records.name: Cole C. Watson
records.summary: Cole C. Watson passed away unexpectedly.
records.body:
The unexpected passing of Cole has left his family, friends, and loved ones in profound shock, still grappling with the heartbreaking loss. He led a vibrant life and had so much more to experience. His departure is a tragedy that has come far too soon. We will always remember the way he affectionately referred to himself as "big husband."

In light of this devastating news, the family kindly requests privacy as they navigate through this difficult time. Cole is survived by his wife Ariel, his children Alex, Atlas and Alyianna, his mother Celeste, his father Chad, and family members Anthony, Byshop, Celeste, and Cody.

records.name: Marion L. Schultz
records.summary: Marion L. Schultz, age 96, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on December 17, 2025.
records.body:
Marion was born on January 15, 1929, in Wausau, to Carl and Mabel (Hanson) Hummel. She was one of six children. Marion's early years were lived in Wausau until the family moved to the countryside, now known as Weston. She graduated from Wausau Senior High School in 1947 followed by Central State Teacher College to earn a diploma to teach in a rural school. Marion received a BA and Master's degrees in education from University of Wisconsin, Stevens Point. A teaching position took Marion to Green Bay where she met Elmer Schultz of Door County. They wed on August 9th, 1958, at Peace Church in Schofield. The happy couple were blessed with the birth of two sons, Carl and David. Sadly, Elmer passed away too soon on May 1st, 1961.

Marion taught preschool and elementary age students for 27 years in Bruce, Marshfield, Green Bay and lastly in Wausau. She was a proud member of the WREA, MCREA, Delta Kappa Gamma Society International and the Gamma chapter of Wisconsin. Over the years, Marion was active at Pilgrim Lutheran Church from being a den mother to Troop 465, Homme Home Rep, and lastly as a Busy Bee Quilter. In the community, Marion served on the Wausau YWCA Solo Parents group and was a long-time member of the YWCA Monday Morning Readers Book Club. Marion's grandfather, Carl Hummel, Sr., was the artist that designed and created the City Seal of Wausau and gifted it to the city in 1913. She enjoyed playing bridge, baking the family traditional German Christmas Stollen (a tradition that continues), her pets and most of all spoiling her five grandchildren.

Marion will be dearly missed and is survived by her two sons Carl (Pat) Schultz, Oconomowoc, WI and their children Tom (Angie) and Becca: David (Janice) Schultz, of Wetmore, Michigan, and their children Mollie (Kevin), Elsie (Jay), Sarah (Kyle). She is further survived by nine great-grandchildren: Luca, Lily, Max, Andrew, Will, Kit, Isla, Archer, (plus one more on the way!), and many nieces and nephews. She was preceded in death by her husband Elmer, her parents Carl and Mabel Hummel, sister Eleanor (LaVell) Berg, and brothers Carl, John (Bernadette), William, and Bert (Betty) Hummel.

A Memorial Service will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, January 3, 2026, at Pilgrim Lutheran Church, 709 Weston Ave, Wausau. Lay minister Eugene Viegut will preside. Visitation will be held from 9:00 a.m. until the time of the service at church. To continue to share memories, a reception will follow the service at church. Memorials may be directed to Pilgrim Lutheran Church and Forest Park Village where Marion resided since 2015.

Marion will be laid to rest next to Elmer, at Little Sister Cemetery, Sister Bay, WI, in the spring of 2026.

records.name: Mark W. Thompson
records.summary: Mark W. Thompson, age 73, of Wausau, died on December 15, 2025.
records.body:
Mark entered the world in Eau Claire, Wi, son of William and Alyce Thompson, on July 3, 1952. In his teen years, he moved to Neenah and later to Palatine, IL. Mark attended Valparaiso University, Indiana, was a member of Tau Kappa Epsilon (TKE) fraternity and graduated with a degree in Civil Engineering.

It was at the University of Valparaiso that Mark met his wife and life partner, Susan. Together they shared 54 years of adventure, hardship, love, and faith in their Lord and Savior, Jesus Christ. They were a great team.

Mark's work ethic, engineering bent, and desire to create manifested early in his life as he always had a project going. In childhood, he developed an elaborate model railroad track with various village scenes. He was a member of Young Life and if there was something to be designed or built, Mark was the guy. He designed and built homecoming floats and when Susan and Mark married while in college, he built furniture and cabinets to outfit their student housing apartment. He was just born with that gift and ability.

After college, Mark and Susan returned to Wisconsin and made their home in Wausau. Mark was a civil engineer at Becker Hoppe for many years and then got an itch to get away from the creating phase and take a more hands-on approach to his trade by becoming the DPW for the then Town of Weston. In 1997, Mark started his own consulting firm, Marathon Technical Services. He was a man of integrity, worked hard and was dedicated to providing quality service at a fair price. Mark always gave more than expected.

As Mark pondered retirement, he began overseeing building projects and donating his engineering services. He was project manager for two major building projects at Covenant Community Presbyterian Church and most recently, project manager for the Bridge Street Mission conversion of the Pied Piper Shoe Factory into a haven for recovery, community support and fellowship. Mark worked tirelessly on these labors of love.

Mark was a pillar in his church, in the community and in his family. He was beloved husband to Susan for 52 years; loving father to Bradley, Andy and Michael Thompson (Holly); grandfather to Dalton Wierzba and Aria Pickering Gross (Parker); UM (Uncle Mark) to many nieces, nephews, and great nieces and nephews. Brother to Paul Thompson (Pam) Louisville, KY and Chris McCormick (Jim) Rolling Meadows, IL; Brother-in-law to Linda and Tony Suminski, New Berlin, WI, Perry and Marge Anderson, Three Lakes WI; dear friend to Tina Spiegel; and Foster Dad to several young men.

The family gathering place – located on Whitefish Lake in Three Lakes, WI, was the site of Chef Mark's fabulous meals and his pancake breakfasts. He was patient with young fisher people, taking them out on his pontoon boat, baiting, untangling and unhooking fish and other fisherman! And UM was the guy who loaded all the chairs into his pickup truck and headed into town early on the 4th of July to set up seats for "the crew".

Our hearts will grieve the loss of our beloved husband, father, grandfather, brother, uncle and friend everywhere we go. He was a man of few words, but his impact was powerful. "In the same way, let your light shine before others, so that they may see your good works and give glory to your Father in heaven" Matthew 5:16 He poured his love out to others in works: in the meals he prepared, in the small acts of kindness he did in anonymity, and in the large undertakings he oversaw for the benefit of the community. He was a humble, unassuming servant.

He worked tirelessly his entire life for the benefit of others and put his wishes on hold. Two years ago, he began to get back to building another model railroad layout that spans a 20'X12' space that he designed and built specifically for that purpose. While trying to recuperate at the Mayo hospital Mark's goal was to be well enough to finish his train room. Sadly, that was not to be.

We now envision Mark helping to layout plans for the New Heaven and New Earth. Mark bore much fruit in his lifetime on earth. He will surely be busy adding rooms to His Father's house. Who knows, maybe there will even be a train room.

Rest well, faithful servant and God Bless you, our beloved one, until we meet again.

A Memorial Service in Mark's honor will be held at 3:00PM on Sunday, January 4, 2026, at Covenant Community Presbyterian Church with Pastor Jim Gates presiding. Visitation will be from 1:00PM until the time of service at the church.

Memorials in Mark's memory may got to Covenant Community Presbyterian Church's "Feed the Flock" campaign or the Bridge Street Mission.

records.name: Ruby F. Fandrey
records.summary: Ruby F. Fandrey, age 87, of Birnamwood, died on December 20, 2025.
records.body:
Ruby was born on August 27, 1938, in Big Falls, the daughter of Henry and Joan (Schroeder) Beyersdorf. After Henry died, Joan married Elmer Bulgrin, and Ruby was very close with her stepfather.

On May 31, 1958, Ruby was united in marriage to Albert Fandrey at St. John Lutheran Church, Wittenberg. He preceded her in death on June 24, 2011.

Ruby and Albert shared a life of hard work and partnership, farming side by side for 30 years. They especially loved polka dancing together.

Ruby worked at Homme Youth and Family for 38 years as a cook and housekeeper.

Family meant everything to Ruby. She cherished family gatherings and time spent with loved ones, whether at home or at the family cabin in Watersmeet, which held many special memories. She also enjoyed traveling, exploring destinations across the United States and Europe, and taking fun trips to the casino with family.

In her younger years, Ruby enjoyed playing softball and shooting pool. Throughout her life, she found peace and happiness in gardening, tending to both flowers and vegetables, and loved watching birds. She also enjoyed cooking and baking.

Ruby had a special place in her heart for her cat, Mitsy, who brought her comfort and companionship.

Ruby is survived by three children, Mike Fandrey, Cheryl Zeinert, and Kevin Fandrey; seven grandchildren, Krystle (Cole) Spiegelberg, April (Brandon) Resch, Shannon (Hailey) Fandrey, Cristy Maltbey, Shawn (Tanya Igl) Zeinert, Andrea (Dave) Rebstock, Kyle (Katelyn Cheyanne) Fandrey; 12 great-grandchildren, Colby, Aubrey and Evelyn Resch, Emersyn Fandrey, Logan Maltbey, Ellie and Kaitlyn Clough, Tatum Igl, Jayden and Mason Zeinert, Vincent and Ali Rebstock; siblings, Bruce (Gayle) Beyersdorf, James (Shirley) Beyersdorf, Joyce (Ron) Frank, David Beyersdorf, Vilas Beyersdorf, Bonnie (Anton) Bender, Gale (April) Beyersdorf, Gene (Diane) Beyersdorf and Milan (Nancy) Beyersdorf; brothers and sisters-in-law, Deborah Beyersdorf, Herbert (Noreen) Fandrey, Marvin Fandrey and Adeline Fandrey and many nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends.

Ruby was preceded in death by her parents, Henry and Joan; step-father, Elmer; husband, Albert; brother, Dale Beyersdorf and many brothers and sisters-in-law.

A Funeral Service will be held at 11 AM, on Saturday, December 27, 2025, at Trinity Lutheran Church, Birnamwood. Vicar Jim Hartleben will officiate. Burial will be in St. Paul Lutheran Cemetery, in the Town of Almon. Visitation will be on Saturday, from 9 AM until the time of service at the church.

records.name: Shirley A. Krayeck
records.summary: Shirley A. Krayeck, age 78, of Stevens Point, passed away peacefully on December 18, 2025.
records.body:
Born on February 7, 1947, in Stevens Point, Wisconsin to the late Frank and Teckla (Bronk) Jamrosz, Shirley was the definition of feisty and sassy. She was a woman who let you know exactly what she thought, but at her core, she was motivated by a deep, selfless love for others.

Shirley's greatest pride was the life she built with her husband, Roger James Krayecki. Their bond was never more evident than during these final five months, as Roger served as her most devoted caretaker, remaining by her side through every challenge. Her children—Kim, Chris, and Carla—were her heart, and she loved Jody and Scott as her own. While she worked over the years at the Holiday Inn, Copps, and helping with bookwork at Kim's Barrel Inn, the only job that ever truly mattered to her was being a full-time grandma and babysitter for her grandkids and great grandkids that she adored and spoiled rotten.

She loved her home along the Wisconsin River, enjoying warm days outdoors, watching the Game Show Network, and listening to Elvis. Whether she was chauffeuring grandkids, traveling to softball games, or attending dance recitals and musicals, Shirley was always their biggest supporter. She was a daily fixture on the phone with her family and cherished every visit with her brothers and sisters.

Shirley is survived by her devoted husband, Roger; her children, Kim (Jody Kluck) Krayecki, Chris Krayecki, and Carla (Scott) Miskowski; siblings Ernie (Sandi) Jamrosz, Ronald (Carol) Jamrosz, and Rosie (Bob)Kieliszewski; her grandchildren, Brooke (Ben) Mahoney, Morgan (Lance) Aeby, Chelsea (Greg Gale) Krayecki, Taylor (TJ) Krayecki, Kyera, and Kaybre Krayecki, as well as many "additional" grandkids she cared for. Her legacy continues in her great-grandchildren, Quinn, Grant (Mahoney), and Miles (Aeby). She was preceded in death by her parents, her sister Diane, and brothers James (Jimmy) and Frank Jr.

A Celebration of Life will be held on her upcoming birthday, February 7, 2026, at the Moose Lodge in Stevens Point with details to follow. Shirley taught us all the power of what it means to love deeply and put family first; she will continue to shine bright in our memories.

records.name: Susan R. Welsh
records.summary: Susan R. Welsh, age 87, of Harshaw, passed away peacefully on December 19, 2025.
records.body:
Sue was born in Wausau, WI on December 7, 1938, to the late Ellis and Martha (Butenhoff) Allds.

Shortly after high school, Sue took a job at "Ma Bell" (Bell Systems), where her and a co-worker, Vicki, plotted to leave Wausau and move to California. In the spring of 1960, Sue and Vicki moved to Whittier, California, where she met another Wausau native, William (Bill) Welsh (Vicki's brother), who after a whirlwind romance she married on June 4th, 1960. They had just celebrated their 60th wedding anniversary when Bill passed away in 2020.

Sue was a stay-at-home mom while Bill was in the military. They lived across the world, including California, Guam, Oklahoma, Spain and Germany, while building their family. Soon after Bill and Sue returned to the Wausau area to raise their family in 1973, Sue took a job at Fiskars and worked there until her retirement in 2001.

Sue and Bill lived in Harshaw after they both retired. She loved being in the Northwoods, working in her yard, feeding the birds and walking on her land with the puppies. She very much enjoyed watching any sporting event – especially college women's basketball, as well as making pies, breads and treats for her family, the church and for the Snowshoe Baseball raffles. Sue loved to spend time spoiling her Grandchildren and Great-grandchildren, and she especially enjoyed attending their school activities and sports events. Sue loved the monthly get togethers she had with her fellow Fiskars retirees. The friendship she had with them was very precious to her.

Sue is survived by her children Christine (Bryan) Kujawa, John (Karen) Welsh and Douglass (Jennifer) Welsh; eight grandchildren, Sasha (Cory) Kesy, Nicholas (Alicia) Schroedel, Austin (Brianna) Welsh, Chloe & Marcus Welsh, Kailey & Kyle Welsh and Riley Welsh; 12 great-grandchildren, Gavin, Emma & Nora Kesy, Natalie & Adalyn Schroedel, Cameron, Drew & Wade Welsh, and Aspen, Zeppelin, Sonny & Stetson Stimac. Sister-in-laws Virginia Allds, Victoria Welsh & Connie Brzezinski, niece's Elizabeth (Brian) Marx, and Tiffany (Jeffery) Voigt.

Sue will be greeted in heaven by her husband Bill, their first child, Mark (2013), her brother Ellis Allds, sister Nancy Heiser and brother-in-law Raymond Heiser, parents Ellis and Martha Allds; and parent-in-laws Charlie and Agnes (Franz) Welsh; Nephew Peter Allds, and Niece Johanna (Bud) Palmer. She will also be welcomed home by her fur babies Tug and Louie, and grand puppies Tia, Maddie and Ariel.

Sue was a fiercely independent and strong-willed woman, and we will miss her so much. Her spunky spirit and quick wit were traits that everyone loved about her. A Memorial Service in Sue's honor will be held at 2:00PM on Saturday, January 10, 2026, at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Ave. Weston, Wisconsin, with Pastor Lance Hoelscher officiating. Visitation will be held from 12:00Noon until the time of service. Sue will be laid to rest with a private interment in June at the Northwoods National Cemetery in Harshaw, Wisconsin.

records.name: William E. Fellner
records.summary: William E. Fellner, age 69, of Schofield, died on December 15, 2025.
records.body:
William was born on February 12, 1956 in Libertyville, IL the son of Frederick Alois and Lorraine Frances (Dolejs) Fellner.

William proudly served his country in the United States Marine Corps. He served from 1973 to 1975 and attained the rank of Corporal.

William is survived by his siblings, Patricia (Dan) Cox, Melanie (Michael) Gurath, Diane Schooley, Frederick J. (Lydia) Fellner, Karl R. Fellner and many nieces and nephews.

William was preceded in death by his parents and a brother-in-law, Dell Schooley.

A Military Honors graveside service will be held at 1:00 PM on Tuesday, December 23, 2025 at Elmwood Cemetery, Antigo.

records.name: Allen D. Burmeister
records.summary: Allen "Al" Burmeister, age 71, of Little Chicago, passed away on December 20, 2025.
records.body:
Allen "Al" Burmeister, age 71, of Little Chicago, Wisconsin, passed away on December 20, 2025, at the Aspirus Hospice House in Wausau.

He was born on March 11, 1954, in Wausau, to Barbara (Williamson) and Henry Burmeister. Allen farmed alongside his parents in his early years, and his entrepreneurial nature later led him to start several businesses including ginseng farming.

Allen enjoyed the simple pleasures of life including good food and good stories. He loved playing sheepshead and dominoes with family and friends, spending time visiting with others, and attending live music whenever he could. Above all he loved his daughters and he cherished time spent with them and their mother, Mary, with whom he remained close. He valued conversation and community, and he will be remembered for the time he shared with those around him.

Survivors include his daughters, Breanna Burmeister and Leah Burmeister; former spouse, Mary Kickbusch; his sister, Helen (Robert) Kuklinski; sisters-in-law, Suzanne and Rene'e Burmeister; and nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents and his brothers, Gary and Robert Burmeister.

A service will be held at 12:00 p.m. on Saturday, January 10, 2026, at St. Peter Lutheran Church (Little Chicago), 135237 County Road A, Marathon. Visitation will take place prior to the service beginning at 9:30 a.m., with a meal to follow the service.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made to St. Peter Lutheran Church or The Neighbors' Place.

We are especially grateful for the compassionate care by Lucy and Aspirus Home Health and that of friend George.

records.name: Charles Johanning
records.summary: Charles "Chuck" Johanning, age 77, of Wausau, passed away on December 15, 2025.
records.body:
Charles "Chuck" Johanning, 77, a beloved husband, son, brother, uncle, and faithful servant of Jesus Christ, entered into the presence of his Savior on December 15, 2025.

Chuck was born in Racine, Wisconsin, to Wallace and Ruth (Bohnsack) Johanning on March 22, 1948. He was raised with a strong foundation in faith and education, attending Zion Lutheran School through the eighth grade before graduating from Wausau High School. He later attended the University of Wisconsin-Madison, where the Lord profoundly changed his life. It was during his time at UW-Madison, through the Navigators campus ministry, that Chuck first received Jesus Christ as his Lord and Savior–a decision that shaped the rest of his life.

Chuck's love for Christ was sincere and enduring. He was a faithful member of Wausau Bible Church for many years and then Immanuel Baptist Church, where he continued to worship, serve, and grow in his walk with the Lord. Chuck had a deep love for reading and studying God's Word. He looked forward to his times at the pizza place where he studied the Word with his longtime friends. In his own quiet way, he shared the Word with others.

In 1992 Chuck met Rita Weiske at a bible study in Wausau. They began dating and were married on June 14th, 1997. Together they shared a life rooted in faith, commitment, and steadfast devotion. God blessed them with many opportunities to travel to special events and to spend precious time with family and friends. They also served together on the Board of Child Evangelism Fellowship for a number of years.

Chuck was known for his steady work ethic and dedication. He was employed for 31 years as a payroll administrator at Van Ert Electric of Wausau, where he was respected for his integrity, reliability, and attention to detail. Earlier in life, Chuck worked as a groundskeeper at Pine Grove Cemetery, which will now become his final resting place.

Chuck was an honest, kindhearted man who enjoyed the simple things in life–gardening, classical music, outdoor grilling, the old-time gospel hymns, decorating the Christmas tree, golfing with his friends, and following the Badgers, Brewers, and Packers. He had a passion for bowling and in his younger years he would bowl every night of the week if he could. He was a student of the game and one of the top bowlers in the area back in the day. He continued to bowl in league play until 2024.

Chuck is survived by his loving wife of 28 years, Rita; sisters-in-law, Sue (Gerald) Johanning, Carol (Leo) Tietz, Gail (Tony) Torelli, Sandra (John) Resop; brother-in-law, Ronald Weiske; nieces, Laura (Tim) Nye, Linda Johanning, Melinda (Jerry) McNally, Adrienne Tietz, Rachel (Dimitrios) Vatakis, Audra (Jay) Thorsten, Amber Torelli, Gina Torelli; nephews, Jimmy (Michelle) Johanning, Andrew (Brittany) Torelli, Jordan (Chelsea) Resop; along with many grand nieces and nephews, cousins, and friends.

Chuck was preceded in death by parents, Wallace and Ruth (Bohnsack) Johanning, parents-in-law, Irvin and Betty (Nigbor) Weiske; brothers, Donald Johanning, Gerald (Sue) Johanning; sister-in-law, Jo Ellen Weiske; niece LeAnn Johanning; nephews John Johanning and Dan Johanning.

A visitation will be held at Immanuel Baptist Church in Wausau on January 2nd from 9:30-10:30 a.m., followed by the funeral service officiated by Pastor Rob Love. A burial service will take place at Pine Grove Cemetery in Wausau. Luncheon will follow at the church.

Chuck was an active supporter and advocate of Child Evangelism Fellowship. Memorial gifts may be made in Chuck's memory to CEF of Wisconsin, 635 Science Dr. Suite 213, Madison WI 53711.

records.name: Daniel M. Krautkramer
records.summary: Daniel "Krauty" M. Krautkramer, age 60, of Marathon, passed away on December 20, 2025.
records.body:
Daniel "Krauty" M. Krautkramer, 60, Marathon, passed away peacefully at his home surrounded by his family and friends on Saturday, December 20, 2025, under the care of Aspirus at Home Hospice.

He was born May 20, 1965 in Marathon, son of the late Arnold and Lorraine (Hornung) Krautkramer. Dan's first marriage was to Kristi Jean Kitsembel on July 14, 1990 at St. Matthew Lutheran Church in Marathon. She preceded him in death on February 8, 2015. Later, he married Lisa Ann Bartz on June 25, 2022 at Rib River Ballroom in Marathon. She survives.

Dan began his working career with Graebel Van Lines in Wausau and later began driving for Coca-Cola on March 28, 1994, where he worked for 30 years until his retirement on June 14, 2024. Among his favorite pastimes, he enjoyed hunting, fishing, cornhole and was an avid Green Bay Packers fan, Wisconsin Badgers fan and Milwaukee Brewers fan. Dan always loved a good party while jamming out to music and spending time with family and friends. Dan's large personality, infectious laugh and giving heart will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved him.

Survivors include his wife, Lisa Krautkramer, Marathon; three children, Stephanie (Joshua) Louis, Kronenwetter, Amanda (Austin) Jaeger, Sun Prairie and Randy Krautkramer, Marathon; four grandchildren, Elaina and Caleb Louis and Leon and Milo Jaeger; his mother-in-law, Barbara Kitsembel, Wausau; eight siblings, Arnold (Mary) Krautkramer, Marathon, Sheri (Dan) Lepak, Wausau, John (Marie) Krautkramer, Marathon, Waine (Candy) Krautkramer, Marvin (Beverly) Krautkramer, Edgar, George (Rhonda) Krautkramer, Wausau, Ronald (Sheri) Krautkramer, Athens and Gary Krautkramer, Kronenwetter; his step-children, Joshua (Renee) Merrigan, Jennifer (Blake) Young and Jeremy Merrigan; his step-grandchildren, Ethan, Autumn, Greyson, Logan and Karsyn; brothers and sisters-in-law, Lani (Brad) Stiede, Jeff (Brenda) Kitsembel, Russell (Amy) Kitsembel and Amy (Jason) Westfall; and many nieces and nephews.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, December 27, 2025 at St. Mary Catholic Church, Marathon. Rev. Msgr. Joseph Diermeier will preside. Burial will be in the parish cemetery. Visitation will be on Friday from 4:00 until 7:00 p.m. at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home in Marathon and again on Saturday from 9:30 a.m. until time of Mass at the church. There will be a rosary service at 7:00 p.m. Friday evening at the funeral home.

A special thank you to Dr. Leon, Aspirus at Home Hospice, especially Kari, Shawn and Alicia, Father Joe, Pastor Mark Gass and the Redeemer family and to all his family and friends who took time from their busy schedules to stop in for a visit, call with a hello or sent a text saying I'm thinking of you. He felt truly blessed to have all of you in his life.

In lieu of flowers, a memorial donation can be made to the Surewood Forest Wishes for Kids, W4240 Pine Pointe Lane, Tomahawk, WI 54487 in memory of Dan.

records.name: Gene E. Sorenson
records.summary: Gene E. Sorenson, age 78, of Rothschild, passed away on December 22, 2025.
records.body:
Gene E. Sorenson, 78, of Rothschild, WI, passed away on December 22, 2025, at Weston Memory Care, surrounded by the love of his family and under the care of St. Croix Hospice. He was known for his legendary patience, sense of humor, quick wit, humility and leaves behind a legacy of teaching, hard work, love for family, and cherished memories.

He was born on February 26, 1947, in Wausau, son of the late Everette and Gladys (Thompson) Sorenson. Gene was a graduate of DC Everest High School, class of 1965. A proud veteran, he served in the United States Navy from 1965 to 1969 as a radio operator on the USS Purdy, USS Charles Roan, and USS Intrepid. He was honored to participate in the Never Forgotten Honor Flight trip.

On August 7, 1971, he married the love of his life, Lou Ann (Zahrt) Sorenson at St. John Lutheran Church, Town of Easton, embarking on a partnership that would last for decades. Gene dedicated 35 years of his professional life to Weyerhauser/Domtar before retiring. Upon retirement, he worked for Jarp Industries part time for several years.

Gene loved the outdoors. He had a wealth of interests including hunting, fishing, logging, bowling, golfing, and watching NASCAR. He also had an artistic side, including writing poetry, drawing, and reading, while also dabbling in upholstery, showcasing his diverse talents and passions. Anything worth doing was worth doing well.

He is survived by his beloved wife, Lou Ann Sorenson; daughters, Cara (Jason) Abt, Penny (Ryan) Mootz, and Gina (Chris) Woodward; grandchildren, Cole (Xianna) Abt, Bekah (Tristan) Carlson, Zach (fiancée Britt) Mootz, Cade Abt, and Vanessa Woodward; great-granddaughter, Eloise Abt; siblings, Judy Seier, Carla (Pat) Sanders, Patti (Ray) Schmidt, and Brad Sorenson; mother-in-law, Shirley Zahrt; brothers and sisters-in-law, Roger Zahrt, David (Deb) Zahrt, Robyn (Kurt) Gacke, Marlene Sorenson; and many nieces and nephews.

In addition to his parents, he was further preceded in death by his father-in-law, George Zahrt; brother, Scott Sorenson; sister-in-law, Roslynn Zahrt; brother-in-law, Russ Seier; and nephew, Wesley Sorenson.

Memorial service will be held at 11:00 A.M. Monday, December 29, 2025, at Mount Olive Lutheran Church, 6205 Alderson St. in Weston, Wisconsin. Rev. Lance Hoelscher will officiate. Military Honors conducted by the American Legion Montgomery-Plant-Dudley Post 10 will take place following the service at the church. Visitation will be from 9:00 A.M. until the time of service on Monday at the church.

The family would like to thank the staff of Weston Memory Care as well as St. Croix Hospice for the care shown to Gene in is final days.

records.name: James Erdman Sr.
records.summary: James "Jim" Erdman Sr., age 68, of Wausau, passed away on December 16, 2025.
records.body:
James "Jim" Erdman Sr. went to be with our Lord and Savior in heaven on December 16, 2025 at the age of 68.

Jim was born on November 29, 1957 to Rev. Leonard and Pauline Erdman in Red Bluff, California. Due to Leonard's career in the ministry, the family moved around until settling in Wausau, Wisconsin. Jim graduated from D.C. Everest High School in 1975. He served in the United States Air Force from 1976 to 1980. Jim spent his career at Wisconsin Public Service as a high voltage electrician and safety trainer until his retirement in 2013.

Jim enjoyed the great outdoors and moved up to the cabin in Woodruff last year. His hobbies included hunting, fishing, carpentry, singing, and especially spending time with his family and friends. His pride and joy were his children and grandchildren. Jim was also very proud to be a veteran and was active in many veteran groups including the American Legion, the Man of Honor Society, and Disabled American Veterans.

Jim is survived by his three children, Alicia (Phil) Davister, Alison (Mike) Waldner, and James Erdman Jr., and his five grandchildren, Ella Erdman, Aiden Waldner, Sawyer Waldner, Jack Davister, and Hank Davister. He is also survived by one sister, Mary (Steve) Shuler, three brothers, John (Coleen) Erdman, Tom (Joan) Erdman, and Pete (Lori) Erdman, and many nieces, nephews, relatives, and friends.

Jim was preceded in death by his parents.

The funeral proceedings will take place on Saturday, January 3rd, 2026 at St. John's Lutheran Church, 164923 County Road Z, Wausau, WI 54403. Visitation will be held from 9 am to 11 am with the funeral service to follow. Burial will take place at a national military cemetery at a later date.

records.name: James M. McHale
records.summary: James "Jim" McHale, age 88, of Wausau, passed away on December 20, 2025.
records.body:
James "Jim" McHale, age 88, of Wausau, passed away on December 20, 2025.

He is survived by his wife, Margaret "Maggie" McHale; his children, Daniel McHale and Caitlin Mack (Andrew); his grandchildren, Elizabeth and Benjamin Mack; as well as other loving relatives and many dear friends. He was preceded in death by his parents, Freda and Vern McHale, and his brother, John McHale.

Jim was born on May 2, in Antigo, Wisconsin. As a young boy, he cherished time spent fishing and golfing at the family cabin on Bass Lake. At the age of 12, Jim experienced the sudden loss of his father. His mother, Freda, remained in Antigo, supported by close friends and the St. John's Parish community. Jim excelled academically and graduated from Antigo High School in 1955.

He went on to attend the University of Wisconsin–Madison, earning a bachelor's degree in History in 1959, a master's degree in 1962, and a Ph.D. in American Foreign Policy in 1970. While at Madison, under The Reserve Forces Act of 1955, Jim served in the Wisconsin Army Reserves from 1958-1966 and received the rank of Captain. He was also active in campus life and was a member of the Sigma Nu fraternity.

Jim began his teaching career at UW – Fox Valley Center in 1964 and, in 1969, accepted a faculty position at the University of Wisconsin–Green Bay. While at UWGB, he directed the Native American Studies program for two years and contributed to numerous historical publications on Wisconsin history.

In 1974, Jim and Maggie moved to Lily, Wisconsin, where they learned to operate a sheep farm. During this time, Jim transitioned careers and became a stockbroker with Smith Barney, while continuing to teach evening courses at UW–Green Bay.

Jim's greatest passions were cheering on the Wisconsin Badgers at Camp Randall and fly fishing on the Wolf and Hunting Rivers. Jim and Maggie also shared a love of travel and spent many years exploring South America, Asia, Africa, the Middle East, and Europe. They also spent 30 years enjoying summers with family and friends at their cabin in Three Lakes.

In 2002, Jim and Maggie sold the farm and moved to Wausau to be closer to family. Jim spent his final years surrounded by close friends and loved ones. He will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved him.

In accordance with his wishes, a private burial will be held.

records.name: Jesse C. Frederick
records.summary: Jesse Charles Frederick, age 50, passed away on December 16, 2025.
records.body:
Jesse Charles Frederick, age 50, passed away on December 16, 2025. He was born on March 13, 1975.

Jesse was the beloved son of the late Patrick Frederick and Susan VanDerGeest.

A proud "Treky" through and through, Jesse had a lifelong love of Star Trek and often wrote imaginative short stories where he cast himself as the fleet commander of the Star-Ship Enterprise. His creativity knew no bounds, and his curiosity about the world—and the universe beyond it—was endless. Jesse was deeply passionate about space, airplanes, and rockets, and he delighted in learning and sharing what fascinated him.

Jesse loved gaming and was known for being very social. He genuinely enjoyed sitting and chatting with people, connecting easily with those around him. His conversations were filled with enthusiasm, wonder, and warmth. Recently, Jesse had been learning to cook with his mom, Susan, who proudly shared that he was getting pretty good at it.

Jesse is survived by his loving mother, Susan VanDerGeest; his stepfather Robert VanDerGeest; his daughter, Kaitlyn Granac; his brothers, Jon and Aaron Frederick; his stepsisters, Sheila (Mark) Winfield, Cortney (Tim) Swan, and Casey VanDerGeest; and many cherished nieces and nephews.

In addition to his father, he was also preceded in death by his sister, Heather Frederick.

Jesse will be remembered for his vivid imagination, his passion for the universe, and the joy he found in connecting with others. His presence, curiosity, and heartfelt enthusiasm will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved him.

A celebration of life will be held at a later date.

records.name: John G. Palutke
records.summary: John Gottlieb Palutke, age 90, of Wausau, passed away on December 23, 2025.
records.body:
John Gottlieb Palutke of Wausau, WI, passed away and went to his heavenly home on Tuesday, December 23, 2025 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital at the age of 90.

He was born on January 11, 1935 in Watertown, WI to immigrant German parents, Albert and Christine Palutke. He attended rural country schools near Wilton, WI and high school in Wilton, then graduated from Central High in La Crosse, WI in 1952. He attended La Crosse State College and Northwestern Bible College in Minneapolis, graduating in 1957. He then attended Bethel Seminary in St. Paul, MN, graduating in 1960.

John married Janis Porter at Trinity Independent Church in North Minneapolis, MN on June 1, 1957. They adopted three children-Cindy, Jeff and Greg, all born in Washington State. Together, they served five pastorates in 36 years, all with the Baptist General Conference (Converge). Churches served were in Puyallup and Custer, WA, Webster, WI, Negaunee, MI and Rhinelander, WI. After retiring, John served five interim ministries in Cumberland, Augusta and Bayfield, WI, as well as Escanaba and Menominee, MI.

In 2004, John and Jan moved to their retirement home near Hurley, WI, where they enjoyed gardening and camping. John loved to travel. In 1990 to 2001, he visited his relatives in Germany and traveled throughout Europe. He and Jan visited all 50 states, plus most of the Providences of Canada.

In 2013, John and Jan sold their country home near Hurley and moved into Cedar Creek Apartments in Rothschild, WI to be closer to a good medical facility and family. In 2016, when Jan was moved into North Central Health Care to receive medical assistance, John moved into nearby Sturgeon Bluff Apartments in Wausau, WI. He was a member of Bethany Baptist Church in Schofield.

John enjoyed reading, especially the Bible and doing Bible studies. He loved people-often visiting them in their homes, churches, nursing homes, jails and homeless shelters; also working with children in church programs and Big Brothers/Big Sisters. In his leisure time, John enjoyed biking, hiking, snowshoeing and cross country skiing.

He is survived by his three children, Cindy (Dan) Mattson, Prescott, WI, Jeff Palutka, Wausau and Greg (Dawn) Palutke, Morrison, CO; nine grandchildren, Lauren (T.J.) Sohre, Zach (Ali) Mattson, Dani (Dan) Burmeister, Sara Rose (Derek) Bolt, Jonathan (Lindsay) Palutke, Cory (Taylor) Ohlson, Chelsea (Steve) Jaco, Tricia (Kevin) Unger and Addie (David) Parkinson; many great-grandchildren and his sister, Ruth Faulhaber, La Crosse, WI. John was preceded in death by his wife, Jan in 2017, his father, Albert in 1994, his mother, Christine in 2000 and his grandson, Sean Schwartz in 2021.

He leaves us with this quote: "I commit myself to God's care in His love for me and trusting in His salvation purchased for me through Christ's suffering and death. I leave those who survive me the comfort of knowing that I have died in this faith and have now joined my Lord in eternal glory."

It is my prayer that all of you who read this will acknowledge "I am a sinner and ask Jesus to forgive me of my sins and believe that Jesus Christ died and rose again to give me eternal life in heaven with Jesus and all those who have gone on before." I really want to see each of you again in Glory.

Funeral services will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Tuesday, December 30, 2025 at Bethany Baptist Church in Schofield. Visitation will be held from 10:00 a.m. until time of service at the church. Burial will be in the Newbold Memorial Cemetery, Rhinelander.

Memorials may be designated to Bethany Baptist Church, the Alzheimer's Foundation or Aspirus Cardiology, Wausau.

records.name: Lawrence Stephen Kober
records.summary: Lawrence Stephen Kober, age 78, of Milwaukee, passed away on December 18, 2025.
records.body:
Lawrence Stephen Kober (Larry), age 78, passed away on December 18, 2025. He was born on September 13, 1947 in Milwaukee WI to Lucille and Henry Kober. He married Carole Stoll on September 19, 1970.

Lawrence is survived by his wife Carole, 4 children Dawn (David) Metzger, Brenda (Tim) Fons, Tina(TJ) Lesnick and Sean (Robin) Kober. Grandchildren Samantha and Macey Fons, Travis Lesnick, Ian and Lillian Metzger, Kirsten, Alayna and Owen Kober, great granddaughter Emerson Kober, brothers Jim(Geraldine), Michael (Debra) and Pat, Sister-in-law: Michelle (Mark) Semmann other relatives and friends.

Lawrence loved his family, gardening landscaping, and wood working.

He was preceded in death by his parents and brother Jerome.

A private Burial will be held at a later date.

Lawrence's favorite charity was Aspirus Hospice Care.

records.name: Rachel L. Buhr
records.summary: Rachel Lee Buhr, age 42, passed away on December 6, 2025.
records.body:
Rachel Lee Buhr, born May 16, 1983, passed away on December 6, 2025.

Rachel was a creative soul who found joy in the little things—collectibles like Legos, rocks, and small dolls that reflected her curiosity and love of detail. She was incredibly crafty and took pride in making things with her hands, especially when those creations could be given to someone she cared about.

Helping others was at the heart of who Rachel was. Whether she was fixing something, teaching someone how to do it themselves, or simply showing up when she was needed, she did so with patience and kindness. She had a heart of gold, even if she was a little rough around the edges. Rachel often put on a brave front, always placing others before herself and making everyone believe everything was okay—even when the world was crumbling around her.

Rachel is survived by her children, Dakota (Emma), Ivan, and Atticus; her grandson, Ezra; her mother, Mary Buhr; her sisters, Pam, Linda, Laurie, Lou, and Amy; her brothers, Randy and Richard; and many beloved nieces and nephews.

She was preceded in death by her father, Ron Buhr.

Rachel will be remembered for her generosity, resilience, creativity, and the quiet strength she carried for those she loved.

A Mass of Christian burial will be held at 11:00 am on January 9, 2026, at St Agnes Catholic Parish 6101 Zinser Street, Weston. Visitation will take place from 10:00 am until the time of the service at the church.

records.name: Allan J. Babl
records.summary: Allan J. Babl, 81, of Wausau, passed away on December 22, 2025.
records.body:
Allan "Al" J. Babl, 81, passed away Monday, December 22, 2025, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born April 11, 1944, in Wausau, son of the late George and Rose (Corazalla) Babl. In 1980, he married Celeste Szymanski at St. Michael Catholic Church, Wausau.

Al graduated from D.C. Everest High School in 1962, earning multiple football scholarships. He chose to attend the University of Wisconsin-Stout, where he completed his education and earned a master's degree. After college, Al moved to Arlington Heights, Illinois, where he spent several years teaching before returning to Wisconsin.

Back home, Al worked alongside his father at George Babl & Sons, then went on to a career as a salesman with Drott Manufacturing. He later became a realtor, working with Century 21 and eventually retiring from First Weber.

Al held a pilot's license and had a lifelong love of flying and travel. He also enjoyed seeing the countryside from his BMW motorcycle. In his younger years, Al was proud to be one of the first members of the ski patrol at Rib Mountain. His many interests included hunting, martial arts, especially Aikido and a playful sense of humor that shone through at Halloween, when he delighted in decorating and dressing up to scare trick-or-treaters.

A devoted hockey dad, Al loved traveling to watch his son Tyler play and was the team's enthusiastic amateur photographer, capturing countless team photos over the years. He was also an avid golfer, with others often joking that he spent as much time searching for golf balls as playing the game.

Survivors include his wife, Celeste; son, Tyler (Cindy Sherfinski) Babl; sister, Christine Gilbreath; sisters-in-law, Sandra Babl, Marta Babl, Carol (Fred) Shindle, Eve (Jack) Keck; brother-in-law, Mark (Lisa) Szymanski; numerous nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends.

Besides his parents, he was preceded in death by brothers, Kenneth, Daniel "Tito", Ronald "Buck", sister, Jean (Tom) Carlson and brother-in-law, Tom Gilbreath.

Funeral service will be 5:00 p.m. Thursday, January 8, 2025, at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth St., Wausau. Visitation will be Thursday from 2:30 p.m. until the time of service all at the Sixth Street funeral home.

In lieu of flowers, a memorial is being established.

The family wishes to express their sincere appreciation to the doctors and nurses at Aspirus Wausau Hospital for the compassionate care given to Al during his time there. Also, to Peterson/Kraemer for their kind guidance during this difficult time.

records.name: Delores L. Brzezinski
records.summary: Delores L. Brzezinski, 89, of Wausau, passed away on December 22, 2025.
records.body:
Delores L. Brzezinski, 89, of Wausau WI passed away at home in her sleep, joining her beloved husband Stanley, son Jeff, and her Savior to eternal joy on December 22, 2025. Released from her struggle with COPD, her breaths no longer labored, she is home and at peace.

She was born April 20, 1936, in Marathon, WI to the late Harry and Odelia Sonnentag. She married Stanley Brzezinski on May 26, 1956.

Delores loved her family deeply and managed to raise hers while also working full time in partnership with Stanely owning and successfully operating Bajet Van Lines for over 35 years. Stanely was the salesman, but Delores was the business brain. She was highly intelligent, a master with numbers, and a very strong independent woman working during the 60's, 70's & 80's in the very male dominated, moving, storage & trucking business. She held her own & through her leadership the business's success provided well for her family. She accomplished this while also cooking, baking & sewing for her busy growing bunch, following her 3 boys all over the state watching them wrestle, play baseball, football & tennis. Delores's family always included a dog or two. Her love for the four-legged kids was lifelong. She often told stories about the best dog ever Buster, and other favorites Pierre & Cocoa.

Delores also liked to have fun. She was an avid card player and participated in many card clubs for over 65 years!! Her last card game just weeks before her passing. She absolutely cherished her lifetime friendships made while playing Crown, Sheepshead & Canasta. She loved spending time with her sisters also playing cards, SHOPPING, and going to the casinos where she used her number skills to often go home a winner. She chatted on the phone often and they still got together for siblings' birthday and Christmas celebrations where Wally joined as well. She was a voracious reader, always a stack of books within her reach. No book was too thick, nor font too small to stop her. She liked historical fiction where she said she enjoyed getting lost in a story but learning something along the way. In recent years, her faith was renewed with an assurance of eternal life and devoted herself to morning devotional readings of God's promises and hope which brought her peace and strength.

A description of Delores would not be complete without including her Milwaukee Brewers fandom. She never missed a game. It brought her so much joy and she was sure she could manage the team better than whomever was in charge at the time! She always challenged herself to learn. At 89, she had a smart phone, apple watch, an Alexa and a Ring doorbell owning more gadgets than many of her kids and grandkids!

Delores is survived by her two sons: Daniel (Karen), Alan (Valerie) Brzezinski, brother Wally Sonnentag, sisters: Rita (Larry) Perrodin, Carol (Dan) Brusky, brother-in-law Alex (Marilyn) Brzezinski, daughter-in-law Rita Kerswill and seven Brzezinski grandchildren: Jonathon, Joshawa, Nikolas, Natalie, Andrew, Danielle & Kyle.

Besides her parents, husband and beloved son Jeffrey, she was preceded in death by sister, Shirley Pospaholla, brother-in-law Walter Pospyhalla & sister-in-law Lucy Sonnentag.

We'd like to give a special thanks to our cousin Terrie. She moved into Delores's beautiful new apartment with her. Delores's greatest fear in her final months was being alone and because of Terrie she never had to be. You are a true Godsend and we will all be eternally grateful for all you did for her.

Services will be held Saturday, January 24th at Peace United Church of Christ in Schofield, WI. The Rev. Vicki Wunch officiating. Visitation will begin at 9:00 am with service starting at 11:00. In lieu of flowers please consider donating to a charity of your choice.

records.name: Patsy M. Resch
records.summary: Patsy M. Resch, 92, of Wausau, passed away on December 22, 2025.
records.body:
Patsy M. Resch, 92, Wausau died Monday, December 22, 2025 while under the care of Aspirus Hospice at Wausau Manor.

She was born November 24, 1933 in Wausau, daughter of the late Leonard and Ethel (Schmitz) Resch.

Patsy loved to watch wildlife and enjoyed being outdoors. She also had a passion for working with the Women's Community and even traveled to Nicaragua for them to speak. Patsy and Dale loved to travel while the kids were young and together up until they divorced.

Patsy was preceded in death by her son, Paul Allen Fichtner, her ex-husband, Dale Allen "Skip" Fichtner, sisters, Mary Joe (Resch) Knapp and Sally Ann Olbrantz (Resch) and her husband John.

She is survived by her son, Marc Allen Fichtner, her sisters, Joan Marie Froehlich (Resch) and her husband, Henry, Sandra Ann Roll and her husband Jim and her brother Leonard George Resch Jr and a brother-in-law, Wayne Knapp.

At Patsy's request there will be no formal services. Burial will be in Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau.

records.name: Alice A. Andreas
records.summary: Alice A. Andreas, 78, of Wausau passed away on December 23, 2025.
records.body:
Alice A. Andreas, 78, passed away Tuesday, December 23, 2025, under the care of Interim Hospice at her home.

She was born January 15, 1947, in Wausau, daughter of the late William and Lillian (Nowitzke) Kortbein. On July 8, 1967, she married Jerald "Jerry" Andreas at St. Mary Catholic Church, Wausau, and he preceded her in death on September 3, 2000.

Alice met Jerry while working at Herberger's in Wausau. Family was always her top priority, and she never missed an opportunity to attend her children's and later her grandchildren's activities and events. She enjoyed dancing, tending to her yard and garden, playing cards, especially cribbage and sheepshead. She also enjoyed putting together puzzles, doing word searches, creating crafts and photography.

Survivors include her daughters, Laurie (Ken Knudsen) Andreas and Margie Andreas; grandchildren, Mickayla (Narcizo) Portilla Andreas, Shane (Angela) Ashton, Brandi (Seth) Crawford, Shawn Andreas, Kassia (Kolby) Schmidt-Andreas, Jason Knudsen and Tyler (Katey) Knudsen; eight great-grandchildren; sister, Christine Cherek, numerous nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends.

Besides her parents and husband, Jerry, she was preceded in death by a son, Bill, siblings, John, Cecilia, and Vera and a nephew, Frank Cherek.

records.name: Barbara A. Barthels
records.summary: Barbara A. Barthels, 87, of Wausau passed away on December 27, 2025.
records.body:
Barbara A. Barthels, 87, of Wausau passed away peacefully at home on Saturday, December 27, 2025.

She was born July 31, 1938, in Wausau, daughter of the late Ervin "Pete" and Grace (Hopperdietzel) Smith. She married Perry Barthels, her husband of 56 years, on April 24, 1965 in a private ceremony at Zion Lutheran Church in Wausau.

After graduating from Wausau High in 1956, Barbara moved to Milwaukee to begin her career. Upon returning to Wausau, she worked for Wausau Insurance. After being a homemaker for many years, she returned to work for the Wausau School District. She was a teacher's aide at Longfellow School and Hawthorne Hills Elementary School, retiring in 2000.

Barbara loved spending time with her family and friends and played a foundational role in the upbringing of her five grandchildren. She enjoyed traveling, reading, cooking for family gatherings, Friday night outings with her sister Joan, and a manhattan at five o'clock.

She was preceded in death by her husband, Perry Barthels, brothers-in-law, Jack Barthels and Roger Pospychala.

Survivors include her children: Rick (Jennifer) Barthels, Wausau, David Barthels, Wausau, and Joni (John) Young, Wisconsin Dells; five grandchildren: Nate, Luke, Elizabeth, Jack, and Sam; one sister: Joan Pospychala; and her nieces and nephews.

records.name: Daniel F. Reichl
records.summary: Daniel F. Reichl, 93, passed away on December 23, 2025.
records.body:
Daniel Francis Reichl, age 93, passed away on December 23rd, 2025 at Aspirus Hospital, Wausau.

Dan was born October 4th, 1932 in Poniatowski, WI to Laura Koepp on the farm that his great-grandfather homesteaded. He attended the Hoffman School, St. Anthony's, and Athens High. In 1951, he started working at the Underwood Veneer Company. In December of 1952, Uncle Sam needed him and he left for basic training at Fort Indiantown Gap Military Reservation in Pennsylvania. After 1 year in Korea he went to Fort Carson, Colorado and was honorably discharged December of 1954. He then returned to work at Underwood Veneer which later sold to Connor Lumber and Land Company. In 1961, he started working for Wisconsin Fuel and Light Company in Wausau. He retired in 1994 as Street Department Superintendent after working there for 33 years.

He married his sweetheart, Shirley Koenig, on November 15th, 1952 at Trinity Lutheran Church. He shared a loving marriage with his wife, whom he cherished deeply, and together they built a home filled with love, laughter, and support. They raised 4 wonderful sons: Steven, Scott, Stuart, and Mark. He worked hard each day for his family, and Shirley was the steady heart of their home, caring for their sons and supporting the life they shared. They lived simply, focused on what mattered most-family. She died July 23rd, 2012 shortly before their 60th wedding anniversary.

Dan was a member of the American Montgomery-Plant-Dudley Post 10 and Veterans of Foreign Wars Burns Post 388. He was privileged to experience the Honor Flight on October 21st, 2013.

Dan enjoyed woodworking, gardening, feeding the squirrels, fishing, and using his beloved 16hp Bolens snowblower. He enjoyed using his skills to help his sons and family with various projects around the house.

In his later years he reunited with a special friend, Marian Braun, who he first met in the 7th grade at St. Anthony's. They enjoyed dancing on Sundays at Sherri Land Ballroom and other locations. They were both members of St. Anne Catholic Church.

Daniel is survived by his 4 Sons: Steve (Barb), Scott, Stu (Sue), and Mark (Jeri Ann); 5 Grandchildren: Jeremiah, Niki, Gretchen, Morgan, and Stephanie; 6 Great-Grandchildren: Samantha, Lincoln, Ashley, Isaac, Breana, and Ryan; 3 Sisters: Ruth Lyon, Gladys Beese, and Betty Jane Wentland; Special Friends: Marian Braun (Companion), Don and Molly Fenhaus, and Sy Hintz; and many other nieces, nephews, other relatives, and friends.

He was preceded in death by Shirley Reichl (Spouse), Laura Koepp (Mother), Clarence Koepp (Step-Father), and Gera Reichl (Daughter-in-Law).

records.name: Daniel H. McKenna
records.summary: Daniel H. McKenna, 91, of Rothschild passed away on December 25, 2025.
records.body:
Thursday, December 25, 2025, another angel earned his wings. Daniel H. McKenna of Rothschild, WI, was a hard-working and outgoing man who was a devoted husband, father, Grandpa Danno, brother, and friend. He was born on February 10, 1934, in Watertown, WI, to Francis Harold and Dorothy M. McKenna (Melius). He was their fourth of seven children. Dan graduated from Mishicot High School on May 29, 1953, and enlisted in the U.S. Army effective January 8, 1954. After his honorable discharge, he then served four years in the Army Reserve.

Daniel married Marlene M. Diederichs on April 21,1956, in a 4:00 p.m. candlelight ceremony at First German Evangelical Lutheran Church in Manitowoc, WI. They then honeymooned in Canada and Niagara Falls. They enjoyed 62 years of marriage before her passing on February 1, 2019.

Dan worked as a welder for several years in the Manitowoc and Brillion, WI, areas and later retired from United Parcel Service (UPS) after working there as a salesman for 27 years.

Dan was an outdoor enthusiast, volunteering for many years as a Boy Scout leader, as well as loving to hunt (deer, elk, turkey, and geese), fish, and snowmobile. He even helped to create the Urbanaires Snowmobile Club. Dan was also an avid card player, enjoying cribbage, canasta, poker, bridge, and sheepshead. He also enjoyed bowling and golfing; although, gambling at the casinos or the machines at local eateries was his favorite pastime. In his later years, he also found enjoyment watching the birds and squirrels as they came to feed at his always-full feeders. He was a member of the Elks Club, Moose Lodge, the American Legion, Ducks Unlimited, the National Rifle Association, the National Wildlife Federation, and held a life membership in the Rocky Mountain Elk Foundation (RMEF).

Daniel and Marlene often wintered in Florida. They traveled to Hawaii for their 25th and 50th wedding anniversaries and enjoyed vacationing in Mexico and Branson, MO. They also completed a driving vacation throughout many northern, western, and southern states in their retirement.

Daniel was best known as Grandpa Danno by his grandchildren and even neighborhood children. Daniel and Marlene had three children together: Kevin of Rothschild, WI; the late Kim (Casey) Jones, née McKenna, of Schofield, WI; and Diane McKenna (formerly Christensen) of Racine, WI. They had eight grandchildren: Kevin II and Travis McKenna; Heather (Stephan) Pfohl, née McKenna; Gwendolyn (Dany) Rizk, née Jones; Owen (Jenna) Jones; Catherine and Ryan Christensen; and Veronica (Landon) Carus, née Christensen. They also had two step- grandchildren: Jennifer (James) McKenna-Sims, née Schmidt, and Lynn (Mike) Coscio, née Schmidt, as well as 14 great-grandchildren, five step-great-grandchildren, and four great-great-grandchildren. Daniel has two surviving siblings: Robert McKenna of Oshkosh, WI, and Eileen Ozga, née McKenna, of St. Petersburg, FL, a sister-in-law Joan McKenna née Hessel of Scottsdale, AZ, and many nieces and nephews.

Daniel was preceded in death by his wife, Marlene; his daughter, Kim; and his brothers and sisters-in-law: James (Myrtle), Terrence (Guadalupe), Roger (Roberta), and Dale as well as his brother-in-law Allan.

records.name: Ellen G. Curtis
records.summary: Ellen G. Curtis, of Phillips, was born June 1, 1949, in Milwaukee, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Ellen G. Curtis, of Phillips, was born June 1, 1949, in Milwaukee, WI, to the late Clarence and Jeanne (Wuerzberger) Liebe, passed away peacefully. Ellen was the heart of her family and a source of unwavering love, strength, and kindness to all who knew her.

She leaves behind her devoted husband, George; her two children, Duane and Dale Jr.; eight cherished grandchildren; and five beloved great-grandchildren. Ellen was also a mother figure to countless others, each of whom will carry a piece of her spirit with them always.

Ellen's legacy lives on through the lives she touched, the love she gave freely, and the family she held so close. She will be deeply missed and forever remembered.

records.name: James H. Hamann
records.summary: James H. Hamann, 84, of Weston passed away on December 27, 2025.
records.body:
James (Jim) Herbert Hamann, 84, went home to heaven, passing away peacefully on December 27, 2025, at Rennes Health and Rehab, under the care of Aspirus Hospice.

Jim was born at home in the Town of Wein (Edgar, WI) to the late Carl and Selma (Pilgrim) Hamann, and was raised on his family's farm and homestead. Jim attended a one room schoolhouse in the Town of Wein and graduated from Edgar High School. With the aid of his sister, Jim met his future wife, Janice, at Edgar High School. They were married on May 4, 1963, and shared 62 years of marriage before his passing.

Jim and Janice had a partnership like no other. Janice showed selfless support for Jim and his work throughout the decades. Together, they enjoyed traveling, both for work and pleasure, working on house and yard projects not only for their homes, but also their children's homes, and created a life built on love, purpose, and shared joy. Jim was an unrelenting advocate for his wife when she was challenged with medical issues.

Jim enjoyed vacations centered around historical or engineering points of interest, from the Panama Canal to the Hoover Dam. A heartfelt thank you to his brother-in-law, Jerry, and Jerry's wife, Diane, for making their adventures so special over the decades.

Jim had a keen interest and natural skill in building construction. Jim joined the team at Wausau Homes in 1973. His work included quality control, plant management, purchasing, corporate materials manager, and safety manager. After his first of many retirements, he, with Janice by his side, traveled around the country setting up and engineering additional Wausau Homes plants. Jim had an unapparelled self-motivation and passion for his work, and a sincere dedication to carrying out the vision of Wausau Homes, Inc. This dedication and work ethic set an example for his children that remains with them today.

In his retirement, he placed immeasurable value on keeping active and being of value. He enjoyed volunteering as a driver for North Central Health Care. He enjoyed breakfasts with colleagues, neighbors, and friends. At the end of each day, Jim enjoyed reading the daily financial and business newspapers, following the national and local news, and tracking markets, investments, and advances in automobile technology. He served on various boards of directors, church council, and committees. He supported multiple non-profit organizations. He was a charter member, firefighter, Secretary, Treasurer, and Lieutenant of the Kronenwetter Fire Department.

Jim, along with Janice, gave their children immeasurable gifts. Jim was selfless when it came to helping his children, grandchildren, and great-grandchildren. He took every opportunity to be a part of their lives. Jim was not only a father, but also a close friend and confidant. He was a true patriarch of the family, still offering advice up until his final weeks.

He is survived by his wife of 62 years, Janice (Gallenberg) of Weston; his three children, Steven (Gayle) Hamann of Medford, Michael (Judy) Hamann of Edgar, and Cathy (Keene) Winters of Wausau; four grandchildren, Daniel (Jessica) Hamann of Edgar, Jorgen Hamann of Wausau, Sarah (Joshua) Aubin of Sussex, and Margaret (Patrick) Reilly of Edgerton. He is further survived by seven great-grandchildren, Izzabella, Adalae, Caroline, Eve, Hannah, Caleb, and Byron.

In addition, he is survived by several siblings and their spouses, including one sister Mary (Lloyd) Schultz of Edgar; one brother, Raymond (Eileen) Hamann of Stratford; and sisters-in-law, Diane Gallenberg of Plover, Thea Hamann of Stratford, Karen Hamann of Stratford, Carol Hamann of Nekoosa, and Catherine Hamann of Custer.

In addition to his parents, he was preceded in death by six brothers, Robert, Charles, David, John, Thomas, and Lloyd; one brother-in-law, Jerome Gallenberg; one sister-in-law, Wilma (wife of Robert); and his beloved Yorkie-Poo, Sammy.

records.name: Janet M. McCabe
records.summary: Janet M. McCabe, 81, of Wausau passed away on December 22, 2025.
records.body:
Janet Mae McCabe (née Dallman), age 81, passed away peacefully on December 22, 2025, in Wausau, Wisconsin, the city where she was born and spent her life.

Born on July 9, 1944, in Wausau, Janet graduated from high school and went on to build a life centered around love, family, and kindness. She was a devoted wife to Terry McCabe and a cherished mother to their children: Sherri (Jeff) Krueger, Cindi (Jeff) Gramling, Terry McCabe Jr., and Jodi (Tim) Boruch.

Janet found her greatest joy in spending time with her family. She especially treasured moments shared wither four grandchildren- Paige, Logan, Dylan, and Zachary- who brought light and laughter into her life. Her warmth and gentle spirit left a lasting impression on all who knew her.

She will be remembered as a loving wife and mother whose kindness touched the lives of many. May her memory bring comfort to those who knew and loved her.

records.name: Leonardo A. Zarko
records.summary: Leonardo A. Zarko, 35, passed away on December 14, 2025.
records.body:
With extremely heavy hearts, we announce the unexpected passing of Leonardo Arthur Zarko, who left this world on December 14, 2025, at the age of 35. Born on June 27, 1990, in Illinois, Leo spent his life bringing joy, creativity, and love to those around him.

Leo was the cherished son of Leonardo Zarko and Jennifer Cocks. His vibrant spirit and compassionate nature made him a beloved friend with the heart of a caretaker—always ready to lend a hand or offer a listening ear. Those who knew Leo will remember him for his infectious laughter, warm heart, and the generous way in which he cared for others.

A talented chef, Leo expressed his love for life through cooking, creating meals that brought people together and turned simple gatherings into celebrations. His passion for the arts and creativity shone through all aspects of his life, enriching the lives of everyone fortunate enough to know him.

Leo's passing is a tremendous loss, and he will be deeply and profoundly missed by all who loved him.

Leonardo is survived by his parents, Leonardo Zarko and Jennifer Cocks, and his partner, Lucas Oliva, along with many friends and loved ones whose lives were forever changed by his presence.

records.name: Robert Pils
records.summary: Robert Pils, 87, of Schofield passed away on December 22, 2025.
records.body:
Robert Pils, Schofield, passed away on Monday, December 22 at Marshfield Medical Center – Weston following a brief battle with lung cancer. He was 87. He was preceded in death by his parents, Herman and Orvilla Pils, brothers in law Ken Seiler (Sylvia), John Seiler (Penny) and Dan Seiler (Betty) and sisters in law Jeannie Zastrow (Ray) and Arlene Sischo (Merrill).

Bob was born June 4, 1938 in Chicago, IL and spent his grade school years growing up alongside his brother and close extended family on Chicago's near north side. Those years included many formative days spent visiting his aunt and uncle's Marathon County dairy farm and grandparents' grain and livestock farm in north central Iowa, playing, fishing and hunting with his cousins and close friends. Bob's family later moved to central Iowa, where he graduated from Marshalltown High in 1956. He returned to Chicago to complete his formal education, graduating from the Art Institute of Chicago and working for a brief time as a commercial artist with IT&T before he was drafted into the US Army, where he served stateside for two years.

Shortly after receiving his honorable discharge, he applied for a graphic artist position with Employer's Mutual (later to be known as Wausau Insurance) where he met his future wife, Bonnie. They were married on September 3, 1966 and spent 59 wonderful years together, a perfect couple in every way. Bob was an incredible father to their two sons, Greg and Andy, and along with Bonnie, brother Chuck's family, and others they spent countless hours hunting, fishing, camping, attending sporting events and a zillion other activities. Every fall they returned to Iowa for pheasant season (Bonnie included!) and to catch up with close family and dear friends. Many good times were had during summer camping vacations exploring national parks throughout the mountain west and during summer holiday weekends with the Seiler clan around the campfire at Gilmore Lake. Bob retired from Wausau Insurance in 1998 and enjoyed a long and happy retirement, traveling the country and great lakes with Bonnie in their camper and internationally with their good friends, and welcoming his daughters-in-law Isabel and Shannon and granddaughter Ally into the family along the way. In 2010, Bob and Bonnie moved into their home on River Street where they enjoyed the beauty of the Eau Claire River and its fish and wildlife and the company of many good neighbors. He was a longtime member of the Wisconsin River Valley Chapter of Trout Unlimited, serving for many years on its board of directors and taking great pleasure in helping with their many stream improvement projects and efforts to introduce young anglers to the sport that gave him and his family so many good times and treasured memories.

Bob was known to many for his good-natured personality, sincerity, warmth, caring and especially his ever-present sense of humor. He had a line for every occasion and wasn't always terribly concerned with saying the socially appropriate thing! He was an avid reader of nonfiction, particularly history, historical biographies and natural history, and had a deep respect and appreciation for wildlife and natural communities that he shared with Bonnie and passed on to his sons. Nothing was more important to him than his family whether close or extended, and in old age he was widely considered the Pils and Kallestad family historian. He is survived by his loving wife, Bonnie, sons Andy (Shannon) and Greg (Isabel), granddaughter Ally, brother Chuck (Linda), special nephew Alan and niece Kristi and their families, and dozens of beloved cousins, nieces, nephews and in-laws.

records.name: Barbara Keller
records.summary: Barbara Keller, age 83, of Wausau passed away at Montview Care Center on December 16, 2025.
records.body:
Barbara was born in Morenci, MI to Rex Tuttle and Esther Tuttle. Barbara married Phillip Keller, the love of her life, of Milan, Ohio on August 26th, 1967.

Barabra is survived by 2 Children: Mark and Michelle Keller. Also, her beloved dog, Sadie Jane and other relatives and friends.

She was preceded in death by Phillip Keller (Spouse), Esther Tuttle (Mother), and Rex Tuttle (Father).

Barabra Keller, a radiant flame, the light of every room she came. A family's core, a gentle guide. With her soulmate Phil, Mark, and Michelle by her side.

A teacher whose joy inspired young hearts, her laughter and warmth were living arts. She threw the parties none forget, with warmth and kindness firmly set.

Though she has gone, her light will stay in every heart she touched each day.

Whether you knew her for a lifetime or just a moment, her impact was undeniable. She taught us the value of kindness, the strength of love, and the beauty of living with an open heart.

A memorial service will be held at 1:00 p.m. on Friday, January 9th, 2026, at Helke Funeral Home, 302 Spruce St, Wausau, WI. Rev. Peter Williamson officiating. Friends and family may meet for a time of visitation from noon until the time of service. The celebration of life will continue at Timekeeper Lounge and Distillery.

Barbara will be laid to rest next to Phil at Gethsemane Cemetery in Minnesota at a later date.

In lieu of flowers the family requests memorial contributions be made to Michelle Keller.

records.name: Dennis A. Schreiber
records.summary: Dennis A. Schreiber, age 77, passed away on December 23, 2025.
records.body:
He was born on December 3, 1948 in Wausau, Wisconsin. Denny lived a life marked by warmth, humor, and a deep love for those around him. He was known for his inviting smile and love of life. Even at 77, he remained full of happiness and joy, continuing to share stories that brought smiles to everyone who knew him.

Dennis is preceded in death by his parents, Arthur and Frances Schreiber, sister Francine (James Strzelecki), and brother-in-law Joseph Szarkowitz.

Dennis is survived by his wife of 54 years, Sharon, daughters Lindsey Wine (Nicholas) and Nicole Duggan (Rory), five grandchildren, Liam (2009), Oliver, Lael, Ellie and Berkeley, sister Sharon Szarkowitz, sisters-in-law Joyce Stieber (John), Nancy Schulz, brothers-in-law Michael Krautkramer, David Krautkramer (Chris), Patrick Krautkramer (Annette), and numerous nieces and nephews.

Dennis attended Wausau East high school and graduated from the University of Wisconsin – Stevens Point in 1971 with a degree in Education. As an Educator, he taught social studies to junior high students and coached the wrestling and football teams, before moving on to other endeavors, partnering to open S&S Insurance in 1985, where he would remain until his retirement in 2014. Dennis was actively involved in the Red Cross, Jaycees and Elks, giving back to his community.

Denny was a devoted husband, father, grandfather, and friend. He took great pride in his family and found his greatest happiness in time spent with family and friends. He was an avid outdoorsman who enjoyed hunting, fishing, hiking, Nordic skiing, biking and snowshoeing. Other interests included painting, reading, harvesting maple syrup, entertaining, and traveling. He had a way of turning everyday moments into meaningful memories. He will be missed.

A Funeral Mass will be held Thursday, January 15, 2026 at 11:00 a.m. at St. Anne Parish, 700 W. Bridge St. Wausau, WI. Visitation will precede the service starting at 10 a.m.

A separate visitation service will be held between 3:00 p.m.-6:00 p.m. on Wednesday, January 14, 2026, at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Avenue, Wausau, WI.

records.name: Gary L. Torzewski
records.summary: Gary L. Torzewski, age 72, of Wausau died unexpectedly at home on December 24, 2025.
records.body:
He was born May 29, 1953, in Wausau, son of the late Eugene and Margaret (Shulta) Torzewski.

Gary graduated from Wausau East High School in 1971, where he played baseball. He also played high school football under coach Win Brockmeyer. A lifelong sports fan, Gary especially loved cheering for the Chicago Bears. After high school, he attended Antigo Technical College, where he studied accounting. Gary proudly served his country in the United States Army and was honorably discharged in September of 1982. He worked in a variety of positions including security, custodial and restaurant work. Fiercely independent and strong-willed, he faced the challenges of multiple sclerosis with determination, refusing to let it define or limit him. Above all, he cherished time spent with family, especially his nieces and nephews, who held a very special place in his heart.

Survivors include his brothers, Dave (Mary) Torzewski and Mark (Barb) Torzewski; nieces and nephews, Kristy (Kevin) Zoromski, Lacey (fiancé Tim Schotz) Torzewski, Trent (Jessica) Torzewski, Troy (Sarah) Torzewski, and Hannah (Tyler) Ninneman; great nieces and nephews, Kiley, Keira, Bryson, Mila and Julius Eugene; numerous dear cousins, aunts, other relatives and friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents, grandparents and numerous aunts and uncles.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated 10:30 a.m. Wednesday, January 7, 2026, at St. Michael Catholic Church, Wausau. Burial will be in St. Michael Cemetery, Wausau. Visitation will be Wednesday from 9:30 a.m. until the time of service all at St. Michael Catholic Church, Wausau.

"I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith."

The family would like to extend their sincere appreciation to Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home for their expertise and compassion during this difficult time.

records.name: Gerald L. Kostka
records.summary: Gerald L. Kostka, age 82, of Wausau passed away peacefully at home on December 29, 2025, after a courageous battle with ALS.
records.body:
Gerald "Jerry" Lee Kostka passed away peacefully at home surrounded by his family on December 29, 2025, after a courageous battle with ALS.

He was born on March 25, 1943, in Wausau to Edna and Albert Kostka. He graduated from Wausau High in 1961 and spent his career at People's State Bank.

In his younger years Jerry competed in many triathlons. He loved riding his Harley and enjoyed golfing with friends. Those who knew Jerry, know that his brilliant smile never faded during his illness and he will continue to smile on forever.

He is survived by his wife, Gloria (Hoss), Son, Tim (Rachael) and Daughter, Tammy (Keith) Hannasch; grandkids, Gunnar Kostka, Payton Hannasch (Jordan), Parker Hannasch, and Isabella Hannasch; sister, Kristine (Mark) Thorne; brothers, Richard Kostka and Tom Kostka and sister-in-law, Lorrie Trimble and sister-in law, Mary Kohnhorst, and many nieces and nephews.

He is preceded in death by his parents, and sister, Judith Kostka and brother-in-law, Wilbur Kohnhorst.

The family would like to thank Aspirus Hospice for their compassionate care and support during Jerry's final days.

A celebration of life will be held in the spring.

records.name: Ida A. Brekke
records.summary: Ida A. Brekke, age 100, of Wittenberg passed away peacefully on December 29, 2025.
records.body:
Ida was born on August 9, 1925, in Elderon, WI, the daughter of the late Emma and Ole R. Dobbe of Rosholt. She was the granddaughter of Berthine Isakson Olson and Jorgen (George) Olson from Elderon, who immigrated from Norway.

Ida graduated from Rosholt High School in 1943. After high school, she began her career at Rosholt State Bank. She later managed the dry goods department at the Red Owl store, traveling by train to Chicago markets to select merchandise, a responsibility she carried out thoughtfully and with care.

Ida married Dr. Alfred Brekke, a veterinarian in Wittenberg, and worked alongside him until his death in 1988. Long before "veterinary technician" was a recognized role, she assisted with procedures and cared for animals with skill and knowledge.

Ida's interests included playing Bridge with the Wittenberg ladies, enjoying the piano, making lefse, and preparing memorable holiday meals – always beginning with the traditional Norwegian prayer, I Jesu Navn.

Preceding Ida in death was her loving husband Alfred Brekke, grandchildren Marne Oestreich and Madeline Brekke, and two brothers and four sisters.

Ida is survived by children Linda (Gene) Oestreich, Jane (Phil) Paige, Jim (Judy) Brekke, Rick (Carolina) Brekke; grandchildren Jon (Beth) Oestreich, Thomas Oestreich, Connor (Jenny) Paige, Hailey (Jonathan Price) Paige, Victoria Paige, Eleanor (Blake) Gottschalk, Henry (Hadar) Brekke-Peer, Sara (Tucker) Maris, and Benjamin Brekke; great-grandchildren Anja Oestreich, Elsa Oestreich, Alice Gottschalk, and Daphne Gottschalk; sister Darlene (Rusty) Knitter and many nieces and nephews.

Friends and family are invited to celebrate Ida's life on Saturday, January 10, 2026 at 11AM at Redeemer Lutheran Church, Wittenberg. A burial will follow at Forest Home Cemetery, Wittenberg. Visitation will be on Saturday from 10AM until the time of service at the church.

records.name: James S. Albrecht
records.summary: James S. Albrecht, age 80, of Wausau passed away on December 15, 2025, from ALS.
records.body:
James "Jim" S. Albrecht passed away surrounded by loving family on Monday, December 15, 2025, from Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS) "Lou Gehrig's disease". His final days were under the care of Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services of Wausau and Pine Crest Nursing Home of Merrill, and the family is grateful to both for the tender care provided to him.

He was born March 4, 1945, in Detroit, Michigan, the son and only child of the late Roland and Marion (Luhta) Albrecht. After graduating from Grinnell College in Iowa, he attended the Institute of Paper Chemistry in Appleton where he received his PhD in Paper Chemistry in 1971. He was the Technical Director of the (then) Mosinee Paper Corporation for 20-plus years. Jim was blessed with beautiful women in his life. On May 16, 1970, Jim married Carolyn Leppla in Waukesha, and while married, they had two daughters together. On June 6, 1992, Jim married Lynette Gantner in Mosinee, WI and his heart was broken when she preceded him in death on April 9, 2006. His joy in life was restored when on September 24, 2011, he married Ellen Fields in Cadott, WI.

Jim devoted himself to supporting and caring for his family, including his extended family of in-laws, stepchildren and grandchildren, foster grandchildren, great grandchildren, and all his precious pets through the years. He enjoyed music, especially at church, and he spent countless hours happily landscaping and gardening. He loved to travel and spend time in nature and at his cottages up north. He relished a very active lifestyle of bicycling, hiking, kayaking, snowshoeing, skiing, wildlife watching, photography, and camping all through his life.
