He was preceded in death by his parents and wife, Lynn. Survivors include his beloved wife, Ellen Albrecht of Wausau, WI and stepdaughter Karen (Phil) Konrath of Thiensville, WI; cherished daughters Erika Albrecht (Jeff Bartnik) of Wausau, WI and Tanya Albrecht of Portland, TN; grandchildren Felipe Mosqueda of Marathon, WI, Rafael Mosqueda of Sandy, UT, and Aria Quiggle of Oshkosh, WI; great grandchildren; and his dear cousins and relations in Ontario, Canada.

Jim was a man of strong faith and was a member with his wife Ellen at Bible Truth Chapel, 1600 N. 1st Ave, Wausau, where visitation will be 9:00-11:00 with a memorial service to follow 11:00 on Friday, January 9, 2026. Rev. David Hart will officiate. Burial will follow at the Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Kronenwetter.

records.name: Janice L. Straub
records.summary: Janice L. Straub, age 66, of Wausau passed away on December 29, 2025.
records.body:
Janice was born on July 23, 1959, in Wausau, the daughter of the late Gerald and Luella (Thurs) Straub.

She found her greatest joy in spending time with her twelve nieces and nephews, faithfully attending their activities, milestones, and celebrations as they grew. In her younger years, Janice also enjoyed crafts and needlework, pursuits that reflected her patience and creativity.

Janice is survived by her siblings: Sue (John Hutchinson) Behling, Linda (Don) Patterson, Diane (Bill) Petersen, and Dave (Laura) Straub; as well as her brother-in-law, Jerry Hetzel. She is further survived by her twelve nieces and nephews and their families.

In addition to her parents, Janice was preceded in death by her sister, Sharon Hetzel.

Private funeral services will be held.

The family extends their sincere gratitude to the caregivers at Compassus Hospice, Sylvan Crossings, Browns Living, and Oak Heights for the exceptional care and kindness Janice received during her illness.

records.name: John C. Weber
records.summary: John C. Weber, age 72, of Wittenberg died unexpectedly on December 24, 2025.
records.body:
John was born on October 27, 1953, in Fond du Lac, the son of Claude and Florence (Zelten) Weber.

John held various jobs throughout his life, working as the director for Calumet County Campground, Homme Youth and Family Programs and most recently, sold his woodworking designs at shows.

John was a man deeply connected to the natural world and to the people around him. He found joy and meaning in the outdoors, where he felt closest to nature and delighted in sharing its quiet miracles and generous gifts with others.

John was a skilled and passionate outdoorsman. He was an avid skier and served on ski patrol. He loved rock climbing, ropes courses, and the art of rope work, taking pride in tying knots and mastering hands-on-skills. Hunting, woodworking, and time spent in his barn or workshop brought him contentment, as did reading a good book after a long day.

A devoted Green Bay Packers fan, John carried his enthusiasm and loyalty into many areas of his life. More importantly, he carried a deep commitment to people-especially troubled youth. John worked as Director of Adventure Base at Homme Youth and Family Programs for many years. Through his work and mentorship, John encouraged others to be their best selves. He was a strong yet gentle leader, a thoughtful listener, and a steady source of guidance for those who needed it most.

John is survived by two sisters, Carol Weber and Jean Weber; special Aunt, Mary Ellen LaComte; special friends, Bonnie Anderson and Mark Steinbach; and many cousins, other relatives and friends.

John was preceded in death by his parents.

Per John's request, no formal services will be held at this time. A Celebration Gathering to remember John will be held at a later date.

records.name: Lorraine M. Mueller
records.summary: Lorraine M. Mueller, age 102, of Wittenberg died on December 27, 2025.
records.body:
Lorraine was born on September 4, 1923, in Milwaukee, the daughter of Joseph and Francis (Schoch) Kuffner.

On October 23, 1943, Lorraine was united in marriage to Lawrence Mueller in Milwaukee. He preceded her in death on September 20, 1999.

Lorraine's early life in Milwaukee was filled with fond memories, as she was raised by her mother, Francis, and stepfather, Jack Redick. She loved roller skating, ice skating, and playing tag football, often spending time in local parks and at South Shore Beach on Lake Michigan, where family picnics were a cherished tradition. It was also during her time at Omar Bakery in Milwaukee that she met the love of her life, Lawrence. The two then moved to Tigerton, where they made a home on a 140-acre farm and raised their family. In her later years, Lorraine took great pride in her hobbies, especially baking for the local deer hunters, crafting over 4,500 necklaces and bracelets, and enjoying trips to the casino. She also enjoyed rummaging and watching the Packers and Brewers. She was crowned Strawberry Queen during her time at Homme Home and was very proud of it. The most rewarding part about her life was watching her children and grandchildren grow up. Lorraine learned in life to always be good to others, kindness is the best thing you can do, have God in your life, do not keep your hands 'idle' and follow the golden rule.

Lorraine is survived by four children, Gail (friend, Brian) Gordon, Debra (David) Boda, Robert (Donna) Mueller and John (Claire) Mueller; eight grandchildren, Bryan, Kelly, Lindy, Laine, Katelyn, Sam, Adam and Katie; four step grandchildren, Kenneth, Nichelle, Elizabeth and Kasey Jo; nine great grandchildren, Audrey, Brooklyn, Addison, Kyla, Reagan, Kenneth, Cameron, Collette and Meadow Mae, along with two more coming in March and five step great grandchildren, Alec, Thomas, Callan, Cora and Aurora.

Lorraine was preceded in death by her husband; parents; brother, George; stepbrother, John; two stepsisters, Irene and Ester and two half-sisters, Marion and Elenore.

A Funeral Service will be held at 1 PM on Friday, January 16, 2026, at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg. Rev. Sharon Fox-Bogen will preside. Burial will be in Union Cemetery, Tigerton in the Spring. Visitation will be on Friday, from 11 AM until the time of service at the funeral home.

records.name: Mary L. Woller
records.summary: Mary L. Woller, age 73, of Wausau passed away on December 27, 2025, at Wausau Manor.
records.body:
Mary L. Woller, beloved mother, mother-in-law, grandmother, sister, aunt and friend, passed away on Saturday, December 27, 2025 at Wausau Manor leaving behind a legacy of love, laughter, and cherished memories. She was born on October 9, 1952 in Wausau, daughter of the late Edmund and Lucille (Lodholz) Budleski and touched countless lives throughout her journey.

Mary's professional life was marked by hard work and dedication. She built a long and accomplished career, working at FedEx, Tommy Docks, Home and Garden, and NTB. Throughout the years she served in a variety of roles, including sales, customer service, general manager and vice president of operations.

While Mary was proud of her professional achievements, her greatest joy came from the love she shared with others. Mary embraced life with joy and enthusiasm and loved with her whole heart. Her life's greatest purpose was making sure those around her felt happy, cared for, and supported. Her family and friends meant everything to her, and she treasured every moment spent together. She especially loved hosting family holiday gatherings filled with laughter, enjoying monthly breakfast outings with her grade school friends, watching short track racing and friendly card games that often lasted until the early morning hours. Mary delighted in trips to Florida, sitting on her deck feeding the birds and watching her sons and granddaughters in sports and other activities. She also cherished the simple pleasures of Sams pizza, spinning the birthday wheel at 2510, attending numerous fish boils and unwinding with a cosmopolitan in hand. Her warmth, generosity, and joyful spirit will be deeply missed and forever remembered by all who knew her.

She is proceeded in death by her parents Edmund and Lucille Budleski and her twin sister Marcia. Survivors include her sons, Quinton Woller, Harshaw and Ryan (Kristin) Woller, Kronenwetter, two grandchildren, Ellie and Maggie Woller and her siblings, Donnie Budleski, Hatley, Diane (Tom) Martin, Mosinee, Dennis Budleski, Schofield and Mike (LeeAnn) Budleski, Hatley and numerous nieces and nephews.

The Funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. Saturday, January 17, 2026 at St. Florian Catholic Church, Hatley. The Rev. Jerome Patric will preside. Burial will be in the parish cemetery at a later date. Visitation will be held on Saturday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service all at the church.

records.name: Nancy J. Wierzba
records.summary: Nancy J. Wierzba, age 87, of Weston passed away peacefully on December 28, 2025.
records.body:
Nancy was born on November 28, 1938, in Madison, Wisconsin, the daughter of Clayton and Eleanor (Linley) Fitzgerald. She was part of the second graduating class of Newman High School in Wausau. On August 24, 1957, Nancy was united in marriage to Eugene Wierzba at St. Mary's Catholic Church in Wausau. He survives.

Nancy was a devoted homemaker whose home was always filled with love. She found great joy in cooking, baking, vegetable and flower gardening, and canning. Above all, she cherished being a mother and raising her children, and she often lovingly cared for other children as well.

A talented, self-taught seamstress, Nancy sewed clothing for her family, vestments for her son and patches on uniforms for the policemen, spending many happy hours in her sewing room creating beautiful items for those she loved. She also enjoyed cross-stitching, hand embroidery, and reading. Nancy was an active and faithful member of St. Agnes Catholic Church in Weston, and her faith was the cornerstone of her life.

Nancy is survived by her loving husband, Eugene Wierzba; her children, Kenneth (Phyllis) Wierzba, Jeffery (Lois) Wierzba, Kelly Fennell, David (Barb) Wierzba, Father Alan Wierzba, and Douglas (Beata) Wierzba; 12 grandchildren; 21 great-grandchildren; and her sisters, Margaret "Peggy" (Darrell) Fritz and Virginia "Ginny" Vanwormer.

She was preceded in death by her parents; a granddaughter, Dawn Marie Meinelt; and a son-in-law, Mark Fennell.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Friday, January 2, 2026, at St. Agnes Catholic Church in Weston. The Most Reverend Gerard W. Battersby, Reverend Alan Wierzba, and Reverend Jerome Patric will concelebrate. A private family burial will take place in the Spring at Gate of Heaven Cemetery in Kronenwetter. Visitation will take place at the church on Friday from 10:00 a.m. until the time of Mass.

records.name: Rita V. Heindl
records.summary: Rita V. Heindl, age 92, of Madison, formerly of Marathon, passed away on December 22, 2025.
records.body:
She was born March 28, 1933 in Marathon, daughter of the late John and Eleanor (Yonker) Vogedes. On June 8, 1953, she married her high school sweetheart, Gerald Heindl at St. Marathon Catholic Church in Marathon. He preceded her in death on July 27, 2025.

Rita began her working career at Employers Mutual Casualty Company as a correspondent for workman's comp and later worked for Marathon Cheese Corp. as a line supervisor. Among her favorite pastimes, she enjoyed reading, baking, cooking, traveling and trivia.

Survivors include her children, Jeffrey (Kim) Heindl of Egg Harbor, Mark (John Lynch) Heindl of Madison, Mike (Jill) Heindl of Shorewood, Kathy (Richard) Marx of Tampa, Florida, and Mary (Randy) Mullen of New Braunfels Texas; grandchildren, Brien (Tasha) Heindl, Leslie (Eric) Hansen, Amy Mullen, Max (Megan) Mullen, Marcus (Amanda) Mullen, Madison Heindl, Nicholas (Casey) Marx, Shanna (Jamie) Larochelle, and Cole (Taylor) Radloff; great grandchildren, Olivia, Callon, Nathan, Jordan, Dillon, Viviann, Rhett and Ellodie; and many nieces and nephews.

Besides her parents and husband, Gerald, she was preceded in death by four brothers, Lester, Hugo, John and Alfred Vogedes; and two sisters, Lucille Freiburger and Doris Fergot.

A funeral Mass for Gerald and Rita will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, January 17, 2026 at St. Mary Catholic Church, Marathon. Rev. Msgr. Joseph Diermeier will preside. Burial will be in the parish cemetery. Visitation will be on Saturday from 9:30 a.m. until time of Mass at the church.

records.name: Adeline A. Wenzel
records.summary: Adeline A. Wenzel, age 96, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on January 2, 2026.
records.body:
Adeline was born on July 21, 1929, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to William and Martha (Schwittay) Baumann. She later married Harold Wenzel and together they shared 35 years of marriage until his passing on May 3, 1986.

She was a member of St. John Lutheran Church in Easton, Wisconsin and was a member of the Ladies Aid. She made countless prayer shawls and volunteered at Bethesda Thrift Store. She was a homemaker and also worked as a home cleaner. Later in life, she worked in the Gardening Department at Farm and Home and at The Wausau Buyer's Guide. She was extremely talented in the kitchen and always made the food for Thanksgiving and Christmas. Adeline enjoyed life on the farm with her husband and children- milking cows, canning, gardening, pickling, making sausage, pies and bread. Turkey stuffing and homemade doughnuts were a family favorite. Most of all, she enjoyed raising her family and spending time with them. She was proud of living independently until the end and even continued gardening until her 90's.

She is survived by her children, Beth (Bob) Doucette of Ringle; Gayle (Jim) Fenhaus of Wausau; Randy Wenzel of Marathon; Marcia (Scott) Habeck of Ringle; Virgil (Angie) Wenzel of Wausau; Karen (Shawn) Opper of Wausau; and Brenda (Rick) Walters of Wausau; grandchildren, Jeremiah (Shannon) Fenhaus, Dan (Jen) Fenhaus and their children Benson and Cora, Rachel (Kevin) Graff and their children Kaleb, Nathan, Annaleigh and Abe, MaDonna (Ryan) Enkers and their children Addison, Axle and Azalea, Matt (friend Becky) Habeck, Mandy (Bruce) Guss, Dustin (Natille) Wenzel and their children Vanessa and Charlotte, Mitchel (Kathleen) Wenzel and their children Hailey and Ryan, Jonathon Wenzel, Jessica Wenzel, Joshua (Maranda) Opper, Amanda (Travis) White and their daughter Magdalin, Isaac Walters, Luke Walters, Elijah Walters and Joseph Walters; and sister-in-law, Marcy Baumann

She was preceded in death by her husband, Harold; parents, William and Martha; siblings, Leslie and Caroline Baumann, Norris and Sylvia Baumann, Ned and Florence Baumann, Florence and Arden Wenzel, Elva and Ed Schreier, and Gaddy Baumann; grandchildren, Tiffany Lyn Opper, Jesse Allan Opper, Sally Marie Habeck, and Baby Boy Fenhaus; and son-in-law, Shawn Opper.

Funeral services for Adeline will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Monday, January 12, 2026, at St. John Lutheran Church, Easton, located at 164923 County Road Z, Wausau, with Pastor Stransky officiating. Friends and family may gather at the church for a time of visitation on Sunday, January 11, from 4:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m., and again on Monday, January 12, from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service. Burial will follow at St. John Lutheran Cemetery, Easton.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made to St. John Lutheran School.

The family would like to thank St. Croix Hospice, Rhianna at Pride TLC, and Pastor Stransky for their care and compassion.

Helke Funeral Home is assisting our family.

records.name: Bernice E. Benedict
records.summary: Bernice E. Benedict passed away peacefully at home.
records.body:
She is preceded in death by her parents, two children; Cheri and Larry Jr., and her husband; Benji Benedict.

She is survived by her daughter; Barbie (Bob) Nitka, granddaughter; Lacey (Jay) Martin; great-grandchildren; Delilah and Richie, Step-daughters; Melody & Brenda, and her Brother; Roger (Tomi) Maedke (Ocala, Florida).

A special thank you to all her friends at her independent living home for always looking out for one another.

Watch out for Deer!

records.name: Cynthia J. Melton
records.summary: Cynthia J. Melton, age 80, of Merrill, passed away peacefully on December 31, 2025.
records.body:
She was born on January 17, 1945, to the late Earl and Jane (Powers) Smith.

Cynthia was first married to Raymond Woller Jr., and together they welcomed three children: Julie, Anthony, and Francie. She later married Chuck Melton, blending their families and adding her daughter, Michelle.

Cindy was not always a perfect person, but who in the world is? She always spoke her thoughts, good or bad. Cindy was a woman who cherished her privacy and the quiet rhythms of life in the comfort of her home. She found joy in watching her favorite TV programs (Gunsmoke, CSI, NCIS), and any other detective-mystery shows. Also, NASCAR. She never missed a race. She knew every detail about every driver and start times. When not watching TV, she would read the 10 different magazines she bought. Fun fact – her daughters received the same magazines (from her).

Known as "Grandma Chuck" by her grandchildren, everyone knew that her house was always stocked with sweets. She had a huge sweet tooth, she never passed up the chance for cinnamon rolls, ice cream or chocolate of any kind – but "NO" peanut butter, —diabetes be damned.

Cindy enjoyed a good game of sheepshead with family and the ladies at Jenny Towers. She would call her daughters and rub it in that she won at BINGO, and how awesome it was that she won a gift card. Cindy was a collector of magnets (her fridge was covered), knickknacks, and trinkets, each one holding a memory. She always had her home totally decorated for every holiday season. She would always buy the window clingy' s for her family's windows.

Years ago, Cindy worked in West Bend at the hospital in the kitchen. She loved to talk to everyone. She also devoted many hours volunteering at St. Mary's Hospital in Rhinelander.

She is survived by her loving children: Julie (Glen) Mathews, Anthony Woller; and Francie Hahn. Her grandchildren: Jana, Britney, Jesse, Trevar (Lisa), Zac (Casey), Alexandra (fiancé Nate), Amanda, Rebecca, Desari, and Bradyn; 14 great-grandchildren; and one great-great-grandchild. She is also survived by her sisters: Bonnie Koelsch, Audrey Zingler, Linda (David) Voelz, and Jill (Steve) Patraw.

In addition to her parents, Cynthia was preceded in death by her husband, Chuck Melton, her daughter, Michelle and brother-in-law Mike.

Cindy will always be remembered.

A memorial service will be held at 12:00 pm on Friday, January 30th, 2026, at St. Stephens United Church of Christ, 903 E. 2nd Street, Merrill, WI 54452. Visitation will take place from 10:00 am until the time of the services at the church. Burial will take place at a later date at the Forest Home Cemetery in Rhinelander.

Family and friends may leave words of remembrance at www.honorone.com.

records.name: David Springer
records.summary: David Springer, age 73, of the town of Easton, passed away peacefully on December 31, 2025.
records.body:
He was born December 28, 1952 in Wausau, the son of Paul and Virginia (Linder) Springer. On August 4, 1973, he married his high school sweetheart, Alice Boser at St. Mary Catholic Church in Marathon.

For more than 48 years, he dedicated himself to his work at Marathon Cheese Corporation. It was more than a job-it was a source of pride and purpose. His strong work ethic was not just something he practiced, but something he lived and passed on to his family.

He lived a life defined by kindness, helping others and humor. He was the kind of man who never missed a birthday call or text, always showed up when someone needed help and could fix just about anything. He learned early to appreciate the land and all it offered. Whether hunting with the grandkids, four wheeling, fishing or simply sitting around the campfire at the cabin, he found peace in the outdoors. Nature was not just a pastime for him…..it was home!

Above all, he was a devoted husband, father, brother, son and most dear to his heart….grandpa! He taught by example how to work hard, keep your word and appreciate the world around you. His presence made people feel welcome and seen and his impact will live on through all the lives he touched.

He is survived by his beloved wife of 52 years, Alice Springer; devoted son, Troy (Tonya) Springer; lovely daughter, Sarah (Derek) Brown; grandchildren, Owen, Audra, Annelise, Bella, Chapman and Channing; his mother, Virginia Springer; brothers, Danny Springer, Ernie (Mary) Springer, Fred (Patty) Springer and Jerry (Brenda) Springer; and many nieces and nephews.

A celebration of David's life will be held from 12:00 until 4:00 p.m. on Sunday, January 18, 2026 at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: John Krukowski Sr.
records.summary: John Krukowski Sr., age 84, of Wausau, passed away on December 18, 2025.
records.body:
John was born in Wausau, WI on March 1, 1941 to John and Magdaline Krukowski. As a young boy he grew up on a farm and developed the foundation for a strong work ethic which he demonstrated his entire life. John graduated from Wausau High in 1959 and joined the Army shortly after graduating. He served his country in the Vietnam war, and while stationed overseas in Germany, he met the love of his life, Gertrud. John and Gertrud were married on July 27, 1965 and soon started their journey together as loving parents. John was honorably discharged from the army after fulfilling 7 years of active service and then pursued a career in auto mechanics that eventually spanned more than 40 years.

He was highly respected by his colleagues in the auto mechanic industry. He also joined the Wisconsin Army National Guard completing more than 23 years of total military service. An accomplishment he was very proud of. He loved getting together for family gatherings which often broke out into a competitive game of sheepshead or bag toss. He was also an avid outdoorsman who loved fishing and hunting. He will always be remembered as a man that could fix or build anything he set his mind to, but most importantly, he will be revered as a loving and compassionate husband, father, grandfather, mentor and friend. John will be missed greatly and remembered fondly by many. From scratching his back on the nearest doorway, to rubbing his belly with both hands after a good meal, we will cherish many of these memories, always.

John is survived by his wife, Gertrud Krukowski and four children: Mary (Ryan) Johnson, Patricia (Dan) Toivonen, John (Jody) Krukowski Jr. and Linda (Perry) Esker, eight grandchildren: Justin, Danny Jr., Samantha, Colton, Ava, Maria, Eli and Dax, and one great- grandchild: Ben, three siblings: Bob (Ann) Krukowski, Paul (Kathy) Krukowski and Charlotte Gantner, and many nieces, nephews and other relatives.

John was preceded in death by his parents John Krukowski and Magdaline Schroeder.

In lieu of flowers, the family encourages donations be made to the Man of Honor Society, P.O. Box 2071, Wausau, WI 54402 or the Wausau American Legion Post 10, 1001 Golf Club Rd., Wausau, WI 54403 to honor John's commitment to helping others in need.

Visitation will be held on Saturday, 10th of January, 2026 from 10:00 to 12:00 PM at Helke Funeral Home.

Services will be held on Saturday, 10th of January, 2026 at 1:00 PM at Restlawn Memorial Park with Chaplain Don Hildebrandt officiating.

records.name: Marlin A. Novitzke
records.summary: Marlin A. Novitzke, age 85, of Edgar, passed away peacefully on December 29, 2025.
records.body:
Marlin was born on January 3, 1940, to Joseph and Lucy (Boehm) Novitzke. On May 26, 1962, he married the love of his life, Mary Imhoff, at St. John's Catholic Church in Edgar. After their wedding, the couple lived in Kenosha for two years before proudly purchasing the family farm established by Marlin's grandparents in 1899, where they built a life centered on faith, family, and hard work.

Marlin's greatest joy was spending time with his family, especially during lively and competitive card games and billiards. He shared his love of hunting, fishing, and gardening with his children and grandchildren. Known for his generosity, he was always willing to lend a helping hand and had a remarkable ability to fix nearly anything. Marlin also had a deep love for animals, including his livestock, cats, and the several dogs he named Scooby over the years.

He enjoyed motorcycling, snowmobiling, and following NASCAR. Marlin and Mary both played the concertina, sharing their love of polka music. They were members of the Jammers and the Central Wisconsin Concertina Club, where Marlin served as president. Through their music, they brought joy to others by performing at nursing homes, parades, and local festivals. Marlin also served his community as a member of the Town of Emmet zoning committee for 20 years and proudly donated more than 10 gallons of blood throughout his lifetime.

Marlin's devotion to his Catholic faith was evident throughout his life; he prayed the rosary even in his final days, and his favorite prayer was to St. Jude. His faith gave him courage and comfort, bringing him peace and touching the lives of everyone who knew him.

He especially cherished time spent with Mary, including UTV rides as they checked on the crops and enjoyed the land they loved so dearly. They also enjoyed attending church picnics and polka festivals together, celebrating his Polish heritage.

Marlin is survived by his beloved wife, Mary; his three children, Robert of Wausau, Lee (Margaret) of Mosinee, and Debra (Robin) Beran of Abbotsford; and a bonus son, Rich Knetter of Mosinee. He is further survived by five grandchildren: Ryan (Elisha Kopca) Beran, Elizabeth (Nick) Dwyer, Jesse Beran, Jared (Amanda) Novitzke, and Bryce Novitzke; and his first great-grandchild of Elizabeth and Nick Dwyer, expected in February; along with many special nieces, nephews, and friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents and his siblings, Joseph, Melvin, James, and Joyce.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held on Friday, January 9, 2026, at St. John's Catholic Church in Edgar, Wisconsin. Visitation will begin at 10:00 a.m., with the Mass to follow at 12:00 p.m. Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Edgar is in charge of arrangements. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Mildred "Milly" Mary Anne Pries
records.summary: Mildred "Milly" Mary Anne Pries, age 87, returned home to Heaven on December 27, 2025.
records.body:
Mildred was born in Antigo, Wisconsin on April 8th, 1938. Mildred was the daughter of the late Frederick and Anna (Adams) Brandt, who resided in Bryant, a rural area outside of Antigo. Milly married Harry Otto Henry Pries on August 24th, 1957, with whom she shared many years of marriage and built a life centered on family, faith, and community.

Milly graduated from Antigo High School and then resided in Bryant, WI., and Antigo, WI before moving to Wausau where she and Harry lived until Harry's passing in 2015. While in Wausau, Milly worked for Marathon County Social Services and was then a housewife. Milly then moved to Cadott, WI, where she resided on her own until 2024, when she moved to The Homeplace of Stanley. Mildred enjoyed dancing, playing cards, listening to Elvis Presley music, watching old TV sitcoms & game shows, and working on crossword and word search puzzles. She enjoyed talking with her longtime friends on the phone and cherished their friendships. She will be remembered for her kindness, quiet strength, simplicity and devotion to her family.

Milly's memory lives on through her family, sons' David (Paula) Pries of Wausau, WI; Daryl (Theresa) Pries of Cadott, WI & grandchildren: Jackie Pries of St. Paul, MN; Justin (Amanda) Pries of Marshal & Dylan (Lindsey) Pries of Wausau, WI, along with Lucy, Marci, Vinny & Gigi Pries of Cadott; and great grandchildren Oliver & Eloise Pries of Wausau & Aubrey & Cooper Pries of Marshall, WI.

Mildred was preceded in death by her husband, Harry (March 19th, 2015); Milly's parents, Frederick & Anna (Adams) Brandt; parents-in-law Otto & Selma (Brennecke) Pries & brother Elmer Brandt of Antigo; brother-in-law Wilhelm (Bill) & Nancy Pries of Salem, Oregon & Vince & sister-in-law Marie Furlott of Salem, Oregon.

Family and friends can honor Milly on Friday, January 9th, 2026, at Christ Lutheran Church, 1300 Townline Road, Wausau, WI. Visitation will begin at 10:30 a.m. followed by a memorial service beginning at 11:00 a.m. officiated by Pastor David Wetmore.

Harry and Mildred will be laid to rest together with their deceased dog, Opie, this Spring at Northwoods National Cemetery, 4520 Lakewood Road, Harshaw, WI., at a date to be determined.

The family would like to extend their sincere thanks to the staff at The Homeplace of Stanley assisted living residences for their compassionate care and companionship, also to St. Croix Hospice for providing in-home healthcare services.

records.name: Patricia A. Nowack
records.summary: Patricia A. Nowack of Marathon City passed away on December 31, 2025.
records.body:
She was born the daughter of her beloved parents William and Dorothy Lang. Pat graduated from Marathon High School in 1960. In 1961, she married Richard E. Nowack, and they raised 4 children: Sandy, of Madison; Ricky (Janis), of Stevens Point; Cheryl, of Eagan, MN; and Jody (Kelly Thomsen), of Wausau. They divorced in 1986.

When she was a child and money was scarce, "Patsy's" father proudly bought her a "piano accordion", which kicked off a lifetime love of country music. Pat had a beautiful voice and if there was a guitar player around she loved performing. She sang everywhere she went.

Pat was courageous and artistic, part wonder woman and part Lucille Ball. It seemed there was nothing she could not do well when she set her mind to it. She was a fabulous gardener and cook, and her dill pickles are legendary. Even while camping, the campsite was raked and her table adorned with a tablecloth, doily, cookie jar, and, often, a big bouquet of lilacs. She was a distinguished employee of American Family Insurance, and was always proud that she strove to treat people fairly.

As a younger woman she and her sister Jeannie enjoyed their Saturday trips to Wausau and just about everything else they did together. Summer camping trips, often with Herb and Shirley Lang, were also highlights.

She valued her children more than anything and often cared for the elderly and children who were not her own. Ultimately, she found Benji, her little white dog, who would be her sidekick for 19 years. Late in life she obsessively did word search puzzles, and she developed a passion for politics.

Pat is survived by her children; granddaughters, April (Chris) Ploenzke and Ashley (Austin) Travis; great-grandsons Logan and Parker Ploenzke; soon-to-be Great-Granddaughter Travis; her sister, Jeannie (Dale) Sauter; and sisters-in-law Shirley Lang, Sandy Lang and Janice Lang, along with many loving nieces and nephews. Her ex-husband Richard (Bernelle) Nowack also survives.

Pat was preceded in death by her parents; brothers Herbert, Laverne, Wayne, David, Robert and Gary Lang; and sisters Sylvia Payne and Vivian (Howard) Jeske. She also lost her dear friend Marjean (Jim) Nowack.

The family wishes to thank the Marathon Fire Department, who saved Pat's life almost 10 years ago. Thanks also to David Martin and the other volunteer drivers who helped get Pat to medical appointments. The family also wishes to recognize the staff at Wood Aven Health and Rehabilitation and St Croix Hospice.

Pat was a woman of faith.

A Memorial Mass will be held at 11:00 a.m. Saturday, January 10, 2026 at St. Mary Catholic Church, Marathon. The Rev. Msgr. Joseph Diermeier will preside. He guided her to great spiritual comfort through the years. Burial will be in Sacred Heart Catholic Cemetery, town of Cassel at a later date. Visitation will be held from 9:00 a.m. until the time of services all at the church. Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Marathon is in charge of arrangements. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

Pat was well loved and will be missed by many.

records.name: Patrick E. Karlen
records.summary: Patrick E. Karlen, age 65, of Marathon, passed away on January 1, 2026.
records.body:
He was born on March 17, 1960 in Medford, son of Gertrude (Dahlke) Karlen, Edgar and the late Ervin Karlen. On April 24, 1982 he married Rebecca Blair at Holy Family Catholic Church, Poniatowski. She preceded him in death on October 10, 2012.

He devoted much of his life to working alongside his father, where he learned the true meaning of craftsmanship, dedication, and pride. Together, they left their mark on countless residential and agricultural buildings, each project a testament to their skill and care. In 1996, Pat started his own construction company. For decades, he poured his heart into building and remodeling homes, garages, decks, and more, earning a reputation for quality work, reliability, and meticulous attention to detail.

His hands shaped more than wood and concrete; they created spaces where families gathered, celebrated, and built memories that will last for generations. His legacy lives on not only in the structures he built, but in the lives enriched by them.

Above all else, he cherished his family. He especially loved spending time with his grandchildren—watching sporting events, attending concerts and plays, and simply being present for the moments that mattered most. Whether cheering from the stands or sitting quietly in the audience, he was their biggest supporter.

He found joy in the simple pleasures of life. He had a lifelong love for John Deere tractors and took great pride in mowing his lawn and caring for his yard, trees, and flowers. His shed was a reflection of who he was – meticulously organized, immaculately cleaned, and always ready for the next project.

He also shared a special bond with his beloved dog, Lucky. Lucky was his constant companion, always close by whether he was working in the yard, spending time in the shed, or relaxing at home, bringing him daily joy and comfort.

Survivors include his mother, Gertrude Karlen, Edgar, his daughter, April (Scott) Kaiser, Mosinee, his fiancé Mary Lou Kurtzweil, Marathon, Mary Lou's children, Tracy (Ryan) Neumann, Sun Prairie, Daniel (Stephanie) Kurtzweil, Wausau and Eric (Jayda) Kurtzweil, Marathon and his grandchildren, Tucker, Noah and Aiden Kaiser, Caleb and Deacon Neumann, Beau and Wade Jahnke and Mara, Lana and Louella Kurtzweil. Pat is further survived by his siblings, Nancy (Tom) Heath, Marathon and Brian (Jenese) Karlen, Marathon and many nieces, nephews and friends.

The Funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. Thursday, January 15, 2026 at St. Mary Catholic Church, Marathon. The Rev. Msgr. Joseph Diermeier will preside. Burial will be in the parish cemetery. Visitation will be held on Wednesday, January 14, 2026 from 4:00 p.m. until 8:00 p.m. and again on Thursday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of services all at the church. Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Marathon is in charge of arrangements. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Thad J. Kinney
records.summary: Thad J. Kinney, age 55, of Weston, passed away on December 18, 2025.
records.body:
He was born July 31, 1970, in Wausau, son of Patrick Sr. and Autumn (Kaufman) Kinney.

Thad had a love if not an obsession, with fishing. He shared this passion with his kids, friends, and sometimes newly made friends at any given fishing location. He was also an avid motorcyclist, especially in his youth. His primary interest in his lifetime was to help people during his work as a Registered Nurse. In addition, he received pleasure and joy in meeting new people and developing friendships. Even as a small child, he was precocious and gregarious with his early speech skills. From a very early age, he would interact with people he met.

Most of all, he loved his children and had many fantastic summers taking them on fishing trips, especially to the Upper Peninsula of Michigan.

Thad is survived by his son, Isaiah Kinney; daughter, Kalie Kinney; his siblings, Patrick (Cheryl) of Wausau; Brian (Margaret) Kinney of Wausau; Lynn (John) Borkenhagen of Rochester MN; Lisa Kinney (Doug Hippe)of Madison; Krista (Kevin) Kulas of Savage MN; Christopher (Connie) Kinney of Lino Lakes MN and Joshua (Tracey) Kinney of Woodbury MN.

He is preceded in death by his parents.

A Celebration of Life will be held at Wausau on the Water, 1300 N River Dr, Wausau, WI 54403, on January 31, 2026, from 2:00-4:00 p.m.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Thomas E. DeLisle
records.summary: Thomas E. DeLisle, age 84, of Mosinee, passed away on December 27, 2025.
records.body:
He was born on January 3, 1941, in Wausau, he was the son of the late Glenn and Ann (Bandoch) DeLisle.

Tom attended Newman High School and went on to study to become a Certified Kitchen Designer, buying his father's business, the DeLisle Company, after Glenn retired. For many years, he worked as a kitchen designer, remodeling countless kitchens and taking pride in his craft. Tom proudly served his country in the US Army Reserve.

He loved taking his family on adventures–camping near the Boundary Waters, trips to Hawaii, summer trips to Door County, and sailing together on Lake Du Bay, Lake Michigan and Superior. After retirement, Tom enjoyed traveling to places such as Madrid, Spain, Mazatlán, Paris and Normandy, France, Jamaica, and the Dominican Republic. Las Vegas was one of his favorite places to visit.

He often said he was "a lucky man" to have had the opportunity to travel, and he remained deeply grateful for all that life had given him.

Survivors include this daughters, Michelle (Ray) Lindner, Chaska, MN and Alisa DeLisle, Madison; Grandchildren, Nicholas Ray and Delaney Ann Lindner; brothers-in-law, Richard "Dick" and Nicholas Krajewski; mother of his daughters, Dianne (Krajewski) Leigh, and many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents, brother, Glenn "Dick" (Katherine) DeLisle; brother-in-law, William "Craig" (Diana) Krajewski; and in-laws, William and Ruth Krajewski.

Funeral services will be held at 11:00 a.m. Thursday, May 14, 2026 at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Avenue, Wausau. Visitation will be on Thursday May 14, 2026 from 9:00 a.m. until time of services all at the Stewart Avenue funeral home. Entombment will be in Holy Angel Mausoleum, St. Michael Cemetery Wausau.

Tom's family would like to express special thanks to the Kronenwetter Police and Fire Departments, TLC Home Care, and Heartland Hospice.

In lieu of flowers please consider a donation to any of the three above-mentioned.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Adeline A. Wenzel
records.summary: Adeline A. Wenzel, age 96, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on January 2, 2026.
records.body:
Adeline was born on July 21, 1929, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to William and Martha (Schwittay) Baumann. She later married Harold Wenzel and together they shared 35 years of marriage until his passing on May 3, 1986.

She was a member of St. John Lutheran Church in Easton, Wisconsin and was a member of the Ladies Aid. She made countless prayer shawls and volunteered at Bethesda Thrift Store. She was a homemaker and also worked as a home cleaner. Later in life, she worked in the Gardening Department at Farm and Home and at The Wausau Buyer's Guide. She was extremely talented in the kitchen and always made the food for Thanksgiving and Christmas. Adeline enjoyed life on the farm with her husband and children- milking cows, canning, gardening, pickling, making sausage, pies and bread. Turkey stuffing and homemade doughnuts were a family favorite. Most of all, she enjoyed raising her family and spending time with them. She was proud of living independently until the end and even continued gardening until her 90's.

She is survived by her children, Beth (Bob) Doucette of Ringle; Gayle (Jim) Fenhaus of Wausau; Randy Wenzel of Marathon; Marcia (Scott) Habeck of Ringle; Virgil (Angie) Wenzel of Wausau; Karen (Shawn) Opper of Wausau; and Brenda (Rick) Walters of Wausau; grandchildren, Jeremiah (Shannon) Fenhaus, Dan (Jen) Fenhaus and their children Benson and Cora, Rachel (Kevin) Graff and their children Kaleb, Nathan, Annaleigh and Abe, MaDonna (Ryan) Enkers and their children Addison, Axle and Azalea, Matt Habeck, Mandy (Bruce) Guss, Dustin (Natille) Wenzel and their children Vanessa and Charlotte, Mitchel (Kathleen) Wenzel and their children Hailey and Ryan, Jonathon Wenzel, Jessica Wenzel, Joshua (Maranda) Opper, Amanda (Travis) White and their daughter Magdalin, Isaac Walters, Luke Walters, Elijah Walters and Joseph Walters; and sister-in-law, Marcy Baumann

She was preceded in death by her husband, Harold; parents, William and Martha; siblings, Leslie and Caroline Baumann, Norris and Sylvia Baumann, Ned and Florence Baumann, Florence and Arden Wenzel, Elva and Ed Schreier, and Gaddy Baumann; grandchildren, Tiffany Lyn Opper, Jesse Allan Opper, Sally Marie Habeck, and Baby Boy Fenhaus; and son-in-law, Shawn Opper.

Funeral services for Adeline will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Monday, January 12, 2026, at St. John Lutheran Church, Easton, located at 164923 County Road Z, Wausau, with Pastor Stransky officiating. Friends and family may gather at the church for a time of visitation on Sunday, January 11, from 4:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m., and again on Monday, January 12, from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service. Burial will follow at St. John Lutheran Cemetery, Easton.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made to St. John Lutheran School.

The family would like to thank St. Croix Hospice, Rhianna at Pride TLC, and Pastor Stransky for their care and compassion.

records.name: Su Ying Thao
records.summary: Su Ying Thao, age 73, of Wausau, passed away on December 22, 2025.
records.body:
Su Ying was born on April 5, 1952 to Ka Pao Thao and Phoua Her. He was the fourth oldest child and the first son.

Su Ying lived in Pennsylvania and North Carolina before settling in Wausau in 1981. During his time in Wausau, he held various jobs as a laborer. He worked at Greenheck for 17 years and retired in 2013.

In his free time, he loved to go hunting and fishing. Su Ying enjoyed going to church and was an active member in the church. He helped with church fundraisers and held different positions within First Hmong Missionary Alliance church, such as an elder on the board and janitor.

Su Ying is survived by wife, Mai Lee Thao, sons, Chong (Pang) Thao, Ching Thao, Xeng (Kelia) Thao, daughters, Mao (David) Chang, Mang (Yer) Lee. Grandchildren, Nhia (Mai Der) Thao, Kimberly, Alexia, Henry, Wyatt, and Olivia. Great Grandchildren, Isabelle, Camila, and Rafael.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Ka Pao and Phoua, and his daughter Peng.

A time of visitation will be held from 9am to 9pm on Saturday, January 24, 2026, 9am to 9pm on Sunday, January 25, 2026 and 9am to 11am on Monday, January 26, 2026 at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Avenue, Wausau.

records.name: Allan Daniel Jirikovec
records.summary: Allan Daniel Jirikovec, 95, of Madison, died peacefully on January 5, 2026.
records.body:
Allan Daniel Jirikovec, age 95 of Madison, died peacefully on January 5th, 2026. Allan was born to Edward and Anna Jirikovec in Manitowoc in 1930. He graduated from Lincoln High School, class of '48, and St. Norbert College in 1952, where he received his B.A. degree in philosophy. He continued his education in theology at St John's Seminary, Collegeville, MN, and was ordained a Catholic priest in St. Francis Xavier Cathedral, Green Bay, in 1957.

He served the people of the Diocese of Green Bay as parish priest in Green Bay, Two Rivers and Omro. He lovingly cared for the sick and dying as a chaplain at Holy Family Hospital in Manitowoc. After 15 years of priesthood, he resigned in 1972 to marry the love of his life, Rita Delmore.

Allan and Rita and her five children then moved to Wausau to start a new life. Allan worked a variety of occupations but liked to think of himself first and foremost as a married priest.

He was an active member of St. Anne's Parish for many years where he and Rita were active in RCIA. For their last several years in Wausau, they worshipped at Our Savior Polish National Catholic Church, where they felt at home and where Allan was accepted as a married priest. The pastor there, Father Marion Talaga, became like a son to Allan and referred to him as "Monsignor".

Allan was a proud member of the Alano Club for many years and fondly called it "an oasis in the desert, a lighthouse in the storm, a holy place where miracles happen."

Allan and Rita enjoyed a long retirement with many cruises and worldly travels, making new friends wherever they went.

Allan is survived by his stepchildren: Margaret Delmore (John), Ruth Diaz de Leon (John), Michael Delmore, Bruce Delmore and Paul Delmore (Laurie), plus 5 cherished grandchildren: Ali, Tony, Tim, Rachel and Ryan.

Allan was preceded in death by his parents, his brothers-John and LeRoy, and his beloved wife, Rita.

A Mass of Christian Hope to celebrate Allan's life will be held at Our Savior National Catholic Church (804 Jackson St, Mosinee) at 11am on Friday, January 30th with visitation starting at 10am.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be made to Our Savior National Catholic Church in Mosinee or the Alano Club of Wausau.

records.name: Brandt Thomas Butler
records.summary: Brandt Thomas Butler, 23, passed away at his home on January 6, 2026.
records.body:
Brandt Thomas Butler, 23, passed away at his home on January 6, 2026.

Brandt was born on August 28, 2002, to Todd and Lori Butler, and was a bright and curious soul from a young age. As a child, he showed an early mind for engineering, spending hours building intricate creations with K'Nex and dreaming up how things worked. That thoughtful creativity stayed with him throughout his life, shaping the way he approached both work and relationships. There was nothing that could stop him from fixing something big or small.

He was known for his sharp mind and love of a good debate, especially when it came to politics and history. Brandt confided in his older sister, Claire, for much of his adult life. Their conversations were filled with tough love and honesty.

Brandt had a deep love for animals and cherished his pets, who brought him comfort and companionship. Deep relationships meant everything to him, and some of his fondest memories included lake life and creating sand castles with family and cousins at his grandpa Tom's.

Professionally, Brandt took great pride in his work. Brandt spent much time involved in the early years of his father's family tree business as an arborist. He went above and beyond showing up early and staying late, committed to his role. Brandt very much valued his fellow co workers and cherished his work relationships, specifically with Kevin and Derek. Later on his younger brother, Blake, joined along side him in the family business. Over time, that partnership grew into a bond of admiration and respect, and Brandt often shared how proud he was of the man his brother had become. Of all his work, he especially loved being a tower climber, a role that matched both his adventurous spirit and his dedication to doing things right. Brandt also spent a short while in his high school years doing janitorial work for the local school district. Tammy was an influential friend and co worker he spoke highly of. Throughout his work life he was a true perfectionist, Brandt was known for the pride he took in every job, no matter how big or small.

Those who knew Brandt will remember his dry sense of humor and his comedic storytelling. He had a contagious laugh which was more of a cackle at times. A sound his family has deeply missed over the recent years.

As he grew older, Brandt developed a love for independence and the open road. One of his greatest accomplishments included road tripping with his camper across the country. He shared fond memories of visiting the Red Woods and seeing the Colorado mountains. He enjoyed taking trips with his beloved girlfriend, Alicia, creating joyful memories. He adored his camper and treasured the freedom it gave him to explore. At home, you could often find him carefully detailing his Ford F-250, taking hours out of his day to make sure it was in "mint" condition as he would always say.

Although Brandt will be remembered for his strong will, determination, and deep care for others, Brandt did not lead an easy life and those close to Brandt knew of his mental health struggles throughout the years.

He will be deeply missed.

Survived by his parents, Todd and Lori Butler; sister, Claire (Cody) Sunie; brother, Blake Butler; girlfriend, Alicia Johnson; aunt, Sara Cross and her son, Danny Socwell; aunt, Terri (Steve) Erickson and their children, Brynn and Adrian; aunt, Amy (Edwin) Davis; maternal grandmother, Karen (Mike) Lawrence; maternal grandfather, Mart (Marie) Butenhoff; paternal grandparents, Tom and Mary Butler; step-grandfather, Al Fedie; and his special dog, Toto. He was preceded in death by his paternal grandmother, Carol Fedie; as well as his beloved dog, Lola.

Brandt's funeral service will be held at 3:00 PM on Sunday, January 11, 2026, at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston. Pastor Leroy Jonas will officiate. Visitation will be held from 1:00 PM until the time of the service at Ascend.

records.name: Fritz Patrick Reiche
records.summary: Fritz Patrick Reiche, 60, of Wausau, passed away on January 4, 2026.
records.body:
Fritz Patrick Reiche, 60, of Wausau, passed away at home on January 4, 2026.

He was born January 22, 1965, the son of James and Dorothy Reiche. He married his wife Latrice. He enjoyed watching football with his best friend of over 50 years, Dan Oliva.

He is survived by four siblings, Debra (Darrell) Wehrman, Sheree (Ken) Schnoor, Cindy (Tim) Palmer, and Scott Waldinger; 12 nieces and nephews, Jeremy (Melissa) Raddatz, Stacy Raddatz, Jon LaFave, Stephanie StAmand, Jenny (Nathan) Gustavus, Garrett (Brittany) Waldinger, Griffin Waldinger, Michelle Reiche, James Reiche, Matthew Reiche, Rylee (Andrew) Dehnal, Will Reiche.

He is preceded in death by his parents and stepmother, Marian Reiche and Latrice, sister Jamie Luer, brothers Mike Reiche and Randy Reiche.

records.name: Kevin D. Kollmansberger
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Kevin D. Kollmansberger, 59, passed away unexpectedly on December 31, 2025.
records.body:
Kevin D. Kollmansberger (known by many as Gibby), age 59, passed away unexpectedly on December 31, 2025.

Kevin was born on November 9, 1966. He was known as the life of the party, with a captivating personality that instantly drew people in. Kevin had a way of making others feel welcome and entertained, leaving a lasting impression wherever he went. He enjoyed fishing, working on cars, and being handy, always willing to fix, build, or help however he could.

Kevin was united in marriage to Kathy Jo Saverda on July 15, 1989, and they had three children. They later divorced in 2005.

Above all, Kevin cherished his family. He is survived by his children: son, Kyle (Leah) Kollmansberger, son, Kasey (Taylor Opelt) Kollmansberger, and daughter, Kelsea (Jesse) Kollmansberger-Preciado. He was a proud grandfather to Laikyn, Kamden, Karson, and Kasen, who brought him immense joy.

Kevin is also survived by his siblings: Kurt (Cindy) Kollmansberger, Renee (Dean) Check, Rhonda Hanson, and Regan (Garrett) Jochimsen; and ex-wife, Kathy; along with many beloved nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Robert and Shirley Kollmansberger.

Kevin will be remembered for his humor, energy, and the way he brought people together. His memory will live in the hearts of all who knew and loved him.

A private service will be held by his family.

records.name: Mark A. Withers
records.summary: Mark A. Withers, 57, passed away peacefully on January 7, 2026.
records.body:
Mark A. Withers, 57, passed away peacefully on January 7, 2026. He was born in Wisconsin Rapids on April 26, 1968.

Mark had an immense love for the Green Bay Packers. Coffee outings with his buddies were among his favorite rituals, with cherished visits to local spots such as Log Cabin, Tri-City, Eagle's Nest, and Red Apple, where laughter and meaningful conversations filled the air.

Mark had a unique talent for capturing the essence of homes, especially log cabins, through his drawings. He also had an appreciation for cars and trucks, particularly Chevys. Mark found purpose in helping others, having taken on the responsibility of driving for the Wausau Daily Herald and transporting patients to their medical appointments-a testament to his heart of gold.

Mark is survived by his mother, Aloha Knetter-Eckes; siblings, Ed Withers, Lona Withers, Lee Withers, Keith (Laurie) Withers, and Mark Knetter; and many nieces, nephews, and coffee buddies Becca and many others. He is preceded in death by his father, Levi Withers; and stepfathers, Jim and Darrell.

A service for Mark will be held at a later date.

We wish to thank all of Aspirus Hospice for their help and care.

records.name: Nancy C. Ross
records.summary: Nancy C. Ross, 87, formerly of Wittenberg, passed away peacefully on January 6, 2026.
records.body:
Nancy C. Ross, 87, formerly of Wittenberg, passed away peacefully on Tuesday, January 6, 2026, at The Pines in Clintonville, WI.

Nancy was born on September 6, 1938, in Winneconne, the daughter of Rexford and June (Freeman) Flanigan. She was united in marriage to Norman Ross in Seattle, WA.

Nancy worked as a beautician for over 40 years, she owned and operated her own salon in Oshkosh, WI and Seattle, WA. She had a creative spirit and found joy in crafting, painting, sewing, gardening, searching for treasures at flea markets and playing her luck at casinos. Nancy also had a deep love for dogs, who held a special place in her heart.

Nancy is survived by her sister, Linda Schlichting; brother, James (Christina) Flanigan; as well as numerous nieces and nephews.

Nancy was preceded in death by her parents; husband, Norman and siblings; Claude 'Dick' Flanigan, Jacqueline 'Jackie' MacKinnis, Glen Flanigan and Gail Flanigan.

A Memorial Service will be held at 11 AM, on Saturday, January 17, 2026, at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg. Visitation will be on Saturday, from 10:30 AM until the time of service at the funeral home.

records.name: Rachael Lea Howen
records.summary: Rachael Lea Howen, 44, left us on December 16, 2025.
records.body:
It's with deep sadness that the family of Rachael Lea Howen, announce that she left us at age 44, on December 16th 2025. She's the youngest daughter of James Howen and Sally Hall. Rachael attended Stevens Point Area schools. She loved working with people and held various jobs over her lifetime. She had a passion for helping others and loved animals, often giving her last dollar to someone who she thought needed it more. Providing those around her with unconditional love and empathy was important to her. She enjoyed tending to her garden, she would dig up her outside garden plants and bring them inside for winter. She couldn't bear to let them die. As teenagers Rachael and her sister would drive around listening to their music loud, while changing the lyrics to fit their current situation. When asked for their favorite memories of their Aunt, her nieces said her laughter that would fill the house, her funny voice, accents, and watching movies together. Her two white cats, she called her girls, Peaches and Jujube, were her pride and joy. At the end of her life, Rachael was able to save five other people through the gift of organ donation.

Rachael leaves behind her parents James (Kelly) Howen, Sally (Joe) Hall. Her son James Hall. Her sister Roxanna (Christopher Schroeder) Howen, Her nieces and nephew Chloe Schroeder, Ramona Kirch, Alex Schroeder and Ava Jolie Kirch. She's also survived by many aunts, uncles and cousins.

She joins in death her sister Marian Marlene, her grandparents Joseph and Ramona Gooch, Marian "Mitzi" Howen. Her uncle Thomas Gooch, Aunts Joyce Wermeling, Brenda Rizzo and Diana Howen

We celebrate Rachael's life everyday by showing compassion and love, being there for each other.

records.name: Rodney J. Klos
records.summary: Rodney J. Klos, 64, passed away on January 7, 2026.
records.body:
Rodney Klos passed away on January 7, 2026, at the age of 64. He was born on February 21, 1961, to the late Allan and Violet (Kramer) Klos.

Rodney graduated from UW Madison with a Bachelor of Science degree in Business and went on to build a career in retail management. He was known for his strong work ethic and leadership, but even more for his outlook on life. Rodney had an unwaveringly positive spirit—always finding the good, even when his health was failing.

Above all else, Rodney cherished his family. He loved spending time with their children and grandchildren, and he found his greatest peace at home with the love of his life, Jackie, and their two beloved fur babies, Yogi and Bam Bam.

Rodney is survived by his loving spouse, Jackie; his five sons, Michael (Donna), Steve (Lindsay), Bob (Hunter), Jonathon (Holly), and Nick (Emily); his 17 grandchildren; his brother, Michael (Sue); and his sister, Kathryn (Mark) Nybo. He is further survived by four nephews; one niece; and one great-niece.

Rodney will be remembered for his kindness, optimism, and the deep love he had for his family. His legacy lives on through all who knew and loved him.

Honoring Rodney's wishes, no services will be held.

records.name: Ardus Jane Schoepke
records.summary: Ardus Jane Schoepke, 92, of Wausau passed away on January 11, 2026.
records.body:
Ardus Jane Beckman (Stone) Schoepke, age 92, passed away peacefully on January 11, 2026, surrounded by love. She lived a long, full life marked by devotion to her family, kindness to others and a quiet strength.

Born on July 30, 1933 in Wausau, WI, she was the daughter of Henry and Mary Beckman. Throughout her life, she was known for her warm heart and unwavering love for those around her.

She was a devoted mother, grandmother and great-grandmother and her family was the center of her world. She took pride in caring for those she loved and she was always there with encouragement, wisdom and a listening ear.

She is survived by her five children: Jamie Mancini (Mark), Terry Stone, Patrick Stone (Cindy), Mark Stone (Donna) and Tracy Stone, three stepchildren: Beth Kaziak (John), Tammy Schoepke and Wes Schoepke (Jane), sisters Jane Robbins and Roxie Krueger (Jim), twelve grandchildren, eleven great-grandchildren and many extended family members and friends who will miss her deeply. She was preceded in death by husbands Patrick Stone and Wayne Schoepke and sister Jeanette Schullo.

The family extends sincere gratitude to Aspirus Wausau Hospital, caregivers and nursing staff for their compassionate care.

A private celebration of life will be held. In lieu of flowers and because of her love of animals, donations may be made to the Humane Society of Marathon County.

She leaves a lasting legacy of love and grace in the hearts of those who knew and loved her.

records.name: Blaine Scott Knowles
records.summary: Blaine Scott Knowles, 82, of Wausau passed away on December 25, 2025.
records.body:
Blaine Knowles of Wausau passed away on December 25, 2025, at Mountain Terrace Assisted Living.

He was born on November 13, 1942, to the late Bill and Phyllis (Twichell) Knowles in Lansing, MI. Blaine graduated from Oswego High School, Class of 1960.

Soon after high school, he joined the United States Air Force. Eventually, he ended his career at the air base near Antigo, WI. He then met his wife, Cheri, and they married shortly after his honorable discharge. They were married on September 18, 1965, in Antigo.

Blaine held a number of jobs over the years, but was always involved with computer science. His final job was at Breakbush in Westfield, WI, where he maintained the computers in the plant that operated the equipment in the plant. After retirement, they moved to Wausau, WI.

He is survived by his sister, Jackie (Neil) Twichell of Eaton Rapids, MI; a niece, Ingrid (James) Benward of MI; and a nephew, Eric Twichell of Portland, OR. He is further survived by two sisters-in-law, La Vonne Duernberger of Wausau and Sandy Duernberger of Antigo; as well as many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his wife, Cheryl (Duernberger) Knowles; parents, R. William and Phyllis A. Twichell; mother and father-in-law, William and Edna Duernberger; sister-in-law, Dolores Marx; brothers-in-law, William C. Duernberger, Ronald Duernberger, and Robert Marx; nephew, Regan Duernberger; and great-nephew, Craig Marx.

Private burial service will be held at a later date at Elmwood Cemetery in Antigo, WI.

records.name: Charles Schmollinger
records.summary: Charles Schmollinger, 72, of Marshfield passed away on January 11, 2026.
records.body:
Charles "Chuck" Schmollinger, age 72, passed away January 11, 2025, surrounded by his loving family, in Marshfield, Wisconsin. He was born in Hillsboro, IL on September 9, 1953.

Chuck lived much of his life in Athens, Wisconsin, where he began his working years as a farmer. Following an illness, he answered a new calling by obtaining his teaching license, a path that allowed him to serve others in a different way. He took pride in supporting both his school and parish communities, living out his faith by becoming a lay minister.

A faith-filled man in every sense, Chuck's beliefs guided his life and the way he cared for others. He was also an avid Green Bay Packers fan, a passion he enjoyed sharing with family. Throughout his life, he bravely fought several illnesses and medical complications, meeting each challenge with quiet strength and perseverance.

Chuck was preceded in death by his parents, Lynn and JoAnne Schmollinger, and a brother, Edward.

He is survived by the love of his life and devoted wife, Cathy; his children Ed (Emily) Schmollinger, Anna (Kevin) Koopman, Angela Schmollinger, Anthony Schmollinger, and Andrew (Abigail) Schmollinger; and his grandchildren Penelope, Zac, Ava, Ella, Clara, Isaac, Jeremiah, Samuel, Violet, and Lilliana, who brought him immense joy. He is also survived by his sister Linda Schmollinger, brother Larry Schmollinger, nieces and nephews.

Visitation will take place at 9:00 a.m. and a funeral ceremony at 11:00 a.m. at St. Thomas Catholic Church in Milan, Wisconsin on Saturday, January 17, 2026.

Chuck will be remembered for his steadfast faith, quiet strength, and the meaningful ways he cared for those around him. He will be deeply missed and lovingly remembered.

The family would like to express their heartfelt gratitude to the countless medical professionals and caretakers at Colonial Center who cared for Chuck and supported the family throughout the years. Their dedication and compassion will always be remembered. In lieu of flowers, donations are encouraged to be made toward the Msgr. John Malik Education Fund at St. Mary's Catholic School in Colby, WI.

records.name: John S. Petroski
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: John S. Petroski, 74, of Hatley passed away on January 10, 2026.
records.body:
John S. Petroski, passed away peacefully at his home in Hatley on January 10, 2026, under the care of Compassus Hospice.

He was born on January 6, 1952, in Wausau, Wisconsin, son of the late Felix and Margaret (Publ) Petroski.

John dedicated over 40 years of his professional life to Gordon Aluminum. He had a deep love for the outdoors, enjoying the simple pleasures of making firewood, hunting, and fishing. John cherished his time spent in the woods, particularly with his special friends Bill and Jody Wodora, creating memories that would last a lifetime.

John's life, however, carried the heavy heartache of losing his cherished son, Adam Petroski. Adam's passing left an indelible mark on John's spirit, shaping his journey in profound ways.

He is survived by his brother, Michael (Gloria) Petroski of Edgar, as well as special friends Bill and Jody Wodora of Hatley, Ann Wodora of Hatley, and Cheryl Rozak of Mosinee. Additionally, John leaves behind several nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents, son, Adam, brother, Dan Petroski, and sister, Patricia (Michael) Proctor.

His family wishes to thank Compassus Hospice for their care in John's final weeks.

Private services will be held in the spring.

records.name: Julie A. Kroeger
records.summary: Julie A. Kroeger, 78, of Wausau passed away on January 12, 2026.
records.body:
Julie Ann (Pfaff) Kroeger, 78, of Wausau passed away Monday January 12th, 2026 at Wood Aven Health and Rehabilitation. She was under the care of Aspirus Hospice Services.

Julie was born October 6th, 1947, in La Crosse, WI to the late Kermit and Sylvia (Harper) Pfaff. She grew up on the family farm, and then moved into the city of Sparta, WI in 1962, Julie attended grade school in a one-room schoolhouse in Daneville, WI. She then attended Sparta High School, graduating in 1965. While there, she met her future husband, Wayne Kroeger. Julie attended college at UW-La Crosse in 1965, 1967, 1969 and graduated in 1970 with a teaching degree. She also attended Oregon State University in 1968; she and Wayne lived in Corvallis, Oregon 1968-1969, while Wayne was in the Air Force.

Julie began her teaching career in 1970, at Sparta Junior High School, where she taught social studies to 8th and 9th graders. When Wayne took a job that brought them to Wausau, Julie worked as a library aide at the D.C. Everest Junior High School. She went on to be a substitute teacher at this location, as well as in the Wausau School District, In 1981, Julie began teaching 4th Grade at St. Anne Catholic School. In 1987, she settled into teaching English and social studies to the 7th and 8th graders at St. Matthew Middle School.

Julie left teaching for a short time. She did retail work at Sears and JC Penney in the former Wausau Center Mall. Julie returned to teaching in 1996, when she began working at NTC. She worked in the ESL program, the GOAL Lab, and the Alternative High School. Rounding out Julie's employment was doing magazine replacement in grocery stores from 2005-2008.

Julie and Wayne were married May 4, 1968 at St. Patrick's Catholic Church in Sparta, WI. They had two sons, Bryan Scott in 1973 and Daniel Mark in 1976. Wayne preceded her in death on January 20th, 2024.

Julie was a wonderful wife, mother, grandmother, mother-in-law, sister, aunt, cousin and friend. She enjoyed teaching, reading, traveling, and listening to music. Her greatest joy was her family, who will greatly miss her.

Survivors include sons Bryan, Wausau, Wisconsin, and Dan (Jen), Wausau, Wisconsin; two granddaughters, Hannah and Chloe, Wausau, Wisconsin; four sister-in-laws, Kathy Pfaff McVicker, Sheldon, Wisconsin; Caroline Wagner, Middleton, Wisconsin; Donna Kroeger, Janesville, Wisconsin; and Diane June, Sparta, Wisconsin; one brother-in-law John (Renata) Kroeger, San Jose, California; as well as many nieces, nephews, cousins, and friends.

Julie was preceded in death by her husband Wayne; parents Kermit and Sylvia (Harper) Pfaff; brother, Mark Pfaff; four brothers-in-law, Conrad Kroeger, Eugene Kroeger, Tom Dwyer and David Wagner; one sister-in-law, Lorraine Dwyer; one nephew, Matthew Kroeger; and one niece, Kara Pfaff.

Funeral services will be held at 11:00am on Saturday January 24th, 2026 at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau. Visitation will take place that morning from 9:00am until the time of the service. Burial will take place at St. Michael Cemetery following the funeral service.

records.name: Kenneth A. Hammes
records.summary: Kenneth A. Hammes, 90, passed away on January 9, 2026.
records.body:
Kenneth August Hammes passed away at the age of 90, on Friday, January 9, 2026 after a short but brave battle with pancreatic cancer. He was a kind man who loved a good joke, his Manhattans, a competitive game of cribbage, the Green Bay Packers, Brewers, Badgers and the Yankees. He would, however, bravely attend games with his children and grandchildren of rival teams such as the Steelers, the Colts, and even the LA Dodgers.

His laughter could fill up a room, and he made a friend wherever he went. He will be missed by all who knew him, but he left everyone with a mountain of memories to cherish.

Ken was born on January 3, 1936, to Leo and Irene (Gronemus) Hammes in La Crosse, WI. He was the oldest of eight siblings. Ken graduated from Bangor High School in 1953 and began working for his uncle at Hammes Truck Sales in La Crosse. In August of 1957 he married Darlene Kneifl, the mother of his three children, Greg, Gary and Sue. They relocated to Wausau where he got a job working for North Central Airlines/Northwest Airlines, retiring in 1993.

Upon Ken's retirement, he started a second career driving motor coach for Lamers Bus Line. His travels allowed him to visit all of the continental United States, as well as most of Canada, including Winnipeg, east to Toronto, Montreal, Quebec, and all of Novia Scotia. He retired a second time in 2014.

Ken enjoyed travel with his longtime companion, Louann Roehr. Their adventures took them to Texas, the Gulf Shores, Palm Springs, and many other destinations.

Ken worked hard to provide his children with opportunities he wasn't able to enjoy in his childhood. He coached his boys in Little League when they were young, encouraging all three kids to be involved in various sports and school activities. He was the president of the DC Everest Parents Athletic Club when his children were in high school, helping to organize fundraisers and parent participation. One of the most memorable things his children will cherish about their father is how he introduced them to the joy of travel, taking advantage of his airline benefits and flying them to places they otherwise would not have seen.

Ken was a member of the Knights of Columbus Council and the Elks Club. He was also on the board of his condominium association for many years, going above and beyond to help his neighbors. He was fondly known by his condo neighbors as "The Mayor."

He will be dearly missed by his three children who adored and respected him immensely: Greg (Debbie) Hammes, Gary (Lisa) Hammes, and Sue (Anthony) Marguleas. His seven grandchildren will also miss his quick wit and humor: Jessica (James) Walker, Kelsey Hammes (Nate Bueche), Katie Hammes (Preston Coates), and Max (Charlotte Queen), Sam, Anna and Jack Marguleas. His three great-granddaughters, Charlotte, Fiona and Tess Walker, loved their Grandpa Ken. Ken is survived by two siblings, his brother Leo Hammes and his sister Renee Hammes, and his loving companion, Louann Roehr. He is preceded in death by his parents and siblings Ronald, David, Robert, Jerome and Mary Ellen Pitz, his second wife, Suzie Fink, as well as many other close relatives.

A funeral Mass will be held on Tuesday, January 20, 2026, at St. Anne Catholic Church in Wausau, Wisconsin. Visitation will be on January 19, 2026, from 4:00 until 7:00 pm at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau and again from 10:00 am until 11:00 am on January 20, 2026 at St. Anne Catholic Church.

The family would like to thank Aspirus Hospice, especially his nurse, Carla, and dear friends Jackie Yeager, M.D. and Sue Soriano for their friendship and care.

In lieu of flowers, donations can be made in Ken's name to Neighbor's Place or Salvation Army.

records.name: Mary L. Woller
records.summary: Mary L. Woller, 73, of Wausau passed away on December 27, 2025.
records.body:
Mary L. Woller, 73, beloved mother, mother-in-law, grandmother, sister, aunt and friend, passed away on Saturday, December 27, 2025 at Wausau Manor leaving behind a legacy of love, laughter, and cherished memories. She was born on October 9, 1952 in Wausau, daughter of the late Edmund and Lucille (Lodholz) Budleski and touched countless lives throughout her journey.

Mary's professional life was marked by hard work and dedication. She built a long and accomplished career, working at FedEx, Tommy Docks, Home and Garden, and NTB. Throughout the years she served in a variety of roles, including sales, customer service, general manager and vice president of operations.

While Mary was proud of her professional achievements, her greatest joy came from the love she shared with others. Mary embraced life with joy and enthusiasm and loved with her whole heart. Her life's greatest purpose was making sure those around her felt happy, cared for, and supported. Her family and friends meant everything to her, and she treasured every moment spent together. She especially loved hosting family holiday gatherings filled with laughter, enjoying monthly breakfast outings with her grade school friends, watching short track racing and friendly card games that often lasted until the early morning hours. Mary delighted in trips to Florida, sitting on her deck feeding the birds and watching her sons and granddaughters in sports and other activities. She also cherished the simple pleasures of Sams pizza, spinning the birthday wheel at 2510, attending numerous fish boils and unwinding with a cosmopolitan in hand. Her warmth, generosity, and joyful spirit will be deeply missed and forever remembered by all who knew her.

She is proceeded in death by her parents Edmund and Lucille Budleski and her twin sister Marcia. Survivors include her sons, Quinton Woller, Harshaw and Ryan (Kristin) Woller, Kronenwetter, two grandchildren, Ellie and Maggie Woller and her siblings, Donnie Budleski, Hatley, Diane (Tom) Martin, Mosinee, Dennis Budleski, Schofield and Mike (LeeAnn) Budleski, Hatley and numerous nieces and nephews.

The Funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. Saturday, January 17, 2026 at St. Florian Catholic Church, Hatley. The Rev. Jerome Patric will preside. Burial will be in the parish cemetery at a later date. Visitation will be held on Saturday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service all at the church.

records.name: Patrick E. Karlen
records.summary: Patrick E. Karlen, 65, of Marathon passed away on January 1, 2026.
records.body:
Patrick "Pat" Karlen, 65, Marathon passed away unexpectedly on Thursday, January 1, 2026 at his home.

He was born on March 17, 1960 in Medford, son of Gertrude (Dahlke) Karlen, Edgar and the late Ervin Karlen. On April 24, 1982 he married Rebecca Blair at Holy Family Catholic Church, Poniatowski. She preceded him in death on October 10, 2012.

He devoted much of his life to working alongside his father, where he learned the true meaning of craftsmanship, dedication, and pride. Together, they left their mark on countless residential and agricultural buildings, each project a testament to their skill and care. In 1996, Pat started his own construction company. For decades, he poured his heart into building and remodeling homes, garages, decks, and more, earning a reputation for quality work, reliability, and meticulous attention to detail.

His hands shaped more than wood and concrete; they created spaces where families gathered, celebrated, and built memories that will last for generations. His legacy lives on not only in the structures he built, but in the lives enriched by them.

Above all else, he cherished his family. He especially loved spending time with his grandchildren—watching sporting events, attending concerts and plays, and simply being present for the moments that mattered most. Whether cheering from the stands or sitting quietly in the audience, he was their biggest supporter.

He found joy in the simple pleasures of life. He had a lifelong love for John Deere tractors and took great pride in mowing his lawn and caring for his yard, trees, and flowers. His shed was a reflection of who he was – meticulously organized, immaculately cleaned, and always ready for the next project.

He also shared a special bond with his beloved dog, Lucky. Lucky was his constant companion, always close by whether he was working in the yard, spending time in the shed, or relaxing at home, bringing him daily joy and comfort.

Survivors include his mother, Gertrude Karlen, Edgar, his daughter, April (Scott) Kaiser, Mosinee, his fiancé Mary Lou Kurtzweil, Marathon, Mary Lou's children, Tracy (Ryan) Neumann, Sun Prairie, Daniel (Stephanie) Kurtzweil, Wausau and Eric (Jayda) Kurtzweil, Marathon and his grandchildren, Tucker, Noah and Aiden Kaiser, Caleb and Deacon Neumann, Beau and Wade Jahnke and Mara, Lana and Louella Kurtzweil. Pat is further survived by his siblings, Nancy (Tom) Heath, Marathon and Brian (Jenese) Karlen, Marathon and many nieces, nephews and friends.

The Funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. Thursday, January 15, 2026 at St. Mary Catholic Church, Marathon. The Rev. Msgr. Joseph Diermeier will preside. Burial will be in the parish cemetery. Visitation will be held on Wednesday, January 14, 2026 from 4:00 p.m. until 8:00 p.m. and again on Thursday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of services all at the church.

records.name: Rev. James F. Schaefer
records.summary: Rev. James F. Schaefer, 90, of Wausau passed away on January 11, 2026.
records.body:
Rev. James F. Schaefer, 90, Wausau, passed away Sunday, January 11, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born February 27, 1935, in the family farmhouse in the township of Lincoln, Buffalo County, Wisconsin, the son of Elmer J. and Evangeline M. (Schmidtknecht) Schaefer. His grandparents were Frank and Anna (Rotering) Schaefer, and Frank and Kate (Theisen) Schmidtknecht. He attended St. Boniface Catholic elementary School in Waumandee, WI (1941-1949), and high school and college education took place at Holy Cross Seminary in LaCrosse, WI (1949-1957). His theological studies were completed at St. John's Seminary and University in Collegeville, MN. On May 20, 1961, he was ordained to the priesthood at Holy Cross Seminary Chapel, LaCrosse, WI by Bishop John P. Treacy. His first priestly assignment was at Sacred Heart Parish, Marshfield, WI as Associate Pastor, 1961-1963. In 1963 he was the temporary administrator of St. Boniface Parish in Waumandee, WI and then Associate Pastor (1963-1970) at St. Anne Parish, Wausau, WI. From 1970-1971 he was Chaplain at St. Joseph's Hospital, Arcadia, WI, and also part-time Associate at St. Stanislaus Parish, Arcadia, WI and temporary administrator of St. Michael's Parish, North Creek, WI followed by being an Associate Pastor at St. Stephen's Parish, Stevens Point, WI, (1971-1973). His first Pastorate was at St. Bartholomew Parish, Mill Creek, WI (1973-1974), followed by a Pastorate at Holy Rosary Parish, Lima, WI (1974-1975) and then assigned as Pastor of St. Louis Parish, Dorchester, WI and remained there until he was granted status as a Senior Priest, retiring in 2005. After retirement he spent many years as a substitute priest in various parishes.
