Fr. Schaefer is survived by two sisters, Joanne of No. Ft. Meyers, FL and Carol of Arvada, CO, an aunt, nieces, and a nephew. He is further survived by the Kozlowski and Quandt families from Wausau, WI who have been family to him for over 60 years.

He was preceded in death by a brother, Charles (1975), his mother, Vange, (1989) and father, Elmer (2002).

Fr. Schaefer was a member of the Third-Degree Knights of Columbus, George Schreier Council No. 1069 and was a member and Past Faithful Friar of the Fourth Degree Knights of Columbus, Pere Rene Menard General Assembly No. 1449. He was also a member of the Catholic Order of Foresters since 1945, served two terms on the Board of Directors of the St. Joseph Priest Fund and was a consultant for the Fifth Diocesan Synod. He participated in several National Bowling Tournaments, was a long-time season ticket holder of the Minnesota Vikings, played semi-pro baseball in the Midwest, bowled, golfed and was a member of various Country Clubs and hunted on the family farm in Buffalo County, WI. He was the proprietor of Padre's Pinery in the township of Bradley, Tomahawk, WI and enjoyed working at the Pinery and on the family farm.

A private funeral Mass will be celebrated at St. Boniface Catholic Church, Waumandee, WI. Burial will follow in the parish cemetery. A public visitation will be held on Saturday, January 24, 2026, from 5:00 p.m. until 6:00 p.m. at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth St., Wausau, WI 54403.

records.name: Rita V. Heindl
records.summary: Rita V. Heindl, 92, of Madison, formerly of Marathon, passed away on December 22, 2025.
records.body:
Rita V. Heindl, 92, Madison, formerly of Marathon, passed away on Monday, December 22, 2025 at Agrace Hospice Care in Madison.

She was born March 28, 1933 in Marathon, daughter of the late John and Eleanor (Yonker) Vogedes. On June 8, 1953, she married her high school sweetheart, Gerald Heindl at St. Marathon Catholic Church in Marathon. He preceded her in death on July 27, 2025.

Rita began her working career at Employers Mutual Casualty Company as a correspondent for workman's comp and later worked for Marathon Cheese Corp. as a line supervisor. Among her favorite pastimes, she enjoyed reading, baking, cooking, traveling and trivia.

Survivors include her children, Jeffrey (Kim) Heindl of Egg Harbor, Mark (John Lynch) Heindl of Madison, Mike (Jill) Heindl of Shorewood, Kathy (Richard) Marx of Tampa, Florida, and Mary (Randy) Mullen of New Braunfels Texas; grandchildren, Brien (Tasha) Heindl, Leslie (Eric) Hansen, Amy Mullen, Max (Megan) Mullen, Marcus (Amanda) Mullen, Madison Heindl, Nicholas (Casey) Marx, Shanna (Jamie) Larochelle, and Cole (Taylor) Radloff; great grandchildren, Olivia, Callon, Nathan, Jordan, Dillon, Viviann, Rhett and Ellodie; and many nieces and nephews.

Besides her parents and husband, Gerald, she was preceded in death by four brothers, Lester, Hugo, John and Alfred Vogedes; and two sisters, Lucille Freiburger and Doris Fergot.

A funeral Mass for Gerald and Rita will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, January 17, 2026 at St. Mary Catholic Church, Marathon. Rev. Msgr. Joseph Diermeier will preside. Burial will be in the parish cemetery. Visitation will be on Saturday from 9:30 a.m. until time of Mass at the church.

records.name: Allen H. Hornung
records.summary: Allen H. Hornung, age 82, of Weston, passed away on January 6, 2026.
records.body:
Allen died from a long battle against cancer.

Allen was born December 3, 1943, son of the late Ervin J. and Marguerite L. Hornung.

Allen served honorably in the Army Reserve as a Drill Sergeant for six years and was stationed in Wausau. He found his true love in Elsiena Moser, whom he married in Marathon, Wisconsin, and together they shared 54 wonderful years of marriage, filled with love and laughter.

Allen was a proud graduate of Marathon High School. He was a hardworking man who dedicated over 40 years of his life to the Wausau Paper Mill.

Survivors include his wife, Elsiena Hornung of Weston; three daughters, Jennifer, Christina and Tammy; one son, Randall; sons-in-law, Ron, Bobby and Matt; four grandchildren, Dylan, Tanner, Chloe and Chase; sisters, Marella, Carol, Kathy and Bonny; brothers, Ronald and Terry; and sisters-in-law, Sandy and Joann. He is preceded in death by his parents; grandson, Tyler; sister, Angelyn; and brothers-in-law, Donald and Donald.

Private family services were held at Restlawn Memorial Park.

records.name: Arlene Wilkowski
records.summary: Arlene Wilkowski of Bevent, passed peacefully on January 10, 2026, age 77.
records.body:
She was born May 31, 1948, in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, to the late Onufry and Helen (Cherek) Charnecki. Arlene graduated from St. Joan Antida High School, Milwaukee. She began working at a bank in Milwaukee eventually retiring after 33 years at Wausau Insurance Co/Liberty Mutual Ins.

On August 26th, 1967, she was united in marriage to Leonard Wilkowski at St. Ladislaus Catholic Church in Bevent. He preceded her in death on July 30th, 2006.

Arlene loved spending time with her family and cherished moments with her children, grandchildren, and great-grandchildren. She crocheted numerous baby blankets and afghans. She loved taking pictures and was always the family event photographer, and unofficial wedding photographer, often sharing her photos. With her quick wit, she always appreciated a clever joke for giggles. Arlene also loved music – procuring a modest collection of oldies, pop, country, and rock. She had a special knack for music trivia. Arlene loved the Green Bay Packers and cheered on the Milwaukee Brewers too.

Arlene is survived by two children, Jerrold (Lynn) Wilkowski and Cindy (William) Brophy; grandchildren, Kyle (Courtney) Wilkowski, Skylin (Josh) Harris and Taylor (Darian) Stankowski; four great-grandchildren, Oli, Bliss, Kylie and Elijah. She is also remembered by sisters-in-law, Adeline Domask, Jeannette Gotchy, Natalie Wilkowski and Yvonne Wilkowski, and many nieces and nephews.

She was preceded in death by her parents; her husband, Leonard; brother, Larry Charnecki; brothers-in-law, George Wilkowski, Edwin Wilkowski and Marvin Wilkowski.

Private family memorial services were held. Burial was in St. Ladislaus Cemetery, Bevent. Sister Mary Ellen Diermeier officiated.

records.name: Beatrice L. Stanton
records.summary: Beatrice L. Stanton, age 92, of Weston, passed away on January 8, 2026.
records.body:
She was born July 16, 1933, in Wausau, the daughter of David and Almeda (Buttke) Stanton.

Bea grew up in Wausau and graduated from Wausau High School in 1951. She then paid her way through college at Concordia University, Chicago. She taught 1st grade for many years in Nashville, Canada, Washington DC, and Maryland. Later in life she began teaching massage to hundreds of students in the Chicago area, many who she still kept in contact with. In her 80's she returned to the Wausau area and enjoyed living in Birchwood Highlands and making many new friends. Once a month she attended a high school class luncheon and loved seeing everyone.

She is survived by her sisters-in-law, Phyllis Stanton, Waupaca and Jeanette Stanton, Tampa, FL. Many nieces and nephews, Sue (Jim) Bochers, Jeff Stanton, Kathy Stanton Liebforth, Scott (Lois) Stanton, Tim Stanton, Mike (Michelle) Stanton, Bruce (Michele) Stanton, Mike (Nichol) Riedel and Brenda (Tim) Kuhn.

She is preceded in death by her parents, brothers, Wallace Stanton and Fat (VR) Stanton and her sister Beverly Riedel and her brother-in-law James Riedel.

As per Bea's wishes there will be no funeral.

The family extends a special thank you to all the staff at Weston Memory Care.

Bea lived life to the fullest and was very independent to the end. She did things on her own terms and loved deeply.

records.name: Brett J. Groshek
records.summary: Brett John Groshek, age 45, of Mosinee, passed away on January 10, 2026.
records.body:
Brett passed away just one-half hour short of one year from being diagnosed with gallbladder and liver cholangiocarcinoma. Brett was born on June 22, 1980, to Wayne Groshek and Lois Long.

Brett truly found himself outdoors. He loved hunting, ice fishing, lake fishing, snowmobiling, and four-wheeling. Some would say that being stuck indoors could make him a bit crabby, as fall and winter were his favorite seasons and where he felt most at home. He enjoyed taxidermy and woodworking and took pride in creating things with his hands. Brett was a loyal Chicago Bears fan and a well-known "honey-do list" procrastinator.

Above all else, Brett was a loving husband and a proud father. His son, Mason, was his right-hand man, and Brett was a devoted "girl dad" to his twin daughters, Olivia and Madilynn, who proudly held his heart.

At the age of 38, Brett made the courageous decision to return to school for Wood Science. While juggling family life and school, he excelled—graduating with high honors, earning five certificates and one associate degree from Northcentral Technical College – Antigo Campus. Through hard work and dedication, he received scholarships each quarter to help offset the cost of his education. Brett went on to work at Featherstone Cabinetry, where he achieved the position of plant manager, and was known as a hardworking employee who was respected and loved by many.

Brett is survived by his wife of 15 years, Nicole; his children, Mason, Olivia, and Madilynn; his father, Wayne (significant other Anna Galecki) Groshek; his mother, Lois (Kevin) Long; his godparents, Lance Romanowski and Gail Lilla; brothers by choice, Troy (Jennifer) Groshek, Jacob Groshek, Connor Groshek, and John (Kim) Fandrey; as well as many aunts, uncles, cousins, and special friends.

He was preceded in death by his father-in-law, Charlie Pinkepank; his paternal grandparents, Herman (Helen) Groshek; and his maternal grandparents, John (Alice) Romanowski.

A Celebration of Brett's Life will be held on Saturday, February 7, 2026, at Lisa's at Lakeside, 214857 County Road Y, Hatley, WI 54440. Family and friends are invited to ice fish any time, because that's what Brett would be doing. Family will greet friends from 12:00 pm to 2:00 pm, with a service to take place at 2:00 pm. Family ask that you bring stories about Brett to share. Food and fellowship will follow the service.

The family would like to thank the entire Aspirus Hospice Team, especially Jennifer Yurish, north team nurse Brenda, as well as nurses Jason, and Shawn Hurt, as they all gave excellent care around the clock. Their attention and care to Brett is truly what helped the family get through these difficult days.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to the family to help offset medical expenses incurred during Brett's illness.

Brett will be remembered for his strength, his love for the outdoors, his dedication to his family, and the many lives he touched along the way.

records.name: Carol F. Kaetterhenry
records.summary: Carol F. Kaetterhenry, age 94, of Weston, passed away on January 16, 2026.
records.body:
She was born November 17, 1931, to the late Myrl and Hazel (Stevens) Pierce.

After graduating from Central High in Sheboygan, she went to work for Leverenz Shoe Company until she married Bud Kaetterhenry on August 27, 1960.

Her life revolved around church and family, caring for her children and grandchildren and running them around their various activities. She had a big vegetable garden for years and loved plastic canvas crafting. We will remember her love for God and compassion for others.

Carol is survived by her two children, Jerome Kaetterhenry and Lori Kaetterhenry; and grandchildren, Elli Wilk, Tyler (Kalli) Kaetterhenry, Amanda (Brent) Pendergrass, Erick (Tina) Jensen, and Ashlee (Hugo) Vargas.

She is preceded in death by her husband; parents; and brothers, Myrl Pierce Jr. and Gordon Pierce.

Per Carol's wishes, there will be a private family entombment.

Thank you to her caregivers at Rennes Health & Rehab, Aspirus Palliative Care and Hospice.

records.name: Donna Rogers
records.summary: Donna Rogers, age 86, of Schofield, passed away on January 12, 2026.
records.body:
She was born on February 15, 1939, to the late John and Jeanette (Jensen) O'Toole.

Donna had a deep love for all things arts and crafts. She found joy and purpose in painting, crocheting, sewing, and needlepointing, and her creativity touched many lives. She was also known for her fondness for scratch-off lottery tickets, always enjoying the simple fun they brought.

Kind and giving by nature, Donna shared her talents generously. She donated handmade blankets to ambulance services for children and supported other local nonprofits, quietly helping those in need. An amazing cook and baker, Donna once owned her own bakery, where her skills and passion shone through every creation. She was also an exceptional seamstress, and many local girls trusted her to make their prom dresses—garments crafted with care, love, and remarkable skill.

Above all, Donna loved her children and was devoted to teaching them independence. She proudly taught her sons not only how to cook, but also how to sew—life skills that reflected her belief in self-sufficiency and creativity for all.

Donna is survived by her children, Duane Tessmer; Don (Sandy) Tessmer; and Brent Rogers (fiancé Erika Henry); Stepchildren, Candy King and Dawn Carpenter; her ten grandchildren; and her great-grandchildren. She is also survived by her siblings, Dan (Joy) O'Toole and Judy (Vern) Nelson.

She was preceded in death by her husband, Vernon; her sons, Dennis and Douglas; and her siblings, Patrick O'Toole and Kathy King.

A memorial service will be held at 3:00 p.m. on Saturday, January 24, 2026, at John J. Buettgen Funeral Home, 948 Grand Avenue, Schofield. Visitation will take place from 2:00 p.m. until the time of services at the funeral home.

A Celebration of Donna's Life will be held from 10:00 am until 2:00 pm on Sunday, January 25, 2026, at 5387 4th Avenue, Pittsville, WI 54466, in the Commons Room.

Donna will be remembered for her generosity, creativity, and unwavering love for her family—stitches of kindness and care woven into every life she touched.

records.name: Ervin E. Resch
records.summary: Ervin E. Resch, age 80, of Weston, died on January 14, 2026.
records.body:
Ervin was born on September 19, 1945, in Birnamwood, the son of Emery and Lorraine (Lenzner) Resch.

On November 11, 1972, Ervin was united in marriage to Patricia (Trish) Haisan at St. Philomena Catholic Church in Birnamwood.

Ervin started out working on his farm for several years. He then became a self-employed mason worker and later worked in manufacturing at Iron Works and HammerBlow. Ervin's greatest pride and joy was his family. He was known for his selflessness and positive outlook on life. Ervin liked to stay busy and was always helping his family with projects if he didn't have his own projects to work on. Some of his favorite moments were spent at the cabin he built at Steven's Lake, where each spring he worked hard to make maple syrup with his children and grandchildren. It was a place that brought him peace and joy. He was an avid NASCAR, Brewers and Packers fan. He also enjoyed hunting and fishing, mostly because it was also precious time with his family.

Ervin is survived by his wife, Trish; six children, Jason (Jenny) Resch, Jenny (Jim Krueger) Resch, Waylon Resch, Travis (Jolene) Resch, Rachel Huebsch, and Cassie (Caleb) Zblewski; 13 grandchildren, Miranda (Ryan) Borgemoen, Dylan Resch, Justin (Teryn) Kuse, Kaitlyn (fiancé, Matthew Szymanski) Kuse, Kylie (Tyler) Thiex, Brendan 'Bucksnort' Resch, Wren Xiong, Allison Resch, Gavin and Emma Huebsch, Carson, Coralee, and Camden Zblewski; six great-grandchildren, Liam and Cole Borgemoen and Kinsley, Tamen, Truett and Tulsen Thiex; brothers and sisters, Judy (Don) Armstrong, Ron 'Berf' (Jonelle) Resch, Shirley (Tom) Schreurs, Ed (Carol) Resch, Dorothy (Ron) Gehring, Norman (Mary) Resch, Fred (Nancy) Resch, Paul (Sharon) Resch, Jean (Michael) Rolnecki, Theresa 'Kitty' (Bob) Vollendorf, Peter (Lori) Resch, Patty (Charlie) Knapp, and Janette (Jerry) Baumann; brother-in-law, John Haisan; and numerous nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends.

Ervin was preceded in death by his parents, Emery and Lorraine, father-in-law, Pete Haisan Sr. and mother-in-law and stepfather-in-law, Marian and Vernon Matsche.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 12 Noon on Wednesday, January 28, 2026, at St. Philomena Catholic Church, Birnamwood. Rev. Vicente Llagas will officiate. Visitation will be on Wednesday, from 9 AM until the time of Mass at the church.

records.name: Gail E. Woods
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Gail Elizabeth Woods, age 83, of Weston, passed away on January 9, 2026.
records.body:
She was born November 22, 1942, to the late Ellsworth and Elizabeth (Goldsworthy) Sigler, in New York.

Gail enjoyed traveling across the country and the world with her family. Reflective of her Native American heritage, Gail loved her wolf collection and dream catchers. She also enjoyed riding motorcycle, shooting and loved going out on Lake Michigan.

She loved her family deeply and will be remembered for her generous spirit.

She is survived by her son, Tom Howard; granddaughter, Karana (Jonathan) Engle; great-grandchildren, J'Bud, Kent, Jonny, Lyn, and Davin.

Per Gail's wishes, no services will be held.

records.name: Gregory A. Opper
records.summary: Gregory Allen Opper, age 69, of Birnamwood, died on January 14, 2026.
records.body:
Greg was born on May 8, 1956, in Wausau, the son of Leonard and Mary (Kastner) Opper.

Greg met the love of his life, Shelley Stadler in April of 1994. They were united in marriage on December 21, 2018. They shared a wonderful 31 years together.

Greg worked for the Village of Birnamwood and then began his trucking career, working for Delano Meverden Trucking and Ron Christiansen Trucking. He then purchased his own truck, becoming an owner/operator of S&G Trucking until June of 2018. His love for trucking drove him back to the next generation of Ron Christiansen Trucking for another round. He plowed and worked for the town of Birnamwood as well.

Greg enjoyed hunting, collecting antiques and reading hunting and trucking magazines. Greg would fix anything for anyone. He loved the outdoors and could be found sitting on his front porch watching and listening to the wildlife and the Brewer game. In the winter, he looked forward to the annual Vintage Snowmobile Ride with friends and family. He enjoyed his time with 'the boys' on Saturday afternoons and Sunday mornings at Chet & Emil's.

Greg is survived by his wife, Shelley; mother, Mary Opper; his daughter, Heather (Seth) Baird, and her children, Brandon (Ally) Norman, and Konner (Liz) Norman; his son, Jason (Katie) Opper and their children, Gaven and Taylor; great-grandchildren, Emmie, Cooper and Wesley Norman; siblings, Patricia Opper of Birnamwood, Christy (Wayne) Clark of Birnamwood, Theresa (Paul) Aas of Hibbing, MN, Keith (Darla) Opper of Wittenberg, Joan (Jim) Bohlman of Arkdale, Kurt (Tina) Opper of Birnamwood and Lori Opper of Weston; sisters-in-law, Denise Olson-Opper and Elise Wolff; mother-in-law, Carol (Richard Smith) Stadler; brothers and sisters-in-law, Ervin (Patti) Stadler Jr., Mark (Julayne) Stadler, Kathy (David) Resch and Vicky (Allan) Beran all of Birnamwood, and Susan Stadler of Shawano; and numerous nieces, nephews, great-nieces and nephews, other relatives and friends.

Greg was preceded in death by his father, Leonard; brothers, Allen, Lawrence and Thomas, father-in-law, Ervin Stadler Sr.

A Celebration of Life will be on Thursday, January 22, 2026, at Chet & Emil's banquet hall in Birnamwood. A Visitation will be from 10 AM until 12 Noon, with a sharing of memories at 12 Noon. Burial will be in St. Boniface Catholic Cemetery, in the Spring.

records.name: Jack Neuens
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Jack Neuens, age 81, of Wausau, passed away on January 15, 2026.
records.body:
Born on April 4, 1944, in Athens, Wisconsin, the son of James and Frances (Scheffler) Neuens. His life was a rich tapestry of laughter, love, and service to others. Jack married the love of his life Mary Ann Westphal on September 17, 1966, and the couple enjoyed nearly 60 years of wonderful marriage together. Jack and Mary Ann spent the majority of their married life living in Edgar for over 34 years. He also was very proud of being a part of many different choirs, starting back in 5th grade and going forward to singing in the church choir as well. He also loved to give a helping hand by volunteering at the welcoming center and various prayer groups at church.

Jack dedicated a significant portion of his working life to roles at Marathon Cheese, Conagra, and ultimately Menzner Lumber, from where he retired. His contributions were not limited to his professional endeavors; they mirrored his beliefs in hard work and community spirit, values that he instilled in his loved ones. Jack's joy in life resonated profoundly beyond the workplace; it flourished in the gardens he nurtured, in the card games that brought friends together, and in the homemade birthday cards he lovingly crafted for his family.

With an ardent passion for gardening and puzzles, Jack found solace and joy in tending to his plants and soaking up the peacefulness of nature. Yet it was his playful sense of humor and penchant for practical jokes that truly defined him, traits he carried gracefully even through his final moments. Up until the very end, he mastered weaving laughter into the fabric of each day, leaving a trail of joy that will be remembered fondly.

Jack took immense pride in his family, who were his greatest joy. Survived by his beloved wife, Mary Ann Neuens, and his children, Barb (Tim) James, Dan (Christine) Neuens, Michelle (Tim) Dahlke, Sarah (Dave) Stenberg, Angie (Thad) Jones, and Jonathan (Sarah) Neuens, and sister Carol (Rod) Westphal, he often recounted stories of their adventures together. Jack rejoiced in the company of his grandchildren: Jessie, Mindy, Zachary, Ashley, Alexandra, David, Nate, Kaytlyn, Stevie, Roscoe, Cindy, Isaac, Cameron, and Devin, as well as his great-grandchildren, Izzy, Henry, and Holly. Each moment spent with them was a treasure, filled with laughter and love, which is how he will remain etched in their memories.

While his family thrived around him, Jack also held dear the memory of those who departed before him, including his parents, James and Frances Neuens, as well as his daughter, Jenny Hack, and son, Tony Neuens. Reflecting on his siblings, Dorie, Geri, Jim, Marge, and Joyce will forever echo in the stories shared amongst those he loved, creating a reverberation of family unity that will endure.

As we gather to celebrate Jack's life, a Mass of Christian Burial will be held on Saturday, January 24, 2026, at St. Matthew Catholic Church, 229 S. 28th Ave. Wausau, WI 54401, at 12:00 PM Noon, with The Very Rev. Sebastian Kolodziejczyk celebrating. Visitation will be held from 9:00 AM until the time of mass at 12:00 PM Noon. Jack will be laid to rest at the Edgar Cemetery at a later date.

Let us remember him warmly, sharing tales of his laughter and kindness, honoring a remarkable man who embraced life with open arms and a heart full of love. Jack's spirit, richly infused with the warmth of family and friends, will always remind us of the joy that comes from laughter, love, and service to others.

The family would like to give special thanks to the Aspirus Hospital – Wausau and to Aspirus Hospice for their loving and caring support to Jack and his family. They would also like to say a special thanks to all of their family and friends who have supported them during this difficult time.

records.name: James Lutzke
records.summary: James Lutzke, age 85, of Wausau, passed away on January 14, 2026.
records.body:
James was born in Wausau, WI to William Lutzke and Clotilda Ashbeck. James is survived by his loving wife of 30 years, Linda Herbert-Lutzke and children from his first wife, Judi Smith: Cindie Zehnder (Dan), Cherie Boldt (Steve), and Jim Lutzke. Grandchildren: Josh Zehnder (Rebekah), Caleb Zehnder, Grace Zehnder, Sam Zehnder (Gina), Jamie Burns (Mario), and Bailey Boldt. Great-grandchildren: Naomi, Josiah, Melody, Addie and Mike Zehnder. Sister: Debra Stennett and other nieces, nephews, relatives and friends. He was preceded in death by his parents and great-grandchildren Jonathan and Lily Zehnder and other loved ones.

Jim loved the outdoors, especially hunting in Montana. He was a true cowboy at heart and never left the house without his hat and boots. He loved sports with football and racing at the top of the list. Just like his mother, he could fix anything, loved to dance and never missed beer time. Jim had a very generous heart and did whatever he could for his family and friends. Though his larger-than-life presence is gone, the memories he created will live on forever in the hearts of those he loved.

A funeral service will be held on January 23, 2026 at 11:00 AM at Brainard Funeral Home-522 Adams Street Wausau, WI with Fr, Janusz Kowalski officiating. Visitation will be from 10:00 AM until the hour of service at the Brainard Chapel.

records.name: John M. Schmidt
records.summary: John M. Schmidt, age 76, of Wausau, passed away on January 11, 2026.
records.body:
John was born on December 22, 1949, the first child of Lawrence and Martha (Wiersma) Schmidt in Woodstock, Illinois. He moved with his family to Colby, Wisconsin when his family purchased a dairy farm in 1959. He graduated from Colby High School in 1968.

He attended school in Milwaukee for one year to be a repair technician and worked for Mittens Appliances until he was drafted for the Vietnam War in 1969. John joked that "this was the only lottery I ever won." He spent roughly one year overseas before returning home.

John was united in marriage to his soulmate, Beverly "Peachie" Peche on October 26, 1974, in Medford, Wisconsin. They started their family and resided in Colby while working the family farm.

In 1979, John and his family moved to Mondovi, Wisconsin where he worked as a technician for Tri-State Breeders. It was here he made lifelong friends while getting to know the farmers and the area which he would call home for the next 38 years.

After realizing his true calling, John started school in 1986 at UW-Eau Claire where he would receive a degree in Elementary Education. He graduated in 1990 and immediately started teaching at Mondovi Public School. He taught every grade from 1st through 7th. In addition, he team taught, coached, and while in middle school, taught science (his favorite subject). He would continue to teach there until his retirement in 2011.

After Bev's retirement, they moved to Wausau in 2017 to be closer to family. John spent these last years making new friends in Wausau, mostly at The Landing where he played ping pong, cribbage, and socialized with the other 55+ members.

John will be remembered for his many hobbies and interests. In addition to being a husband, father, grandfather, cousin, uncle, and friend, he was a veteran, bartender (known for his Old Fashions), ball player, bowler, fisherman, hunter, gardener, and guitarist. So many people had the privilege of hearing him play guitar, including his friends at The Landing and residents at local nursing homes. But his most favorite time was spent with his two young grandsons: eating chocolate-chocolate-chip muffins and reading countless books.

John is survived by his wife of 51 years, Beverly "Peachie" of Wausau; two children, Josh (Rebecca) Schmidt of West Allis and Jessica Schmidt of Wausau; and two beautiful grandsons, Oliver and Dylan. He is further survived by his siblings, Peggy Erickson of Medford, Susan (Peter) Jecevicus of Colby, Linda Geiger of Marshfield, Rocky (Bobbi) Schmidt of Phillips, Jeri (Bill) Schmidt of Abbotsford, Judy (Mark) Sebold of Phillips, and Carol (Dan) Beyer of Medford; and many nieces, nephews, other relatives, and friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents; in-laws, Leonard and Rosella Peche; niece, Amy Schmidt; and brother-in-law, Alan Peche.

He was good man.

Visitation will begin at 1:00 p.m. on Saturday, February 7, 2026, at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Avenue, Wausau. A time to share stories and celebrate John's life will take place at 4:00 p.m. followed by military honors accorded by the American Legion Montgomery Plant Dudley Post #10.

The family requests memorial donations be made to the Never Forgotten Honor Flight (www.neverforgottenhonorflight.com) or Guitars 4 Vets (www.guitars4vets.org).

records.name: John W. Bentz
records.summary: John W. Bentz, age 83, passed away peacefully on January 12, 2026.
records.body:
He was born on October 13, 1942, the son of the late Franklin and Laona (Wenzel) Bentz.

John lived life with enthusiasm, kindness, and a sense of humor that could light up any room. He had a lifelong love of traveling and stock car racing, and in his younger years proudly raced Mopar at tracks in Plymouth and Francis Creek—memories he cherished and enjoyed sharing.

Known for his generous heart, John was always willing to lend a hand to anyone in need. His kindness was matched only by his quick wit and remarkable sense of humor. Above all else, family meant everything to him. He always looked forward to family gatherings and especially treasured time spent with his children, grandchildren, and great-grandchildren.

John was preceded in death by his wife, Darlene Bentz; his daughter, KiAnn Flenor; and his two sisters, Trudy and Sandy.

He is survived by his sons, Kevin (Jamie) Bentz and Keith (Jackie Czaikowkski) Bentz; his eight grandchildren; his nine great-grandchildren; and his loving partner of 23 years, Barbara Dallman, who stood faithfully by his side.

John will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved him, but his legacy of love, laughter, and devotion to family will live on in the hearts of those he leaves behind.

A celebration of life for John will be held on Sunday, February 8, 2026, from 1:00 pm until 5:00 PM at the Eagle's Club, 1703 S. 3rd Ave, Wausau.

The family would like to thank the staff at Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services for the extraordinary care they gave John.

records.name: Kathleen A. Harris
records.summary: Kathleen A. Harris, age 68, passed away peacefully on January 13, 2026.
records.body:
She was born on September 20, 1957, to the late Marvin and Mary (Ewing) Jelinek.

Kathleen had a deep love for life's simple pleasures. She enjoyed going fishing, playing cards with her brother and sister-in-law, and spending quiet time cross-stitching. She was a devoted fan of Westerns—especially those starring John Wayne—and rarely missed an episode of her favorite medical dramas. Nursing was more than a career for Kathleen; it was her calling, and she was passionate about caring for others.

Known for her kind and generous heart, Kathleen would do anything for anyone. She never asked questions when someone needed help—she simply showed up. Her compassion, selflessness, and unwavering support touched countless lives.

Above all else, Kathleen loved her family. She cherished every moment spent with her grandchildren and held them close to her heart.

Kathleen is survived by her daughter, Jessica (AJ) Napiwocki; her grandchildren, Carter, Reed, and Kelsey; her "pseudo-grandchild," Robyn; and her siblings, Michael (Bonnie), David (Marcella), Victor (Marilyn), Jeff (Jane), Gretchen, and Tom (Rose) Jelinek. She is further survived by several nieces, nephews, great-nieces, and great-nephews.

She was preceded in death by her parents and her niece, Jenny.

A celebration of life will be held this Summer in Richland Center, where family and friends will gather to honor Kathleen's life, her compassion, and the love she shared so freely.

records.name: Lon R. Wunsch
records.summary: Lon (Lonnie) R. Wunsch, age 60, of Athens, passed away on January 12, 2026.
records.body:
Lon was born on April 26, 1965 in Wausau, WI. He was a devoted husband, a loving father and grandfather, and a steady presence to family and friends who were fortunate enough to know him. He valued hard work, loyalty, and taking care of the people, he loved.

Above all else, Lon cherished his family. He shared a deep and enduring love with his wife, Tracy, and took great pride in his family. His strength, live out loud humor, and willingness to help others will be remembered always.

Lon is survived by his wife and best friend Tracy Riehle; stepdaughter, Nicole (Jesse) Petroski; grandchildren, Bane, Ranea, Easton; chosen children; TJ (Marissa) Gorski, Trish Gorski, Zach Hallas, grandchildren; Tye, Lili, Ava, and Ryder, brothers, Pat (Diane) Wunsch, Jeff (Lisa) Wunsch, nieces & nephews; Lisa (Andy), Ben (Trish), Ashley, great niece and great nephews Lydia, Will, Leo, Charolette, Harper, Isabella, Chloe and Aunt Eleanor. He was preceded in death by his parents Lawerence & Joan and sister Kathleen Wunch-Fehl.

Lon was a devoted husband and a loyal, steady presence to those who knew him. He also shared a special bond with his beloved dog, Bella, who was never far from his side and brought him constant companionship.

Lon was a proud retired heavy equipment operator and longtime member of Local Union 139, where he dedicated many years to hard work and craftsmanship. He took great pride in his work and the union that supported his career.

Outside of work, Lon had a true passion for NHRA drag racing and NASCAR, never missing a chance to follow the sport he loved. Whether watching or traveling to a race, both sports brought him genuine joy.

A celebration of life will be held on June 27th, 2026 at the Eagle's Club, 1703 S 3rd Ave., Wausau, WI, at 3:00 p.m.

Lon's legacy lives on in the lives he touched and the love he gave so freely. He will be deeply missed and forever remembered.

records.name: Mark D. Welbes
records.summary: Mark D. Welbes, age 79, passed away on January 7, 2026.
records.body:
He was born on January 5, 1947, in Illinois and spent most of his life in Wisconsin, a place he proudly called home—except for a brief adventure in Alaska, where he went in search of "the big buck."

Mark worked in the dairy industry throughout his life, with over 20 years as a filed technician for DHIA, where he met many people who became lifetime friends.

An avid hunter and fisherman, Mark loved the outdoors and always had a story to tell—especially about the "big ones" that got away, or the tree limbs he managed to catch along the way. In his younger years, Mark enjoyed playing baseball and carried that love of the game with him throughout his life.

Mark was a natural storyteller—quick with a joke, full of stories, and always good for a laugh. He leaves behind many memories that his family will continue to share and cherish.

He is survived by his brothers, George Welbes Jr. (Pamela), and John Welbes (Clarice); family members, Andrew (Lillie) Curran and Melanie (Ray) Easterlin; as well as several cousins, nieces and nephews. He was preceded in death by his parents, George and Cecilia (Vogl) Welbes.

Mark will be deeply missed and fondly remembered.

The family would like to thank both Bedrock Health Care Services of Abbotsford and Hope Hospice and Palliative Care of Medford for their care and support of Mark while he is in their care; as well as his neighbors and friends who were always there for him.

Per Marks wishes no services will be held.

records.name: Nancy L. Vera
records.summary: Nancy L. Vera, age 87, of Mosinee, passed away peacefully on January 15, 2026.
records.body:
She was born on November 8, 1938, in Wausau, WI. Daughter to the late Louis and Helen (Tyskiewicz) Kwiatkowski. She married Ernest Vera on February 8, 1958, in Milwaukee, WI, he preceded her in death in 2008. Nancy and Ernie owned and operated the "Up The Hill Bar" in Pike Lake for 14 years until 1995. After they moved to Mosinee she began a career with Hancock Fabrics to keep her in fabric for the hundreds of clothes and costumes she loved to make for all the grandchildren and great grandchildren.

Nancy was a devout Catholic who raised her children to know and follow Jesus which brought her great peace and joy. She had a servant's heart always caring and giving to those she saw in need and volunteering her time, talents, and cookies for countless events at St. Paul's. Nothing brought a broader smile to her face than spending time with her grandchildren and great grandchildren playing, reading, coloring, and of course, making cookies and knitting afghans for all of them, including some of her children's friends. Nancy was known for baking many types of Christmas cookies enjoyed year-round. Empty cookie tins often made their way back to her for refills with kind notes of thanks and appreciation.

Nancy is survived by her children; Ernest (Lynn) Vera of Sunderland, England, Mark (Noel) Vera of Edmond, OK, Steve Vera, Jenny Vera of Hatley, Kathryn (James Walter) Mottl of Plainfield, IL, Theresa (Bobby Lee) Hoffmann of Hatley, Ann (Tom) Ahles of Wausau. Grandchildren, Robert, Dana, Jessica, Cheyanne, Dustyn, Adam, Danielle, Sydney, ReHanah, Savanah, Laura, Garrett, Neil, and Heather; great-grandchildren, Elaina, Nicholas, Isabella, Harrison, Alicia, Melody, Sierra, Hudson, Jax, Leah, Jameson, Avaylah, and Breven. Nancy is also survived by siblings John (Janice) Kwiatkowski of Powell WY, Jean Oppermann of Wausau, Louis (Janie) Kwiatkowski of McFarland, and Charles (Frances) Kwiatkowski of Columbia, SC; as well as a brother-in-law, Terry Klegin and many nieces, nephews and Uncle Tom Tyskiewicz.

Besides her parents and husband Ernie, Nancy was preceded in death by her sister, Alice Klegin who survived 39 years after receiving a kidney from Nancy.

A Mass of Christian Burial will take place at 11:00 a.m., on Tuesday, January 20, 2026, at St. Paul Catholic Church, Mosinee. Father Aaron Becker will officiate. Burial will take place at Gate of Heaven Cemetery. Visitation will be from 9:00 a.m., until the time of service, all at the church.

The family thanks the doctors and nurses of the Marshfield Medical Center ICU who provided amazing care and compassion to Nancy and family.

records.name: Ruth M. Krugler
records.summary: Ruth M. Krugler, age 93, of Merrill, passed away on January 13, 2026.
records.body:
She was born on March 1, 1932 in Merrill to the late Julius and Marie (Schwartz) Kuckhahn. She was baptized at Trinity Lutheran Church in the Town of Berlin and later confirmed on April 14, 1946. She grew up on the family dairy farm in the town of Corning where she attended Corning School and then graduated from Merrill High School with the class of 1950. She married Leroy H. Krugler on September 2, 1951, a union lasting 56 years. He passed away on November 25, 2007. In 2011 she met William Frederick, and they exchanged marriage vows on June 30, 2012 at St Paul Lutheran Pine River. He passed away on July 23, 2015.

Ruth worked as a bookkeeper for Knispel & Latzig John Deere dealership for over 20 years, until her retirement. Ruth was passionate about her family genealogy, self-publishing several books documenting the family tree.

She was a woman of strong faith and dedication to her Lord, being very active in her church bible study, ladies aid, and helping at the fair stand. Ruth was a very faithful, loving, and generous woman. She was known to play cards, work on puzzles, and enjoy live music and plays in the area.

Ruth is survived by her children: Bonnie Wilkum of Kiel, WI, Julie (Michael) Henrichs of Merrill, Steven (Stacia) Krugler of Junction City, WI and David (Tina) Krugler of Marshfield, WI, grandchildren: Jason (Sue) Breitzman, Robyn (Luke) Titel, Erin (Alex) Tyink, Shawn (Kathryn) Henrichs, Scott (Melissa) Henrichs, Andrew Krugler, Allison (Brandon) Westholm, Justin Krugler, and Samantha Krugler, great grandchildren: Olivia, Cosette, Jayden, Marissa, Elora, Travis, Talon, Jesse, Jordan, Odin, Otto, Daniel, Ameliah, Wallace, and Wesley, her stepson Mark Fredrick (Chansa) and 2 step grandchildren. She is further survived by her sister: Rosemarie Given, brother-in-law: Art (Lu) Krugler. She was preceded in death by her parents, husbands: Leroy Krugler and William Frederick, son-in-law: Craig Wilkum and brother-in-law: Fred Krugler.

The funeral service for Ruth will be held on Friday, January 30, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. at St. John Lutheran Church, Merrill. Pastor Neely Owen will officiate. Visitation will be held on Friday, January 30, 2026, from 10:00 a.m. until the time of the service, also at the church. Entombment at Merrill Memorial Park will immediately follow the service.

In lieu of flowers, the family requests that donations in Ruth's memory be directed to St. John Lutheran Church, Merrill.

A special thank you to the staff at Bell Tower Residence and Aspirus Palliative Care in Wausau. The care and compassion shown to Ruth and her family was much appreciated.

records.name: Amber Kathleen Gilman
records.summary: Amber Kathleen Gilman, age 37, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on January 15, 2026.
records.body:
Amber Kathleen Gilman, age 37, passed away peacefully and went to her Heavenly home on January 15, 2026.

Amber was born on July 25, 1988, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to John Gilman and Connie Broome. She worked at Great Lakes Cheese for ten years, where she was known for her dedication and strong work ethic.

Above all else, Amber was a devoted mother. Her children were the center of her world, and she cherished every moment spent with them. She loved experiencing new adventures with her children and creating memories that will be forever held close to their hearts.

Amber's favorite color was purple, and she found great peace and happiness fishing at the family cabin in Eagle River. Time spent on the water fishing brought her comfort and joy. Amber was a beautiful soul whose smile could brighten any room and whose kindness left a lasting impression on everyone she met.

Amber is survived by her parents, John Gilman and Connie Broome; her sisters, Amanda and Alexandria; her half-sister, Erin; her stepbrother, Cory; her children, McKayla, Ayden, Sophia, Hunter, Gunner, Kain, and Levi; and her significant other, Xeng.

She was preceded in death by her stepmother, Tracey Pfantz.

A Celebration of Life will be held at John J. Buettgen Funeral Home in Schofield, Wisconsin, on Sunday, January 25, 2026. Visitation will begin at 1:00 p.m., followed by the service at 2:00 p.m.

records.name: Carol L. Sell
records.funeral_home: Helke Funeral Home and Cremation Service
records.summary: Carol Leone Sell, age 79, of Wausau, passed away on January 16, 2026.
records.body:
Carol Leone Sell, 79, of Wausau, passed away on Friday, January 16, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Medical Center.

She was born on January 18, 1946, in Buffalo County, Wisconsin, to the late John and Esther (Stumprude) Olson. She attended high school at Eleva-Strum High School class of 1964. Carol was employed at Northcentral Technical College as a secretary prior to her retirement.

Carol enjoyed, playing cards and candy making. Most of all she loved spending time with her great-grandchildren who always referred to her as GITYH (grandma in the yellow house).

She is survived by her children: daughter, Trisha Downing, of Wausau, and son, Sean Sell, of Phoenix, Arizona; grandchildren, Sabrina (Pagels) and Cassie Beasley; great-grandchildren, Romeo, Skye, Liam, and Kingston; her siblings, Florence Deustcher and Dennis (Sharon) Olson; and many nieces, nephews, friends, and other family members.

She was preceded in death by her parents; and her siblings: Florence Olson, Violet Cleasby, Lorraine Bauer, Delores Bauer, Jerome Olson, Russell Olson, Leone Olson, Edith Cleasby, and Doris Hansen.

The family would like to thank Aspirus for their wonderful care through the years.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made to organization of your choosing, in Carol's name.

In accordance with Carol's wishes, cremation has taken place and no services will be held.

records.name: Charles O. Sigris
records.summary: Charles Oscar Sigrist, age 82, passed away peacefully on January 14, 2026.
records.body:
Charles Oscar Sigrist, who left this world peacefully on January 14, 2026, at the age of 82. Born on February 1, 1943, in Jersey City, NJ, Charles was a remarkable man who touched the lives of everyone he met.

Charles will be fondly remembered as a devoted husband to his wife, Irene Sigrist, with whom he shared an incredible 58 years of love and companionship. Their bond was a beautiful example of partnership and commitment, a testament to the love they nurtured through the years.

He served in the Navy and carried the values of duty and service throughout his life. He was not just an accountant by profession; he was a true handyman, always ready to lend a helping hand to friends and neighbors. With a deep passion for history and philosophy, Charles was an avid reader whose inquisitive mind sparked many delightful conversations. He could often be found diving into a fascinating book or sharing his profound insights on the mysteries of life.

His gentle spirit, infectious laughter, and ability to make everyone feel welcome will be profoundly missed. He leaves behind his beloved wife, Irene Sigrist; sister-in-law, Christine (Juan) Fernandez; nephews, Alex Fernandez and Daniel Fernandez; and many extended family and friends.

records.name: Jeanne L. Baranowski
records.summary: Jeanne L. Baranowski, age 67, of Wausau, passed away on January 14, 2026.
records.body:
Jeanne L. Baranowski, 67, of Wausau, passed away on Wednesday, January 14, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

She was born on July 27, 1958, in Milwaukee, to William and Darlene (Magrecke) Raebel. Jeanne was united in marriage to Danny Baranowski on July 9, 1979, in Wausau. Together they started a family and Jeanne loved and provided for them at home while Danny worked. She also worked over the years at Mountain Lanes, Dale's Weston Lanes, and North Star Lanes where she became friends with many people. Jeanne also enjoyed 3D painting shirts and spending time outdoors fishing. Most of all she loved and cherished the time spent with her twin granddaughters.

Survivors include her husband, Danny Baranowski; daughter, Ashlee (Timothy) Bishop; and twin granddaughters, Anyanka and Serendipity Bishop.

She was preceded in death by her mother, Darlene "Ski" Raebel; brother, Craig Raebel; and grandparents, Walter "Shorty" and Helen "Charlie" Magrecke.

A celebration of life will be planned for this spring/summer.

records.name: Joan Blair
records.summary: Joan Blair, age 91, of Wausau, passed away on January 16, 2026.
records.body:
Joan (Felten, Samse) Blair, age 91, went to be with her Lord and Savior Jesus Christ on January 16, 2026 with her family by her side at the Aspirus Hospice House in Wausau.

She was born on September 15, 1934 to the late Paul and Amanda Felten, in DePere, WI. She was a shy and quiet girl growing up on a farm where she enjoyed riding her horse, Babe.

After graduation from DePere High School, Joan studied to be a medical transcriptionist and then worked at Sheboygan Memorial Hospital. It was in Sheboygan that her best friend, Marlys, set her up on a blind date and met the man she would marry, Ronald Samse on May 5, 1956.

Together they had three children, Jeffrey, Cheryl and Steven, and she spent years raising her children before returning to her career as a medical transcriber at Wausau Medical Center. She was a faithful and loving wife to her husband, Ron, for 40 years until his death in 1996. Before his death, they enjoyed a few years of retirement at "the cottage" they built together on Lake Sherwood in Nekoosa, WI, while wintering in Sebring, FL.

Joan met R. Morton Blair at a grieving class in Sebring, FL and they were married in 1997. They spent the next 28 years traveling the world until his death in 2025. When they were not traveling, she enjoyed quilting, playing her digital piano, playing bridge, and was active in the Golf Hammock Women's Club and the Sebring Women's Club. Joan was a member of St. John's United Methodist Church of Sebring, FL where they had many friends.

After Morton's death, Joan "came back home" to Wisconsin where her children, grandchildren and great grandchildren reside. She took up residence at Acorn Hill Senior Living in Mosinee, WI, where she made new friends and was known for her quiet sweet demeanor.

Joan is survived by her brother, Roger (Dottie) Felten; three children, Jeffrey (Sheryl) Samse, Cheryl (Peter) Schneider, and Steven (Leslie) Samse; grandchildren, Bridget (Ryan) Friberg, Michelle (Bryan) Schueller, Paul (Alicia) Schneider, Sam (Kim) Schneider, Philip (Becky) Xanatos, Leah Schneider, Owen (Shosie) Samse, and Laura Samse; and six great grandchildren; as well as many nieces, nephews and friends.

She is preceded in death by one child, Cynthia; her husband, Ronald Samse; husband, R. Morton Blair; sister, Ruth (Gordon) Pedersen; sister-in-law, Darlene Samse and Janice (Donald) Schaetz.

Funeral Service will be held on Friday January 23, 2025 at 11:00 AM at Immanuel Baptist Church (152111 Tulip Ln, Wausau, WI 54401) with Pastor Rob Love Officiating. Visitation will be from 10:00 AM until the hour of service at the church. Burial will be at a later date at Restlawn Memorial Park Cemetery.

The family would like to thank the caretakers at Acorn Hill Senior Living in Mosinee and the compassionate care team at the Aspirus Hospice House.

records.name: Mary A. Nowicki
records.summary: Mary Ann Nowicki, age 92, of Athens, died peacefully on January 15, 2026.
records.body:
Mary Ann Nowicki, 92, Athens, died peacefully with family at her side on January 15, 2026.

She was born August 8, 1933, in the town of Rietbrock, daughter of the late Joe and Stella (Handlosky) Nowicki. She loved her dogs and growing up on a farm there were many escapades with her cousins Johnny and Bobby and sister, Helen that raised the hair on their father's heads.

She worked in Wausau for a few years and in January 1955, she went to Milwaukee to work at Briggs and Stratton for 31 years serving on the union elections committee for a number of years.

When bowling caught her interest, she bowled from 1960 – 2011 and entered 25 national tournaments all around the country and served as league secretary organizing many tournaments.

Mary Ann was the "extreme couponer" in the 80's finding the best bargains in the city! When her trunk was full she would head home to Athens where everybody enjoyed trunk shopping.

She loved giving people rides to the airport, taking families to Brewers games, the zoo or wherever.

Survivors include her three sisters, Helen Bogaczyk, Chris (John) Delago, Bernice Manhardt and three brothers, Joe (Jane) Nowicki, Ed (Mary) Nowicki and Richard Nowicki; many beloved nieces and nephews and many greats.

She was preceded in death by her parents and siblings, Phyllis (Mel) Mader and Theresa Schwartz.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated 11:00 a.m. Saturday, January 24, 2026, at Holy Family Catholic Church, Poniatowski. Rev. Alan Wierzba will preside. Burial will be in the parish cemetery. Visitation will be Saturday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service all at Holy Family Catholic Church, Poniatowski.

In lieu of flowers, Mary Ann would appreciate donations to be given to St. Anthony Catholic School or the Athenian Living Community where she received outstanding, loving care.

The family expresses their heartfelt gratitude to the Athenian and to St. Croix Hospice for everything.

records.name: Rickie Geno
records.summary: Rickie Geno, age 72, of Wausau, passed away on January 18, 2026.
records.body:
It is with heavy hearts that we share the passing of Rickie Geno, affectionately known as Rick, who passed away on January 18, 2026, in Wausau, Wisconsin, at the age of 72.

Born on January 27, 1953, in Waukon, Iowa, the son of Leonard & Ruth (Hager) Geno. Rick was blessed with a remarkable sense of humor. His love for life was reflected in his many hobbies and interests. An outdoorsman at heart, Rick found immense joy in hunting, fishing, and camping. He was always eager to share these experiences with family and friends. He was also a die-hard sports fan, unwaveringly cheering on the Green Bay Packers.

Family was at the core of Rick's being. He took immense pride in his ability to fix anything but, more importantly, he cherished the time spent with his loved ones, especially his grandchildren. Rick's legacy of love and dedication will forever be in their hearts. He is survived by his beloved wife, Helen Geno, and their daughters, Julie (Jim) Lueck, Angie (Ben) Boese, Allie (James) Michka, and Amber Geno. Rick's spirit lives on in his grandchildren: Trent (fiancé Alaina Rupple) Geno, Erykah Robinson, Bria Robinson, Autumn Lueck, Josh Boese, Jon Boese, Aurora Boese, Noah Michka, and Luna Michka, each of whom brought him boundless joy. Rick is also remembered by his loving sisters, Ivy Saenz, Julie (John) Geno and Marsha (Annette Kronberg) Geno. Rick is also survived by nieces, nephews, other relatives, his closest friends, "Shaggy", Dennis Lynch, and Dennis Bradley, and many of his other hunting and fishing buddies.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Leonard and Ruth Geno, his brother, Cliff Geno, and brother-in-law, Ben Saenz.

A memorial service to honor Rick's life will be held on Saturday, February 21, 2026, at 11:00 AM at St. Peter Ev. Lutheran Church, 1351 Grand Avenue, Schofield, WI. Visitation will be held from 10:00 AM until the time of service at 11:00 AM, with a lunch to follow.

Rick leaves behind a legacy of love, laughter, and resilience, and while he may no longer walk among us, his memory will forever remain in our hearts.

records.name: Ronald H. Gut
records.summary: Ronald H. Gut, age 85, passed away peacefully on January 18, 2026.
records.body:
Ronald H. Gut, 85, passed away peacefully on January 18, 2026. He was born on March 28, 1940, to the late Herman and Mabel (Garske) Gut.

Ronald graduated from Wausau High School and went on to attend the University of Wisconsin–Stevens Point, where he earned his bachelor's degree. It was there that he met the love of his life, Linda Smith—a relationship clearly meant to be, as Linda crossed the ocean from Hawaii to attend UW–Stevens Point in Wisconsin.

Known affectionately as "Big Tug," Ronald had a deep love for music, especially polkas, and enjoyed playing the accordion. He was an avid sports fan who proudly cheered on the Green Bay Packers, Milwaukee Brewers, and Wisconsin Badgers.

Ronald will be remembered for his love of family, music, and deer camp, and for the joy and laughter he brought to those who knew him.

Ronald dedicated 30 years of service to Wausau Insurance, working as a claims specialist before retiring. He was a member of the Maine Lions and the American Legion Montgomery-Plant-Dudley Post 10, where he enjoyed fellowship and community involvement.

He is survived by his beloved wife, Linda; his children, Julie Esser, Jeff (Audrey) Gut, and Ron "Little Tug" Gut Jr.; seven cherished grandchildren; and four great-grandchildren, with another great grandbaby on the way.

Visitation will be held from 3:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. on Tuesday, January 27, 2026, at John J. Buettgen Funeral Home, 948 Grand Avenue, Schofield. Military honors will take place at 3:30 p.m. at the funeral home.

In lieu of flowers, please remember to be KIND.

records.name: Stefanie Geiger
records.summary: Stefanie Geiger, age 81, of Wausau, passed away on January 16, 2026.
records.body:
Stefanie Geiger, 81, passed away on January 16, 2026, at Aspirus Hospice House in Wausau, Wisconsin. Stefanie was born in Enkenbach, Germany on January 19, 1944, to Stefan Henrich and Elisabetha Henrich nee Lorenz. She was educated in Germany as a bookkeeper. She married Dennis Frank Geiger on January 20, 1966, in Enkenbach, Germany. The family moved to Wausau in 1973.

Stefanie loved her family. She is preceded in death by her husband, Dennis Frank Geiger. She is survived by four children, including her sons, Dennis (Jane) Geiger, Steve (Patti) Geiger, and two daughters, Pamela (Tod) Tesch, Susi Geiger. She was a proud grandmother to six grandchildren, Samuel (Ashleigh) Tesch, Emily (Boaz) Rodriguez, Max (Anna) Geiger, Sofie (Jonathan) Miller, Andrew Geiger, and Ellie Geiger; two step-grandchildren, Olivia LaFevre, Michael LaFevre; and three step-great-grandchildren.

She is survived by siblings, including Georg Henrich, Helma Doll, Adelwine Henrich, and is preceded in death by Elisabeth Stumpf, Irma Weissmann, Karola Henrich, Martin Henrich, Richard Henrich.

She is survived by her sisters- and brothers-in-law: Joan Schmidt, Janice Geiger, Marlene Behnke, Bonnie (Gilbert) Slivicke, Sue Fluegge, Beth (Kent) Fluegge, and preceded in death by: Donald Schmidt, Gerald Geiger, Jerome Behnke, Dan Fluegge, Daniel Kleman, Gloria Kleman, Joe and Catherine Geiger. She is also survived by many nieces and nephews.

In addition to her love of family and friends, her passions were knitting, writing, and volunteering. When her kids were young, she volunteered at Franklin Elementary School PTO, Wausau-Merrill German Club, and St. Michael Catholic Church. Throughout the years, she knitted colorful hats, sweaters, mittens, socks and more for family and friends. She will be remembered by colleagues from Northcentral Technical College as an administrative assistant and for writing speeches for reunions and celebrations even into her retirement. She also wrote poems for friends and family on their birthdays and for members of her "Good Grief" group which she attended each Tuesday as much as possible since 2013 with her friend and sister-in-law Marlene Behnke.

She was a member of Saint Michael Catholic Church in Wausau where she had volunteered as a catechist when her children were young. A celebration of her life will take place Thursday evening, January 22, 2026, at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau, with visitation from 4-7 p.m. The funeral Mass will be celebrated by Rev. Tom Lindner at St. Michael Catholic Church, 611 Stark Street, Wausau, on Friday, January 23, 2026, with visitation beginning at 10:00 a.m. followed by the Mass at 11:00 a.m. A lunch and fellowship to follow.

We appreciate the care she was given throughout the years at Aspirus Wausau Hospital and in recent days at Aspirus Hospice House in Wausau.

records.name: Theresa A. Wysocki
records.summary: Theresa Ann Wysocki, age 83, passed away peacefully on January 16, 2026.
records.body:
Theresa "Terri" Ann Heil Wysocki, 83, passed away peacefully at Aspirus Wausau Hospital on Friday, January 16, 2026, surrounded by family.

Theresa was born on January 10, 1943 in Waukegan, Illinois to Ervin and Rosina (Diedrich) Heil. She was the second of eleven children. In 1950, the family moved to a farm near Marathon. Theresa attended St. Rose Convent in La Crosse for high school and later graduated from Viterbo University with a degree in Education.

After eleven years in the convent, Theresa decided to pursue a new path and moved to Milwaukee with four friends where she taught elementary school in West Allis. On October 3, 1970, she married Ronald Wysocki in Marathon. The couple lived in Milwaukee until they built their home in Port Washington in 1979.

In 1972, Theresa became self-employed with the Figurette Company, eventually becoming a regional manager, a role that allowed her to travel. She was an active member of the Senior Center in Port Washington where she taught Mahjong and served on the board. Ron and Theresa were married for over forty years before he preceded her in death on January 11, 2011.

In 2018, Theresa moved to Wausau to be closer to family. She joined The Landing in Wausau where she taught Mahjong and Mexican Train. In 2022, she moved to Renaissance Assisted Living, where she continued teaching games and participated in many activities.

Theresa was known for her outgoing personality, her love of meeting new people, and her ability to make everyone feel welcome.

Theresa is survived by her siblings: Richard (Lynn) Heil, Weston; Arnold (Luanne) Heil, Wausau; James (Janet) Heil, Venetia, PA; Rose Nerad, Bellingham, WA; Roger (Joanne) Heil, Wausau; Eugene (Paula) Heil, Marathon; Barbara (Rodney) Roeder, Weston; Margaret (Barry) Busse, Wausau; sister-in-law, Carol Heil, Wausau; and brother-in-law, Greg Stark, Edgar; godson, Bradley (Cory Voigt) Heil, Ringle; as well as many nieces and nephews and friends.

She was preceded in death by her husband; her parents; her in-laws, Frank and Genevieve Wysocki; her sister, Elaine Heil-Stark; and her brother, Dennis Heil.

The family wishes to extend heartfelt thanks to Renaissance Assisted Living and the Aspirus ICU for the compassionate care given to their sister, Theresa.

A Celebration of Life will be held on Wednesday, February 4, 2026, at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Avenue, Wausau. Visitation will be from 9:30 a.m. until the time of services at 11:00 a.m. Burial will take place in the spring.

records.name: Vicky L. Ramsey
records.summary: Vicky L. Ramsey, age 76, of Marathon, passed away peacefully on January 17, 2026.
records.body:
Vicky L. Ramsey, 76, Marathon passed away peacefully on Saturday, January 17, 2026 at Meadows of Marathon while under the care of Heartland Hospice.

She was born February 26, 1949 in Wausau, daughter of the late Jerome and Pearl (Church) Brickner.

Vicky worked in many roles throughout her career. She was a licensed beautician, spent several years in data entry, and most recently in customer service and sales. She had a knack for building relationships with customers.

Vicky enjoyed spending countless hours planting flowers, oil-painting, ceramics, travelling, sewing, photography, and spending time with her grandchildren.

She saw beauty in almost everything she looked at. She had an adventurous and thrill seeking personality. She never passed up an opportunity to parasail, zipline, or take a dip in the ocean. She enjoyed traveling to other countries to experience their culture and explore their surroundings. She will be remembered as someone who never met a stranger, big-hearted, and dedicated to God. Her love for her family and God was unending.

Survivors include her children, John (Patty) Ramsey, Grayslake, IL and Jody (Melissa) Ramsey, Weston, WI, five grandchildren (Tia, Dylan, Blake, Joey, and Ethan), her siblings, Carol (Jim) Studzinski, Lorraine Wilichowski, Ginger Weiks and Gail (Steve) Studinski and many nieces and nephews.

Besides her parents she was preceded in death by her brothers, Wayne, Ray, and Jerry Brickner and a sister, Romayne Breaman.

The Funeral Mass will be celebrated at 1:00 p.m. Friday, January 23, 2026 at St. Mary Catholic Church, Marathon. The Rev. Msgr. Jospeh Diermeier will preside. Burial will be in the parish cemetery. Visitation will be held on Friday from 11:00 a.m. until the time of services all at the church.

records.name: David Allen Svacina
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: David Allen Svacina, born May 7, 1947, in Rice Lake, Wisconsin.
records.body:
"It was his smile. His smile drew me to him like a moth to a flame. He was the entire package. Smart as a whip. Handsome. Charming. Funny. And most important of all, Decent, Kind, and Caring." (Darlene)

David Allen Svacina was born May 7th, 1947, in Rice Lake, WI, to Reynold and Florence Svacina. He graduated from Rice Lake H.S. (1965) and UW River Falls (1969).

During his senior year at River Falls, he fell madly in love with his future wife, Darlene Marie Gagnon, and they were married in the chapel on campus on October 25, 1969.

Dave's first job was as a bank examiner for the State of Wisconsin, followed by district manager for H&R Block. He partnered with another tax professional and presented at seminars around the country. Dave recognized the need for a full time tax and accounting business in town, so he founded Wausau Tax and Accounting. Later, Dave worked at Peoples State Bank and was tasked with starting their Investment Center. He retired in 2007 as Executive Vice President. After retirement, he worked part-time at a community bank reviewing loans.

Dave was a graduate of Leadership Wausau, was a longtime member and office holder for the Wausau Noon Optimists, and served on the Executive Committee of Farm Progress Days. He was a member and president of the Board of Directors of the Wisconsin Valley Fair for 18 years. Dave also served on the Board of Directors for The Women's Community and a local community bank.

Dave was a proud hunter and took a special trip with his son Dustin to northern Quebec and came home with two caribou. Later, he brought home an elk from New Mexico. Dave, Darlene, and Dustin harvested several deer from their land. He loved to fish and took trips to the Great Lakes and caught halibut in Alaska.

Dave was adept at sports, as a lap swimmer, tennis player, cyclist, hiker, and his favorite sport, golf. He was most proud of his 32 that he shot during league play at Rib Mountain Golf Course during league play on July 7, 1996.

Dave was the best host-father to countless exchange students from all over the world. We usually hosted young women and sometimes, on arrival, were fearful of the "man of the house." Inevitably, they fell in love with his beautiful smile, and they ended up teasing each other mercilessly.

David is survived by his wife of 56 years, Darlene; son, Dustin Svacina, and partner Keli Kleinschmidt, and grandson, Gavin Svacina; daughter, Dixie Swartwood, and son-in-law, Gary Swartwood, Lake Zurich, IL, and granddaughters, Lucy and Delilah Swartwood. Three brothers preceded him in death: Leigh, Dale and Don Svacina.

records.name: Diane Bemis
records.summary: Diane Bemis, age 85, passed away on January 23, 2026.
records.body:
Diane Bemis, age 85, passed away on January 23, 2026.

Diane was born in Saukville, WI to Frank and Anna Hiber. Diane married Clarence Bemis of Adell, WI in 1983 in Belguim WI.

She enjoyed riding her motorcycle with her husband all across the United States. later in life Diane lived at Renaissance in Weston and enjoyed many activites including knitting, crocheting, and jig saw puzzles, Diane especially enjoyed spending time with family and friends and will be missed by all that knew her.

Diane is survived by: (3)Children: Cathie Mason, Todd (Cindy) Scheftgen, Lori (Randy) Klopp, (6)Grandchildren, (2)Great-Grandchildren: (2)Sisiter: and many other nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends.

She was preceded in death by parents, Her husband: Clarence Bemis, and Brother: Jack Hiber.

records.name: Jean Ann Anderson
records.summary: Jean Ann Anderson, age 77 of Wausau, passed away on January 22, 2026.
records.body:
Jean Ann Anderson, age, 77 of Wausau, passed away peacefully on Thursday, January 22, 2026 at Pride TLC Therapy and Living Campus.

She was born on February 1, 1948 in Minot, North Dakota, a daughter of Herbert and Eleanor (Peake) Guenthner. She was married to Rodney Anderson.

Jean embraced a life with a warmth and kindness that left a lasting impression on all who knew her. A true homebody, Jean devoted her life to her family and created a nurturing environment that joyfully revolved around her kids and grandkids. They were the light of her life, bringing her immense joy and pride. Jean possessed an unwavering ability to listen and support, always offering a comforting presence and wise counsel when needed. Her children and grandchildren were not just part of her life; they were her greatest legacy.

Known for her steadfast nature and positive spirit, Jean accepted life's challenges with quiet confidence and a positive attitude. She was a kind soul, genuinely caring for others and finding joy in the simple pleasures of life, such as snacking on tiny bits of chocolate and peanut butter. Her passion for reading murder mystery novels was only rivaled by her commitment to fostering close relationships, whether at home, with friends, or while working at Target and her other jobs. Though not a social butterfly, she possessed a unique ability to bring people together, creating bonds that reminded us all of the importance of connection and community.

Jean remained a universally positive force for her family, embodying a silent strength that inspired those around her. Her quiet yet firm approach to life taught many the value of resilience and kindness in the face of life's ups and downs.

She is survived by, her children: Jenny (Keli'i Rice) Anderson, Aaron (Kristen) Anderson; grandchildren: Brooklyn Hess, Olivia Hess, Victoria Anderson, William Anderson; siblings: William Guenthner, and John Guenthner. She is preceded in death by, her parents: Herbert and Eleanor Guenthner; husband: Rodney Anderson; and siblings: Janice Guenthner, and Dewey Guenthner.

records.name: Stephen Anthony Martin
records.summary: Stephen Anthony Martin, age 70, passed away on January 22, 2026.
records.body:
Stephen Anthony Martin, 70, passed away on Thursday, January 22, 2026, at Ken Crass Memorial Home in Merrill.

Stephen was born on January 27, 1955, in Wausau, the son of Arthur and Ida (Goldsmith) Martin.

Stephen attended the EACH day programming, the Adult Day Services at North Central Health Care Facility and the Aurora Day Programming.

Stephen was preceded in death by his parents and his brother, Scott.

Stephen's laugh, smile and joyful personality will be missed by all of those who knew him. On behalf of the family and his guardian, we would like to extend our gratitude to the staff of Ken Crass Memorial Home and Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services.

records.name: Alice V. Weiler
records.summary: Alice V. Weiler, 94, died Thursday, January 22, 2026, under the care of Hope Hospice at her home.
records.body:
Alice V. Weiler, 94, died Thursday, January 22, 2026, under the care of Hope Hospice at her home.

She was born December 9, 1931, in Wausau, daughter of the late William and Margaret (Niewolny) Kremsreiter. On December 5, 1951, she married Donald J. Weiler at St. John Catholic Church, Edgar and he preceded her in death on September 16, 2006.

Alice loved gardening, canning, cooking, baking, camping, fishing, playing cards, polka dancing and spending time with her family.

Survivors include her children, Bonnie (Terry) Leffel, Richard Weiler, Donna Jean Weiler, Rita (David) Stevens, Calvin Weiler, Gail Weiler, Tammy Weiler and Julie (Scott) Jensen; daughter-in-law, Donalyn Weiler; 36 grandchildren, 79 great-grandchildren, 25 great great-grandchildren; siblings, Shirley (Tom) VonLoh, Diane Dahl, Lola (Gary) Radloff, Judy Riemer, Mary Lou Kremsreiter, Ronald (Marlene) Kremsreiter, Raymond (Kathy) Kremsreiter, Dennis (Rita) Kremsreiter and Harlen (Lori) Kremsreiter.

Besides her parents, Alice was preceded in death by her husband, Donald, children, Allen "Butch", Linda, Pam and Loralyn "Lorrie", grandchildren, Shawn and Kurt Wogernese and Dustin Weiler, great-grandchild, David Weiler, and siblings, William Kremsreiter Jr., Roger Kremsreiter and Eugene Kremsreiter, son-in-law, John P. Cullen.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at Noon Monday, February 2, 2026, at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens. Rev. George Graham will preside. Visitation will be Monday from 10:00 a.m. until the time of service all at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens.

The family wishes to extend their sincere and profound gratitude to Hope Hospice for the exceptional care provided to Alice. Your compassion, professionalism, and unwavering dedication brought comfort and dignity to her during a difficult time, and the family is deeply appreciative of all that you did.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Bruce L. Clark
records.summary: Bruce L. Clark, 68, of Wausau, Wisconsin, died on January 25, 2026, after a 25-year battle with polymyositis.
records.body:
Bruce Lowell Clark, of Wausau, WI, died on January 25, 2026, after a 25-year battle with polymyositis – an autoimmune disease. Bruce was born in Wausau on June 9, 1957 – the oldest child of Dolores Ellen (Comer) Clark and Robert Lynn Clark, Sr.

Music was the core of Bruce's life: he played trumpet in both the Wausau Story Drum & Bugle Corps, Wausau East High School's Tophatters jazz band, and Wausau Concert Band. He also played trumpet and accordion with the Jay Tlusty Orchestra for many years. Bruce was a consummate educator, having taught music lessons – particularly on trumpet, flute, guitar, voice, and piano – to hundreds of students over 50 years.

Bruce was proud of the fact that, even though it took 27-1/2 years, he eventually completed his Bachelor of Music Education degree from UWSP. Bruce then went on to successfully complete three graduate degrees including a Master of Arts in Music Education, Master of Divinity in Pastoral Care, and Master of Arts in Religion.

Bruce was a kind, gentle, and artistic soul. He began creating macrame sculptures while in high school and continued to produce unique and beautiful works of macrame art throughout his life. Bruce embraced an active outdoor life and enjoyed scuba diving, skiing, and bicycling as long as his health allowed. His love of animals meant that Bruce's home was always graced with furry companions.

In the 1980's, Bruce moved to Minocqua, WI and opened The Minocqua Music Studio. He later founded the North Central Wisconsin Conservatory for the Arts which became the renowned Campanile Center for the Arts. Bruce eventually returned to Wausau and continued teaching private music lessons through The Music Studio until just before his death. Of his many accomplishments, Bruce was most proud of his son Seth, and he considered raising Seth to be the greatest honor and privilege of his life.

Bruce was preceded in death by his parents, his youngest brother (Steven Hale Clark), and his stepdaughter (Emily Willegal). Bruce is survived by his son, Seth Emanuel Clark (Kateleigh Hewins), two grandchildren (Emmeline Hewins Clark and Anthony Hewins Clark), and his brothers Jeffery Alan Clark and Robert Lynn Clark, Jr. (Sheri). Bruce will be deeply missed by his family, his friend Sheila Garske, and his many former students.

At Bruce's request, no public funeral or service will be held. Donations in memory of Bruce can be made to the Humane Society of Marathon County, 7001 Packer Dr., Wausau, WI 54401 or at https://catsndogs.org.

records.name: Daniel O. Dirks
records.summary: Daniel O. Dirks, 72, of Colby, Wisconsin, passed away on January 22, 2026, under the care of Compassus Hospice.
records.body:
Daniel O. Dirks, age 72, passed away on Thursday, January 22, 2026, in Colby, WI, while under the care of Compassus Hospice at Waterford at Colby.

Born on October 21, 1953, in Berlin, WI, Dan was the son of the late Oscar and Leila (Richter) Dirks. He spent his early childhood in Tustin, WI, before relocating to Marathon, WI, where he was raised following his father's career transfer.

Dan graduated from Marathon High School in 1971 and went on to earn an Electronics degree from North Central Technical College in 1973. He began his career at the Hobart Company, later accepting a position with Schulmerich Carillons, where he resided in Alabama and served the southern United States. Following years of professional service, he returned to Madison to pursue further education, completing an Electrical Engineering degree in 1992. His subsequent employment included positions at Norlight Telecommunications in LaCrosse, WI, and MCI in Naperville, IL, both pioneering companies in fiber optics during the 1990s. Upon retirement, he settled in Wausau, WI, contributing to the community as a handyman for area seniors and caring for his brother Ron until he was no longer able.

Throughout his life, Dan maintained a strong passion for electronics, building his own stereo system and amassing a substantial LP and movie collection. An admirer of science fiction, particularly Star Trek, he collected related films and series and enjoyed classic cinema. He demonstrated a strong commitment to preserving family photographs. He cherished time with family, taking joy in seeing photographs of his nieces and nephews. Although known for his reserved demeanor, he was deeply caring toward those close to him.

He is survived by his siblings: Ken (Jean) Dirks of Tomahawk; Justine (Robert) Stolze of Ringle; Nancy (Mark) Loker of Oshkosh; Annette (Patrick) Krautkramer of Marathon; as well as numerous nieces, nephews, great-nieces, great-nephews, and extended family members.

Dan was preceded in death by his parents, his sister Diane Blume, his brother Ronald Dirks, and his nephew Jeremy Sonntag.

Funeral services will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, February 28, 2026, at St. Matthew Ev. Lutheran Church in Marathon, officiated by Rev. Jon W. Hadler. Burial will occur at a later date in St. Matthew Lutheran Cemetery, Marathon. Visitation will take place from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service at the church. Arrangements are being managed by Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Marathon. Online condolences may be shared at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Joann M. Budnik
records.summary: Joann M. Budnik, 85, of Schofield, Wisconsin, was called home on January 25, 2026.
records.body:
Joann Mary Budnik, 85, Schofield, WI, was called home to her Lord on January 25, 2026.

Joann was born May 6, 1940, the daughter of Late Sam and Katie (Kasper) Budnik. She grew up on a farm in the town of Reid; she was the youngest of eleven siblings. Joann attended school in Mosinee, WI. She then went on to work at Marathon Battery and then Crestline Windows until her retirement in 2002.

Joann was a great Daughter, Sister, Sister-in-law, Aunt, Friend, and caregiver, graciously taking care of her mother Katie for many years. She was a zesty Lady who liked to shop, take day trips to the casino and Joann took pride in keeping her yard mowed and planting her favorite flowers in the spring. She always enjoyed getting together for a good game of poker or guts.

Joann's true passion was her dogs she cared for over the years. She was a Dog Mom who loved her dogs and treated them like her children.

Joann was an active member of St. Ladislaus Church in Bevent, WI.

Joann is survived by many nieces, nephews, great nieces, great nephews and a special Sister-in-Law, Betty (Richard) Budnik.

Joann is preceded in death by her parents and siblings, Emma, Donald, Edward, Stanley, Irene, Annie, Joseph, Richard, Sam, and Ray.

A Mass to Celebrate Joann's Life will be Monday, March 9, 2026, at St. Ladislaus Catholic Church, Bevent at 11am. There will be a visitation starting at 10am until the time of the Mass.

We would like to thank the staff at Lili Gardens Assisted Living for their Special Care, Compassion and Love they gave our Aunt Joann while in their care. A special Thank You to Heartland Hospice. We deeply appreciate the kindness and support and exceptional care given to Joann.

Wishing you eternal Peace, Aunt. You were a steady presence and a loving soul. Thank you for the memories, may your journey onward be full of Peace.

Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg is assisting the family with arrangements. Memories and messages of support may be shared at schmidtschulta.com.

records.name: Joni McEwen
records.summary: Joni McEwen, 71, passed away peacefully on January 19, 2026.
records.body:
