Joni McEwen passed away peacefully on January 19, 2026, at the age of 71. She was born on March 27, 1954, to the late Robert and Betty (Sullivan) McEwen.

Joni was a devoted mother to her sons, Justin and Jason, and a loving grandmother to Lillith Paige. She took immense pride and joy in being a great-grandmother to Harlow, who held a very special place in her heart. Joni is also survived by her sisters, Deborah and Karlene, and by her beloved fur baby, Oliver, who brought her daily comfort and companionship.

A quiet and gentle soul, Joni was known for being a very good cook, for her love of relaxing with the Hallmark Channel, and being a huge fan of the Milwaukee Bucks and Green Bay Packers. She had a caring heart and spent much of her life as a caretaker, finding fulfillment in caring for her two clients, Daniel and Steven, whom she genuinely enjoyed and looked after with compassion.

Joni was deeply loved by her family and those who knew her. She will be remembered for her kindness, her nurturing spirit, and the love she gave so freely. She will be greatly missed and forever held in the hearts of those she leaves behind.

Family and friends may leave words of remembrance at MWCS.WS

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family.

A celebration of Joni's life will be held at a later date.

The family wishes to thank the Aspirus Wausau Hospital Emergency room team for their efforts in trying to save Joni.

records.name: Kathleen A. Kraklow
records.summary: Kathleen A. Kraklow, 76, passed away peacefully on January 23, 2026.
records.body:
Kathleen Kraklow, age 76, passed away peacefully on January 23, 2026. She was born on December 15, 1949, to the late Thomas and Loretta (Simcakoski) Fisher.

Kathleen was a kind, caring, and generous soul who found joy in life's simple pleasures. She loved doing crossword puzzles, playing bingo, and going out to eat with family, friends, and cousins. She had a deep love for animals—especially dogs—and often took in strays and rescues, giving them care, comfort, and a safe place to call home.

Kathleen was always willing to help anyone in need and looked out for her neighbors as if they were family, serving as her own kind of neighborhood watch. She also enjoyed feeding and watching the birds and rabbits, finding peace and happiness in nature.

She is survived by her loving children: Jenny (Tim Bluem), Thomas (significant other Vicki Zabawa), and Carmen (significant other Leon Liebl); her cherished grandchildren, Jasmine Lilyquist, Colton Dahms, and Brian (Michelle) Dahms; and her adored great-grandchildren, Bennett and Madeline Dahms.

Kathleen was preceded in death by her parents; her sister, Darlene Schroda; and her brother, Richard Fisher.

She will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved her, and remembered for her compassion, generosity, and unwavering care for both people and animals.

A celebration of Kathleen's life will be held at a later date.

Family and friends may leave words of remembrance at MWCS.WS

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family.

records.name: Kathleen K. Bush
records.summary: Kathleen K. Bush, 83, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on January 27, 2026, at Pride TLC in Weston.
records.body:
Kathleen Kay Bush, 83, passed away on Tuesday, January 27, 2026, at Pride TLC in Weston, under the care of Interim Hospice. She was born on July 19, 1942, in Wausau to Mark and Laura Synnott (née Kennedy).

On February 17, 1963, Kathleen married Thomas Bush. The couple later divorced in 1993. On July 29, 1994, she married David Lechnir at her sister's home in Cheyenne, Wyoming. David preceded her in death on November 20, 2003.

Kathleen was raised by proud Irish parents who encouraged a love of reading, current events, and lively, informed political discussions around the dinner table. She graduated from Wausau High School in 1960 and later attended Wausau Technical College.

While raising her family, Kathleen was deeply involved in her community. She served as a Cub Scout den mother, taught Sunday school, edited a children's newspaper, and sat on the board of the First Universalist Church. She was an active member of numerous committees for the D.C. Everest School District and served as a parent representative on the Curriculum Council. She was also a past member of the Wausau Junior Woman's Club and the League of Women Voters.

Kathleen enjoyed a 17-year career with the Marathon County Public Library, retiring in 2007. Throughout her life, she loved to travel and, due to her Irish heritage, especially treasured a trip to Ireland. In retirement, she traveled with her special friend John Gaulke, her lifelong friend Mary Marquardt, and her niece Laura Stevens. She visited 42 states, including Hawaii, as well as Puerto Rico and the Bahamas.

Kathleen is survived by her sons, Kraig Bush (Christa) of Wildwood, Missouri, and Kurt Bush of Beaver Dam; her grandsons, Michael Bush (Elizabeth) and Nicholas Bush (Grace), both of St. Louis, Missouri; two great-grandchildren, Evelyn and Noah Bush; her sister, Marjorie Christianson of Minneapolis, Minnesota; as well as two nephews and three nieces.

She was preceded in death by her parents, Mark and Laura Synnott; her former husband, Thomas Bush; her husband, David Lechnir; her sisters, Mary Goodin and Patricia Sullivan; and her niece, Susan Seamonson.

In keeping with Kathleen's wishes, a private burial will be held at a later date at Restlawn Memorial Park in Wausau. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com. Memorials may be given in her name to the Salvation Army or to a charity of the donor's choice.

records.name: Lee Ann Burton
records.summary: Lee Ann Burton, 87, of Schofield, Wisconsin, passed away on January 21, 2026.
records.body:
Lee Ann Burton passed away on January 21,2026. She will be missed by her loving children, Blake, Brad, Scott and Jenny and their families.

Lee Ann was born, in Schofield Wisconsin, to Earl and Alice Heinrich on August 13, 1938. She was lovingly supported by her extended family of aunts, uncles, and cousins. Lee Ann was the youngest of 3 siblings, Alan Heinrich, Lois Manthe. She met her husband Terry Burton while attending DC Everest High School. They married and she moved with him to Madison Wisconsin. They eventually returned to Schofield where they raised their 4 children. She was an active member of Wausau Curling Club and Wausau Country Club. She enjoyed traditional rug hooking and knitting. She knit many pink beanies for the Aspirus cancer area. She was very close to her siblings and made many trips "east" to the casino with them. There were many wonderful memories made at the family cabin on Lake Nokomis and Crystal Lake Campgrounds. She was actively involved with her grandchildren's lives babysitting, sleepovers, grandma trips, and supporting all their activities.

She is preceded in death by the love of her life, Terry Burton, her parents and siblings. She is survived by her children, Blake (Sandy) and their sons Blaze and Ben, Brad (Cheri) and their daughters Emily (Jeff) and Katherine (Nick) and her 2 great grandchildren Eliot and Rowan, Scott (Amy) and their children Cade, Connor, Kellen, and Quinn, and her daughter Jennifer Soley.

Her family would like to thank Primrose Memory Care staff and St. Croix Hospice for their compassionate care she received.

Visitation will be held at Brainard Funeral Home – Everest Chapel, 5712 Memorial Ct, Weston, WI, on Thursday, February 5th, 2026, from 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM. Private burial of ashes will be on February 6th. Arrangements are with Brainard Funeral Home and Cremation Center.

records.name: Ricky O. Svennes
records.summary: Ricky O. Svennes, 52, of Wausau, Wisconsin, peacefully passed away on January 23, 2026, after a battle with brain cancer.
records.body:
After a life filled with love and kindness, Ricky Odell Svennes, peacefully left this earth for a better place on Friday, January 23, surrounded by his loved ones. He was 52 years young.

Rick was born on August 14, 1973, to Phyllis (Bjerke) and Arvid Svennes. He grew up on a farm in Toronto, South Dakota, the youngest of eight siblings in a proud Norwegian family. Rick cherished his upbringing in a small community and loved helping on the farm alongside his siblings.

Rick graduated from Deubrook High School in White, South Dakota, in 1991, and went on to attend South Dakota State University, earning a Bachelor of Science degree in Business Economics. He continued his education with a Master of Business Administration from Baker University and also held a Master's Certificate in Project Management.

Rick began his professional career in 1997 at Wausau Financial Systems as an Implementation Specialist, a role that relocated him from South Dakota to Wisconsin. While in Wisconsin, he met the love of his life and future wife, Christine. His career in information technology later took him to Dallas, Texas, and Overland Park, Kansas, before returning to Wisconsin in 2004. At the time of his passing, Rick was happily employed at Optum as a Senior Technical Program Manager.

Rick and Christine were married on August 28, 1999, in Stevens Point, Wisconsin. Together they built a wonderful life and raised two beautiful daughters, Linnea and Annika, in Wausau. Rick was deeply committed to giving back to his community, especially to the Catholic Church and Newman Catholic Schools, where he passionately volunteered his time on the finance committee, education commission, and strategic planning committees.

Rick loved sharing his knowledge and enthusiasm for sports by coaching his daughters in soccer, volleyball, and basketball. He developed a special love for volleyball and went on to coach at the high school level for Newman Catholic for six years. A devoted sports fan, Rick was especially passionate about the Green Bay Packers and was well known for making his family endure his legendary Packers parody songs during timeouts, a tradition that began while he and Christine lived in Dallas and became a cherished family memory.

One favorite story recalled by his family is a camping trip to Madeline Island, where they found themselves on the service-less sands of Lake Superior wishing for music. When asked what he had downloaded, Rick proudly played an hour of Packers parodies as he and Annika tossed a frisbee in the freezing water, while Christine and Linnea laughed from the shore.

Rick treasured his seven siblings and their close-knit bond, faithfully catching up through weekly Zoom calls and eagerly anticipating their annual week-long summer reunions.

Rick truly loved life. He was a special human being with a wonderful sense of humor and a gift for making people smile with his classic dad jokes. He loved golfing with his wife and daughters, camping in Door County and other Wisconsin state parks, enjoying outdoor activities with his family, playing catch with Annika, hiking with Linnea, working in the yard with Christine, and relaxing on their patio alongside Princess, the family dog. He was a very intelligent man and problem solver, he was always happy to help his girls, and many others he loved, with any issue.

Rick also found joy in supporting athletes of all ages, helping at camps and organizing elementary and middle school summer volleyball leagues at Newman, instilling a love for the game in countless young athletes.

After being diagnosed with brain cancer in March of 2025, Rick faced his journey with Norwegian wit, humor, and a gracious heart. Throughout his fight he remained comedic and selfless, helping his family stay strong and supportive with endless hours of laughter.

The family would like to extend their heartfelt gratitude to the many friends, neighbors and loved ones who supported them through this journey. Special thanks to UW Health Neurology, Aspirus Cancer Center, and Home Health for the compassionate care given to Ricky.

Survivors include wife, Christine (Glodowski) Svennes, daughters, Linnea Svennes (Jacob Hause) and Annika Svennes. Brothers, Allen (Pat) Svennes, Lanny (Gayleen) Svennes and Philip (Jodie) Svennes. Sisters, Peggy Raine, Lori (Keith) Willemssen, Sue (Curt) Nomeland and Mary (Mike) Shupe. Mother-in-law, Tharsilla Glodowski, brothers-in-law, Phillip (Katie) Glodowski and Matthew (Jolene) Glodowski. Sisters-in-law, Georgann Mackie, Patrice Wasmundt, and Leanne Jeidy. Along with many nieces and nephews that he adored. Rick is preceded in death by his parents, Arvid and Phyllis Svennes.

He will be missed forever.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 10:30 a.m. on Tuesday, February 3, 2026, at Resurrection Catholic Church, 621 2nd Street, Wausau, with Rev. Allan Slowiak presiding. Visitation will begin at 8:00 a.m. until the time of service, all at the church. Private burial will take place at a later date. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Robert C. Campo
records.summary: Robert C. Campo, 79, passed away peacefully after a 12-year battle with lung cancer.
records.body:
Robert C. Campo passed away peacefully at the age of 79 after a 12-year battle with lung cancer, having been exposed to Agent Orange during his service time in Vietnam. Bob was in the United States Navy as a Seabee.

Bob was born on July 6, 1946, in Milwaukee, WI. Bob was a retired Master plumber and a member of the Plumbers Union. The two loves of Bob's life were his two dogs, Blear and Emma, who brought him much joy and comfort. His favorite thing to do was golfing – he went almost every day.

Bob was in a courtship with his wife, Melodee (Schley) Campo, for over 17 years before they were married in 2001.

Bob is survived by his wife, extended family, and many friends.

Per his wishes, no services will be held.

The family wishes to thank Rennes Health and Rehab Center, Weston, Inner Peace Caregiving, and Cornerstone Caregiving for helping care for Bob the last few years.

Memorials may be directed in Bob's honor to his favorite dog rescue organization, Bullies n Friends, S4492 Belter Rd, North Freedom, WI 53951 (608) 393-6843.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Ronald G. Hahn
records.summary: Ronald G. Hahn, 85, of Edgar, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully at home on January 24, 2026.
records.body:
Ronald G. Hahn, age 85, of Edgar, passed away peacefully at home surrounded with family love on Saturday, January 24, 2026.

Ron, born on November 28, 1940, was the beloved son of Elmer and Viola (Weber) Hahn. He grew up grounded in family values that stayed with him throughout his life.

On June 5, 1964, Ron married Carolyn Landreth, with whom he shared 61 years of marriage marked by devotion, hard work, and commitment to family.

Ron proudly served in the United States Air Force from 1962 to 1966. Following his military service, he worked for Wisconsin Fuel & Light Company before beginning his teaching career at Edgar Elementary School, where he taught for 24 years. He was also active in the Experimental Aircraft Association (EAA), American Legion Post 393, Cub Scouts Pack 437, Boy Scout troop 458 (teaching several merit badges,) Cub Scouts Chuckwagon Skills Day, Central Wisconsin Antique Steam and Gas Engine Club, and many years with church involvement reflecting his strong belief in service, community, and mentorship.

Ron is survived by his wife of 61 years, Carolyn, brothers Ken (Bev) Hahn, David Hahn, Harlan Hahn, and Tim (Dawn) Hahn; his children Deborah (Benjamin) Hoffman and Dustin (Amy) Hahn; and his grandchildren Bobbi (Alex) VanHandel, Benjamin (Brenda) Hoffman, Tyler Hoffman, Morgan Hahn, and Holly Hahn, along with four great-grandchildren Alek, Ana, Alarik and Amanda. He is also survived by many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Elmer and Viola (Weber) Hahn; his daughter Nicholle Hahn; his brother Steven Hahn; and his sister-in-law Lois (Wenzel) Hahn.

A funeral service for Ron will be held on Saturday, January 31, 2026, at 11:30 AM at St. John Lutheran Church, 119415 Huckleberry Road, Edgar, Wisconsin 54426, with Reverand Michael Mathey presiding. Visitation will be held at church from 9:00 AM until the time of service at 11:30 AM. Ron will be laid to rest following the service at St. John Lutheran Cemetery – Easton.

Ron will be remembered for his steady presence, dedication to family, and quiet strength. He leaves behind a legacy of service, love, and integrity that will continue through those who knew and loved him. The family would like to extend a big Thank You to Aspirus Hospital, the rehab units he has been in over the past year, and Compassus Home Care and Hospice who have given their fine care and concern every step of the way.

Memorials will be shared with UW Transplant Clinic and Parkinson's Foundation.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the Hahn family with arrangements. You may sign the family guestbook at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Theresa L. VanLieshout
records.summary: Theresa L. VanLieshout, 67, of Rothschild, Wisconsin, died peacefully on January 27, 2026, after several years of battling cancer.
records.body:
Theresa L. VanLieshout, 67, died peacefully on January 27, 2026, after several years of bravely fighting cancer. She is the daughter of the late James and Phyllis VanLieshout, who raised her and her five brothers and sisters in Rothschild, Wisconsin. Theresa often spoke about the joy she had growing up in this community; swimming at the local pools, riding bikes, and getting into good-natured trouble with the other neighborhood kids.

Theresa graduated from D.C. Everest High School in 1977, and while attending school, competed in track, gymnastics, and swimming. In track, she won several state competitions and set school records, some of which she still held when her children attended high school. She was among the inaugural class of athletes honored as members of Everest's Wall of Fame. Theresa stayed connected to Everest athletics in her adult life, working as a videographer for the Everest football team for many years and creating highlight reels for players looking to play college football.

Although she didn't think of herself as one, Theresa was a trailblazer in many ways. She was among the first female athletes to be offered an athletic scholarship for college. In adulthood, she pursued a career in the then male-dominated fields of technical direction and videography. While working at WAOW, she was the first woman at the station to direct a live news broadcast. She went on to work as a videographer for Discover Wisconsin, where she traveled across the state capturing both the natural and cultural beauty that make up Wisconsin.

She also greatly enjoyed the outdoors by walking, paddle boarding, and golfing. Her family will (selfishly) miss her fantastic cooking, kindness, and deep well of empathy and care for others.

Theresa is survived by her husband, Doug Purdy, her two children: Natasha VanLieshout, and Andrew (Joey Ninabuck) Zuidema, her granddaughter: Mel Ninabuck, her two sisters: Bonnie (Dick) Shane, and Ann (Jim) Hinner, her two brothers: Bob (Joan) VanLieshout, and Paul VanLieshout, her father and mother in law: Fred (Nina) Schroeder, her brother and sister in law: Michael (Kelly) Schroeder, and many beloved nieces, nephews, cousins, and friends.

She was preceded in death by her brother: Bill VanLieshout, and her parents: James (Phyllis) VanLieshout.

Further details regarding a memorial service for Theresa will be provided at a later date.

records.name: Douglas R. Brown
records.summary: Douglas R. Brown, age 65, of Edgar, passed away on January 26, 2026.
records.body:
He was born January 22, 1961, in Wausau, son of Janice (Hamann) Brown, Edgar and the late Ronald Brown.

Doug grew up working on his parents' farm and continued doing that for his first few years after graduation. He did a variety of jobs before eventually finding himself delivering and setting up in-home/hospice care equipment. He enjoyed helping people, listening to their stories, and showing them the compassion God gave him. While not helping others, Doug was an avid sports fan and enjoyed cheering on his nieces and nephews in their endeavors. He could be found every weekend cheering on the Packers, Brewers, Bucks, and Badgers.

Doug was also an active member of his church, and loved his church family. He served as church president along with other duties throughout his life.

Survivors include his mother, Janice Brown, Edgar, his siblings, Larry (Wendy) Brown, MN, Beverly (Todd) Schwantes, Oconto Falls, Bruce (Jamie) Brown, Kansas and Stacey (Mark) Van Hoy, Edgar and many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his father Ronald Brown and grandparents.

Funeral Services will be held at 11:00 a.m. Friday, February 6, 2026, at St. John Lutheran Church-High Steeple, 119415 Huckleberry Road, Edgar. The Rev. Michael Mathey will officiate. Burial will be in St. John Lutheran Cemetery at a later date. Visitation will be held on Friday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of services all at the church.

records.name: Eldri Guldan-Zahn
records.summary: Eldri Guldan-Zahn, age 73, passed away on January 28, 2026.
records.body:
I was born on June 6, 1952, in Rhinelander, Wisconsin, to Maryellen (Houg) and Malcolm Guldan Jr. I am the oldest of five children. I loved growing up there. I met my best friend, Dolly (Jaroski) Loduha, when we were three years old, and we're still best friends. I loved school, and not many people had the same level of fun I had in high school with my friends. When I was a junior, my parents moved to Wausau. The second night there on a blind date, I met the love of my life, Steve Zahn. I was 16, and he was 17. Fifty-seven years later, we're still together and very much in love. We had two daughters, and I stayed home until the youngest started school. Then, I went to work at Wausau Insurance and stayed there for ten happy years. In 1988, an opportunity arose to purchase a business in Green Bay, and we seized it. We ran it for almost 30 years before selling and retiring.

In 2001, while I was "room grandma" for our granddaughter's second grade class, I met an eight-year-old boy who was in foster care, and his parental rights were being terminated. Steve and I became foster parents at almost 50. We decided to adopt him, and at the age of nine, he was ours. With him, we were given a second chance at parenthood, and it was absolutely wonderful. We became friends with a diverse group of new people and served as the Bay Port Athletic Booster Club for five years. I wish I could have kept track of how many quarts of Bay Port Chili I made. The help and support we received from other parents was phenomenal. The teachers we encountered in the Howard-Suamico School District went above and beyond, helping our son transition from special education to achieving straight A's in seventh grade and being inducted into the National Honor Society as a senior, all while participating in three sports. We cannot thank them enough! There was never a prouder mother, watching him graduate from college with that gold cord around his neck, all the while being a starter in two sports all four years.

In 2014, we did an adult adoption on our oldest granddaughter, Stephanie, and she completed our family. We attended her college graduation in 2016 from UWEC, exactly 100 years after my grandfather graduated from Eau Claire Normal School as a precursor to UWEC.

In May of 2017 a man walked into our business after we had met that morning with our accountant on making a plan to sell our business and retire and told us his uncle from Wausau wanted to buy it all – the land and buildings in the Howard Industrial Park, the equipment, and most importantly, would keep all our employees. Less than twelve weeks later, the sale was completed, and we headed to Florida to find a vacation home. In a few days, we found the community of our dreams, The Meadows, along with our beautiful home on the pond. Little did we know we would also find a new family. I would tell people that maybe I died of my 2015 cancer and now I was in heaven. For the next eight years, I had a perpetual smile on my face, even as I faced my own mortality; it still made me smile and feel loved and cherished. Thank you, my friends. I love you all.

I was preceded in death by my beloved grandparents, Alfred and Esther (Edwardson) Houg. I was lucky enough to have my grandma until I was 48, and I cherished every moment we had together. My other grandparents, Malcolm Sr. and Elizabeth (Scherer) Guldan also preceded me in death, along with my parents and my brothers, Joseph Guldan, and Malcolm (Buzz) Guldan III. In addition, I was preceded by my in-laws, Harold and Shirley (Hafeman) Zahn.

I am survived by my beloved husband, whom I affectionately referred to as Mr. Z, our children, Jennifer (Karl) Hansen, Katherine (Andy) Sickinger, Marshall (Michele Grzywa) Zahn, and Stephanie (Paul Balcita) Hansen. We have four much-loved grandchildren, Chelsea (Evan Ford) VanDenElzen, Olivia Held, Henry Held, and Bryson Zahn. Also surviving me is my precious sister, Tracy (William Vaughan) Guldan, sisters-in-law, and many cousins whom I cherish.

A memorial service will be held at Carlson Funeral Home in Rhinelander, Wisconsin at 1 PM on Friday, June 5, 2026. My ashes will be buried alongside my great grandmother for whom I was named, Eldri Houg, at Forest Home Cemetery in Rhinelander.

records.name: Ervin A. Boehm
records.summary: Ervin A. Boehm, age 89, of Edgar, passed away on January 26, 2026.
records.body:
Ervin was born in Edgar, WI on September 2, 1936 to John and Pauline (Meixner) Boehm, being the 4th of 6 children. Ervin married Clarice Langhoff of Merrill, Wisconsin on May 6, 1961.

He loved to fish and hunt with his sons, travel with his wife, attend his children's events and spend time with his extended family. He especially liked to spend time at the land and cabin, making firewood, tending to Christmas trees and doing field work. Ervin retired from Wausau Papers after 38 years.

He is survived by his wife and they were blessed with 4 children: Judy (Brian) Radke, Russ (Jeannine), Tim (Tracy), Jean (Scott) Dailey. In addition, Ervin has 9 grandchildren (James Dailey, Kailie Boehm, Steven Dailey, Louis Boehm, Kaitlyn Boehm, Ty Radke, Jack Boehm, Korey Boehm, and Timothy Dailey), 3 great-grandchildren (Easton Dailey, Claire Dailey and Landon Dailey) He is also survived by his brother Clarence (Mary Ann), sister in laws JoAnn Boehm and Shirley Borchardt, sister Loretta Stanton and many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents, father and mother in law Louis and Viola Langhoff, his son Russ, sisters Rose (Charles) Lang, Evelyn (John) Lewandowski, brother Lawrence Boehm, brother in laws Gary Stanton, Louis (Geraldine) Langhoff, LaVerne Borchardt, Sister in laws Margery (Bill) Klammer Buffington, Vernette (Milton) Kleinschmidt, Isabelle (Bob) Hoff, Jean (Lawrence) Buboltz, Marion (Dick) Simon, Florence (David) Langhoff.

Funeral Mass will be held on Thursday, February 12, 2026 at 11:00 AM at St. Anne Catholic Church (700 West Bridge Street, Wausau WI 54401). Fr. Tom Lindner will officiate. Visitation will be held on Thursday, February 12, 2026 from 9:00 AM until 11AM at the church. Burial will be at 2:30 PM at Snow Hill Cemetery.

records.name: Karl H. Middelhauve
records.summary: Karl Heinrich Middelhauve, age 93, of Wausau, passed away on January 27, 2026.
records.body:
Born on November 20, 1932, in Siegen, Germany, Karl was shaped by faith, discipline, and a lifelong curiosity about how things worked. He often shared the story of being the last one into the bomb shelter on December 24, 1944, when their family home and shoe store business—along with the Christmas goose, all went up in flames.

In 1954, Karl immigrated to the United States and later became a U.S. citizen. In 1957, he met Ruth Helene Fricke in Passaic, New Jersey. Her family had also immigrated from Eisleben, East Germany. That same year, Karl enlisted in the U.S. Army and was stationed in Germany from 1957 to 1959. Karl and Ruth were married on July 30, 1960, beginning a marriage grounded in faith, perseverance, and devotion.

Karl's professional life reflected his love of precision and craftsmanship. He worked as a consultant to the wood industry, including Martin Guitar and Steinway Piano. His keen interest in Mercedes-Benz automobiles grew into Classic Car Restoration, LLC, through which he became world-renowned as a master mechanic and restorer of the Mercedes-Benz 600 and 6.3-powered automobiles. A longtime member of the MB100 Mercedes Club, he also served as editor of the club's newsletter, Lode Star. To Karl, his work was his hobby, and his hobby was his work. In 1986, one of the three-ton vehicles he was restoring nearly claimed his life when an air-suspension system exploded and dropped the car onto his chest. From then on, every restoration was more than a technical challenge—it was an act of stewardship, reflecting the redeeming grace he knew through Christ. He loved the work God entrusted him with until the business was sold in 2023.

Those who knew Karl remember his quiet, reflective nature, always there to listen. He loved hymns, Friday night pizza, and laughter, especially when recalling stories of driving cross-country in his restored MB600 hearse. His sweet tooth was legendary. His generosity was extraordinary. He freely shared his time, talents, and treasure, delighting in blessing others unexpectedly. Even the family dog, Louie, loved staying by his side, knowing he would be included too.

Karl had a steady way of pointing others to God—accepting hardship as a character shaper, humbly admitting his failures, giving thanks in all circumstances, and trusting that God was always with us, always.

He is survived by his wife, Ruth, of Wausau, Wisconsin, and their four daughters: Christine Westburg (Paul), of Texas, with sons Jordan and Jesse; Claudia Keller, of Pennsylvania, with children Sophia, Steven, Sammy, and Aliyah; Audrey Felten (David), of Wisconsin, with sons Hans and Conrad; and Margaret Ross (Travis), of North Carolina, with children AJ, Chloe, Stefan, and Sebastian. He was a proud grandfather of twelve grandchildren and nearly six great-grandchildren. He is also survived by his sister, Jutta, of Pakistan, and his brother, Frieder, and sister-in-law, Doerte, of Germany.

A private family celebration of life will be held for Opa, as he was affectionately known by so many.

records.name: Ray C. Ellenbecker
records.summary: Raphael "Ray" C. Ellenbecker, age 95, of Athens, passed away on January 27, 2026.
records.body:
He was born on February 23, 1930, in the town of Johnson, Ray grew up on the family farm…land he proudly purchased from his parents and lovingly maintained as it became a century farm. On September 23, 1953, he married the love of his life, Caroline Mueller, at St. John Catholic Church in Wuertzburg. They shared 65 wonderful years together before her passing in 2019.

Ray lived a full and vibrant life. A skilled carpenter and contractor, he left his mark throughout the area by building countless barns and structures that still stand strong today. Never one to sit still, he embraced every opportunity to enjoy the outdoors, especially hunting and fishing trips with his family. Ray also had a knack for finding treasures at auctions and could strike up a friendly conversation wherever he went.

Family meant everything to Ray. He took tremendous joy in cheering on his grandchildren and great-grandchildren at wrestling matches, football games, and ball games in Milan. He loved shooting pool, catching up with friends, and making his rounds at the Athens Fair. His presence was warm, welcoming, and unmistakably Ray.

Ray is survived by his eight children: Randy (Debra) Ellenbecker of Texas; Jeff (Louise) Ellenbecker of Athens; Patsy (Bob) Brunke of Athens; Pam (Les) Dickinson of Athens; Lori Blake of Athens; Brad (Angela) Ellenbecker of Iowa; Todd (Londa) Ellenbecker of Athens; and Mitchell Ellenbecker of Weston. He leaves behind 21 grandchildren, 32 great-grandchildren, 3 great-great-grandchildren, and numerous nieces and nephews—all of whom carry forward his legacy of love and laughter.

He is preceded in death by his parents; his wife Caroline; his grandson Jeremiah Blake; his son-in-law Bill Blake; his daughter-in-law Traci Ellenbecker; and 16 siblings.

A Funeral Mass celebrating Ray's life will be held at Noon on Thursday, February 5, 2026, at St. Anthony Catholic Church in Athens, with Father George Graham presiding. Burial will follow at Calvary Cemetery. Visitation will take place on Wednesday from 4:00–7:00 p.m., followed by a Rosary at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home in Athens. Additional visitation will be at the church on Thursday from 10:30 a.m. until the time of service. A lunch will be served in the Catholic school basement following the burial.

records.name: Russell J. Hinsa
records.summary: Russell "Russ" Hinsa, age 73, of Tomahawk, passed away on January 28, 2026.
records.body:
He was born on May 25, 1952, in Prentice, Wisconsin, son of the late Henry E. and Edna (Tapani) Hinsa. Russ was happily married to Wendy Carlson on July 30, 1977, at the First Lutheran Church in Prentice. Their partnership was built on love and companionship, often reflected in their time spent together while Russ worked as an over-the-road truck driver, frequently traveling with Wendy by his side. His CB Handle, "Cranberry," was a nod to his first job at a cranberry marsh, and it stuck with him throughout his life. As their family grew with the addition of two daughters, Russ shifted his focus to sales to spend more time at home with them.

Russ's journey through life was marked by a dedicated career in the logging and forestry industry. He began as an equipment operator and gained invaluable experience that led him to positions in sales and appraisal. When his daughters were young, he transitioned from over-the-road driving to sales with Scaffidi Truck Center in Tomahawk for many years, followed by Ponsse North America in Rhinelander, which allowed him to connect with his Finnish heritage through several trips to Finland. Subsequently, he went on to utilize his extensive knowledge of logging and forestry equipment to start Hinsa Equipment and Appraisal, a venture he continued to be actively involved in until his passing.

He cherished spending time with others, finding joy in good conversations over coffee. Many of the relationships that began as customers became life-long friendships for Russ. He loved his home on Lake Nokomis and all the joys that came with living on the water. He also enjoyed the camaraderie and time spent with friends and family at "the cabin." He was an avid dog lover and always had at least one furry companion by his side, most recently his beloved dog, Saimaa. He enjoyed racing and driving just about anything – his trucks, boat, UTV, or his Harley-Davidson.

He is survived by his devoted wife of 48 years, Wendy Hinsa of Tomahawk; daughter Lacey (Matt) Piekarski of Stevens Point; granddaughters Emilia, Eliza, and Ella; sister Alice (Ron) Nichols of Madison; brother Edward Hinsa of Madison; and many nieces and nephews. Additionally, he is remembered by his mother-in-law, Betty J. Carlson of Prentice; sister-in-law Cheryl (Tom) Griffiths of Kronenwetter; and brother-in-law Warren D. (Rachel) Carlson of Prentice.

He was preceded in death by his parents, beloved daughter Krista Caley Hinsa, sister Anita Raasch, brother-in-law Harv Raasch, sister-in-law Georgia Hinsa, father-in-law Warren E. Carlson, and many cherished friends.

Memorial service will be held at 11:30 A.M. on Saturday, February 7, 2026, at Grace Lutheran Church, 108 W. Somo Ave., Tomahawk. Rev. Kathy Tulman and Rev. Haley Halsted will officiate. Visitation will be from 9:00 A.M. until the time of service at the church.

records.name: Sharon J. Schedler
records.summary: Sharon J. Schedler, age 90, passed away on January 28, 2026.
records.body:
Born just half an hour after her twin sister, Shirley, on November 20, 1935, in Athens, Wisconsin, Sharon was the daughter of Fred and Lorna Mootz. She grew up in Athens, where she attended school and formed lifelong friendships.

On February 5, 1955, Sharon and Bob were married. Together they built their home and family in Wausau. Sharon was a devoted wife, a kind and nurturing mother, and the heart of her household. She found great joy in a home filled with the laughter and warmth of family gatherings.

Sharon enjoyed her close-knit circle of friends, including her lifelong card-playing group from the Athens area. She was an active participant in a women's Bible study at St. Mark's, where her faith and friendships deepened. In her later years, she appreciated the caring staff and welcoming community at Athenian Living, where she spent her final year surrounded by comfort and companionship.

Sharon is survived by her sister, Shirley (Robert) Huebner; her children, Mark (Stacia) Schedler of Eagan, MN, David (Janet) Schedler of Oshkosh, WI, Bruce Schedler of Wausau, and Beth (Brian) Bisgrove of Wausau; nine grandchildren; ten great-grandchildren; along with many nieces, nephews, extended family members, and dear friends.

She was preceded in death by her husband, Robert, and her parents, Fred and Lorna Mootz.

Sharon's legacy lives on in the love she poured into her family, the friendships she nurtured, and the quiet faith that guided her life. She will be deeply missed and fondly remembered by all who knew her.

Funeral Service will be held on Thursday February 5, 2026 at 11:00 am at St. Mark's Lutheran Church (600 Stevens Dr, Wausau, WI 54401) Pastor Eric Hauan will officiate. Visitation will be held on Thursday February 5, 2026 from 9:30 am until the hour of service at the church. Burial will be at Stettin Cemetery at 2:00 PM.

records.name: Bradley R. Olds
records.summary: Bradley "Brad" R. Olds, age 44, of Kronenwetter, passed away unexpectedly at his home on January 26, 2026.
records.body:
Bradley "Brad" R. Olds, 44, Kronenwetter, passed away unexpectedly at his home on Monday, January 26, 2026, surrounded by his family.

Brad was born on March 20, 1981, son of the late Richard and Karen (Perkins) Olds. He graduated from Mosinee High School in 1999 where he met his future wife, Dawn Raczek, and they married on August 30, 2003, at St. Francis Xavier Catholic Church, she survives.

Brad had a personality bigger than life, and a sense of humor and the ability to entertain others to match. He had a great big smile and was so outgoing. He could talk to anyone and everyone he met like he had known them forever and could tell stories like no other. The enthusiasm and excitement he had for the things he enjoyed in life was palpable and contagious. Brad loved anything to do with skiing, traveling, and music, and nothing made those things even better than enjoying them with his beautiful wife and children.

Besides his wife Dawn, Brad is survived by his children, Olivia, Wyatt, and Tesla Olds. He is further survived by his stepfather, John Pasko, sister Stacie Olds and her children Destiny, Keegan, and Avah, sister Billie Olds, brother Kylie Olds, parents-in-law Anthony and Cheryl Raczek, brother-in-law John (Carol) Raczek, and their children Emma, Elizabeth, Micah, Jubilee, and Anthony, sister-in-law Julie (Andy) Zarnowski and their children Allison and Joseph, and sister-in law Lisa (Peng) Qiang, and their children Sierra, Mylah, and Jun.

Brad was preceded in death by his parents, Richard Olds and Karen Olds.

A Memorial Service will be held at 12:30 p.m., on Friday, February 13, 2026, at Beste Funeral Home, Mosinee. Father Aaron Becker will officiate. Visitation will be from 10:30 a.m. until the time of service, all at the Funeral Home.

records.name: Daniel F. Wood
records.summary: Daniel F. Wood, age 84, of Birnamwood, died on January 28, 2026.
records.body:
Daniel F. Wood, 84 of Birnamwood, died on Wednesday, January 28, 2026, at Wisconsin Veterans Home at King in Waupaca, under the care of Compassus Hospice.

Daniel was born on November 11, 1941, in Antigo, the son of Daniel and Irene (Kosbab) Wood.

Along with his brothers Stanley and Orville 'Pete', Daniel owned and operated a farm for many years. In addition to farming, he drove truck and worked at the local feed store. Daniel enjoyed driving around and listening to music, and in recent years he looked forward to his Friday meals with family. He deeply cherished his time spent with family.

Daniel is survived by two sisters, Lois Owen and Iris (Joseph) Steinke, as well as numerous nieces and nephews.

Daniel was preceded in death by two sisters, Caroline Garbisch and Bernice Wood; five brothers, Stanley Wood, James (Agatha) Wood, Orville 'Pete' (Delores) Wood, Roger (Stella) Wood and Julius Wood and brother-in-law, Wayne Owen.

A Funeral Service will be held at 11 AM on Saturday, February 7, 2026, at St. Paul Lutheran Church, Birnamwood. Rev. Matthew Christians will preside. Visitation will be on Saturday, from 9 AM until the time of service at the church. Immediately following the service, Military Rites will take place in the church parking lot, conducted by Darling-Gunderson Post #341, American Legion.

records.name: Dorothea A. Woller
records.funeral_home: Helke Funeral Home and Cremation Service
records.summary: Dorothea A. Woller, age 96, of Wausau and formerly of Tomahawk, passed away peacefully on January 31, 2026.
records.body:
Dorothea A. Woller, age 96, of Wausau and formerly of Tomahawk, passed away peacefully on Saturday, January 31, 2026, at Applegate Terrace.

She was born on October 10, 1929, in Stratford, Wisconsin, to the late Otto and Alma (Natzke) Steidinger. Dorothea attended school in Rib Falls and went on to work her first job at the Edgar Basket Factory.

On October 10, 1950, she was united in marriage to Loyd Woller in Wausau. He preceded her in death in 2010. Loyd and Dorothea moved several times throughout their lives and enjoyed meeting new people. While living in Seattle, Washington, she worked at Boeing Aircraft. As they started their family, she enjoyed being a homemaker and was a loving mother to their son, Rhys. She especially enjoyed spending her free time doing handiwork, particularly needlework.

Dorothea is survived by her beloved son, Rhys Woller; sister-in-law, Carol Steidinger and many other friends and family members.

She was preceded in death by her husband, Loyd; parents; parents-in-law, Sigmund and Adela (Kasten) Woller; brothers, Arthur (Elaine) Steidinger and Arnold Steidinger.

Funeral services will be held at 11:00 am on Monday, February 9, 2026, at Christ Lutheran Church, 1300 Townline Rd, Wausau, WI with Pastor David Wetmore officiating. Friends and family may greet at the church for a time of visitation from 9:00 am until the time of service with a reception to follow.

Burial will take place at Faith Lutheran Cemetery South in the Town of Maine.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made in Dorothea's name to a charity or organization of choice.

The family would like to thank Interim Hospice and Applegate Terrace for their care and compassion.

records.name: Eleanor L. Krull
records.summary: Eleanor LaVonne Krull, age 89, of Birnamwood, died on January 30, 2026.
records.body:
Eleanor LaVonne Krull, 89 of Birnamwood, died on Friday, January 30, 2026, at Rennes Health & Rehab, Weston, under the care of her family and Compassus Hospice.

LaVonne was born on March 6, 1936, in Underhill, the daughter of Walter and Ella (Lutzow) Green.

On October 15, 1955, LaVonne was united in marriage to Lyle Krull in Birnamwood. He preceded her in death on June 19, 2024.

LaVonne drove school bus for the Wittenberg-Birnamwood School District for 46½ years. She enjoyed traveling, camping, and going on the annual girls' flower-shopping trips. LaVonne especially loved watching and feeding the birds. Most importantly, she cherished spending time with her children, grandchildren, and great-grandchildren, and faithfully attended their sporting events.

LaVonne is survived by her children, Paula (Ron) Sopiarz of Mosinee, Peter (Sharon) Krull of Marshall and Chris (Shirlyn 'Toots') Krull of Birnamwood; she is also survived by her grandchildren, Cory (Sarah), Ashley (Neal), Brittany (Mario), Becky (Shane), Jon (Beth), Jeremy (Lindsay), Nick (Taylor), Corey, Matthew (Robyn) and Tyler (Kelly); and her great-grandchildren, Sydney, Sam, Axle, Vincent, Keegan, Karson, Stella, Beckett, Kipton, Chase, Colt, Crew, Cooper, Harper, Levi, Everett, Carter and Camden, as well as many other relatives and friends.

LaVonne was preceded in death by her husband of 68 years, Lyle; parents; a daughter, Stacy; siblings, Wallace Green, Edward 'Jack' Green, Ken Green, Richard Green and Robert Green.

A funeral service will be held at 11AM on Saturday, February 7, 2026, at Trinity Lutheran Church, Birnamwood. Vicar Carolyn Jolitz will preside. Burial will be in Forest Cemetery, Birnamwood. Visitation will be on Friday, from 4-7PM at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Birnamwood, and again on Saturday, from 9AM until the time of service at the church.

The family would like the thank the staff at Rennes Health & Rehab and Compassus Hospice for their exceptional care given to LaVonne.

records.name: Ellen G. Curtis
records.summary: Ellen Grace Liebe Basterash Curtis, age 76, of Phillips, passed away peacefully at home on December 28, 2025.
records.body:
Ellen Grace Liebe Basterash Curtis, 76, passes away peacefully at home on December 28, 2025, surrounded by her loving family George, Amber, Bryan and Email.

Ellen was born on June 1, 1949, in Milwaukee, WI, daughter to the late Clarence and Jeanne (Wuerzberger) Liebe. Ellen was a pillar of strength and wisdom to all who knew her. She touched the lives of countless people with her kindness, generosity, and unwavering love. Her legacy will forever be etched in our hearts.

Ellen was a devoted wife, mother, grandmother, and great-grandmother. She is survived by her husband George T Curtis of Phillips, her children Dale (Skip) Basterash of Aurora, CO., Duane Basterash of Waukesha, WI., Bryan Curtis of Phillips, WI., Emily Curtis of Rib Lake, WI., Amber Curtis of Phillips, WI. Her 14 grandchildren, her 18 great-grandchildren, 5 sisters, countless extended family and friends.

Throughout her life, Ellen was known for her passions and talents. She loved her houseplants and outdoor plants. Her love for the wildlife that surrounded her. Her love for baking brought warmth and joy to our family gatherings.

Her legacy will live on through the memories we shared, the values she instilled in us, and the love she gave. We take solace in knowing that she is now at peace, reunited with loved ones who have gone before her, her late husband Dale Basterash Sr. and her 5 brothers.

Rest in peace Ellen (Mom, Sis, Sissy & Aunt Sis). You will forever be our guiding light.

records.name: James M. Niewolny
records.summary: James "Jimbo" M. Niewolny, age 78, of Edgar, died on January 29, 2026.
records.body:
James "Jimbo" M. Niewolny, 78, of Edgar, died Thursday, January 29, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born January 28, 1948, in Marshfield, son of the late Myron and Mary (Schrauth) Niewolny. Jimbo married Betty Buetow and they later divorced but remained friends.

For many years Jimbo was the butcher at the Edgar IGA. He was also a volunteer fireman in Edgar for 27 years, loved watching sports, especially baseball and football and in his younger years he loved to bowl. Jimbo enjoyed the simple things in life, and nothing was better than ripping a few rip tickets at a favorite bar or cheering on the Packers and the Brewers.

Survivors include his son, Travis (Erin) Niewolny of Edgar and grandson, Mason; stepdaughter, Tina (Steve) Seidl of Wausau and their children, Madelyn and Hanna Seidl; siblings, Bob Niewolny of Tomahawk, Karen (Dennis) Dallman of Edgar, and Mike Niewolny of Edgar; and nieces and nephews.

Besides his parents he was preceded in death by his brother, Bruce Niewolny.

A Memorial Mass will be celebrated at Noon, Friday, February 6, 2026 at St. John Catholic Church, Edgar. The Rev. Alan Wierzba will preside. Visitation will be held on Friday from 10:00 a.m. until the time of services at the church.

records.name: Katelin M. Blaschka
records.summary: Katelin Mae Blaschka, age 31, of Kronenwetter, passed away on January 29, 2026, following a battle with triple-negative breast cancer.
records.body:
On Thursday, January 29th, 2026, Katelin Mae (Zebro) Blaschka passed away at Marshfield Medical Center – Marshfield, surrounded by her family. Following a nearly four-year battle with triple-negative breast cancer, she met this battle with unmatched and inspiring courage and resilience leaving a lasting imprint on the hearts of all who knew and loved her in her 31 years of life.

Katelin was born in Marshfield to William Zebro and Brenda Cheyka on November 24, 1994. From an early age, it was clear that Katelin's personality and beautiful heart matched her beautiful appearance. As a sister to Gerrick Zebro, family vacations were common and often included hobbies on the water like boating and water skiing as well as camping and UTV's with Mike and Amy Zebro. Other hobbies included Tomah tractor pulls and a fascination with the paranormal.

She graduated from D.C. Everest Senior High School in 2013 and pursued higher education for not only one, but two bachelor's degrees; first in Biology from University of Wisconsin – Stevens Point and the second in Nursing from Rasmussen University. As strong-willed as she was in her studies, she was equally driven to take care of her patients across the state as an Emergency Room Nurse in various Level 1, 2, and 3 Trauma Centers like UW Health in Madison, Abbot Northwestern Hospital in Minneapolis, St. Vincent Hospital in Green Bay, Ascension All Saints Hospital in Racine, Howard Young Medical Center in Woodruff, Marshfield Medical Center in Marshfield, and most recently, Marshfield Medical Center in Weston, where she grew up. A social co-worker, it was no surprise for Katelin to again make many new friends. Loved especially for her unique sarcasm and her incredible skill as a RN, she was inspirational to many on her team as she remained dedicated to her career throughout her cancer diagnosis. She especially took pride in mentoring new nurses and her peers. As a lifelong learner Katelin set goals to continue her education in medicine and would, without doubt, be wearing a white coat in the future. It was a special honor to be visited by the Wisconsin Nurses Honor Guard in Katelin's final days.

Growing up, Katelin's beautiful and natural smile and energetic personality made it easy for her to make friends. While she could be a goofball and knew how to have a lot of fun, she was as equally smart and focused. At age 14, she would meet and start dating her future husband, Spencer Blaschka. They were married in Wausau on July 21, 2018. Their life together was full of love for each other as well as for their family and friends. Green Bay Packer Games were a favorite way to spend time together as well as cheering on the Brewers, Bucks and Badgers, especially around March Madness. Katelin was always happy around food. She was especially good at cooking (not baking) and had to document her plate in pictures – in her words, "phone eats first!" New-Recipe-Sunday was a favorite day for Katelin and Spencer to spend time together in the kitchen and try her creations. Family and friends would often get a text from Katelin inviting them for bonfires and driveway drinking – her way of spending quality time with those who mattered most. Katelin and Spencer made trips to countless destinations over the years, visiting family and new sights. Their last trip to Tennessee included the Great Smoky Mountains, which offered Katelin calm and beautiful scenery and quality time with her husband.

Katelin's life was full – made in part by her courageous and determined mindset for what came her way but also in the way she touched the lives around her. Her smile, laughter, and beautiful heart will be remembered in the hearts of all who knew her.

She is survived by her husband, Spencer Blaschka of Kronenwetter; father, Bill (Kristi Strelcheck) Zebro of Black River Falls; mother, Brenda (Scott Noonan) Cheyka of Weston; brother, Gerrick (Paige) Zebro of Neenah; step-brother, Michael (Desi) Noonan of Neenah; nieces and nephew, Sophia and Aubree Zebro and Phelix Noonan of Neenah; mother and father-in-law, Shawn and Jenny Blaschka of Ringle; brother-in-law, Andrew Blaschka of Wausau; grandfather, Lee Zebro of Tomah; grandfather, Lenny (Dawn) Cheyka of Wausau; and grandmother, Paulette (John, her Cancer Buddy) Allman of Weston; as well as uncles, aunts, and cousins. She will be especially missed by her dogs Hank, Cash, and Behr and her cat, Titan. She is preceded in death by her stepmother, Eveline Zebro; and her grandmother, Julie Zebro.

A Celebration of Life will be held on Sunday, February 15, 2026, from 1:00 – 5:00 PM at Trailside Sports Bar & Grill, 8101 Schofield Avenue, Weston.

records.name: Laura Louise Woellner
records.summary: Laura Louise Woellner, age 88, of Athens, passed away peacefully at her home on February 1, 2026.
records.body:
Laura Louise Woellner, 88, of Athens, passed away peacefully at her home while surrounded by her family on Sunday, February 1, 2026, under the care of Hope Hospice.

She was born May 28, 1937 to Edwin and Lillian (Kautzer) Dahl. On August 3, 1954, she married Victor Woellner at St. Anthony Catholic Church in Athens. They lived on the family farm that she ran for many years.

Laura enjoyed working for the Halsey Township, sewing, quilting, cooking, baking, her pets, dining out, playing Sheepshead and family get togethers.

She is survived by her children, Florence (Glen) Kreft, Marathon, Sue (Claude) Kreft, Athens, Debbie (Kirk) Simon, Athens, Sarah (Mike) Young, Athens, Brian (Jodi) Woellner, Wausau and Kent (Brenda) Woellner, Athens; many grandchildren; many great-grandchildren; many great-great-grandchildren; and her beloved sister, Betty Auner, Athens.

She was preceded in death by her parents, her husband, Victor Sr.; two sons, Victor Jr. and Ronnie; her daughter, Bonnie; and many other dear family members.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. on Friday, February 6, 2026 at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens. Rev. George Graham will officiate. Burial will be in the Calvary Cemetery in Athens. Visitation will be on Friday from 9:00 a.m. until time of services at the church.

In lieu of flowers, the family ask you to please "pay it forward" to someone you love in honor of their mother, Laura.

records.name: Richard Petzke
records.summary: Richard Petzke, age 60, of Wausau, passed away on January 31, 2026.
records.body:
Richard Petzke, age 60, of Wausau, Wisconsin, a cherished husband, beloved father, and devoted brother, passed away on January 31, 2026, at Aspirus Hospital surrounded by his loving family.

Born on November 17, 1965, in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, the son of Dick and Diana (Biegel) Petzke. Richard lived a life filled with laughter, love, and a profound commitment to his family and friends.

As an HR recruiter by profession, Richard was known for his infectious personality and his ability to connect with people from all walks of life. His colleagues and clients alike were touched by his funny, outgoing, and caring nature. He dedicated himself not only to his work but to building meaningful relationships that extended beyond the office.

Richard took great pride in his personal achievements, including his role as Vice President of membership for the Milwaukee Chapter of Muskies Inc., There he was a member for 45 years and shared his passion for fishing and the great outdoors. He found joy in hunting and was an avid card player, often rallying friends and family around the poker table for an evening filled with competition and camaraderie.

Family was at the heart of Richard's life. He is survived by his loving wife, Cindy Hoerter-Petzke, who was his partner through all of life's adventures. He was a devoted step-father to Rylan (Miranda) Hoerter and Cailee Hoerter, and a proud grandfather to Ryder, Harrison, Theo, and Gabriel. Each of his grandchildren held a special place in his heart, and he cherished the time spent creating memories with them. His faithful companion Buck, our Labrador Retriever will always be looking for him to come home.

Richard is also survived by his sisters, Michelle (William) Morse, Colleen (Todd) Dorman, and Nicole (Matt) Moskaluk, as well as his brother, Robert (Daelyn) Petzke. Each sibling brought unique joy and companionship to his life, and the bond they shared will forever be a testament to his loving nature. He is also survived by many nieces, nephews, other relatives and many friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents, who instilled in him the values of kindness and resilience. Their love and teachings lived on in Richard, shaping him into the incredible person he became.

A memorial service to honor Richard's life will take place on Saturday, February 7, 2026, at 2:00 P.M., at First English Lutheran Church, 402 N 3rd Ave, Wausau, WI, with Pastor Mary Zupansic presiding. Visitation will be held from 1:00 P.M. until the time of service at 2:00 P.M. at church.

As we gather to celebrate Richard's life, we remember a man who brought joy and light into every room he entered. His legacy will continue to weave through the hearts of all who knew him, reminding us to cherish every moment and embrace the laughter that life has to offer. Richard Petzke will be deeply missed but will always remain a beloved part of the lives he touched.

records.name: Ronald W. Uttecht
records.summary: Ronald W. Uttecht, age 80, of Birnamwood, died on January 31, 2026.
records.body:
Ronald W. Uttecht, 80 of Birnamwood, died on Saturday, January 31, 2026, at Rennes Health & Rehab, Weston, under the care of Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice.

Ron was born on October 10, 1945, in Wausau, the son of William and Sarah (Diestler) Uttecht.

Ron was a 1963 graduate of Birnamwood High School and proudly served his country in the United States Army from 1965 to 1967. In 2017, Ron had the honor of participating in the Never Forgotten Honor Flight, an experience he deeply cherished.

On June 14, 1969, Ron was united in marriage to Darlene Schmidt in Birnamwood. She preceded him in death on June 24, 2014.

Following his military service, Ron worked in construction for 12 years and then dedicated 26 years to tile work, employed by Wausau Tile, Birnamwood Tile, and Resch's Tile. Although he retired early at the age of 55, Ron remained active and hardworking. He enjoyed spending time in the woods and took great pride in helping his children build homes. Ron loved hunting, fishing, and playing cards. He had a fondness for chocolate and Diet Coke and was always one to give people a hard time

Ron is survived by three children, Jeannie (Dan) Larson, Christy Barden and John (Sara) Uttecht; five grandchildren, Stephanie, Olivia, Jackson, Gracyn and Ashlyn; siblings, Suzanne Schmidt, Carol Schmidt and Sandra (Ronnie) Stillman, as well as many nieces and nephews.

Ron was preceded in death by his wife, Darlene; parents; son-in-law, Shannon Barden; brother, James, in infancy; brothers-in-law, Milton Schmidt and Edward Schmidt.

A Funeral Service will be held at 2PM, on Saturday, February 7, 2026, at Church of the Nazarene, Mattoon. Rev. Bill L. Jones will preside. Burial will be in Evergreen Cemetery, Town of Almon in the Spring. Visitation will be on Saturday, from 12NOON until the time of service at the church. Following the Funeral Service, Military Rites will take place in the church parking lot, conducted by Darling-Gunderson Post #341, American Legion.

The family encourages those attending the service to wear flannels or plaids in Ron's honor. Any memorial donations received by the family will be donated to the Never Forgotten Honor Flight organization.

records.name: Betty L. Stone
records.summary: Betty Lou Hotchkiss-Collins-Stone, 71, passed away on January 14, 2026.
records.body:
Betty attended Antigo High School and got her GED in 1974.

She retired from working at Schroeder Potato Farm, where she had work for 20 plus years.

Betty was born in Rhinelander WI to Charles W. Hotchkiss (1/19/1890 – 6/27/1983) and Theresa C. Olinski-Hotchkiss "Spoon" (9/14/1920 – 10/2/1999). She was the 2nd youngest of 7 children.

Betty gave birth to her daughter Tami Jo Hotchkiss on December 21st, 1975. She then gave birth to her son Perry D. Stone March 5th, 1983, and married his father, Dan D. Stone on 7/9/1988 in Rhinelander WI at the Hodag Country Music Festival. Which gave Betty 3 stepchildren from Dan's first marriage, Danny Stone JR., Steve Stone, and Wanda Stone-Kesson-Graff. Betty and Dan divorced 12/5/2008.

Betty was preceded in death by both of her parents, a brother Ronald D. Hotchkiss "Whitey" (1/4/1947 – 3/21/1981), 2 sisters Lila J. Hotchkiss-Helsinger-Lucht (1/11/1942 – 5/10/2008) and Julia E. Hotchkiss-Soper (1/6/1944 – 10/26/2022), 2 half-sisters Marie E. Hotchkiss-Jalowiec (5/8/1921 – 3/23/2004) and Loraine Hotchkiss (3/16/1918 – 11/22/1918), and 2 half-brothers Eugene L Hotchkiss (4/12/1924 – 11/29/1975) and George C. Hotchkiss (8/1/1920 – 1/15/1995), and one nephew Todd W. Phelps Sr. (9/1/1966 – 8/26/1984)

Betty is survived by both of her children, a brother Richard G. Hotchkiss "Red", 2 sisters Grace H. Hotchkiss-Phelps, Bonnie J. Hotchkiss-Hoeft and 10 nieces / 7 nephews A granddaughter Mariah Kopp and a great grandson

Betty enjoyed her country music while drinking her Old-Style beer and going to music concerts like Hodag Country Music Festival in Rhinelander WI, Tuck Pence, and Jackpine Jamboree at Wildlife Bar in Birnamwood WI. She collected and washed specific rocks for her rock garden. She loved having fires and would sleep outside next to the fire. She enjoyed nude gardening. With her OCD she would do everything in "3's". She saved change and would spend a $1 before she would pay with change. She loved Betty Boop and raccoons and had a nice collection. She loved the outdoors.

Betty was a laidback, no rush person. She loved her family and friends, always helping people that were down and out, if she could. She was a free spirit.

Betty's wishes were not to have an event. The family is discussing having a Celebration of Life on July 25, 2026, place unknown currently.

records.name: Corby L. Erickson
records.summary: Corby Lynn Erickson, 69, passed away on February 1, 2026.
records.body:
She was born on January 5, 1957, and was the only child to the late Merlin and Verna (Dahlke) Radant. Growing up on their family farm in the town of Stettin, Corby was a lifelong member of Trinity Evangelical Lutheran Church of Stettin. Corby graduated from Wausau West High School in 1975, and later married Mike Erickson on May 17, 1980.

Corby worked at Fleet Farm until retiring and then did part time work at Bautch Chiropractic. Besides gardening, Corby had a love of crafting such as sewing, ceramics, quilting, and decorating. She loved being involved with her daughter in 4-H. Corby was a cat person and after building their first home she picked out her first "not a barn cat." As a true country girl, she always enjoyed country music.

Survivors include her husband, Michael; daughter, Jamie (Spencer) Hupf; and granddaughter, Murphy.

A memorial service will be at 1:00 p.m. on Saturday, May 16, 2026, at Trinity Evangelical Lutheran Church (Town of Stettin), 232280 120th Avenue, Wausau, with Rev. William Ostrem officiating. Private family burial will take place in Pine Grove Cemetery, Wausau, in February.

In lieu of flowers, a memorial donation can be made to your local animal shelter or to Trinity Evangelical Lutheran Church, Stettin.

records.name: Geraldine C. Hopperdietzel
records.summary: Geraldine Hopperdietzel, 80, passed away on February 2, 2026.
records.body:
Geraldine was born in Wausau to Leonard and Iola Paul. Geraldine grew up with a strong sense of family and faith.

In 1969, she married the love of her life, Lyle, at First English Lutheran Church. Together they built a life filled with love, laughter, and community. Geraldine was a proud member of her church and the VFW Post #388 Women's Auxiliary. She dedicated many years to caring for others through her work at Marywood Nursing Home.

Geraldine had a gentle spirit and a generous heart. She enjoyed playing Bingo, time spent at her camper in Tomahawk, and the simple joy of being with those she loved.

She leaves behind her sons, David and John (Beth), who will forever cherish her memory. She is reunited in heaven with her parents, her husband Lyle, and her siblings, Ervin Paul and Anna Smith.

A funeral service will be held February 10th, at 12 noon at Helke Funeral Home, Wausau, with a visitation from 11am up until the time of service. Pastor Mary Zupansic will officiate. Geraldine will then be laid to rest at Restlawn memorial Park.

records.name: Jewel F. Goodwin
records.summary: Jewel F. Goodwin, 87, passed away on January 28, 2026, in Wausau, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Jewel F. Goodwin, affectionately known as Jo, was born on September 19, 1939, in Ft Smith, Arkansas, Jo brought warmth and vitality into the world from the very beginning. Her spirited nature and outgoing personality made her a memorable presence to all who knew her.

Jo shared over five decades of companionship with her beloved husband and life partner of 52 years, Lee R. Yusten of Westboro, WI. Together, they built a life filled with enduring love and shared memories.

She was a devoted mother to her children: Rochelle Cherek (Wally) of Wausau, Wisconsin; Lyle Qualls (Solita) of Florence, Colorado; and loved her daughter Lora Adams (Mike) of Kingman, Arizona. She was a Mother, Grandmother, and Great-Grandmother to several grandchildren. Her family was central to her life, and she held each member close to her heart.

Jo was preceded in passing by her son Daniel "Dano" Qualla (Denver, CO), daughter Teresa Phelan (Stoughton, WI), and Marie Bruggs (Russ) (Medford, WI). Their memories remained an important part of her life story.

In her quiet moments, Jo found joy in tending to her garden and challenging herself with crossword puzzles, card games, and reading- simple pleasures that reflected both her nurturing spirit and sharp mind.

Jo's vibrant personality left a lasting impression on everyone she encountered. Her legacy lives on in the hearts of those who loved her.

records.name: Lois A. Schultz
records.funeral_home: Helke Funeral Home and Cremation Service
records.summary: Lois Ann Schultz, 82, of Wausau, died on January 30, 2026.
records.body:
She was born on May 21, 1943, in Wausau, a daughter of George and Irene (Frank) Schultz. She attended school at Trinity Lutheran and Wausau East High School. She worked for the Wausau Paper Mill for 37 years before her retirement in 1998. Lois was a lifelong resident of Wausau and she loved her family and friends. She enjoyed teaching a bible class for neighborhood children and was considered the "church auntie" to fellow members and their children whom she claimed as part of her family. She was a member of Gathered To The Name Of The Lord Jesus in Mosinee, Wisconsin. She traveled the U.S. and abroad taking family and friends on her adventures. She enjoyed having game day with family and hosting holidays.

She is survived by her sister, Marion Froemming; sister-in-law, Barbara Schultz, as well as several nieces, nephews, extended relatives and friends. She is preceded in death by her parents, sisters, Caroline Koehler and Barbara Jean Schultz; brother, Roger Schultz; brothers-in-law, James Koehler and Don Froemming; nephew, Thomas Koehler.

Funeral service will be held, Saturday, February 7, 2026, at 2:00 PM at Helke Funeral Home and Cremation Service, 302 Spruce St., Wausau, WI. Visitation will be held for one hour prior to the service at the funeral home. Interment will be held in the Pine Grove Cemetery in Wausau. In Lieu of Flowers, memorials are preferred to The Lord's Work at Bible Truth Publishers, PO Box 649, Addison, Illinois, 60101.

records.name: Lois Ruether
records.summary: Lois Ruether, 82, of Wausau, passed away on January 31, 2026.
records.body:
Lois was born May 14, 1943, to Herald and Hermandina Sommerfeldt. She married Robert "Bob" Ruether on January 26, 1963, at Our Savior's Lutheran Church, where they remained members. Together, Lois and Bob had three amazing children whom they loved very much. Lois worked at Aspirus Wausau Hospital for 42 ½ years before retiring in May 2008. Lois loved and lived to spend as much time as possible making memories with her husband, children, grandchildren and great grandchildren. She also enjoyed spending time with her siblings, in-laws and many friends. Lois was a member of the Wausau Eagles Club Ladies Aux. and had volunteered for the Badger State Games for over a decade.

Lois is survived by her three children, Laurie (Paul) Hitts, Tammy (Rich) Beilke and Brian (Wendy Schneider) Ruether; 11 Grandchildren and 23 Great-Grandchildren; siblings, Shirley (Corky) Nowak, Nancy Manowski, Leta Klumpyan; and many in-laws, nieces, nephews, and friends. She is preceded in death by her husband; parents; sisters Betty Viergutz and Jean Gehrke; brothers, Vernon Sommerfeldt and Harold "Buzzy" Sommerfeldt and other in-laws.

A funeral service will be held February 18, at 11am at Our Savior's Lutheran Church, 703 Flieth Street, Wausau, with a visitation from 9am up until the time of service. Rev. Wranovsky will officiate. Lois will then be laid to rest at Restlawn memorial Park.

In lieu of flowers, please consider donating to Devos Children's Hospital in Grand Rapids, Michigan.

We wish to thank the staff at Mount View Care Center and Aspirus Hospice Care for the kindness and compassion they showed to Lois and our family.

records.name: Marie L. Hall
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home and Cremation Care
records.summary: Marie L. Hall, 95, of Weston, passed away on February 1, 2026.
records.body:
Marie was born on May 23, 1930, in Ludington, Michigan. She lived a long, meaningful life marked by quiet strength, creativity, and deep love for her family.

She was preceded in death by her beloved husband of 33 years, Donald A. Hall, with whom she shared a devoted and enduring partnership.

Marie is lovingly survived by her daughters, Kerri Schuiling and Debra Klotz (Deb); her grandchildren, Mary Czaplewski (Mike), Sean Cummiskey, and Sarah Humpula (Galen); and her cherished great-grandchildren, Spencer, Quinn, and Kaylin Czaplewski, and Gryffin and Lincoln Humpula. She is also survived by her sister, Judy, along with many extended family members and friends who were touched by her gentle presence.

Professionally, Marie worked for many years as an executive secretary for various companies, a role that reflected her intelligence, organization, and dedication. Prior to her retirement, she served at Grand Valley State University, where her professionalism and warmth were deeply valued.

Beyond her career, Marie was a beautiful and talented watercolor artist, finding joy and expression through painting. She also had a lifelong passion for reading, often losing herself in books that reflected her curiosity and love of learning. These quiet pursuits brought her great peace and fulfillment.

Marie will be remembered for her grace, creativity, and the love she poured into her family. Her legacy lives on in the generations she nurtured and the beauty she created.

A private family service will be held at a later date.

records.name: Mary Ann Howard
records.summary: Mary Ann Howard, 96, of Wausau, passed away on January 29, 2026.
records.body:
Born on November 2, 1929, in Wausau, WI, she was the daughter of Clarence Albert Beilke and Ella (Knorr) Beilke. She was raised on the family farm and was baptized and confirmed at St. Paul's Lutheran Church in Naugart. Mary Ann graduated from Wausau High School in 1947 and she earned an associate degree in secretarial services from Spencerian College in 1949.

She worked many years with Employers Insurance of Wausau, retiring in 1992. Following retirement, Mary Ann remained active in her community, volunteering with her church, local nursing homes, as a poll worker for local elections, and at Aspirus Hospital. She was a member of the VFW Auxiliary, the American Legion Auxiliary and other community service organizations.

On October 2, 1954, Mary Ann married Donald Edwin Howard at St. Paul Lutheran Church in Naugart. They made their home in Wausau throughout their 67 years of marriage and were longtime members of Good Shepherd Lutheran Church. Together, they enjoyed traveling and were able to tour all 50 states as well as parts of Canada, Mexico, Europe, Australia, and New Zealand. They enjoyed square dancing and spending time with family, friends, and neighbors.

Donald preceded Mary Ann in death in 2022. Mary Ann cherished her children and grandchildren.

She is survived by her daughter, Sheila Misso (Gary Roberton) of Rochester, MN; her son, Daniel Howard (Karen) of Charlotte, NC; four grandchildren-Christina Misso, Angela Misso, Matthew Howard, and Jonathan Howard; and one great-grandchild, Ellanora Misso.

She honored her elders and ancestors and was preceded in death by her husband, parents, and her brother, David Beilke. Mary Ann loved God and always knew Jesus would care for her.

Her funeral service will be held on Friday, February 27, 2026, at 11:00 AM at Good Shepherd Lutheran Church (930 Edgewood Rd, Wausau, WI), officiated by Pastor Nicole Behnke-Welke. Visitation will take place at the church from 10:30 AM until the time of service. Burial will follow at Pine Grove Cemetery.

records.name: Paul Krukowski Sr.
records.summary: Paul Krukowski Sr., 81, passed away on January 25, 2026.
records.body:
Paul was born in Wausau, WI on April 28th, 1944 to John and Magdaline Krukowski. He graduated from Wausau High and upon graduating he went to work for SNE/ Crestline, where he was a window maker for 30+ years. Paul and his wife Kathy, loved traveling the country together. They always traveled by car and enjoyed seeing the country from coast to coast together. In his free time he was an avid woodworker, building anything and everything for anyone. He was a lover of the outdoors, fishing and gardening are those of his favorite hobbies. Paul always would go out of his way to help and be there for anyone. He will always be remembered as a kind, caring and compassionate husband, father, grandfather, and friend.

Paul is survived by his wife, Katherine Krukowski, his son: Peter Krukowski, and Katherine's three children: Lisa (Gary) Hettinga, Scott (Betsy) Pagel, Chad (Sarah) Pagel, seven grandchildren: Ashley, Nicholas, Dakota, Dallas, Daytona, Mackenzie, and Madison and four great-grandchildren: Layla, Lucy, Zoey, and Zach, two siblings: Bob (Ann) Krukowski, and Charlotte Gantner, and many nieces, nephews and other relatives.

Paul was preceded in death by his parents John Krukowski and Magdaline Schroeder, son Paul Jr. Krukowski, and brother John Krukowski Sr.

In accordance with Paul's wishes, no funeral service will be held, a private celebration of life will take place at a later date.

records.name: Richard A. Green
records.summary: Richard "Dick" Green, 86, of Schofield, passed away on January 24, 2026.
records.body:
Richard Allen Green was born July 9, 1939 to the late John and Genevieve (Gorecki) Green. He attended D.C. Everest High School and graduated in 1957. He was a great athlete and played basketball, football, baseball and track.

He went on to serve in the United States Army and was stationed at Fort Leonard Wood, MO and Fort Lewis, WA. After serving in the Army, he married Jeanette "Corky" (Pregont) Green and had two children, Jodi (Wausau) and Patrick (Hastings, MN).

As a well-known businessman in Schofield, he was an insurance agent and owner of Green's Insurance Agency. Along with his siblings, owner and operator of Green's Professional Building.

Dick will be remembered for his outgoing fun personality and his love for family and friends. He was proud of his grandchildren, Rylee (Woodbury, MN) and Manny (Crookston, MN).

He is survived by his sister, Rita Suske and preceded in death by his brother Donald and sister Therese.

A Memorial Service for Dick will be held on Saturday, February 21, 2026 at 11:00 AM at Brainard Funeral Home Everest Chapel (5712 Memorial Court, Weston WI 54476) with Fr. Janusz Kowlaski officiating. Visitation will be held on Saturday, February 21, 2026 From 10:00 AM until the hour of service at Brainard Funeral Home Everest Chapel.

records.name: Susan J. Kroeplin
records.summary: Susan J. Kroeplin, 79, of Wausau, passed away on January 25, 2026.
records.body:
She was born in Wausau, WI to Edward W. H. Kroeplin and Bertha W. Kroeplin (Boettcher). Susan was never married.

Susan loved to travel and spend time in church as often as possible. She also loved to watch old movies and game shows.

She was preceded in death by Edward W. H. Kroeplin and Bertha W. Kroeplin (Boettcher).

Service will be held on Tuesday February 10, 2026 at 11:00 AM at Restlawn Memorial Park Cemetery Chapel. (235962 N Troy St, Wausau, WI 54403) with Chris from St. Pauls United Church of Christ. officiating. Burial will follow after the commital at Restlawn Memorial Park Cemetery.

records.name: Thomas S. Kokke
records.summary: Thomas S. Kokke, 72, of Oshkosh, died on February 2, 2026.
records.body:
Tom was born on October 25, 1953, in Milwaukee, the son of Gerald and Mary Jane (Davis) Kokke.

Tom graduated from JFK Prep School in 1972 and University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee in 1979, with a degree in economics and political science. He worked as a corrections officer for 30 years. After retirement, he loved spending time at Wildlife Campground with his significant other, Barb Lutz and friends. Tom enjoyed motorcycling and helping others when needed, whether it be plowing fields or cutting trees.

Thomas is survived by two children, Adam Kokke and Erin Kokke; two sisters, Kitty Kokke Wood and Sally Hall, as well as many lifelong friends.

Thomas was preceded in death by his parents; furry companion, Cal, who he was looking forward to reuniting with, and many friends.

A Celebration of Life to honor Tom's life will take place from 4-7 PM on Thursday, February 12, 2026, at The Bar, 825 N Washburn Street, Oshkosh, WI 54904.

In lieu of flowers, the family asks that donations be made to your local humane society in honor of Thomas.

records.name: Troy C. Turley
records.summary: Troy Charles Turley, 58, passed away on January 16, 2026, in Marshfield, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Troy was born February 4, 1967, in Huntington Beach, California, to Penny Markum and Jerry Turley.

From an early age, Troy approached life with spirited curiosity and a deep dedication to his passions. He grew up in Turlock, California, where he attended Turlock High School. There, his involvement in sports—especially soccer and baseball—not only highlighted his athletic abilities but also ignited lifelong passions that brought him great joy.

In 1997, Troy moved to Marshfield, Wisconsin, where he built a meaningful life and lasting friendships.
