In recent months, he made his home in Stratford, Wisconsin, embracing an active and creative lifestyle. He found peace and excitement in cooking and the outdoors, particularly kayaking and fishing. Often sharing these moments with friends and family.

Troy was an avid gamer, music lover, and had a special talent for sound systems. One of his favorite pastimes was installing speakers in cars.

Troy is survived by his maternal Aunts and uncle Debbie and Barry Parsons, and Peggy Gumm. His children, Nicole Turley, Makayla Babb, Kelli Turley Jessica Martinez, Penny Turley and Stepchildren Karri and Chad Lockhart. His siblings, Jerilyn Hasenohrl, James Turley, Tammy Creach (Donnie), Colette Ghan, Penny Flippins (Dave) and his grandchildren. Baily Turley, Hope Rene, Grayson Benette, Cody and Ayden Babb, Phoebe and Luca Dickmann. He will also be dearly missed by his nephews and nieces. Cory Harden, Timothy and Richard Small, Niki Dodd (Matthew) and many others.

Troy will be fondly remembered for his infectious enthusiasm, generous spirit, and the joy he found in music, and the outdoors. He leaves behind a legacy of passion, creativity, and connection that will be cherished by all who knew and loved him.

A celebration of Troy's life will be held in the late spring, when friends and family will gather to share memories and honor his vibrant spirit.

records.name: Kenneth J. Thario
records.summary: Kenneth J. Thario, 73, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on February 4, 2026.
records.body:
Kenneth "Ken" J. Thario, 73, Wausau, passed away peacefully on Wednesday, February 4, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born June 7, 1952 in Oconto Falls, son of the late Joseph and Loretta (Colson) Thario.

Ken worked as a computer programmer for many years and then as a trucking parts specialist.

Ken was an avid sports enthusiast. In addition to playing softball himself, he coached the 9 -12 year old division of the Wausau Area Girls Softball Association for 20 years, impacting many lives with his encouragement, support, and visits to Dairy Queen.

He faithfully supported the Green Bay Packers year after year regardless of their record.

He enjoyed attending Wausau East High School sporting events, cheering on the Lumberjacks in a variety of sports.

Ken was an active member of First United Methodist Church in Wausau. As a man of strong faith he enjoyed sharing his insight and knowledge as the leader of the Adult Sunday School Class. He served as an usher as well as the church treasurer for many years.

Survivors include his sisters, Kathy (Bob) Rice, Batavia, IL and Kandy (Richard) Herra, Elburn, IL and a nephew, R.J. (Sydney) Herra, Iowa.

Besides his parents he was preceded in death by an infant sister, Karen.

records.name: Marilyn A. Smith
records.summary: Marilyn A. Smith, 84, of Rothschild, passed away peacefully on January 27, 2026.
records.body:
Marilyn A. (Shaw) Smith, 84, of Rothschild, passed away peacefully on January 27, 2026, at the Aspirus Wausau Hospital. Marilyn was born on April 23, 1941 to Raymond and Renetta (Kostus) Shaw.

Family meant everything to Marilyn. Known as "Nama" by her grandchildren and great-grandchildren, Marilyn made every holiday and birthday special for them. Her celebrations included decorations for all occasions, the world's tastiest apple pie, and activities like scavenger hunts and decorating cutout cookies. "Mickey" loved to laugh with her sisters and niece Renee in frequent phone calls that brought her great joy. Marilyn cherished family time at Bluegill Lake where swimming, fishing, and campfires filled each day with carefree fun.

A music lover, Marilyn was a member of the D.C. Everest Songspinners choir, and she received a prestigious scholarship for her beautiful voice. She continued singing often, especially at church, first at St. Therese Parish, where served as a secretary, and then as a charter member of St. Mark Parish. She enjoyed her lovely garden and, for a short time, shared flower arrangements and humorous conversations as she worked in a floral shop. Marilyn cared for others deeply, and her devotion will be greatly missed.

Marilyn is survived by her children, Kimberly (Keith) Rosenow and Klint (Jessa) Smith; grandchildren Dustyn Smith, Kyrianna Smith, and Adria Smith; great-grandchildren Jeremiah, Athena, Blake, and Avery; sister Joanne (Donald) Smith; sister-in-law Carol Shaw; brother-in-law Floyd Goytowski; and many nieces and nephews.

She is preceded in death by her husband, Lawrence Smith; parents, Raymond and Renetta; mother- and father-in-law John and Lucy (Becker) Smith; brother Donald Shaw; sister Debra Goytowski Robl; brothers- and sisters-in-law Wayne (Sharon) Smith, Geraldine (Albert) Pregont, and Kathleen (Neil) Spatz; grandsons Derryk Clark and Dyllan Smith; and nephew Richard Shaw.

records.name: Michael A. Mueller
records.summary: Michael A. Mueller, 70, of the town of Wien, passed away unexpectedly on February 2, 2026.
records.body:
Michael A. Mueller, 70, of the town of Wien, passed away unexpectedly on Monday, February 2, 2026, at his home. He was born on August 11, 1955, to the late Lawrence and Charlotte (Tatroe) Mueller. Michael fully embraced the dedication and hard work that it took to be a dairy farmer. He also took pride in his field work and yard work at the farm. In his free time Michael enjoyed watching wrestling.

Survivors include his sisters, Karen Sigmund of Fenwood, Prudy Lang (Rod Nowack) of Rib Mountain, Linda (James) Lang of Rib Mountain, and Debra Adamski of Marathon; brothers, Patrick (Donna) Mueller of Mosinee, Lyle Mueller of Edgar, and Timothy Mueller of Fenwood; and many nieces, nephews, great-nieces, and great-nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Lawrence and Charlotte; brothers, Clyde Mueller, Rodney Mueller, and Dennis Mueller; sister-in-law, Sharon Mueller; and brothers-in-law, Donald Sigmund and Donald Adamski.

records.name: Peter D. Johnson
records.summary: Peter D. Johnson, 63, of Wabeno, died unexpectedly at his home on February 4, 2026.
records.body:
Peter D. Johnson, 63 of Wabeno, died unexpectedly at his home on Wednesday, February 4, 2026.

Peter was born on October 1, 1962, in Woodruff, the son of Walter and Eleanor (Long) Johnson. Peter was in Boy Scouts growing up. He was a true outdoorsman. He loved nature and spending time walking in the woods. Peter was filled with compassion for others and would help anyone in need. He was a woodsman and would help others by taking care of their yards, cutting their grass and making firewood.

He was a social butterfly and everyone in the community knew him. He had a love for Indian Chief Harley Davidsons and was an avid bicyclist. He was a competitive bicyclist and earned the name 'Billy Goat' as he mastered the inclines. Peter was very funny and loved having fun, enjoying life, eating, watching Netflix and going for rides. Above all, he loved spending time outdoors where he always found peace.

Peter is survived by his siblings, nieces and nephews, Walter Johnson Jr. of Berkley, CA, Crystal (Daniel) Deschinny of Laona, Candice (Alan) Johnson of Apple Valley, MN, Jaiden, Skylar and Heyla, great nieces and nephews and other relatives and friends.

Peter was preceded in death by his parents; grandparents, Rose and Pete Johnson and Elizabeth and Nat Long; brother, Marcus; niece Alaytra, and great niece, Adaysha.

records.name: Sondra L. Feirn
records.summary: Sondra L. Feirn, 70, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on January 30, 2026.
records.body:
Sondra Lee Feirn, well known as Sony, age 70, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on her 70th birthday on January 30, 2026, at the Hospice House in Wausau.

Sony was born on January 30, 1956, in Wisconsin, the daughter of Irvin and Geraldine (Weldner) Feirn.

Per Sony's wishes no formal services will be held.

records.name: Wade A. Helke
records.summary: Wade A. Helke, 57, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on February 1, 2026.
records.body:
Wade A. Helke, 57, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on February 1, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital, surrounded by his loving family and his devoted wife, Lynn. He went home to be with his Lord and Savior, held in the prayers and presence of those who loved him most.

Wade was born on September 5, 1968, to the late Wallace and Arlene (Kottke) Helke. The youngest of seven children, he was proudly known as the baby of the family, a role he carried with both humor and heart throughout his life. Growing up in a large, close-knit family shaped his steady loyalty and deep love for those around him.

On June 26, 1993, Wade was united in marriage to the love of his life, Lynn (Buchberger), at St. Michael's Church in Wausau. Together they built a beautiful life over 32 years of marriage, grounded in faith, partnership, and unwavering devotion to each other. Lynn was his greatest joy and constant companion.

Wade believed in working hard and loving deeply. After an array of jobs, he found his true passion in building. As a craftsman and woodworker, he was meticulous in every detail and took great pride in his work. His strong work ethic and keen eye for excellence led him to build many Parade of Homes in the Wausau area, with his business, Helke-Rakow, each one a testament to his skill and integrity.

He loved staying busy outdoors, whether hunting, fishing, or snowmobiling. Wade was also an avid sports fan who rarely missed a game. He cheered faithfully for the Milwaukee Brewers and the Milwaukee Bucks, but especially loved the Green Bay Packers.

He absolutely loved dogs, especially his soul dog, Tucker, his faithful chocolate lab. The bond they shared was one of pure companionship and joy.

Wade was blessed with a lifelong best friend, Kevin Mikula. Their friendship endured through every season of life, remaining strong and steady from boyhood through adulthood, a rare and treasured gift.

In his later years, Wade faced the challenges of an autoimmune disease that limited his mobility. Through this journey, Dr. Matthew Clancy, Dr. Thomas Stoffel, and Dr. Vera Bocoun, became an important and steady presence in Wade's care. Wade deeply valued the compassion, dedication, and expertise these providers brought to his treatment. Even as his body grew weaker, his spirit and determination remained strong. He continued to spend time in his shop, offering advice, guidance, and a helping hand on building projects for those he loved. He was always willing to share his knowledge and lend his steady presence. His resilience and quiet strength were evident to all who knew him.

Wade was incredibly proud of his nieces and nephews. He followed their accomplishments, in sports, careers, and life, with genuine excitement and encouragement. Their successes were his pride and joy.

He will be deeply missed by his loving wife, Lynn; his siblings, Dennis (Kristin) Helke of Mosinee, Gary (Pamela) Helke of Wausau, Donovan (Lynn) Helke of Mosinee, Wesley Helke of Wausau, Dwight (Lori) Helke of Wausau, and his favorite sister, Gayle (Jerry) Woller of Wausau; his mother-in-law, Arlene Buchberger of Wausau; sister-in-law, Michele (Andy) Kettner; brother-in-law, Todd Buchberger; and many beloved nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Wallace and Arlene Helke; his father-in-law, Donald Buchberger; and his cherished dog, Tucker.

The family would like to extend their heartfelt gratitude to the doctors, nurses, and healthcare team at Aspirus Wausau Hospital for their compassionate and exceptional care, specifically Dr. Matthew Clancy and the MSICU staff. Their kindness and dedication brought comfort during a deeply difficult time.

Wade will be remembered for his steady hands, his generous heart, his loyalty, and the quiet strength that carried him through life. The homes he built will stand for years to come, but the love he built within his family will endure forever.

records.name: Betty J. Zych
records.summary: Betty J. Zych, 95, of Stetsonville, died peacefully on February 4, 2026.
records.body:
Betty J. Zych, 95, died peacefully on February 4, 2026, under hospice care at Stone Crest Residence. She was born February 27, 1930, in Stetsonville, WI.

Betty graduated from Medford High School in 1948. She went on to attend beautician school in Wausau and later managed her own beauty salon. She married Michael Zych in 1952, and together they shared 62 years of devoted marriage until his passing. In 1974, she completed Waitress Training NTC 1 and worked in various local restaurants, ultimately finishing her career many years later at The Palms. Betty was a dedicated homemaker and loving mother who enjoyed polka dancing and traveling with her husband, Mike. She took great pride in her marriage, in raising her five children, in creating a warm and welcoming home. Her strong Lutheran faith brought her comfort throughout her life.

Betty will be remembered by her children: David (Patty) Zych, Sun Lakes, AZ; Barbara (Jim) McGeough, Lincoln, CA; Brian (Melody) Zych, Kronenwetter; and Dona (Tom, Colonel, USAF, Retired) Kauth, Dover, DE; granddaughter Christine (Jason) Budnik, Chandler, AZ; great-grandchildren Stella, Sophie, and Piper; and her sister, Kathleen (Wayne) Stark, Gig Harbor, WA, along with many nieces and nephews.

She was preceded in death by her husband, Michael Zych; her daughter, Patricia Rose; and her brother, Don Richawlski.

A private family memorial will be held in her honor. In lieu of flowers, the family appreciates donations to Homme Home Foundation-Forest Park Village, 604 S. Webb St., Wittenberg, WI 54499, or St. Peter Evangelical Lutheran Church-Wausau Campus.

We want to thank the Interim Health team/Hospice nurses as well as all of the staff at The Gardens and Stonecrest for their compassionate care over the past year.

records.name: Jeanne M. Downer
records.summary: Jeanne M. Downer, 73, passed away peacefully on January 30, 2026.
records.body:
Jeanne M. Downer, age 73, passed away peacefully on January 30, 2026. She was born on September 3, 1952, the daughter of the late Donald and Joyce (Holland) Sullivan.

Jeanne married the love of her life, Glen, on Christmas Eve in 1986 in her parents' yard – a day filled with love and meaning that perfectly reflected her heart. Jeanne found joy in life's simple pleasures: boating on the pontoon, fishing, and going for rides in the car, even if only for half an hour. No matter the length of the trip, she always made sure to thank Glen for taking her.

She loved shopping, was unfailingly, and had a genuine love for everyone she met. Above all, Jeanne cherished her family and the time she spent with them.

Jeanne is survived by her devoted husband, Glen; her beloved cat, Murphy; and many nieces, nephews, and cousins who will miss her dearly. In addition to her parents, she was preceded in death by her twin brother, Dennis.

Jeanne will be remembered for her kindness, gratitude, and the love she shared so freely with those around her.

A celebration of life with Jeanne's friends and family will be held at Waube Lodge in Lakewood. Date and time are to be determined.

records.name: Kerry K. Juvonen
records.summary: Kerry K. Juvonen, 71, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on January 31, 2026.
records.body:
Kerry (Podboy) Juvonen, 71, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed peacefully from this world on the morning of January 31, 2026, receiving her long-awaited and complete healing in the presence of her Savior, Jesus Christ.

Kerry was born on November 2, 1954, in Waukegan, Illinois, the beloved daughter of Dorothy Podboy and the late Raymond Podboy. She was raised in a close-knit family alongside her sisters and brothers and cherished her family's rich Slovenian heritage. During elementary school, Kerry and her siblings were invited by neighbors to attend a local Baptist church and Camp Grow—an experience that laid the foundation for her lifelong faith in God.

Kerry possessed a vibrant and compassionate spirit. She graduated from Evanston Hospital School of Nursing in 1975 and devoted her professional life to caring for others as a registered nurse. She served patients at Victory Memorial Hospital in Waukegan, Illinois, and later at Wausau Hospital.

On December 15, 1979, Kerry married the love of her life, Anders "Andy" Juvonen. Their marriage of 46 years was marked by faith, devotion, and a deep commitment to family, serving as a steady anchor throughout her life. After a brief time in West Allis, in 1980 Andy and Kerry settled in Wausau, where they raised their three children: Laura, Ellen, and Erik.

Grounded in her faith, Kerry lived out her beliefs through active involvement in both her church and the community. She was a gracious hostess who delighted in bringing friends, family, and neighbors together—often around meals lovingly prepared in her home. Kerry was a deeply engaged and devoted mother. She loved the Lord and intentionally encouraged her children's faith, guiding them to know God through church life and Bible camp. She also volunteered regularly in her children's classrooms and faithfully supported their academic, musical, and athletic pursuits.

Music was a lifelong passion and constant joy for Kerry. She played piano, flute, and French horn; sang in choirs; and filled her home with music. She faithfully nurtured this love in her children through piano and drum lessons, voice instruction, and choir participation. Time spent with her sisters was often marked by spontaneous harmonies—especially "Sisters" from White Christmas. Kerry also had a love for the outdoors and an adventurous spirit. She enjoyed swimming in lakes, exploring the woods with her children in every season, cross-country and downhill skiing, and spending treasured time at the Podboy family cabin on Rock Dam Lake and the Juvonen cabin on Presque Isle Lake.

In 1992, at the age of 37, Kerry was diagnosed with glioblastoma, stage IV brain cancer, and given a grim prognosis. Against all odds, following surgery and treatment, she miraculously entered remission and was blessed with nearly 34 additional years of life. Each day with Kerry was a testimony to God's grace and healing power and a source of hope to those who knew her. She set an extraordinary example of what it means to trust God and live joyfully. Her smile, laugh, and goofy sense of humor brightened the lives of many. When people noticed her arm in a sling or the limp in her step and asked, "What happened?" she would playfully reply, "You should see the other guy." Though she experienced lasting physical limitations that eventually left her reliant on a wheelchair, she met each day with gratitude, grace, and quiet determination, rarely complaining.

Kerry and her family made many lasting memories over the years. Most recently, she cherished time spent with her faithful husband, Andy, family, and friends. She especially delighted in regular outings to Laura's home, where time with her grandchildren brought her immense joy and pride.

Kerry will be remembered for her steadfast faith, joy in the face of adversity, contentment, and enduring love for her family. She will be deeply missed by all who knew her, and her memory will remain a lasting blessing to those whose lives she touched.

The family extends their heartfelt gratitude to the many caregivers who compassionately cared for Kerry throughout the years, most recently the dedicated staff at Wausau Manor and Aspirus Hospice. They are also deeply thankful for the loyal friends and family members who faithfully visited, encouraged, and supported Kerry—walking alongside her and her family through every season of her life with love, prayer, and kindness.

Kerry was preceded in death by her father, Raymond Podboy; her father- and mother-in-law, Alex and Margaret Juvonen; and her nephew, Karl Bodnar.

She is survived by her husband, Anders Juvonen; her mother, Dorothy Podboy; her siblings: Betsy (Mike) Lonchar, Scott (Christine) Podboy, Tina (Dale) Laughner, Jonathan Podboy, and Amy Podboy Reynolds; her sister-in-law, Angela Juvonen; her sister- and brother-in-law, Annette and Ken Bodnar; and many nieces, nephews, and dear friends.

Her legacy continues through her beloved children: Laura (Adam) VanNoord, Ellen Juvonen, and Erik Juvonen; and through her adored grandchildren: Edan, Charlie, Annika, Luke, Silas, and Reuben VanNoord.

A celebration of Kerry's life will be held on March 21, 2026, at Restoration Church, 2125 Franklin Street, Wausau, Wisconsin. Visitation will begin at 2:00 p.m., the memorial service at 3:00 p.m., with a dinner to follow.

In lieu of flowers, donations can be made in Kerry's name to Monk Botanical Gardens of Wausau, WI. The family will select a bench and planting in her memory. This is the woods Kerry and Andy used to walk through together with their children.

records.name: Kimberly D. Bahrs
records.summary: Kimberly D. Bahrs, 53, passed away on January 20, 2026.
records.body:
Kimberly Bahrs, age 53, passed away on January 20, 2026. She was born on November 19, 1972, to William and Brenda (Marlett) Nelson.

Kimberly was a devoted partner, loving mother, sister, and friend. She shared 37 years of her life with her partner, Keith Ligman, building a life filled with love, laughter, and cherished memories.

She was the proud mother of Justin, Dillion, Jordan, Jared, Tyler, and Jonathan. Kimberly's love for her children was constant and unwavering, and her family meant everything to her.

Kimberly had a joyful spirit and a great sense of humor. She was known for her good laugh, her ability to make up words, and her love of talking with friends and family. She found happiness in the simple things—sitting and watching her favorite shows, collecting dolphin and angel figurines, going to old car shows, enjoying sweets, and sipping on Dr. Pepper. She also loved spending time outdoors, going on hikes, and visiting waterfalls. She was all about peace, love, and rainbows.

She is survived by her partner, Keith Ligman; her children; mother, Brenda; her stepbrother, Steve; Aunt, Heather; and Cousin, Nick.

Kimberly was preceded in death by her father, William Nelson; her infant son, Michael; and her aunt, Carol.

Kimberly will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved her. Her warmth, humor, and loving heart will live on in the memories she leaves behind.

records.name: Nancy L. Koehler
records.summary: Nancy L. Koehler, 76, of Edgar, passed away peacefully on February 5, 2026.
records.body:
Nancy L. Koehler, 76, Edgar, passed away peacefully on Thursday, February 5, 2026 at Marshfield Medical Center in Marshfield with her family by her side.

She was born December 3, 1949 in Wausau to Victor and Dolores (Ritger) Schueller. On February 11, 1972, she was united in marriage to Randal W. Koehler. He survives.

Nancy was a woman of deep faith and prayed the rosary daily. Among some of her favorite pastimes, she enjoyed baking, especially sweet treats, traveling with her family-her favorites were trips to the East coast, many camping trips and playing games such as dominoes and sheepshead. She liked to sew, and in her younger years, made many clothing items for her children. She enjoyed shopping, especially with the girls, always looking for a bargain. Nancy was an avid reader and in her retirement years, she frequented the local library. Most of all, she loved her family. She would do anything for her grandchildren. Spending time together during holidays, birthdays celebrations, grandchildren's events and being together no matter what the occasion was the highlight of her life.

Working for the post office in multiple locations for 24 years, Nancy knew the importance of a handwritten note. She had a way of touching so many relatives and friends with a "card in the mail" for a special occasion, just to say she was thinking of them, and always to send thanks for any kindness shown to her. She was very generous and always thought about others before herself. She will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved her.

Survivors include, her loving husband, Randal; three children, Luke (Jamie) Koehler, Edgar, Lindsey (Scott) Kroening, Marathon and Bridget (Brandon) Kaiser, Edgar; eight grandchildren, Malana, Brooks and Taya Koehler, Caleb and Jemma Kroening, Isabella, Amelia and Maxwell Kaiser; six siblings, Patty Steffeck, Stevens Point, Michael (Marie) Schueller, Edgar, Ron (Barb) Schueller, Kent, WA, Jan (Jerry) Jacobs, Wisconsin Rapids, Gary (Cathy) Schueller, Edgar and Kathy (Troy) Bargender, Stratford; and many nieces and nephews.

She was preceded in death by her parents and her brother-in-law, Michael Steffeck.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 12:00 p.m. (Noon) on Friday, February 13, 2026 at St. John Catholic Church, Edgar. Rev. Allen Wierzba will preside. Visitation will be on Thursday from 4:00 until 7:00 p.m. and again on Friday from 10:00 a.m. until time of Mass all at the church. There will be a rosary service at 7:00 p.m. Thursday evening following visitation.

records.name: Christopher C. Batka
records.summary: Christopher C. Batka, 59, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on February 9, 2026.
records.body:
Christopher C. Batka, 59, passed away February 9, 2026 at Marshfield Medical Center, Weston.

He was born May 6, 1966, in Chicago, Illinois. He served his country in the United States Army. On July 23, 2022 he married Rebecca "Becki" Nordrum.

Survivors include his wife, Becki Batka; his son, Christapher Batka(Ashley) and his grandchildren; his stepchildren, Cody Alsteen (Abby and Cooper) and Tianna Daniels (Joseph); his father figure, boss, mentor, and close friend Joe Jordan (Peg); and many of his close friends.

Chris was known for his strong work ethic, generous heart, and unwavering loyalty to the people he loved. He found joy in working with his hands, especially restoring cars, a passion he developed over many years. He loved spending time outdoors and cherished fishing dates with his wife, Becki. Chris had a way of making those around him feel supported and valued, whether through a helping hand, shared laughter, or quiet companionship. He will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved him.

A celebration of life will be at a later date. No services will be held at this time.

records.name: Darlene V. Kell
records.summary: Darlene V. Kell, 92, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on February 8, 2026.
records.body:
Darlene V. Kell passed away at the age of 92 on Sunday February 8, 2026. She was born on April 11, 1933 in Wausau, WI to the late George and Evelyn (Moderow) Krueger.

Darlene went to Wausau area schools and graduated in 1951 from Wausau High School, this is where she met Derald Kell who became her husband. They married on December 1, 1951 and were married for 71 years until Derald's passing in 2023. Together, Darlene and Derald raised four boys, Steven, Russell, David and Kevin.

Darlene was a member of First Methodist Church in Wausau her entire life. She was a greeter for many years.

Darlene enjoyed gambling, trips to California to visit family and loved fishing. She also enjoyed walking every day and visiting with people, she was definitely a social butterfly. She would say that she had the gift of gab to anyone.

She is survived by two sons, Russell (Diane) of Marathon, WI and David (Leela) of Weston, WI; daughters-in-law, Staci of Prescott, AZ and Cheri Kell Wausau, WI; grandchildren, Brian Kell (Rachelle), Jason Kell and Vivian Kell; great-grandchildren, Ty Kell and Trevor Kell; and her siblings, Judy (Dan) Wadzinski, Tom Krueger and Richard (Kim) Krueger.

She was preceded in death by her parents, George and Evelyn Krueger; husband, Derald Kell; two sons, Steven Kell and Kevin Kell; and her siblings, Carol Rhyner, Leila Howland, Janice Normand, Diane Goetz and Ronald Krueger.

Funeral services will be held at 3:00 p.m. on Saturday, February 21, 2026 at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau. Rev. Rebecca Borke will officiate. Visitation will be on Saturday from 1:00 p.m. until time of services at the funeral home. Private burial will be held at Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau at a later date.

records.name: Elaine M. Langhoff
records.summary: Elaine M. Langhoff, 89, of Hillsboro, formerly of Athens, Wisconsin, passed away on February 8, 2026.
records.body:
Elaine M. Langhoff, 89, of Hillsboro, formerly of Athens, passed away on Sunday, February 8, 2026, at Milestone Senior Living in Hillsboro under the care of St. Croix Hospice.

She was born on March 27, 1936, to the late Henry and Inga (Johnsurd) Halvorson in Fenwood. Elaine was baptized at home on May 14, 1936, and confirmed on July 3, 1949, at St. Peter Lutheran Church in Fenwood. Elaine attended Fenwood Grade School, Edgar High School, and beauty school in Wausau. She was united in marriage to Roger Langhoff on November 4, 1961, at St. Stephen Lutheran Church in Wausau. She was a member of Christ United Church in Athens and was also a former president of the American Legion Auxiliary and WWT Homemakers Club.

Survivors include her daughters, Roxanne (John) Glassford of Marathon and Holly (David) DeWitt of Tomah; Step-son, Scott (JoAnna) Langhoff of San Diego, California; grandchildren, Cassandra Glassford (Kris Bucholtz) of Stevens Point, Cole (Holly) Glassford of Oshkosh, Bryan (April) Langhoff of Clintonville, Brittany (Kyle) Kast of Tomah, Nicole (Scott) Sobczak of Yuba, Jordan DeWitt of Ontario, Allison (Jason) Borchardt of Wausau, and Sarah (Kip) Krueger of Wausau; great-grandchildren, Ethan, Sharlette, Trysten, Dante, Luna, Levi, Courtney, David, Cassius, Finton, and a great-grandbaby due in February 2026; a great-great-grandchild due in April 2026; and sister, JoAnn Swanson of Abrams.

She was preceded in death by her husband, Roger; daughter, Michelle; parents, Henry and Inga; and siblings, Doris, Betty, Orvis, Helen, Arnold, Melvin, Irving, Florence, and Christine.

A funeral service will be at 11:00 a.m. on Friday, February 13, 2026, at Christ United Church, Athens, with Rev. Teri Hanson officiating. Visitation will begin at 4:00 p.m. on Thursday, February 12, at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Athens, and conclude at 7:00 p.m. Visitation will continue on Friday, beginning at 9:30 a.m. until the time of service, all at the church. Private family burial will take place in Restlawn Memorial Park, town of Texas.

records.name: George Paul Klimowicz
records.summary: George Paul Klimowicz, 92, of Iola, Wisconsin, passed away on February 9, 2026.
records.body:
George Paul Klimowicz, 92, passed with grace and strength on February 9th, 2026, in Iola, Wis. Born at home on Strongs Ave in Stevens Point, Wisconsin on April 20th, 1933, He was the celebrated son of Catherine & Henry Klimowicz. George lived his life guided by a sincere devotion to Jesus Christ and a commitment to serving others for the glory of God.

George is survived by his loving wife and best friend Helen Akin Klimowicz. Their unconventional partnership was deeply meaningful, grounded in independence, joyful companionship, and unwavering devotion. Together, they raised four children: Henry (Brooke) Klimowicz, Katherine (Terry) Simenson, Karolyn (Brent) Halstead, and Khristine (Jeff) Schwoeppe. George was a loving father and a proud grandfather to Alexandria, Johanna, Jack, Wesley, Ryan, Emily, Eloise, and Sarah. He was also blessed with great?grandchildren: Obadiah, Ashley, Harper, and Hank, as well as two soon?to?arrive great?grandchildren—a baby girl and a baby boy who will carry his name, George.

After moving to Milwaukee, WI. George graduated from the University of Wisconsin–Madison and after serving in the US Army worked as a Certified Public Accountant for the State of Wisconsin Tax Department. He carried himself with integrity and high standards in his profession and was known for leaving no tax unaccounted for—a reputation that did not always endear him to the corporations he audited.

It is said that life is not defined by its beginning or its end, but by the dash in-between—and George lived his "dash" to the fullest. A diverse hobbyist and lifelong science enthusiast, never afraid to try new things or continue learning. George enjoyed 50 years in retirement giving him plenty of time to explore his passions. George embraced life to the end performing his final clarinet concert in 2025 and dancing daily with friends and family. He will be fondly remembered for his welcoming smile, sharp and unfiltered wit, infectious humor, and boundless love of life. George lived by his self-proclaimed motto, "Always leave them laughing."

Being outdoors and staying active were especially important to George, and his favorite place in the world was his land on Sunset Lake. It was a place rich with memories—from attending Boy Scout camp there as a young boy, many summers spent camping with his family. At Sunset Lake, George swam across the lake daily into his 90s and windsurfed well into his late 80s. The lake was a place of peace and reflection, where he felt deeply connected to God and to his family.

His legacy endures through the many lives he touched and through the quiet example of a man who sought daily to live in a way that honored God.

George, you will be missed. We will see you soon on the other side of the lake, George. Please keep dancing until we get there.

In lieu of flowers, donations can be made in George's name to the Sunset Lake Association.

records.name: Janeel Mae Westberg
records.summary: Janeel Mae Westberg, 73, of Schofield, Wisconsin, passed away on February 8, 2026.
records.body:
Janeel Mae Westberg, 73, of Schofield, passed away at Aspirus Wausau Hospital on February 8, 2026.

Janeel was born in Wausau to the late Alvin and Marcella (Ninnemann) Kell on May 13, 1952. She married Gary L. Westberg on December 13, 1976, and raised two beautiful daughters.

She enjoyed reading books, being around her cats, and playing card games as a family. She also enjoyed working in early childhood education.

She is survived by her husband of 49 years, Gary; daughters, Tammy Westberg and Sarah Westberg; sister, Julaine Raduechel; and sister-in-law, Marlys Kell. She is preceded in death by her brother, Jerome Kell; brother-in-law, Harvey Raduechel; and parents Alvin and Marcella Kell.

Service will be held at 11:00 AM on Tuesday, February 17, 2026, at Restlawn Memorial Park Chapel Mausoleum. Pastor Jim Gates will officiate.

records.name: Janet L. Mohr
records.summary: Janet L. Mohr, 75, passed away peacefully on January 29, 2026.
records.body:
Janet L. Mohr, age 75, passed away peacefully on January 29, 2026. She was born on February 9, 1950, the daughter of the late Earl and Esther (Stroik) Steinke.

Janet was a loving and caring woman who was always ready to do whatever it took to help her family. She found joy in working outside and tending to her garden, a place where she felt most at peace. Above all, Janet especially cherished the time she spent with her grandchildren, who brought her immense happiness and pride.

She is survived by her children, Mike (Desi), Carmen, and Amanda (Mike); her five grandchildren, Kayla, Aryanna, Brittney, Kassy, and DomiNick; her three great-grandchildren, Hadley, Jaylee, and Ember; and her brother, Ken Steinke. She is further survived by many nieces, nephews, and cousins who will miss her dearly.

In addition to her parents, Janet was preceded in death by her infant brother, John Michael, and her brother, Gordon.

Janet's kindness, devotion to family, and gentle spirit will be fondly remembered by all who knew and loved her.

A celebration of life will be held at a later date.

records.name: Karen Louise Gleisner
records.summary: Karen Louise Gleisner, 71, of Rib Mountain, Wisconsin, passed away on February 9, 2026.
records.body:
Karen Louise Gleisner (Bunde) passed peacefully from the arms of her loving husband Mark, into the loving arms of God on February 9th, 2026. She was born on April 15, 1954, to William and Geraldine (Krigsvold) Bunde in Eau Claire, Wisconsin. Karen was an EC Memorial HS "Old Abe" and an EC University "Blue Gold" graduating in 1976 with a BFA in Graphic Design.

Karen met her love and partner in life Mark in Milwaukee, and were married on April 27, 1991, joyfully gaining an instant stepdaughter Laura.

Karen had a 30-year career in television as a Graphic Designer and Art Director. She started at WITI-6 in Milwaukee, then moved to WDTN-2 in Dayton, OH, and finally returned to Milwaukee at WTMJ-4 where she spent 21 years as Art Director.

Karen always had an interest in learning and exploring different forms of visual arts and was an active member of several artist organizations including Cream City Calligraphers and Paper Arts, the Manito Art League and the Wisconsin Valley Art Association. After retiring in 2006 and later moving to Rib Mountain, WI, she started creating and showing her hand-cut paper sculptures, winning numerous awards.

When she was not working on her art pieces, she enjoyed working out at the "Y" and hanging out at The Landing. Karen also enjoyed spending summers at their cabin in Manitowish Waters, boating, fishing for Crappies, watching "her" white deer raise her fawns and especially entertaining her grandchildren with her games and treasure hunts.

Karen is predeceased by her parents, her nephew Scott Olson and her sister-in-law Greta Russel. Karen is survived by her husband Mark, step-daughter Laura (Tom) Stevens, grandchildren William, Henry and Victoria, her siblings Sandra Olson, Barbara (Tom) Olson and Alan Bunde, niece Megan (Sam) Hunt, sister-in-Law Gail (Barry) Thompson, nephews Joe and Mike (Stephanie) Thompson and numerous other relatives.

The family thanks the doctors and staff at Aspirus Cancer Center, Froedtert Cancer Center and Aspirus Hospital Wausau for their compassionate care during her cancer journey.

A Memorial Service will be held on Friday, February 20, 2026, at 12:00 P.M. at St. Andrew Lutheran Church, 150202 County Road NN, Wausau, WI 54401, with a visitation at church, from 11:00 A.M. up until the time of service. Pastor Jack Busche will officiate. A luncheon will be served after the memorial service.

records.name: Linda Lou Grawien
records.summary: Linda Lou Grawien, 76, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on February 4, 2025.
records.body:
Linda Lou Grawien, age 76, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed on Wednesday, February 4, 2025, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital, in Wausau. She was born on November 5, 1949, in Merrill, Wisconsin, the daughter of Robert and Doris (Bruns) Yaeger. On April 27, 1968, she married Lloyd Grawien at St. Paul's Lutheran Church in Pine River.

Linda was a kind, caring person who never knew a stranger. She could talk to anyone and would often be a calming influence in any situation without even trying. She enjoyed cooking, reading, sewing, and was a proud member of Trinity Lutheran Church in Wausau. She is survived by her husband, Lloyd; her beloved sons, Todd Grawien and Danny (Cindy) Grawien, and her two grandchildren, Jordan (Morgan) and Jayden.

She is preceded in death by her parents, Robert and Doris, and one sister, Melody.

A Memorial Gathering of family and friends will be held on Thursday, February 19th, 2026, from 4:00 PM – 8:00 PM at Helke Funeral Home and Cremation Service, 302 Spruce St. Wausau, WI. Inurnment will be held in Restlawn Memorial Park Cemetery in Wausau.

records.name: N. June Plant
records.summary: N. June Plant, 93, of Kronenwetter, Wisconsin, passed away on February 8, 2026.
records.body:
N. June Plant, 93, of Kronenwetter, passed away peacefully on February 8, 2026, at her home.

June was born on June 4, 1932, to the late Alfred and Bessie (Sefton) Zich. She married R. Ross Plant on April 12, 1952, in Wausau, WI.

June was a wonderful wife and mother. She was always practical and had a wonderful sense of humor. June enjoyed traveling and camping, visiting many of the National parks, as well as many fishing trips to Canada with friends and family. She loved to spend time in the northwoods and the winters in Florida.

June had many interests. She made beautiful quilts and afghans that she gifted to her children and grandchildren. She was an avid reader and she enjoyed gardening and loved her flowers. And you would have never seen her without a dog close by as she and Ross had many dogs over the years.

She is survived by her husband Ross, of nearly 74 years. She is also survived by her children; son, Michael (Cynthia) Plant; and daughters, Cheryl (Paul) Jahns, Susan (Rick) Paszkot, and Barb (Keith) Friedlein; grandchildren, Kelly (Wade) Puntney, Christopher (Kirby) Plant, Matthew Plant, Brian (Amy) Jahns, Andy (Carly) Jahns, Aimee (Kyle Buehler) Paszkot, Jonathan (Jaewoo) Paszkot, David (Piper) Pozorski, Laura Friedlein, and Lucas Friedlein; great-grandchildren, Lana, Ethan, Tenley, Harlow, Henry, Violet, Emily, William, Ryan, Rhys, Lucy, Vincent, and Trinity. She is further survived by her brother-in-law, Wayne Plant; and former sister-in-law, Diane Plant. She was preceded in death by her sister and brother-in-law, Joyce (Jack) Schuetz.

A memorial service will be planned in the future.

records.name: Nancy L. Jirovec
records.summary: Nancy L. Jirovec, 77, passed away peacefully on February 5, 2026.
records.body:
Nancy L. Jirovec, age 77, passed away peacefully on February 5, 2026. She was born on July 14, 1948, the beloved daughter of the late Carl and Lennea (Luoma) Hill.

On August 11, 1973, Nancy married the love of her life, Ronald Jirovec, beginning a partnership that spanned more than 52 years. Together they built a life centered on God, love, companionship, and family, and welcomed one son, Chad, who was a great source of pride and joy to Nancy.

Nancy enjoyed the simple pleasures of life. She loved going to the movies and especially cherished traveling around the country with Ronald. Some of her happiest moments were spent on the road, side by side, exploring new places together by car and enjoying the journey as much as the destination.

Nancy is survived by her devoted husband, Ronald; her son, Chad; her sister, Barbara Bush; her brother, Douglas Hill; brother and sister-in-laws; Wayne (Kathy) Jirovec, Barbara (Tryg) Torgerson, Lana (Glenn) Seefeldt; and several nieces and nephews who will remember her fondly. She was preceded in death by her parents, Carl and Lennea Hill.

Nancy will be remembered for her quiet strength, her love for family, and the deep bond she shared with those closest to her.

Her presence will be greatly missed by all who knew and loved her.

The family would like to express their appreciation to Compassus Hospice, Weston Memory Care, and all of her medical staff.

Memorial service for Nancy will be held at Wausau Bible Church at 1300 Grand Avenue on Friday, February 13th: visitation 9 – 10:30, service at 11:00 o'clock with luncheon to follow. In lieu of flowers memorials may be given to Bridge St. Mission, Wausau, Wisc.

records.name: Randall B. Arnett
records.summary: Randall B. Arnett, 74, of Rothschild, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly on February 6, 2026.
records.body:
Randall B. Arnett, 74, of Rothschild, WI, passed away unexpectedly on February 6, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born on June 5, 1951, in Beloit, Wisconsin, son of the late James "Carroll" and Edith (Evans) Arnett. On May 17, 1985, he married the love of his life, Dawn Musson in Rhinelander.

Randall attended Harper College in Palatine, Illinois. He began his career in sales with Coca Cola, where he devoted 25 years of service before embarking on a new chapter with his wife, Dawn. Together they fulfilled a dream by opening the Coffee Haus Inn in Schofield. Later, after moving their business to St. Petersburg, Florida for four years, they returned to Wisconsin to be closer to family. Randall continued his commitment to sales for over a decade with Schwan's food service until his retirement in 2013.

Randall was known as "all in" when it came to his hobbies. In his younger years, he was a fishing fanatic, often embarking on trips to Canada in pursuit of walleye. He held a particular fondness for the Northwoods of Wisconsin. In later years, Randall became actively involved with the International Pipe Smokers Club. His participation included attending their yearly convention in Chicago, where he enjoyed meeting in person with friends he formed through their Facebook club, which met online every Saturday. Family held a special place in Randall's heart, and his cherished grandchildren will fondly remember the silly side of his personality most.

Randall is survived by his wife, Dawn Arnett of Rothschild; daughter, Jenny (Mathias Welle) Fox of Rothschild, son, Ryan (Deirdra Moon) Arnett of Little Chute, son, Nathan (Germaine) Fondie of Green Bay, and daughter, Tina Arnett; his four beloved grandchildren, Savannah, Zakkary, Madilynn, and Michelle; brothers-in-law, Jeffrey (Mary) Musson and Shane (Tina) Musson, both of Rhinelander, and George Kojis of New Zealand; nephew, Theo Parker; and his cherished cat, Pickles, along with many other relatives and friends who were fortunate to know him.

In addition to his parents, he was further preceded in death by his sister, Carlen Arnett.

records.name: Ronald Bruce Peters
records.summary: Ronald Bruce Peters, 87, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully.
records.body:
Ronald Bruce Peters, 87, passed away peacefully in the home in Wausau that he had built by hand with his wife, his father-in-law and brother-in-law in 1967 and where he had lived for almost 60 years. He was surrounded by family in his living room, exactly as he had desired. Ronnie had a years-long illness of COPD and congestive heart failure which he continued to fight with all his strength.

Ronnie was born December 11, 1938 in his parents' home in rural Town of Stettin, Wisconsin. He was the fourth of eight siblings in his family. On February 24, 1961, he married the love of his life, Faye Janet Grebe. They met at Wausau High School when Faye was 14 and Ronnie was 16 and were married for almost 65 years. Faye cared for Ronnie during his long illness showing an amazing Christian example of love and devotion. Ronnie told all who would listen how much he loved Faye and how lucky he was to have her as his wife.

Ronnie worked for 39 years at Wausau Metals in various factory supervisory roles and made lifelong friends with many of his co-workers.

Ronnie loved to hunt and fish, especially on Clear Lake near Tomahawk, Wisconsin, where his in-laws Fred and Shirley Niemeyer owned a cottage and in Florida with his nephew Jerry Chancellor. Although he was no longer able to hunt due to his physical condition and the loss over the years of many of his beloved hunting partners, he fished all through his 80s, moving to a pontoon when he could no longer step down into a fishing boat. Ronnie also loved to play cards with his friends and family, especially cribbage, pinochle and sheepshead. Everyone wanted to be Ronnie's card partner, not necessarily because he was a skilled player or unusually lucky—although he was—but because having him as a partner made the game more fun. Ronnie was a huge Packers and Cubs fan and also enjoyed retiring to bed early to read biographies and history.

Ronnie had a gift for making people laugh—he was always telling jokes, making good natured "smart aleck" remarks or pulling goofy faces. He continued this playful attitude until the end, making jokes with the nurses at the hospital. He started silly traditions, sneaking up behind family members on Easter to crack dyed eggs on their heads. He would strike up conversations with total strangers and leave them smiling. He also loved to tell stories and would entertain—and sometimes shock—his grandchildren with crazy tales of his rambunctious and rowdy youth with his four brothers while Faye would shake her head. Ronnie never took himself very seriously, but he was intensely serious about loving and protecting his family, often saying "If you don't have family, you have nothing." He loved to hear about the accomplishments and activities of his nephews, nieces, grandchildren and great grandchildren and was so proud of all of them.

Ronnie was also blessed with a lifelong best friend, Hank Radtke, who predeceased Ronnie. Hank and Ronnie's relationship was special and Ronnie was fond of saying that in all their lives, he and Hank never had an argument. Hank was like a fifth brother to Ronnie.

Ronnie was preceded in death by his parents, Alfred and Helen Peters; his brothers Alfred "Fritz" Peters, Kenneth Peters, William Peters, and Gary Peters; his sisters Patricia Peters and Karen Peters; and many of his brothers- and sisters-in-law.

Ronnie is survived by and will be deeply missed, by his loving wife Faye; his daughters Peggy (Jay) Reich and Nancy (Travis) Henke; his son Troy (Amy) Peters; his grandchildren Ryan (Tina) Reich, Nathan (Michelle) Reich, John Reich, Jessica Henke, Zachary Henke, William Peters, Caroline Peters, Phoebe Peters; his great-grandchildren Wyatt Reich, Caston Reich and Charlotte Reich; his sister Kristine (Gus) Gollhardt; his sisters-in law Irene (Gary) Peters and Shirley (Fred) Niemeyer; and many nieces, nephews and cousins. Ronnie's family would like to thank dedicated Cubs fan Dr. Bart Isaacson and the staff of Aspirus Wausau Hospital for all the wonderful care given to Ronnie in the past years of his fight with COPD and congestive heart failure. Ronnie deeply appreciated the personal care and compassion of these doctors, nurses and staff.

Service will be held Saturday, February 14, 2026 at 12 p.m. at Wausau Bible Church, 1300 Grand Avenue, Wausau, WI 54403, with the Reverend Chris Juvinall officiating. Visitation hours will be from 10 a.m. until the time of the service at Wausau Bible Church. All funeral arrangements are being handled by Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home. Memorials may be directed to the family.

records.name: Rose Marie Krohn-Koehler
records.summary: Rose Marie Krohn-Koehler, 92, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on February 1, 2026.
records.body:
Rose Marie Krohn-Koehler, 92, of Wausau, WI, passed away peacefully on February 1, 2026, at Prairie Place Assisted Living and Memory Care in Ripon, Wisconsin.

Rose was born on September 23, 1933, in Wausau to Melvin and Myrtle (Plautz) Arnette. She was born again by water and the Word at her baptism at the Church of Saint Mary in Wausau on December 10, 1933, and later confirmed in the faith begun by her baptism on March 2, 1952, at Our Savior's Lutheran Church in Wausau. She married Earlin Krohn in 1953 and Bob Koehler in 2001.

A woman of deep faith, Rose was actively involved in church life throughout her years. She sang in church choirs, taught Sunday School, and served on the Planned Giving committee at St. Peter Lutheran Church. It is there that she was a member for the last four decades. She was also a member of the Lutheran Women's Missionary Society.

She had a diverse professional career, including work in medical offices, retail jewelry sales, for a title company, and as a realtor. Her care and thoroughness in her fifteen-year career as a real estate agent in the Wausau area and her serving as a member of the Wausau Board of Realtors brought her a fulfilled sense of accomplishment.

She loved gardening, the arts, cheering on the Packers and Brewers, decorating elaborately at Christmas, baking cookies, hosting holiday meals, and she regularly enjoyed the tasty Friday night fish fry.

Rose is survived by her children: Joey (Lisa) Krohn of Wimberley, TX; Jay (Jacqueline) Krohn of Minneapolis, MN; Jud (Barbara) Krohn of Pickett, WI; and her step-daughter Patricia "Trish" (Donald) Schuman of Morgantown, WV; and grandchildren and great grandchildren Joshua (Leah) Krohn and their daughter Harper and son James; Naomi (Gus) Martinez and their daughter Tess and son Ethan; Jared Krohn, Garrett Krohn and Austin Krohn; Thomas Krohn and Lance (Eleanor) Sylvan; Haley (Adam) Grimwood and Braeden Krohn; Theodore Schuman and Shawna Laity; as well as many nieces, nephews, extended family members, and fellow church members and friends.

She was preceded in death by her parents; husbands, Earlin Krohn and Robert Koehler; sisters, Lenore Gertschen and RuthAnn Jahns; brother-in-law, Gerald Gertschen; stepson, Robert Allan Koehler; and grandson, Trevor Elias Krohn.

The family thanks the staff of Prairie Place Assisted Living, Compassus Hospice, and Ascend Funeral Home for their compassionate and attentive care.

A worship service is planned for 11:00 AM on Monday, March 2, 2026, at St. Peter Ev. Lutheran Church, 1351 Grand Avenue Schofield, WI 54476. Pastor Kevin Schnake will officiate. Visitation will be held from 9:00 AM until the time of service at the church. A luncheon will follow the service at the church. Interment will be in Restlawn Memorial Park, Town of Texas.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be given to the Student Assistance Fund at St. Peter Lutheran Church, Schofield, WI; The American Macular Degeneration Foundation; the Performing Arts Foundation of the Grand Theatre (Wausau); and Thai Village, Inc. [PO BOX 132 Deerfield, WI, 53531]

records.name: Todd David Wanta
records.summary: Todd David Wanta, 63, of Las Vegas, Nevada, died at his home on February 8, 2026.
records.body:
Todd David Wanta, age 63, died at his home in Las Vegas, Nevada on Sunday, February 8, 2026. He was born on May 23, 1962 in Wausau, Wisconsin to David Wanta and Nancy Kurszewski Wanta of Bevent. Todd grew up in Bevent and attended the Wittenberg-Birnamwood schools. He married Julie Oleson of Hatley, Wisconsin 45 years ago in 1980, and they had two daughters.

Todd and Julie ventured into the greyhound racing business in 1995 and had a kennel in Wisconsin Dells, Wisconsin; later moving to a larger track in Hudson, Wisconsin. They trained and raced the greyhounds for five years and had success, especially with one of their dogs who proved to be a champion and held numerous records at the track. This was an intense business for them that required hard work seven days a week, but they enjoyed it because of their love for the animals.

It ended in the year 2000, when Todd was diagnosed with the progressive, crippling disease, Multiple Sclerosis. He was 38 years old at the time and decided he did not want to wait to fulfill one of his dreams, which was to move to Las Vegas to join his mother and brother in the family business which was owning and operating three apartment complexes of over 400 units. He sold the dogs and the kennels and his home in Kronenwetter, Wisconsin, took his wife and two daughters and headed west. He was able to help with the physical work for 10 years, until his disease, again, made it too much of a struggle. He retired in 2015.

Todd enjoyed living in Las Vegas with all of its interesting things to do. He loved football and was an excellent handicapper. Every year, he entered a 17-week football tournament sponsored by Station Casinos with hundreds of other entrants. It required that he choose the winning team of five games each week, and the year he won first place, he took home a large jackpot. This became more than a hobby for Todd; it became a challenge. He spent many hours each week studying and preparing his entries.

It was eleven years ago when Todd lost his ability to walk. The disease then seemed to accelerate until he could no longer stand, nor could he sit nor could he bend his elbows. He became totally disabled and had been bedridden since 2023. Multiple sclerosis had won the battle that Todd had fought for 25 years – a fight well fought.

Todd had been treated by numerous doctors throughout his illness. He doctored in Marshfield and Wausau, Wisconsin, the Mayo Clinic in Rochester, Minnesota, the Lou Ruvo Center for Brain Health in Las Vegas, Nevada, and neurology specialists in Santa Barbara and Los Angeles, California.

Yet, Todd never lost his pleasant disposition, his great sense of humor, and he always had a smile for everyone. He was not only very kind, but he was a sensitive soul and hated being a burden and confined to the chair. He would feel so sorry for others who were actually in better condition than he was. His life was cut short, but yet he did manage to have fun and enjoy the time he had here because he had so many fun and interesting things to do.

Survivors include his wife, Julie Wanta of Las Vegas, his father, David Wanta (Jennie Awana) of Schofield, Wisconsin, his mother Nancy Dudley of Las Vegas, daughters Amanda Wanta and Angela Wanta of Las Vegas, one brother Troy (Ginger) Wanta of Las Vegas, and grandsons Trentin and Casen Wanta and Cash Bruns, all of Las Vegas.

"He couldn't walk; now he can run!"

records.name: Dean A. Bohlman
records.summary: Dean A. Bohlman, 76, of Eland, passed away peacefully at his home on Thursday, February 12, 2026.
records.body:
Dean A. Bohlman, 76, of Eland, passed away peacefully at his home on Thursday, February 12, 2026, surrounded by his loving family and under the compassionate care of LeRoyer Hospice.

Dean was born on September 3, 1949, in Wausau, to Howard and Marjorie (Frohman) Bohlman. He proudly served his country in the United States Army and was honorably discharged.

On October 21, 2006, Dean was united in marriage to Joanne Bielen, who remained his loving partner and best friend.

Dean was a skilled craftsman and the owner and operator of Bohlman Construction. Over the years, his talents shaped countless apartment buildings, duplexes, businesses, nursing and CBRF facilities, and homes throughout Wisconsin, Colorado, and Hawaii. He especially cherished time spent in his shop, where he transformed simple pieces of lumber into treasured works of art — crafting furniture, signs, and beautiful live-edge bar tops that will remain lasting reminders of his creativity and skill.

An avid outdoorsman, Dean found peace in nature. He enjoyed hunting, fishing, and trapping, and he had a deep appreciation for wildlife. Though he loved animals of all kinds, dogs held a special place in his heart — especially his beloved Rufus, Dexter and Lily. Dean also enjoyed the occasional trip to the casino, but his greatest joy came from spending time with his family, particularly his grandchildren.

Dean is lovingly survived by his wife, Joanne Bielen-Bohlman; his children, Cory (Carrie) Bohlman of Wittenberg, Calley (Ryan) Mackenzie of Poulsbo, WA, Sam Bohlman of Sun Prairie, and Casey (Alicia) Bohlman of LaCrosse; four grandchildren, Riley (Kylie) Bohlman, Colton (Paige) Bohlman, Louie Mackenzie, and Eli Bohlman; his sister, Pam Daffron of Eland; his cherished dog, Lily; and many other relatives and friends. He was preceded in death by his parents.

records.name: Eric J. Lindstrum
records.summary: Eric J. Lindstrum, 61, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly from cardiac arrest on February 10, 2026.
records.body:
Eric J. Lindstrum, 61, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly from cardiac arrest on February 10, 2026. He was born on March 17, 1964, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to Mary Ann Lindstrum and the late Stephen Lindstrum. He grew up in Athens, Wisconsin, where he attended Athens High School and graduated in 1982. Eric married the love of his life, his wife, Karen Kelly, on April 27, 1991. They were best friends, partners in everything, and were always laughing together, creating a life filled with love and family.

Eric devoted 30 years of his career to Greenheck Fan Corporation, where he worked as a team lead and was known as one of the hardest working people his family and coworkers knew. He retired last year, leaving behind a legacy of dedication, steadiness, and quiet leadership.

Above all, Eric loved his family. He found joy in both the big milestones and the small, everyday moments spent together. He enjoyed watching football and basketball, impressively knowing every player. He loved golfing, especially the time he got a hole in one. He had a deep love for the outdoors as an avid deer hunter and fisherman. He even caught a shark in Puerto Vallarta. Eric loved to go to supper clubs and would often repeat the saying, "This is the best ____ I've ever had." Years ago, he also cherished the time he spent coaching his young daughters in softball, creating memories that his family will always hold close. Eric was one of the kindest people you could ever meet. He was friendly to everyone, level headed in any situation, and known for a dry sense of humor that those closest to him cherished.

Eric is survived by his loving wife, Karen Lindstrum; his daughters, Kelsey (Brad) Borchardt and Alissa (Michael) Powell; and his beloved grandchildren, Bryson Borchardt and Brooklyn Borchardt. He is also survived by his mother, Mary Ann Lindstrum; his siblings, Kris (Chris) Barber and Sandi (Keith) Fierek; his sisters-in-law, Kim Kelly and Kathy Dobson; along with many nieces, nephews, and other extended family members and friends who will miss him dearly.

He was preceded in death by his father, Stephen Lindstrum, and his father-in-law and mother-in-law, Donald and Nancy Kelly.

Eric's family takes comfort in their faith, knowing that God loved him and that he is now in heaven.

records.name: Jeanette Bresnahan
records.summary: Jeanette (Jan) Bresnahan, 87, passed away on February 8, 2018, at her home.
records.body:
Jeanette (Jan) Bresnahan, 87 passed away on February 8th, 2018, at her home under the care of Compassus Hospice Service and loving family.

She was born to the late William and Arlene Rohloff December 30th, 1938.

Jan married Pat Bresnahan at St Mary's Church on February 8th, 1958. Love is a powerful thing – Pat passed away one day after their anniversary in 2018 and Jan passed on their anniversary. They both cherished their marriage and would not leave this world before another year of celebration. This speaks to the powerful bond they shared together. Jan truly lost the love of her life. For over 60 years they did everything together and were inseparable, Jan missed him dearly.

Jan became a very strong and independent woman after the loss of her husband Pat. She was always busy doing something to occupy her time. Most notably she enjoyed spending time tending to her garden, numerous flower beds and doing lawn care. She could always be seen riding on her beloved John Deere 'tractor'.

Jan was very dedicated to her family and church but did work a few jobs early on in life. She worked as a waitress at Palm's Supper Club and Lo-mar's (currently BB Jacks.) It wasn't until the kids started to grow up that she realized her true love, interior design. Her husband Pat liked to fix things, and she knew how to make a home beautiful. They worked well together as a team as they proudly refurbished 18 homes across the Wausau area.

She is survived by her children Pat (Terri) Bresnahan, Jill (Roger) Reisner, & John Bresnahan. Grandchildren: Jenny Trzebiatowski, Brad (Abby) Dobeck, Heidi Desjarlais, Jack Bresnahan, Kadine Bresnahan, Megan Bresnahan, & great grandchildren: Reese, Grey, Rylan, Isaiah, Vaughn, & Emily. Nieces & nephews and many dear friends.

She is preceded in death by her husband Pat, and daughter Kim Nelson.

Mom always said, "If you have something nice, take care of it". Jan Instilled these values within her family… appreciating and taking care of the people and life around her. Rest in peace mom, thank you for being a loving mother, friend, and mentor.

records.name: Lloyd J. Jolin
records.summary: Lloyd J. Jolin, 69, of Wittenberg, died on Thursday, February 12, 2026, at his home.
records.body:
Lloyd J. Jolin, 69 of Wittenberg, died on Thursday, February 12, 2026, at his home, under the care of his family and Interim Hospice.

Lloyd was born on June 7, 1956, in Rhinelander, the son of Vernon and Annis (Jackson) Jolin.

On August 5, 1978, Lloyd was united in marriage to Marcia Stewart in Tigerton.

Lloyd worked for many years as a laborer at Morgan Doors and alongside his brothers in Jolin Plastering and Jolin Lawncare. He was a beloved member of the Wittenberg community where many people knew him from The Olde Dime Store. He could be found talking to everyone and was known as one of the "coffee clutch guys" at the Tigerton Café.

Lloyd enjoyed hunting and fishing, passions he was unable to pursue in recent years. We can only imagine he is now making up for lost time with his brother Bob, laughing together until they cry. He loved watching the deer and birds that gathered outside his patio window and treasured time spent with his furry companions, Bud and Duncan. Above all, Lloyd deeply valued time with his family.

Lloyd is survived by his wife, Marcia; daughter, Laura; son, Mike; sisters, Mary (Floyd) Johnson, Emmie (Dennis) Clauson; brothers, Ken Jolin, Jon Jolin, Jerry Jolin, Don (Kathy) Jolin and Vern Jolin Jr.; brothers and sisters-in-law, Debra Walsh, Mary Harris and Dennis (Linda) Stewart, as well as numerous nieces and nephews that he helped raise.

Lloyd was preceded in death by his parents; brothers, Ed Jolin and Bob (Rose) Jolin; sisters, Evelyn Voight and Charlotte Fisher; father and mother-in-law, Jim and Sarah Stewart, and brothers-in-law, David Stewart and Dale Harris.

records.name: Carl A. Totzke
records.summary: Carl A. Totzke, age 82, passed away on February 12, 2026, at House of the Dove in Marshfield, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Carl A. Totzke went to be with his son, Jeremy, on Thursday, February 12, 2026, at House of the Dove, Marshfield, WI. He was born April 22, 1943 in Wausau, WI to Albert and Agnes Totzke. Carl lived a life defined by service, family and community roots.

Carl graduated from Edgar High School in 1961. He proudly enlisted in the United States Army, serving in active duty from 1961 to 1963. Carl returned home in 1963 to work alongside his father on the family farm, while also driving a milk truck, continuing a strong tradition of hard work and dedication to rural life.

In 1965 Carl married the love of his life, Jean Paul, in Edgar, WI. Together they built a life centered on family and community while raising four children.

Carl was deeply service minded and took great pride in giving back. Over the years he served his community in many roles including the Frankfort town board, the Edgar School board and as treasurer of his church. He was a member of the Marathon County Holstein board, a founding member of the Frankfort Freedom Riders, served on the Midland Cooperative board, member of the Wausau Area Jaycees and a proud lifetime member of the VFW. Family was at the heart of Carl's life. He loved to attend his children's activities and was a proud supporter of Edgar High School athletics. Carl was especially proud of his role as a grandfather to Matthew, Brienna, Cameron and Grayson. He cherished attending their events and spending time with each of them creating special memories along the way. One particular meaningful experience was sharing the Never Forgotten Honor flight with his granddaughter Brienna, and experience he held close to his heart.

Carl is survived by his wife of 60 years, Jean, his children, Cathy (Rob), Jeff (Jess) and Anthony (Annette); his grandchildren, Matthew (Kayley), Brienna (Tyler), Cameron (Cayden) and Grayson; his great grandson, Theo Albert and his special grand dog, Yeti. He is further survived by his sisters, Jane, Kaye and Kris; a brother, Aaron and numerous nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his son, Jeremy; his parents, Albert and Agnes Totzke and his brother, John.

Carl will be remembered for his quiet unwavering service, his smile and friendly waves and the genuine care he showed to his family, friends and community.

The funeral service will be held at 12:00 noon, Saturday, February 21, 2026 at Trinity Lutheran Church, Athens, WI. Rev. Mark Schwalenberg will preside. Visitation will be held from 9:30 am until the time of the service all at the church on Saturday.

records.name: Darlene V. Kell
records.summary: Darlene V. Kell, age 92, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on February 8, 2026.
records.body:
Darlene V. Kell passed away at the age of 92 on Sunday February 8, 2026. She was born on April 11, 1933 in Wausau, WI to the late George and Evelyn (Moderow) Krueger.

Darlene went to Wausau area schools and graduated in 1951 from Wausau High School, this is where she met Derald Kell who became her husband. They married on December 1, 1951 and were married for 71 years until Derald's passing in 2023. Together, Darlene and Derald raised four boys, Steven, Russell, David and Kevin

Darlene was a member of First Methodist Church in Wausau her entire life. She was a greeter for many years.

Darlene enjoyed gambling, trips to California to visit family and loved fishing. She also enjoyed walking every day and visiting with people, she was definitely a social butterfly. She would say that she had the gift of gab to anyone.

She is survived by two sons, Russell (Diane) of Marathon, WI and David (Leela) of Weston, WI; daughters-in-law, Staci of Prescott, AZ and Cheri Kell Wausau, WI; grandchildren, Brian Kell (Rachelle), Jason Kell and Vivian Kell; great-grandchildren, Ty Kell and Trevor Kell; and her siblings, Judy (Dan) Wadzinski, Tom Krueger and Richard (Kim) Krueger.

She was preceded in death by her parents, George and Evelyn Krueger; husband, Derald Kell; two sons, Steven Kell and Kevin Kell; and her siblings, Carol Rhyner, Leila Howland, Janice Normand, Diane Goetz and Ronald Krueger.

Funeral services will be held at 3:00 p.m. on Saturday, February 21, 2026 at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau. Rev. Rebecca Borke will officiate. Visitation will be on Saturday from 1:00 p.m. until time of services at the funeral home. Private burial will be held at Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau at a later date.

records.name: Dorothy R. Wickkiser
records.funeral_home: Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home of Birnamwood
records.summary: Dorothy R. Wickkiser, age 93, of Kempster, passed away peacefully on February 13, 2026.
records.body:
Dorothy R. Wickkiser, 93, of Kempster, passed away peacefully in her sleep on Friday, February 13, 2026, at Autumn Lane in Birnamwood.

Dorothy was born on November 27, 1932, in Mattoon to Henry and Marion (Clauss) Wickman. She graduated from Mattoon High School and later moved to Milwaukee, where she worked at AC Spark Plug.

On February 18, 1956, she was united in marriage to Kenneth Casson Sr. While Kenneth served in the United States Air Force, the couple was stationed at several bases in Europe. They returned to Racine in 1958 and lived and worked on King Ehrlich's horse ranch. In 1959, they purchased a dairy farm on Red River Road near Mattoon. In addition to helping manage the farm, Dorothy worked at the lumber mill in Birnamwood, the Mattoon Veneer Mill, and on Alan Andrus's farm. Kenneth preceded her in death on October 22, 1968.

On January 1, 1971, Dorothy married Raymond Wickkiser Sr. Together they later enjoyed retirement in Kempster on beautiful Kimball Lake. Raymond preceded her in death on February 12, 2009.

Dorothy found great joy in her family, gardening, canning, and breeding AKC German Shepherds. She also cherished her beloved kitty, Nala. She was a faithful member of St. Matthew's Church in Deerbrook.

Dorothy is lovingly survived by her daughters, Deb (Tom) Kessen of Birnamwood and Mary (Dennis) Dewey of Bryant; her sons, Kenneth Casson Jr. of Bowler and Raymond Wickkiser Jr. of Kempster; her son-in-law, Daniel Fandrey; and two stepdaughters, Linda and Nancy and their families.

She is further survived by her grandchildren: Rabecka (Mike) Saal, Melissa Fandrey, Amy (Tyler) Casson, Jenni (Kyle) Kessen, Jazmyn (Steve) Wickkiser, Natalie and Shantel Dewey, Donna (Bob) Casson, Laura (Todd) Wildenberg, Jamie Casson, Jason Casson, Shawn Casson, Makayla (Ryan) Waldvogel, Olivia (Adam) Wickkiser, and Alex (Jade) Wickkiser; great-grandchildren, Dakota Witz, Patrick (Amber) Witz, Henry Frahm, Baby Cruz (Jazmyn and Steve), Case, Cash and Cadence Wildenberg, Dominick Silva, Emery and Jovie Casson, and Lilyanna, Callum, and Finley Casson; great-great-grandchildren, Mason Witz-Staubs, Hayden Witz and Baby Witz; along with many other relatives and friends.

Dorothy was preceded in death by her parents, Henry and Marion Wickman; her brother, Robert; two sisters in infancy, Iris and Rose; her husbands, Kenneth Casson Sr. and Raymond Wickkiser Sr.; her daughter, Rosemarie Fandrey; her son, Joseph Wickkiser; her stepson, John Wickkiser; her great-grandchildren, Richard and Genevieve Witz; and her great-great-grandchild, Renezzmay Rose Willums Witz.

A Funeral Service will be held at 11:30 a.m. on Wednesday, February 18, 2026, at Chet & Emil's Hall in Birnamwood, with a luncheon to follow. Rev. Clifford Kessen will officiate. Visitation will be held on Wednesday from 10:00 a.m. until the time of service at the hall. Burial will take place in the spring at Woodlawn Cemetery in Mattoon.

Dorothy's family would like to thank the staff at Autumn Lane and ADRC Home Health Staff for the wonderful care they provided.

In honor of Dorothy's love of gardening and perennials, the family kindly asks that memorial tributes include sending or bringing perennials.

records.name: James J. Wojciechowski
records.summary: James "JJ" J. Wojciechowski, age 90, of Schofield, Wisconsin, passed away on February 11, 2026.
records.body:
JJ was born in Schofield, WI to John and Helen Wojciechowski. JJ married Lola Wojciechowski of Weston, Wisconsin on September 26, 1959 in St. Therese.

JJ was a truck driver for 30 years with ForeWay Express. He served honorably in the National Guard for 10 years. JJ loved bluegill fishing and spending time with his grandchildren.

JJ is survived by his loving wife Lola; daughters, Jane Wirezba, Faye (Jeff) Grosskreutz, Kay (Matt) Wojciechowski; grandchildren Joan (Brandon) Cummings, Joe Wirezba; brother, Llyod (Gerry) Wojciechowski and other family and friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents, John and Helen Wojciechowski, Parents-in-law, Joe and Viola Stoltz and son-in-law Jim Wirezba.

Private graveside services will be held.

records.name: Jean Wiedeman
records.summary: Gloria "Jean" Wiedeman (nee Niedzwiecki), age 85, of Wausau, passed away on January 23, 2026.
records.body:
Jean Wiedeman (nee Niedzwiecki), aged 85 of Wausau, passed away on Friday, January 23rd, 2026 at her home surrounded by her children.

She was born on Friday, December 13th, 1940 in Munising, Michigan; to the late John and Alma (nee Erickson) Niedzwiecki.

On July 25th,1959, Jean married the love of her life, Marshall. They were blessed to have had nine children. Over the years, Jean and Marshall operated numerous businesses together, including: a deli, a discount store in Merrill and a successful painting company. She worked at Hurd Millwork for many years until her retirement in 2007. During her retirement, she and her husband helped countless numbers of veterans and homeless individuals, in addition to helping many more charities and good causes. She loved searching for treasures at thrift stores and rummage sales and was always raring to go shopping. She also enjoyed reading and working puzzle books.

Jean lived a full life with the love of her family and friends.

Jean is survived by her children: Wayne Wiedeman, Cami Kramer, Marshall J. Wiedeman (Barb), Dennie Wiedeman, Christine Opichka (Dennis), Kitty Wiedeman (Nathan) & Alan Wiedeman; grandchildren: Joshua Kramer, Mark Nemke, Erik Wiedeman, Keith Wiedeman, Jeremy Kramer, Megan Thiel, Carmen Ebert, Devin Opichka, Samantha Thiel, Jasper Wiedeman & Wendy Howell; great-granchildren: Davon Kramer, Madelynn Opichka, Olive Wiedeman, Owen Wiedeman & Brooke Ebert; Many, many in laws, cousins, nephews and nieces. Her favorite kid, Maestro (Cat).

Jean was preceded in death by: her husband, Marshall T.; her parents, John & Alma Niedzwiecki; her sister, Mary Ann; infant daughter, Wendy; daughter, Merry Star; granddaughter, Sheri Kramer; son in law, Timothy Kramer; daughter in law, Renee & granddaughter inlaw, Norma.

The family would like to thank Aspirus Hospice for all of their help, patience and loving care during her final days.

A celebration of life is planned for a later date.

records.name: Kathleen Stuchlak
records.summary: Kathleen (Klein) Stuchlak, age 63, passed away peacefully on February 8, 2026.
records.body:
Kathleen (Klein) Stuchlak was a beloved wife, mother, sister, nana, and friend. Kathy passed away peacefully on February 8th, 2026, at the age of 63. Kathy was born February 13th, 1962, and raised in Chicago, Illinois.

Kathy was well known for her love and support of her sons Sam and Noah, her boisterous laugh, never-ending sass, and the love she had for her grandchildren. Kathy was like another mother to her sons' friends and gave them the same love, support, and kindness she showed to her own family. She was a woman who loved to live vicariously through other people's adventures and encouraged everyone she loved to live their lives in a way that made them the happiest.

Kathy is preceded in death by Alan and Elaine Kaminski; She is survived by her husband, Joseph; their two children, Sam (Elana), Noah (Jordan); as well as her sister Carol (Robert), her nephews, Jason and Matt, and her great nephews Kendrix and Javion. She was the proud Nana of two grandchildren, Hadleigh and Ethan. She was loved by more people than we can ever name.

Join us for a celebration of life at Moundview Golf Course on Friday, Feb 27th, 2026. The celebration will start at 1:00. We will be serving light finger foods and soft drinks. Moundview is located at 1930 14th Ct, Friendship, WI 53934.

records.name: Neil Roger O'Brien
records.funeral_home: Brainard Funeral Home and Cremation Service
records.summary: Neil Roger O'Brien, age 83, of Kronenwetter, Wisconsin, died on February 9, 2026.
records.body:
Neil Roger O'Brien, age 83, of Kronenwetter, Wisconsin, died, Monday, February 9, 2026, at Aspirus Hospital in Wausau.

He was born on May 30, 1942, in Bessemer, Michigan, a son of Raymond and Lillian (Gartman) O'Brien. On January 15, 1965, he married Colleen Ann Pierce in Wausau. Neil was a Night Maintenance Supervisor for the Wausau YMCA. He had a true love for the great outdoors and the Northwoods; hunting, fishing and spending time at his home away from home cottage in Boulder Junction were some of his happiest times. He was once a winner for the Musky Classic. Known for his notorious storytelling, being a "professional fishing guide" and top notch BS'ing, he will be missed by all who knew and loved him.

He is survived by his wife, Colleen; daughters, Maureen Mueller (Todd Stelter) and Shawn (Robert) Koehler; four grandchildren, Trevor, Brennan, Natalie, Christian and his sister, Janet (Chuck) Wirt. He is preceded in death by his parents, and brother, Donald O'Brien.

A gathering of family and friends will be held on Saturday, March 14, 2026, from 11:30 AM – 12:30 PM at the Wausau Elks Lodge, 414 Scott Street, Wausau, WI. Military Honors will be held at 12:30 PM, and a luncheon to follow.

records.name: Phyllis M. Henneman
records.summary: Phyllis Marie Henneman, age 95, of Hamburg, died on February 13, 2026.
records.body:
Phyllis Marie Henneman, age 95, died Friday, February 13, 2026 at Stone Crest, Wausau. She was born on August 2, 1930 in Wausau, daughter of the late Paul and Katherine (Neuman) Tessmer. On June 30, 1951 she married Roger Henneman. He preceded her in death in 1987. Phyllis was a lifelong Hamburg resident.

Survivors include two sons, Gary (Gloria) Henneman and Wayne (Suzy) Henneman; five grandchildren, Scott (Blair) Henneman, Laura (Brandt) Hunter, Zach Barker, Ryan Henneman and Nick Henneman and two great grandchildren, Olivia and Liam Hunter.
