She was preceded in death by her parents, husband and two brothers, Harold Tessmer and Dale Tessmer.

A Memorial Service will be held at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 312 Caroline Street, Athens, on Friday, February 27, 2026 at 3:00 pm. Burial will be in the Athens Village cemetery. Visitation will be from 1:00 pm Friday until the time of the service at the funeral home in Athens.

records.name: Timothy A. Baumann
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Timothy A. Baumann, age 56, of Wausau, passed away on February 12, 2026.
records.body:
Timothy A. Baumann, age 56, passed away on February 12, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center, Weston, Wisconsin.

He was born on August 15, 1969. Tim spent his life in Wausau, the son of Elgart and Eunice (Wilde) Baumann. Tim married the love of his life, Beverly Kriehn, on November 14, 1998, and the couple enjoyed 26 years together until her passing in December 2024.

Tim was a proud graduate of Wausau West High School after beginning his education at Trinity Lutheran School. He possessed a warped sense of humor and a steel trap memory. Tim was a lover of history and genealogy. He also enjoyed sharing his made from scratch baked goods with family and friends alike.

A passionate enthusiast of polka music, Tim was a member of the Central Wisconsin Polka Club and a member and former Vice President of the Wisconsin Polka Hall of Fame. His love for music extended beyond just listening; he actively participated in singing in the choir at Trinity Lutheran Church, where he also served on the Altar Guild. Tim was a life member of the Elks Lodge 248 and member of Wausau Eagles Club Aerie 251.

Tim is survived by his mother, Eunice Baumann; sister, Brenda Bolin; sister, Angela Burton; and nephew, Andrew Bolin.

He was preceded in death by his father; his wife, Beverly Baumann; his grandparents, Erwin & Viola (Ringle) Wilde and Elgart & Luella (Sturm) Baumann; and brother-in-law Wayne Burton, and his aunts and uncles.

A funeral service for Tim will be held on Thursday, February 19, 2026, at 11:00 AM at Trinity Lutheran Church, 501 Stewart Avenue, Wausau, WI 54401, with Pastor Jim Mayland presiding. Visitation will be held from 9:00 AM until the time of service. Burial will be at Restlawn Memorial Park.

In lieu of flowers, donations can be made in Tim's name to the Elks Good Fellowship or the Eagle's Community Easter Dinner.

records.name: Angel Gabriel Vargas Hernandez
records.summary: Angel Gabriel Vargas Hernandez, age 20, of Wausau, passed away on February 11, 2026.
records.body:
Angel Gabriel Vargas Hernandez, 20, passed away on February 11, 2026, as the result of a car accident. Though his time on earth was far too short, Angel filled his years with purpose, passion, and deep love for those around him.

Angel was the beloved son of Arnulfo and Carmen (Hernandez) Vargas. He was a humble, generous, and genuinely kind young man whose presence brought comfort and joy to family and friends alike. Angel had a special appreciation for the finer details in life—especially his well-loved collection of cologne. He never left the house without a spritz, always wanting to look sharp and smell his best. It was just one of the many ways he expressed pride in himself and respect for others.

He found peace and happiness outdoors, especially when hunting, and he valued the quiet strength that nature offered. Angel was also an exceptionally talented barber at Modern Gents in Wausau. With his modern spin on a classic cut, he built not only an impressive client base but a loyal following. His chair was more than a place for a haircut—it was a place of connection, laughter, and friendship. His clients quickly became friends, and his work reflected both his skill and his heart.

Above all, Angel cherished his family and remained grounded in his faith. His love for those closest to him was unwavering, and he carried that love into every part of his life.

He is lovingly survived by his parents, Arnulfo and Carmen; his brother, Mauricio; his sister, Neidy; his girlfriend, Arely; his maternal grandmother, Rosa Maria Rojas; his paternal grandmother, Maria Concepcion Juarez; and many aunts, uncles, cousins, clients, and dear friends who will forever hold his memory close to their hearts.

He was preceded in death by his brother, Oscar, and by both of his grandfathers.

A Funeral Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Monday, February 23, 2026, at St. Matthew Catholic Church, 229 S 28th Ave, Wausau. The Very Reverend Sabastian Kolodziejczyk will officiate.

A Rosary service will be held at 2:00 pm on Sunday, February 22, 2026, at John J Buettgen Funeral Home-Mosinee Chapel, 900 Old Hwy 51, Mosinee, WI.

Visitation will take place from 2:00 to 4:00 p.m. on Sunday, February 22, 2026, at John J. Buettgen Funeral Home, 900 Old Hwy 51, Mosinee, WI, and again on Monday, February 23, 2026, from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service at the church.

Angel's life reminds us to love deeply, work passionately, and carry ourselves with quiet confidence. His memory will live on in every fresh cut, every walk in the woods, and every lingering scent of cologne that brings him to mind.

records.name: Brenda S. Plautz
records.summary: Brenda S. Plautz, age 66, of Wausau, passed away on February 14, 2026.
records.body:
A Memorial Service will be at 11:00 a.m., Friday, February 27, 2026 at Zion Lutheran Church in Wausau for Brenda S. Plautz, age 66, who passed away on Saturday, February 14, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center in Weston. Pastor Steven Gjerde will officiate. Visitation will be from 9:00 a.m.-11:00 a.m. on Friday at the church. Arrangements are entrusted to Helke Funeral Home in Wausau.

Brend was born on June 4, 1959, in Wausau to Thomas and Shirley (Wandtke) Callahan. On August 9, 1986, she was united in marriage to Jeffery "Jeff" Plautz at Zion Lutheran Church in Wausau. Together, they built a life rooted in love and faith, spending their years in the community they both cherished.

Brend worked at Wausau Hospital before dedicating herself to supporting her family's business, Woodcraft Renew-A-Kitchen. She was a loving and devoted wife, mother, daughter, and grandmother whose strong Christian faith guided her life and was instilled deeply in her children.

She found great joy in the outdoors and in time spent with family. Brenda loved camping trips, relaxing at the family cottage in Minocqua on Booth Lake, hiking, kayaking, pontooning, snowshoeing, and skiing during the winter months. Above all, family meant everything to her. She wholeheartedly supported and loved her children in countless ways. Brenda's wonderful sense of humor and warm personality made her the life of the party, and she will be deeply missed by all who knew her.

Brend is survived by her beloved husband, Jeff Plautz of Wausau; her mother, Shirley Callahan of Weston; her children, Sara (Paul) Wajgel of Oak Creek, Ethan (Whyatt) Bristol of Wausau, and Andy (Bri) Plautz of Dallas, Texas; her siblings, Bruce Callahan of Wausau, Brian (Judy) Callahan of Wausau, Beth Callahan of Oshkosh, and Brad Callahan of Iron River, Michigan; her granddaughter, Ariyah Rae Plautz-Bristol; many cherished nieces and nephews; and her loving dog, Riley.

She was preceded in death by her father; her nephew, Kyle Callahan; and her beloved dog, Reggie.

The family extends a heartfelt thank you to the compassionate staff of the Oncology Unit at Marshfield Medical Center in Weston for their exceptional care and support.

records.name: Carol R. Wangen
records.summary: Carol R. Wangen, age 95, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on February 7, 2026.
records.body:
Carol Rae Wangen, 95, passed away peacefully on February 7, 2026, at Silver Lace Memory Care by Primrose in Weston, WI.

Carol was born on August 9, 1930, in Menomonie, WI, the daughter of the late Ione Wood Kaunzner and Lloyd "Kandy" Kaunzner.

Carol married her high school sweetheart, Joseph "Don" Wangen on August 15, 1952. They celebrated 70 years of marriage before his passing in August of 2022. Together they welcomed four children, Candia, Nancy, Bill, and Ann.

Carol was a tomboy in her younger years. She enjoyed many sports including playing football with her future husband. She spent many days swimming by the Eau Claire dam. She was often bird hunting with her dad and her dog Chief. She also helped her parents care for her sister Nancy.

Carol and Don lived in many cities throughout their years with one of their favorites being Ashland, WI by Lake Superior. Carol and Don were in a dancing club and enjoyed the many holiday parties with friends. Carol was also on the ski patrol at Mt. Ashwabay, Indianhead Ski Resort, and Whitecap Ski Resort. She made many memories of skiing with her family and friends throughout the years. Carol, along with Don and their children, would spend time in their cabin, rafting down the White River. Carol and the family often picnic at Long Lake and spent many days swimming in Lake Superior.

Carol received her teaching degree after raising her four children. She taught at multiple schools throughout her career with the local schools being Weston Elementary and her favorite school, Rothschild Elementary. She had a passion for teaching young children, especially reading. She was the fun teacher who when the kids needed a break would bring out her foam ball and let the kids play dodgeball in the classroom. Carol enjoyed spending many days decorating her room and planning special activities for the children. She had the special privilege of teaching two of her grandchildren as well. Carol had many teaching friends whom she adored their visits and reminiscing with.

After retiring from teaching, she enjoyed many trips with Don and the grandchildren. One of her favorites was the white sandy beaches of Destin, Florida. She visited often her daughter Kandy and grandson's Jon and Chris in Colorado and Pennsylvania as well as daughter Nancy and granddaughter Kendra in Texas. She attended many sporting events for grandson Bill and granddaughter Kali as well as taking them to visit family out of state. Carol loved dogs especially her West Highland Terriers, Ellie, Lily and Wesley. Carol was blessed with two great-grandchildren, Tucker and Ila who meant the world to her.

Survivors include her daughters; Kandy, Minneapolis; Nancy, Houston; Ann (Tom), South Carolina. Carol had 5 grandchildren; Bill, Kali (Randy), Jon (Brenna), and Chris (Sammy). Two great grandchildren; great-grandson; Tucker and great-granddaughter; Ila.

Carol is preceded in death by husband Don, son; Bill, son-in-law; Don, granddaughter; Kendra, and sister; Nancy.

The family would like to give a special thank you to all the wonderful staff at Silver Lace Memory Care for the compassionate care they gave Carol in her final days as well as Brittany, R.N., at Interim Hospice Care.

No services will take place, as Carol requested, but we do appreciate your love and support.

In lieu of flowers, donations may be made to Marathon County Human Society.

records.name: Charmaine J. Burnett
records.summary: Charmaine J. Burnett, age 95, passed away peacefully on February 17, 2026.
records.body:
Charmaine Joan (Ohrmundt) Burnett, age 95, passed away peacefully on February 17, 2026, leaving behind a lifetime of love, laughter, and a legacy rooted firmly in the great outdoors.

She was at her happiest with her hands in the dirt, tending to her garden. A week was not complete without meeting cherished friends to play bridge, going out for lunch, or attending book club. Her faith and belief in Jesus were an important part of her life. She was proud of all her various volunteer efforts throughout her life. She and her husband opened their hearts and home, taking in foster children, giving them love and a safe home.

Dogs held a special place in her heart, and she never met one she didn't like (or one that didn't quickly become her friend).

Her favorite place in the world was the family cabin up north on the lake. There, she found joy in fresh air, quiet mornings sipping a cup of coffee, playing games with her children and granddaughters, always with a dog at her side.

Known for her sharp wit and sassy spirit, Charmaine spoke her mind, kept people on their toes, and made life more interesting just by being herself. She was strong, independent, and unapologetically so – right to the end.

She is deeply loved and will be dearly missed by her family, friends, and anyone lucky enough to have crossed her path. Her memory lives on in blooming gardens, loyal dogs, northern lakes, and every moment we choose to enjoy life boldly and honestly – just like she did.

Charmaine is survived by her daughters, Lynn (Edward) Klein and Jane Burnett; son, Jon Burnett; granddaughters, Abby, Emily, Mallory, Jourdan, Liz, and Madison; as well as six great-grandchildren. She is preceded in death by her husband, Eugene Burnett; parents, Hugo and Clara (Trebus) Ohrmundt; and an infant sister.

Private Interment will be held in Pine Grove Cemetery.

records.name: Clemen Shelbrack Jr.
records.summary: Clemen Shelbrack Jr., age 88, of Wausau, passed away on February 16, 2026.
records.body:
Clemen Shelbrack Jr.,88, passed away Monday, February 16, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center, Marshfield.

He was born June 26, 1937, in Menasha, son of the late Clemen and Clara (Ostrowski) Shelbrack. On October 24, 1959, he married Gloria Radtke at St. Michael Catholic Church, Wausau.

Clemen worked as a carpenter, dedicating his skill and craftsmanship to building and creating with his hands. He served his country in the United States Army. Clemen loved tinkering with cars and nothing made him happier than cruising down the road on his Harley-Davidson.

He enjoyed traveling, exploring new places, and making memories along the way. He also liked trips to the casino, where he could relax, have a little fun, and enjoy the excitement of the games.

Above all, Clemen simply loved living life to the fullest, working hard, riding free, and savoring every moment.

Survivors include his wife, Gloria Shelbrack; children, Laurie (Cyril) Kloos, James (Deann) Shelbrack, Mark Shelbrack and Lynda (Jim) Kroening; grandchildren, Alicia Cervenka, Janessa (Steven) Cronin, Jayda Shelbrack, Alexa Shelbrack; step-grandchildren, Jeremy (Laurie) Kloos, Bryan Kloos and Ashley Morga; great-grandchildren, Emilee and Abigail Cronin; siblings, Clarence Shelbrack, Richard Shelbrack, Jayne Steiner and Bonnie (Jim) Crochiere; close friend, Fritz Blaskowski; numerous nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends.

Besides his parents, he was preceded in death by a son, Scott Shelbrack, sister, Carol Toftum and brother, Jack Shelbrack.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated 10:30 a.m. Friday, February 20, 2026, at St. Michael Catholic Church, Wausau. Entombment will be in Garden of Eternal Rest, Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau. Visitation will be 9:00 a.m. until the time of service all at St. Michael Catholic Church.

records.name: Donovan D. Helke
records.summary: Donovan D. Helke of Mosinee passed away after a courageous battle with cancer.
records.body:
Donovan D. Helke, of Mosinee, passed away peacefully after a courageous battle with cancer, surrounded by his family, with his devoted wife, Lynn, holding his hand. He went home to be with his Lord and Savior, leaving behind a legacy of devotion, generosity, and laughter that will never be forgotten.

Donovan was born to the late Wallace and Arlene (Kottke) Helke and was the middle child of seven children, growing up alongside five brothers and one sister in a close-knit family that remained the foundation of his life. From a young age, Donovan's determination and spirit were evident. In his youth, he was a two-time Wausau Record Herald City Marbles Champion, proudly earning a Schwinn 10-speed bicycle for each of his championship victories, an accomplishment he fondly remembered.

He graduated from the University of Wisconsin–Madison in 1978 with a degree in Physical Education and a Handicap Certificate, beginning a career marked by hard work, adaptability, and entrepreneurial spirit. Donovan held a variety of roles throughout his life, including substitute teacher, Graebel Movers employee, UPS worker, and later earning his Life and Health Insurance License while working for Prudential Securities. He ultimately found his calling when he opened his own agency, Compass Insurance and Compass Solutions, where he focused on financial planning. Donovan took great pride in helping others prepare for their futures. Even after his retirement on December 31, 2021, his passion never faded, and he continued working and consulting alongside his handpicked successor, Cody Hanke, until his passing.

On July 22, 1978, Donovan was united in marriage to the love of his life, Lynn Burns. Together they built a beautiful life rooted in love, faith, and shared memories. Donovan especially cherished the time they spent together on the golf course, where they created countless moments of joy side by side.

Donovan loved being outdoors, whether cutting wood, walking in the woods hunting, or spending time at his beloved cabin, where he found peace and happiness in nature. He especially looked forward to his cherished annual deer hunting camp, where evenings were filled with sheepshead games, laughter, and a few cocktails among family and friends. In his younger years, he was an excellent athlete who loved racquetball and basketball, always eager to sprint down the court for a fast-break layup during Friday night games and City League play. After enduring several knee surgeries, his passion shifted to golf. He became an avid golfer and proudly formed his own Thursday night league, the Big-Dog Golf League, where his friends and family gathered to play, compete, and share in the camaraderie he loved so much.

He was an avid sports fan who faithfully followed the Milwaukee Brewers, Milwaukee Bucks, and most especially the Green Bay Packers. Watching the games brought him joy, and he celebrated and endured each season with loyal dedication.

Donovan was a very generous man, not only financially, but also with the most precious gift, his time. He cared deeply for his parents, taking them to countless medical appointments in their later years, and was always there for any family member who needed help, guidance, or support. He loved his nieces and nephews dearly, proudly supporting their many activities and fundraisers and encouraging them to save, invest, and plan for their futures. He took great pride in their accomplishments. He also had a wonderful sense of humor. Nearly every conversation with Donovan, whether in person or on the phone, ended in laughter. His warmth and wit made others feel at ease and brought joy to those around him. Donovan was meticulous in all aspects of his life. He was a proud record keeper, maintaining countless binders documenting everything from his clients' financial plans to the history of family golf tournaments. His organization reflected the care and dedication he brought to everything he did.

He will be deeply missed by his loving wife, Lynn; his siblings, Dennis (Kristin) Helke of Mosinee, Gary (Pamela) Helke of Wausau, Wesley Helke of Wausau, Dwight (Lori) Helke of Wausau, and his favorite sister, Gayle (Jerry) Woller of Wausau; his mother-in-law, Joanne Burns; his brothers-in-law, Robert Burns Jr. and James Burns; and many beloved nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Wallace and Arlene Helke; his beloved brother, Wade Helke; his father-in-law, Robert Burns Sr.; his sister-in-law, Barb Szczetaniak.

The family extends their heartfelt gratitude to Dr. Bart Isaacson, Dr. Adedayo Onitilo, research nurse Barbie Gildenzopf, RN, and the compassionate staff in the Marshfield Emergency Department and ICU for their exceptional care, kindness, and support.

Donovan will be remembered for his generous heart, his sharp mind, his unwavering dedication to his family, and his ability to bring laughter wherever he went. The lives he touched, the wisdom he shared, and the love he gave will remain in the hearts of those who knew him forever.

A memorial service will be held on Monday, March 2, 2026, at 12:00 PM at Mount Olive Lutheran Church, 6205 Alderson St., Weston, with a social hour to follow. There will be no visitation. Pastor Lance Hoelscher will officiate.

records.name: Joanne M. Baehman
records.summary: Joanne M. Baehman, age 91, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on February 15, 2026.
records.body:
Joanne Margaret Baehman, 91, a resident of Wausau for 68 years, died peacefully on Sunday, February 15, 2026, at Golden Years of Lake Geneva. Joanne was born October 1, 1934, in Augusta, WI, to Ben and Josephine Johnson.

After high school and Eau Claire Technical School, she moved to Milwaukee where she met and married Gerald (Jerry) Baehman on February 15, 1958. Upon moving to Wausau that year, the couple opened Baehman's Beauty Salon and State College of Beauty, and Joanne met strangers in the Newcomers Club who became lifelong friends. With a passion for community service, her advice to anyone feeling down was to 'get out and do something for someone and soon you won't have any troubles.' A long-time volunteer at The Woodson Art Museum, she carried a folder of notes to share local recommendations with visitors, promoting all that Wausau had to offer. She was active in Antique Study Club, Good Earth Garden Club, Friends of the Library, Genealogical Society, Good Shepherd Lutheran Church, and many other organizations. Joanne loved homemaking, reading, journaling, history, gardening, thrifting, socializing, basket weaving, and spending time with her three grandchildren. The common thread of her interests was the intentionality she brought to every endeavor. She believed in the art of letter writing, scripting notes every Monday morning at her dining table to friends and family (Joanne famously refused to put outgoing mail in her mailbox, instead driving it to the post office until age 90, so as not to burden the mail carrier – "they already have so much to do!").

Joanne is survived by two sons, Jack (Stacy) and John (Jeannie); grandchildren, Kent (Amber), Kate, and Claire (Chris) Devore; two great-grandchildren, and beloved nieces, nephews, and friends. She was preceded in death by husband, Jerry; seven brothers and sisters; and daughter-in-law, Ciri.

The family thanks Dr. Krista Scharer, Primrose Retirement Community, Golden Years of Lake Geneva, and Badger Hospice for their loving care.

Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Wausau, is handling arrangements for a private memorial service.

records.name: Libby L. Mosher
records.summary: Libby L. Mosher, age 88, passed away peacefully at home on February 10, 2026.
records.body:
Libby Mosher, age 88, passed away peacefully at home, on February 10, 2026.

Libby was born in Antigo, WI to Joseph and Anna (Blatecky) Prochaska on October 19, 1937. Libby married Eugene Mosher of Antigo, Wi on December 1, 1956.

Prior to her retirement, Libby enjoyed owning and managing Mosher Synder Drug store alongside Gene. Much of her and Gene's free time was spent at their lake home on Post Lake tackling home improvement projects and tending to her beautiful gardens. In addition to enjoying quality time with her children and grandchilden, her favorite hobbies included craft projects such as sewing and knitting (which she taught to a few of her grandchilden!). Lastly, she loved playing bridge with her close friends in Wausau for over 50 years.

Libby is survived by her children Diane (William) Cralley, Mark (Susan) Mosher, Cheryl (Kevin) Loos, Jill (Steve) Kickhaver, and Scott Mosher; 13 grandchildren and 8 great children.

She was preceded in death by her loving husband Eugene Mosher; two sons, Eugene Mosher Jr. and Curtis (Amy) Mosher; and parents Joseph Prochaska and Anna Prochaska.

Services for Libby Mosher will be held at 12:00 P.M. on Monday, February 23, 2026, at Brainard Funeral Home, Wausau Chapel, 522 Adams Street, Wausau, WI 54403, with Joy Nelson-Jeffers officiating. Visitation will start at 10:00 A.M. until the time of service. A private burial will follow services.

records.name: Pamela J. Matt
records.summary: Pamela J. Matt, born September 5, 1964, passed away leaving behind a love of music and quiet strength.
records.body:
Pamela Jean Matt, born September 5, 1964, in Illinois, passed away leaving behind a love of music, and quiet strength. She was the beloved daughter of the late Duane and Cletus (Pearson) Matt.

Pamela dedicated much of her working life to housekeeping in the service industry. She took pride in her work.

She found joy in life's simple and meaningful pleasures. Pamela loved to sing and play guitar, filling her home with music. She was an avid reader who could often be found immersed in a good Stephen King novel—especially enjoying the thrill of seeing the story come alive later on the big screen. A true coffee connoisseur, she appreciated a perfectly brewed cup and the comfort it brought during quiet moments of reflection.

She is survived by her daughter, Brandy Boda; and son, Joey; her sister, Beth; her brother, Phillip; and her three adored grandchildren, Lilith, Zyin, and Aiko.

Pamela's memory will live on in the songs she sang, and the stories she shared.

records.name: Theresa Graap
records.summary: Theresa Graap, age 75, passed away peacefully on January 30, 2026.
records.body:
Theresa Graap, age 75, passed away peacefully on January 30, 2026. She was born on July 24, 1950, the daughter of the late Gus and Anna (Wanka) Zastrow.

Theresa married the love of her life, Gerald Graap, on April 24, 1981. Gerald preceded her in death on August 22, 1992. Together, they shared a devoted marriage and were blessed with one daughter, Linda, who was the center of Theresa's world.

Theresa was a factory worker at Northern Door, where she worked for many years as a molding operator. In retirement, she truly embraced life. She loved watching television, was extremely social, and thrived on being around people-always the life of the party. Theresa was a passionate Green Bay Packers fan and especially enjoyed talking sports with her son-in-law, Jeremy, loved Daniel O'Donnel and Polka's.

Known for her caring heart, Theresa deeply loved her people and was always checking in to make sure everyone was okay. She was a wonderful cook and baker, generously sharing her talents with family and friends. She also cherished her special friendship with the "Merrill-Go-Round" bus drivers, who affectionately called her "trouble," often announcing they were "picking up trouble" when they arrived-something that never failed to bring a smile.

Theresa will be lovingly remembered by her daughter, Linda (Jeremy) Nass; her brother, Jerry (Sue) Zastrow; her beloved grand-puppy, Izzy Bella; and her many dear friends at Jenny Towers.

She was preceded in death by her husband, Gerald Graap, and her sister, MaryAnn Zastrow.

Theresa's warmth, humor, and vibrant spirit will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved her.

A celebration of Theresa's life will be held at a later date.

records.name: Tobias Hanford
records.summary: Tobias "Toby" Hanford, age 47, passed away peacefully on February 13, 2026.
records.body:
Tobias "Toby" Hanford, 47, passed away peacefully at Wausau Aspirus Hospital on Friday, February 13. He was born March 1, 1978 in Rensselaer, Indiana to Daniel and Caroline Hanford.

He graduated from Calumet High School in Calumet, Michigan. He moved to the Wausau area shortly after graduating high school. In his younger years he loved to play basketball and golf. He was an avid culinary enthusiast, sports fan and loved the Indiana Hoosiers, San Antonio Spurs, Miami Dolphins, and Chicago White Sox.

He was preceded in death by his father, Daniel, his grandparents, and numerous aunts and uncles. He is survived by his mother Caroline Hanford, brother Cody "C.J." (Mary) Hanford, various aunts and uncles along with numerous close friends.

According to his wishes, there will be no memorial service. In lieu of flowers, the family wishes you donate to the Marathon County Humane Society.

records.name: Violet M. Hackbart
records.summary: Violet M. Hackbart, age 100, of Wausau, passed away on February 13, 2026.
records.body:
Violet Mae (Radke) Hackbart, age 100, passed away on February 13, 2026, at Aspirus Hospice House, just a month after celebrating her 100th birthday. Born on January 14, 1926, she was the fifth of eight children born to Paul and Bertha (Goetsch) Radke.

Violet was a strong, determined woman who always made the best of any situation. In order to attend high school, Violet and her sister, Grace, moved into an apartment in Wausau as it was too far to commute from their family farm. After graduating, during World War II, Violet traveled with two friends to Washington, D.C., where she worked at the FBI, reading fingerprint cards.

On September 13, 1947, Violet was united in marriage to Norton Hackbart at Trinity Lutheran Church, Town of Stettin. Together, they moved to Milwaukee, where they raised three children: Bonnie, Lois, and Roger. As her children entered high school, Violet pursued beauty school and worked as a beautician for the rest of her life, continuing to cut family members' hair until she was 99 years old. When the family moved back to Wausau, Violet opened and operated two beauty shops. She, along with her daughter Bonnie, Lois, and daughter-in-law Faye, also co-founded the House of Fashion clothing store, which they successfully ran for 16 years.

Violet and Norton enjoyed traveling, visiting Germany several times, as well as other countries. They were active members of the Polka Club, German Club, Pommerscher Verein, and a card club. Violet also loved trips to the casino. She cherished spending time with her grandchildren at their cottage in Tomahawk and enjoyed taking them to rummage sales. The arrival of her great-grandchildren brought even more joy to her life.

Violet is lovingly survived by her children: Bonnie (Jim) Von Rueden of Wausau, Lois (Pete) Kufahl of Ringle, and Roger (Pam) Hackbart of Marathon. She is also survived by her grandchildren: Amy (Brad) Lund of Cheyenne, WY; Brian (Benjamin) Von Rueden of Dresden, Germany; Matthew (Janelle) Kufahl of Hayward, WI; Jessica (Adam) Janisch of Albert Lea, MN; Jeremy (Bridget) Hackbart of Milwaukee, WI; and Stacey (Richard) Borre of Rockton, IL. Additionally, she is survived by step-grandchildren Alyssa (Jay) Forss of South Range, MI; Quinn (Ivy) Forss of St. Paul, MN; and Grant (Becca) Forss of Poynette, WI. Her great-grandchildren include Claire, Carson, Angus, Myers, Scarlett, and Dax Lund; Elias Von Rueden; Kayleigh and Jenna Kufahl; Connor and Jack Janisch; Melanie and Abigail Borre; and Beatrice Violet Hackbart. She is also survived by many nieces, nephews, friends, and extended family.

Violet was preceded in death by her husband of 57 years, Norton; her infant son, Rodney; daughter-in-law, Faye; and all seven of her siblings.

Violet was determined to remain in her home and successfully did so until she was over 99 years old. The family would like to extend their heartfelt thanks to Pride TLC, Interim Home Hospice, and Aspirus Hospice House for the wonderful care she received in her final year.

Funeral services will be held at 1:00 PM on Monday, March 2, 2026, at Helke Funeral Home (302 Spruce St, Wausau). Pastor Karoline Schwantes will officiate. Friends and family are invited to greet the family at the funeral home for a time of visitation from 11:00 AM until the time of service.

Violet will be laid to rest next to Norton and Rodney at Restlawn Memorial Park.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations can be made to St Stephen Lutheran Church Wausau, Wisconsin in Violet's name.

records.name: Edward R. Tuckett
records.summary: Edward R. Tuckett, age 86, of Marinette, Wisconsin, passed away on February 14, 2026.
records.body:
Bob was born in Marinette WI on August 12, 1939, to the late Wilfred & Lillian (Mallett)Tuckett.

Bob was employed by Weyerhaeuser from 1969-2003 and retired as a warehouse lead. He enjoyed taking two trips a year to Arizona to visit his daughter. Bob loved his Green Bay Packers, gambling trips with friends & going out for weekly fish fry's with others. He always enjoyed making everyone laugh he came into contact with.

He is survived by his daughter Ann (Gerry) Meissner; stepdaughter Carrie McTavish; grandsons, Damien (Liz) Tuckett, and Simon (Katie) McTavish; along with several great grandchildren.

He was preceded in death by both his parents; his wife Priscilla; two brothers, Jim, and Harold (June) Tuckett; one sister Charmis Noel (Donald); and grandson Trenton Krueger.

A mass of Christian burial will be held at 11:00 am on Thursday, February 26, 2026, at St Theres Catholic Church, 113 Kort St, W., Rothschild, WI. Visitation will take place from 10:00 am until the time of the services at the church.

Family and friends may go to www.honorone.com to leave words of remembrance.

records.name: Marcia L. Spacher
records.summary: Marcia L. Spacher, age 79, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on February 9, 2026.
records.body:
Marcia was born on September 24, 1946, in Wausau, Wisconsin, the daughter of Aloise and Betty (Duggan) Sowinski. She married her best friend, Gregg Spacher, in 1967, and the couple shared 34 years of marriage marked by devotion and companionship until his passing in 2001.

Marcia was a devoted nurse, a role she took great pride in throughout her life. She worked as a nurse in Wisconsin, Kansas, Michigan, California, and Texas, caring for patients across many communities. Later in her career, she served in leadership and management roles in healthcare, including hospice care where she led and supported a team, and as the director of a nursing home in Texas. Caring for others was not just her profession but part of who she was.

She had a lifelong love of animals and found comfort in their companionship throughout her life, including her dogs, who remained by her side until her final days. Marcia was also a talented and creative crafter who enjoyed cross-stitching, crocheting, and knitting. Through her knitting, she made hats for cancer patients, a quiet and meaningful way she expressed care for others.

Marcia was active in her faith community at St. Agnes Catholic Church in Weston, where she participated in the choir and the prayer shawl group. She appreciated life's quieter pleasures and valued routine, simplicity, and dependability. She did not seek attention, but she had a steady presence and a thoughtfulness to those close to her came to rely on. She valued her relationships and stayed closely connected to family, friends, and her church community, including her dear friend Sally, with whom she shared a close friendship for more than 30 years, and Kathy, who was also a cherished part of her life. As a nurse, mother, and sister, she found meaning in caring for those closest to her.

Marcia is survived by her caring son, Dan (Timmy) Spacher; her sister, Sally Driscoll; and her brother, Paul (Ginny) Sowinski.

She was preceded in death by her parents, Aloise and Betty Sowinski; her husband, Gregg Spacher; and her sisters, Marlys and Linda.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held on Wednesday, February 25, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. at St. Agnes Catholic Church, 6101 Zinser Street, Weston, Wisconsin, with Fr. Patric celebrating. There will be no formal visitation; please feel free to arrive a few minutes early.

In lieu of flowers, donations can be made to the Saint Judes Children's Hospital in Marcia's honor.

records.name: Melvin R Matti
records.summary: Melvin Roger Matti, age 87, of the Town of Day, passed away on February 17, 2026.
records.body:
A celebration of Melvin's life will be held from 12:00 pm until 3:00 pm on Saturday, March 7, 2026, at the Town of Day Hall, 122518 County Road C. Stratford, WI 54484.

Family and friends may go to MWCS.WS to leave words of remembrance.

records.name: Arnold Drewek
records.summary: Arnold "Arnie" Drewek, 81, left his earthly home on February 17, 2026.
records.body:
Arnold "Arnie" Drewek left his earthly home to be with his heavenly Father on Tuesday, February 17, 2026. It was a long-awaited event that lasted 81 years.

Arnie loved God, his family and country. He diligently studied the bible to gain truth and wisdom. He served his country for four years in the U.S. Air Force.

Arnie was a long-time truck and bus driver. He especially liked talking to the young children. He also delivered packages for UPS.

The highlight of his bus driving career was successfully performing the Heimlich Maneuver on one very frightened student.

Visitation will be held from 10:00 until 11:00 a.m. on Wednesday, February 25, 2026 at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home in Marathon, with full military honors to follow conducted by the American Legion Post No. 469, Marathon.

The family wishes to thank Heartland Hospice and Meadows of Marathon for their compassionate care of Arnie.

records.name: Friedrich G. Kirsch
records.summary: Friedrich G. Kirsch, 87, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on February 18, 2026.
records.body:
Friedrich "Gigs" Gerhard Kirsch passed away peacefully February 18, 2026 in Wausau, Wisconsin. Gerhard was born February 13, 1939 to Karl and Babette (Bach) Kirsch in Finschafen, New Guinea.

During his childhood, Gerhard and his family moved from New Guinea to Brisbane, Australia, where he graduated from high school. Gerhard became an elementary school teacher, and while teaching in Lae, New Guinea, he met Virginia Marquardt whom he married 61 years ago. Not one to stand still long and wanting to experience more, he and Virginia moved around teaching in Iowa and Wisconsin, as well as schools overseas, including Karachi, Pakistan; São Paulo, Brazil; Zaire, Africa; and Lima, Peru. Upon retirement, Gerhard and Virginia settled in Wausau, close to their sons and family.

Gerhard loved teaching, especially third grade. He made sure that his students received the careful attention that they needed. He also was a Boy Scout leader and bravely led his troop out for winter camping! He coached baseball, basketball and soccer, where he cheered for his teams. Always a sports lover, Gerhard truly enjoyed watching the Packers, Bucks, and the Badgers.

Gerhard had a great zest for life. He loved being with his family and friends. And they knew that if they needed help, Gerhard would be the first one there. He was an avid gardener and was rarely found without a book in hand. Gerhard collected stamps and coins and hats and many other things. Some of Gerhard's favorite moments were spent at Black Oak Lake, where he appreciated all that nature provided, whether it was bird watching or even just making firewood.

While settling in Wausau, Gerhard became a grandfather, Opa to them. He cherished his time with his grandchildren and was very proud of each of them. Each held a special place in his heart. He loved to joke around and always took the opportunity to teach them something new.

Gerhard is survived by his wife, Virginia, his sons Charles (Deb) Kirsch and Fred (Joan) Kirsch, his grandchildren: Robyn Kirsch, Christopher Kirsch, Carson Kirsch and Allison Kirsch, his three sisters: Marianne Blason, Hedwig Schulz and Heidi Linsenmeier, as well as his sisters-in-law Heidi Kirsch, Katy (Dieter) Harle, Helen (Jim) Skindlov and brothers-in-law Steve Marquardt and Joel (Marion) Marquardt and many nieces and nephews.

Gerhard is preceded in death by his parents, his brother Karl Heinz Kirsch, his brothers-in-law Eric Linsenmeier, George Blason, Eric Schulz and John Marquardt, and his sister-in-law Lynn Marquardt.

A Memorial Service for Gerhard will be held Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 11:30 a.m. at St. John Lutheran Church, 5304 North 44th Avenue, Village of Maine. Visitation will begin at 10:00 a.m. at the church, continuing until the time of service. A luncheon will follow.

records.name: JoAnn Sowinski
records.summary: JoAnn Sowinski, 85, of Wausau, passed away on February 20, 2026.
records.body:
JoAnn Sowinski, age 85, of Wausau, passed away on Friday, February 20, 2026, at Aspirus Hospice House in Wausau.

JoAnn was born on July 2, 1940, in Wausau, Wisconsin, the daughter of Wesley Barttelt and Mary Ann Barttelt. Her father, Wesley, left to serve his country and was killed in action during World War II. She was later adopted by her mother's second husband, Elmer Wimmer Sr.

On October 8, 1960, JoAnn married the first love of her life and father of her children, Chester "Chet" Sowinski Jr.. They shared 25 wonderful years together until his passing in 1985. She later met Anton "Tony" Podhora, her devoted partner for nearly 41 years.

JoAnn spent most of her career as a factory worker, dedicating 34 years to Marathon Rubber Products until its closing, followed by more than a decade at Kolbe Windows & Doors.

She had a lifelong love for the Northwoods and treasured every moment at the cottage she and Chet purchased. Countless weekends were spent there with family and friends. When her grandchildren were young, she delighted in feeding the "coons" day-old treats just to watch them wander up at night—then waking everyone to witness their feisty antics. It quickly became a cherished highlight of every trip.

JoAnn also enjoyed her frequent casino visits, quiet afternoons spent bird watching, and long, heartfelt phone conversations with family. Holidays were always about being together; Christmas Eve was first celebrated at Grandma's house, and later the tradition continued at Jim's as the family grew and welcomed great-grandchildren.

JoAnn is survived by her partner, Tony Podhora; her son, James "Jim" (Dorianne) Sowinski; grandchildren Beth (John) Hopperdietzel, Rebecca, Amy, Mike (Misty), and Ashley Sowinski; great-grandchildren Dakota (Sam), Santana (Kylor), Quinton, ToriLynn (Hunter), Austin, Landon, Zachary, Nickalas, Jackson, and Annaleese; and great-great-grandchildren Bellamy, Hazel, and one expected in June 2026.

She is also survived by Tony's sons Tony (Christy) Jr., Scott (Mary), and Robert (Rob); Tony's grandchildren Jeffrey (Asta), Kerrie, Zach, and Max; her brothers Gene, Elmer Jr. (Judy), Keith (Cindie), David, and Patrick Wimmer; and her sisters Rose (Steve) Miller and Barb (Ken) Zunker.

She was preceded in death by her parents, Wesley Barttelt, Mary Ann and Elmer Wimmer Sr.; her husband, Chet Sowinski; her children Donna and Robert "Bobby" Sowinski; and her great-grandson, Coltyn Sowinski.

A visitation will be held from 10:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. on Friday, March 6, 2026, at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau, with burial to follow at Restlawn Memorial Park.

records.name: Kaitlyn D. Bronson
records.summary: Kaitlyn Dale Bronson, 32, passed away on February 19, 2026.
records.body:
Kaitlyn Dale Bronson, 32, passed away on February 19, 2026. She was born on April 29, 1993, in Germany to Theodore and Kari (Scheunemann) Bronson.

Kaitlyn's greatest love and proudest role was being a mother. Her daughter, Kaylani, was her heart, her best friend, and her most important person. Not a night passed without Kaitlyn telling her, "I love you to the moon and stars and back and to infinity and beyond."

Kaitlyn lived her life boldly and unapologetically. She loved movies, especially Cinderella and The Aristocats, and had a special fondness for Marie from Aristocats. She adored her Lisa Frank cheetah print, spontaneous hair makeovers, make-up, music, and dancing. Kaitlyn never entered a room quietly — whether it was her loud hair, loud clothes, or loud voice, her presence was always known and always felt.

She had a gift for music, able to sing and rap with confidence and skill. Once, she entertained her brother Corey with a hilarious "full talking rap" over the phone while he drove three hours home from Milwaukee. She loved to dance and sing and was always ready with a quick wit and a comeback.

Kaitlyn especially loved teasing her dad with spirited political debates, getting him all worked up before flashing a grin and asking, "Are you big mad, or little mad?" — instantly making him laugh and, in her mind, winning the argument every time.

She graduated from D.C. Everest Senior High School with the Class of 2011, where she also played softball. Though she always dreamed of attending beauty school, her stubborn independence wouldn't allow her to accept help paying for it, and she kept that dream close to her heart.

Kaitlyn was the favorite aunt — always ready for pool trips, games, and playful trash talk. She was often heard saying, "Don't play with me, play with your friends." She loved her cats, supported autism awareness, and treasured her nieces and nephews deeply.

Her kindness knew no limits. Kaitlyn would "give the shirt off her back," even when she had little to give. She saw beauty in everyone, stood up for the underdog, and was fiercely loyal — truly her siblings' ride-or-die. She made people feel like she had everything, even when she had nothing. She was bright, eclectic, beautiful inside and out, with a sense of humor that could light up the darkest room.

Kaitlyn is survived by her parents, Theodore and Kari Bronson; her beloved daughter, Kaylani; the love of her life, Sean Smith; her siblings, Nicole (Jeffery) Eaton, Corey (Leigh) Bronson, and Tiffany (Jonathan) McKnight; her cherished nieces and nephews, Bryce and Bryton Childers, Brandi Eaton, McKenzie and Roman Bronson, Erin, Aliyah, Camryn, Rhylee, and Lyla; along with many loving aunts, uncles, and cousins.

She was preceded in death by her maternal and paternal grandparents.

Kaitlyn's life was vibrant, loud, loving, and unforgettable — just like she was. Her spirit will continue to dance in the hearts of all who loved her.

A celebration of Kaitlyn's life will take place on Friday, February 27, 2026, at the Rothschild Village Hall, 211 Grand Ave, Rothschild, WI 54474 from 12:00 pm until 9:00 pm. Kaitlyn's wish was to celebrate in her own way, with God. The family's wishes were for a celebration featuring rainbow colors, cheetah print, or Lisa Frank themes.

records.name: Ray Vollmer
records.summary: Ray Vollmer, 95, residing at Primrose Retirement Community of Wausau, died on February 17, 2026.
records.body:
Ray Vollmer, 95, residing at Primrose Retirement Community of Wausau, died on Tuesday, February 17, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born on July 29, 1930, in Tigerton, the son of George and Anna (Bloechl) Vollmer.

Ray graduated from High School in 1948 in Birnamwood. He attended a barber college in Green Bay but decided that being a barber was not his calling. Thus, he volunteered to join the Navy in 1955. He attended boot camp at the Great Lakes Naval Training Center near Chicago. From Great Lakes, Ray was sent to Norfolk, Virginia, the home port of the 6th Fleet, the U. S. Navy's operational unit and staff of U. S. Naval Forces.

With this assignment, Ray was aboard the U. S. S. Chloris, an aircraft repair ship. Later, Ray worked for the naval intelligence department in Londin, England, under the guidance of Admiral Wright, commander-in-chief of the U. S. Atlantic Fleet. At this time, Admiral Wright was also involved in the overseeing of the modernization and readiness of the U. S. Atlantic Fleet during the Cold War.

After examinations and obtaining top-secret clearance, Ray was placed at the Pentagon, the headquarters of the United States Department of Defense in Arlington, Virginia. At the Pentagon, Ray was involved with IBM technology for a couple of years.

When he left the Pentagon in the mid-60's, he worked in Fond du Lac in the food and drug department for A. C. Nielsen Company, a global marketing research firm.

Ray bought a dairy farm and also a beef farm, both in Shawano County, near his hometown. While running the farms, he also worked for Banner Banks in Birnamwood/Antigo/Hatley. He later worked at the University of Wisconsin-Eau Claire in the Management Information Technology Department for about 18 years.

Ray is survived by his sister, Nancy Mueller of Hatley; nieces and nephews, Debra (Dennis) Rew of Birnamwood, Diane (Alan) Schultz of Shawano, Michelle (Steve) Turney of Birnamwood, Trudy (John) Hill of Hortonville, Matt Vollmer of Hortonville, Mary Vollmer of New London, Colleen Vollmer of Milwaukee, Nicole Vollmer of Stoughton, Virgil (Carrie) Mueller of Mosinee, Dawn (Michael) Peschel of Madison and Becky (Perry) Kasprak of Hatley; and many great nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends.

Ray was preceded in death by his parents; sister, Jeanette (Norman) Opper; brother, Milan (Vivian) Vollmer; brother-in-law, Virgil Mueller and niece-in-law, Bonnie Vollmer.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 am on Saturday, February 28th, 2026, at St. Philomena Catholic Church, Birnamwood. Father Vicente Llagas will preside. Burial will be in the parish cemetery. Visitation will be held on Saturday from 9:00 am until the time of service at the church. Following the Funeral Mass, Military Rites will be take place in the church parking lot, conducted by Darling-Gunderson Post #341, American Legion Birnamwood.

records.name: Rhonda Schmid
records.summary: Rhonda Schmid, 71, of Athens, formerly of Green Bay, passed away on February 19, 2026.
records.body:
Rhonda Schmid, age 71, of Athens, formerly of Green Bay, was called home by her Lord on Thursday, February 19, 2026, at The Athenian, Athens, WI.

The former Rhonda Jean Retzlaff was born on September 28, 1954, in Tigerton, WI to Eugene and Leona (Steinke) Retzlaff. She graduated from Tigerton High School in 1972. Rhonda retired from Georgia-Pacific (previously Fort Howard) after over 30 years. She loved being around kids and driving school bus for many years as well.

Rhonda was incredibly passionate about her faith, church, and community. She loved giving back in any way she could. She helped with church activities such as LWML, VBS and the annual Christmas Cookie walk (no one really knows how many cookies she has made). Throughout her life she enjoyed painting, reading, sewing, crafting, ceramics, stained glass making, and cheering on the Packers, Badgers and Brewers. When she wasn't busy working or crafting, she enjoyed camping and traveling but especially spending time with her family.

Rhonda's desire to help others continued even when she moved into assisted living. She became the self-appointed welcomer to new residents and those that didn't partake in group activities, encouraging others to join in. She has taught new crafting skills, played Dominos and board games, called bingo and helped assemble countless puzzles. Her artistic creativity and passion for gardening were truly amazing. She would take any opportunity she could to make someone's day brighter and that will never be forgotten.

Rhonda is survived by her children Stacy (Steve) VanDerLinden, Athens, WI, and Nancy (Allen Boucher) Schmid, Luxemburg, WI. Brother, Jim Retzlaff, Tigerton, WI. Grandchildren, Alanah (Wade), Seth, Haley (significant other, David), Karra (Fiancé, Nicholas), Olivia (Fiancé, Lane), and Jacob. Great grandchildren, Atlas, Eleanor, Maddox, and Amara. And her beloved little dog Izzy.

She is preceded in death by parents Leona and Eugene Retzlaff; sister, Sheri Retzlaff; daughter, Shanda Daehnert; and long-time boyfriend, Tony Maciulis.

Funeral service will be held Saturday, February 28th at 11:30 a.m. at Trinity Lutheran Church, 301 Elm Street, Athens. Rev. Mark Schwarz will officiate. Friends may call on Saturday from 9:30 a.m. until time of service at the church.

records.name: Scott E. Trull
records.summary: Scott E. Trull, 86, of Rothschild, passed away on February 20, 2026.
records.body:
Scott was born on May 28, 1939, to Shirley and Lucille (Brewster) Trull in Waupaca County. He graduated from Nekoosa High School in 1958. Scott proudly served our country in the United State Air Force in 1958 and received a medical honorable discharge, but volunteered for "grab barracks" for a couple of years.

He attended the University of Guelph in Ontario, Canada, from 1960-1961, receiving an Ontario Diploma of Horticulture. Scott also took several classes through UW-Madison in professional Horticulture. He attended Trinity Christian College in Palos Heights, IL, for four years receiving an honorary Bachelor's degree toward pastoral counseling. Later in life, Scott did Certified Nurse's Assistant Training through NTC.

Scott was united in marriage to Patricia Emmes in 1963 in Wisconsin Rapids and later divorced. He then was married to DiAnne Keck Kuklinski in 1978 in Wausau until she passed away in 2002. Scott then married Arlene Gross Trewyn Braithwaite on October 27, 2007, in Wausau.

Scott worked numerous jobs throughout his life. He started out by doing an apprenticeship in greenhouse operations and management in Burlington, IA, from 1961-1963. After his apprenticeship, he worked at Leap's Greenhouse in Wausau, WI, as a manager and grower from 1963-1965. Scott worked at K Mart in Wausau in various departments until becoming the manager of the garden department for a few years. He then worked for the City of Wausau/ Marathon County Parks Departments as a Horticulturist. He also worked part time for years at Andy Borguine's greenhouse on Birch street in Schofield and part time for years at Ed Prohaska's nursery in Rothschild off Weston Avenue. Scott then worked at NTC in Wausau as call staff in Horticulture for 20 years, then eight years as an Agriculturology instructor. He was proud to be instrumental in working, helping, and teaching the Hmong population gardening skills. He developed the first sustainable agriculture program in Wisconsin which is now a Master's Degree program at the University of Wisconsin. He was appointed by the State Department of Agriculture to be on the first Organic Gardening Certification Board. Scott started the New Directions Biblical Counseling Center in Wausau and Mosinee helping others for 20 years from 1980-2000 as a licensed pastoral counselor. He was sent out as a local missionary through the Great Lakes Baptist Conference. He was also active in starting The Neighbors' Place. Lastly, Scott was a shuttle bus driver/greeter for Aspirus Hospital in Wausau from 2005-2011.

Scott will be remembered for his active creativity, making a motor bike when young for his transportation and using it to make store deliveries. He and a fellow student were the school artists and he won a trip to art school in Minneapolis, MN, where he met his teacher, Charles Schulz, who became famous for creating the comic, "Peanuts". Since he had a knee replaced after his military service, he wasn't able to bend that knee. Therefore, he made a rowing bike, an airboat, campers, a lawnmower with a rider behind, and gardening planters to help those with physical disabilities plus teaching others how to use their cane to defend and exercise. He held a Black Belt from the CaneMasters International Association. He was a wonderful storyteller, had a great sense of humor, and loved sharing his strong faith in God. He was an inventor (having a patent for a Connector Block and various copyrites), a handyman, and had experience being a meat cutter, deputy, firefighter, and he enjoyed meteorology. Scott was very musically talented and shared that talent being in quartets and playing the harmonica (once happily meeting the Harmonicats). He was always welcoming of others and will be missed by all who knew him.

Scott is survived by his wife, Arlene Trull of Rothschild; children, Pamela (John) Schneider of DePere, WI, and Amy (Robert) Umland of Schofield, WI; stepchildren, Ben (Kris) Kuklinski of Merrill, WI, Annette (Rick) Block of Wittenberg, WI, Keith (Connie) Trewyn of Phillips, WI, Barry (Vicky) Braithwaite of Somerset, WI, and Brandon (Loyda) Braithwaite of Shorewood, MN; grandchildren, Hailey King, Blake Lepel, Jayden Lepel, Chase Lepel, and Jordan (Jo) Umland; step grandchildren, Aron (Ellie) Joles, Anthony Rowe, Shawn (Amanda) Trewyn, Alexis (Joel) Block, Ryan (Erin) Trewyn, Hannah (Dylan) Elliott, Grace Braithwaite, Lily Braithwaite, Ayla Braithwaite, and Hahmi Braithwaite; and 6 great grandchildren.

Memorial Service will be at 11:00 am, Friday, March 6, 2026, at Highland Community Church-Weston Campus. Pastor Dan MacDonald will officiate and burial will be at a later date at Maple Grove Cemetery in the Village of Maine. Visitation will be from 4:30 pm-7:00 pm, Thursday, March 5, at Brainard Funeral Home in Weston and will continue on Friday from 9:30 am-11:00 am at the church.

In lieu of flowers, memorials are preferred to Highland Community Church.

A special thank you to Interim Healthcare Hospice for all of their care and assistance.

records.name: Donna M. Bolton
records.summary: Donna M. Bolton, 91, passed away peacefully on February 15, 2026, at Rennes Health and Rehab in Weston, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Donna M. Bolton, 91, passed away peacefully on February 15, 2026, at Rennes Health and Rehab in Weston, Wisconsin.

Born on December 30, 1934, to the late James and Olive (Roberts) Brown, Donna lived a life filled with music, faith, family, and joy. She had a deep love for music and shared that passion generously with others. Donna formed a musical program with musical seniors and created Show Case of the Stars, who brought comfort and happiness to nursing homes, assisted living facilities, long-term care centers, and churches through their performances. The group was so well loved that they recorded CDs of their music, preserving the joy they shared with so many.

Donna was steadfast in her faith and wrote in her medical directive that she had lived a very good and happy life — a statement that reflected her grateful heart and positive spirit. She found great pride in her family and cherished every moment spent with them.

She is lovingly survived by her children, Georgia Fischer, Juan Girard, and Adrienne Clark; her grandchildren, Danielle, John, Erin, Adam, Ryan, and Christina; ten great-grandchildren; one great-great-grandchild; and her siblings, Betty Qualheim, Lorraine Beyer, and Harold Brown.

In addition to her parents, Donna was preceded in death by her husband, Ed Bolton; her daughter, Rhonda; and her siblings, Bernice Zajec and James Brown.

Per Donna's wishes, no funeral service will be held.

Donna's warmth, music, and unwavering faith will be deeply missed but fondly remembered by all who knew and loved her.

records.name: Gerald Thomas Schneider
records.summary: Gerald "Gerry" Thomas Schneider, 69, of Wausau, passed away on February 22, 2026 at Oak Heights Assisted Living in Kronenwetter.
records.body:
Gerald "Gerry" Thomas Schneider, 69, Wausau, passed away on Sunday, February 22, 2026 at Oak Heights Assisted Living in Kronenwetter.

He was born December 18, 1956 in Mundelein, IL, son of the late Paul and Florence (Gruener) Schneider.

He is survived by four brothers, James (Vicky) Schneider, Robert (Laura) Schneider, Joseph Schneider and Thomas (Elaine) Schneider, 14 nieces and nephews; and many great nieces and great nephews.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 12:00 p.m. on Saturday, February 28, 2026 at Holy Name of Jesus Catholic Church, Wausau. Rev. Woodrow Pace will preside. Visitation will be on Saturday from 11:00 a.m. until time of Mass at the church. Burial will be held at the Ascension Catholic Cemetery in Libertyville, IL at a later date.

The family would like to extend a heartfelt thank you to all the staff at Oak Heights Assisted Living for all the wonderful care, love and support you gave to Gerry over the past two years.

records.name: Joanne M. Kostka
records.summary: Joanne M. Kostka, 88, formerly of Weston, died on February 18, 2026 at Vesta Memory Care in Brookfield.
records.body:
Joanne M. Kostka, 88, formerly of Weston, died on Wednesday, February 18, 2026 at Vesta Memory Care, Brookfield, WI under the care of Agrace Hospice.

Joanne was born on October 10, 1937 in Galloway, the daughter of Walter and Bernice (Lutzewitz) Tryba.

On February 20, 1960, Joanne was united in marriage to Valerian Kostka at St. Joseph Catholic Church, Galloway. He preceded her in death on December 19, 2021.

Joanne graduated from St. Mary's Nursing School in 1958 and was a nurse at various clinics for 20 years. She enjoyed cooking, and trying new recipes in cookbooks. Val and Joanne had many friends and loved hosting dinner parties and going out to eat. Joanne especially loved the time spent with her family and will be deeply missed.

Joanne is survived by her children, Ellen (Michael) Lucey and Steven (Kira) Kostka; grandson, Joseph (Bri) Lucey and great-grandchildren, D.J. Lucey and Mason Lucey; brother-in-law, James 'Loyal' (Karen) Kostka and sister-in-law, Sue (Jack) Wanta.

Joanne was preceded in death by her parents; husband; and sister, Betty (Russ) Hanson.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11AM on Friday, March 20, 2026 at St. Ladislaus Catholic Church, Bevent. Fr. Augustine Bentil will officiate. Burial will be in the Parish cemetery. Visitation will be on Friday from 9AM until the time of Mass at the church.

records.name: Michael C. D'Acquisto
records.summary: Michael C. D'Acquisto, born November 5, 1962, passed away on February 16, 2026.
records.body:
Michael C. D'Acquisto, born November 5, 1962, to the late Nancy Bishop and John D'Acquisto, passed away on February 16, 2026.

Above all else, Michael was a devoted husband to Leslie, the love and true reason for his life. He cherished their life together and found his greatest joy simply in being her partner. Their marriage was the center of his world and the foundation of his happiness.

Michael was a great dad to his loving English Shepard, Abigail. They were great pals and spent their days together. She is very sad and keeps looking for her dad.

Michael was also a proud patriot who loved and served his country with honor and dedication. He was a veteran of the United States Air National Guard, 128 Air Refueling Wing, serving from January 24, 1985, until 2008. During his distinguished military career, he served in Desert Storm, Operation Noble Eagle, and Iraqi Freedom. His service afforded him the opportunity to travel the world—an experience he deeply treasured. He carried immense pride in his country and in the role he played in protecting it. He continued his patriotic beliefs as a proud member of the Peplin VFW 8280.

A graduate of Muskego High School, Michael went on to earn his Bachelor of Science degree from the University of Wisconsin–Milwaukee. He and Leslie made their home in Milwaukee for 25 years before moving to their dream property—12.97 acres in the Town of Bergen on the Eau Plaine Reservoir. There, they created their own sanctuary. Surrounded by nature and the peace he so valued, Michael found true contentment and happiness on the property.

Michael had a deep love for sports and was an avid collector of sports memorabilia. He was a lifelong fan of the Milwaukee Brewers and the Green Bay Packers, with his love for the Packers beginning in the 1960s. In quieter moments, he enjoyed collecting stamps, working on crossword puzzles, and listening to music.

Michael will be remembered for his steadfast devotion to Leslie, his unwavering patriotism, and the quiet joys that filled his life. His legacy is one of love, service, and loyalty. He will be deeply missed and forever remembered.

He is survived by his loving wife, Leslie; his brothers-in-law, Stephen (Sarrah) Oliver, George (Mary Clare) Gary, and Robert (Rita) Gary; sister-in-law, Sarah (Will) Burggraf; mother-in-law, Marcia Oliver; and father-in-law, Bob Olafson.

A celebration of Michael's life will take place at a later date.

records.name: Nicholas James Bierman
records.summary: Nicholas James Bierman, 42, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on February 19, 2026.
records.body:
With hearts both broken and deeply grateful for the gift of his life, we announce that Nicholas James Bierman, 42, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on February 19, 2026, entering into the presence of his Lord and Savior. He was surrounded by the compassionate and tender care of his devoted wife and caregiver, Sarah, along with the extraordinary team at Interim Hospice.

Nick was born on March 14, 1983, to James Bierman and Lisa (Breyer) Fischer. He attended Wittenberg-Birnamwood High School, where he proudly competed in football, basketball, and baseball all four years, graduating in 2001. He went on to earn his degree from Northcentral Technical College in Autobody Industrial Paint and CNC Machining. Nick dedicated many years of loyal service to Greenheck Fan Corporation in Schofield, where he was known for his strong work ethic, steady skill, and unwavering reliability.

Above all, Nick was a devoted husband and father. His greatest joy was his beloved wife, Sarah, and their five boys: Cody (Cienna) Graf of Montana, parents of Abel, Clayton, and Malia Rose; Joseph Graf of Wisconsin; Kory (Katie) Graf of Wisconsin; and Colten and Beau, who remain at home under the loving care of their mother. His family was his pride, his purpose, and his greatest accomplishment.

Nick found peace and renewal in the outdoors. He loved fishing, hunting, and camping, especially at Moose Lake, Mud Lake, Harrison Hills, and countless other treasured waters and woods. Whether casting a line at sunrise, walking quietly through the trees, or sitting beside a campfire surrounded by laughter, Nick found joy in God's creation and in sharing those moments with family and friends.

Nick is further survived by his grandmother, Beverly Bierman, Birnamwood; parents, James (Trina) Bierman and Lisa (Randal) Fischer, Birnamwood, WI; his sisters, Alison Fischer, California; Jenna (Ryan Bune) Bierman and their daughters Brynn and Adalynn, Wisconsin; Miranda Morris and her son Grayson, Illinois; Melanie Fischer, Eau Claire, WI; and Rebecca Bierman and her daughters, Rayah, Avery, and Carmella, Birnamwood, WI.

He is also survived by his mother-in-law, Diane Frazier, Wausau; brother-in-law Paul (Keely) Chadha and their children Emmeline, Dylan, Tennessee, and Vaughn, South Dakota; and many more loving aunts, uncles, cousins, extended family members, and dear friends.

He was preceded in death by his grandparents, Paul Bierman Jr., Glen and Virginia Breyer, and Philip and Edna Fischer; his uncle, Dennis Clark; and his cousin, Garrett VanderGrinten.

A Funeral Service will be held at 11:00 AM on Saturday, February 28, 2026, at Bible Truth Chapel, 1600 N. 1st Avenue, Wausau. David Hart will officiate. Visitation will be held from 5:00 to 7:00 PM on Friday, February 27, 2026, at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston, and again from 10:00 AM until the time of service on Saturday at the church. Interment will be private.

In lieu of flowers, memorial contributions may be made to Nick's wife, Sarah Bierman, for the continued care, support, and future needs of their children.

records.name: Bradley D. Mroczenski
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Bradley D. Mroczenski, 59, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on February 24, 2026.
records.body:
Bradley D. Mroczenski, affectionately known as Brad, passed away peacefully on Tuesday, February 24, 2026, at Aspirus Hospital – Wausau surrounded by his loving children.

Born on December 15, 1966, in Wausau, Wisconsin, the youngest child of John and Arlene (Hardt) Mroczenski. He spent his life cultivating a legacy of love, creativity, and dedication to his family and community.

Brad was deeply rooted in Wausau, where he developed a passion for the outdoors, finding joy in hunting and golfing. His love for sports was evident; he relished attending various sporting events, often sharing these moments with family and friends. A devoted father, he attended all of his children's school and sporting events, always cheering them on from the sidelines, embodying the role of an encouraging and supportive parent. His zest for life extended beyond sporting events to include woodworking and other creative pursuits.

Above all, Brad cherished spending time with his family. He was especially proud of his two children, son Dylan (Jacey) Mroczenski and daughter Madison (Brandon Rotolo) Mroczenski, who were the light of his life. His joy continued with the arrival of his grandson Daxton Mroczenski, who brought him immense happiness.

Brad's connections with his siblings were also a source of pride and strength. He is survived by his brothers Steve (Annette) Mroczenski, Rick Mroczenski, and Jeff (Laura) Mroczenski, as well as his sister Jean (Larry) Rothmeyer and sister Karen Schenzel. These relationships shaped him into the beloved family man he was, and he will be dearly missed by all who had the privilege of knowing him.

He is preceded in death by his parents.

In this difficult time, the memory of Brad's laughter, generosity, and love will remain in the hearts of his family and friends. He leaves behind a rich tapestry of memories that will continue to inspire and bring comfort to those he touched throughout his life. May he rest in peace.

records.name: Dean S. Gille
records.summary: Dean S. Gille, 64, passed away on February 25, 2026.
records.body:
Dean S. Gille, born on November 29, 1961 (age 64), to Lee and Nancy (Engel) Gille, passed away on February 25, 2026, at Aspirus Howard Young Hospital, leaving behind a legacy of creativity, curiosity, and laughter. A man with a brilliant mind and an adventurous spirit, Dean approached life with enthusiasm and ingenuity.

Dean had a deep love for the outdoors and could often be found fishing or ice fishing, patiently waiting for the next bite while enjoying the peace of nature. He was a devoted NASCAR fan, especially of Dale Earnhardt Sr., and rarely missed a chance to watch a race. His passion for adventure extended to gold mining, magnet fishing, HAM radio, and even storm chasing — pursuits that reflected both his love of discovery and his fearless curiosity.

A gifted creator and builder, especially during his career doing autobody repair and custom paint work, Dean's intelligence truly shined through his projects. Among his many accomplishments, he designed and built an underwater drone and even wrote the program to operate it — a testament to his remarkable talent and determination. He loved sharing his creations and enthusiastically explaining how they worked to anyone willing to listen. His excitement was contagious.

Dean also had a special place in his heart for his beloved Huskies. He treasured their companionship and the joy they brought to his life.

Known for his awesome sense of humor and easygoing nature, Dean was always fun to be around. He had a way of making people laugh and feel at ease, leaving lasting impressions on all who knew him.

He is survived by his parents, Lee and Nancy Gille; his brothers, Kery Gille, Darin (Kati) Gille, and Kris (Billie Jo) Gille; his nephews, Andrew Gille, Justin (Jessica) Gille, Brendan (Morgan) Gille; one great-nephew and two great-nieces. He is further survived by his former wife and her family.

Dean will be remembered for his sharp mind, adventurous heart, and the laughter he brought into the lives of others.

records.name: Deborah A. Gassner
records.summary: Deborah A. Gassner, 74, of Wausau, Wisconsin, died on February 24, 2026.
records.body:
Deborah A. Gassner, 74, died February 24, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

She was born April 26, 1951, in Poughkeepsie, New York, daughter of the late George and Anna Mae (Hoffman) Jones. On October 27, 1979, she married Jon Gassner at Holy Name Catholic Church, Wausau.

Deb worked over 40 years at Marathon Cheese; she was never one to be afraid of hard work. She enjoyed traveling and the wind in her hair on the back of the motorcycle with her husband and friends. Her favorite cycle trip was to Sturgis. She loved taking walks and animals, especially puppies. Her family was always number one and she was the kindest person you could ever meet.

Survivors include her husband, Jon; children, Michelle (Chad) Huff and Shawn (Savannah) Gassner; grandson, Cameron Gassner; siblings, Charles Jones, Dianne (Stan) Kaminski, Joseph (MaryJo) Jones, Ann Marie Jones and Teresa (Steven) Boorse; many nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends.

She was preceded in death by her parents and a brother, Keith Jones.

records.name: Donald A. Voermans
records.summary: Donald A. Voermans, 70, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on February 24, 2026.
records.body:
Donald "Don" Albert Voermans, age 70, passed away peacefully on Tuesday, February 24, 2026, surrounded by family.

A devoted son, brother and friend, Don was known for his generosity and sense of humor. Born in Wausau, Wisconsin, he was a craft woodworker and took great pride in creating things built to last.

He loved spending time with family, particularly his nieces and nephews. He also enjoyed fishing, bow hunting, hockey, the Green Bay Packers and retelling stories of his past. His care and loyalty touched everyone who knew him.

Don is preceded in death by parents, Donald and Rita Voermans; and survived by brothers, Jeffrey (Ann) Voermans and Gerald (Beth) Voermans; four nieces and nephews, and six grand nieces and nephews.

A visitation will be held at St Michael Catholic Church, Wausau, WI on Friday, March 6, 2026 at 9:30 am. A Mass of Thanksgiving will be held at 10:30 am, followed by a luncheon in the parish hall. Burial at St Augustine Catholic Cemetery in Harrison, Wisconsin will follow immediately after the luncheon.

records.name: Eleanor J. Nueske
records.summary: Eleanor J. Nueske, 94, of Shawano County, passed away on February 26, 2026.
records.body:
Eleanor J. Nueske, age 94, of Shawano County, passed away peacefully on Thursday, February 26, 2026, in Birnamwood.

Eleanor was born on June 27, 1931, to Edward and Lillian (Breaker) Foth. On March 30, 1953, she was united in marriage to Elmer Nueske in Michigan. Together they shared 69½ years of marriage before Elmer preceded her in death in 2022. Their life together was rooted in love, hard work, and devotion to family.

Eleanor worked at a papermill in Neenah and later at Lord's Tax Firm in Manawa. She and Elmer also dedicated many years to working their dairy farm before retiring. A wonderful homemaker, Eleanor loved to cook, bake, and can. She delighted in collecting recipes and cookbooks and enjoyed watching cooking shows. Her kitchen was a place of warmth and welcome.

Eleanor also found great joy in watching and feeding her birds. Each year, she patiently waited for their return in the spring, a quiet ritual that brought her comfort and happiness.

Known as a kind and gentle soul, Eleanor deeply loved her family and cherished every moment spent with them. Her greatest joy was serving THE LORD. She loved gathering with her children, grandchildren, and great-grandchildren, creating memories that will be treasured for generations.

She is lovingly survived by her children, Kris (Mark) Neuendorf and Roger (Stacey) Nueske; daughter-in-law, Cheri Nueske; nine grandchildren, Stephanie (Albert) Gillis, Justin (Jannah) Neuendorf, Sabrina (Nick) Tews, Angela (Jeremy) Benson, Cassie (Justin) Kressel, Derek (Ashley) Nueske, Kelsie (Robert) Schoneck, Ashley (Zack) Russell, and Chase Nueske; and twelve great-grandchildren, Cole, Ayla, Riley, Ethan, Liam, Avery, Chloe, Brinley, Brityn, Beau, Callum, Callan, and one on the way.

She is further survived by her siblings and in-laws, Norma Naef, Kenneth (Joannie) Foth, Bill (Sylvia) Nueske, and Bob Nelson, along with many nieces, nephews, cousins, and dear friends.

Eleanor was preceded in death by her parents; her beloved husband, Elmer; her son, Darrell; and her siblings and in-laws, Lauretta (Harry) Kassube, Maranda (Warren) Sykes, Harvey (Clara) Foth, Howard (Doris) Foth, Marjorie (Alvin) Krisher, Vivian (Elmer) Marx, Allen Foth, Eugene (Pearl) Foth, Dorothy Gehrke, Lyle (Joan) Foth, and Conrad Naef.

A Funeral Service will be held at 12:00 noon on Tuesday, March 3, 2026, at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home in Wittenberg. Visitation will take place from 10:00 a.m. until the time of service. Burial will follow at Forest Home Cemetery in Wittenberg.

records.name: Gerald E. Gehrmann
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Gerald Edward Gehrmann, known as Gehrmo, of Superior, Wisconsin, passed away in 2026.
records.body:
It is with heavy hearts that we announce the passing of Gerald Edward Gehrmann (Gehrmo), whose life was marked by strength, kindness, and unwavering dedication to those he loved.

Gerald was born in Superior, Wisconsin, on 26, July 1947, and grew up with a strong sense of responsibility and compassion that would define his life. He graduated from East High in Superior in 1965 and from UW Superior in 1969. In 1969, he enlisted in the Armed Forces, the Army division. He served two tours as an Infantry Sergeant in Vietnam from November 4, 1969, to June 16, 1971.

After his dedication to our country, Jerry taught Physical, Health, and Driver's Education in Port Washington, WI, for two years. He went on to build a meaningful career as a salesman. He started at Clover Belt Dairy in Hayward before dedicating forty-six years to Anheuser Busch, where he was known as "The Bud Man" and respected for his integrity, work ethic, and steady leadership. As a tribute to Jerry's service in Vietnam, he was chosen for the honor flight to Washington, D.C., on May 13, 2023.

Above all, Gerald cherished his family and their time spent together. On October 3rd, 1981, he married the love of his life, Karen. They shared so much love together, including rides out west on their Harley to see the mountains and their sons, raising many litters of puppies, and at the end of the day, enjoying a cold beer. Karen also blessed Jerry with six more children. He loved them unconditionally as his own, with favorite memories of camping at the Eau Plaine, kiddie cocktails at Colonial Ballroom, and even raking the woods to keep the backyard tidy.

He was a devoted husband, father, grandfather, great-grandfather, brother, and friend, always ready with wise advice. He was a steady hand, with witty, sarcastic humor and unbeatable resilience. He found joy in life's simple pleasures, such as fishing, hunting pheasants and deer, his lawn, and creating maple syrup at the Bear Track Inn. He will be remembered for his dedication to his country, quick wit, and impact on others.

Gerald's cherished memories live on with his children, Mike (Melody) Crisman, Peggy (Dennis) Bindl, Dave (Beckie) Crisman, Kim (Butch) Suchomski, Daniel (Shellie) Crisman, Todd Gehrmann, Troy (Amy) Gehrmann; twelve grandchildren; seven great-grandchildren; his sister, Mary Roulston; as well as extended family and friends who will deeply miss his presence.

He was preceded in death by the love of his life, Karen; parents, Edward and Evelyn Gehrmann; son, Gregory Crisman; grandson, Chase Crisman; and nephew, David Gnoza; as well as six trusted furry friends, Bud, Mic, Sport, Sarge, Lily, and Parker, who are laid to rest at The Bear Track Inn.

Memorial service will be held at 11:00 AM on Saturday, March 7th, 2026, at Mount Olive Lutheran Church, 6205 Alderson Street, Weston. Pastor Lance Hoelscher will officiate. Visitation will be held from 9:00 AM until the time of the service at the church. Military Honors will be accorded by the American Legion Honor Guard of Montgomery-Plant-Dudley Post 10. Interment will be in Northwoods National Cemetery in Harshaw.
