In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to the Never Forgotten Honor Flight or the VFW.

Gerald's legacy lives on in the lives he touched, the lessons he shared, and the love he gave so freely. He will be remembered always and missed beyond measure.

records.name: Nancy L. Heil
records.summary: Nancy L. Heil, 89, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on February 17, 2026.
records.body:
Nancy Heil, age 89, passed away on February 17, 2026. She passed peacefully with her daughter by her side. Nancy was born on September 30, 1936, in Wausau, Wisconsin to the late Charles and Ida (Haufshildt) Brown.

Nancy graduated from Wausau West High School in 1955 and married Dennis Heil on December 26, 1955. Nancy and Dennis lived for a short time in Mobile, AL while Dennis was in the military. After Dennis had served his 4 years, they returned to Wausau. Not long after Nancy went to work full-time for Wausau Supply company, while Dennis started the family business Denny's Standard. Nancy started working with her husband when he purchased another location for Denny's Standard and opened Northway Sports. Once they were back down to one station Nancy went to work for CIT Financial, where she worked her way up to manager of the finance company and trained many employees who had success in the financial industry. Nancy joined Dennis running the family business again after they opened a new c-store and service station, Denny's Auto Tech on Stewart Avenue. Even after the sudden death of her husband, she continued working at the business until it's closure.

Nancy had an unbelievable work ethic and truly loved working. Even in retirement she continued to work for Randlin Homes into her 80s!

Nancy lived a full and vibrant life, she loved traveling! She didn't have any fear of new places! In the mid 60's she drove with her sister, two 5-year-old kids, and a baby out to Syracuse NY, to visit her niece, Diane. She went on to visit Syracuse New York again after her niece had married her husband, Bill. Nancy and family made many trips to Milwaukee to visit her brother George and his wife Eleanor. Once Dennis joined retail gasoline dealers, they made trips all over the state, they also traveled to Boston, Kentucky, California, Oregon, and Seattle for national conventions. As a family they traveled up through Canada and down the east coast, to Puerto Vallarta Mexico, Disneyland and Disneyworld, Hawaii, various Caribbean cruises, California, the Catalina islands, as well as many trips to Las Vegas once her niece moved there. Nancy also traveled to watch her daughter Dee dance throughout her ballroom dance career. She also traveled with her sister Sharon, brother George and wife Eleanor, to Branson and up through South Dakota into the black hills.

When Dennis was alive the family had a cottage on Lake Nokomis, after that she had a permanent campsite up north where she made many memories.

Nancy loved shopping, once you got her in a store you would be there for more than a couple hours. She also loved going out to dinner, she would have gone every night of the week is she could have. She especially loved going to the casino and playing bingo. She and sister Sharon went on the bus to casinos quite regularly.

Nancy had a lifelong love of collecting dolls, her collection containing somewhere around 380 Barbies, 250+ porcelain baby dolls, and all the buggies, strollers, and highchairs to go with them.

Nancy loved spending time with family and truly enjoyed any family gathering. She loved talking politics with Dee's husband Doug! She was very happy to enjoy watching her daughter and grandchildren grow and to see them find healthy, happy marriages and on good paths in life. She found a new joy when her great-grandson Alexander was born and had wanted to meet the new grandbabies that will be born soon. But that was not God's plan.

Known for honesty and straightforward nature, Nancy never left anyone guessing where she stood- but her words always came from a place of love and sincerity.

She is survived by her daughter, Denise (Doug) Heil-Herbst; her grandchildren, Erica (Geoff) Mondroski and Logan (Kassie) Heil; and her great-grandson, Alexander Mondroski. She is also survived by sister-in-law Cheryl Heil, her niece Diane (Bill) Omara who was like a sister, and her best friends. She survives many nieces, nephews, and their children, all of whom she considered immediate family.

Nancy was preceded in death by her beloved husband, Dennis A. Heil; her son Dennis J. "DJ" Heil; her siblings, Sharon (Leonard) Frank, Phyllis (Victor) Bradfish, Gordon (Joyce) Brown, Violet (Wally) Martens, George (Eleanor) Brown, and Charles (Clara) Brown, and brother-in-law Richard Heil.

Memorial service will take place at 11:00 AM on March 7, 2026, at Restlawn Memorial Park. There will be a celebration of life immediately following the services at Hutches on Stewart Avenue.

Nancy's warmth, honesty, adventurous spirit, and unwavering love for her family will be deeply missed and forever remembered.

records.name: Richard A. Seefeldt
records.summary: Richard A. Seefeldt, 97, of Eland, Wisconsin, died on February 26, 2026.
records.body:
Richard A. Seefeldt, 97 of Eland, died on Thursday, February 26, 2026, at home, in the care of his loving family and Aspirus Hospice Care.

Rich was born on May 13, 1928, at the farmhouse, the son of Paul and Irma (Hegewald) Seefeldt.

On June 3, 1950, he was united in marriage to Hilda Tabbert, where they spent 56 years together; she preceded him in death on December 30, 2006.

Rich farmed in Elderon with his wife and son until retirement in 1992, when his son took over the farm. He still helped work the fields for many years after.

To say Rich loved hunting is an understatement. If there was a season, he was hunting for it. He started at a young age with squirrels and deer. He spent many years running coyotes and bears with his hound dogs, his favorite being Sam. He took many trips out west to hunt elk and Canada for moose. His true passion came when they introduced turkeys to the hunt. He shot his last turkey at age 96. In the evenings, you could see him driving around to check on the turkeys. Later in life, he spent summers up on Pine Lake fishing for blue gills. As much as he enjoyed hunting, he loved the "after the hunt", with a cold beer and tomato juice or enjoying an Old Fashioned. Many had the pleasure of being called 'Little Darlin' or 'Sweetie' with a wink and a smile. He enjoyed bowling, playing cards, gardening, canning his tomato juice and pickles, and of course, cutting wood. Rich also gave back to his community as a member of the Wittenberg-Birnamwood School Board for many years.

He is survived by his son, Rick (Kathy) Seefeldt of Eland; four granddaughters, Heather (Robert) Yenter of Hatley, Jaime (Jeremy) Resch of Eland, April (Lance) Loonsfoot of Kronenwetter and Jenna (Donnie) Fahey of Hatley; eight great grandchildren, Tyler (Anna) and Abby Giddings, Dalton and Vivian Resch, Jolene Loonsfoot and Malayna, Kendra and Kase Fahey; one sister, Diane (special friend, Terry) Ploeger, as well as many nieces and nephews.

Rich was preceded in death by his parents, Paul and Irma Seefeldt; wife, Hilda; brothers, Del and Ray Seefeldt; sisters, Marge Beckwith, Elaine Krueger and Grace Mathie and special friend, Marie Nievinski.

A Funeral Service will be held at 11AM on Saturday, March 7, 2026, at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg. Mr. Dan Cnossen will preside. Burial will be in Evergreen Rest Cemetery, Elderon. Visitation will be on Friday, from 4-7PM, at the funeral home, and again on Saturday, from 9AM until the time of service at the funeral home.

records.name: Arlene Theresa Henrichs
records.summary: Arlene Theresa Henrichs, age 63, of Athens, passed away on February 26, 2026.
records.body:
Arlene died peacefully following a 32-year fight with kidney disease, on Thursday, February 26, 2026, at the Marshfield Medical Center in Marshfield, WI. Arlene was born on October 11, 1962, to Howard and Dorothy (née Literski) Murkowski. On October 16, 1982, she married Thomas D. Henrichs in Athens. After graduating with an associate's degree from Northcentral Technical College, Arlene worked in the Graphics/Communications department at Marathon Electric, specializing in page layout and stripping, as well as working the switchboard. She began work at Color Vision Printing in 1992 and remained part of the family there until retiring in January from her position as Bindery Supervisor.

In her early years, she played volleyball in high school and was a good softball player for Kraft Lanes. After her diagnosis, Arlene lived a full life, not letting the disease slow her down. She was in a golf league for years and placed several times in the American Legion Annual Golf Tournament in Wausau. She enjoyed road-tripping with her husband to major league baseball games and national parks, playing bingo, gardening, cooking maple syrup, spending time with her grandkids, and organizing Easter egg hunts at her family's syrup shack. Arlene also hosted the annual Hamburg Open Croquet Tournament at the A.T. & T. Country Club for over forty years.

Survivors include her husband Tom, Athens; three children: Kyle (Megan Gregory) Henrichs, St. Louis, MO, Eric (Monica) Henrichs, Denver, CO, and Michaela (Heath) Gaedtke, Ringle; four grandchildren: Howard Henrichs, Denver, and Genevieve, Gabrielle, and Larkin Gaedtke, Ringle; eight siblings: Mary (Bill) Koranda, New Berlin, Karen (Steve) Ostovich, Duluth, MN, Jerry (Linda) Murkowski, Mike Murkowski, Tom Murkowski, Myron (Cindy) Murkowski, all of Athens, Steve (Tina) Murkowski, Kiel, and Angie (Dean) Ellenbecker, Athens; and many other relatives and friends. She was preceded in death by her parents, Howard and Dorothy; and her sister Rita.

Family and friends are welcome to pay their respects at a visitation to be held on Friday, March 6, 2026, from 4:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Athens, and again on Saturday, March 7, from 10:00 a.m. at St. Anthony Catholic Church until the time of the service. A funeral Mass will be held in memory of Arlene at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, March 7, 2026, at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens. Rev. George Graham will preside. Private family burial will take place at a later date.

records.name: Frank J. Schuelke Jr.
records.summary: Frank J. Schuelke Jr., age 76, of Wausau, passed away on February 23, 2026.
records.body:
Frank proudly served his community as a Major with the Wausau Emergency Police, volunteering for over 40 years with dedication and pride. He was known as someone who could fix just about anything and was always willing to help a friend with a project.

He had a lifelong love of tractors and machinery repair, enjoyed watching NASCAR, working on puzzles, attending fish fries, and never missed a chance for all-you-can-eat boneless wings. Frank valued time with family and friends and was happiest when lending a hand.

He is survived by his brothers, David, Albert (Peggy), Scott (Jackie), Edward (Janine), Thomas (Maria); nieces, nephews and many other family and friends, with special recognition to his best friends, Linda, Larry, Bob, Dewey, Steve, Jeff and Olivia.

He was preceded in death by his parents.

A Funeral Service will be held 12:00 PM, on Thursday, March 5, 2026, at Helke Funeral Home, 302 Spruce St, Wausau, WI 54401. Michael Juedes will preside. Visitation will be held from 9:00 AM to the time of service, at the funeral home. Entombment will follow at Restlawn Memorial Gardens.

records.name: Verna Mae Schremp
records.summary: Verna Mae Schremp, age 94, of Mosinee, passed away on February 26, 2026.
records.body:
Verna Mae Schremp passed away peacefully with her family by her side, on Thursday, February 26, 2026, under the care of St. Croix Hospice.

Verna was born in Marathon, WI on May 9, 1931, daughter of the late Edward & Frieda (Loos) Lang. She was the first born of five children. Her family moved to the Antigo, WI area where she grew up attending St. Johns Elementary School, followed by her graduation from Antigo High School. While in high school Verna met the love of her life, Herb Schremp. She and Herb were married on December 28, 1949, at St. Johns Catholic Church in Antigo. They eventually moved to Mosinee, WI, where they raised their six children. Along with being a loving wife and mother, Verna also worked in healthcare at Aspirus Hospital until her retirement. She especially enjoyed caring for the elderly.

Her favorite past times included gardening and reading. Exploring new books at a bookstore was one of her favorite things to do. Verna took great pride in her children and her home and welcomed all who visited, including all the family pets. She has given all of her family so many wonderful memories of the many holiday gatherings we enjoyed together and are so blessed to have had.

Verna's faith was an important part of her life. She was a member of St. Paul Catholic Church for over 70 years and a member of St. Monica's Society. She and Herb also enjoyed many wonderful years of retirement together, especially with their many trips and time spent up north.

Verna is survived by two sisters, Evelyn Hoard and Ruth Kosteloc and her sister-in-law Bernice Lang. She is also survived by her children, Sandra (Tom Hecox) Jacobson, John (Mary) Schremp, Connie (Rory) McKellips, Bill (Kelly) Schremp. She is further survived by daughters-in-law Cheri Schremp and Karen Schremp Schinker. Vernas surviving grandchildren include Colleen Ostrowski, Brad Jacobson, Brent Jacobson, Kelly Schmidt, Kristy Hecox, Jennifer Schremp, Kristen Schremp, Kayln Schremp, Nick Schremp, Courtney Kuklinski, Melissa Holtz, Brooke Bargender, Greg Schremp, Jeff Schremp, Nick Thomas, Cory Jarocki, Eric Schremp and Alex Schremp. Verna was blessed with 31 great-grandchildren who gave her much joy, in addition to another little great-grandson arriving in Spring. Verna was also blessed with many nieces and nephews who she and her late husband Herb always enjoyed spending time with.

She was preceded in death by her husband Herb Schremp in 2021 after 71 years of marriage. Verna is also predeceased by her two sons, Kenneth Schremp and James Schremp; grandson, Ryan Schremp; and son-in-law, James Jacobson. She is further preceded in death by her brother Ralph Lang and her sister Elaine Artymiuk.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 10:30 a.m., on Friday, March 6, 2026, at St. Paul Catholic Church, Mosinee. Father Aaron Becker will officiate. Burial will take place in Mosinee Cemetery. Visitation will be held from 8:30 a.m., until the time of service, all at the church.

records.name: Barbara Joan Vignali
records.summary: Barbara Joan Vignali, 87, passed away in Wausau, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Barb was born March 24, 1938 in Wausau, WI to Eleanor and Charles Anderson and was later warmly welcomed and raised by her foster parents, Frank and Mabel Grisa. Barb went to her eternal glory on March 1, 2026 surrounded by the love of her family and a life well lived.

Barb graduated from Merrill High School, carrying with her the strong values of hard work, faith, and family that shaped her entire life. On June 6, 1959, Barb married her sweetheart, Richard Vignali in Merrill Wis. Together, they built a wonderful life filled with laughter and love, raising their family and working side by side on their Wausau-area farm. Their partnership was built on a never wavering faith in Jesus Christ, dedication to church and family. Over the years there were plenty of smiles, some tears and always a shared sense of adventure.

Barb had a zest for life and a variety of interests. She enjoyed crafting, gardening, and she found special joy in music-playing both the flute and the piano and singing. Her faith was at the heart of her days, and she was happily involved in her church community and proud to sing with the Wausau Insurance Choir. Barb loved spending time with her grandchildren and great grandchildren and was always behind the camera capturing their moments throughout the years.

Balancing work and home, Barb was not only a wonderful partner on the farm and a devoted mom but also worked as an overnight security agent at Wausau Insurance. In later years, Barb cared for several elderly people spending days and nights caring for their needs and sharing her love and her faith in God with them. A very special time of life for Barb and Richard was during the time they were involved at Willow Springs Garden in Wausau working in the kitchen, flower beds and setting up weddings, special occasions and family get togethers. It was at Willow Springs Gardens that a surprise 50th wedding anniversary was held for them. The love for Barb and Dick by family and friends was evident by the turn out of over 200 people attending. Many of those who came to celebrate were neighbors from the Village of Maine who had known them for several years.

Above all, Barb was an "all-in" mother and grandmother-always engaged, endlessly supportive, and immensely proud of her grandchildren and great grandchildren. Her warmth and love will be cherished for generations.

Barb leaves behind a family who adored her: her children, Mike Vignali and Jenne (Jeff) Martens; her grandchildren Kathleen (Marty) Vanderstappen, Daniel Vignali, Vincent Vignali, Andrea (Dylan) Fleischman, Ryan (Janel) Martens, Anastassia Martens, and Alyssa Martens; and her great-grandchildren Orion, Bradley, Theodore, and Eleanor Martens. Siblings, Alice Nichols, Sandy (Bill) Schultz, half-sister Gail Gore, Edith McNeely Price, and Verna Burton.

Barb was preceded in passing by her loving husband, Richard Vignali; her sisters Judy Seehafer and Mary Pagel; brothers Dick Anderson and Reverend Robert Warnke, foster parents Frank and Mabel Grisa, and foster siblings; Ruthie Grisa King, Mildred Rooney, Mark Eldon Grisa,

records.name: Brian D. Campbell
records.summary: Brian D. Campbell, 66, of Mosinee, Wisconsin, passed away.
records.body:
Brian D. Campbell, 66, of Mosinee, WI, passed away February 28, 2026, at his home from natural causes.

He is survived by his mother, Joan M. Campbell, and siblings: Edward Campbell, Sean Campbell, Dennis (Becky) Campbell, and Colleen Campbell, as well as nieces and one nephew.

He was preceded in death by his father, Roderick J. Campbell.

records.name: Edmund "Ed" Moriarity
records.summary: Edmund "Ed" Moriarity, 82, passed away in Wausau, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Edmund "Ed" Moriarity, 82, passed away on February 23, 2026, in Wausau, Wisconsin. He was born on August 10, 1943, in Eau Claire, Wisconsin.

Ed spent much of his life in the Eau Claire, Milwaukee, and Sun Prairie areas. He worked for AE Rogers and later Clark Lift, where he served as a manager. He was known for his strong work ethic, direct manner, and steady leadership, and he took pride in the work he did.

Ed is survived by his brother, Mike (Char) Moriarity; his daughter, Chris (Steve) Moriarity Zupek; his daughter, Cory (Amy Pemperin) Moriarity; his stepson, Greg (Eva) Allen; his stepdaughter, Amy (Jim) Anderson; and his stepdaughter-in-law, Heather Allen. He is also survived by his grandchildren: Laura (Steve) Sorenson, Alexander (Beth) Zupek, Mykel Moriarity, Claire Allen, Liam Allen, Abby (Cory) Kramshuster, Andrew (Sarah) Fabris, Anna (Adam) Fischer, and Mathew Anderson, as well as 12 great-grandchildren.

He was preceded in death by his wife, Maureen Moriarity; his son, Eddie Moriarity; his stepson, Buddy Allen; his mother, Colleen Arnsdorf Moriarity; his father, Edmund Moriarity; and both sets of grandparents.

Ed had a long-standing interest in cars and spent many hours working on and talking about them. He enjoyed fishing, golfing, and spending time outdoors. He and Maureen traveled over the years and spent time scuba diving, experiences he appreciated.

Ed cared about his family and took pride in their accomplishments. He lived life on his own terms and leaves behind a large family who will remember him in their own ways.

records.name: Henrietta B. O'Brien
records.summary: Henrietta B. O'Brien, 89, passed away in Schofield, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Henrietta B. O'Brien, 89, Schofield passed away peacefully on Saturday, February 28, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

She was born October 20, 1936 in Mineral Point, daughter of the late John and Catherine (Schweitzer) Mick.

For many years Henrietta worked at Federal Mogul in Schofield. She also was a waitress at Gulliver's in Rib Mountain for many years and enjoyed working with the people and staff there.

Some of her favorite pastimes included spending summers on Lake Tomahawk, crocheting, getting into Packer pools, taking an occasional trip to the casino, bowling and spending time with the girl's birthday group.

Survivors include her children, Mary Clark, Texas, Randy (Teri) O'Brien, Arizona and Jeff O'Brien, Schofield, three grandchildren, Ryan (Alyssa) Clark, Cory O'Brien and Nicole Slowikowski, four great grandchildren Siara, Aurora, Kyan and Andriella and numerous nieces and nephews. Henrietta is further survived by her sisters-in-law, Eileen Mick and Joanne Mick, a brother-in-law, Patrick "Gary" (Betty) O'Brien and her dear and special friends, Michael and Jill Nezworski, affectionately known as "Moms Earth Angels" by family.

Besides her parents she was preceded in death by her former husband, Francis and numerous siblings.

records.name: James "Jim" Kernosky
records.summary: James "Jim" Kernosky, 99, passed away in Wausau, Wisconsin.
records.body:
James "Jim" Kernosky 99, passed away in Wausau, Wisconsin on February 26, 2026, at Aspirus Hospice House. Jim was born on December 30, 1926, in Grayling, Michigan to Joseph and Marie (Endricks) Kiersnowski. Jim attended Grayling High School and dropped out at the age of 17 to serve his county in the US Army during World War II, serving with the US Army in the 97th Infantry Division as a Private First Class. He took great pride his military service. After working several jobs in the Detroit area auto industry, Jim decided to finish his high school education and enrolled in Michigan State where he completed an accounting degree. He was employed for 25 years as a field auditor for Wausau Insurance. After his retirement, he worked part-time at Kinzie & Green for 25 years.

Jim married his wife Marilyn (Rock) in 1962 and together they lovingly raised twin boys, Peter and Paul and a daughter, Ann in the Riverview neighborhood of Wausau. Jim was a long-time member of the YMCA and enjoyed running. At the age of 58 he decided to try lap swimming. He swam a mile most days up until his 90th birthday. Jim also loved spending winters at his condo in Peoria, Arizona and continued to spend time there through the age of 97 when the trips became difficult for him. With the help of his son Pete, he was able to live very independently up until a week before his 99th birthday.

Jim had a huge heart and the ability to form an instant connection with almost everyone he met, and he became a good friend to many. His sharp mind, playful attitude, sense of humor, and love of life stayed with him up until his time of passing. Jim enjoyed watching all sports and reading; he particularly enjoyed World War II History and John Grisham novels.

His love and heart live on through his daughter Ann Schwieger (Ted), seven grandchildren and seven great grandchildren. He was proceeded in death by his wife Marilyn (2014) and his sons Paul (2017) and Pete (2025).

records.name: Marcella R. Hargraves
records.summary: Marcella R. Hargraves, 91, passed away in Wausau, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Marcella R. Hargraves, 91, Wausau, passed away peacefully on Tuesday, March 3, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

She was born June 23, 1934 in Gaylord, MN, daughter of the late Elmer and Sophia (Stencil) Kaldunski. On August 14, 1952, she married Marvin Hargraves at St. Anthony's Rectory in Athens, WI. He preceded her in death on July 20, 2014.

She was employed at the former 29 Super Market in the bakery department where she retired after 28 years of employment. She then enjoyed doing lots of baking at home for her family, especially at Christmas, making all kinds of cookies and different kinds of sweet breads. She also enjoyed decorating her home inside for all the holidays, especially for Christmas.

Marcella was a longtime member of St. Anne Catholic Church, where she was an active volunteer at many different functions, among them was a Eucharistic Minister, Usher, Liturgy Assistant and taught Faith Formation Class for a few years. She also worked at St. Anne's famous fish fries and St. Anne's fest. She also volunteered for many years at Aspirus Wausau Hospital for the Teddy Bear Clinic, also was Mrs. Claus for the volunteer's Christmas Tea at the hospital and Mrs. Claus for the Festival of Trees for over 20 years. Marcella and Marvin enjoyed trips to Germany, Florida, Michigan, Texas and Missouri.

Marcella is survived by her children, Michael (Cathleen) Hargraves, Weston, Marie Strasser, Barbara Merck and Sarah Hargraves, all of Wausau; son-in-law, Mark Peters, Georgia; six grandchildren, Andrea (Len) Moon, Georgia, Elizabeth (Richard) Higgins, Sheboygan, Erik Peters, Fitchburg, Jason (Becca) Hargraves, Merrill, Michelle (Ken) Kaminski, Hatley and Amy (Adam) Frei, Mosinee; nine great-grandchildren, Matthew, Marly, Emma, Bobbi, Brittany, Leeland, Alexandra, Theodore and John Paul; sister-in-law, Donna Kaldunski, Rib Falls; and many nieces and nephews.

Marcella was preceded in death by her parents, husband, Marvin; one daughter, Mary Jane Peters; one son-in-law, Larry Strasser; two sisters, Rosella Adams and Marjorie Diers; two brothers, Elmer and Daniel Kaldunski; one sister-in-law and two brothers-in-law.

records.name: Marlene June Bremer
records.summary: Marlene June Bremer, 85, of Medford, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully.
records.body:
Marlene June Bremer, 85, of Medford, passed away peacefully on February 26, 2026, at The House of the Dove under the care of Compassus Hospice in Marshfield, Wisconsin.

Marlene was born on June 14, 1940, in Medford, Wisconsin to Hilbert and Alda (Clausnitzer) Frey. After spending her early years in Medford, Marlene married Ron Bremer in 1959. Together, they shared nearly 65 years of marriage until his passing on January 6, 2024.

As a proud military wife, Marlene traveled with Ron during his years of service, living in Okinawa, Japan; Fort Bragg, North Carolina; Marinette, Wisconsin and Fort Benning, Georgia. Marlene and her daughters returned to Medford during 2 of Ron's 3 tours in Vietnam. After Ron's retirement the couple returned to Medford. Marlene worked as an administrative assistant for Enerquip, Prochnow Trucking and Hurd Millwork until her retirement in 2005.

Marlene's passion for life extended to many hobbies. She was an active member of the Twisted Threads Quilt group creating award-winning quilts, sang in the choir and played handbells at Immanuel Lutheran Church. She often sang and played guitar as part of a duo with Carey Hartwig for 16 years at local care and resident facilities. She also bowled with the women's league for many years. She and Ron loved country dancing and were members of the Central Wisconsin Country Dancers for 27 years. Additionally, Marlene was very dedicated to her church where she served on the finance committee, attended to fellowship and helped serve for events. She enjoyed skiing, hiking, gardening, painting, and biking with Ron, and walking the river walk in Medford. Her and Ron were snowbirds, traveling to Texas for 13 years in their camper, also traveling to many other places throughout the US.

Marlene is survived by her daughters, Susan Bremer (Geoff Eaton) of Dorset, England and Stephanie (Ron) Hayton of Medford. She is further survived by her siblings, Marvin (Dorothy) Frey, Mark (Patricia) Frey, Michael (Gloria) Frey, Marshall (Anne) Frey, and Marvel Bakri, along with their children, grandchildren, and many other relatives from both the Frey and Bremer families. She is also survived by her sister-in-law, Avis Lebiecki.

She was preceded in death by her beloved husband, Ron Bremer, and her parents, Hilbert and Alda Frey.

records.name: Nancy J. DeHaven
records.summary: Nancy J. DeHaven, 75, passed away in Wausau, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Nancy J. DeHaven, 75, Wausau, passed away peacefully on Friday, February 27, 2026 at Rennes Health and Rehab Center in Weston, under the care of Aspirus at Home Hospice.

She was born on July 24, 1950 in Wausau to Arnold and Marie (Newman) Pergalski. Nancy worked as an administrative assistant for many insurance companies and banks in the area. Among her favorite pastimes, she loved to watch TV, especially game shows.

Survivors include her son, Greg DeHaven; sister, Carol (Duane) Gau; three grandchildren, Vince, Vanessa and Valerie; her great-grandchildren; nieces, Julie Perry, Kim Pergalski and Tina Kleist; nephew, Kevin Pergalski; and sister-in-law, Doreen Pergalski.

She was preceded in death by her parents; Arnold and Marie; brother, James Pergalski; and sister, Barbiera Pergalski.

records.name: Samson V. Thao
records.summary: Samson V. Thao, 75, passed away in Wausau, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Samson V. Thao, age 75, passed away on February 22, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born on June 15, 1950, in Laos to Yong Thao and Shoua Her.

A devoted man of faith, Samson served as a pastor in California in the early 1990's and remained strong in his faith in God throughout his life.

He was a loving husband, father, and grandfather who cherished his family.

Samson was preceded in death by his wife, Ruth X. Thao, and two sons, Kong and Seth.

He is survived by his children: Panhia, Long, Xeng, Sher, Chang, Vang, Chua, Zer, Lei, Susan, Tang, and Malinda.

records.name: Sharon Kay Philipp
records.summary: Sharon Kay Philipp, 87, of Wittenberg, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully.
records.body:
Sharon Kay Philipp, 87 of Wittenberg, passed away on Sunday, March 1, 2026, at Rennes Rehab Center. Sharon was born in Elderon on February 25, 1939, the daughter of Arthur and Merna (Kaufman) Carlson. On April 25, 1959, Sharon married John Philipp and they raised three boys together, Gregory, Jeffrey and Steven. John loved telling stories of the days that he and Sharon were in grade school, and he liked to play jokes on her and pulled her pig tails. He said he knew one day he would marry her and he did. They were happily married for 59 years until John passed away on September 5, 2018.

Following graduation from Birnamwood High School, Sharon worked at Employers Mutual Insurance of Wausau. She left her employment to devote her time to raising the three boys and was a loving mother. While John worked at FWD Seagrave in Clintonville, Sharon was a partner in their two businesses, Philipps Greenhouse and the Island Bar, which they operated for many years. They had fond memories of their customers and formed many close friendships.

After closing those businesses, they cared for a number of foster children for Lutheran Social Services, while John continued working at FWD Seagrave and in 1979, Sharon then went to work for the Wittenberg Birnamwood School District Support Staff for 21 years until her retirement. She loved working with her co-workers and hearing about the exploits of the school children.

Sharon enjoyed camping, snowmobiling, attending Country Hodag music festivals, listening to country music and gardening. What she enjoyed most was spending time with her children, grandchildren and extended family gatherings.

Sharon will be truly missed by her family and friends.

Sharon is survived by her children, Gregory Philipp, Jeffrey (Sharla) Philipp and Steven Philipp; six grandchildren: Jonathon (fiancée Rachel Willems) Philipp, Lisa (Josh) Mulder, Carrie (Jay) Meydam, Nicole (fiancé Devyn Raab) Philipp, Amber (Jordan) Hull and Hailey (Shannon) Fandrey; great grandchildren: Aaron, Josiah, Samuel and Arthur Mulder, Ayden and Issac Meydam, Maddelyn, Levi and Landon Hull and Emersyn Fandrey, and was able to welcome her newest great granddaughter, Willow Kay Raab, born on 2-19-26, and another great grandchild (Fandrey) on the way. Also survived by two sisters, Donna (Stan) Stankowski and Tina Carlson; four brothers, Melvin (Janet) Carlson, Randy (Joanne Arts) Carlson, Rick (Laura) Carlson and Ron (Vicki) Carlson. Sharon is further survived by John's sisters; Rosemary Fierek, Ann Weller, Janice Marquardt, Jo Ann Winkler and a brother, Michael Philipp and many nieces and nephews.

Sharon was preceded in death by her parents, Arthur and Merna, her husband John, one brother Doug Carlson and sister-in-law Chris Carlson; John's sisters; Kay Gee, Mary Staszak, Alice Herrman, Michelle Ebel, Jean Osflag, Barbara Pozorski and Lucille Hinkfuss and Johns parents, Michael Sr. and Anna Philipp.

The family would like to thank Ann Mesich, Wittenberg, for the thoughtful and loving home care that she gave Sharon during her illness and taking her to doctor appointments, and neighbors, Bob Steigerwald, for snow blowing Sharons driveway, and Jenny and Wade Schram, for watching over Sharon's property. Also, to Aspirus Wausau Hospital and Rennes Hospice for their care and support.

records.name: Theresa Ann Helgeson
records.summary: Theresa Ann Helgeson, 71, passed away in Wausau, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Theresa Ann Helgeson, 71, passed away peacefully on March 1, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital, surrounded by the love of her family.

Born on October 13, 1954, in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, Theresa was the beloved daughter of the late Orley J. Laufenberg and Madeline (Belt) Laufenberg. From an early age, Theresa carried a caring spirit that would define her life's work and the way she loved those around her.

Theresa dedicated her career to nursing, a profession that perfectly reflected her generous heart and sharp mind. She worked in emergency medicine, geriatrics, pediatrics, and everything in between. She had a gift for caring for others—always determined to figure out what was wrong and how she could help fix it. She was direct, honest, and strong; you never had to wonder what Theresa was thinking. Her patients, colleagues, and loved ones all knew they were in capable, compassionate hands.

On February 14, 1981, she married the love of her life, Dennis Helgeson—ensuring, as she would joke, that he would never forget their anniversary. Their love story spanned 50 beautiful years total. Forty-three years ago, Dennis took a city girl and brought her to the country, where Theresa discovered her true peace. When Dennis bought her a small horse ranch, it became her sanctuary. There, surrounded by open skies and the steady rhythm of hooves, she found her center. She loved riding her Buckskin and her Paint, feeling the world settle into place with each ride, and always being entertained by her "silly" draft horse. Even in the past decade, when physical challenges made life more difficult, her family lovingly helped her into the saddle—where she would once again feel like herself and could do anything.

Above all, Theresa treasured her role as "Oma." She was devoted to her children and grandchildren, attending every event and cheering them on with pride. She baked beautiful birthday cakes and special occasion cakes, pouring her creativity and love into each one. Just this past year, she carefully baked and decorated a stunning cake for her and Dennis's 45th wedding anniversary—determined, as always, to make it perfect.

Theresa also found joy in tending her flower gardens, filling her yard with color and beauty. Through her gardening, arts and crafts, baking, nursing, and riding, she created beauty and comfort wherever she went.

She is lovingly survived by her devoted husband, Dennis; her children, Denelle Helgeson and Cody (Sara) Helgeson; her cherished grandchildren, Dakota and Lillymae; her siblings, James (Holly) Laufenberg and Barb (Kevin) Laufenberg; and many extended family members and friends who will miss her deeply.

In addition to her parents, Theresa was preceded in death by her 17-month-old son, Derek, and a baby lost to miscarriage—both held forever in her heart.

Theresa's legacy is one of strength, devotion, and unwavering love. She cared fiercely, spoke honestly, loved deeply, and lived fully. Her family finds comfort knowing she is at peace, perhaps riding freely once again.

records.name: Yuying B. Lo
records.summary: Yuying B. Lo, 22, passed away in Wausau, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Yuying B. Lo, a remarkable and one-of-a-kind person with a pure heart and pure soul, whose vibrant spirit radiated warmth and compassion to everyone around him, passed away peacefully on Thursday, February 26, 2026, at Wausau Manor, surrounded by his loving family.

Yuying was born on March 24, 2003, in Appleton, Wisconsin. The son of Shwa Lo and Mai Thor. He is leaving behind a profound legacy etched in the hearts of his family and friends. From an early age, Yuying nurtured a fervent dream of becoming a firefighter, a testament to his courageous heart and desire to serve his community. His journey began at Wausau East High School, where he not only excelled academically but also cultivated a rich array of interests and friendships. His natural exuberance made him a beloved figure among his peers, where he was celebrated for being caring, fun, and outgoing.

In addition to his academic pursuits, Yuying found solace in various hobbies that complemented his zest for life. His love for Pho, a favorite dish, often brought joy to the dinner table, where laughter resonated in the company of family. Fishing with his grandpa and uncles was another cherished pastime, allowing him to forge meaningful memories that would last a lifetime. Music infused his life with rhythm and joy, while the color blue brought a sense of tranquility to his vibrant existence. Above all, Yuying was a devoted family member, embracing the role of the second oldest sibling with great pride. He stood as the first man of the house, a guardian figure who helped raise his brothers and sisters. Yuying dedicated himself to ensuring their well-being and happiness, always willing to lend a helping hand. His boundless support kept his family united, and his unwavering presence was a source of strength for those around him.

An active member of the Church of Latter-Day Saints, Yuying's faith in God shaped his character, inspiring him to volunteer tirelessly within his community. His commitment to service revealed the depth of his compassion. Yuying's nurturing spirit and genuine kindness touched lives beyond his immediate family, making indelible marks in the hearts of those fortunate enough to know him.

Yuying is survived by his loving mother, Mai Thor, his siblings, Shaina, Nai, Summer, Kai, and Selena, as well as Eli. His absence is keenly felt, yet his memories and the love he fostered will forever resonate in the lives he has impacted.

Though his time on this earth was fleeting, Yuying's legacy of love, service, and dedication to family remains a testament to the extraordinary individual he was. May his spirit continue to inspire those he left behind to embody the same caring nature and commitment to community that he so passionately demonstrated throughout his life.

records.name: Ardith May Parsells
records.summary: Ardith May Parsells, 82, of Wausau passed away on March 3, 2026.
records.body:
Ardith May Parsells, passed away peacefully on Tuesday, March 3, surrounded by the love of her family.

Born on December 10, 1943 in Antigo, WI she was the daughter of Alfred and Edna Rotell. On July 30, 1961, she married the love of her life, Richard Parsells, and together they built a life centered on family, kindness, and unwavering support for those around them.

Ardith will be remembered most for her deep love of family and the special role she played as a grandmother and great-grandmother. Her home was a place where everyone felt welcome—often filled with the comforting smell of homemade bread, stories from years past, and the sound of laughter. She took great joy in traveling to new places, particularly to watch her grandchildren's hockey games, soccer matches, cheer competitions and music concerts, always fully invested as their biggest supporter.

Throughout her life, she worked at UMR in various positions, where she was known for her hard-work and humor. Outside of work, she enjoyed baking, trips to the casino, and most of all time spent with her family. Whether she was sharing family recipes, or offering words of encouragement, she had a quiet way of making others feel cared for and valued.

She is survived by her loving children, Penny Parsells, Greg Parsells (Shelly), and Pam Waldinger (Shawn); her cherished grandchildren, Molly Parsells, Sarah Parsells, Adam Parsells (Jaedyn), Brayden Waldinger and Jordyn Waldinger; and her great-grandchildren Acker, Pax, and Bosco Parsells.

She was preceded in death by her beloved parents, her husband, her siblings Jo-Ellen Weber and George Rotell; and her daughter in law Shelly.

A small family memorial service will be held on March 13, from 10-12 at Brainard Funeral Home in Wausau. Family and friends are invited to gather to celebrate a life filled with love and devotion.

In lieu of flowers, the family asks that memorial contributions be made to K-tech Charities: https://k-techkleening.com/k-tech-charities/

Though she is deeply missed, her family finds comfort in the many memories she leaves behind and in the love she shared so freely with all who knew her.

records.name: Bonnie K. Bernarde
records.summary: Bonnie K. Bernarde, 57, of Birnamwood passed away on March 2, 2026.
records.body:
Bonnie Kay Bernarde, 57, of Birnamwood, passed away on Monday, March 2, 2026, at Aspirus Hospital in Wausau, where she was welcomed into heaven and reunited with her father.

Bonnie was born on March 19, 1968, and lived her life with a vibrant spirit, a generous heart, and a deep love for her family. She was united in marriage to the love of her life, Jon Bernarde, and together they built a life full of love, laughter, and family. Bonnie was a proud and devoted mother who raised two wonderful sons, and nothing brought her greater joy than watching them grow.

For most of her adult life, Bonnie worked as a Certified Nursing Assistant, dedicating herself to caring for others with compassion and kindness. Her nurturing spirit extended far beyond her profession — it was simply who she was.

Bonnie embraced life fully. She loved snowmobiling, riding UTVs while jamming to her favorite music, and she was a huge Sasquatch believer. Yet above all else, Bonnie treasured time spent with her family. Simply enjoying everyday moments together, those were the times she cherished most.

Bonnie will be deeply missed by her loving husband of 27 years, Jon Bernarde; her two sons, Keegan (Randi) Bernarde of Birnamwood and Keenan (Codi) Bernarde of Birnamwood; and her beloved grandchildren, Kezmai, Paisliee, and Grainger of Birnamwood. She is further survived by her mother, Elsie Matz of Birnamwood; her brothers, Rick Matz of Ringle and John (Shirley) Matz of Goodman; her sisters, Debbie (Doug) Koeller of Birnamwood, Phyllis (Jerry) Clauss of Birnamwood, and Brenda (Manl) Malueg of Antigo; as well as many cherished nieces and nephews.

She was preceded in death by her father, Eugene Matz; her mother-in-law, Betty Bernarde; her father-in-law, David Bernarde; her niece, Melissa Matz; and one precious child lost to miscarriage.

A Celebration of Life honoring Bonnie will be held on Saturday, March 14, 2026, at 4:00 p.m. at Tangle Horn Bar in Eland, Wisconsin. Family and friends are invited to gather to share stories, memories, and laughter in remembrance of a woman whose love and light will continue to shine in the hearts of all who knew her.

Those who wish to share memories or photographs are warmly encouraged to bring them to the celebration.

Bonnie's legacy lives on in the family she adored, the lives she touched through her kindness and the joy she brought into every room she entered. She will be forever loved and never forgotten.

Family and friends may go to MWCS.WS to leave words of remembrance.

records.name: Doris A. Heil
records.summary: Doris A. Heil, 90, of Village of Rib Mountain passed away on March 3, 2026.
records.body:
Doris A Heil 90, Village of Rib Mountain, died Tuesday March 3, 2026 at Dimensions Living, with her loved ones at her side. She was born March 14, 1935, in Marathon WI, daughter of the late Aloys and Frances (Hornung) Lemmer. On May 12, 1956, she married Conrad Heil, her high school sweet heart, at St. Mary Catholic Church in Marathon WI. Conrad preceded her in death December 12, 2024, they were married 68 years.

Doris had a short career at Marathon Electric, then began her true joy, starting a family. Their six kids brought her happiness and delight.

Doris was instrumental in caring for their farm, animals, garden, many experiments with flowers, fruit trees and her huge vegetable garden. Her flower beds were spectacular. She always had flowers, berries and veggies to share with beloved family and friends. The kids were helpful with all the farm chores. And in summer after we finished weeding our rows, or bailing hay, we were treated with a swim at the gravel pits.

Doris always had a kind and heart warming word for everyone she met. She was active as room mother and field trip chaperone for all her kids at Holy Name. She continued her service at Holy Name with a variety of activities including Summer Fun and giving communion to the elders of the parish.

Doris was blessed with an extra special bonus of many grandchildren. She adored each of them and spent as much time with them as she could. Grandma was always ready to play badminton, croquet, fishing game, piano, cards, and board games. Also the annual Christmas performance with angle wings for all the grands and Grandma. Each and all were truly the light of her life.

Survived by five children Jeff (Judy Wenckaitis) Heil, Linda (Jeff) Olcott, Theresa Becker, Bob (Dodi Grulkoski) Heil, all of Rib Mountain, WI. Jim (Olga Kireeva) Heil, of Lake Jackson, TX. Also daughter in law Debra (Gary) Heil. And 12 grandkids, and 13 great grandkids. Surviving siblings James (Karla) Lemmer, Athens, Barbara (Robert) Murkowski, Edgar, and George Lemmer, Wausau. Preceded in death by son Gary W Heil.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated 11:00 a.m. Friday March 13th, 2026 at Holy Name of Jesus Catholic Church, Wausau, WI. Father Woodrow H Pace will preside. Visitation will be Friday at 10:00 a.m. until the time of services, burial at 12:30 at Rest Lawn and lunch at Holy Name at 1:00.

records.name: Gary L. Buch
records.summary: Gary L. Buch, 76, passed away on February 28, 2026.
records.body:
Gary Lee Buch, 76, passed away surrounded by the love of his family on Saturday, February 28, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born on December 10, 1949, to the late Earl and Arlene (Koenig) Buch. At the end of Gary's working life, he worked as a Project Engineer for Jacobs Engineering. His time with the company took him on many great travels, including a trip to France.

Gary was a proud father, grandfather, great-grandfather, brother, and friend whose warmth and humor left a lasting impression on everyone he met. He was the devoted father of Cara and Chad. Nothing brought him greater pride than his family. He cherished his role as Grandpa to Nicholas Gestner, Caitlynn, and Alyssa, and Great-Grandpa to Colbie, Ian, Alina and Brayden. His loving, endearing smile made everyone feel like they were the center of his world.

Gary shared a special bond with his son, Chad, especially over the game of pool. He loved playing and took great pride in teaching Chad how to handle a cue stick, passing along not just skill but time, laughter, and memories that will last forever.

A loyal fan through and through, Gary cheered faithfully for the Green Bay Packers and the Milwaukee Brewers. Whether watching a game at home or talking sports with friends, he was always ready with commentary, enthusiasm, and plenty of spirited debate.

Gary was truly a social champion. He loved striking up conversations, telling jokes, and making people smile. His humor and easygoing nature drew others in, and his presence could brighten any room. He also enjoyed simple pleasures—going for walks, golfing, spending time outdoors, and connecting with friends and neighbors along the way.

One of Gary's greatest passions was sports cars. He loved everything about them—looking at them, talking about them, reading about them, and especially driving them. He was especially proud of his 280Z, which brought him immense joy and countless stories. For Gary, a drive wasn't just a trip; it was an experience.

He is survived by his children, Cara (Tim) Gestner and Chad Buch; his grandchildren, Nicholas Gestner, Caitlynn (Justin) Major, and Alyssa Buch; his great-grandchildren Jacey Major, Colbie and Ian Gestner, and Alina and Brayden Hernandez; his sister, Debbie Buch; and his sister-in-law, Debbie Buch.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Earl and Arlene Buch, and his brother, Darrel.

Gary will be remembered for his humor, his love of family, his passion for cars and sports, and the way his smile made everyone feel special. His legacy lives on in the laughter he inspired, the lessons he shared, and the deep love he had for his family. He will be dearly missed and forever cherished.

A funeral service will be held at 2:00 pm on Friday, March 13, 2026, at John J Buettgen Funeral Home, 948 Grand Ave, Schofield. Visitation will take place from 12:00 pm until the time of services at the funeral home. Julie Eder will officiate. Burial will take place in the Stettin Christian Cemetery.

Family and friends may go to www.honorone.com to leave words of remembrance.

records.name: Vickie L. Ginsburg
records.summary: Vickie L. Ginsburg, 74, of Wausau passed away on March 2, 2026.
records.body:
Vickie Ginsburg of Wausau, WI, age 74, passed away on March 2, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital surrounded by her loving family, after battling health issues for several years. She was born on September 17, 1951 in Danville, Virginia, the daughter of Robert and Dorothy (Bonton) Pelz. Vickie was the eldest of five children.

She was married for twenty-five years to Ronald J. Knetter in Edgar, WI.

Vickie was a fierce and devoted wife, mother, stepmother, sister, Nana, and Great Nana whose love for her family knew no bounds. Her family was her greatest strength and her proudest joy. She cared deeply for those she loved, and her happiest moments were always spent surrounded by family – laughing, sharing stories, and creating memories that will be cherished forever, especially all of us dancing in the kitchen.

On July 22, 2007, she married Steven Ginsburg of Wausau, WI, resulting for both of them in a beautifully happy and successful second marriage.

In her earlier years, Vickie was an active and engaged member of her community, generously giving her time and talents to various organizations. Her dedication and strong work ethic carried throughout her life. After remarrying and moving to Wausau, she continued her successful career as a pharmacy technician, a role she held with pride and commitment until her retirement.

She was preceded in death by her parents.

She is survived by her husband Steven, her children Christina (Travis) Dobizl and April (Tyler) Smith, step-children Lisa (Tim) Wiencek and David Ginsburg, grandchildren Mariah (Eric Myszka) Kohl, Rebecca (Geoff Framke) Kohl, Christopher Kohl, and Connor Smith, step-granddaughter Luna Wiencek, step-grandchildren Shannon (Jodi Jean LaForge) Devine, CJ (Jackii) Smith, Austin Paszek, Rylee (Dustin) Studinski, Ethan Dobizl, great grandson Kolton Myszka, step-great granddaughters Edyn Smith, Maisie and Chesney Framke, and Calliope Studinski, brother Wayne (Cheryl) Pelz, sister Deborah (Curtis) Boyd, and brothers Robert (Virginia) Pelz and Brian Pelz, along with many nieces and nephews.

Vickie will be remembered for her strength, loyalty, and unwavering devotion to her family. Her legacy lives on in the lives she touched, the memories she created, and the love she gave so freely. She will be deeply missed and forever held in the hearts of those who knew and loved her.

Great Thanks go to Aspirus Wausau Hospital and their wonderful staff for taking such great care of her and all of us during her final hospitalization.

A Memorial Funeral Service will be held 11:00 AM, on Monday March 16, 2026, at Helke Funeral Home, 302 Spruce St, Wausau, WI 54401, led by Tyler Smith. Visitation will be held from 10:00 AM to the time of service at the funeral home.

records.name: Jon W. Kumbera
records.summary: Jon W. Kumbera, 68, of Wausau, passed away March 4, 2026.
records.body:
Jon W. Kumbera, 68, Wausau, passed away Wednesday, March 4, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born October 24, 1957, in Wausau, son of the late Eugene and Angeline (Boerke) Kumbera. On July 30, 1983, he married Kathryn Kayhart in Wausau.

Jon worked in sales and was known for his strong work ethic and gift of gab. In his free time, he enjoyed fishing, hunting, working in the yard and watching the birds. Jon was very handy and took great pride in building his family home with the help of relatives. He loved woodworking and collecting antiques, always appreciating craftsmanship and history. When his sons were young, Jon rarely missed their soccer games, track meets, and other sporting events, cheering them on with pride. He was also a devoted fan of the Milwaukee Brewers, Green Bay Packers and the Wisconsin Badgers.

Survivors include his wife, Kathy Kumbera; sons, Kyle and Jared Kumbera; sisters, Mary Jo Tilton and Jeanne Coppins; brother-in-law, Tom Kayhart; uncle, Robert Kumbera; numerous nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents and father and mother-in-law.

Visitation will be Friday, March 13, 2026, from 4:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau.

The family wishes to extend their sincere appreciation to the staff at Aspirus Wausau Hospital for their kind, compassionate care given to Jon.

records.name: Kenneth H. Lang
records.summary: Kenneth H. Lang, 77, passed away March 4, 2026.
records.body:
Kenneth H. Lang passed away at the age of 77 on March 4, 2026.

Ken was who you called first when something broke, wouldn't start, trees needed chopping, or you didn't know what to do. Armed with his trademark impatience, creative genius, and competitive streak, he approached every problem with an intent to win. Sometimes his tinkering resulted in designing and building the biggest wood splitter in the area, and other times it knocked out power to an entire city block, which was most likely followed with his trusty phrase "well dammit."

He was born on August 8, 1948, to Rosemary and Hugo Lang. He grew up on the family farm in Cassel and attended school in Marathon. Ken served in the U.S. Army during the Vietnam War repairing airplanes. He later attended Blackhawk Technical School in Janesville where he met Kathy. They married in 1972, settled near Poniatowski, and created a life filled with devotion and good-natured teasing. After his service, he worked at Murray Machinery and JARP Industries as a Maintenance Electrician. Since he was never able to sit still, he also grew ginseng with his family for many years. Nights and weekends in the garage and ginseng garden gave his kids life-long lessons about hard work, determination, and essential life skills.

In his later years, dementia stole his words. Slowly the word "grass" took the place of any word he couldn't speak, creating unforgettable moments. Conversations during this time were hard (and at times may have sounded illegal) but that made his stubbornness, smart-aleck smile, and laugh so very precious.

Ken's lifelong friendship with Gene and Carol, later joined by Kathy, brought decades of laughter and treasured visits, even through his illness, and it meant the world to him, for which the family is deeply grateful.

Ken leaves behind the love of his life and wife of 53 years, Kathy; their four children, Kris (Dale), Carl, Karen (Matt), and Diane (Brad)—who will carry on his unique sense of humor, curiosity, and his love of being right. He is also survived by his siblings Robert (Colette), Ronald (Pam), Judy (Knicker), Cheryl (Mark), Sandy (Todd), Debbie (Bruce), and Laurie (Greg), and was a proud grandfather to Brandon (Jess), Emily (Max), Irie (Grant), Dylan, Trevor, Braxton, and Alex, and great-grandfather to Lillian.

The family would like to thank the staff at Dimensions Living in Rib Mountain and Interim HealthCare for their loving care and kindness—no matter how spicy Ken got. They made the darkest days easier and helped both Ken and a fiercely independent family learn to accept help.

A visitation will be held on Wednesday, March 11, 2026, from 4:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Avenue and again on Thursday, March 12, from 10:00 a.m. at St. Anne Catholic Church, Wausau, until the time of the service. A funeral Mass will be held at 11:00 a.m., Rev. Tom Lindner will preside. Burial with military honors provided by American Legion Alois Dreikosen Post 469 at St. Mary's Catholic Cemetery, Marathon, will follow.

In lieu of flowers, the family asks that you bring a copy of your best Kenny story, donate to a memory care organization, or a gift card to benefit the staff and friends he made at Dimensions Living.

records.name: Thomas J. Evjue
records.summary: Thomas J. Evjue, 76, passed away March 5, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas (Tom) Evjue passed away at Stone Crest Residence in Wausau on March 5, 2026.

Tom was born on June 24, 1949, in Madison to Douglas and Arleen Ringle Evjue. He graduated from Wausau High School where he enjoyed being in the band as well as playing drums for the jazz ensemble, The Top Hatters. He graduated from the University of Wisconsin Madison, earning a bachelor's degree in education.

Tom spent much of his adult life in Florida where he worked as a Science and Math teacher. He had a passion for teaching and was proud of the students whose lives he touched throughout his career. While in Florida he married Stephanie Leach where they shared many years together until her passing in 2010.

Several years ago, Tom returned to Wausau to help care for his mother. During his life, he enjoyed fishing, reading, cooking, working with exotic animals and spending time in nature.

He dearly loved his chihuahuas throughout the years : Chance, Spike, Tila and Stoney. He also enjoyed keeping up with the lives and accomplishments of his nieces and nephews.

Tom is survived by his sisters Lynn Cupelli of Madison and Cathy (Lynn) Taschler along with his nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents, his wife Stephanie, his brother Robert (Bob) Evjue and his sister- in- law, Karen (Eggebrecht) Evjue.

A private family burial will be held at Pine Grove Cemetery.

The family would like to extend their heartfelt gratitude to the staff at The Gardens and Stone Crest, as well as Interim Health Care Hospice, for their kindness, support, and compassionate care.

records.name: Todd Alan Johnson
records.summary: Todd Alan Johnson, 58, of Rib Mountain, WI, died March 5, 2026.
records.body:
Todd Alan Johnson, 58, of Rib Mountain, WI, died Thursday, March 5, 2026 at Aspirus Hospital in Wausau, WI, during his third battle with cancer.

He was born August 22, 1967, in Sherman, TX, son of William Howard and Rose Ellen (Kruit) Johnson. Todd graduated from Wausau West High School in 1985 and earned his Mechanical Design degree from Northcentral Technical College in Wausau in 1987.

After graduation he worked as a design engineer at Oshkosh Truck Company until 1994, then served as a process engineer and process engineering supervisor with A?&?B Process Systems in Stratford, WI, through 2019. From 2019 to 2020 he was a senior process engineer for CRB in Marshfield, WI, before joining Membrane Process & Control as a senior process engineer and project manager. In 2025, he worked as a senior project manager with Complete Filtration Resource in Marshfield, WI, until his passing.

He married Lynne Hoffmann at Holy Name of Jesus Parish in Wausau on June 10, 1995.

Todd's true passion was engineering. He thought of his job as more than work but a way to show his talents of creativity. Even when not working, he often spent his time creating items around the house. This included a greenhouse and, in the days before his death, tip?downs for ice fishing. He further enjoyed every second he could in the woods and on the water. He had countless stories of times spent fishing with his father-in-law, father, sons, high school friends, and his coworkers. He had a passion for hunting, not only for the trophy buck, but the opportunity to enjoy God's outdoor creations. His favorite outdoor activities were bow hunting in Three Lakes, salmon fishing in Baileys Harbor, and fishing in Canada with his friends. He enjoyed playing his Gibson Les Paul, often pretending he was a member of the band Rush.

Todd is survived by his wife Lynne; two sons Nathan (Allison Samuel) of Verona and Zachary of Rib Mountain; one brother Scott (Dawn) of Rothschild; parents William & Rose of Rib Mountain; and his dog companion Molly. He is preceded in death by his beloved dog Lily. He also leaves behind numerous cousins, aunts and uncles, a sister-in-law, nieces and nephews, and friends.

Memoiral Mass will be held on Friday, March 20, 2026 at 12:00 PM at Holy Name of Jesus Catholic Church (1104 S 9th Ave, Wausau, WI 54401) Fr. Woodrow Pace will officiate. Visitation will be held on Friday, March 20, 2026 from 10:00 AM until the hour of Mass.

The family wishes to express sincere gratitude and thanks to Dr. Peterson, the hospitalists, various APPs, and nursing staff who cared for him over the past few years and gave him more time to spend doing what he loved.

records.name: Bernice G. Corsten
records.summary: Bernice Gladys (Sandberg) Corsten, born August 25, 1943, in Granite Falls, Minnesota.
records.body:
Bernice Gladys (Sandberg) Corsten was born on August 25, 1943 in Granite Falls, MN to Elmer and Gladys Sandberg. She was raised in Granite Falls along with her siblings, Mavis, Dorothy, James, Clara, Shirley, Keith, and Joyce. Bernice was united in marriage to Roland Charles Corsten on October 28, 1967. She was an incredible mother, who was always supportive of her children, Sean, Eric, and Neil. On top of raising a family, she also was host to fifteen exchange students. Bernice was a certified Reiki Master and instrumental in bringing Laughter Yoga to the community. Her other passions included golfing, cross country skiing, curling, ukulele, tennis, and choir at the University. But her greatest gift may have been making connections through substantive conversations. Bernice's strong spiritual energy and intuition was felt by many throughout her family, friends, and community. She will be truly missed by all who knew her.

Bernice is survived by her husband, Roland; children, Sean T. Corsten, Eric C. Corsten, and Neil S. Corsten; grandchildren, Savannah Corsten, Nicholas Corsten, Tiffany Bredeck, Tarina Bredeck, Ray Corsten, Piper Corsten; great grandchildren, Mason Zimick, Ashton Zimick, Keyson Bredeck, Cameron Bredeck, and Olin Granger.

There will be a time to share laughter, love, and memories of Bernice on Saturday, August 1, 2026 at Riverside Park, 100 E Sherman St., Wausau, WI 54401.

records.name: Emilie M. Kappel
records.summary: Emilie Marie Kappel, 89, of Marshfield, passed away with family by her side.
records.body:
Emilie Marie Kappel, 89, of Marshfield, passed away with family by her side, under the loving care of Three Oaks Health Services and Heartland Hospice.

Emilie was born June 14, 1936, in Colby to Walter and Nina (Riemer) Loos. She received her education in Colby. She was united in marriage to Edward Kappel on April 27, 1957, in Marshfield.

Emilie was employed by St. Joseph's Hospital for 33 years as a nursing assistant. After retirement, she volunteered for many years at Hannah Center. She was a member of Christ the King parish in Spencer. Nothing brought more joy to Emilie than her children and her grandchildren and great-grandchildren. She was always our Grandma Emmy. Polka dancing was a highlight for Emilie and Ed, as well as traveling and playing cards. Mom loved her pets, most recently with Oliver, until she moved to Three Oaks Health Services. Then she had Patches, Annie, and Kitty, her forever stuffed animals.

Emilie is lovingly survived by her children, James (Cindy) Kappel of Lake Dubay, and Sharon (Tim) Meyer of Marshfield. She is also survived by her grandchildren, Jenna (Eric) Drengler of Ringle, Tyler (Allisa) Brock of Waunakee, Logan (Katrina) Brock of Athens. Great-grandchildren, Danica, Brooklyn, and Kaitlyn Drengler. Step-grandchildren Candy (Jake) Zschernitz, Crystal (Brian) Varsho, Jeremy Meyer, Megan Meyer. Step-great-grandchildren, Kaya (Blade) Begert, Keigan and Breyer Zschernitz, Riley, Ted, Maple Schultz-Becker, and Brianna and Ayden Varsho. Emilie is further survived by her brother, Dean (Ann) Loos of Colby.

She was preceded in death by her husband, Edward; her infant daughter, Sandra Ann; her parents, Walter and Nina Loos; her sisters, Marcella Sauer and Lorraine Morrow; and her brother, Darrell Loos.

Services will be held at 11:00 am, Saturday, March 21, 2026, at Christ the King Catholic Church, 306 S Lasalle St, Spencer, WI 54479. Visitation will take place from 10:00 am until the time of services at the church.

Honorary pallbearers will be her grandchildren, Jenna and Eric Drengler, Tyler and Alissa Brock, and Logan and Katrina Brock.

The family wishes to extend our most gratitude to the staff at Three Oaks Health Services and Heartland Hospice. Their love and compassion went beyond anything we could imagine.

records.name: Gene Steidinger Sr.
records.summary: Gene Steidinger Sr., 96, of the Town of Stettin, passed away on March 9, 2026, surrounded by family.
records.body:
Gene Steidinger Sr. went to his eternal rest on Monday March 9, 2026, surrounded by family. He will be greeted in heaven with outstretched arms by his wife, Betty and son, Tom.

He was born November 13, 1929, on the Steidinger Farmstead in the Town of Stettin to the late Carl and Albertine (Roeder) Steidinger. On September 10, 1952 he married the love of his life Betty Ruth Buttke at Trinity Lutheran Church, Wausau.

Gene farmed his entire life in the Town of Stettin. He loved his family and was very proud of them. His grandfather, Ludwig Steidinger, was a German immigrant and Gene was the last surviving member of the 3rd generation of the family tree to live in America. He was extremely proud to be a member of the last German-speaking confirmation classes at Trinity of Stettin and at the time of his passing he was the church's oldest living member. He also loved eating, some of his favorites included boiled dinner with pork hocks, head cheese, liver and blood sausages in addition to many sweets, but in particular Betty's graham cracker pie.

Gene was especially fond of Belgian draft horses. He purchased his first registered mare in 1960, one of the first in Central Wisconsin. Many of his "vacations" involved attending draft horse events. For many years Gene and Betty rarely missed a spring or fall of Waverly, Iowa horse auctions. He loved showing his horses at local fairs in Wausau, Athens, Neilsville, Marshfield, and several years at the Minnesota State Fair. His draft horse travels also included attending the North American Belgian Championship shows in Toronto, Ontario, Lexington, Kentucky, Columbus, Ohio, Brandon, Manitoba and Indianapolis, Indiana.

He is survived by four of his children, sons Gene Jr. and Brian Steidinger both of the Town of Stettin, and twin daughters, Cindy (Doug) Breyer and Sandy (Randy) Schmidt both of Birnamwood. Five grandchildren, Cody (Kimberly) Schmidt, Dillon Breyer, Derek (Katelyn) Breyer, Jeremy (Trisha) Schmidt, Linda Piszczor and seven great-grandchildren, Tori, Bree, Zachary, Aaron, Brayden, Morgan, and Ethan as well as many nieces and nephews.

Gene Sr. was preceded in death by his parents Carl and Albertine, his wife Betty, and son Tom. Gene was further preceded in death by his siblings, Rheinhold (Eleanore) Steidinger, Elsie (Walter) Semerau, Esther (Norman) Fellbaum, Erna (Rudolph) Thurs, Sadie (Alfred) Strei, Verna (Lawrence) Hanke, Hilton (Elaine) Steidinger and Vilas (Phyllis) Steidinger, and brother and sister-in-laws: Mildred (Ervin) Graveen, Donald (Helen) Buttke, and Donna (Albert) Neuman.

Funeral Service will be held on Saturday March 14, 2026 at 12:00 PM at Trinity Lutheran Church in Stettin. (232280 N 120th Ave, Wausau, WI 54401) Pastor Will Ostrem will officiate. Visitation will be held on Saturday March 14, 2026 from 10:00 AM until the hour of service at the church. Burial will follow at the Stettin Christian Cemetery.

The family would like to extend a special thank you to the many family and friends for their wonderful visits throughout the years.

records.name: Grant O. Daniels
records.summary: Grant "Butch" "Eddie" O. Daniels Jr., 79, passed away on March 9, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.
records.body:
Grant "Butch" "Eddie" O. Daniels Jr., age 79, passed away on March 9, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital. Grant was born in Milwaukee, WI to the late Grant and Dorothy (Arendt) Daniels.

After graduating high school, Grant had a dedicated career to the United States Postal Service and United States Navy. Grant married Debbie Kreager of Wausau, Wisconsin on October 19, 2013, at St. Francis Xavier Catholic Church, in Merrill, WI.

Grant loved to ride his ATV with friends and family or going dancing. He was an avid deer hunter and fisherman, often traveling to go fishing. He enjoyed playing music around local areas of the community. Grant was always there for his family and would find any reason to get together.

Grant is survived by his wife, Debbie Daniels; sister, Nancy Hindal; aunt, Beverly Wiloff; nieces, nephews and many other family and friends.

In addition to his parents, Grant is preceded in death by his brother-in-law Robert Hindal.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 12:30 PM on Saturday March 21, 2026, at St. John the Baptist Catholic Church, N4887 Hwy 17, Bloomville, WI 54435. Father John Long will officiate. Visitation will be held at Helke Funeral Home, 302 Spruce St, Wausau, WI 54401, on Saturday March 21, 2026, from 9:00 AM to 11:30 AM. Directions will be provided from Helke Funeral Home to St. John the Baptist Catholic Church.

In Lieu of flowers donations can be made to the charity of your choice.

records.name: James L. McMenamin
records.funeral_home: Brainard Funeral Home and Cremation Center
records.summary: James Leo McMenamin, 71, of Cornell, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully surrounded by family.
records.body:
James (Jim) Leo McMenamin passed away peacefully, surrounded by his family, on March 7, 2026, at the age of 71. Born and raised in Cornell, Wisconsin, on August 16, 1954, he was the only son among six siblings and cared deeply for his sisters. On September 29, 1973, he married Sally Jean Hakes, and together they raised four children.

Jim enlisted in the United States Army, serving his country for 12 years before being honorably discharged. He later worked as a mechanic until retirement. He cherished his role as the beloved babysitter to his 11 grandchildren, sharing his wisdom and love generously. In his spare time, Jim enjoyed helping others, seeing movies, camping, fishing, and hunting alongside his family. An active member of the VFW and American Legion in Cornell, he later joined the Wausau chapters upon moving back to the area.

Jim will be remembered for his generous spirit and willingness to assist anyone in need. Despite personal difficulties, he always went above and beyond for others. He adored all babies and had a special gift for making them smile and play with ease. He was a member of Bethlehem Lutheran Church (Kronenwetter).

Jim is survived by his loving wife of 52 years, Sally Jean McMenamin; his children, James (Regina) McMenamin Jr., Denise (Chris) Hardman, Toni (Randy) Schilling, and Jennifer (Dennis) Plante; 11 grandchildren, Shannan, Nicole (Tristin), Elena (Ryan), Neil, James III, Felicity, Logan, Piper, Landon, Alivia, and Mckenzie; two great-grandchildren, along with four bonus great-grandchildren; and five sisters, Mary (Ron) Hartzell, Jeannine McMenamin, Colleen (Dan) Berg, Grace (Jeff) Skrzeczkowski, and Kate (Lylas) Oestreich. He also leaves behind many brothers and sisters-in-law, nieces, nephews, and cousins.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Raymond and Genevieve (Flood) McMenamin; his mother- and father-in-law, Russell and Donna Hakes; and his brother-in-law, Michael Hakes.

A celebration of life will be held at Bethlehem Lutheran Church, 1901 Kowalski Rd, Kronenwetter, on March 27, 2026. Visitation will begin at 10:00 a.m., with the service at 11:00 a.m., followed by lunch. Burial will take place at Northwoods National Cemetery at a later date.

The family would like to thank Dr. Kyle Dettbarn of Oak Leaf Clinic, Eau Claire for his care and compassion through the last several years.

records.name: John W. Dahlie
records.summary: John William Dahlie, 72, of Weston, passed away on March 9, 2026, after his courageous battle with cancer.
records.body:
John William Dahlie, 72, of Weston, WI passed away March 9, 2026, after his courageous battle with cancer. He was born in Marshfield, WI on September 17, 1953, to the late Norman and Carol (Ohm) Dahlie. He married Katherine Jean Claire Seeger in 1977.

John graduated from Phillips High School and the University of Wisconsin-Eau Claire, where he earned his bachelor's degree in environmental public health. He continued his education and graduated from Marquette University where he earned his Doctor of Dental Surgery (DDS). Following graduation, John worked in Milwaukee for a year before moving to Shawano, WI where he practiced dentistry for over 40 years. He enjoyed regaling his patients with his wide array of material including tales from his farm, hunting stories, little known facts, and dad jokes. He also frequently asked questions while patients' mouths were open, which was likely for his entertainment. John was a great storyteller, and his favorite topic was his family, proudly sharing updates on his children's and grandchildren's lives and accomplishments. He loved them beyond words and watching their numerous activities was one of his favorite things to do.

John was a dedicated member of Divine Savior Lutheran Church in Shawano for 44 years, before becoming a member of Forgiven Lutheran Church in Kronenwetter. At Divine Savior, he held various leadership roles in the church council and school board and was a regular usher and greeter. In his limited free time, he enjoyed managing his "hobby" farm, where he and his family raised a variety of animals. He also spent a significant amount of time fixing fence, chasing the cows that got through the "fixed" fence, and eventually selling the cows because they wouldn't stay inside the fence. John was an avid hunter, who just enjoyed being in the woods. He and Kathy also loved taking trips and cruises after the kids left the house.

John was an amazing husband, father, and grandfather and emphasized to his family to remain faithful to God. He will be dearly missed, but the sting of death is gone because he is with God in heaven.

He is survived by Kathy, his wife of 48 years; five children, Andrew (Michelle) Dahlie, David (Melanie) Dahlie, Beth (Luke) Krueger, Emily (Jesse) Stodall, and Michael Dahlie; his ten grandchildren Tyler, Tanner, Mason, Brianna, Johanna, Ethan, Eli, Carson, Adelynn, and Charlotte; brother Dr. Peter (Kathi) Dahlie; sister Kathy Meyer; mother-in-law Shirley Seeger; sister-in-law Trisha Dahlie; brother-in-law Rick (Roberta) Seeger, and nieces and nephews.

He is preceded in death by his parents Norman and Carol, brother Bob, father-in-law Rev. Richard Seeger, brother-in-law Rick Meyer, and sister-in-law Wendy Dahlie.

A Funeral Service will be held at 11 AM on Saturday, March 21, 2026, at Divine Savior Lutheran Church in Shawano. Rev. Fred Berger and Rev. Joseph Neuberger will officiate. Visitation will be on Friday, March 20, 2026, from 4 PM until 7 PM at the church. Visitation will continue on Saturday, from 9 AM until the time of service at the church.

In lieu of flowers, the family request's contributions be made to the Wisconsin Lutheran Seminary's Reverand Richard M. Seeger Scholarship memorial fund.

records.name: Kathleen M. Jaecks
records.summary: Kathleen M. Jaecks, 86, of Wausau, passed away on March 6, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.
records.body:
Kathleen M. Jaecks, 86, Wausau passed away on Friday, March 6, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

She was born July 8, 1939 in Wausau, daughter of the late Frederick and Verona (Wachholz) Jaecks. Kathleen graduated from Wausau High School in 1957.

Kathleen had a long working career with the Wausau Medical Center as an insurance claims specialist. She was also a lifelong member of St. Stephen Lutheran Church in Wausau where she was a Sunday School teacher for many years. Kathleen loved her sisters and their children and loved spending time with them all.

Survivors include her sister, Elizabeth (Ron) Kordus, Weston and a brother Richard "Dick" (Sue) Jaecks, Woodstock, GA as well as numerous nieces, a nephew, great nieces and a great nephew and a great great nephew.

Besides her parents she was preceded in death by a sister, Grace (Gordon) Berg.

Funeral Services will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Monday, March 16, 2026, at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau. The Rev. Karoline Schwantes will officiate. Burial will be in Pine Grove Cemetery, Wausau. Visitation will be held on Monday from 9:30 a.m. until the time of services all at the Sixth Street funeral home.

The family wishes to express their sincere gratitude to all the staff at Aspirus Wausau Hospital for the outstanding care they all gave Kathleen during her stay at the hospital.

records.name: LaVern 'Curley' Meurett
records.summary: LaVern 'Curley' Meurett, 88, of Merrill, passed away on March 8, 2026, at Aspirus Merrill Hospital.
records.body:
LaVern 'Curley' Meurett, 88, of Merrill, passed away Sunday, March 8, 2026, at Aspirus Merrill Hospital.

LaVern was born April 22, 1937, in Mosinee, son of Mark and Ellen (Jenks) Meurett. After high school, Curley enlisted in the United States Army. He served during the Korean War and was honorably discharged on April 23, 1957. Curley was proud of his service to our country and was able to participate in the Never Forgotten Honor Flight on April 24, 2017.

Curley married Mavis Brandenburg on October 3, 1959. She preceded him in death on June 22, 2002. Curley worked as an interior and exterior painter and decorator. At the beginning of his career, he and his brother designed and painted billboards by hand. Curley was very creative and enjoyed making stained glass and mosaics. Curley enjoyed the outdoors be it golfing, fishing, gardening, berry picking, or making maple syrup. In his younger years, he was a well-known fastpitch softball pitcher. Curley also enjoyed working indoors making sauerkraut, canning, baking cakes and cookies, making candy, and making Tom & Jerry and Hot Buttered Rum mixes. Curley was an animal lover.
