Survivors include his children, Laurie (Pete Rynes) Meurett of Minneapolis and Dan (Lori) Meurett of Stevens Point; grandchildren, Molly, Lilabel, Sarah, Jason, Satchel, Jake, and Nathan; great granddaughter, Josie; and sisters, Susan Mueller of Minocqua and Sally Siegler of Wadena, MN.

Curley was preceded in death by his wife, Mavis Meurett; parents, Mark and Ellen Meurett; son, Dean (Connie) Meurett; brothers, Burleigh 'Burl' (Catherine 'Kate) Meurette, James (June) Meurett, and William 'Willy' (Mary) Meurett; brothers-in-law, Wayne Mueller and Leonard 'Len' Siegler; nephews, Matthew Meurett, Michael Meurett, and Chandler Schaffhausen; and significant other, Barbara Bakke.

A celebration of life will be held at a later date.

records.name: Mary Boruta
records.summary: Mary (McLeod) Boruta, 64, of Arbor Vitae, passed away on February 24, 2026, in Wausau.
records.body:
Mary (McLeod) Boruta, age 64, of Arbor Vitae, WI passed away on February 24, 2026, in Wausau, WI surrounded by her loved ones. Mary was born on December 27, 1961, in Chicago, Illinois, to Donald and Mary. Mary's early years were spent alongside her siblings, Judy, John, Paul, and her late brother, Donald.

On August 15, 1987, Mary married the love of her life, Michael Boruta, in Chicago. Together, they shared over 38 years of marriage, marked by love, laughter, and an unwavering bond. Their enduring partnership is a testament to their commitment to one another and their family. Together, they raised three sons—Patrick, Joseph, and James—in a home filled with warmth, humor, and the occasional misadventure often resulting in some busted drywall. Their long-time home on Lowden Lane was often filled with friends and family as the doors and refrigerator were always open to anyone who wanted to visit.

Mary's professional journey restarted after years at home raising her children. She spent over 25 years at The Randhurst Home Depot in Mount Prospect where she was known for her unwavering dedication to her customers and the genuine friendships she cultivated with her coworkers. Her infectious and voluminous laughter and warm smile were the constants that brightened the days of those around her. Her commitment to the store and the community they served was no more evident than her longtime participation in The Kid's Workshop and the numerous store events she participated in and organized.

Outside of work, Mary was a woman of diverse interests and passions. She was an avid gardener, finding peace and joy in nurturing her plants, flowers and vegetables. Her role as a devoted hockey mom saw her cheering passionately from the stands of rinks across the Midwest and Canada, supporting her three sons with unyielding enthusiasm and love.

Mary also cherished her role as an expert Harley passenger, sharing countless adventures with her beloved Michael on the open road. After a long and prosperous life raising children and working in the Chicagoland area, Mary and Mike retired to a home in Arbor Vitae, Wisconsin. There they enjoyed new adventures and friendships in the tranquility of the Northwoods.

Mary was a woman who truly knew how to celebrate life. Her love for parties and family gatherings was unmatched, and her presence was always the center of any occasion. Her legacy of joy, kindness, and hospitality will live on through her surviving family: her devoted husband, Michael; her sons and their spouses, Patrick and Deanna, Joseph and Hillary, and James and Lisa; her three siblings Judy, John, and Paul; and her eight treasured grandchildren, Scarlet, Olivia, Donavon, Lena, Zinnia, Grey, Luke and Joseph and sixteen nieces and nephews each of whom brought her immeasurable joy and pride.

Mary is preceded in death by her parents, Donald McLeod and Mary and John Schwartz, and her brother, Donald.

A celebration of Mary's life will be held on Sunday, April 26, 2026, at The Red Barn, 303 E. Kensington Road, Mount Prospect, IL 60056, from 1-6 PM, where all who knew her can come together with food and drinks and share in the joy of her memory. In lieu of flowers, the family has asked to instead contribute to a scholarship fund in Mary's name that will support students attending Harper College in Palatine, IL; the Mary Boruta Endowed Scholarship.

May she rest in peace, surrounded by endless gardens and the laughter of those she loved.

records.name: Raymond G. Dundore
records.summary: Raymond G. Dundore, 63, of Birnamwood, died on March 5, 2026, at his home.
records.body:
Raymond G. Dundore, 63, of Birnamwood, died on Thursday, March 5, 2026, at his home, under the care of his family and Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services.

Ray was born on March 4, 1963, in Racine. On October 31, 1981, he was united in marriage to Jennifer Hubbard at Kingdom Hall in Elderon.

Ray was a member of The Kingdom Hall of Jehovah's Witnesses in Rib Mountain.

Ray worked as a Journeyman Carpenter and later as a Superintendent for the The Samuels Group for 25 years until his retirement. Ray also had a natural talent as a handyman. He enjoyed building, framing, and taking on woodworking projects, always having an eye for improvements and new projects. Whether helping family or working on something around the house, Ray took pride in creating and fixing things.

Ray loved to travel and especially enjoyed his camping trips out west with his family. Together they discovered many favorite places throughout the Midwest that they loved to revisit with their camper. Ray and Jenny also shared a love of cruising, taking ten cruises together to destinations throughout the Caribbean, Mexico, the Panama Canal, and Puerto Rico.

An avid outdoorsman, Ray enjoyed deer and turkey hunting, watching and feeding birds, and making maple syrup, with a specialty being in bourbon barrels. One of his most cherished traditions was the annual trip to the races in Brainerd with his brother, and family and friends, a trip he faithfully attended every year since 1989, missing only one race. Ray also enjoyed listening to classic rock music. He especially cherished time spent with his wife, children and grandchildren.

Ray is survived by his wife, Jenny; his children, Zach (DeAnna) Dundore and Cassy (Jay) Hynes; two grandchildren, Quinton and Vincent; his mom, Doris Dundore; siblings, Tim (Melinda) Harmann, Kimberly Shields and Sally Hegewald and many nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends.

Ray was preceded in death by his unborn grandchild, Wesley.

A Funeral Service will be held on March 20, 2026 at the Kingdom Hall of Jehovahs Witnesses in Rib Mountain. Visitation will be from 1-3pm, with the funeral talk at 3pm. The talk will be live streamed on a zoom meeting and available to anyone who wishes to watch. Reach out to family for zoom information.

records.name: Robert R. Schug Sr.
records.summary: Robert "Bob" R. Schug Sr., 84, of Athens, passed away peacefully at his home on March 7, 2026.
records.body:
Robert "Bob" R. Schug Sr., 84, Athens, passed away peacefully at his home surrounded by his family on Saturday, March 7, 2026.

He was born March 7, 1942 in the town of Johnson, son of the late George and Dorothy (Weiler) Schug. On June 29, 1963, he married Joanne Klimpke at St. Anthony Catholic Church in Athens. She survives.

In 1960, Bob graduated from Athens High School and furthered his education, earning a degree from Delco Training School in Milwaukee. His first job was working as a mechanic for Norb's Enco Service in Milwaukee. He moved back to Athens, was married and began working for Marathon Electric in Wausau. Bob and his family then relocated to Milwaukee, where he owned and operated Bob's Enco Service. He moved his family back to Athens and bought a farm in the town of Halsey where he farmed for 43 years and also ran his own farm equipment business called B & J Equipment, then began working for Schuette Metals for 17 years as a welder. He retired at the age of 70.

Following retirement, he moved to the village of Athens and traveled with Joanne all over the country, including a trip to Alaska, where they celebrated their 50th wedding anniversary at the Santa Claus House in Anchorage. Among his favorite pastimes, he enjoyed fishing and hunting with his sons, sons-in-law and grandkids. One of his most favorite things to do was bird watching.

Survivors include, the love of his life, Joanne Schug; eight children, Sandy Neubauer, Bob Schug Jr., Lisa (Pete) Kroening, Paul (Amanda) Schug, Scott Schug, Kenneth (Janae) Schug, Jennifer (Cory) Mauer and Jessica (John) Sommer; 23 grandchildren; six great-grandchildren; eight siblings, Caroline Beran, Joan Hargraves, Pat (Norb) Boesl, George (Jean) Schug Jr., Dorothy Gajewski; Delmar (Charlotte) Schug, Sherry (Jim) Literski, and Pearl (Lyle) Guralski; one sister-in-law, Joline Schug; two Godsons, Nick Klimpke and Gary Gajewski; and many nieces and nephews.

Besides his parents, he was preceded in death by daughter, Julie Drinka; infant daughter, Cindy Schug; daughters-in-law, Tonya Schug and Jowanda Schug; two siblings, Delbert Schug and Sally Whaples; and brothers-in-law, Howard Whaples, Frank Beran, Lawrence Gajewski and Clem Hargraves.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, March 14, 2026 at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens. Rev. George Graham will preside. Burial will be in the Calvary Cemetery, Athens. Visitation will be on Friday from 4:00 until 6:00 p.m. at church and again on Saturday from 9:00 a.m. until time of Mass at the church. There will be rosary service at 6:00 p.m. Friday evening at the church.

records.name: Verna M. Schalow
records.summary: Verna Mae Schalow, 84, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on March 5, 2026.
records.body:
Verna Mae Schalow, age 84, passed away peacefully on March 5, 2026.

Verna was born in Wausau, WI to Alfred and Helen Wendt. Verna married Robert J Schalow of Wausau, Wisconsin on August 30th, 1962 at Trinity Lutheran in Wausau, WI.

Verna started her working career at Lemke Cheese in Wausau and then transitioned to the Wausau School District in their Food Service Department. She worked at a few of their schools as she followed her boys as they grew up and retired from the Food Service Department at Wausau West High School.

Verna loved her hummingbirds and watching old westerns on TV. She loved spending time with family and friends. Her and her husband Bob would spend summer weekends with her boys up north fishing and she also enjoyed deer hunting with the family.

Verna is survived by her son Dean (Maria) Schalow; 9 Grandchildren – Jaysy Schalow, Dakota Schalow, Skyler (Austin) Gilkenson, Hunter (Elizabeth) Schalow, Jordyn (Ashley) Schalow, Bryana Schalow, Hayley Schalow, Brandyn Schalow, Alyson (Robert) Petersen; 13 Great Grandchildren – William Schalow, Alexis & Eliza Rogers, Connor & Murphy Hancock, Isabella, Owen & Violet Schalow, Marina & Mariyana Robinson, Ariya & Kyrie Schalow, Khamari Schalow, and Jakson Petersen; 1 Sister Lois (Gary) Hoffman, and 1 Brother Donald (Inge) Wendt.

She was preceded in death by her husband Robert J Schalow, Clark Schalow (Son), Alfred Wendt (Father) Helen Wendt (Mother) Lyle Wendt (Brother ) Phyllis Venner (Sister), Kenneth Wendt (Nephew), Gary Hoffman (Brother in Law), Calista Wendt (Sister in Law).

A Celebration of Life will be held at The Town of Merrill Community Center located at W4594 Progress Ave, Merrill, WI 54452, on Saturday May 16, 2026.

records.name: Wai Man Lee Quong
records.summary: Wai Man Lee Quong, "Benny," 81, passed away at home on March 7, 2026.
records.body:
Wai Man Lee Quong, "Benny, "was born on March 26, 1944, in Kaiping, China, to the late Min Quong and Mee (Lee) Quong. He passed away at home on March 7, 2026. Wai Man grew up in the Philippine Islands.

Wai Man married LiLi Li in 1991, with whom he shared a devoted and enduring partnership. Together they raised their son, Michael, who was a great source of pride and joy in his life.

After earning a business degree, Wai Man discovered that his true passion was in the kitchen. He pursued a career as a chef, where his creativity, dedication, and love of food allowed him to bring happiness to many through his cooking.

Outside of work, Wai Man found joy in the simple pleasures of life. He loved fishing, tending to his garden, and watching television—especially cooking shows that continued to inspire his culinary interests. Known as a wonderful conversationalist, he had a gift for engaging others with warmth, humor, and thoughtful discussion.

Wai Man is survived by his beloved wife, LiLi Li; his son, Michael; and three sisters.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Min and Mee Quong; four brothers; and two sisters.

Wai Man will be remembered for his love of family, his passion for cooking, and the many meaningful conversations he shared with those around him. His memory will live on in the hearts of all who knew and loved him.

records.name: Audrey E. Lemmer
records.summary: Audrey E. Lemmer, 87, passed away peacefully on March 11, 2026.
records.body:
Audrey E Lemmer, 87, passed away peacefully with family by her side on Wednesday March 11th 2026 under the care of St. Croix Hospice.

She was born August 17, 1938 in Wausau, daughter of the late Herbert and Myrtle (Burk) Leitermann.

Audrey was the only child who grew up on a family farm in Marathon. She spent more than 35 years working in medical records at Bone and Joint Clinic, where she was known for her dedication and care. Audrey was a faithful member of St. Anne Catholic Church in Wausau. She loved cooking and celebrating holidays and birthdays with her family, always bringing people together for special occasions. Audrey also enjoyed playing piano. Throughout Audrey and Orville's 40 year partnership they enjoyed going for fish fry every Friday night with a traditional old fashioned, they enjoyed watching Nascar and Packer Games, camping at Wisconsin Valley Fair, attending church picnics and traveling the world. Audrey and Orv have traveled to places such as Germany, Hawaii, Italy, Branson, Las Vegas, Disney World, Washington DC and the list goes on.

She shared a deep bond with her Grandchildren and cherished every moment spent with them; Casey (Kaitlynn), Mackenzie, Conrad, Cade, Chloe (Dee), and Great Grandson, Luka.

Survivors include her son, Craig (Heidi) Lemmer, daughter, Lana (Dave) Brounacker and Grandchildren.

She is preceded in death by her parents, her beloved partner, Orville, and her loyal dog, Buddy.

Funeral service will be 11:00 a.m. Monday, March 23, 2026, at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau. Visitation will be Monday from 10:00 a.m. until the time of service all at the Sixth Street funeral home. Burial will be in Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Barbara J. Callahan
records.summary: Barbara J. Callahan, 97, passed away peacefully on March 13, 2026.
records.body:
Barbara Jane Callahan, age 97, passed away peacefully on March 13, 2026. She is no longer suffering and is with her lord and reunited with her family in heaven.

Barbara was born on March 8, 1929, to the late Robert and Ida (Erdman) Smith. She was born a twin with her sister Virginia (Smith) Canfield, who she is now reunited with.

Barbara married her husband, Patrick Callahan, on November 20, 1948. They lived happily for 50 years, before Patrick sadly passed on September 5, 1999.

Barbara was known for her love of traveling as well as watching and attending NASCAR races. Her active nature was very prevalent in her life. She lived on Lake Wausau for many years, where she loved snowmobiling and going on pontoon rides. Any time outside in nature was a true pleasure for Barbara.

Even in her later years of life, Barbara was heavily involved and active member in her community. She was a member of the Wausau Women's Club as well as the Wausau Senior Choir. Not only that, but she spent a lot of time participating in the local church choirs: Pilgrim Lutheran Church choir and Bethleham Lutheran Church Choir.

Barbara is survived by her daughter, Katherine Callahan; nieces, Sara (Philip) Duellman and Susan (Kent) Larson; and great niece and nephews, Nicholas (Niki Quillman) Jarntowski, Joshua (Ashley) Jarntowski, Jacob Duellman, Lizabeth Larson, and Duke Larson. They will all miss her dearly.

Barbara will move on to join her parents, husband, twin sister, and brother, Robert Smith Jr. in heaven, who preceded her in death.

Barbara's family wants to give thanks to Corrine at Inclusa, the Care Partners staff, and Amanda at Interim Hospice as well as the rest of the staff.

Per Barbara's wishes, there will be no formal service.

Family and friends may go to MWCS.WS to leave words of remembrance.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family.

records.name: Beverly A. Vandervoort
records.summary: Beverly A. Vandervoort, 73, of Merrill passed away on March 6, 2026.
records.body:
Beverly A. Vandervoort, 73, of Merrill, passed away at Seasons of Life Hospice, on March 6, 2026.

She is survived by her children, Heather Majetic, Christina (Nick) Wendt, James (Angie) Reiche, Matthew (Michela) Reiche, and Michelle Reiche; as well as many grandchildren and great-grandchildren.

She was preceded in death by her parents, John and Edith Vandervort; son, Jeremy; as well as her son-in-law, Chuck Majetic.

Beverly was happiest where the trees were tall, the water was calm, and a fishing pole rested in her hands. Camping under open skies and waking up to the quiet sounds of nature brought her a kind of peace that few things could match. Whether it was casting a line into the water or sitting by a campfire, she found joy in life's simple moments.

Her dogs were never just pets—they were her loyal companions, adventure partners, and constant sources of love. They followed her through countless walks, camping trips, and lazy afternoons, always close by, where they belonged.

She also had a well-known love for rings. If something sparkled, she probably owned it—or planned to. Each ring told a story and wearing them was just one more way she expressed her vibrant personality.

She will be remembered for her adventurous spirit, her love for the outdoors, her devotion to her dogs, and the little flashes of sparkle on her fingers that perfectly matched the brightness she brought into the lives of those around her.

Her memory will live on in the quiet mornings by the water, the warmth of a campfire, the wag of a happy dog's tail, and every bit of sparkle that catches the light.

A private celebration of Beverly's life will take place at a later date.

Family and friends may leave words of remembrance at MWCS.WS

records.name: Brian M. Bessette
records.summary: Brian M. Bessette, 59, of Wausau passed away unexpectedly on March 8, 2026.
records.body:
Brian Bessette, age 59, of Wausau, Wisconsin passed away unexpectedly on Sunday, March 8, 2026.

He was born on September 12, 1966, in Wisconsin, the son of Clayton and Janice (Zeinert) Bessette. Brian led a life imbued with warmth, kindness, and an unwavering dedication to his family and friends.

As a self-employed entrepreneur, Brian's work ethic was unmatched. He was known as a hard worker who would extend his helping hand to anyone in need, embodying a spirit of generosity and compassion that touched the lives of many. Throughout his life, he lived life to the fullest, embracing every moment with a zest that inspired those around him.

Brian was a proud father to Alex (Kourtney) Bessette, Ryan Bessette, Lily Bessette, and Jack Bessette. His role as a son, brother, father, uncle, and grandfather brought him immense joy, and these were among his proudest accomplishments. He cherished every lesson, every memory, and every laugh shared with his loved ones. Alongside his children, he created lasting traditions, often adventurous days spent fishing, hunting, and camping that will be deeply missed by them all.

His hobbies reflected his vibrant personality; an avid fisherman and hunter, Brian relished the tranquility of nature and the thrill of the outdoors. Every fishing trip and hunting excursion was not just about the activity itself, but also about the time spent together with family. A true card game enthusiast, he exhibited prowess as a "cribbage shark" and enjoyed playing chess, where his strategic mind shined through. An avid reader and devout Christian, Brian shared stories that brought laughter and joy to everyone around him, reminding us always to find light, even in our darkest moments.

Brian is survived by his beloved mother, Janice Northcott; his children, 5 grandchildren, his brothers Craig (Shelly) Bessette, James Bessette, and Andrew (Emily) Bessette; and sisters Kari (Doug) Weisshahn and Kristi (Phil) Harms. He also leaves behind cherished aunts Bonnie Matz and Marianne (Perry) Theix, many nieces, nephews, other relatives and many friends.

He was preceded in death by his father, Clayton Bessette; his stepmother, Dorothy Bessette; his stepfather, William Northcott; grandparents; Aunt Jane Maki and Uncle David Zeinert.

A memorial service for Brian will take place on Tuesday, March 17, 2026, at 11:00AM at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston, Wisconsin 54476, with Pastor Lance Hoelscher presiding. Visitation will be held from 10:00AM until the time of service at 11:00AM.

A life well lived, Brian leaves behind a legacy of love, laughter, and indelible memories. As we remember his character and spirit, we find comfort in knowing that his influence will forever remain in the hearts of those who were fortunate to know him. Brian will be dearly missed, but his spirit will live on in every heartfelt story and cherished moment shared.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the Bessette Family with arrangements. You may sign the family guestbook at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Donald W. Brandt Jr.
records.summary: Donald W. Brandt Jr., 71, of Wausau passed away unexpectedly on March 10, 2026.
records.body:
Donald William Brandt, Jr., 71, of Wausau, passed away unexpectedly on March 10, 2026, peacefully at home.

Don was born on December 9, 1954, to Donald Sr. and Joan "Frannie" (Coffer) Brandt.

He is survived by his son Jason and daughter-in-law Jolene Brandt, daughter Jayme and son-in-law Ryan Anklam, two grandchildren, Leland and Lochlan. Niece Ashley Vial and Nephew Zack Sobacki.

He is preceded in death by his loving parents and his sister, Cheryl Brandt.

Don was happily retired. He has always had a zest for life. His passions included riding his motorcycle, going up north fishing, watching wildlife, and tinkering in his garage.

He will be missed by all who knew him.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family at this time. Online condolences may be expressed at MWCS.WS

records.name: Flora E. Christner
records.summary: Flora E. Christner, 84, of Marshfield passed away peacefully on March 7, 2026.
records.body:
Flora E. Christner, 84, Marshfield passed away peacefully on Saturday, March 7, 2026. Her family is grateful for the wonderful care given by Heartland Hospice and the staff at Three Oaks, Marshfield.

She was born on December 7, 1941 in Marshfield, daughter of the late Anton and Emma (Altmann) Kraemer. On August 4, 1962 she married John Christner in Marshfield. He preceded her in death on September 14, 2004.

For many years Flora worked side by side with her husband in various enterprises, including her last position as the office manager at Charles Apartments, a historic building that she and John owned and operated together in Marshfield.

Flora was a devoted wife and mother. She loved cooking and baking, especially for Holidays. Her Catholic faith sustained her through life's difficulties. People were drawn to Flora's sense of humor and her beautiful laugh. Some of her favorite pastimes included going on vacations with John, especially New York City where she loved to attend Broadway shows and traveling to Las Vegas at least two or three times a year.

Survivors include her beloved daughter, Barbara Christner, Marshfield, her two sisters, Rosemary Adler, Appleton and Rita Gassner, Wausau, her in-laws, Mike and Bonnie Christner, Marshfield and numerous nieces and nephews.

Flora was preceded in death by her parents and husband, three brothers, Paul, Richard and Thomas Kraemer and a sister, Arline Stangl.

The Funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. Monday, March 23, 2026 at Our Lady of Peace Catholic Church, Marshfield. The Rev. Douglas Robertson will preside. Burial will be in Hillside Cemetery, Marshfield. Visitation will be held on Monday from 10:00 a.m. until the time of service at the church. Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Wausau is in charge of arrangements. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Helen A. Brenton
records.summary: Helen A. Brenton, 99, of Wausau passed away peacefully on March 14, 2026.
records.body:
Helen Ann Brenton, aged 99, of Wausau, passed away peacefully following a brief illness on March 14, 2026.

Helen was an 'all star' wife, mother, grandmother, friend, listener, musician, educator and Christian.

Helen was born in Clinton, Iowa to Ralph and Harriet White. She graduated from the University of Iowa as a music major. At U of I she met her future husband, Frank "Les". They moved to Wausau with their one year old son, Steve, in 1953, and proceeded to have three more children, Ann, Laura and Betsy.

Helen was largely a stay-at-home mom for her children. She also directed choirs at several Wausau churches. Helen started her music teaching career in 1967, a position she held for twenty years.

Helen and Les retired in 1987 and traveled extensively, spent winters in Arizona and spoiled their six grandchildren and later four great grandchildren. They were extremely active and visible in the Wausau community.

Helen was a beautiful listener to her children, grandchildren, and great grandchildren. She would genuinely want to know how they were doing and what brought joy or sadness. She had a caring tenderness with her family and they felt loved unconditionally in her presence. They also loved her red lipstick which she wore until the end.

In addition to family, the center of Helen's life was Wausau's First Presbyterian Church where she and Les were active members for many decades. Her faith in Jesus Christ deepened as the years went by and the chair that she sat in daily was surrounded by devotionals and her bible.

Helen's family wishes to express appreciation to caregivers at Forest Park and the Gardens and to a long list of caring and supportive friends whose company she cherished, especially following Les' death in 2017. The love and support of this group cannot be understated.

Helen's family will schedule a memorial service later in the year.

Memorials suggested to Wausau Conservatory of Music Brenton Scholarship Fund.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting family at this time. Online condolences may be expressed at MWCS.WS

records.name: James A. Parsons
records.summary: James A. Parsons of Merrill, formerly of Wausau, passed away peacefully on March 13, 2026.
records.body:
James "Jim" Alan Parsons, Merrill, formerly of Wausau, passed away peacefully on Friday, March 13, 2026 with his two favorite girls by his side, if we do say so ourselves.

Jim led a long life filled with service to our country, a construction worker, and a supervisor within the finishing room at Wausau Papers.

Jim was drafted into the U.S. Army, was stationed at Fort Knox and later at Fort Hood where he was one of the troops who made their way to Fort Stewart to be deployed to Cuba in 1962. Jim sat on a plane and was going to be one of the first to go in and build roads.

After the Army, Jim came back home, attended North Central Technical School and studied drafting, skills he later used to build his first home. Along the way Jim married Rosalie Blarek on May 21, 1960. They had two children, Scott Parsons and Vicki (George) Wilde. Jim was also a grandpa to Elizabeth (Austin) Leverity and Alan Parsons and a Bonus Grandpa to Allison (Chris) Lannigan, Marissa (Logan) Wilke and Morgan Wilde.

Jim loved puttering around the yard, riding his lawn tractor and being in his garage. He spent a great amount of time helping Scott bend pipe for his custom motorcycle business and generally chatting it up with the guys at the shop.

Jim was preceded in death by his parents, Grace and Elmer Parsons, brothers, David and Gordon (Patricia) Parsons, Rosalie who passed away in 2008 and Scott, his son who passed in 2018. Losing Scott was the hardest thing he could have experienced in his life, he was never the same after.

Jim was a quiet and gentle soul who will be deeply missed. He was a great father, grandpa, who was known for the world's best chocolate chip pancakes.

Funeral Services will be held at 11:30 a.m. Thursday, March 19, 2026 at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 6th Street, Wausau. Burial will be in Maple Grove Cemetery, town of Maine. Visitation will be held on Thursday from 9:30 a.m. until the time of services all at the Sixth Street funeral home. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

Our family would like to give special thanks to Abby who was part of his care team and Mackenzie, Alysa and Joanie from the hospice team who "fought" over who was Jims's favorite "girlfriend". Their care has truly been noticed and appreciated by the family. The family also would like to extend a sincere thank you to Dr. Ann Witmer at the VA for the exceptional care she gave to Jim as well.

Jim will be deeply missed by all.

records.name: Janis D. Mootz
records.funeral_home: Helke Funeral Home and Cremation Service
records.summary: Janis D. Mootz, 84, of Kronenwetter passed away peacefully on March 11, 2026.
records.body:
Janis D. Mootz, 84, of Kronenwetter, passed away peacefully on Wednesday, March 11, 2026, at St. Clare's Hospital in Weston, surrounded by her loving family.

Janis was born on May 5, 1941, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to the late James and Leila Mae Jackson. She lived in the Wausau area her entire life and was a 1959 graduate of the former Wausau High School. On September 16, 1961, she married LaVern Mootz, at Trinity Lutheran Church in Wausau. With whom she shared 50 years of marriage until his passing on June 26, 2012.

Family was the heart of Janis's life. She was a devoted mother, grandmother, and great-grandmother who treasured every moment spent with her loved ones. She had a passion for sewing, knitting, and crocheting, ensuring that each child, grandchild, and great-grandchild received a handmade Afghan. Janis also enjoyed bargain shopping to find special clothing for her family and cherished monthly lunches with her three childhood friends.

Janis is survived by her children, Steven (Martina) Mootz of Weston, Beth (Calvin) Wong of Elmhurst, Ill, and Jill Krzmarcik of Mosinee; grandchildren, Breanna, Brady, Hannah, Emma, Noah, Megan, Adam, Katelyn, Jessica, and Ben; great-grandchildren, Maxwell, Madilyn, Mia, Myles, Jerrin, Jace, Ellie, Briella, Walter, Ivy, Bentley, and Malakai; brother James and sister Betty; as well as many cousins and close friends.

She was preceded in death by her husband, LaVern; parents; parents-in-law, Harry and Leona (Bahr) Mootz; granddaughter Ashley; sister Lois; nephew James; and her cousins Stretch, Renee, Dave, Darla, and Dana.

Per Janis's wishes, a private graveside committal service will be held at Maple Grove Cemetery, Town of Maine.

The family would like to extend their heartfelt thanks to the doctors and staff at St. Clare's Hospital for the compassionate care they provided to Janis.

records.name: Jennifer J. Burns
records.summary: Jennifer J. Heal-Burns, 49, passed away on March 13, 2026 following a courageous 13-year battle with cancer.
records.body:
Jennifer J. Heal-Burns passed away Friday, March 13, 2026, following a courageous 13-year battle with cancer, at Aspirus Hospice House in Wausau. She was 49 years old.

Jenny was born on December 10, 1976, to Jeff Heal and Joyce Walter in Viola, Wisconsin. The family moved to Hatley, and Jenny graduated from D.C. Everest Senior High School in 1995. Jenny then attended State College of Beauty Culture in Wausau.

For more than 10 years, Jenny worked as a hair stylist for Cost Cutters in Rothschild, where her gift for conversation was on display to everyone she took care of. She loved visiting with her clients, listening to them, especially older ladies and gentlemen, and loved being there for them like family. The bonds she formed at work would still last decades later. A self-proclaimed "people person", Jenny worked in customer service for Banner Banks in Hatley. For another 10 years, she was known as a friendly individual who would always go above and beyond what was expected of her.

In 2013, Jenny was diagnosed with liposarcoma. As she would reflect on her diagnosis, she was not afraid. Never wanting any attention, her great sense of humor gained the affection of so many friends who admired how she handled life with her unique personality.

On October 22, 2016, she married Christofer L. Burns, and moved to Plainfield. Their hobbies together included loving their pets, fishing, rummage sales, gardening, and canning and selling their own pickles. There, she worked her favorite job at Union Telephone, meeting great co-workers who turned into great friends. Christofer passed away on August 29, 2025, as a result of a motor vehicle accident.

Jenny is survived by her parents, Joyce (Clyde) Patterson and Jeff (Dawn) Heal; brother, Jay (Stacy) Heal; special nephew, Blake Heal; as well as her great friends, Heather Tulickas, Trudy Wanichek, and Shannon Volden-Schultz. She is preceded in death by her husband, Christofer.

Words of Remembrance for Jenny will begin at 1:00PM on Saturday, March 28, 2026, at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston, WI 54476. An Open House Gathering will continue after the words until 3:00PM at Ascend.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be made in Jenny's honor to the Aspirus Hospice House.

Jenny's last message to everyone she loved and cared for is to "Be Kind".

Ascend Funeral Home and Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the Heal-Burns Family with arrangements. You may sign the family guestbook at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: John Llewellyn
records.summary: John Llewellyn, 68, passed away on March 11, 2026.
records.body:
It is with profound love and deep sorrow that I inform everyone of the passing of my husband, John Llewellyn, who left us on March 11, 2026, to be with our Lord and Savior.

John was more than a husband – he was my best friend, my confidant, my spiritual champion, and my greatest blessing. His kindness, humor, and unwavering strength touched everyone who knew him. Whether it was through his warm smile, his incredible wisdom, or his willingness to help without hesitation, he made the world brighter simply by being in it.

Born in Deerborn, MI, John lived a life filled with purpose and generosity. His greatest passion was his love for Christ, and he never missed an opportunity to spread God's word. John not only served Christ, but his country as well as an Army PV2, stationed in Germany. It was there that he met his first wife, Renata, and had his children, Ray and Lindsay. He also developed a passion for golf and found joy and contentment on the course.

As a Certified Public Accountant, John worked for many different companies in many different states. He was instrumental in assisting hospital systems by performing their Medicare cost reports which saved them hundreds of thousands of dollars.

John has touched and changed so many lives, starting with me, his devoted wife, Christine Llewellyn. Brought together by God during some difficult life changes each of us were going through, we were married in 2010, and almost never spent a day apart.

His son, Ray, is married to Jessica, and they have three wonderful children, Chase, Kamden, and Abigal. Lindsay, his beautiful daughter, also has three incredible children, Avi, Maci, and Griffin. We are all going to miss him terribly.

Until God calls me home, John will live through me. I hope the love he has given me will pass through me onto other lives that I touch, especially my children, Cole, Amber, Cassidy, Sydney and Brooke, my two precious grandbabies, Auden and Owen, my parents Bud and Beverly, and my fabulous sister, Kay. And let's not forget our two dogs, Dante and Chica, whom John treasured with all his heart.

Save a place for me, my love. I'll see you soon.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting family at this time. Online condolences may be expressed at MWCS.WS

records.name: Marjorie D. Nimz
records.summary: Marjorie D. Nimz, 99, of Wausau, Wisconsin passed away on March 14, 2026.
records.body:
On March 14, 2026, Marjorie D. Nimz, age 99, of Wausau, Wisconsin passed away. She has gone to meet the love of her life, Lynn M. Nimz, who died in 2011.

Surviving family members include two children, Holly (Dan) Bembenek, Scott (Peggy) Nimz and daughter-in-law Kris Nimz. Grandchildren include Sara (Mike) Ogurek, Lisa Nimz and Ryan Nimz. Great-grandchildren include Jered and Jake Ogurek. Sister-in-law Lilly Buchholz along with nieces and nephews, survive.

Marjorie is preceded in death by her son, Gerald and brother, Bob Buchholz. Marjorie's (Marge's) long life was filled with well-known attributes in sewing, crafting, cooking and master gardening.

She crafted teddy bears from reclaimed mink coats, baked specialty cakes for weddings and anniversaries, and grew every type of orchid imaginable. In later years, her gardening focused on tomatoes, growing "just a few plants from seeds" to giving away over fifty plants every spring. She volunteered and served the DAV, the Talent Shop, Meals on Wheels, and St. Stephen Church circles. She loved the sunshine and her backyard wildlife. Spending time up north and visiting a casino were travels she considered "therapy".

In later years she enjoyed phone calls and visits, whether planned or a surprise. She also appreciated getting precious cards, especially the ones Mickey sent her. She kept in touch with her former neighbors Diana and Pam, enjoyed her visits with Pastor Jen, Ryan and his breakfast sandwiches, and groundskeepers Jack and Linda. She exchanged daily phone calls with her late friend Betty. She often joked about seeing friends at the "imaginary" Charlie's Bar. Perhaps her earthly name for heaven, we hope to meet her there one day for her homemade potato salad, peanut bars, and delicious lemon meringue pie.

A private interment will take place at Restlawn Memorial Park.

records.name: Melva E. Nahring
records.summary: Melva E. Nahring, 102, of Fenwood passed away on March 9, 2026.
records.body:
Melva Nahring (Mueller) at the age of 102 joined her husband, Vernon, on March 9, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital with her children beside her.

Melva was born on February 20, 1924 in the Town of Wien to Edwin Sr and Alma Mueller (Grosskrutz) the oldest of 8 children. She went to the local one room schoolhouse and graduated from 8th grade. Melva was baptized, attended Sunday School and was confirmed at St. John ELCA on Hwy N, Edgar. At the same church, on June 25, 1947 she married her sweetheart, Vernon, and kept her promise to him for 73 years. They were blessed with four children, Dianne, Janet passed away as an infant, DuWaine and Dawn.

After their marriage they joined St. Peters Lutheran Church, Fenwood where mom was active in choir, women's aid group, was the janitor, on altar guild and also belonged to the Fenwood Homemakers.

Her working career started at Marathon Electric until marriage and family became her next full-time job. She again went back to work at the Edgar Basket Factory which is now Edgar Standard Company. Her job was sewing liners for basinets, picnic baskets, doll furniture, etc. Sewing was her pride and joy. She became Fenwoods seamstress. If anyone had a sewing problem, needed something hemmed, a pocket put in a new pair of slacks or how about sewing a wedding dress? Well, she could do it all! She made several prom dresses, wedding dresses, miniature bride dresses and the list could go on. Thanks, mom, for sewing our wedding dresses.

Between sewing and gardening she had time to can fruit, make jelly, wine and bake. For our holiday dinner she would make apple pie (no one got that recipe), pumpkin pies (she'd say, just following the recipe on the pumpkin pie can), we knew that wasn't true. Another thing everyone looked forward to was our birthdays. We always got a cool whip container of fresh baked chocolate chip cookies (her recipe). Then there was a time you would get a call, 'I just made a lazy daisy or a chocolate cake with peanut butter frosting, you want to come over and join me'?

Mom is survived by 3 children, 9 grandchildren, 16 great grandchildren and 1 great great grandchild. Dianne Bauman and her family, Gwen (Ed) Zajac and their son Nick; Ken (Debbie) Bauman and his daughters, Chelsea (Wylie) Frahm, and Savannah (Will Raatz); Keith (Cassi) Bauman and their children, Korbyn (Shynia Stahel) and their daughter Lainey, Chase and Collins; Duwaine (Terry Weber-Nahring) Nahring and his sons Tyler (Janalee) and their sons, Caiden and Deklan; Eric (Dana) and their children, Claire, Colt and Camryn; Dawn (Burt) DeYoung and their family, Brenda Polivka (friend Amy); Brian (Jessie) Polivka and their children, Jeret, Jaxon and Brynn; Becky (Darrel Schwartz) Polivka and their sons, Branson and Hudson; and Jennifer DeYoung. The first gift any of the grandchildren received was a homemade baby blanket made by mom. Mom was also survived by one brother, Elgart Mueller of Edgar.

She was preceded in death by her parents, daughter, and husband, her brothers, Edwin (Sonny), Frankie, Allen, Elton; sisters, Darlene (Dotte), Arline, her sister in laws Shirley Mueller, Anita Mueller, Eleanor Mueller, and Angeline Woldt. Brother in laws, Ervin Mueller, Sr, Willard Wiesman, Lester Koehler, Lewis Woldt and son in law Gary Bauman, two infant granddaughters Beth Polivka and Connie DeYoung.

The village of Fenwood must have interesting things to offer their residents, as Vernon and Melva Nahring found it. They were the ones that lived there the longest and both reached the age of 102. Dad had found a new home for him and mom that is beautiful and peaceful. He waited five years with open arms for mom to join him. Congrats dad and mom in finding that beautiful and peaceful home with our Lord in heaven.

Funeral services for Melva will be 11:00 am Saturday, March 21, at Hope Lutheran Church, 224556 State Hwy 97, Edgar. Reverend Andrew Cave will officiate. Burial will be in the Fenwood Cemetery. Visitation will be on Saturday, from 9:00 until time of service at the Church.

Special thank you to Aspirus Hospital for the good care you gave our mom while she was there. She was always comfortable and well taken care of. The nurses and doctors were very concerning and compassionate.

Thank you, mom, for the apple pies, pumpkin pies and chocolate chip cookies. You will be missed dearly.

records.name: Raymond Sleck
records.summary: Raymond Sleck, 90, passed away on March 8, 2026.
records.body:
Raymond "Ray" Sleck, 90, passed away on March 8, 2026.

Ray was born on February 13, 1936, to the late Walter and Mary (Pheifer) Sleck. Throughout his life, Ray was known for his quick mind, and the simple joys that brought happiness.

Ray loved the outdoors in Northern Wisconsin. Camping, hunting, and fishing were among his favorite pastimes. He also had a passion for cars which led to a long career in the auto industry. He never turned down a good game of checkers. Ray enjoyed living in Las Vegas for over 30 years. He especially enjoyed the challenge of the casino Baccarat tables and the bright lights of the city.

Music and karaoke were a special part of Ray's life. He had a beautiful singing voice and loved country western songs. Friends and family will fondly remember how he would often answer the phone with a song, and how he made it a tradition to call loved ones on their birthdays to sing "Happy Birthday."

Ray had a big heart for animals, especially dogs. Over the years he adored many, but held a special place in his heart for his four beloved companions. At home, he took pride in keeping things tidy and neat. He also enjoyed good food and loved going out for ribs and fried chicken. Quiet evenings watching Hallmark movies—especially Christmas movies—were among the simple pleasures he treasured most.

Ray is survived by his wife of 37 years, Denise, whom he married in 1989. He is also survived by his children, Anthony, David, Roger (Cyndi) and Kathy; as well as many grandchildren and great-grandchildren who will carry forward his memory.

In addition to his parents, Ray was preceded in death by his first wife, Leilani Kerner; one brother; and two sisters.

Ray will be remembered for his songs and the joy he found in life's everyday moments. His memory will live on in the hearts of those who knew and loved him.

Family and friends may go to MWCS.WS to leave words of remembrance.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family.

records.name: Robert J. VanLanen
records.summary: Robert J. VanLanen, 67, of Weston passed away on March 8, 2026.
records.body:
Robert John VanLanen, known to most as "Big Bob," passed away on March 8, 2026, at his home in Weston, Wisconsin, surrounded by the love of his family and under the care of Aspirus Hospice. He was 67 years old and had faced a short but courageous battle with a progressive disease.

Bob was born on September 13, 1958, in Eland, Wisconsin, to William and Mary VanLanen. He lived a full life defined by hard work, a big personality, and an even bigger heart.

Bob graduated from Wittenberg-Birnamwood High School and went on to build a life rooted in grit and determination. A skilled welder by trade, he was also an entrepreneur who never stopped pursuing new ventures. Over the years he owned and operated several local businesses, including Rainbow Snow, The Sugar Shack, and The Judy Peterson Dance Studio. No matter what project he took on, Bob approached it with determination, skill, and pride.

Bob married Diane in 1979 and quickly became an important father figure to her two sons, Ronald and Robert. In 1985, they welcomed their first and only child together, Krystle. In 2025, Bob experienced one of the greatest joys of his life when he became a grandfather to his beloved granddaughter, Delaney.

To his family and friends, Bob was someone you could lean on and believe in. He was a devoted husband, father, grandfather, brother, and friend whose generosity, humor, and ability to fix just about anything left a lasting impression on everyone who knew him. Whether it was repairing a broken machine, solving a tough problem, or simply lending a helping hand, Bob was always there.

He loved spending time with his family, cheering on the Green Bay Packers, golfing with his close friends, hunting and being outdoors, and taking memorable trips to Mexico with those he loved most. Known for his quick wit and sarcasm, Bob balanced his humor with a deeply generous heart. He was thrifty when it came to himself but incredibly giving when it came to helping others.

Bob is survived by his beloved wife, Diane (Holmes); his daughter, Krystle (Nelson) Drees; his cherished granddaughter, Delaney; and his stepson, Robert Ramsey. He is also survived by his siblings, Cindy (Dan) Borcherding, Russell (Janice) VanLanen, and Gregory (Cindy) VanLanen, along with many nieces, nephews, extended family members, and friends who will miss him dearly.

He was preceded in death by his parents, William and Mary VanLanen; his stepson, Ronald Ramsey; his mother-in-law, Irene Holmes; his father-in-law, Edwin Holmes; and his uncle, Jack VanLanen.

A Celebration of Life will be held on Sunday, March 29, 2026, at 1:00 PM at Trailside Bar. Family and friends are invited to gather, share stories, and honor the life of a man who meant so much to so many.

"All our times have come… Seasons don't fear the reaper, nor do the wind, the sun or the rain."

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the VanLanen family with arrangements. You may sign the family guestbook at www.ascendweston.org

records.name: Theodore P. Haack Jr.
records.summary: Theodore P. Haack Jr., 80, of Wausau passed away peacefully on March 11, 2026.
records.body:
Theodore "Ted" P. Haack, Jr. a man of steady loyalty, sharp sense of humor, and a legendary love for sports and the open road, passed away peacefully at his home in Wausau on Wednesday, March 11, 2026.

Ted's story began in Marshfield on May 1, 1945. It was there he found his two greatest passions: basketball and his high school sweetheart, Cheryl Meissner. After playing for UW-Marathon, Ted transferred to UW-Madison, after which he and Cheryl married on August 20, 1966. For nearly 60 years, they were a team, building a life in Wausau that was defined by family, hard work, and the occasional cross-country adventure.

To Ted, life was meant to be experienced. When his four children were young, Ted was famous for "carting" the family across the country. These weeks-long road trips were more than just vacations. They were a rite of passage, filled with mapped-out routes, roadside landmarks, and a lifetime of shared, humorous stories that the family continues to fondly recall during quiet moments together.

During his long career with Wausau Insurance, Ted was known for his dedication, eventually transferring to the Milwaukee office in 1996. But when he retired in 2002, the pull of Central Wisconsin was too strong to ignore. He and Cheryl returned to Wausau, the place that had always truly felt like home.

Ted was the ultimate Wisconsin sports fan. If there was a game on, you knew where to find him. Whether Ted was cheering on his beloved Badgers or meticulously organizing his vast collection of sports cards, his enthusiasm was infectious. Ted didn't just collect cards. He collected the stories and connections that came with them, spending countless hours and connecting with fellow hobbyists, fueled by a genuine enthusiasm that never faded.

Above all, Ted was the cornerstone of his family. He is survived by his wife, Cheryl; their children, Brian, Bradley, Jeffrey, and Jennifer; and his three grandchildren, Cassandra, Emma, and Evan. He was also the proud great-grandfather of Vincent. He was preceded in death by his parents, Theodore, Sr. and Verna Haack.

A celebration of Ted's life and his final "road trip" will be held at 1 pm on Tuesday, March 24, 2026, at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Ave, Wausau, WI 54401.

Visitation will be held from 11 am to 1 pm until the time of the service at the funeral home. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: William L. Dahlke
records.summary: William L. Dahlke, 84, passed away peacefully on February 26, 2026.
records.body:
William (Bill) L Dahlke passed away peacefully on February 26, 2026, at age 84, surrounded by his loving family. Bill was under the care of Compassus Hospice and Amethyst Health of Wausau.

He was born May 9, 1941, to William L and Fern A (Poppe) Dahlke. After high school graduation, Bill attended the UW Marathon County campus for 2 years and he served in the Army National Guard for 6 years. Bill met Margarete Graefe while working part time in his father's shoe repair business. They were married August 1, 1964, and were blessed with 2 children, Connie (Jim) Brown and Curt Dahlke, 2 grandchildren Katelyn (Henry) and Bono (Emily), and 2 great-grandchildren Chloe and Lewis.

Bill was employed full time by Wausau Area Transit System (WATS) and was dedicated to providing compassionate customer care until his retirement. His career choice showcased his love for cars, and following retirement he kept busy delivering car parts, reminiscing on his favorite cars from the 1950's. Bill was also passionate about music. His talent for singing led him to participate in the Wausau and Stevens Point Barbershop Choruses, a barbershop quartet, St Mark's church choir, and most recently the Rib Mountain Lutheran church choir. When Bill wasn't working or singing, he loved taking his family's dogs for walks. There wasn't a "bad" dog out there according to Bill; he seemed to form a connection with every furry friend he came to know.

Aside from his career and hobbies, Bill had a deep faith and felt service to God was his number one priority. His testament to God was clear; for Bill had the kindest heart and most generous spirit.

Bill was preceded in death by his parents and grandparents. He is survived by many other relatives and friends.

The funeral service will be held Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 11:00AM at Rib Mountain Lutheran Church, located at 227150 Harrier Ave., Rib Mountain, WI 54401, with Rev. Dwayne Lueck presiding. Visitation will be held from 10:00AM until the time of service. Bill will be laid to rest at Restlawn Memorial Park in the town of Texas with full military honors provided by VFW Post 388 Honor Guard.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the Dahlke Family with arrangements. You may share your condolences at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Aubrey Charles "Porky" Fish
records.summary: Aubrey Charles "Porky" Fish, 83, formerly of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away with his wife by his side.
records.body:
Growing up in Wausau, Aubrey loved participating in any and all sports. From his early love of Little League Baseball, downhill skiing, (which he did into his 80's), to competitive ski jumping. He was an outstanding athlete. His love for ski jumping earned him numerous awards including Longest Jump and Most Graceful in tournaments around the upper midwest.

He attended Wausau High School where he served as Class President, was a member of the 1960 State Championship Basketball Team, went to the WI State Tennis Tournament, and was the recipient of the American Legion Gold Medal for Outstanding Athlete. He was also inducted as a member of the Wausau East HS Hall of Fame.

Aubrey attended the University of Wisconsin-Madison, later transferring to UW – Stevens Point on a football scholarship, where he was named to the All-Conference Football Team twice. He was a member of the University Diving/Swim Team and was a two-time Wisconsin State AAU Diving Champion.

After graduating from college, Aubrey managed a United Parcel Service distribution center in southeastern Wisconsin. He left UPS to begin his insurance career with Wausau Ins. Co. serving as a Benefits Mgr. for the Twin City Region. He earned a CLU Designation and went on to work as a Life/Benefit Mgr. for a national insurance company.

Aubrey was beloved by all who had the good fortune to know him. He had a full arsenal of jokes and somehow never forgot any of them. He loved to tell stories. Many of his friends had heard some of his "A. Fish" stories many times but somehow they got funnier each time he told them. He loved making people laugh.

Patti and Aubrey spent their time between Minnesota and Arizona. He enjoyed nothing more than spending time with his family and friends. He loved traveling, playing tennis, golfing, skiing and, of course, was an avid Packer and Badger fan.

Aubrey is survived by his loving wife of 51 years, Patti (Hayes) Fish, his daughter, Sara (Scott) Martinka, and sons Aubrey, Brett (Ellen) and Scott and grandchildren Jonny and Lainey Martinka, Brandon (Amanda) May, Dawson and Scottie Fish and Allie (Jatin) Bansal, brothers Bill (Sharon) and Ron (Betty) Fish, and brother-in-law Mike (Jan) Hayes. Aubrey was preceded in death by his parents, Aubrey and Lucille (Sippl) Fish, his brothers and sister Jerry, Norman and Sally (Fish) Mergen and granddaughter Elena Fish.

Aubrey was an amazing person, kind-hearted, generous, warm, loving, incredibly funny, and a constant prankster. He filled our every day with stories, surprises, sunshine and lots of laughter. He was a loyal friend, brother, husband, dad and Bapa. We will all miss him so very much.

records.name: Judith A. Brewster
records.summary: Judith "Judy" A. Brewster, 86, passed away Wednesday, March 18, 2026, under the care of Compassus Hospice at Wellington Assisted Living, Wausau.
records.body:
She was born March 2, 1940, in Coleman, Michigan, daughter of the late Albert and Ardis (Koontz) Riegle. On August 8, 1959, she married her high school sweetheart, Wayne Brewster in Coleman, Michigan. He preceded her in death on December 13, 2012.

Judy grew up always having horses and the family spent much of their time moving to different dairy farms, where she embraced the hard work and rewards of renovating and country living. She loved being outdoors whether mowing the lawn or tending to her many flowers, especially her petunias. Judy and her family took pride in growing their own vegetables and preserving them through canning. She was also a gifted baker, known for her delicious pies that became a favorite among family and friends.

Survivors include her children, Angela (Jim) Kanitz, Lisa (Bruce) Stein, Steven (Dee) Brewster, John (Tonya) Brewster, Amanda (Will) Heiner; brother, Richard (Pat) Riegle; many grandchildren, great-grandchildren, nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends.

Besides her parents, she was preceded in death by a sister, Barbara Davison.

records.name: Kathleen C. Tornow
records.summary: Kathleen Tornow, age 100, went home to be with her Savior, Jesus Christ on March 13, 2026.
records.body:
Kathleen was born in Neenah, WI to Carroll and Adeline (Fahrenkrug) Rogers.

Kathleen graduated from Neenah High School in 1943. She was part of an all-women's singing group called Chaminade Women's Chorus and took voice lessons at Lawrence University.

She met her future husband, Norman R. Tornow in 1948, after he returned from military service in World War II. They were married on October 8, 1949 in Menasha, WI.

She was preceded in death by Carroll Rogers (Father), Adeline (Fahrenkrug) Rogers (Mother), and Norman R. Tornow (Spouse).

Kathleen and Norman had four children, Kristine (Don) Rasmussen-Weston, WI, Gail (Roger) Duck-Abilene, TX, Thomas (AnnaMarie) Tornow-Wisconsin Rapids, WI and Theodore (Ruth) Tornow-Surprise, AZ.

And grandchildren…Joel (Collette) Duck, John Duck (Karine Trudel), Jason (Ali) Duck, Nathan (Hannah) Duck, Gregory (Amanda) Duck, Anders (Amy) Rasmussen, Kara (George) Willett, Marta (Joshua) Thunder, Zachary (Jessica) Tornow, Krista (Kevin) Lien, Brett Tornow, Lucas Tornow and Steven Pennent. And added to these wonderful grandchildren are her great grands, 23 at this point. She loved them all very much and made sure that each of them received birthday cards and as much love as she could put in one of those cards. Not to mention her prayers for every one of them.

Her nieces and nephews were also well loved and prayed for.

Her walk with the Lord was one of reading Our Daily Bread and her Bible. We all rejoice that she is with her Lord and Savior and in His presence.

records.name: Leroy Tollar
records.summary: Leroy (Butch) Tollar, age 84, of Wausau passed away peacefully on March 17th at Aspirus Hospital under Hospice Care.
records.body:
He was born December 26, 1941, in Wausau, Wisconsin to Florence Nelson and Arthur P. Tollar.

He is survived by his children Jenny Abraham (Lance,) Mandy Vogel (Jesse), William Tollar, and Leroy Tollar; granddaughter Caroline; and grandson Daniel; as well as his close friend Brian Doerr.

Butch was a very simple and old fashion man. In his younger years, he enjoyed hunting and fishing. And in the last few years, he enjoyed doing his puzzles with the help of his "special" care giver.

Great thanks go to Wausau Hospital and their wonderful staff for the compassionate care they provided to Butch in his most recent hospitalization.

records.name: Logan Daniel Roloff
records.summary: Logan Daniel Roloff, age 26, of Weston, passed away on March 13, 2026.
records.body:
Logan was born on December 1, 1999, in Wausau to Dan and Christy (Raatz) Roloff. He was the younger brother to Alexis who was not only his sibiling but a trusted friend and confidant, they shared an unbreakable bond. Alexis passed away on April 30, 2019.

Logan grew up in Weston and attended D.C. Everest High School. He graduated in 2018 and attended University of Wisconsin Stevens Point where he received an Associates Degree. Logan was a hard worker and started working summers at the age of 15 for Scott's Enterprises. After receiving his degree, he then became a full time employee with Riverview Construction, as a Lead Maintenance Technician. He was known for his strong work ethic and dedication who formed lasting friendships with those he worked beside. He had a gift of turning the ordinary work moments into stories worth telling, many times unforgettable and hilarious.

Logan very much enjoyed the outdoors. He loved snowboarding and had an adventurous spirit always eager to seek out new challenges. He enjoyed golfing and fishing with his buddies, he was always up for a friendly wager. He enjoyed many hunting seasons with family on their property in Athens. Logan even shot a bear at age 12 when he was selected for a youth mentor hunt. Whether it is was camping, yearly vacations, or just being together, family was important. Logan also enjoyed playing pool and attending Brewers games.

Logan will be remembered for being selfless, kind hearted, having a great sense of humor, and his empathy for others. Most importantly, Logan would do anything for anyone and always put others before himself. Logan will truly be missed by all who knew him.

Logan is survived by his loving parents, Dan and Christy; paternal grandparents, Dick and Carol Roloff of Weston; maternal grandmother, Shari Raatz of Edgar; uncle, Jon (Kathy) Raatz; aunt, Vicki (Jason) Roloff; cousins, Hunter (Heather), Mitchell (Nikki and their son, Callahan), Will (Savannah), Ethan, Katja, Mya, Landon, and Atticus. He was preceded in death by his sister, Alexis; maternal grandfather, William Raatz; uncle, Matt Raatz; and the family dog, Owen Wilson.

records.name: Russell Earl Proctor
records.summary: Russell Earl Proctor, age 69, passed away on Friday March 13th, 2026.
records.body:
He leaves behind a legacy of quiet strength, devotion, and integrity.

For 38 years, Russell dedicated himself to his career at Weyerhauser as a Journeyman electrician. Russell was a family man, who balanced life's demands with his deep passion for the open road, finding freedom and joy riding and working on his motorcycles. He was a leader among his brothers, and wore his colors with Pride.

Russell was a devoted father, and cherished spending time with his son Zechariah teaching him how to safely shoot, as well as with his daughter Brooke – often taking motorcycle rides to explore state parks.

A student of military history, he held a deep respect for those who served. He was guided by a strong faith, and maintained a reverence for God through all seasons of life. In his later years, he served others through Alcoholics Anonymous, offering support and leadership to those battling addiction.

Though a traumatic brain injury changed his path, Russell fought with courage and taught us the true meaning of resilience. Those who knew Russell will remember his humor and kindness, which shone through even during the toughest days.

records.name: William Henry Hamlin
records.summary: William "Bill" Henry Hamlin, age 64, of Wausau, passed away unexpectedly on March 15, 2026.
records.body:
Born on April 13, 1961, in Ely, MN, Bill was the son of William "Luke" Werner Hamlin and Mary Lee (Dietzler) Hamlin.

He graduated from Tomahawk High School in 1979, and then Bill attended the University of Wisconsin Stout. He graduated with a Bachelor of Science Degree in Industrial Technology – Product Development in 1985 and also received his Associates of Applied Science Degree – Mechanical Design 1982. Bill worked in the consulting engineering and manufacturing fields as a designer for electrical systems and controls, water treatment skid systems, HVAC systems, and structural detailing at Siemens Water Technologies, Lang Associates, Clark Dietz, and Wausau Windows and Walls. Bill most recently was employed at Steel King Industries of Stevens Point as a Senior Designer of pallet racking systems. Bill was an all-in employee, taking on the difficult projects and prided himself in providing training and mentoring the newer employees.

Bill was a man of many passions including: summer family trips to Ely, MN to canoe, fish and visit his mother who vacationed there for the summer, camping with family and friends, collecting and riding motorcycles, snowmobiling in the winter, and a blood bank donor of more than 100 units in total. Bill dreamed of retiring and starting his own brewery, he enjoyed making his beer and recently began making wine to share. He didn't care if it was an award-winning beer, he only cared that someone could enjoy it.

One of his greatest joys was being a part of the boys' boy scouting adventure. Bill was a Cubmaster for Pack 462 and instrumental in helping restart Pack 400 and Troop 400. He was a parent committee member in Troop 409 for a total of 25 years witnessing firsthand our sons attain the rank of a Life Scout and two as Eagle Scouts. Bill stayed on as an adult leader to offer leadership for Troop 400 after his sons finished scouting. He built many relationships with leaders and scouts along the way that he cherished.

Bill was often known as the guy who would go out of his way to help a friend or someone in need. He was a faithful Christian man seeing the best in everyone with a great sense of humor. He was most proud of his sons and the men they have become. He was a devoted husband and father who cherished time with his family and his dog, Maisie.

Survivors include his loving wife of 36 years, Melody (Schickert); his children, Hunter Hamlin (fiancée, Lauren Lonski) of Milwaukee, David Hamlin of Wausau, and Dylan Hamlin of Wauwatosa; siblings, Helen (Rick) Schroder of Hugo, MN, Phil Hamlin of Tomahawk, and Rob (Kathy) Hamlin of Mantorville, MN; in-laws, Larry (Toni) Schickert, Lance (Carmen) Schickert of Gretna, NE, Lonny (Stacy) Schickert of Rhinelander; and many nieces, nephews, other extended family members, and friends who will miss him dearly. He was preceded in death by his father, William "Luke" Hamlin.

records.name: Andrew John Plath
records.summary: Andrew John Plath, 68, of Wausau, Wisconsin, died after a two-year battle with cancer.
records.body:
Andrew John Plath, 68, of Wausau, WI, died Saturday, March 14, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital after a two-year battle with cancer.

Andy, as he was known by most people, was born December 28, 1957, in Wausau, WI, the son of Robert Plath and Marion (Werner) Plath. He graduated from Wausau West High School, then earned a Bachelor of Arts in International Studies from the University of Wisconsin-Eau Claire, followed by an Associate Degree in Insurance from Northcentral Technical College.

Andrew's willingness to step into leadership roles began in high school, when he was elected President of the Student Council in 1975. He states in one of his blog posts: "My service in the Student Council, particularly as President, taught me many things. For one, if you want change, you must work for it to make that change happen."

He spent his entire career in production with Kolbe Windows & Doors, but outside his career, Andy's passions lay elsewhere. He loved photography, hiking, biking, and his church, Trinity Lutheran. He combined many of his passions. He took photographs primarily of nature, but he also captured photos while hiking and biking to many places.

His passions led him to serve on the bike and pedestrian subcommittee of the Wausau Area Metropolitan Planning Organization and the City of Wausau's Bike and Pedestrian Advisory Committee. He was delighted with the development of the River Edge Walkway. He was active with Wausau Wheelers and enjoyed the camaraderie of riding with fellow bikers. He loved Rib Mountain, the Ice Age Trail, Wausau's riverfront, and Wausau Whitewater.

He spent many years supporting the Wausau Christian Outreach for the Visually Impaired. This ministry provides an opportunity for blind and visually impaired people to experience fellowship and social activities, to network with others and learn about available resources, and to share in the Gospel of Jesus Christ and grow in faith.

Andy also spoke fondly of the Band of Brothers from Trinity, an organization whose goal is to develop, disciple, and mentor men to lead in their families, workplaces, and communities.

He created a blog on Medium: https://andrewplath26.medium.com/, where he wrote on many topics and combined his photos. In one called the River of Life, Andy wrote: "It is a question of age. I look old, but I do not feel old. I still want to look for new possibilities, but time is running out. The biological clock is now at 8:00 pm. The end of life is coming quicker than we could possibly think. In time, God will call me home."

Andy is survived by his sister, Julie, and her two sons, Timothy and Daniel, as well as a great niece, Willow, all in Wausau, and multiple cousins. He was preceded in death by his father, mother, and brother-in-law, as well as numerous other grandparents, aunts, uncles, and cousins.

A memorial service (Celebration of Life) will be held at Trinity Lutheran Church, 501 Stewart Avenue, Wausau, WI, on April 25, tentatively scheduled for 11 am. More details will follow and be updated via Facebook.

records.name: Bradley J. Goessl
records.summary: Bradley J. Goessl, 61, of Wausau, was taken to his Lord and Savior on March 16, 2026.
records.body:
Bradley "Brad" Goessl, age 61, was taken to our Lord and Savior on March 16, 2026.

Brad was born on June 6, 1964, in Marshfield, Wisconsin, to Robert and Mary Goessl. A graduate of Medford High School, he went on to earn a degree in Police Science from Northcentral Technical College. Brad began his career serving his community as a Sheriff's Deputy for Vilas County before transitioning into his lifelong passion for building. For 30 years, he was a dedicated general contractor and an award-winning builder and dealer for Wisconsin Homes.

On June 22, 1996, Brad married Holly Hintze in Wausau, Wisconsin. Together, they shared a life full of adventure, with their favorite memories being the many cruises they took together, where Brad loved exploring new environments, trying different foods, and meeting new people.

An active member of his community, Brad was a proud member of the Wausau Elks Lodge #248. He was always the first to step forward to help with charity events, and he especially cherished playing Santa for the Elks' Good Fellowship program, delivering gifts to children during the holidays. Brad also served as an assistant curling coach and worked with the Little Rice Volunteer Fire Department.

An avid hunter and fisherman, Brad found great joy in the outdoors. He was also a enthusiastic card player who never turned down a game of poker, blackjack, or cribbage with friends.

Brad is survived by his loving wife, Holly; his mother, Mary J. Goessl; his sister, Mary Beth Bixby (Wayne); and his brothers, Mark Goessl (Kathy) and Michael Goessl (Mary), nieces and nephews: Laura, Adam, Chelsea, Lindsey, Jacqueline, Jonathon, Jacob, and Jordan and several grand nieces and nephews. He is further survived by brother-in-law, Herbert O. Hintze (Rose); sister-in-law, Haley Hintze; niece, Jessi Drill (David); nephew, Christopher.

He was preceded in death by his father, Robert Goessl; his sister, Mary Michelle "Shelly" Goessl; his grandparents; several aunts and uncles; and his in-law, Herbert and Alice Hintze.

Visitation will be held on Friday, March 27, 2026, from 10:00 AM to 1:00 PM at Brainard Funeral Home (522 Adams Street, Wausau, WI). A Lodge of Sorrows service will be held during this time.

A Celebration of Life will follow at 2:00 PM at the Wausau Elks Lodge #248 (414 Scott Street, Wausau, WI).

If you would prefer to make a donation, in Brad's memory, his favorite charity was the Special Olympics of Taylor County.

records.name: Dianne Krajewski DeLisle Leigh
records.summary: Dianne Krajewski DeLisle Leigh passed away on March 5, 2026, in Madison, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Dianne Krajewski DeLisle Leigh passed away on March 5, 2026 surrounded by her daughters and son-in-law in the comfort of her home in Madison, Wisconsin.

Dianne grew up in Rothschild, WI and graduated in 1959 from DC Everest High School. She worked as a bank teller after graduation for a while in Wausau. She Married Tom DeLisle in 1962. She and Tom moved to St. Anthony Village, Minnesota and Dianne worked in the dermatology department at the University of Minnesota. When they moved back to the Wausau area Tom worked for his father Glenn DeLisle and Dianne worked for the DeLisle Company doing accounting.

After she and Tom divorced Dianne went back to school to fulfill her dream of higher education. She spent two years at the University of Wisconsin Extension Marathon County, then moved to Madison to attend classes at the University of Wisconsin Madison, ultimately receiving a Masters in Social Work from the UW Madison. Dianne worked for the VA in Madison for a time. Then she got a position with Dane County Child Protective Services and worked there for years.

Dianne met Terry Heiden at St. Anthony Retreat Center in Marathon City, Wisconsin. Terry went to pursue a third college degree at the UW Madison and he and Dianne reconnected. Terry also became a social worker in Dane County. They had a lot in common. She married Terry in 1993 and had 16 years with him before Terry passed away in 2009.

After Dianne retired from Dane County Human Services, she worked for the Aging and Disability Resource Center, was a member of the Area on Aging Advisory Council and was a court appointed Guardian.

Dianne is preceded in death by her parents, Bill and Ruth Krajewski, her brother William Craig Krajewski, sister-in-law Diana Krajewski, sister-in-law Sue Krajewski, her first husband Thomas Edward DeLisle, her second husband Terrence Dennis Heiden and her sister-in-law Sister Jolene Heiden, SSND School Sisters of Notre Dame.

Dianne leaves behind two daughters, Michelle Lindner (Ray), and Alisa DeLisle, two grandchildren Nicholas Lindner and Delaney Lindner, two brothers Richard Krajewski and Nicholas Krajewski and many nieces and nephews.

Dianne was a force of nature. She always did everything she could for others. She was a wonderful example for the entire family.

A Memorial Service will be held at 11:00 a.m. Friday, May 15, 2026 at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Avenue, Wausau. Visitation will be held from 9:00 a.m. on Friday, May 15, 2026 until the time of services all at the Stewart Avenue funeral home. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Glen A. Engebretson
records.summary: Glen A. Engebretson, 86, passed away on March 21, 2026, at Aspirus Hospital in Wausau.
records.body:
Glen A. Engebretson, 86, passed away on March 21, 2026, at Aspirus Hospital in Wausau. Glen was born on February 20, 1940, to the late Bert and Edith (Perry) Engebretson in Wausau. On June 26, 1965, Glen married the love of his life, Jeanette Wolf, at St. Therese Catholic Church in Rothschild. The couple shared 60 beautiful years together.

Glen was a 1958 graduate of Wausau High School. Glen dedicated 39 years to Northern Auto/CARQUEST, serving as a store manager. He also devoted 48 years of service as a volunteer fireman and EMT with the Rothschild Fire Department, retiring as Assistant Fire Chief. Glen was active in Scouting as a Cubmaster, enjoyed singing in the church choir, and spent many years pursuing a passion for HAM radio.

Glen is survived by Jeanette and three children: Brian (Amy) Engebretson; Julie (Jason) Barnum; and Brenda Engebretson, all of Wausau. Glen is also survived by two grandchildren, Rebecca Engebretson of Stevens Point and Nathanial (Ashley) Engebretson of Mosinee; two great-grandchildren, Leylynn and Colter; one brother, Walter (Margo) Engebretson; and many nieces and nephews. Glen was preceded in death by one son, Jeffrey, and two brothers, Bernard and Lester.

Funeral services will be held on March 27, 2026, at 10:30 a.m. at St. Michael's Catholic Church, 611 Stark Street, Wausau, with Rev. Al Slowiak officiating. Visitation will be held at the church from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service at the church. Burial will follow at Gate of Heaven Cemetery in Kronenwetter.

The family is deeply grateful to the staff at Mount View Care Center for the compassionate care provided to Glen.

Family and friends may leave words of remembrance at www.honorone.com

records.name: Jared J. Gorski
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Jared J. Gorski, 52, of Weston, was welcomed home to heaven on March 7, 2026.
records.body:
Confident in the promise that we are all Children of God, Jared J. Gorski was welcomed home to heaven on Saturday, March 7, 2026, at the age of 52. He passed peacefully at his home in Weston, surrounded by love and faith. Waiting to greet him were his mother Sharon; his brother, Jody; several grandparents, and many cherished family members and friends.

Born on April 6, 1973, to Leroy and Sharon Gorski, Jared grew up with his siblings Jennifer and Jody in a home filled with laughter, hard work, and a love of family. A devoted father, he took great pride in his daughter Ryean, who was the joy of his life.

Jared was known for his boundless generosity and his willingness to help anyone in need. A gifted mechanic, he could fix just about anything with a motor, and he especially loved classic muscle cars. One of his fondest memories was a trip to Tennessee as a teenager with his dad and grandpa to buy his first restoration-project car, back in the days of paper maps and spur-of-the-moment adventures.

A graduate of Wausau East High School, Jared later earned a welding certificate from Northcentral Technical College. Whether repairing, building, or creating, he was happiest when working with his hands. His craftsmanship and kindness touched countless lives.

Jared is survived by his daughter, Ryean (Hunter); his father, Leroy (Deb); his sister, Jennifer (Tim); as well as many loving aunts, uncles, cousins, nieces, nephews, and friends.

Private services and a celebration of life will be held at later dates.

The family invites everyone to honor Jared's memory in their own meaningful way – by lending a helping hand, taking a drive in a classic car, or simply sharing a smile with someone in need. He will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved him.

You may sign the family guestbook at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: John L. Rakow
records.summary: John L. Rakow, 82, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully at home.
records.body:
John Rakow, 82, passed away peacefully at home on March 20, 2026.

Born on December 17, 1943, in Wausau, Wisconsin, John was the son of the late Lewis and Ella (Jansean) Rakow. He grew up in Wausau and attended Wausau High School. On September 5, 1969, he married the love of his life, Susan Mildbrandt of Sturgeon Bay, in Madison. They shared 56 years of devoted marriage.

In 1969, following the passing of his father, John took over the family farm—a role that became one of his greatest lifelong passions.
