After selling his cows in 1993, he spent 14 years driving milk truck for Mullins Cheese until he was diagnosed with pancreatic cancer. True to his resilient spirit, John fought his illness with determination. Never one to sit still, he later worked for the Village of Maine, handling road maintenance and snow removal. When he wasn't needed by the Village, he could often be found helping neighboring farmers with fieldwork, driving tractor, or mowing yards around the community. Generosity came naturally to John; he was always ready to lend a hand without hesitation or complaint.

John proudly served the Town of Maine Fire Department for more than 40 years. He also groomed trails for the Trailmates Snowmobile Club, where he was a longtime member.

In his free time, John enjoyed fishing, snowmobiling, hunting, traveling, restoring model trains, cooking, and spending time with friends and family. He was a devoted husband, father, and grandfather whose happiest moments were spent gathering loved ones for holidays, birthdays, or any reason he could find to celebrate. Known for his warm laugh, easy conversation, and unmatched old fashioneds, John made everyone feel welcome. After retiring, he especially cherished day trips with friends to fish, snowmobile, or discover new restaurants.

John is survived by his wife, Susan (Mildbrandt) Rakow; his four children, Mike (Shelley) Rakow, Lori Rakow, Debbie (Jamie) Loiz, and Amy Rakow; and his three grandchildren, Alex Rakow, Adysen Loiz, and Skyler Loiz, all of Wausau. He is also survived by a large and loving extended family, including sisters and brothers in law, nieces, nephews, and cousins. He was preceded in death by his parents; his brother, Thomas; his sister, Ruth; his niece, Kristin; his brother in law, Jerry; and his sister in law, Sherry.

A Celebration of Life will be held on Thursday, March 26, 2026, at 11:00 AM at St. John Evangelical Lutheran Church (5304 N. 44th Avenue, Wausau, WI 54401). Pastor Maggie Westaby will officiate. Visitation will take place at the church from 9:00 AM until the time of service, with lunch to follow. Burial will be held at a later date at Berlin Cemetery.

The family extends heartfelt gratitude to the dedicated and compassionate staff at Aspirus, and a special thank you to the team at Aspirus At Home – Hospice for their exceptional care and support.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to St. John Evangelical Lutheran Church of Wausau, a charity of your choice, or you may plant a tree in John's memory.

records.name: Karen Y. Weeks
records.summary: Karen Y. Weeks, 82, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on March 17, 2026.
records.body:
Karen Y. Weeks, age 82 of Wausau, passed away peacefully on Tuesday, March 17th, 2026 while at Sylvan Crossing.

Born March 31, 1943 in Carlton County in Minnesota in her aunt's home. Karen was the daughter of Einer Polo and Dorothy (Wiener) Polo. She grew up in a rural home in Adolf MN. She had 3 siblings: brother Thomas Polo, sisters Wendy Polo (Kronenwetter), and Joanne Polo (Duluth MN).

She married David Bouyear in May of 1962. David served in the US Airforce, they had 3 children together. There were many moves over the years, including a few years in Okinawa. Then to Kincheloe Airforce base, where she started fostering children, along with working at an office on base. This is where she met Ralph Weeks. Ralph was gobsmacked with Karen. Karen set clear boundaries he would have to take on her kids and the foster kids. So after divorcing David, she married Ralph and moved to Mosinee. The family started working with Marathon county Social Services to do foster care here. She met Jim Prozinski and they decided to open a group home. She had issues with her mail carrier and decided to apply at the USPS and got a job in Wausau. So they moved to Wausau to start a new job and put together a group home. They fostered over 500 children over 50 years. She met so many great people over the years and they remained as friends.

Survivors are her children David Bouyear Jr. (Carol Davidowski), Lisa Reynolds (Kris), Lance Bouyear (Christine). Sisters Wendy Polo and Joanne Polo. Grandchildren David Bouyear III (Misti), Josh Reynolds (Brittany), Brendan Reynolds (Haley), Sasha Bouyear, and Antone Bouyear.

She was preceded in death by her parent Einer and Dorothy Polo, brother Thomas Polo, husbands David Bouyear and Ralph Weeks, and stepsons Cash Weeks and Russell Weeks.

A celebration of life will be scheduled later this year.

Family and friends may go to MWCS.WS to leave words of remembrance.

records.name: Patricia A. Seubert
records.summary: Patricia A. Seubert, 93, of Marathon, passed away at her home.
records.body:
Patricia A. Seubert, 93, of Marathon, passed away on Thursday, March 19, 2026, at her home. She was born to the late Alois and Esther (Dehlinger) Seubert in Wausau on January 3, 1933.

Patricia grew up in Marathon and graduated from Marathon High School. After completing school, she began her job with Wisconsin Public Service in Wausau. She would walk five blocks from her home to take the train to get to work in Wausau. After many years of dedicated service, she retired in 1995. In her free time, she enjoyed golfing, knitting, crocheting, counted cross stitch, crafts, baking, playing cards, and volunteering at St. Mary's. Over the years she was also an avid bowler and would travel nationally for tournaments and tours as a part of the National Women's Bowling Association. She loved the socializing and the friends that she made when bowling and playing cards.

Survivors include many cousins and extended family. She was preceded in death by her parents and sister, Sr. Charlotte Seubert, FSPA.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:30 a.m. on Wednesday, April 1, 2026, at St. Mary Catholic Church, Marathon, with Rev. Msgr. Joseph Diermeier presiding. Visitation will begin at 10:00 a.m. until the time of service, all at the church. Burial will follow in the parish cemetery. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Reuben H. Erickson
records.summary: Reuben H. Erickson, 84, formerly of Bowler, passed away on March 9, 2026.
records.body:
Reuben H. Erickson, 84, formerly of Bowler, passed away on Monday, March 9, 2026, at Evergreen Health Services in Shawano. He was born on April 6, 1941, in Tigerton, the son of Carl and Dorothy (Denny) Erickson.

Reuben proudly served in the United States Army from 1967 until 1973. He enjoyed deer hunting and fishing and was an avid Green Bay Packers fan who also liked watching Westerns. Reuben especially cherished family gatherings and cookouts.

Reuben is survived by his siblings, Diane (John) Cerveny, of Shawano and Ralph (Cindy) Erickson of Bowler; sisters-in-law, Grace Erickson of Kenosha and Barb Erickson of Shawano; brother-in-law, Donald Wurth of Gresham; special friends, Kevin and Sue Meverden and many nieces, nephews and other relatives.

Reuben was preceded in death by his parents; brothers, Charles, Roger, Herbert, Gary and Andy (Pam) Erickson; sisters, Jean Behnke and Karen Wurth and special friend, Carol Meverden.

A Funeral Service will be held at 1 PM on Saturday, March 28, 2026, at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg. Rev. Clifford Kessen will officiate. Visitation will be from 11 AM until the time of service at the funeral home. Military Rites will follow the funeral service in the funeral home parking lot.

records.name: Sergio E. Ortiz
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Sergio E. Ortiz, 85, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly on March 3, 2026.
records.body:
Sergio E. Ortiz, age 85, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly on Tuesday, March 3, 2026.

Sergio was born on February 25, 1941. He spent his entire life spreading the good word. He was an evangelist and minister and a devoted Seventh-day Adventist. He would spread God's word to anyone who had an ear to listen. Sergio dedicated his life as a follower of Christ and wanted everyone that he knew to know about God's word.

You may sign the family guestbook at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Shirley Buffington
records.summary: Shirley Buffington, 96, passed away peacefully on March 21, 2026.
records.body:
Shirley Buffington, 96, passed away peacefully on March 21, 2026, under the care of Compassus Hospice. She was born on February 14, 1930, in Marshfield, Wisconsin, to the late Alois and Theresa (Schroeder) Graettinger.

Shirley was united in marriage to George Buffington on June 23, 1951. He preceded her in death on July 25, 2005. Together they built a life centered on family, faith, and community.

She was a devoted mother to Jane (Henry) Lamovec; a loving grandmother to Patricia (Mike) Marquardt and Robert (Megan) Lamovec; a cherished great-grandmother to Sabrina (Scottie Subert) Heil and Mikaila (Jacob Weitz) Heil; and a proud great-great-grandmother to Hudson and Jebediah.

Shirley is further survived by her sister, Betty Borisch; and her brother-in-law, Bill (Peggy) Buffington.

She was preceded in death by her husband, George; her parents; her sister, Audrey Trudeau; and her brother, Jim Graettinger.

Shirley dedicated 41 years of service to the Marshfield Clinic in Marshfield, where she was known for her strong work ethic and kindness. Eight years ago, she moved to Wausau to be closer to her daughter. She made her home at Primrose, where she formed many meaningful friendships and enjoyed participating in activities. Shirley especially loved listening to Green Bay Packers games.

The family would like to extend their heartfelt gratitude to the staff at Primrose and Compassus Hospice for their compassionate care and support.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Friday, May 1, 2026, at St. Therese Catholic Church. Visitation will take place from 10:00 a.m. until the time of service at the church. Burial will take place at Gate of Heaven Cemetery in Marshfield at a later date.

Shirley will be deeply missed and lovingly remembered by all who knew her.

Family and friends may leave words of remembrance at www.honorone.com

records.name: Carol J. Burzinski
records.summary: Carol J. Burzinski, age 88, passed away on March 23, 2026.
records.body:
Carol Burzinski was welcomed into the arms of Jesus and reunited with her husband, Gordy, on March 23, 2026, at Mount View Care Center, surrounded by family and friends while under the care of Compassus Hospice in Wausau.

She was born on November 12, 1937, to the late Elmer and Martha Sickler. On November 13, 1971, she married Gordon in Rothschild, Wisconsin, beginning a life together filled with love, laughter, and cherished memories.

Carol was a member of Mt. Calvary Lutheran Church. She found joy in flower gardening, fishing up north with her family, trips to the casino, and watching the birds and wildlife in her backyard. She also loved bowling and spending time with those she loved most. Carol had a big, generous heart and was always eager to lend a helping hand. She especially enjoyed assisting her daughter with school projects, from assembling treat bags for students to labeling thousands of books for Rollin' Readers.

She is lovingly survived by her son, David Burzinski; her daughter, Lynn Burzinski; her brother, James (Tricia) of Tucson, Arizona; as well as many nieces, nephews, cousins, and dear friends.

In addition to her parents and her husband, Gordy, she was preceded in death by her brother, Wayne Sickler.

Carol's family extends their deepest gratitude to the staff at Mount View Care Center, as well as to dear family friends Joan, Jim, and Suzy, and to Hank, her hospice nurse, for the compassion, kindness, and exceptional care they provided to Carol and her family. Heartfelt thanks are also extended to the staff at Ascend Funeral Home, and to Pastor Lance, Pastor Jordan, and Deacon Ray for their support and guidance.

Hopefully, heaven is ready for this spunky little firecracker. We will miss you dearly, Mum.

The funeral service will be held at 11:00 AM on Monday, March 30, 2026, at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston. Pastor Lance Hoelscher will officiate. Visitation will be held from 9:30 AM until the time of the service at Ascend. Interment will follow in Pine Grove Cemetery, Wausau.

records.name: David W. Niewolny
records.summary: David W. Niewolny, age 80, passed away on March 22, 2026.
records.body:
My heart is broken beyond repair. Today, March 22, 2026, after almost thirty-eight years together, I lost the man I have loved since the first day I met him, David Niewolny. We married on September 14, 1990.

David W. Niewolny was born May 19, 1945, at 12:15 am in Wausau Memorial Hospital to his very proud parents, Frank and Ilma (Krienke) Niewolny. He weighed in at a whomping 6 pounds 13 ounces.

Dave was blessed with two sons, Todd (Shelli) Niewolny, and Tony (Wendy) Niewolny. Four grandchildren, Allyssa (Ryan) Dassow, Colton (Nicole) Niewolny, Trevor Niewolny, and Lexi (Sye) Mannel. Five great-grandchildren, Laikynn and Rowan Dassow, Bentley, Briggs and Beau Mannel, and baby to be Niewolny. They all survive. In addition, he is survived by his wife Susan F. (Westfall) Niewolny, and brother, Thomas (Patricia) Niewolny.

A celebration of Dave's life will take place at a later date.

records.name: James D. Chesley
records.summary: James D. Chesley entered into eternal life on March 19, 2026.
records.body:
It is with heavy hearts that we announce that Jim Chesley entered into eternal life on March 19, 2026.

Born in 1947 to Leona and Dale Chesley, Jim graduated from Madison West and attended UW-Madison. In 1966, he met Nancy Podrez from Wausau and swept her off her feet. Married in 1967, they shared 60 years of love, laughter, and an unwavering bond.

In 1969, Jim joined the Air Force and was stationed at Laughlin AFB in Texas for four years. They enjoyed Texas so much that they remained there for another 38 years before moving back to Wausau in 2011. In 1973, they were blessed with twins, Todd and Wendy. Their joy grew over the years with their loving grandchildren, Alec, Haley, and Samara, and their great-granddaughter, Kathryn.

Jim prospered in management and sales, working and playing with equal passion. An active man, he spent every chance he could motorcycling, boating, and water skiing with his family. Following in his footsteps, Todd and Wendy were skiing and riding their Honda 50s by the age of five. Jim was a wonderful husband and father; for him, a perfect day was simply spending time with his family.

Together, Jim and Nancy traveled across Texas, California, Colorado, and the East Coast. Favorite trips included Japan, Hawaii, Nassau, San Juan, St. Thomas, and Washington D.C. Jim was a kind and patient man who enjoyed life to the fullest, a quality witnessed by everyone who knew him.

Jim was preceded in death by his parents and his brother, John.

Per his wishes, no formal services will be held.

He will be greatly missed by all of his family and friends. Rest in peace, dear Jim.

We would like to extend a sincere thank you to Linda Fosick and the caring staff at Cedar Creek Manor in Mosinee.

records.name: Janet R. Salmi
records.summary: Janet R. Salmi, age 79, passed away peacefully on March 23, 2026.
records.body:
Janet R. Salmi, age 79, passed away peacefully on March 23, 2026. She was born on October 23, 1946, to the late Edward and Dorothy (Schneider) Bokelberg.

Janet lived a vibrant and joyful life, full of energy, love, and devotion to her family. In her younger years, she enjoyed camping and fishing with her father, experiences she cherished deeply. She was also a member of a professional water ski team and participated in figure roller skating, reflecting her adventurous and active spirit.

Janet had a true zest for life. She loved to gamble, sing, and dance, and was always ready for a good time. She had a well-known sweet tooth and rarely passed up her favorite candies. She took great pride in her appearance, never leaving the house without her hair and makeup done, and she kept a pristine home that reflected her care and attention.

She had a deep love for animals, especially her two beloved dogs. Janet also enjoyed traveling to warm destinations, with some of her favorite trips being to Las Vegas, Mississippi, and Florida, where she made many cherished memories with her husband.

Above all else, Janet's greatest joy was her family. She treasured every moment spent with her children, grandchildren, and great-grandchildren. Her kindness, caring nature, and unwavering love will be deeply missed by all who knew her.

Janet is survived by her loving husband of 36 years, whom she married on December 15, 1990; her children, Holly (Greg) Smith, Machelle, and Denny (Mary); 18 grandchildren; and four great-grandchildren.

She was preceded in death by her parents, Edward and Dorothy Bokelberg, and her brother, Alan.

Janet lived her life grounded in faith. She prayed daily, never missed a day of thanksgiving, and carried her beliefs with quiet strength and devotion throughout her life.

A celebration of Janet's life will be held at a later date.

records.name: Julie K. Gault
records.summary: Julie Kae Gault, age 45, of Wausau, passed away on March 24, 2026.
records.body:
Julie Kae Gault, 45, Wausau, passed away peacefully on Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at House of the Dove in Marshfield.

She was born July 5, 1980 in Merrill, daughter of Edward and Karen (Kippenhan) Gault. For many years, Julie worked as a tax consultant in the Wausau area for H & R Block.

She is survived by her father, Ed; sister, Jessica (Matthew) Tingue; niece, Desiree Tingue; and her significant other, Joseph Thorp and his daughter, Chani.

She was preceded in death by her mother, Karen.

Funeral services will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at St. Paul's United Church of Christ in Wausau. Pastor Chris Ewan will officiate. Visitation will be on Tuesday from 9:30 a.m. until time of services at the church.

records.name: Kenneth W. Boneske
records.summary: Kenneth W. Boneske, age 91, of Athens, passed away on March 17, 2026.
records.body:
Kenneth W. Boneske, 91, of Athens, passed away peacefully on Tuesday, March 17, 2026, at his home. He was born on March 20, 1934, in Medford, to the late Arnold and Margaret (Merwin) Boneske.

Kenneth worked for many years as a dairy farmer and was also employed at Jahnke Construction. After his retirement, he drove road grader for the towns of Bern and Holton. When he wasn't working, he enjoyed his 32 tractors. Kenneth served his country in the U.S. Army in the late 1950's.

Survivors include his brother, Roy (Pat) Boneske of Athens; sister, Darlene Zietlow; stepson, Randy (Chris) Apfelbeck of Edgar; stepdaughter, Crystal (Perry) Williamson of Athens; daughters, Connie Boneske (fiancé, Randy Murkowski) of Athens and Cherie Boneske of Colby; 11 grandchildren, Stephanie (Jim) Halvorsen of Edgar, Jeremy (Kristina) Apfelbeck of Edgar, Matt (Karen) Apfelbeck of Edgar, Tiffany (Travis) Hart of Rozellville, Ciera (Josh) Grueneberg of Berlin, Jessie Venzke of St. Petersburg, Florida, Jackie (Patrick) Venzke of Edgar, Kimberly Venzke of Athens, Stevie (Jenna) Boneske of Cornell, Shane Boneske of Wausau, and Selena Boneske of Athens; and 14 great-grandchildren.

He was preceded in death by his parents; brother, Jerry Boneske; and brother-in-law, Don Zietlow.

Services will take place later this year. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

The family would like to thank the first responders for their care and support.

records.name: Mary B. Stencil
records.summary: Mary B. Stencil, age 62, of Wausau, passed away on March 12, 2026.
records.body:
Mary B. Stencil (nee Detert), 62, of Wausau, passed away on Thursday, March 12, 2026.

Mary was born on July 14, 1963, in Wausau, the daughter of the late David and Kathleen (Warosh) Detert. She spent her life in the Wausau area surrounded by family and friends who meant the world to her. On August 20, 1991, Mary married Duane Stencil in Las Vegas, Nevada. Together they shared many years of marriage, laughter, and adventure until his passing on October 31, 2018. They especially enjoyed motorcycle riding and spending time on the open road. Mary dedicated nearly 40 years of her career to Liberty Mutual, formerly Wausau Insurance Company, where she built many lasting friendships and was known for her reliability and commitment to her work.

Following her retirement, she worked at County Market to stay active, where she enjoyed connecting with members of the community. Mary had a strong and spirited personality; she could be wonderfully stubborn at times, a trait those who loved her understood and appreciated. She enjoyed the simple pleasures in life, including spending time with family and relaxing with a cold Point beer. Mary was famously lucky in Packer pools and at the gambling machines at her favorite local taverns, a source of many jokes among her family and friends. More than anything, Mary loved her family. She was a proud mother, an involved grandmother, and a loving great-grandmother who treasured the time she spent watching her grandchildren grow.

Mary is survived by her daughter, Danielle (Eric) Gesell of Wausau; her grandchildren, Jarek (Hope Uttecht) and Marley; her step-grandchildren, Eric and Lily; and one great-grandson, Oakland. She is further survived by her siblings, Barbara (Lawrence Becht) Perry of Lublin, WI; Debra (Jim) Blair of Weston, WI; Lori (Wade) Parnell of Waunakee, WI; and Barry Detert of Wausau, WI; along with many nieces, nephews, extended family members, and friends.

In addition to her parents, she was preceded in death by her husband, Duane Stencil.

At Mary's request, there will be no formal funeral services. A Celebration of Life will be announced at a later date.

records.name: Randall M. Frank
records.summary: Randall M. Frank, age 63, of Brokaw, died on March 20, 2026.
records.body:
Randall "Randy" M. Frank, 63, Brokaw, died Friday, March 20, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital. Randy was born January 24, 1963, to the late Henry "Hank" and Ruthelle (Wedepohl) Frank. He was a 1981 graduate of Wausau East High School and earned a Bachelor's Degree in Zoology from the University of Wisconsin – Madison and a Master's Degree in Public Health and Community Health Education from the University of Wisconsin – LaCrosse.

After college, Randy developed a strong interest in staying healthy and maintaining his fitness, which led him to become an avid cyclist. Through his passion, he embarked on many incredible adventures, traveling to places like Italy, Switzerland, Germany, and other destinations around the world. He also enjoyed memorable trips out West with his sister Rochelle, where they explored nature together and discovered as many as 800 waterfalls.

Randy had a deep love for animals and gardening, a passion instilled in him by his parents that continued throughout his life. As a child, his family even raised bantam chickens as pets, and at one point, Randy had a pet chipmunk. He was also well known as a lead salesman for Rosemurgy and Ballweg Toyota.

Randy enjoyed spending time with his family at the cottage in Harrison Hill, where he could often be found reading, doing crossword puzzles, fishing, and swimming in the lake. He cherished the quiet moments spent relaxing and taking in the beautiful nature that surrounded him.

Randy is survived by his sisters, Rochelle Frank and Renae (Mitch) Butler; and extended family and friends. He was preceded in death by his parents, Henry and Ruthelle Frank; and his grandparents, Elmer and Dorothea Wedepohl.

The funeral service will be held at 11:00 AM on Friday, March 27, 2026, at St. Peter Ev. Lutheran Church – Wausau Campus, 2822 North 6th Street, Wausau, WI 54401. Pastor Jeffrey Mahnke will officiate. Visitation will be held on Thursday, March 26th from 5:00 to 7:00 PM and again on Friday from 10:00 AM until the time of service; all at the church. Interment will be in Restlawn Memorial Park.

Special thanks to the nurses and doctors at Aspirus Hospital and Dr. Patrick Allen and staff; Close Family and Friends – Pat and Russ Baumann, Melissa and John Krasowski Family and the dental staff, Kris (Butler) Mohr, Sandy and Norm Prouty, and Mikey Wedepohl, Mary Brieske, Jane Janke, and Dave Johnson.

Memorials may be directed to: St. Peter Ev. Lutheran Church, Friends of Rib Mountain State Park to improve the trails or Monk Garden to improve garden growth, Vilas Park Zoo in Madison, WI.

records.name: Rolynn Barbara Hand
records.summary: Rolynn Barbara Hand, age 69, of Kronenwetter, Wisconsin, passed away on March 17, 2026.
records.body:
Rolynn Barbara (Jantz) Hand, age 69, passed away on March 17, 2026 peacefully at home.

Rolynn (Ro) was born April 6, 1956 in Milwaukee, Wisconsin to Roy and Marilyn (Laufer) Jantz. She attended grade and high school in Germantown, Wisconsin. After graduating, she attended University of Wisconsin Milwaukee. Ro worked at Briggs and Stratton in Milwaukee for 31 years where she made many life-long friends. From 2006-2014 she worked at Walmart at the West Bend and Hartford stores. In 2014 Ro and her mother, Marilyn, moved to Kronenwetter, Wisconsin where she worked at the Wausau Walmart for a short time before retiring. She made many close friends in the Wausau area. Ro loved to socialize.

Ro will be remembered for being selfless, kind-hearted, having a great sense of humor, and empathy for others. Ro would do anything for anyone and always put others before herself. Ro will truly be missed by all who knew her.

Ro is survived by her loving aunt Teri (Theresa) Miller of West Bend, her cousins Jo Miller of West Bend, Jena Maronie of St. Petersburg, Florida, Marti Laufer of Watertown and many more cousins. She had a large extended family. Ro was also survived by her beloved golden retriever, Cody and many friends.

Ro was preceded in death by her parents Roy and Marilyn (Laufer) Jantz and several cousins.

Ro will be buried at Peaceful Pines Pet Memorials & Family Sanctuary beside her mother, Marilyn and all their beloved dogs at a future date followed by a celebration of life. We will reach out to friends and family with the details.

Ro's favorite charity was the Humane Society.

records.name: Allan Hatz
records.summary: Allan Hatz, 61, of Neillsville, passed away peacefully.
records.body:
Allan Hatz, age 61, of Neillsville, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully. He was born on June 1, 1964, in Merrill to the late John and Berdina (Luedtke) Hatz.

Allan had a lifelong passion for aviation. A skilled pilot, he found great joy in flying and dedicated much of his time to restoring antique airplanes. His love for the skies and craftsmanship reflected his adventurous spirit and attention to detail.

He is survived by his brothers, Lyman (Judy) Hatz and Clifford Hatz; his sister, Barbara Hatz; his nephews, Wyat Hatz and Jordan Lasky; and his niece, Angela (David) Jirovec. He will be deeply missed by his family and all who knew him.

Allan was preceded in death by his parents and his brother, Aaron Hatz.

His memory will live on in the hearts of those who loved him and in the skies he so dearly cherished.

records.name: Arthur J. Garcia
records.summary: Arthur J. Garcia, known as "Tudy," 45, passed away on March 21, 2026.
records.body:
Arthur Garcia, known to so many as "Tudy," passed away on March 21, 2026, at the age of 45, leaving behind a legacy that cannot be measured in time, but in love, laughter, and the countless lives he touched. He was more than a son, a father, a fiancé, a brother, and a friend—he was a presence, a feeling, a soul that made life brighter just by being in it. His absence leaves a space that can never truly be filled, but his memory will live on in every heart that had the privilege of knowing him.

Arthur was a man who embraced life fully. He loved music and finding joy and meaning in the words. He loved being surrounded by family and friends, finding joy in togetherness, in laughter, and in the simple moments that many take for granted. He had a way of making people feel welcome, like they belonged, like they mattered. Whether it was sitting around talking, joking, or just enjoying each other's presence, those were the moments he valued most. He didn't need anything extravagant—just real love and real people.

He had a deep passion for tattooing, something that was more than just a skill—it was a part of who he was. Through his art, Arthur expressed emotion, culture, and identity. Every piece he created carried meaning, and those who wore his work carried a part of him with them forever. He took pride in what he did, pouring his time, focus, and heart into every detail. His art was not just seen, it was felt.

Outside of his work, Arthur found happiness in the simple and meaningful parts of life. He loved cooking, bringing people together over food, creating moments filled with warmth and connection. The kitchen, like his art, was another way he showed love. He also found peace in nature—taking walks, clearing his mind, and appreciating the quiet beauty of the world around him. In those moments, he was able to just be himself, grounded and at ease.

Arthur was incredibly smart, with a natural understanding of life and people. He had a sense of humor that could light up any room, the kind of laugh that was contagious and unforgettable. He could make people smile even when they didn't feel like it, and he had a way of lifting others without even realizing it. But beyond the laughter, he had a deep and caring heart. He was someone people could go to, someone who listened, who understood, and who never turned his back on those he loved.

In his journey through life, Arthur found a relationship with God that became a meaningful part of who he was. His faith gave him strength during difficult times and brought him a sense of peace and purpose. It grounded him, helped him grow, and reminded him of what truly mattered. Through that faith, he built a beautiful family and created bonds rooted in love, forgiveness, and understanding. Before his passing, he left those he loved with a blessing—something to hold onto, a reminder of his love, his faith, and the impact he would always have in their lives.

Above everything else, Arthur's greatest pride was his family. He loved deeply, fully, and without hesitation. His daughter, Destiny, was his heart—his reason, his joy, and his greatest blessing. The love he had for her was unconditional and unbreakable, a love that will continue to live on within her forever. He was a devoted partner to his fiancé, Sabrina Garza, building a life filled with love, hope, and shared dreams. He was a son who loved his mother, Maria Garcia, with all his heart, and a brother who shared an unbreakable bond with Michelle, Cristina, Anthony, and Alex. His love extended to his entire family, including his brothers-in-law Marcos, Victor, Martin, and Andrew Garza, and his father-in-law Martin Garza.

Arthur's life was not defined by how long he lived, but by how deeply he loved and how much of himself he gave to others. He was real, he was genuine, and he was someone who left a lasting impact on everyone he met. His presence, his laughter, and his love will be missed beyond words, but never forgotten.

He is survived by his loving mother Maria Garcia; his sisters Michelle and Cristina; his brothers Anthony and Alex; his fiancé Sabrina Garza; his beloved daughter Destiny; his brothers-in-law Marcos, Victor, Martin, and Andrew Garza; his father-in-law Martin Garza; and many extended family members and friends who will forever carry his memory in their hearts. He was preceded in death by his sister, Irene Garcia.

Though he is no longer here in the way we wish he could be, Arthur "Tudy" Garcia lives on—in the love he gave, the family he built, the faith he found, and the memories that will never fade. His life was a blessing, his presence a gift, and his memory will remain forever.

records.name: Carol A. Dexter
records.summary: Carol A. Dexter, 70, of Oakdale, Minnesota, died on March 18, 2026.
records.body:
Carol Ann Dexter died at the young age of 70 on March 18, 2026 in Oakdale, Minnesota. Our colorful companion Carol has left on the wind.

Born in Wausau, Wisconsin on January 8, 1956 to John Dexter and Mary Ann (Urmanski) Dexter, Carol graduated from Wausau East High School in 1974. She married Keith Bogut in Minneapolis on September 5, 1987, and in 2000 they built a home in Lake Elmo, Minnesota. Carol completed a nursing degree in 1980 at the University of Wisconsin, and received a Masters of Public Health degree in 1992 from the University of Minnesota.

Carol was a knowledgeable nurse, a competent medical manager, a caring friend to many, an ambitious gardener, and a prolific collector of color. Carol was proud of her passion for color, which she displayed in her kitchen, on her walls, out in her garden, in her clothes, and in her humor. She was the creator of fun and a builder of friendships.

Carol and Keith were avid travelers, visiting many national parks, and the Boundary Waters canoe wilderness often. Rib Mountain was a mandatory visit on trips to Wausau. Alaska was another favorite. A debilitating illness left Keith unable to do so in later years, and he died in November 2022.

Carol is survived by sister Joni (Denny) Jaecks, Niles, Michigan, and brother Peter Dexter, Ridgecrest, California. Preceding her in death were sister Kathy Dexter, brother David Dexter, her husband, and her parents. She leaves the comfort of a great circle of friends gathered in her years living in Wausau & Madison, Wisconsin, and Minneapolis & Lake Elmo, Minnesota. She also leaves her two kitty companions, Neutron and Photon, who moved in with her after the passing of brother David.

Carol greatly appreciated the kind care she received at Boulder Ponds Senior Living in Lake Elmo, and The Pillars Hospice Home in Oakdale.

In later years Carol wanted, more than anything, to travel to "see the world" (wide arm gestures). If you do so to any interesting parts of the globe, please take her along – in pictures, in memories, or in a tiny vial. She once wrote that in death, "I will finally be free to go where the wind takes me."

Do good things in her memory. Consider supporting local efforts for animal rescue, teaching children about the arts, and protecting environmental treasures.

A celebration of life is planned for 4pm on Saturday, April 18 at the Lake Elmo Event Center, Lake Elmo, Minnesota.

records.name: Carol A. Hettinga
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Carol A. Hettinga, 85, of Schofield, passed away peacefully on March 4, 2026.
records.body:
Carol Hettinga, age 85, passed away peacefully on Wednesday, March 4, 2026, in the loving care of her daughter and staff at the Aspirus Hospice House.

Carol was born in Wausau on December 21, 1940, to William and Florena Heidemann. She graduated from Wausau High School in 1960 and married Roland Hettinga on April 17, 1964. Together, they built a life in Schofield, raising their three daughters and sharing 52 years of marriage before Roland's passing in 2016.

As her children grew, Carol worked part-time in the community and later provided childcare in her home.

Carol's greatest joy was her family, but she also lived a life full of simple pleasures – time at the family cabin, fishing, bowling and dart leagues, Friday afternoons playing cards at Schofield City Hall, and trips to the casino.

Carol is survived by her 3 daughters, Sherri Crawford of Auburn, WA, Cindy (Russell) Schilling of Marathon, WI, and Connie (Andrew) Heise of Wausau, WI; three grandchildren, Cameron Schilling, Brooke and Alivia Heise; and sister-in-law, Marcia Keck of Loris, SC.

In addition to her parents, Carol was reunited in heaven with her husband, Roland Hettinga; son-in-law, Richard Crawford; brothers, David (Jean) Heidemann and Willis (Betty) Heidemann; sister, Elaine (Donald) Utecht; and brothers and sisters-in-law: Larry (Judy) Hettinga, Morris Keck.

Carol will be remembered for her love of family, her kindness, and the many simple moments she shared with those around her.

The family would like to extend their heartfelt thanks to Bell Tower, Aspirus at Home Hospice, and Aspirus Hospice House for the wonderful care she received in her final years.

A celebration of life for Carol will be held this summer.

records.name: Francie Skyles
records.summary: Francie Skyles, born November 25, 1976, passed away after a courageous eight-year battle with cancer.
records.body:
Francie Skyles, born November 25, 1976, in Antigo, Wisconsin, to Timmon and Ruth (Koeppel) Tveten, passed away after a courageous eight-year battle with cancer.

Francie was a bright light in every life she touched—someone who always found the good in people and situations, and whose positivity was truly contagious. She had a deep love for music and was a devoted fan of the band "Barenaked Ladies." In the kitchen, she was an amazing cook, bringing people together through meals made with care and joy. She was also known as a fierce cribbage player, never backing down from a good game.

An animal lover at heart, Francie shared a special bond with her beloved German Shepherd, Schaffen. Her warmth, laughter, and unwavering optimism made her a source of comfort and strength to those around her, even through her long and difficult illness, which she faced with grace and resilience until the very end.

Francie is survived by her loving husband, Ken; her parents, Timmon and Ruth Tveten; her brother, Christopher (Haley); her sister, Charlotte (Jesse) Seymour; and many extended family members and friends. She was preceded in death by her stepdaughter, Shelby Casper.

Francie will be remembered for her kindness, her strength, and the light she brought into the world. Her memory will live on in the hearts of all who knew and loved her.

records.name: Gerald Baker
records.summary: Gerald Baker, born August 25, 1951, passed away leaving behind a legacy of hard work, devotion to family, and appreciation for life's simple pleasures.
records.body:
Gerald Baker, born August 25, 1951, to the late Edward and Delores (Wolf) Baker, passed away leaving behind a legacy of hard work, devotion to family, and appreciation for life's simple pleasures.

In August 1971, Gerald enlisted in the United States Army and served in Korea. He dedicated himself to his country with honor and pride until his discharge in May 1978.

In January 1984, Gerald married the love of his life, Cynthia Rusch. Together, they built a life centered on love, companionship, faith, and quiet joys. Gerald was a mechanic by trade and had a natural talent for working on anything with an engine. He loved tinkering in the garage, always finding satisfaction in fixing and building with his hands.

Outside of his work, Gerald found happiness in gardening, hunting with his children, taking peaceful rides in the country with Cynthia, and relaxing at home watching his favorite Westerns and Animal Planet. He was a simple man who found true contentment in the little things in life.

Above all, Gerald cherished his family. Nothing meant more to him than spending time with his grandchildren, especially attending their sporting events and cheering them on with pride. His steady presence and quiet support will be deeply missed.

Gerald retired from Kolbe & Kolbe, where he dedicated over 30 years of service.

He is survived by his children, Dayna (Lucas) Rodemeier, Kyle Baker, and Joshua (fiancée Anne Morales) Diaz; ten grandchildren; two great-grandchildren; his sister, Cheryl Lippert; his brothers, Larry, Gary, and Jimmy (Mary) Baker; as well as many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his beloved wife, Cynthia; his parents; his granddaughter, Byanka; and his brother-in-law, Wayne Lippert.

Gerald will be remembered for his strong work ethic, his love of family, and the gentle, steady way he lived his life.

records.name: Josephine A. Susnowski
records.funeral_home: Brainard Funeral Home and Cremation Center
records.summary: Josephine A. Susnowski, 78, passed away on March 22, 2026.
records.body:
Josephine Anne Susnowski, aged 78, passed away on Sunday, March 22, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

The Mass of Christian Burial will be held at St. Mary's Roman Catholic Oratory, 408 Seymour St, Wausau, WI 54403, on Thursday, April 9, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. Friends and family may greet the family for a time of visitation from 10:00 a.m. until the time of the service.

Following the Mass, Josephine will be laid to rest next to her beloved Bruno at Restlawn Memorial Park.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made to Neighbors Place or an organization of choice in Josephine's name.

Josephine will be remembered for her warmth, kindness, and the love she shared with family and friends.

records.name: Larry A. Peterson
records.summary: Larry A. Peterson, 66, of Wausau, passed away on March 25, 2026.
records.body:
Larry A. Peterson, 66, of Wausau, passed away on Wednesday, March 25, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center in Marshfield after a courageous battle with cancer. Facing his illness with strength and dignity, Larry made the brave decision to pass on his own terms, ensuring his final moments were filled with peace and comfort and surrounded by the love of his family.

He was born on June 25, 1959, in Medford, the son of Charles and Ruth (Anderson) Peterson. Larry worked for HSU Ginseng in Wausau for 37 years, until his recent retirement. He often spoke candidly about the challenges of his work, but it was clear he had a deep-rooted pride in what he did.

Larry had a deep appreciation for the outdoors and an adventurous spirit, finding joy in motorcycle rides along scenic roads, time on the golf course, camping, and exploring nature. Hunting was one of his greatest passions, especially the camaraderie and lasting memories shared at the shack. In recent years, he found great enjoyment in traveling throughout Wisconsin, taking in the sights and exploring new things while on spontaneous adventures. Most of all, he cherished the time he spent with his family and friends. Larry held a special place in the hearts of many, and he had a unique way of making others feel seen, cherished, and loved.

Survivors include his father, Charles Peterson; fiancée, Carole Greene; three children, Jessica (Joe) Scott, Dustin (Kris) Peterson, and Brooke (Jade) Arnold; four grandchildren, Josslyn Schellinger, Kinley Peterson, Finn Arnold, and Nora Arnold; 11 siblings, Deb Dahl, Mike (Beverly) Peterson, Pat (Roger) Hamus, Cindy (Russell) Hamus, David (Leta) Peterson, Dale (Chris) Peterson, Darla Peterson, Kevin (Stacey Kilty) Peterson, Kenny (Amy) Peterson, Jamie (Nikki) Peterson, and Chucky (Carrie) Peterson; two brothers-in-law, George Velon and Ron Haas; sister-in-law, Audrey Peterson; and many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his mother, Ruth Peterson; three siblings, Char Velon, Mark Peterson, and Julie Haas; one brother-in-law, Lance Dahl; and one nephew, Parker Peterson.

Funeral services will be held at 2:00 p.m. on Thursday, April 2, 2026, at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Avenue, Wausau. Visitation will be on Thursday from 11:00 a.m. until the time of the service at the funeral home.

Memorials may be directed to his family to be donated in Larry's memory to a hunting organization serving youth and underserved persons.

records.name: Lynn M. Duberstein
records.summary: Lynn M. Duberstein, 63, of Weston, died on March 13, 2026.
records.body:
Lynn M. Duberstein, 63, Weston, died Friday, March 13, 2026, knowing her loving family and friends came to support her through her end-of-life journey. She was born Tuesday, February 5, 1963, the beloved daughter of Ronald and Margaret (Condon) Buchacek. On July 19, 1985, she married Kenneth (Ken) Duberstein celebrating their 40 years with a fun road trip together.

Lynn was a loving wife, mother, grandmother, daughter, sister, auntie and great auntie Wynn. She cherished her children and grandchildren and especially loved celebrating family birthdays and anniversaries, truly valuing the importance of these special occasions. She preferred tropical weather and fondly reminisced about the years spent living in a gated community in Lakeland Florida. Lynn traveled on many adventures with her family seeking warmer climates to stroll along the ocean or whale watch in Maui. Lynn enjoyed going out to eat and spending time chatting over delicious food at local favorites like Pinewood, Pit Stop, Hammer Down, Casa Mezcal, The Mint Café, 2510, and Gorski's. Lynn's greatest passion was following the Dallas Cowboy Cheerleaders reality series and their pursuit of making it. She often recounted details of traveling with Ken that took them all over the country.

Lynn felt fortunate to work as a Health Care Aid at Cedar Ridge and attend to the needs of residents. While in Lakeland, she worked as a gate security attendant and had a blast buzzing around the complex in her golf cart. She was an active member of the VFW Ladies Auxiliary, supporting their programs as a volunteer.

Lynn made friends easily and was a caring friend. She especially loved her best friends Joyce and Jessie. Lynn had a true gift she gave to others by being present to listen and offer her kind heartfelt support. She also had immense empathy and genuinely cared for the wellbeing of others. Her family and friends will treasure the many fun times spent in her company laughing, celebrating Thanksgiving and Christmas holidays, dancing at concerts, holding grand babies, shopping at Sally's and Ulta Beauty, and talking on the phone to catch up on everyone's plans. She taught us a lot, and conversations with her remain alive in our heart and minds.

Lynn had resilience and strength that could not be squelched while enduring multiple treatments for breast cancer that lasted over a decade. Lynn was beautiful and took pride in her appearance, often sharing her knowledge of living a healthy lifestyle.

Survivors include her husband Ken; Weston, children, Joshua Neitzke, Weston, Jason Neitzke, Wausau, Marlena Neitzke, Rothschild, Vanessa (Kris) Chowning, Schofield, step children, Beth (Jim) Allen, Mosinee, Kenny Duberstein, Mosinee, Genia Duberstein, Mosinee, grandchildren, Jonathon Chowning and Catherine Elizabeth Irene Knetter, mother, Margaret Buchacek, Mosinee, siblings, Beth Knetter, Wausau, Frank (Julie) Buchacek, Green Bay, Sally (Patrick) Medvecz, Wausau, Brian (Tracy) Buchacek, Rothschild, Molly (Jeff) Breitenbach, Kronenwetter, Lea (Glen) Loppnow, Mosinee, sister-in-law Jeanette (Dennis) Pierson, Mosinee, Cheryl (Scott) Pilch, Mosinee, nieces and nephews, Derek (Susan) Knetter, David (Morgan) Knetter, Kelly (Andre) Carriveau, Ashley (Todd) Olson, Brandon, Brice and Mary Buchacek, Thomas, Ronald, and Christopher Medvecz, Alex and Kate Buchacek, Elle Breitenbach, Amy (Chase) Crowell, and Amanda Loppnow, great nieces and great nephews Lyla Knetter, Henri and Colt Carriveau, and Anton Knetter.

Preceded in death by her father Ronald Buchacek, brother-in-law Mark Knetter, father-in-law Max and mother-in-law Lola Duberstein, brother-in-law Mark Duberstein

Funeral Mass at St. Therese Catholic Church located at 113 Kort St. Rothschild, will begin at 11:00am on Thursday, April 9, 2026, Fr. Janusz Kowalski will preside. Lynn's visitation at the church will begin at 10:00am until the time of service, a luncheon will be served following mass at the church. Burial will follow lunch at Gate of Heaven Cemetery Veteran Memorial Section located at 1399 Gate of Heaven Drive, Kronenwetter.

records.name: Roger L. Stasko
records.summary: Roger L. Stasko, 68, of Wausau, passed away on March 22, 2026.
records.body:
Roger Stasko, 68, passed away unexpectedly on March 22, 2026. Roger was born in Stevens Point, WI to the late Roger and Kathryn "Kitty" (Strike) Stasko.

Roger began his career in 1979 with Wausau Insurance, later Liberty Mutual, where he worked as a Software Engineer in the Information Systems Department. Over 40 years, he found his work both challenging and rewarding, with a particular passion for designing and developing software applications. He retired in 2019.

In 1982, he married Lois Edwards after meeting while working on a systems project. Their marriage was defined by enduring love and partnership, and they would have celebrated their 44th anniversary in September 2026.

Roger had a lifelong love of sports. He played football and basketball in high school and college and later enjoyed softball with coworkers in a Wausau Insurance league. He was also an avid cross-country skier and completed the American Birkebeiner twice. An accomplished tennis player, his greatest passion was golf. He enjoyed playing courses throughout the state, was a member of the Stevens Point Country Club, and took memorable golf trips to destinations including Scottsdale, Arizona; Palm Springs, California; Gulf Shores, Alabama; and Myrtle Beach, South Carolina.

He and Lois also shared a love of travel, especially annual spring trips to warm destinations such as Cozumel, Cancun, and Ixtapa, Mexico; Punta Cana; and Puerto Rico. Roger especially valued time spent with close friends, enjoying conversation, laughter, and shared experiences.

Roger will be remembered for his warmth, wit, and appreciation for life's simple pleasures. He will be deeply missed.

Roger is survived by his loving wife, Lois; sister, Donna (Gary) Plahmer, Stevens Point, WI; sister-in-law, Judy (Bobby) Morgan, Reidsville, NC; brother-in-law, Mark (Laine) Edwards, Carrollton, TX; nieces and nephew, Zach (Nataly) Morgan, Deforest, WI, Hannah Morgan, Reidsville, NC, Faith Edwards, The Colony, TX, Karalyn Plahmer, Stevens Point, WI, and many other family and friends.

Funeral services will be held at 11:30 AM on Saturday March 28, 2026, at First English Lutheran Church, 402 N. 3rd Ave, Wausau, WI 54401. Reverend Mary Zupansic will officiate. Visitation will be held at church from 10:00 AM until the time of services. For those unable to attend in person the services will be livestreamed.

A special thank you from the family to the staff of Rennes Health and Rehab Clinic for the support and care they displayed to Roger.

In lieu of flowers donations can be made to First English Lutheran Church or a charity of your choice.

records.name: Arleen Claire Wurman
records.summary: Arleen Claire Wurman, 82, of Wausau, passed away on March 26, 2026.
records.body:
Arleen Claire Wurman, beloved wife, mother, grandmother, great grandmother, and sister, passed away March 26, 2026 at the age of 82. She lived a life full of love, laughter, and a fierce devotion to the people she held dear.

Born in New York City on September 28, 1943 to Milton and Paula Edelman, Arleen was the youngest of their three children. She met Len in 1961 and they were married in 1963. When Len's career took them to a few cities, including Minneapolis, they both fell in love with the upper Midwest and they settled in Wausau in 1975 where they have lived ever since.

First and foremost, Arleen was a dedicated wife and mother. Over the years, she discovered a love of knitting and excelled at it; each of her grandchildren treasures the beautiful, elaborate baby blankets she created to celebrate their births. She worked at The Knitting Nook with her best friend, Evie Rosen, where she was a fixture, sharing her passion and skill to inspire and teach dozens of fellow knitters.

She also loved to cook, and collected recipes as well as kitchen tools and gadgets-her family often joked she could open her own kitchen store with her impressive collection. From heaps of latkes to enormous holiday turkeys to all types of wild game, she mastered recipes of all kinds to share around a table full of family and friends.

Arleen was a woman of faith, and honoring Jewish traditions was a cornerstone of her life. She and Len have been members of Mount Sinai for 50 years, during which she held numerous leadership and board positions.

Whether cheering as a lifelong Green Bay Packers fan or starting a new knitting project, Arleen brought joy, enthusiasm, and a tremendous sense of humor to everything she did. A voracious reader, she kept a steady stream of books coming into her house and back out again to friends- yet another way in which she kept closely connected to her community and family.

She is survived by her husband of 63 years, Len; her sister, Charlotte Kardos; her three children, Peter (Nancy), Nancy, and Scott (Jill); grandchildren Tim (Jordan), Erik (Sam), Ben, Matt (Julie), Becka, Tucker, and Piper, and her adorable great-granddaughter, Tessa.

She will be deeply missed by all who knew her, and remembered with love and joy.

Funeral services were held on Monday, March 30th at Mount Sinai Congregation in Wausau, followed by the burial at Wausau Hebrew Cemetery.

In lieu of flowers please send donations to Mount Sinai Congregation or a charity of your choosing.

records.name: Florence Marie Beese
records.funeral_home: Helke Funeral Home and Cremation Services
records.summary: Florence Marie Beese, 94, of Wausau, passed away on March 25, 2026.
records.body:
Florence Marie (Reed) Beese went to Heaven to be with Jesus on March 25th, 2026. She was 94 years old. Florence was the second child of The Late Henry and Laura (Clark) Reed, born on April 8th, 1931, at St. Mary's Hospital in Wausau. She received her grade-school education in one-room-schools, attending both Olsen in the Town of Mosinee and Hardwood Ridge in the Town of Marathon. She graduated from Mosinee High School with the class of 1949, and went on to college at North Central Technical College (NTC). Florence graduated in 1970 with a degree in Business Administration, majoring in accounting.

Florence married the love of her life, Friedrich (Fred) Beese at St. Stephen Lutheran Church Parsonage on October 17th, 1953, and had one child. Fred proceeded her in death on February 16th, 2002. She stayed at home as long as she could in the house that Fred built back in 1956.

Florence worked in various places before she started working at Wausau Insurance (now Liberty Mutual) where she worked for 15 years, retiring in 1986.

She loved to travel, having been to most of the 50 states, as well as Europe and South Africa; as well as working in her Rose Garden. Florence was also very fond of the genealogy of the Beese / Bramer families, even completing a Beese Genealogy Book.

Florence was a very active member of the community, and will be remembered for being a member of St. Stephen Lutheran Church for more than 60 Years; Elder for Social Concerns Committee Council (4 yrs); Women of the ELCA Secretary and Treasurer; Dorcas Circle/Women of St. Stephen helping with funeral lunches; Jolly Friends/Young at Heart Member program coordination; Marathon County Genealogical Society Program Coordination; Daughters of the American Revolution (DAR) Membership; and Retired Women of Wausau Insurance and the Retired Good People of Wausau Insurance. She was also a member of the Franklin School Mother's Club and took part in their "Stunt-night" several times.

Survivors are a beloved son David Beese, Two Grandchildren: John Beese (Heather) of Florida; Jeannette Mueller (Troy) of Appleton, Four Great-Grandchildren: Jonathan and Justin Beese of Florida and Natalie and Zachery Mueller of Appleton WI; Two Sisters: Anna Tanck of Wausau; Martha Walters of Kronenwetter; Brother: Edward Reed of Eldron, WI; Sister-in-Law: Esther (The late Charles Reed) of Wausau, WI and many nieces, nephews, cousins and friends. Preceded in death by her Husband Friedrich (Fred) Beese, Parents Henry and Laura (Clark) Reed, Brother Charles Reed, Grandparents Joseph and Delia (Anderson) Reed; Charles and Ella (Durkee) Clark; Parents-in-law Edward and Laura (Bramer) Beese; Brothers and Sisters-in laws Emil and Edith Beese, Otto and Myrtle Beese, and Arthur and Florence Beese.

A Funeral Service will take place on Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at St. Stephen Lutheran Church at 12:00 pm (512 McClellan Street, Wausau, WI, 54403) Pastor Karoline Schwantes will be officiating. A Visitation will take place from 10:00 AM until the time of the service.

The Family would like to extend a special Thanks to Wood Aven Health and Rehab for taking care of Florence the past two years.

Florence loved animals, especially her cat, so in Memorial please direct donations to the Humane Society of Marathon County.

records.name: Joseph Kauzlaric
records.summary: Joseph Kauzlaric, 83, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on March 26, 2026.
records.body:
Joseph (Joe) Kauzlaric, 83, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on March 26, 2026.

Joe was born August 27, 1942, in Eagle River, Wisconsin to the late John and Catherine Kauzlaric. Growing up on the farm in Eagle River, he had fond memories with his siblings and friends playing hockey, swimming and hunting. Even after moving away, he had a had a deep connection to the beauty and tranquility of the Northwoods.

Joe played hockey throughout his life – on the frozen lakes of Eagle River to rinks throughout the Midwest. He enjoyed coaching and watching hockey and spent many years sharpening skates for young hockey players. He truly loved the game and community it built. His dedication left a lasting impact on many young players he mentored over the years.

Joe served his country as a medic in the Vietnam War with the United States Army. The experience had a lasting impact on many aspects of his life. He carried pride in his service and patriotism with him throughout his life.

Joe eventually met the love of his life, Eloise, and they were married for more than 25 years. Together they had three children and had many happy memories of time spent together – fishing as a family and at the hockey rink. Eloise preceded Joe in death on July 10, 2005.

Joe had an incredible work ethic and worked in the underground utilities field for many years with North States Utilities. He sincerely enjoyed the camaraderie of those he worked with.

He was known as a genuinely kind and generous person – someone who would help anyone in need without hesitation. Joe found joy in life's simple pleasures. One of his greatest traditions was organizing the family's annual Eagle River fish fry, where he proudly took on the role of host and fryer, ensuring everyone had a great time.

Joe is survived by his children, Scott (Tera), Ryan (Katie), and Daniel; his grandchildren, Lilee and Iris; his great-grandchild, Layton; and his siblings, John (Barb), Florence "Flossy," and Edith. In addition to his wife, Joe is preceded in death by his parents and multiple siblings.

Joe will be remembered for his warmth, generous nature, jovial spirit and wit.

The family would like to thank the staff at Our House Senior Living, Village Caregiving, and St. Croix Hospice for their compassionate care.

A memorial service will be held at 12:00 pm on Friday, April 17, 2026, at John J Buettgen Funeral Home, 948 Grand Ave, Schofield. Visitation will take place from 10:00 am until the time services at the funeral home. Military honors will be given by American Legion Montgomery-Plant-Dudley Post 10.

records.name: Linda Lou Kennedy
records.summary: Linda Lou Kennedy, 89, of Mosinee, passed away peacefully on March 28, 2026.
records.body:
Linda Lou Kennedy, age 89, passed away peacefully on March 28, 2026, under the compassionate care of hospice services at Acorn Hill.

Linda was born on May 2, 1936. In 1956, she married her beloved husband, Arnold. During his service in the United States Army, the couple moved to Virginia, where their son Jeffrey was born. They later returned to Mosinee, where they planted their roots and built a life centered on family and community.

She is survived by her two sons, Jeffrey (Mindy) Kennedy and Douglas (Christine) Kennedy. She was a proud grandmother to nine grandchildren: Bobbie Jo (Brooke) Kennedy, Blair (Arlen) Hoen, Kyle (Kate) Kennedy, Morgan (Taylor) Schleh, Lucas (Megan) Ziemanski, Austin (Alycia) Kennedy, Logan (Kaitlyn) Kennedy, Carson Kennedy, and Jackson Kennedy. She was further blessed with seven great-grandchildren: Braydon Hoen, Paige Hoen, Mia Schleh, Aubrey Kennedy, Haisley Kennedy, Millie Kennedy, and Callum Kennedy.

Linda is also survived by her siblings, Bettie (Jerry) Denny, Mary (Kathy) Stoiber, and Allen Stoiber.

She was preceded in death by her beloved husband, Arnold, and her parents, Clarence and Isabel Stoiber.

Linda found joy in life's simple pleasures. She loved playing cards and occasionally visiting the casino. Family gatherings were especially dear to her, and she expressed her love through delicious home-cooked meals that brought everyone together. She worked for many years at the Mosinee Airport and was a lifelong member of St. Paul Catholic Church.

Linda cherished sharing stories of her life's adventures, especially vacations with her sisters and brother, creating memories that she lovingly passed down through her storytelling. Her family will forever remember her giving and loving heart and her willingness to help others.

The family extends their heartfelt gratitude to Linda and the staff at Acorn Hill for their compassionate care, as well as Amanda and the staff at Interim for the exceptional support they provided.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 12:00 p.m. on Thursday, April 9, 2026, at St. Paul Catholic Church, 603 4th Street, Mosinee. Visitation will take place from 10:00 a.m. until the time of service at the church. Burial will follow at St. Paul Catholic Cemetery.

Linda's warmth, kindness, and love for her family will be deeply missed and forever cherished.

records.name: Michael Allan Wilde
records.summary: Michael Allan Wilde, 53, of Merrill, passed away on March 26, 2026.
records.body:
Michael "Mike" Allan Wilde, 53, Merrill, passed away on March 26, 2026.

He was born May 5, 1972 in Wausau, son of the late Jerome and Nancy (Ringwelski) Wilde.

He graduated from Merrill Sr. High School in 1990. Mike worked the majority of his career at Walmart for 31 1/2 years followed by a few years alongside the company delivering for Walmart Spark and was loved by all that worked with him for his sense of humor and work ethic. "He was a great guy." He was always ready for that fun sarcastic comment. He loved playing hockey and golf in his younger years and was an avid sports fan, namely football and baseball, but enjoyed following all sports and attending games.

Mike's love for music and strong friendships took him to the annual RockFest in Cadott WI. He also was a large Merrill history buff—finding old photos and comparing them to the new one's he would take, sharing them with the community. You can find some of his photos on the Facebook group: Merrill, Wisconsin Scrolling Down Memory Lane. Mike worked hard away from his job to give his 2 daughters, who he loved more than anything, many beautiful skating rinks as they grew up and upkept on a large above ground swimming pool in the summer.

Survivors include his daughters, Mya and Ahna Wilde, Merrill, his siblings, Gary Wilde, Weston, Brenda (Robert Sr.) McNeil, Fence, and George (Vicki) Wilde, Merrill, his former wife, Colette (Tracey) Wilde-Sawczuk, Merrill, and several nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his infant son, Nolan, his sister-in-law Dara Wilde, his parents Nancy and Jerome (Sr.) Wilde and his cat Oreo who was his best buddy.

Funeral Services will be held at 11:30 a.m. Thursday, April 9, 2026 at the VFW of Merrill, 601 Johnson Street, Merrill, WI. Visitation will be held that same Thursday from 9:30 a.m. until the time of services also at the Merrill VFW. Burial will follow the service at Merrill Memorial Park.

records.name: Arthur F. Koepke
records.summary: Arthur F. Koepke, age 99, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on March 28, 2026.
records.body:
On March 28, 2026, Arthur "Art" Koepke, age 99, of Wausau, WI, didn't just pass on, he went home to the Lord and to join his wife, Mary, other loved ones, and friends who have been waiting patiently for him.

Arthur Francis Koepke was born July 11, 1926, to Dr. Arthur T. Koepke and Alma Luick Koepke Holzhauer in Milwaukee, WI. He graduated from Wauwatosa High School in 1944. He married the best wife and mother, Mary June Schaefer, on November 15, 1952. Together they created a beautiful family of 6 children, 11 grandchildren and 1 great grandchild.

Art joined the Navy right after high school and was ready to give his all to the country he loved. Unfortunately, he contracted Scarlet Fever and saw his navy buddies go off to WWII. He studied pharmacy and served as a hospital corpsman in the Navy and always held veterans and their service close to his heart. He was active in the Montgomery Plant American Legion Post #10 and was a past president of the Wausau Serra Club and Wausau Centurions. A man of faith, he was twice elected Grand Knight of the Knights of Columbus Sacred Heart Council #4628.

After attending the University of Wisconsin, he had 35 years of dedicated service to Kraft Foods Company, retiring as Retail Sales Zone Manager for Northern Wisconsin. He built lasting friendships and took great pride in his work. He was an outstanding salesman and truly proud of the young men and women he hired and trained, especially those who are still with Kraft today. He received many awards and won multiple contests for selling product, enabling him and Mary to go on many award trips. This began a lifelong love of traveling and seeing many different countries; Ireland, Poland, Germany, Australia and Canada, to name a few.

Art was a big fan of the Green Bay Packers, Saint Louis Cardinals, and Notre Dame football. He was blessed with a fantastic memory and a great sense of humor. Art was an ardent Philatelist who was very proud of the binders containing the many stamps he had bought, collected and organized. This hobby enabled him to acquire a wealth of knowledge about many subjects. He prided himself on knowing something about everything and enjoyed playing the Super Quiz from the Wisconsin State Journal-many times getting the top score amongst the family and all who played. He was a huge fan of Jeopardy, and often, but not all the time, was able to answer the Final Jeopardy question. Art enjoyed the monthly Poker games with the old neighbors from Kronenwetter. He was never known to turn down a game of Hearts. Anyone who played with him knew he ALWAYS tried to shoot the moon but rarely got it.

Over the years, Art went on many fishing trips with his two sons, brother John, and close friend, Jim Morgan. His favorite spot was the Chippewa Flowage. Pictures prove it wasn't always the fish that made the trips fun, it was the camaraderie, card games, and his outfits. Art, along with Mary, had a passion for playing golf and even carried the clubs in the trunk of the car in case they had time for a quick round. Both Art and Mary were devoted Packer fans and handed down the love of football to their children and grandchildren. Most importantly, Art and Mary shared with their children the love of God and the joy in the weekly Mass. He was very supportive of Sacred Heart Missions in Southaven, MS and was proud to have a leaf on their Tree of Life.

Together, Art and Mary lived an excellent example of a successful marriage and taught their children what dedication means for parents. Art passed on an excellent work ethic when it came to the working world and when it came to jobs around the house, always saying "Don't do the job twice, do it right once."

Art is lovingly survived by his children Stephen (Susan) Koepke, Mary Dunn, Jane (Donnie) Alvis, Carol (Rick) Goecke, and Peter (Lizzy) Koepke; his grandchildren, Patrick, Shannon, Ryley, Abbey, Jeffrey (Brittany), Connor (Brandy), Kyle, Hannah (Devon Fontenot), Hazel, Charlie and Tommy; and 1 great grandchild, Gunnar. He leaves behind his brothers, John (Pat) Koepke and Mark (Cis) Holzhauer, and in-laws, Carol Meganck, Geri Barwick, and John (Rosie) Schaefer. He will be missed by many extended family members and friends.

He is preceded in death by his wife, Mary; infant son, Arthur III; brother, Bob; sister, Betty (Don Koleson); stepsister, Virginia "Gini" Holzhauer; his parents, Dr. Arthur and Alma; and numerous other relatives.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be at Saint Therese Catholic Church, Rothschild, WI, at 11:00 a.m. Visitation from 10:00 a.m. until the time of service. In lieu of flowers, memorials made in Art's name can be sent to: Sacred Heart Southern Missions, 6050 Hwy 161, Walls, MS 38686-9099 or The Alzheimer's Association, Wausau, 505 S 24th Avenue, Suite 304, Wausau, WI 54401.

Dad, there is no more pain, no more grief, only love and comfort for eternity.

records.name: Gary J. Glenetski
records.summary: Gary J. Glenetski, age 70, passed away peacefully on March 27, 2026.
records.body:
Gary Glenetski, a beloved husband, father, brother, grandfather, great-grandfather, and cherished friend, passed away peacefully on March 27, 2026, in the home he built with his wife and faithful friend Gizzy at his side.

Gary was born on July 17, 1955, in Wausau, Wisconsin. The son to Leonard and Mary (Bursel) Glenetski. Gary married the love of his life Connie Stahl on August 7, 1993, and the couple enjoyed 32 years together. An honorable man, Gary served his country proudly in the United States Marine Corps and continued his commitment through over 20 years with the Wisconsin National Guard. His dedication extended beyond service; it shaped his character, instilling in him a profound sense of loyalty and camaraderie.

Gary's vibrant personality brightened every room he entered. Known for his sharp wit and outgoing nature, he carried a joyful spirit that was infectious. Whether spending a sunny afternoon pontooning or engaging in a friendly game of Chicken Bingo on the Rainbow Flowage, he was always full of life and enthusiasm. A true sports enthusiast, Gary was a major Green Bay Packers fan, in the fall on any Packer Sunday your guaranteed to find Gary at the Packer Play House, usually out coachingfrom the coach of course, and an avid hunter and fisherman who found peace and excitement in the great outdoors. One could often find him watching the birds and deer with a contented smile, music playing in the background, embodying a love for the simple pleasures that life offered.

Gary's greatest joy was being surrounded by his "special friends," often with a beer in hand and his beloved wife, Connie, by his side. He treasured the company of his family and friends, giving generously of his time and heart.

He is survived by his loving wife, Connie Glenetski; his sons, Nicholas (April) Glenetski and Johnathan Glenetski; his stepsons, Joshua (Amanda) Maltbey and Aaron (Molly) Maltbey; his siblings, Blaine Glenetski, Joe (Gloria) Glenetski, and Cathy Baumann; and his grandchildren, Nicholas Jr., McKayla, Adelyne, Lily, Ivan, Elenore, Xander, Brennan, Adahlia, and Lenox. Gary was also the proud great-grandfather to Hendrix, Barrett and Evera. He is also survived by Gizzy his beloved dog of almost 10 years and they were best buddies; you would often see them roaming the neighborhood on the golf cart together. The family's loss is profound, yet their gratitude for sharing in Gary's remarkable life shines through their tears.

He was preceded in death by his parents and his brothers Mark Glenetski, David Glenetski, and Richard Glenetski, whose memories live on in the stories and hearts of those who loved Gary.

A memorial service to honor Gary's life will be held on Saturday, April 11, 2026, at 12:00 Noon, at Ascend Funeral Home, 2809 Jelinek Ave. Weston, WI, 54476, with Pastor Lance Hoelscher presiding. Visitation will take place from 10:00AM until the time of service at 12:00 Noon. Full Military Honors will take place following the service.

In lieu of flowers, donations may be made to Gigi's Playhouse in Gary's name in honor of our grandson Lenox Maltbey.

records.name: Helen V. Busch
records.summary: Helen V. Busch, age 93, a long-time resident of Stevens Point, Lake DuBay, and Wausau, died peacefully on March 19, 2026.
records.body:
Helen Busch (nee Rauschelbach), 93, a long-time resident of Stevens Point, Lake DuBay, and Wausau, died peacefully on Thursday, March 19, 2026 in Wausau.

A memorial service will be held at St. John's Lutheran Church, 2291 Church Road, Dancy, WI Wednesday, April 22 with the Reverend Doctor Timothy Roser officiating. Visitation will commence at 12:30 PM with the service following at 1:30 PM. A reception and reflection of Helen's life will follow the service.

Helen was born in Sheboygan on September 10, 1932 to Hugo and Martha Rauschelbach, the second of four daughters. She grew up in Mount Clemons, MI until the family returned to Wisconsin, moving to Cedarburg in 1946.

Helen attended Concordia Teachers' College in River Forest, IL in 1952. While studying at Concordia, Helen met her future husband, Robert W. Busch. They married in June, 1953 and remained together until his death in 2015.

In 1967, Helen and her family moved to Stevens Point, where she returned to her studies at the University of Wisconsin Stevens Point. She graduated with a B.S. in Education in 1970. She also earned a Master's degree in Education from the same university.

A lifelong educator, she spent most of her career teaching at Kennedy Elementary School in Junction City.

Helen was a lifelong member of the Lutheran Church, Missouri Synod.

Helen is survived by her sisters Martha Doepel, Yukon, OK and Claire Hilgendorf, Appleton; her three sons: John (Kathy), Stevens Point; Kurt (Katharine), Seattle, WA; and Paul (Kris), Weston; seven grandchildren and nine great-grandchildren.

records.name: Mary J. Krueger
records.summary: Mary J. Krueger, age 77, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on March 29, 2026.
records.body:
Mary Krueger, age 77, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on March 29, 2026, at home surrounded by her loving family.

Born on March 8, 1949, in Woodruff, Wisconsin, the daughter of Victor and Mary (Paquette) Evenson. Mary married the love of her life Don Krueger on December 21, 1968, and the couple enjoyed 57 years together. Mary lived a life marked by love, devotion, and a zest for the simple joys that filled her days.

Mary was a cherished wife, loving mother, and doting grandmother. She shared her life with her husband, Donald Krueger, who stood by her side through all the seasons of her life. They built a family rooted in warmth and togetherness, including their daughter, Rebecca (James) LaMarche, and son, Steven (Sarah) Krueger. Mary's heart was especially attuned to the delight of spending time with her grandchildren: Austin LaMarche, Ashley LaMarche, and Owen Krueger, all of whom brought immeasurable joy to her life.

Those who knew Mary remember her fondness for collecting giraffes, a unique hobby that reflected her whimsical spirit and appreciation for the distinctive beauty in life. She had a passion for family gatherings, particularly cherishing the moments spent 'up north,' where nature's tranquility complemented her peaceful and nurturing presence.

Mary was preceded in death by her parents, her brother Thomas Evenson, and her in-laws, Leslie and Jeanette Krueger. Their memories live on through the family she lovingly nurtured and the many lives she touched with her kindness.

To honor Mary's memory, a memorial service will be held on Saturday, April 18, 2026, at 3:00PM, at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston, WI 54476. Visitation will be held from 2:00PM until the time of service at 3:00PM.

Mary Krueger's legacy is one of unwavering love, joyful companionship, and cherished family bonds. Her presence will be deeply missed, yet her spirit will forever remain a guiding light for those fortunate to have known her.

The family would like to give a special thank you to Aspirus Hospice for their care, and a major thank you to nurse Cathy and hospice social worker Jen for all of their help and care to Mary and the entire family.

records.name: Ronald Sem
records.summary: Ronald Sem, age 84, passed away peacefully on March 8, 2026.
records.body:
Ronald Sem, age 84, passed away peacefully at home on March 8, 2026, surrounded by family. Ron, born in 1941 to Ida and Kenneth Sem, survived by his loving wife, Diane, and his sons, Michael and Matthew. Ron is also survived by his sisters, Frances Mcllroy and Delores Urevick. He was preceded in death by his brother, Kenny.

Ron attended Minot High School and the University of North Dakota, graduating in 1964. He took his first teaching job in Duluth. In 1968, they moved back to North Dakota and Ron got his master's degree in teaching at UND in 1969. In 1970, he became employed as a marketing instructor in Wausau. After teaching there for 24 years, he became Dean of the Business Department in 1994, a position he held until his retirement in 2000. After retirement, Ron began attending many political activities and events. As part of his activism, he would send letters to the editor of the local paper and to members of political offices.

Ron played basketball at Minot High School and received a scholarship to play basketball for 4 years at UND. His love of sports continued throughout his life after his playing days were over, as evidenced by many family activities that were centered around sports. Ron and his family attended many professional football, baseball, basketball, and tennis events. In addition, through Ron's efforts, his family was able to attend their first NCAA basketball tournament in Minneapolis. They also had regular family game nights, which usually became quite lively. Ron loved his boys dearly and used their mutual participation in sports to teach them life lessons they have relied on since childhood.

Ron met his wife in Grand Forks while at UND. They started dating in 1960 and were married in Fall of 1962, a partnership that would last many years. They enjoyed playing golf, tennis, and racquetball together. They also loved riding their bikes all around the Wausau area. In addition, they took pleasure dining out and playing cards in the company of their friends.

Besides his accomplishments and recreational pursuits, Ron will be forever remembered as a kind and caring man with a wonderful sense of humor. A celebration of his life will be held this summer. His family would like to thank the teams of Heartland Hospice for all of the care they provided, along with the John J Buettgen Funeral Home for their assistance.

records.name: Christopher John Schauder
records.summary: Christopher John Schauder, 58, of Tomahawk, Wisconsin, died peacefully at his home.
records.body:
Christopher John Schauder, age 58, died peacefully at his home in Tomahawk, Wisconsin, on March 31, 2026.

Born on January 22, 1968, in Rhinelander, Wisconsin, Chris was a graduate of Rhinelander High School, class of 1986, and attended the University of Wisconsin-Madison. He built his career and much of his adult life in Evergreen, Colorado, where he served as New Service and Environmental Manager for the Evergreen Water Department for more than twenty years — work that reflected his deep commitment to the land and communities he loved. In 2022, he returned to the Northwoods of Wisconsin to be close to his family, and the lake outside his window suited him just fine.

Chris made his living in service to his community, but he made his life on the water, in the mountains, and among the people — and dogs — he loved.
