He was a fisherman at heart, at home on the lake with a line in the water and no particular hurry. He loved the Colorado mountains — Kenosha Pass, the summit of Mount Evans — and live music was the thread that ran through it all, never more perfectly than on the stage at Red Rocks under an open sky. He found his way to the Cubby Bear Bar in Chicago on New Year's Eve, 1993, where he shared his first kiss with Marcy — proof that the best nights always have a good soundtrack. He was a dedicated Green Bay Packers fan, humble about it in the way only a true fan can be, though those who followed him on game days knew to watch for a single, satisfied "Woot" after every win. He was devoted to his dogs, Comet, Groovy, Luna, and Petey, each of whom knew exactly where to find him. He lived fully and without apology, and he brought everyone around him along for the ride.

He will be remembered for his fierce independence, his dry humor, his loyalty to the people he loved, and his quiet, unwavering courage in the face of a battle he faced with his eyes open and his head up. He fought esophageal cancer the way he did everything — on his own terms.

Chris was preceded in death by his beloved wife and soulmate, Marcy Newman; his maternal grandparents, Mary and John Sharples; and his paternal grandparents, Herb and Carol Schauder.

He is survived by his parents, Pat and Phil Schauder of Rhinelander; his favorite sister, Rita Marquardt and her husband Karl of the Wausau area; his brother, Mark Schauder and his wife Kathleen of Madison; sister-in-law Elissa Newman-Karhoff and her husband Gary of Scottsdale; his nieces and nephews, Nicole Briskie (Ryan), Matt Marquardt (Elizabeth), Gavin and Wyatt Schauder, and Lucas and Ricky Grobart; his uncle, Bob Jaeckin; his uncle, Jack Sharples; his aunts, Lynda Lukowski and Phoebe McIntyre; cousins around the world; and lifelong friends — Paul, Keeter, and Kyle — who made his world richer than he would ever admit.

According to Chris's wishes, there will be no immediate memorial service. A Celebration of Life will be held later this summer, giving people the time they need before gathering — just as he and Marcy had done before. Details will be shared when available.

The family wishes to express their deepest gratitude to the teams at Compassus Hospice and Aspirus Cancer Center in Rhinelander, whose compassion, skill, and dedication to Chris's comfort and dignity made an immeasurable difference during his final months. He was in extraordinary hands, and his family will not forget it. The family also wishes to thank Hildebrand Funeral Home for their care and professionalism during this difficult time.

In lieu of flowers, the family asks that donations be made in Chris's memory to the Colorado Gynecologic Cancer Alliance (CGCA) — a cause that was deeply important to both Chris and Marcy. CGCA provides emotional and financial support to Coloradans diagnosed with gynecologic cancers and was close to Marcy's heart during her own fight. Donations may be made at ovariancancercolorado.org

records.name: Donald F. Lenzner
records.summary: Donald F. Lenzner, 72, of Weston, passed away on March 28, 2026.
records.body:
Donald Lenzner, age 72, of Weston, passed away on March 28, 2026, surrounded by his three loving daughters, under the care of Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services and Rennes Health and Rehab Center.

Donald was born in Wausau, Wisconsin, to the late Roy and Victoria (Krall) Lenzner. He graduated from Wausau West High School in 1972 and went on to earn his Associate Degree in Marketing from Northcentral Technical College. He was known to many as Don or Donnie.

Donald had a deep love for the outdoors and especially enjoyed hunting and fishing. He was known for getting up early—often before sunrise—to head out for a day of fishing or hunting. He cherished time spent with his good friends and family, traveling on hunting trips throughout the United States and fishing on Lake Superior. He also enjoyed going for drives, taking in the quiet and simple moments along the way.

Donald had a playful side and could be quite the jokester at times, bringing laughter to those around him. He also enjoyed playing bingo and the simple joy of being around others. He loved going for walks and was known for his friendly nature, often greeting those he passed with a warm "Have a good day." A loyal fan, Donald loved watching the Green Bay Packers and rarely missed a game.

Above all, Donald was a protector at heart—steadfast, strong, and devoted to his three girls, always looking out for them with unwavering love.

Donald is survived by his daughters, Jaime Kelly, Staci (Paul) Kamke, and Tiffani (Chris) Plautz; grandchildren, Maizi and Emma; siblings, Carol Jean Krummel, Dale (Lauren) Lenzner, Gloria Lenzner Ruether, Sharon (David) Larsen; sister-in-law, Karen Lenzner; companion, Marilyn Moser; nieces and nephews; and extended family and friends.

He was preceded in death by his father, Roy Lenzner; his mother, Victoria Lenzner; brother, Roger Lenzner; and his brother-in-law James Krummel.

A Funeral Mass will be held on Friday, April 10, 2026, at 10:30 a.m. at St. Anne Catholic Church, 700 W. Bridge Street, Wausau, WI 54401, with Fr. Tom Lindner officiating. Visitation will be held from 9:30 a.m. until the time of Mass at the church. Burial will follow at Restlawn Memorial Park Cemetery.

His legacy of love and protection will remain with his family always.

records.name: Gene Robert Emmerich
records.summary: Gene Robert Emmerich, 71, lovingly known as "Doc", of the Village of Maine, passed away unexpectedly on March 28, 2026.
records.body:
Gene Robert Emmerich, 71, lovingly known as "Doc", passed away unexpectedly at his home in the Village of Maine on Saturday, March 28, 2026.

Gene was born on October 5, 1954, to Lester and Erna (Bungert) Emmerich in Wausau. Gene was an award-winning quilter who enjoyed crafting beautiful quilts for family, friends, and his collection. Moreover, he did needlepoint and loved making candy to bring to work. He participated in many bowling tournaments in Merrill and Reno. He was happiest in his greenhouse at home, gardening and planting flowers.

Gene is survived by his wife, Ruth Ann Emmerich; mother, Erna Emmerich; siblings, Linda (Christopher) Nikolai, Jackie (Randy) Woldt, Scott Emmerich, Tom (Sherrie) Emmerich, Patti (Pat) Champine, and James (Diane Fischer) Emmerich; as well as many nephews, nieces, and godchildren. He is preceded in death by his father, Lester.

In honor of Gene's wishes, private services are being held.

records.name: Jess Patrick Wakely
records.summary: Jess Patrick Wakely, 54, of Rhinelander, passed away on April 1, 2026.
records.body:
There will be a Celebration of Life at a later date for Jess Patrick Wakely, age 54, who passed away on April 1, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital. Jess was born on February 18, 1972 in Rhinelander, WI to Thomas and Katherine (Hankey) Wakely.

Jess is survived by his wife, Tammy; daughter, Jade; brothers, Mike, Mitch, Chris, and John; sisters, Christine, Jessica, Ana and Vicki; and many nieces and nephews.

Jess will be missed by all who knew him.

records.name: Kathleen A. Basil
records.summary: Kathleen "Kathy" A. Basil, 71, of Wausau, died unexpectedly at her home on March 19, 2026.
records.body:
Kathleen "Kathy" A. Basil, 71, of Wausau, died unexpectedly at her home on Thursday, March 19, 2026.

She was born December 23, 1954, in Wausau, WI, the daughter of the late Norman and Lorraine (Leffel) Reuter. On December 27, 1975, she married Robert "Bob" Basil at Sacred Heart Catholic Church in Polonia. He preceded her in death.

For over 30 years, Kathy worked for the Marathon County Public library in Wausau. She especially enjoyed working with the bookmobile and kids.

Among her favorite pastimes, she enjoyed spending time at the cottage up north on Sand Lake and caring for children in her home.

Survivors include her brother, Wayne (Cyndi) Reuter, of Wausau; and her sister, Rose (Dean) Markofski, of Schofield; and nieces and nephews.

Besides her parents and her husband, she was preceded in death by her sister, Patricia Reuter.

No funeral services will be held.

records.name: Mary Sue Paul
records.summary: Mary Sue Paul, 70, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on April 1, 2026.
records.body:
Mary Sue Paul, age 70, of Wausau, Wisconsin passed away peacefully on April 1, 2026, after her courageous battle with lung cancer. She was surrounded by family and snuggled in by Peanut, her canine companion of 13 years.

Mary was born in Marshfield, Wisconsin to Richard and Shirley Paul. Following family moves to Hinsdale, Illinois and Lansing, Michigan, the family settled in Wausau, and she graduated from Wausau East High School in 1974.

Her life and career were in Wausau where she served as a home health personal care assistant to people with disabilities, injuries and age-related challenges. With her big heart, Mary loved to care for and provide comfort to her clients, young and old. With professionalism, empathy, and optimism, she personified caregiving and inspired many people.

Mary was predeceased by her mother Shirley Gies Paul, her father Richard Paul and stepmother Joan Banals. Mary is survived by her stepmother Kathleen Paul of Albert Lea MN, sister Barbara Paul (Tom) of Pebble Beach CA, brothers Mike Paul (Cathy) of Edgerton WI, and William Paul of Seaside CA, stepbrothers Jeff Naden (Becky) of Pflugerville TX and David Naden (Linda) of Hillsborough NC, uncle Jim and aunt Yvonne Gies of Stevens Point WI, aunt Betty Gies of Watertown, WI, aunt Audrey Paul of KY, and many cousins, nieces and nephews.

Mary loved her friends and gave them her unwavering support – something that they will always remember. She treasured her constant companion, Peanut, and he was devoted to her. When Mary was the recipient of kindness and caring, she sent it back out into the world ten-fold. When life brought challenge, she still shared her love. This is her legacy.

Mary's family is grateful to so many people. We loved seeing her with her dedicated friends, who especially supported her through this cancer journey. We thank her many healthcare providers, including Dr. Niaz Haque and the team at Aspirus Cancer Center. Aspirus Hospice, especially the loving gentle care at Aspirus Hospice House in Wausau, was magnificent. It was beautiful to see how their dedicated and tender care helped release a lightness and happiness in Mary in her final weeks. And to Peanut's new "forever family," we send our deep appreciation and love.

At Mary's request, there will be no services, and her ashes will be interred near family in Albert Lea, MN. In lieu of flowers, please donate in her memory to the Aspirus Hospice House or the Marty Bus (Community Outreach Task Force) – and use this moment to give a big hug to the health care providers and caregivers who play a crucial role in the lives of people in need.

records.name: Peggy Marie Weinkauf
records.summary: Peggy Marie Weinkauf, 65, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on April 1, 2026.
records.body:
Peggy Marie Weinkauf (Knoblock), 65, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully surrounded by her family, on Wednesday, April 1, 2026, at the Aspirus Hospice House.

Peggy was born on January 23, 1961, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to Dan Knoblock Sr. and Doris (Seehafer) Springer. She graduated from Wausau East High School with the Class of 1979 and later married the love of her life, Randall Weinkauf, on October 29, 1983, at Trinity Lutheran Church in Merrill, Wisconsin.

Peggy was a dedicated homemaker who devoted her life to raising her six children. She took great pride in her family and was a fierce protector of those she loved. Her faith was strong, and it guided the way she lived her life, always placing family at the center of everything she did.

She had a gift for bringing people together and loved hosting family gatherings and campfires. Peggy found joy in decorating her home and made every holiday special, especially Christmas and Easter. Christmas was always a big celebration filled with presents and laughter, while Easter brought her famous large Easter egg hunts that her family will never forget. She also had a passion for collecting dolls from the 1960s and built an impressive collection over the years. Peggy cherished the time spent at her vacation home in Englewood, Florida, where she enjoyed making memories with friends and family.

Peggy also had a love for the color pink, her absolute favorite, and it felt especially fitting to those who knew her that she passed under a Pink Full Moon.

In addition to her devotion to her family, Peggy proudly served on the Board of Directors for River View Construction, Inc., where she held the role of Vice Chair.

Peggy is survived by her loving husband, Randall Weinkauf; her children, Jesse (Marlo) Turner, Wesley Weinkauf, Heidi (Jeremiah) Dehnel, Angelina (Andrew) Hoss, and Dakota Heath; her grandchildren, Stella Turner, Autumn Weinkauf, Brett Dehnel, Deacon Dehnel, Miley Dehnel, Charles Mistlebauer, Vivienne Mistlebauer, Owen Hoss, and Asher Hoss; brothers and sisters, Jenny (Craig) Erickson, Dan (Jacki) Knoblock, Josh (Jessica) Springer, Rebecca (Jeremy) Peru, Amber Alsteen, and Kary (Josh) Schwie; and many nieces and nephews.

She was preceded in death by her son, Riley Weinkauf; her father, Dan Knoblock Sr.; her grandparents, Norman and Ethel Knoblock, and William and Loretta Seehafer; her nephew, Cameron Springer; and Jesse's father, Curt Turner.

A funeral service will be at 11:00 a.m. on Friday, April 10, 2026, at Highland Community Church, 1005 North 28th Avenue, Wausau. Visitation will begin at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau, on Thursday, April 9 at 4:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. and continue at the church on Friday, beginning at 9:30 a.m. until the time of service. Private family burial will take place in Restlawn Memorial Park.

Peggy will be remembered for her unwavering love for her family, her strong faith, and the warmth she brought to every gathering. Her presence will be deeply missed, but the memories she created will live on in all who knew and loved her.

The family would like to thank the Aspirus Hospice House for their amazing and loving care.

records.name: Ronald J. Walters
records.summary: Ronald J. Walters, 75, of the town of Texas, passed away on March 27, 2026.
records.body:
Ronald J. Walters, 75, of the town of Texas, passed away on Friday, March 27, 2026, at his home, surrounded by his family, and under the care of Interim Hospice.

He was born on September 5, 1950, in Wausau, to the late Frank and Helen (Thorn) Walters. Ron was united in marriage to Deborah Zimmerman on December 31, 1983, at Zion Lutheran Church in Wausau. He was a devoted husband, father, and grandfather who deeply valued the life he built with Deb and their family.

Ron was a kidney transplant recipient multiple times throughout his life, receiving his first transplant in 1979. He carried a profound gratitude for the gift of life and the years he was able to share with those he loved.

He proudly partnered with his brother, Jim, in running the family farm for many years. Together with his wife and family, he experienced the hard work, risks, and rewards of dairy and beef farming, as well as crop production. Ron also served as a board member for AMPI for 15 years, a role that allowed him to travel and attend conferences—often bringing his family along and creating many meaningful memories together. Over the years, he held various jobs, including his longest tenure with L & L Propane. Ron enjoyed staying active and social—playing softball, bowling, and shooting pool at Homestead. Above all, he loved supporting his children and grandchildren at their sporting events, rarely missing an opportunity to cheer them on.

He is survived by his wife, Deborah; children, Samantha (Corey) Sanders, Melissa (Andrew) Krueger, Joe (Sam) Walters, and Tom Walters; grandchildren, Evelynn, Sophia, Asher, Hinley, Sylvia, Alaina, and Clancy; siblings, Mary Jo (Bob) Prahl, Terri Fronek, Jim (Kay) Walters, and Mike (Jean) Walters; along with many nieces, nephews, and cousins.

He was preceded in death by his parents.

A Funeral Mass will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Thursday, April 9, 2026, at St. Michael Catholic Church, 611 Stark Street, Wausau, with Rev. Tom Lindner presiding. Visitation will begin at 10:00 a.m. until the time of service at the church. A celebration of life will follow the Mass at Homestead on Hwy 52 in Nutterville.

The family extends their heartfelt gratitude to the team at Interim Hospice for their compassionate care and support, to Dr. Abrego with Marshfield Clinic Nephrology for his dedicated and attentive care, and to the staff at UW Hospital and Clinics in Madison for their exceptional care and guidance throughout Ron's transplant journey over the years.

records.name: Susan M. Grosskreutz
records.summary: Susan M. Grosskreutz, 84, of Hudson, formerly of Edgar, passed away peacefully on March 29, 2026.
records.body:
Susan M. Grosskreutz, Hudson, formerly of Edgar, passed away peacefully on Sunday, March 29, 2026 while under the care of Lakeview Hospice at Christian Community Home, Hudson.

She was born on October 13, 1941 in Milwaukee, daughter of the late Raymond and Sophie (Pawluczak) Marzynski. On March 28, 1978 she married Otto "Hunce" Grosskreutz. He preceded her in death on March 7, 2016.

Susan's career in elementary education spanned nearly 35 years, with the majority of her time spent at the Edgar School District, where she taught 3rd grade. Her teaching extended beyond the classroom, as she continued to impart her knowledge at the care center where she dedicated her final three years. A passionate gardener, Susan indulged in canning all the fruits and vegetables she grew. Her deep-rooted connection to farming was evident when she purchased a farm and managed ginseng fields, a venture that predated the widespread recognition of such pursuits.

Susan's hobby of Polish paper cutting, known as "Wycinanki," left an indelible mark on many lives, adorning every graduation, Christmas, and birthday card with her exquisite creations. Her cooking skills were exceptional. She used instinct and observation instead of measuring instruments, memorizing the recipes by taste and appearance. She had great pride and interest in her Polish heritage leading her to researching and publication, sharing this history with everyone she believed should know. Family meant the world to her and enjoyed every opportunity to gather.

Survivors include her sister, Jessica Delwiche, River Falls, nieces: Amy Klein (Kevin), Sara Marzynski, Hayden Marzynski (Bernice Hernandez), nephews, Luke Delwiche (Katie), Nick Delwiche (Stacy) Daniel Marzynski (Brenda)

Besides her parents and husband, she was preceded in death by her brothers, Mark and John and a brother-in-law, Jerome Delwiche.

A Memorial Service will be held at 11:00 a.m. Saturday, April 25, 2026 at St. John Catholic Church, Edgar. The Rev. Alan Wierzba will preside. Burial will be in the parish cemetery. Visitation will be held on Saturday from 9:30 a.m. until the time of services all at the church.

records.name: Vicki L. Erdmann
records.summary: Vicki L. Erdmann, 76, of Weston, passed away peacefully on March 29, 2026.
records.body:
Vicki L. Erdmann, 76, Weston, passed away peacefully on Sunday, March 29, 2026 at her home with her husband, Bob by her side, under the care of Aspirus at Home Hospice.

She was born March 6, 1950 in Pawnee, Oklahoma, daughter of the late Gerald and Mary Lynn (Fisher) Kloxin. On January 1, 1985, she married Robert "Bob" Erdmann in Wausau. He survives.

For 10 years, Vicki was a waitress for Wausau Mine Company in Wausau, a job she truly loved. Among her favorite pastimes, she liked crafting, craft shows and spending time with her family, especially her grandchildren. She also enjoyed remodeling projects throughout home.

Survivors include her husband, Bob; three children, Machelle (Greg) Eschmann, Rhett Adams and Brigit Adams; step-daughter, Rachel Gable; five grandchildren; three step-grandchildren; and her sister-in-law, Candy Kloxin.

She was preceded in death by her parents and brother, Gary Kloxin.

In honor of Vicki's request, private services will be held.

records.name: Clarence R. Pochinski
records.summary: Clarence R. Pochinski, 60, of Mosinee, passed away April 1, 2026.
records.body:
Clarence R. "Duzer" Pochinski, 60, Mosinee, died Wednesday, April 1, 2026 surrounded by his loved ones at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born on November 12, 1965, son of the late Virginia (Buchkowski) Pochinski and Clarence Pochinski, Hatley. On April 19, 1986 he married Jean Rucinski in Mosinee.

Duzer, known to many by his nickname but born Clarence, lived a life defined by quiet strength, deep faith, and unwavering devotion to his family. A man who put God first in all things, Duzer was a devoted Christian who knew Jesus as his Lord and Savior, and he carried that faith with him in the way he lived, loved, and served others.

He shared 40 wonderful years of marriage with his high school sweetheart, Jean—a partnership built on love, commitment, and shared values. Together, they created a life centered around family, faith, and simple joys.

Duzer was a thoughtful and intelligent man, known for his honesty and depth. Though naturally quiet, his words carried weight; when he spoke, it mattered. He possessed a remarkable memory for dates and historical trivia, and he approached life with both curiosity and a strong work ethic. He spent many years working at Hatley Veneer and later retired from Domtar after more than 30 years of dedicated service.

Above all, Duzer was a family man. He was a loving father and an exceptional grandfather whose presence left a lasting impact on those closest to him. His son fondly remembers how proud his Father was of his military service. His daughter cherished the honor of being walked down the aisle by her father, a memory she holds close to her heart. Another cherished memory of his daughter is when she was having a bad day and Dad visited her at work, bringing a small gift and giving her a hug, a simple but meaningful way of lifting her spirits.

To his grandchildren, Duzer was a constant and comforting presence. He spent countless hours playing baseball with them, teaching them how to ride snowmobiles, and encouraging their imaginations. Walks to the mailbox—though long—became treasured moments of conversation and connection. He loved watching them leave for school in the mornings and greeting them when they returned, often stepping outside just to say hello. Evenings were often spent sharing popcorn and stories, creating memories that will be cherished forever.

He took great pride in attending his grandchildren's sporting events, always present, always supportive. Whether on the sidelines or at home, he invested his time fully in the lives of those he loved. Duzer found joy in the simple things—laughter, conversation, and togetherness—and he made those around him feel valued and seen.

Despite a lifelong battle with diabetes, Duzer never complained. His resilience and quiet perseverance were a testament to his character.

Duzer will be remembered as a man of faith, integrity, and deep love. He was greatly loved and will be profoundly missed by his family, who will carry his memory forward in the values he lived by and the moments they shared. His legacy is one of love, faith, and family—exactly as he would have wanted.

Survivors include his wife, Jean Pochinski, Mosinee, his father, Clarence Pochinski, Hatley, his children, Niki (Al) Pearcy, Mosinee, and Brad (Maeghan) Pochinski, Mosinee, his grandchildren, Seth, Leon and Cid Berger, Bella, Naomi and Bryce Pearcy and Braylon, Eric, Odin and Asher Pochinski, his brother, David (Sandy) Pochinski, Wausau, and his sister, Angel (Matt) Brabender, Hatley, and three nephews, Kyle Pochinski, and Dilan and Devan Brabender.

Besides his mother he was preceded in death by a nephew, Dustin Pochinski.

Funeral services will be held at 11:00 a.m. Saturday, April 11, 2026 at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau. The Rev. Arnold Allison will officiate. Burial will be at a later date in Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau. Visitation will be held on Saturday from 9:30 a.m. until the time of services all at the Sixth Street funeral home.

records.name: James E. Ausloos
records.summary: James E. Ausloos, 80, of Kronenwetter, passed away March 31, 2026.
records.body:
James E. "Jim" Ausloos, 80, of Kronenwetter, WI, passed away peacefully on Tuesday, March 31, 2026. He was born April 8, 1945, a twin to Jerry, to Elmer Sr. and Rosalia (Gross) Ausloos in Plymouth, WI.

Jim graduated from New Holstein High School in 1963 and later earned an Associate's degree from Moraine Park Technical College in 1968. He married Marie Steffes at Holy Cross Church in Mount Calvary, WI, on April 15, 1967.

A devoted family man, Jim prioritized time with his family, coaching each of his children in youth baseball and enjoying travel with friends and relatives. He treasured evenings and holidays spent playing cards and games with extended family and friends.

He is survived by his wife, Marie; daughter, Dede Schwerin of Cecil; sons, Mike Ausloos of Kronenwetter and Dave (Amy) Ausloos of Menomonee Falls; grandchildren, Reid Schwerin, Gage, Drew, and Cam Ausloos; twin brother, Jerry (Kathy) Ausloos; sisters, Sharon Koppang and Sandi (Roy) Schwoerer; and sisters-in-law, Lorraine Karls, Ceil (Joe) Schaefer, Annie (John) Schmitz, Judy (Dr. Joe) Reising, and Dr. Jeanne (Dory) Hart Steffes.

He was preceded in death by son-in-law, Steve Schwerin; parents, Elmer Sr. and Rosalia; brother, Elmer Jr.; parents-in-law, Ben and Virginia Steffes; and brothers and sisters-in-law, Grant Koppang, Elmer and Betty Steffes, and Gerry Karls; nephew, Steve Butz; great-nephew, Kenny Coye, Jr.

Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 12:00 PM on Tuesday, April 7, at St. Mark Catholic Parish, 602 Military Road, Rothschild, WI 54474. The Rev. Janusz Kowalski will officiate. Visitation will be held from 10:00 a.m. until the time of service at the church. A luncheon will immediately follow the service at St. Mark Elementary School. Interment will be private.

records.name: Janet M. Stroik
records.summary: Janet M. Stroik, 89, of Wausau, passed away March 29, 2026.
records.body:
Janet M. Stroik, 89, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully, surrounded by her family, on March 29, 2026, in Wausau. She was born on November 28, 1936, in Chicago, Illinois, to Walter and Eleanor Lundgren.

Jan graduated from Calumet High School and Moser Business School in Chicago. She began her career at Spiegel and Illinois Bell Telephone before relocating to Wausau. There, she worked at Marmet Corp., where she met her husband, Vic Stroik, and later at Engineered Curtainwall. She went on to serve as a legal assistant at Trembath, Griffin, Stevens & Onan, and retired in 2003 as office manager at Grischke Law Office. In retirement, she continued working part-time at Kohl's in Wausau and in Arizona, where she wintered with her longtime companion, Gene Habeck.

A devoted mother and grandmother, Jan raised her family while attending First United Methodist Church in Wausau and later became actively involved at Mt. Olive Lutheran Church in Weston. She volunteered with hospice for many years, always finding joy in helping others, whether by driving people to appointments, shopping, visiting, or simply being there when needed. She was the life of the party, who loved a good gathering and made sure every occasion included plenty of food and laughter. Nothing brought her greater joy than time spent with her family.

In her retirement years, Jan and Gene enjoyed golfing, traveling, and socializing with friends. Most recently, Jan resided on the fourth floor at Mount View Care Center in Wausau, where the compassionate care team cared for her and treated her like family.

Jan is survived by her three children, Sandy (John) Feldmann, Bruce (Diane) Stroik, and Chuck (Wendi) Stroik; her grandchildren, Eric (Andrea) Feldmann, Corey Cielinski, Jacob (Alexis) Stroik, Ethan (Jordan Murphy) Stroik, Nicole Stroik, and Caleb Stroik; and her great-grandchildren, Micah, Grayson, Emma, and Penelope.

She was preceded in death by her husband, Vic Stroik (1982); her parents; and many dear aunts, uncles, cousins, and in-laws.

Jan leaves behind a legacy of love, generosity, and deep devotion to family that will never be forgotten. She will be deeply missed and lovingly remembered by all who knew her.

The family extends heartfelt thanks to the entire Mount View team for their extraordinary kindness, as well as to her lifelong friend, Sandy Barden, who remained faithfully by her side through every challenge.

Funeral services will be held at 11:00 AM on Thursday, April 30, 2026, at Mt. Olive Lutheran Church, 6205 Alderson Street, Weston, WI 54476. Pastor Lance Hoelscher will officiate. Visitation will be held from 9:00 AM until the time of the service at the church. In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be directed to a charity close to her heart.

records.name: Jay L. Kolbeck
records.summary: Jay L. Kolbeck, 68, of Wausau, passed away March 31, 2026.
records.body:
Jay Kolbeck, of Wausau, passed away on March 31, 2026, peacefully in his home after a long battle with cancer. Jay was born on June 8,1957 to Jerry and Joan (Krueger) Kolbeck. He graduated from Wausau East High School in 1975. Jay married Julie Arps on June 2, 1979. Together they raised two wonderful daughters Jessi and Kelli. Jay leaves behind a legacy that will live on in the hearts of all who knew him.

He was the kind of person who would drop everything without hesitation to help someone in need. Whether it was a neighbor, a friend, or family member, his instinct was always to lend a hand, offer support, and make life a little easier for others. His selflessness was not something he spoke about—it was simply who he was.

Above all, Jay cherished his family. He was a devoted husband and father who deeply loved his wife, Julie, and their daughters, Jessi and Kelli. One of his greatest joys was spending time with his grandchildren, especially at his favorite place—the cottage up north. He took immense pride in watching them participate in their activities and treasured every moment spent together. Jay also found great happiness in the outdoors, enjoying hunting and fishing and sharing those passions with the people he loved most.

He is preceded in death by his parents, Jerry and Joan Kolbeck, his in-laws Louie and June Arps and brother-in-law Kevin Arps.

He is survived by his wife, Julie Kolbeck; his children, Jessi (Scott) Towle, and Kelli (Willie) Vetter; his grandchildren, Nolan, Connor and Emmett Towle, and Madison, Aria, and Cole Vetter. He is further survived by his 5 siblings Jamie (Neal) Treu, Jill (Randy) Forer, Joy (Steve) Henrich, Jeff (Missy) Kolbeck, John (MaryJo) Kolbeck, two sister-in laws, Debbie Overbeck, Shelley Arps, and brother-in law Greg Arps and many nieces and nephews.

He will be remembered for his generous spirit, his steady presence, and the deep love he had for his family. His impact cannot be measured in words, but in the countless lives he touched through his kindness.

Jay will be deeply missed and forever remembered by his beloved wife, family, and all who had the privilege of knowing him. A celebration of Jay's life will be held at a later date. In lieu of flowers, the family kindly requests that memorial donations be made to the American Cancer Society in Jay's name.

records.name: Paul K. Csencsics
records.summary: Paul K. Csencsics, 77, of Wausau, passed away April 2, 2026.
records.body:
Paul Karl Csencsics, 77, a beloved husband, father, grandfather, and friend, passed away peacefully on April 2, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital. Born on January 30, 1949, in Wausau, WI, Paul lived a life rich with dedication, humor, and kindness.

Growing up in Wausau, Paul was known early on for his athletic talents. He was an incredible swimmer and cross-country runner during his time at Wausau Senior High School. His first job was a lifeguard at the McDonald Pool in the summer, which helped him develop his love of water and community. After graduation, from 1968 to 1970, Paul served in the U.S. Army during the Vietnam Era. Beyond his athletic talents, Paul had many roles throughout his life. He spent 27 memorable years working at "the bowling alley," a place where countless locals came to know him for his kind heart, vast knowledge of the bowling industry, and his quick wit. He then worked for American Welding and Gas for 17 years. This type of dedication to every job he had serves as an incredible example of his work ethic.

Anyone who knew Paul would describe him as joking, kind, and always willing to help others. You might ask him, "How are you doing?" and be on the receiving end of one of his legendary one-liners, like "Are you writing a book? Make me chapter 1." When asking for advice from him, he might respond, "I may not always be right, but I am never wrong." It brought smiles to many faces, whether from his children or strangers he met at the grocery store on Saturday mornings. His life lesson also included the value of hard work and financial responsibility. From little on, the request was to save half of a paycheck, a wisdom that still lives on. These sayings, just like Paul himself, were instantly familiar and warmly comforting.

Additionally, Paul's hobbies and interests included cheering on the Packers, Brewers, and Badgers, playing cribbage, and spending quality time with his beloved dog Annie. Paul and Paulette enjoyed their trips to baseball games to watch their rivals play one another, where Dad's team usually came out with the win. These simple pleasures reflected his kind nature and his love of a competitive game and good company.

He was married to Paulette (Schulz) Csencsics for almost 48 years. Together, they raised three children: Vanessa (Jeffery) Sieg of Bloomer, WI; the twins Ryan Csencsics of Mosinee, WI; and Rob Csencsics of Madison, WI. Paul's pride extended to his grandchildren, Avery and Gage Sieg, and he was equally fond of his grand-dog, Tucker. Four years ago, Paulette convinced Paul to take a trip to the Marathon County Humane Society, where they met Annie. He was instantly won over and knew she was the one. They became best friends by regularly sharing snacks and a recliner together.

Paul was preceded in death by his father, Paul Csencsics; his mother, Delores Csencsics; his sister, Fran Landrath; brother-in-law Darryl Landrath; brother-in-law Larry Newby; step-mother, Ileane Csencsics; and his parents-in-law, Alfred and Norma Schulz.

The family expresses heartfelt gratitude to the Comfort Care team at Aspirus for their incredible care and compassion during Paul's final days. Their kindness provided much comfort to Paul and his family.

A private family service and interment is taking place to honor Paul's wishes. In lieu of flowers, please consider a donation to the Marathon County Humane Society in Paul's name.

records.name: Richard Kay Wick
records.summary: Richard Kay Wick, 87, of Merrill, passed away April 1, 2026.
records.body:
Richard Kay Wick passed away April 1, 2026, at age 87. He was born May 5, 1938, in Merrill, WI, to Percy T. Wick and Viola (Polivka) Wick. He graduated from Merrill High School in 1956, and received his Electrical Engineering Degree from UW Madison in 1960. He put his degree to good use designing controls for the Command Module of the Apollo space program. When the Apollo program ended, he became an engineering instructor at NorthCentral Technical Collage, a position he held until his retirement.

On June 12, 1965, he married Sharon Rose Goetsch at Trinity Lutheran Church. They enjoyed 60+ years of marriage.

Richard loved playing cards. He was a avid Cribbage player and passed his love of the game to his children and grandchildren. His sons-in-law and grandsons-in-law learned the game upon becoming members of the family. Skunking Dad/Grandpa was a badge of honor in the family.

Richard enjoyed traveling with family and friends, especially camping along Lake Superior. After retirement, he and Sharon traveled extensively. Their retirement trip was a summer-long road trip to Alaska that they took in their pickup truck equipped with a topper camper. (And they were still married when they returned home.) Over the years they enjoyed many trips in the US and Europe, and a trip to Australia and New Zealand holds a special place in their memories.

Richard and Sharon were snowbirds for 19 years, spending their winters in an RV park in Rockledge, FL. They enjoyed watching dolphins and manatees, attending old car shows, watching rocket launches, and reading their books on a beach.

One of Richard's favorite places was the family cabin. He and Sharon spent as much time there as possible during the summers, and his kids and grandkids love to swim, kayak, and fish on the lake. The Hazelhurst Pub and Minocqua Pizza Company were favorite places for meals.

Richard is survived by his wife, Sharon Wick; daughters Patty (Alan) Harper and Julie (Jeff) Kania; grandchildren Amy (Nathan) Gunderson, Matthew Harper, Gregory Kania (Joely Baxter), and Jacob Kania (Olivia Keating); brother Gerald (Anne) Wick; sister-in-law Yvonne (Herbert) Meshun, and many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents; father- and mother-in-law Arnold and Mable Goetsch; infant daughter Jennifer Lynn; and several sisters-in-law and brothers-in-law.

A celebration of life will be held at 4:00 p.m., Saturday, April 25, 2026 at St. Mark's Lutheran Church in Wausau. Visitation will be from 2:00 p.m. – 4:00 p.m. prior to the service at the church.

Donations may be made to the St. Mark's Lutheran Church "Built in Love" building campaign or a charity of your choice.

records.name: Sandra L. Schilling
records.summary: Sandra L. Schilling, 85, of Wausau, passed away April 3, 2026.
records.body:
Sandra L. Schilling, 85, Wausau, passed away peacefully following a brief illness on Friday, April 3, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

She was born February 6, 1941 in Merrill, daughter of the late Arno and Verda (Goetsch) Kniess. Sandra was baptized on March 11, 1941, marked with the cross of Jesus forever.

On June 10, 1983, she married Arthur Schilling Jr. in Las Vegas, Nevada. Together, they owned and operated Schilling Plumbing for over 30 years. They traveled often, including trips to Hawaii, Aruba, Alaska and took several cruises.

Sandra really enjoyed her get-togethers with her sons, Kevin and Kirk Schmitt, and their families. All her grandchildren enjoyed spending time with grandma at the plumbing shop. A good time was had by all.

Sandra also worked at Stettin Mutual Insurance for many years, making various long lasting friendships.

In her younger years, she liked to bowl and golf. Sandra bowled in many Pin Buster tournaments, being proud of her 600 series. Sandra taught Sunday School for many years. She enjoyed participating in school Stunt Nights. She loved to laugh, dance, read, and was very independent. Sandra had a loyal circle of friends who always had her back.

Sandra will be remembered for her generosity and empathy for others. She remembered those in need year-round. Sandra lived by the motto, "You can't help everyone, but you can help someone!"

Sandra is survived by her son, Kirk Schmitt (Gigi); Renae Nachman (Jobe), Randi Klos (Bryant); brother, Richard Kniess (Debra); sister, Cindy Johnson (J.J.); brother-in-law, William Schilling; grandchildren, Ryan (Megan), Reanna (Jordan), Olivia (Curtis), Blakney (Ryan), Arabella, Matt (Casie), Emilie (Andrew), Alex and Mike; and great-grandchildren, Silas, Ashtyn, Lily, Isla and Julian.

She was preceded in death by her son, Kevin Schmitt and her husband, Arthur.

Funeral services will be held at 11:30 a.m. on Monday, April 13, 2026 at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau. Visitation will be on Monday from 10:00 a.m. until time of services at the funeral home. Entombment will be at Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau.

records.name: Susan E. Bousley
records.summary: Susan E. Bousley, 85, of Wausau, passed away April 1, 2026.
records.body:
Susan "Sue" Elmira Bousley, age 85, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on Wednesday, April 1, 2026, at Our House Senior Living in Wausau. She was born on September 4, 1940, to the late Benjamin and Mildred (Pratt) Rossenbach, in Schofield, WI. She attended and graduated from Everest High School in Wausau. Sue met the love of her life, Robert Bousley, and the two were united in marriage on June 18, 1960 in Schofield, WI. Sadly, he preceded her in death on November 2, 2010.

Sue was an organized and caring person that worked as a CNA and Unit Clerk for some time, before worked for Aspirus at the switchboard, which she went on and did for over 30 years, until her retirement. She looked forward to camping trips with her family and spending the day out on the lake fishing in Phillips or Boulder Junction. Sue was very crafty and creative, and would crochet and knit many items, including prayer shawls and hats for cancer patients. She also enjoyed snowmobiling, and spending time with her "Texas Moonlighters", friends and neighbors. Sue would take in some relaxing time around the bonfire and spending time with her family. Later in life, she enjoyed putting puzzles together and coloring. She is remembered as a caring woman, that was generous and had a forgiving, warm soul. She will be missed dearly and never forgotten.

Sue is survived by her children: Michael (Elaine) Bousley, Tina (Kris) Graefe and Timothy Bousley, grandsons: Benjamin (Kaylee) Graefe and Tyler (Cheyanne) Graefe, along with many nieces and nephews. She was preceded in death by her parents, husband: Robert Bousley, brother: Delbert (Helen) Rossenbach and sister: Barbetta (Al) Passow.

The memorial service for Sue will be held on Saturday, April 25, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. at Helke Funeral Home in Wausau. Visitation will be held on Saturday, April 25, 2026, from 9:00 a.m. until the time of the service, also at the funeral home.

The family wishes to thank the devoted and caring staff at Our House Senior Living in Wausau. Because of their compassion and support, family was put at ease knowing that Sue was being well taken care of.

records.name: Carl E. Peterson
records.summary: Carl E. Peterson, age 77, passed away peacefully on April 3, 2026.
records.body:
Carl Edward Peterson, age 77, passed away peacefully on April 3, 2026. He was born May 6, 1948, to the late Harry and Dorothy (Anderson) Peterson. Carl lived a full and meaningful life marked by devotion to family, country, and the great outdoors.

A proud Army veteran, Carl served his country for two years with honor and humility. His patriotism continued long after his service, as he remained an active and dedicated member of the American Legion, where he found camaraderie and a shared sense of purpose among fellow veterans.

Carl was happiest under an open sky — whether casting a line on a quiet lake at sunrise or tracking through the woods in the fall. His love of fishing and hunting wasn't just about the sport; it was about being in nature and finding peace.

Carl is survived by his long-time life partner, Linda Novotny; his sister, Christine Morrison; his children, Sadie Dekens, Julie Krolczyk, and David Peterson; and many grandchildren.

He is preceded in death by his loving parents and his brother, Lloyd Peterson.

He will be remembered as a caring friend, a devoted family man, and a proud American who lived his values every day.

records.name: Earl C. Spreeman Jr.
records.summary: Earl C. Spreeman Jr., age 57, of Merrill passed away on April 1, 2026.
records.body:
Earl C. Spreeman Jr., 57, of Merrill passed away on April 1, 2026 at Aspirus Hospital in Wausau. Earl was born to Earl Sr. and Sally (Cotter) Spreeman on November 21, 1968 in Appleton, WI. He was a man whose greatest joy in life was his children, whom he loved deeply and unconditionally. His pride in them was evident in every story he shared and every moment he spent by their side.

He cherished time with his family above all else. Whether gathered around the dinner table, sharing laughter during holidays, or simply enjoying each other's company, those moments meant everything to him.

An avid outdoorsman, he found peace and happiness in nature. He loved hunting and fishing, often rising early to greet the quiet of the morning woods or the calm of the water. These were not just hobbies to him, but passions that brought him closer to the world around him and to those he shared them with.

He also had a love for motorcycles and the freedom of the open road. Riding was one of the ways he felt most alive, embracing both adventure and reflection with every mile.

He will be remembered for his strong spirit, his unwavering love for his family, and the countless memories he created with those fortunate enough to know him. Though he is no longer with us, his legacy lives on in the lives he touched and the love he gave so freely.

He is survived by his two children, Megan Spreeman, Merrill, and Robert (Amanda Altenburg) Spreeman, Merrill, two sisters, Shirley Spreeman, La Crosse, and Sherry Lebo, Green Bay, one uncle, William Spreeman Sr. (Suring) and many nieces and nephews. He was preceded in death by his parents, Earl Spreeman Sr., Sally (Cotter) Spreeman and his brother, Steven Spreeman.

A Celebration of Life will be held at a later date.

records.name: Franklin J. Walters
records.summary: Franklin J. Walters, age 86, passed away on April 5, 2026.
records.body:
Franklin "Frank" J. Walters, 86, passed away Sunday, April 5, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born February 4, 1940, in Edgar, son of the late Frank and Mildred (Borchardt) Walters. On May 26, 1962, he married Carol Hamann in Edgar and she preceded him in death on May 2, 1993. On June 16, 2000, he married Judy Grabau (Madison) in Wausau.

Frank dedicated more than 75 years of his life to his craft as a carpenter, mastering every aspect of home construction from roofing to plumbing, electrical work, and general handyman projects. His skill and work ethic left a lasting mark on countless homes and buildings. Frank seemed to know someone everywhere he went. He had a genuine gift for connecting with others and was always ready to lend a helping hand.

Outside of his work, he found joy in the simple pleasures of life. He loved being outdoors, enjoying a campfire or tending to his yard, especially his flowers. He traveled to 35 states and into Canada and also enjoyed many casino trips.

Frank was a devoted fan of the Packers, Brewers, and NASCAR, and rarely missed the opportunity to cheer on his grandchildren at their sporting events. He cherished time spent with family and friends, whether playing a competitive game of Sheepshead, making Christmas candy, decorating cutout cookies, or mixing up his signature grasshopper drinks. Never missed a Friday night fish fry with a brandy old fashioned.

Survivors include his wife, Judy Walters; children, Rick (Christine) Walters, Randall (Julia) Walters and Steven (Debi) Walters; grandchildren, Kelsey (Dylan) Swords, Allyson (Joey) Hnath, Mason (Shannon) Walters, Emilee (Sean) Anderson and Braden Walters; great-grandchildren, Noah Hnath, Maverik and Rhett Walters, and Logan Anderson; brothers, Gary (Betty) Walters, Greg Walters and LaRon (Julie) Walters.

Besides his parents, and wife, Carol, he was preceded in death by a brother and sister-in-law, Doug (Mary) Walters and a granddaughter, Liliana Walters.

Funeral service will be 11:00 a.m. Friday, April 10, 2026, at St. Mark Lutheran Church, Wausau. Rev. Eric Hauan will officiate. Burial will be in Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau. Visitation will be Friday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service all at St. Mark Lutheran Church, Wausau.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Hildegard C. Szarkowitz
records.summary: Hildegard C. Szarkowitz, age 97, of Weston passed away on March 7, 2026.
records.body:
Hildegard "Hilde" C. Szarkowitz, age 97, passed away on March 7, 2026 in Weston, WI. Hilde was born on January 8, 1929, the youngest of nine children. Preceding her in death were her Polish immigrant parents John Szarkowitz and Bernice (Baron) Szarkowitz and her eight brothers and sisters, Julia, Jospeh, Thomas, Anthony and Henry Szarkowitz, and Veronica Mirek, Mary Dorshak, and Rosalie Slusarski.

As a young girl Hilde lived on the family farm in the Town of Weston, WI with her parents and siblings. She attended school in a one room schoolhouse located across the road from their family farm. Hilde grew up to be a beautiful ambitious young woman, who at an early age left Wisconsin to pursue a modeling career in New York City.

In the 1960's Hilde moved to Milwaukee, WI where she lived for over 50 years. She began working as a secretary at a local school. In 1978 Hilde started work at the Milwaukee County Courthouse where she held various positions for more than 26 years, retiring in 2004 at the age of 75.

Hilde was always on the go. In her spare time, she attended classes at Marquette University, was an election worker for the City of Milwaukee, sold some Avon, played a little bingo at the Potawatomi Casino, and scratched her fair share of Wisconsin Lottery tickets.

Hilde traveled frequently. She drove back to Wausau, took the Amtrak to Chicago, and flew to Dallas. Being a devout Catholic Hilde made several trips to Medjugorje, Bosnia, a major Catholic pilgrimage site. Hilde was never happier than when she had a rosary in one hand and a current issue of Vogue magazine in the other.

Hilde loved to dine out with family and friends every chance she got. Holiday meals were always a special event for her.

Remembered as stubborn, sassy, witty, and lovable, Hilde passed away on Saturday morning March 7, 2026 in her room at Pride TLC Assisted Living which is located in what is now the Village of Weston. Pride is across the street from where the Szarkowtiz family farm and one room schoolhouse stood 97 years earlier.

A Memorial Mass will be celebrated at 10:30 a.m. Thursday, April 16, 2026 at St. Michael Catholic Church, Wausau. The Rev. Allan L. Slowiak will preside. Private family burial will be in Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Kronenwetter at a later. Visitation will be held on Thursday from 9:30 a.m. until the time of services all at the church. Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Wausau is in charge of arrangements. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

The family would like to thank everyone for their care, perseverance and friendship.

records.name: James D. Zastrow
records.funeral_home: Brainard Funeral Home and Cremation Center
records.summary: James D. Zastrow, age 88, of Rothschild passed away on April 4, 2026.
records.body:
James David Zastrow, age 88, of Rothschild, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully at home on April 4, 2026, surrounded by his loving family.

He was born on September 2, 1937, in Milwaukee, Wisconsin. James graduated from D.C. Everest High School, Class of 1956, and proudly served his country in the United States Air Force, attaining the rank of E-1 First Class. While in the Air Force, Jim and Rosemary (Arrowood) were married here in Wausau on June 7, 1958, and lived in Shreveport, Louisiana until the end of his service.

Following his military service, James, Rosemary, and the twins came back to the Wausau area. He worked in several different companies over the years, including Sears and Drott. James then worked as a field engineer at Zimpro/Siemens Water Technologies until his retirement in 1999. Following his retirement, Jim worked as a consultant. James enjoyed all the traveling around the world he experienced at his jobs, especially when Rosemary could join him.

James worked to build his house and raise his family on the land he loved. James enjoyed his time with his family and friends, including the time he spent hunting, fishing, and working in the woods. He was an avid Green Bay Packers fan. Jim loved his church family at Redeemer Lutheran Church, and had served on the council there.

James is survived by his loving wife, Rosemary Zastrow; his children, Kelly (Craig) Zweck, Tracy Rucinski, Kevin Zastrow, Craig Zastrow, and Bryan Zastrow; 15 grandchildren; and 13 great-grandchildren. He was preceded in death by his parents, Louis and Gertrude Kunzman, and his parents-in-law, Samuel and Velva Arrowood.

A memorial service will be held on Saturday, April 11, at 11:00 a.m. at Redeemer Lutheran Church, 6404 Old Costa Lane, Schofield, WI 54476, with Pastor Cortright officiating. Friends and family may gather for a visitation from 9:00 a.m. until the time of the service. Following the service, military honors will be provided.

Interment will take place at Pine Grove Cemetery at a later date.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made to Redeemer Lutheran Church or the Cancer Research Institute.

The family would like to thank the nurses and caregivers from Aspirus at Home, and Aspirus Hospice Care, for their care and support.

James will be remembered for his service, his love of the outdoors, his devotion to family, and the lasting impact he made on those around him.

records.name: John W. Bandock
records.summary: John W. Bandock, age 77, of Wausau passed away on April 4, 2026.
records.body:
John William Bandock, 77, of Wausau, WI, passed away peacefully on April 4, 2026. Born on April 12, 1948, John was a lifelong resident of Wausau and a proud graduate of Wausau High School.

A devoted husband, father, and brother, John is survived by his beloved wife, Norma; his daughters, Diane Husom (Joshua) and Rachel (Adam Dunn); and his brother Bill. He was predeceased by his parents, Bernard and Ruth (née Supple), as well as his brothers Robert and James.

John honorably served his country in the U.S. Army during the Vietnam War, where he earned two Purple Hearts for his bravery and sacrifice. His Army Commendation Medal for Meritorious Service exemplified his courage and dedication to others.

John was a member of St. Stephen's Lutheran Church in Wausau. A passionate Green Bay Packers fan through thick and thin, John also enjoyed walking and bicycling throughout Wausau. His humor, kindness, generosity, and loving nature left a lasting impression on all who knew him.

A memorial service will be held at 4:30 p.m. on April 18, 2026 at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 N. Sixth Street, Wausau and will be livestreamed on the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home Facebook page. Visitation will be on Saturday from 3:00 p.m. until time of services at the funeral home. In lieu of flowers, donations may be made to Special Olympics Wisconsin in John's memory.

John's legacy of love and laughter will forever remain in the hearts of those who were fortunate enough to know him. May his memory bring comfort to all who mourn his passing.

records.name: Judith G. Silvia
records.summary: Judith G. Silvia, age 82, of Huntersville, NC passed away on November 20, 2025.
records.body:
HUNTERSVILLE, NC – Judy Silvia, a devoted mother, cherished grandmother, and beloved sister, passed away peacefully on Thursday, November 20th, at Novant Health Huntersville. She was 82.

Born in Wausau, Wisconsin, to Anton and Florence Murr, Judy carried the warmth and strength of her Midwest roots throughout her life. She established a successful career as a dedicated realtor in Northern Virginia, where she was known for her exceptional professional integrity, commitment to her clients, and ability to help countless families find their homes.

A faithful Catholic, Judy found strength and comfort in her faith throughout her life. Her greatest joy, however, was her family.

Judy is survived by her two loving sons, Leo (Megan) of Huntersville, NC, and Scott (April) of Fort Mill, SC; her two devoted sisters Joan Langlois of Appleton, WI, and Marilyn (Don) Benz of Kronenwetter, WI; her six beloved grandchildren, who were the light of her life; and her beloved nieces and nephews.

She was preceded in death by her parents, Anton and Florence Murr and one sister – Jan Murr Chevella.

A private family service will be held at a later date. Details regarding memorial contributions will be shared by the family.

records.name: Leon Zieglmeier
records.summary: Leon Zieglmeier, age 85, passed away on March 30, 2026.
records.body:
Leon Zieglmeier, 85, passed away on March 30, 2026, at Banner Baywood Medical Center in Mesa, Arizona. Leon was born on October 24, 1940, to the late Martin and Mildred (Anvoots) Zieglmeier in Wausau. On July 2, 1960, Leon married the love of his life, Carol Kraimer, at Holy Name Catholic Church in Wausau. The couple shared 57 beautiful years together.

Leon was a 1958 graduate of D.C. Everest High School. Leon was an Auto Mechanic for most of his career. He also devoted 25+ years of service as a volunteer fireman and EMT with the Schofield Fire Department. Leon truly enjoyed his late career and retirement job driving School Bus for the D.C. Everest School District. He and Carol loved camping, beginning with family trips in a tent and progressing to a permanent campsite for many years following retirement. In his younger years he embraced the Wisconsin winters by enjoying snowmobiling with family and friends almost every weekend. The last seven years he has happily avoided Wisconsin winters in Apache Junction, Arizona. Leon has been a lifelong member of St. Therese Parish, Rothschild WI. He was also a member of the Knights of Columbus Sacred Heart Council #4628, the Knights of Columbus bowling league in his younger years, and the Wausau Interchurch Dartball league. He enjoyed playing many different card games with family and friends (as long as you didn't lead a trump).

Leon is survived by son Todd (Jacqueline) Zieglmeier, grandchildren Paul Zieglmeier, Scott (Kasondra) Zieglmeier, great granddaughters, Brynlee, Blakeley, and Brielle Zieglmeier; son Alan (Theresa) Zieglmeier, grandchildren, Sara (Matt) Hall, Mark (Ashley) Zieglmeier, Anne (Aaron) Gurholt, great grandchildren, Martin and Caroline Hall; daughter Jean (Steven) Kupczak, grandchildren Nicholas Kupczak, John Kupczak, Michael Kupczak. He is also survived by special friend Sharon Bohnert.

Leon was preceded in death by his wife Carol, parents Martin and Mildred Zieglmeier, infant sister Therese Zieglmeier, and brother Tracy Zieglmeier.

Funeral services will be held at 11:00 am on Tuesday, April 14, 2026, at St. Therese Catholic Church, 113 W Kort Street, Rothschild, with visitation taking place from 9:00 am until the time of the services at the church. Visitation will also take place from 4:00 pm until 6:00 pm on Monday, April 13, 2026, at John J Buettgen Funeral Home, 948 Grand Ave, Schofield, WI with a Rosary at 6:00 pm. Burial will follow at Gate of Heaven Cemetery in Kronenwetter. John J Buettgen Funeral Home is assisting the family.

Family and friends may leave words of remembrance at www.honorone.com

records.name: Leta F. Ebben
records.summary: Leta F. Ebben, age 95, of the town of Holton passed away on April 4, 2026.
records.body:
Leta F. Ebben, 95, of the town of Holton, passed away on Saturday, April 4, 2026, at home, surrounded by her loving family and under the care of Heartland Hospice.

She was born on January 29, 1931, to the late Anton and Regina (Yessa) Hartl. Leta graduated from Stratford High School in 1948 and began her working years at Thorp Finance Corporation in Wausau and Thorp. On May 8, 1954, she married Leonard Ebben who preceded her in death on April 24, 2013.

Following their marriage, Leonard and Leta lived in Milwaukee from 1954 to 1955 before purchasing a farm in the town of Frankfort. Together they farmed until 1980 when they moved to Edgar. While living in Edgar, Leta worked at the Edgar Library and later, in 1988, began working at the Bank of Edgar where she remained until her retirement in 1999.

In 1990 Leta and Leonard purchased a home in the town of Holton. Leta took great pride in her large garden filled with beautiful flowers. She was known for baking many wedding cakes for family and friends. During the Christmas season, Leta and her grandchildren made hundreds of cookies each year to share with loved ones. Her other favorite pastimes were reading and putting together puzzles.

Survivors include her five children, Brenda (Mark) Towsley, Gerald (Marlene) Ebben, Joan (Damien) Bondi, James Ebben, and Harvey (Lindsay) Ebben; 12 grandchildren, Robert Kang, Casey Kang, Joseph Bondi, Abby (Karl) Repay, Ramana (Benny) Gutierrez, Nyssa Towsley (Michael Reis), Adric Towsley, Jenna (Kenneth) Riesen, Ryan Ebben, Logan Ebben, Amelia Ebben, and Elliot Ebben; great-grandchildren, Leta-Ray, Everly, Barrett, Karly, and Nathaniel; one sister-in-law; two brothers-in-law; and many nieces and nephews.

In addition to her husband and parents, she was preceded in death by four sisters, Marie Totzke, Agnes Totzke, Laura Mielke, and Helen Brunner; brother, Leopold; and their spouses.

A funeral service will be at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, April 11, 2026, at Bethlehem Lutheran Church, 233500 County Road E, Milan, with Rev. Michael Mathey and Rev. Mark Schwalenberg officiating. Visitation will begin at 9:00 a.m. until the time of service, all at the church. Burial will take place in the Edgar Cemetery. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

The family would also like to extend their heartfelt thanks to Heartland Hospice and Marshfield Senor Services for their kindness and the compassionate care for Leta.

records.name: Maxine C. Huebner
records.summary: Maxine C. Huebner, age 76, of Wausau passed away in 2026.
records.body:
Maxine Brose was born in Wausau, WI on December 27, 1949 to James and Susan (Hooker) Brose. She graduated from Wausau Senior High School in 1968. As a teenager, she was a loyal member of Job's Daughters. Maxine lived in Wausau all of her life and worked in the Housekeeping department at Wausau Hospital for many years. Maxine was united in marriage to Charles Huebner on October 9, 1976 at the former Calvary Baptist Church in Schofield. She enjoyed helping to plan her High School Class Reunions. Maxine had an immense love for nature, animals, and spending time with her family. She was a caregiver for her daughters, Ruth and Joy, and was involved with their activities throughout their lives. Maxine was so proud of her daughters and loved them dearly.

Maxine is survived by her daughters, Joy Huebner of Wausau and Ruth Huebner of Wausau; sisters, Evelyn Johnston of Jackson, WI and Jamie (Robert) Fink of Cleveland, WI; brother-in-law, Richard (Joni Spies) Huebner of Columbia Heights, MN; sister-in-law, Kathleen Huebner of Wausau, and several nieces and nephews.

She was preceded in death by her parents; husband, Charles; sister, Nancy Gast; and brother-in-law, William Johnston.

Funeral Service will be at 11:00 a.m., Friday, April 17, 2026 at Highland Community Church in Wausau. Visitation will be one hour prior to the service at the church. Burial will be at Restlawn Memorial Park.

In lieu of flowers, memorials are preferred.

A special thank you to the Aspirus Hospice House and to a lifetime friend, Ann Waraksa, for all of their care.

records.name: Nancy J. Bedessem
records.summary: Nancy J. Bedessem, age 89, passed away peacefully on April 4, 2026.
records.body:
Nancy Bedessem (Chapiewsky), age 89, passed away peacefully at Aspirus Palliative Care on Saturday, April 4th. She left behind a legacy of love, music, and devotion to family.

Nancy was born in 1936 in Bangor, Wisconsin. She attended Lutheran Middle School and graduated from Bangor High School in 1955. One highlight Nancy remembered fondly was winning a singing duet competition with her best friend, Nancy Rea, which led to competing at the state level. Shortly thereafter, she moved to Wausau, Wisconsin, where she began the life she would build with her beloved husband, Jim Bedessem. This St. Patrick's Day in 2026, they celebrated their 70th wedding anniversary. Together, they shared a loving marriage and raised four children.

Nancy was a devoted mother to Scott Bedessem (deceased), Robyn Bedessem (Tom), Julie (Chris) Zelich, and Brad (Kathy) Bedessem. Her family was the center of her life, and she found her greatest joy in time spent with her grandchildren, Meghan, Paige, Ethan, and Ava, and her cherished great-grandchildren, Ellie and Gianna.

Music was woven deeply into Nancy's life. A gifted and passionate musician, she played the piano, accordion, clarinet, and guitar, and loved to sing. Her home was often filled with music, reflecting both her talent and her joyful spirit.

Several interests enriched Nancy's days. She was an active and faithful member of Pilgrim Lutheran Church in Wausau, where she found spiritual fulfillment. She was also known for her meticulous housekeeping, which was a point of pride for her. Lastly, she was a dedicated hockey mom and an enthusiastic fan of the Minnesota Wild and the Wisconsin Badgers, rarely missing an opportunity to cheer on her teams.

For more than 40 years, Nancy and Jim treasured summers camping on Lake Nokomis with close friends. Those years were filled with laughter, friendship, and memories that became some of the most cherished moments of her life.

Nancy will be remembered for her loving heart, her musical gifts, her devotion to family, and the warmth she brought to those around her. She leaves behind a family who will forever hold her memory close and honor her life through the love she so generously shared.

Our family would like to thank Aspirus Hospital and the compassionate nurses and doctors of Aspirus Palliative Care for the comfort and dignity they provided Nancy in her final days.

records.name: Pauline T. Holl
records.summary: Pauline T. Holl, age 92, of Forest Park Village, Wausau passed away on April 4, 2026.
records.body:
Pauline Teresa Holl, age 92, of Forest Park Village, Wausau, WI, peacefully passed away on April 4, 2026.

Pauline was born on October 10, 1933, in Quinnesec, Michigan, the daughter of Fred and Eleanor (Johnson) Warmuth.

After attending schools in Quinnesec and Kingsford, she moved to Milwaukee for work, where she met the love of her life, Carl. They were united in marriage on September 6, 1952, at Gesu Catholic Church in Milwaukee. Together, they raised two children, building a life centered on family, hard work, and laughter, and shared 55 years of marriage until Carl's passing on April 25, 2008. Carl and Pauline lived in Brookfield and Milwaukee for a short time before moving to Leopolis, where they owned and operated "The Old Ranch House" tavern for 45 years.

Some of the most cherished memories were made at The Old Ranch House, where she loved to entertain. Her beautiful singing voice would carry throughout the bar, a reflection of her vibrant spirit and the joy she brought to everyone around her. Pauline enjoyed entertaining alongside Carl at their bar and created a place where everyone felt welcome.

Her faith was a guiding light throughout her life, giving her strength and helping her to fully appreciate every moment and every blessing. She loved spending time with her family and friends, sharing a cup of coffee, laughs over bingo, a good dessert, watching her favorite Milwaukee Brewers, and traveling the world.

Pauline was a member of St. Mary's Catholic Church in Leopolis, where she also sang in the choir and was a member of the Council of Catholic Women (CCW), where she loved serving funeral meals and working at St. Mary's Autumn Fest. She was also a member of St. Michael Catholic Church and later St. Matthew Catholic Church in Wausau.

Pauline is survived by her son and his spouse, Robert (Jane) Holl of Wausau, and her son-in-law, Mark Geiger of Francis Creek, eight grandchildren: Natasha (Bryan) Hanneman, (Children: Anthony, Samuel, Rayla, Savina, Maximilian, and Mary Rose); Ashley (Paul) Michlig, (Children: Jackson, Parker); Stephanie (Jerome) Wotachek, (Children: Juniper, Mabel); David Jon (Alexandria) Chupita, (Children: Hudson, Everleigh); Michael (Sarah) Chupita, (Child: Claire); Jolene (Jake) Funk, (Children: Colton, Caleb); Kristy (Jeff) Hahn, (Children: Emma, Anna); Lee (Katie) Geiger, (Children: Braelynn, Reagan). She is also survived by her great-grandchildren, nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends.

Pauline was preceded in death by her husband Carl, daughter Mary Ann, parents, sister Gertrude Lundamo, brother Fred Warmuth Jr., and other relatives.

Funeral Mass will be held on Saturday, April 11, 2026, at St. Mary's Catholic Church in Leopolis, WI. Visitation will be held at the church from 9:00 to 11:00 am, with the Mass to follow. Fr. Edward Looney and Fr. Vincent will officiate. Burial will take place at St. Mary's Cemetery.

We extend heartfelt thanks to the staff at Forest Park Village for making Pauline feel at home. We also sincerely thank the Marshfield Medical Center Emergency Department staff and the entire care team who provided her compassionate care, as well as the staff at Wausau Manor and the Compassus hospice care team for their comfort and support.

Pauline's spirit and love of life will be remembered and celebrated by her family, friends, and all whose lives she touched.

records.name: Sarah L. Ruffi
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Sarah L. Ruffi, age 58, of Wausau passed away on April 18, 2026.
records.body:
A personal letter from Sarah Ruffi's husband, Dwight:

I guess you could call this a love letter—because that's really what it is. A love letter to my wife, Sarah, the life we built together and the most difficult eight months our family faced. In July, we learned that Sarah had stage 4 glioblastoma. The surgeon said matter-of-factly that she had six months to live. We said, "Unacceptable!" From that moment, nothing else mattered except Sarah, loving her fiercely while finding hope to beat the monster living in her head. It was all hands-on deck!

Our four boys, Sarah lovingly called, "the men", without hesitation became part of her care team. Tony, Mac, Nic, and Joey, along with their girlfriends brought joy and laughter into even the hardest days. They also ran errands and took on some of the toughest jobs caring for their mom—while still maintaining their own businesses.

Along the way, many people facing brain cancer reached out to us about the protocols we were using to help shrink the tumor. Sarah used her Woman Warrior Lawyer Facebook page to share and answer questions, offer hope, and thank everyone for their prayers. Her faith never wavered. She always said, "God is great all the time."

Wausau Mayor Doug Diny gave Sarah one of the best days during her struggle. He honored Sarah by making her 58th birthday, March 31st, Sarah Ruffi Day, for all her work helping many local organizations and other volunteer work in the community. The City Hall room was packed, and she couldn't believe all the community support. Sarah always said, "We're put on this earth to help others."

In Sarah's last couple days, she slept with the sound of our kids, family and friends around her bed. We read some of over a hundred birthday cards sent during Sarah's birthday month. The cards came from people across the country and the world—including our 37 foreign exchange students from countries including: Russia, Jordon, Denmark, and Brazil. Stories about Sarah filled the room. There was joy—because Sarah was all about living life to the fullest!

Now the memories flood my mind. I met Sarah at a volleyball game, and we hit it off right away. On June 24, 2000, we were married. At the time, I had a construction business, and she was a new attorney. When our first born came along–daycare wasn't for us, and I stayed home to raise the boys while my wife built her law practice. Sarah always had my back and didn't miss a beat being my wife and the boys' mom!

Once, in a deposition, a lawyer asked Sarah who she was. She answered, "I'm a daughter, a lover, a mother, a wife, who just happens to be a lawyer." That was Sarah, fierce and compassionate. She gave her clients personal care and was tough in the courtroom—but her priority was always her family.

Summers I'd take the boys to our little Northwoods cottage in Gile, Wisconsin, population 249, who are the most beautiful people in the world. Sarah juggled work to make sure she had three days with us on the weekends. Gile is our peaceful place, and it's where we'll always feel closest to Sarah.

I always teased Sarah that I was one of the original Bee Gees. Their song, "To Love Somebody" says what I'm feeling these days:

"I want my life to be lived with you.
Lived with you.
There's a way everybody say
To do each and every little thing.
But what does it bring
If I ain't got you, ain't got you?"

Please join our family to celebrate Sarah's life April 20th, 2026, along with Sarah's parents Theodore and Cecelia Rudolph, brother Joseph (Jennifer Rudolph), sister Jenny Kelly (Paul Kelly), friends and many others who loved Sarah. Visitation will be held from 3:00 P.M. until 7:00 P.M. on Monday, April 20, at The Living Well Faith Community Church, 1707 McIntosh St. Wausau. A memorial service will take place at 7:00 P.M. The service will also be livestreamed.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be given to her family.

Sarah gave us a lifetime of love in the years we had. And that love will carry us forward—always.

"Like, click and subscribe" – Sarah

records.name: Steven G. Oelke
records.summary: Steven G. Oelke, age 71, passed away on March 31, 2026.
records.body:
Steven Glenn Oelke age 71, passed away at Aspirus Wausau Hospital on March 31st. Steve was born on January 30th, 1955, to the late Glenn & Elaine (Buttke) Oelke in Wausau, WI.

In Steves' younger years he enjoyed playing slow-pitch, throwing horseshoes and spending time with his two children, Miranda & Stetson.

Upon retirement, Steve worked at the U.W. Stevens Point Power Plant.

Steve enjoyed hunting, fishing, and watching the Packers & Brewer games. He also enjoyed playing cribbage and sheepshead. He was president of the Lions Club out of Rib Falls.

Steve was a proud grandpa to seven grandchildren!

Steve is survived by his girlfriend Tracey Butterud (Wausau) and his two children Miranda (Seth) Erickson of Athens and Stetson (Amanda) Oelke of Tomahawk. Seven grandchildren Hunter, Ava & Bryn Erickson and Montgomery, Octavia, Hartley & Mavis Oelke. One brother-in-law John Young of Wausau.

Steve is preceded in death by his parents Glenn & Elaine Oelke and his sister Suzanne Young.

There will be no services at this time, Burial will be at a later date in the town of Stettin.

records.name: Cynthia Marie Goldbach Smith
records.summary: Cynthia Marie Goldbach Smith, 69, of Rothschild, passed away on April 6, 2026.
records.body:
She was born January 4, 1957, at St Mary's Hospital in Wausau, WI. She graduated from Marathon High School and then University of Wisconsin – Madison with a degree in dietetics. She went on to work for UW Hospitals and Clinics as a registered dietician and later helped open an eating disorders clinic at Marshfield Clinic.

From a young age she was a gifted athlete, achieving a world championship in Baton Twirling. She was also an accomplished flutist, singer, and writer. She loved to travel, scuba dive, and all things Irish and turquoise. Above all, she loved her family dearly and cherished her time with her grandchildren.

Cindy is survived by her mother Iris Goldbach, Marathon, WI; her two sons, Bradley (Mary) Smith, Dunedin, FL, and Dr. Daniel (Sophia Ruff-Berganza) Smith, Wausau, WI; one niece, Hannah Goldbach, Washington DC; two nephews, Collin Goldbach, Boston, MA, and Sam Goldbach, Oconomowoc, WI; and her four grandchildren Nora, Owen, Addie, and Sally.

She was preceded in death by her father, Robert Goldbach; her brother, John Goldbach; her grandparents, John and Alva Mateofsky; her grandparents, Joseph and Anna Goldbach.

Mass of Christian Burial will be held at noon on Saturday, April 11, 2026 at Nativity of the Blessed Virgin Mary (St. Mary's) Catholic Church in Marathon, WI. Reverend Monsignor Joseph Diermeier will preside. Burial will be in the parish cemetery. The family will receive friends at the church from 10am to 11:45 prior to the start of mass.

records.name: Darrin G. Stanke
records.summary: Darrin G. Stanke, 59, passed away on April 3, 2026.
records.body:
Darrin was born in Wausau, WI to John Stanke and Marlene Stanke. He was raised in Bevent, WI, and obtained his degree in Civil Engineering from the University of Wisconsin – Platteville. Darrin married Jenny (Tepp) Stanke of Wausau, Wisconsin on November 5, 1994 in Bevent, WI.

Darrin worked in the construction industry, with strong contributions to bridge building in the upper Midwest. He retired from his career in 2022 and was able to pursue his three major passions: cars, camping, and guns.

After being raised with an interest in tinkering and cars, Darrin never stopped working on vehicles. Whether it was maintaining his commuter car or restoring a one-of-a-kind vehicle, cars was one of his lifelong passions. Darrin was well-known in the Mopar community (some even calling him the "Godfather of A12") after fully restoring two 1969 Dodge A12 Superbee's to OEM condition. One of those vehicles was a one-of-a-kind floral top. He won numerous awards at shows with each of his project cars. Both of these vehicles are now on display in museums. Later in his life, he enjoyed the modern day Mopar vehicles, owning a Hellcat and Demon Challenger. His knowledge, expertise, attention to detail, and friendly demeanor built him a strong presence in the Mopar community that will be missed.

In 2005, his wife Jenny convinced him to go on a family camping trip to Glacier National Park. What started as hesitancy to pack up the kids and travel across the country soon turned into the exciting family trips each year. Darrin dove into the world of camping and traveled to various locations with his truck, camper, and eventually with an additional vehicle – usually a Jeep Wrangler. Darrin and his wife, along with the kids when they could join, traveled to 33 National Parks across the nation.

Darrin was an avid sportsman, traveling across the Midwest to hunt pheasant each year and compete in sporting clay competitions. He was actively involved with the Wausau Skeet & Trap Club, including serving as President, Past President, and most recently, Vice President.

Amongst all these passions, Darrin always made time for his biggest priorities: family and friends. He showed up for them day in and day out, offering valuable advice and being a strong shoulder to cry on. Sometimes, it just meant offering up some of his classic "bumper stickers" as he called them.

He was preceded in death by John Stanke (Father). Darrin is survived by his wife, Jenny, and his three children Zach, Andrea, and Grace. Darrin's Celebration of Life will be held on April 11, 2026 from 9:00 AM to 10:00 AM at St. Therese Catholic Church. An engine rev tribute will take place at 9:50 AM. The Requiem Mass begins at 10:00 AM, and lunch will follow in the church basement.

records.name: David D. Yenter
records.summary: David D. Yenter passed away unexpectedly on April 3, 2026.
records.body:
He was preceded in death by his parents, David Yenter and Jeanette (Groshek) Wagner.
