David is survived by his sister, Julie (Mike) Stroik; his niece, Wendy (fiancé Andrew Johnson) Stroik; his great-nephew, Calum; and many aunts, uncles, cousins, and friends.

David lived life on his own terms and was truly one of a kind. He had a deep love for rock 'n' roll and a natural musical talent, playing both the trumpet and piano. He was an avid Green Bay and Brewers fan, never wanting to miss a game.

He will be remembered for his unique spirit and the wide circle of friends he built from all walks of life.

A celebration of David's life will be held at a later date.

records.name: Margo A. Rychtik
records.summary: Margo A. Rychtik, born February 27, 1945, is remembered for her warmth and kindness.
records.body:
Margo Rychtik, born on February 27, 1945, to the late Elmer and Agnes (Mercek) Godersky, has lived a life defined by warmth, resilience, and a deep appreciation for the simple joys around her. Known for her beautiful personality and ever-present smile, Margo has touched the lives of many through her kindness and genuine spirit.

She found happiness in life's quiet moments-reading a good book, spending time outdoors, tending to her garden, and enjoying the thrill of a game of chance. These passions reflected her vibrant and spirited nature, as well as her love for both peace and excitement.

Margo is survived by her children, Sue (Gary), Tony, Chris (Christine), and Francis; nine grandchildren; and ten great-grandchildren, all of whom carry forward her legacy of love and strength. She is also survived by her siblings, Rosemary Godersky, Butch (Caroline) Godersky, and David Godersky; and her sister-in-law, Vi Godersky.

She was preceded in death by her daughter, Marlene Rychtik; her sister, Sarah (Jerry) Habaneck; and her brother, Richard.

Her life continues to be a testament to the enduring power of family, compassion, and joy.

A remembrance of Life will be held at a later date.

records.name: Phyllis A. Lingford
records.summary: Phyllis A. Lingford, 98, passed away on April 4, 2026.
records.body:
She was born on May 3, 1927 in Biron, to the late William and Bessie (Staples) Sherman. Phyllis grew up in Wisconsin Rapids and graduated from Lincoln High School. In 1946 she married Donald Lingford, who preceded her in death in 2010.

Following their marriage, Donald and Phyllis lived in Milwaukee, before returning to Wisconsin Rapids around 1950. She loved playing piano, and dancing to big band music. After Don's retirement, they took several road trips around the country. Moving to the Wausau area, they became avid walkers in the local parks, especially Rib Mountain and Marathon Park. They dined often at local restaurants and enjoyed a stroll to The Mint Café after walking in the Mall. 2510 Restaurant was a go to for birthdays and special occasions.

She is survived by daughters Linda Evans, Marshfield, and Kathleen Hagen, Kronenwetter, four grandchildren, and 10 great grandchildren.

Besides her parents and husband, she was preceded in death by son John, two brothers and one sister, and son-in-law Gary Evans.

We are so grateful for all the care and kindness she received from everyone at Cedar Creek Manor and Compassus Hospice.

A private family entombment will take place at Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau at a later date.

records.name: Roger Joseph Bertram
records.summary: Roger Joseph Bertram, 68, of Schofield, passed away on April 6, 2026.
records.body:
He was born January 21, 1958 in Fond du Lac, son of the late Alfonse and Juliana (Wettstein) Bertram. On June 20, 1987 he married Carla Skrzypchak at St. Agnes Catholic Church, Weston. Together they had two children; Jacklyn and Vincent.

Roger began his career in broadcasting as a radio DJ and local sports announcer before spending many years in sales for TV and radio where he built lasting relationships throughout the community. A man of deep faith, Roger was a devoted Catholic and active member of St. Therese Church, where he generously volunteered during weekly Mass and special events. He loved hunting and fishing, especially opening weekend trips and salmon fishing on Lake Michigan, and cherished camping with his family throughout Wisconsin. As a loyal Wisconsin sports fan, he rarely missed cheering on the Green Bay Packers or listening to Brewers games on the radio. In later years, he enjoyed traveling the country with his wife, visiting family, and making new memories. Roger also had a special place in his heart for his dogs, especially his beloved yellow lab, Elle, and the family's golden retriever, Ruby. Above all, he will be remembered for his larger-than-life personality and his love of being surrounded by people sharing stories, laughter, and making every gathering more memorable.

Survivors include his loving wife, Carla Bertram, his children Jacklyn (Peter) Van Nortwick, and Vince (Amy) Bertram, two grandsons Jack and Benjamin Bertram; his sister Kathy (Joe) Schumacher and his twin sister Ruth (Eric) Schumacher; his mother-in-law Donna Skrzypchak; and two sisters-in-law Sue Bertram and Candice Koch-Wetzel, and numerous cousins, nieces, and nephews.

Besides his parents he was preceded in death by his brothers Norm and John Bertram; his father-in-law David Skrzypchak; and his brother-in-law Todd Koch.

A Memorial Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. Friday, April 24, 2026 at St. Therese Catholic Church, Rothschild, WI. The Rev. Janusz Kowalski will preside.

Visitation will be held on Thursday, April 23, 2026 from 4:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 6th Street, Wausau and again on Friday from 9:30 a.m. until the time of services at the church.

records.name: Winifred Elizabeth Kaiser
records.funeral_home: Brainard Funeral Home and Cremation Center – Everest Chapel – Weston
records.summary: Winifred Elizabeth Kaiser, 99, of Weston, died on April 3, 2026.
records.body:
Winifred was born April 25, 1926, in Marshfield to the late John and Lillian (Cizek) Lensmire. Winnie grew up in the Marathon area and worked in the family cheese factory, where she aspired to become a cheese maker. She married Virgil "Butch" Kaiser on September 24, 1947 at St. Mary Catholic Church in Marathon.

Winnie was a loving wife, mom, farmer, factory worker and ginseng grower. She loved walking, playing cards, shooting buckets with her grandkids and dancing.

Survivors include her children, Jackie (Larry) Meyer of Holcombe, Peter Kaiser of Lansing, MI, Al (Michele) Kaiser of Wausau, Deb (Marc) Wolding of Amherst, Randy (Lisa) Kaiser of Wausau, and Kim Kaiser of Fort Worth, Texas; grandchildren, Troy (Sherry) Meyer, Justin (Jennifer) Meyer, Chris (Danyelle) Kaiser, Ryan (Jami) Kaiser, Adam (Monica) Kaiser, Anna (Erik) Christenson, Brook (Josh) Nickel, Adam McCarthy, Jordan Selbo, Spencer (Jenny) Selbo, Paige (Mason Reisdorph) Selbo and Jessica Wolding; and 18 great-grandchildren.

Also surviving is her sister, Joan Wirkus; brother, John Lensmire; sisters-in-law Anna Mae Rasmussen and Marge Krautkramer, and many nieces and nephews.

Winnie was preceded in death by her husband, Virgil (Butch) Kaiser, daughter-in-law Donna Kaiser, granddaughter Erin Wolding, parents Lillian and John Lensmire, brothers, Warren (Grace) and Calvin (Alvina) Lensmire, sister Mae (Lee) Gliniecki and sister-in-law Lynn Lensmire and many other in-laws.

Funeral service will be at 11:00 AM, Thursday, April 16, 2026 at St. Agnes Catholic Church, 6101 Zinser Street, Weston, WI 54476. Visitation will be from 9:00 AM until the time of service at the church. Burial will be in St. Agnes Cemetery.

records.name: Coleen K. Erdman
records.summary: Coleen K. Erdman, 66, passed away on April 9, 2026.
records.body:
After many years suffering from aggressively progressing dementia, Coleen Kay (Schwalenberg) Erdman passed away on Thursday, April 9, 2026 in her room at Cedar Creek Manor at the age of 66. Now the Lord has mercifully ended her suffering. She died in the sure and certain hope of the resurrection to eternal life through her Lord Jesus Christ, a faith she shared with others at every opportunity.

Coleen was born on September 13, 1959 in West Bend. She was baptized into Christ on October 18, 1959 at Trinity Freistadt Lutheran Church. She graduated from Winona State University in 1981 with a BS in Therapeutic Recreation (working with special needs children and in nursing homes). She married John Erdman on June 9, 1984 at St John Lutheran Church, Wausau.

Over her life, she had a passion for many different church groups and activities, including: leading various women's and youth Bible Studies, numerous church choirs, homeschooling associations, scrapbooking, sewing, First Light Puppets (both puppeteer and puppet maker), and Bow the Knee. She loved nativity scenes, and at one time her collection had more than 100 unique sets. She was an extrovert who was always making new friends and wanted to greet everyone with a hug.

Coleen is survived by her husband of 41 years, John, her children Michael (Rachael) and Laura (Walter), and grandchildren Edmund, Paul, and Thomas. She is also survived by her siblings, Lee (Vicky) Schwalenberg, Larry (Linda) Schwalenberg, Maxine Schwalenberg, and Dean (Amy) Schwalenberg.

She was preceded in death in death by her father Elroy and mother Mabel.

records.name: Patricia A. Schreiner
records.summary: Patricia A. Schreiner, 74, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on April 9, 2026.
records.body:
Patricia "Pat" Audrey Schreiner, age 74, of Weston, WI, passed away unexpectedly on Thursday, April 9, 2026, at the Marshfield Medical Center – Weston, with her family by her side. She was the daughter of the late Wayne and Myrtle (Paul) Long and was born in Oak Park, IL. Pat graduated from Proviso West High School, Hillside, IL and went on to take some courses at junior college. She found her soulmate and best friend, Virgil Schreiner, and the two were united in marriage at Divine Infant, Westchester, IL, on May 20, 1972.

Leaving behind dreams of becoming a teacher, Pat embraced another dream of having a large family and devoted her life to being a wife and mother of five children. Pat worked at the Wausau Daily Herald for 7 years, and then went on to work for American Family Insurance in the claims department for the next 24 years. She had an incredible work ethic and was a selfless woman. Family was her priority in life, and she cherished her time with her children and grandchildren. Family and friends treasure the gifts she gave them from her love of sewing, working in her flower and vegetable gardens and orchard, and playing the piano. Pat put a lot of love into feeding her family by preparing dinners nightly and making and decorating custom birthday cakes. Pat was a member at Saint Therese Catholic Church in Rothschild, WI and volunteered her talents with the stained glass committee guiding the efforts to design and install all 28 windows. She had a strong faith and was a devoted Christian. Pat is remembered as a kind and faithful woman, whose infectious laughter would ring, bringing joy to any room she graced. She will be greatly missed and never forgotten.

Pat leaves behind her loving husband of 54 years: Virgil Schreiner of Weston, WI, son: Mark (Sue) Schreiner of Sussex, WI, daughter: Linnea (Kevin) Lannon of Granger, IN, son: Michael (Carla) Schreiner of Cedarburg, WI and daughters: Melanie Schreiner and Michelle Schreiner, both of Melrose, MA, grandchildren: Stephen Schreiner, Gwyneth and Greta Lannon, and Elsa, Ethan and Gabriel Schreiner, along with brother: Thomas (Terri) Long of Downers Grove, IL, numerous nieces, nephews and cousins, and was a beloved sister-in-law to: Doreen (Leroy) Riehle, Bernadine (Kenneth) Pollack and Judith (David) Weix. She is further survived by her loved and spoiled German Shepherd: Saber. Pat is preceded in death by her parents.

records.name: Raymond W. Woller
records.summary: Raymond W. Woller, 83, of Merrill, Wisconsin, passed away on April 12, 2026.
records.body:
Raymond Wesley Woller, 83, of Merrill, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on April 12, 2026, following a short but courageous battle with cancer, in the comfort of his home and under the compassionate care of Aspirius Hospice.

He was born on July 17, 1942, in Merrill, Wisconsin.

He married the love of his life, On January 18, 1964 and dedicated over 30 years of his working life to SEMCO, before retiring in 2008.

Raymond is survived by his loving wife, Janet; his son Steven (Katrina) Woller; his son Allen"Tripp" (Kim) Woller and Son In Law Nick Lecher, seven grandchildren; and twelve great-grandchildren. His family was his greatest pride, and he treasured every moment spent with them.

He is also lovingly remembered by his extended family and was preceded in death by his daughters, Beverly Woller and Lynn Woller Lecher; his parents Raymond Ardith (Oleson) Woller Sr, brother and sister in law Kenneth Rita (Hoffman) Woller.

He loved deer hunting, fishing, and golf, finding peace in the outdoors and companionship in shared time with family and friends. He spent many hours working on cars for demolition derbies alongside his sons, proud to watch them earn trophies and carry forward the excitement of the track.

Some of his favorite moments were spent with his grandchildren, where stories, laughter, and time together meant everything. He also enjoyed playful fishing rivalry, especially with his daughter-in-law Trina. He would proudly try to outfish her, sometimes even "helping" the competition by throwing a good catch back just to make it interesting. Despite his best efforts, the fish often had other plans, and he was known to end up with hooks tangled in trees more often than not, much to everyone's amusement.

Raymond will be remembered for his resilience, his humor, his warmth, and the steady way he showed love through presence and action. His life was a reflection of devotion to family, hard work, and the joy found in everyday moments.

He passed in the place he felt most at peace, his home, surrounded by love, dignity, and comfort.

Raymond will be deeply missed and forever remembered by his family and all who had the privilege of knowing him.

records.name: Wyatt B. Christenson
records.summary: Wyatt B. Christenson, infant son of Ben and Jenny Christenson, passed away on April 6, 2026.
records.body:
Wyatt Benjamin Christenson, infant son of Ben and Jenny (Luthi) Christenson, passed away in the arms of his parents on Monday, April 6, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

In addition to his parents, Wyatt is survived by his siblings, Ethan, Asa and Carson Christenson; grandparents, Mark and Julie Christenson and Troy and Vannessa Luthi; aunts and uncles, Katie (Marcus) Wenger, Adam (Julia) Christenson, Charlie (Lisa) Christenson, Mary (Jonathan) Brubaker, Seth (Hannah) Christenson, Rachel Christenson, Maranda (Caleb) Knobloch, Jordan (Andrea) Luthi, Matt Luthi, Jacob Luthi, Mark Luthi, Mary (Alex) Feather, Julia (Devon) Peach and James Luthi, as well as great grandparents and many cousins.

Wyatt was preceded in death by his aunt, Rose Christenson and cousin, Kieran Christenson.

records.name: Arden James Emmerich
records.summary: Arden James Emmerich, 91, of Mosinee, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on April 8, 2026.
records.body:
Arden James Emmerich, 91, and Donna Mae Emmerich, 91, both of Mosinee, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully within days of each other—Donna on Saturday, April 4, 2026, and Arden on Wednesday, April 8—surrounded by love and now reunited for eternity.

Lifelong partners they met in 1952, at a 4-H roller skating party in Rothschild, where Arden crashed the event with friends and spotted the prettiest girl on the floor, Donna. A year later they married, building 73 years together, marked by their March 12, 2026, anniversary.

Arden, born in Mosinee to James and Elva Emmerich and raised on a nearby farm, shouldered family responsibilities early after his father's plane crash when Arden was 6 years old. Donna, born in Wausau to Elroy and Dorthea Utecht and raised on a Town of Maine dairy farm, brought warmth and tireless energy to their life in Mosinee.

Arden graduated from UW-Stevens Point in chemistry and paper science, starting at Mosinee Paper Corporation before launching Hom Mart Realty with Donna in 1973. Together they built Emmerich Properties into one of central Wisconsin's largest residential and commercial landlords, with Arden also running construction companies, gas stations, a trailer park, fuel delivery, and ginseng/Christmas tree farming. Donna managed leasing, lawn care, and merchandising, and delighted customers for 20 years at their family's Christmas tree farm.

Arden loved work's possibilities, advocating for small businesses as president of state builders and realtor associations. He skied with Rib Mountain Patrol for 20 years, flew his plane, golfed, rode horses, hunted, danced, and walked Mosinee's streets greeting neighbors.

Donna cherished dancing, travel, her flower garden, feeding the birds, reading, playing cards, and daily walks that brightened everyone's day—her homemade chocolate chip cookies a family favorite.

Together, they raised four children, and their lives were guided by a deep love of family that remained their highest priority.

Active for 73 years at Mosinee United Methodist Church, they served faithfully and helped others, from tenants to church family. Longtime members of Wausau Country Club and Indian Head Golf Course, they gathered often with friends.

To continue their Legacy of community support, Arden and Donna established a charitable fund in 2015 through the Community foundation of Central Wisconsin. Emmerich Properties Charitable Trust each year awards grants to local organizations that promote education, youth development and assistance for those in need.

They are survived by Ardens sister, Marita Wagner (Appleton); sister-in-law Gwen Emmerich (Menomonee Falls); And Donnas Siblings; Shirley Baumann (Summit Lake), Burnell and Jeanette Utecht (Town of Maine), Nancy Chervenka (Neenah), Michael and Mary Utecht (Cicero, IN); children Jeff and Heddy Emmerich (Norwich, CT), Susan Lang (Wausau), Cheryl and Tim Paulson (Mosinee), daughter-in-law Jan Knox (Columbia Falls, MT), family friend Roger Lang (Wausau); grandchildren Madelin Lang, Jake Lang, McKenna Emmerich, Ben Paulson, Michael Grillo, family friend Danni Niles; great-grandchildren Mason, Rowan, Tristin, Oona; many nieces, nephews, and friends.

Preceded in death by son Scott Emmerich (2018).

A joint celebration of their lives will be held at 11:00 a.m., on June 26, 2026, at the Mosinee United Methodist church, Mosinee. Pastor Timothy Gurlea will officiate. Burial will take place at Mosinee Cemetery. Visitation will be from 9:00 a.m., until the time of service, all at the church.

In their honor, consider helping someone in need, feeding the birds, planting flowers, or baking cookies.

The family would like to extend a heartfelt thanks to Aspirus Hospice House, Wausau, for their wonderful and compassionate care of Donna and Arden.

records.name: Christopher Cortese
records.summary: Christopher Cortese passed away on April 11, 2026, at Aspirus Hospital in Wausau, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Born on March 20, 1981, in Duluth, Minnesota, the son of Michael and Sandra Cortese. Christopher dedicated his life to those he loved most, always placing family as his highest priority. His radiant spirit and analytical mind left a lasting impression on all who knew him.

Christopher was a man of many passions who delighted in shared moments and intellectual challenges. He was a sought-after trivia teammate, an avid board game player, and a dedicated comic book collector. He was a passionate basketball fan, especially college games. He also found great joy in music and movies. He was a self-proclaimed "foodie"; however, he cherished his mother's cooking/baking over any meal out.

Family was everything to Christopher. The center of his world was his niece, Sophie. He is lovingly remembered by his parents, Michael & Sandra Cortese; his sisters, Jamie and Sara Cortese; and his cherished niece, Sophie. Christopher's legacy also lives through his aunts; Lisa (Charlie) Okroi, Maria Cortese, Teresa (Dale) Patterson, Bonnie Kalchev, and his uncle Ron (Janet) Cortese. He is also survived by many cousins, other family, and many friends.

Christopher is preceded in death by both sets of grandparents; Orlando & Elizabeth Cortese and Gene & Jane Abrahamsen.

A celebration of Christopher's life will be held on April 25, 2026, from 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston, WI 54476. We are holding a gathering to honor the man whose kindness, witty humor, and unwavering love touched us all. This will be an opportunity for us to come together and celebrate the beautiful moments he created, which will never fade from our hearts.

Christopher's life, rich with love, laughter, and loyalty, remains an enduring testament to the power of family and friendship.

records.name: Dale E. Brown
records.summary: Dale E. Brown, born June 3, 1952, passed away after a battle of cancer.
records.body:
Dale passed away after a battle of cancer. He was born June 3, 1952 to the late Thomas and Audrey Brown.

Dale married Pat (Jahns) on April 26th they lived in Rockford, Illinois raising their girls. They moved to Gleason in 1996. Dale worked at Northern Wire and Lincoln Wood where he retired from in 2018.

Dale enjoyed tinkering in the garage, barn or around the yard. He also loved watching the grandkids grow. Dale liked showing the girls and grandkids how to do things. He especially enjoyed the time he had with his great-grandkids.

He is survived by his wife Pat, two beautiful girls Jennifer (Chris), Stephanie (Beaver), grandkids Christian (Jaydin), Kallie (Tanner D.), Tanner, great-grandkids, Emerson and Blair also by his two special brothers and three sisters.

Dale is proceeded in death by his daughter Michelle, his mother and father, mother and father-in-laws and several Brothers and sisters.

Celebration of life will be June 6th at Gleason Lions Park 11:00 to 2:00.

records.name: Donna Mae Emmerich
records.summary: Donna Mae Emmerich, 91, of Mosinee, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on April 4, 2026.
records.body:
Arden James Emmerich, 91, and Donna Mae Emmerich, 91, both of Mosinee, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully within days of each other—Donna on Saturday, April 4, 2026, and Arden on Wednesday, April 8—surrounded by love and now reunited for eternity.

Lifelong partners they met in 1952, at a 4-H roller skating party in Rothschild, where Arden crashed the event with friends and spotted the prettiest girl on the floor, Donna. A year later they married, building 73 years together, marked by their March 12, 2026, anniversary.

Arden, born in Mosinee to James and Elva Emmerich and raised on a nearby farm, shouldered family responsibilities early after his father's plane crash when Arden was 6 years old. Donna, born in Wausau to Elroy and Dorthea Utecht and raised on a Town of Maine dairy farm, brought warmth and tireless energy to their life in Mosinee.

Arden graduated from UW-Stevens Point in chemistry and paper science, starting at Mosinee Paper Corporation before launching Hom Mart Realty with Donna in 1973. Together they built Emmerich Properties into one of central Wisconsin's largest residential and commercial landlords, with Arden also running construction companies, gas stations, a trailer park, fuel delivery, and ginseng/Christmas tree farming. Donna managed leasing, lawn care, and merchandising, and delighted customers for 20 years at their family's Christmas tree farm.

Arden loved work's possibilities, advocating for small businesses as president of state builders and realtor associations. He skied with Rib Mountain Patrol for 20 years, flew his plane, golfed, rode horses, hunted, danced, and walked Mosinee's streets greeting neighbors.

Donna cherished dancing, travel, her flower garden, feeding the birds, reading, playing cards, and daily walks that brightened everyone's day—her homemade chocolate chip cookies a family favorite.

Together, they raised four children, and their lives were guided by a deep love of family that remained their highest priority.

Active for 73 years at Mosinee United Methodist Church, they served faithfully and helped others, from tenants to church family. Longtime members of Wausau Country Club and Indian Head Golf Course, they gathered often with friends.

To continue their Legacy of community support, Arden and Donna established a charitable fund in 2015 through the Community foundation of Central Wisconsin. Emmerich Properties Charitable Trust each year awards grants to local organizations that promote education, youth development and assistance for those in need.

They are survived by Ardens sister, Marita Wagner (Appleton); sister-in-law Gwen Emmerich (Menomonee Falls); And Donnas Siblings; Shirley Baumann (Summit Lake), Burnell and Jeanette Utecht (Town of Maine), Nancy Chervenka (Neenah), Michael and Mary Utecht (Cicero, IN); children Jeff and Heddy Emmerich (Norwich, CT), Susan Lang (Wausau), Cheryl and Tim Paulson (Mosinee), daughter-in-law Jan Knox (Columbia Falls, MT), family friend Roger Lang (Wausau); grandchildren Madelin Lang, Jake Lang, McKenna Emmerich, Ben Paulson, Michael Grillo, family friend Danni Niles; great-grandchildren Mason, Rowan, Tristin, Oona; many nieces, nephews, and friends.

Preceded in death by son Scott Emmerich (2018).

A joint celebration of their lives will be held at 11:00 a.m., on June 26, 2026, at the Mosinee United Methodist church, Mosinee. Pastor Timothy Gurlea will officiate. Burial will take place at Mosinee Cemetery. Visitation will be from 9:00 a.m., until the time of service, all at the church.

In their honor, consider helping someone in need, feeding the birds, planting flowers, or baking cookies.

The family would like to extend a heartfelt thanks to Aspirus Hospice House, Wausau, for their wonderful and compassionate care of Donna and Arden.

records.name: Larry I. Kufahl
records.summary: Larry I. Kufahl, 84, went home to be with the Lord on April 11, 2026.
records.body:
Larry Irven Kufahl went home to be with the Lord on the morning of April 11, 2026. Larry was a faithful servant who loved the Lord with all his heart and was a devoted husband, father and grandpa. Larry was born on August 30, 1941, the 3rd oldest child to Irven & Dorothy Kufahl, and was 84 years old.

Larry met the love of his life when he was 26 years old through his church youth group. He knew on their first date that he would marry Patti. From an early age, Larry knew he wanted a wife and children. During his college and Army years, when he had not yet met the right person, Larry would say that God knew what He was doing, "I have someone for you; you just need to wait and be patient. She is not ready yet." Larry and Patti were married on August 10th, 1968, and shared a truly blessed marriage for 57 years. By the way they looked at one another, cared for each other, showed affection, and always put each other first, you knew their marriage was something truly special.

As a father, Larry was beyond measure—a loving and devoted Dad who would do anything for his children, setting a beautiful example of how to live life and be thankful in all situations.

Larry had an engineering mind in all things. He graduated from UW–Stout at the top of his class. This led to his vocational passion of design and mechanical engineering which he used while working at Marathon Electric. After retiring, Larry continued doing what he loved by starting his own business, Unique Innovations, where he designed and built industrial automation machines. There was not a problem Larry could not solve with a little ingenuity and hard work.

Larry enjoyed, above everything, spending time with his family – he always said how thankful he was that we all lived so close. He also enjoyed working in his garage, gardening and landscaping, riding his motorcycle in his younger years, volunteering at church, and helping Patti create many beautiful church banners. He also always enjoyed a good glass of room temperature root beer. Larry could fix or build almost anything imaginable. He was extremely talented at repairing salvaged vehicles and always lovingly spoiled Patti by giving her his newest vehicle to drive. Larry had a beautiful voice and loved singing for special church events, weddings and funerals, and with the Barber shoppers. As his family grew, Larry especially enjoyed attending his grandchildren's sporting events and would sometimes want to "try out" their sport and find a way to be involved. The kids loved that every visit to Grandma and Grandpa's always ended with a big goodbye wave from the window—a small tradition filled with so much love.

Larry was baptized and confirmed at Trinity Lutheran Church in the Town of Wausau, also where he married Patti. When "Little Trinity" closed, they were blessed to become members at Christ Lutheran Church where they were lovingly welcomed by their new church family.

Larry lived a full and blessed life. During Sunday family gatherings, he could often be heard saying, "You can't comprehend how much I love my family," and we would always respond, "We know Dad—and we love you too, so much."

Until we see you again
Your loving family

Larry is survived by his loving wife, Patti Kufahl; his children and their families—Nicole (Kirt) Schubring, Tina (Jeff) Kurtz, and Cory (Amanda) Kufahl; and his eight cherished grandchildren: Regan (Adam) Decker, Kaylene (Nick) Kurtz, Teilyn (Jahi) Schubring, Kadin Kurtz, Jayden Kufahl, Bentley Kufahl, Eli Kufahl and Emily Kufahl.

Larry was preceded in death by his parents, Irven and Dorothy Kufahl, and his siblings, Bruce Kufahl and Gail Kufahl. He is further survived by his sisters Diane Borchardt and Faye Kufahl, brother Tom Kufahl, and sister-in-law Donna Kufahl, and by many other beloved relatives and friends.

A funeral service for Larry will take place on Monday, April 20, 2026, at 11:00AM at Christ Lutheran Church, 1300 Townline Road Wausau, WI 54403, with Rev. David Wetmore officiating. Visitation will be held at church from 9:00AM until the time of service at 11:00AM. Following the service a meal will be provided at 12:30PM at Mount Olive Lutheran Church, 6205 Alderson Street, Weston, WI 54476. Larry will be laid to rest at Mechanics Ridge Cemetery in the town of Wausau on Tuesday, April 21, 2026, a funeral procession will leave from Christ Lutheran Church in Wausau leaving church at 10:30AM.

records.name: Leone A. Preuss
records.summary: Leone A. Preuss, 96, of Birnamwood, died on Monday, April 13, 2026.
records.body:
Leone A. Preuss, 96 of Birnamwood, died on Monday, April 13, 2026, at Aspirus Langlade Hospital.

Leone was born on January 12, 1930, in Eland, the daughter of Royal and Linnie (Parfitt) Meverden.

On September 11, 1954, Leone was united in marriage to Kenneth Preuss in Pine City, MN. He preceded her in death on August 29, 2018.

In her earlier years, Leone enjoyed attending horse shows and watching fast-pitch softball games with her husband. She was an avid fan of the Milwaukee Brewers and Green Bay Packers. Leone also loved playing cards and bingo, and she especially cherished opportunities to go out to eat with her children.

Leone is survived by two sons, Paul (Cheryl) Preuss and William (Susanne) Preuss; five grandchildren, Shelly (Josh) Kasper, Steve (Leslie) Preuss, Billie Jo Preuss, Shaun (Kaylee) Preuss and Kayla (Mitch Tischauser) Hanke; 16 great grandchildren; two great-great grandchildren and sister-in-law, Jean Braunel.

Leone was preceded in death by her husband; parents; infant grandson, Randall; one brother, Merville (Lucille) Meverden and two sisters, Verna (Daniel) Corrigan and Loretta (Edward) Wyne.

A Funeral Service will be held at 11 AM on Tuesday, April 21, 2026, at St. Paul Lutheran Church, Birnamwood. Rev. Matthew Christians will preside. Burial will be in Forest Cemetery, Birnamwood. Visitation will be on Tuesday, from 9:30 AM until the time of service at the church.

records.name: Mai L. Thao
records.summary: Mai L. Thao, 71, of Wausau, passed away on March 29, 2026.
records.body:
Mai Lee Thao, age 71, of Wausau, passed away on March 29, 2026.

Mai was born on August 2, 1954 to Joua Lue Lee and Yer Vang. She was the third of four children.

Mai married her husband Su on December 13, 1973. Mai and Su immigrated from Laos to the United States in 1980. They first lived in Pennsylvania and then moved to North Carolina. They settled in Wausau in 1981. Mai worked as a teacher's aide for the Wausau School District and also helped translate for Hmong families during home visits for 16 years. After leaving from the Wausau School District, she worked in the production department at Fiskars for five years. When Fiskars closed their Wausau location, Mai became a business owner selling clothes. Mai retired in 2018.

One of her favorite hobbies was gardening. She harvested many kinds of vegetables and shared it with her loved ones. Another hobby of Mai's was going fishing. She especially loved going fishing with Su. Mai loved going to church and was an active member in the church. She helped with church fundraisers.

Mai is survived by sons, Chong (Pang) Thao, Ching Thao and Xeng (Kelia) Thao; daughters, Mao (David) Chang and Mang (Yer) Lee; grandchildren, Nhia (Mai Der) Thao, Kimberly, Alexia, Henry, Wyatt, and Olivia; and great-grandchildren, Isabelle, Camila, and Rafael.

She was preceded in death by her parents, Joua Lue Lee and Yer Vang; her husband, Su; and her daughter, Peng.

Visitation will be held from 9am to 9pm on Saturday, May 2, 2026, 9am to 9pm on Sunday, May 3, 2026 and 9am to 11am on Monday, May 4, 2026, all at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Avenue in Wausau.

records.name: Mavis M. Thurs
records.summary: Mavis M. Thurs, 94, of Weston, passed away on Friday, April 10, 2026.
records.body:
Mavis Mae Thurs, age 94, of Weston, passed away on Friday, April 10, 2026, at Lili Gardens in Weston, surrounded by her loving family and while under the care of Heartland Hospice. She was born in the town of Berlin on March 14, 1932, and was the daughter to the late Harold & Alice (Pagel) Kriewald. Mavis was baptized into God's family on March 27, 1932 and confirmed into the faith on April 14, 1946. She met the love of her life, Harvey Thurs, at a dance at Schmidt's Ballroom, and the two exchanged marriage vows on September 19, 1953. Sadly, Harvey preceded her in death on March 11, 2023.

Harvey and Mavis ran the family farm until 1965. At that time, they moved to Weston, WI. Mavis worked at Larson's Cleaners, and as an assembler at Hoffer's Glass Company until her retirement. After retiring, she was a beloved lunch lady at D.C. Everest. Mavis had also been a groundskeeper year-round for Mt. Calvary Lutheran Church. First and foremost in her life, was her faith and devotion to her Lord and Savior and her precious family. She and Harvey had built a cottage up north in 1976, and treasured time up there fishing, swimming at Sandy Beach, rollerblading, and having ice cream at The Windmill. Mavis and Harvey also took fishing trips to Canada. Mavis looked forward to trips with Harvey, whether it was to Ireland, Holland, Hawaii, Washington D.C. or traveling to Germany with the Pommerscher Verein German Club. She also enjoyed more local trips, such as going to the Fireside Theatre, as well as attending Brewers and Woodchucks games. Mavis was well-known for her infectious smile, her positive attitude and her strong faith in God. Everyone loved her the moment they met her. Mavis was a social butterfly and enjoyed going on walks, especially with her walking club at Lili Gardens. She was an excellent baker, making many fruit cakes and cut out cookies and treats for family and friends. She enjoyed making cross stitch towels, working on puzzles, playing cards, reading a good book and watching game shows on television. Mavis adored her grandchildren and great grandchildren and would attend their sporting events every chance she had. Mavis loved to watch the birds at her feeders, and would especially enjoy seeing her cardinals coming to visit. She took many trips to the strawberry fields and went cherry picking, and she loved tending to her flower gardens. Mavis will be sadly missed, but her smile and love will last forever in the hearts of her family and friends. Have an ice cream drumstick in memory of Mavis!

Mavis leaves behind her children: Cheryl Utecht, Kim (Jill) Thurs, Char (Jeff) Marcell and Amy (Pete) Jaglinski, her 10 grandchildren: Lucas (Sara) Utecht, Heather (Jared) Grusznski, Mike (Jessica) Thurs, Sarah (Jacob) Bartsch, Nicole (Mike) Riemer, Tara (Garrett) Lancelle, Tanner (Shannon), Bryce (Madison), Seth, and Kyle Jaglinski, her precious 14 great grandchildren: Coen and Anna Utecht, Jude and Adelynn Grusznski, Jacob and Madilyn Thurs, Caleb and Aubrie Riemer, Gavin, Isabelle and Jasper Lancelle, Joseph Bartsch, and Jackson and Samuel Jaglinski, with another great grandchildren due in June, brother: William (Marilyn) Kriewald, brothers-in-law: Elgart Thurs, Harland Schubring and sister-in-law: Anne Kriewald.

She is preceded in death by her parents: Harold & Alice Kriewald, husband: Harvey, brother: Gerald Kriewald, sister: JoAnne Schubring, son-in-law: Ken Utecht, in-laws: Gustav & Gertrude Thurs, brothers-in-law: Wilmar (Alladine) Thurs, Norman Thurs and Orville (Iris) Thurs.

The funeral service for Mavis will be held on Monday, April 20, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. at Mt. Calvary Lutheran Church in Rothschild. Pastor Phyllis Smoot with officiate. Visitation will be held on Sunday, April 19, 2026, from 4:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. and again on Monday, April 20, 2026 from 9:30 a.m. until the time of the service, all at the church. Entombment at Restlawn Memorial Park will take place.

The family wishes to extend their heartfelt thanks and appreciation to the staff members and many friends of Mavis's at Lili Gardens. She loved her time spent there. They also want to thank Heartland Hospice for their care and compassion.

records.name: Victor J. Richards
records.summary: Victor J. Richards, 76, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on March 28, 2026.
records.body:
Victor was born in Wausau to Edward Sr and Valeria Richards on Sep 28, 1949. He graduated from Wausau High School in 1968. As a scholar-athlete, Vic was a top baseball pitcher, football team captain, and the senior class president. In 1972, he graduated from the University of Wisconsin-Eau Claire with a Bachelor of Arts in comprehensive business management and was a proud member of the Tau Kappa Epsilon fraternity.

Giving back to his community was a defining part of Vic's life. Volunteering at the Boys and Girls Club of Wausau was especially meaningful to him. Vic's efforts to help the community youth were recognized by awards, including the From the Heart Award and Volunteer of the Year. Victor also cared for his mother in her later years, allowing her to remain at home-a reflection of his deep loyalty and compassion.

Victor was a great neighbor, a committed community member, and truly selfless. After every snowstorm, he could be found running his snowblower around the neighborhood block, helping anyone who needed it. Generosity was a constant thread in his life; he would do anything for anybody, often putting others before himself.

Victor is survived by his two sons, Chad (Bentley) Richards and Brian (Andrea) Richards, and his grandchildren Charlotte, Stella, and Carter. He is also survived by his siblings Charles (Nancy) Richards, Edward Jr (Sue) Richards, and Arlene Bube, along with many nieces, nephews, and extended family members.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Edward Sr and Valeria Richards, and by his brother, Jerry Richards Sr.

On Saturday, April 25, a visitation will open at 11 am with a noon funeral mass at Holy Name Catholic Church. Following the mass, Vic will be interned at Restlawn Cemetery in proximity to his parents. Vic was devoted to his faith and believed in eternal life.

The family extends heartfelt gratitude to the staff and loved ones who cared for Victor over the years. He was cherished by many and received exceptional care from Wausau Manor and the Aspirus Hospital staff. Despite challenges, Victor's genuine heart and commitment to his community never wavered. Through decades of volunteering and service, he devoted much of his life to supporting organizations and positively shaping the future of Wausau.

In place of flowers or donations, the family asks that you honor Victor's memory by helping a neighbor or supporting a local organization that aligns with your values-continuing Vic's legacy of making the community a better place for all.

records.name: Clifton G. Jones
records.summary: Clifton G. Jones, 71, passed away on April 13, 2026.
records.body:
Born in Norco, California on October 22, 1954 and went to be with the Lord on April 13, 2026. Guy was 71 years old.

Greeting Guy in Heaven are Parents Clifton M Jones & Darla M Horine. Brother Larry Jones. Wife Karen who lost her battle to cancer.

He leaves behind a daughter Desirae Jones & Zac of Mesa, Arizona. Son Cody Jones Ahwatukee, Arizona Grandchildren Mckayla, Evan, Axton, Emma, all residing in Arizona. Sister Janet Lawrence & Charles Birnamwood, Wisconsin. Nephews-Gregory Lawrence & Holly Wausau, Wisconsin. Timmy Davis, Kyle Jones, Sonny Jones, Mississippi. Nieces-Melissa Ramsey & Jody Weston, Wisconsin, Christy Jones Residing in Bloomington, California. Many great nephews and nieces. His first wife Jean Linaburg Swan who remained his friend until the end.

Guy was an explorer and loved to fish. He went to High School at Eisenhower High School, Rialto, CA. He served in the Army in 1972-1975.

Following his father's trade of hanging drywall and a wondering spirit he moved to La Grande, Oregon where he put down roots for over two decades making many great friends! He then moved on to Arizona so he could be closer to his adult children and grandchildren.

Last May he suffered a stroke leaving him paralyzed on his right side but he kept fighting to live and in October moved a final time to live with his sister in Wisconsin. He received services from Aspirus at Home. Thank you Lynn, Diane and Connie for your support during Guy's time of need. You became part of his family.

Guy passed away at Compassus House of the Doves, Marshfield, Wisconsin. A special thank you to all the nurses who showed him kindness and compassion his final 3 days on earth. Kristin, Keri, Carmen and Beth will always have a special place in our hearts. You were all Godsends for a time such as this!

Cremation will be at Brainard/Helke Funeral Home Weston. Funeral at Restoration Church Birnamwood, Wisconsin. Pastor Joshua Weix, our Pastor, Shepherd and Friend will conduct the service at a date to be determined.

Guy's ashes will be laid to rest along side his late wife, Karen and Father Cliff Jones in La Grande, Oregon by his daughter Desirae and friend Chipper.

records.name: Debra "Debbie" Ann Henning
records.summary: Debra "Debbie" Ann Henning, 70, of Iron River, Michigan passed away peacefully on April 14, 2026.
records.body:
Debra "Debbie" Ann Henning, of Iron River, Michigan, was born on April 11, 1956, and passed away peacefully with her family by her side on April 14, 2026, at the age of 70.

Debbie spent much of her life caring for and feeding those around her, a reflection of her generous and nurturing spirit. She worked for many years as a cook and in the deli and bakery at local grocery stores. Many in the community fondly remember visiting Main Street Café, where they enjoyed her delicious, home-cooked breakfasts.

She found great joy in the simple pleasures of life-camping, sitting around a campfire, and spending time with loved ones, especially her lifelong friend, Marsha. Debbie loved the holidays, particularly Christmas. Her home was always beautifully decorated, and her Christmas tree was her pride and joy each year. She also had a playful, competitive side and delighted in winning her family's quarters during lively games of left, right, center.

Debbie looked forward to the Henning family's annual Memorial Day camping weekend, where she was always counted on to bring her famous pasta salads-especially her cucumber dill, a favorite among her nieces. She enjoyed listening to music and watching her favorite movie series, The Lord of the Rings and Harry Potter.

Above all, Debbie deeply loved her family, even if she didn't always say it out loud. Her daughter, Jody, and her grandchildren, Allison and Mason, were her entire world.

records.name: Donna R. Skrzypchak
records.summary: Donna R. Skrzypchak, 80, of Weston passed away peacefully on April 12, 2026.
records.body:
Donna R. Skrzypchak, 80, Weston, passed away peacefully on Sunday, April 12, 2026 while under the care of Interim Hospice at Primrose Memory Care, Weston.

She was born on June 20, 1945 in Wausau, daughter of the late Delbert and Marion (Gavitt) Sickler. On October 2, 1965 she married David Skrzypchak at Sacred Heart Catholic Church, town of Cassel. He preceded her in death on March 6, 2024.

Donna devoted her early married life to caring for her home and family. Shortly after graduating from Wausau High School, she moved to Milwaukee, where she worked at a cookie factory. Later, when her youngest child began school, she returned to the workforce at Tupperware/Lakeland Sales, where she remained for 14 years. Always hardworking and dependable, she then began a long and fulfilling career as Assistant Deli Manager at IGA in Rib Mountain, serving the community for 18 years before retiring.

Donna's heart was always in the kitchen, and cooking was her greatest joy. She treasured time spent bowling in her league with her best friends and was a proud member of the Red Hat Society. She loved casino trips with her sisters and friends, and shopping was a favorite pastime – especially when a good sale was involved. True to form, Donna never missed one. More than anything, she will be remembered for her warmth, her laughter, and the joy she found in spending time with those she loved.

Survivors include her two daughters, Carla Bertram, Schofield and Candice (Todd) Wetzel, Schofield, four grandchildren, Jacklyn (Peter) VanNortwick, Vince (Amy) Bertram and Lucas and Olivia Koch, 2 great grandchildren, Jack and Benjamin Bertram, one sister, Cheryl (Jim) Buttke, Wausau and one brother, Loren Sickler and numerous nieces and nephews.

Besides her parents and husband, she was preceded in death by two sisters, Merna (Dave) Kleman and Joan (Sid) Hunter and two sons-in-law, Roger Bertram and Todd Koch.

The Funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. Tuesday, April 28, 2026 at St. Therese Catholic Church, Rothschild. The Rev. Janusz Kowalski will preside. Burial will be in Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Kronenwetter. Visitation will be held on Tuesday, April 28, 2026 from 9:00 a.m. until the time of services all at the church.

Donna's family would like to extend their sincere and heartfelt gratitude to the staff at Primrose Memory Care for the loving and compassionate care they provided her during the past three years.

records.name: John C. Kasten
records.summary: John C. Kasten, 70, of Wausau passed away peacefully on April 6, 2026.
records.body:
John Carl Kasten, 70, of Wausau, WI passed away peacefully on Monday April 6th after a brief stay at Aspirus Hospital.

John was born in Wausau, Wisconsin in 1955 to Alvin "Al" and Lucille "Lou" Kasten. He was a student at Wausau East High School. After high school he had various jobs but ultimately settled into a life of Truck Driving, working for various companies throughout the greater Wausau area over the course of his career. There wasn't a stretch of the US Interstate Highway System that he didn't know like the back of his ever-tanned hand.

John was hard-working, generous and good for a joke, be it appropriate or not. He cared deeply about the people in his life and was always willing to lend a helping hand. He loved his family and the city where he was born and was always up for a game of cribbage.

John could be stubborn at times and was not afraid to give tough love. He was very thankful for his sobriety, often encouraging others to evaluate their relationship with alcohol. He worked hard to maintain his sobriety and his family is tremendously proud of this achievement. May 27th would have marked 19 years.

John is survived by his wife Diane Garr, his daughter Nicole Kasten (Ryan Gonzalez), grandchildren Saffron and Cyrus Gonzalez of Madison, WI, daughter Lindsay Kasten of Milwaukee, WI, mother of his daughters Jackie Kasten of Wausau, WI, sisters Karen Kun of Omaha, NE, Margaret "Peggy" Lawonn of Wausau, WI, sister-in-law Bonnie Kasten of Wausau, WI as well as many nieces, nephews, and cousins.

He was preceded in death by his parents, his brother, Danny Kasten and brothers-in-law Wayne Lawonn and Joe Kun.

A Celebration of Life will be held on Saturday, May 16, 2026, from Noon to 5:00p, at Marathon Park – Wausau – "Big Kitchen" Shelter.

records.name: John S. "Jack" Igers
records.summary: John S. "Jack" Igers, 87, passed away peacefully on April 9, 2026.
records.body:
John S. "Jack" Igers, 87, passed away peacefully on April 9th, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospice House.

Jack was born in 1938 in Melrose, Minnesota, the third of four children to John and Florence Igers. He attended St. Boniface Elementary School in Melrose before continuing his education at Crosier High School in Onamia, MN, graduating in 1956.

In 1965, Jack was ordained as a Catholic priest with the Crosier Fathers and Brothers. While serving at the North Dakota State Hospital in Jamestown, North Dakota, he met Irene Jaeger. The two were united in marriage in 1971 at St. Anne's Church in Kimball, Minnesota. Shortly thereafter, they moved to Iowa City, Iowa, where Jack attended the University of Iowa and earned his Master's degree in Social Work in 1973.

Later that year, Jack and Irene made their home in Wausau, Wisconsin. Jack devoted 28 years of service to North Central Health Care Center, where he worked compassionately with those in need until his retirement in 2001.

He is survived by his son, Brad Igers (Megan James) of Lynnwood, Washington, and his cherished granddaughter, Harper.

Jack was preceded in death by his beloved wife, Irene; his son, Shawn Igers; and his sisters, Jane Igers, Barb Igers, and Mary Arndt.

Jack will be remembered as a kind, caring, and charitable man who touched many lives. He found great joy outdoors, especially during his annual pheasant hunting trips to North and South Dakota. An avid basketball player known for his tenacious rebounding skills, he regularly attended morning pick-up games at the YMCA where he played until late into his 70s. Jack was well known and loved in his neighborhood, often stopping to visit with friends and neighbors on his daily walks and bike rides with his revered dogs.

He gave generously of his time as a hospice volunteer for over 20 years, offering comfort, companionship, and meaningful conversation to those nearing the end of life. Jack had a warm presence and a wonderful sense of humor that will be deeply missed by all who knew him.

Services will be held at St. Anne Catholic Church on Friday, May 29, 2026. Visitation will start at 9:00 a.m. until the time of Mass at 11:00 a.m. Burial will follow to Maple Grove Cemetery in the township of Maine.

records.name: Ralph E. Heil
records.summary: Ralph E. Heil, 69, of Wausau passed away suddenly on March 26, 2026.
records.body:
Ralph E. Heil, 69, Wausau passed away suddenly on Thursday, March 26, 2026, at his home.

He was born on December 17, 1956 in Marshfield, son of the late LeRoy and Agnes (Writz) Heil.

Ralph worked for Salzman Roofing and Siding for 26 years, was self-employed for 10 years and for 13 years he worked at Fleet Farm in Wausau, retiring in April 2023.

Ralph is survived by three sons, Trevett, Travis, Matthew, 10 grandchildren, his siblings, Rose Ann (Gene) Jacob, Mosinee, Marie Kiczula, Ill., Janet (Tomas) Sabaduquia, Ill. and Margaret (Keith) Ziegel, Marathon, a sister-in-law, Gladys Heil and numerous nieces and nephews.

Besides his parents he was preceded in death by his life partner, Julie Alft and a brother, Jospeh.

A Celebration of Life will be held on Friday, May 15, 2026, from 2:30 p.m. until 4:00 p.m. at which a meal will be served all at the Wausau Labor Temple, 318 South Third Avenue, Wausau, WI, 54401. Private family interment will take place at Memorial Chapel Mausoleum, Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau.

records.name: William M. Hussong
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: William M. Hussong, 86, passed away on April 13, 2026.
records.body:
On April 13, 2026 William (Bill) Hussong went to join his parents and younger brother Dave in the presence of his Lord and Savior, Jesus Christ.

Bill was born on August 29, 1939 at Wausau Memorial Hospital to W. Myron and Ethel (Draeger) Hussong. He graduated from Wausau High School (1957) and then Concordia Teacher College in River Forest, IL (1961). He went on to earn his Masters Degree from the University of Oklahoma (1967). Additional graduate work was done at Adelphi University, Hofstra University, Columbia University, Northwestern University and several National Radio and Optical astronomy observatories around the county. He was a member of the Phi Betta Kappa honors society.

His first job out of college was teaching sciences and mathematics courses at Long Island Lutheran High School in Glen Head (N.Y.). He enjoyed teaching and interacting with students as coach of the J.V. baseball and football teams. In 1967 had returned to his Alma Mater (now Concordia University of Chicago) as professor of earth/space sciences where he also taught science education classes. He developed and lead field study courses to Mexico, Hawaii and several areas of the United States mainland. He later taught some of the same classes at College of DuPage where he served for a time as Associate Dean of the Natural and Applied Sciences and Mathematics Division. During this time he also served for many years as an adjunct professor at Elmhurst College and Dominican University. Bill spent a total of 56 years in education before his retirement.

Bill met the love of his life, Jan (Bok) Hussong, a traveling Physical Therapist through a mutual friend at church. They were married on April 9, 1983 at Grace Lutheran Church in River Forest, IL and made their home in Elmhurst before later moving to Wheaton. God blessed their loving marriage with two wonderful and healthy children, Ethan (1984) and Whitney (1986). After retirement they moved to Wausau to be closer to Bill's younger brother Dave, who was diagnosed with two forms of aggressive cancer.

Bill enjoyed a full life after retirement with included traveling with his wife, time spent with family, Bible studies, playing 3-13 and Mexican Train or other games, enjoying the Brewers and Packers (where he and his son had gold package season tickets), reading and a deep interest in Native American culture (especially their settlements and effigy mound systems).

His is survived by his wife Jan and his children Ethan (Elizabeth Forest) Hussong of Chicago and Whitney (Lenny Roberts) of Eden, N.C. He was proceeded in death by his parents and younger brother.

A memorial service honoring Bill's life will be held at St. Mark's Lutheran Church at 600 Stevens Drive, Wausau, Wisconsin on Tuesday, June 23, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. with Pastor Eric Hauan officiating. Visitation will take place from 10:00 a.m. until the time of service at the church. Bill will be laid to rest at Pine Grove Cemetery following the service.

records.name: Barbara Jean Kloppenburg Fabel
records.summary: Barbara Jean Kloppenburg Fabel, 83, of Wausau, WI, passed away on April 18, 2026.
records.body:
Barbara Jean Kloppenburg Fabel, (née Zurawski), 83, passed away on April 18, 2026, in Wausau, WI. She was born October 4, 1942, in Stevens Point, WI, to the late Chester Zurawski and Virginia Clayton Zurawski.

Barb attended Stevens Point area schools, graduating in 1960 from P.J. Jacobs High School – a place where she formed treasured friendships that would last her entire lifetime. After graduation, she enrolled at the University of Wisconsin – Stevens Point where, with a desire to teach math, she earned a degree in education.

She worked briefly as a schoolteacher in Stevens Point before she moved to Wausau and focused on raising her daughters, Sue and Jane Kloppenburg. Once both girls were in school, Barb re-entered the workforce at Wausau Insurance, where she spent over two decades in various roles. Her professional journey then led her to the Twin Cities to work for Parade of Homes magazine before eventually returning to the Wausau area, where she forged a new career path at Wausau Financial.

In 1992, she married Warren Fabel, and for 33 years, the couple built a life that celebrated every season-spring and summer in Wausau, WI on local golf courses, and autumn and winter in either Tucson, AZ, or Destin, FL.

In addition to being an avid golfer, Barb was an expert seamstress and accomplished quilter. Her sewing skills resulted in lovingly crafted prom and wedding gowns for both of her daughters. She was an active and longtime member in Wausau's Pine Tree Quilters Guild, and her quilting skills were nationally recognized at the prestigious annual quilt show in Paducah, KY. Her many quilts have been passed down and will be cherished for generations to come.

And if you knew Barb, you knew this: she loved to laugh. A lot. She had a quick sense of humor, a sparkling wit, and a love for a good turn of phrase. Whether with her family, cherished high school friends, golf partners, quilting pals, or good friends from Wausau Insurance, there was always plenty of laughter when you were with her.

Along with her parents, Barb was predeceased by her husband (Warren Fabel), her sister (Margaret Konkle), and her brother (Chet Zurawski).

She is survived by her daughters Sue (Scott) Graf of Mosinee, WI, and Jane (Gerard) Lagos of St. Paul, MN; her grandchildren Josh (Alex) Graf, Christian (Hannah) Graf, Julia (Maria) Lagos, Eric Lagos, and Olivia Lagos; great-grandchildren Rebekah, Rowan, Simeon, and Milo Graf; nieces Anne (Chad) Rogalski, Nicole Youngstrom, and nephew Nick (Andrea) Zurawski, as well as numerous cousins.

Additionally, she is survived by Warren's children: Kim (Jim) Fairman, Kristi Fabel, Kerry (Scott) Ray, Kirk (Ellen Moran) Fabel, and Kyle (Carey Anderson) Fabel.

The family extends heartfelt thanks to North Central Health Care (NCHC) and the staff at the Mount View Care Center for their exceptional care and support over the years.

There will be an open house and celebration of life for Barbara Jean at Brainard Funeral Home, 5712 Memorial Court in Weston, WI 55476 on May 2 from 11:00 to 3:00. The family invites friends to stop by to share memories and view a collection of her quilts. There will be a private family service at a later date.

Memorial donations can be sent to either: North Central Health Care (NCHC), c/o Melissa Stockwell, 2400 Marshall St. Wausau, WI 54403 (memo Legacies – Life Enrichment) or to: Pine Tree Quilters – Education, PO Box 692, Wausau, WI 54402.

records.name: David R. Theis
records.summary: David Robert Theis, 72, of Wausau, WI, passed away on April 18, 2026.
records.body:
David Robert Theis, age 72, passed away Saturday, April 18, 2026, at his residence. He was born April 2, 1954, to the late Robert and Rose (Stochek) Theis in Wausau, WI. Linda attended Wausau East High School, graduating with the class of 1974.

David worked for several years at Custom Cleaners and a variety of other jobs in the Wausau area.

David was preceded in death by his parents; a sister, Linda Theis Siegmann; and a niece, Elisa Siegmann.

Survivors include his brother-in-law, Paul Siegmann; a nephew, Arick (Pamela) Siegmann; and other friends and relatives.

records.name: Dennis A. Narlock
records.summary: Dennis A. Narlock, 79, of Mosinee, passed away on April 16, 2026.
records.body:
Dennis A. Narlock, 79, Mosinee, passed away peacefully, on Thursday, April 16, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital, surrounded by his loving family.

Dennis was born on February 24, 1947, in Wausau, WI, one of nine children of the late Lambert and Margaret (Skarwecki) Narlock. After attending St. Therese school and graduating from D.C. Everest High in 1965, he married Janis Rowell, she survives.

Dennis loved his family unconditionally. He was a very proud man and would downplay his accomplishments. Not many people knew, but Dennis would've been 20 years sober this year. Dennis should've owned stock in Pepsi with as much as he drank. Dennis' his favorite color was "flannel" and he was an expert at cutting trees, dragging them out of the woods with the multitude of tractors he owned and planting gardens well over an acre. He thoroughly enjoyed weekly trips to the casino with his Janis, a long phone and a good laugh with Cheryl and shooting the bull with Joe over coffee, watching Kelly Clarkson. Another time that stood out to Dennis was with his son Tim, when he harvested his biggest deer, the smile on his face said it all.

Dennis worked in the paper industry as a machine tender for 43 years retiring from Weyerhaeuser/Domtar. He served his country in the U.S. Air Force during the Vietnam War, servicing planes. Dennis was a member of the Knights of Columbus in Mosinee.

Dennis is survived by his wife, Janis, his children Cheryl (Travis) Newman, Joe (Jolin Stewart) Narlock, Tim (Jill) Narlock, grandchildren Tyler, Emily, Adam (Brooke), Alyssa (Alan), Breanna, Sadie, Leo and Nola, 5 great-grandchildren and one step-grandson Calvin. Dennis is further survived by his siblings Mike (Judy Neuman) Narlock, Cathy (Pete) Peil, Marilyn (Bob) Stogbauer, Mary Lou (Dan) Krautkramer, sisters-in-law Mary Kratwell-Narlock, Nancy Narlock and Gail Narlock, brothers-in-law Philip Rowell and Daniel (Jerry) Rowell, as well as many nieces, nephews and other extended family.

Besides his parents, Dennis was preceded in death by brothers Terry, Ronald, Gary and John, sisters-in-law Mary Narlock, Sharon Rowell and Karen Rowell and brother-in-law David Rowell, nephew Douglas Narlock.

A Celebration of Life will take place at 11:00 a.m., on Friday, April 24, 2026, at Beste Funeral Home, Mosinee. Monsignor Joseph Diermeier will officiate. Burial will take place at Gate of Heaven Cemetery. Visitation will be from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service, all at the Funeral Home. Online condolences may be directed to the family at www.bestefh.com.

records.name: Grace C. Brickner
records.summary: Grace C. Brickner, 85, of Marathon, WI, passed away on April 19, 2026.
records.body:
Grace Carol Brickner, 85, Marathon passed away peacefully on Sunday, April 19, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital, with her family by her side.

She was born on September 13, 1940, in Marathon, daughter of the late Matthew and Marie (Blume) Wagner. On April 11, 1959, she married Jerome E. Brickner at St. Mary's Catholic Church, Marathon. He preceded her in death on March 9, 2022.

Grace lived her life rooted in a deep and unwavering Catholic faith, finding strength, purpose, and comfort in her relationship with God. Through quiet example and steadfast devotion, she and her husband, Jerry, passed these values on to their children, teaching them the importance of prayer, compassion, and service to others. Faith was not only something Grace believed, but something she lived each day, leaving a lasting spiritual legacy that continues to guide and inspire her family.

For over 27 years, Grace was the secretary at Brickner Motors in Little Chicago. Grace always said she liked working there because she loved the boss. She enjoyed spending time at the cottage with her family. Grace also enjoyed hunting with her husband; she was very proud of a successful hunt for a black bear. Fishing in Canada was a favorite family trip for many years. Grace never said no to a game of marbles and seldom lost. Most importantly, she enjoyed spending time with her children, grandchildren and great grandchildren.

Survivors include her children: Jerome M. III (Jane) Brickner, Wausau and children, Janessa Grace (Tom) Ress and children, Ashton and Janea, Jerry IV (Hannah) Brickner and daughter, Ivy; Michael (Cheryl) Brickner, Tomahawk and daughter, Michaella; Steve (Linda) Brickner, Tomahawk and children, Steven (Katie) Brickner and children, Paityn and Abigail, Melissa (Kurt) Seubert and children, Madalynn, Quinnlynn, Conrad, and Gabriel, Shawn (Joey Greshowak) Brickner, Melanie (Scott) Heinz and children, Alexander and Raelynn Grace; Maria (Tom) Olson, Wausau and children, Thomas (Heidi) Olson, Michael (Kaylee) Olson and children, Alyssa, Braylee and Hudson, and Grace (Lucas) Somma; her siblings, Lucille (Jim) Sorenson, Madison and Mary (Jeff) Flamino, Mosinee and many nieces and nephews.

Besides her parents and husband, she was preceded in death by an infant son, Bryan, granddaughters, Tara and Kendra Brickner and siblings, Dorothy Klumpyan, Joyce Seubert and Kenneth Wagner.

The Funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. Monday, April 27, 2026, at St. Mary's Catholic Church, Marathon. The Rev. Msgr. Joseph Diermeier will preside. Burial will be in the parish cemetery. Visitation will be held at St. Mary's Catholic Church, Marathon, on Sunday, April 26th from 3:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. A rosary service will follow at 7:15 p.m. Sunday evening. Visitation will begin on Monday, April 27th at 9:00 a.m. and continue until 10:45 a.m. In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to Gifts from Heaven or St. Mary's Catholic School. Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Marathon oversees arrangements. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: John Schremp
records.summary: John Schremp, 74, passed away on April 20, 2026.
records.body:
John Schremp, 74, passed away peacefully at North Crest in Stevens Point, under the care of St. Croix Hospice, on April 20, 2026.

He was born in Antigo, WI, the second oldest of Herb and Verna Schremp. He graduated from Mosinee High School.

He attended Marquette University, graduating in Civil Engineering along with a masters in Structural Engineering. He met his wife, Mary, through the Marquette chorus. They married August 19, 1972, 18 months to the day after their first date.

They owned and operated Architectural Products of Wausau (APW) for 36 years, moving to the area from Seattle. He was well known in the state for putting up many hospitals, airports, and UW buildings. He enjoyed doing box luncheons with his daughter, Jennifer.

John employed many and was grateful for their help, especially his office manager, Shelley Rahlf and his project manager, Chad Patoka.

He coached both girls and boys soccer teams. John was active in Indian Guides and Indian Princesses. We as a family spent countless hours boating, both in Seattle and on Lake DuBay. He was active in high school musicals and the Mosinee community theater. He loved gardening with his daughter, Kristen, and attending the grandkids events was always special. One of his favorite things was watching the glorious sunsets over Lake DuBay.

The family wishes to thank the staff at North Crest in Stevens Point and the St. Croix Hospice team, along with Dr. Onitilo, Dr. Fox. And Dr. Evans, for giving him 5 more years.

A special thanks to Father Jim Trempe for his many visits. Thank you to his brother, Bill, his sister, Sandy and Mary's brother, Tom, for their support.

He is survived by his wife, Mary, of 53 years, daughters: Jennifer Schremp of Appleton, WI and Kristen Schremp (Sean Johnson) of Neenah, WI, grandchildren: Hannah, Alex and Ryan Heran, along with Addisyn Schremp. He is further survived by his brother, Bill (Kelly) Schremp, sisters: Sandra Jacobson (Tom Hecox) and Connie (Rory) McKellips, brother-in-law: Thomas Rader, sister-in-laws: Karen (Steve) Schremp Schinker and Cheri Schremp (Ken Kamenick) and many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents: Herb and Verna Schremp, son: Ryan Schremp, brothers: Jim and Ken Schremp, brother-in-laws: Jim Jacobson and Larry Rader, sister-in-laws: Marna and Diane Rader, in-laws: Violet and Felix Rader, and his wife's foster parents: Delos and Lucella Paul, and lastly, good friends, Doug Nesbitt, Floyd Wisniewski, Joe Slezak, Jerome Cherek, Tony Hintz and Bill Bohn.

Go with a smile, John, for you are loved by many. That is all any of us can ask for in this life.

The visitation for John will be held on Friday, May 8, 2026, from 4:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. at Brainard Funeral Home, Wausau. The Mass of Christian Burial will be held on Saturday, May 9, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. at St. Francis Xavier Catholic Church, Knowlton, with a visitation from 8:30 a.m. until 10:30 a.m., also at the church. Father Jim Trempe will officiate. Burial will immediately follow the Mass and take place at the Mosinee Cemetery, with a luncheon at Pinewood in Mosinee to follow.

Brainard Funeral Home & Cremation Center of Wausau is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.brainardfuneral.com.

records.name: Josiah Juedes
records.summary: Josiah Juedes was born still on April 16, 2026, at 34 weeks.
records.body:
"Yet you do not know what your life will be like tomorrow. For you are just a vapor that appears for a little while, and then vanishes away."— James 4:14

Like a vapor that rises softly and slips quietly into heaven, Josiah Juedes graced this world for only the briefest of moments — yet left an imprint upon the hearts of his family that will endure for a lifetime. Born still on April 16, 2026, at 34 weeks, Josiah arrived and departed in the same sacred breath, carried from his mother's arms into the arms of Jesus.

Though Josiah never took first breaths or first steps, his life was woven into the fabric of his family from the very moment of his becoming. He was known. He was wanted. He was cherished. Our hearts find comfort in knowing that this earthly life is but a vapor, while life with God has no end.

Josiah is cherished by his devoted parents, Jacob and Madelyn Juedes, who love him with tender grace, heavy hearts, and unwavering faith. He is also cherished by his brothers, Jackson and Wesley, who will grow up knowing their family holds a piece of heaven — a little brother who watches over them always. His name will be spoken with both joy and tears, and he will forever remain the third son in this family's story.

He is lovingly remembered by his grandparents, James and Joanne Juedes, Dean Prohaska, and Chris and Mary John; great-grandparents, Ronald and Marie Juedes, Dolores Prohaska, and Bonnie Bishell; along with many loving aunts, uncles, and extended family.

He was preceded in death by his great-grandparents Wilfred and Betty Heindl, Edward Prohaska, and Lyle Langenhahn.

The funeral service for Josiah will take place on Saturday, April 25, 2026, at 3:00PM at The Village Church, 1485 Village Way Dr, Kronenwetter, WI. Pastor Leroy Jonas will officiate. A private burial will follow. In lieu of flowers, memorials to the family to assist with medical expenses are appreciated.

The same God who knit Josiah together in the womb now holds him in perfect peace. He was never lost; he simply arrived home ahead of the rest.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Larry G. McDonald
records.summary: Larry Gene McDonald, 82, formerly of Neillsville, Wisconsin, passed away on April 16, 2026.
records.body:
Larry Gene McDonald, age 82, formerly of Neillsville, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on April 16, 2026, at the House of Dove in Marshfield.

Larry was a devoted husband, father, grandfather, and great-grandfather who was known for his hardworking nature and love for fixing and building things. On July 23, 1983, he married the love of his life, Eldray "Elly" McDonald, and together they built a life centered around simple joys caring for their home and yard.

He dedicated 38 years of service as the patrolman to the Township of Pine Valley in Neillsville, where he was respected for his strong work ethic and commitment to his community.

Larry had a passion for tinkering in his garage, especially working on his beloved 1950 Chevy pickup. He found peace and happiness spending countless hours on his 20-acre property, which he and Elly referred to as Paradise Acres on Sidney Avenue, where he enjoyed the outdoors and the satisfaction of working with his hands.

He is survived by his loving wife, Elly McDonald; his former wife Sandy McDonald and their five children: Doug (Becky) McDonald, Lorrie (Jerry) Fredrick, Mike (Zandra) McDonald, Todd McDonald, Amanda (Jerry) Schmidt; Stepchildren, Chris North and Doug (Angie) North. Brothers, David (Donna) McDonald and Gary (Charlene) McDonald as well as many grandchildren, great-grandchildren, and great-great-grandchildren who will carry on his legacy. He was preceded in death by his parents: Francis and Gladys McDonald, a Grandson: Joesph Salisbury, a stepson: Randy North, a brother, Robert McDonald and an infant sister Pamela McDonald

Larry will be remembered for his quiet strength, the pride he took in caring for his home and yard, his love of vintage vehicles, and his ability to fix just about anything. He will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved him.

A graveside burial service is currently being planned.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family. Words of remembrance may be left at MWCS.WS

records.name: Thomas S. Kokke
records.summary: Thomas S. Kokke, 72, of Oshkosh, died on February 2, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas S. Kokke, 72 of Oshkosh, died on Monday, February 2, 2026, at Hospice House, Wausau, under the care of Aspirus Hospice and Comfort Care.

Tom was born on October 25, 1953, in Milwaukee, the son of Gerald and Mary Jane (Davis) Kokke.

Tom graduated from JFK Prep School in 1972 and University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee in 1979, with a degree in economics and political science. He worked as a corrections officer for 30 years. After retirement, he loved spending time at Wildlife Campground with his significant other, Barb Lutz and friends. Tom enjoyed motorcycling and helping others when needed, whether it be plowing fields or cutting trees.

Thomas is survived by two children, Adam Kokke and Erin Kokke; two sisters, Kitty Kokke Wood and Sally Hall, as well as many lifelong friends.

Thomas was preceded in death by his parents; furry companion, Cal, who he was looking forward to reuniting with, and many friends.

A Celebration of Life to honor Tom's life took place on Thursday, February 12, 2026, at The Bar, 825 N Washburn Street, Oshkosh, WI 54904.

Another Celebration of Life will be held at 3 PM on Saturday, June 13, 2026, at Wildlife Bar & Campground, 183327 Schwede Lane, Birnamwood, WI 54414. A Memorial Ride will take place from 12 NOON until 3 PM.

In lieu of flowers, the family asks that donations be made to your local humane society in honor of Thomas.

Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg is assisting the family with arrangements. Memories and messages of support may be shared at schmidtschulta.com.

records.name: Catherine M. Burnett
records.summary: Catherine M. Burnett, 75, of Rothschild, passed away on April 21, 2026.
records.body:
Catherine "Cathy" M. Burnett, 75, Rothschild passed away peacefully surrounded by her family on Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at UW Health University Hospital in Madison.

The family would like to thank Dr. Govari and his staff at UW Health Carbone Cancer Center in Madison. They would also like to thank Dr. Peterson and his staff at Aspirus Wausau Cancer Center.

She was born on July 1, 1950 in Wausau, daughter of the late Leonard and Alice (Kordus) Sattler. On May 19, 1973 she married Dale Burnett Jr. at St. Mark Catholic Church in Rothschild. He survives.

Her working career included staying at home raising her children, Drott/JI Case, Professional Consultants and Peterson Kraemer Funeral Home, before retiring from the YMCA.

Her hobbies included a love of traveling with her husband, spending quality time with her grandchildren, reading, golfing, yearly vacations up-north in Tomahawk with her family, spending time on the pontoon at her lake home "camper" on Mayflower Lake, playing cards and spending time with her many friends.

She is survived by her loving husband Dale Burnett Jr. of Rothschild. Her 3 children, Lori Heitkamp of St. Michael, MN, Andrew (Melissa) Burnett of Rothschild and Brian (Dawn) Burnett of Marinette. Her 8 grandchildren: Zachary, Brenden Heitkamp of St. Michael, MN. Melanie Burnett of Blaine, MN, Trevor Burnett of North Fond du Lac, Carter, Devin, Dexter and Miranda Burnett, all of Rothschild and her siblings, Sally (Morgan) Holzapfel of Nashville, TN, Peggy (Scott) Smith of Rothschild, and Joel Sattler of Rothschild.

Besides her parents she was preceded in death by a brother, Ron Sattler.

Funeral Services will be held at Noon, Friday, May 1, 2026 at Mount Olive Lutheran Church, Weston. Private family burial will be at Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Kronenwetter. Visitation will be held on Friday, May 1, 2026 from 9:00 a.m. until the time of services all at the church. In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to UW Health Carbone Cancer Research.

records.name: Douglas Baumann
records.summary: Douglas Baumann, 76, of Town of Wausau, passed away on April 22, 2026.
records.body:
Douglas "Doug" Baumann, 76, Town of Wausau, passed away on Wednesday, April 22, 2026, at home surrounded by his family, just 37 days after being diagnosed with metastatic prostate cancer. He was under the care of his loving wife of 53 years, Sharon and Aspirus Hospice and Comfort Care. He was born May 14, 1949, in Wausau, the son of the late Eugene and Bernice (Kranz) Baumann. Doug attended Zion Lutheran school and graduated from Wausau High School in 1967. On June 10,1972 he married Sharon Lev at Zion Lutheran Church, Wausau. Doug and Sharon had dated for 5 years and even separated states for a short time before getting married, but it was true love and they were together though his last breath.

A millwright at the Weyerhaeuser Paper Mill in Rothschild for 34 years, Doug retired at the age of 55. After retirement he and Sharon spent many years camping "Up North" and traveling. They planned trips in their motor home alone and with family and visited many states spending extended time in places like North Carolina and Florida. They also went out west several times seeing our national parks and camping along the way. Doug also started raising beef to share with his children, becoming a farmer at the age of 60.

Doug loved to go fast! When his family was young, they spent many hours snowmobiling and four wheeling. He was an active member of the Nutterville Sno Doggers(Nuts) for many years helping them get their 1st new Tucker, and groomed countless miles of trails. His family would put on thousands of miles each winter always trying to hit that "1000 mile" mark during Christmas break alone each year. The family rarely stopped to go inside, instead they took breaks along the trail, dad ways had a Kit Kat or Hershey bar in his pocket as a treat to share and they would be off again for more adventures.
