In 1989 at the age of 40, Doug took up motocross. This was a passion that carried on throughout the rest of his life. Starting with a father-son relay race on a borrowed bike at Hixton, WI. That race grew to be a lifestyle; the family spent most weekends at a racetrack somewhere in district 16 between April and October. He also practiced every day on his practice track in the back yard. Doug made many friends along the way and earned many trophies and jackets. He also broke many bones gaining the nick name "Gimpy" by his family, neighborhood kids, and race friends. He often bragged about his 17 surgeries and multiple pieces of hardware, rods, plates and screws used to put him back together. He passed his need for speed and love of motocross to his son, grandsons and even great-grandson.

Doug was a devoted father and grandfather. He attended every softball game, soccer game, swim meet, wrestling meet, ice skating competition, band concert, track meet, tractor pull or motocross event they competed in. Sometimes packing up the motor home and traveling great distances with Sharon, to just be there. He was very handy and built playground sets, bear cages, did home repairs and upgrades, fixed bikes, snowmobiles, go-carts and helped with maintenance of vehicles. He truly did anything for his children and grandchildren. He could often be found in his shop with his buddy Dewbus, his springer spaniel, working on a project. Doug planned every vacation with his family spending many hours camping, fishing, water skiing and tubing during the summer in the state forest campgrounds of northern Wisconsin. During the last several summers, after his first broken femur slowed his motocross activities a bit, he and Sharon found enjoyment riding UTVs. They spent many hours and miles exploring together in the UP and northern Wisconsin.

Survivors include his wife, Sharon Baumann, Town of Wausau. His children; Bradley (Barb) Baumann, Wausau and Kristie (Chris) Winter, Merrill. His grandchildren; Tanner Steele, Cassie Krueger, Matthew Meverden, Wausau; Amber Winter (Ryder Depies), Emily Winter, Logan Meverden (Hatti Learned), Merrill. Great-grandchildren Arthur Hogan and Aspyn Steele, Wausau. Sister Roxane (Jim) Jensen, Longmont CO and brother Gary Baumann (Jodi Wescott), Kohler WI. He also leaves behind his best friend and companion, Dewbus. Besides his parents he was preceded in death by his brothers-in-law Jim Jensen and William Lev.

The family would like to extend a special thank you to Doug's hospice nurse Kathy for her kindness and compassion during his final days. The family also wishes to thank Pastor Scott Gustafson with Trinity Lutheran Church in Merrill for his thoughtfulness.

Following Doug's wishes the family will mourn privately and there will be no formal memorial services.

records.name: John Kow Bentil
records.summary: John Kow Bentil, 91, passed away on February 6, 2026.
records.body:
John Kow Bentil, 92, passed away peacefully on February 6, 2026, at home. John was born on December 28, 1934, to Opanyin Kwesi Bentsir and Obaapanyin Adjoa Ampenyiwa of Kojokrom near Sekondi, in the Western Region of Ghana.

John was enrolled at Essikado Catholic Primary School 1951 at an advanced age of 17. He continued at Essaman Catholic and finally completed his elementary education at Roman Catholic, Ketan in 1960, making his education in 9 years instead of 10 years due to his advanced age and his cleverness.

All this while getting himself educated during his advanced age, John was always working hard with his late father on the farm while assisting his mom with Fante kenkey preparation.

John was a devoted husband to Grace Bentil whom he married on January 10th, 1963. He was a loving father to his six children, Augustine, Martha, Sylvester, Alice, Rose, and Monica.

All this helped "Old Ben, New Ben" to develop his own sense of work ethic in farming, home chores, kenkey selling. This gave him an affection for farming, that took him, his wife and children to follow his stead as he farmed.

John was employed by the Ghana Railways as a pipe fitter at the Carriage & Wagon sector from October 1961 until retiring in September 1989.

John was not only a hardworking man but also a man of deep faith. This is chronicled in his own archived notes that he was a founding member of Sacred Heart Church. He never got tired serving God as a Sacred Heart parishioner, Chorister, St. Anthony member, and Sacred Heart Confraternity member. John could be described as the face of the Catholic Church in Kojokrom. His commitment to God in the Catholic church shaped his own spiritual growth as well as many of his family members, leading to his first-born son becoming a Catholic priest.

John served in faith, served with humility and led in love. He never wanted to engage in any conflict with anyone, a man of peace. John will be remembered for his commitment to righteousness, grace and unwavering trust in God's promises. The family finds peace in knowing that he is now in eternity, resting with his Maker. His love, wisdom and devotion will forever remain in the hearts of those he touched.

The late John Bentil is survived by his loving wife, Grace Bentil, and his six children Rev. Augustine Bentil (Fr. Gus), Martha Bentil, Sylvester Bentil, Alice Bentil, Rose Bentil, and Monica Bentil.

A combined Memorial Service will be held on Thursday, May 14, 2026, at noon at St. Joseph Parish, Galloway. There will be refreshments and a meal served.

records.name: Laron Thomas Erickson
records.summary: Laron Thomas Erickson, 69, of Bancroft, passed away on April 21, 2026.
records.body:
Laron Thomas Erickson, of Bancroft, passed away on April 21, 2026, at the House of Dove Hospice House in Marshfield. He was born on September 27, 1956, in Wisconsin Rapids to the late Noral and Marjorie (Pohl) Erickson.

Laron grew up in Plainfield, Wisconsin, where he attended school. Following his education, he worked a few odd jobs before beginning a long and dedicated career with B&B Paving. He spent over 40 years working alongside his brother-in-law and his favorite nephew, Jim Benjamin Jr., until his retirement. Laron was a hardworking and dependable employee who rarely missed a day of work. He was his brother's right-hand man, known for keeping equipment running and tackling any mechanical issue that came his way.

A self-taught mechanic, Laron had a passion for restoring his "old Fords" and Oliver tractors. He also loved gardening and took great pride in his harvest, canning as much as he could each season.

Music was another of Laron's joys. He appreciated all kinds, but especially enjoyed bluegrass, The Beatles, and the Eagles. He often spent time with family "playing DJ" while everyone gathered together. Laron also enjoyed target practice in his backyard, especially when his children and grandchildren joined him.

He had a unique sense of humor and a quiet wit that those closest to him cherished. Laron loved sharing stories with his grandchildren—many of which they continue to tell today.

He is survived by his daughters, Angela Kleman (John Mallek) and Alycia (Mike) Firkus; five grandchildren, Benjamin (Courtney) Berndt, Patrick (Paige) Kleman, Nicholas Kleman, Marshall Firkus, and Abby Firkus; and one great-grandson, Jett Berndt. He is further survived by his sister, Natalie Erickson; and his loyal companion of the past six years, his dog, Novah.

The family would like to extend a sincere thank you to Compassus Hospice for the care they provided to Laron.

A private memorial service will be held at a later date. Family and friends may leave words of remembrance at mwcs.ws.

records.name: Ricky Allen Carman
records.summary: Ricky Allen Carman, 63, of Wausau, passed away on April 8, 2026.
records.body:
Ricky Allen Carman, age 63, of Wausau, passed away on Wednesday, April 8, 2026, at his home, after a short, but courageous battle with cancer. He was born on November 29, 1962, to the late Clarence and Darla Carman, in Minneapolis, MN. He attended and graduated from Mankato High School. He then went on to attend classes to become a boiler technician. Ricky met Vicki Flynn and fell in love. The two exchanged marriage vows on July 1, 2005 in Wausau, WI. She survives.

Ricky was a hard worker and worked right up until his death. He was employed in the janitorial department at the Marshfield Hospital in Weston for many years. Ricky enjoyed classic cars and loved to go to the Iola car show. He was a dedicated family man that adored his grandchildren. He was a huge Harry Potter fan, and collected many related items. Ricky bravely faced and fought his illness. He will be dearly missed, but the memories of the good times will remain with his family.

Ricky is survived by his loving wife: Vicki Carman, son: Scott (Allondra) Carman, daughter: LeeAnn Carman, step-daughters: Cassandra Pahls, Katrina Flynn and Jessica (Taylor) Rosa and 7 grandchildren: Zane, Sylas, Sierra, Oliver, Gabryel, Matthew and Kyanna. He is preceded in death by his parents, step-father: Ralph and brother: Rocky.

No services will be held.

records.name: Adam C. Celing
records.summary: Adam C. Celing, 47, died on April 12, 2026.
records.body:
Adam had to leave us following his two-year fight against colon cancer.

Adam was born an old soul, yet he lit up the world around him with his quick wit, often leaving us in fits of laughter. He was a talented thespian and graphic designer, but what he loved most were his family and friends.

Adam took his role as the oldest child to heart, often to the dismay of his brothers Evan and Ian, and his sister, Erin. He loved them fiercely and protectively.

In college he met his best friend, Renee Gaspard, who shortly thereafter became his wife and "forever person." Adam and Renee went on to create their legacy with sons Gabriel and Bennet, and daughter Magdalyn.

Adam has gone to hang out with his grandparents, Bill and Irma McIntee, and Edwin and Helen Celing; his Uncle Larry Celing, and his great-grandmother Elizabeth McIntee.

He is survived by his wife Renee, and his children Gabe, Ben, and Maggie; his mom and dad, Mary and Don Celing; his partners in crime, Evan (Kristin), Ian (Hannah), and Erin (Peter); his Aunt Barbara Prouty; Uncle Matt (Ann) McIntee; "Aunt and Uncle" Penny and Ken Swanson; Uncle Peter (Barbara) McIntee; in-laws Roger and Tyanne Gaspard, brother-in-law Aaron Gaspard; niece and nephew, Madison and Michael Celing; cousins Patrick and William McIntee, and Annie (Ross) McIntee Pimental, Will McIntee; "cousins" Jennifer Rael and Jim Swanson; his Friendsgiving family, his college family, his many friends and co-workers, and friends he hadn't yet met.

records.name: Carl Henry Slagle
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Carl Henry Slagle, 80, of Birnamwood, Wisconsin, died on April 18, 2026.
records.body:
Carl Henry Slagle was born on July 20, 1945, in Park Falls, Wisconsin, the son of Guyrus and Clara (Sonto) Slagle. Carl graduated from Park Falls High School in 1963 and later from Northcentral Technical College in 1965, with an associate's degree in mechanical design. After graduating from NTC, Carl was drafted into the U.S. Army and proudly served during the Vietnam conflict as a pharmacy specialist. Shortly after the conclusion of military service, Carl returned home to marry the love of his life, Darlene Seehafer, on February 16, 1968. Although Carl made a living in the industrial field and later by operating his own home inspection service, he made a life through his commitment to faith and family. He devoted his years to serving others through his steadfast faith. In his early years, he taught Sunday school and volunteered as a youth group leader at his church. In his later years, he encouraged his children and grandchildren in their walks of faith. Carl enjoyed hunting, fishing, and gardening, often sharing these activities with those he loved most. Carl was a beloved husband, father, grandpa, great-grandpa, and friend to many. His kind and gentle spirit reflected the love of Christ in all his relationships.

Carl is survived by their daughters, Tammy (Jeremy) Strassburger and Tina (Mario) Meverden; their grandchildren, Jacob (Taneal) Strassburger, Tiffany (Larry) Daffinson, Zachary (Jessica) Strassburger, Sawyer Meverden, Hudson (Kristi) Meverden, and Lincoln (Anna) Meverden; their great-grandchildren, Noelle Strassburger, Elora Strassburger, Loretta Strassburger, Porter Daffinson, Clara Daffinson, Oliver Daffinson, Ivory Strassburger, Luke Strassburger, Laine Meverden, and Maisie Meverden; brothers and sisters, Shirley Erickson, Helen Dezotell, Gary (Sandy) Slagle, Mary Literski, Doris Springer, and John Seehafer; sister-in-law, Molly Seehafer; and many nieces and nephews.

Carl was preceded in death by his parents and his brother-in-law, Larry Erickson.

records.name: Catherine "Cathy" M. Burnett
records.summary: Cathy M. Burnett, 75, of Rothschild, died on April 21, 2026.
records.body:
Catherine "Cathy" M. Burnett was born on July 1, 1950 in Wausau, daughter of the late Leonard and Alice (Kordus) Sattler. On May 19, 1973 she married Dale Burnett Jr. at St. Mark Catholic Church in Rothschild. He survives.

Her working career included staying at home raising her children, Drott/JI Case, Professional Consultants and Peterson Kraemer Funeral Home, before retiring from the YMCA.

Her hobbies included a love of traveling with her husband, spending quality time with her grandchildren, reading, golfing, yearly vacations up-north in Tomahawk with her family, spending time on the pontoon at her lake home "camper" on Mayflower Lake, playing cards and spending time with her many friends. Some of Cathy's favorite pastimes included volunteering with the Special Olympics where she was a volunteer during the Winter Olympics and was also a coach for the snowshoe races. She also loved playing cards, especially Canasta and loved being part of the Card Club. In her earlier years she was on a bowling league with Dale at Mountain Lanes and dear to her heart was her knitting group at St. Mark Catholic Church.

She is survived by her loving husband Dale Burnett Jr. of Rothschild. Her 3 children, Lori Heitkamp of St. Michael, MN, Andrew (Melissa) Burnett of Rothschild and Brian (Dawn) Burnett of Marinette. Her 8 grandchildren: Zachary, Brenden Heitkamp of St. Michael, MN. Melanie Burnett of Blaine, MN, Trevor Burnett of North Fond du Lac, Carter, Devin, Dexter and Miranda Burnett, all of Rothschild and her siblings, Sally (Morgan) Holzapfel of Nashville, TN, Peggy (Scott) Smith of Rothschild, and Joel Sattler of Rothschild.

Besides her parents she was preceded in death by a brother, Ron Sattler.

records.name: Darlene Ruth Slagle
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Darlene Ruth Slagle, 77, of Birnamwood, Wisconsin, died on April 18, 2026.
records.body:
Darlene Ruth Slagle was born on December 28, 1948, in Wausau, Wisconsin, the daughter of William and Loretta (Hintz) Seehafer. Darlene graduated from Wausau East High in 1967. She met her life-long love, Carl, at the age of fifteen after spotting him strolling downtown Wausau and following him back to his apartment. Since that first meeting, Carl and Darlene were inseparable. Darlene longingly waited for Carl to return from military service before joining him in marriage soon after Valentine's Day in 1968. Two daughters – the result of their love story – followed shortly after. Darlene devoted her years to her home, husband, and children before entering the retail work world. With Carl's support, she operated "Once and Again Consignments" in Birnamwood, Wisconsin for over 24 years. Darlene loved the consignment world, working closely with her vendors and enjoying those who frequented the shop. She considered them all her friends and took immense joy in the conversations she had with each. Darlene enjoyed decorating, thrifting, and crafting, but her Savior Jesus was nearest to her heart. Like Carl, she too loved her God and family fiercely.

Darlene is survived by their daughters, Tammy (Jeremy) Strassburger and Tina (Mario) Meverden; their grandchildren, Jacob (Taneal) Strassburger, Tiffany (Larry) Daffinson, Zachary (Jessica) Strassburger, Sawyer Meverden, Hudson (Kristi) Meverden, and Lincoln (Anna) Meverden; their great-grandchildren, Noelle Strassburger, Elora Strassburger, Loretta Strassburger, Porter Daffinson, Clara Daffinson, Oliver Daffinson, Ivory Strassburger, Luke Strassburger, Laine Meverden, and Maisie Meverden; brothers and sisters, Shirley Erickson, Helen Dezotell, Gary (Sandy) Slagle, Mary Literski, Doris Springer, and John Seehafer; sister-in-law, Molly Seehafer; and many nieces and nephews.

Darlene was preceded in death by her parents; her brothers, Gerald Seehafer and James Seehafer; brothers-in-law, George Literski and Rick Springer; and sister-in-law Elaine Seehafer.

records.name: Gina M. Brunner
records.summary: Gina M. Brunner, 45, of Wittenberg, died on April 22, 2026.
records.body:
Gina M. Brunner, 45 of Wittenberg, died unexpectedly, on Wednesday, April 22, 2026, as the result of a car accident.

Gina was born on June 25, 1980, in Oshkosh, the daughter of Dale 'Bones' & Joan (Hall) Brunner.

Gina was currently employed at Amron as a chemical attendant. Gina enjoyed reading and listening to audiobooks, especially fantasy and romance. She loved visiting waterfalls and going to the casino with her mom, Joan. Most importantly, Gina cherished time with her family and often planned game nights with her children, where they played cards and board games together.

Gina is survived by her mother, Joan; seven children, Adriana (Fernando Zavaleta) Paredes, Jose Paredes, Angel (Mariely) Lopez, Oscar (Elise) Lopez, Flor Lopez, Ayden Brunner and Kylie Brunner; three grandchildren, Freddy Jr. Gallardo, Gabriel Zavaleta, Evander Lopez, and one more on the way; siblings, Lori Burks, Billy (Lisa) Partridge and Shane (Michelle) Brunner, as well as many nieces, nephews, relatives and friends.

She was preceded in death by her father.

records.name: Henry "Hank" Blarek, Jr.
records.summary: Henry "Hank" Blarek, Jr., 80, of Hatley, died on April 24, 2026.
records.body:
Henry "Hank" Blarek, Jr., age 80, of Hatley died unexpectedly Friday, April 24, 2026.

He was born February 5, 1946, to the late Henry Sr., and Lucille (nee Tryba) Blarek. On April 23, 1966, he married the love of his life, Bonnie Miller in Wausau at St. Paul Lutheran Church, and years later renewing their vows in the Catholic faith at St. Florian's in Hatley.

A proud U.S. Army veteran, Hank served a tour in Vietnam with the 500 Engineer Corps, achieving the rank of E-5 Sergeant. His commitment to fellow veterans never faded. He was an active member of the American Legion Post 471 in Hatley and the VFW-Post 8068 in Elderon, and enjoyed delivering Christmas baskets for the VFW-Post in Ringle/Hatley. He volunteered countless hours with Man of Honor Society supporting veteran causes through raffles and events.

Hank dedicated 45 years of his career to Greenheck Fan, where he worked as a welder before retiring, leaving behind a legacy of hard work and commitment. He found joy in the outdoors and the work of his hands, living a life shaped by patience, skill, and quiet determination. A successful hunter and excellent fisherman, he had a style all his own when it came to finding fish, one that seemed guided as much by instinct as experience. He was an awesome pool player, known for his sharp eye and steady hand at the table. In his spare time, he took pride in restoring International Harvester tractors, bringing old machines back to life with care and craftsmanship.

Hank was truly a "man for the people." He served his community as a supervisor before being elected chairman of the Town of Ringle, a position he held for 26 years. Even in his final days, he remained connected to his town, driving through the devastation left by a recent tornado in the place he loved so deeply.

Faith was a cornerstone of Hank's life and was a lifelong member of Knights of Columbus. He attended St. Ladislaus Catholic Church where he was an active member of the Men's Society and Eucharistic Minister.

Devoted husband of 60 years of Bonnie Blarek. Loving father of Penny Blarek, and John Sr., (Jane) Blarek. Adoring grandfather of Ashley (Sean) Schramm, Steven (Scott) Augustine, Tia Growcock, Meranda (Aaron Mehlberg) Growcock, Kasey (Steve Larson) Blarek, Amber Blarek, and John Jr., (Kendra Socha) Blarek. Doting great-grandfather of Ayden, Jaxon, Kearson, Nolan, Jennifer, Brentley, and Huxley. Kind brother of Therese "Terri" (the Late Jim) Miller, James (Rita) Blarek, JoAnn (James) Davidowski, Barbara Morgan, George (Diane Weston) Blarek, Mark Blarek, the late Carleen, and the late Marlene. Fond brother-in-law of the late Lyle Miller, Noreen (Herbert) Fandrey, and Gary (Joyce) Miller. Caring father-in-law of Steven (Cari Behar-Growcock) Growcock and Stacey Blarek. Cherished friend of Bobby Walkowski, Chris Fuge, Tony Robl, Jim Streeter, and the Mott Family of Two Rivers. Loyal uncle, cousin, mentor, and friend to so many.

Hank will be remembered as a devoted husband, proud father and grandfather, great-grandfather, loyal friend, and a man who lived fully and gave generously. Whether casting a line, lining up a pool shot, or lending a hand, Hank left his mark wherever he went. His life reminds us to love fiercely, work hard, serve others, and never pass up the chance for one more game, one more laugh, or one more lap around the track.

records.name: Joan M. Gliniecki
records.summary: Joan M. Gliniecki, 86, died on April 22, 2026.
records.body:
Joan M. Gliniecki, 86, passed away peacefully on April 22, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital. She was born on May 9, 1939, to the late Alvin and Mary (Jurkowski) Tomczik.

Joan was united in marriage to Theodore Gliniecki on April 11, 1959. Together they built a life centered on family, hard work, and caring for others. He preceded her in death.

After graduating from high school, Joan pursued her passion for beauty and people by earning her cosmetology certification. She spent her life as a hairdresser, continuing to work until the remarkable age of 85. To Joan, her clients were more than appointments—they were friends. She treasured the conversations, the laughter, and the chance to be part of their lives.

Joan had a joyful spirit and loved the simple pleasures—an afternoon of bingo, a trip to the slot machines, or a lively card game surrounded by family and friends. Her home was often filled with the smell of fresh baking and the sounds of gatherings she so loved to host.

Her generosity was one of her greatest gifts. Joan gave freely of her time, always ready to lend a hand or simply sit and visit with someone who needed company. She volunteered often, especially at Marywood Convalescent Center, and made sure her children understood the importance of giving back. Each Christmas, she quietly made a difference by helping families in need, a tradition that reflected her kind and giving heart.

Joan loved flowers and found joy in their beauty, but nothing meant more to her than her family. Her children, grandchildren, and great-grandchildren were the center of her world, and she took every opportunity to be part of their lives.

She is survived by her children, Brian (Jackie) Gliniecki, Michelle (Jesse) Angell, and Lori (Jack) Hehling; ten grandchildren, Desiree (Cory), Darlene (Eric), Colleen, Alyssa (Rob), Tianna (Dan), Brittany, Robert (Erin), Amanda, Ted, and Colton; and great-grandchildren, Laylanee, Olivia, Oliver, Wally, Dominic, Aiden, Emsley, and Ashton. She is further survived by her special friend, Bernell Ellenbecker; sisters-in-law, Marie Tomczik, Gertrude Gabrielski and Gloria Milanowski; as well as several nieces and nephews.

In addition to her parents and husband, Joan was preceded in death by her fiancé, John Wolfe; siblings, Jim and Rosie Tomczik; and her grandson, Chase Howe.

records.name: Olga M. Kuck
records.summary: Olga M. Kuck, 105, of Wausau, formerly of Athens, Wisconsin, died on April 21, 2026.
records.body:
Olga M. Kuck, 105, Wausau, formerly of Athens, WI, passed away peacefully on Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at Wausau Manor, under the care of Compassus Hospice.

She was born March 13, 1921 in the Town of Goodrich, WI, daughter of the late Fred and Friederike (Radke) Roesler. On November 30, 1939, she married Edwin A. Kuck at Trinity Lutheran Church in Athens. He preceded her in death on May 6, 2006.

Olga truly enjoyed being a homemaker, in addition to working on the family mink ranch and small dairy farm. Some of her favorite hobbies were flower gardening and watching the animals play around in her yard. Olga also spent many hours crocheting items for her family.

Survivors include, her son, Roger (Beth) Kuck; four step-grandchildren, Kimberli (Troy) Rubenzer and Brenda (Rich) Rubenzer; four great step-grandchildren, Mia, Stella, Sophia and Lily Rubenzer; one sister-in-law, Jeanette Roesler, Milwaukee; and many nieces and nephews.

Besides her parents and husband, Edwin, she was preceded in death by her son, Robert Kuck; six brothers, Edwin, Henry, Ervin, Ernest, Walter and Julius Roesler; and sister, Lydia Thuot.

records.name: Carol J. Anderson
records.summary: Carol Jean Anderson, 88, passed away on April 17, 2026, in Mosinee, Wisconsin.
records.body:
On April 17, 2026, Carol Jean Anderson (88 years old), after listening to hours of Christian hymns, songs, and a Christian message called "Reaching for the Prize", breathed her last, surrounded by family.

Carol was born in Freeport, Illinois, to Harry Hill and Marjorie Ruth (Unzicker) Johnson, in Freeport, Illinois, on October 17, 1937, at St Francis Hospital.

Carol graduated high school in Freeport, Illinois, in 1955. As a Freshman, she was awarded Class Queen. As a sophomore, she was elected the Most Beautiful of the class. As a Sophomore, her picture was sent to a contest Eddie Fisher sponsored. Carol came in second place. Carol was a cheerleader her Junior and Senior years.

Carol married her high school sweetheart, Gerald (Jerry) Burton Allen, on October 29, 1955. They had four sons before Jerry's passing in 1980. Carol then married Gary Lee Anderson on July 10, 1981. Gary passed away in 2017.

Carol had attended a week-long school on teaching Sunday School to 4- and 5-year-olds, which started on a path of teaching Sunday School at 4 different churches over a span of 25 years.

Carol was well-known for her service at the church – as a Sunday School teacher, Church Counsel leader, Women's Retreat leader, prayer leader, and for suggesting an Alzheimer's Group.

Carol was well known for her many years of service in Avon. She was an Avon Lady in Mosinee for 50 years, gaining many friends and relationships. Over the years, Carol was awarded 1st in Sales in her District for 7 years, 2nd or 3rd in Sales for 8 years, and winning the Spirit of Avon Award- the first to achieve that award.

Carol was preceded in death by her parents, Harry and Marjorie Johnson, her husbands, Jerry Allen and Gary Anderson, her sister, Peggy Johnson and son at birth, Douglas Patrick Allen.

She is survived by her brothers, Harry (Bub) Johnson and James (Jim) Johnson (Marcia). Sons Michael (Mike) Allen (Regina), David (Dave) Allen, Charles (Chuck) Allen (Karen).

Grandchildren, Robyn (Andrew) Kayes, David Allen, Josh Murray, Aaron Murray, Ty Murray, Holly Neeley, Heidi (Freddie) Stouffer, Carl (Stephanie) Wanserski, Mark (Rachael) Allen, Paige (Matthew) Ross. Great-grandchildren, Zychray (Zee) Kayes, Gamaya (Maya) Kayes, Dominic Murray, Skyler Murray, Connor Murray, Diana Murray, Leeland Neeley, Olive Neeley, Liza Neeley, Zoey Stouffer, Paizley Stouffer, Finn Stouffer, Mavis Stouffer, McKinley Wanserski, Roslyn Wanserski, Monroe Wanserski, Maverick Wanserski, Nataliee Ross, Chloe Ross, Jade Ross, Kate Ross.

Nieces and Nephews, Karen Johnson, Susanna Johnson, Mandy Moss, Ryan Armagost, Jaime Stepan, Jessica Leseberg, and Mackenzie (Tyler) Olsen.

Carol will be remembered for her love of the Packers, the Milwaukee Brewers, bird-watching, creating albums of family members, and most of all, remembering birthdays and anniversaries by mailing beautiful cards to all the special people in her life. She will be deeply missed.

The family would like to thank the staff at Aspirus Hospice House for the excellent, compassionate care they gave Carol.

A Memorial Service will take place at 10:00 a.m., on May 30, 2026, at Mosinee United Methodist Church. Pastor Timothy Gurlea will officiate. Visitation will be from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service, all at the church. A luncheon will follow the service.

records.name: Damian J. Baracca
records.summary: Damian J. Baracca, 48, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly on April 19, 2026.
records.body:
Damian James Baracca, 48, passed away unexpectedly on April 19, 2026, in Wausau, Wisconsin.

He was born on May 5, 1977, in Poughkeepsie, NY, son of James and Daria "Debbie" (Burg) Baracca. Damian was a man who found joy in the kitchen, having worked in various restaurants across Wisconsin and the Hudson Valley in New York. Easily making friends wherever he went, Damian's warm and inviting nature earned him a wide circle of friends across the nation. His personality shone through in his zest for life—whether riding motorcycles, relishing nature walks, or indulging his love for music and hot rods. More recently, his spiritual journey had taken center stage, as he devoted himself to his church with aspirations to become a deacon.

Survivors include, his three children: Ashley "Cheyanne" (Joe) Vanfossen of California, Mikey Baracca, and Jade Baracca-Barringer of New York; four grandchildren; his father, James Baracca (along with his 2 beloved huskies, Apollo and Diamond) of New York; his two brothers, Jordan (Staci) Baracca of Wittenberg and Jeremy (Susan Gentile) Baracca of New York, and his lifelong friend Chris Vasquez of New York. Damian was especially close to his nephews and niece—Alex, Justin, Marissa and Kaylee—who held a special place in his heart.

Though we mourn the loss of Damian, we find comfort knowing he is warmly welcomed in heaven by his mother, Daria "Debbie" Baracca, and his grandparents, Lawrence and Catherine Burg, and Virgil and Lorraine Baracca.

A memorial service will be held at 11:00 A.M. on Saturday, May 16, 2026, at Gilead Church, 1102 N 1st Ave, Wausau, WI. Rev. Salvador Fuentes and Rev. Jennifer Fuentes will officiate. Visitation will be from 10:00 A.M. until the time of service at the church.

The last text on Damian's phone reads: "I can't leave my church. God put me there for a reason. I don't know what the reason is, but He'll show me in time."

records.name: Dawn Osness
records.summary: Dawn Osness, 69, of Merrill, Wisconsin, passed away on April 27, 2026.
records.body:
Dawn Osness, age 69, of Merrill, passed away on April 27, 2026, at Woodland Court under hospice care.

She is lovingly remembered by her domestic partner of many years, Mark Mensching; her daughter, Breanne Semling (Scott); her son, Josh Osness (Ashton); her granddaughters, Ally Sus (Jayden), Ashlynn Semling, and Kendel Semling; and her grandson, Archer Osness. She is further survived by her brother, Basil Woller (Valerie), and her sister, Lorie Kurtzweil (Chrys).

Dawn was preceded in death by her sister, Beth, and her parents, Kenneth and Elida Woller.

Dawn cherished time spent with family and friends. She was an avid bowler for more than 50 years and enjoyed many trips to the casinos with friends. She took great pride in her long career at Weinbrenner, where she worked for over 40 years.

The family extends their heartfelt thanks to the staff at Aspirus Health and Woodland Court for their compassionate care.

At her request, there will not be a funeral service. A celebration of life will be held at a later date.

records.name: Donald R. Marten
records.funeral_home: Helke Funeral Home and Cremation Care Center
records.summary: Donald R. Marten, 86, of Ringle, Wisconsin, passed away on April 26, 2026.
records.body:
Donald Marten, aged 86, passed away on Sunday, April 26, 2026, at Pine Meadows Assisted Living. He was born on July 21, 1939, in Ringle, Wisconsin, to Reno and Eva (Merriam) Marten.

Donald served honorably in the United States Army and later worked for Marathon Electric for 42 years before retiring in 2002.

He most enjoyed hunting, fishing, and spending time outdoors cutting wood, he loved listening to old country music, his favorite artist Don Williams on his record players.

He is survived by his children: Jeanine Manthe and Keith (Tracy) Marten. Four grandchildren; Missy (Travis) Olds, Rick (McKenzie) Manthe, Amber Marten (Fiance' Alex Jaeger) and Kyle Marten and three great-grandchildren; Johnny Dunning V, Jack Manthe and Hader Olds.

He was preceded in death by his parents: Reno and Eva Marten and five siblings; Gertrude Marten, Carol Bolzak, Lowell (Willie) Marten and Shirley Schram.

Burial will take place at Forestville Cemetery in Ringle, Wisconsin.

Family and friends may pay their respects during a time of visitation at Helke Funeral Home Thursday, April 30th from 10am to Noon, their will be a short service at Noon and proceed to burial site of Forestville Cemetery in Ringle, WI with Military Honors.

The family would like to express their sincere gratitude to Joleen at Interm Hospice Care and the Pine Meadows Assisted Living for their years of support and care.

records.name: Gordon D. Lowe
records.summary: Gordon Douglas Lowe, born April 16, 1950, passed away in 2026.
records.body:
Gordon Douglas Lowe was born on April 16, 1950, to Pauline Gabbard and, as best anyone could determine, Winston Lowe… we think.

His father was something of a mystery—appearing mostly in name and later reappearing under variations that hinted at aristocracy or generational wealth. None of it checked out. Gordon only seldom saw him. He grew up mostly only hearing stories of his father—many of them under different names—which may have contributed to his independence and his instinct to question a good story.

His mother, a mail-order bride from Kentucky—not the exotic European origin one might expect, but no less determined—raised him through what would now be called a difficult childhood. At the time, it was simply called life. Gordon did not dwell there. He moved forward, and he kept moving.

In March of 1972 he met the love of his life, Barbara Billington… a feeling that was not entirely mutual. After waiting a year to try again, chance had reunited them back together. Gordon wanted to get married on the 4th of July, but Barb convinced him to wait, tying their future to the arrival of Comet Kohoutek, declaring that if they were still together when it passed in December 1973, they would marry.

They were. So, on December 28, 1973, he took Barbara to the courthouse and made it official. (The comet, famously underwhelming. The marriage, not.)

Gordon spent many years working as a maintenance mechanic at Aspirus Wausau Hospital—back when it was Saint Mary's and Memorial Hospital, and later as it became Aspirus. When the new facility was being built, Gordon worked third shift and took full advantage of the freedom that comes with it, occasionally riding a skateboard through the halls—because sometimes efficiency looks like momentum, and sometimes it just looks like fun.

Although he was not a doctor, Gordon delivered all four of his children at home—working at the hospital, he had a general sense of how things worked and saw no need to call for backup.

He worked overtime when needed—picking up factory labor and other jobs along the way—but work was never what defined him.

What did define him, at least in part, was a deep and enduring love of history and politics. Gordon had a remarkable ability to produce obscure facts at exactly the right (or wrong) moment—earning him, in family circles, a reputation not unlike Cliff Clavin. Whether you asked or not, he likely had an answer—and if he didn't, he had a theory.

He loved motorcycling and boating, though his boating skills were… ambitious. Backing up a trailer remained a lifelong challenge, and at least one outing ended with a near miss and the confirmed loss of an iPhone.

On a family trip to the Green River in Colorado, Gordon found himself caught in the current. Assessing the situation, he calmly waved to his family and called out, "It's been nice knowing you." His family, unwilling to accept this conclusion, shouted instructions from shore until Barbara delivered the most effective one: "Mike, you're a Boy Scout—save your father." With some effort, a tree branch, and a renewed commitment to staying alive, Gordon made it out.

In 2006, Gordon was diagnosed with Parkinson's disease. The progression was long, but his response never changed. He did not complain. He did not dwell. He kept going.

He is survived by his wife, Barbara, and their four children: Anza D'Antonio (husband Jason) and their children Kara McLaughlin (husband Sean, 2 children Lilah and Liam), Mario, Dorthea, and Valentina; Michael Lowe (wife Heidi) and their sons Torin and Thomas; Paula Lowe (daughter Matilda); Francine Askew (fiancé Chris DeJoie) and her children Isaiah and Evangeline Askew. He is also survived by a very naughty cat named Bobby, who remains unapologetic.

Gordon had an unwavering faith—not loud or performative, but steady. It shaped how he lived, how he endured, and how he loved. Through the long progression of Parkinson's, it gave him peace that did not depend on circumstances.

He believed in God, trusted in His promises, and lived his life with the quiet assurance that this world was not the end of the story.

That faith is the inheritance he leaves behind—carried forward in the lives of his children and grandchildren.

And now, having finished his race, he is home.

A Celebration of Life will be held for Gordon Saturday May 2nd at Trails End Bar from 1:00-3:00 pm.

records.name: Janet L. Falkowski
records.summary: Janet L. Falkowski, 86, of Ringle, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on April 25, 2026.
records.body:
Janet L. Falkowski, 86, Ringle, passed away peacefully on Saturday, April 25, 2026 at her home.

She was born September 5, 1939 in Wausau, daughter of the late John and Marion (Prahl) Dittbrender. On May 25, 1963, she married Joseph "Joey" Falkowski in Wausau. He preceded her in death on November 4, 2005.

Janet began her career working at the Brokaw Paper Mill and then began working in the blueprint room for Drott Manufacturing in Schofield, which became J.I. Case. After the plant closure, Janet transferred to their plant in Fargo, ND, until her retirement. She then moved back home and took a part time job in the mail room at Marshfield Clinic in Wausau where she worked for 17 years.

Janet truly enjoyed traveling and would not hesitate to go on a trip with family or friends whenever she had the opportunity. She took several trips to Alaska with her cousin Faye to visit family, went on bus tours on the east coast, and always enjoyed traveling back to North Dakota any chance she had, especially with her special friend, Debbie.

Janet was well known for her ability to sew. She sewed many wedding dresses, dresses for bride maids, made curtains for Ethan Allen, made her own line of cabbage patch kids outfits, baby bibs and made many clothing items for her family. She also helped two generations of 4-H kids with sewing and crafting at her home, a "hobby" she truly enjoyed. She was always adventuring into a new sewing project and used her sewing machine regularly up until her last days. She will be forever known for sewing patches on "everyone's pants". Janet also enjoyed quilting, making many baby quilts using the quilting frames she got from her grandma.

Janet was a social person and very well known. It was hard to take her somewhere and not run into someone that knew her. Her smile and quick wit was enjoyed by all. Wednesdays were her favorite days. She waited for her midday call to confirm her 3:00 p.m. pick up to travel to Kluck's Bar for pizza, beer and sign in. Janet was always the life of the party.

She loved spending time with her children, grandchildren and great-grandchildren. She always made time to watch their sporting events, dance recitals, musicals, plays, concerts and taking the grandchildren to music lessons. She also attended the Wisconsin Valley Fair to support her grandchildren in the animal barns and events and in her younger years she was elected the Wisconsin Valley Fair Farm Bureau Queen.

She will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved her.

Survivors include three children, Karen (Monty Behreandt) Frederick and her family, Kullen Patterson, Kamron Vogt and Keela Coppersmith and her children Liam, Caleb and Sadie, Allen Falkowski, and Leon (Jennifer) Falkowski and their children, Hunter and Alex; four siblings, Petie (Millie) Dittbrender, Ted Dittbrender, Linda (Randy) Ostrowski and June Dittbrender; sister-in-law, Donna Dittbrender; and many nieces and nephews.

Besides her parents and husband, Joey, she was preceded in death by three brothers, Gary, Roger and Sport Dittbrender.

Funeral services will be held at 1:00 p.m. on Wednesday, May 6, 2026 at St. John Lutheran Church, 164923 County Road Z, Wausau (Town of Easton). Rev. John Stransky will officiate. Burial will be in the church cemetery. Visitation will be on Tuesday from 4:00 until 8:00 p.m. at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau and again on Wednesday from 11:00 a.m. until time of services at the church.

records.name: Joseph M. Horban
records.summary: Joseph M. Horban, 72, of Schofield, Wisconsin, passed away on April 27, 2026.
records.body:
Joseph "Joe" Michael Horban, age 72, of Schofield, WI, passed away on Monday, April 27, 2026, at Pine Meadows Assisted Living in Schofield, while under the care of Compassus Hospice. He was born on October 29, 1953 in Chicago, IL and was the son to the late John C. and Patricia E. (Pappas) Horban. He married Bonnie Gorichs on June 24, 1978 in Medford, WI. She survives.

When he was younger, Joe enjoyed going fishing and for bike rides. He was a collector of various knick knacks and loved being around dogs and horses! He was a talented musician with the concertina and the accordion. Joe had a heart of gold and enjoyed helping people in his community, especially the elderly, and he loved making people laugh. He was a kind-hearted man that will be dearly missed by all who knew him.

Joe leaves behind his wife, Bonnie, brother: John (Diane) Horban Jr. and sister: Renee Zeske. He was preceded in death by his parents, brother: Jonathan Horban and sister: Sandra Horban.

The memorial service for Joe will be held on Tuesday, May 5, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. at Helke Funeral Home, 302 Spruce Street, Wausau, WI 54401. Pastor Rebecca Borke will officiate. Visitation will be held on Tuesday, May 5, 2026 from 9:00 a.m. until the time of the service, also at the funeral home.

records.name: Reuben 'Duke' Frahm
records.summary: Reuben 'Duke' Frahm, 95, of Ringle, Wisconsin, died on April 25, 2026.
records.body:
Reuben 'Duke' Frahm, 95, of Ringle, died on Saturday, April 25, 2026, at Aspirus Hospice House in Wausau.

Duke was born on December 4, 1930, in the Town of Bern, the son of George Sr. and Anna (Klein) Frahm.

On November 3, 1956, Duke was united in marriage to Myrna Ullenbrauck at Our Savior's Lutheran Church, Wausau.

Duke was a United States Army Veteran and a member of the Ringle-Hatley VFW Post #8342.

Duke worked as a carpenter for various companies. He enjoyed deer hunting and woodworking, often making bird feeders and bird houses.

Duke is survived by his wife, Myrna; two sons, Mark (Kathy) Frahm and Michael Frahm; three grandchildren, Heather (Devon) Harms, Jacob (Jessica) Frahm and Brittany (Alex Radtke) Frahm; two great-grandchildren, Carson Harms and Hunter Frahm.

Duke was preceded in death by his parents; daughter in infancy, Diane Marie; brothers, John "Hunts", Marvin "Sons", LeRoy "Bobby", Orville "Toots", George "Ki", Harold "Bip", Leonard Sr. "Bups", and one brother in infancy, Arthur; sisters; Dorothy "Ditty" and Florabell "Honey".

A Funeral Service will be held at 11 AM on Wednesday, May 6, 2026, at Grace Evangelical Lutheran Church in Ringle. Rev. James Mattek will officiate. Burial will be in Evergreen Rest Cemetery in Elderton. Visitation will be on Wednesday, from 9AM until the time of the service at the church.

records.name: Richard R. Thayer Sr.
records.summary: Richard R. Thayer Sr., 87, of Birnamwood, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on April 26, 2026.
records.body:
Richard R. Thayer Sr., 87 of Birnamwood, passed away peacefully on Sunday, April 26, 2026, at Oak Park Place, Menasha.

Richard was born on September 9, 1938, in Milwaukee, the son of Tom Sr. and Harriet (Jerzakowski) Thayer.

Richard married Delores Stanke on September 3, 1960, at St. William's Catholic Church in Eland, celebrating 65 years together.

Richard was a 1957 graduate of Wittenberg High School. He dedicated 37½ years to Kimberly-Clark, working as a papermaker and foreman. He also proudly served in the Army Reserve for 24 years, retiring with the rank of Master Sergeant. Richard received the Milwaukee Journal Award for Outstanding Soldier. In 2016, he was honored to participate in the Never Forgotten Honor Flight. After retirement, he volunteered with the DNR as a hunter safety instructor. He loved playing Santa every year for his friends' families and especially for his own grandchildren and great grandchildren.

Richard is survived by his wife, Delores; son, Rick (Carla) Thayer; daughter, Pam (Dusty) Canoy; daughter-in-law, Mary Beth (Chris) Brandl; grandchildren, Kayla (Josh) Tursky, Brittany (Dan) Paulson, Brandon (Allison) Thayer, Grant (Courtney) Thayer and Tyrell (Brittany) Reiman; great grandchildren, Oliviana and Noah Tursky, Liam and Brooks Paulson and Sophie and Cullen Thayer, and sister, Beverly 'Sis' Stock.

Richard was preceded in death by his parents; son, Dennis Thayer; three brothers, Orville (Kay) Thayer, Don (Jan) Raddatz and Tom (Jane) Thayer and a brother-in-law, John Stock.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11 AM, on Monday, May 11, 2026, at St. Florian's Catholic Church, Hatley. Rev. Jerome Patric will preside. Inurnment will be in Greenlawn Memorial Park, Neenah. Visitation will be on Monday, from 9 AM until the time of Mass at the church. Following the Mass, military rites will be conducted by the Darling-Gunderson Post #341, American Legion, in the church parking lot.

The family would like to extend their deepest gratitude to the compassionate staff at Oak Park Place and Bristol Hospice for their exceptional care and support during Richard's final days.

records.name: Ronald L. Westcott
records.summary: Ronald L. Westcott, 88, of Edgar, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on April 26, 2026.
records.body:
Ronald (Ron) L. Westcott, 88, of Edgar, WI, passed away peacefully on April 26, 2026, surrounded by his loving family, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

Ron was born on July 11, 1937 in Wausau, to the late Daniel and Veronica Westcott. Ron served in the US Navy and Naval Reserves for 8 years and was a Gunner's Mate, Third Class, stationed on the U.S.S. Helena and served in the Korean War. He participated in the Honor Flight in 2022.

Ron worked as a union plumber and pipefitter and has been a proud member of UA Local 434 for over 65 years. He worked on many large-scale projects across Wisconsin. Ron was a dedicated, hard worker and made lifelong friends during his career.

He is survived by his wife, Mary Ann (Heldt); his beloved children, Tracey, Troy (Renee), Trent (Sheila), Travis, and Tammy, whom he shared with his first wife Joan Fearson, half sibling to Ron's children, Karlan (Dana) Dietsche, and step children, Tammy (Russell) Westfall and Ricky (Troy Moore) Heldt; grandchildren, Julien, Chelsea (Greg), Abbi (Zach), Eli (Journey), Christopher (Ali), and Allen; great grandchildren; nieces and nephews; and great nieces and nephews, and siblings Patricia Suman and Tim (Clarice). Ron was preceded in death by his granddaughter, Nora, nephew Logan, his parents, and cherished siblings Mike (Karla), Jaqueline Hustedt, and Richard (Darlene).

Ron and Mary Ann were married December 19, 1992 and enjoyed over 33 years of marriage. They met at the Labor Temple, where Mary Ann was the secretary for the Plumbers and Pipefitters Union. He asked her out for a drink and the rest is history. They traveled extensively after their retirements and visited England, France, and many areas in the U.S. Ron and Mary Ann spent wonderful times together, spending time with family and friends and later spending quiet evenings at home playing rummy. They shared a bottle of champagne on every holiday.

Ron loved and valued his family more than anything. He was an excellent host and loved to entertain, everyone was always welcome. Ron's family have wonderful memories of holidays at his house. His 4th of July celebrations were legendary with huge firework displays, great food, friends, and of course, family. He was an intelligent and charismatic man, who had the best laugh. Ron recently said, "I probably ate too much, drank too much, and smoked too much, but I've had a hell of a good time".

He enjoyed hunting, fishing, and wood working. He just completed 9 birdhouses for a park in Milwaukee. Ron loved to read, particularity westerns by Louis L'Amour, a fire in the backyard, and his nightly Kessler's and water.

Ron was a devoted father, grandfather, husband, brother, and uncle, who will be dearly missed. In honor of him, love your family, host over the top holiday celebrations, and always remember to have a good time.

A funeral service will be held Saturday, May 2, at Trinity Lutheran Church, 501 Stewart Ave., Wausau. Visitation will be from 10 am-11am with a service at 11 am. Lunch to follow the service. Burial will take place at Zion Lutheran (St. John's) Cemetery, Little Chicago.

records.name: Tyrone Harris
records.summary: Tyrone Harris, 63, passed away on April 22, 2026.
records.body:
Tyrone Harris (Ty, Bone) 63 passed away 4/22. He faced serious health problems this past fall but was on the road to recovery, making his passing all the more unexpected and deeply felt.

Tyrone was a quiet presence, someone who didn't seek attention but was always there in the background, when it mattered most. He had a steady, dependable nature and calm spirit. He had a gift for the simple things including grilling-his chicken was especially well-loved by family and friends. He was mechanically inclined and took pride in helping keep cars running, fixing what he could.

Though he was born and raised in Chicago, he found his own way of appreciating the outdoors and came to accept a more 'country' way of life. Enjoying fishing, watching fireworks from the boat, and walking Punkin in Marathon Park.

He also enjoyed spending time at a local bar, sharing laughs, betting in local sporting pools, and playing radio contests hoping to win it big someday—but those who knew him would say he already had with his wife of 32 years, Michelle. Tyrone also shared a special bond with Punkin, their beloved Beagle. In recent years he came to warmly accept the presence of dogs in his life, often helping Michelle babysit pets for family. Most recently Diesel who repaid his kindness by sleeping on his feet. Both dogs brought comfort, companionship and lasting joy to his life.

Tyrone is survived by his wife Michelle, two daughters JoJo (Wausau) and Quita, grandchildren Jalen, Jeremiah, Jasmine, and Jordon (Buddha). Brothers Chad, Ron, and Reggie (all of Chicago). He is proceeded in death by siblings Charles, Ed, Wanda and son TJ.

Tyrone's life was defined not by loud moments but by loyalty, love and presence. His final gift was being an organ donor. A selfless gift that reflects the quit generosity he showed throughout his life. He will be deeply missed and forever remembered.

records.name: Wade B. Johnson
records.summary: Wade B. Johnson, 36, of Minocqua, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly on April 24, 2026.
records.body:
Wade B. Johnson, 36, of Minocqua, passed away unexpectedly on April 24, 2026. He was born on November 13, 1989, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to Bruce and Gail (Schlais) Johnson.

Wade graduated from Wausau East High School in 2008. Following high school, he attended Fox Valley Technical College to pursue a career as a lineman. He later worked as an apprentice lineman for MDU in North Dakota and also earned certification as a service fitter. Wade recently returned to Wisconsin, where he was employed by Togstad Construction, working as a directional borer installing underground fiber optics.

Wade had a kind and generous soul and made friends easily wherever he went. He was a dedicated and hardworking individual who never hesitated to lend a hand to anyone in need. Whether helping family, friends, or neighbors, Wade was always there when called upon.

He cherished deer hunting with his father every November and enjoyed spending time fishing with his father and grandfather, especially during their annual fishing trips. Wade also loved rucking and taking long walks with his beloved dogs, Hammy and Stella. He adored his two youngest nephews, Wyatt and Noah, and treasured the time he spent with them—teaching them new things and listening to their stories.

Wade is survived by his parents, Bruce and Gail Johnson of Minocqua; his grandmothers, Grace Schlais of Medford and Margaret Johnson of Wausau; his sisters, Tara Hubbard of Ogema and Melissa (Dan) Fenske of Wausau; and his nephews, Brandon (Hayley) Kariger of Elk Mound, Ethan (Mikaela) Kariger of Rothschild, Isaiah and Kai Hubbard of Ogema, and Wyatt and Noah Fenske of Wausau.

He was preceded in death by his grandfathers, Galen Schlais and LeRoy Johnson.

A Funeral Service for Wade will be held at 11:00am on Saturday, May 9, 2026, at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg. Rev. Maggie Westaby will officiate. Visitation will be held on Saturday beginning at 9:00am until the time of service. Burial will be held at a later date in Evergreen Rest Cemetery, Elderon.

records.name: Leonard Kawleski
records.summary: Leonard "Lenny" Kawleski, 67, passed away at home on April 5, 2026.
records.body:
Leonard "Lenny" Kawleski, 67, passed away at home on April 5, 2026.

Lenny found joy in life's simple pleasures. He enjoyed golfing, reading, and woodworking, and especially valued the time he spent with family and friends. Whether on the golf course or around a cribbage board, he shared laughter, stories, and companionship with those around him.

He will be remembered for his love of golf and cribbage, as well as the easygoing spirit he brought into every room.

In accordance with Lenny's wishes, no funeral service will be held.

records.name: Loretta Robl
records.summary: Loretta Robl, 72, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly on April 19, 2026.
records.body:
Loretta Robl, age 72, of Weston, WI, passed away unexpectedly on 04/19/2026.

Loretta was born at her family's home in Hatley, WI, on 08/25/1953 to Jennifer and John Oleinik. She was the youngest of nine children.

Loretta had a passion in life for cooking and baking. She owned and ran Mom's Cafe in Schofield, WI, for 14 years. Her friends and family always looked forward to any meal she was preparing.

Loretta loved finding new art and craft projects to do with her granddaughter, Malanyah, as she was growing up. In the summer, you could find her outside gardening or inside frying up the fish her husband Jeff caught. Loretta also enjoyed fishing. When her son Justin was growing up, their favorite thing to do was go fishing. As he got older, they loved getting together to try new recipes.

Loretta had a son, Justin (Jeni) Oleinik, and their daughter Malanyah.

She also had a son-in-law, Ross (Tara) Robl, and their two children, Ryland and Elizabeth, and a stepdaughter, Renee Robl.

She is survived by her husband, Jeff Robl; sister, Rose Gusman; and brothers, Vern and Thomas Oleinik.

Her family will miss her terribly; the world just isn't the same without her hugs and laughter.

There will be a celebration of life to honor Loretta at a later time.

records.name: Marvin R. Kroening
records.funeral_home: Brainard Funeral Home and Cremation Center
records.summary: Marvin R. Kroening, 91, passed away on April 27, 2026.
records.body:
Marvin R. Kroening, age 91, passed away on Monday, April 27, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born on March 14, 1935, in Stratford, Wisconsin, to Emil and Eleanor (Pember) Kroening. Marvin graduated from Stratford High School in 1953 and attended the University of Wisconsin-Madison from 1953 to 1954. He enlisted in the U.S. Air Force in 1954 and, after completing basic training, attended the Army Language School in Monterey, California, where he graduated as a Korean linguist.

Marvin served in Korea from 1956 on the island of Pyong Young Do, located 125 miles north of the DMZ, 5 miles offshore, for one year. From 1957 to 1958, he was with Security Services in the U.S. Air Force in San Antonio, Texas. After his military service, Marvin returned to school, attending the University of Wisconsin-Marathon County campus in the spring of 1959 and the fall semester at UW-Madison. Following his education, Marvin began a long and successful career with Hoffer Glass Company in Wausau, Wisconsin.

In 1960, Marvin married Judith Ulrich. They shared a life full of love along with having three children. They were married 66 years. He is remembered fondly for his kindness, generosity, dedication and love to his family and friends.

Marvin is survived by his loving wife, Judy; children, Brian (Lisa) Kroening, Kurt (Sue) Kroening and Sheri (Mark) Pankow; grandchildren, Sam, Nicholas, Ellie (Kroening), Aaron, Austin, Marissa (Mohr) and Jesse (Williams) great-grandchildren, Everett, Violet (Hammond) and sister, Marjorie (Dennis) Redetzke. He is further survived by many nieces, nephews, family, and friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Emil and Eleanor (Pember) Kroening; his parents-in-law, Arnold and Marie Ulrich and brother Donny.

A memorial service will be held at 3:00 PM on Thursday, May 7, 2026, at Brainard Funeral Home and Cremation Center, 522 Adams Street, Wausau, Wisconsin, with Pastor Jack Busche officiating. Family and friends are invited to greet the family for a time of visitation from 1:30 PM until the time of the service at the Funeral Home. Military honors will follow the service.

records.name: Paul D. Jones
records.funeral_home: Helke Funeral Home & Cremation Services of Wausau
records.summary: Paul D. Jones, 81, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on March 6, 2026.
records.body:
Paul Dean Jones, 81, of Wausau, Wisconsin — passed away on March 6, 2026. He was born on June 10, 1944, in Dubuque, Iowa.

Paul loved the friends he made throughout the Northwoods community. Paul's connections extended throughout many paths, including LandArt, a business he owned with his brother, Tom. Paul also contributed greatly through his involvement with the Marathon Jail project, the Monk Gardens, and the Wausau Chamber of Commerce. Though Paul's most cherished friendships came through hunting at the "Trout Pond" and his time on the golf course.

Paul will be remembered for his loyalty, generosity, and love of Liberty. He is survived by his Wife, Deborah Miller; Sons Chris (Lala) and Brian (Sherry) and their families; Brother Tom Jones (Anne); Sister Doris Wilson (Jim); and his beloved cat, Irwin.

A Celebration of Paul's Life will be held in Door County, Wisconsin, on June 20, 2026.

records.name: Robert D. Schwartz
records.funeral_home: Brainard Funeral Home & Cremation Center of Weston
records.summary: Robert "Bob" Schwartz, 88, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on April 24, 2026.
records.body:
Robert "Bob" Schwartz, 88, passed away peacefully with his loving wife and children by his side the morning of April 24, 2026.

Bob was fortunate to have lived a very full, meaningful life and was a man who truly exemplified the "work hard, play hard" mentality. The youngest of four children, Bob was born on May 16, 1937 to Lillian and Frank Schwartz, where his strong work ethic began to take shape on his family's farm in Neva, Wisconsin. There, he kept many animals and loved to stop at the cheese factory for curds on the two-mile walk home from school with his siblings. At just 13 years old, Bob started drumming for a local polka band his brother Gene played trumpet in, and by the time he graduated from Antigo High School in 1955, Bob was playing drums and tuba (sometimes simultaneously!) with The Neva Band, playing popular local dance jobs. Fitting then, that his first date with his wife of 69 years, Mona, was to a polka dance. Their courtship started with a note in study hall, but Mona wasn't yet allowed to date. After graduation, Bob mailed her a postcard from his travels out west, and when he returned stopped by the dime store where Mona worked to finally ask her out. After a year of dating, Bob bought a diamond ring and stashed it in his aunt's refrigerator for safekeeping until he proposed on Christmas Day in 1956. After they married on April 13, 1957, the couple lived in Milwaukee and had three children: Dee Dee, Tamee, and Robert Jr.

Bob spent eight years in the Army Reserves, while working for Allen-Bradley and UPS as a mechanic and trainer. Then he was promoted to supervisor and the family moved to Weston in 1964. He continued playing in polka bands, most notably with The Jolly Gentlemen and later with the Lee Gardner Band and the Firemen Five, touring around the state and often bringing his family along to polka shows and parades on weekends. In 1970, Bob started as Branch Manager — then later, as District Manager — for Auto Glass Specialists until his retirement in 1999.

While Bob worked hard throughout his career, he was equally dedicated to making the most of his time off. The family took many trips around the country and to the Bahamas, and spent countless hours entertaining and relaxing around their backyard pool. Bob and Mona also enjoyed many fun trips with Bob's brother Gene and his wife Pat over the years. In 1984, Bob and Mona bought land and — with the help of family and friends — built a cabin, which became the homebase for many decades of cross-country ski parties, hunting camps, and times to just wander the woods and enjoy the great outdoors. Bob always loved to keep busy and work with the land. He cut and shaved the log banisters and beams in the cabin, built six log beds by hand, and built a 20-foot garden bridge for one of his daughters. He loved teaching his kids and grandkids how to hunt, how to use tools, and how to respect the land. Everyone looked forward to his cabin cooking, like his famous potatoes that would simmer in a cast iron kettle over a campfire for hours, but his biggest specialty was sweet corn. Bob became known as The Corn Man, taking his old half-barrel rig to all kinds of events where he'd shuck hundreds of steamed ears with his big leather mitts and dunk them in melted butter before finishing them off with salt. The kids would often have contests to see who could eat the most.

Bob also enjoyed helping his family cook maple syrup, cold-smoke venison sausage, and going on fishing trips out on Lake Michigan. Bob and Mona always had a blast on their trips to casinos — especially their favorite, North Star Mohican Casino in Bowler — where the lucky duo won many jackpots. They even had a slot machine in their basement that was a source of fun for many generations growing up.

Bob loved making people laugh; he always had perfect timing with a joke, or a clever prank up his sleeve. On Christmas, he couldn't wait to present a perfect gag gift, and his bellowing laugh always filled the room. He also had a knack for knowing how to relate to anyone he spoke with. He was always able to find a connection, and make anyone he encountered feel comfortable, which is just one of the many reasons he will be sorely missed, but forever remembered, in this life.

Bob is survived by his loving wife of 69 years, Mona Schwartz; his three children, Dee Dee (Bob) Jahnke, Tamee (Dick) Wanta, and Bob Jr. (Laura Vogel-Schwartz); his nine grandchildren, Scott (Coretta) Jahnke, Craig (Annette) Jahnke, Briana Rupel, Christopher Rupel, Keith (Patti) Wanta, Jason (Angela) Wanta, Adrianna (Brett) Leach, Alexander (fiancé Brittany) Schwartz, and Ryan Schwartz; his six great-grandchildren, Trevor (Kayla) Jahnke, Jennica (Zack) Back, Zachary (Kassandra) Jahnke, Miranda (Nolan) Rodriguez, Jack Wanta, and Lillian Leach; his seven great-great-grandchildren, Nora & Elena Jahnke, Lakyn Back, Raelyn & Madilyn Jahnke, and Lane & Evelyn Rodriguez; his sisters-in-law, Patricia Schwartz, Betty Pizl, Yvonne (Lloyd) Svacina, Vickie (Wenzel) Husnick, and many nieces and nephews.

Bob is preceded in death by his parents, Frank & Lillian; his siblings, Norman, Helen Jean (Tom) Thomae, and Eugene; his sister-in-law, Donna Debroux; and his brothers-in-law, Clarence Debroux and Duane Pizl.

Bob's family would like to extend special thanks to the fourth-floor staff at Marshfield Medical Center in Weston, the Rennes Health & Rehab 200-wing staff, and Compassus Hospice for their care and compassion during Bob's final weeks.

Per Bob's wishes, there will be a private celebration of life held at a later date.

In lieu of flowers, the family asks that memorial donations be made instead to the Michael J. Fox Foundation for Parkinson's Research. Donations can be made in Bob's name at www.give.michaeljfox.org.

records.name: Roman Michalski
records.summary: Roman Michalski, of Milwaukee, Wisconsin, passed away on April 26, 2026.
records.body:
Roman Michalski, of Milwaukee, Wisconsin, passed away on April 26, 2026, and leaves behind a legacy of kindness, humor, and unwavering devotion to his family.

Born in Milwaukee on February 19, 1943, to the late Alois and Stella (Pirog) Michalski, Roman lived a life grounded in love, resilience, and appreciation for the simple joys. He was a devoted father to Russell, Jeffrey, and Daniel, and a proud grandfather to Jon, Megan, Jacob, Christopher, and Justin.

Roman found great happiness in fishing, watching ballroom dancing, and playing cards—especially sheepshead and cribbage. During his sons' younger years, he rarely missed an opportunity to attend their Lake band events, offering enthusiastic support and encouragement from the sidelines. He truly enjoyed spending time fishing with all three of his sons, most especially Daniel. As they grew into adulthood, he took immense pride in their military service and in all they accomplished, never missing a chance to express his admiration.

Known for his forgiving nature, Roman had a remarkable ability to find the positive in any situation. His warm, sometimes mischievous sense of humor and his generous heart left a lasting impression on everyone who knew him.

He will be deeply missed and lovingly remembered for his lighthearted spirit and enduring compassion.

Roman is survived by his sons, Russell and Jeffrey (Toni) Michalski; his grandchildren, Jon Michalski, Megan (Taylor) Austin, Jacob Michalski, and Christopher Michalski; his brother, Walter (Sandy) Michalski; brothers-in-law, Tom Peterman and Ron Peterson; and his sister-in-law, Roberta Michalski.

He was preceded in death by his beloved wife, Mary; his son Daniel; his brother, Robert; grandson Justin, daughter-in-law, Elizabeth; and his sisters-in-law, Ann Peterson and Audrey Peterman.

His memory will live on in the hearts of his family and all who were fortunate to know him.

A celebration of Roman's life will be held at 12:00 pm on Saturday, May 16, 2026, at St. Romans Catholic Church, 1710 Bolivar Ave, Milwaukee, WI 53221. Visitation will take place from 11:00 am until the time of services at the church.

records.name: Arlyce E. Drews
records.summary: Arlyce E. Drews, 92, of the town of Easton, passed away on May 3, 2026.
records.body:
Arlyce E. Drews, 92, town of Easton, passed away on Sunday, May 3, 2026, at Rennes Health and Rehab, Weston, under the care of Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services.

She was born in Mattoon on November 1, 1933, to the late Raymond and Doris (Salvesen) LeGault. Arlyce was united in marriage to Warren Drews at Bethany Lutheran Church, Aniwa, on May 7, 1955.

Arlyce worked for many years, until her retirement, as a Teacher's Aide at the elementary schools in the Wausau School District. She also loved and took care of her family. She was involved in church activities including the Library Guild and Vacation Bible School at Zion Lutheran Church. She enjoyed working with children and they adored her. Arlyce enjoyed cooking, baking, and reading.

Survivors include her children, Cheryl (Robert) Phillips and Scott Drews; grandson, Jarod Drews and his partner, Jodi Stroad; great-grandson, Carter; and sisters-in-law, Althea Drews and Elaine Drews. She was preceded in death by her husband, Warren; parents, Raymond and Doris LeGault; sisters, Phyllis Jolin and Shirley Nowak; brother, Lynn LeGault; granddaughter, Chelsie Marie Drews; brother-in-law, Arnold Drews Jr.; sister-in-law, Juliet Drews; father-in-law, Arnold Drews Sr.; and mother-in-law, Erna (Pagel) Drews.

The funeral service will be at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, May 16, 2026, at Zion Lutheran Church, 238145 Star Road, Aniwa (Town of Easton) with Rev. Jim Mayland officiating. A luncheon follows the service. Burial will be in Restlawn Memorial Park. Visitation will begin on Friday, May 15 at 4:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. and continue on Saturday at 10:00 a.m. until the time of service, all at the church.

records.name: Austin D. Olson
records.summary: Austin D. Olson, 21, of Little Black, passed away on April 28, 2026.
records.body:
Austin Davis Olson, age 21, of Little Black, passed away unexpectedly at his home on Tuesday, April 28, 2026.

Austin was born on June 3, 2004, in Merrill Wisconsin to Todd Olson and Jennifer Hanson. He graduated from Medford Senior High School in 2022. Following graduation, Austin continued working at Gold Diggers Scrap & Salvage until November 2022 before pursuing a career in farming at Pine Creek Dairy, where he found joy in hard work and life on the farm.

Austin was a true gearhead who loved anything involving wrenching, working on vehicles, tractors, and four-wheeling. He enjoyed spending time outdoors, especially around a campfire surrounded by family and friends. Above all, Austin cherished being a father and loved spending time with his son, Bennett.

Austin will be deeply missed by his father, Todd (Stacy) Olson of Medford; his mother, Jennifer Hanson of Eau Claire; his siblings, James (Erica) Madden of Merrill, Robert Brannen of Wausau, Erin Kraemer of Medford, Cody Hamm of Medford, Sara (Elijah) Blume of Medford, Alex Olson (McKenzie Spalo) of Stevens Point, Collin Turonie of Stevens Point, Jordan Olson of Medford, and Jayce Olson of Medford; and his beloved son, Bennett Olson of Eau Claire. He is also survived by his nieces and nephews: Carter, Vayda, Oaklyn, Luke, Walter, and Tucker.

Austin was preceded in death by his grandparents, Mary Kraemer, Don and Linda Sparby.

Austin's kind heart, sense of humor, and love for his family and friends will be forever remembered by all who knew and loved him.

A celebration of life will be held at 3:00PM on Saturday, June 6, 2026, at Free Spirit of Medford. A benefit ride will take place at 10:00AM on Saturday, June 6, 2026, leaving from Free Spirit of Medford. N7382 Hwy 13, Medford, WI.

records.name: David D. Yenter
records.summary: David D. Yenter, 69, of Mosinee, passed away on April 3, 2026.
records.body:
David Darrell Yenter, 69, of Mosinee, passed away at home unexpectedly on April 3, 2026.

Dave was born on January 23, 1957, to David E. Yenter and Jeanette (Groshek) Wegner in Milwaukee.

He was preceded in death by his parents.

David is survived by his sister, Julie (Mike) Stroik; his niece, Wendy (fiancé Andrew Johnson) Stroik; his great-nephew, Calum; and many aunts, uncles, cousins, and friends.

David lived life on his own terms and was truly one of a kind. He had a deep love for rock 'n' roll and a natural musical talent, playing both the trumpet and piano. He will be remembered for his unique spirit and the wide circle of friends he built from all walks of life.

A celebration of David's life will be held on May 30, 2026, at 12:00 pm for family at Gorski's, 450 Orbiting Drive, Mosinee. Father Marion Talaga will officiate. Another Celebration of Life will be held at Hodges Place 2, on May 17, 2026, from 2:00 pm until 6:00 pm.

In lieu of flowers, donations may be made to NAMI (National Alliance on Mental Illness) or to Prevent Suicide, Marathon County.

records.name: Deb Wanta
records.summary: Deb Wanta, 72, of Bevent, passed away on May 1, 2026.
records.body:
Deb Wanta, age 72, of Bevent, WI passed away peacefully on the morning of May 1, 2026, after a courageous seven-month battle with pancreatic cancer. She was surrounded by the love of her family and her devoted husband of 54 years, Ron, this August.

Deb's life was filled with love, laughter, and a deep commitment to those around her. She cherished spending time with Ron, whether running the bar, dabbing cards at bingo, sharing laughter over card games, or tackling projects together. She found joy in the pages of a good book, creating lasting memories with her grandchildren, and enjoying a turtle sundae. In her younger years, Deb was known for her skills as a pool shark, her prowess on the softball field, her ringers in the pits, ripping it up on three wheelers, canning chaos, and her sharp-shooting in trap. Her infectious laughter still echoes across the waters from memorable fishing trips with her beloved aunts and uncles. After retiring from the Post Office with some dear friends made, she had more time to enjoy her hobbies.

Deb is survived by her husband, Ron; her children, Clay (Kurtis), Clint (Gail), and Heidi; her siblings, Chris (Gale), Jimmy (Diana), Lynn (Steve), Lisa Wanta, Ben Z., and Teri Tranetzke; her grandchildren, Isaac, Cade, Evan, Willem, Matilda, Benton, Atlas; and one great-grandchild, Jett. Deb was preceded in death by her parents, Benedict Ziolkowski and Gladys (Yenter); her siblings, Lori (Dale) Burazin, Cory Glenda Z., Brian (Pam) Z.; and nephew and godchild, Derek Kizewski.

Debbie always put the needs of family and friends above her own. Kind, caring, considerate, and of course, funny, Deb's cheerful spirit and warm hospitality were unmatched. Her memory will be cherished by all who knew her.

A special thank you to the staff at Aspirus Hospice House and Ascend Funeral Home for the heartfelt ways they've helped during this journey.

To honor Deb, the family will be hosting a Celebration of Life on Saturday, June 13th at Family Tradition Bar in Bevent beginning at 2:00 PM. In the spirit of her remembrance, you are invited to bring a dish to share as we commemorate Debs' life.

records.name: Elaine M. Dabler
records.summary: Elaine M. Dabler, 81, of Rothschild, passed away on May 2, 2026.
records.body:
Elaine M. Dabler, 81, passed away peacefully at home on May 2, 2026 surrounded by her loving family and under the gentle care of Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services.

Born on May 13, 1944, to the late John and Marjorie (Gavitt) Gusman, Elaine's life was defined by her devotion to those she loved. On July 3, 1965, she married the love of her life, Orville Dabler. Together, they built a life in Rothschild, where Elaine dedicated herself to raising their three children. Once her children were grown, she shared her friendly nature with the community as a teller for Connexus Credit Union.

In retirement, Elaine and Orville embraced the "snowbird" lifestyle, traveling in their fifth-wheel camper to Ocklawaha, Florida, to escape the Wisconsin winters. Following Orville's passing, Elaine found peace and happiness living on the lake, where she cherished quiet afternoons on the pontoon.

Known for her generous spirit, Elaine delighted in "spoiling" her friends and family. Whether it was through a thoughtful gift or a kind word, she made sure those around her felt valued. She was a lifelong lover of dogs and found great companionship in her "furry children" over the years. Above all, she was a kind, loving matriarch who treasured every moment spent with her children, grandchildren, and extended family and friends.

She is survived by:
Children: Robert (Tedra), Kris (Todd) Opal, and John (Brita).
Grandchildren: Jenna (Casey), Austin (Kelsey), Asha (Rylee), and Augie.
Great-Granddaughter: Nola Grace.
Furry Companion: Luna.
Extended Family: Many beloved nieces and nephews.

Elaine was preceded in death by her parents, her husband, Orville; her brothers, Robert, John "Jack," and Gary; her nephew, Jonathon; and her beloved dog, Lola.

Private family services will be held to honor Elaine's life.

records.name: Jennifer S. Moran
records.summary: Jennifer S. Moran, 50, of Wausau, passed away on April 30, 2026.
records.body:
Jennifer Streff Moran, lovingly known as Jennie, born on October 8, 1975, in Canby, Minnesota, passed away on April 30, 2026, in Wausau, Wisconsin. She was 50 years old.

Jennie spent her early years on her family farm in Canby, where her love for the outdoors took root. She graduated from Canby High School in 1994 and continued her education at the University of Minnesota Morris, earning a degree in communications. Throughout high school and college, she was an active choir member and often performed solo or in ensembles with her beautiful alto voice. On her birthday, October 8, 1999, in Minneapolis she met the love of her life, Tim. They were married two years later on October 19, 2001. After spending a few years in Cleveland, they eventually settled in Wausau where they raised their two daughters, Natalie and Alison.

Growing up on a hog farm was probably the trigger that led to her life-long love of gettin' dirty and working with her hands. It makes sense then, that Jennie found immense joy working in her garden, planting flowers, and mowing the lawn. Every Thursday for the past eight years, rain or shine, you could find her doing all things farming at Red Door Family Farm where she was considered family. Jennie enjoyed cooking seasonally from her CSA and believed in sustainability. This conscientiousness led her to find The Good News Project, Inc where she spent her Fridays supporting their e-CYCLE for electronics recycling program. Further putting her hands to work, Jennie found great satisfaction "working with her guys" at Habitat for Humanity on Tuesdays where she would intake drop off items such as microwaves, tools and light fixtures, and then clean them to verify that they were working safely before being donated.

Jennie was passionate about health and fitness, especially if the activity brought her outdoors. She was comfortable being alone but never turned down an opportunity to workout with a friend. Jennie was also a long-time member of The Wausau Curling Center, where she ran their Learn to Curl program for many years. Many of Wausau's finest curlers curl because of Jennie. She was a master thrifter and her friends were often the lucky benefactors of her keen eye, finding thrifted treasures waiting for them on their doorstep.

Above all, family was the center of Jennie's world. Her daughters were her pride and joy.
