Cheering them on at swimming, traveling with them, and experiencing the simplest moments at home made her the happiest. She took tremendous pride in watching her daughters grow into loving, compassionate, independent women.

Jennie is survived by her husband, Tim, and their two daughters, Natalie and Alison. She also leaves behind her parents, Chally and Chuck Streff; her sister Erin Streff, and Erin's husband Tumaini with their children Musa, Noel, and Ila; her in-laws John and Jan Moran; her brother-in-law Chris Moran and his wife Shannon with their children Roman, Maxwell, and Rose; and her sister-in-law Julie Moran.

Jennie will be remembered for her beautiful smile and quick wit. Her legacy is one of compassion, bravery and unwavering devotion to her family, friends and community. Jennie's presence brought light and laughter to those around her. She will be deeply missed. Good Curling, Jennie!

A Celebration of Life will be held on May 9, 2026, from 2:30 PM to 5:30 PM at The Wausau Curling Center, 1920 Curling Way, Wausau, Wisconsin.

In lieu of flowers, please consider supporting the organizations that Jennie held close to her heart, Habitat for Humanity and Good News Project INC. As well, consider supporting Red Door Family Farm by signing up for their CSA or stopping by their stand at the Wausau Farmers Market. Tell them Jennie sent you.

records.name: Julia A. Magrecke
records.summary: Julia A. Magrecke, 84, of Wausau, passed away on May 3, 2026.
records.body:
Julia Ann Magrecke (née McCarten and then Nelson) passed away peacefully on May 3rd, 2026. She was surrounded by all of her children.

Julia was born to Edgar and Sophia McCarten on June 28th, 1941, in Wausau, Wisconsin. Her father had been a barber; her mother, a beautician, whose salon occupied the opposite side of the same room as Edgar's barber shop. Julia worked there, too, as a beautician in her youth giving patrons "permanents."

Julia received her elementary education at Saint James Catholic School, later attending Wausau High School from which she graduated in 1959. She was a popular child for her sense of humor – an inheritance from her father, who, being a barber, enjoyed access to limitless zingers. Her ebullient spirit, natural athleticism, and heavily ingrained work ethic made Julia an excellent cheerleader (though it is quite possible she might have enjoyed a different sport had one been available to girls at the time).

Julia had many passions. She was a lifelong angler, having spent much of her childhood fishing with her father and growing up on the shores of Lake Wausau and the Rib River. She was a talented cook, whose urge to feed friends and family overruled whether they were actually hungry or not. She was a great fan of British comedies and crime dramas, as well as Jeopardy! (She would never decline a phone call from a loved one, though the caller in question could always sense Julia's vague air of vexation if they were interrupting Trebek o'clock.) She grew up a devout Catholic, and held only slightly less devotion to the Packers and the Badgers. She enjoyed her career at Marathon Electric, where she began working after having been a stay-at-home mom. She also enjoyed gambling and playing cards, and, more recently, bingo which made her many friends at the senior living community where she spent her final years.

But more than anything, Julia loved to laugh. If you knew her, then you can surely imagine her laughter as you read this. Keep that thought with you. Cherish it. It is a laugh that will ring out no longer save for in our hearts.

Julia was preceded in death by her parents, first husband Charles Nelson (with whom she had five children), second husband Roger Magrecke; and her siblings Michael McCarten, Elizabeth (McCarten) Michlig, and John McCarten. She is mourned in death by her children, Thomas Nelson, John Nelson (wife Suzie Nelson), Joe Nelson (wife Jennifer Nelson), Jennifer Nelson, and Shannon Nelson-Lee (husband John Lee); grandchildren Nicole Lahti, Katelyn Leher, Evelyn Nelson, Hayley Nelson, Madeline Nelson, Michael Nelson, Olivia Nelson, Sarah Nelson, Heidi Schmidt-DesJarlais, and Jenna Slaby; Mary Lou McCarten (wife of sibling, John McCarten), and her darling cat Boo-Boo.

A Memorial service will be held on Saturday, May 9th, 2026, at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau, WI 54403. Visitation hours are 1:00 to 3:00 PM, followed by an open invitation for anyone to share stories about Julia's life. Catered food will be provided.

records.name: Rita J. Stieber
records.summary: Rita J. Stieber, 96, of Weston, passed away on May 2, 2026.
records.body:
Rita J. Stieber, 96, passed away Saturday, May 2, 2026, under the care of St. Croix Hospice at Pride TLC Therapy & Living Campus, Weston.

She was born March 25, 1930, in Marathon, daughter of the late Elmer and Eleanor (Erdmann) Leitermann. On July 1, 1950, she married Charles Stieber at St. Mary Catholic Church, Marathon and he preceded her in death on January 5, 2015.

Rita dedicated over 40 years to volunteering at St. Anthony Spirituality Center. She was an active member of the Lay Franciscans and the PCCW and served St. Mary Catholic Church in Marathon as a choir member, lay minister, cafeteria worker and playground supervisor. Some of her hobbies included bowling (state tournament), ceramics, and embroidery. Rita and Charles loved traveling in their retirement. They traveled all over the U.S. and Canada, enjoying an Alaskan cruise on one of their trips. They also visited Europe with fellow farmers, checking out how German farming methods were different from ours.

Survivors include her children, Cathy (Dave) Joswiak, Huntley, Illinois; Fran (Ed) Flanders, Rothschild; Lynette (Fran) Heil, Rib Mountain; Don (Lynn) Stieber, Marathon; grandchildren, Joel (Laura) Stieber, Greg (Katrina) Stieber, Dianne (Paul) Leffel, Nicole (Chris Daniels) Joswiak, Stephannie (Nathan) Parris, Nathan (Cat Paccasassi) Joswiak, Becky Flanders, Chad Flanders, Kelly (Jason) Schaeffer, Amy (Mike Fleishmann) Peter, David (Telia) Peter, Ben (Lisa) Heil and Adam (Hannah) Heil; 18 great-grandchildren; sister, Charlotte Seubert; sister-in-law, Henrietta Stieber; many nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends.

Besides her parents and husband, Charles, she was preceded in death by a grandson, Myron Stieber, brother and sister-in-law, Allan (Marcy) Leitermann, brothers-in-law, Joseph Seubert Jr., James Stieber and Jerome (Mary) Stieber.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated 11:30 a.m. Saturday, May 9, 2026, at St. Mary Catholic Church, Marathon. Rev. Msgr. Joseph Diermeier will preside. Burial will be in the parish cemetery. Visitation will be Saturday from 9:30 a.m. until the time of service all at St. Mary Catholic Church, Marathon.

records.name: Wayne R. Spulak
records.summary: Wayne R. Spulak, 71, of Wausau, passed away on May 1, 2026.
records.body:
Wayne Ray Spulak, 71, Wausau, was called to heaven on Friday May 1, 2026, surrounded by his family at home.

He was born November 27, 1954, in Wausau, son of the late Walter and Gertrude (Sulstrom) Spulak.

He enjoyed the simple things in life, tinkering in his garage, working on his vehicles, trimming his lawn and watching sports, especially the Green Bay Packers and Wisconsin Badgers. His children were taught at a young age when a game was on you don't talk to him, unless there was a commercial and then you better be quick. Holidays with his family, where board games were a must, and blasting his music loud enough you couldn't hear yourself think. He always said that's how it reaches your soul and heals what's burdening you. Black Sabbath was a constant in his house.

At the age of 18 he started working at Norlen Incorporated until he retired at the age of 65. He was proud of the fact that he was able to stay with the company for 47 years. He had a strong work ethic and instilled in his children and grandchildren that hard work builds character and a healthy mind.

Survivors include his children, Amber (Billy) Kasat, Wausau; Justin (Heather) Spulak, Stevens Point; grandchildren, Logan (Nikki), Ariel, Blaze Kasat and Levi Spulak; great-grandchildren, Kamryn, Lukas and Oaklee Kasat; Siblings, Gary (Jackie) Spulak, Weston; Cheryl Wunderlich, Germantown.

Besides his parents, he is proceeded in death by a brother Joseph who passed away at birth and a brother-in-law Keith Wunderlich.

There will be a Celebration of Life on Saturday May 30, 2026 from 1p-4p at the Eagle's Club, 1703 South Third Avenue, Wausau, WI. 54401

The family would like to thank Dr. Peterson and his amazing staff at the Aspirus Cancer Center, as well as the wonderful Aspirus Hospice team for all their wonderful care, guidance and reassurance.

records.name: Amanda N. Lehman
records.summary: Amanda N. Lehman, 86, of Ringle, died on May 5, 2026.
records.body:
Amanda was born in Hartville, OH on May 8, 1939; she died on May 5, 2026, in Ringle, WI, in her home, surrounded by her family. She was married to the late John A. Lehman. The couple lived in Middlebury IN, Belle Center OH, and Ringle WI.

Amanda loved the Lord and was zealous about trying to help others to love Him also. She made a commitment to follow Jesus for as long as she lived. She was a good wife, mother, grandmother and great grandmother. She was an excellent homemaker, teacher, and advisor.

Amanda is survived by her children: Nathan (Barbara) Lehman, James (Ida) Lehman, Marjorie Lehman, John Jr (Susan) Lehman, Titus Lehman, Dorothy Lehman, Marlin (Gina) Lehman, Kenneth (Megan) Lehman, Elmer (Lindsey) Lehman and Lydia Ann (Shane) Holt, 35 grandchildren and 29 great grandchildren, and Casey Patton, Scott Patton & Kylie Kitson-Shoemaker as extended family. Sibling: Joe & Mary Coblentz, in-laws: Anna Coblentz, Laura Coblentz, Rosanna Coblentz, Laura Mast and Emma & Ben Troyer.

Preceded in death: Her husband, John A. Lehman, her parents Noah & Lydia Ann Coblentz, step-mother Anna Mast-Coblentz, siblings Anna Mary & John Detweiler, Orin & Vera Coblentz, Dan & Elsie Coblentz, Esther & Wilmer King, Earl & Sarah Coblentz, Emma & Atlee Miller, John Henry Coblentz, Vernon Coblentz, Nettie & Noah Coblentz, Roy Coblentz, Eva, Fannie Mast, Edna Mast, Levi Mast, John Mast.

Visitation will be held from 4:00 P.M. to 7:00 P.M. Thursday, May 7, 2026, at the Cedar Creek Hotel & Conference Center, 1100 Imperial Ave, Rothschild, WI 54474. Funeral Services for Amanda will be at 9:30 A.M., on Friday, May 8, 2026, at the Cedar Creek Hotel and Conference Center with a final viewing following the service. Burial will take place in Forestville Cemetery, 171006 County Road N, Ringle, Wisconsin. Pallbearers will be her sons Nathan, James, John Jr, Titus, Marlin, Kenneth, & Elmer. Lunch will be served at Cedar Creek following the burial. Everyone is welcome.

records.name: Brad R. Weber
records.summary: Brad R. Weber, 61, passed away on May 3, 2026.
records.body:
He was born August 5, 1964, to the late Dale and Charlotte Weber.

He is survived by his daughter, Brittney (Alec) Repic and son, Bradley Weber; three grandchildren, Xander Repic, Silas Weber and Kaya Weber; longtime companion, Kim Weber. He is further survived by his six brothers and sisters.

Besides his parents, he was preceded in death by a sister, Diane Louze.

A celebration of life will be planned at a later date.

records.name: Clara A. Guenther
records.summary: Clara A. Guenther, 94, was welcomed into the arms of Jesus on May 3, 2026.
records.body:
She was born on July 22, 1931, in Brokaw, WI, the daughter of the late Glen and Selma Campbell. Clara and the late Allan Guenther were married for 64 years. As a great grandma she obtained her GED. She enjoyed sharing her Christian faith. She was very patriotic. Clara's hobbies included making handicrafts, watching sports on TV, and keeping up with her daughters, grandchildren, and great grandchildren. Clara kept her calendar full of social events. Her friends described her as always positive and spunky.

Clara is survived by 6 daughters: Linda (Lyle), Cherryl (John deceased), Pamela (Thomas deceased), Brenda (Mike), Sheila (Scott), and Lisa (Mike). She is further survived by 11 grandchildren, 18 great grandchildren, a brother Jerry (Dolly), many nieces and nephews.

Besides her parents and her husband, she was preceded in death by a son: Steven, sisters: Helen and Betty, brothers: Glen Jr. and Clifford, and a grandchild: Andrew.

A Celebration of Clara's life will be held on Thursday, May 14, 2026.

All events will be held at:
Highland Community Church
Weston Campus
6615 County Road J
Schofield, WI 54476
715-842-5683
Open House 3:00 pm – 4:00 pm
Memorial Service 4:00 pm – 5:00 pm
Open House 5:00 pm – 6:00 pm

The family is very grateful for the love and support given to her throughout her entire life. In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to the church in Clara's name for the Vacation Bible School Program. Please make checks payable to: Highland Community Church, 1005 N 28th Avenue, Wausau, WI 54401-2498.

records.name: Diane C. Connor
records.funeral_home: Brainard Funeral Home & Cremation Center of Weston
records.summary: Diane C. Connor passed away in 2026.
records.body:
Diane Caroline Connor was born on December 19, 1955, in Greenville, Wisconsin, to Richard and Virginia Peters. The middle child of nine, Diane grew up on a farm where she learned the values of hard work, humility, and quiet strength—qualities beautifully modeled by her mother, who remained her greatest support throughout her life.

Diane met the love of her life, Bill Connor, on a blind date, and they were married in July of 1977. Together, they quickly grew their family, welcoming four children—three daughters, Kessa, Jonah, and Holly, and finally a son, Judd.

Diane's heart's desire was always to be a stay-at-home mother, a role she fulfilled with exceptional devotion and grace. When a brain injury left Bill unable to work, Diane courageously stepped into the role of provider for her family. She carried this responsibility with perseverance and without complaint. It was during this difficult season that Diane came to know Jesus personally—a relationship that became the foundation of her strength and endurance.

Throughout her life, Diane faithfully modeled kindness, patience, gentleness, and unwavering faith. Like her mother, she was quiet in spirit, yet her life radiated compassion and love. She loved Jesus deeply, and she loved others exceptionally well. Her children and grandchildren were her whole world, and she would set aside anything to be there for them at any moment.

Even during her most difficult battle with cancer, Diane continually returned to the words of Scripture, often repeating:

"Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to Him, and He will make your paths straight." —Proverbs 3:5–6

Diane's deepest desire, even in her death, was that the gospel be proclaimed. She would want everyone reading this to know what it means to have a personal relationship with Jesus Christ—to declare Him as Lord and to live a life that honors and glorifies Him.

Diane was preceded in death by her parents, Richard and Virginia Peters; her in-laws, Gene and Jean Connor; her brother, Mark Peters; her sister, Kay Tiffin; her brother-in-law, Richard Pompa; her sister-in-law, Donna Peters; her nephew, Joseph Peters; and her niece, Bobbi Jo Bain Connor.

She is survived by her beloved husband, William Connor; her children, Kessa (Chris) Erickson, Jonah (Brad) Kosmalski, Holly (Craig) Petersen, and Judd Connor; and her eight treasured grandchildren: Austin, Antek, Davis, Eli, Sami, Jace, Calla, and Brennan.

Her legacy of faith, love, sacrifice, and quiet strength lives on through all who were blessed to know her.

records.name: Judith M. Paszek
records.summary: Judith M. Paszek, 78, of Wausau, passed away on May 5, 2026.
records.body:
Judy was born on October 9, 1947, to Paul and Margaret Loehmer of Tomahawk. On February 10, 1968, she married Claude Paszek. They went on to raise their three children in the house they built and loved in the town of Stettin. Gardening was one of Judy's favorite things to do. She was very proud of her flower gardens and loved puttering around in the yard.

Judy worked for many years at Citizens Bank and then went on to work at various other customer service jobs.

After the kids were grown and her husband retired, Claude and Judy went traveling all over the US and into Canada. Camping was another one of her favorite activities. They would even take their grandkids along just for the fun of it.

Judy was known for her stubbornness and doing things her way. She was also not very graceful, as all the trips to the ER in her lifetime can tell you. She was nice enough to hand those two qualities down to all the generations that followed her. She was definitely one of a kind and was loved by everyone.

Judy is survived by her children: Paula (John) Gavinski, Bevent, Brian Paszek, Gilbert, AZ, and Leann Ninabuck, Edgar; grandchildren: Ashley (Justin), Raquel (Matt), Cody, Michaela, Alexandra, Christopher (CJ), Lillie and Adelyn and their mother, Ann; 12 great-grandchildren and 2 great-great-grandchildren; her older brother, Tom Loehmer; her youngest sister, Chris (Himey) Kincaid and their son Nick; and assorted cousins and friends.

She was preceded in death by her husband Claude, her younger sister Pat, her granddaughter Courtney, her sister-in-law Louise and Louise's sons Kirk and Chad, her niece Val, and her dogs Penny, Tasha, and Lady Jane.

Thank you to all of the staff at Primrose who made her feel loved and to all of the staff with Moments Hospice who made her last few days as comfortable as possible.

Mom, even though Alzheimer's and dementia robbed you of your golden years, you were still golden to us. We're sure you could hear us when we were spending the last few days with you and you were probably laughing right along with us or shaking your head at us. We love you and miss you and look forward to seeing you and Dad again in the future when both of you will be happy and healthy.

Funeral services will be at 11:00 a.m. on Monday, May 11, 2026 at Saint Andrew Lutheran Church, Rib Mountain. Rev. Jack Busche will officiate. Burial will be in Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau. Visitation will be on Sunday from 4:00 until 6:00 p.m. at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Avenue in Wausau and again on Monday from 10:00 a.m. until time of services at the church.

records.name: Kathy K. Wistrom
records.summary: Kathy K. Wistrom, beloved mother, grandmother, sister, mentor, and friend, passed away on May 6, 2026.
records.body:
Born on March 3, 1954, to Fred and Virginia (Tietz) Zastrow, Kathy lived a life centered on family, community, and her passion for youth baseball.

After graduating from high school, Kathy met the love of her life, Gary Wistrom. The two were married in 1974 and built a wonderful life together, raising their children and later cherishing every moment spent with their grandchildren.

For more than 20 years, Kathy and Gary dedicated themselves to the Wausau Area Youth Baseball program. While Gary coached, Kathy became the heart of the league, helping run the organization and supporting countless young athletes and families. Beyond her own family, Little League Baseball was Kathys greatest joy. She loved coaching and encouraging children, cheering them on through their years in the league, and making an impact on their lives that lasted well into adulthood. Her dedication and service earned her the honor of being inducted into the Little League Hall of Fame.

In later years, Kathy especially enjoyed spending time with her family, watching birds from home, and creating lasting memories with her children and grandchildren.

Kathy is survived by her children, Erika (Dan) Staffeil, Travis (Shaina) Wistrom, and Ryan (Megan) Wistrom; her cherished grandchildren, Cole, Jenna, Brooke, Klaire, Gracie, Ember, Bria, Chanelle, Julian, and Natalee; her mother, Virginia Zastrow; siblings, Linda Schroeder, Donna Kufahl, Vicki Detert, Elroy (Sharon) Zastrow, and Arlen Zastrow; along with many nieces, nephews, extended family members, and dear friends.

She was preceded in death by her beloved husband, Gary; her father, Fred; brothers, Wayne Zastrow and Garry(Janet) Zastrow; brothers-in-law, Dick Schroeder and Bruce Kufahl; and her sister, Robin Zastrow.

A visitation will take place on Monday, May 11, 2026, from 3:00 p.m. until 6:00 p.m. at John J. Buettgen Funeral Home, 948 Grand Avenue, Schofield, Wisconsin.

Kathy will be remembered for her generous heart, unwavering support of others, and the countless lives she touched both on and off the baseball field. Her legacy of kindness, dedication, and love for family and community will live on in all who knew her.

records.name: Loren D. Sickler
records.summary: Loren D. Sickler, 76, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on May 3, 2026.
records.body:
Loren was born on January 10, 1950, in Wausau, Wisconsin, the son of Delbert and Marion (Gavitt) Sickler. A proud veteran, Loren served honorably in the United States Air Force, a commitment that exemplified his sense of duty and patriotism. His service was a cornerstone of his identity, reflecting the values of honor, discipline, and loyalty that guided his life.

Throughout his years, Loren enjoyed simple yet fulfilling hobbies that brought joy and relaxation. He was known among friends and family for his skill and enthusiasm in playing pool, a pastime that often-gathered loved ones around for friendly competition and shared laughter. In his earlier years, he found great pleasure in hunting and fishing, embracing the outdoors with a spirit of adventure and respect for nature. Additionally, Loren had an affinity for scratch-off tickets, adding a lighthearted thrill to his daily routines.

Loren is survived by his sisters Sheila Rebernick and Cheryl (Jim) Buttke, who cherished him dearly. He was preceded in death by his parents, his sisters Merna (Dave) Kleman, Joan (Sid) Hunter, Dona (David) Skrzypchak, and his brother-in-law James Rebernick, whose memories he carried with him always.

He will be deeply missed and fondly remembered by all who had the privilege of knowing him.

Per Loren's wishes, no formal services will be held.

records.name: Richard J. King
records.summary: Richard J. King, 71, of Rothschild, passed away peacefully on May 5, 2026.
records.body:
Rick was born in Milwaukee, WI in September, 1954 to Alfred and Elenore King.

Rick attended James Madison High School in Milwaukee, but felt his learning would be best served through travel and proceeded to hitchhike (because you could in 1972!) 'Down South'. He quickly discovered that he needed to find work in order to eat and found a variety of work along the way – baling hay, picking oranges, running carnival rides (sleeping quarters were in the Merry-Go-Round horse truck), and ended up in Job Corps in North Carolina. There he learned to cook and was very proud of his culinary skills – that is, until one unfortunate meal of "barbeque eggs" – which were pink and really inedible, but has provided lots of laughs since. He eventually traveled back to Milwaukee and his second night in town met a nursing student at a neighborhood bar. The second date, he informed her "I'm going to marry you!" She was a bit more than doubtful, but a brief 8-months later, his prediction came true. Rick married the "love of his life" Mary (Klemp) in August, 1974. A daughter, Tracy – the "second love of my life" was born the following year.

Rick worked as a machine operator for most of his life starting at Marathon Electric, then in Daytona Beach and Phoenix, Arizona. He loved all types of sports and could cite stats and scores better than anyone and is counted among the diehard Green Bay Packers fans. He loved telling stories and could have the room in hysterics with his descriptions of events or situations. He loved date nights with Mary – this was often simply having a few beers on the patio or in the garage – as evidenced by the "Rick's Bar & Garage" neon sign – a Father's Day gift from Tracy. He loved classic Rock & Roll – preferably at full volume, and his morning routine was to put on his earbuds, turn on some tunes, have a cup (or 4!) of coffee and a "few" cigarettes. He put a bench he made on the backyard patio and that was his favorite spot to carry out his morning routine along with enjoying his backyard and all the nature. He was always in awe that deer and turkeys were in his own backyard and seemed to have a special relationship with a chipmunk (he named "Alvin") who would often come and sit at his feet. Phone calls with his mom were always started with a cheerful greeting in Polish.

He loved playing 'scratchers' and discussing the finer points with his lifelong friend/'brother', Preston Brock. Rick never met a stranger and frequently we heard "your husband/dad/brother is quite the character!" meaning that in the best way possible because he was welcoming to all and was often the life of the party. He loved going out to eat – especially the Palms Supper Club where he enjoyed the friendship and fun with all the staff.

Most of all he loved his family. He often said he was the "luckiest man in the world" and forever blessed with his wife and daughter. He will be more than deeply missed in their lives and those of his family and friends.

Rick is survived by his wife, Mary, Rothschild; daughter, Tracy King (Jason Steele), Fort Lauderdale, FL; his mother, Elenore King; sisters, Mary Giustso and Susan King; and brother, John King all of Florida; sister and brother-in-law, Zona and Joel Lemanski, Marathon, WI; and extended family that includes nephews, a niece, and a great niece. He is also survived by his canine companion, Coco and grand-dog, Otis. Rick considered his lifelong friend, Preston Brock as his brother along with the 'sisters-in-law' Ruth Brock and Mary Collins. In their family he was affectionately known as 'Rick Brock'.

Rick was preceded in death by his father, Alfred King; grandparents; and several aunts, uncles, and cousins.

Rick had distinct ideas as to how he wanted to live his life, and he had very firm beliefs in how he should be cared for at end-of-life. Beginning with Kathy Weix-Klippel, NP at Marshfield Clinic – Merrill who helped him to realize his wishes with a consult to Compassus and Apollo Senior Care – Palliative Care. Jessie Ryan-Geiger, NP supported his wishes for a year and did her level best to make him as comfortable as possible. Our gratitude is beyond measure for their care and support for his journey on his terms. The transition to hospice care was seamless and we wish to express our deepest appreciation and thanks to his Compassus family who have been with us these past several months. The nurses, especially Hank, RN and Chaplain Mark who provided Rick with spiritual care – he looked forward to every visit. And, Tia, CNA who quickly became his "angel". Your care and compassion are forever treasured.

A graveside service will be held at 11:00 A.M., Wednesday, May 27, 2026, at Merrill Memorial Park Cemetery. Chaplain Mark Lewis will officiate.

Rick loved his rescue pups. In lieu of flowers, please consider a donation to the local Humane Society or a dog rescue organization.

records.name: Robert J. Redmann
records.summary: Robert J. Redmann, 87, passed away on April 6, 2026.
records.body:
Bobby was Born on November 11, 1938, at home in Edgar, to Hazel & Fred Redmann. Bobby grew up surrounded by family and friends. Bobby served his country in the United States Marine Corp as a Corporal and was discharged in1958. Bobby worked at several jobs with local businesses, Carl Johnson, Johnson Silo, Joe Burger, Stratford Builders and Marve Holbach, Holbach Concrete. Bobby was especially proud of the work he completed and often spoke of the different barns, silos etc. that he helped build. Bobby was an avid reader, he enjoyed collecting books, coins, and Jim Beam decanters and even emptied a few. Bubu lu also enjoyed socializing down at the bar with friends and neighbors.

Bobby was preceded in death by his sisters and brothers, Annetta Behrendt, Betty Jahnke, Charles, Fred (Fritz), Ernest, Hazel (Sis), and Frank (Bud).

Bobby is survived by his sister-in-law, Germaine Redmann, numerous nieces and nephews, and a wide circle of friends.

Everyone who knows Bobby will remember him fondly.

Bobby received excellent care at the Colby Colonial Center. Thank you to the wonderful staff for their exceptional care, humor, and patience with his expressive personality.

A memorial & internment service with military honors will be held June 6, 2026, at Athens Cemetery at 2:00 p.m. Family and friends are welcome to attend.

In lieu of flowers, the family requests that donations be made to the Athens WI VFW in Bobby's memory. Bobby will be deeply missed and always remembered.

records.name: Ronald E. Buedding
records.summary: Ronald E. Buedding, 82, of Wausau, passed away on May 1, 2026.
records.body:
Ronald "Ron" Buedding passed away May 1, 2026, following a courageous battle with cancer.

Born in Wausau, Wisconsin, on February 28, 1944, Ron was the son of Wilbert and Leona (Sowinski) Buedding. After graduating from Wausau High in 1962, he proudly served four years in the U.S. Navy, including a two-year assignment in Scotland. After returning home, Ron married Jean Fehl on April 8, 1967. They recently celebrated their 59th wedding anniversary. Ron was employed at Wausau Papers for 37 years before his retirement.

Ron was a true Wisconsinite and embraced the four seasons. During daylight hours, you would rarely find him indoors. Being an outdoor enthusiast, Ron especially enjoyed riding his bike, walking, fishing, and perfectly manicuring his yard. The birds never went hungry, and the snow hardly had a chance to touch the ground with Ron around. Some of Ron's happiest moments were spent relaxing "up north," where he shared endless stories and laughter with his family. He also loved reading, doing crossword puzzles, and his morning coffee with dear friends.

Ron is lovingly remembered by his wife, Jean "Jeanne" Buedding; his son, Dan (Tara) Buedding; his daughter, Heather (Kirk) Polkinghorne, and his granddaughter, Norah Polkinghorne; his siblings, Dennis (Nancy) Buedding, Mike Buedding, Bob (Anne) Buedding, Sue Beck, Randy (Kathy) Buedding, and Becky Felver; his in-laws, Vicki Buedding, Judy (Dennis) Jehn, Mary (Joe) Spychalla, and Mary Fehl; and many nieces and nephews.

Ron is now reunited with his parents; his brother, John Buedding; his sister, Lori Buedding; his father and mother-in-law, John and Verna Fehl; and his brothers-in-law, Jeff Felver, Bill Beck, and John Fehl.

A celebration of life will be held on Thursday, May 14, 2026, from 10:00 AM until 12:00 PM at Brainard Funeral Home Wausau Chapel (522 Adams Street, Wausau, WI 54403)

A heartfelt thank you to the Aspirus Cancer Center and the Oncology and Palliative Care Staff at Aspirus, whose kindness and compassion meant more than words can say.

records.name: Siegfried K. Lenk
records.summary: Siegfried K. Lenk, 76, of Wausau, passed away on May 5, 2026.
records.body:
He was born on June 20, 1949, in Gottingen, Germany, son of the late Friedrich "Fritz" and Lieselotte "Lottie" (Kretchmer) Lenk. Sieg served in the United States Air Force from 1972 until 1977.

Survivors include his wife of 57 years, Kay (Madson) Lenk of Wausau; his brother, Wolfgang Lenk of Merrill; sisters-in-law, Linda (Kim) Clouse of Rapid River, MI, and Bunny (John) Sjogren of Gladstone, MI; brother-in-law, Dan Sullivan of Merrill, and many nieces and nephews.

In addition to his parents, he was further preceded in death by his mother and father-in law, Jim and Sylvia Madson; and sisters-in-law, Nancy Sullivan and Moni Lenk.

A celebration of his life will be held at a later date. In lieu of flowers, memorials may be given to the Humane Society.

records.name: Willard "Will" Nyquist
records.summary: Willard "Will" Nyquist, 24, died on May 3, 2026.
records.body:
Will was born December 2, 2001, to Jeffery and Angie (Berth) Nyquist. He thrived when they moved to the family farm where he grew up with a deep appreciation for hard work, family, and the outdoors.

After graduating high school, Will attended UW–Platteville. During his time there, he became an active member of the Baja Club, where he applied his passion for using his hands to build things. He took great pride in hauling the dune buggies across the country to compete in events, always eager for the next challenge.

Following college, Will began working for Servpro, a job he truly loved. He often said, "Every day is a great day for restoration," a reflection of both his work ethic and his outlook on life. He was a hands-on, gritty individual, who found satisfaction in helping people. There was no job too dirty… except for that one freezer…

Will's first vehicle was a truck that he loved and threw himself into learning how to fix. Fixing, building and creating brought him great satisfaction. Other favorite projects included his fishing boat and his grandfather's Allis-Chalmers tractor. He loved his motorcycle and the sense of freedom it gave him on country roads.

He enjoyed deer hunting, paintball, target shooting and hanging out with his buddies—anything that allowed him to get a little dirty and enjoy the outdoors.

Will had a generous heart, and was always ready to lend a hand, especially to his parents and grandparents. He was looking forward to standing beside his brother as Best Man, a role his family knows he would have filled with humor, sincerity, and a perfectly off-the-cuff speech.

Above all, Will was grounded in his faith and is now free in the presence of His Savior, Jesus Christ.

Will is survived by his loving parents, Jeff and Angie Nyquist; his brother, John (fiancée Abigail); his maternal grandparents, Chuck and Gail; his paternal grandmother, Gail; as well as many aunts, uncles, cousins, friends, and his beloved furry friend, Harper.

He was preceded in death by his grandfather, Steve, and his loyal companion, Benny.

Will is fondly remembered for being gritty, quick to help, gentle, tenacious, a man of few words, even tempered, even keeled, steady, unflappable, full of integrity, and having high character. His presence, strong hands, kind heart, and unwavering willingness to help others will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved him.

Will died doing what he loved, with his boots on.

Visitation will be at 2 PM on Sunday, May 17, with a service to follow at 3 PM at Woodlands Church (190 Hoover Ave., Plover, WI 54467). Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family. Words of remembrance may be shared at MWCS.WS

In lieu of flowers, tax-deductible gifts in honor of Will can be given to the Fort Wilderness Stables Project. Will enjoyed many adventures he experienced at this year-round Christian camp. To give by mail: "Fort Wilderness" / Will Nyquist Memorial, Box 715, McNaughton WI 54543, 715.277.2587,
https://fortwilderness.givevirtuous.org/donate/memorials

records.name: Willis W. Allmandinger
records.summary: Willis W. Allmandinger, 96, passed away in Wausau on May 1, 2026.
records.body:
Will was born in Fort Wayne, Indiana in 1929. He attended Southside High School, and went onto to attend Purdue University. Will served in the US Army during the Korean War. Will went to work in banking at Lincoln National in Fort Wayne, IN, First National Bank in Neenah, WI as a Vice President in Trust and Estate Planning on to M&I Bank in Marshfield before retiring.

Will was preceded in death by his parents Herman and Zelma Allmandinger, and daughter Amy Allmandinger. He is survived by his son Byron.

Will has been known to friends and acquaintances as an engaging, compassionate, individual who viewed life with optimism, hope, his strong faith and thankfulness.

Donations can be made to St. Vincent DePaul or Habitat for Humanity in Will's name.

An informal celebration service will be held at a later time.

records.name: Daniel P. Strehlow
records.summary: Daniel P. Strehlow, age 71, of Weston, passed away peacefully on May 7, 2026.
records.body:
Daniel P. Strehlow, 71, of Weston, passed away peacefully on Thursday, May 7, 2026, at the Hospice House in Wausau, surrounded by the love of his family.

Dan was born on November 15, 1954, in Wausau, the son of Gerald and Joyce (Heller) Strehlow. He spent his life in the area he proudly called home. On May 14, 1977, he was united in marriage to Margaret Johnson at Holy Name Catholic Church in Wausau. Together they shared nearly 49 years of marriage.

For many years, Dan was a familiar and respected presence on the basketball courts of the Wausau area as a referee. His love of the game extended beyond the whistle—he was a longtime member of the YMCA, where he enjoyed noon basketball games, workouts and visiting with friends.

Dan was an avid sports fan and enjoyed watching the Brewers, Packers, Bucks, and Badgers games. Him and Margaret could often be found rooting for the home team on the sidelines of local high school basketball and football games.

Dan is survived by his loving wife, Margaret Strehlow of Weston; his children, Shawn (Meagan) Strehlow, and their children, Owen and Sydney. Ryan (Jackie) Strehlow and their children, Rae Ann, Jordon, and Jemma. He is further survived by his siblings, Randy Strehlow of Superior, Steve (Linda) Strehlow of Rib Mountain, and Karen (Chris) Kyle of Rib Mountain; as well as many nieces and nephews who will remember him fondly.

Dan is also survived by his in-laws: Don and Jan Johnson, Richard and Bonnie Johnson, David and Carol Johnson, Mary and Tom Schepp, Sharon and Gary Boushley, and Rose LeMay, all of whom held a special place in his life.

He was preceded in death by his parents and his sister-in-law, Cheryl Strehlow.

A Memorial Mass will be held at 10:30 a.m. Thursday, May 14, 2026 at St. Michael Catholic Church, Wausau with the Rev. Tom Lindner presiding. Burial will be at Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Kronenwetter. Visitation will be held on Thursday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service.

records.name: Jeanette M. Riehle
records.summary: Jeanette M. Riehle, age 84, formerly of Wausau, WI, died peacefully on May 6, 2026, at Strake Court Assisted Living in Conroe, TX.
records.body:
Jeanette M. Riehle 84, formerly of Wausau, WI, died peacefully on Wednesday May 6, 2026, at Strake Court Assisted Living in Conroe, TX.

She was born June 8, 1941, in Athens, WI, the only child of Arthur and Adela (Miller) Nowicki. Part of a multi-generational musical family, she loved playing piano accordion in the family band and giving lessons to locals. She graduated in 1959 from Athens High School and held several secretarial positions in Wausau. On July 17, 1964, she married Bernard A. Riehle at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens. Jeanette co-founded and managed their business, Riehle Plumbing, Heating and Electric, until they divorced in 1982.

In 1983 Jeanette moved with her daughter, Tiffany, to Houston, TX, where she tirelessly raised her, engaging in various activities. She worked for many years as a dedicated and skilled administrative assistant in healthcare. Jeanette retired from the big city to quieter life in Conroe to pursue interests in needlecrafts, scrapbooking, and fishing. She also loved music, cars, reading, and baking cookies with her granddaughter, Kaylin!

Jeanette lived her life with strong determination, fierce independence, and tenacious love of family, taking great pride in their successes.

Survivors include her daughter, Tiffany Colarusso, son-in-law, Todd Colarusso, and granddaughter, Kaylin Colarusso, of Atlanta, GA.

Besides her parents, she was preceded in death by her ex-husband, Bernard, on May 21, 2024.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 10:30 a.m. Thursday, May 14, 2026 at Sacred Heart Catholic Church, Conroe, Texas. Visitation will be held one hour prior to the Mass beginning at 9:30 a.m. at the church. Jeanette will be laid to rest at Restlawn Memorial Park in Wausau, WI at a later time.

records.name: Mark E. Lyon
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Mark E. Lyon passed away unexpectedly on May 6, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center in Weston, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Mark E. Lyon, passed away unexpectedly on May 6, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center in Weston, Wisconsin.

He was born on January 26, 1948, in Wausau, Wisconsin, son of the late Lewis and Loretta (Stieber) Lyon. He was married to Barb (Tratz) Lyon for 27 years until her passing in 2016.

Mark's military service is a distinguished chapter of his life. He began his honorable service in 1967, by joining the United States Marines during the Vietnam Era. Throughout his tenure, he earned several significant accolades, including the Combat Action Ribbon, National Defense Service Medal, Vietnam Service Medal with Star, and the Vietnam Campaign Medal. Following his time in the Marines, Mark continued serving his country by transferring to the Wisconsin Army National Guard, dedicating an additional 20 years to military service. His commitment and valor stand as testament to his character and sense of duty.

Beyond his military career, Mark was a long and short haul truck driver. He also served on the Rothschild Volunteer Fire Department for many years. Most recently, he devoted himself to caring for the greens as a lawn caretaker at Tribute Golf Course in Wausau, a role that allowed him to merge his love of golf with rewarding work.

Mark was greatly involved in the Man of Honor Society, finding joy especially in their annual picnic and golf outing. Golf was his favorite pastime, and he was an active member of a league at Rib Mountain Golf Course for many years. His enthusiasm extended to dabbling in RC planes and drones, enjoying the thrills of the National Hot Rod Association, and bowling in earlier years. Mark cherished casino trips with his Army friends and enjoyed bow hunting. A passionate sports fan, he delighted in cheering on the Green Bay Packers, Milwaukee Bucks, Wisconsin Badgers, and watching NASCAR races.

He is survived by his two daughters, Traci (Dan) Behrens of Rothschild, and their children, Danielle Behrens of Rothschild, Austin (Allie) Behrens of Savage, Minnesota, and Bryce (Alivia Garnes) Behrens of Queen Creek, Arizona; and Vicki Scheibe of Weston; step-children, Trisha (Jody) Wanta of Wittenberg and Amy Tratz of Wausau; sister-in-law, Ruth Lyon, along with many step-grandchildren, and several nieces and nephews.

In addition to his parents and his wife, he was preceded in death by his siblings, Geraldine (Walter) Voight, Don Lyon and his daughters Laurie Ellis and LouAnn Gille; and his brother Stan (Jan) Lyon.

A private family celebration of life will be held this summer.

records.name: Richard D. Roloff
records.funeral_home: Brainard Funeral Home & Cremation Center of Weston
records.summary: Richard "Dick" D. Roloff, age 78, of Weston, passed away unexpectedly at his home on May 6, 2026.
records.body:
Richard "Dick" D. Roloff, age 78, of Weston, passed away unexpectedly at his home on Wednesday, May 6, 2026. He was born on June 29, 1947, to the late Wilbert and Gladys (Blossom) Roloff in Wausau, WI. Dick attended and graduated from Wausau East in 1965 then went to NTCwhere he received an Associates Degree in Accounting. After college graduation he was drafted into the United States Army. He bravely served his country from November 13, 1967 until September 19, 1969, and was honorably discharged on November 13, 1973. Dick met Carol Easker, the love of his life and his soul mate, and the two exchanged marriage vows on April 10, 1971 in Wausau, a union lasting over 55 years.

He began his working years helping his father at the famly Piggly Wiggly, in Schofield until the business was sold. He then worked in various types of management positions at manufacturing companies throughout his adulthood. Including Drott Manufacturing, where he was an inventory control analyst; Marmet Manufacturing in production management and Imperial Industries in quality and safety control. Dick also worked at Dale's Weston Lanes as an event coordinator until 2020.

Dick is remembered as a meticulous man, that was very organized and had a great sense of humor. During his younger years, Dick enjoyed camping and fishing, as well as coaching his son Dan in Little League. He was an avid bowler, golfer, deer hunter and looked forward to watching all of the Wisconsin sports teams. He took pride in maintaining a well-kept yard and paid close attention to every detail. He loved his pets – his faithful dog Tippy, and his two cats Bailey and Norman. Dick was a member of the American Legion, and was a proud patriotic man. He was entertaining, loving and generous, an all-around good guy that could get along with anyone! He enjoyed his daily meetings at Kelly Club where he considered himself "The Mayor". Dick will be sadly missed by his loving family and friends, but never forgotten.

Dick leaves behind his wife: Carol Roloff of Weston, son: Dan (Christy) Roloff of Weston, daughters: Michelle (Jason) Witz of Mosinee, with their daughters: Amanda (Jason) Riedel and Skylar Witz (Dylan Block) and Vickie Roloff (Jason Levine) of Oregon, WI, with their son: Atticus Levine, brother: John (Nancy) Roloff of Wausau and sister-in-law: Jane Seubert, as well as many nieces and nephews and good friends. He was preceded in death by his parents, grandchildren: Alexis and Logan, and brothers: Larry (Sue), Lloyd (Rosie), and Jim Roloff.

The Celebration of Life for Dick will be held on Sunday, May 17, 2026, from 1:00 p.m. until 3:30 p.m., with military honors being conducted at 3:30 p.m., all at Brainard Funeral Home in Weston.

The family wishes to thank all of the caring and compassionate emergency responders that came to assist Dick.

In lieu of flowers the family requests donations be made to the Alexis P. & Logan D. Memorial fund.

records.name: Todd Mathew Pierce
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Todd Mathew Pierce, age 64, of Rothschild, WI, passed away on May 9, 2026.
records.body:
Todd Mathew Pierce, age 64 (1962-2026) of Rothschild, WI, passed away on May 9, 2026, at his home in Rothschild under the care of Aspirus Hospice, after a battle with stomach cancer.

Todd was born on January 24, 1962, in Milwaukee, WI, to the late Donald and Rita (Larson) Pierce.

Todd attended Stillwater and De Pere high schools and graduated from UW-Green Bay in 1988 with a double major in economics and regional analysis. Todd worked as a City Planner for a number of years. He also did landscaping and groundskeeping.

Todd enjoyed genealogy, travel, the beach, reading westerns, having a beautiful looking yard, hiking, and spending time with his wife. He was known for his great smile, helpfulness, and sense of humor.

Todd is survived by the love of his life and wife of 31 years, Patrice Anne (Stoddart) Pierce. He is also survived by 7 siblings, Donn (Mary) Pierce of Oshkosh, WI; Kim Pierce of De Pere, WI; Kirk (Mary) Pierce of Bowling Green, KY; Julie (Mark) Denil of Greensboro, NC; Gwen (Terry) Fritch of Lakewood, WI; Kelly (Susan) Pierce of Marathon, WI; Kent (Julianne) Pierce of Green Bay, WI, many nieces and nephews, and other family and friends.

He is preceded in death by his parents, Don and Rita (Larson) Pierce, grandparents, Ole and Loretta (Werlein) Larson, and Roscoe and Clara (Junker) Pierce.

A memorial service will be held at 4:00 PM on Saturday, May 16, 2026, at Ascend Funeral Home, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston, WI. Visitation will be held from 2:00 until the time of the service at Ascend. An interment service at Sacred Heart Catholic Cemetery in Mondovi, WI, will be at a later date.

records.name: David G. Svetlik
records.summary: David G. Svetlik, 78, passed away on May 8, 2026.
records.body:
Dave Svetlik was born on September 21, 1947. He left this world on May 8, 2026, surrounded by his family. He grew up in Neillsville, Wisconsin, with five siblings, where he enjoyed fishing on the Black River, a passion he later shared with his children.

Dave met the love of his life, Cheryl, and they were married on January 5, 1980. They moved to Kronenwetter with Cheryl's children, Laura and David, that same year and lived together in their home for 46 years. Dave and Cheryl welcomed their children, John and Paul, into their family in the coming years, followed closely by their grandchildren, Wes, Mike, and Leanna.

Dave, Cheryl, and their children loved to sail around the Door County Peninsula and road trip across the country, visiting the majority of the National Parks. Dave showed up for everyone, his children, nieces and nephews, grandchildren, and great-grandson, Abe, to whom he was a proud "Grampy". He loved spending time with his granddaughters, Sara and Katie. He and Katie enjoyed lunch together nearly every week.

Dave was a father figure to anyone who needed one and helped several family members find their lifelong careers. It didn't matter the scope of the job; if you needed Dave's help, he was there. Dave could fix or build anything. He loved to share that knowledge with his family, his students, and anyone he could help.

For many years, Dave owned his own heating and cooling business before joining Northcentral Technical College as an electromechanical faculty member for almost twenty years. It was common for former students to recognize him in the community, expressing their appreciation for his guidance.

Upon retirement, a favorite way to spend his time was "bumming" with Cheryl, especially to the Mead Wildlife Area to watch migrating birds. He looked forward to seeing the sandhill cranes, trumpeter swans, and geese each spring. He recently revived an annual tradition of fishing in Canada with his son John and grandson Andrew.

Dave spent hours in his woodshop with Paul, building everything from wooden boats to fine furniture. He recently started choosing to spend time playing with his grandson Frankie, over cutting dovetail joints with Paul. He loved maintaining trails at the land where he built a cabin with his own hands and spending time boating in his handmade wooden boat.

In the last few years, Dave spent a lot of his time researching and writing social justice and progressive political opinion pieces for local news organizations.

He was an easy man to love and will be missed greatly by everyone who knew him.

Dave is survived by his wife, Cheryl; his daughter, Laura (Gary Erickson) Rose-Erickson; and sons, John Svetlik (Mandy Yang) and Paul (Amanda Ley) Svetlik. He loved his grandchildren, Wesley Rose (Jamie Lenzner), Michael (Ashley) Rose, Leanna (Tim) Holzem, Sara Williams, Katie Williams, Andrew Svetlik, and Francis Ley-Svetlik; and his great-grandson, Abraham Holzem. He was also survived by his siblings, Jeanne (Ed) Kubicki, Carolyn (Hal Ehrenreich) Michalski, Alan (Penny) Svetlik; sister-in-law, Rita Svetlik; brothers-in-law, Mark (Cindy) Miller and Michael Miller; and many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Frank and Catherine Svetlik; his brothers, John and Gerry Svetlik; his in-laws, Robert and Jeanne Miller; and his son, David Williams.

records.name: Gene R. Hellman
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Gene Russell Hellman, 84, passed away on May 5, 2026.
records.body:
Gene Russell Hellman, 84, passed away on May 5, 2026, leaving behind a legacy of faith, integrity, wisdom, and deep love for family and friends.

Gene grew up in Dollar Bay, Michigan, and attended Michigan Technological University, where he earned degrees in Applied Physics and later an MBA. It was during his college years in the Houghton/Hancock area that he met the love of his life, Ella Jean "Ginger" Hellman, at the Coffee Cup restaurant. Gene had been told by a friend that he should ask Ginger on a date. When he stopped in to ask her out, she told him she needed to study. Never discouraged, Gene suggested she bring a flashlight to the movie so she could do both. That quick wit and persistence began a love story that lasted more than 60 years after they married in 1964.

Together, Gene and Ginger built a beautiful life centered on faith, family, hard work, and community. They began married life in Dayton, Ohio, later moved to married student housing at Michigan Tech, and eventually made their permanent home in the Wausau, Wisconsin area, living in Rib Mountain, Stevens Point, and finally Kronenwetter.

Gene's professional life reflected both his intelligence and determination. He began his career with General Motors before returning to graduate school. He later worked for IBM selling computer systems, then for Wausau Insurance in data security. In 1987, Gene began what became the defining chapter of his professional life when he joined Lutheran Brotherhood, later Thrivent, as a financial advisor. He built lasting relationships through his honesty, wisdom, and unwavering integrity.

A man of strong faith, Gene was one of the key founders of Bethlehem Lutheran Church in Kronenwetter in the 1980s. His commitment to serving others extended into the community, where he dedicated many years of service to the D.C. Everest School Board, including leadership roles on the board.

Gene was known for his quick wit, remarkable memory, and gift for storytelling. He seemed able to recall vivid details from experiences decades earlier, and every story carried a lesson, a moral, or a piece of timely advice. He genuinely loved people and seemed to run into someone he knew everywhere he went.

Outside of work and service, Gene cherished time spent with family. He loved fishing and deer hunting with his sons and lifelong friends each fall at "Bucksnort Lodge" in Merrill, Wisconsin. He also enjoyed horseback riding at Mackinac Island with his daughter, golf, and cross-country skiing. Together, Gene and Ginger spent countless hours on the road visiting the people they loved, creating memories with family and friends across Michigan, Minnesota, Ohio, Colorado, and beyond.

One of the places closest to Gene's heart was the family cottage on Portage Lake in Michigan, simply known as "Camp," which he built by hand with his father. Camp became the center of countless family memories — taking saunas, trips to Dreamland, picking rocks along Lake Superior, and gathering together across generations. Gene especially loved creating memorable experiences for his grandchildren, including an elaborate treasure hunt that began with a mysterious map washed ashore in a bottle, a story the family will treasure forever.

More than anything, Gene loved Ginger and his family. He is survived by his beloved wife, Ella Jean "Ginger" Hellman; his children, Melany Kaiser (John), Matt Hellman (Patty), and Thad Hellman (Jennifer); and his treasured grandchildren, Caleb, Alina, Jacob, Bethany, Ben, Kaley, Treyton, and Lewis.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Russell and Edith Hellman, and his sister, Nancy Pfauth.

Gene will be remembered for his wisdom, humor, and the steady way he showed up for the people he loved. His stories, guidance, and presence will be deeply missed.

records.name: James "Hugger" Craig
records.summary: James "Hugger" Craig, 79, of Wittenberg, passed away on May 10, 2026.
records.body:
James "Hugger" Dell Craig, 79, of Wittenberg, passed away on Sunday, May 10, 2026, at Autumn Lane in Birnamwood under the care of LeRoyer Hospice.

Jim was born on October 19, 1946, in New London, Wisconsin, to Edward and Irene Orpha (Button) Craig. He was the third of five children: Joan Dumbleton, Jock Craig, Janice Jones, and Jill Brown. His early years were spent in Royalton, Wisconsin, where he, Jan, and Jill spent countless hours enjoying the local river.

Jim graduated from Tomorrow River High School in 1964. Much of his adult life was spent working as a cook at various area restaurants. He later worked for 21 years as a Nursing Assistant at the Wisconsin Veterans Home, retiring in 2001. Shortly after retirement, Jim moved to the Wittenberg area, where he lived in the apartment located behind the home of his sister, Jan.

Before suffering a stroke in March of 2009, Jim was an avid singer who enjoyed performing at weddings and local events. He was a longtime member of the Waupaca Community Chorus. Music remained one of his greatest passions, and he enjoyed concerts, playing the piano, and taking Suzuki violin lessons. Jim also found joy in fishing, gardening, volunteer work, sewing for charity, and competing in floral arranging.

Jim is survived by his sisters, Jan (Tom) Jones and Jill (Phillip) Brown; sister-in-law, Pat Craig; special niece, Sharon Rustad, and several nieces, nephews, great-nieces, and great-nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents; his siblings, Joan (Larry) Dumbleton and Jock Craig; and his niece and nephew, Erika Jones and Jacob Craig.

The family would like to thank the staff at Autumn Lane and LeRoyer Hospice for their kind and compassionate care given to Jim.

records.name: Marisa M. Oliva
records.summary: Marisa M. Oliva, 36, of Wausau, passed away unexpectedly on May 9, 2026.
records.body:
Marisa Marie Oliva passed away unexpectedly at her home on Saturday, May 9th, 2026.

Born on September 3, 1989, to Tony and Julie Oliva, Marisa lived life in a way that was uniquely her own. She was funny, stubborn, loving, hardworking, and unforgettable to everyone who knew her. She had a beautiful smile, a huge heart, and a personality that could fill any room. And while she was famously always running late, she was always there when the people she loved truly needed her.

More than anything, Marisa loved her two boys, Jaxson Anthony Furger and Cameron Daniel Oliva. They were her greatest pride, her deepest love, and the center of her world. Being their mom brought her the greatest happiness in life. She also adored her beloved cat, Kylo, who was truly part of the family and brought her comfort, companionship, and unconditional love.

Marisa spent many years working alongside her family at Carmelo's and most recently at Oliva's at Gulliver's Landing. No matter where she worked, she worked hard and cared deeply about the people around her. She shared countless memories, laughs, and long days with family, friends, and coworkers who became family.

Like many, Marisa faced personal struggles in life, but through it all she continued to love deeply and care fiercely for those around her. Her family chooses to remember the loving mother, daughter, sister and friend who meant so much to so many.

She is survived by her parents, Tony and Julie Oliva; her beloved sons, Jaxson Anthony Furger and Cameron Daniel Oliva; her sisters, Kim (Chris) Garski and Amanda (Jesse) Riege; her nephews, Evan, Jacob, and David Riege; her paternal grandmother, Joan Oliva; her maternal grandfather, Ken (Polly) Day; along with many loving aunts, uncles, cousins, extended family members, and dear friends.

She was preceded in death by her maternal grandmother, Ellen Day; her paternal grandfather, Carmelo Oliva; and her uncle, Frank Oliva.

The loss of Marisa leaves an emptiness that can never truly be filled, but her love, laughter, and memory will live on forever in the hearts of those who loved her.

records.name: Roger E. Poblitz
records.summary: Roger E. Poblitz, 80, of Wausau, died peacefully on May 11, 2026.
records.body:
Roger E. Poblitz, 80, of Wausau, died peacefully on Monday, May 11, 2026, at Aspirus Hospice House in Wausau, with his family by his side.

He was born on August 31, 1945, in Vancouver, Washington, to the late Earl and Adelaide (Smith) Poblitz. He graduated from Ripon High School and later married Sharon Buchholz on September 11, 1965.

In 1971, they moved to Wausau. Roger worked for the United States Postal Service for over 40 years. His greatest joys in life were spending time with his grandchildren, attending different fairs and festivals with family and friends, and fishing with his grandson. He also had a love for music.

Survivors include his two daughters, Michelle (Jesse) Bornfleth and Melanie (Bart) Riddell; one grandson, Evan Paul Holtz; one granddaughter, Chloe Rose Holtz; and one step-grandson, Erik Scott Riddell.

He was preceded in death by his wife, Sharon Poblitz; and parents, Earl and Adelaide Poblitz.

The family would like to extend a special thank you to the staff of Aspirus Hospice House for the compassionate and kind care that Roger received.

records.name: Sarah J. Jacob
records.summary: Sarah J. Jacob, 46, passed away unexpectedly on May 9, 2026.
records.body:
Sarah J. Jacob, 46, passed away unexpectedly on Saturday, May 9, 2026.

She was born March 6, 1980, in Wausau, daughter of Larry and Pamela (Phillips) Duellman. In June of 2002, she married James Jacob at St. Mary Catholic Church, Marathon.

Sarah was a loving wife and mother who enjoyed working with 4-H kids and doing crafts. She also enjoyed watching family and friends play cornhole.

Survivors include her husband, James Jacob; children, Daniel and Leroy Jacob and Dakota Merriam; parents, Larry and Pamela Duellman; sister, Jennifer Duellman; brother, Phillip (Sara) Duellman; father and mother-in-law, Gene and Rose Ann Jacob; numerous other relatives and friends.

records.name: Shelley J. Boda
records.summary: Shelley J. Boda, 73, of Elderon, died on May 10, 2026.
records.body:
Shelley J. Boda, 73 of Elderon, died on Sunday, May 10, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

Shelley was born on June 21, 1952, in Wausau, the daughter of Raphael 'Pat' and Jean (Larson) Gunderson.

On August 2, 1986, Shelley was united in marriage to John Boda, at Zion Lutheran Church, Eland.

Shelley enjoyed gardening, feeding and watching the hummingbirds, spending time with friends and family, especially her grandchildren, and cheering on her favorite sports teams—the Wisconsin Badgers, Milwaukee Brewers, and Green Bay Packers. She could often be found at local baseball games, playfully heckling the umpires. In earlier years, she enjoyed softball and bowling. Shelley also dedicated time to teaching Sunday School and Vacation Bible School at Zion Lutheran Church in Eland.

Shelley is survived by her husband, John; children, Rick (Cathy) Boda, Robert (Diane) Boda and Rachael (Johnnoy) Boda-Holness; five grandchildren, Whitney (Brittney) Boda, Briana (Eric) Bergsbaken, Brennan Boda, Kylee Boda and Ryker Boda; one great granddaughter, Kinzlee Bergsbaken; siblings, Donna Gunderson, Peggy (Marty) Benes, Ray Gunderson, Wanda Gunderson and David Gunderson; brothers-in-law, David (Debbie) Boda and Gregg Hugill, as well as many nieces and nephews.

Shelley was preceded in death by her parents; brother, Mark; father and mother-in-law, Anthony and Laura Boda and sister-in-law, Lynn Hugill.

records.name: Thomas A. Tetting
records.summary: Thomas A. Tetting, of the Town of Wescott, passed away peacefully on May 9, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas Alan Tetting, of the Town of Wescott, passed away peacefully surrounded by his family on Saturday, May 9, 2026, after fighting a tough battle with a form of pulmonary fibrosis known as Interstitial Lung Disease.

Tom was born on August 1, 1951, and raised in Pewaukee by the late Kenneth and Dorothy (Moll) Tetting.

Tom dedicated his life to public service and education. He began his career as a police cadet and correctional officer, working his way through the ranks to become Police Chief in the Waukesha County area. He later served as Municipal Judge for the Village of Pewaukee for many terms. In addition to his distinguished law enforcement and judicial career, Tom was a criminal justice and law enforcement instructor at Waukesha County Technical College for approximately 25 years until his retirement.

Tom earned his bachelor's degree from University of Wisconsin – Milwaukee and his master's degree from University of Wisconsin – Whitewater. He was also a proud graduate of the FBI National Academy in Quantico, VA. An achievement that reflected his lifelong commitment to professional excellence and serving those around him.

In August of 1996, he was united in marriage to Barbara (Smith) Tetting, and together they celebrated nearly 30 happy years of marriage. They were blessed with two sons, Caden Tom and Tristan Alan Tetting. The family later relocated to Shawano Lake, where they spent countless hours fishing, boating, and simply enjoying life together.

In his free time, Tom enjoyed his hobby farm north of Shawano, where he spent many hours cutting wood and riding his beloved tractors. He also loved bike riding, traveling to Florida each summer with his family, and was an avid reader.

But his absolute greatest joy in life was spending time with his two sons.

Tom is survived by his loving wife, Barbara (Smith) Tetting; his two sons, Caden Thomas Tetting and Tristan Alan Tetting, all of Shawano; his sister, Marge (Jim) Gozdowiak of Mukwonago; his brother, Keith (Nancy) Tetting of Mukwonago; his father-in-law, Walter Smith; brother-in-law, DuWayne Smith; and nieces and nephews Robert Smith, Tiffany Smith, Thomas Tetting, Tracy Tetting, and Travis Tetting, and their families.

Tom was preceded in death by his parents, Kenneth and Dorothy Tetting; his mother-in-law, Consuelo Smith; and his brother-in-law, Walter Smith.

The family would like to extend heartfelt thanks to the Shawano Ambulance Service, as well as all of their family and friends, for their love and support.

records.name: Thomas I. Maltbey
records.summary: Thomas I. Maltbey passed away on May 7, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas "Tom" Irving Maltbey took his last breath on May 7, 2026, with his loving wife and daughters by his side.

Thirty-seven years ago, Tom received the incredible gift of a kidney from his brother, Tim, a gift that gave him many more years with the people he loved most. Throughout his life, Tom faced more than his share of health challenges, but he met them with quiet strength, determination, and courage. Until the very end, he did everything he could to stay healthy, heal, and recover. His body had endured so much, and though it could no longer keep up, his spirit never stopped fighting.

Tom was an incredible man, strong, humble, patient, caring, and deeply devoted to his family. He was not a man of many words, but when he spoke, his words came from the heart. His advice was honest, meaningful, and often came with his unmistakable ability to call out the obvious. He was the rock of his family, steady and dependable, and his love was shown through his actions every single day.

Tom was the first All-State football player at Wausau West High School, where he played both sides of the ball. It was also during high school that he met the love of his life, Sharon, at just 16 years old, and, true to Tom's determined nature, he didn't take "no" for an answer. Together, they built a beautiful life and shared 50 years of marriage.

Tom worked for Greenheck Fan for more than 30 years before retiring in 2016. Outside of work, he found joy in the outdoors, especially hunting and fishing.

As a devoted girl dad, he happily passed those passions on to his daughters, always willing to share his lunch, and, of course, his candy bars. He loved sports, spent time coaching softball, and took great pride in supporting his girls in anything they chose to do.

One of Tom's greatest joys was being a grandfather. He was extremely proud of his five grandchildren, Cole, Wes, Will, Samantha, and Allie, and rarely missed one of their activities. Whether he was cheering from the sidelines, watching from the stands, or simply showing up with quiet pride, his presence meant everything.

Tom also loved riding his Harley, especially alongside his best friend, Larry. Those rides brought him happiness, freedom, and memories he treasured. Tom will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved him. His strength, loyalty, quiet humor, and steady love will live on in the family he cherished so much.

Tom is survived by his high school sweetheart and wife of 50 years, Sharon; his daughters, Jamie (Jason) Polley and Kendra (Danny) VanSlyke; his grandchildren, Wes and Samantha Polley, and Cole, Will, and Allie VanSlyke; his sister, Vicky Pingel; his brother, Tim (Wendy) Maltbey; brothers-in-law, Ross Wood, Marv Schael, and Bob McCabe; sister-in-law, Jayne Wood; and many beloved nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Dorothy and Victor Maltbey; his in-laws Kenneth and Geraldine Wood; his sisters, Sandy McCabe, Shron Schael, and Marcia Maltbey; and his brothers-in-law, Michael Wood, Jeff Wood and Dick Pingel.

records.name: Bernard J. Richards Jr.
records.summary: Bernard J. Richards Jr., age 70, of Wausau, passed away on May 11, 2026.
records.body:
Bernard (Bernie) Richards was a man of few words and a gentle, giving heart; he was as wonderfully set in his ways as he was generous to anyone in need.

Bernie, age 70, passed away on May 11, 2026, in Wausau, following a courageous battle with ALS. He was born to the late Bernard Sr. and Ellen (Huntington) Richards.

On January 25, 1993, Bernie married Susan Richards in Las Vegas. Together, they shared a life of simple Wisconsin pleasures-heading "up north" to the cottage, enjoying Friday night fish fries, and traveling. Susan preceded him in death, but the memories of their time together remained a cornerstone of his life.

A quiet man who expressed his love through actions rather than grand speeches, Bernie was happiest when he was giving to others. He was a devoted fan of the Milwaukee Brewers and cherished the time spent attending and watching games with his son, Reid. He also had a passion for live music and theater, constantly scouting for tickets to concerts and shows just so he could treat his loved ones to an evening out.

In his later years, Bernie found companionship and adventure with Joanne Sullivan. In the time they shared, they saw the world together, traveling to Alaska, Ireland, Florida, Arizona, and across the American West. Throughout his illness, Bernie found constant comfort and support in his beloved cat, Guinness, who rarely left his side.

Bernie is survived by his significant other, Joanne Sullivan, his three children, Amy (Paul) Gasch of Trempealeau, Holly Raasch (Nate Nguyen) of Fridley, MN, and Reid Richards of Wausau; his three granddaughters, Elin and Aria Gasch, and Olivia Nguyen; and his sister, Ann Quaschnick.

He was greeted in heaven by his wife, Susan; his parents, Bernard Sr. and Ellen; and his siblings, Debbie Moeller, Lois Kleiber, and Ronald Richards.

A memorial service will be held on Friday, May 22, 2026, at 11:00 am at Helke Funeral Home (302 Spruce Street, Wausau, WI 54401 with Deacon Erv Burkhardt officiating. Visitation will be held on Friday May 22, 2026 from 9:30 am until the hour of service at the Helke.

records.name: Carol L. Zoromski
records.summary: Carol L. Zoromski, age 68, of Stevens Point, died on May 13, 2026.
records.body:
Carol Lynn Zoromski, 68, of Stevens Point, died on Wednesday, May 13, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

Carol was born on May 22, 1957, the daughter of Donald and Laura (Hackley) Kern.

Carol worked in data entry and accounting, holding numerous jobs with various companies. She enjoyed crafting, sewing and quilting.

Carol is survived by her son, James Zoromski; brother, Donald (Miladis) Kern; sisters, Linda (Barry) Myers, Janet (Dan) Kranski and Sue (Leo) Johnson; and numerous nieces and nephews.

Carol was preceded in death by her parents.

According to Carol's wishes, no services will be held.

records.name: J.D. Tackett
records.summary: J.D. Tackett, age 72, passed away on May 9, 2026.
records.body:
It is with heavy hearts that we announce the passing of JD Tackett, age 72, who passed away peacefully on May 9, 2026.

JD was born on May 15, 1953, in Pikeville, Kentucky to J.C. and Gladys (Hall) Tackett where his roots and love for family began. As a young boy he moved to Michigan with his mom, dad, and sister.

JD was a man who enjoyed life. He had a passion for fishing, loved fast cars, and was one of the biggest Elvis Presley fans you could ever meet. Whether he was listening to Elvis's music, talking about classic cars, or spending time by the water with a fishing pole in hand, JD found joy in the simple things and shared that joy with those around him.

Above all, JD loved his family deeply. He leaves behind his children, Angie (Brian), Jay (Sarah), Tiffany and Amber (Shane); his beloved grandchildren, Justin, Logan, Jordan, Heaven, and Izaiah; his wife, Linda Tackett and first wife Linda McGregor. He also leaves behind many extended family members and friends who will forever cherish the memories they shared with him.

JD will be remembered for his personality, his love of music and Elvis, and the laughter and stories he brought to those around him. His presence will be deeply missed, but his memory will live on in the hearts of all who knew and loved him.

He is proceeded in death by his mother, his father and his sister, Ellen.

records.name: Norma J. Nelson
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Norma J. Nelson, age 85, of Wausau, passed away on May 11, 2026.
records.body:
Norma Jean Nelson (née Nyland) age 85, passed away on Monday, May 11, 2026, at her home in Wausau.

Norma was born on June 11, 1940, in Fosston, Minnesota, the daughter of Helmer and Emma (Melbo) Nyland. A proud graduate of Wausau East High School in 1958, she married the love of her life, Dennis Nelson, on June 7, 1958, and the couple enjoyed 57 years together before his passing in 2015. Norma pursued her passion when she obtained a degree as a surgical technician from NCTI. She worked at Memorial Hospital in Labor and Delivery, where she loved taking care of the mommas and babies. Prior to her time at Memorial Hospital, she was the first female bartender in Wausau and worked alongside her sister, Marian, at their family bar; Jim's Bar. She later worked at Fiskars, from which she retired in 1999.

Norma, affectionately known as "Stormin Norman" to her close friends due to her spirited and fiery nature, was a woman of vibrant character. She cherished her friendships and shared countless joyful moments with her beloved friends, Janette Woodward and Lynn Byrd, with whom she embarked on yearly "fishing trips" and many other adventures. Norma and Dennis had a cabin in Harshaw, on Birch Lake, where they spent much of their retirement until Dennis' passing. After Dennis passed, Norma and her best friend, Joan Wood, bought a winter home in Lakeland Florida; a place where she cherished spending time with her good friends who became like a second family to her. She shared her mother's love of quilting and various needle crafts, loved to read and shop and did many puzzles.

Central to Norma's heart was her devotion to spending time with her family at birthday parties, cookouts, and other gatherings. Her constant companion was Little Sammy, her little rotten dog, who she loved dearly.

The tears we have are for us, as we know she is no longer in pain and in a good place. She will be deeply missed by her children, Kathy Foote, Denise Layman, Kurt (Marci) Nelson, and Craig (Tuesday) Nelson; her 11 grandchildren, Keenan Foote, Carla Glodowski, Becky Heinrich, Rachel (Dustin) Altfillisch, Brittany (Josh) Wiechmann, Emily (Mitch) Baumann, Teague Layman, Calli (Anthony) Catanese, Taylor (Chad) Pemberton, Alicia Nelson, and Ashlynd Nelson. She is also survived by her 21 great-grandchildren and her brother, Ole Nyland.

She is preceded in death by her parents; husband, Dennis; and sisters, Marian Waldinger Reiche and Diane Nyland Plante.

A memorial service for Norma will be held on Saturday, May 23, 2026, at 1:30 PM at Mount Olive Lutheran Church, 6205 Alderson St. Weston, WI 54476. Pastor Lance Hoelscher will officiate. Visitation will be held from 12:00PM until the time of service at the church. Norma and Dennis will be laid to rest at Pine Grove Cemetery at a later date.

records.name: Peter Anthony Philipp
records.summary: Peter Anthony Philipp, age 85 passed away on May 15, 2026.
records.body:
Peter Anthony Philipp, 85, passed away peacefully on May 15, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center Weston, with his loving wife Kay by his side.

Peter was born on April 1, 1941, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to the late Eugene and Mary Ann (Kostuck) Philipp. As a child, Peter attended several schools as his family moved throughout different areas, eventually graduating from Newman High School in Wausau in 1959.

Peter proudly served his country in the United States Air Force from 1963 to 1967. Following his military service, he became a dedicated employee of the Schofield Post Office, first working as a walking mail carrier and later driving a rural route until his retirement. After retiring from the Post Office, he briefly worked at East Bay in Wausau.

Peter had a lifelong love for building and flying radio-controlled airplanes and helicopters, he also enjoyed bird hunting and fishing with family and friends, his hobbies brought him much joy and many friendships. He was also a member of American Legion Post 10 in Wausau.

Family was deeply important to Peter. He was a loving father figure to Gregory Hintz and his wife Alana and Anya whom called him Grandpa. He treasured the relationships he built throughout the years and remained devoted to those he loved.

On September 1, 1984, Peter married Darlene Tanck. Together they enjoyed spending time camping up north and creating many happy memories. Darlene preceded him in death on January 17, 2006.

Peter later married Kay (Robinson) Peterson on May 19, 2007, in Schofield. They had first met years earlier as two couples on a retirement trip to Boston sponsored by Liberty Mutual, where both Kay and Darlene were employed before retirement. Peter and Kay reconnected through the Covenant Community Presbyterian Church, where Kay and her late husband, Clare “Pete” Peterson, were members. Peter and Kay shared many wonderful years together traveling throughout the United States, often on organized bus tours. They also enjoyed several trips to Hawaii with dear friends Shirley and Mark Summers and made five memorable trips to Europe. Of all the places he visited, Ireland remained Peter’s favorite.

Peter will be lovingly remembered for his gentle spirit, devotion to family, quiet sense of humor, and appreciation for life’s simple joys. He especially adored his precious kitty, Daisy, who faithfully “herded” him around the house and brought him constant companionship.

He is survived by his beloved wife, Kay; several relatives and friends; and his cherished cat, Daisy.

Peter was preceded in death by his parents; his first wife, Darlene; his sister, Patty Bennett; brother-in-law, Watson “Corky” Bennett; brother-in-law, David Tanck, Darlene’s twin brother; and Kay’s first husband, Clare “Pete” Peterson, who passed away in 2005 after illness.

The family extends a special thank you to the compassionate staff of Compassus Hospice for their care and support.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made to Covenant Community Presbyterian Church for their building fund or to the American Cancer Society

John J Buettgen Funeral Home is assisting the family. Family and friends may leave words of remembrance at www.honorone.com

records.name: Eugene Charles Field
records.summary: Eugene Charles Field, age 78, of Wittenberg passed away on May 17, 2026.
records.body:
