Eugene C. Field, 78 of Wittenberg, died unexpectedly on Sunday, May 17, 2026, at his home.

Eugene was born on June 25, 1947, in Pontiac, IL, the son of Eugene and Hope (O’Higgins) Field.

On July 1, 1965, Eugene was united in marriage to Sheryl Close. They later divorced.

Eugene was drafted for Vietnam and was fortunate enough to only serve in Germany. He was in the Army Corp of Engineers under the U.S. Army Engineer Command Europe in Frankfurt, Germany, with a rank of specialist E-4. After returning home in the summer of 1972, he was employed with Material Service Corporation in Joliet, Illinois, where he was a machinist for a number of years, working on very large rebuilds, such as the Joliet Downtown Drawbridges.

He left the Joliet area in the mid to late 80's, partnering with an Army buddy, Gary Thompson, to grow ginseng in the Wittenberg area. He retired later from farming, but continued living on the last farm he used for ginseng.

Eugene was a very practical and pragmatic man who possessed remarkable intelligence despite having little formal education. He lived his life entirely on his own terms, which was only appropriate that his favorite song was ‘I Did It My Way.’ Eugene took pride in being very connected to the Earth. He could walk through a cedar forest in the middle of a moonless night and know exactly where he was. Eugene could start fires with nothing, sneak up on deer and really connected with hunting and farming.  He loved chess and was able to teach his son, Charlie, the game. Later in life, he became very interested in Harley Motorcycling and liked interacting with events associated with that. Eugene also loved traveling to Mexico and made more than 25 trips there over the years.

Eugene is survived by his son, Charlie Field; four grandchildren; three great-grandchildren; half brothers and sisters; partner and friend, Marianne Schiefer of Stevens Point and many friends. He was preceded in death by his parents.

No services will be held.

Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg is assisting the family with arrangements. Memories and messages of support may be shared at schmidtschulta.com.

records.name: Charles F. Fox
records.summary: Charles F. Fox, age 81, of Vesper passed away on May 18, 2026.
records.body:
Charles F. Fox, 81, of Vesper passed away peacefully at his home on May 18, 2026.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 am on Tuesday, May 26, 2026 at St. Joachim Catholic Church, Pittsville with rosary at 8:30 am and visitation from 9:00 am am until the time of service. Rev. David Ashbeck will officiate. Burial will follow in the parish cemetery with his grandsons; Austin Fox, Cedric Neve, Adam Sturgis, Eli Fox, and Codi Fox, serving as pallbearers. Buchanan/Rembs Funeral Home, Pittsville is assisting the family.

Charlie was born at home on June 27, 1944, to Donald and Lillian (Grimm) Fox. He spent his early years working on the farm. Following graduation from Pittsville High School in 1962, he attended tech school to learn masonry. On January 2, 1965, he married Patricia Schara at SS Peter and Paul Catholic Church in Wisconsin Rapids.

After their marriage, he and Patricia moved to Racine, and he continued to work as a mason. He and Patricia moved back to Central Wisconsin, eventually buying a hobby farm where they raised their five children. He worked various jobs throughout his life, including owning Fox Construction and American Dream Realty. He retired from USPS in 2021 at the age of 77.

Charlie had many pastimes throughout his life. He enjoyed trap shooting and racing snowmobiles and 4-wheelers. When he wasn’t racing, he was watching his family race. He played fast and slow pitch softball. His family has many fond memories of cheering him on as he played in leagues and tournaments. An avid hunter, he encouraged all of his children to hunt. He also enjoyed fishing at his back pond with his children, grandchildren, and great-grandchildren. His happiest days were when he was spending time with his family and friends.

Charlie was very involved with Ducks Unlimited, starting several chapters in our area. He was recently recognized by DU for his many years of service. He served on the Board for the Farm Bureau. Central WI State Fair was his favorite time of year as he looked forward to scooping ice cream at the Farm Bureau stand each year. He belonged to the Wisconsin Cranberry Association, owning his own marsh for nearly 30 years.

Charlie is survived by his wife, Patricia; his children, Charles (Diane) Fox II of Vesper, Lisa (Terry) Sobczak of Stevens Point, Tracy (Don) Neve of Wisconsin Rapids, Wendy (Paul) Sturgis of Vesper, and Mike (Tina) Fox of Vesper. His grandchildren; Austin, Caitlyn, Erica, and Ashley Fox; Rachel (Justin Jens) Neve and Cedric Neve, Christine (Eathen) Aden, Adam (Jenna) Sturgis, Miranda (Evan) Foust, Rebecca (fiancé Ethen Meissner) Sturgis; Eli, Codi, and Franki Fox. Great-grandchildren, Henry, Aly, and Charley Aden; Elliot Sturgis.

He is further survived by his siblings and in-laws, Donald “Butch” (Dawn) Fox, Diane (Bob) Zuebella, Fran Fox, Juliana (Russ) Roy, and Peggy Grutzik.

He was preceded in death by his parents, his siblings Jeannette Engelbright, Dale Fox, and Dolores Robus; and one grandson, Rahli Fox. His in-laws, Donald Engelbright and Judy Fox; Russell and Rose (Pelot) Schara, Russell (Kathy) Schara, Rose Ann (Denis) Crowns, and Barbara (David) Huisheere.

Charlie enjoyed visiting with all those who called, stopped by the house, and shared pictures and memories on Facebook. You made his day.

records.name: David L. Kaminski
records.summary: David L. Kaminski, age 75, of Mosinee passed away on May 18, 2026.
records.body:
David L. Kaminski, 75, Mosinee passed away peacefully on Monday, May 18, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born March 7, 1951 in Wausau, son of the late Wallace and Genevieve (Koback) Kaminski. On November 24, 1979 he married Carol Weinke at St. Paul Catholic Church, Mosinee. She survives.

For 37 years Dave was a carpenter at Wausau Homes in Kronenwetter. Dave liked to do wood working, he liked to do puzzles, and enjoyed admiring classic cars. Dave loved to spend time with family and friends. He loved helping organize family reunions and deciding what he would make for raffles. He also liked to play dominoes and play cards. He enjoyed volunteering at the Polish Fest in the Popcorn and Ice Cream stand each year, and at many other church functions. He was a big fan of Elvis and loved to collect memorabilia and attend Elvis impersonator shows.

Survivors include his wife Carol Kaminski, Mosinee, his children, Stanley Phillips Jr., Menominee and Bonnie (Matthew) Cortright, Wausau, two grandchildren, Jackson and Kade Phillips and his siblings, Gene Kaminski, Rothschild and Donnie Kaminski, Wausau and many nieces and nephews.

Besides his parents he was preceded in death by his siblings, Barney (Karen) Kaminski, Caroline Kaminski, Virginia (Jimmy) Waters, Barbara Salyers, Marlene (Lenny) Kell, Dennis(Dottie) Kaminski, Susie (Jerry) Fischer and sister-in-law Ramona Kaminski.

Funeral Services will be held at 11:00 a.m. Friday, May 29, 2026 at Our Savior National Catholic Church, Mosinee. The Rev. Marion Talaga will preside. Burial will be in the church cemetery. Visitation will be held on Thursday May 28, 2026 from 4:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau and again on Friday from 10:00 a.m. until the time of services at the church. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Florence M. Ziegel
records.summary: Florence M. Ziegel, age 90, of Wausau passed away on May 16, 2026.
records.body:
Florence M. Ziegel, 90, of Wausau passed away on Saturday May 16, 2026 at Rennes Health and Rehab Center in Weston. She was born on February 12, 1936 in the town of Ringle to the late Walter and Julia Mijal. Florence was the eleventh of twelve children that grew up on the family farm in Ringle. She graduated from Wittenberg-Birnamwood High School in 1954.

On October 4, 1958, she married Gerald Ziegel at St. James Catholic Church in Wausau. They were blessed with 64 years of marriage before Jerry passed away on January 4, 2023. Together they enjoyed traveling, fishing, birdwatching and family vacations up north each summer. They planted a beautiful flower garden in the backyard, which was one of Florence's favorite places to be in summer.

Florence was a stay at home mom to raise their four children. Once all of the kids were in school, she began working part time jobs and eventually worked full time at Kolbe & Kolbe until she retired in 1998. She was a devoted wife and dedicated mom. She was overjoyed when the grandchildren (and great grands) arrived. She loved to make them their favorite foods and cheered them on in their sports and activities. Listening to polka music was a favorite pastime and she treasured any time she could spend with family and friends. She was a longtime member of Holy Name Catholic Church where she was a member of St. Catherine's Counsil. She also was a volunteer at their annual Summerfun picnic and funeral lunches.

Most of all she loved spending time with Jerry, her kids and grandkids. She was happiest when her house was full, she could cook a meal, share drinks and conversation and laughter filled the room.

Survivors include her children, Jim (Dawn) Ziegel, Jeff (Lori) Ziegel, Donna (Jon) Martin and her son-in-law Todd Hass; grandchildren, Megan, Matt, Amie (Jason), Josh (Cassie), Sheena (Adam), Travis, Larissa (Maggie), Brett (Lauren), Jarrod, Joseph (Kailin) and Alexandria (Dylan); great-grandchildren, Owen, Olivia, Jaedyn, Staci, Elowen and Baby Z due this fall. She is further survived by sisters-in-law, Janice Ruplinger, Rosemary Groth and Beverly Ziegel; and many nieces, nephews and special friends.

She was preceded in death by her husband Jerry, her daughter Sandy, her parents and siblings, Eleanor (Felix) Groshek, Mickey (Bernice) Mijal, Ed (Ceil) Mijal, Stanley (Lil) Mijal, Joseph (RoseAnn) Mijal, Jean (Stanley) Pierzchalski, John (Kay) Mijal, Helen (Leon) Sondelski, Tony (Pat) Mijal, Lucy (Joe) Muzynoski, Ann (Earl) Strehlow.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. on Thursday, May 21, 2026 at Holy Name Catholic Church in Wausau. Rev. Woodrow Pace will preside. Visitation will begin at 9:30 am until the time of service at the church. Entombment will take place in the Memorial Chapel Mausoleum at Restlawn Memorial Park, town of Texas.

The family would like to extend a special thank you to Fr. Martin for your visits, prayers and guidance. Also, thank you to family and friends that visited her throughout her years and the Mijal boys for sharing your musical talents. She cherished each moment shared. Sara, you are a true blessing sent from above. And lastly, to the staff at Rennes, who we entrusted with her care, your patience and support during her years there, especially during this last difficult week, was appreciated more than words can express. Also, a special thank you to the staff at Aspirus at Home Hospice who helped care for our Mother during her final days.

Rest in peace Mom and Granny ! Until we meet again …

records.name: John Phillip Singer
records.summary: John Phillip Singer, age 81, of Weston passed away on May 18, 2026.
records.body:
John Phillip Singer passed away peacefully in the early morning on May 18, 2026, with his sister, Shirley, at his side.

John was born on November 18, 1944, in Milwaukee, WI, to the late Charles and Adeline (Fearson) Singer. He attended St. Paul’s School in Mosinee, St. Mary’s School in Marathon, and graduated from Marathon High School in 1963. John served in the United States Army beginning August 12, 1965, where he served in Vietnam at the rank of SP4 until being honorably discharged in 1967.

Following his military service, John began working with Crestline Company in Wausau, which was then purchased in 1981 by Sentry Insurance along with the Vetter Company in Stevens Point, giving it the name SNE. In 1991, the company moved to Mosinee, and in 2001, it was again sold to the Weather Shield Company. He continued to work for the company in Mosinee until his retirement in 2007, accomplishing 40 years of service.

John was a member of the American Legion Roy L. Drew Post 492 in Rothschild. He was on a pool league with the 19th Hole in Schofield and enjoyed watching old western TV shows. He was also a fan of the Milwaukee Brewers, Green Bay Packers, and enjoyed NASCAR. John made many new friends after moving to Renaissance Assisted Living in March 2024, joining with others in the many activities offered, such as bingo, happy hour, listening to musicians, and occasional trips to the casino.

John is survived by sisters, Shirley (Edward) Princl of Weston, WI, Kathleen Asterita of Mesa, AZ, and Margaret (Richard) Pesmark of Peshtigo, WI; brothers, Charles Singer of Wausau, WI, and Gerald (Diane) Singer of Mesquite, NV; special Goddaughter, Amber Myska; and many nieces and nephews. He is preceded in death by his parents; brothers, Robert, Richard, Dennis Singer; and sister, Judith (Singer) Buntrock; as well as several doggie companions throughout the years.

A Memorial Service will be held at 11:30 AM on Friday, May 29, 2026, at Ascend Funeral Home and Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston. Pastor Lance Hoelscher will officiate. Visitation will be held from 10:00 AM until the time of service at the funeral home. Interment will be in Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Kronenwetter, with Military Honors accorded by the United States Army and American Legion Montgomery-Plant-Dudley Post 10 Honor Guard.

The family would like to thank the staff at Renaissance Assisted Living in Weston and Compassus Hospice for the wonderful care they gave John, especially during his final weeks.

Ascend Funeral Home of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Larry Bergh
records.summary: Larry Bergh, age 71, of Merrill passed away on May 10, 2026.
records.body:
Larry Bergh, age 71, passed away peacefully on May 10, 2026. He was born on December 27, 1954, to Evelyn McWilliams and Leonard Bergh Sr.

After getting his GED, he headed off to the Navy. When he came home, he was a cook, settled down in Merrill, and married in 1976. He then worked for Franzen Brothers Circus for a short while, returning home to be a cook, then settled into over-the-road trucking for 33 years.

He is survived by his daughters, Colleen (Richard) Martiny and Karen Bergh. Grandchildren Dakota (Kathleen) Martin, Rusty Martin, Lillian Martin, Shawana (Conor) Martin, Blaze (Brianna) Martiny, great grandchildren Sophia, Emmalynn, Waverly, Phoebe, Nephew Ray Carawan, and many other nieces and nephews.

Larry is preceded in death by his parents and brothers Dennis Bergh, Bernard "Buck" Bergh and Leonard Bergh Jr.

A celebration of life will take place at a later date.

records.name: Lois Ann Nehls
records.summary: Lois Ann Nehls, age 89 passed away on May 18, 2026.
records.body:
Lois Ann Nehls, a beloved mother, grandmother, and sister passed away on Monday, May 18, 2026 at the Rennes Health and Rehab in Rhinelander.  Lois was born on Tuesday, February 16, 1937 in Rhinelander, the oldest daughter of Clarence and Nora (Kussman) Christian.  She was 89 years old.

Lois graduated from Rhinelander High School in 1955 and began work as a teller at the First National Bank in Rhinelander.  It was there at work where she received the devastating news of her father’s early passing.  A few months later, Lois married John (Jack) Nehls on August 3, 1957.  They had five children.  After her third child Lois left her teller position to focus on raising the family.  Later, she began working at Campbells Clothing Store and ultimately returned to First National where she retired in May 1999.  Lois was a life-long member of Zion Evangelical Luthern Church where for many years, she enjoyed singing in the choir.

Lois enjoyed being surrounded by her family, whether it be celebrating family holiday or birthday gatherings, rocking an infant grandchild, or taking boat rides on the Wisconsin River.  She loved looking through her many cookbooks or browsing online for new recipes, dancing to music, especially a good polka, and always looked forward to enjoying a fabulous meal at one of the many wonderful area supper clubs.

She is survived by her five children, Chris (Donna) Nehls, Corey Nehls, Carla (James) Kuczmarski, Craig (Heidi) Nehls, and Carin (Jon) Sandeman; her twelve grandchildren, John, Ryan, Jenna, Tyler, Jordan, Kaitlynn, Jackson, Heaven, Daray, Grace, Ruby, and Ava, plus, a great grandson, Riley, all who brought her joy; her two sisters, Charolotte (Jan) Payne and Anna (Steve) Schnell; and many nieces and nephews (Lisa, thanks for your visits).  She was proceeded in death by her father and mother, stepfather, Edward Underwood, ex-husband, Jack Nehls, and two nephews, Timothy and Trent LeClaire.

The Nehls family deeply appreciates the care, compassion, and kindness the Rennes staff gave to Lois.  There were several special caregivers who truly touched her heart – you know who you are, and we thank you.  The family would also like to thank Aspirus Hospice for bringing comfort during her final weeks.

Visitation for Lois will be held on Thursday, June 11, 2026 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM, with Memorial Services following at 11:00 AM, all at the Zion Evangelical Lutheran Church. Reverend Karl Schultz will be officiating. Interment will be in the Newbold Memorial Cemetery. You may leave your private condolences for the Nehls family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Nehls family.

records.name: Penny Hommerding
records.summary: Penny Hommerding, age 78, of Merrill passed away on May 18, 2026.
records.body:
It is with heavy hearts that we announce the passing of Penny Lee Hommerding, age 78, of Merrill, who left us on Monday, May 18, 2026, at her home.

Penny was born April 30, 1948, in Merrill, daughter of Arthur ‘Shorty’ Burrows Jr. and Ramona (Juedes) Burrows. She graduated from Merrill High School with the class of 1966. On April 29, 1973, Penny married Michael Hommerding.

Penny was truly the heart of her family, always making it a point to cheer on her kids and grandkids at every sporting event. She even took on the role of coaching Little League softball for several years. Her grandkids and great-grandkids meant the world to her, and she looked forward to bringing them treats every Monday after grocery shopping and sharing Saturday morning breakfasts with the whole family.

A big fan of the Milwaukee Brewers and Green Bay Packers, Penny also had a soft spot for Elvis. She loved her flower gardens and took pride in making sure they were beautifully decorated. Cooking and hosting holiday gatherings was something she poured her heart into, bringing everyone together.

Penny was a dedicated member of St. Stephens United Church of Christ in Merrill, where she was an active participant in the funeral lunch committee, counted money, and assisted with the monthly services at Jenny Towers. She also lent a helping hand with the free summer lunch program offered by local churches in the area.

Survivors include her husband, Michael Hommerding of Merrill; sons, Robert (Joyce) Hommerding and Christopher Hommerding (Tina) of Merrill; grandchildren, Nathanial, Ethan (Marisa), and Jesse; great-grandchildren, Oaklynn and Hayden; brother, Mike (Joy) Burrows of Merrill; sisters-in-law, Sharon Detert, Sue (Joel) Wendt, and Shirley Gremler all of Merrill; and several aunts, uncles, nieces, and nephews. Penny was preceded in death by her parents; her in-laws, Bill and Dorothy Hommerding, Willie Detert, and Bob Gremler.

The memorial service will be held at 11:00 A.M., Friday, June 5, 2026, at St. Stephens United Church of Christ, Merrill. Rev. Michael Southcombe will officiate. The family will receive friends beginning at 9:00 A.M. that day at church. As a true tribute to Penny, please wear your favorite Brewers or Packers apparel to the visitation and funeral.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences are encouraged and may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be made in Penny’s name to St. Stephens United Church of Christ – 903 E. Second St., Merrill, WI 54452.

records.name: Phyllis "Cricket" Lorrain Kretschmer
records.summary: Phyllis "Cricket" Lorrain Kretschmer, age 90, of Merrill passed away on May 18, 2026.
records.body:
Phyllis "Cricket" Lorrain Kretschmer, age 90, passed away on May 18, 2026.

Cricket was born in Richland Center, WI to the late Benjamin and Lelah (Harris) Murphy on February 17, 1936. Cricket married Joe Kretschmer on December 15, 1985.

Cricket loved to go square or round dancing with Joe or her friends. She enjoyed sewing, reading and camping with her family. She was an amazing cook and would bake often for her family or her church congregation at the Christ United Methodist Church in Merrill.

She was preceded in death by her parents, Benjamin and Lelah Murphy; brother, Bill Murphy; daughter, Cynthia Booth; and sisters-in-law, Mae Kretschmer and Gloria Dorger.

Cricket is survived by her husband Joe; daughters, Kathleen (Thomas) Hammack and Gail (Timothy) Thompson; step-son, Joseph Kretschmer; step-daughter, Amy (Anthony) Bavinck; many grandchildren, many many great-grandchildren, and countless other family and friends.

Visitation will be held on Tuesday, May 26, 2026, at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral Home, 301 S Eagle Dr, Merrill, WI 54452, from 11:00 A.M. to 1:00 P.M. Following Cricket's wishes a small prayer service will be held at the end of visitation at 1:00 P.M.

records.name: Robert K. Schmitt
records.summary: Robert "Bob" Kenneth Schmitt, age 95, of Menomonee Falls, Wisconsin passed away on April 24, 2026.
records.body:
Robert "Bob" Kenneth Schmitt, 95, of Menomonee Falls, Wisconsin passed away peacefully during the early morning of April 24th, 2026.

Bob was born in Milwaukee, WI on March 27th, 1931 to Arthur and Hilda Schmitt. At a young age, he experienced the loss of his mother, Hilda, and was lovingly raised by his stepmother, Pearl.

He proudly served his country as a U.S. Army Veteran during the Korean War and carried that pride throughout his life. In later years, he was honored to participate in an Honor Flight and was invited back to South Korea, a meaningful experience he treasured deeply. Bob remained committed to supporting fellow veterans and was an active and dedicated member of the VFW, including his local Post 382.

Bob was married to the love of his life and his "beauty", Kay, for the last 67 years. Together they enjoyed hosting lively parties with family and friends, antiquing adventures, and trying their luck gambling. Side by side they truly lived life to the fullest.

He was a proud and devoted father to his three sons, Daniel, Gary, and Gregory. Nothing brought Bob more joy than being a grandfather to his six grandchildren and a great-grandfather to his three great-grandchildren. He embraced his role as Grandpa wholeheartedly and took great pride in being present, supportive, and loving in their lives.

Bob will be remembered for his gift of storytelling, his warm sense of humor, and his ability to make everyone around him laugh. he loved sharing stories, reminiscing about the past, and bringing joy to family gatherings. His warmth, wisdom, and playful spirit made him the best dad and grandpa anyone could ask for and a beloved presence to all who knew him.

He was preceded in death by his parents and many cherished friends and family members.

Bob's legacy lives on through the family he loved so deeply. He will be missed beyond words and remembered always for his love, laughter, and the countless memories he leaves behind.

A service will be held at St. Mary's Cemetery, N88 W136 Main St., Menomonee Falls, Wisconsin 53051, on Friday, May 29th, at 11:00 am. Please gather at the mausoleum.

records.name: Roger Alvin Baumgart
records.summary: Roger Alvin Baumgart, age 88 passed away on May 18, 2026.
records.body:
Roger Alvin Baumgart, 88, of Merrill, passed away peacefully on May 18, 2026, surrounded by his loving family and under the compassionate care of hospice at Bell Tower Residence.

Roger was born in 1937 to Alvin and Lillian Baumgart. He graduated from Merrill Senior High School in 1955. On November 12, 1960, he married the love of his life, Donna Marie Nelson. Together they shared more than 65 wonderful years of marriage, building a life centered on love, family, and faith. Roger was a member of Our Savior’s Lutheran Church and was baptized, confirmed and married at Bethlehem Lutheran Church both in Merrill.

Roger was a devoted husband, father, grandfather, and great-grandfather. He and Donna raised two children, Brian and Lisa, whom he loved deeply and guided through life with quiet strength and enduring wisdom. Though a man of few words, Roger’s love and pride for his family were always evident.

His greatest joy came from spending time with his family, especially his grandchildren and great-grandchildren. His eyes would light up at the mention of their names, and he took immense pride in following their activities, milestones, and accomplishments.

Roger proudly served in the Army National Guard and was deployed to Fort Lewis in Tacoma, Washington, during the Berlin Crisis. After returning home, he worked at the IGA Grocery Store before beginning a long career with Asbestos Local 127, where he worked for more than 35 years.

A talented athlete throughout his life, Roger loved playing baseball for the Merrill Giants and Rangers. He also enjoyed fast pitch and slow pitch softball, city league basketball, bowling, and later in life, pitching horseshoes and playing golf. In retirement, Roger and Donna spent more than 25 years wintering in Orange Beach, Alabama, creating treasured memories and enjoying many visits with family.

Roger was preceded in death by his parents; his brothers, Beryl, Larry, and LaVern Baumgart; and his sister, Gloria Noel.

He is lovingly survived by his wife, Donna; his son, Brian (Lori) Baumgart; and his daughter, Lisa (Brad) Peck, grandchildren, Amanda (Jon) Washa, Brad (Stephanie) Baumgart, Trevor (Megan) Peck, and Molly (fiancé Morgan Rode) Peck; and his beloved great-grandchildren, Maci and Tucker Baumgart, and Aoife and Seamus Peck. Roger is further survived by his brothers, Dick (Gay) and Virgil (Marilyn) Baumgart; his sister, Mary Rutell; his sisters-in-law, Julie Baumgart and Dianne Jach; and many nieces and nephews who will all remember him with love and gratitude.

Roger will be remembered for his quiet kindness, steady presence, strong work ethic, and deep love for his family. His legacy lives on in the many lives he touched.

A funeral service for Roger will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, June 6, 2026, at Our Saviour’s Lutheran Church in Merrill, with Pastor Justin Smoot officiating. Visitation will take place from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service.

Roger’s family would like to extend their heartfelt gratitude for the exceptional care and compassion provided by the staff at Bell Tower Residence and Interim Hospice.

Donations in Roger’s name may be made to Bell Tower Residence or the Community Food Pantry of Merrill.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation of Merrill is assisting the family with the arrangements. Online condolences are encouraged and may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Steven B. Petri
records.summary: Steven B. Petri, age 69, of Weston passed away on May 18, 2026.
records.body:
Steven B. Petri, 69, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on Monday, May 18, 2026, at Edgewater Haven in Port Edwards, Wisconsin under the care of Heartland Hospice.

He was born on July 20, 1956, in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, son of the late Delmar and Helen (Hotchkiss) Petri. Steven married his high school sweetheart, Cheryl Cramer on May 22, 1976, in Milwaukee. Their union spanned nearly 53 years, filled with shared adventures and love. A proud veteran, Steven served honorably in the United States Army from 1976 to 1979, including six months stationed in Germany. His military service was a source of deep pride throughout his life.

As a long-haul truck driver for an impressive 42 years, Steven traveled over two million miles without a single accident, a testament to his skill and dedication behind the wheel. He truly loved being on the road, finding joy in every journey. After returning from Texas in 1989, he devoted 16 years to the city of Wausau, working in various roles within public works.

Steven was known for his vibrant personality — a jokester and captivating storyteller, he brought warmth and laughter wherever he went. At the nursing home, he earned the affectionate nickname “the music man” because of his love for diverse musical styles. Concerts and country festivals were some of his favorite places to create happy memories with his family. Steven cherished traveling across America with Cheryl, delighting in discovering special cafés along the way. He enjoyed camping, fishing, and spending quality time with his grandchildren.

Survivors include his wife, Cheryl Petri of Weston; three children, Steven (Kerry Martin) Petri, Jr. of Cottage Grove, Wisconsin; Shannon (David) Cronce of Wausau; and Jason (Morgan Brace) Petri of Sarasota, Florida; his four grandchildren, Aubrey Anne, Ava Ann, Owen Michael, and Logan Steven.  He also leaves behind several nieces and nephews, including Jessica (Tony) Sun of Dallas, Texas, and his sister-in-law Nga Goodhal of Brownsville, Texas, who was a great help and support during his final months.

He was preceded in death by his parents and five siblings: Gary, Karen, James, Delmar, and Susan.

A private family service will be held.  Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements.  Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Carol Jean Ashby
records.summary: Carol Jean Ashby, age 78, of Wausau passed away on May 19, 2026.
records.body:
Carol Jean Ashby, age 78, passed away on Tuesday, May 19, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital, Wausau, Wisconsin.

She was born on October 15, 1947, in Huron, South Dakota, the youngest child of the late Frank and Evelyn (Love) Kearns. For over two decades, Carol was a familiar and friendly presence at the Hwy 51 Truck Stop in Merrill, where she served as manager. She was known not only as a diligent and hardworking individual but also as someone who genuinely cared for the many people she encountered daily.

Carol had a great love of birds and amassed a treasured collection of bird figurines. In her leisure, she enjoyed trips to the casino, playing bingo and Texas Hold’em, as well as traveling. Family was the cornerstone of her life, and she loved attending family reunions and gatherings, and delighted in spending quality time with her girls and grandchildren. She was also an enthusiastic supporter of Wisconsin sports teams, often found cheering for the Packers and Brewers with spirited pride.

Carol is lovingly remembered by her three daughters: Jeannine (Curly) Marg of Custer, South Dakota; Denise (Carl) Viviano of Wausau; and Kimberly (Robert) Teske of Marathon; as well as one stepdaughter, Heather (Roy) Freda of Glendale, Arizona. Her legacy continues through her beloved grandchildren: Bethany, Renee (Jacob), Erin (Mitchell), Katlyn (Derek), Christopher (Hannah), Samantha, Billy (Bradly), Robert (Amanda), Bobby (Krystal) Lillianna, and Patrick (Alexandra); and her nine great-grandchildren: Chloe, Oaklynn, Ava, Roman, Hayden, Bennett, Daniel, Soren, and Hudson. She is further survived by two brothers, Dale Kearns of Panama City Beach, Florida, and Duane (Angie) Kearns of Mesick, Michigan, as well as the many nieces and nephews she dearly loved.

She was preceded in death by her parents, her sister Beverly Castagno, and her brothers Darrell (Sonny) Kearns and Donald Kearns.

Memorial service will be held at 11:00 A.M. on Wednesday, May 27, 2026, at Mount Olive Lutheran Church, 6205 Alderson Street, Weston, Wisconsin. Pastor Jordan Balk will officiate. Visitation will take place from 10:00 A.M. until the time of service at the church.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements.  Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Jim Wenzel
records.summary: Jim Wenzel, age 79, of Weston passed away on May 19, 2026.
records.body:
Jim Wenzel, age 79, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on Tuesday, May 19, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center - Weston, surrounded by his loving family.

Jim was born on April 25, 1947, in Wausau, Wisconsin, the son of Glenn and Leatrice (Poeske) Wenzel. He proudly served his country in the National Guard, a commitment that underscored his deep sense of duty and patriotism. Beyond his military service, he was devoted to his local community, contributing his leadership as a Village Trustee for the Village of Weston a role he held with dedication and pride.

An avid outdoorsman, Jim found great joy in hunting and fishing, and cherished time spent at his second home on the lake up north in St. Germain. The natural world was a sanctuary for him, where he could embrace peaceful moments and create lasting memories. He was also a passionate sports fan, delighting in cheering for Wisconsin’s beloved teams: the Packers, Brewers, and Bucks.

Jim’s warm and approachable nature made it easy for him to connect with others; he could hold a conversation with anyone, whether a longtime friend or a stranger. Jim sold plywood and ran propane deliveries, which he was excellent at because of his ability to communicate and talk with anyone. Above all, he treasured spending time with his parents, whose memory he deeply honored throughout his life.

He is survived by his daughter Lynn (Tim) Halambeck; son Brad (Sarah) Wenzel; sister Sue Garnsworthy; and grandchildren Megan Halambeck, Benjamin Halambeck, Andrew Wenzel, and Dillon Morrisey. Jim is preceded in death by his parents and brother-in-law Stan Garnsworthy.

The community and loved ones remember Jim as a man whose integrity, kindness, and vibrant personality touched many lives. As we reflect on his life, we hold dear the moments shared and the legacy he leaves behind a legacy rooted in service, family, and a passion for life.

A private family service will be held. Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Judith "Judie" Kay Urbanek
records.summary: Judith "Judie" Kay Urbanek, age 79, of Wausau passed away on May 19, 2026.
records.body:
Judith “Judie” K. Urbanek, 79, of Wausau, passed away unexpectedly on Tuesday, May 19, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

Judie was born October 28, 1946, in Oshkosh, daughter of the late Norman and Katherine (Rollins) Dahlke. On December 19, 1970, she married Dennis Urbanek.

A dedicated foreign language teacher, Judie shared her love of learning with countless students throughout her career. Zion Lutheran School, Wausau East High School, and St. Mary’s Oratory (where she taught the pre-seminary program for several years) were all fortunate to have her on their faculties. In addition to her classroom teaching, she was a German teacher and generously volunteered her time as a tutor to ensure her students' success.

Judie was also deeply involved in her Zion Church community, serving faithfully as a reader and as a devoted member of the choir. Outside of her work, she found great joy in gardening, traveling, and reading. Above all, she cherished time spent with her friends and her wonderful family, especially her grandchildren.

Survivors include her husband, Dennis Urbanek; her children, Edward (Rebecca) Urbanek, Elise Purves, and Tim Urbanek; her beloved grandchildren, Maxwell, Malcolm, and Mirabelle Urbanek, and Orla Purves; her sister, Kathie (Gary) McFarlane; and numerous nieces, nephews, other relatives, and friends.

She was preceded in death by her parents.

A funeral service will be held at 11:00 AM on Monday, June 1, 2026, at Zion Lutheran Church, Wausau. Visitation will be held at the church on Monday from 9:30 AM until the time of the service.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made in Judie’s name to Zion Lutheran Church.

records.name: Scott Robert Durkee
records.summary: Scott Robert Durkee, age 63, of Mosinee passed away on May 19, 2026.
records.body:
Scott Durkee, age 63, of Mosinee, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on Tuesday, May 19, 2026, at the Aspirus Hospice House surrounded by his loving family and friends.

Scott was born on April 22, 1963, in Wausau, Wisconsin, the son of Wallace and Marjorie (Hoskin) Durkee. A proud graduate of Wausau West High School in 1981, Scott's commitment to education continued as he earned his bachelor's degree in accounting from Upper Iowa University in 2007. His professional journey was distinguished by over 24 years of dedicated service at United Healthcare, where his work ethic, positive attitude, and humor left a lasting impression on co-workers and customers alike. He married the love of his life Jeaninne Rux in 2006.

Scott's 23 years of service in the Army Reserve, culminating in the rank of Sergeant, reflected his deep sense of duty and discipline. Those who served with him remember a man of integrity, courage, and steadfast loyalty.

Beyond his professional achievements and military service, Scott was known for his infectious sense of humor and the big smile that greeted everyone he met. He was a passionate supporter of the Green Bay Packers, Milwaukee Brewers, Milwaukee Bucks, and Wisconsin Badgers. He was often seen cheering with a bottle of Miller Lite in hand. His love of the outdoors drew him to camping adventures shared with friends, family, and his beloved dogs. Music, a lifelong passion, provided rhythm and joy to his everyday life. Scott sang and played bass guitar in several bands including Taceyon, 3 on Fire, Kliff Klavon, After You, and 5 Mile Scream.

One of Scott’s proudest accomplishments was his remarkable bowling career, during which he scored an incredible fourteen perfect games, earning him a well-deserved induction into the United States Bowling Congress Wausau Hall of Fame in March 2024. This honor speaks not only to his skill but to the dedication and perseverance that defined his approach to life.

Scott is survived by his wife of 20 years, Jeaninne Durkee; his collies Edith Ann and Rogan, his sister Gena Geng, brothers Dave (Kay) Durkee, Doug Durkee, and Dale (Renee) Durkee; and brother-in-law Mike (Jan) Rux and several nieces and nephews.

He is preceded in death by his parents, grandparents and his beloved dogs, Brady, Casey, Tucker, Jordy and Blaze.

A celebration of life service for Scott will be held on Saturday, June 20, 2026, at 11:00AM at Restlawn Memorial Park Chapel, 235962 North Troy Street, Wausau, WI 54403, Visitation will take place from 10:00AM until the of service at 11:00AM. Military Honors will follow the service.

Scott's family would like to extend a sincere Thank You to everyone at Aspirus Hospice in Home Care and the Aspirus Hospice House for 13 weeks of amazing compassionate care.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Alfred "Alfie" F. Hoefs
records.summary: Alfred "Alfie" F. Hoefs, age 78, of Stratford passed away on May 20, 2026.
records.body:
Alfred “Alfie” Hoefs, 78, Stratford, passed away on Wednesday, May 20, 2026 at his home.

A celebration of life will be held at the Country Aire Banquet Center from 1-4pm on Thursday June 4th.

Alfie was born on April 30, 1948 in Marshfield to Charles and Alma (Reichert) Hoefs. He graduated from Stratford High School. He was united in marriage to Tina Laessig on December 8, 1990.

Alfie was a hardworking dairy farmer whose life revolved around family and friends. A true outdoorsman, he could often be found making firewood, making maple syrup, backhoeing and digging, or buying machinery at auctions. Known as quite the talker, Alfie never met a stranger that he couldn’t talk to and loved gathering with family and friends for breakfasts. Sunday mornings often included listening to the Sunday Polka Jamboree. His grandchildren held a special place in his heart, and he cherished every opportunity to be involved in their lives, especially when it meant giving tractor rides. Alfie also took pride in caring for his lilac and snowball bushes.

He is survived by his wife, Tina, sons, Joey (Sara) Hoefs, Randy (Mia) Hoefs, Brian (Jasmine) Hoefs, and Tim, and grandchildren, Zoey, Zara, Norah, Clayton, Creed, Morgan, and Quinn.

He is further survived by his sisters, Ione (Wilmer) Landwehr of Stratford and Diane (Leon) Lueddecke of Unity, and sister in law, Lorraine Hoefs.

He was preceded in death by his parents, brother, John Hoefs, sisters, Ruth Correll and Edna Landwehr, brother in law, Ivan Landwehr, and nephews, John Jr. and Donald Hoefs.

records.name: Ann L. Fondow
records.summary: Ann L. Fondow, age 84, of Merrill passed away on May 20, 2026.
records.body:
Ann Fondow, age 84, a resident of the Town of Pine River, passed away at Aspirus Hospice House in Wausau on Wednesday, May 20, 2026. Ann was born to Charles and Lorraine Steinke on March 30, 1942. She was a proud “Yooper” who graduated from Kingsford High School and Eastern Michigan University.

After graduating from Eastern Michigan University, Ann was the fine women’s fashion buyer for the HC Prange department stores, a children’s librarian, a substitute teacher for the Green Bay Public Schools and then was hired as a full time teacher, first for an at-risk program for high school students and then as a Family and Consumer Science teacher. A few years before retirement, Ann started a health services academy and taught CNA and medical terminology courses. In addition to her many other talents, Ann had a remarkable sense of style, and she made clothes look good on her.

Ann had two brothers, Charles and Roy, who preceded her in death. Ann is survived by her son, Jerome Charles Glaeser, Jerome’s French Bulldog, Poppy Starr, cousins Cynthia Steinke and Kathy Dillhey, sisters in law Patricia Steinke and Jane Steinke, and five nephews, including Charles, Jeff, and Steve Steinke and Jared and Roy Steinke. She is also survived by her husband, Terry Fondow.

A memorial service will be held on Saturday, May 30, 2026, at St. Stephens, UCC, Merrill, Wi at a time to be determined.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation of Merrill is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences are encouraged and may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com.

records.name: Carol Lattimer
records.summary: Carol (Riehle) Lattimer, 94, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on May 5, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.
records.body:
Carol (Riehle) Lattimer, 94, Wausau passed away peacefully on May 5, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital with family by her side.

She was born August 20, 1931, in Wausau, daughter of Carl Klimek and Dorothy (Klimek) Pabst.

Carol worked at American Can/James River Corp, and was the first woman supervisor on a production line, for the majority of her work career. She was an avid Green Bay Packer fan and watched all of the regular games, with her mother until she passed, and still continued to watch even with her failing eyesight. She loved the casinos and the game show network. She would never forget a birthday and would always make it a point to send a card.

Survivors include six children, Cassandra Nelson, Alan Riehle, Deborah Riehle, Cynthia Riehle, Gary (Tina) Riehle and Lori Oliva; sister, Catherine Klimek; brother, Robert (Nancy) Klimek; eight grandchildren and eight great-grandchildren, along with many nieces and nephews.

She was preceded in death by her first husband, Frederick Riehle, sisters, Barbara Schilling and Marcia Grisa, sons-in-law, Bruce Nelson and Frank Oliva, brothers-in-law, Anton Schilling and Wally Grisa.

Mother will be greatly missed by her family and all that knew her.

A celebration of life is planned for June 14, 2026, from 1:00 pm until 3:00 pm at Oliva's at Gulliver's Landing located at 151701 Gadwall Lane, Wausau.

records.name: Dauna Faye Gollhardt
records.summary: Dauna Faye Gollhardt, age 91, of Rhinelander passed away on May 20, 2026.
records.body:
Dauna Faye Gollhardt, age 91 of Rhinelander, passed away peacefully on May 20th at the Friendly Village nursing home in Rhinelander.

Dauna was born on November 9th, 1934 to Allen and Alma Hermann in Sheboygan, Wisconsin. She was married to Gene Gollhardt for 61 years. Dauna enjoyed spending time golfing and traveling with her husband. Together they enjoyed traveling around the country with friends and visiting family. They spent most of their winters in Florida as snowbirds. Dauna loved her family very much. She often held endearing nicknames for her family members and in turn she herself earned a title as "the Pajama Grandma".

Dauna; is survived by her two daughters, Susan and (Kevin) Zuege, Eureka Mt, Karen (Bill) Anderson, Rhinelander WI, three grandchildren, Nathan (Kirsten) Zuege, Lyndhurst Ohio, Kyle Zuege, Painsville Ohio, and Jared Zuege (Amanda Cain), and great-grandsons, Everett Zuege, Garrett Zuege, and great-granddaughter, Isla Zuege. Dauna is further survived by nieces, nephews, other family members and friends.

Dauna is preceded in death by her husband Gene Gollhardt, two brothers, Jerry and Wallace Hermann, sister Sandra Fickau, and grandson, Ian Thomas Zuege.

The family would like to extend a special thank you to Howard Young Hospital in Woodruff, Aspirus Hospice, and Friendly Village Nursing Home in Rhinelander for such exceptional care, comfort, and compassion.

A private family celebration of life will be held at a future date. You may leave your private condolences for the Gollhardt family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is handling the arrangements for the family.

records.name: David L. Kaminski
records.summary: David L. Kaminski, 75, of Mosinee, passed away peacefully on May 18, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.
records.body:
David L. Kaminski, 75, Mosinee passed away peacefully on Monday, May 18, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born March 7, 1951 in Wausau, son of the late Wallace and Genevieve (Koback) Kaminski. On November 24, 1979 he married Carol Weinke at St. Paul Catholic Church, Mosinee. She survives.

For 37 years Dave was a carpenter at Wausau Homes in Kronenwetter. Dave liked to do wood working, he liked to do puzzles, and enjoyed admiring classic cars. Dave loved to spend time with family and friends. He loved helping organize family reunions and deciding what he would make for raffles. He also liked to play dominoes and play cards. He enjoyed volunteering at the Polish Fest in the Popcorn and Ice Cream stand each year, and at many other church functions. He was a big fan of Elvis and loved to collect memorabilia and attend Elvis impersonator shows.

Survivors include his wife Carol Kaminski, Mosinee, his children, Stanley Phillips Jr., Menominee and Bonnie (Matthew) Cortright, Wausau, two grandchildren, Jackson and Kade Phillips and his siblings, Gene Kaminski, Rothschild and Donnie Kaminski, Wausau and many nieces and nephews.

Besides his parents he was preceded in death by his siblings, Barney (Karen) Kaminski, Caroline Kaminski, Virginia (Jimmy) Waters, Barbara Salyers, Marlene (Lenny) Kell, Dennis(Dottie) Kaminski, Susie (Jerry) Fischer and sister-in-law Ramona Kaminski.

Funeral Services will be held at 11:00 a.m. Friday, May 29, 2026 at Our Savior National Catholic Church, Mosinee. The Rev. Marion Talaga will preside. Burial will be in the church cemetery. Visitation will be held on Thursday May 28, 2026 from 4:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau and again on Friday from 10:00 a.m. until the time of services at the church.

records.name: Dorothy K. Rathjen
records.summary: Dorothy "Dottie" Kim Rathjen, 66, passed away on May 2, 2026.
records.body:
Dorothy "Dottie" Kim Rathjen, 66, passed away on May 2, 2026. Born on July 3, 1959, Dottie lived a life rooted in faith, compassion, service, and creativity, leaving a lasting impression on all who knew her.

Dottie was a lifelong learner who pursued her education with dedication and purpose. She graduated from Northcentral Technical College in 1979 with an Associate of Arts degree in Secretarial Science with an emphasis in Medicine. In 1984, she achieved her Certified Professional Secretary (CPS) designation, reflecting her professionalism and commitment to excellence.

Continuing her education, Dottie graduated from Wartburg College in 1987 with a degree in Business Administration emphasizing Management. During her time at Wartburg, she was an active and respected member of campus life. She belonged to Lambda Beta Phi, served on the Educational Policy Committee as a student representative to the faculty, participated in the chapel choir, and received the prestigious Dell Award for Peace and Justice, an honor recognizing a senior dedicated to the integration and acceptance of all members of society.

Throughout her career, Dottie served in many meaningful roles, including transcriptionist, medical secretary, executive assistant, law enforcement/E911 dispatcher, and volunteer Emergency Medical Technician. In every position, she brought compassion, professionalism, and a servant's heart.

Dottie found her greatest joy and peace in her faith and in the life of St. John's Episcopal Church. The serenity and sacred rhythms of church life were a constant source of comfort and fulfillment for her.

Outside of church, Dottie embraced life's simple and creative joys. She loved gardening, music, and the companionship of her beloved dogs. Her artistic spirit was evident in her many creative pursuits, including drawing, knitting, crocheting, chalk art, and a wide variety of crafts that she lovingly shared with others.

Dottie is survived by her mother, JoAnn Rathjen; her sisters, Joy Rathjen of Eau Claire, Wisconsin, and Kay (Alan) Tulppo of Eagle River, Wisconsin, and nephew Bernard Schoneberg (Eau Claire, Wisconsin) along with many extended family members, friends, and members of her church community who will deeply miss her gentle spirit and caring heart.

She was preceded in death by her father, Henry Rathjen; her grandparents, Bernard and Dorothy Pfister and Henry and Erna Rathjen; and her uncle, Duane Pfister.

Dottie's life was a testament to kindness, faith, acceptance, and service. Her quiet strength, creativity, and compassionate heart will be remembered always.

records.name: E. Wyleen High
records.summary: E. Wyleen High passed away.
records.body:
Wyleen was above all a God-loving woman. She was a loving sister to Marion, LaVon, Lois, Joanne, and Jean, all preceding her in death.

She was an amazing wife, mother, grandmother, and aunt.

Wyleen spent many years volunteering for multiple organizations including being a Girl Scout leader and a church worker. She served as an officer in the women's groups of both parishes she belonged to. For as long as she was physically able, she worked at all church events. She was a church organist for many years.

She was an avid birder; she organized a birding club which had more than 70 active members when she retired. During the 10 years she lived in the Northwoods she held a monthly women's breakfast in her home.

She was a Registered Nurse. She left nursing only when she became a single mother of 3 young children and wanted a job that would have her at home when her children were home. She became a teacher's aide at Brookfield Central High School where she met Clark. They became best friends and had a marriage that lasted more than 30 years ending only at her death.

Wyleen was a seamstress and made most of her children's clothes through elementary school. She was a quilter and received many ribbons. She was a crafter and had her own machines in her husband's shop. She did wood carving.

She served as the 1969 State Chairperson for the American Heart Association campaign.

Overall, Wyleen was just a wonderful human being, and she will be missed tremendously.

records.name: Gerald L. Tritten
records.summary: Gerald L. Tritten, 81, passed away peacefully on May 16, 2026, at Care Partners Assisted Living in Weston.
records.body:
Gerald L. Tritten, 81, passed away peacefully on Saturday, May 16, 2026 at Care Partners Assisted Living in Weston under Hospice Care.

He was born May 18, 1944 in Wausau, son of the late Lawrence and Anna (Zimmerman) Tritten. On April 1, 1978 he married Charlotte Hackbarth in Wausau. She survives.

Gerald and Charlotte lived in the Merrill area until his declining health 3 years ago and was moved to Care Partners, not quite a year ago.

Gerald started working at Connors in Wausau until they closed their doors and then he went to work at the Town of Texas Quarry. Following that he went to work in the maintenance department at Lintec until his retirement at 62.

Gerald loved hunting and fishing with a passion and had a deep love for his tractors. He also enjoyed sitting on the porch and watch Nature pass bye. Gerald loved shooting the breeze with anyone that came around.

Survivors include his wife of 48 years Charlotte Tritten, Wausau, one sister, Mary Schepp, Wausau, five sister-in-law, (AKA) the Hackbarth girls, Linda (Dale) Barttelt, Sheboygan, Karen (Roger) Erdman, Wausau, Debbie Hackbarth, Wausau, Denise (Ken) Sala, Wausau and Brenda (Dave) Blaubach, Wausau, two aunt-in-laws, Virginia (Gilbert) Erdman and Adeline Krause and 22 nieces and nephews, Susie and Patrick, Sandy, Lisa, Matthew, Linda, Amanda and Ted, Randy and Reba, Kasey and Megan, Travis and Kelly, Jamie and Josh, Cale and Sara, Cody and Zack, Reanna and Rahul. He is further survived by 13 great nieces and nephews, Lucas, Jacob, Wheylan, Jolene, Clara, Emma, Addyson, Grace, Kylee, Croy, Selena, Allen and Hunter.

Gerald was preceded in death by a brother-in-law, Butch Schepp and one sister-in-law, Rose Hackbarth.

At Gerald's request there will be no formal services. In lieu of flowers memorials for Gerald may be directed to Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau, WI, 54403.

records.name: Gordon P. Salmi
records.summary: Gordon Paul Salmi, 85, passed away on May 9, 2026.
records.body:
Gordon Paul Salmi, age 85, passed away on May 9th, 2026. He was born on July 7th, 1940, to the late Reino Salmi and Josephine Johnson in Dollar Bay, Michigan. He grew up in Dollar Bay where he worked on the family farm.

In his younger years, Gordon proudly served in the military. He later built a legacy through his work in construction, helping build bridges across the United States. Gordon took immense pride in his work and the many places his career allowed him to see.

He shared a beautiful marriage of 36 years with his late wife, Janet, who recently preceded him in death. Together they enjoyed traveling, praying daily, and living a life grounded in deep faith and love.

Gordon was known for his gift of storytelling and his warm conversations with everyone he met. He often shared what he believed were the three keys to life: "Eat right, exercise, and be happy." He also had a special love for his dogs, whom he spoiled dearly and treated like family.

He was preceded in death by; his parents Reino Salmi and Josephine Johnson, wife Janet Salmi, daughter Cheryl Salmi, and son Paul Salmi.

Gordon is survived by his children, Brenda Harper, Richard Salmi, Beverly Raisanen, Kris Lepisto, and Travis Salmi; his niece, Melody Holmes; his grandchildren, extended family, and many friends who will miss his wisdom, humor, and loving heart.

records.name: Janette C. Jenson
records.summary: Janette Choitz Jenson, 86, of Edgerton, passed away peacefully on May 7, 2026, at her home in Verona.
records.body:
Janette Choitz Jenson, of Edgerton, passed away peacefully on Thursday, May 7, 2026, at her home in Verona with her family by her side.

Janette was born in Edgerton, WI on January 3, 1940, to the late Martin and Georgina Burkhardt. Her early years were spent growing up in Merrill, WI where she graduated from Merrill High School in 1958. She attended Wisconsin State College in La Crosse, where she met the love of her life, Carl Choitz. Janette and Carl married on April 28, 1962, and settled in the Edgerton/Whitewater area. In November of 1964 they welcomed their only daughter, Carin. Carl was a young teacher and coach when he was tragically killed in a traffic accident in January of 1965. From that day on, Janette demonstrated extraordinary strength, courage and perseverance as she continued her education and received both a Bachelor's and Master's degree in Education. She taught in the Edgerton and Milton School Districts for 28 years, retiring in 2000.

Post-retirement Janette kept remarkably busy, devoting her time and talents to volunteerism in the community, her many crafts and hobbies, and spending time with family and friends. She was a dedicated member of Central Lutheran Church in Edgerton where she volunteered with the Krazy Quilters sewing club and for other special functions. Her volunteerism extended to the American Cancer Society's Relay for Life, an annual event she organized for a number of years, as she was a breast cancer survivor herself. Janette was a 'Jill of all trades'; there weren't too many hobbies or crafts that she didn't try over the years. Some of her favorites were vegetable and flower gardening, canning and freezing vegetables, woodworking, caning chairs, knitting and crocheting, cross stitching, and making greeting cards, just to name a few. Her most accomplished craft, however, was sewing. Janette was an adept seamstress; in her early years she predominantly made clothes, and after her retirement she took up quilting. She loved her weekly sewing clubs and annual quilting retreats, and the hundreds of quilts and projects she created over the decades are treasured gifts.

Janette led an active life and was involved in a bowling league and women's golf league in her middle age. She was also an avid lifelong Badgers, Packers and Brewers fan. Janette was fortunate to travel to Europe, Alaska and Hawaii and many places in between. She was blessed with many close friends and neighbors throughout her life. But what Janette treasured most in her life was the time she got to spend with her family, particularly her three grandkids. She cherished every holiday tradition, celebrated all of life's milestones, cheered the loudest at every sporting event, and was devoted to loving and supporting everything else in between-she was always there for her family.

Janette is survived by her daughter, Carin (Dan) Higgins of Verona, WI.; grandchildren, Andrew (Amanda) Higgins of Stoughton, WI., Lindsay (Jonathan) Palutke of Lodi, WI., Erica (Sean) Casey of Chicago, IL.; two great-grandchildren, Peter Higgins and Maria Higgins; her brother, Martin (Kathy) Burkhardt of Wausau, WI.; and two nephews and their families. She was preceded in death by her parents and her husband.

The family would like to extend a special thank you to the nurses and staff with Agrace HospiceCare, Fitchburg that provided comfort for Janette, during her final days.

A memorial service will be held at ST. JAMES LUTHERAN CHURCH, 427 S. Main St., Verona, WI at 11:30 a.m. on Thursday, May 21, 2026, with Pastor Sarah Schilcher presiding. A luncheon will be served at the church following the service. Family and friends who wish to view the service via LIVESTREAM may visit Janette's obituary page at www.gundersonfh.com at the time of the service and click on "Watch Now" in the Webcast section. Visitation will be held at the church from 10 a.m. until the time of the service on Thursday. A private burial will take place at Woodlawn Cemetery in Plymouth, WI, on Friday, May 22, 2026.

In lieu of flowers donations may be made in Janette's honor to the Carbone Cancer Center – University of Wisconsin-Madison and Agrace HospiceCare, Fitchburg, WI.

records.name: Joyce J. Langner
records.summary: Joyce Jean (Schroeder) Langner, 86, of Medford, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly on May 7, 2026.
records.body:
Joyce Jean (Schroeder) Langner, 86, of Medford, WI, passed away unexpectedly on Thursday, May 7, 2026, at Aspirus Medford Nursing and Rehabilitation Center.

She was born in Merrill, WI, on August 24, 1939, the daughter of Charles and Rosella Gryskiewicz.

She married Frank Schroeder Sr. October 18th, 1958. They made their home in Butternut, WI, on the Schroeder family farm until an untimely wood's accident took her husband's life on March 25, 1974.

She later moved her children to Park Falls, where she found love again and married a wonderful man, Ben Langner, who raised her children as his own. They shared 36 blessed years together before his passing in 2020.

She raised 6 children, worked as a nurse's aide, was a farm wife, and volunteered at the Medford nursing home reading the bible to residents. She loved family gatherings, cookouts, and dancing to polka music. Her faith was strong. She loved her church and to share Gods word.

She was preceded in death by her husband's Frank Schroeder Sr (1974), Ben C. Langner (2020), her parents, Charles and Rosella Gryskiewicz; her son, Ricky Charles; and two sisters, Pat Frank and JoAnn Richardson.

She is survived by her son, John (Kathy) Schroeder of Butternut, WI; her son, Frank Schroeder Jr. of Mercer, WI; her daughter, Lori (Joel) Brost of Medford, WI; her daughter, Barbara (Richard) Rienow of Park Falls, WI; her son, Mike Schroeder of Butternut, WI; and her son, Joseph Schroeder of Park Falls, WI. She is also survived by eight grandchildren and twelve great-grandchildren, all of whom she loved dearly with all her heart. 2 sister in-laws, Pauline (Bill) Fenzl and Dorthy (John) Luhtala of Park Falls, numerous nieces, nephews, and other family and friends.

Celebration of life will be held on June 20, 2026, at 751 Grace Way 1st Baptist church in Medford, WI 54451. Family gathering will start at 9:00a.m. The church service will be at 10:00a.m. with lunch to follow.

records.name: Kevin Joseph Schuster
records.summary: Kevin Joseph Schuster, age 53, of Schofield passed away on May 20, 2026.
records.body:
Kevin Joseph Schuster, 53, passed away unexpectedly on Wednesday, May 20, 2026, in Schofield.

Kevin was born on January 13, 1973, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to Kenneth and Kathleen (Woznicki) Schuster. He graduated from D.C. Everest Senior High in 1991.

From a young age, Kevin found a passion for baking, which evolved into a love for everything related to food - its creation, its presentation, and its service. His career spanned working in restaurants, restaurant management, and ownership; most recently in food service sales. Special to him were trips to Golden Corral with his daughter, Madisyn, for the eighth wonder of the world - the chocolate fountain.

Kevin enjoyed mountain bike racing with Madisyn, as well as spending time at his cottage on White Potato Lake. There, you could find Kevin engaging in pontoon rides, fishing, ice fishing, and socializing with friends. Over the years, many family camping trips were shared with his siblings and extended family.

Kevin is survived by his daughter, Madisyn Schuster (fiance, Alex Legatt) of Prior Lake, MN; mother, Kathy Schuster of Weston; his siblings, Jamie (Scott) Lerch of Weston, Craig (Kate) Schuster of Wausau, and Cindy (Nate) Hall of Weston; nieces and nephews, Keri (Zach) Neuner of Wausau, Kayla (Isaac) Frieders of Kronenwetter, Ashton Schuster of Charlotte, NC, Colton, Marcus, and Kirsten Hall, all of Weston, Casper and Linus Schuster of Wausau; great-nephews, Theo Neuner and Cal and Remi Frieders; and step-sons, Tyler Krzanowski and Blake Wozniak. He is further survived by many friends and extended family other relatives and friends. He is preceded in death by his father, Kenny Schuster.

An Open House Celebration of Life will be held from 4:00 - 7:00 PM on Friday, May 29, 2026, at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston, WI 54476. In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to the National Pancreas Foundation.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Leslee J. Boers
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Leslee Jeyne Boers, age 62, died on May 12, 2026, following a four-year battle with COPD.
records.body:
Leslee Jeyne Boers, age 62, died Tuesday, May 12, 2026, following a four-year battle with COPD.

Leslee was born on October 4, 1963, to Patricia Boers in Antigo. She attended Antigo High School and North Central Technical College. She spent most of her life working and managing in retail, including The Cutlass Motor Lodge, AC Liquidators, Goodwill, and others.

Leslee was also a caretaker for many, including her various exotic pets, which included ferrets and skunks. Leslee spent her final weeks in life learning God's word and was surrounded by family and friends.

Leslee is survived by her son, Cody (Patti) Boers of Aniwa; her brother, Tom (Sue) Boers of Antigo; her great-niece, Alexis Paszek of Wausau; her four grandchildren; her nieces and nephews; and her pet skunk, Bebop.

Private services are being held.

records.name: MaryAnn H. Luther
records.summary: MaryAnn H. Luther, age 84 passed away on May 20, 2026.
records.body:
MaryAnn H. (Faillaci) Luther, 84, passed away May 20, 2026.

She was born September 28, 1941, daughter of the late Santo and Frances (Campo) Faillaci. On October 11, 1969 she married Melvin Luther Jr. and he preceded her in death on March 16, 2020.

Survivors include her daughter, Malanie Burley; grandchildren, Isabella Javidan, Benjamin Javidan and Charles Sparrow.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated 4:30 pm Friday, June 26, 2026 at St. Michael Catholic Church, Wausau. Rev. Tom Lindner will preside. Visitation will be Friday from 3:30 pm until the time of service all at St. Michael Catholic Church, Wausau.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Patricia A. Schoenherr
records.funeral_home: Brainard Funeral Home & Cremation Center of Wausau
records.summary: Patricia A. Schoenherr, born in Mauston, Wisconsin, passed away on May 14, 2026.
records.body:
Patricia (Pat) Anne Schoenherr, went home to Heaven on May 14. Pat is survived by daughters Susan Stratta (Paul Bass) of Omaha, Nebraska and Kim Ramquist (James) of Bloomington, Minnesota and sons Christopher (Diane) of Rochester, Minnesota, and Kent (Beth) of West Lakeland Township, Minnesota and grandchildren Michael and Erin Stratta, Grayson (Nicole) Vangen, Raela and Lowell R. (Elizabeth) Schoenherr and great-grandchildren Reed, Wyatt, Rizbeth, Joyce, Ethan and Leah Patricia. She was preceded in death by her parents, and by her husband of sixty-two years, Lowell E. Schoenherr.

Pat was born in Mauston, Wisconsin on April 4, 1930, to Patrick and Mary Brunner (Nate). She met her husband Lowell while attending the University of Wisconsin – Madison. She finished both her bachelor's and master's degrees years later while working full time and being an amazing mother and wife.

Pat was part of the original on-air team at WAOW, Channel 9 in Wausau when the station began broadcasting in 1965. After her TV career she spent over two decades teaching communications at what is now North Central Technical College. After she and Lowell retired, they enjoyed traveling together.

She will be remembered as a loving wife, wonderful mother and a great friend to the wide circle of ladies she routinely met with for investment club, golf and lunch.

The family extends a special thanks to her caregivers, Angie, Courtney and Angela, whose care allowed her to stay in her beloved condo overlooking Wausau's west side until her passing.

A Mass of Christian burial will take place on June 12 at 11:00am at St. Mathew's Catholic Church, 227 South 28th Avenue, Wausau, Wisconsin. A reception with light refreshments will follow immediately after Mass. Pat will be interred with her husband at Arlington National Cemetery at a later date.

Memorials may be directed to a charity of choice.

records.name: Ralph Rutkowski
records.summary: Ralph Rutkowski, 87, of Shantytown, passed away on May 14, 2026, at Cardinal Grove in Whiting under hospice care.
records.body:
Ralph Rutkowski of Shantytown passed away at Cardinal Grove in Whiting on May 14, 2026, under the care of hospice.

Ralph was born in Shantytown on September 21, 1938, on the Bentley homestead to Nick and Iva (Bentley) Rutkowski.

He went to Countyline School and graduated from Rosholt High School in 1957, and attended UW Stevens Point.

He worked on the family farm, bused kids to Wausau, and was a Postal Carrier for Stevens Point Post Office.

Ralph loved feeding deer, birds, and his cats. He was an avid reader and spent most of his life doing what he loved: painting and creating artwork.

He is survived by one sister, Carol Zick, sister-in-law Anne Rutkowski and many nieces and nephews.

Ralph is preceded in death by 4 brothers, Chester, Charles, Irv, and Harold, and 5 sisters, Margie, Dorothy, Patty, Lorraine, and Arlene.

A private graveside service will be held at a later date.

records.name: Robert J. Henning
records.summary: Robert J. "Bob" Henning, 83, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on May 3, 2026.
records.body:
Robert J. "Bob" Henning, 83, of Wausau, WI, passed away on May 3, 2026, surrounded by his loving family following a courageous battle with cancer. Known to many as "Hammer," Bob lived a life defined by faith, family, and an unwavering commitment to community service.

Born in Milwaukee to Walter Henning and Mary (Nepp) Carriveau, Bob grew up in Grafton, where he graduated from Grafton High School. He attended UW-Stout, where he met the love of his life, Yvonne Peterson. The two were married on May 7, 1966, at Big Elk Creek Lutheran Church and eventually settled in Wausau to raise their three daughters.

Bob's passion for education led to a distinguished 35-year career at Northcentral Technical College (NTC). He was remembered by colleagues and students alike as a dedicated mentor who championed every student's success. A staunch advocate for his profession, he was deeply involved in the Central Wisconsin Uniserv Council (CWUC) and the Wisconsin Education Association Council (WEAC), continuing his service well into retirement as the President of WEAC-Retired.

Bob's leadership extended through countless organizations. He was a devoted member of Immanuel Lutheran Church, serving on the church council and as a graduate of the lay ministry school. His community impact was felt through his roles as District Governor for the Rib Mountain Lions Club, Exalted Ruler of the Wausau Elks Lodge, and Board President for both the Lions Eye Bank of Wisconsin and Homme Home.

For many in Wausau, Bob was the spirit of Christmas. For years, he portrayed Santa Claus for the Wausau Christmas Parade and local gatherings, always donating his "Santa proceeds" to the Elks Lodge Fellowship program to provide gifts for families in need.

An accomplished woodworker, Bob found great joy in his workshop, creating everything from animal carvings to furniture. He shared this passion by founding the Legacy Woodworking Academy to teach others the art of wood turning and carving. Even in retirement, he stayed busy helping others through his handyman business, "Bob's Extra Hand".

Bob was preceded in death by his beloved wife, Yvonne (Peterson) Henning; mother Mary (Nepp) Carriveau; father Walter Henning; brothers Ronald Nepp, William Henning, Jerald Henning, and Dennis Henning; and sister Marilyn (Henning) Kayl.

He is survived by his three daughters: Laura (Thomas) Olson, Elizabeth Henning (Brian Bornheimer), and Amy Henning. He also leaves behind two grandchildren, Emma and Hannah Olson; brothers, Edward Henning, Ervin Ruhe, Sr., and Norman Ruhe, Jr.; sisters Eadie (Henning) Berndt, Carol (Ruhe) Wollner, Sharon (Ruhe) Varisco; and a large community of nieces, nephews, and friends.

All services will take place at the Elks Lodge, 414 Scott St, Wausau, WI, on Saturday, May 30th. The visitation will begin at 10:00 AM with celebration of life service following at 12:00 PM.

In lieu of flowers, the family requests donations be made in Bob's name to the Legacy Woodworking Academy, Homme Home Foundation, or the Lions Eye Bank of Wisconsin.

records.name: Steven B. Petri
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Steven B. Petri, 69, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on May 18, 2026, at Edgewater Haven in Port Edwards, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Steven B. Petri, 69, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on Monday, May 18, 2026, at Edgewater Haven in Port Edwards, Wisconsin under the care of Heartland Hospice.

He was born on July 20, 1956, in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, son of the late Delmar and Helen (Hotchkiss) Petri. Steven married his high school sweetheart, Cheryl Cramer on May 22, 1976, in Milwaukee. Their union spanned nearly 53 years, filled with shared adventures and love. A proud veteran, Steven served honorably in the United States Army from 1976 to 1979, including six months stationed in Germany. His military service was a source of deep pride throughout his life.

As a long-haul truck driver for an impressive 42 years, Steven traveled over two million miles without a single accident, a testament to his skill and dedication behind the wheel. He truly loved being on the road, finding joy in every journey. After returning from Texas in 1989, he devoted 16 years to the city of Wausau, working in various roles within public works.

Steven was known for his vibrant personality — a jokester and captivating storyteller, he brought warmth and laughter wherever he went. At the nursing home, he earned the affectionate nickname "the music man" because of his love for diverse musical styles. Concerts and country festivals were some of his favorite places to create happy memories with his family. Steven cherished traveling across America with Cheryl, delighting in discovering special cafés along the way. He enjoyed camping, fishing, and spending quality time with his grandchildren.

Survivors include his wife, Cheryl Petri of Weston; three children, Steven (Kerry Martin) Petri, Jr. of Cottage Grove, Wisconsin; Shannon (David) Cronce of Wausau; and Jason (Morgan Brace) Petri of Sarasota, Florida; his four grandchildren, Aubrey Anne, Ava Ann, Owen Michael, and Logan Steven. He also leaves behind several nieces and nephews, including Jessica (Tony) Sun of Dallas, Texas, and his sister-in-law Nga Goodhal of Brownsville, Texas, who was a great help and support during his final months.

He was preceded in death by his parents and five siblings: Gary, Karen, James, Delmar, and Susan.

A private family service will be held.

records.name: Dorothy Ann Meyer
records.summary: Dorothy Ann Meyer, age 95, of Hewitt passed away on May 21, 2026.
records.body:
Dorothy Ann Meyer, 95 of Hewitt, WI died peacefully Thursday morning, May 21, 2026.

Mass of Christian Burial will be at 10:30am Saturday, May 30, 2026 at St. Michael’s in Hewitt, co-celebrated by Fr. Angi and Fr. Raj. Burial will take place in the parish cemetery. Visitations will be from 4-7pm Friday, May 29, 2026 at Rembs Funeral in Marshfield and again on Saturday at the church from 9:30am until service time.

Dorothy was born in Marshfield, WI on January 25, 1931 to Edmund and Elizabeth (Lucille Deiler) Beining. She attended St. Andrew’s Parochial School in Rozellville, WI. She was united in marriage to the love of her life, Harold J.Meyer on September 5, 1955 at Sacred Heart Catholic Church in Marshfield. Dorothy lived her entire married life and the years following Harold’s death on April 3, 2013 in the home that she and Harold built in Hewitt.

Dorothy worked at Jimmy’s Cafe, where she met Harold, and continued working there before she started her most precious job—being a mother. She held this job proudly for over 60 years. Dorothy was a strong, caring, big-hearted and social person. She enjoyed crocheting and was an accomplished seamstress, knitter and crafter. Each summer you could find Dorothy in her garden and preserving her prized produce, which she willingly shared with children, grandchildren, and friends. She was a long time member of St. Michael’s Catholic Church where she was involved with funeral dinners and PCCW along with the local Hewitt Boy Scouts and Hewitt Area Fire Department. When Dorothy wasn’t watching or listening to her favorite sports teams, you could find her helping out a neighbor or friend in various capacities including her children’s newspaper sub.

Dorothy was preceded in death by her husband Harold. She is survived by her five children, Barbara (James Waters) Meyer of Horsham, PA; Carol (Kim) Meyer-Boldon of LaCrosse, WI, Paul (Judy) Meyer of New Brighton, MN; Roger Meyer of Hewitt; and Mary Meyer of LaCrosse, WI and six grandchildren who will serve as pall bearers. They are Kathryn Boldon, Anne Meyer, Adam Meyer, Erin Meyer, Emilie (fiancé, Joe Alt) Meyer, and Isaac Meyer. She is further survived by one sister, Virginia Bangart of Stratford; two brothers Richard (Lisa) Beining of Tucson, AZ and Ed (Karen) Beining of Sheboygan Falls, WI; and sisters-in-law Betty Beining of Howard’s Grove, WI and Audrey Meyer of Marshfield. Besides her parents and husband, she was preceded in death by brothers, Donald (Joan), Jerry, Terrence; brothers-in-law James Bangart, Herbert Meyer and Norbert (Donna Meyer-Frane) Meyer.

The family would like to extend special thank yous to Larry and Kathy Updyke for their watchful eye and great friendship shown to Dorothy and also niece, Janet Meyer, for playing chauffeur and providing companionship.

Dorothy’s three daughters and one son would also like to thank the other son, Roger, for the care and nurturing given to our mother, enabling her to be self-sufficient while keeping her dignity since the death of our father.

In lieu of flowers, memorials will be designated at a later date.

records.name: Emmett Myles Smith
records.summary: Emmett Myles Smith, age 0, of Rhinelander passed away on May 21, 2026.
records.body:
Emmett Myles Smith, eleven-day old infant son of Austin Smith and Fiona Redmond of Rhinelander passed away May 21, 2026 at the Marshfield Medical Center in Marshfield from complications of extreme prematurity. Myles Emmet, his twin brother preceded him in death eight days prior on the 13th.

Although his life was short, these few days were filled with love. Besides his parents, he is survived by his older brother Riley and his grandparents: Tara & Michael Redmond, Chad & Simona Smith, and Eri & Jay Martin who will remember him and his brother with love.

Eleven days of grace, eleven days of light, You held our world, so pure and bright. Your tiny hands, your gentle breath, A love that triumphs over death.

You did not stay to walk the miles, Or share with us a lifetime's smiles, But in those hours you softly slept, You claimed the promises we kept.

So brief your stay, so deep your trace, An angel's touch, a fleeting grace. Though arms are bare and tears must fall, You were our joy, our life, our all.

records.name: Lori Ann Heise
