records.summary: Lori Ann Heise, age 62 passed away on May 21, 2026.
records.body:
Lori Ann Heise, age 62, passed away on Thursday, May 21, 2026, at Seasons of Life Hospice House in Woodruff, WI., following a month of wonderful, constant and compassionate care.   She was surrounded by those that loved her dearly, including family and her 2 best friends Byron Lee, who took such good care of her during her final months at home and Marlene Rudolph, who rarely missed a day visiting with Lori at the Hospice House, making sure she was not alone and had everything she needed.

Lori always had a smile on her face as remembered by all those that she met.  Her love of nature and plants and animals was evident as she planted flowers every year in front of her apartment building and tended to them throughout the summer.  She also loved birds no matter what kind, always making sure the pigeons and mourning doves outside her window had food and water.  She could be seen almost any time of year enjoying a bike ride throughout the city.  She loved her kitty, Ian, who was her constant companion.  Her latest passion was volunteering at the Nativity Thrift Store, where she had many special friends that meant a lot to her and became like family.

Lori was born on August 29, 1963, to Keith and Rita Heise, both of whom predeceased her.  She was also predeceased by her brother John Heise, and both sets of grandparents, Hubert and Matilda Damask and August and Othelia Heise.  Lori graduated from Rhinelander High School in 1981.

Lori is survived by her sister Cindy Kaechele, brother Jeffrey C Heise, brother-in-law David Kaechele, Sisters in law Penny Varnava and Marge Heise and many beloved nieces and nephews and all their children.  Lori is also survived by many aunts, uncles and cousins.

Lori’s family would like to thank everyone for the love, prayers and support shown throughout her life.  Again, we would especially like to thank all the nurses at the Seasons of Life Hospice House for their gentle and compassionate care of our cherished Sister.

You may leave your private condolences for the Heise family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is handling the arrangements for Lori's family.

records.name: Marjorie "Marge" A. Giles
records.summary: Marjorie "Marge" A. Giles, age 91, of Marshfield passed away on May 21, 2026.
records.body:
Marjorie “Marge” A. Giles, 90, Marshfield, passed away on Thursday, May 21, 2026 at Stoney River Assisted Living, Marshfield.

A memorial service will be held at 10:30 am on Wednesday, May 27, 2026 at Rembs Funeral Home, Marshfield with visitation from 9:30 am until the time of service. Deacon Mueller will officiate. Burial will follow at Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Marshfield.

Marge was born on November 25, 1934 to Raymond and Theresa (Goldbach) Heiting.

She attended grade school and high school in Marshfield. After high school she went to work at the Marshfield Clinic where she was employed as a medical transcriptionist. She worked for the Clinic her entire career. She was united in marriage to Gary Giles on May 5, 1973 at Sacred Heart Catholic Church, Marshfield. She lovingly stayed by his side until his passing on July 26, 2011.

Marge enjoyed bowling and attending sporting events - especially her son and grandkids. She was also an avid Green Bay Packers fan. Later in life she enjoyed coffee and donuts with her friends, playing sheepshead, traveling, and going to the casino.

She is survived by her son, Brian (Elizabeth) Giles of Marshfield and grandchildren, Jeremiah, Trenton, and Alexander Giles.

She is further survived by her brother, Ronald (Donna) Heiting, sister, Shirley (David) Egner, all of Marshfield, sister-in-law, Joan Heiting of Wausau and many nieces and nephews.

She was preceded in death by her parents, husband, and brothers, Gerald, James, and Kenneth Heiting.

The family would like to extend a special thank you to Stoney River and Heartland Hospice - especially Julie and Courtney.

records.name: Mark 'Swede' Lindstrom
records.summary: Mark 'Swede' Lindstrom, age 71, of Merrill passed away on May 21, 2026.
records.body:
Mark “Swede” T. Lindstrom, 71, of Merrill, passed away peacefully on May 21, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center -Weston.

Born on July 30, 1954, in Appleton, Mark was the son of John ‘Jack’ and Dorothy (Tebo) Lindstrom. He graduated from Merrill High School in 1972 and proudly served in the United States Army. After his service, Mark moved back to Merrill and worked for the railroad for several years. He then relocated to Green Bay for a few more years before he eventually moved back to Merrill and started working for the Merrill Water Utility and Merrill Street Department. He retired after 28 years with the water utility and street department.

On July 7, 2001, he married Vicki Rusch in Milwaukee, and they enjoyed their time together. Mark, affectionately known as "Swede," enjoyed spending many hours fishing. He was also an avid fan of the Green Bay Packers, Milwaukee Brewers, and Wisconsin Badgers, always ready to cheer on his teams.

Survivors include his wife, Vicki Lindstrom of Merrill; son, Casey (Bridget) Lindstrom of Denver, CO; step daughters, Rachel (Robert) Secora of Merrill and Sara (Seth) Clark of Merrill; grandchildren, Barrett Lindstrom of Denver, CO, Riley (Makayla) Secora of Merrill, Trevor (Miranda) Myers of La Crosse, Shailee Myers (fiancé Anthony Forsberg) of New London, and Decilyn Clark of Verona; great-granddaughters, Zelda and Aurora Secora of Merrill; sisters, Gale Janquart of Merrill, Wendy Lindstrom of Irma, Jane (Greg) Stevenson of Irma, and Amy (Conrad) Sternot of Port Huron, MI; brothers, Steve (Mary) Lindstrom of Green Bay and Dan (Pam) Lindstrom of Indiana; Vicki’s siblings, Sharon Horenberger of New Berlin, Deborah Bradford of Glendale, Jean (Bob) Miller of Louisville, KY, David Schupmann of Glendale, Tim Schupmann of San Antonio, TX, Jon (DeAnna) Schupmann of Milwaukee, and Angela (Jim) Poitz of New Berlin; and many nieces and nephews.

Mark was preceded in death by his parents, Jack and Dorothy Lindstrom; father-in-law and mother-in-law, Rev. Arthur and Dorothy Schupmann; sister-in-law and brother-in-law, Nancy and Jeff Villwock; and brothers-in-law, Mike Horenberger and Willie Bradford.

In accordance with Swede’s wishes, no formal service will be held.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Robert "Bob" Joseph Faber
records.summary: Robert "Bob" Joseph Faber, age 57, of Spencer passed away on May 21, 2026.
records.body:
Robert “Bob” Joseph Faber May 17, 1969 – May 21, 2026

Robert “PeeWee” Faber, age 57, of Spencer, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly at home on May 21, 2026.

Born on May 17, 1969, in Neillsville, Wisconsin, Bob was the cherished son of Joseph and Jean (Strey) Faber. After graduating from Loyal High School in 1987, he pursued his passion for electrical work, beginning his career at Wisconsin Homes. Through years of dedication and hard work, Bob became a Master Electrician and proudly established his own business, Faber Electric. He was known not only for his skill, but also for his generosity — always willing to lend a hand or offer guidance to friends, neighbors, and customers alike.

In 1989, Bob met Tracy Boushack in Marshfield, and together they built a loving family. Bob and Tracy were blessed with two daughters, Lauren and Haley, who brought him endless joy and pride.

Bob cherished the outdoors, whether boating on calm waters, hunting in the woods, or competing in long-range shooting. He loved a good challenge and approached life with the same passion and precision he brought to his work.

He is survived by his wife, Tracy; his daughters, Lauren and Haley; his parents, Joseph and Jean Faber; his sisters, Tammy Heil (Kurt) and Sandra Leonhard (Gerard); and his brothers, Edward and Tracy (Tanneberg) Faber, Steven and Jessica (Nelson) Faber. He is also survived by his favorite in-laws, Debbie and Paul Boushack; his brothers-in-law, Greg and Ayme (VerKilen) Boushack and Jeremiah Boushack; many cherished nieces and nephews; and his beloved fur babies, Kipton, Winnie, and Alaska.

Bob’s warmth, humor, smart mouth, and unwavering commitment to family and community will be remembered by all who knew him. Most especially, he will be remembered by those who loved him for always saying, “Don’t tell me what to do.”

A celebration of Bob’s life will be held on June 27th, 2026 in the Marshfield Junior Fair Building, Marshfield Wisconsin, from noon to 3pm.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family at this time.  Online condolences may be expressed at MWCS.WS

records.name: Ronda Lou Rydbom
records.summary: Ronda Lou Rydbom, age 68 passed away on May 21, 2026.
records.body:
Ronda Lou Rydbom, 68 of Wausau peacefully went to be with the Lord on Thursday, May 21, 2026.

Ronda was a strong woman who faced life and all its challenges with grit and determination. Ronda loved spending time crafting many projects for her kids and grandkids. She enjoyed lunch and dinner dates with her best friend, Susan, at the local diners in town. She also enjoyed being the friendly, smiling face of all whom she greeted at NCHC, as part-time post-retirement work.

Ronda is survived by her four children Marci (Chet Klave ), Brandi (Scott Kawlewski ), Lynsi (Dan Ravn), and Zackary Bradford; her brothers Rodney and Roy Rydbom; and her biggest blessings, her five grandchildren; Logan, McKenna, Zoey, Oaklee, and Ellie. Ronda also left behind her beloved cat, Baby. Ronda was preceded in death by her mother, Norma; father, Dale; and sister, Rene.

By choice of the family and Ronda's wishes there will not be a formal service or memorial. Her children have planned to remember their mother with family and friends this summer with a "Remembering Ronda" Celebration at Jojo's Jungle on Sunday, August 2nd, 2026, from 1-4 pm. We welcome all that would like to share their fond memories of Ronda.

Thank you to Brainard Funeral Home for their assistance and guidance in this most challenging time.

"I'll love you forever, I'll like you for always, as long as I'm living, my baby you'll be." - Robert Munsch

records.name: Rosemary Farchione
records.summary: Rosemary Farchione, age 94, of Weston passed away on May 21, 2026.
records.body:
With broken hearts we announce the passing of Rosemary “Dolly” Farchione, who peacefully passed away in her sleep at the age of 94. Although her passing was sudden and unexpected, we find comfort in knowing she experienced no pain and was at home, surrounded by family, just as she would have wanted. Rosemary was a proud Italian wife, mother, grandmother, great-grandmother, sister, and friend. She was the heart and soul of her family. She was and remains the matriarch, the foundation upon which generations were built, and the reason so many of us understand the true meaning of unconditional love, loyalty, and family. Her family was her life’s greatest accomplishment and her greatest joy. She devoted herself completely to those she loved, protecting them fiercely and loving them without limits. She is survived by her beloved husband, Dominic Farchione, age 95. Together, they were approaching an incredible milestone of 75 years of marriage. Anyone who knew them together knew that there is no love greater than theirs. She also leaves behind her loving children: Katherine Miller; Debbie Habel and her husband, Donald; Dominic Farchione and his wife, Veronica; Deann Farchione and her fiancé, Kyle; and Michelle Ruenger, whom she lovingly embraced as her adopted daughter. Rosemary was also blessed with 10 cherished grandchildren and 10 adored great-grandchildren, all of whom were the center of her world. She is also survived by her beloved brother, Tony Gianfermi, whom she loved dearly. To know Rosemary was to know a woman with a heart larger than life. She loved people and her warmth made everyone feel like a long time friend regardless of who they were. She’s known for chatting on the phone for hours, her warm sense of humor, and her ability to turn around anyone’s bad day. Rosemary was never negative, and always knew the right things to say. She was always the last one to hang up, never ending a conversation without countless “I love you’s.” Rosemary’s happy place was her kitchen, full of family laughing at the table for hours. She was an incredible cook and would be often found leaning over the kitchen sink cutting up vegetables in her hand. Some of our greatest memories were made gathered around her table, sharing stories, laughter, and meals prepared with love. She loved her Lifetime movies and classic films and was quite the night owl, often staying up far later than anyone else. She was famous for trying to convince her daughter to pull all-nighters with her, refusing to let the evening end before one more conversation, one more movie, or one more laugh. She also loved her hard candies and you could always find a stash in her purse. Her kitchen table, her voice on the phone, her stories, her traditions, and her unwavering love brought generations together. She taught us that family is not simply something you have. It is something you protect, cherish, and hold close every day. She was our anchor, our storyteller, our protector, and our guide. While we mourn her loss, we find comfort in knowing she has been reunited in Heaven with those she loved so dearly. She joins her beloved son, James Farchione; her brothers, Michael Gianfermi, Donald Gianfermi, Frank Gianfermi, Eddie Gianfermi, Johnny Gianfermi; and her sister Joann Murney. She has also reunited with her cherished mother, Lucy Gianfermi, and her dad, Arduino; her beloved mother-in-law, Marietta and father-in-law, Vincenzo. We can only imagine the joyful reunion that welcomed her home. Losing her leaves a space that can never truly be filled. Yet her legacy lives on in her children, grandchildren, great-grandchildren, and all who were fortunate enough to be loved by her. We will hear her in our conversations, see her in our traditions, and feel her presence always. We miss her deeply. We love her endlessly. And we will carry her memory with us always. Rest in peace, Nana. The family you loved so fiercely will continue to honor you, cherish you, and keep your spirit alive for generations to come.

Private services are being held. Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org

records.name: Theodore Clintsman
records.summary: Theodore Clintsman, age 91 passed away on May 21, 2026.
records.body:
Theodore (Ted) Clintsman passed away peacefully on May 21, 2026 at Mount View Care Center in Wausau. He was 91 years old.

Ted was born in Greenwood, WI on July 8, 1934 - the 5th of 6 siblings. In his 20’s, he moved to Waukesha, WI and worked at various factories. In 1972, he and his family moved to Wausau, WI. After working at Wausau Iron Works / LOED as a machinist and assembler for 20 years, he decided to open a used furniture and appliance store – Budget Resale. There he not only could pursue his passion for sales and working with his hands; he could also chat with his customers – something else he enjoyed.

When not at his store, Ted faithfully met with his buddies for coffee and for a swim at a therapy pool. He was friendly and talkative - quick to greet others and, as he used to like to put it, “give them grief”. He also loved spending time up at his camper in Tomahawk and later, Birnamwood - where he spent most of the time relaxing by the campfire and socializing with other campers.

In 2014, when Ted decided to close Budget Resale for good, the local paper interviewed him. In that article, he talked about his joy in serving and visiting with his customers and his sales philosophy: “be fair with people and treat them like you want to be treated”. That was Ted –was how he operated his business and who he was as a person.

Ted is survived by his sons, Gregory (Judy) and Douglas (Linda), by his grandsons, Justin (Rebecca), Sean (Meghan), Evan (Phoenix), and Daniel (Taryn) and by his sister Nell. Preceding him in death were his parents, Fred and Luella Clintsman, his sisters Marie, Irene, Shirley, and his brother, Dale.

A visitation will be held at St. Mark's Lutheran Church, 600 Stevens Dr, Wausau, Wisconsin, on Friday, June 12, at 10:30 am until the time of service at 12:00 officiated by Pastor Eric Hauan.

Family and friends are invited to share words of remembrance and condolences online at www.honorone.com.

records.name: Carol J. Ashby
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Carol Jean Ashby, 78, passed away on May 19, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.
records.body:
Carol Jean Ashby, age 78, passed away on Tuesday, May 19, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital, Wausau, Wisconsin.

She was born on October 15, 1947, in Huron, South Dakota, the youngest child of the late Frank and Evelyn (Love) Kearns. For over two decades, Carol was a familiar and friendly presence at the Hwy 51 Truck Stop in Merrill, where she served as manager. She was known not only as a diligent and hardworking individual but also as someone who genuinely cared for the many people she encountered daily.

Carol had a great love of birds and amassed a treasured collection of bird figurines. In her leisure, she enjoyed trips to the casino, playing bingo and Texas Hold'em, as well as traveling. Family was the cornerstone of her life, and she loved attending family reunions and gatherings, and delighted in spending quality time with her girls and grandchildren. She was also an enthusiastic supporter of Wisconsin sports teams, often found cheering for the Packers and Brewers with spirited pride.

Carol is lovingly remembered by her three daughters: Jeannine (Curly) Marg of Custer, South Dakota; Denise (Carl) Viviano of Wausau; and Kimberly (Robert) Teske of Marathon; as well as one stepdaughter, Heather (Roy) Freda of Glendale, Arizona. Her legacy continues through her beloved grandchildren: Bethany, Renee (Jacob), Erin (Mitchell), Katlyn (Derek), Christopher (Hannah), Samantha, Billy (Bradly), Robert (Amanda), Bobby (Krystal) Lillianna, and Patrick (Alexandra); and her nine great-grandchildren: Chloe, Oaklynn, Ava, Roman, Hayden, Bennett, Daniel, Soren, and Hudson. She is further survived by two brothers, Dale Kearns of Panama City Beach, Florida, and Duane (Angie) Kearns of Mesick, Michigan, as well as the many nieces and nephews she dearly loved.

She was preceded in death by her parents, her sister Beverly Castagno, and her brothers Darrell (Sonny) Kearns and Donald Kearns.

Memorial service will be held at 11:00 A.M. on Wednesday, May 27, 2026, at Mount Olive Lutheran Church, 6205 Alderson Street, Weston, Wisconsin. Pastor Jordan Balk will officiate. Visitation will take place from 10:00 a.m. until the time of service at the church.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Eugene C. Field
records.summary: Eugene C. Field, 78, of Wittenberg, died unexpectedly on May 17, 2026.
records.body:
Eugene C. Field, 78 of Wittenberg, died unexpectedly on Sunday, May 17, 2026, at his home.

Eugene was born on June 25, 1947, in Pontiac, IL, the son of Eugene and Hope (O'Higgins) Field.

On July 1, 1965, Eugene was united in marriage to Sheryl Close. They later divorced.

Eugene was drafted for Vietnam and was fortunate enough to only serve in Germany. He was in the Army Corp of Engineers under the U.S. Army Engineer Command Europe in Frankfurt, Germany, with a rank of specialist E-4. After returning home in the summer of 1972, he was employed with Material Service Corporation in Joliet, Illinois, where he was a machinist for a number of years, working on very large rebuilds, such as the Joliet Downtown Drawbridges.

He left the Joliet area in the mid to late 80's, partnering with an Army buddy, Gary Thompson, to grow ginseng in the Wittenberg area. He retired later from farming, but continued living on the last farm he used for ginseng.

Eugene was a very practical and pragmatic man who possessed remarkable intelligence despite having little formal education. He lived his life entirely on his own terms, which was only appropriate that his favorite song was 'I Did It My Way.' Eugene took pride in being very connected to the Earth. He could walk through a cedar forest in the middle of a moonless night and know exactly where he was. Eugene could start fires with nothing, sneak up on deer and really connected with hunting and farming. He loved chess and was able to teach his son, Charlie, the game. Later in life, he became very interested in Harley Motorcycling and liked interacting with events associated with that. Eugene also loved traveling to Mexico and made more than 25 trips there over the years.

Eugene is survived by his son, Charlie Field; four grandchildren; three great-grandchildren; half brothers and sisters; partner and friend, Marianne Schiefer of Stevens Point and many friends. He was preceded in death by his parents.

No services will be held.

Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg is assisting the family with arrangements. Memories and messages of support may be shared at schmidtschulta.com.

records.name: James J. Furmanek
records.summary: James (Jim) Joseph Furmanek of Goose Creek, South Carolina, died on May 15, 2026.
records.body:
James (Jim) Joseph Furmanek of Goose Creek, South Carolina died Friday, May 15, 2026.

He was born on May 10, 1950 in Wausau, Wisconsin, to Ambrose and Violet Furmanek. Jim graduated from Wausau High School and had a distinguished 20-year career in the Air Force.

Upon retirement, he worked an additional 20 years with McDonnell Douglas and Boeing with focus on building, launching, and maintaining the C17 Globemaster.

Jim is survived by his wife, Elizabeth (Liz), sisters Bridget Litza, Shirley Van Slate (husband Jim Ludwig), brother John (wife Nola), and seven nieces and nephews.

Jim's greatest joys were driving his Corvette, playing golf, and watching the Packers and Brewers on his giant TV.

In accordance with his wishes, Jim will be buried in Wausau. In lieu of flowers, family requests that donations be made to St. Jude Children's Research Hospital. Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Wausau is in charge of arrangements. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Jim Wenzel
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Jim Wenzel, 79, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on May 19, 2026.
records.body:
Jim Wenzel, age 79, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on Tuesday, May 19, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center – Weston, surrounded by his loving family.

Jim was born on April 25, 1947, in Wausau, Wisconsin, the son of Glenn and Leatrice (Poeske) Wenzel. He proudly served his country in the National Guard, a commitment that underscored his deep sense of duty and patriotism. Beyond his military service, he was devoted to his local community, contributing his leadership as a Village Trustee for the Village of Weston a role he held with dedication and pride.

An avid outdoorsman, Jim found great joy in hunting and fishing, and cherished time spent at his second home on the lake up north in St. Germain. The natural world was a sanctuary for him, where he could embrace peaceful moments and create lasting memories. He was also a passionate sports fan, delighting in cheering for Wisconsin's beloved teams: the Packers, Brewers, and Bucks.

Jim's warm and approachable nature made it easy for him to connect with others; he could hold a conversation with anyone, whether a longtime friend or a stranger. Jim sold plywood and ran propane deliveries, which he was excellent at because of his ability to communicate and talk with anyone. Above all, he treasured spending time with his parents, whose memory he deeply honored throughout his life.

He is survived by his daughter Lynn (Tim) Halambeck; son Brad (Sarah) Wenzel; sister Sue Garnsworthy; and grandchildren Megan Halambeck, Benjamin Halambeck, Andrew Wenzel, and Dillon Morrisey. Jim is preceded in death by his parents and brother-in-law Stan Garnsworthy.

The community and loved ones remember Jim as a man whose integrity, kindness, and vibrant personality touched many lives. As we reflect on his life, we hold dear the moments shared and the legacy he leaves behind a legacy rooted in service, family, and a passion for life.

A private family service will be held. Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: John P. Singer
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home and Cremation Care
records.summary: John Phillip Singer passed away peacefully on May 18, 2026.
records.body:
John Phillip Singer passed away peacefully in the early morning on May 18, 2026, with his sister, Shirley, at his side.

John was born on November 18, 1944, in Milwaukee, WI, to the late Charles and Adeline (Fearson) Singer. He attended St. Paul's School in Mosinee, St. Mary's School in Marathon, and graduated from Marathon High School in 1963. John served in the United States Army beginning August 12, 1965, where he served in Vietnam at the rank of SP4 until being honorably discharged in 1967.

Following his military service, John began working with Crestline Company in Wausau, which was then purchased in 1981 by Sentry Insurance along with the Vetter Company in Stevens Point, giving it the name SNE. In 1991, the company moved to Mosinee, and in 2001, it was again sold to the Weather Shield Company. He continued to work for the company in Mosinee until his retirement in 2007, accomplishing 40 years of service.

John was a member of the American Legion Roy L. Drew Post 492 in Rothschild. He was on a pool league with the 19th Hole in Schofield and enjoyed watching old western TV shows. He was also a fan of the Milwaukee Brewers, Green Bay Packers, and enjoyed NASCAR. John made many new friends after moving to Renaissance Assisted Living in March 2024, joining with others in the many activities offered, such as bingo, happy hour, listening to musicians, and occasional trips to the casino.

John is survived by sisters, Shirley (Edward) Princl of Weston, WI, Kathleen Asterita of Mesa, AZ, and Margaret (Richard) Pesmark of Peshtigo, WI; brothers, Charles Singer of Wausau, WI, and Gerald (Diane) Singer of Mesquite, NV; special Goddaughter, Amber Myska; and many nieces and nephews. He is preceded in death by his parents; brothers, Robert, Richard, Dennis Singer; and sister, Judith (Singer) Buntrock; as well as several doggie companions throughout the years.

A Memorial Service will be held at 11:30 AM on Friday, May 29, 2026, at Ascend Funeral Home and Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston. Pastor Lance Hoelscher will officiate. Visitation will be held from 10:00 AM until the time of service at the funeral home. Interment will be in Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Kronenwetter, with Military Honors accorded by the United States Army and American Legion Montgomery-Plant-Dudley Post 10 Honor Guard.

The family would like to thank the staff at Renaissance Assisted Living in Weston and Compassus Hospice for the wonderful care they gave John, especially during his final weeks.

records.name: Judith K. Urbanek
records.summary: Judith "Judie" K. Urbanek, 79, of Wausau, passed away on May 19, 2026.
records.body:
Judith "Judie" K. Urbanek, 79, of Wausau, passed away unexpectedly on Tuesday, May 19, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

Judie was born October 28, 1946, in Oshkosh, daughter of the late Norman and Katherine (Rollins) Dahlke. On December 19, 1970, she married Dennis Urbanek.

A dedicated foreign language teacher, Judie shared her love of learning with countless students throughout her career. Zion Lutheran School, Wausau East High School, and St. Mary's Oratory (where she taught the pre-seminary program for several years) were all fortunate to have her on their faculties. In addition to her classroom teaching, she was a German teacher and generously volunteered her time as a tutor to ensure her students' success.

Judie was also deeply involved in her Zion Church community, serving faithfully as a reader and as a devoted member of the choir. Outside of her work, she found great joy in gardening, traveling, and reading. Above all, she cherished time spent with her friends and her wonderful family, especially her grandchildren.

Survivors include her husband, Dennis Urbanek; her children, Edward (Rebecca) Urbanek, Elise Purves, and Tim Urbanek; her beloved grandchildren, Maxwell, Malcolm, and Mirabelle Urbanek, and Orla Purves; her sister, Kathie (Gary) McFarlane; and numerous nieces, nephews, other relatives, and friends.

She was preceded in death by her parents.

A funeral service will be held at 11:00 AM on Monday, June 1, 2026, at Zion Lutheran Church, Wausau. Visitation will be held at the church on Monday from 9:30 AM until the time of the service.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made in Judie's name to Zion Lutheran Church.

records.name: Marlyn A. Opyd
records.summary: Marlyn Alice (Farrand) Opyd, 88, passed away on May 13, 2026.
records.body:
Marlyn Alice (Farrand) Opyd, age 88, passed away on May 13, 2026, at her daughter's home under hospice care. Marlyn was born July 1, 1937, in West Kraft right outside Tomahawk, WI, the youngest of eight children to Arthur Burdett Farand and Grace Julia (Thompson) Farrand.

When Marlyn was born, they forgot the "i" in her first name on her birth certificate. She decided to keep the "i" out forever, because she liked it like that. She attended Fulsher grade school in Tomahawk and at fifteen moved to Ohio with her mother where she graduated from Fairborn High School. Her first job was working at a daycare at the Wright Patterson Airforce base, where she cared for the children of the first seven astronauts.

After high school, Marlyn moved to Chicago and worked at Zenith Television Corporation. Later, she moved to Wausau, where she met her husband, Walter L. Opyd. They eloped and married in Red Wing, MN on July 11, 1959. They made their home in Wausau and raised their two daughters Dawn and Debra. Together they shared 40 years of marriage before Wally's passing on March 11, 2000.

Mom most valued being a homemaker and mother. She volunteered at her children's school, belonged to the church circle, and was a Girl Scout co-leader. Later, she worked at Employers Insurance, where she made many dear friends. After retiring, she began a fun job working on tour buses as a hostess and travel coordinator – a job she absolutely loved. She enjoyed traveling, meeting people, and talking with everyone she encountered.

Marlyn made a point of getting to know the workers at the places she frequented and knowing them all by name, at Kwik Trip, the pharmacy, the grocery store, everywhere she went, she got to know everyone. She loved chatting with people in her community. For many years, Mom and our dad, Wally, were season ticket holders for Wausau Timbers/Wausau Woodchucks minor league baseball teams in Wausau. Mom enjoyed watching sports, especially the Brewers, Packers, and state tournaments.

We always laughed that Mom could do anything. She would read craft and cookbooks or magazines and teach herself how to do everything. She liked to make meals, bake desserts, can jam, pickles, fruit, and vegetables. She could quilt, craft, crochet, knit, and sew. Mom loved to smelt, fish for bluegills, and was so proud of catching salmon and canning it herself. We grew up on homemade baked bread! At Christmas, there were sometimes 30 different kinds of cookies she baked. Her rule was that you could not eat any cookies until they were all counted. She always wanted to know how many cookies came from each batch.

Mom always found time to play board games like Scrabble or Racko or play cards. A visit with Marlyn was never complete unless a few rounds of cards were played: Canasta, Kings Corner, Rummy or most likely Three-Thirteen.

There was always a holiday to celebrate or a gathering to dress up for. Mom adored being with her family, friends, and neighbors. She was kind, loving, and generous to all. She made sure everyone was included and invited. There were always extra seats at the table, and if gifts were given, everybody received something to open.

Mom loved to laugh, giggle, hug, and have a great time. Wherever she went, she made friends and was one of the friendliest people you would ever meet. Strangers quickly became friends. She mailed her Christmas cards early so friends, nieces and nephews would receive hers first and smile. For eight years before becoming ill, she devoted all her time to care for an elderly friend. When you were Marlyn's friend, you were a friend forever.

She is survived by her two daughters Dawn (Opyd) Stencil (Joseph) of McFarland, WI and Debra Opyd of Peoria, IL. Grandchildren, Rachel Stencil (Jeffrey Baswell) of Euharlee, GA, Paul (Harmony) Stencil of Madison, WI, Christi Stencil of Citrus Heights, CA and Holly (Andrew Benson) of Bloomfield, CT. Great grandchildren, Desiree' Stencil, GA, Bethany Benson, CT, Eve Stencil, and Kaleb Stencil of Madison.

Marlyn was the last of her eight siblings and their spouses: Mildred (Frank) Suffal, AK, Melroy (Marie) Farrand, Tomahawk, Dorothy (former Robert) Winker, OH, Helen (Shannon O'Brien, AK, / former Norman Metcalf, Prentice), Margaret (Edwin) Roberts, MO, Allan (Myrna 'Jean'), Milwaukee and Leland (Delores) Farrand, WA. She was the last of Walter's six siblings and their spouses: Rose (Anthony) Lenart, IL, Mary (Albert) Szczecina, IL, Joseph (former Mary) Opyd, IL, Charlotte (Edward Wojciechowski), Milwaukee, Michael (Stephanie) Opyd, IL, and very close sister and brother-in-law Lottie (Cornelius) Guenther of Mosinee.

The family would like to thank the staff of Brighton Hospice for their exceptional love, comfort, and care during mom's last year.

The funeral mass is 11:00 A.M. on Friday, June 12th, 2026, at St. Michael Catholic Church, 611 Stark St., Wausau, WI. Rev. Thomas Lindner will preside. Visitation will be from 10:00 A.M. until mass. Lunch will be served in the adjoining church hall. A private interment will take place at Restlawn Memorial Park in the Garden of Eternal Rest, 235962 N Troy St., Wausau, WI on a future date.

Peterson Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau, is in charge of arrangements. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

In lieu of flowers, consider a red kettle donation at Christmas. Our parents' home flooded in the 1960s and their young family lost everything. The Salvation Army rescued them and helped them get back on their feet. We were always taught to never pass a kettle without putting a donation in.

Please feel free to wear your favorite hat. Marlyn loved to get together, have fun and wear hats.

records.name: Mary J. Paulson
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Mary Jean Paulson, 82, of Rockland, Michigan, passed away peacefully on May 16, 2026.
records.body:
Mary Jean Paulson, age 82, of Rockland, Michigan, passed away peacefully on May 16, 2026, in Wausau, Wisconsin, leaving behind a legacy of resilience, independence, fierce love, and unforgettable spirit.

Born and raised in Rockland, Mary lived life entirely on her own terms. She spent many years in the Chicago area, living in Des Plaines and Arlington Heights, before returning home to the farm she loved so deeply in Rockland. The farm was more than a home to Mary – it was her heart. There, surrounded by horses, dogs, cats, flowers, and the quiet rhythms of country life, she built the life she wanted with grit, determination, and hard work.

Mary attended Northland College in Ashland, WI, earning her B.A. in English in 1965. She earned her Master of Arts in Education from Northern Michigan University in 1970. She went on to teach English and special education at Ontonagon Area High School, where she remained until her retirement in 2008. She believed deeply in standing up for the kids who needed someone in their corner. She touched countless lives as a teacher, advocate, mentor, and steady source of support.

To know Mary was to know a woman who was unapologetically independent. She was stubborn in the best and most legendary ways, determined that anything worth doing should be done correctly – usually her way. For years, she cut, raked, and baled her own hay herself, long after many people would have handed the work over to someone else. Her lifelong love of horses defined so much of who she was, and caring for her animals brought her tremendous joy. Her faithful German Shepherd, Gretchen, remained a constant companion.

Mary had a sharp wit and a memorable way of teaching life lessons. She reminded those around her never to make assumptions, and Thursdays were always greeted with a little extra enthusiasm and one of her trademark sayings that family and friends will never forget.

One story especially captures her spirit. When county workers came through trimming back trees on French Shanty Road near her home, Mary tied a ribbon around a beloved old elm tree and posted a sign declaring it protected by the "Tree Huggers of America." The tree remained standing. Anyone who knew her knows exactly how that conversation probably went.

Though tough as nails on the outside, Mary loved deeply. She cherished family visits to the farm, sitting outside in the summer shade or relaxing in the screenhouse while watching the world around her. Spring flowers brought her happiness, especially crocuses, lilacs, peonies, trilliums, and roses. She enjoyed gatherings with her Red Hat friends and treasured time spent with friends, cousins, nieces, and nephews throughout the years.

More than anything, Mary loved her family. She was a devoted mother to her daughter, Amanda, and a proud mother-in-law to Josh. Being "Nana" to her granddaughter Eliin brought her enormous joy, and she remained a constant source of love, strength, encouragement, and unwavering support to those closest to her.

Mary was preceded in death by her parents, Edwin and Harriet (McCracken) Paulson; her sister, Evelyn McGinn; and her brother, Keith Paulson.

She is survived by her daughter Amanda and son-in-law Josh Myrblom; granddaughter Eliin; siblings Laura (the late Chris) Olson, Brenda (Gary) Poisson, and David Paulson; cousins Patty McCracken, Peggy (the late Russell) Hoeft, and Colleen (Royce) Mort; nieces Lindsey Poisson, Karen (Andrew) Linton; nephews Kelly (Beth) Olson, Christopher (Laila) Poisson, Jonathon Poisson, John (Kay) McGinn, and Michael McGinn, as well as numerous great- nieces and nephews, friends, former students, and all who were fortunate enough to know her.

A private family gathering will be held at a later date.

The family would like to extend their heartfelt gratitude to the staff at Aspirus Hospice House for the compassionate care, comfort, and kindness they provided to Mary and her family.

In honor of Mary's lifelong love for animals, memorial donations may be made to the Ontonagon County Animal Protection (OCAP), P. O. Box 315, Ontonagon, MI 49953.

Mary will be remembered as vibrant, feisty, courageous, loving, and impossible to forget – a woman who stood her ground, loved fiercely, worked tirelessly, and taught those around her how to be strong.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Peter A. Philipp
records.summary: Peter Anthony Philipp, 85, passed away peacefully on May 15, 2026.
records.body:
Peter Anthony Philipp, 85, passed away peacefully on May 15, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center Weston, with his loving wife Kay by his side.

Peter was born on April 1, 1941, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to the late Eugene and Mary Ann (Kostuck) Philipp. As a child, Peter attended several schools as his family moved throughout different areas, eventually graduating from Newman High School in Wausau in 1959.

Peter proudly served his country in the United States Air Force from 1963 to 1967. Following his military service, he became a dedicated employee of the Schofield Post Office, first working as a walking mail carrier and later driving a rural route until his retirement. After retiring from the Post Office, he briefly worked at East Bay in Wausau.

Peter had a lifelong love for building and flying radio-controlled airplanes and helicopters, he also enjoyed bird hunting and fishing with family and friends, his hobbies brought him much joy and many friendships. He was also a member of American Legion Post 10 in Wausau.

Family was deeply important to Peter. He was a loving father figure to Gregory Hintz and his wife Alana and Anya whom called him Grandpa. He treasured the relationships he built throughout the years and remained devoted to those he loved.

On September 1, 1984, Peter married Darlene Tanck. Together they enjoyed spending time camping up north and creating many happy memories. Darlene preceded him in death on January 17, 2006.

Peter later married Kay (Robinson) Peterson on May 19, 2007, in Schofield. They had first met years earlier as two couples on a retirement trip to Boston sponsored by Liberty Mutual, where both Kay and Darlene were employed before retirement. Peter and Kay reconnected through the Covenant Community Presbyterian Church, where Kay and her late husband, Clare "Pete" Peterson, were members. Peter and Kay shared many wonderful years together traveling throughout the United States, often on organized bus tours. They also enjoyed several trips to Hawaii with dear friends Shirley and Mark Summers and made five memorable trips to Europe. Of all the places he visited, Ireland remained Peter's favorite.

Peter will be lovingly remembered for his gentle spirit, devotion to family, quiet sense of humor, and appreciation for life's simple joys. He especially adored his precious kitty, Daisy, who faithfully "herded" him around the house and brought him constant companionship.

He is survived by his beloved wife, Kay; several relatives and friends; and his cherished cat, Daisy.

Peter was preceded in death by his parents; his first wife, Darlene; his sister, Patty Bennett; brother-in-law, Watson "Corky" Bennett; brother-in-law, David Tanck, Darlene's twin brother; and Kay's first husband, Clare "Pete" Peterson, who passed away in 2005 after illness.

The family extends a special thank you to the compassionate staff of Compassus Hospice for their care and support.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made to Covenant Community Presbyterian Church for their building fund or to the American Cancer Society

John J Buettgen Funeral Home is assisting the family. Family and friends may leave words of remembrance at www.honorone.com

records.name: Thomas D. Wetterneck
records.summary: Thomas Dean Wetterneck, 77, of Schofield, passed away on May 20, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas Dean Wetterneck, 77, of Schofield, WI, passed away on May 20, 2026.

Born in Wausau, WI, Tom was a beloved husband, father, and grandfather.

He attended the University of Wisconsin-Stevens Point and married the love of his life, Jan Wiesman, in January 1970. He worked at Marathon Electric as a sales engineer for over 30 years.

He was a big Wisconsin sports fan and also watched professional golf.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Morris and Lois (nee Olson) Wetterneck, and his sister, Susan Aschbrenner.

He is survived by his wife, Jan, his daughter Tosha and son-in-law David Brenneis and their children Faith (21) and Liana (13), and his son Chad and daughter-in-law DeeDee and their children Sion (15), Rowan (14) and Asher (11).

The family is holding private services. Donations to St. Jude Children's Research Hospital, Tom's favorite charity, are appreciated.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting family at this time. Online condolences may be expressed at MWCS.WS

records.name: Wesley L. Manning
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Wesley "Wes" Lee Manning, 81, passed away peacefully on May 17, 2026.
records.body:
Wesley "Wes" Lee Manning, 81, passed away peacefully on May 17, 2026, at home with his beloved wife Judy and dog Abby by his side.

Wes was born on June 3, 1944, at Wausau Memorial Hospital to Daniel Sr. and Wilma (Blattert) Manning. He grew up on the family farm in the Town of Easton and graduated from Wausau High School in 1963 — a milestone he marked with characteristic wit, joking that he "went all 3 school days of the week." On November 11, 1967, he married the love of his life, Judy Shanak, at St. Paul's United Church of Christ in Wausau, Wisconsin, beginning a partnership spanning 59 years.

After graduation, Wes served in the United States Army. Following his honorable discharge, he briefly worked at the Brokaw Paper Mill before finding his true calling behind the wheel. For 31 years, Wes drove over-the-road for Schneider National, earning membership in the prestigious Two Million Mile Club and collecting multiple safe driving awards along the way. He was a proud member of Teamsters Local 662. Wes loved the road deeply — his natural gift for directions earned him the nickname "Human GPS" from a friend. He could always tell you the best route to wherever you were headed, with worthy stops along the way. He raved about the great pastrami, bread, and other food he enjoyed on runs out east.

After retiring from trucking in 2000, Wes couldn't sit still. He found himself tinkering in the garage, working in the yard, labeling expiration dates on food with duct tape and a Sharpie, and reorganizing kitchen cupboards with a system only he fully understood — signs of a man stir crazy without his work. Fortunately, he soon found his next calling as groundskeeper at St. Mary's Oratory in Wausau, where he mowed lawns, planted flowers, shoveled sidewalks, and tended to the grounds with pride and genuine care.

Wes was a blue-collar man through and through — unpretentious, grounded, and proud of it. His sharp wit and mischievous streak could be traced all the way back to his childhood and never really left him. He was known for one-liners with a smirk and twinkle in his eye, able to get a laugh out of just about any situation. He was generous to his core, showing up for people he loved in quiet, steady ways.

In his later years, Wes and Judy settled in Palm Beach Gardens, Florida, where he traded in blue jeans for shorts. They enjoyed exploring and spending time at the Thirsty Turtle, their favorite local spot for food and drink. After some time in Florida, they returned to Wisconsin. He and Judy loved to travel and made regular trips to Minnesota to visit Brad and his family. He shared a love of racing events with Jeff and made memories on the road that his family will carry with them always.

Wes was preceded in death by parents, Daniel Sr. and Wilma Manning; in-laws, Nick and Maralee Shanak; and siblings, Daniel Manning Jr., Wendy (LeRoy) Moseng, and Perry Manning.

He is survived by his wife, Judy Manning and devoted dog companion Abby; sons Jeff (Julie) Manning and Brad (Ivy) Manning; grandchildren Lucy and Vivian Manning; brother-in-law Gary (Kelly) Shanak and sisters-in-law Audrey (Arlo) Schlund, and Shirley Manning; along with many nieces and nephews; and grand pets.

The family extends heartfelt thanks to Judy's sister, Wes's sister-in-law, Audrey Schlund, for her unwavering support through Wes's health challenges and final days. They also wish to express their deep gratitude to the hospice team at Aspirus — especially Jason and Hope — for their compassionate care provided during Wes's final months.

In keeping with Wes's wishes, cremation will take place, and no funeral will be held. A private committal ceremony for immediate family will take place at Restlawn Memorial Park.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Allen D. Henricks
records.summary: Allen D. Henricks, age 90 passed away on May 23, 2026.
records.body:
Allen Dale Henricks, 90, of Marathon, passed away on Saturday, May 23rd, 2026, leaving behind a lifetime of stories, resilience, humor, and memories that will continue to live on through his family.

Allen was born July 13th, 1935, in Sheboygan, Wisconsin, the oldest of three children, son of Arthur and Laura (Mahner) Henricks.

Born two months premature and weighing just 4½ pounds, Allen entered the world at a time when care for premature infants was far more limited. During his earliest days, his family kept him warm near an open oven set on low heat — a family story Allen later used, with his dry sense of humor, to explain why he was “always half-baked.”

He spent his childhood in Sheboygan before moving to Green Bay as a young teenager. Allen learned the value of hard work early in life. At age 12, he carried a paper route in Green Bay and later spent weekends and summers helping with the family’s dairy delivery business. Those early experiences helped shape the hardworking, practical man he would become. He also had a natural curiosity for mechanics and problem-solving, once completely disassembling and reassembling three motorcycles.

Allen proudly served in the United States Air Force before beginning a long career in education. He taught English for many years, later earned his master’s degree in Library Science, and worked as a librarian at North Central Technical Institute until his retirement in 1996.

Allen and his first wife, Miriam (Nelson) Henricks, built their life together while raising their three children, John, Charles, and Sheryl. In June of 1969, tragedy struck when Miriam passed away in a traffic accident, leaving Allen to continue raising his children while navigating the heartbreak of becoming a young widower.

Allen later married Judith Green. Together, they purchased a farm on Schoolhouse Lane and built a new life in the country, where they raised their family and welcomed two daughters, Allyson and Elizabeth. Allen and Judith later divorced.

Allen later married Nancy Wohld, and together they shared 43 years of life, family, and memories. Nancy survives him.

One of Allen’s most meaningful later-life experiences was traveling to Washington, D.C., on Honor Flight #51 on April 14, 2025, accompanied by his daughter Sheryl. It was a special opportunity to honor his service in the United States Air Force and to share a memory they had together.

Outside of work, Allen loved the outdoors and especially treasured time spent at his cabin in Marinette County. He enjoyed fishing, hunting, playing cards, and spending time up north.

Allen had a dry sense of humor. He could deliver a joke or a story with such a straight face that people were often left wondering whether he was serious. He enjoyed the quiet laugh, the perfectly timed comment, and the kind of humor that sometimes landed only after you had a moment to think about it. His family will remember not only the stories he told, but also the look in his eye when he knew he had gotten the last laugh.

Allen will be remembered for his intelligence, humor, resilience, storytelling, and quiet strength. From surviving a difficult, premature birth during the Great Depression to serving his country, raising a family, teaching students, helping others through education and libraries, and building a life filled with both hardship and joy, Allen lived with perseverance, curiosity, and character.

Allen was preceded in death by his parents, Arthur and Laura (Mahner) Henricks; his first wife, Miriam; his sister and brother-in-law, Kay and Jack Lauterbach; his brother-in-law, Dick Martin; and his eldest son, John Henricks.

Allen is lovingly remembered by his wife, Nancy Wohld Henricks; his children, Charles (Tammy) Henricks, Sheryl Henricks, Allyson (Pete) McKensey, and Elizabeth Henricks; his grandchildren, Paul, Matthew John, Matthew Charles, Dylan, Marlena, Connor, Danielle, Nicholas, and Cayllah; and his sister, Helen (Dick) Martin.

A funeral service will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Tuesday, June 9, 2026, at Rib Falls United Methodist Church, 136700 Bearskin Trail, Marathon. Visitation will be held from 9:30 a.m. until the time of the service at the church. Pastor Tim Gurlea will officiate. Interment will follow at Rib Falls Methodist Cemetery in Rib Falls, Wisconsin, with military honors accorded by the United States Air Force and American Legion Alois Dreikosen Post #469. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Beverly Joyce Eisenman
records.summary: Beverly Joyce Eisenman, age 95 passed away on May 23, 2026.
records.body:
Beverly J Eisenman, age 95, went home to Jesus on May 23, 2026 and has now been reunited with her loving husband, Harold. Beverly was born in Wausau, WI on September 23, 1930 to Chester and Alma (Tapper) Green. Beverly married Harold Eisenman on May 2, 1953 at St Stephen Lutheran Church in Wausau. They shared 72 wonderful years together.

Beverly was a devoted wife, mother, grandmother and great grandmother who' s greatest pleasure was spending time with her family. She was the manager of the hot lunch program at Riverview School for many years. Her love of the Lord was evident in her involvement at Good Shepherd Lutheran Church, baking hundreds of Christmas cookies for the yearly bake sale and preparing and serving numerous funeral luncheons. Beverly is survived by her two daughters Lynn (Lee) Schoepke, Laurel (Dave) Schmieder; grandchildren, Nicole (Jeff) Starck, Megan (Wyatt) Thompson, Eric (Heather) Schmieder, Ryan (Jamie) Schmieder; great-grandchildren, Ethan Starck, Braxton, Sawyer and Asher Thompson, Noah and Ella Schmieder and Mason, Elin and Camden Schmieder. She is further survived by many nieces, nephews, family and friends.

She was preceded in death by her beloved husband Harold Eisenman, parents, parents-in-law, and siblings Helen Hoff, Clifford Green, Wesley Green and Earl (Dick) Green. A Memorial Service will be held on Wednesday June 17, 2026 at 11:00 am at Good Shepherd Lutheran Church, (930 Edgewood Rd., Wausau, WI) with Rev. Nicole Behnke-Welke officiating. Visitation will be held on Wednesday June 17, 2026 from 10:00 AM until the hour of service at the church. .

The family would like to thank the wonderful staff from Mt. View Care Center for their comfort, care and compassion and also for the wonderful care given by the staff of Aspirus Palliative Care and Aspirus Hospice.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations can be made in Beverly's name to Good Shepherd Lutheran Church.

records.name: Dale D. Seymour
records.summary: Dale D. Seymour, age 78, of Wausau passed away on May 23, 2026.
records.body:
Dale D. Seymour, age 78, of Wausau, passed away on Saturday, May 23, 2026, while at Rennes Rehab Center in Weston. He was one of eight children, born to the late Donald and Ruth (Lodholz) Seymour in Wausau, WI. Dale attended school in Wausau, before joining the United States Navy, to proudly and bravely serve his country for over 20 years in Japan, Vietnam, Spain, Washington D.C. and Florida, until he was honorably discharged in May 1985. Dale was a patriotic man and loved his country and his family fiercely. He married Ginny Quaderer in Gibraltar in 1970. A loving union lasting 52 years, until her death in 2022.

His family and military service were near and dear to his heart. Dale enjoyed hunting, fishing and UTV rides.

Dale leaves behind his partner/care giver: Joreen Jahsmann, daughter: Stacey (Edward) Benzing of Weston, with their children: Tyler (Rachael), Brandan and Justin, son: Scott (Lori) Seymour of Marathon, with their children: Derick (Kelsi), with their son: Grayson, Jared and Andrea, 7 siblings: Allen (Carol), Chuck (Leona), Suzanne, Ron (Laura), Patty Liederman (Charlie), Jeff (Stacey) and Terry (Connie). His is further survived by his loving brothers and sister-in-laws: Jerry (Willie) Quaderer, Bob (Lynn) Quaderer, Mike Quaderer, Joy Haase, and Judy Quaderer, along with numerous nieces, nephews and good friends. He was preceded in death by his parents: Don and Ruth Seymour, mother and father-in-law: Ardith and Roy Quaderer, wife: Ginny and brother-in-laws: Alan Beck and David Quaderer.

The funeral service for Dale will be held on Friday, May 29, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. at Brainard Funeral Home in Weston. Pastor Karoline Schwantes will officiate. Visitation will be held on Friday, May 29, 2026, from 9:00 a.m. until the time of the service, also at the funeral home. Burial and military honors will be conducted at Pine Grove Cemetery immediately following the service.

In lieu of flowers, donations are to be directed to the Marathon County United Way organization.

The family wishes to express their heartfelt appreciation to the staff at the Aspirus IMC, Palliative and Oncology departments for their care and dedication in caring for Dale. They also wish to thank the wonderful staff at Rennes for making his stay as nice as possible, and to Compassus Hospice for their support and help during his last days.

Brainard Funeral Home & Cremation Center of Weston is honored to assist Dale’s family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.brainardfuneral.com.

records.name: Norbert Peter Novitzke
records.summary: Norbert Peter Novitzke, age 100, of Athens passed away on May 23, 2026.
records.body:
Norbert P. Novitzke, 100, Athens, died Saturday, May 23, 2026, at Athenian Living Community, Athens.

He was born June 23, 1925, in the town of Emmet, the son of the late Benjamin and Katherine (Knetter) Novitzke. He married Helen Frieders on Oct. 5, 1948. She preceded him in death on Sept. 16, 2018.

Norbert was a WWII Navy veteran who served in Pearl Harbor with clean up and rebuilding. He and Helen farmed and operated a cattle business in the town of Hamburg for more than 40 years where he met many people who he would eventually call his friends. He had a passion for gardening which continued into his last days at Athenian where he was still growing some tomato plants for their garden. Norbert was definitely happiest while playing cards, fishing or hunting with family and friends. His sharp mind helped pass on many stories that we will forever remember with sometimes a gasp and always a chuckle.

Survivors include a son, Terry (Brenda) Novitzke, Arbor Vitae; a daughter, Clarice (Jim) Zenner, Medford; eight grandchildren; 15 great-grandchildren; and seven great-great-grandchildren. He was preceded in death by a son, Mark Novitzke, four brothers and a sister.

Services will be at 11 a.m. on Friday, May 29, 2026, at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens. The Rev. George Graham will officiate. Entombment will be in Restlawn Cemetery, Wausau. Visitation will be from 9 a.m. Friday until time of services at the church. Beste Funeral Home, Mosinee, is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be directed to the family at www.bestefh.com

The family wishes to extend a special thanks to the staff at Athenian Assisted Living, VA Home Health and St. Croix Hospice.

records.name: Norman Koch
records.summary: Norman Koch, age 95, of Merrill passed away on May 23, 2026.
records.body:
Norman J. Koch, 95, of Merrill, passed away peacefully on May 23, 2026, at Aspirus Merrill Hospital. Born on July 15, 1930, in Merrill to Norman R. and Edna (Secard) Koch. After graduating from Merrill High School, he studied drafting at vocational school, eventually dedicating 33 years to sales with Church Mutual Insurance. He married the love of his life, Rita Steinfest, on June 28, 1952, at St. John the Baptist Catholic Church in Bloomville. Together, they created countless memories dancing the night away at local halls, where they loved to polka, fox trot, jitterbug, and waltz. They also enjoyed getting out on their motorcycle rides with friends in their younger days. Rita, a cherished partner, passed away just three years prior, on May 24, 2023.

Norman was known for his generous spirit, often bringing home treats from work trips—whether it was a candy bar or a dress for his daughters. Through his sales with Church Mutual, he was awarded many trips. He loved to take Rita on these trips, exploring beautiful destinations like Hawaii, Mexico, the Bahamas, and Florida. Norman enjoyed hunting in Colorado for elk and locally for whitetail with his sons. He enjoyed fishing and golfing in his earlier years too. Norman took pride in working on cars, tackling construction projects, and crafting cabinetry with precision. His love for Western books and movies reflected his adventurous spirit.

Survivors include his children, Steve Koch of Merrill, Cheryl (Ryan) Plautz of Merrill, David (Lisa) Koch of Wausau, and Dawn (Steve) Hintze of Merrill; grandchildren, Ashley (Steven) Sanford, Andrew (Amber) Plautz, Jacob (Olivia) Hintze, Ben Hintze, and Kaitlin Koch; great-grandchildren, Sebastian and Stella Sanford, Aidan, Addison, and Anderson Plautz, Avery, Chase, and Jonah Hintze, and Blade and Rhett Hintze; and siblings, Tom (Jean) Koch of Virginia, Jerry (Joan) Koch of Merrill, and Kathleen Paral of Green Bay.

Norman was preceded in death by his wife, Rita Koch; parents, Norman and Edna Koch; brother, Richard Koch; father-in-law and mother-in-law, Frank and Katherine Steinfest; and brother-in-law, Eugene Steinfest.

The funeral service will be held at 11:00 A.M., Monday, June 1, 2026, at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation – 903 E. Third St., Merrill. Rev. Greg Laska will officiate. Entombment will take place in Merrill Memorial Park Mausoleum. The visitation will begin at 9:00 A.M. Monday at the funeral home.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Donald D. Zamzow
records.summary: Donald D. Zamzow, age 90, of Schofield passed away on May 24, 2026.
records.body:
On May 24, 2026, Donald Dale Zamzow went to meet his Savior at the age of 90. His family is saddened to announce his passing.

Don was born on July 23, 1935, in Merrill to Herbert and Mildred (Utech) Zamzow.

He is survived by his beloved wife of 68 years, Lou Ann; his four children, Michael Zamzow, Laurie Rufledt (James), Jayne Theo (Andrew), and Thomas Zamzow (Brenda); and his cherished grandchildren, Benjamin (Marina), Samuel (Julie), Hannah (Dennis), Emily (Robert), Rebekah (River), Molly (Garret), Katarina (William), Arianna (Dean), Steven, Geena (Gene), and Julia. He is also survived by his ten great-grandchildren, whom he loved very much, Otto, Ivana, Adeline, Rowan, Harrison, Houston, Bode, Archer, and two on the way. Don also leaves behind his brother, DuWayne Zamzow; sisters-in-law, Marlene Pruss and Bonita Buttke; brothers-in-law, Roger Tepe and Allen Buttke; along with many nieces, nephews, extended family members, and dear friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Herbert and Mildred (Utech) Zamzow; his sister, Judith Tepe; sisters-in-law, Gladys Zamzow, Elaine Oelke, and Karen Buttke; and brothers-in-law, Glenn Oelke and Merlin Pruss.

Don married his high school sweetheart, Lou Ann, on October 26, 1957. In 1957, he joined the United States Army Reserves. Their story began on a school bus and blossomed into a lifetime of love, faith, and devotion. Together, they raised four children and created countless treasured memories through family gatherings, summer vacations at Little Spider Lake, and annual Thanksgiving sausage-making traditions that became beloved family events.

Don and Lou Ann were devoted Green Bay Packers season ticket holders since 1963 and rarely missed a game. One of their favorite memories was attending the historic Ice Bowl. They also faithfully supported their children through many school sporting events, musicals, and concerts over the years.

Raised on a dairy farm, Don developed a strong work ethic and lifelong interests through his involvement in 4-H. He graduated from Wausau High School in 1953 and later attended the University of Wisconsin–Madison, where he proudly played tuba in the UW Marching Band.

Don carried a deep passion for his Pomeranian heritage and worked tirelessly to preserve the Plattdeutsch (“Low German”) language and traditions. In 1994, he became the founding president of the Pommerscher Verein of Central Wisconsin and also served on the board of the Max Kade Institute. He organized heritage trips to northern Germany (formerly Pomerania), helping many reconnect with their roots. One of his most meaningful experiences was standing on the exact land from which his ancestors emigrated.

The love of music was a big part of Don’s life. Inspired by his mother, who played organ in church, and his father, who performed in a polka band, Don began playing music with friends during high school noon-hour polka dances. He later played tuba with the Jerry Goetsch Orchestra for 42 years. Their Sunday morning television shows on Channel 7 gained a devoted following and eventually led to the creation of Polka Productions, a successful travel company that took musicians and travelers around the world. Don especially loved attending the Oktoberfest in Munich, Germany.

In 2012, Don was inducted into the Polka Hall of Fame. He also played with the Wausau City Band for an extraordinary 70 years. Sharing his love of music with the community, he founded the Bull Falls Blasmusikanten and Tuba Christmas performances enjoyed by many at Bull Falls Brewery. He also directed the Brass Choir at St. Peter Lutheran Church for many years.

Don faithfully served his community through his involvement with the Wausau Noon Optimist. Professionally, he worked as an IT specialist at Drott Manufacturing in the data processing department before founding his own software company, Government & Industry Technologies (GIT), later joined by his son, Michael. Together, they also founded Bull Falls Brewery, known for its German-style craft beers. Don was also a proud supporter of the Never Forgotten Honor Flight.

Don found comfort and strength in God’s promise: “And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come back and take you to be with me, that you also may be where I am.” — John 14:3

Faith was always central to Don’s life. He served the Lord with gladness and touched countless lives through his kindness, music, heritage, and unwavering devotion to family and community.

Don had a way of seeing the good in everyone.

He will be deeply missed by his family and all who knew and loved him.

Funeral service will be held at 12:00 Noon on Wednesday, June 3, 2026, at St. Peter Ev. Lutheran Church, 1351 S. Grand Avenue, Schofield. Pastor Jeffrey Mahnke and Grandson, Pastor Benjamin Zamzow, will officiate. Visitation will be held from 9:00 AM until the time of service at the church. Interment will be in St. Paul's Lutheran Cemetery, Naugart. A reception of family and friends will follow at the Town of Berlin Community Center. In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to the His Children Our Mission Fund for the expansion of St. Peter Lutheran School.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Patricia Ann Wood
records.summary: Patricia Ann Wood, age 91 passed away on May 24, 2026.
records.body:
Patricia Ann Wood, age 91, peacefully passed away on Sunday, May 24, 2026, at

Aspirus Hospital in Wausau with members of her family by her side. Pat was born on

January 2, 1935, to Dave and Eva (Franks) Krause in Lake Linden, Michigan.

In 1953 Pat graduated from Lake Linden High School. After high school she married

William (Bill) Wood on October 17th, 1953, in Lake Linden. In 1954 Pat and Bill moved

to Alaska due to his military service. They returned to Houghton, Michigan, while Bill

attended Michigan Technological University.

In 1963 they moved to Rhinelander, Wisconsin, where Bill was employed by the State

of Wisconsin as a forester. Pat’s role was homemaker which involved raising four boys

and miscellaneous home duties. As the boys got older she had several jobs outside

the home. She first worked as a cook for the Newbold Elementary School and later

worked at one of the local grocery stores.

However, by far Pat’s most important job was supporting her husband and taking care

of her four boys while creating a loving environment with lots of excellent home-cooked

meals. Pat’s true legacy was more than what most would call “just a housewife.” She

was one of her generation’s strong women who could “do it all,” from being a loving

wife and mother, disciplinarian, scheduler, teacher, cook, home cleaning and repairs,

and with four boys a medical expert for small wound care. She loved to sew and bake

cookies.

Pat always made the holidays special for her children and grandchildren by organizing

family dinners and gatherings. Cookies and a wide variety of baked goods were always

available for all to enjoy. Pat will be fondly remembered for her dedication to her family

and for being a kind and loving wife to Bill and a caring mother and grandmother to her

children and grandchildren.

Pat is survived by her sons, James (Jean) Wood of Lake Tomahawk, Timothy Wood of

Arbor Vitae. Thomas (Bobbi Sue) Wood of Houghton, MI, daughter-in-law Jolie Wood of

Mosinee, grandchildren Andrew, Kevin, Joshua, Anna, Daniel, Ben, Joseph; great

-grandchildren William, Jocelyn, Tommy; niece Gretchen (Jim) Brogan of Ontonagon,

MI., nephew Don (Glenna) Brinkman of Lake Linden, MI., nephew Louis (Carol)

Chandonais of Lake Linden, MI., and other family and many friends.

Pat was preceded in death by her husband, Bill, infant son Michael, son Ronald, and

sisters Hasseltine (Tina) Brinkman and Margaret Chandonais, also niece Carol (Marty)

Dennis and nephew David (Mary Ann) Chandonais.

A private family service will be held for Pat at the Carlson Funeral Home. You may leave

your private condolences for the Wood family at www.carlsonfh.com.

The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Wood family.

records.name: Florence A. Marvin
records.summary: Florence A. Marvin, age 80 passed away on May 25, 2026.
records.body:
Florence A. Marvin, 80, of Wausau, passed away on Monday, May 25, 2026, at The Meadows of Marathon, under the care of Heartland Hospice. She was born in Wausau on October 25, 1945, to the late Anthony Sr. and Isabelle (Zieglmeier) Marvin.

Florence attended St. Mary’s School and later graduated from Newman Catholic High School, both in Wausau. She also received a degree in bookkeeping from technical college. For 20 years, Florence worked at Marathon Electric in Wausau, followed by 22 years at St. Anne Catholic Church, Wausau, as parish secretary. She thought of her work at St. Anne’s as her “dream job.” Florence was a strong supporter of the Humane Society of Marathon County, and a passionate sports fan – she always cheered for her Brewers, Packers, and Badgers!

Survivors include: close friends, Dorothy “Dotty” Schumacher and Debbie Lemke, extended family, and friends. Florence was preceded in death by her parents, Anthony Sr. and Isabelle Marvin; brother, Anthony “Pudge” Marvin Jr.; and grandparents, Louis and Anna Zieglmeier.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. on Wednesday, June 17, 2026, at St. Anne Catholic Church, 700 West Bridge Street, Wausau, with Rev. Tom Lindner presiding. Visitation will be at the same location and will begin at 9:00 a.m., June 17th, until the time of service. Lunch will follow. The burial will be at 10:00 a.m. on Thursday, June 18, at Restlawn Memorial Park. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

Please consider memorial donations to the Humane Society of Marathon County in lieu of flowers.

records.name: Gladys Eileen Margetta
records.summary: Gladys Eileen Margetta, age 102, of Wausau passed away on May 25, 2026.
records.body:
Gladys Eileen Margetta was peacefully called home on Monday, May 25th, 2026, at Mount View Care Center in Wausau, Wisconsin. She was born February 10, 1924, to the late August and Brita Johnson of Bessemer, Michigan. Gladys was one of eight children raised on a 38-acre family farm on Johnson Rd. in Bessemer, Michigan. She grew up in the home that her father built. When Gladys was three years old her mother, Brita, died and her father remarried Linda Johnson. Linda helped raise Gladys and her siblings, teaching them many skills including a skill that would stay with Gladys her entire life, sewing. Gladys would often reminisce about her happy childhood and what fun she had with her brothers and sisters.

In 1938, during the great depression, Gladys met Bob (Robert) Margetta while ice skating with her sister Aggie. Bob and his friend, Tom Korpi, walked Gladys and Aggie home and, following that chance meeting, Gladys and Bob dated for several years.

In 1942 Gladys worked at the Kellogg’s company in Battle Creek Michigan. This was the same year that Bob was drafted into the Armed Forces. The war needed K- Rations and Gladys’ job on the assembly line was to fill a 5 x 8 box with canned meat, two packages of crackers, matches and 4 cigarettes. Before Bob could leave for training camp, he had one thing to do, and that was to ask Gladys to marry him. Bob and Gladys eloped in a courthouse ceremony on October 13, 1942, in Battle Creek, Michigan. Along with their two witnesses, they celebrated with one of the first Sara Lee cakes ever made as their wedding cake. Gladys’ dad, August, approved of her marriage to Bob and their honeymoon was spent on their family farm.

Gladys was very devoted to her husband and followed him across the country before he was shipped overseas. After training at Camp McCoy was complete, they moved to Central Point, Oregon. The day Bob left for the war was the same day their daughter, Jeanie Marie, was born. The year was 1944. After Bob left to serve our country, Gladys moved back home to live with her sister Aggie, who helped raise Jeanie until Bob returned home on September 19th, 1945. Gladys and Jeanie were waiting at the train station when he returned and together, they started their life in Bessemer, Michigan. On September 3rd, 1946, they welcomed a son, James Michael. Gladys and Bob built their very own home on Lake Road in Ironwood Michigan in 1954. It was there they enjoyed decades of family memories.

For many years Gladys worked as a waitress at Korpi’s Cafe in Ironwood, Michigan, the restaurant owned by her sister Aggie and brother-in-law, Tom Korpi. She was a hard worker, and her family remembers visiting her often at the restaurant.

Gladys was so proud of her husband, his service in WWII and his work ethic. We find it very fitting she was called home on Memorial Day. Gladys and Bob were deeply in love and set a wonderful example for their families. They were blessed with 53 wonderful years of marriage. After Bob’s passing in 1995, Gladys moved to Schofield, Wisconsin, to be closer to family.

Gladys loved teaching her grandchildren, great grandchildren and great- great grandchildren how to play cards, cook, bake and sew. She will forever be remembered for her cardamon biscuit and pasties. She was an affable hostess, the coffee was always ready, the kitchen table set with tasty goodies and the conversation always warm and friendly. She loved to watch cooking and home decorating shows on TV and rearrange her furniture. It was not unusual to visit her one week and the following week her apartment would be arranged completely different. She enjoyed watching the Packers, Brewers and many golf tournaments. She would lend a hand to whoever needed it or offer words of wisdom about life if you asked. Her life was simple but very richly blessed because of her love for her family.

Gladys was an independent woman, a “tough ole’ gal” as she was affectionately called by her son-in-law, Don, and lived on her own until the age of 99. In her final years she spent quiet days at Mt. View Care Center. She enjoyed the beautiful view over Lake Wausau and would often comment about the birds that would fly by. Despite her eyesight and hearing challenges, she remained kind and cheerful offering to hold your hand or give a hug and sweet smile to all who cared for her. She was truly our GEM.

Gladys is survived by her son, James (Johanna) Margetta of Appleton, Wisconsin, and their family: grandsons Robert John (Gina) Margetta of Appleton, and James Mathew (Jill) Margetta of Marinette; greatgrandchildren Janelle (Josh) Bestul, Korey (Nicholas) Kolz, Jakob Margetta, and Jenna Margetta; and great-great-granddaughter Liliana Kolz.

She is further survived by her son-in-law, Don Aspinwall of Schofield, Wisconsin, and the families of Don and his late wife, Jeanie. This includes granddaughter Gretchen (Lee) Kluz of Ringle, granddaughter Sarah (Kent) Kwick of Kronenwetter and granddaughter Amy (Steve) Zondlo ( Schofield ); great-grandchildren Brittany (Nic) Lotzer, Paige (Steven) Button, and Hope (Mike) Rebman, Jordan Kwick, McKenzie ( Jordan ) Baltz, Brandon Kwick and Jack Zondlo ; great-great-grandchildren Gavin, Emmett, Finnley Lotzer, August Button, Peter and Penelope Rebman and Tennessee Jean Baltz.

Gladys was preceded in death by her beloved husband, Robert (Bob) Margetta (1995); her cherished daughter, Jeanie Marie Aspinwall (2011); and her seven siblings, Elsie Berg, Ada Holla, Jennie Jacobson, Eaner Johnson, Clarence Johnson, Agnes Korpi, and Kathleen Lanctoe.

A private family service will be held at Hillcrest Cemetery in Bessemer, Michigan at a later date.

“Jesus said to her, ‘I am the resurrection and the life. The one who believes in me will live, even though they die;'” – John 11:25

records.name: James Donald Davidowski
records.summary: James Donald Davidowski, age 79, of Hatley passed away on May 25, 2026.
records.body:
James D. Davidowski, 79 of Hatley, died on Monday, May 25, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

James was born on January 12, 1947, in Milwaukee, the son of Leo and Wanda (Windorski) Davidowski.

On May 8, 1971, James was united in marriage to JoAnn Blarek at St. Florian’s Catholic Church, Hatley.

James worked at SNE, formerly Crestline, for many years in production. He loved refinishing furniture and woodworking. James could build just about everything and especially enjoyed making toys for his grandchildren.

James is survived by his wife, JoAnn; two children, Janice (Lynn Jr.) Roe of Alma Center and Leon (Kelly) Davidowski of Hatley; five grandchildren, Calvin (Courtney) Roe, Aron Roe, Alek Roe, Taleigh (Lincoln) Davidowski and Hayden Davidowski; siblings, Joanie (Richard) Kegal and Harold (Roxanne) Davidowski and sister-in-law, Maryanne Davidowski.

James was preceded in death by his parents and siblings, Robert Davidowski, Leon Davidowski, Judy (John) Bydynkowski and Sharon (Gene) Pogorzelski.

A Funeral Service will be held at 11AM on Friday, June 12, 2026, at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg. Fr. Jerome Patric will preside. Burial will be in St. Florian’s Catholic Cemetery, Hatley. Visitation will be on Friday, from 9AM until the time of service at the funeral home.

Memories and messages of support may be shared at schmidtschulta.com.

Other Links

Lucille Blarek - mother-in-law

James Miller - brother-in-law

records.name: Roger Louis Sandquist
