records.summary: Roger Louis Sandquist, age 84 passed away on May 25, 2026.
records.body:
Roger Louis Sandquist , 84, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on May 25, 2026, at University Hospital in Madison.

Roger was born April 3, 1942 to the late Marvin and Hazel Sandquist. He grew up in the countryside outside of Wausau and was the big brother to Karen(John), Lyle(the late Sherry) ,and the late Rose Marie. After high school, he joined the Air Force and served overseas in England and North Africa.

He was a man of unwavering commitment to everything in his life. He married Karen Lehman on September 2, 1967 and remained devoted to her until the end.

He worked at Wisconsin Box for 43 years until he retired in 2009.

He was greatly involved in the lives of his only child, Lisa, and his 4 grandchildren, Isabella, Dailey, AJ, and Taylor. He loved the Brewers and the Packers and watched nearly every game. He also enjoyed putting together puzzles and had amassed quite a collection. Most of all, he never failed to give a wide smile and a laugh to everyone he met.

He was very grateful to everyone who helped him as he struggled with his health in recent years , with a special thanks to his nephew, Randy, who provided a helping hand whenever called.

A funeral service for Roger will be held on Monday, June 1, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. at Brainard Funeral Home, 522 Adams Street, Wausau, WI. A pastor from Zion Lutheran Church will officiate. Friends and family may gather at the funeral home for a time of visitation from 10:00 a.m. until the time of service. Entombment will take place at Restlawn Memorial Park with full military honors. In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made to the American Cancer Society. Brainard Funeral Home and Cremation Service, (715) 845-5525 , is honored to be assisting the Sandquist family.

records.name: Roger Joseph Stieber
records.summary: Roger Joseph Stieber, age 90 passed away on May 26, 2026.
records.body:
Roger Joseph Stieber, 90, Wausau, formerly of Marathon, passed away peacefully on Tuesday, May 26, 2026 at Marshfield Medical Center, Weston. He was born May 20, 1936 in Marathon, son of the late Henry and Anna (Lemmer) Stieber. On July 26, 1958, he married Agnes Rose Weber at St. Boniface Catholic Church, Aniwa, WI. She preceded him in death on May 15, 2014.

Roger was a Veteran and served in the United States Army. For many years, he worked as a machine operator for Wausau Paper Corporation until his retirement. Among his favorite pastimes, he enjoyed playing the stock market, talking politics and spending time with his family. Roger will be remembered for his beautiful smile, quick wit and wonderful sense of humor. He was always ready to offer good advice and a funny story. Most of all, he loved spending his retirement years in Florida with his wife, Agnes.

Survivors include four children, Dean (Robin) Stieber, James Stieber, Brian Stieber and Lynn Stieber; eleven grandchildren; nine great-grandchildren; five siblings, Dennis (Leisa) Stieber, Francis (Cindy) Stieber, Pauline (Johnny) Ivaska, Rose (Gary) Stubbe and Rita (Rich) Rennie; and many nieces and nephews.

Besides his parents and wife, Agnes, he was preceded in death by his grandson, Elliot Stieber; and two sisters, Imelda (Don) Connick and Shirley (Herold) Helvey.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. on Wednesday, June 3rd, 2026, at Holy Name Catholic Church,1104 S 9th Ave, Wausau. Rev. Woodrow Pace will preside. Visitation will be June 3rd, 2026, from 9:30 a.m. until time of Mass at the church. Burial will be at St. Mary Cemetery, 1206 Co. Rd. B, Marathon.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Sherman Edward Stock
records.summary: Sherman Edward Stock, age 98, of Hales Corners passed away on May 26, 2026.
records.body:
Sherman Edward Stock (Michaelis), of Hales Corners, passed away on May 26, 2026, at the age of 98, in the care of his daughter Kate Milani and son-in-law Hamid Milani, in Wausau, WI.

Sherman was born in Milwaukee and graduated from Rufus King High School, where he played football and sang in the choir. During that time, he met the love of his life, Sally, and they were married in 1951. He attended Marquette University, earning a bachelor’s degree in business administration. After graduation, Sherman enlisted in the United States Army and was stationed in Oklahoma, Texas, and Northern New York. Following his military service, he returned to Marquette University to attend law school, earning his Juris Doctor in 1958.

Sherman devoted much of his career to public service, working as legal counsel to U.S. Senator Gaylord Nelson for 18 years. He and Gaylord shared a close friendship, and they worked together on several significant projects, including the founding of Earth Day and a trip to Biafra to provide humanitarian relief during the Nigerian Civil War. He then worked for Governor Tony Earl as Veterans Affairs Advisor.

Artistic expression was a lifelong passion for Sherman. He created numerous paintings and drawings and was an award-winning political cartoonist under the pen name K. Cots. Sherman also showcased his artistic talents as a resident of the Village of Hales Corners and member of the Hales Corners Historical Society, contributing clay figures, dioramas, murals, and a portrait of Jim Ryan, the late village president. He remained civically engaged throughout his life, writing consistently about politics, often through letters to the editor. Known for his quick wit and dry sense of humor, he was rarely without a clever remark.

He enjoyed fishing, playing cribbage, following baseball, basketball, and football, and traveling with his wife Sally. Together, they visited multiple countries in Europe, Australia, Fiji, New Zealand, and South Africa. For many years, they spent summers with family at Pasha Lake in Ontario, Canada, and winters in Hawaii and Florida. They were long-time members of the Greenfield Avenue Presbyterian Church.

A dedicated Democrat, Sherman believed in civil rights, environmental and consumer protections, and bipartisan cooperation to improve the lives of working-class Americans. Upon his retirement as Communications Director for the Wisconsin Academy of Trial Lawyers, the organization celebrated him as “Defender of the Bill of Rights and Friend of the People.”

He was preceded in death by his brother and sister-in-law, Grover and Hazel Stock; daughter, Anne Weber; and his wife of 70 years, Sally Stock. He is survived by his son, Christopher Stock (wife Ellen); daughter, Kathryn Milani (husband Hamid); son-in-law, Jeff Weber, husband of the late Anne Weber; grandchildren Wendi Horcos, Sahar Milani, Holly Weber, Emily Guill, Billy Weber, Chris Stock, Jr., Rebecca Stock, and Michael Stock; great-grandchildren Liam Devan, Teagan Farris, and Faye Farris; and many extended family members and friends.

No services are planned at this time.

Memorial donations may be made to the Nelson Institute for Environmental Studies at the University of Wisconsin - Madison. https://50.nelson.wisc.edu/our-stories/sherman-stock/

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Andrea J. Anderson
records.summary: Andrea J. Anderson, 77, of Mosinee, passed away peacefully on May 3, 2026.
records.body:
Andrea J. Anderson, 77, Mosinee, passed away peacefully on Sunday, May 3, 2026, while under the care of St. Croix Hospice at Pine Meadows, Mosinee.

She was born October 25, 1948, in Wausau, the daughter of the late Clayton and Shirley (Rasmussen) Brandenburg. After graduation from Wausau High School in 1967, Andrea worked at Mercy Hospital in Janesville for 25 years. She is a former member of First United Methodist Church and worked in the office at times. Being amazing at crocheting and knitting blankets, Andrea provided many for friends and family.

Andrea is survived by her niece, Jena (Eustice) Kratz, Greenville, WI.

Graveside services will be at Pine Grove Cemetery, Wausau.

records.name: Brittney S. Moniz
records.summary: Brittney S. Moniz, 35, of Janesville, Minnesota, passed away on May 13, 2026.
records.body:
Brittney S. (Miilu) Moniz, 35, Janesville, MN, passed away peacefully surrounded by family on Wednesday, May 13, 2026, at Moffitt Cancer Center in Tampa, Florida.

She was born April 9, 1991, in Wausau, daughter of Kathy (Quednow) Miilu, Wausau and the late Thomas Miilu. Brittney graduated from University of Minnesota Duluth with a Biochemistry degree where she met her husband Zach. On June 6th, 2020, she married Zachary Moniz in Wausau, Wisconsin . He survives.

Brittney was employed as a Supplier Quality Engineer at Medline. She had a passion for finding solutions and helping her team succeed. Brittney loved cheering on her Green Bay Packers even having so many Minnesota Viking friends!! But her greatest joy in life were her two children.

Brittney enjoyed being outdoors every chance possible. Between long walks, boat days, yearly trips to Leech Lake, and ice fishing at multiple lakes across Minnesota and Wisconsin. You could always catch her enjoying God's creations.

She was involved in Girl Scouts for many years and received the Silver award.

Brittney graduated from Wausau West high school in 2009. During high school Brittney played softball along with track and field. She was also a football manager and curled all four years. In her senior year of curling she was the skip (captain of the team). Her team was playing in the championship game for high school state curling. Brittney made an incredible shot to win the title. Her brother Brandon is still mad at her that she got one and he didn't.

Survivors include her husband, Zachary, Janesville MN, her beautiful children, Hank 4 and Camryn 2, at home, her mother, Kathy Miilu, Wausau, her brother, Brandon Miilu, Goddaughter Leighton Warner, Godson Benjamin Banks, many aunts, uncles, cousins and friends.

Brittney was preceded in death by her father Thomas Miilu, brother Kyle Miilu, grandparents Helen and Joseph Quednow and Mildred and Walter Miilu. Also Uncles Gene Ostrowski, Dale Quednow, Tom Landree and David Jones.

A Memorial Service will be held at 11:00 a.m. Saturday, May 30, 2026, at St. Matthew Catholic Church, Wausau. The Rev. Robert Thorn will preside. Burial will be in Maple Grove Cemetery, Town of Maine. Visitation will be held on Saturday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of services all at the church. There will be a celebration of life June 27th 12 pm at Indian Island winery in Janesville, Minnesota.

In lieu of flowers the family asks for donations to the Leptomeningeal Cancer Foundation to further research of this terrible disease. https://lmcancer.org/donate/

records.name: Dale D. Seymour
records.summary: Dale D. Seymour, 78, of Wausau, passed away on May 23, 2026.
records.body:
Dale D. Seymour, age 78, of Wausau, passed away on Saturday, May 23, 2026, while at Rennes Rehab Center in Weston. He was one of eight children, born to the late Donald and Ruth (Lodholz) Seymour in Wausau, WI. Dale attended school in Wausau, before joining the United States Navy, to proudly and bravely serve his country for over 20 years in Japan, Vietnam, Spain, Washington D.C. and Florida, until he was honorably discharged in May 1985. Dale was a patriotic man and loved his country and his family fiercely. He married Ginny Quaderer in Gibraltar in 1970. A loving union lasting 52 years, until her death in 2022.

His family and military service were near and dear to his heart. Dale enjoyed hunting, fishing and UTV rides.

Dale leaves behind his partner/care giver: Joreen Jahsmann, daughter: Stacey (Edward) Benzing of Weston, with their children: Tyler (Rachael), Brandan and Justin, son: Scott (Lori) Seymour of Marathon, with their children: Derick (Kelsi), with their son: Grayson, Jared and Andrea, 7 siblings: Allen (Carol), Chuck (Leona), Suzanne, Ron (Laura), Patty Liederman (Charlie), Jeff (Stacey) and Terry (Connie). His is further survived by his loving brothers and sister-in-laws: Jerry (Willie) Quaderer, Bob (Lynn) Quaderer, Mike Quaderer, Joy Haase, and Judy Quaderer, along with numerous nieces, nephews and good friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents: Don and Ruth Seymour, mother and father-in-law: Ardith and Roy Quaderer, wife: Ginny and brother-in-laws: Alan Beck and David Quaderer.

The funeral service for Dale will be held on Friday, May 29, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. at Brainard Funeral Home in Weston. Pastor Karoline Schwantes will officiate. Visitation will be held on Friday, May 29, 2026, from 9:00 a.m. until the time of the service, also at the funeral home. Burial and military honors will be conducted at Pine Grove Cemetery immediately following the service.

In lieu of flowers, donations are to be directed to the Marathon County United Way organization.

The family wishes to express their heartfelt appreciation to the staff at the Aspirus IMC, Palliative and Oncology departments for their care and dedication in caring for Dale. They also wish to thank the wonderful staff at Rennes for making his stay as nice as possible, and to Compassus Hospice for their support and help during his last days.

records.name: Darrell W. Boyd
records.summary: Darrell W. Boyd, age 73 passed away on May 27, 2026.
records.body:
Darrell W. Boyd, 73, of Athens, passed away on Wednesday, May 27, 2026, at Aspirus Medford Hospital.

He was born on April 30, 1953, in Litchfield, Illinois, to the late Elmer and Mary (Davis) Boyd. As Darrell was growing up, he lived in several states and graduated from Ponderosa High School in Shingle Springs, California. Darrell was united in marriage to Sherri Passehl on May 18, 1990, at St. Thomas Catholic Church in Milan. She preceded him in death on March 28, 2010.

Darrell worked for many years at Stratford Builders and later as a forklift operator at Marathon Cheese. Outside of work, he loved to spend time outdoors hunting, fishing, and taking in nature at the pond near his home. He enjoyed and adored the companionship of his Black Lab, Jake. Darrell cherished the time with his family and grandchildren, and attended as many sporting events and activities that he could. He loved supporting his grandchildren. He also attended sprint car races in Shawano and Antigo with his son, Craig.

Survivors include his children, Casey (Paul) Zarnke, James Boyd, and Craig (Sarah) Boyd; grandchildren, Kamden, Kalei, and Kincaide Zarnke and Callum and Scarlette Boyd; sister, Linda (Mark) Ellenbecker; brother, William Boyd; brothers-in-law and sisters-in-law, Udell (Kari) Passehl, Tim Passehl, Sandy (Jay) Schmidt, Kevin (Barb) Passehl, Sue (John) Wood; and many nieces, nephews, relatives, and friends.

He was preceded in death by his wife, Sherri Boyd; parents, Elmer and Mary Boyd; brother, Charles (Rita) Boyd; and father-in-law and mother-in-law, Urban and Elizabeth Passehl.

The graveside service will be at 4:00 p.m. on Friday, June 12, 2026, at Wuertzburg Cemetery, 116746 Wuertzburg Road, Athens (Town of Johnson). A Celebration of Life will begin at 5:30 p.m. at the home of Craig Boyd, 217010 County Road M, Stratford. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Donald Dale Zamzow
records.summary: Donald Dale Zamzow, 90, passed away on May 24, 2026.
records.body:
On May 24, 2026, Donald Dale Zamzow went to meet his Savior at the age of 90. His family is saddened to announce his passing.

Don was born on July 23, 1935, in Merrill to Herbert and Mildred (Utech) Zamzow.

He is survived by his beloved wife of 68 years, Lou Ann; his four children, Michael Zamzow, Laurie Rufledt (James), Jayne Theo (Andrew), and Thomas Zamzow (Brenda); and his cherished grandchildren, Benjamin (Marina), Samuel (Julie), Hannah (Dennis), Emily (Robert), Rebekah (River), Molly (Garret), Katarina (William), Arianna (Dean), Steven, Geena (Gene), and Julia. He is also survived by his ten great-grandchildren, whom he loved very much, Otto, Ivana, Adeline, Rowan, Harrison, Houston, Bode, Archer, and two on the way. Don also leaves behind his brother, DuWayne Zamzow; sisters-in-law, Marlene Pruss and Bonita Buttke; brothers-in-law, Roger Tepe and Allen Buttke; along with many nieces, nephews, extended family members, and dear friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Herbert and Mildred (Utech) Zamzow; his sister, Judith Tepe; sisters-in-law, Gladys Zamzow, Elaine Oelke, and Karen Buttke; and brothers-in-law, Glenn Oelke and Merlin Pruss.

Don married his high school sweetheart, Lou Ann, on October 26, 1957. In 1957, he joined the United States Army Reserves. Their story began on a school bus and blossomed into a lifetime of love, faith, and devotion. Together, they raised four children and created countless treasured memories through family gatherings, summer vacations at Little Spider Lake, and annual Thanksgiving sausage-making traditions that became beloved family events.

Don and Lou Ann were devoted Green Bay Packers season ticket holders since 1963 and rarely missed a game. One of their favorite memories was attending the historic Ice Bowl. They also faithfully supported their children through many school sporting events, musicals, and concerts over the years.

Raised on a dairy farm, Don developed a strong work ethic and lifelong interests through his involvement in 4-H. He graduated from Wausau High School in 1953 and later attended the University of Wisconsin–Madison, where he proudly played tuba in the UW Marching Band.

Don carried a deep passion for his Pomeranian heritage and worked tirelessly to preserve the Plattdeutsch ("Low German") language and traditions. In 1994, he became the founding president of the Pommerscher Verein of Central Wisconsin and also served on the board of the Max Kade Institute. He organized heritage trips to northern Germany (formerly Pomerania), helping many reconnect with their roots. One of his most meaningful experiences was standing on the exact land from which his ancestors emigrated.

The love of music was a big part of Don's life. Inspired by his mother, who played organ in church, and his father, who performed in a polka band, Don began playing music with friends during high school noon-hour polka dances. He later played tuba with the Jerry Goetsch Orchestra for 42 years. Their Sunday morning television shows on Channel 7 gained a devoted following and eventually led to the creation of Polka Productions, a successful travel company that took musicians and travelers around the world. Don especially loved attending the Oktoberfest in Munich, Germany.

In 2012, Don was inducted into the Polka Hall of Fame. He also played with the Wausau City Band for an extraordinary 70 years. Sharing his love of music with the community, he founded the Bull Falls Blasmusikanten and Tuba Christmas performances enjoyed by many at Bull Falls Brewery. He also directed the Brass Choir at St. Peter Lutheran Church for many years.

Don faithfully served his community through his involvement with the Wausau Noon Optimist. Professionally, he worked as an IT specialist at Drott Manufacturing in the data processing department before founding his own software company, Government & Industry Technologies (GIT), later joined by his son, Michael. Together, they also founded Bull Falls Brewery, known for its German-style craft beers. Don was also a proud supporter of the Never Forgotten Honor Flight.

Don found comfort and strength in God's promise: "And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come back and take you to be with me, that you also may be where I am." — John 14:3

Faith was always central to Don's life. He served the Lord with gladness and touched countless lives through his kindness, music, heritage, and unwavering devotion to family and community.

Don had a way of seeing the good in everyone.

He will be deeply missed by his family and all who knew and loved him.

Funeral service will be held at 12:00 Noon on Wednesday, June 3, 2026, at St. Peter Ev. Lutheran Church, 1351 S. Grand Avenue, Schofield. Pastor Jeffrey Mahnke and Grandson, Pastor Benjamin Zamzow, will officiate. Visitation will be held from 9:00 AM until the time of service at the church. Interment will be in St. Paul's Lutheran Cemetery, Naugart. A reception of family and friends will follow at the Town of Berlin Community Center.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to the His Children Our Mission Fund for the expansion of St. Peter Lutheran School.

records.name: Jim Wenzel
records.summary: Jim Wenzel, 79, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on May 19, 2026.
records.body:
Jim Wenzel, age 79, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on Tuesday, May 19, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center – Weston, surrounded by his loving family.

Jim was born on April 25, 1947, in Wausau, Wisconsin, the son of Glenn and Leatrice (Poeske) Wenzel. He proudly served his country in the National Guard, a commitment that underscored his deep sense of duty and patriotism. Beyond his military service, he was devoted to his local community, contributing his leadership as a Village Trustee for the Village of Weston a role he held with dedication and pride.

An avid outdoorsman, Jim found great joy in hunting and fishing, and cherished time spent at his second home on the lake up north in St. Germain. The natural world was a sanctuary for him, where he could embrace peaceful moments and create lasting memories. He was also a passionate sports fan, delighting in cheering for Wisconsin's beloved teams: the Packers, Brewers, and Bucks.

Jim's warm and approachable nature made it easy for him to connect with others; he could hold a conversation with anyone, whether a longtime friend or a stranger. Jim sold plywood and ran propane deliveries, which he was excellent at because of his ability to communicate and talk with anyone. Above all, he treasured spending time with his parents, whose memory he deeply honored throughout his life.

He is survived by his daughter Lynn (Tim) Halambeck; son Brad (Sarah) Wenzel; sister Sue Garnsworthy; and grandchildren Megan Halambeck, Benjamin Halambeck, Andrew Wenzel, and Dillon Morrisey. Jim is preceded in death by his parents and brother-in-law Stan Garnsworthy.

The community and loved ones remember Jim as a man whose integrity, kindness, and vibrant personality touched many lives. As we reflect on his life, we hold dear the moments shared and the legacy he leaves behind a legacy rooted in service, family, and a passion for life.

A private family service will be held.

records.name: John D. Seitz Sr.
records.summary: John D. Seitz Sr., age 75, of Stratford passed away on May 27, 2026.
records.body:
John Donald Seitz, Sr., 75, of Stratford, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly at his home on Wednesday, May 27, 2026.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, June 20, 2026, at St. Joseph Catholic Church in Stratford. Visitation will begin at 9:00 a.m. at the church prior to the Mass. A livestream of the service will be available on John’s obituary page at rembsfh.com. Burial will follow at St. Joseph’s Cemetery in the Town of Eau Pleine, Marathon County where military honors will be conducted by VFW Mueller-Hintz Post #1866 - Marshfield. Sauter/Rembs Funeral Home, Stratford is assisting the family.

John was a caring husband, father, grandfather, brother, and friend. He was born on January 28, 1951, to Marvin and Lorraine (Poeschel) Seitz. He met his high school sweetheart, Jane Krause, while attending Stratford High School. Following graduation, he enlisted in the United States Army.

He proudly served in the United States Army from 1969 to 1972, assigned to the 173rd Airborne Brigade, 503rd Infantry Battalion, deployed to Vietnam, and was awarded the Bronze Star. After returning home, John and Jane were united in marriage on October 30, 1971, at St. Joseph Catholic Church.

John loved spending time on the land “Out Home.” The family mink farm became the place where he and Jane built their first home and grew their family. He found joy in life’s simple pleasures—long conversations around a fire, tending gardens for his famous horseradish, and collecting treasures. It was truly his happy place. The family later moved into town, but his heart remained “Out Home”.

John valued family, hard work, and humor. He would drop what he was doing to lend a hand to anyone in need. His quick wit made friends wherever he went. He was always ready with a warm hug or handshake, a good joke, or a playful prank, bringing laughter and joy to those around him.

John is survived by his wife, Jane Seitz, Stratford; four children, John Jr. (Cathy) Seitz of Stratford, WI, Tommy Seitz of Stratford, WI, Katie (Heather) Seitz of Milwaukee, WI, Candice (Chad) Bolding of Auburn, AL; and 6 grandchildren, Zack, John III and Olivia Seitz, Benjamin, Matthew and Samuel Bolding.

He is further survived by his siblings, Judy (Peter) Beining, Jerry (Jan) Seitz, sister-in-law, Lois Seitz, Susan Alfredson, Mary (Joe) Mayer, and Rosy Frank, and many nieces and nephews. He also loved his Krause Family, Bob Krause, Lois (James) Wagner, and Mary Martens. He was preceded in death by his parents, brother Clifford Seitz, and in-laws Robert & Mercedes Krause, Darvin Martens, Terry Frank, Gary Alfredson, and Bea Ann Krause.

In lieu of flowers, a memorial fund has been established.

Memorials may be sent to: Rembs Funeral Home Attn: Family of John Seitz, Sr. 300 S. Oak Ave. Marshfield, WI 54449

records.name: Judith J Dillon
records.summary: Judith J Dillon, age 76 passed away on May 27, 2026.
records.body:
Judith J. Dillon, age 76, passed away on May 27, 2026, and is now reunited for eternity with the love of her life, her husband, Allen Dillon, who preceded her in death on April 5, 2026.

Judith was born in Wausau, Wisconsin, to the late LeRoy Sr. and Pearl (Doepke) Schoepke. She and Allen shared more than 50 wonderful years of marriage, building a life centered on love, family, and devotion.

Above all else, Judith was a full-time mom, and her boys were the center of her world. She dedicated her life to caring for them, always making sure they had everything they needed and reminding them through her actions every day how deeply they were loved. Her sons will forever remember her as an amazing mother who was always there for them with love, support, and kindness.

Judith found great joy in the outdoors. She could often be found spending hours tending to her yard, working in her garden, or planting flowers that brought beauty to those around her. She also worked part time at Maple Ridge Farms, where she enjoyed her time and the people she worked with.

She had a special love for animals, especially her beloved cat, Scrappy, and dog, Macy, who brought her endless companionship and happiness.

Judith is lovingly survived by her four sons, Allen Dillon (significant other, Tracie), Daniel Dillon, Andrew Dillon, and Robby Dillon; her grandson, David Dillon; and her siblings, Pearl (Roger) Janikowski, LeRoy Jr. (Debbie) Schoepke, and Debra (Michael) Conklin; and her furry companion Macy.

In addition to her parents, and husband, Judith was preceded in death by her furry companion, Scrappy.

She will be deeply missed and lovingly remembered by all who knew her.

Family and friends may leave words of remembrance at MWCS.WS

records.name: Kathryn Elaine Solchenberger
records.summary: Kathryn Elaine Solchenberger, age 79, of Wausau passed away on May 27, 2026.
records.body:
Kathryn Elaine Solchenberger, age 79, was born in Eureka, Kansas, on February 5, 1947, to Claude and Gladys Gibson. She graduated from high school in Eureka and married her first husband and moved to California. She had three boys and a girl. Her first marriage ended in divorce, and she married her second husband and had another son. She was married two other times. In 2017, she married her current husband, Will. She lived in many places but finally came to Wausau, WI. There she worked as a cook at Forest Park Village.

She is survived by Bob Weems, Milwaukee, Mike Weems, Michigan, Kathy Weems, Oklahoma and Daniel McMullen, Wausau, six grandchildren, four boys and two girls, two of the boys live in Wausau and two other great grandchildren.

Kathryn liked to go by her middle name, Elaine. In the last couple of years, her eyesight progressively got worse and she enjoyed going to the Lion’s camp in Rosholt. For her honeymoon trip, she went to Hawaii, the big island, to visit her husband Will’s cousin. She also enjoyed a trip to Florida to visit the Everglades and Key West, St. Louis to visit her cousin Betty Joe, Branson and Yellowstone.

Per family request, no services will be held.

In lieu of flowers, please make a donation to the Feeding America Foundation.

Helke Funeral Home & Cremation Services of Wausau is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences are encouraged and may be made at www.helke.com.

records.name: Kevin Joseph Schuster
records.summary: Kevin Joseph Schuster, 53, of Schofield, passed away unexpectedly on May 20, 2026.
records.body:
Kevin Joseph Schuster, 53, passed away unexpectedly on Wednesday, May 20, 2026, in Schofield.

Kevin was born on January 13, 1973, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to Kenneth and Kathleen (Woznicki) Schuster. He graduated from D.C. Everest Senior High in 1991.

From a young age, Kevin found a passion for baking, which evolved into a love for everything related to food – its creation, its presentation, and its service. His career spanned working in restaurants, restaurant management, and ownership; most recently in food service sales. Special to him were trips to Golden Corral with his daughter, Madisyn, for the eighth wonder of the world – the chocolate fountain.

Kevin enjoyed mountain bike racing with Madisyn, as well as spending time at his cottage on White Potato Lake. There, you could find Kevin engaging in pontoon rides, fishing, ice fishing, and socializing with friends. Over the years, many family camping trips were shared with his siblings and extended family.

Kevin is survived by his daughter, Madisyn Schuster (fiance, Alex Legatt) of Prior Lake, MN; mother, Kathy Schuster of Weston; his siblings, Jamie (Scott) Lerch of Weston, Craig (Kate) Schuster of Wausau, and Cindy (Nate) Hall of Weston; nieces and nephews, Keri (Zach) Neuner of Wausau, Kayla (Isaac) Frieders of Kronenwetter, Ashton Schuster of Charlotte, NC, Colton, Marcus, and Kirsten Hall, all of Weston, Casper and Linus Schuster of Wausau; great-nephews, Theo Neuner and Cal and Remi Frieders; and step-sons, Tyler Krzanowski and Blake Wozniak. He is further survived by many friends and extended family other relatives and friends. He is preceded in death by his father, Kenny Schuster.

An Open House Celebration of Life will be held from 4:00 – 7:00 PM on Friday, May 29, 2026, at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston, WI 54476.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to the National Pancreas Foundation.

records.name: LaVerne Helen Mussell
records.summary: LaVerne Helen Mussell, age 96 passed away on May 27, 2026.
records.body:
LaVerne Helen Mussell, a beloved mother, grandmother, and friend, passed away peacefully on May 27, 2026, at the age of 96. Born on April 19, 1930, in Hartford, WI, she was the daughter of Harry and Leona Licht.

LaVerne lived a full and vibrant life, marrying her soulmate, Robert Mussell, on July 10, 1948. Robert passed away on September 25, 2010, but their love and memories lived on in the hearts of their family.

LaVerne spent many years co-owning the family business, Tomahawk Tire Center. LaVerne found joy in playing the piano, organ, and guitar. She was also an avid puzzler who enjoyed putting together jigsaw puzzles.

Her love for nature was evident in her fondness for cardinals and hummingbirds, while her enthusiasm for the Green Bay Packers was unmatched. LaVerne was known as the local flag lady on Thielman Street, further showcasing her dedication to her community. A devoted Catholic, she was an active member of St. Francis Xavier Church in Merrill.

LaVerne is survived by her children, Gary (Debra Shipway) Mussell, Barbara (Dennis) Hopfensperger, and Steve (Jenny) Mussell-grandchildren, James Hopfensperger, Kristine (Matthew) O'Brien, Ashley (Brian) Andrew, Cody Mussell, and Ryan Mussell. great-grandchildren, Thea Kay Andrew, Skylar Rose Andrew, Cora Lynn O'Brien, and Amy Rose O'Brien. LaVerne is also survived by brother-in-law Paul Mussell and her many nieces and nephews, who all held a special place in her heart.

LaVerne was preceded in death by her parents, her brothers Henry (Esther) Licht and Robert "Bobby" (Diane) Licht, as well as her sisters-in-law, Phyllis Mussell and Augusta (Robert) Leu, and her brother-in-law Alfred (Marion) Mussell.

In lieu of flowers, the family asks that donations be made in LaVerne's name to the Lincoln County Animal Shelter.

A special thank you to the staff at Bell Tower and Chaplain John for their compassionate care.

A Visitation will be at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, 301 Eagle Dr., Merrill, Wisconsin from 4pm-7pm on Thursday, June 18, 2026. Mass of Christian burial will be held at St. John the Baptist Catholic Church in Bloomville, on Friday, June 19 at 11am.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: MaryAnn H. Luther
records.summary: MaryAnn H. Luther, 84, passed away on May 20, 2026.
records.body:
MaryAnn H. (Faillaci) Luther, 84, passed away May 20, 2026.

She was born September 28, 1941, daughter of the late Santo and Frances (Campo) Faillaci. On October 11, 1969 she married Melvin Luther Jr. and he preceded her in death on March 16, 2020.

Survivors include her daughter, Malanie Burley; grandchildren, Isabella Javidan, Benjamin Javidan and Charles Sparrow.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated 4:30 pm Friday, June 26, 2026 at St. Michael Catholic Church, Wausau. Rev. Tom Lindner will preside. Visitation will be Friday from 3:30 pm until the time of service all at St. Michael Catholic Church, Wausau.

records.name: Paul J. Frahm
records.summary: Paul J. Frahm, age 59, of Wausau passed away on May 27, 2026.
records.body:
Paul J. Frahm, 59, Wausau passed away on Wednesday, May 27, 2026 at his home.

He was born January 23, 1967 in Wausau to Gary and Maxine (Zurow) Frahm, Wausau.

For many years Paul was a delivery driver at Krueger Floral, Kronenwetter. He later went to work at the Rib Mountain Greenhouse. Prior to his retirement because of health issues he enjoyed working at the Duane L. Corbin shooting range as a rangemaster.

Some of Paul’s favorite pastimes included playing softball at Sunnyvale, fishing, hunting and shooting bow at Windy Hill.

Survivors include his parents, Gary and Maxine Frahm, Wausau; his children, Courtney, Tanner and Tyler; his siblings, Trenny (Jim Foss) Frahm, Weston and Shawn Frahm, Wausau; two nephews and a niece.

Graveside services will be held at 10:30 a.m. Saturday, June 6, 2026, at Calvary Cemetery, Athens. The Rev. Msgr. Joseph Diermeier will officiate.

Paul’s family would like to extend their sincere and heartfelt gratitude to Kent Spiegel and his family for their friendship and continued support through the years.

Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Wausau is in charge of arrangements. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Robert Joseph Faber
records.summary: Robert "Bob" Joseph Faber, 57, of Spencer, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly on May 21, 2026.
records.body:
Robert "PeeWee" Faber, age 57, of Spencer, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly at home on May 21, 2026.

Born on May 17, 1969, in Neillsville, Wisconsin, Bob was the cherished son of Joseph and Jean (Strey) Faber. After graduating from Loyal High School in 1987, he pursued his passion for electrical work, beginning his career at Wisconsin Homes. Through years of dedication and hard work, Bob became a Master Electrician and proudly established his own business, Faber Electric. He was known not only for his skill, but also for his generosity — always willing to lend a hand or offer guidance to friends, neighbors, and customers alike.

In 1989, Bob met Tracy Boushack in Marshfield, and together they built a loving family. Bob and Tracy were blessed with two daughters, Lauren and Haley, who brought him endless joy and pride.

Bob cherished the outdoors, whether boating on calm waters, hunting in the woods, or competing in long-range shooting. He loved a good challenge and approached life with the same passion and precision he brought to his work.

He is survived by his wife, Tracy; his daughters, Lauren and Haley; his parents, Joseph and Jean Faber; his sisters, Tammy Heil (Kurt) and Sandra Leonhard (Gerard); and his brothers, Edward and Tracy (Tanneberg) Faber, Steven and Jessica (Nelson) Faber. He is also survived by his favorite in-laws, Debbie and Paul Boushack; his brothers-in-law, Greg and Ayme (VerKilen) Boushack and Jeremiah Boushack; many cherished nieces and nephews; and his beloved fur babies, Kipton, Winnie, and Alaska.

Bob's warmth, humor, smart mouth, and unwavering commitment to family and community will be remembered by all who knew him. Most especially, he will be remembered by those who loved him for always saying, "Don't tell me what to do."

A celebration of Bob's life will be held on June 27th, 2026 in the Marshfield Junior Fair Building, Marshfield Wisconsin, from noon to 3pm.

records.name: Roger Louis Sandquist
records.summary: Roger Louis Sandquist, 84, of Wausau, passed away on May 25, 2026.
records.body:
Roger Louis Sandquist, 84, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on May 25, 2026, at University Hospital in Madison.

Roger was born April 3, 1942 to the late Marvin and Hazel Sandquist. He grew up in the countryside outside of Wausau and was the big brother to Karen(John), Lyle(the late Sherry) ,and the late Rose Marie. After high school, he joined the Air Force and served overseas in England and North Africa.

He was a man of unwavering commitment to everything in his life. He married Karen Lehman on September 2, 1967 and remained devoted to her until the end.

He worked at Wisconsin Box for 43 years until he retired in 2009.

He was greatly involved in the lives of his only child, Lisa, and his 4 grandchildren, Isabella, Dailey, AJ, and Taylor. He loved the Brewers and the Packers and watched nearly every game. He also enjoyed putting together puzzles and had amassed quite a collection. Most of all, he never failed to give a wide smile and a laugh to everyone he met.

He was very grateful to everyone who helped him as he struggled with his health in recent years , with a special thanks to his nephew, Randy, who provided a helping hand whenever called.

A funeral service for Roger will be held on Monday, June 1, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. at Brainard Funeral Home, 522 Adams Street, Wausau, WI. A pastor from Zion Lutheran Church will officiate. Friends and family may gather at the funeral home for a time of visitation from 10:00 a.m. until the time of service.

Entombment will take place at Restlawn Memorial Park with full military honors.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made to the American Cancer Society.

records.name: Rosemary Farchione
records.summary: Rosemary "Dolly" Farchione, 94, passed away peacefully at home.
records.body:
With broken hearts we announce the passing of Rosemary "Dolly" Farchione, who peacefully passed away in her sleep at the age of 94. Although her passing was sudden and unexpected, we find comfort in knowing she experienced no pain and was at home, surrounded by family, just as she would have wanted.

Rosemary was a proud Italian wife, mother, grandmother, great-grandmother, sister, and friend. She was the heart and soul of her family. She was and remains the matriarch, the foundation upon which generations were built, and the reason so many of us understand the true meaning of unconditional love, loyalty, and family. Her family was her life's greatest accomplishment and her greatest joy. She devoted herself completely to those she loved, protecting them fiercely and loving them without limits.

She is survived by her beloved husband, Dominic Farchione, age 95. Together, they were approaching an incredible milestone of 75 years of marriage. Anyone who knew them together knew that there is no love greater than theirs.

She also leaves behind her loving children: Katherine Miller; Debbie Habel and her husband, Donald; Dominic Farchione and his wife, Veronica; Deann Farchione and her fiancé,m,  Kyle; and Michelle Ruenger, whom she lovingly embraced as her adopted daughter. Rosemary was also blessed with 10 cherished grandchildren and 10 adored great-grandchildren, all of whom were the center of her world.

She is also survived by her beloved brother, Tony Gianfermi, whom she loved dearly.

To know Rosemary was to know a woman with a heart larger than life. She loved people and her warmth made everyone feel like a long time friend regardless of who they were. She's known for chatting on the phone for hours, her warm sense of humor, and her ability to turn around anyone's bad day. Rosemary was never negative, and always knew the right things to say. She was always the last one to hang up, never ending a conversation without countless "I love you's."

Rosemary's happy place was her kitchen, full of family laughing at the table for hours. She was an incredible cook and would be often found leaning over the kitchen sink cutting up vegetables in her hand. Some of our greatest memories were made gathered around her table, sharing stories, laughter, and meals prepared with love.

She loved her Lifetime movies and classic films and was quite the night owl, often staying up far later than anyone else. She was famous for trying to convince her daughter to pull all-nighters with her, refusing to let the evening end before one more conversation, one more movie, or one more laugh. She also loved her hard candies and you could always find a stash in her purse.

Her kitchen table, her voice on the phone, her stories, her traditions, and her unwavering love brought generations together. She taught us that family is not simply something you have. It is something you protect, cherish, and hold close every day. She was our anchor, our storyteller, our protector, and our guide.

While we mourn her loss, we find comfort in knowing she has been reunited in Heaven with those she loved so dearly. She joins her beloved son, James Farchione; her brothers, Michael Gianfermi, Donald Gianfermi, Frank Gianfermi, Eddie Gianfermi, Johnny Gianfermi; and her sister Joann Murney. She has also reunited with her cherished mother, Lucy Gianfermi, and her dad, Arduino; her beloved mother-in-law, Marietta and father-in-law, Vincenzo. We can only imagine the joyful reunion that welcomed her home.

Losing her leaves a space that can never truly be filled. Yet her legacy lives on in her children, grandchildren, great-grandchildren, and all who were fortunate enough to be loved by her. We will hear her in our conversations, see her in our traditions, and feel her presence always.

We miss her deeply. We love her endlessly. And we will carry her memory with us always.

Rest in peace, Nana. The family you loved so fiercely will continue to honor you, cherish you, and keep your spirit alive for generations to come.

Private services are being held.

records.name: Russell William Running
records.summary: Russell William Running, age 65, of Sugar Camp Wisconsin passed away on May 27, 2026.
records.body:
Russell William Running, age 65 of Sugar Camp Wisconsin passed away on Wednesday May 27, 2026 at Aspirus Hospital in Wausau. He was born in Appleton, Wisconsin on October 10, 1960, the son of James and Shirley (Jenneman) Running.

Russ graduated from Three Lakes High School in 1979. He then went on to work at Watercraft, Shorewood, and Three Lakes Marine.

Russ met his future wife Debby at her summer residence while working on her boat. Then they became a regulars at Brigg’s Bar in Three Lakes. They were married August 17, 1991, and soon welcomed their children Kacey and Brandon who are the pride and joy of their lives.  Their family was completed over the years with their labs Bandit, Shadow, Cinder, Royal, and Jessie who Russ loved so much.

Russ owned and operated Running’s Yamaha - John Deere dealership for 22 years. He then went to work at the Three lakes School District with their custodial team until his retirement in 2025.

Russ enjoyed anything racing including Nascar, dirt ovals, and vintage snowmobiles. He had no greater joy then building and working on Vintage Race sleds for both Kacey and Brandon to race.

Russ will lovingly be remembered by many as “Hoytz” or “Rad Dude” and even “Honey” but to all who new him he will forever be called Friend.

Russ is survived by his wife Debby, Daughter Kacey (Felicia Schuster) Running, Son Brandon (Sam) Running, His Father Jim (Kathy) Running, Brothers Rick (Lisa) Running, Mike (Traci) Running, Sister Lisa (Jack) Wales, Sister-in-law Donna (Jay) Teagle, Brother-in-law David (Lissy) Moore. As well as many beloved Aunts, Uncles, Nieces, Nephews, and Cousins.

He is Preceded in death by his mother Shirley Running, Brother and sister-in-law Tim and Cheryl Running, Mother-in-Law Jane Moore.

A celebration of life and luncheon will be held on Saturday July 11th 11am-2pm at the Sugar Camp Town Hall. The Family would like to extend a special thank you to friends Jessica Czaplinski, and Toni Swendson for help caring for Russ.

Russ’s kind and gentle heart will live on with us all.

“Make It your ambition to lead a quiet life, to mind your own business and to work with your hands.” 1 Thessalonians 4:11a

You may leave your private condolences for the Running family at www.carlsonfh.com.  The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Running family.

records.name: Sally Ann Puent
records.summary: Sally Ann Puent, age 72 passed away on May 27, 2026.
records.body:
Sally Ann Puent, passed away Wednesday, May 27, 2026, at Aspirus Hospice House, Wausau, after a lengthy battle with cancer.

She was born May 2, 1954, in Green Bay, daughter of the late Donald and Lois (Stoeckly) Fellenz. On May 5, 1973, she married Robert “Bob” Puent Jr. at St. Wenceslaus Catholic Church in LaCrosse.

In 1972, Sally graduated from Aquinas and went on to Mid-State Technical School in Stevens Point, where she obtained her LPN in nursing while Bob was attending UWSP. She later continued her education at Nicolet Technical College in Rhinelander, earning her RN while both of her boys were in college.

Sally cherished every moment spent with her children and grandchildren. Together they enjoyed sledding, swimming, and trips to the playground, and she proudly chauffeured her grandchildren to countless appointments, sports practices, and ballet practice. She greatly enjoyed attending her grandchildren’s school programs, sports events, and ballet performances. Sally also enjoyed card games, card tricks, board games, and following the “March Madness” bracket challenge with her family and grandchildren, especially the playful “trash talk” with her grandsons. She loved cooking and baking, and always looked forward to decorating Christmas cookies with her grandchildren, using plenty of sprinkles. Her banana bread was always in high demand. She also enjoyed knitting, crocheting and doing crossword puzzles. Poker games with her brothers were always special occasions for her.

She loved visiting Lake Superior and exploring the restaurants and waterfalls along the North Shore. Sally was also active in her faith community, playing in the bell choir at St. Mark Catholic Church.

Her infectious smile, laughter, and sense of humor will be dearly missed by her family and all who knew and loved her.

Survivors include husband, Bob Puent; sons, Brian (Jodi) Puent and Kevin (Ciolagh) Puent; grandchildren, Damien, Griffin, Everett, Addison, Aubrey, Emmett, Clayton and Elsie; brothers, Matt (Pam) Fellenz, Marty (Joanie) Fellenz, Myron (Tammy) Fellenz, Steve (Gina) Fellenz and Paul (Michelle) Fellenz; sister-in-law, Patti Fellenz; numerous nieces, nephews, and other relatives and friends.

Besides her parents, she was preceded in death by her brothers, Mike and Mark Fellenz.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated 11:00 a.m. Monday, June 8, 2026, at St. Mark Catholic Church, Rothschild. Rev. Eric Mashak will preside. Visitation will be Monday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service, all at St. Mark Catholic Church, Rothschild.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Scott R. Durkee
records.summary: Scott Durkee, 63, of Mosinee, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on May 19, 2026.
records.body:
Scott Durkee, age 63, of Mosinee, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on Tuesday, May 19, 2026, at the Aspirus Hospice House surrounded by his loving family and friends.

Scott was born on April 22, 1963, in Wausau, Wisconsin, the son of Wallace and Marjorie (Hoskin) Durkee. A proud graduate of Wausau West High School in 1981, Scott's commitment to education continued as he earned his bachelor's degree in accounting from Upper Iowa University in 2007. His professional journey was distinguished by over 24 years of dedicated service at United Healthcare, where his work ethic, positive attitude, and humor left a lasting impression on co-workers and customers alike. He married the love of his life Jeaninne Rux in 2006.

Scott's 23 years of service in the Army Reserve, culminating in the rank of Sergeant, reflected his deep sense of duty and discipline. Those who served with him remember a man of integrity, courage, and steadfast loyalty.

Beyond his professional achievements and military service, Scott was known for his infectious sense of humor and the big smile that greeted everyone he met. He was a passionate supporter of the Green Bay Packers, Milwaukee Brewers, Milwaukee Bucks, and Wisconsin Badgers. He was often seen cheering with a bottle of Miller Lite in hand. His love of the outdoors drew him to camping adventures shared with friends, family, and his beloved dogs. Music, a lifelong passion, provided rhythm and joy to his everyday life. Scott sang and played bass guitar in several bands including Taceyon, 3 on Fire, Kliff Klavon, After You, and 5 Mile Scream.

One of Scott's proudest accomplishments was his remarkable bowling career, during which he scored an incredible fourteen perfect games, earning him a well-deserved induction into the United States Bowling Congress Wausau Hall of Fame in March 2024. This honor speaks not only to his skill but to the dedication and perseverance that defined his approach to life.

Scott is survived by his wife of 20 years, Jeaninne Durkee; his collies Edith Ann and Rogan, his sister Gena Geng, brothers Dave (Kay) Durkee, Doug Durkee, and Dale (Renee) Durkee; and brother-in-law Mike (Jan) Rux and several nieces and nephews.

He is preceded in death by his parents, grandparents and his beloved dogs, Brady, Casey, Tucker, Jordy and Blaze.

A celebration of life service for Scott will be held on Saturday, June 20, 2026, at 11:00AM at Restlawn Memorial Park Chapel, 235962 North Troy Street, Wausau, WI 54403, Visitation will take place from 10:00AM until the of service at 11:00AM. Military Honors will follow the service.

Scott's family would like to extend a sincere Thank You to everyone at Aspirus Hospice in Home Care and the Aspirus Hospice House for 13 weeks of amazing compassionate care.

records.name: Theodore Clintsman
records.summary: Theodore Clintsman, 91, of Wausau, passed away peacefully on May 21, 2026.
records.body:
Theodore (Ted) Clintsman passed away peacefully on May 21, 2026 at Mount View Care Center in Wausau. He was 91 years old.

Ted was born in Greenwood, WI on July 8, 1934 – the 5th of 6 siblings. In his 20's, he moved to Waukesha, WI and worked at various factories. In 1972, he and his family moved to Wausau, WI. After working at Wausau Iron Works / LOED as a machinist and assembler for 20 years, he decided to open a used furniture and appliance store – Budget Resale. There he not only could pursue his passion for sales and working with his hands; he could also chat with his customers – something else he enjoyed.

When not at his store, Ted faithfully met with his buddies for coffee and for a swim at a therapy pool. He was friendly and talkative – quick to greet others and, as he used to like to put it, "give them grief". He also loved spending time up at his camper in Tomahawk and later, Birnamwood – where he spent most of the time relaxing by the campfire and socializing with other campers.

In 2014, when Ted decided to close Budget Resale for good, the local paper interviewed him. In that article, he talked about his joy in serving and visiting with his customers and his sales philosophy: "be fair with people and treat them like you want to be treated". That was Ted –was how he operated his business and who he was as a person.

Ted is survived by his sons, Gregory (Judy) and Douglas (Linda), by his grandsons, Justin (Rebecca), Sean (Meghan), Evan (Phoenix), and Daniel (Taryn) and by his sister Nell. Preceding him in death were his parents, Fred and Luella Clintsman, his sisters Marie, Irene, Shirley, and his brother, Dale.

A visitation will be held at St. Mark's Lutheran Church, 600 Stevens Dr, Wausau, Wisconsin, on Friday, June 12, at 10:30 am until the time of service at 12:00 officiated by Pastor Eric Havan. A luncheon will follow the service.

records.name: Joyce Wojcik
records.summary: Joyce Wojcik, age 92, of Pittsville passed away on May 28, 2026.
records.body:
Joyce Wojcik, 92, Pittsville, passed away on Thursday, May 28, 2026 at Aster Assisted Living, Marshfield.

A memorial service will be held at 11:00 am on Wednesday, June 3, 2026 at Sauter/Rembs Funeral Home, Stratford with visitation from 9:00 am until the time of service. Christopher Ewan will officiate. Burial will follow at Ebenezer United Church of Christ Cemetery, Town of Cleveland, Marathon County.

Joyce was born on May 29, 1933 in Stratford to John and Alinda (Wiesman) Landwehr. She graduated from Stratford High School.

Joyce was united in marriage to Donavan Bean on August 12, 1952 at Ebenezer United Church of Christ. He passed away on October 5, 1984. She married Frank Wojcik on May 5, 1990. He died on September 18, 2022.

Joyce enjoyed playing Aggravation, doing jigsaw puzzles, knitting dish cloths, and quilting. Most of all, she loved being with her grandkids, great grandkids, and her friends at Aster.

She is survived by her children, Christy (Gordon) Elsasser of Pittsville, Kevin (Rebecca) Bean of Crestwood, KY and Cindy (Steve) Redmond of Arpin, step daughter, Ann Cahak of Rhinelander, 9 grandchildren, and 18 great grandchildren.

She was preceded in death by her parents, husbands; sisters, Mildred Hilgeman, Angeline Koehler, Ruth Yoder, and Irene Kuyoth; and brothers, Roland, Lawrence, Benjamin, and Roger Landwehr.

The family would like to extend a special thank you to the entire staff at Aster.

records.name: Paul Michalski
records.summary: Paul Michalski, age 71, of Wausau passed away on May 28, 2026.
records.body:
Paul Anthony Michalski, age 71, was called home to be with his Lord and Savior on May 28, 2026, after a courageous battle with small-cell lung cancer. He was born on February 18, 1955, to Justin and Pearl (Sowinski) Michalski in Milwaukee, Wisconsin.

Paul graduated from Milwaukee’s Saint Alexander Grade School in 1969 and Pulaski High School in 1973. He earned a Bachelor of Science degree in Electrical Engineering from Marquette University in 1977 and a Master’s degree in Engineering Management from the Milwaukee School of Engineering (MSOE) in 1980. In 1988, he obtained his Professional Engineer license.

Paul made his home in the Wausau area, where he built a successful career and devoted himself to his family. He and Linda (Lambert) Michalski were blessed with three children, who remained a source of great pride and joy throughout his life. On November 11, 2011, Paul married Sharon Modia, and together they shared many years of love, faith, and companionship.

Paul dedicated more than 35 years of service to Wisconsin Public Service Corporation, retiring in 2015. For much of his career, he served as the Substation Construction and Maintenance Manager for the utility’s North Central Wisconsin region.

Paul lived life with passion and conviction. He was never shy about sharing his opinions, especially when it came to politics and current events, and he enjoyed spirited discussions with family and friends. While he was not always one to express affection with words, he showed his love in other ways. One of his greatest joys was planning memorable vacations, creating opportunities for his family to spend time together and build lasting memories. He took pride in bringing people together and creating experiences that would be remembered for years to come.

One of Paul's favorite hobbies was genealogical research. Through years of dedicated work, he built an Ancestry family tree containing more than 20,000 individuals. His research connected relatives, preserved family histories, and assisted hundreds of people in discovering their roots. His life's work in genealogy will remain a lasting gift to future generations.

Paul dearly loved his two long-haired Dachshunds, Keena and Bukie, who rarely left his side and were constant companions throughout the years, especially during his four-year battle with cancer. Keena and Bukie, who knew him simply as "Pops," will miss him dearly.

Paul is survived by his beloved wife, Sharon (Modia) Michalski; the mother of his children, Linda (Lambert) Michalski; his daughters, Lindsay (Nick) Larson and Amanda (Thomas) Hardy; and his son, Andrew Michalski. He was a proud Dzia Dzia to Jovie, Anders, and Ansel Larson. He is survived by his stepson, Tyler Kenowski, and his stepdaughter, Krystal Kenowski.

Paul is also survived by his dear siblings, Camille Mueller, Justin (Lori) Michalski, Richard Michalski, and Jan (Andria) Michalski, as well as many beloved nieces, nephews, cousins, and extended family members.

Paul was preceded in death by his parents, Justin and Pearl (Sowinski) Michalski; his brother-in-law, Carl Mueller; his sister-in-law, Loretta (Watkins) Michalski; and his nephew-in-law, Major Erik Boyce, USMC.

A visitation will be held at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston, WI 54476, on Friday, June 5, 2026, from 4:00 to 6:00 p.m. Visitation will continue at St. Mark's Lutheran Church, 600 Stevens Drive, Wausau, WI 54401, on Saturday, June 6, 2026, from 10:00 a.m. until the funeral service at 11:00 a.m., with Pastor Eric Hauan officiating. Family and friends are invited to a luncheon at St. Mark's Lutheran Church following the funeral service. Paul's committal and entombment will be held on Monday, June 8, 2026, at 10:00 a.m., at Restlawn Memorial Park.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made to St. Mark's Lutheran Church of Wausau, St. Michael Catholic Church of Wausau, or the Marathon County Humane Society.

A special thank you to Dr. Christopher Peterson for the outstanding care, compassion, and support he provided to Paul over the years. The family is also deeply grateful to the wonderful staff of the Aspirus Oncology and Cardiac units, as well as the IMC, ICU, Palliative Care, and Hospice teams. Their skill, kindness, and positive attitudes helped Paul through many medical challenges and meant so much to him and his loved ones.

Paul's faith, devotion to family, generosity, and passion for preserving family history leave a legacy that will continue to inspire those who knew and loved him.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Thomas "Tom" Louis Fahey
records.summary: Thomas "Tom" Louis Fahey, age 80, of Edgar passed away on May 28, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas Louis Fahey of Edgar, passed away on May 28, 2026, at the age of 80, surrounded by his loving family at his home in Tomahawk.

Tom was born on June 5, 1945, in Marshfield to Donald and Elizabeth (Koller) Fahey. He grew up living at Cedar Hedge Cheese Factory in the township of Emmet. Tom graduated from Edgar High School in 1963 and wed Mary Ellen (Guralski) Fahey on June 14,1969, at Saint John the Baptist Parish. Together they shared 56 years of blessed marriage, raising their five children: Janet, Julie, Jimmy, Don, and Mary Lou.

Tom resided in Edgar, where together he operated Pine Valley Cheese Factory for 20 years. He then went on to become an entrepreneur of sorts owning and operating both Westside and Brooklyn Hill Mobile Home Parks, Fahey’s Apartments in Weston, and the Mosinee Laundry Mat. He attended the short-course at UW-Madison and graduated with his cheesemaking degree. For the past 10 years, he spent his time in quiet contentment on Lake Alice in Tomahawk, where he could drink his coffee and view God’s beauty looking out his bay window to see the morning sunrise, ripples on the lake, and be mesmerized by all the wildlife that surrounded him.

Tom was a 30-year kidney transplant survivor, receiving a kidney from his sister Jean, creating a bond stronger than any other between a brother and sister.

Tom was a handy man of all sorts who could fix anything. He strongly believed in educating his children on the tools needed for both on the job and in life (or to at least run and get the tools for him). In Tomahawk, he spent his time in his garage, feeding the bluejays, orioles, squirrels, and watching NASCAR. The best part of his day was sitting on his deck overlooking the water, chatting with his loving wife Mary.

Being a community member of Edgar, he served on the Edgar Village Board, a member of the Edgar Lions Club, Krecji City Cruisers Snowmobile Club, and served on the Saint John the Baptist church board, where he was a lifelong member.

Tom is survived by his wife, Mary, their children: Janet (Matt) Baumann – Bryce (Lindsey) Schilling, Olivia (Noah) Swita, Owen Baumann, and Olysta Baumann; Julie Fahey (Tony Bong); Jimmy (Tammy) Fahey – Hailey (Josh) Elmhorst, Austin Fahey: Don (Jenna) Fahey – Malayna, Kendra, Kase; and Mary Lou (Brad) Zettler – Eli, Erika, Emma Lou, and Ethan. Two great grandchildren: Mallory Schilling and Palmer Swita. He is also survived by siblings: Michael (Mary Katherine) Fahey, Mary Lynn (Kenny) Hoernke, Ann (John) Napierala, and Jean (Mark) Fonferek; in-laws: Glenroy (Diane) Guralski, Terra Lee Guralski, Jean (John) Sattler, Gerald (Sandy) Guralski, Judy (Wayne) Novak, Steve (Carol) Guralski and many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Donald and Elizabeth Fahey; in-laws, John and Irene Guralski; brothers-in-law LeRoy Guralski, Michael Guralski and sisters-in-law, Rose, Louise, and Elsie Guralski; nephews Frank Guralski, Luke Guralski, Timothy Hoernke, niece Melinda Guralski, close cousin Richard Esp, and still born infant son Joseph.

A funeral Mass will be held at 11:00a.m. on Friday, June 5, 2026, at St. John the Baptist Catholic Parish in Edgar. Burial will take place at the parish cemetery. Visitation will be at the church from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service at 11:00 a.m. The honor of pallbearer belongs to Bryce Schilling, Owen Baumann, Austin Fahey, Kase Fahey, Eli and Ethan Zettler.

Special thanks to Don and the team from Davita Dialysis in Woodruff, Wisconsin, where dad was receiving treatment and to Jenn, RN, and the Aspirus Hospice team who supported him during his final days.

In lieu of flowers, a memorable fund is being established in his memory.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Thomas Yang
records.summary: Thomas Yang, age 62, of Wausau passed away on May 28, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas Yang, 62, Wausau, died Thursday, May 28, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born May 15, 1964 in Laos, the son of Pao Yang and Yia Lor.  Survivors include his wife, Kaosheng Vang; three children, Dao Yang, Douang Yang and Angelina Yang.

Funeral services will be held on Saturday, June 13, 2026 with visitation beginning at 8:00 a.m. until services at 11:30 a.m. at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Avenue in Wausau.  Burial will be in Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Allen Dale Henricks
records.summary: Allen Dale Henricks, 90, of Marathon, passed away on May 23, 2026.
records.body:
Allen Dale Henricks, 90, of Marathon, passed away on Saturday, May 23rd, 2026, leaving behind a lifetime of stories, resilience, humor, and memories that will continue to live on through his family.

Allen was born July 13th, 1935, in Sheboygan, Wisconsin, the oldest of three children, son of Arthur and Laura (Mahner) Henricks.

Born two months premature and weighing just 4½ pounds, Allen entered the world at a time when care for premature infants was far more limited. During his earliest days, his family kept him warm near an open oven set on low heat — a family story Allen later used, with his dry sense of humor, to explain why he was "always half-baked."

He spent his childhood in Sheboygan before moving to Green Bay as a young teenager. Allen learned the value of hard work early in life. At age 12, he carried a paper route in Green Bay and later spent weekends and summers helping with the family's dairy delivery business. Those early experiences helped shape the hardworking, practical man he would become. He also had a natural curiosity for mechanics and problem-solving, once completely disassembling and reassembling three motorcycles.

Allen proudly served in the United States Air Force before beginning a long career in education. He taught English for many years, later earned his master's degree in Library Science, and worked as a librarian at North Central Technical Institute until his retirement in 1996.

Allen and his first wife, Miriam (Nelson) Henricks, built their life together while raising their three children, John, Charles, and Sheryl. In June of 1969, tragedy struck when Miriam passed away in a traffic accident, leaving Allen to continue raising his children while navigating the heartbreak of becoming a young widower.

Allen later married Judith Green. Together, they purchased a farm on Schoolhouse Lane and built a new life in the country, where they raised their family and welcomed two daughters, Allyson and Elizabeth. Allen and Judith later divorced.

Allen later married Nancy Wohld, and together they shared 43 years of life, family, and memories. Nancy survives him.

One of Allen's most meaningful later-life experiences was traveling to Washington, D.C., on Honor Flight #51 on April 14, 2025, accompanied by his daughter Sheryl. It was a special opportunity to honor his service in the United States Air Force and to share a memory they had together.

Outside of work, Allen loved the outdoors and especially treasured time spent at his cabin in Marinette County. He enjoyed fishing, hunting, playing cards, and spending time up north.

Allen had a dry sense of humor. He could deliver a joke or a story with such a straight face that people were often left wondering whether he was serious. He enjoyed the quiet laugh, the perfectly timed comment, and the kind of humor that sometimes landed only after you had a moment to think about it. His family will remember not only the stories he told, but also the look in his eye when he knew he had gotten the last laugh.

Allen will be remembered for his intelligence, humor, resilience, storytelling, and quiet strength. From surviving a difficult, premature birth during the Great Depression to serving his country, raising a family, teaching students, helping others through education and libraries, and building a life filled with both hardship and joy, Allen lived with perseverance, curiosity, and character.

Allen was preceded in death by his parents, Arthur and Laura (Mahner) Henricks; his first wife, Miriam; his sister and brother-in-law, Kay and Jack Lauterbach; his brother-in-law, Dick Martin; and his eldest son, John Henricks.

Allen is lovingly remembered by his wife, Nancy Wohld Henricks; his children, Charles (Tammy) Henricks, Sheryl Henricks, Allyson (Pete) McKensey, and Elizabeth Henricks; his grandchildren, Paul, Matthew John, Matthew Charles, Dylan, Marlena, Connor, Danielle, Nicholas, and Cayllah; and his sister, Helen (Dick) Martin.

A funeral service will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Tuesday, June 9, 2026, at Rib Falls United Methodist Church, 136700 Bearskin Trail, Marathon. Visitation will be held from 9:30 a.m. until the time of the service at the church. Pastor Tim Gurlea will officiate. Interment will follow at Rib Falls Methodist Cemetery in Rib Falls, Wisconsin, with military honors accorded by the United States Air Force and American Legion Alois Dreikosen Post #469.

records.name: Annette Booher-Malcolm
records.summary: Annette Booher-Malcolm, age 82, of Marshfield passed away on May 29, 2026.
records.body:
Annette Booher-Malcolm (82) was called into the arms of her Savior on May 29, 2026. Daughter of the late Revs. Carl and Mercedes Booher, she was born August 30, 1943, in Des Moines, Iowa.

Annette graduated from 8th grade in Prairie City, IA, and from high school at Omaha Central in 1962. She obtained a Bachelor's Degree in Comprehensive Music Education from UW–Eau Claire in 1966 and later her Master's Degree in Education, with a Gifted/Talented emphasis, from UW–Stevens Point.

From 1966–69 she taught elementary and junior-high band at Woodrow Wilson Junior High in Manitowoc, and from 1969–98 she briefly taught elementary general music and then band at D.C. Everest Junior High in Schofield. During the 1988–89 school year, Annette was awarded D.C. Everest Junior High Teacher-of-the-Year.

Predeceasing her, in addition to her parents, were her husband, Dr. Harlen Malcolm; a brother, Rev. Donald; and sisters-in-law Nancy and Norma Jean.

Her Booher family survivors include brothers Louis and Steven D. (Marlene); nieces Tami (Brian) Terpstra, Teresa Maynard, and Becca (Dan) Kempenich; and nephews Steven N. (Cindy), Trent (Kathy), Brad (Michelle), David (Mary Ann), and Matt, as well as multiple great, and great great nieces and nephews.

A memorial service will be held at 4:00 pm on Saturday, August 22, 2026 at Rembs Celebration Center, 250 S. Oak Ave., Marshfield.

records.name: Dale  W Ziebell
records.summary: Dale  W Ziebell, age 76 passed away on May 29, 2026.
records.body:
Dale Ziebell, 76, passed away peacefully at home on May 29, 2026, under the care of Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services, with his loving wife and family by his side.

Born on May 22, 1950, to the late Walter and Doretta (Steinberger) Ziebell, Dale lived a life centered on family, laughter, and making memories with those he loved most.

Dale shared a special bond with his wife, JoAnn. Together they enjoyed fishing and camping, creating countless cherished memories along the way. They also loved traveling to carnivals throughout the state, never letting distance stand in the way of an adventure—even if it meant driving 150 miles one way.

Known for his mischievous spirit, Dale loved playing jokes on his wife, children, and grandchildren. His playful sense of humor brought smiles to everyone around him, and he continued sharing that joy right up until his final days. Above all, Dale was a devoted husband, a loving father, and an amazing grandfather who treasured every moment spent with his family.

JoAnn will especially remember Dale for his peaceful nature, loving heart, and gentle spirit. His kindness, patience, and unwavering love will remain in the hearts of all who knew him.

Dale is survived by his beloved wife, JoAnn; his children, Christopher (Sarah) Ziebell, Jason (Kelley) Goertz, and Jennifer (Todd) Schifelbine; and his grandchildren, Tabatha Ziebell, Emery Ziebell, Elizabeth Leverty, Adam Goertz, Candace Goertz, Tyler Goertz, Olivia Goertz, Natasha James, and Raven Love. He is further survived by many nieces, nephews, other relatives, and friends.

In addition to his parents, Dale was preceded in death by his son, Brian, and six siblings.

A celebration of Dale’s life will be held at a later date.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family. Family and friends may leave words of remembrance at MWCS.WS.

Dale’s legacy of love, laughter, and gentle kindness will be forever remembered by all who were blessed to know him.

records.name: Douangchay Vilaysane
records.summary: Douangchay Vilaysane, age 48 passed away on May 29, 2026.
records.body:
Douangchay Jai Vilaysane, born on September 15, 1977, in Ban Tin Pier Laos, passed away peacefully at home on Friday, May 29, 2026, surrounded by the love of her friends and family.

Jai went to Franklin Elementary school, John Muir Middle, and graduated from East High School in 1996. Jai enjoyed singing karaoke, eating, and playing cards with her friends. She was such a joker, always bringing laughter and people together.

She is survived by her father, Phay Vilaysane; her mother, Deune Vilaysane; sister, Malay; brother, Aenoy; partner, Larry; nephews, Martez, Marlon, Kargie, Madden, and Mackenzie; niece, Karlesia; and great-niece, Zaviyah.

Funeral services will begin at 10:00 a.m. until 3:00 p.m. on Friday, June 5, 2026, at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: James D. Davidowski
records.summary: James D. Davidowski, 79, of Hatley, died on May 25, 2026.
records.body:
James D. Davidowski, 79 of Hatley, died on Monday, May 25, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

James was born on January 12, 1947, in Milwaukee, the son of Leo and Wanda (Windorski) Davidowski.

On May 8, 1971, James was united in marriage to JoAnn Blarek at St. Florian's Catholic Church, Hatley.

James worked at SNE, formerly Crestline, for many years in production. He loved refinishing furniture and woodworking. James could build just about everything and especially enjoyed making toys for his grandchildren.

James is survived by his wife, JoAnn; two children, Janice (Lynn Jr.) Roe of Alma Center and Leon (Kelly) Davidowski of Hatley; five grandchildren, Calvin (Courtney) Roe, Aron Roe, Alek Roe, Taleigh (Lincoln) Davidowski and Hayden Davidowski; siblings, Joanie (Richard) Kegal and Harold (Roxanne) Davidowski and sister-in-law, Maryanne Davidowski.

James was preceded in death by his parents and siblings, Robert Davidowski, Leon Davidowski, Judy (John) Bydynkowski and Sharon (Gene) Pogorzelski.

A Funeral Service will be held at 11AM on Friday, June 12, 2026, at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg. Fr. Jerome Patric will preside. Burial will be in St. Florian's Catholic Cemetery, Hatley. Visitation will be on Friday, from 9AM until the time of service at the funeral home.

records.name: Jeffrey Michael Zdroik
records.summary: Jeffrey Michael Zdroik, age 69, of Rhinelander passed away on May 29, 2026.
records.body:
Jeffrey M Zdroik, age 69, of Rhinelander, died on May 29, 2026, at the Wausau Aspirus Hospital. He was born in Rhinelander, Wisconsin on November 15, 1956, the son of Blaise and Helen (Aschenbrenner) Zdroik. He attended schools in Rhinelander, graduating from the Rhinelander High School in 1975. After a few years of college, he went to work for the Rhinelander School District and became the building and grounds supervisor for the district. He was with the school district for 36 years, retiring in 2023.

Jeff married the love of his life, Cheryl Fisher on August 12, 1978 and they were blessed with over 47 years of marriage.

Jeff enjoyed designing and then building things and working on cars. When he was asked how it is going, he would respond "Normal' until his retirement when the response changed to "every day is a Saturday" He will be missed by his family and his friends.

Jeff is survived by his wife Cheryl (Fisher), his sons Brandon (Amy Dodge) Zdroik and Cameron (Cassi Oestreich) Zdroik, his grandson Oliver, his sisters Gloria Brady and Barb (Steve) Gillis, nieces and nephews. He was preceded in death by his parents, his sister Darlene and his brother Roddy.

Private family services will be held for Jeff. You may leave your private condolences for the Zdroik family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home is serving the Zdroik family.

records.name: Judith J. Dillon
records.summary: Judith J. Dillon, 76, of Wausau, passed away on May 27, 2026.
records.body:
Judith J. Dillon, age 76, passed away on May 27, 2026, and is now reunited for eternity with the love of her life, her husband, Allen Dillon, who preceded her in death on April 5, 2026.

Judith was born in Wausau, Wisconsin, to the late LeRoy Sr. and Pearl (Doepke) Schoepke. She and Allen shared more than 50 wonderful years of marriage, building a life centered on love, family, and devotion.

Above all else, Judith was a full-time mom, and her boys were the center of her world. She dedicated her life to caring for them, always making sure they had everything they needed and reminding them through her actions every day how deeply they were loved. Her sons will forever remember her as an amazing mother who was always there for them with love, support, and kindness.

Judith found great joy in the outdoors. She could often be found spending hours tending to her yard, working in her garden, or planting flowers that brought beauty to those around her. She also worked part time at Maple Ridge Farms, where she enjoyed her time and the people she worked with.

She had a special love for animals, especially her beloved cat, Scrappy, and dog, Macy, who brought her endless companionship and happiness.

Judith is lovingly survived by her four sons, Allen Dillon (significant other, Tracie), Daniel Dillon, Andrew Dillon, and Robby Dillon; her grandson, David Dillon; and her siblings, Pearl (Roger) Janikowski, LeRoy Jr. (Debbie) Schoepke, and Debra (Michael) Conklin; and her furry companion Macy.

In addition to her parents, and husband, Judith was preceded in death by her furry companion, Scrappy.

She will be deeply missed and lovingly remembered by all who knew her.

records.name: Roger Joseph Stieber
records.summary: Roger Joseph Stieber, 90, of Wausau, formerly of Marathon, passed away on May 26, 2026.
records.body:
Roger Joseph Stieber, 90, Wausau, formerly of Marathon, passed away peacefully on Tuesday, May 26, 2026 at Marshfield Medical Center, Weston. He was born May 20, 1936 in Marathon, son of the late Henry and Anna (Lemmer) Stieber. On July 26, 1958, he married Agnes Rose Weber at St. Boniface Catholic Church, Aniwa, WI. She preceded him in death on May 15, 2014.

Roger was a Veteran and served in the United States Army. For many years, he worked as a machine operator for Wausau Paper Corporation until his retirement. Among his favorite pastimes, he enjoyed playing the stock market, talking politics and spending time with his family. Roger will be remembered for his beautiful smile, quick wit and wonderful sense of humor. He was always ready to offer good advice and a funny story. Most of all, he loved spending his retirement years in Florida with his wife, Agnes.
