Survivors include four children, Dean (Robin) Stieber, James Stieber, Brian Stieber and Lynn Stieber; eleven grandchildren; nine great-grandchildren; five siblings, Dennis (Leisa) Stieber, Francis (Cindy) Stieber, Pauline (Johnny) Ivaska, Rose (Gary) Stubbe and Rita (Rich) Rennie; and many nieces and nephews.

Besides his parents and wife, Agnes, he was preceded in death by his grandson, Elliot Stieber; and two sisters, Imelda (Don) Connick and Shirley (Herold) Helvey.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. on Wednesday, June 3rd, 2026, at Holy Name Catholic Church,1104 S 9th Ave, Wausau. Rev. Woodrow Pace will preside. Visitation will be June 3rd, 2026, from 9:30 a.m. until time of Mass at the church. Burial will be at St. Mary Cemetery, 1206 Co. Rd. B, Marathon.

records.name: Sherman Edward Stock
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Sherman Edward Stock, 98, of Hales Corners, passed away on May 26, 2026.
records.body:
Sherman Edward Stock (Michaelis), of Hales Corners, passed away on May 26, 2026, at the age of 98, in the care of his daughter Kate Milani and son-in-law Hamid Milani, in Wausau, WI.

Sherman was born in Milwaukee and graduated from Rufus King High School, where he played football and sang in the choir. During that time, he met the love of his life, Sally, and they were married in 1951. He attended Marquette University, earning a bachelor's degree in business administration. After graduation, Sherman enlisted in the United States Army and was stationed in Oklahoma, Texas, and Northern New York. Following his military service, he returned to Marquette University to attend law school, earning his Juris Doctor in 1958.

Sherman devoted much of his career to public service, working as legal counsel to U.S. Senator Gaylord Nelson for 18 years. He and Gaylord shared a close friendship, and they worked together on several significant projects, including the founding of Earth Day and a trip to Biafra to provide humanitarian relief during the Nigerian Civil War. He then worked for Governor Tony Earl as Veterans Affairs Advisor.

Artistic expression was a lifelong passion for Sherman. He created numerous paintings and drawings and was an award-winning political cartoonist under the pen name K. Cots. Sherman also showcased his artistic talents as a resident of the Village of Hales Corners and member of the Hales Corners Historical Society, contributing clay figures, dioramas, murals, and a portrait of Jim Ryan, the late village president. He remained civically engaged throughout his life, writing consistently about politics, often through letters to the editor. Known for his quick wit and dry sense of humor, he was rarely without a clever remark.

He enjoyed fishing, playing cribbage, following baseball, basketball, and football, and traveling with his wife Sally. Together, they visited multiple countries in Europe, Australia, Fiji, New Zealand, and South Africa. For many years, they spent summers with family at Pasha Lake in Ontario, Canada, and winters in Hawaii and Florida. They were long-time members of the Greenfield Avenue Presbyterian Church.

A dedicated Democrat, Sherman believed in civil rights, environmental and consumer protections, and bipartisan cooperation to improve the lives of working-class Americans. Upon his retirement as Communications Director for the Wisconsin Academy of Trial Lawyers, the organization celebrated him as "Defender of the Bill of Rights and Friend of the People."

He was preceded in death by his brother and sister-in-law, Grover and Hazel Stock; daughter, Anne Weber; and his wife of 70 years, Sally Stock.

He is survived by his son, Christopher Stock (wife Ellen); daughter, Kathryn Milani (husband Hamid); son-in-law, Jeff Weber, husband of the late Anne Weber; grandchildren Wendi Horcos, Sahar Milani, Holly Weber, Emily Guill, Billy Weber, Chris Stock, Jr., Rebecca Stock, and Michael Stock; great-grandchildren Liam Devan, Teagan Farris, and Faye Farris; and many extended family members and friends.

No services are planned at this time. Memorial donations may be made to the Nelson Institute for Environmental Studies at the University of Wisconsin – Madison.

records.name: Susie Marie Bohne
records.summary: Susie Marie Bohne, age 67, of Rhinelander passed away on May 29, 2026.
records.body:
Susie Marie Bohne, age 67, of Rhinelander, passed away on May 29,2026, at her home, surrounded by her loving family. She was born February 1,1959 in Rhinelander, the daughter of Albert and Marie(Arrand)Reinke.

Susie was born with a compassionate soul and found contentment in simply being present in each moment. She took great pride in knowing people trusted her for her honesty. She always saw the good in people and was quick to forgive. She believed in second chances (sometimes even 3 or 4). When she smiled or laughed it was always from the heart and the world felt like a better place when she did.

She married her high school sweetheart after she graduated and they were blessed with two sons and almost 48 years of marriage. She chose to be a stay at home mom because she felt being there for her family was her most important job and her fulfilment in life. She quickly became the neighborhood mom where they all could come and grab a bite or just hang out. They all knew she was home if they ever needed help or just wanted company. when she did decide to work out of the house, it was something she could do with her children when they were small. She delivered bi weekly papers on a rural route. she would load up her civic and her boys and it became a "shoot the tubes adventure". After her boys had grown older she worked for her sister Pat, bussing tables at the Sportsman cafe. She then helped provide care for a local elderly woman.

Throughout her life she was a caregiver. Whether it was caring for her husband and children or lending an ear to a troubled family member or friend of her children. From moving her mother in law in to live with them while she battled dementia to helping her own mother stay in her own home until the age of 103. Susie lived by a very simple set of standards, family first, tell the truth, be kind and obey the law. She was never so busy that she wouldn’t stop to help and be kind to person or animal.

Her family was her life. She enjoyed being busy in her back yard and watching the wildlife, cheering for the Green Bay Packers, believing they were never to far behind to win. She simply just enjoyed life.

Susie is survived by her husband Dennis, her sons Eric (Jacquie)Bohne and Brandon Bohne. Her two granddaughters Adalyn and Alena Bohne, sisters Marian(William)Fourt and Debra(Randy) Slack, brothers in law Randall(Marybeth)Bohne, Robert(Rebeca) Petonquot and brother in law Douglas Mueller, along with her pic(partner in crime) niece Bobbie Bennett and many other nieces and nephews. She came from a large family and loved them all dearly. She was preceded in death by her granddaughter Elise Bohne, her mother and father, brother David Reinke, sisters Patricia Wales, Shirley Mueller and Rebecca Petonquot, her brothers in law Jack Wales, William Fourt and many nephews and nieces. There will be no service per Susie’s request. A celebration of life will be scheduled at a later date. A thank you to all family, friends and neighbors for their support and prayers during this difficult time.

You may leave your private condolences for the Bohne family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Bohne family.

records.name: Thomas Lee Wardall
records.summary: Thomas Lee Wardall, age 83 passed away on May 29, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas Lee Wardall, a beloved husband, father, grandfather, great-grandfather, and friend, passed away peacefully on May 29, 2026, at the age of 83. Born on December 10, 1942 to Clarence and Ellen Wardall, in Merrill, Wisconsin.

In 1962, Thomas married Betty Gryskiewicz, and together they built a life filled with adventure, laughter, and cherished memories. They enjoyed traveling, particularly to South Padre Island during the winter months, and shared a passion for camping and water skiing at Lake Tomahawk. An avid outdoorsman, Tom loved to hunt and shoot guns and spend time at their log cabin on their hunting land.

Thomas served his country honorably in the U.S. Army from 1960 to 1966. After his service, he dedicated 40 years to Church Mutual, where he furthered his education by earning his CPCU certification and worked his way up to be an Assistant Vice President. Active in his community, he was a proud member of the Lion's Club, served on the Merrill Area Public Schools school board from 1982 to 1990, and was a member of the board for North Central Technical College from 1983 to 1992. He once held the position of president of the Kiwanis Club, reflecting his commitment to service and leadership.

Thomas is survived by his devoted wife, Betty Wardall; his son, Alan (Marie) Wardall, and daughter-in-law, Janet Wardall; his grandchildren, Jared (Brianna) Wardall, Aubrey (Brent) Leech, Morgan Wardall, Aleysa Wardall, and Ryan (Elena McMenamin) Wardall; as well as great-grandchildren Wesley Thomas Wardall and Bennett Bradley Leech. He also leaves behind his brother, Gary (Peggy) Wardall, and numerous nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents, his infant daughter Nicole Wardall, his son Lee Wardall, and his brother Harold Wardall.

The family wishes to extend heartfelt gratitude to the doctors, nurses and staff at Marshfield Medical Center in Weston and the hospice staff at Compassus for their compassionate care.

Funeral services will be held Friday, June 19, 2026 at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, 301 Eagle Dr, Merrill, Wisconsin, at 11am. Visitation will be from 9am-11am at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation.

In lieu of flowers, donations can be made in Thomas' name to Tunnel to Towers Foundation , the Wounded Warriors Project , or St. Jude's Children's Research Hospital .

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: James "Jim" Edward Balge
records.summary: James "Jim" Edward Balge, age 68, of Mosinee passed away on May 30, 2026.
records.body:
James “Jim” E. Balge, 68, Mosinee, went to heaven on May 30, 2026, at his home, surrounded by his loving wife.

Jim was born on September 9, 1957, son of the late William and Joyce (Kaskavitch) Balge. Jim was known for his strong work ethic and appreciation for the simple pleasures of life. A true outdoorsman, he found great joy in hunting, fishing, and camping, always eager to spend time in nature. He enjoyed working on cars, assembling model cars, and attending car shows, where he could admire craftsmanship and share his passion with others. Jim also had a curious mind and a love for travel, taking a special interest in learning the history of the places he visited. Whether exploring the outdoors, turning a wrench in the garage, or discovering stories from the past, Jim approached life with enthusiasm, dedication, and a genuine appreciation for the world around him.

Jim married Bonnie Swan, and together they shared 36 wonderful years of marriage. Building a life centered on love and family. Jim was a kind, loving and quiet person, who was caring and always willing to help others.

He is survived by his beloved wife, Bonnie Swan Balge; daughter, Ashley Wolfe; granddaughter, Laney Wolfe; sister, LuAnn (Garret) Teske; niece, Pamela (Russel) Emon; nephew, Brian (Amy) Anderson; six sisters-in-law and many more family.

A Celebration of Life will be held on Sunday, September 6, 2026, at 1:00 p.m., at Jim and Bonnie’s home in Mosinee. Beste Funeral Home, Mosinee, is honored to be assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be directed to the family at www.bestefh.com.

A very heartfelt and special Thank You, to Linda & Al, Nicole, Cecelia, Evalina, Pam & Russ, Peggy & Al, Syndie, Sonja, Scarlet & Layla, Craig, Karen, and Karla, for helping the Balge family with care.

records.name: Karen Rose Breske
records.summary: Karen Rose Breske, age 86, of Hatley passed away on May 30, 2026.
records.body:
Karen Rose Breske, 86, Hatley, passed away peacefully on Saturday, May 30, 2026 at Primrose Memory Care in Weston, under the care of Interim Hospice.

She was born October 8, 1939 in Hatley, daughter of the late Edward and Velma (Doty) Woznicki. On April 25, 1959, she married Roger M. Breske at St. Florian Catholic Church, Hatley. He preceded her in death on April 3, 2012.

For 53 years, Karen and Roger built a life together grounded in love, hard work, and family. As business owners, they worked side by side and shared in the success they created together.

Karen was a loving and devoted mother and grandmother who cherished every moment with her family. She enjoyed hosting family get-togethers and creating lasting memories for those she loved. Known for her gentle and patient nature, she treated her grandchildren with unwavering kindness and never raised her voice to them. Her love, warmth, and compassion will be deeply missed. Her presence made family feel like home.

Survivors include her four children, John (Heather) Breske, Antigo, Jim (Beth) Breske, Hatley, Jodi (Mike O’Neil) Breske, Weston and Jill Bricco, Hatley; 13 grandchildren, Lindsey, Tiffany (Cody), Stephanie (Frank), Dana (Chad), Shannon (Jerry), Erin (Bryce), Cassi (Matt), Timothy, Carli (Riley), Taylor, Halle (Evan), John (Maddie) and Makailah; 12 great-grandchildren, Skylar, Leesa, Evalee, Mason, Brice, Grayson, Stetson, Ruby, Peyton, Miles, Teagan and Thomas; six siblings, Lee (Kathy) Woznicki, Susan Klingbile, Carol (Wendell) Silvius, Thomas (Roberta) Woznicki, Daniel (Brenda) Woznicki and Donald (Cathy) Woznicki; two sisters-in-law, Patti Woznicki and Patty Woznicki; three brothers-in-law, Ron Geurink, John (Peggy) Breske and Dan Breske; and many nieces and nephews.

Besides her parents and husband, Roger, she was preceded in death by four siblings, Gary, Alan and Clifford Woznicki and Diane Geurink.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, June 6, 2026 at St. Florian Catholic Church, Hatley. Rev. Jerome Patric will preside. Burial be in the St. Philomena Cemetery. Visitation will be on Friday from 4:00 until 7:00 p.m. at the church and again on Saturday from 9:00 a.m. until time of Mass. There will be a rosary service at 7:00 p.m. Friday evening.

The family would like to thank the staff and Primrose Memory Care and the staff at Interim Hospice for their loving and compassionate care.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Lillian M. Buchholz
records.summary: Lillian M. Buchholz, age 96, of Wausau passed away on May 30, 2026.
records.body:
Lillian M. Buchholz, 96, Wausau, passed away on Saturday, May 30, 2026 at Stone Crest in Wausau.

She was born March 8, 1930 in Rothschild, WI, daughter of the late Anton and Casimira (Olinski) Falkowski. She married Robert Buchholz in Minnesota, he preceded her in death in 2015.

Among her favorite pastimes, she enjoyed making Christmas cookies, reading, attending stock car races and short track races, visiting casino’s, spending time at the family cottage up North and most recently, travelling with her daughter, Renee.

Survivors include her daughter, Renee (Kevin) Malak, Wausau; and her grandson, Wesley (Allison Cross) Malak.

Besides her parents and husband, Robert, she was preceded in death by her son, Jeffrey Buchholz; one sister and four brothers.

Per Lillian’s request, private entombment will be at Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Marion A. Hintzel
records.summary: Marion A. Hintzel, age 94, of Marshfield passed away on May 30, 2026.
records.body:
Marion A. Hintzel, age 94, of Marshfield, passed away on Saturday, May 30, 2026 at Marshfield Health Services.

Marion was born on July 14, 1931 in Green Bay, WI, to Thomas and Gladys (Francois) Mahoney. Marion and her parents moved to Chicago when she was nine and she received her education in Illinois schools.

She was united in marriage to Donnie C. Zarbock of Villa Park, Illinois on December 19, 1953. He passed away suddenly in May of 1974. She later married William H. Hintzel of Lansing, IL. After Bill’s retirement they moved to Charlotte, NC. Bill died there in 1995. Following Bill’s death, Marion came to Marshfield to live with her friends, Frank and Pat Jacobitz.

Marion was employed in office and administrative positions.

She is survived by her children, Eric Zarbock of Denver, CO, and Kurt Zarbock of Willmington, NC and stepson, Terry (Pat) Hintzel of Willmington, IL. She is also survived by three grandchildren, Astara, Neil and John Zarbock.

She was preceded in death by her parents and her husbands.

records.name: Michael J. Dailey
records.summary: Michael J. Dailey, age 78 passed away on May 30, 2026.
records.body:
Michael J. Dailey, 78, passed away Saturday, May 30, 2026, under the care of Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services at Cedar Creek Manor, Kronenwetter.

He was born December 1, 1947, in Freeport, Illinois, son of the late Joseph and Arlene (Bottorf) Dailey. He grew up in Pecatonica, Illinois, a close-knit farming community where he spent much of his youth working on family farms and exploring the countryside on horseback. On July 12, 1969, he married Julie Kathryn Woods, his high school sweetheart, in Freeport, Illinois.

Following graduation from his beloved St. John’s University, Michael built a successful career with Traveler’s Insurance before continuing his work with Wausau Insurance as claims manager, RMA and Home Office. When he wasn’t working, he could often be found outdoors pheasant hunting in Northern Illinois, deer hunting in Northwestern Wisconsin, and fishing in Canada. His many trophy mounts serve as lasting reminders of the great hunts, memorable trips, and deep appreciation he had for the outdoors.

A devoted sports fan, Michael faithfully followed St. John’s, Notre Dame, and the Chicago Bears, celebrating their victories and enduring their losses with equal passion. He was also an avid boater and lifelong water-ski enthusiast who cherished every opportunity to be on the water with family and friends. Known for his gift of storytelling, Michael could often be found sharing memories, laughter, and the occasional tale over an Old Style beer. His warmth, humor, and ability to bring people together will be remembered by all who knew and loved him.

Above all, Michael was a devoted husband, father, and grandfather. He took immense pride in his six grandchildren and treasured every opportunity to attend their activities, celebrate their accomplishments, and share in their lives. He also maintained lifelong friendships with many of his childhood and hometown friends from Freeport, Illinois, along with those he met during his time as a student and football player at St. John’s. Their shared memories, laughter, and enduring bond remained a cherished part of his life throughout the years.

Survivors include his wife, Julie Dailey; son, Sean (Kathleen) Dailey; daughter, Jennifer (Dean) Haugo; grandchildren, Maura, Sean Patrick, and Shannon Dailey, and Megan, Michael, and Katherine Haugo.

Besides his parents, he was preceded in death by a brother, Thomas Dailey.

The family would like to express their heartfelt gratitude to the compassionate caregivers of Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services and the dedicated staff at Cedar Creek Manor for the kindness, dignity, and exceptional care they provided to Michael during his final days. Their support and compassion brought great comfort to Michael and his family.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. Monday, June 8, 2026, at St. Matthew Catholic Church, Wausau. Rev. Robert Thorn will preside. Visitation will be Monday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service, all at St. Matthew Catholic Church, Wausau. Burial will take place at a later date in St. Mary Catholic Cemetery, Pecatonica, Illinois.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to the Alzheimer's Association, www.alz.org.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Steven D Sonderleiter
records.summary: Steven D Sonderleiter, age 80 passed away on May 30, 2026.
records.body:
Steven Dale Sonderleiter, 80, passed away peacefully at home on May 30, 2026, surrounded by his children and his faithful companion, Janice, while under the compassionate care of Compassus Hospice.

Steven was born on November 8, 1945, in Los Angeles, California, to the late Dale and Mary (Bracy) Sonderleiter. He was raised in the Garwin, Iowa, area alongside his siblings Connie, Paula, Diane, and Kenneth.

On August 12, 1966, Steven married Carol Ann Reitenbaugh. Together they established their home in Le Grand, Iowa, and were blessed with four children: Lori Sonderleiter of Jasper, Tennessee; twins Steven Sonderleiter and Stephanie Sonderleiter of Marshalltown, Iowa; and Kristi Sonderleiter of Montour, Iowa.

Steven proudly served his country in the United States Army Reserves. He dedicated many years of his working life to Harker's Meats in Le Mars, Iowa. After relocating to Wisconsin, he continued his career with Mullins Cheese until his retirement.

A talented craftsman and mechanic, Steven had a lifelong passion for motorcycles. He enjoyed building, customizing, and riding motorcycles, earning recognition and an award for a custom chopper he created. He also loved working on vehicles and was always happiest when he had a project in the garage.

While living in Wisconsin, Steven was fortunate to meet Janice Haase, who became his devoted companion and partner for many years. Their time together brought him great happiness and companionship.

Steven is lovingly survived by Janice; his sisters, Diane, Paula, and Connie; Steven's children, Lori, Steven, Stephanie, and Kristi; as well as numerous grandchildren and great-grandchildren who will cherish his memory.

In addition to his parents, Steven was preceded in death by his younger brother, Kenneth.

In accordance with Steven's wishes, cremation has taken place. Private family graveside services will be held in Marshalltown, Iowa.

The family extends their heartfelt gratitude to Compassus Hospice for the exceptional care, kindness, and support provided to Steven and his loved ones during his final days.

Mid-Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family with arrangements. Family and friends are invited to visit MWCS.WS to share condolences and words of remembrance.

records.name: Thomas A Traub
records.summary: Thomas A Traub, age 79 passed away on May 30, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas Arthur Traub passed away peacefully at Aspirus Langlade Hospital in Antigo, Wisconsin on May 30, 2026, after suffering a massive stroke on May 8.

Tom was born in Milwaukee, Wisconsin on December 1, 1946, to Arthur and Catherine (Schutz) Traub. He graduated from John Marshall High School in 1965. On November 12, 1966, he married Geraldine Wickland at Our Lady of Sorrows Catholic Church in Milwaukee. Their union produced five children. Tom was a carpenter and home builder in Southeastern Wisconsin for over 45 years and a life-long Green Bay Packer fan, attending many games with friends. Tom was a resident of Antigo for the past 13 years.

Tom was preceded in death by his parents, Arthur and Kathryn Traub; son, Michael Allen Traub; brother-in-law, Lawrence Koty.

Tom is survived by his ex-wife, Geraldine; daughters, Juliette Ellyn Hoffman (Mike Rouse), Patricia Anne (John) Logue; sons, James Edward (Kristen) and Daniel Arthur (Ashley); foster daughter, Connie Kuphal; sister, Carol Koty; brothers-in-law John Wickland and Robert Wickland; sister-in-law, Georgianna (Jim) Whelan; nieces and nephews; special friend, Manish Patel.

Tom is further survived by grandchildren, Nicole Traub, Danielle Fails, Ariel Leaf, Shaina Warchol, Nate Rouse, Jake Heine, Timothy Traub, Trinity Logue, Matthew Traub, Alexander Traub, Andrew Traub and Evan Logue; great-grandchildren, Kaden Leaf, Abigail Fails, Madyson Leaf, Charlotte Fails, Caroline Gilbert, Lucien Leaf, and Oliver Gilbert.

The family would like to thank the entire Aspirus staff for their kindness and care during Tom's stay and their visits.

Per Tom’s wishes, the family will hold a private memorial at a later date.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family. Family and friends may go to MWCS.WS to leave words of remembrance.

records.name: Betty M. Severt
records.summary: Betty M. Severt, age 91 passed away on May 31, 2026.
records.body:
Betty M. Severt, 91, formerly of Merrill, passed away Sunday, May 31, 2026, at Care Partners Assisted Living under the care of St. Croix Hospice.

Betty was born May 31, 1935, in Merrill, daughter of the late Peter and Dorothy (Kurth) Horgen. Betty grew up in the Newwood area. She graduated from Merrill High School. On December 24, 1954, Betty married Gordon ‘Chub’ Severt. He preceded her in death on October 30, 1988. Betty helped her husband run their restaurant ‘Kozy Korner’ in the 70’s. She and Chub also owned and operated Merrill Evergreen for twenty years. They provided wholesale flowers to local florists, designed wedding and funeral pieces, and sold roping and wreaths during the Christmas season.

Betty worked for 12 years at Merrill Manufacturing and another 17 years at Wire Products, both in Merrill. In retirement, Betty volunteered at Pine Crest Nursing Home for five years. She also enjoyed traveling and sightseeing in the Southern and Western states, playing keyboard, doing word search puzzles, and watching NFL football, especially the Packers. She liked watching her favorite movie, The Blind Side, and listening to polka music. She loved going to watch her grandsons’ baseball games and granddaughters’ band concerts and dance shows. In her spare time, she would take coffee breaks or a round of golf with her friend Halena and play card games with her friend Keith. Betty also enjoyed making and decorating wreaths and making decorative sprays for the cemetery. Betty loved cooking for her family and was open to making any meal requested by them.

Survivors include two daughters, Tammy (Ted) Benolken, of Otsego, MN, and Cindy (Todd Rolaff) Vachavake, of Schofield; four sons, Greg (Kaye) Severt, of Florida, James Severt, of Merrill, Jeff (Jackie) Severt, of Boise, ID, and Todd Severt, of Merrill; one daughter-in-law, Lisa Severt, of Colorado Springs, CO; fourteen grandchildren; five step grandchildren; twelve great-grandchildren; and six step great-grandchildren. Besides her parents and husband, Betty is preceded in death by one son; one grandson; and one granddaughter.

The family would like to thank St. Croix Hospice staff for all the care and support they offered Betty and her family. They would also like to thank Kendra, Kaitlin and the rest of the staff at Care Partners for the wonderful care they provided Betty over the years.

Per Betty’s request, there will be no services. Inurnment will take place at Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation of Merrill is assisting the with arrangements. Online condolences are encouraged and may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Jeffry Scott Wyland
records.summary: Jeffry Scott Wyland, age 73 passed away on May 31, 2026.
records.body:
Jeffry Scott Wyland, passed away on May 31, 2026, at the age of 73. Born on August 2, 1952, in Merrill, Wisconsin, Jeffry was the son of Clifford and Arleigh Wyland.

Jeffry married his beloved wife, Diane Pike, on November 14, 1980, at St. Francis Xavier Catholic Church in Merrill. Together, they shared a passion for crafting and woodworking, often participating in local craft fairs, bringing their creations to life. Though a private man by nature, Jeffry knew how to light up a room with his infectious sense of humor, especially when he was with Diane. They created a wonderful life together, filled with laughter and love.

He had a love for fishing and hunting and enjoyed taking fishing trips to Green Bay and Michigan.

Professionally, Jeffry was a dedicated supervisor and stockman at Herd Windows and Doors Manufacturing and Greenheck. He kicked off his career as a youth counselor for a Catholic charity, reflecting his kind-hearted spirit and desire to help others.

He is survived by his devoted wife, Diane, and siblings Reed Wyland, Beth Jankowsky, Ann Barnett, James Wyland, and Christopher Wyland. He also leaves behind several nieces and nephews.

Jeffry was preceded in death by his parents, his sister-in-law Judy Wyland, and nephew Jeremy Wyland.

A celebration of life will be held at a later date.

In lieu of flowers, contributions can be made in Jeffry's name to the Lincoln County Humane Society.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Kenneth A. Fuller
records.summary: Kenneth A. Fuller, age 81, of Stratford passed away on May 31, 2026.
records.body:
Kenneth A. Fuller, 81, Adams, formerly of Stratford, passed away on Sunday, May 31, 2026 at Rosebrook Assisted Living, Adams.

Ken was born on November 12, 1944 in Marshfield to Robert and Mae (Maguire) Fuller. He attended Stratford Area Schools and, following high school, entered the U.S. Marine Corps, serving his country until his honorable discharge in 1968. Ken owned and operated Fuller’s Tree Service.

He is survived by his brother, Thomas (Mary) Fuller of Stratford and sister, Helen Breit of Stratford.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Robert and Mae Fuller, sister, Joanne Viegut, brother, Robert Fuller, and brother in law, Kenneth Breit.

A private family service will be held to honor Ken’s life.

records.name: Wayne A Tryba
records.summary: Wayne A Tryba, age 72 passed away on May 31, 2026.
records.body:
Wayne Allen Tryba, age 72, died May 31st, 2026, peacefully at home.

Wayne was born on September 29th, 1953, to Joseph and Adeline (Wilk) Tryba. He attended D.C. Everest High School and North Central Technical College. He was a diligent and knowledgeable worker at Mill’s Fleet Farm in Wausau for 35 years.

Wayne was a perfectionist and very mechanically minded. Since he was always learning new things, he was able to fix many different machines. His son (Ben) and he restored two motorcycles.

He enjoyed a variety of hobbies which included riding his motorcycle, playing pool, throwing horseshoes and fishing. He often would be in search of the perfect recipes and would go out of his way to help others.

Wayne is survived by his mother, Adeline; his son, Ben; his brothers Gary and Larry Tryba; and his sister Christine (Greg) Jandrt. He was preceded in death by his wife, Ninette (Woytasik); his father; two sisters, Sandra Alsteen and Karen Tryba; and one brother, Jim Tryba.

No services will be held per Wayne’s request.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family. Friends and family may leave words of remembrance at MWCS.WS.

records.name: Annette Malcolm
records.summary: Annette Booher-Malcolm, 82, was called into the arms of her Savior on May 29, 2026.
records.body:
Annette Booher-Malcolm (82) was called into the arms of her Savior on May 29, 2026. Daughter of the late Revs. Carl and Mercedes Booher, she was born August 30, 1943, in Des Moines, Iowa.

Annette graduated from 8th grade in Prairie City, IA, and from high school at Omaha Central in 1962. She obtained a Bachelor's Degree in Comprehensive Music Education from UW–Eau Claire in 1966 and later her Master's Degree in Education, with a Gifted/Talented emphasis, from UW–Stevens Point.

From 1966–69 she taught elementary and junior-high band at Woodrow Wilson Junior High in Manitowoc, and from 1969–98 she briefly taught elementary general music and then band at D.C. Everest Junior High in Schofield. During the 1988–89 school year, Annette was awarded D.C. Everest Junior High Teacher-of-the-Year.

Predeceasing her, in addition to her parents, were her husband, Dr. Harlen Malcolm; a brother, Rev. Donald; and sisters-in-law Nancy and Norma Jean.

Her Booher family survivors include brothers Louis and Steven D. (Marlene); nieces Tami (Brian) Terpstra, Teresa Maynard, and Becca (Dan) Kempenich; and nephews Steven N. (Cindy), Trent (Kathy), Brad (Michelle), David (Mary Ann), and Matt, as well as multiple great, and great great nieces and nephews.

A memorial service will be held at 4:00 pm on Saturday, August 22, 2026 at Rembs Celebration Center, 250 S. Oak Ave., Marshfield.

records.name: Beverly Marie Koss
records.summary: Beverly Marie Koss, age 94, of Weston passed away on Jun 1, 2026.
records.body:
After 94 remarkable years of life, Bev closed her eyes for the final time on June 1, 2026 after a tough 10+ years of Dementia. Beverly was born in Chippewa Falls, WI on December 8, 1931 to Lawrence and Theresa (Zwiefelhofer) Revor. Bev married Duane Rugg of Chippewa Falls,WI Jan 1952, in San Juan, Puerto Rico. She then married Jim Koss of Mosinee, WI April 1989 in Schofield, WI.

Having witnessed nearly a century of history, her greatest blessings were seeing her children and grandchildren begin their own journeys. Bev lived a full and adventurous life, retiring from K Mart after 20+ years. Her hobbies included: sewing for her kids and grandkids, crocheting afghans, cooking, and traveling the world. You could not put anything past her, her photographic memory was amazing and her spiciness for life was unforgettable.

She was preceded in death by both parents: Lawrence and Theresa Revor, her husband's: Duane in 1986 and Jim in 2012, sisters: Dorothy Geissler, Betty Lokranz, Darlene Bowe, and very special sister in law: Beaty Wheeler.

She is survived by a sister: Joanne Shilha, Children: Debbie- San Francisco, CA, Jerry,- Marlborough, MA, Sue- Weston WI and Steve (Pam)- Bellevue, NE; Step-Children Sue (Ken) Smith- Jacksonville, FL, Linda (Lee) Garvey- Freedom, WI, Mike (Sara) Koss- Wausau, WI, and Karrie McGreevey- Marshfield, WI. Grandchildren: Theresa (Todd), Sonja (Josip), Marisa (Rob), Stephanie (Tj), Christopher, Ilse, and Caitlynn, Step-Grandchildren: Gina (Jason), Nate (Carla), Katie (Mike), Nick (Blair), Jennifer (Chris), Jeff (Jennifer), Andrew, Catherine (Jake), Aaron, and 11 Great Grandchildren, and 10 Step-Great Grandchildren.

In honor of her passion for life there will be "One Heck of a Party!

To celebrate her life Saturday August 29, 2026

Contact the family for time and location or email at Honorbmk@gmail.com.

Our family would like to extend a heartfelt thank you to her caregivers, nurses, and social workers at Renaissance and Rennes. Your kindness and patience brought so much comfort to Mom. Special thank you to her Hospice team, Haley, Carrie, and Will ( who was in the right spot at the right time.), as well as Brainard Funeral Home for assisting us during this difficult time.

We will forever be grateful.

records.name: Caitlin O'Heaney
records.summary: Caitlin O'Heaney, 73, passed away on May 18, 2026.
records.body:
It is with great sadness the family of actress Caitlin O'Heaney (aka Kathleen Helen Heaney) announces her peaceful passing on May 18, 2026, in Tarrytown, New York, at the age of 73. Her dear friend, Peter Davis, was by her side.

Born in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, and raised in Whitefish Bay, Caitlin was passionate about acting from an early age. At 17, she was awarded a full scholarship to The Julliard School's Drama division.

Caitlin worked extensively in theatre over the years, including on Broadway with Katherine Hepburn and Juilliard classmate Christopher Reeve. Her television credits include the roles of Anna Marie Hollyhock in Norman Lear's sitcom Apple Pie (1982), Sara Stickney White, the female lead in ABC's Tales of the Gold Monkey (1982), in addition to numerous guest roles spanning the late 1970's to the early 1990's.

Caitlin is known among horror fans for her lead role as Amy Jensen in 1980's cult classic He Knows You're Alone. She also appeared in Stephen Spielberg's Three O'Clock High as the popular character, Miss Farmer.

Caitlin will be remembered for her keen wit, charming sense of humor, her notable beauty, and as a passionate environmentalist and animal rights activist. In the early 90's she joined GreenPeace, USA where she worked onboard ship as a cook's assistant and deckhand. Over the years, Caitlin was mom-extraordinaire to an endless collection of rescue dogs, cats, her beloved horse, donkey, and even sheep. She was inspiring in her commitment to always do her best to leave the lightest footprint on this planet as possible. One of her proudest moments was being part of Beacon, NY's World Water Day Celebration in 2010 where she performed her original, anti-war, environmentalist song titled "Who Have We Freed?" with singer/songwriter Pete Seeger.

Caitlin is survived by sisters Susan Maureen Norton Emrey (Robert Emrey) and Coleen Heaney Kennedy (Michael Juers), along with nephews Robert Lambeth, Patrick Norton (Katie Runyon), Kevin J Kennedy, Ryan Juers (Jennifer Juers) and great nephews Bradley J Kennedy, Nicholas Juers (Savanna Juers) and great nieces Myla Katherine Kennedy, Kayla Juers, Vanessa Juers and Naomi Norton.

Donations to animal welfare groups, your local animal shelter, or environmental organizations made in Caitlin's memory would be greatly appreciated.

records.name: Cecilia M. Hintz
records.summary: Cecilia M. Hintz, age 96, passed away on May 17, 2026.
records.body:
Cecilia M. Hintz, age 96, passed away on May 17, 2026.

Cecilia was born November 21, 1929 in Wausau, WI to the late Alvina (Breske) and John J. Hintz.

Cecilia graduated with the class of 1947 from Wausau Sr. High. She received her Bachelor's degree from the University of Wisconsin – Green Bay and was employed there in administration and as an academic and career counselor, retiring after 30 years of service. She was a member of the UW – Green Bay Alumni Association, UW – Green Bay Retiree Association and a member of the Catholic Daughters of the Americas, Court St. Rose 1062 for more than 75 years.

Cecilia was preceded in death by her parents, her brother Henry T. Hintz, her sister Esther M. Kipp, an infant nephew John, nephew James, and great nephew Justin.

Survivors include two nephews: Timothy (Sandy) Kipp and Thomas (Nola Hoen-Kipp) Kipp. Also surviving are 5 great nephews: Nathan (Jean), Bryce (Julie), Ryan (Michelle), Brandon (Karri) and Joel (Lacey); three great-great nieces: Emma, Harper and Quinn; five great-great nephews: Anderson, Broderick, Hudson, Wells and Logan; and many special relatives and friends.

A visitation will take place at St. Francis Xavier Cathedral, 740 S. Monroe Ave, Green Bay, WI, from 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM on Wednesday, June 3, 2026. A Mass of Christian Burial will follow at 11:00 AM.

Burial will take place on Wednesday, June 3, 2026 at 2:45 PM at Restlawn Cemetery in Wausau, WI.

In lieu of flowers, Cecilia's family would encourage you to donate to the UW Green Bay Foundation.

records.name: Dale W. Ziebell
records.summary: Dale Ziebell, 76, passed away peacefully at home on May 29, 2026.
records.body:
Dale Ziebell, 76, passed away peacefully at home on May 29, 2026, under the care of Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services, with his loving wife and family by his side.

Born on May 22, 1950, to the late Walter and Doretta (Steinberger) Ziebell, Dale lived a life centered on family, laughter, and making memories with those he loved most.

Dale shared a special bond with his wife, JoAnn. Together they enjoyed fishing and camping, creating countless cherished memories along the way. They also loved traveling to carnivals throughout the state, never letting distance stand in the way of an adventure—even if it meant driving 150 miles one way.

Known for his mischievous spirit, Dale loved playing jokes on his wife, children, and grandchildren. His playful sense of humor brought smiles to everyone around him, and he continued sharing that joy right up until his final days. Above all, Dale was a devoted husband, a loving father, and an amazing grandfather who treasured every moment spent with his family.

JoAnn will especially remember Dale for his peaceful nature, loving heart, and gentle spirit. His kindness, patience, and unwavering love will remain in the hearts of all who knew him.

Dale is survived by his beloved wife, JoAnn; his children, Christopher (Sarah) Ziebell, Jason (Kelley) Goertz, and Jennifer (Todd) Schifelbine; and his grandchildren, Tabatha Ziebell, Emery Ziebell, Elizabeth Leverty, Adam Goertz, Candace Goertz, Tyler Goertz, Olivia Goertz, Natasha James, and Raven Love. He is further survived by many nieces, nephews, other relatives, and friends.

In addition to his parents, Dale was preceded in death by his son, Brian, and six siblings.

A celebration of Dale's life will be held at a later date.

Dale's legacy of love, laughter, and gentle kindness will be forever remembered by all who were blessed to know him.

records.name: Deloris Eleanor Seefeldt
records.summary: Deloris Eleanor Seefeldt, age 93, of Tigerton passed away on June 1, 2026.
records.body:
Deloris E. Seefeldt, 93 of Tigerton, passed away on Monday, June 1, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center, Weston.

Deloris Eleanor Seefeldt was born on June 4, 1932, in the Town of Little Wolf, Waupaca County, to William and Alice (Polley) Flater.

Deloris was united in marriage on June 13, 1953 to Howard C. Seefeldt at St. Paul’s Lutheran Church, Manawa. The couple dairy farmed in the Town of Wyoming and the Town of Fairbanks for most of their lives. Deloris loved farming, especially feeding and caring for the calves. In 1996 they sold the farm to their oldest son.

Deloris was a longtime member of Peace Lutheran Church, Split Rock, where she was a member of the Ladies Aid. In her early years, she enjoyed snowmobiling. She loved baking, canning, picking berries and gardening. Other favorite pastimes included mowing the lawn, spending time outdoors, going for drives through the countryside, visiting family members and watching the birds. Deloris will be remembered most as a loving mother and a proud grandmother.

Survivors include five children, Douglas (Karin) Seefeldt of Tigerton, Debra Seefeldt of Weston, Dawn Kersten of Tigerton, Delbert Seefeldt of Tigerton and Darold (Julie) Seefeldt of Bear Creek; daughter-in-law, Theresa Seefeldt of Clintonville; seven grandchildren, Wyatt, Paul (Tracy), Joshua, Ashley, Nicole (fiance', Austin), Matthew and Jesse and five great grandchildren, Hailey, Rylee, Abigail, Preston and Lydia. She is further survived by nieces, nephews and friends.

Deloris was preceded in death by her husband, Howard; parents; son, Daniel; son-in-law, Dennis Kersten; grandson, Justin; brother, Edwin (Kathy) Flater; sisters, Margaret (Robert) Bailey and Ardella (Arthur) Pethke; sister-in-law, Gladys (Edward) Prahl and three brothers-in-law, Harold (Evelyn), Harvey (Rosalie) and Lester (Marie) Seefeldt.

A Funeral Service will be held at 11AM on Friday, June 5, 2026, at Peace Lutheran Church, Split Rock. Rev. Ryan Halmstad will officiate and burial will follow in the parish cemetery. Visitation will be on Friday, from 9AM until the time of service at the church.

Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg is assisting the family with arrangements. Memories and messages of support may be shared at schmidtschulta.com.

records.name: Karen R. Breske
records.summary: Karen Rose Breske, 86, of Hatley, passed away on May 30, 2026.
records.body:
Karen Rose Breske, 86, Hatley, passed away peacefully on Saturday, May 30, 2026 at Primrose Memory Care in Weston, under the care of Interim Hospice.

She was born October 8, 1939 in Hatley, daughter of the late Edward and Velma (Doty) Woznicki. On April 25, 1959, she married Roger M. Breske at St. Florian Catholic Church, Hatley. He preceded her in death on April 3, 2012.

For 53 years, Karen and Roger built a life together grounded in love, hard work, and family. As business owners, they worked side by side and shared in the success they created together.

Karen was a loving and devoted mother and grandmother who cherished every moment with her family. She enjoyed hosting family get-togethers and creating lasting memories for those she loved. Known for her gentle and patient nature, she treated her grandchildren with unwavering kindness and never raised her voice to them. Her love, warmth, and compassion will be deeply missed. Her presence made family feel like home.

Survivors include her four children, John (Heather) Breske, Antigo, Jim (Beth) Breske, Hatley, Jodi (Mike O'Neil) Breske, Weston and Jill Bricco, Hatley; 13 grandchildren, Lindsey, Tiffany (Cody), Stephanie (Frank), Dana (Chad), Shannon (Jerry), Erin (Bryce), Cassi (Matt), Timothy, Carli (Riley), Taylor, Halle (Evan), John (Maddie) and Makailah; 12 great-grandchildren, Skylar, Leesa, Evalee, Mason, Brice, Grayson, Stetson, Ruby, Peyton, Miles, Teagan and Thomas; six siblings, Lee (Kathy) Woznicki, Susan Klingbile, Carol (Wendell) Silvius, Thomas (Roberta) Woznicki, Daniel (Brenda) Woznicki and Donald (Cathy) Woznicki; two sisters-in-law, Patti Woznicki and Patty Woznicki; three brothers-in-law, Ron Geurink, John (Peggy) Breske and Dan Breske; and many nieces and nephews.

Besides her parents and husband, Roger, she was preceded in death by four siblings, Gary, Alan and Clifford Woznicki and Diane Geurink.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, June 6, 2026 at St. Florian Catholic Church, Hatley. Rev. Jerome Patric will preside. Burial be in the St. Philomena Cemetery. Visitation will be on Friday from 4:00 until 7:00 p.m. at the church and again on Saturday from 9:00 a.m. until time of Mass. There will be a rosary service at 7:00 p.m. Friday evening.

The family would like to thank the staff and Primrose Memory Care and the staff at Interim Hospice for their loving and compassionate care.

records.name: Kathryn E. Solchenberger
records.summary: Kathryn Elaine Solchenberger, 79, of Wausau, passed away in 2026.
records.body:
Kathryn Elaine Solchenberger, age 79, was born in Eureka, Kansas, on February 5, 1947, to Claude and Gladys Gibson. She graduated from high school in Eureka and married her first husband and moved to California. She had three boys and a girl. Her first marriage ended in divorce, and she married her second husband and had another son. She was married two other times. In 2017, she married her current husband, Will. She lived in many places but finally came to Wausau, WI. There she worked as a cook at Forest Park Village.

She is survived by Bob Weems, Milwaukee, Mike Weems, Michigan, Kathy Weems, Oklahoma and Daniel McMullen, Wausau, six grandchildren, four boys and two girls, two of the boys live in Wausau and two other great grandchildren.

Kathryn liked to go by her middle name, Elaine. In the last couple of years, her eyesight progressively got worse and she enjoyed going to the Lion's camp in Rosholt. For her honeymoon trip, she went to Hawaii, the big island, to visit her husband Will's cousin. She also enjoyed a trip to Florida to visit the Everglades and Key West, St. Louis to visit her cousin Betty Joe, Branson and Yellowstone.

Per family request, no services will be held.

In lieu of flowers, please make a donation to the Feeding America Foundation.

records.name: Lawrence "Larry" Beckley
records.summary: Lawrence "Larry" Beckley, age 72, of Harshaw passed away on June 1, 2026.
records.body:
Lawrence "Larry" Beckley, age 72, of Harshaw, passed away on June 1, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital. He was born on July 21, 1953, in Rhinelander, Wisconsin, the youngest son of John and Doris (Retherford) Beckley.

Larry was a 1971 graduate of Rhinelander High School. Shortly after graduation, he made his way to Milwaukee, where he worked at Central Steel and Wire Company and welcomed three daughters into the world. In the early 1980s, he returned with his family to Harshaw, the place he had called home since childhood. He later worked at Webster Well Drilling for a number of years before his health forced him into early retirement.

In his younger years, Larry enjoyed gardening, hunting, and fishing. NASCAR was a fixture in his home throughout his life, and if you stopped by, chances are it was on the television. As rheumatoid arthritis made the outdoor hobbies harder over time, he could often be found keeping his friend Jay company out at the garden.

Larry had good people around him throughout his life, and those friendships carried him through more than he would have ever admitted.

Larry is survived by his daughters Maggie (Bud) Schmidt and Lindsey (Kris) Kennedy, both of Rhinelander; his grandchildren Mikhail, Lisa, Joe, Carly and Josie; his siblings Terry (Barb) Beckley, Corie (Bob) Kettner, Debra Lehne and Dixie (Sparky) Stimart; many nieces and nephews; and other extended family. He was preceded in death by his parents, John and Doris Beckley, and his youngest daughter, Amy Woodruff.

The family would like to extend their sincere gratitude to the nursing staff at Aspirus Wausau Hospital for the compassionate care shown to Larry during his final hours.

Visitation for Larry will be held on Monday, June 15, 2026 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM with Memorial Services following at 11:00 AM, all at the Carlson Funeral Home. Interment will follow in the Union Grove Cemetery in Harshaw, Wisconsin. You may leave your private condolences for the Beckley family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Beckley family.

records.name: Michael J. Dailey
records.summary: Michael J. Dailey, 78, passed away on May 30, 2026.
records.body:
Michael J. Dailey, 78, passed away Saturday, May 30, 2026, under the care of Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services at Cedar Creek Manor, Kronenwetter.

He was born December 1, 1947, in Freeport, Illinois, son of the late Joseph and Arlene (Bottorf) Dailey. He grew up in Pecatonica, Illinois, a close-knit farming community where he spent much of his youth working on family farms and exploring the countryside on horseback. On July 12, 1969, he married Julie Kathryn Woods, his high school sweetheart, in Freeport, Illinois.

Following graduation from his beloved St. John's University, Michael built a successful career with Traveler's Insurance before continuing his work with Wausau Insurance as claims manager, RMA and Home Office. When he wasn't working, he could often be found outdoors pheasant hunting in Northern Illinois, deer hunting in Northwestern Wisconsin, and fishing in Canada. His many trophy mounts serve as lasting reminders of the great hunts, memorable trips, and deep appreciation he had for the outdoors.

A devoted sports fan, Michael faithfully followed St. John's, Notre Dame, and the Chicago Bears, celebrating their victories and enduring their losses with equal passion. He was also an avid boater and lifelong water-ski enthusiast who cherished every opportunity to be on the water with family and friends. Known for his gift of storytelling, Michael could often be found sharing memories, laughter, and the occasional tale over an Old Style beer. His warmth, humor, and ability to bring people together will be remembered by all who knew and loved him.

Above all, Michael was a devoted husband, father, and grandfather. He took immense pride in his six grandchildren and treasured every opportunity to attend their activities, celebrate their accomplishments, and share in their lives. He also maintained lifelong friendships with many of his childhood and hometown friends from Freeport, Illinois, along with those he met during his time as a student and football player at St. John's. Their shared memories, laughter, and enduring bond remained a cherished part of his life throughout the years.

Survivors include his wife, Julie Dailey; son, Sean (Kathleen) Dailey; daughter, Jennifer (Dean) Haugo; grandchildren, Maura, Sean Patrick, and Shannon Dailey, and Megan, Michael, and Katherine Haugo.

Besides his parents, he was preceded in death by a brother, Thomas Dailey.

The family would like to express their heartfelt gratitude to the compassionate caregivers of Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services and the dedicated staff at Cedar Creek Manor for the kindness, dignity, and exceptional care they provided to Michael during his final days. Their support and compassion brought great comfort to Michael and his family.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. Monday, June 8, 2026, at St. Matthew Catholic Church, Wausau. Rev. Robert Thorn will preside. Visitation will be Monday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service, all at St. Matthew Catholic Church, Wausau. Burial will take place at a later date in St. Mary Catholic Cemetery, Pecatonica, Illinois.

records.name: Paul A. Michalski
records.summary: Paul Anthony Michalski, 71, was called home on May 28, 2026.
records.body:
Paul Anthony Michalski, age 71, was called home to be with his Lord and Savior on May 28, 2026, after a courageous battle with small-cell lung cancer. He was born on February 18, 1955, to Justin and Pearl (Sowinski) Michalski in Milwaukee, Wisconsin.

Paul graduated from Milwaukee's Saint Alexander Grade School in 1969 and Pulaski High School in 1973. He earned a Bachelor of Science degree in Electrical Engineering from Marquette University in 1977 and a Master's degree in Engineering Management from the Milwaukee School of Engineering (MSOE) in 1980. In 1988, he obtained his Professional Engineer license.

Paul made his home in the Wausau area, where he built a successful career and devoted himself to his family. He and Linda (Lambert) Michalski were blessed with three children, who remained a source of great pride and joy throughout his life. On November 11, 2011, Paul married Sharon Modia, and together they shared many years of love, faith, and companionship.

Paul dedicated more than 35 years of service to Wisconsin Public Service Corporation, retiring in 2015. For much of his career, he served as the Substation Construction and Maintenance Manager for the utility's North Central Wisconsin region.

Paul lived life with passion and conviction. He was never shy about sharing his opinions, especially when it came to politics and current events, and he enjoyed spirited discussions with family and friends. While he was not always one to express affection with words, he showed his love in other ways. One of his greatest joys was planning memorable vacations, creating opportunities for his family to spend time together and build lasting memories. He took pride in bringing people together and creating experiences that would be remembered for years to come.

One of Paul's favorite hobbies was genealogical research. Through years of dedicated work, he built an Ancestry family tree containing more than 20,000 individuals. His research connected relatives, preserved family histories, and assisted hundreds of people in discovering their roots. His life's work in genealogy will remain a lasting gift to future generations.

Paul dearly loved his two long-haired Dachshunds, Keena and Bukie, who rarely left his side and were constant companions throughout the years, especially during his four-year battle with cancer. Keena and Bukie, who knew him simply as "Pops," will miss him dearly.

Paul is survived by his beloved wife, Sharon (Modia) Michalski; the mother of his children, Linda (Lambert) Michalski; his daughters, Lindsay (Nick) Larson and Amanda (Thomas) Hardy; and his son, Andrew Michalski. He was a proud Dzia Dzia to Jovie, Anders, and Ansel Larson. He is survived by his stepson, Tyler Kenowski, and his stepdaughter, Krystal Kenowski.

Paul is also survived by his dear siblings, Camille Mueller, Justin (Lori) Michalski, Richard Michalski, and Jan (Andria) Michalski, as well as many beloved nieces, nephews, cousins, and extended family members.

Paul was preceded in death by his parents, Justin and Pearl (Sowinski) Michalski; his brother-in-law, Carl Mueller; his sister-in-law, Loretta (Watkins) Michalski; and his nephew-in-law, Major Erik Boyce, USMC.

A visitation will be held at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston, WI 54476, on Friday, June 5, 2026, from 4:00 to 6:00 p.m. Visitation will continue at St. Mark's Lutheran Church, 600 Stevens Drive, Wausau, WI 54401, on Saturday, June 6, 2026, from 10:00 a.m. until the funeral service at 11:00 a.m., with Pastor Eric Hauan officiating. Committal and entombment will follow at Restlawn Memorial Park Cemetery. Family and friends are invited to a luncheon at St. Mark's Lutheran Church following the committal service.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made to St. Mark's Lutheran Church of Wausau, St. Michael Catholic Church of Wausau, or the Marathon County Humane Society.

A special thank you to Dr. Christopher Peterson for the outstanding care, compassion, and support he provided to Paul over the years. The family is also deeply grateful to the wonderful staff of the Aspirus Oncology and Cardiac units, as well as the IMC, ICU, Palliative Care, and Hospice teams. Their skill, kindness, and positive attitudes helped Paul through many medical challenges and meant so much to him and his loved ones.

Paul's faith, devotion to family, generosity, and passion for preserving family history leave a legacy that will continue to inspire those who knew and loved him.

records.name: Paul J. Frahm
records.summary: Paul J. Frahm, 59, of Wausau, passed away on May 27, 2026.
records.body:
Paul J. Frahm, 59, Wausau passed away on Wednesday, May 27, 2026 at his home.

He was born January 23, 1967 in Wausau to Gary and Maxine (Zurow) Frahm, Wausau.

For many years Paul was a delivery driver at Krueger Floral, Kronenwetter. He later went to work at the Rib Mountain Greenhouse. Prior to his retirement because of health issues he enjoyed working at the Duane L. Corbin shooting range as a rangemaster.

Some of Paul's favorite pastimes included playing softball at Sunnyvale, fishing, hunting and shooting bow at Windy Hill.

Survivors include his parents, Gary and Maxine Frahm, Wausau; his children, Courtney, Tanner and Tyler; his siblings, Trenny (Jim Foss) Frahm, Weston and Shawn Frahm, Wausau; two nephews and a niece.

Graveside services will be held at 10:30 a.m. Saturday, June 6, 2026, at Calvary Cemetery, Athens. The Rev. Msgr. Joseph Diermeier will officiate.

Paul's family would like to extend their sincere and heartfelt gratitude to Kent Spiegel and his family for their friendship and continued support through the years.

records.name: Sally A. Puent
records.summary: Sally Ann Puent passed away on May 27, 2026.
records.body:
Sally Ann Puent, passed away Wednesday, May 27, 2026, at Aspirus Hospice House, Wausau, after a lengthy battle with cancer.

She was born May 2, 1954, in Green Bay, daughter of the late Donald and Lois (Stoeckly) Fellenz. On May 5, 1973, she married Robert "Bob" Puent Jr. at St. Wenceslaus Catholic Church in LaCrosse.

In 1972, Sally graduated from Aquinas and went on to Mid-State Technical School in Stevens Point, where she obtained her LPN in nursing while Bob was attending UWSP. She later continued her education at Nicolet Technical College in Rhinelander, earning her RN while both of her boys were in college.

Sally cherished every moment spent with her children and grandchildren. Together they enjoyed sledding, swimming, and trips to the playground, and she proudly chauffeured her grandchildren to countless appointments, sports practices, and ballet practice. She greatly enjoyed attending her grandchildren's school programs, sports events, and ballet performances. Sally also enjoyed card games, card tricks, board games, and following the "March Madness" bracket challenge with her family and grandchildren, especially the playful "trash talk" with her grandsons. She loved cooking and baking, and always looked forward to decorating Christmas cookies with her grandchildren, using plenty of sprinkles. Her banana bread was always in high demand. She also enjoyed knitting, crocheting and doing crossword puzzles. Poker games with her brothers were always special occasions for her.

She loved visiting Lake Superior and exploring the restaurants and waterfalls along the North Shore. Sally was also active in her faith community, playing in the bell choir at St. Mark Catholic Church.

Her infectious smile, laughter, and sense of humor will be dearly missed by her family and all who knew and loved her.

Survivors include husband, Bob Puent; sons, Brian (Jodi) Puent and Kevin (Ciolagh) Puent; grandchildren, Damien, Griffin, Everett, Addison, Aubrey, Emmett, Clayton and Elsie; brothers, Matt (Pam) Fellenz, Marty (Joanie) Fellenz, Myron (Tammy) Fellenz, Steve (Gina) Fellenz and Paul (Michelle) Fellenz; sister-in-law, Patti Fellenz; numerous nieces, nephews, and other relatives and friends.

Besides her parents, she was preceded in death by her brothers, Mike and Mark Fellenz.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated 11:00 a.m. Monday, June 8, 2026, at St. Mark Catholic Church, Rothschild. Rev. Eric Mashak will preside. Visitation will be Monday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service, all at St. Mark Catholic Church, Rothschild.

records.name: Sharon J. Sefton
records.summary: Sharon J. Sefton, age 74, of Wausau passed away on Jun 1, 2026.
records.body:
Sharon Jean Zarda Sefton passed away on Monday, June 1, 2026 in Wausau after a series of medical setbacks.

Sharon was born on June 17, 1951 in Wausau. She attended Irving, Holy Name and St. Michael grade schools and graduated from Wausau East High School in 1970. Her career at the Marathon County Public Library began before her high school graduation.

Before and after retirement, Sharon loved spending time with her family, sharing recipes on Facebook, watching the Game Show Channel and everything Elvis! She leaves behind her daughter, Rachel (Kris) Woller, grandson, Dennis Woller, granddaughters, Kianna Parker and Taija Burns, sister Joyce (Tom) Naps, stepsister Marcia Kosmerchock, niece Tracy Zarda Lader and nephews, David Zarda, Keith Naps and Joseph Naps.

Preceding Sharon in death are her parents, LaVern (Bud) and Jeanette Kosmerchock; former husband, Dennis Sefton and brothers, Gerald Seliger and Richard (PeeWee) Zarda.

As accordance with her wishes, Sharon will be cremated and interred with her parents at Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau.

Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Wausau is in charge of arrangements. In lieu of flowers memorials may be directed to the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 6th Street, Wausau, WI 54403 to help cover funeral costs. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Stuart C. Bender
records.summary: Stuart C. Bender, age 67, of Hamburg passed away on Jun 1, 2026.
records.body:
Stuart C. Bender, 67, Hamburg, passed away on Monday, June 1, 2026 at his home with his wife, Karen by his side.

He was born August 29, 1958 in Belvidere, IL, son of the late Wayne and Lou (Swail) Bender. On November 1, 1988, he married Karen Holz in Las Vegas and together they enjoyed 37 years of marriage.

Stuart attended Wausau East High School and worked as an over the road truck driver for many years. He loved his acreage, planting trees and growing the land with Karen. He was passionate about growing things and creating artistic pieces from nature.

He is survived by his loving wife, Karen; one son, Eric Bender; his brother, Stacy (Suzie) Bender of Colorado; eight brothers and sisters in law, Deb (Dennis) Hibray, Sue (Mark) Pohlkamp, Steve Holz, Chris (Larry) Jacques, Theresa Gideon, Kathy (Gordy) Karnopp, David Holz and Gretchen (Todd) Bedore; and many nieces and nephews, extended family and friends.

Funeral services will be held at 12:00 p.m. (Noon) on Monday, June 8, 2026 at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau. Rev. Joy Nelson-Jeffers will officiate. Visitation will be on Monday from 10:00 a.m. until time of service at the funeral home. Burial will be held at Restlawn Memorial Park at a later date.

The family would like to extend a special thank you to Lily, Kaylee, Mary and Adam with Aspirus at Home Hospice for the wonderful care and support for Stuart.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petesonkraemer.com

records.name: Theodore Clintsman
records.summary: Theodore (Ted) Clintsman, 91, passed away peacefully on May 21, 2026.
records.body:
Theodore (Ted) Clintsman passed away peacefully on May 21, 2026 at Mount View Care Center in Wausau. He was 91 years old.

Ted was born in Greenwood, WI on July 8, 1934 – the 5th of 6 siblings. In his 20's, he moved to Waukesha, WI and worked at various factories. In 1972, he and his family moved to Wausau, WI. After working at Wausau Iron Works / LOED as a machinist and assembler for 20 years, he decided to open a used furniture and appliance store – Budget Resale. There he not only could pursue his passion for sales and working with his hands; he could also chat with his customers – something else he enjoyed.

When not at his store, Ted faithfully met with his buddies for coffee and for a swim at a therapy pool. He was friendly and talkative – quick to greet others and, as he used to like to put it, "give them grief". He also loved spending time up at his camper in Tomahawk and later, Birnamwood – where he spent most of the time relaxing by the campfire and socializing with other campers.

In 2014, when Ted decided to close Budget Resale for good, the local paper interviewed him. In that article, he talked about his joy in serving and visiting with his customers and his sales philosophy: "be fair with people and treat them like you want to be treated". That was Ted –was how he operated his business and who he was as a person.

Ted is survived by his sons, Gregory (Judy) and Douglas (Linda), by his grandsons, Justin (Rebecca), Sean (Meghan), Evan (Phoenix), and Daniel (Taryn) and by his sister Nell. Preceding him in death were his parents, Fred and Luella Clintsman, his sisters Marie, Irene, Shirley, and his brother, Dale.

A visitation will be held at St. Mark's Lutheran Church, 600 Stevens Dr, Wausau, Wisconsin, on Friday, June 12, at 10:30 am until the time of service at 12:00 officiated by Pastor Eric Hauan.

Family and friends are invited to share words of remembrance and condolences online at www.honorone.com.

records.name: Thomas A. Traub
records.summary: Thomas Arthur Traub passed away peacefully on May 30, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas Arthur Traub passed away peacefully at Aspirus Langlade Hospital in Antigo, Wisconsin on May 30, 2026, after suffering a massive stroke on May 8.

Tom was born in Milwaukee, Wisconsin on December 1, 1946, to Arthur and Catherine (Schutz) Traub. He graduated from John Marshall High School in 1965. On November 12, 1966, he married Geraldine Wickland at Our Lady of Sorrows Catholic Church in Milwaukee. Their union produced five children. Tom was a carpenter and home builder in Southeastern Wisconsin for over 45 years and a life-long Green Bay Packer fan, attending many games with friends. Tom was a resident of Antigo for the past 13 years.

Tom was preceded in death by his parents, Arthur and Kathryn Traub; son, Michael Allen Traub; brother-in-law, Lawrence Koty.

Tom is survived by his ex-wife, Geraldine; daughters, Juliette Ellyn Hoffman (Mike Rouse), Patricia Anne (John) Logue; sons, James Edward (Kristen) and Daniel Arthur (Ashley); foster daughter, Connie Kuphal; sister, Carol Koty; brothers-in-law John Wickland and Robert Wickland; sister-in-law, Georgianna (Jim) Whelan; nieces and nephews; special friend, Manish Patel.

Tom is further survived by grandchildren, Nicole Traub, Danielle Fails, Ariel Leaf, Shaina Warchol, Nate Rouse, Jake Heine, Timothy Traub, Trinity Logue, Matthew Traub, Alexander Traub, Andrew Traub and Evan Logue; great-grandchildren, Kaden Leaf, Abigail Fails, Madyson Leaf, Charlotte Fails, Caroline Gilbert, Lucien Leaf, and Oliver Gilbert.

The family would like to thank the entire Aspirus staff for their kindness and care during Tom's stay and their visits.

Per Tom's wishes, the family will hold a private memorial at a later date.

records.name: Thomas L. Fahey
records.summary: Thomas Louis Fahey, 80, of Edgar, passed away on May 28, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas Louis Fahey of Edgar, passed away on May 28, 2026, at the age of 80, surrounded by his loving family at his home in Tomahawk.

Tom was born on June 5, 1945, in Marshfield to Donald and Elizabeth (Koller) Fahey. He grew up living at Cedar Hedge Cheese Factory in the township of Emmet. Tom graduated from Edgar High School in 1963 and wed Mary Ellen (Guralski) Fahey on June 14,1969, at Saint John the Baptist Parish. Together they shared 56 years of blessed marriage, raising their five children: Janet, Julie, Jimmy, Don, and Mary Lou.

Tom resided in Edgar, where together he operated Pine Valley Cheese Factory for 20 years. He then went on to become an entrepreneur of sorts owning and operating both Westside and Brooklyn Hill Mobile Home Parks, Fahey's Apartments in Weston, and the Mosinee Laundry Mat. He attended the short-course at UW-Madison and graduated with his cheesemaking degree. For the past 10 years, he spent his time in quiet contentment on Lake Alice in Tomahawk, where he could drink his coffee and view God's beauty looking out his bay window to see the morning sunrise, ripples on the lake, and be mesmerized by all the wildlife that surrounded him.

Tom was a 30-year kidney transplant survivor, receiving a kidney from his sister Jean, creating a bond stronger than any other between a brother and sister.

Tom was a handy man of all sorts who could fix anything. He strongly believed in educating his children on the tools needed for both on the job and in life (or to at least run and get the tools for him). In Tomahawk, he spent his time in his garage, feeding the bluejays, orioles, squirrels, and watching NASCAR. The best part of his day was sitting on his deck overlooking the water, chatting with his loving wife Mary.

Being a community member of Edgar, he served on the Edgar Village Board, a member of the Edgar Lions Club, Krecji City Cruisers Snowmobile Club, and served on the Saint John the Baptist church board, where he was a lifelong member.

Tom is survived by his wife, Mary, their children: Janet (Matt) Baumann – Bryce (Lindsey) Schilling, Olivia (Noah) Swita, Owen Baumann, and Olysta Baumann; Julie Fahey (Tony Bong); Jimmy (Tammy) Fahey – Hailey (Josh) Elmhorst, Austin Fahey: Don (Jenna) Fahey – Malayna, Kendra, Kase; and Mary Lou (Brad) Zettler – Eli, Erika, Emma Lou, and Ethan. Two great grandchildren: Mallory Schilling and Palmer Swita. He is also survived by siblings: Michael (Mary Katherine) Fahey, Mary Lynn (Kenny) Hoernke, Ann (John) Napierala, and Jean (Mark) Fonferek; in-laws: Glenroy (Diane) Guralski, Terra Lee Guralski, Jean (John) Sattler, Gerald (Sandy) Guralski, Judy (Wayne) Novak, Steve (Carol) Guralski and many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Donald and Elizabeth Fahey; in-laws, John and Irene Guralski; brothers-in-law LeRoy Guralski, Michael Guralski and sisters-in-law, Rose, Louise, and Elsie Guralski; nephews Frank Guralski, Luke Guralski, Timothy Hoernke, niece Melinda Guralski, close cousin Richard Esp, and still born infant son Joseph.

A funeral Mass will be held at 11:00a.m. on Friday, June 5, 2026, at St. John the Baptist Catholic Parish in Edgar. Burial will take place at the parish cemetery. Visitation will be at the church from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service at 11:00 a.m. The honor of pallbearer belongs to Bryce Schilling, Owen Baumann, Austin Fahey, Kase Fahey, Eli and Ethan Zettler.

Special thanks to Don and the team from Davita Dialysis in Woodruff, Wisconsin, where dad was receiving treatment and to Jenn, RN, and the Aspirus Hospice team who supported him during his final days.

In lieu of flowers, a memorable fund is being established in his memory.

records.name: Larry J. Wild
records.summary: Larry J. Wild, age 80 passed away on Jun 2, 2026.
records.body:
Larry J. Wild, 80, of Athens, passed away on Tuesday, June 2, 2026, at home.

He was born in southern Wisconsin in the town of York on December 19, 1945, to the late Henry and Grace (Krull) Wild. Larry was united in marriage to Susan McCarron on December 16, 1978, in Medford. She preceded him in death in 2004.

For many years Larry was an over-the-road truck driver and delivered fruit and vegetables across the United States. Over the years he drove for E.L. Murphy and Northstar Transport. Trucking was Larry’s life and when he was home he enjoyed spending time with his friends and family.

Survivors include his sister, Dolly Bartz; many nieces and nephews; and his good friends, Florence (Randy) Volzka; C.J.; and their grandchildren, Ezrie and Emrick.

He was preceded in death by his wife, Susan; parents, Henry and Grace; siblings, Henry, Babe, David D., David A., Richard, and Jean; and stepson, Scott.

Private graveside service and burial will be at Holy Rosary Cemetery, Medford. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

A special thank you to Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services and to the Athens Fire Department for the excellent care Larry received.

records.name: Nancy A. Annen
records.summary: Nancy A. Annen, age 86 passed away on Jun 2, 2026.
records.body:
Nancy Anne Annen of Sun Lakes, AZ passed away at Copper Creek Inn Memory Care on Tues June 2, 2026 after a 6-year battle with dementia. She was 86 years old. Nancy was born in Merrill, Wisconsin on July 27, 1939, the youngest of 5 children to Frank Sr. and Ester Robl. Ester passed away 9 months after Nancy’s birth. Nancy was then adopted by Arthur and Ida Horgen of Irma, Wisconsin.

Nancy graduated from Merrill High School in 1957. Then she received her associate degree in 1959 from Madison Business College.

On June 16, 1962, Nancy and Carl came together in Matrimony. They welcomed their daughter into the world on March 29, 1964.

Nancy was employed by Farmco and then retired from Cuna Mutual Insurance after 34 years of employment as a meeting planner.

Carl and Nancy’s journey together took them from Madison, Wisconsin to Sun Lakes, Arizona in 1999. Even in the hottest summers, it was a decision Nancy never regretted. Nancy enjoyed gardening, golf and collecting crosses.

She is survived by her husband of 63 years, Carl Annen; her daughter Peggy Annen; her son Joseph DeGeorge; her grandson Justin Joyce (Jess). Also proceeded by Frank Sr and Ester Robl, Arthur and Ida Horgen, Frank Jr Robl, Douglas Robl, Evelyn Johnson, and Irene McKinney.

Her life will be celebrated by many and she will be greatly missed by her friends and loved ones.

A graveside service will be held June 23, 2026 at 12:00 PM at St. Francis Xavier Catholic Cemetery in Merrill, Wisconsin. After the service, a reception will follow at Northwoods Veterans Post in Merrill, WI

In lieu of flowers, contributions can be made in Nancy’s memory to Maricopa County Humane Society (www.azhumane.org)

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation of Merrill is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences are encouraged and may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com.

records.name: Bellatrix Q. Czerniak
records.summary: Bellatrix Q. Czerniak, age 1, of Spencer passed away on Jun 3, 2026.
records.body:
Bellatrix Quinn Czerniak, 19 months, of Spencer died early the morning of Wednesday, June 3rd, 2026 in the Pediatric ICU at Marshfield Medical Center surrounded by her family.

A private service will be held.

Bella was born October 25, 2024 in Madison to Kristina Hansen and Matthew Czerniak. She fought a long hard battle with congenital heart failure since birth. In defiance of her illness she loved to smile and play, and especially loved watching Peppa Pig. Bella also enjoyed playing with her sister, Sigrid. She passed from this world peacefully with her mom, dad, and sister at her side. She will be missed and will always remain in our hearts.

Bella is lovingly survived by her parents Kristina Hansen & Matthew Czerniak, her sister Sigrid Czerniak, and her brother Koffee Hansen.

She is preceded in death by her grandparents: Bernadine & Gerald Ziehlke, Sarah & Jerome Czerniak, Richard Hansen, and John & Regina Lang

The family wishes to extend a special thanks to the Madison Children’s Hospital and the Marshfield Children’s Hospital for all they have done over the last year and a half.

In lieu of flowers please consider donating in Bella’s memory to the Ronald McDonald Charities.

records.name: Beverly J. Eisenman
records.summary: Beverly J. Eisenman, age 95, of Wausau, passed away on May 23, 2026.
records.body:
Beverly J Eisenman, age 95, went home to Jesus on May 23, 2026 and has now been reunited with her loving husband, Harold.

Beverly was born in Wausau, WI on September 23, 1930 to Chester and Alma (Tapper) Green. Beverly married Harold Eisenman on May 2, 1953 at St Stephen Lutheran Church in Wausau. They shared 72 wonderful years together.

Beverly was a devoted wife, mother, grandmother and great grandmother who's greatest pleasure was spending time with her family. She was the manager of the hot lunch program at Riverview School for many years. Her love of the Lord was evident in her involvement at Good Shepherd Lutheran Church, baking hundreds of Christmas cookies for the yearly bake sale and preparing and serving numerous funeral luncheons.

Beverly is survived by her two daughters Lynn (Lee) Schoepke, Laurel (Dave) Schmieder; grandchildren, Nicole (Jeff) Starck, Megan (Wyatt) Thompson, Eric (Heather) Schmieder, Ryan (Jamie) Schmieder; great-grandchildren, Ethan Starck, Braxton, Sawyer and Asher Thompson, Noah and Ella Schmieder and Mason, Elin and Camden Schmieder. She is further survived by many nieces, nephews, family and friends.

She was preceded in death by her beloved husband Harold Eisenman, parents, parents-in-law, and siblings Helen Hoff, Clifford Green, Wesley Green and Earl (Dick) Green.

A Memorial Service will be held on Wednesday June 17, 2026 at 11:00 am at Good Shepherd Lutheran Church, (930 Edgewood Rd., Wausau, WI) with Rev. Nicole Behnke-Welke officiating. Visitation will be held on Wednesday June 17, 2026 from 10:00 AM until the hour of service at the church.

The family would like to thank the wonderful staff from Mt. View Care Center for their comfort, care and compassion and also for the wonderful care given by the staff of Aspirus Palliative Care and Aspirus Hospice.

In lieu of flowers, memorial donations can be made in Beverly's name to Good Shepherd Lutheran Church.

records.name: Clement Joseph Weis
records.summary: Clement Joseph Weis, age 93, of Marshfield passed away on Jun 3, 2026.
records.body:
Clement J. "Clem" Weis, 93, of Marshfield died Wednesday, June 3rd, 2026 at the Marshfield Medical Center.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11am on Tuesday, June 9th at Our Lady of Peace Catholic Church. Music will be provided by Cindy Penker and the Resurrection Choir. The family welcomes relatives and friends for a period of visitation at the church from 9am until time of Mass. Burial will follow at St. Andrew’s Parish Cemetery in Rozellville. Military Honors will be performed at the cemetery by the Stratford VFW Post 6352.

Clem was born November 22, 1932 on the family farm in the Town of Day to Eugene and Mary (Wilhelm) Weis. He was the 8th of 9 children born to the couple.
