Clem attended school at St. Andrew’s through the 8th grade, and graduated from Stratford High School 3rd in his class in 1951. He enlisted into the Army in 1954 and served as a company clerk until his honorable discharge in December of 1955.

He was united in marriage to the late former Miss Antonette “Toni” Hebert September 14, 1957 at St. Mary’s Catholic Church in Colby. She sadly passed November 29, 2000. Clem married Marci Sessions a few years later, she recently passed away. Clem and Toni took over his parents dairy farm following their marriage and raised three children on the property before retiring from farming in 1989. Clem was a talented woodworker and cook, his family remembers many barbeques and enjoying his homemade fudge. He had also sold insurance for Catholic Family and was involved with Meals-on-Wheels for over 10 years. Most recently he had been a regular fixture at the senior center where he loved playing sheepshead and smear 6-days a week.

Clem is survived by his children: Chris Feltz of Stratford, Brian (Donna) Weis of Anchorage, AK, and Jeni (Scott) Schoenherr of Marshfield; grandchildren: Megan (Wade Behrens) Feltz and Symone (Craig) Kirsch, and two great-grandchildren: Theo and Forrest Kirsch. Clem is further survived by his dear sister Virginia Johnson of Marshfield.

He was preceded in death by his parents, his wife Antonette, daughter Mary Rose in infancy, son-in-law Larry Feltz, and his seven older siblings: Sister Mercita Weis, Harold Weis, Norbert Weis, Melvin Weis, Dorothy Hamus, Marie Walter, and Donald Weis.

The family wishes to extend their deepest gratitude to the staff of Wells Nature View on Adams and the Palliative Care Staff at Marshfield Medical for the kindness they've shown Clem.

records.name: Darrell W. Boyd
records.summary: Darrell W. Boyd, age 73, of Athens, passed away on May 27, 2026.
records.body:
Darrell W. Boyd, 73, of Athens, passed away on Wednesday, May 27, 2026, at Aspirus Medford Hospital.

He was born on April 30, 1953, in Litchfield, Illinois, to the late Elmer and Mary (Davis) Boyd. As Darrell was growing up, he lived in several states and graduated from Ponderosa High School in Shingle Springs, California. Darrell was united in marriage to Sherri Passehl on May 18, 1990, at St. Thomas Catholic Church in Milan. She preceded him in death on March 28, 2010.

Darrell worked for many years at Stratford Builders and later as a forklift operator at Marathon Cheese. Outside of work, he loved to spend time outdoors hunting, fishing, and taking in nature at the pond near his home. He enjoyed and adored the companionship of his Black Lab, Jake. Darrell cherished the time with his family and grandchildren, and attended as many sporting events and activities that he could. He loved supporting his grandchildren. He also attended sprint car races in Shawano and Antigo with his son, Craig.

Survivors include his children, Casey (Paul) Zarnke, James Boyd, and Craig (Sarah) Boyd; grandchildren, Kamden, Kalei, and Kincaide Zarnke and Callum and Scarlette Boyd; sister, Linda (Mark) Ellenbecker; brother, William Boyd; brothers-in-law and sisters-in-law, Udell (Kari) Passehl, Tim Passehl, Sandy (Jay) Schmidt, Kevin (Barb) Passehl, Sue (John) Wood; and many nieces, nephews, relatives, and friends.

He was preceded in death by his wife, Sherri Boyd; parents, Elmer and Mary Boyd; brother, Charles (Rita) Boyd; and father-in-law and mother-in-law, Urban and Elizabeth Passehl.

The graveside service will be at 4:00 p.m. on Friday, June 12, 2026, at Wuertzburg Cemetery, 116746 Wuertzburg Road, Athens (Town of Johnson). A Celebration of Life will begin at 5:30 p.m. at the home of Craig Boyd, 217010 County Road M, Stratford.

records.name: David Allen Svacina
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: David Allen Svacina, born May 7, 1947, in Rice Lake, Wisconsin, passed away in 2026.
records.body:
David Allen Svacina was born May 7th, 1947, in Rice Lake, WI, to Reynold and Florence Svacina. He graduated from Rice Lake H.S. (1965) and UW River Falls (1969).

During his senior year at River Falls, he fell madly in love with his future wife, Darlene Marie Gagnon, and they were married in the chapel on campus on October 25, 1969.

Dave's first job was as a bank examiner for the State of Wisconsin, followed by district manager for H&R Block. He partnered with another tax professional and presented at seminars around the country. Dave recognized the need for a full time tax and accounting business in town, so he founded Wausau Tax and Accounting. Later, Dave worked at Peoples State Bank and was tasked with starting their Investment Center. He retired in 2007 as Executive Vice President. After retirement, he worked part-time at a community bank reviewing loans.

Dave was a graduate of Leadership Wausau, was a longtime member and office holder for the Wausau Noon Optimists, and served on the Executive Committee of Farm Progress Days. He was a member and president of the Board of Directors of the Wisconsin Valley Fair for 18 years. Dave also served on the Board of Directors for The Women's Community and a local community bank.

Dave was a proud hunter and took a special trip with his son Dustin to northern Quebec and came home with two caribou. Later, he brought home an elk from New Mexico. Dave, Darlene, and Dustin harvested several deer from their land. He loved to fish and took trips to the Great Lakes and caught halibut in Alaska.

Dave was adept at sports, as a lap swimmer, tennis player, cyclist, hiker, and his favorite sport, golf. He was most proud of his 32 that he shot during league play at Rib Mountain Golf Course during league play on July 7, 1996.

Dave was the best host-father to countless exchange students from all over the world. We usually hosted young women and sometimes, on arrival, were fearful of the "man of the house." Inevitably, they fell in love with his beautiful smile, and they ended up teasing each other mercilessly.

David is survived by his wife of 56 years, Darlene; son, Dustin Svacina, and partner Keli Kleinschmidt, and grandson, Gavin Svacina; daughter, Dixie Swartwood, and son-in-law, Gary Swartwood, Lake Zurich, IL, and granddaughters, Lucy and Delilah Swartwood. Three brothers preceded him in death: Leigh, Dale and Don Svacina.

Memorials in Dave's name may be directed to The Women's Community, 3200 Hilltop Avenue, Wausau, WI 54401.

Dave's Celebration of Life will be held from 3:00 – 5:00 PM on Saturday, June 20th, 2026, at The Meeting Hall in Marathon Park, 1201 Stewart Ave., Wausau.

records.name: Deloris Eleanor Seefeldt
records.summary: Deloris Eleanor Seefeldt, age 93, of Tigerton, passed away on June 1, 2026.
records.body:
Deloris E. Seefeldt, 93 of Tigerton, passed away on Monday, June 1, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center, Weston.

Deloris Eleanor Seefeldt was born on June 4, 1932, in the Town of Little Wolf, Waupaca County, to William and Alice (Polley) Flater.

Deloris was united in marriage on June 13, 1953 to Howard C. Seefeldt at St. Paul's Lutheran Church, Manawa. The couple dairy farmed in the Town of Wyoming and the Town of Fairbanks for most of their lives. Deloris loved farming, especially feeding and caring for the calves. In 1996 they sold the farm to their oldest son.

Deloris was a longtime member of Peace Lutheran Church, Split Rock, where she was a member of the Ladies Aid. In her early years, she enjoyed snowmobiling. She loved baking, canning, picking berries and gardening. Other favorite pastimes included mowing the lawn, spending time outdoors, going for drives through the countryside, visiting family members and watching the birds. Deloris will be remembered most as a loving mother and a proud grandmother.

Survivors include five children, Douglas (Karin) Seefeldt of Tigerton, Debra Seefeldt of Weston, Dawn Kersten of Tigerton, Delbert Seefeldt of Tigerton and Darold (Julie) Seefeldt of Bear Creek; daughter-in-law, Theresa Seefeldt of Clintonville; seven grandchildren, Wyatt, Paul (Tracy), Joshua, Ashley, Nicole (fiance', Austin), Matthew and Jesse and five great grandchildren, Hailey, Rylee, Abigail, Preston and Lydia. She is further survived by nieces, nephews and friends.

Deloris was preceded in death by her husband, Howard; parents; son, Daniel; son-in-law, Dennis Kersten; grandson, Justin; brother, Edwin (Kathy) Flater; sisters, Margaret (Robert) Bailey and Ardella (Arthur) Pethke; sister-in-law, Gladys (Edward) Prahl and three brothers-in-law, Harold (Evelyn), Harvey (Rosalie) and Lester (Marie) Seefeldt.

A Funeral Service will be held at 11AM on Friday, June 5, 2026, at Peace Lutheran Church, Split Rock. Rev. Ryan Halmstad will officiate and burial will follow in the parish cemetery. Visitation will be on Friday, from 9AM until the time of service at the church.

Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg is assisting the family with arrangements.

records.name: Douangchay Jai Vilaysane
records.summary: Douangchay Jai Vilaysane, born September 15, 1977, in Ban Tin Pier, Laos, passed away on May 29, 2026.
records.body:
Douangchay Jai Vilaysane, born on September 15, 1977, in Ban Tin Pier Laos, passed away peacefully at home on Friday, May 29, 2026, surrounded by the love of her friends and family.

Jai went to Franklin Elementary school, John Muir Middle, and graduated from East High School in 1996. Jai enjoyed singing karaoke, eating, and playing cards with her friends. She was such a joker, always bringing laughter and people together.

She is survived by her father, Phay Vilaysane; her mother, Deune Vilaysane; sister, Malay; brother, Aenoy; partner, Larry; nephews, Martez, Marlon, Kargie, Madden, and Mackenzie; niece, Karlesia; and great-niece, Zaviyah.

Funeral services will begin at 10:00 a.m. until 3:00 p.m. on Friday, June 5, 2026, at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau.

records.name: Gladys Eileen Margetta
records.summary: Gladys Eileen Margetta passed away on May 25, 2026, at Mount View Care Center in Wausau, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Gladys Eileen Margetta was peacefully called home on Monday, May 25th, 2026, at Mount View Care Center in Wausau, Wisconsin. She was born February 10, 1924, to the late August and Brita Johnson of Bessemer, Michigan. Gladys was one of eight children raised on a 38-acre family farm on Johnson Rd. in Bessemer, Michigan. She grew up in the home that her father built. When Gladys was three years old her mother, Brita, died and her father remarried Linda Johnson. Linda helped raise Gladys and her siblings, teaching them many skills including a skill that would stay with Gladys her entire life, sewing. Gladys would often reminisce about her happy childhood and what fun she had with her brothers and sisters.

In 1938, during the great depression, Gladys met Bob (Robert) Margetta while ice skating with her sister Aggie. Bob and his friend, Tom Korpi, walked Gladys and Aggie home and, following that chance meeting, Gladys and Bob dated for several years.

In 1942 Gladys worked at the Kellogg's company in Battle Creek Michigan. This was the same year that Bob was drafted into the Armed Forces. The war needed K- Rations and Gladys' job on the assembly line was to fill a 5 x 8 box with canned meat, two packages of crackers, matches and 4 cigarettes. Before Bob could leave for training camp, he had one thing to do, and that was to ask Gladys to marry him. Bob and Gladys eloped in a courthouse ceremony on October 13, 1942, in Battle Creek, Michigan. Along with their two witnesses, they celebrated with one of the first Sara Lee cakes ever made as their wedding cake. Gladys' dad, August, approved of her marriage to Bob and their honeymoon was spent on their family farm.

Gladys was very devoted to her husband and followed him across the country before he was shipped overseas. After training at Camp McCoy was complete, they moved to Central Point, Oregon. The day Bob left for the war was the same day their daughter, Jeanie Marie, was born. The year was 1944. After Bob left to serve our country, Gladys moved back home to live with her sister Aggie, who helped raise Jeanie until Bob returned home on September 19th, 1945. Gladys and Jeanie were waiting at the train station when he returned and together, they started their life in Bessemer, Michigan. On September 3rd, 1946, they welcomed a son, James Michael. Gladys and Bob built their very own home on Lake Road in Ironwood Michigan in 1954. It was there they enjoyed decades of family memories.

For many years Gladys worked as a waitress at Korpi's Cafe in Ironwood, Michigan, the restaurant owned by her sister Aggie and brother-in-law, Tom Korpi. She was a hard worker, and her family remembers visiting her often at the restaurant.

Gladys was so proud of her husband, his service in WWII and his work ethic. We find it very fitting she was called home on Memorial Day. Gladys and Bob were deeply in love and set a wonderful example for their families. They were blessed with 53 wonderful years of marriage. After Bob's passing in 1995, Gladys moved to Schofield, Wisconsin, to be closer to family.

Gladys loved teaching her grandchildren, great grandchildren and great- great grandchildren how to play cards, cook, bake and sew. She will forever be remembered for her cardamon biscuit and pasties. She was an affable hostess, the coffee was always ready, the kitchen table set with tasty goodies and the conversation always warm and friendly. She loved to watch cooking and home decorating shows on TV and rearrange her furniture. It was not unusual to visit her one week and the following week her apartment would be arranged completely different. She enjoyed watching the Packers, Brewers and many golf tournaments. She would lend a hand to whoever needed it or offer words of wisdom about life if you asked. Her life was simple but very richly blessed because of her love for her family.

Gladys was an independent woman, a "tough ole' gal" as she was affectionately called by her son-in-law, Don, and lived on her own until the age of 99. In her final years she spent quiet days at Mt. View Care Center. She enjoyed the beautiful view over Lake Wausau and would often comment about the birds that would fly by. Despite her eyesight and hearing challenges, she remained kind and cheerful offering to hold your hand or give a hug and sweet smile to all who cared for her. She was truly our GEM.

Gladys is survived by her son, James (Johanna) Margetta of Appleton, Wisconsin, and their family: grandsons Robert John (Gina) Margetta of Appleton, and James Mathew (Jill) Margetta of Marinette; greatgrandchildren Janelle (Josh) Bestul, Korey (Nicholas) Kolz, Jakob Margetta, and Jenna Margetta; and great-great-granddaughter Liliana Kolz.

She is further survived by her son-in-law, Don Aspinwall of Schofield, Wisconsin, and the families of Don and his late wife, Jeanie. This includes granddaughter Gretchen (Lee) Kluz of Ringle, granddaughter Sarah (Kent) Kwick of Kronenwetter and granddaughter Amy (Steve) Zondlo ( Schofield ); great-grandchildren Brittany (Nic) Lotzer, Paige (Steven) Button, and Hope (Mike) Rebman, Jordan Kwick, McKenzie ( Jordan ) Baltz, Brandon Kwick and Jack Zondlo ; great-great-grandchildren Gavin, Emmett, Finnley Lotzer, August Button, Peter and Penelope Rebman and Tennessee Jean Baltz.

Gladys was preceded in death by her beloved husband, Robert (Bob) Margetta (1995); her cherished daughter, Jeanie Marie Aspinwall (2011); and her seven siblings, Elsie Berg, Ada Holla, Jennie Jacobson, Eaner Johnson, Clarence Johnson, Agnes Korpi, and Kathleen Lanctoe.

A private family service will be held at Hillcrest Cemetery in Bessemer, Michigan at a later date.

records.name: Jane P. Cappell
records.summary: Jane P. Cappell, age 93, of Wausau, passed away on May 16, 2026.
records.body:
Jane P. Cappell, formerly Dovenbarger, passed away peacefully at home on May 16, 2026, at the age of 93.

Jane was born on April 5, 1933, at Norwegian American Hospital in Chicago, Illinois, to Dorothy and Dr. George Cappell, DDS. Though her early years were shaped by the Great Depression, World War II, and the loss of her father when she was just 14 years old, Jane always remembered her childhood as a happy one. From an early age, she showed both a talent and love for art, eventually earning a scholarship to The Art Institute of Chicago.

After her art education, Jane attended Ripon College, where she met the first of what she fondly called her "two amazing husbands," Earle "Swede" Swanson. Together they moved to Marshfield, Wisconsin, where Swede owned a furniture company and where they raised four daughters.

In Marshfield, Jane's spirit of service began to flourish. She founded the "Pink Ladies" volunteer organization at St. Joseph's Hospital and became deeply involved in the community. Living in a medical community sparked her own interest in healthcare, leading her to earn her RN certification from St. Joseph's School of Nursing. She was especially proud of receiving one of the top scores in the state on her final nursing examination. Jane began her nursing career in the intensive care unit, where caring for critically ill patients and their families became deeply meaningful work to her.

In 1981, Jane married William "Bill" Dovenbarger, MD, blending their families and creating an even larger circle of children and grandchildren. Jane later worked as an RN assistant to the medical director of Security Health Plan. Together, she and Bill volunteered with Doctors Without Borders in St. Lucia, sharing their compassion and skills with others in need.

After retirement, Jane and Bill moved to Venice, Florida, where they built a home and enjoyed ten wonderful years of golf, boating, and visits from family and friends. Eventually, Wisconsin called them home once again, and they settled in Wausau.

Jane quickly became involved in the Wausau community through volunteer work and civic organizations. She was active with the Marathon County Historical Society, Antiques Study Club, Good News Project, and the Leigh Yawkey Woodson Art Museum, where she served as a docent. Jane and Bill were benefactors of the 1916 Society at Marshfield Clinic and members of First United Methodist Church. She also held a special place in her heart for the Golden K Kiwanis Club, where she served as president until shortly before her passing.

Art remained one of Jane's greatest joys throughout her life. She was a gifted oil painter who continued painting well into her nineties.

She also loved to travel and was fascinated by cultures around the world, especially the history and traditions of Indigenous Americans here in the United States.

Jane embraced life fully and beautifully. Proud of her Norwegian heritage, she was known for her impeccable style, perfectly kept hair and nails, and her ability to make even a trip to the grocery store feel like an occasion. She loved shopping of every kind, and few things delighted her more than finding a hidden treasure at Goodwill. Jane was an avid football fan, a gourmet cook, a gracious entertainer, and deeply passionate about classical music.

Above all else, Jane cherished her family. She treasured time spent with her children and grandchildren, as well as Bill's children and grandchildren, and took special joy in the lives of her great-grandchildren. She rarely missed a birthday or holiday celebration with "little" Jane, Anna, and Will Duros. Having her great-granddaughter carry her name brought her enormous joy in her later years.

One of Jane's favorite quotes was from Mark Twain:

"Let us live so that when we come to die even the undertaker will be sorry."

Those who knew Jane can surely say she accomplished exactly that.

In addition to her parents, Jane was preceded in death by her first husband, Earle "Swede" Swanson; her second husband, William "Bill" Dovenbarger, MD; Bill's sons, Vance and Kurt Dovenbarger; and her sister, Joan Becker.

Jane is survived by her children: Kristine Duros (John) of Kronenwetter, Wisconsin, and their children Nick (Helen), Alex (Alexandra), and Jack (Kathryn); Shelley Glinski (the late Don) of Eau Claire, Wisconsin; Karen Swanson of Wausau, Wisconsin; and Ann Silberman (Peter) of Northfield, Illinois, and their children Leah (Didi), Ben (Elena), and Eva (Parker).

She is also survived by Bill's children: Diane Meissner (the late Jerry) of Chili, Wisconsin, and their children Josh (Sherri), Jenny (Matt), Tiffany (Danny), and Ashley (David); Eric Dovenbarger (Cynthia) of Palm Bay, Florida, and their children Tyler and Aiden; as well as grandsons William, son of the late Vance, and Kade, son of the late Kurt. Jane was also blessed with ten Dovenbarger great-grandchildren and three Duros great-grandchildren. She is also survived by her beloved dog, Opie.

Those wishing to make memorial contributions are encouraged to do so at the Wausau Golden K (Kiwanis Club) 1707 Marquardt Rd, Wausau, WI, 54403.

A Memorial Service will be held on Saturday, June 27, 2026, at First United Methodist Church, 903 North 3rd Street, Wausau, Wisconsin 54403. Visitation to begin at 11:00 a.m., followed by the Service at 11:30 a.m. A luncheon will follow, with location to be announced.

records.name: Ronda Lou Rydbom
records.summary: Ronda Lou Rydbom, age 68, of Wausau, passed away on May 21, 2026.
records.body:
Ronda Lou Rydbom, 68 of Wausau peacefully went to be with the Lord on Thursday, May 21, 2026.

Ronda was a strong woman who faced life and all its challenges with grit and determination. Ronda loved spending time crafting many projects for her kids and grandkids. She enjoyed lunch and dinner dates with her best friend, Susan, at the local diners in town. She also enjoyed being the friendly, smiling face of all whom she greeted at NCHC, as part-time post-retirement work.

Ronda is survived by her four children Marci (Chet Klave), Brandi (Scott Kawlewski), Lynsi (Dan Ravn), and Zackary Bradford; her brothers Rodney and Roy Rydbom; and her biggest blessings, her five grandchildren; Logan, McKenna, Zoey, Oaklee, and Ellie. Ronda also left behind her beloved cat, Baby. Ronda was preceded in death by her mother, Norma; father, Dale; and sister, Rene.

By choice of the family and Ronda's wishes there will not be a formal service or memorial. Her children have planned to remember their mother with family and friends this summer with a "Remembering Ronda" Celebration at Jojo's Jungle on Sunday, August 2nd, 2026, from 1-4 pm. We welcome all that would like to share their fond memories of Ronda.

Thank you to Brainard Funeral Home for their assistance and guidance in this most challenging time.

records.name: Steven Dale Sonderleiter
records.summary: Steven Dale Sonderleiter, age 80, passed away on May 30, 2026.
records.body:
Steven Dale Sonderleiter, 80, passed away peacefully at home on May 30, 2026, surrounded by his children and his faithful companion, Janice, while under the compassionate care of Compassus Hospice.

Steven was born on November 8, 1945, in Los Angeles, California, to the late Dale and Mary (Bracy) Sonderleiter. He was raised in the Garwin, Iowa, area alongside his siblings Connie, Paula, Diane, and Kenneth.

On August 12, 1966, Steven married Carol Ann Reitenbaugh. Together they established their home in Le Grand, Iowa, and were blessed with four children: Lori Sonderleiter of Jasper, Tennessee; twins Steven Sonderleiter and Stephanie Sonderleiter of Marshalltown, Iowa; and Kristi Sonderleiter of Montour, Iowa.

Steven proudly served his country in the United States Army Reserves. He dedicated many years of his working life to Harker's Meats in Le Mars, Iowa. After relocating to Wisconsin, he continued his career with Mullins Cheese until his retirement.

A talented craftsman and mechanic, Steven had a lifelong passion for motorcycles. He enjoyed building, customizing, and riding motorcycles, earning recognition and an award for a custom chopper he created. He also loved working on vehicles and was always happiest when he had a project in the garage.

While living in Wisconsin, Steven was fortunate to meet Janice Haase, who became his devoted companion and partner for many years. Their time together brought him great happiness and companionship.

Steven is lovingly survived by Janice; his sisters, Diane, Paula, and Connie; Steven's children, Lori, Steven, Stephanie, and Kristi; as well as numerous grandchildren and great-grandchildren who will cherish his memory.

In addition to his parents, Steven was preceded in death by his younger brother, Kenneth.

In accordance with Steven's wishes, cremation has taken place. Private family graveside services will be held in Marshalltown, Iowa.

The family extends their heartfelt gratitude to Compassus Hospice for the exceptional care, kindness, and support provided to Steven and his loved ones during his final days.

records.name: Stuart C. Bender
records.summary: Stuart C. Bender, age 67, of Hamburg, passed away on June 1, 2026.
records.body:
Stuart C. Bender, 67, Hamburg, passed away on Monday, June 1, 2026 at his home with his wife, Karen by his side.

He was born August 29, 1958 in Belvidere, IL, son of the late Wayne and Lou (Swail) Bender. On November 1, 1988, he married Karen Holz in Las Vegas and together they enjoyed 37 years of marriage.

Stuart attended Wausau East High School and worked as an over the road truck driver for many years. He loved his acreage, planting trees and growing the land with Karen. He was passionate about growing things and creating artistic pieces from nature.

He is survived by his loving wife, Karen; one son, Eric Bender; his brother, Stacy (Suzie) Bender of Colorado; eight brothers and sisters in law, Deb (Dennis) Hibray, Sue (Mark) Pohlkamp, Steve Holz, Chris (Larry) Jacques, Theresa Gideon, Kathy (Gordy) Karnopp, David Holz and Gretchen (Todd) Bedore; and many nieces and nephews, extended family and friends.

Funeral services will be held at 12:00 p.m. (Noon) on Monday, June 8, 2026 at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau. Rev. Joy Nelson-Jeffers will officiate. Visitation will be on Monday from 10:00 a.m. until time of service at the funeral home. Burial will be held at Restlawn Memorial Park at a later date.

The family would like to extend a special thank you to Lily, Kaylee, Mary and Adam with Aspirus at Home Hospice for the wonderful care and support for Stuart.

records.name: Thomas Lee Wardall
records.summary: Thomas Lee Wardall, age 83, of Merrill, Wisconsin, passed away on May 29, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas Lee Wardall, a beloved husband, father, grandfather, great-grandfather, and friend, passed away peacefully on May 29, 2026, at the age of 83. Born on December 10, 1942 to Clarence and Ellen Wardall, in Merrill, Wisconsin.

In 1962, Thomas married Betty Gryskiewicz, and together they built a life filled with adventure, laughter, and cherished memories. They enjoyed traveling, particularly to South Padre Island during the winter months, and shared a passion for camping and water skiing at Lake Tomahawk. An avid outdoorsman, Tom loved to hunt and shoot guns and spend time at their log cabin on their hunting land.

Thomas served his country honorably in the U.S. Army from 1960 to 1966. After his service, he dedicated 40 years to Church Mutual, where he furthered his education by earning his CPCU certification and worked his way up to be an Assistant Vice President. Active in his community, he was a proud member of the Lion's Club, served on the Merrill Area Public Schools school board from 1982 to 1990, and was a member of the board for North Central Technical College from 1983 to 1992. He once held the position of president of the Kiwanis Club, reflecting his commitment to service and leadership.

Thomas is survived by his devoted wife, Betty Wardall; his son, Alan (Marie) Wardall, and daughter-in-law, Janet Wardall; his grandchildren, Jared (Brianna) Wardall, Aubrey (Brent) Leech, Morgan Wardall, Aleysa Wardall, and Ryan (Elena McMenamin) Wardall; as well as great-grandchildren Wesley Thomas Wardall and Bennett Bradley Leech. He also leaves behind his brother, Gary (Peggy) Wardall, and numerous nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents, his infant daughter Nicole Wardall, his son Lee Wardall, and his brother Harold Wardall.

The family wishes to extend heartfelt gratitude to the doctors, nurses and staff at Marshfield Medical Center in Weston and the hospice staff at Compassus for their compassionate care.

Funeral services will be held Friday, June 19, 2026 at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, 301 Eagle Dr, Merrill, Wisconsin, at 11am. Visitation will be from 9am-11am at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation.

In lieu of flowers, donations can be made in Thomas' name to Tunnel to Towers Foundation, the Wounded Warriors Project, or St. Jude's Children's Research Hospital.

records.name: Darlene "Dolly" Mary Tanck
records.summary: Darlene "Dolly" Mary Tanck, age 72 passed away on Jun 4, 2026.
records.body:
Darlene “Dolly” Mary Tanck, a beloved mother, grandmother, great-grandmother, and friend, passed away on June 4, 2026, at the age of 72. Born on January 21, 1954, in Merrill, Wisconsin, to Geno and Florence Bosi.

Dolly was the proud owner of Dolly's Daycare, where she dedicated herself to caring for children and babies from 1991 until her retirement in 2026. She loved every moment spent with the kids, filling her daycare with laughter and warmth. Beyond her professional life, Dolly was a creative spirit. She enjoyed baking—Saturdays were reserved for this passion, along with cleaning—and made cards for every occasion, bringing joy to her family with her thoughtful creations.

A homebody, Dolly found happiness in gardening, sewing, knitting, and all the "Grandma hobbies" that she so loved. She was happiest during family gatherings, where her homemade wine was always a hit. Dolly had a special fondness for camping trips with her family and was known for her spring and fall cleaning rituals.

Dolly treasured her time with her late husband, Lonnie Tanck, whom she married on April 17, 1971. Their bond was unshakable, and she missed him dearly after his passing on January 21, 2024. Together, they created a loving home filled with warmth and laughter.

Dolly is survived by her daughters, Toni (Cory Wilk) Smith and Brenda (Brian) Burkhardt; her grandchildren, Brittany (Christian) Steinke, Logan (Kaylee) Smith, Vanessa Smith, Matthew Burkhardt, and Elizabeth Burkhardt; great-grandchildren Ava, Marjorie, and River; her mother-in-law, Anna Tanck; and her siblings, Jean Goodell, Nancy Drew, Kathy Iwen, and Karen (Brian) Grund, sisters-in-law, Gail (Terry) Glisch and Glenda (Terry) Modrzejewski, along with many nieces and nephews.

Dolly is preceded in death by her loving husband Lonnie, her parents, her in-laws Al Tanck, Joyce and Alvin Goestch, and her brothers-in-law Francis "Lefty" Goodell, Arthur Drew, and Russell Iwen.

The Mass of Christian burial will be held at St. Francis Xavier Catholic Church in Merrill on June 12, 2026, at 1:00 PM, with a public visitation taking place at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, 301 Eagle Dr. Merrill, Wisconsin, that same day from 10:00 AM until 12:30 PM.

In honor of Dolly’s vibrant spirit and loving nature, her family encourages everyone to remember her by embracing joy in their lives. As she always said, “Be Happy.”

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Karl L. Schreiner
records.summary: Karl L. Schreiner, age 68 passed away on Jun 4, 2026.
records.body:
Karl Schreiner, 68, of Athens Wisconsin passed peacefully from his home to his eternal rest on June 4th. A humble and kind man Karl will be fondly remembered by all who knew him. He spent 35 years, supporting farmers throughout the area with their needs and caring deeply about their families. Many people will recall his passion for raising whitetails. Deep kinship was forged with many Deer friends across four decades. Spring fawns, peanut treats, and antler watch hours were quality time. AI and ship outs were adventurous and boisterous moments for which he was grateful to kindred spirits who showed up every year. Karl was a quiet problem solver always willing to help anyone no matter his personal cost, a man you could count on who didn’t expect anything in return. Karl‘s extensive family will miss his steady presence, gentle eyes, and loving heart. He desired peace rather than strife and always chose his actions with that intent. Karl cherished sharing enjoyment of the simple things in life. A clear crisp morning in the forest, listening intently to catch the first glimpse of a magnificent buck strolling through. The twitch of the line as a fat trout left the stream to join him for breakfast. The joy of a well tended food garden nourishing both body and spirit. A good hound’s companionship. A rock, solid and timeless, with stories untold, quiet yet beautiful…

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. on Friday, June 19, 2026, at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens, with Rev. George Graham presiding.  Visitation will begin at 4:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. on Thursday, June 18 and continue on Friday beginning at 9:00 a.m. until the time of service at the church.  Private burial will take place.  Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Maryann Halverson
records.summary: Maryann Halverson, age 73, of Summit Lake passed away on June 4, 2026.
records.body:
Maryann Halverson, age 73, of Summit Lake, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on June 4, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital with her family by her side.

Maryann was born on March 25, 1953, in Rhinelander, Wisconsin, the daughter of Ervin and Lucille (Neu) Rupple. She was a woman whose life was marked by love, warmth, and an extraordinary spirit that touched all who had the privilege of knowing her.

Maryann was deeply devoted to her family, and her greatest joy resided in her grandchildren. Known for having the biggest heart, she was often described as a true ray of sunshine in the lives of those around her. Her affection extended beyond her human family to her beloved animals; she had a special bond with her horses, donkey, and dogs. An enduring image of Maryann is one of her being barefoot outdoors, embracing the simple, unpretentious pleasures of life. Her kindness and generous nature made her a wonderful person, admired and cherished by friends and family alike.

Maryann is lovingly survived by her husband, Rodney Halverson, her daughter Heather (Ben) Kessler, her son Jason (Kaylyn) Halverson, and her grandchildren: Emma Kessler, Marley Halverson, and Memphis Halverson, who were truly the light of her life. Her family circle also includes her siblings, Ervin (Sandy), Gloria (Don), George, Alvin (Sue), sister-in-law Joanne, Jerry (Terry), and many nieces, nephews, other relatives, and friends. Together, they carry forward the loving legacy Maryann created.

She was preceded in death by her beloved parents and siblings, Ann, Alan, and Judy. Their memories remain cherished and forever part of Maryann’s story.

Maryann Halverson’s life was a testament to love, kindness, and the simple joys that make a life meaningful. She will be remembered always for her warm spirit and the profound impact she had on all who knew her. Her presence will be deeply missed, but her memory will continue to shine brightly in the hearts of her family and friends.

A celebration of Maryann's life will be held on Saturday, June 20, 2026, from 2:00-5:00 pm at the Upper Pavilion(C) - Veterans Memorial Park (N8375 Park Rd, Deerbrook, WI 54424). Please wear blue, Mom's favorite color, as we honor the life of Maryann.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Mildred Abt
records.summary: Mildred Abt, age 85, of Ringle passed away on June 4, 2026.
records.body:
Mildred V. (nee Thanig) Abt, more commonly known as Midge, age 85, of Ringle, Wisconsin passed away peacefully at home on Thursday, June 4, 2026, under the care of her family.

She was born on November 14, 1940, in the town of Emmet. She was the daughter of the late Justin and Ruth (Nechuta) Thanig. She married the love of her life Ronald J Abt of Ringle on August 21, 1959, and the couple shared 66 wonderful years together. Midge drove school bus in the D.C. Everest Area School District for 52 years. She was also employed with the Marathon County Commission of Aging (MCCA) food service for many years.

Midge's firm foundation and first love was her Lord, rooted from her childhood. One of her greatest joys was baking for family and friends and loving on her grand and great-grandchildren. Her extraordinary loving spirit split over to everyone who knew her, as she took great joy in giving to everyone she met.

Midge is survived by her husband Ron, her children; Lisa (Bob) Glapinski and Ronald (Laura) Abt, her 7 grandchildren, and 14 great-grandchildren. She is also survived by many nieces, nephews, other relatives and many friends. She is preceded in death by her daughter Carlene (Steve) Hohn.

Funeral service will be held at 11:00 AM on Friday, June 12, 2026, at Redeemer Ev. Lutheran Church, 6404 Old Costa Lane, Weston, WI, 54476. Pastor Ryan Cortright will officiate. Visitation will be held from 5:00 - 7:00 PM on Thursday, June 11th at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston, and again on Friday from 10:00 AM until the time of service at the church. Interment will be in Forestville Cemetery.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Ronald D Sutheimer
records.summary: Ronald D Sutheimer, age 92 passed away on June 4, 2026.
records.body:
Ronald D. Sutheimer, 92, died Thursday, June 4, 2026, at Willowbrook Senior Living in Stevens Point, WI.

Ronald was born August 8, 1933, to the late Walter and Loretta (Persike) Sutheimer, in the town of Lanark, WI. He graduated from high school in Amherst, WI, in May of 1951.

He married June (Damrau) Sutheimer on January 17, 1952. He divorced in 1982. He remarried Ethelyn (Bull) Sutheimer on April 30, 1994.

Ronald was preceded in death by his parents, siblings Maynard Sutheimer, Arleigh (Mildred) Sutheimer, grandsons Keith Sutheimer, Travis Sutheimer, Michael Higgins, Nathanial Bull, son Christopher Bull and great grandson Nicholas Benz.

Ron is survived by his wife Ethelyn (Bull) Sutheimer, children Dwynne (Ann) Sutheimer, Gene Sutheimer, Dale Sutheimer, Patricia (Patsy) Sutheimer, Vicki (Dale) Higgins, Penny Sutheimer, Sadie (Larry) Dassow, 15 grandchildren, 38 great grandchildren, and 9 great, great grandchildren.

Ronald worked at Nelsonville Creamery and Pavelski Potato routes with June. He then got into the Soo Line Railroad as an engineer until they sold out. After retiring from the railroad, he went to work for his son, Dwynne Sutheimer Trucking as a dispatcher and driver. He then drove truck for Wysocki Potato Farm.

Ronald’s interests and hobbies were spending time with family and friends, hunting, fishing, camping, traveling, hosting family reunions and working on projects in his garage. He also enjoyed going to Spooner with Lyn to see her parents Gladys and Bill Fritz.

Ronald was in the Waupaca Motorcycle Club, the Lions Club, the Elks Club, a Jr. Warden at Intersession and St. Olaf’s, volunteered as a driver at the Lincoln Center, volunteered with a youth fishing group, and went with a mission group with Lyn after hurricane Katrina.

Services for Ronald will be held on Saturday, June 20, 2026, at St. John the Evangelist Episcopal Church, 320 Oak St., Wisconsin Rapids, WI 54494. Visitation will be from 10-11am, with the service starting at 11am, with Father David Klutterman officiating.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family. Words of remembrance may be left at MWCS.WS

records.name: Edward "Eddie" Jacob Millard
records.summary: Edward "Eddie" Jacob Millard, age 1, of Pittsville passed away on Jun 5, 2026.
records.body:
Edward “Eddie” Jacob Millard, 1, Pittsville, passed away unexpectedly on Friday, June 5, 2026 at his home.

Eddie was born on June 3, 2025 in Wisconsin Rapids to Jacob and Rachel (Hahn) Millard.

Eddie was ready to go places - walking tall, climbing and exploring. He enjoyed trying to chase and play on the playground. He liked to clap and cheer on others with an enthusiastic “YEAH!” His first word was his sister’s name.

Eddie’s favorites, beyond his daddy’s tossing and beard tickles, was watching what his sister was into. He thought she hung the moon. They enjoyed building block towers and knocking them down! Who can scream louder, chasing bubbles, and splashing in baths. Eddie loved fruit, meat and anything good to eat! Cars, puppies and chocolate milk.

He is survived by his parents Jacob and Rachel, beloved older brother Connor Black, and sweet older sister Millie.

He is further survived by great grandparents Char and Glen Geer, Grammy Tammy Millard and grandparents Thomas and Gretchen Hahn, loving aunts and uncles Glen (Kim) Geer, Curtis (Michelle) Millard, William (Shannon) Millard, Jo Ann Millard, Lisa Altman, Ronald (Hailey) Hahn, close friends John and Katie Hoffman, his Godparent Trio of Rebecca & Nickolas Schmit and John Robert Hoffman, as well as a vast collection of precious cousins, friends and community.

He was welcomed to his heavenly home by his namesake, grandfather Edward E. Millard, grandmother Christine Ann Keech, and great grandparents Walter and Caroline Keech & Walter and Virginia Hahn.

A Gathering to Share Memories of Eddie is to be held from 5:00 - 8:00 pm on Sunday, July 19 at Wildwood Station Pavilion. Children are welcome and encouraged to attend.

records.name: Joan E. Jarosz
records.summary: Joan E. Jarosz, age 66, of Rothschild passed away on Jun 5, 2026.
records.body:
Joan Ellen Jarosz, age 66, of Rothschild, WI, passed away on Friday, June 5, 2026. She was born to Harry and Judith Jarosz on August 31, 1959.

Joan loved to create masterpieces with her hands, especially crocheting and sewing. She was deeply involved in giving back to her community and helping others through things like Bible Quizzing and the Get Ahead program. She was a champion of others, loving them into their greatest potential.

Joan is survived by her brother Ken (Carolyn) Jarosz of Equality, Alabama; sister Amy (Richard) Emmert, of Mason, Ohio; Nieces and Nephews: Aaron Jarosz, Emily (Chris) May, Andrea (Jason) Harris, Krista Emmert, and Jade Emmert.

She was preceded in death by: her parents, Harry and Judith Jarosz.

The family would like to thank Sue Krcma and members of the Lighthouse Pentecostal Church, Marge and Bill Tice, and Linda Emens for loving her so much.

Services will be held at the Lighthouse Pentecostal Church, 1525 Townline Rd, Wausau, WI 54403, on Saturday, June 13, at 10:00 a.m. Pastor Ron Schuenke will officiate. Burial will be at Mosinee Cemetery immediately following the service.

Brainard Funeral Home & Cremation Center of Weston is honored to be assisting Joan’s family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.brainardfuneral.com.

records.name: Margaret A. Anderson
records.summary: Margaret A. Anderson, age 76, of Marshfield passed away on Jun 5, 2026.
records.body:
Margaret A. Anderson, 76, Marshfield, passed away on Friday, June 5, 2026 at her home.

She is survived by her daughter, Roberta Fallin (Beloit).

She was preceded in death by her husband, John.

records.name: Ruth A. Wallace
records.summary: Ruth A. Wallace, age 97, of Marshfield passed away on Jun 5, 2026.
records.body:
Ruth Ann Wallace passed away on June 5, 2026 at 97 years of age. Family was at her side at the time of her passing. Ruth would have been 98 years old on June 10, 2026.

Graveside services will be held at Oak Grove Cemetery in La Crosse, WI. on Saturday, June 13th at 1 pm. Reverend Maxine Ashley presiding.

Ruth lived her entire life in La Crosse, WI until November of 2022 when she moved to assisted living in Marshfield, WI.

Ruth was a kind, generous woman who valued family above all else. She provided endless care to many she loved including her parents, her husband, and multiple other family members.

She attended First Baptist Church in La Crosse, WI her entire life until her move to Marshfield. She was a faithful member and servant.

Ruth loved to sew, knit, play bridge and her home was her pride and joy. Her children Sue and Mary were her top priority and later also were grandchildren and great grandchildren. She shined at being both Mother and Grandma.

She is survived by her daughters, Sue Ferguson (Rod), Mary Green (Rick), grandchildren Scott Ferguson (Heather), and Sarah Lenten (John); great grandchildren, Nathan Courteau; Nolan and Ava Ferguson; Sam and Damian Lenten; one niece and three nephews.

She will be missed by all who knew and loved her and she holds a special place in our hearts forever.

Memorials may be sent to Compassus Hospice, Marshfield, WI., or the charity of your choice.

records.name: Scott Daniel Wozniarski
records.summary: Scott Daniel Wozniarski, age 66, of Rhinelander passed away on June 5, 2026.
records.body:
Scott Daniel Wozniarski, age 66, of Rhinelander, died on June 5, 2026 at his home. He was born on July 1, 1959 in New Berlin, WI to Daniel and Mary(Sczymanski) Wozniarski.

Scott attended schools in New Berlin and graduated from New Berlin High school in 1977.  After high school he began working as a mechanic in the Milwaukee area. In the early 1990’s Scott moved to Rhinelander and continued working as a small engine mechanic.

Scott enjoyed fishing, gardening, Nascar, and his favorite hobby was collecting Hot Wheels. His collection was impressive to say the least, he donated many to the kids and schools in the community.  Scott loved his family and enjoyed spending time with them as much as he could.

Scott is survived by his daughters Shannon Wendorf of Oak Creek, and Dawn Wozniarski of Waukesha, son Eric Wozniarski of South Milwaukee, grandchildren Dominick, Christian, Nevaeh, Chance and Reagan, great grandchild Kingsly, mother Mary Wozniarski, sisters Debbie(Raymond(Rocky)) Richards, and Diana(James) Deprey, brother Dale(Colleen) Wozniarski, other family and many friends.

He was preceded in death by his father Daniel.

There will be no services at this time for Scott. You may leave your private condolences for the Wozniarski family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home(715-369-1414) is handling the arrangements for the family.

records.name: Sharon Jean Zarda Sefton
records.summary: Sharon Jean Zarda Sefton, 74, of Wausau passed away on June 1, 2026.
records.body:
Sharon Jean Zarda Sefton passed away on Monday, June 1, 2026 in Wausau after a series of medical setbacks.

Sharon was born on June 17, 1951 in Wausau. She attended Irving, Holy Name and St. Michael grade schools and graduated from Wausau East High School in 1970. Her career at the Marathon County Public Library began before her high school graduation.

Before and after retirement, Sharon loved spending time with her family, sharing recipes on Facebook, watching the Game Show Channel and everything Elvis! She leaves behind her daughter, Rachel (Kris) Woller, grandson, Dennis Woller, granddaughters, Kianna Parker and Taija Burns, sister Joyce (Tom) Naps, stepsister Marcia Kosmerchock, niece Tracy Zarda Lader and nephews, David Zarda, Keith Naps and Joseph Naps.

Preceding Sharon in death are her parents, LaVern (Bud) and Jeanette Kosmerchock; former husband, Dennis Sefton and brothers, Gerald Seliger and Richard (PeeWee) Zarda.

As accordance with her wishes, Sharon will be cremated and interred with her parents at Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau.

records.name: Wayne Allen Tryba
records.summary: Wayne Allen Tryba, 72, died peacefully at home on May 31, 2026.
records.body:
Wayne Allen Tryba, age 72, died May 31st, 2026, peacefully at home.

Wayne was born on September 29th, 1953, to Joseph and Adeline (Wilk) Tryba. He attended D.C. Everest High School and North Central Technical College. He was a diligent and knowledgeable worker at Mill's Fleet Farm in Wausau for 35 years.

Wayne was a perfectionist and very mechanically minded. Since he was always learning new things, he was able to fix many different machines. His son (Ben) and he restored two motorcycles.

He enjoyed a variety of hobbies which included riding his motorcycle, playing pool, throwing horseshoes and fishing. He often would be in search of the perfect recipes and would go out of his way to help others.

Wayne is survived by his mother, Adeline; his son, Ben; his brothers Gary and Larry Tryba; and his sister Christine (Greg) Jandrt. He was preceded in death by his wife, Ninette (Woytasik); his father; two sisters, Sandra Alsteen and Karen Tryba; and one brother, Jim Tryba.

No services will be held per Wayne's request.

records.name: William "Bunker" C. Luder
records.summary: William "Bunker" C. Luder, age 81, of Marshfield passed away on Jun 5, 2026.
records.body:
William “Bunker” C. Luder, 81, Marshfield, passed away on Friday, June 5, 2026 at House of the Dove, Marshfield.

Funeral services will be held at 1:30 pm on Friday, June 12, 2026 at Rembs Funeral Home, Marshfield with Rev. Douglas Robertson officiating. Burial will follow at Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Marshfield.

Visitation will take place from 4:00 pm - 7:00 pm on Thursday, June 11 and from 12:30 pm until the time of service on Friday, all at Rembs Funeral Home, Marshfield.

William was born on December 5, 1944 in Marshfield to Carl and Ruth (Boehm) Luder. He was united in marriage to Barbara Hunt on September 4, 1971 in Beloit.

After owning and operating Luder's Lumberyard until its closure in 1986, he continued his career with A&B Process Systems, where he worked until retirement. He found his greatest joy outdoors, spending countless hours caring for and enjoying his property. An avid hunter and fisherman, he was a lifetime member of the NRA and deeply appreciated the beauty of nature and the traditions of the outdoors. He was also a proud member of the Fraternal Order of Eagles #624 and the Republican Party. Throughout the years, he enjoyed participating in local bowling and softball leagues, forming many lasting friendships along the way. He also loved to travel, creating cherished memories with family and friends through adventures near and far. His strong work ethic, community involvement, love of the outdoors, and devotion to family and friends will be remembered by all who knew him.

He is survived by his wife, Barbara, son, Ryan (Lori Lae) of Marshfield, daughter, Lori Ann of Madison, and sister in law, Margaret Peggy Hunt of Beloit.

He was preceded in death by his parents.

Big thanks to all his friends for all the memories and good times, he'll have a cold one ready for you.

records.name: Gary Anthony Podjaski
records.summary: Gary Anthony Podjaski, age 80, of Weston passed away on Jun 6, 2026.
records.body:
Gary Anthony Podjaski, 80, passed away on Saturday, June 6, 2026. He was born December 4, 1945 at St. Mary’s Hospital in Wausau to Anthony and Katherine (Wroblewski) Podjaski of Hatley.

Gary was raised on the family farm on Bass Lake Road, which he purchased from his mother after his father’s death. He graduated from Wittenberg High School in 1964. He enjoyed snowmobiling and shooting pool.

Gary married Judith “Judy” Ziegel on October 22, 1977 at Saint Florian’s Catholic Church in Hatley. They farmed until 1987 when they moved to Kronenwetter, later building a home in Weston, WI. He retired from J&D Tube Benders in Weston in 2015.

After retirement he struggled with Parkinson’s and Alzheimers, but still enjoyed working outside on his lawn and taking walks to visit with neighbors and anyone he met along the way. He most enjoyed watching his grandchildren play ball.

Gary is survived by his wife Judy; son, Tony (Jennifer) Podjaski, of Marathon; daughter, Tracy (Mark) Fenske, of Tigard, Oregon; grandchildren, Allison and Harvey, of Marathon; sister, Yvonne Shakula, of Hales Corners; brother, Donald Podjaski, of Weston; brother-in-law, Lyle (Patty) Ziegel, of Wausau; sister-in-law, Marie Podjaski, of Ringle; godson, Mark Shakula, of Milwaukee, and several nieces and nephews.

In addition to his parents, he was preceded in death by his in-laws, Harry and Audrey Ziegel, brother, Myron Podjaski and brother-in-law, David Shakula.

A mass of Christian Burial will take place at Saint Florian’s Catholic Church, 500 Church Lane Rd, Hatley, WI, 54440, on Monday, June 15, 2026, at 11:00 am. Before the service there will be a visitation at church from 9:30 am until 11:00 am. Burial will follow services at Saint Florian’s Catholic Cemetery.

We would like to thank the staff of Sylvan Crossing Memory Care in Stevens Point for their care and compassion and to Moments Hospice for his care these last few months. All of you became part of our family and we knew he was in good hands.

records.name: Susan Jean Johnson-Fenhaus
records.summary: Susan Jean Johnson-Fenhaus, age 79, of Wausau passed away on Jun 6, 2026.
records.body:
Susan J. Johnson-Fenhaus, age 79, of Wausau, passed away on Saturday, June 6, 2026, at Colonial Health Services, Colby.

Susan was born on July 24, 1946, in Wausau, to Marvin H. and Edna K. (Kratwell) Plautz. Susan attended Wausau High School, graduating June 6, 1964. Susan was united in marriage on June 15, 1974, to Mark J. Johnson at St. Stephen Lutheran Church, Wausau.

Together Susan and Mark were active parents raising two sons, Drew and Kevin, and happily attended everything from school events to year-round sports. Susan's professional career spanned time at Wausau Insurance, J R Brushert Jewelers, Brokaw Credit Union, and finally with Greenheck, for over 20-years, retiring in 2009.

Susan was a lifelong dog lover, caring endlessly for her two successive sets of Bichon Frise's. In retirement Susan enjoying attending craft sales with friends, collecting jewelry, riding her Harley-Davidson and caring for her beloved dogs. In her later years, Susan married Bill Fenhaus August 11, 2006, sharing a life together until his passing on May 16, 2023.

Susan is survived by her sons, Drew (Amy Downs) of Richmond, California, and Kevin, of Wausau and her cousin, Sandy (Ron) Borth, Scandanavia. She was preceded in death by her husband Mark, and parents Marvin and Edna.

The family would like to thank the staff of Colonial Health Services in Colby, and North Shore Hospice for providing Susan heartfelt care and compassion in her final years and days.

A celebration of life will be held at Helke Funeral Home, 302 Spruce Street, Wausau, WI at 11:00 AM, on Friday, June 26, 2026, with visitation prior, from 10:00 AM until the time of services. The family will have a private burial following services.

In lieu of flowers, the family requests donations be made to your local pet shelter or animal rescue.

records.name: Jerome Fisher
records.summary: Jerome Fisher, age 81, of Schofield passed away on June 7, 2026.
records.body:
Jerome Fisher, 81 years old, died Sunday, June 7, 2026, at his home in Schofield.

He was born on October 5, 1944, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to the late Doris Fisher-Hollman. He married Jeanette M. Kramer, who preceded him in death on April 23, 2025.

He retired from Greenheck, where he worked for nearly 40 years.

Some of his favorite pastimes included watching racing, watching the wildlife on their properties, going up north to the cottage, hunting, fishing, and spending time with friends at Down The Hill bar.

He is preceded in death by his mother, Doris; and his loving wife, Jeanette.

He is survived by his three children, Jeff Fisher, Jenny Fisher (Matt Dunlop), and Julie (Jeff) DeClark; three grandchildren, Marissa (Joe) Okrzesik, Jaden Spatz, and Dominic DeClark; as well as one great-grandchild, Carter Okrzesik.

Private services are being held. Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Robert "Mickey" Grassl
records.summary: Robert "Mickey" Grassl, age 0 passed away on Jun 7, 2026.
records.body:
Robert “Mickey” Grassl, 89 of Mosinee, WI was called to rest June 7th, 2026. He was born in July of 1936 to the late Robert and Lula (Krieg) Grassl.

In his teens Mickey was a valuable member of the Mosinee High School hockey team, Mosinee Papermakers, as well as an exceptional boxer. After joining the Air Force, Mickey and his wife, Nancy (Sparhawk), married in November of 1958. Nancy followed Mickey during his time in the Air Force as an aircraft mechanic; they welcomed their first son while Mickey was stationed in Florida. The couple returned to their roots in Mosinee, where their family continued to grow, raising 6 children (having lost 1 son during infancy). Mickey baptized many of his children at Saint Francis Xavier, where he ushered and attended service with unwavering faith. In his early days as a father, Mickey worked both at the Mosinee Papermill and as a school bus driver, while also remaining active with the Air Force during drill weekends. Mickey began his career at the Mosinee Papermill as a Spare Hand; he later received education and on-site training to become an electrician. As an electrician, he was known for practical innovations and strong work ethics. Mickey dedicated nearly 45 years to the Mosinee Papermill before retiring in 2008. Throughout his life Mickey enjoyed many hobbies including watching football (cheering for the Packers), downhill skiing, and biking. He also enjoyed traveling and made several trips out of state and locally within Wisconsin to visit or support his family.

At home, Mickey’s family knew him to be loving and compassionate. He was soft-spoken, but his actions spoke volumes. Mickey’s family felt his love for them when he meticulously cared for their cars by brushing them free of snow or cleaned their windshields before he hugged them goodbye and said, “watch your step.” At family gatherings, an empty plate in your hand was an opportunity for Mickey to offer you more food and he always had a beer or a cup of coffee to share. Animals and people flocked to Mickey. It was common to find Mickey on the floor among the grandchildren and their toys or surrounded by family pets. He took exceptional pride in buying 40-pounds of bird seed at a time, guiding their family cats and dogs into or out of the house or coaxing frogs and toads out of their driveway to keep them safe.

Mickey’s life was a life of kindness and service. He served his country in the Air Force, his church as an usher, and his family as a son, brother, husband, father, grandfather, great-grandfather, and uncle, for which he will be missed dearly. Mickey is survived by his wife of 67 years, Nancy (Sparhawk); his sister, Joyce Creske; his children, George (Sandy) Grassl, Cathy (Jeff) Walker, Cheryl (Eric) Barszcz, Pam Asmundsen, Heidi Bloomer, Joe (Laura) Grassl, and his niece, Debbie (Doug) Bemowski. He is also survived by many grandchildren, great-grandchildren, nieces and nephews. Mickey was preceded in death by his brother, John “Jack” Grassl, two sisters, Virjean Grzadzielewski, Gloria Dinon, and infant son, Andy Grassl.

Mickey will be honored by brief visitation and service both at Beste Funeral home, Mosinee, on June 12th, 2026. Visitation begins at 10:00am, with service following at 11:00am. The Rev. Aaron Becker will officiate. Guests are welcome to join the family at 12:30pm at the Knowlton Townhall for lunch at the completion of service. Online condolences may be directed to the family at www.bestefh.com

records.name: Scott D. Schafer
records.summary: Scott D. Schafer, age 58, of Wausau passed away on Jun 7, 2026.
records.body:
Scott (Scottie) Dean Schafer, Wausau, peacefully passed away on 6/7/26 surrounded by his loving family after a 13-month courageous battle with Grade 4 Glioblastoma Brain Cancer at the age of 58 under the care of St. Croix Hospice. He was born on January 25, 1968, to the late Lyman Sr. and Doris (Holeton) Schafer on their 21st wedding anniversary, the youngest of 7 boys.

Scott grew up in Antigo, WI with his older brothers doing all the things that boys do. It was often said that his mom was a saint to deal with the Schafer boys. He got into playing music with his older brothers and their friends and spent many years playing the drums in multiple bands, including Water Works which he shared many stories about their “gigs”. Scott also picked up pool and, more so, darts at a young age (his parents would have to go to the bars with him so he could play because he wasn’t of age) and he was a pretty good shot. He went to multiple state dart tournaments and played in leagues for many years meeting (and beating) many friends.

He graduated from Antigo High School in 1986. Scott had plenty of stories about his high school years which usually included driving around in someone’s car, having a beer or two, and just having fun. He cherished the friendships that he made in high school that lasted forever. Immediately following high school, Scott followed in the footsteps of his dad and brothers Jerry, Pete and Dan and joined the military. He proudly served 28 ½ years in the Army National Guard achieving the rank of First Sergeant and the nickname “Scooter”. Scott did 2 tours to Iraq and held multiple jobs during his time in the military including being a tanker, cavalry, operations sergeant, communications, convoy security and detainee operations. Scott created many friendships during his time in the military, some which lasted decades, including meeting his wife, Andrea Fisher, in the middle of the woods at Fort McCoy. They were married in Wausau on December 13, 2008. Retiring in 2014 as the First Sergeant of the Antigo unit was a full circle moment, as that’s where he began his career.

Besides the military, Scott worked for Pomp’s in Antigo for multiple years then as the Scheduling Manager of Apogee in Wausau for over 18 years until his medical retirement in January 2026. He made amazing friendships along the way and was one of the hardest and devoted workers anyone had ever met. Outside of work, Scott enjoyed hunting, fishing, camping, traveling with Andrea, watching, attending, participating in and coaching sports, spending time with family and friends, and curling. He loved being “papa” to his grandkids and playing with them, teaching them so much. Scott was a long-time member of the Wausau Curling Club which he enjoyed very much and always wanted to get back to it if his health would allow him to. Scott and Andrea hosted an interclub bonspiel together for the last 10 years which was always a highlight of his year.

Scott liked to say that he was not a very social guy, often calling himself “anti-social”. He said it so much so that his cousin Steve started a special photo album on his phone of incidents of Scott being “anti-social”. Scott could talk to anyone and make friends at the drop of a hat. Once you got him talking, it was hard to get him to stop; he always had a story to tell. Scott would rarely raise his voice; he would talk and talk which would drive his girls crazy! He loved to joke, make up funny sayings and songs, and started Floral Shirt Fridays at Apogee which (we hope) is still a trend today. His favorite day of the week was Tuesdays which he deemed as “Hump Day Eve”. He would say, “We celebrate all the eves, New Year’s Eve, all hallows eve, heck, we even celebrate Christmas Eve and Santa Clause comes on Christmas Eve so why not have a reason to celebrate every week?”

Scott and Andrea were not only married; they were best friends. They spent many nights staying up until the sun rose just talking or listening to music and Andrea cared for him day in and out for the last year and a half. Andrea’s love and devotion to Scott was unwavering, and likewise. Their message to everyone who finds their person is to keep making memories. Andrea did everything that she could in the last year and a half to make sure Scott and her had as many memories as they could possibly have; a WI Badger football game, a trip to Seattle including a train trip back, a WI Badger Basketball game, a Bucks game, a trip to Florida, and multiple rides in the truck which all included a lot of ice cream stops (vanilla malts, chocolate chip cookie dough or mint chocolate chip). There were many days that lead into nights and sunrises, just having “back deck sitting time” which is what they enjoyed the most.

Scott is survived by his loving wife and best friend, his Andrea, Wausau. His daughters, Jenny (Dustin Drake), Wausau, Deana (Jessey) Richey, Antigo, and Adriana, Madison. His grandchildren, River Drake, Tobias Schafer, Koltin Richey, and Graylin Richey. He is further survived by his in-laws, Howie and Terri Fisher. Five of his older brothers, Ron, Antigo, Jerry (Deb), Lake Charles, LA, Pete, Madisonville, KY, Rick (Shayne), Antigo, Dan (Cindy), Aniwa. Brother-in-laws John Zich, Lancaster, WI and Garrett Peterson, Wausau. His princess puppy girl, Morgan. Very special cousins and friends, Steve and Cassie Cerny, Wausau who have helped Scott and Andrea immensely over this past year along with many nieces, nephews, aunts, uncles, extended family, cousins and friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents, his mother-in-law, Becky Fisher, his oldest brother, Lyman Jr., his niece, Tina Mullins, and nephew, Benny Kerstetter.

Scott’s family would like to thank all of their friends and family that have helped them over the last year and a half. Dr. Platta and Dr. Asquith and the amazing staff at Aspirus Cancer Center. His Neurosurgeon Dr. Dey and his Neuro-Oncologist Dr. Bhatia and the staff at UW Health. NP Ann Wittwer, her nurse Jessica and all the staff at the VA in Wausau and Tomah. Holly, Ashley and the other caring staff at St. Croix Hospice and all the other amazing care givers we have had along the way.

A Celebration of Scott’s life will be held at Noon, Saturday, June 20, 2026 at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau. Visitation will begin at 9:00 a.m. until the time of services all at the Sixth Street funeral home. In lieu of flowers a memorial fund has been established in Scott’s name. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

In the words of Scott “Life is too short to be unhappy, find your happy and make as many memories as possible that you can.”

records.name: Darlene "Dolly" Mary Tanck
records.summary: Darlene "Dolly" Mary Tanck, 72, passed away on June 4, 2026.
records.body:
Darlene "Dolly" Mary Tanck, a beloved mother, grandmother, great-grandmother, and friend, was born on January 21, 1954, in Merrill, Wisconsin, to Geno and Florence Bosi.

Dolly was the proud owner of Dolly's Daycare, where she dedicated herself to caring for children and babies from 1991 until her retirement in 2026. She loved every moment spent with the kids, filling her daycare with laughter and warmth. Beyond her professional life, Dolly was a creative spirit. She enjoyed baking—Saturdays were reserved for this passion, along with cleaning—and made cards for every occasion, bringing joy to her family with her thoughtful creations.

A homebody, Dolly found happiness in gardening, sewing, knitting, and all the "Grandma hobbies" that she so loved. She was happiest during family gatherings, where her homemade wine was always a hit. Dolly had a special fondness for camping trips with her family and was known for her spring and fall cleaning rituals.

Dolly treasured her time with her late husband, Lonnie Tanck, whom she married on April 17, 1971. Their bond was unshakable, and she missed him dearly after his passing on January 21, 2024. Together, they created a loving home filled with warmth and laughter.

Dolly is survived by her daughters, Toni (Cory Wilk) Smith and Brenda (Brian) Burkhardt; her grandchildren, Brittany (Christian) Steinke, Logan (Kaylee) Smith, Vanessa Smith, Matthew Burkhardt, and Elizabeth Burkhardt; great-grandchildren Ava, Marjorie, and River; her mother-in-law, Anna Tanck; and her siblings, Jean Goodell, Nancy Drew, Kathy Iwen, and Karen (Brian) Grund, sisters-in-law, Gail (Terry) Glisch and Glenda (Terry) Modrzejewski, along with many nieces and nephews.

Dolly is preceded in death by her loving husband Lonnie, her parents, her in-laws Al Tanck, Joyce and Alvin Goestch, and her brothers-in-law Francis "Lefty" Goodell, Arthur Drew, and Russell Iwen.

The funeral service will be held at St. Francis Xavier Catholic Church in Merrill on June 12, 2026, at 1:00 PM, with a public visitation taking place at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, 301 Eagle Dr. Merrill, Wisconsin, that same day from 10:00 AM until 12:30 PM.

In honor of Dolly's vibrant spirit and loving nature, her family encourages everyone to remember her by embracing joy in their lives. As she always said, "Be Happy."

records.name: Dave Wanta
records.summary: Dave Wanta, age 86, of Wausau passed away on June 8, 2026.
records.body:
Dave Wanta passed away peacefully, surrounded by family at Mount View Care Center in Wausau, Wisconsin, on June 8, 2026, at the age of 86. He was born in Bevent on May 27, 1940, to the late Anton and Mattie (Cebula) Wanta. He fought a courageous battle with Parkinson's disease for several years.

Dave married Nancy Kurszewski of Bevent in 1961, and they had two sons during their marriage. They divorced in 1988.

He was a mason contractor by occupation and a musician at heart. Music was in Dave's blood, and his first love was the concertina. He was a band member with Norm Dombrowski's band, the Happy Notes of Stevens Point, Wisconsin, as well as the Polka Knights of Mosinee, Wisconsin, and the Polka Stars of Stevens Point, Wisconsin.

Dave loved to golf and also to travel, especially to Las Vegas to see his sons. Bingo and trips to the local casinos were among his favorite pastimes.

He is survived by his significant other, Jennie Awana of Schofield, WI; son, Troy (Ginger) Wanta of Las Vegas, NV; granddaughters, Mandy and Angie Wanta; and great-grandsons, Trentin, Casen, and Cash, all of Las Vegas. He is further survived by his brothers, Jack (Sue) Wanta and Ron Wanta, all of Bevent; goddaughter, Heidi Krause of Green Bay; along with many close friends and relatives.

He was preceded in death by his parents; his son, Todd; sister, Adeline; brother-in-law, Kelly Wanta; sister-in-law, Debbie Wanta ; and nephew, Bryan Wanta.

The family would like to extend our heartfelt gratitude to Dave's significant other, Jennie, for all the love and care she gave to Dave over the past 30 years. Thank you also to the wonderful staff at Mount View Care Center for the wonderful care, love, and support to Dave and the family. You will all be in our hearts forever.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 AM on Friday, July 17, 2026, at St. Ladislaus Catholic Church, Bevent. The Rev. Augustine Bentil will preside. Visitation will be held from 9:00 AM until the time of Mass at the church. Interment will be in the Parish Cemetery, with a meal to follow at the church.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Gary Lee Winn
records.summary: Gary Lee Winn, 81, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on April 30, 2026.
records.body:
Gary Winn had an impressive career in the Army, received several honors for his time in Vietnam, and later went on to continue his occupation as a CPA.

In 1969, Gary married Robyn Parmelee. They were married for 52 wonderful years. They displayed an inspiring example of how to support one another through love and life. Together they pursued a passion of travel and photography. As members of several art groups they participated in many shows & received multiple awards for their work.

Gary and Robyn were both incredibly kind, tender and loving humans. They lived a very content life, enjoyed being with each other and hosting friends and family. Together they raised three girls and instilled their love of travel with a lifetime filled of joyful memories.

Gary had a jovial sense of humor and will be remembered for his 'dad jokes' and ability to make others smile. His engaging personality intrigued people and he loved finding connections with others while sharing stories about his photography.

A Memorial Art Show will be held in Gary's honor, at his home, instead of a traditional funeral, on June 27th 2026. Both Gary and Robyn's art will be on display and offered to those who attend. A donation box will be available for Rennes Health & Rehab Center, Facility, The Good News Project Inc, & Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services.

records.name: Jeanette "Jean" Priebe
records.summary: Jeanette "Jean" Priebe, age 85 passed away on June 8, 2026.
records.body:
Jeanette Florence “Jean” Priebe met Jesus face to face on June 8, 2026, at the age of 85. Jean was born on May 9, 1941, in Parrish, Wisconsin, to the late Lonzie and Lucy Sparks.

Jean married William “Bill” Priebe, on September 17, 1960. He has been waiting for her in heaven since 2017. Jean was also preceded in death by her brother, Arland Sparks.

Jean is survived by her children: Mark (Diane) Priebe, Julie (Gary) Fisher, Katy (Mark) Sweigart, and Christy (Joseph) Fehlen. She also leaves behind her beloved sister and best friend, Nancy Melau. Jean was a proud grandmother to her thirteen grandchildren: Matt (Jessica) Zeratsky, Tara (Tom) Lund, Bill (Tracy) Priebe, Tori (Skye) Fisher-Keough, Olivia (Dylan) Fisher, Zachary (Stephanie) Sweigart, Adria (Michael) Quiroz, Annie (Emilio) Rodriguez, Hope (Paul) Ramseyer, Amelia (Jonathan) Fehlen-Wagenknecht, Elliot (Faith) Fehlen, Samuel (Kenzi) Fehlen, and Ruby Fehlen. She also leaves behind twelve great grandchildren and another on the way.

Jean held a number of jobs over the years, but her favorite role was that of a friend. She loved to swoop in and help wherever she could. She was a warrior for the underdog. She was an active member of Grace Foursquare Church and participated in and led countless Bible studies over the years. Jean loved cooking, reading, and her family. She loved nothing more than having everyone home.

The family wishes to extend their heartfelt gratitude to the residents and staff of Grace Lodge for making Jean's last months truly enjoyable.

A celebration of Jean’s life and meal will take place at the Family Life Center at the Grace Foursquare Church, on Monday, July 6th, 2026 from 12:00 PM to 1:45 PM. All are welcome. You may leave your private condolences for the Priebe family at www,carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Priebe family.

records.name: Maryann Halverson
records.summary: Maryann Halverson, 73, of Summit Lake, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on June 4, 2026.
records.body:
Maryann Halverson was born on March 25, 1953, in Rhinelander, Wisconsin, the daughter of Ervin and Lucille (Neu) Rupple. She was a woman whose life was marked by love, warmth, and an extraordinary spirit that touched all who had the privilege of knowing her.

Maryann was deeply devoted to her family, and her greatest joy resided in her grandchildren. Known for having the biggest heart, she was often described as a true ray of sunshine in the lives of those around her. Her affection extended beyond her human family to her beloved animals; she had a special bond with her horses, donkey, and dogs. An enduring image of Maryann is one of her being barefoot outdoors, embracing the simple, unpretentious pleasures of life. Her kindness and generous nature made her a wonderful person, admired and cherished by friends and family alike.

Maryann is lovingly survived by her husband, Rodney Halverson, her daughter Heather (Ben) Kessler, her son Jason (Kaylyn) Halverson, and her grandchildren: Emma Kessler, Marley Halverson, and Memphis Halverson, who were truly the light of her life. Her family circle also includes her siblings, Ervin (Sandy), Gloria (Don), George, Alvin (Sue), sister-in-law Joanne, Jerry (Terry), and many nieces, nephews, other relatives, and friends. Together, they carry forward the loving legacy Maryann created.

She was preceded in death by her beloved parents and siblings, Ann, Alan, and Judy. Their memories remain cherished and forever part of Maryann's story.

Maryann Halverson's life was a testament to love, kindness, and the simple joys that make a life meaningful. She will be remembered always for her warm spirit and the profound impact she had on all who knew her. Her presence will be deeply missed, but her memory will continue to shine brightly in the hearts of her family and friends.

A celebration of Maryann's life will be held on Saturday, June 20, 2026, from 2:00-5:00 pm at the Upper Pavilion(C) – Veterans Memorial Park (N8375 Park Rd, Deerbrook, WI 54424). Please wear blue, Mom's favorite color, as we honor the life of Maryann.

records.name: Mildred V. Abt
records.summary: Mildred V. Abt, 85, of Ringle, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully at home on June 4, 2026.
records.body:
Mildred V. (nee Thanig) Abt, more commonly known as Midge, was born on November 14, 1940, in the town of Emmet. She was the daughter of the late Justin and Ruth (Nechuta) Thanig. She married the love of her life Ronald J Abt of Ringle on August 21, 1959, and the couple shared 66 wonderful years together. Midge drove school bus in the D.C. Everest Area School District for 52 years. She was also employed with the Marathon County Commission of Aging (MCCA) food service for many years.

Midge's firm foundation and first love was her Lord, rooted from her childhood. One of her greatest joys was baking for family and friends and loving on her grand and great-grandchildren. Her extraordinary loving spirit split over to everyone who knew her, as she took great joy in giving to everyone she met.

Midge is survived by her husband Ron, her children; Lisa (Bob) Glapinski and Ronald (Laura) Abt, her 7 grandchildren, and 14 great-grandchildren. She is also survived by many nieces, nephews, other relatives and many friends. She is preceded in death by her daughter Carlene (Steve) Hohn.

Funeral service will be held at 11:00 AM on Friday, June 12, 2026, at Redeemer Ev. Lutheran Church, 6404 Old Costa Lane, Weston, WI, 54476. Pastor Ryan Cortright will officiate. Visitation will be held from 5:00 – 7:00 PM on Thursday, June 11th at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston, and again on Friday from 10:00 AM until the time of service at the church. Interment will be in Forestville Cemetery.

records.name: Princeton Alexander Ferguson
records.summary: Princeton Alexander Ferguson, age 0, of Weston passed away on June 8, 2026.
records.body:
Princeton Alexander Ferguson was born still on Monday, June 8, 2026, to his mother, Ariana Ferguson, at Marshfield Medical Center - Weston.

These Are My Footprints

Author Unknown

These are my footprints, so perfect and so small.

These tiny footprints never touched the ground at all.

Not one tiny footprint, for now, I have my wings.

These tiny footprints were meant for other things.

You will hear my tiny footprints in the patter of the rain.

Gentle drops like angels' tears, of joy and not from pain.

You will see my tiny footprints in each butterfly’s lazy dance.

I’ll let you know I’m with you if you give me just a chance.
