You will see my tiny footprints in the rustle of the leaves.

I will whisper names into the wind and call each one that grieves.

Most of all, these tiny footprints are found in mommy’s heart;

for even though I’m gone now, we’ll never truly part.

Princeton is loved by his mother, Ariana; grandma, Crystal (Todd Jones) Dalbec; aunt, Jazelle Ferguson; uncle, Treyvon Jones; and great-grandmothers, Bonnie (Dwayne Ricci) Wadinski and Elaine Dalbec. He is preceded in death by his aunt, Lexi Dalbec.

Funeral service will be held at 6:30 PM on Wednesday, June 17, 2026, at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston, WI, 54776. Visitation will be held from 5:00 PM until the time of service at Ascend.

Princeton's family wishes to extend their gratitude to the Birthing Center staff at Marshfield Medical Center - Weston for their special love and care.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Whitney R. Lindwall
records.summary: Whitney R. Lindwall, age 94, of Wausau passed away on Jun 8, 2026.
records.body:
Whitney R. Lindwall, 94, Wausau, passed away on Monday, June 8, 2026 at Mount View Care Center, Wausau, under the care of St. Croix Hospice. He was born on June 5, 1932 in Hinsdale, Illinois, the son of the late Ralph and Nanna (Swanson) Lindwall. He married Beatta “Bea” Allemann on March 17, 1956 at Zion Lutheran Church, Minneapolis.

Whitney was a Medical Specialist in the U.S. Army having completed Medical Field Service School Training at Fort Sam Houston, San Antonio, TX, and achieved the rank of First Lieutenant. He also served as Team Captain of the U.S. Army Pistol Team. After completing his doctorate in Food Science at the University of Minnesota he began his career in food science and product development holding key positions at Schweigert’s, General Mills and International Multifoods. His hobbies and interests were many, and included traveling, home remodeling, landscaping, gardening, collecting antiques, playing Bridge and Scrabble, and extensively studying our family’s genealogy.

Survivors include his wife, Bea, Wausau; one daughter, Karen (Theran) Rusch, Wausau; two grandsons, Matthew (Diana) Rusch, Papillion, NE, and David Rusch, Wausau; two great-grandsons, Finnigan and Liam, three great-granddaughters, Eliany, Diannelys, and Adielys, and his nieces and nephews. Besides his parents, he was preceded in death by one daughter, Laura Lee, and one sister, Carol Holm.

No public services will be held in accordance with Whitney’s wishes. Private burial will take place at a later date at Fort Snelling National Cemetery in Minnesota. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

Donations preferred to Rib Mountain Lutheran Church, 227150 Harrier Ave, Wausau, WI 54401.

records.name: Christine Marie Lambert
records.summary: Christine Marie Lambert, age 65 passed away on June 9, 2026.
records.body:
Christine Marie Lambert, 65, passed away peacefully on June 9, 2026, while under the compassionate care of Compassus Hospice at the House of the Dove.

Christine was born on December 20, 1960, in Indiana to the late Donn and Ginette (Pirot) Rethlake. She grew into a woman known for her kindness, generosity, and unwavering devotion to her family and community.

For many years, Christine served as a bus driver for the Mosinee School District. She truly loved her work and cherished the opportunity to care for the children entrusted to her each day. Ensuring their safety and brightening their mornings and afternoons brought her tremendous joy and fulfillment.

Family was the center of Christine's life. She was a devoted wife, loving mother, and proud grandmother whose greatest happiness came from spending time with those she loved. She had a remarkable gift for making others feel cared for and valued, always putting the needs of others before her own no matter where she was or what she was doing.

Christine was especially passionate about being a grandmother. She treasured every moment spent with her grandsons and eagerly anticipated the arrival of another grandson. As the family's unofficial photographer, she could always be found behind the camera, capturing precious memories at gatherings, celebrations, and everyday moments. Through her photographs, she preserved countless memories that her family will cherish for generations.

On her journey through life, Christine met and married the love of her life, Carl Lambert. The couple was united in marriage in 1990 and spent the next 36 years loving and caring for one another through life's many joys and challenges. Their marriage was a beautiful example of devotion, partnership, and enduring love.

Christine is survived by her beloved husband, Carl; her children, Jared (Tahlia) Lambert and Sarah Lambert; her cherished grandsons, Henry and Harvey; and grandson number three, who is expected to arrive on July 10, 2026. She is further survived by her siblings, Dorothy, Matt (Sharon), and Sherri (Bill); as well as many other relatives and friends who will deeply miss her.

In addition to her parents, Christine was preceded in death by her sister, Mary (Gary); her uncle, Ralph; and her aunt, Josette.

Christine's children wish for her to be remembered for her kind and caring heart, her selflessness, and the love she shared so freely with everyone around her. Her warmth, compassion, and gentle spirit touched countless lives and will remain in the hearts of all who knew her.

A memorial service will be held at 11:00 am on Saturday, June 27, 2026, at Good News Church, 145919 Lowland Lane, Mosinee. Visitation takes place from 9:00 am until the time of services.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family. Words of remembrance and condolences may be shared at MWCS.WS.

records.name: Gerald A. Brzezinski
records.summary: Gerald A. Brzezinski, age 71, of Wausau passed away on Jun 9, 2026.
records.body:
Gerald (Gary) Alan Brzezinski, 71, died June 9, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital, surrounded by his family.

He was born March 2, 1955 in Wausau, son of the late Henry and Dorothy (Dombrowski) Brzezinski.

Gary was a 1973 graduate of Newman High School, Wausau. He began his career as a groundskeeper at the former Employers Insurance in Wausau. Later, he began a 30-year career at Lee Yawkey Woodson Art Museum in Wausau as a groundskeeper.

Gary was a big Green Bay Packers fan and never missed watching or listening to a game. He also enjoyed watching NASCAR races over the years. His love for the outdoors was a great complement to his chosen profession.

Survivors include his siblings: Rev. Hilary Brzezinski, O.F.M., Greenwood, Miss., Norb Brzezinski, Wausau, Deb (Ken) Dorshorst, Mosinee, Jerry (Hollie) Brzezinski, Green Bay and Cindy (Paul) Prior, Lake Elmo, Minn., nieces and nephews: Ryan (Jodi) Dorshorst, Jenna (Dave) Coldwell, Danielle (Eric Lindbergh) Brzezinski, Zachary Brzezinski, Patrick (Mary Teresa) Prior and Molly (Matthew) Freidel; seven great-nephews: Theodore, Arthur and Simon Dorshorst, Hank Coldwell, Beckett Lindberg, Albert Prior and Walter Freidel; two great-nieces: Lucy Freidel and Lidwina Prior; aunts: Bea Dombrowski, Lucille Dallman, Joyce Gagas, and many cousins on the Brzezinski and Dombrowski sides.

Besides his parents, he was preceded in death by his brother, Craig.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 10:30 a.m. on Tuesday, June 16, at St. Michael Catholic Church, 611 Stark St., Wausau, with viewing from 9-10:30 a.m. at the church.  Rev. Allan Slowiak and Deacon Peter Burek will concelebrate.  There will be a visitation on Monday, June 15, from 4-7 p.m. with a rosary at 7 p.m. at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 N. 6th St., Wausau.  Burial will be at St. Michael Catholic Cemetery, Wausau.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Judith Laura Laduron
records.summary: Judith Laura Laduron, age 84 passed away on June 9, 2026.
records.body:
Judith (Judi) Laura Laduron (Manecke)

Judi was called home to be with the Lord on June 9th, 2026 at the age of 84.

Born on April 20, 1942, in Wausau, Wisconsin, Judi was the daughter of the late Vincent and Vitalia Manecke. She graduated from Wausau East High School in 1960 and later relocated to Milwaukee, where she met her future husband and love of her life, Gerard (“Jerry”) Laduron, on a blind date. They were united in marriage on May 26, 1962, in Milwaukee, where they began their life together and started to raise their family.

Judi began her professional career with Federal Pacific Electric, a division of Reliance Electric and in 1983, she and Jerry returned to Wausau, where she found employment at L&S Electric. During this time, they also spent many enjoyable weekends and vacations at their cottage in Crooked Lake, Wisconsin and could often be found “Gone Fishing”.

The cottage eventually became their full-time residence where Judi continued to commute to Wausau, WI or Menominee, MI for work until her retirement in 2004. They enjoyed countless hours with family and dear friends, as well as many memorable days spent fishing. Following retirement, they embraced every opportunity to travel, exploring various states with their fifth wheel and boat, seeking out favorite fishing spots and visiting loved ones along the way.

Jerry preceded her in death on April 6, 2006, just one month shy of their 44th wedding anniversary. In the years that followed, Judi remained deeply engaged in her community, generously dedicating her time to organizations including St. Mary of the Lake Christian Women, the Crooked Lake Volunteer Fire Department Women’s Auxiliary, the Crooked Lake Sportsman’s Club, and the Crooked Lake/Mountain Lions Club.

She also treasured the friendships she built through her involvement with the Mountain Red Hatters, the Mountain Slick Chicks, and the Crooked Lake Ladies card club—connections she greatly missed after returning to Wausau in 2015.

A devoted sports fan, Judi enthusiastically supported the Green Bay Packers and Milwaukee Brewers. Her spirited cheering was well known and could often be heard throughout the neighborhood.

Survivors include Jerry and Judi’s five children: Laura Anderson; Gerard (“Butch”) Laduron Jr.; Debra Smith; Susan (Ray Jansen) Grunwald; and Jeff (Heather) Laduron. She is further survived by eight grandchildren: Michael, Whitney, Scott (Amanda), Amanda (Mitchell), Greg, Jennifer (Aaron), Mellisa, and Nick (Abby); eight great-grandchildren: Michael, Sierra, Mason, Vinnie, Marshall, Mitchell, Chevelle, and Bella; and one great-great-grandchild, Jasper.

Judi is also survived by her Brother and Sister in-laws Vern and Geri Laduron, Mary Graesing, Karen Volkman, and Darlene Manecke; as well as numerous nieces and nephews, including Christopher (Mary) Graesing, James Laduron, Ken (Cheri) Laduron, Steve (Michelle) Laduron, Dean (Julia) Volkman, Kristy Marienthal, Carie (Jeff) Hoeser, Dawn (Ricky) Snyder, Kelley Niederhauser, Dave (Sara) Manecke, Dawn (Rob) Simpson, Ann (Adam) Victor and many dear friend and cousins that she cared about deeply.

She was preceded in death by her parents, Vincent and Vitalia Manecke; her husband Jerry’s parents, Harold and Alice Griebeler; her brother, David Manecke; grandchildren Timothy Gendron, Matthew Gendron, Jessica Smith, and Vincent Laduron; great-grandson Kolton Lemma; brothers-in-law Wayne Graesing, Jerome Volkman, and Fred Winters; sister-in-law Georgia Winters; nephews Robert Graesing and Ty Marienthal; and niece Denise Mascari.

A Mass of Christian burial will take place at 11:30 AM on Friday, June 19, 2026, at St. Marks Catholic Parish, 602 Military Road, Rothschild, WI. Visitation will take place from 9:30 until the time of services at the church. Burial will take place at 1:00 PM on Saturday, July 11, 2026, at Mountain Cemetery, 14097 Silver Hill Road, Mountain, WI 54149.

The family extends their sincere gratitude to Dr. Christopher Peterson, Oncology Specialist at Aspirus Cancer Center, as well as Haley Breaker, Lacie Beltran and the entire Aspirus Hospice nursing team, for their compassion, dedication, and exceptional care during this difficult time. Your support and kindness brought great comfort to our mother and to all of us, and it is deeply appreciated.

Judi has peacefully departed to be reunited with Jerry, together “gone fishing” once again.

John J Buettgen Funeral Home is assisting the family. Family and friends may leave words of remembrance at www.honorone.com

records.name: Matthew William Reif
records.summary: Matthew William Reif, age 54, of Verona, WI passed away on Jun 9, 2026.
records.body:
Matthew William Reif, age 54, of Verona, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly on Tuesday, June 9, 2026. He was born on January 16, 1972, in Tarrytown, New York, to Bill and Mary Nell Reif. Most of his growing up years were spent in Wausau but included many visits to northern Minnesota to visit grandparents and Reif, Randall, and Hoolihan cousins.

A 1990 graduate of Wausau East High School, Matt went on to receive a Bachelor of Arts degree from Hamline University in St. Paul, Minnesota. He was captivated by music at an early age. He loved to sing and knew all the lyrics from his childhood favorite "Bad Bad LeRoy Brown" to all the music he later came to love. Matt was a talented pianist and spent countless hours at his many keyboards performing and sharing his passion with others. Over the years he played in multiple bands in college, and in Phoenix, Wausau, and Madison. Lately, he was devoting time to composing original music as well as recording his own versions of music by his favorite musicians.

Matt and Holly Schmidt were married in June of 2006, and their family grew to include three boys, Myles, Evan, and Nolan. Matt and Holly lived in Madison while Matt worked for Music Notes and Holly taught Music at the Wingra School. They returned to Wausau in 2013, and Matt worked for Wausau Coated Products for two years. Subsequently, the family moved to Madison where Matt took on the role of a stay-at-home dad and performed with a local Madison band.

More than anything else, Matt cherished being a father to Myles, Evan, and Nolan and was recently excited about introducing them to the sports he loved as a child. He was deeply committed to supporting, encouraging, and loving his three boys and treasured every moment he spent with them.

Matt was preceded in death by his maternal grandparents, Alice and Jerry Hoolihan, and his paternal grandparents, Jane and Myles Reif. He is survived by his wife and the mother of his three children, Holly Reif of Verona, Wisconsin; his sons, Myles, Evan, and Nolan, his parents, Bill and Mary Nell Reif, his brothers, Ben Reif (Amy), Will Reif (Laura), and nieces and nephews, Patrick, Benjamin, Allie, and Christopher Reif and Shea, Ryan, and Billie Reif, all of Wausau., as well as beloved cousins, aunts and uncles, many extended family members, dear friends, fellow musicians, and all whose lives were touched by knowing him.

Matt was kind, thoughtful, sensitive, empathetic, and creative, traits which were evident from the start. Most importantly he will be remembered for his devotion to family and particularly his three boys.

Visitation will be from 4pm to 5pm at the Church of the Resurrection in Wausau on Friday, July 10. The service will begin at 5p m followed by a reception at the Wausau Club. All are welcome.

Memorials may be directed to a charity of your choice or to the Community Foundation of North Central Wisconsin for the "Matt Reif Music Scholarship" for students of Wausau East, Wausau West, and Newman High School. The scholarship will also support younger musicians who need help attending the Wausau Conservatory of Music.

.

records.name: Ojars Victor Rekis
records.summary: Ojars Victor Rekis, age 82, of Town of Rock passed away on Jun 9, 2026.
records.body:
In Loving Memory of Ojars Rekis 1944 – 2026

With profound sadness and deep gratitude for a life well lived, we announce the passing of Ojars Rekis, a devoted husband, father, grandfather, great-grandfather, farmer, businessman, and faithful Christian whose legacy will continue to live on through the many lives he touched.

Ojars was born in Latvia in 1944 to Konstancia (Velkme) Rekis and Oskars Rekis. His earliest years were marked by the hardships of World War II. His father, Oskars, served in the Latvian military and tragically lost his life near a concentration camp in Russia . During these difficult circumstances, Ojars, along with his mother and grandfather, escaped Latvia and spent approximately four and a half years in a refugee camp in Germany.

Through perseverance and hope, the family was eventually sponsored and immigrated to the United States, where they began a new chapter of life on a farm, with a widower Carl and his children, in Minnesota.

After his mother remarried to Carl Smith, the family settled in the Marshfield, Wisconsin area. Ojars then attended the University of Wisconsin–Stevens Point, where he furthered his education before embracing the farming lifestyle that would become his life’s work.

Following the guidance of his stepfather, Ojars ventured into farming and quickly established himself as an innovative and hardworking agricultural leader.

After marrying the love of his life Marlene(Casperson) in 1969 he built not only a successful farming operation but also a remarkable life. Together they shared 57 years of marriage, a partnership rooted in faith, love, dedication, and unwavering commitment to family.

Their farming operation flourished through years of hard work, growing to include registered cattle, approximately one hundred head of milking livestock, and many acres of cropland. Ojars and Marlene’s dedication to agriculture earned them recognition, including the prestigious Outstanding Young Farmer of the Year Award. Yet, despite the accomplishments and accolades, Ojars greatest pride was never found in the farm itself—it was found in the family they built together.

Ojars was the proud father of six children: Cortney (Jerry Dieringer), Angela (Bruce Shearer), Ryan, Brandon (Heidi Engen), Kristin, and Craig. He was also blessed with a growing family of grandchildren whom he loved dearly: Victoria (Josh Grorich), Marissa(James Newkirk) Miranda (Brayden DeVries), Rhya, Cierra, Harper, Bob, Jackson, Grayson, Samson, Taggart, and Dagny.

His joy multiplied with the arrival of his great-grandchildren: Terrah, Everlyn, Emmett, Killian, and a precious baby Newkirk on the way.

The Rekis family farm was more than a business—it was a way of life. Together, the family raised cattle, tended crops, cultivated gardens, and built a close-knit family rooted in Christian values. Their home was filled with laughter, hard work, and simple joys. Whether organizing kickball games, friendly races across the yard, hosting family parties, or gathering around the dinner table, Ojars cherished every opportunity to bring his family together.

Ojars loved farming and business. He enjoyed managing operations. He took great pride in teaching his children the value of hard work, entrepreneurship, responsibility, and perseverance. He believed in creating opportunities. This entrepreneurial spirit is what led to the transition from cows to cars launching his next chapter as the founder of Tiger Motors. He proudly operated for over 30 years.

But perhaps what people will remember most about Ojars was his ability to make others smile.

No interaction was too small. Whether speaking with a cashier, a banker, a server, or a stranger he had just met, Ojars looked for ways to brighten someone’s day. A joke, a story, a kind word, or a playful comment—he delighted in making people laugh and feel valued.

Of all the people he loved, none held a more special place in his heart than his wife, Marlene. He spoke often of her beauty, her strength, her love for Christ, and the wonderful mother she was to their children. Their marriage was a shining example of devotion, partnership, and enduring love.

Ojars also held a deep love for his mother and remained devoted to her throughout her life, spending time with her regularly and honoring the sacrifices she made for their family.

Above all else, Ojars was a man of faith. A devoted Christian, he believed that God was the foundation of everything worthwhile in life. He attended church faithfully every Sunday at Bluffview Church and truly valued the relationships he made there. He instilled those values in his children and grandchildren, the importance of faith and family . He believed the greatest purpose in life was to love God and love people. One shining quality Ojars had was his ability to bring God into conversations with whoever he felt needed it. He touched so many lives by showing and sharing his love of Christ.

Ojars lived a life defined by resilience, generosity, humor, faith, and unconditional love. His legacy is found not only in the farm he built, but in the family he nurtured, the values he taught, and the countless lives he impacted with his kindness and encouragement.

Though he will be deeply missed, his family finds comfort in knowing that his faith has led him home and that his love, wisdom, and example will continue to guide future generations.

“Well done, good and faithful servant.”

Ojars memory will forever remain in the hearts of those who knew and loved him. May his legacy of faith, family, hard work, and joy continue to inspire all who were blessed to share in his life. ❤️

The wake will be at Rembs Funeral Home in Marshfield on June 23 from 4-8pm

Visitation will continue on June 24 starting at 10am at Bluff View Church in Arpin. Followed by a celebration of life service at 11am and a luncheon to follow at the church.

records.name: Robert "Bob" E. Pfaff
records.summary: Robert "Bob" E. Pfaff, age 80 passed away on Jun 9, 2026.
records.body:
Bob E. Pfaff, 80, Wausau, passed away on Tuesday, June 9, 2026. He was a caring and devoted husband, a loving father, and a treasured grandfather whose absence will be felt by all who knew him.

Bob was born on August 11, 1945, in Medford, Wisconsin, to Arthur and Edna Pfaff. On August 16, 1975, he married Barbara Ovel and together they had two daughters, Cindy and Laura. He is further survived by his granddaughter, Emma; his sister, Lucille Miller; four sisters-in-law, Pat Wunsch, Kathy Johnson, Joan Pfaff, and Ann Ovel; and numerous nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents; his brother, John Pfaff; and three brothers-in-law, Leonard Miller, Dan Ovel, and Jack Wunsch.

Bob was a veteran of the United States Army and served during the Vietnam War. He worked as a heavy equipment mechanic for various construction companies throughout his 41-year career, retiring at the age of 62.

Bob was an avid fisherman who enjoyed fishing, especially on the Great Lakes. He was also an accomplished hunter, enjoying both bow and rifle hunting. He liked playing pool, listening to music, attending live shows and concerts, and watching stock car races. He had a passion for anything with an engine, especially cars, tractors, and construction equipment. He also enjoyed camping, road tripping throughout the country, and trying his luck "pulling levers" at the casino.

Bob was a kind and generous man who was always willing to offer his help and share his knowledge when needed. Larger than life, he had a warm and welcoming nature that made everyone feel comfortable. He was an amazing person whose kindness, humor, and presence left a lasting impact on all who knew him.

A celebration of life will be held beginning at 1:00 p.m. on Sunday, July 26, 2026, at Big Eau Pleine County Park, Enclosed Shelter #19 (main shelter near the beach), 135695 Eau Pleine Park Road, Mosinee. Please join the family for lunch and a time together to remember and celebrate Bob. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Barbara A. Binder
records.summary: Barbara A. Binder, age 81, of Marshfield passed away on Jun 10, 2026.
records.body:
Barbara A. Binder, 81, formerly of Marshfield, passed away peacefully on Wednesday, June 10, 2026 at Benedictine Living Community in Northfield, MN.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 am on Saturday, June 20, 2026 at Corpus Christi Catholic Church, Bakerville where a gathering of family and friends will be from 9:30 am until the time of service. Father Nathan and Deacon Ray Draeger will officiate. Burial will take place in the parish cemetery. Rembs Funeral Home is assisting the family.

Barb was born on March 23, 1945 in Wausau to Frederick and Martha (Beran) Blanke. She was united in marriage to Donald A. Binder on December 27, 1969 at St. John’s Catholic Church in Marshfield.

Barb enjoyed gardening, where she lovingly tended to her plants and flowers, and took pride in crafting homemade wine to share with family and friends. She had a talent for quilting and sewing, often creating thoughtful, handmade items that will be cherished for years to come. Baking was another passion, filling her home with warmth and delicious treats. She also enjoyed fishing and treasured the peaceful moments it brought. Above all, she valued time spent with her family, grandchildren and great grandchildren, creating lasting memories through her love, guidance, and care.

She is survived by her daughters, LeAnne (Thomas) Murphy of Clear Lake, MN, Jolene (Chris) Chapp of Northfield, MN, and Melanie (John) Gawronski of Oconomowoc, son, Mark (Maureen) Reinhardt of Neillsville, 10 grandchildren, Gavin, Keegan and Nolan Murphy, Ceci and Sam Chapp, London Gawronski, and Nicole, Lauren, Ethan, and Luke Reinhardt, and 7 great grandchildren.

She is further survived by her sister, Janice Blanke of Quebec, Canada.

She was preceded in death by her parents, son, Keith, husband, Don, and sister, Jean Holland of Plover.

Memorials will be designated at a later date.

records.name: Beverly M. Koss
records.summary: Beverly M. Koss, age 94, passed away on June 1, 2026.
records.body:
After 94 remarkable years of life, Bev closed her eyes for the final time on June 1, 2026 after a tough 10+ years of Dementia. Beverly was born in Chippewa Falls, WI on December 8, 1931 to Lawrence and Theresa (Zwiefelhofer) Revor. Bev married Duane Rugg of Chippewa Falls,WI Jan 1952, in San Juan, Puerto Rico. She then married Jim Koss of Mosinee, WI April 1989 in Schofield, WI.

Having witnessed nearly a century of history, her greatest blessings were seeing her children and grandchildren begin their own journeys. Bev lived a full and adventurous life, retiring from K Mart after 20+ years. Her hobbies included: sewing for her kids and grandkids, crocheting afghans, cooking, and traveling the world. You could not put anything past her, her photographic memory was amazing and her spiciness for life was unforgettable.

She was preceded in death by both parents: Lawrence and Theresa Revor, her husband's: Duane in 1986 and Jim in 2012, sisters: Dorothy Geissler, Betty Lokranz, Darlene Bowe, and very special sister in law: Beaty Wheeler.

She is survived by a sister: Joanne Shilha, Children: Debbie- San Francisco, CA, Jerry,- Marlborough, MA, Sue- Weston WI and Steve (Pam)- Bellevue, NE; Step-Children Sue (Ken) Smith- Jacksonville, FL, Linda (Lee) Garvey- Freedom, WI, Mike (Sara) Koss- Wausau, WI, and Karrie McGreevey- Marshfield, WI. Grandchildren: Theresa (Todd), Sonja (Josip), Marisa (Rob), Stephanie (Tj), Christopher, Ilse, and Caitlynn, Step-Grandchildren: Gina (Jason), Nate (Carla), Katie (Mike), Nick (Blair), Jennifer (Chris), Jeff (Jennifer), Andrew, Catherine (Jake), Aaron, and 11 Great Grandchildren, and 10 Step-Great Grandchildren.

In honor of her passion for life there will be "One Heck of a Party!"

To celebrate her life Saturday August 29, 2026

Contact the family for time and location or email at Honorbmk@gmail.com.

Our family would like to extend a heartfelt thank you to her caregivers, nurses, and social workers at Renaissance and Rennes. Your kindness and patience brought so much comfort to Mom. Special thank you to her Hospice team, Haley, Carrie, and Will

records.name: Davis  Milton Prei
records.summary: Davis  Milton Prei, age 84, of Rhinelander passed away on June 10, 2026.
records.body:
Davis M Prei, age 84, of Rhinelander, died on June 10, 2026 at the Rennes Health and Rehab Center. He was born on June 19, 1941 in Wausau, Wisconsin the son of Milton and Juanita (Simon) Prei. He attended schools in Wausau, graduating from the Wausau East High School and then attended MTC.

Davis moved to Rhinelander where he was employed by K Mart, then Nehls Webster Well Drilling and then Rhinelander Well Drilling. His greatest joy in life came from the time he spent on the Rainbow Flowage with family and friends. He loved to fish on the flowage.

Davis is survived by his sister Kathleen Prei brother Gene Prei and his nieces. He was preceded in death by his parents and his brother William Prei.

Private family services will be held for Davis. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Prei family.

records.name: Ellanora Louise Bessey
records.summary: Ellanora Louise Bessey, age 99 passed away on Jun 10, 2026.
records.body:
Ellanora L. Bessey, age 99, of Merrill, passed away Wednesday, June 10, 2026.

Ellanora was born April 23, 1927, in the Town of Corning, daughter of Henry and Martha (Voigt) Kienbaum. She married John E. “Jack” Bessey on May 22, 1948, at St. Stephens United Church of Christ, Merrill. He preceded her in death on February 8, 1991, while residing in Texas.

After their marriage, Ellanora and Jack lived in Wisconsin Rapids for a short time before moving to California. They lived in California for 25 years and then moved to New Mexico where they lived for six years. In 1982, they moved to Texas. Ellanora resided in Texas until April of 2014, when she moved to Merrill to be closer to her siblings.

Ellanora worked as a cook at various restaurants, and while in New Mexico, she leased a gourmet crepe restaurant. Ellanora worked in Alaska at Denali National Park for nine summers. Most of her work was done at the gift shop at the park. The last summer Ellanora spent in Alaska was 2008.

Ellanora enjoyed cooking and baking, especially birthday cakes for family members. She also enjoyed sewing and knitting and taught knitting class in California. Ellanora volunteered at a hospital in Longview, Texas and was a 50+ year member of the Eastern Star Masonic Lodge in California.

Survivors include her son, David Bessey of Woodville, Texas; grandson, Kevin (Kendra) Bessey of The Woodlands, Texas; brother, Gordon Kienbaum of Birchwood, Wisconsin; four great-grandchildren, Clara, Cayman, Coralee, and Chance; two great-great-grandchildren, Wrenlee and Everleigh; step-grandson, Quentin (Stephanie) Trautner of Winnsboro, Texas; step-great-grandchildren, Laney, Kason, Kinley, and Addison; daughter-in-law, Virginia Bessey of Longview, Texas, and several nieces and nephews.

Ellanora was preceded in death by her husband; parents; daughter, Tamara Dillard; granddaughter Brandy Baldridge; step-grandson, Robert Coleman; sisters, Rosemary (Art) Reichelt and Dorothy (Edwin) Bahlow; brother, Henry (Eldina) Kienbaum; sister-in-law, Louise Kienbaum.

Visitation will be held at St. Stephens United Church of Christ in Merrill, on Saturday, June 27, 2026, from 9 am to 11 am, Funeral service to follow at 11 am.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Jerome Fisher
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Jerome Fisher, age 81, of Schofield, died on Sunday, June 7, 2026.
records.body:
Jerome Fisher, 81 years old, died Sunday, June 7, 2026, at his home in Schofield.

He was born on October 5, 1944, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to the late Doris Fisher-Hollman. He married Jeanette M. Kramer, who preceded him in death on April 23, 2025.

He retired from Greenheck, where he worked for nearly 40 years.

Some of his favorite pastimes included watching racing, watching the wildlife on their properties, going up north to the cottage, hunting, fishing, and spending time with friends at Down The Hill bar.

He is preceded in death by his mother, Doris; and his loving wife, Jeanette.

He is survived by his three children, Jeff Fisher, Jenny Fisher (Matt Dunlop), and Julie (Jeff) DeClark; three grandchildren, Marissa (Joe) Okrzesik, Jaden Spatz, and Dominic DeClark; as well as one great-grandchild, Carter Okrzesik.

Private services are being held. Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Joan E. Jarosz
records.funeral_home: Brainard Funeral Home & Cremation Center of Weston
records.summary: Joan E. Jarosz, age 66, of Rothschild, Wisconsin, passed away on Friday, June 5, 2026.
records.body:
Joan Ellen Jarosz, age 66, of Rothschild, WI, passed away on Friday, June 5, 2026. She was born to Harry and Judith Jarosz on August 31, 1959.

Joan loved to create masterpieces with her hands, especially crocheting and sewing. She was deeply involved in giving back to her community and helping others through things like Bible Quizzing and the Get Ahead program. She was a champion of others, loving them into their greatest potential.

Joan is survived by her brother Ken (Carolyn) Jarosz of Equality, Alabama; sister Amy (Richard) Emmert, of Mason, Ohio; Nieces and Nephews: Aaron Jarosz, Emily (Chris) May, Andrea (Jason) Harris, Krista Emmert, and Jade Emmert.

She was preceded in death by: her parents, Harry and Judith Jarosz.

The family would like to thank Sue Krcma and members of the Lighthouse Pentecostal Church, Marge and Bill Tice, and Linda Emens for loving her so much.

Services will be held at the Lighthouse Pentecostal Church, 1525 Townline Rd, Wausau, WI 54403, on Saturday, June 13, at 10:00 a.m. Pastor Ron Schuenke will officiate. Burial will be at Mosinee Cemetery immediately following the service.

records.name: Judith L. Laduron
records.summary: Judith L. Laduron, age 84, was called home to be with the Lord on June 9, 2026.

Judi was called home to be with the Lord on June 9th, 2026 at the age of 84.

Judi is also survived by her Brother and Sister in-laws Vern and Geri Laduron, Mary Graesing, Karen Volkman, and Darlene Manecke; as well as numerous nieces and nephews, including Christopher (Mary) Graesing, James Laduron, Ken (Cheri) Laduron, Steve (Michelle) Laduron, Dean (Julia) Volkman, Kristy Marienthal, Carie (Jeff) Hoeser, Dawn (Ricky) Snyder, Kelley Niederhauser, Dave (Sara) Manecke, Dawn (Rob) Simpson, Ann (Adam) Victor and many dear friend and cousins that she cared about deeply.

She was preceded in death by her parents, Vincent and Vitalia Manecke; her husband Jerry's parents, Harold and Alice Griebeler; her brother, David Manecke; grandchildren Timothy Gendron, Matthew Gendron, Jessica Smith, and Vincent Laduron; great-grandson Kolton Lemma; brothers-in-law Wayne Graesing, Jerome Volkman, and Fred Winters; sister-in-law Georgia Winters; nephews Robert Graesing and Ty Marienthal; and niece Denise Mascari.

The family extends their sincere gratitude to Dr. Christopher Peterson, Oncology Specialist at Aspirus Cancer Center, as well as Haley Breaker, Lacie Beltran and the entire Aspirus Hospice nursing team, for their compassion, dedication, and exceptional care during this difficult time. Your support and kindness brought great comfort to our mother and to all of us, and it is deeply appreciated.

Judi has peacefully departed to be reunited with Jerry, together "gone fishing" once again.

records.name: Karl Schreiner
records.summary: Karl Schreiner, age 68, of Athens, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on June 4, 2026.
records.body:
Karl Schreiner, 68, of Athens Wisconsin passed peacefully from his home to his eternal rest on June 4th.

A humble and kind man Karl will be fondly remembered by all who knew him. He spent 35 years, supporting farmers throughout the area with their needs and caring deeply about their families. Many people will recall his passion for raising whitetails. Deep kinship was forged with many Deer friends across four decades. Spring fawns, peanut treats, and antler watch hours were quality time. AI and ship outs were adventurous and boisterous moments for which he was grateful to kindred spirits who showed up every year

Karl was a quiet problem solver always willing to help anyone no matter his personal cost, a man you could count on who didn't expect anything in return. Karl's extensive family will miss his steady presence, gentle eyes, and loving heart. He desired peace rather than strife and always chose his actions with that intent. Karl cherished sharing enjoyment of the simple things in life. A clear crisp morning in the forest, listening intently to catch the first glimpse of a magnificent buck strolling through. The twitch of the line as a fat trout left the stream to join him for breakfast. The joy of a well tended food garden nourishing both body and spirit. A good hound's companionship. A rock, solid and timeless, with stories untold, quiet yet beautiful…

Date and times of funeral services to be announced.

records.name: Larry J. Wild
records.summary: Larry J. Wild, age 80, of Athens, passed away on Tuesday, June 2, 2026.
records.body:
Larry J. Wild, 80, of Athens, passed away on Tuesday, June 2, 2026, at home.

He was born in southern Wisconsin in the town of York on December 19, 1945, to the late Henry and Grace (Krull) Wild. Larry was united in marriage to Susan McCarron on December 16, 1978, in Medford. She preceded him in death in 2004.

For many years Larry was an over-the-road truck driver and delivered fruit and vegetables across the United States. Over the years he drove for E.L. Murphy and Northstar Transport. Trucking was Larry's life and when he was home he enjoyed spending time with his friends and family.

Survivors include his sister, Dolly Bartz; many nieces and nephews; and his good friends, Florence (Randy) Volzka; C.J.; and their grandchildren, Ezrie and Emrick.

He was preceded in death by his wife, Susan; parents, Henry and Grace; siblings, Henry, Babe, David D., David A., Richard, and Jean; and stepson, Scott.

Private graveside service and burial will be at Holy Rosary Cemetery, Medford. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

A special thank you to Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services and to the Athens Fire Department for the excellent care Larry received.

records.name: Marlene D. Heinz
records.summary: Marlene D. Heinz, age 83, of Wausau passed away on Jun 10, 2026.
records.body:
Marlene D. Heinz, 83, Wausau, passed away on Wednesday, June 10, 2026 at her home.

She was born October 2, 1942 in Wausau, daughter of the late Aloysius and Anna (Lang) Schilling. On July 7, 1962, she married the love of her life, Milton “Smokey” Heinz in Wausau. He preceded her in death on June 23, 2025.

Marlene’s greatest passion was gardening. She loved tending to her flowers both indoors and outdoors. She liked knitting, crocheting and hosting family gatherings for all holidays, birthdays and family reunions. Marlene was a longtime member of Holy Name Catholic Church and enjoyed donating items for several church events. Her faith was strong and she was always by her husband, Smokey’s side.

Survivors include two special nieces, Lisa (Brian) Buchberger and Chris Wintersteen; and many nieces, nephews, relatives and friends.

Besides her parents and husband, Smokey, she was preceded in death by six siblings, George Schilling, Armella Bendele, Orville Schilling, Evelyn Winter, twin sister-Darlene Kapitz and Gary Schilling.

Funeral services will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Thursday, June 18, 2026 at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau. Rev. Woodrow Pace will officiate. Burial will be in Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau. Visitation will be on Thursday from 9:00 a.m. until time of service at the funeral home.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Richard "Dick" James Austin
records.summary: Richard "Dick" James Austin, age 89 passed away on Jun 10, 2026.
records.body:
Richard (Dick) J. Austin, 89, of Rib Mountain, passed away June 10, 2026 at Aspirus Hospice House. Dick was born August 11, 1936 in Sturgeon Bay, WI and moved to Wausau in 1956 to attend Wausau Vocational School. Dick's career spanned over 60 years in various roles, working primarily for the Schuette companies in Wausau. He married Sandra (Sandy) in 1960 and their son Jeffrey was born in 1961.

Dick was an avid golfer and fan of the Packers and Brewers. He also enjoyed a good Friday fish fry and morning coffee with the guys at The Red Apple and Mosinee Cafe. He and Sandy were longtime supporters of the Humane Society of Marathon County. As a memorial to his late wife, Dick provided the vision and drive that resulted in the creation of Sandy's Bark Park in Rib Mountain.

He is survived by his son, Jeff (Jennifer) Austin of Minocqua; his sister, Jayne (Mark) Baumann; nieces and nephews, Jon (Dawn) Marsh, Julie (Randy) LeMieux, all from Sturgeon Bay and Jacob Baumann of Plover. He is also survived by friend Mary Cole of Rib Mountain and many great nieces, nephews and cousins. He was preceded in death by his wife, Sandy; brother, John; and parents, Walter and Lucille.

In keeping with Dick's wishes, there will be no funeral service. In lieu of flowers, please consider a donation in his name to the Humane Society of Marathon County or Aspirus Hospice House where he received the best of care.

records.name: Ronald D. Sutheimer
records.summary: Ronald D. Sutheimer, age 92, died on Thursday, June 4, 2026.
records.body:
Ronald D. Sutheimer, 92, died Thursday, June 4, 2026, at Willowbrook Senior Living in Stevens Point, WI.

Ronald was born August 8, 1933, to the late Walter and Loretta (Persike) Sutheimer, in the town of Lanark, WI. He graduated from high school in Amherst, WI, in May of 1951.

He married June (Damrau) Sutheimer on January 17, 1952. He divorced in 1982. He remarried Ethelyn (Bull) Sutheimer on April 30, 1994.

Ronald was preceded in death by his parents, siblings Maynard Sutheimer, Arleigh (Mildred) Sutheimer, grandsons Keith Sutheimer, Travis Sutheimer, Michael Higgins, Nathanial Bull, son Christopher Bull and great grandson Nicholas Benz.

Ron is survived by his wife Ethelyn (Bull) Sutheimer, children Dwynne (Ann) Sutheimer, Gene Sutheimer, Dale Sutheimer, Patricia (Patsy) Sutheimer, Vicki (Dale) Higgins, Penny Sutheimer, Sadie (Larry) Dassow, 15 grandchildren, 38 great grandchildren, and 9 great, great grandchildren.

Ronald worked at Nelsonville Creamery and Pavelski Potato routes with June. He then got into the Soo Line Railroad as an engineer until they sold out. After retiring from the railroad, he went to work for his son, Dwynne Sutheimer Trucking as a dispatcher and driver. He then drove truck for Wysocki Potato Farm.

Ronald's interests and hobbies were spending time with family and friends, hunting, fishing, camping, traveling, hosting family reunions and working on projects in his garage. He also enjoyed going to Spooner with Lyn to see her parents Gladys and Bill Fritz.

Ronald was in the Waupaca Motorcycle Club, the Lions Club, the Elks Club, a Jr. Warden at Intersession and St. Olaf's, volunteered as a driver at the Lincoln Center, volunteered with a youth fishing group, and went with a mission group with Lyn after hurricane Katrina.

Services for Ronald will be held on Saturday, June 20, 2026, at St. John the Evangelist Episcopal Church, 320 Oak St., Wisconsin Rapids, WI 54494. Visitation will be from 10-11am, with the service starting at 11am, with Father David Klutterman officiating.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family. Words of remembrance may be left at MWCS.WS

records.name: Scott D. Schafer
records.summary: Scott D. Schafer, age 58, of Wausau, passed away on June 7, 2026.
records.body:
Scott (Scottie) Dean Schafer, Wausau, peacefully passed away on 6/7/26 surrounded by his loving family after a 13-month courageous battle with Grade 4 Glioblastoma Brain Cancer at the age of 58 under the care of St. Croix Hospice. He was born on January 25, 1968, to the late Lyman Sr. and Doris (Holeton) Schafer on their 21st wedding anniversary, the youngest of 7 boys.

Scott grew up in Antigo, WI with his older brothers doing all the things that boys do. It was often said that his mom was a saint to deal with the Schafer boys. He got into playing music with his older brothers and their friends and spent many years playing the drums in multiple bands, including Water Works which he shared many stories about their "gigs". Scott also picked up pool and, more so, darts at a young age (his parents would have to go to the bars with him so he could play because he wasn't of age) and he was a pretty good shot. He went to multiple state dart tournaments and played in leagues for many years meeting (and beating) many friends.

He graduated from Antigo High School in 1986. Scott had plenty of stories about his high school years which usually included driving around in someone's car, having a beer or two, and just having fun. He cherished the friendships that he made in high school that lasted forever. Immediately following high school, Scott followed in the footsteps of his dad and brothers Jerry, Pete and Dan and joined the military. He proudly served 28 ½ years in the Army National Guard achieving the rank of First Sergeant and the nickname "Scooter". Scott did 2 tours to Iraq and held multiple jobs during his time in the military including being a tanker, cavalry, operations sergeant, communications, convoy security and detainee operations. Scott created many friendships during his time in the military, some which lasted decades, including meeting his wife, Andrea Fisher, in the middle of the woods at Fort McCoy. They were married in Wausau on December 13, 2008. Retiring in 2014 as the First Sergeant of the Antigo unit was a full circle moment, as that's where he began his career.

Besides the military, Scott worked for Pomp's in Antigo for multiple years then as the Scheduling Manager of Apogee in Wausau for over 18 years until his medical retirement in January 2026. He made amazing friendships along the way and was one of the hardest and devoted workers anyone had ever met. Outside of work, Scott enjoyed hunting, fishing, camping, traveling with Andrea, watching, attending, participating in and coaching sports, spending time with family and friends, and curling. He loved being "papa" to his grandkids and playing with them, teaching them so much. Scott was a long-time member of the Wausau Curling Club which he enjoyed very much and always wanted to get back to it if his health would allow him to. Scott and Andrea hosted an interclub bonspiel together for the last 10 years which was always a highlight of his year.

Scott liked to say that he was not a very social guy, often calling himself "anti-social". He said it so much so that his cousin Steve started a special photo album on his phone of incidents of Scott being "anti-social". Scott could talk to anyone and make friends at the drop of a hat. Once you got him talking, it was hard to get him to stop; he always had a story to tell. Scott would rarely raise his voice; he would talk and talk which would drive his girls crazy! He loved to joke, make up funny sayings and songs, and started Floral Shirt Fridays at Apogee which (we hope) is still a trend today. His favorite day of the week was Tuesdays which he deemed as "Hump Day Eve". He would say, "We celebrate all the eves, New Year's Eve, all hallows eve, heck, we even celebrate Christmas Eve and Santa Clause comes on Christmas Eve so why not have a reason to celebrate every week?"

Scott and Andrea were not only married; they were best friends. They spent many nights staying up until the sun rose just talking or listening to music and Andrea cared for him day in and out for the last year and a half. Andrea's love and devotion to Scott was unwavering, and likewise. Their message to everyone who finds their person is to keep making memories. Andrea did everything that she could in the last year and a half to make sure Scott and her had as many memories as they could possibly have; a WI Badger football game, a trip to Seattle including a train trip back, a WI Badger Basketball game, a Bucks game, a trip to Florida, and multiple rides in the truck which all included a lot of ice cream stops (vanilla malts, chocolate chip cookie dough or mint chocolate chip). There were many days that lead into nights and sunrises, just having "back deck sitting time" which is what they enjoyed the most.

Scott is survived by his loving wife and best friend, his Andrea, Wausau. His daughters, Jenny (Dustin Drake), Wausau, Deana (Jessey) Richey, Antigo, and Adriana, Madison. His grandchildren, River Drake, Tobias Schafer, Koltin Richey, and Graylin Richey. He is further survived by his in-laws, Howie and Terri Fisher. Five of his older brothers, Ron, Antigo, Jerry (Deb), Lake Charles, LA, Pete, Madisonville, KY, Rick (Shayne), Antigo, Dan (Cindy), Aniwa. Brother-in-laws John Zich, Lancaster, WI and Garrett Peterson, Wausau. His princess puppy girl, Morgan. Very special cousins and friends, Steve and Cassie Cerny, Wausau who have helped Scott and Andrea immensely over this past year along with many nieces, nephews, aunts, uncles, extended family, cousins and friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents, his mother-in-law, Becky Fisher, his oldest brother, Lyman Jr., his niece, Tina Mullins, and nephew, Benny Kerstetter.

Scott's family would like to thank all of their friends and family that have helped them over the last year and a half. Dr. Platta and Dr. Asquith and the amazing staff at Aspirus Cancer Center. His Neurosurgeon Dr. Dey and his Neuro-Oncologist Dr. Bhatia and the staff at UW Health. NP Ann Wittwer, her nurse Jessica and all the staff at the VA in Wausau and Tomah. Holly, Ashley and the other caring staff at St. Croix Hospice and all the other amazing care givers we have had along the way.

A Celebration of Scott's life will be held at Noon, Saturday, June 20, 2026 at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau. Visitation will begin at 9:00 a.m. until the time of services all at the Sixth Street funeral home. In lieu of flowers a memorial fund has been established in Scott's name. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

In the words of Scott "Life is too short to be unhappy, find your happy and make as many memories as possible that you can."

records.name: Susan J. Johnson-Fenhaus
records.summary: Susan J. Johnson-Fenhaus, age 79, of Wausau, passed away on Saturday, June 6, 2026.
records.body:
Susan J. Johnson-Fenhaus, age 79, of Wausau, passed away on Saturday, June 6, 2026, at Colonial Health Services, Colby.

Susan was born on July 24, 1946, in Wausau, to Marvin H. and Edna E.E. (Kratwell) Plautz. Susan attended Wausau High School, graduating June 6, 1964. Susan was united in marriage on June 15, 1974, to Mark J. Johnson at St. Stephen Lutheran Church, Wausau.

Together Susan and Mark were active parents raising two sons, Drew and Kevin, and happily attended everything from school events to year-round sports. Susan's professional career spanned time at Wausau Insurance, J R Brushert Jewelers, Brokaw Credit Union, and finally with Greenheck, for over 20-years, retiring in 2009.

Susan was a lifelong dog lover, caring endlessly for her two successive sets of Bichon Frise's. In retirement Susan enjoying attending craft sales with friends, collecting jewelry, riding her Harley-Davidson and caring for her beloved dogs. In her later years, Susan married Bill Fenhaus August 11, 2006, sharing a life together until his passing on May 16, 2023.

Susan is survived by her sons, Drew (Amy Downs) of Richmond, California, and Kevin, of Wausau and her cousin, Sandy (Ron) Borth, Scandanavia. She was preceded in death by her husband Mark, and parents Marvin and Edna.

The family would like to thank the staff of Colonial Health Services in Colby, and North Shore Hospice for providing Susan heartfelt care and compassion in her final years and days.

A celebration of life will be held at Helke Funeral Home, 302 Spruce Street, Wausau, WI at 11:00 AM, on Friday, June 26, 2026, with visitation prior, from 10:00 AM until the time of services. The family will have a private burial following services.

In lieu of flowers, the family requests donations be made to your local pet shelter or animal rescue.

records.name: Thomas Yang
records.summary: Thomas Yang, age 62, of Wausau, died on Thursday, May 28, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas Yang, 62, Wausau, died Thursday, May 28, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born May 15, 1964 in Laos, the son of Pao Yang and Yia Lor. Survivors include his wife, Kaosheng Vang; three children, Dao Yang, Douang Yang and Angelina Yang.

Funeral services will be held on Saturday, June 13, 2026 with visitation beginning at 8:00 a.m. until services at 11:30 a.m. at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Avenue in Wausau. Burial will be in Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Wynn Owen Jones
records.summary: Wynn Owen Jones, age 94, of Wausau passed away on June 10, 2026.
records.body:
Wynn O. Jones, 94, passed away on June 10, 2026, at Stonecrest Residence in Wausau, WI.

Wynn was born on March 16, 1932, in Oshkosh, WI, to Gomer “Pappy” D. Jones and Grace “Dot” (Means) Jones. He graduated from Horicon High School and went on to attend Carroll College in Waukesha, WI and later graduated from the University of Wisconsin - Platteville. Wynn was also a Korean War Veteran who served in the U.S. Navy as a Seaman based in the Pacific from 1951 to 1953. In May of 2019, he had the privilege of taking the Never Forgotten Honor Flight out of Wausau, WI.

On December 19, 1953, Wynn married Joan A. Knutson. He loved to tell people the story of how they “met.” Joan was one of his sister Jean’s bridesmaids, and he had been assigned to “take care of her.” He would smile and tell people that he never stopped. They were married for 71 years and had three boys: Paris O., Hugh E. and Wynn “Casey” O. Jones, II. Wynn and Joan raised them in Schofield, WI, retired and moved to Rhinelander, WI in 1997 and wintered in Hot Springs Village, AR as “snowbirds” for 30 years. They also really enjoyed travelling. Included in their international adventures were over 25 cruises as well as trips with each of their 6 grandchildren for all their high school graduation presents.

Wynn worked in sales and laboratory design with Milwaukee Equipment Co. for 23 years before becoming their president. In 1982, he started Wynn O. Jones & Associates, Inc., grew it successfully, remained involved until 2002, and his company continues to flourish. He was also very active in the community, including Rotary, elder at First Presbyterian Church of Wausau, WI, member of Village Bible Church, Hot Springs Village, AR, and as a founding member of Covenant Community Presbyterian Church, Schofield, WI. He was a faithful choir member in all the churches he attended.

Family was always very important to Wynn, who was also affectionately called “Taid”, which means grandfather in Welsh. His Welsh heritage was not the only thing that bound the Jones family together. In the 1920s, his father, “Pappy” purchased land on a lake near Rhinelander, WI, where 6 generations have come together over the years, built cottages and homes, and continued family traditions; playing cribbage, picking blueberries, playing kick-the-can, getting thrown in the lake with your clothes on when you turn 12, and getting a silver dollar when you swim the lake’s channel without assistance. Relatives travel to Jones Point from a variety of states for picnics on all three summer holidays, where homemade ice cream is served and the doxology sung, just as it has been for over 100 years.

Wynn loved to work with his hands and was always ready to start or help with a project. He was an outdoorsman who played golf, flew private planes, loved watersports, and was an avid hunter and fisherman. He was also known for always having a Golden Retriever by his side, taking a daily “morning dip” in the lake and playing cribbage with Joanie every night before supper. Wynn made sure to invest time in playing with his grandchildren/great-grandchildren and regularly charted their growth by marking it on a door frame. Most importantly, Wynn was a man of faith and said at his and Joanie’s 50th wedding celebration, “If you have Jesus Christ, you can get through…just anything.”

Remaining to cherish Wynn’s memory are his wife Joan; and their sons, Paris (Lora-jean), Hugh (Diane), and Casey (Cindy) Jones; their grandchildren, Nichole (Scott) Anderson, Gwen (Dan) Rizk, Leah (Jed) Lechleitner, Evan (Christy) Jones, Owen (Jenna) Jones, Bennett (Christina) Jones, and 17 precious great-grandchildren. He is preceded in death by his parents, Gomer and Grace Jones; his two sisters, Sue (Bruce) Gohl and Jean Radke; and brother-in-law Tom Radke.

A memorial gathering to celebrate his life on earth and now in Heaven with His Lord and Savior will be held at 11:00 AM on Monday, June 22, 2026, at Covenant Community Presbyterian Church, 1806 Weston Avenue, Schofield, WI. Pastors Jim Gates and Larry McKensie will officiate. Visitation will be held from 10:00 AM until the time of the service at the church. Military Honors will be accorded immediately following the service, with a luncheon to follow in the church fellowship hall. Inurnment will take place in Pine Grove Cemetery, Wausau, at a later date.

In lieu of flowers, donations in Wynn’s memory can be made to Covenant Community Presbyterian Church.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Chad Robert Seivert
records.summary: Chad Robert Seivert, age 50, of Weston passed away on June 11, 2026.
records.body:
Chad Robert Seivert, 50, of Weston, passed away on Thursday, June 11, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital after a short but courageous six-month battle with stage four liver cancer.

Chad was born on January 3, 1976, in Wausau, to Robert and Patricia (Koenig) Seivert. He was baptized and confirmed at First English Lutheran Church in Wausau, a graduate of the D.C. Everest Senior High School class of 1994, and further pursued his studies at Northcentral Technical College

From his earliest days, Chad showed a remarkable mechanical mind and a generous spirit. For nearly a decade, Chad worked as a skilled Tool and Die Maker at Gordon Aluminum.

To those closest to him, he was a wonderful son and a special friend to many. Known affectionately as the “Mayor of Schofield,” he knew and loved so many people and was quick to make friends wherever life took him. His great sense of humor and open heart drew countless people into his life. The numerous long-lasting friendships are a testament to the warmth and kindness he radiated.

Family was central to Chad’s life. He was always there for his parents, treasuring the time spent together. Chad enjoyed fishing from an early age, especially with his dad and best friend, Bryan. Chad also had a passion for his Harley-Davidson motorcycle and had a knack for building or fixing anything. As an avid Green Bay Packers and Wisconsin Badger Fan, Chad hosted many fun Packer parties. He was especially proud of his home, where he took care of beautiful renovations; but his pride and joy was the bar he handmade from barn wood. It was beautiful!

A dream close to Chad’s heart was his land in the Upper Peninsula of Michigan, where he had hoped to someday build a cabin and enjoy a peaceful retirement surrounded by nature. He leaves behind countless memories, friendships, and the legacy of a hardworking, loving individual whose humor, love, and zest for life will be terribly missed

He is survived by his parents, Bob and Pat Seivert, Ringle; sister, Kristi; uncle, Tim (Alma) Koenig; aunts, Kathy Ellis, Joanne Woodrich, and Joleen Seivert; significant other, Sheila Beyer; best friend, Bryan DeLucia; special friend, Kelly Hicks; as well as several cousins and extended family. He is further survived by his cats, Ernie and Venus. Chad is preceded in death by his grandparents; uncles, Mike, George, and Glenn; and cousins, Chi and Josh.

A Memorial Service will be held at 1:00 PM on Saturday, August 1, 2026, at First English Lutheran Church, 402 N 3rd Avenue, Wausau, WI 54401. Pastor Mary Zupansic will officiate. Visitation will be from 11:00 AM until the time of the service at the church. A Celebration of Life reception will follow on Saturday at Hutch's Kelly Club, 4810 Ross Ave., Weston, from 2:30 - 5:30 PM.  In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed in Chad's honor to First English Lutheran Church or the American Cancer Society.

The family wishes to extend their gratitude to the Aspirus Oncology Staff as well as the staff in the MSICU at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: David M. Hoheisel
records.summary: David M. Hoheisel, age 58, of Hewitt passed away on Jun 11, 2026.
records.body:
David M. Hoheisel, 58, Hewitt, passed away on Thursday, June 11, 2026 at Marshfield Medical Center.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 am on Friday, June 19, 2026 at St. John’s Catholic Church, Marshfield with visitation from 9:30 am until the time of service. Rev. Joseph O’Hara will officiate. A livestream of the service will be available on David’s obituary page at rembsfh.com. Rembs Funeral Home is assisting the family.

David was born on September 11, 1967 in Norway, MI to Robert and Mary (Yourkoski) Hoheisel. He graduated from Norway High School and Michigan Tech University. He was united in marriage to Christa Dunbar on December 12, 1997 in Gaylord, MI.

David was an avid outdoorsman. His love of the outdoors extended to spending time at camp, fishing, hunting, or simply sitting around a campfire with family and friends, sharing stories and creating memories. The outdoors was where he felt most at peace and where many of his happiest moments were spent.

His passion for the outdoors also led him to a career in the composite panel industry, which he affectionately described by saying that he “took round trees, chopped them up, and made them flat.” Over the course of a distinguished 35-year career, David became a highly respected wood scientist and particleboard specialist. His expertise, dedication, and contributions to the industry earned him the admiration and respect of colleagues throughout the field.

Family was extremely important to David, and he would do absolutely anything to support those he loved. He was a devoted husband, father, grandfather, brother, and friend whose kindness, generosity, and steady presence could always be counted on. Whether offering a helping hand, words of encouragement, or simply showing up when needed, David consistently put the needs of others before his own.

He is survived by his wife, Christa, sons, Adam Hoheisel of Hewitt, Nicholas (Brianna) Hoheisel of Big Bend, and Lucas (Lisa Lacoursiere - fiancé) Hoheisel of Rapid City, SD, one grandchild, Wesley, and one on the way.

He is further survived by his parents, Robert and Mary, sisters, Kathy (Jeff) Barnett of Ann Arbor, MI, Carol (Tim) Mooney of Toledo, OH, and Susan (Mike) Bovee of Wisconsin Rapids, and brothers, Daniel (Debra) Hoheisel of Marshfield and Karl (Patsy) Hoheisel of Hudson, IL.

The family would like to extend a special thanks to Arauco-North America and the School District of Spencer for their unwavering support, along with the Marshfield Medical Center Oncology and Palliative Care teams.

records.name: Arthur W. Haynes
records.summary: Arthur W. Haynes, age 78, of Weston passed away on Jun 12, 2026.
records.body:
Arthur “Art” Warren Haynes, 78, Weston, died Friday, June 12, 2026 at Marshfield Medical Center in Weston.

He was born January 23, 1948 in Lawrence, Massachusetts, son of the late Arthur and Arlene (Willard) Haynes. He moved to Orange County, California where he fell in love with the sun, sand and Carol Ann Miller, his sweetheart of nearly 55 years. They married on a sunny day in Garden Grove, California on August 11, 1973.

He was a dependable and committed employee of the California State Hospital system where he worked in facility operations, retiring after 40 years of service and relocating with Carol to Wisconsin. Early retirement afforded him the opportunity to spend time doing what he loved. He was an avid fisherman, with an impressive collection of over 300 fishing rods and reels and a story to go along with each. He also loved sports of every kind, but was a die-hard LA Dodgers fan and was looking forward to a three-peat World Series performance this October. He was coach for many youth sports teams and was a natural.

Art was a loving family man with a strong Christian faith. He received the Lord in his life in 1972, baptized in Pirates Cove in Corona Del Mar, California. He lived an honest life of hard work and generosity and will be remembered as someone who never met a stranger. He was not a man of few words and could talk your ear off, which made him even more endearing.

Survivors include, his wife of almost 53 years, Carol Haynes, Weston; two children, Dennis Russell, Gays Mills, Wisconsin and Melissa Haynes, St. Louis, Missouri; brother, David Haynes, Arizona; sister Shirley Kelley, Arizona; two sisters-in-law, Kathy Vanderhoof, Ogema and Eva Nichols, Wausau; numerous beloved nephews and nieces as well as his cats Scooter and Patches.

Besides his parents, he was preceded in death by two siblings, Nancy Bartlett and Skip Haynes.

A celebration of Arthur’s life will be held on Friday June 19, 2026 at Christ Community Church, 8100 Alderson Street, Schofield. Rev. Arnold Allison will officiate. Visitation will be at 10:00am with service following at 11:00 am at the church.

In lieu of flowers, please consider a donation to the Pitch In For Baseball and Softball https://pifbs.org/ an organization supplying sports gear to youth sports teams in need.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Barbara J Beck
records.summary: Barbara J Beck, age 82 passed away on June 12, 2026.
records.body:
Barbara J Beck (nee Kappler), 82, a long-time resident of Marshfield, WI passed away at Woodland Court Elder Services of Merrill, on June 12, 2026. Barbara was born September 1943 to Alvin and Hazel Kappler in Jackson, MI. She was a graduate of Manchester High School class of 1961. She married the love of her life, John Beck, on May 18, 1974.

Barbara was an exchange student in the Netherlands upon graduation, and she volunteered many years for Youth for Understanding, including serving on the National Volunteer Advisory Council. She worked in tourism and hospitality for many years including as a travel agency manager, and hotel sales and banquet director. Barbara was a Board Member of the Marshfield Convention and Business Bureau for 12 years and was a volunteer at many events. She also worked as Personnel Officer and Employee Benefits Manager at Valley Bank in Appleton.

Barbara was active in Business & Professional Women/USA for many years. She received many awards including State Woman of Distinction 2005, Regional Woman of the Year 2006, State Member of Inspiration 2007, and State & National Issues Management Award 2007.

Barbara was an active member of Immanuel Lutheran Church in Marshfield and a member of the Women’s Guild. She was a proud member of the Daughters of the American Revolution.

Survivors include her husband John Beck of Merrill, brothers-in-laws David (Betty) Beck of Menomonee Falls, Keith Beck of Wausau, and sisters-in-law Mary (Dale) Gohdes of Wausau, Suzanne Beck of Aniwa; and numerous nieces and nephews. She was preceded in death by her parents, Alvin and Hazel Kappler, brother James Kappler, and brothers-in-law Robert and Allen Beck, and nephew James Beck.

A Celebration of Life Service will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, June 20, at Immanuel Lutheran Church in Marshfield, Wisconsin. Family and friends may call at the church beginning at 9:30 a.m. until the time of service. Private burial will be at Central Wisconsin Veterans Memorial Cemetery in King, Wisconsin at a later date.

The family would like to thank the staff of Pine Crest NH, Woodland Court Elder Services, and St Croix Hospice for their kind attentions to Barbara. In lieu of flowers, donations to Immanuel Lutheran Church, Marshfield are appreciated.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family. Family and friends may go to MWCS.WS to leave words of remembrance.

records.name: Christine M. Lambert
records.summary: Christine Marie Lambert, 65, passed away peacefully on June 9, 2026.
records.body:
Christine Marie Lambert, 65, passed away peacefully on June 9, 2026, while under the compassionate care of Compassus Hospice at the House of the Dove.

Christine was born on December 20, 1960, in Indiana to the late Donn and Ginette (Pirot) Rethlake. She grew into a woman known for her kindness, generosity, and unwavering devotion to her family and community.

For many years, Christine served as a bus driver for the Mosinee School District. She truly loved her work and cherished the opportunity to care for the children entrusted to her each day. Ensuring their safety and brightening their mornings and afternoons brought her tremendous joy and fulfillment.

Family was the center of Christine's life. She was a devoted wife, loving mother, and proud grandmother whose greatest happiness came from spending time with those she loved. She had a remarkable gift for making others feel cared for and valued, always putting the needs of others before her own no matter where she was or what she was doing.

Christine was especially passionate about being a grandmother. She treasured every moment spent with her grandsons and eagerly anticipated the arrival of another grandson. As the family's unofficial photographer, she could always be found behind the camera, capturing precious memories at gatherings, celebrations, and everyday moments. Through her photographs, she preserved countless memories that her family will cherish for generations.

On her journey through life, Christine met and married the love of her life, Carl Lambert. The couple was united in marriage in 1990 and spent the next 36 years loving and caring for one another through life's many joys and challenges. Their marriage was a beautiful example of devotion, partnership, and enduring love.

Christine is survived by her beloved husband, Carl; her children, Jared (Tahlia) Lambert and Sarah Lambert; her cherished grandsons, Henry and Harvey; and grandson number three, who is expected to arrive on July 10, 2026. She is further survived by her siblings, Dorothy, Matt (Sharon), and Sherri (Bill); as well as many other relatives and friends who will deeply miss her.

In addition to her parents, Christine was preceded in death by her sister, Mary (Gary); her uncle, Ralph; and her aunt, Josette.

Christine's children wish for her to be remembered for her kind and caring heart, her selflessness, and the love she shared so freely with everyone around her. Her warmth, compassion, and gentle spirit touched countless lives and will remain in the hearts of all who knew her.

A memorial service will be held at a later date.

records.name: Eleanor "Nora" V. Miranda
records.summary: Eleanor "Nora" V. Miranda, age 83, of Marshfield passed away on Jun 12, 2026.
records.body:
Eleanor “Nora” V Miranda, 83, of Marshfield, WI, was called to her heavenly home on June 12, 2026.

Nora was born on March 17, 1943, in the Philippines to Eusebio and Cleotilde (Dimalibot) Villanueva. She was the 7th of 13 siblings. She was united in marriage with Warren L. Miranda on January 21, 1967, at The Church of Saint Philomena in Livingston, New Jersey.

Nora is lovingly survived by her husband Warren and their three children, Warren “Sonny” (Jill) of Marshfield, WI, Earl (Rebecca) of Fargo, ND and Karla (Josh) Leggett of Oshkosh, WI. Nora also has ten grandchildren and 3 great grandchildren. Words cannot describe how much she will be missed.

Known as Nor, she will be missed dearly by her sisters and brothers: Mely Lee of Calapan, Philippines, Eloisa (Emiliano) Galvez of Toronto, CA, Clemencia (Alex) Tenorio of Houston, TX, Rose (Antonio)Villacorta of Houston, TX, Grace (Jose) Rubia of Socorro, Philippines, Vee (Patrick) Snyder of Houston, TX, Nestor (Lani) Villanueva , San Jose Occidental Mindoro, Philippines, and Jimmy (Alma) Villanueva of Houston, TX. She is also a dear aunt to many.

Nor will also be missed by her lifelong Marshfield Filipino family, Fernando and Rachel Bersalona, Constante and Amelia Avecilla, Nemensia (and Victor) Ejercito and Senan and Menchu Siasoco.

She is preceded in death by her parents, sisters Noemi and Nieves, brothers Romulo, Eduardo and father-in-law Raymundo, mother-in-law Ambrosia and many loved brother and sister-in-laws.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at Our Lady of Peace Catholic Church, Marshfield, WI on Friday, June 19, 2026, at 11:00 am, with visitation starting at 9:00 am. Fr Robertson will officiate. A livestream of the service will be available on Nora’s obituary page at the time of service. Interment will be at Hillside Cemetery, Marshfield, WI at a later date.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be made to Our Lady Of Peace and Columbus High School.

Our family would like to thank the caring, compassionate staffs at the Marshfield Medical Center and Rembs Funeral Home for the care they provided our beloved mother and wife.

“Eternal rest grant unto Eleanor, O Lord, and let perpetual light shine upon her. May her soul, and the souls of all the faithful departed, through the mercy of God, rest in peace. AMEN”

Livestream Link:

https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=2518908008556174

records.name: Florence A. Marvin
records.summary: Florence A. Marvin, 80, of Wausau, passed away on May 25, 2026.
records.body:
Florence A. Marvin, 80, of Wausau, passed away on Monday, May 25, 2026, at The Meadows of Marathon, under the care of Heartland Hospice. She was born in Wausau on October 25, 1945, to the late Anthony Sr. and Isabelle (Zieglmeier) Marvin.

Florence attended St. Mary's School and later graduated from Newman Catholic High School, both in Wausau. She also received a degree in bookkeeping from technical college. For 20 years, Florence worked at Marathon Electric in Wausau, followed by 22 years at St. Anne Catholic Church, Wausau, as parish secretary. She thought of her work at St. Anne's as her "dream job." Florence was a strong supporter of the Humane Society of Marathon County, and a passionate sports fan – she always cheered for her Brewers, Packers, and Badgers!

Survivors include: close friends, Dorothy "Dotty" Schumacher and Debbie Lemke, extended family, and friends. Florence was preceded in death by her parents, Anthony Sr. and Isabelle Marvin; brother, Anthony "Pudge" Marvin Jr.; and grandparents, Louis and Anna Zieglmeier.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. on Wednesday, June 17, 2026, at St. Anne Catholic Church, 700 West Bridge Street, Wausau, with Rev. Tom Lindner presiding. Visitation will be at the same location and will begin at 9:00 a.m., June 17th, until the time of service. Lunch will follow. The burial will be at 10:00 a.m. on Thursday, June 18, at Restlawn Memorial Park. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Gary Anthony Podjaski
records.summary: Gary Anthony Podjaski, 80, of Weston, passed away on June 6, 2026.
records.body:
Gary Anthony Podjaski, 80, passed away on Saturday, June 6, 2026. He was born December 4, 1945 at St. Mary's Hospital in Wausau to Anthony and Katherine (Wroblewski) Podjaski of Hatley.

Gary was raised on the family farm on Bass Lake Road, which he purchased from his mother after his father's death. He graduated from Wittenberg High School in 1964. He enjoyed snowmobiling and shooting pool.

Gary married Judith "Judy" Ziegel on October 22, 1977 at Saint Florian's Catholic Church in Hatley. They farmed until 1987 when they moved to Kronenwetter, later building a home in Weston, WI. He retired from J&D Tube Benders in Weston in 2015.

After retirement he struggled with Parkinson's and Alzheimers, but still enjoyed working outside on his lawn and taking walks to visit with neighbors and anyone he met along the way. He most enjoyed watching his grandchildren play ball.

Gary is survived by his wife Judy; son, Tony (Jennifer) Podjaski, of Marathon; daughter, Tracy (Mark) Fenske, of Tigard, Oregon; grandchildren, Allison and Harvey, of Marathon; sister, Yvonne Shakula, of Hales Corners; brother, Donald Podjaski, of Weston; brother-in-law, Lyle (Patty) Ziegel, of Wausau; sister-in-law, Marie Podjaski, of Ringle; godson, Mark Shakula, of Milwaukee, and several nieces and nephews.

In addition to his parents, he was preceded in death by his in-laws, Harry and Audrey Ziegel, brother, Myron Podjaski and brother-in-law, David Shakula.

A mass of Christian Burial will take place at Saint Florian's Catholic Church, 500 Church Lane Rd, Hatley, WI, 54440, on Monday, June 15, 2026, at 11:00 am. Before the service there will be a visitation at church from 9:30 am until 11:00 am. Burial will follow services at Saint Florian's Catholic Cemetery.

We would like to thank the staff of Sylvan Crossing Memory Care in Stevens Point for their care and compassion and to Moments Hospice for his care these last few months. All of you became part of our family and we knew he was in good hands.

records.name: Gerald A. Brzezinski
records.summary: Gerald Alan Brzezinski, 71, of Wausau, died on June 9, 2026.
records.body:
Gerald (Gary) Alan Brzezinski, 71, died June 9, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital, surrounded by his family.

He was born March 2, 1955 in Wausau, son of the late Henry and Dorothy (Dombrowski) Brzezinski.

Gary was a 1973 graduate of Newman High School, Wausau. He began his career as a groundskeeper at the former Employers Insurance in Wausau. Later, he began a 30-year career at Lee Yawkey Woodson Art Museum in Wausau as a groundskeeper.

Gary was a big Green Bay Packers fan and never missed watching or listening to a game. He also enjoyed watching NASCAR races over the years. His love for the outdoors was a great complement to his chosen profession.

Survivors include his siblings: Rev. Hilary Brzezinski, O.F.M., Greenwood, Miss., Norb Brzezinski, Wausau, Deb (Ken) Dorshorst, Mosinee, Jerry (Hollie) Brzezinski, Green Bay and Cindy (Paul) Prior, Lake Elmo, Minn., nieces and nephews: Ryan (Jodi) Dorshorst, Jenna (Dave) Coldwell, Danielle (Eric Lindbergh) Brzezinski, Zachary Brzezinski, Patrick (Mary Teresa) Prior and Molly (Matthew) Freidel; seven great-nephews: Theodore, Arthur and Simon Dorshorst, Hank Coldwell, Beckett Lindberg, Albert Prior and Walter Freidel; two great-nieces: Lucy Freidel and Lidwina Prior; aunts: Bea Dombrowski, Lucille Dallman, Joyce Gagas, and many cousins on the Brzezinski and Dombrowski sides.
