The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 10:30 a.m. on Tuesday, June 16, at St. Michael Catholic Church, 611 Stark St., Wausau, with viewing from 9-10:30 a.m. at the church. There will be a visitation on Monday, June 15, from 4-7 p.m. with a rosary at 7 p.m. at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 N. 6th St., Wausau.

records.name: Judith L. Laduron
records.summary: Judith Laura Laduron, 84, of Wausau, was called home to be with the Lord on June 9, 2026.
records.body:
Judith (Judi) Laura Laduron (Manecke)

Judi was called home to be with the Lord on June 9th, 2026 at the age of 84.

Born on April 20, 1942, in Wausau, Wisconsin, Judi was the daughter of the late Vincent and Vitalia Manecke. She graduated from Wausau East High School in 1960 and later relocated to Milwaukee, where she met her future husband and love of her life, Gerard ("Jerry") Laduron, on a blind date. They were united in marriage on May 26, 1962, in Milwaukee, where they began their life together and started to raise their family.

Judi began her professional career with Federal Pacific Electric, a division of Reliance Electric and in 1983, she and Jerry returned to Wausau, where she found employment at L&S Electric. During this time, they also spent many enjoyable weekends and vacations at their cottage in Crooked Lake, Wisconsin and could often be found "Gone Fishing".

The cottage eventually became their full-time residence where Judi continued to commute to Wausau, WI or Menominee, MI for work until her retirement in 2004. They enjoyed countless hours with family and dear friends, as well as many memorable days spent fishing. Following retirement, they embraced every opportunity to travel, exploring various states with their fifth wheel and boat, seeking out favorite fishing spots and visiting loved ones along the way.

Jerry preceded her in death on April 6, 2006, just one month shy of their 44th wedding anniversary. In the years that followed, Judi remained deeply engaged in her community, generously dedicating her time to organizations including St. Mary of the Lake Christian Women, the Crooked Lake Volunteer Fire Department Women's Auxiliary, the Crooked Lake Sportsman's Club, and the Crooked Lake/Mountain Lions Club.

She also treasured the friendships she built through her involvement with the Mountain Red Hatters, the Mountain Slick Chicks, and the Crooked Lake Ladies card club—connections she greatly missed after returning to Wausau in 2015.

A devoted sports fan, Judi enthusiastically supported the Green Bay Packers and Milwaukee Brewers. Her spirited cheering was well known and could often be heard throughout the neighborhood.

Survivors include Jerry and Judi's five children: Laura Anderson; Gerard ("Butch") Laduron Jr.; Debra Smith; Susan (Ray Jansen) Grunwald; and Jeff (Heather) Laduron. She is further survived by eight grandchildren: Michael, Whitney, Scott (Amanda), Amanda (Mitchell), Greg, Jennifer (Aaron), Mellisa, and Nick (Abby); eight great-grandchildren: Michael, Sierra, Mason, Vinnie, Marshall, Mitchell, Chevelle, and Bella; and one great-great-grandchild, Jasper.

Judi is also survived by her Brother and Sister in-laws Vern and Geri Laduron, Mary Graesing, Karen Volkman, and Darlene Manecke; as well as numerous nieces and nephews, including Christopher (Mary) Graesing, James Laduron, Ken (Cheri) Laduron, Steve (Michelle) Laduron, Dean (Julia) Volkman, Kristy Marienthal, Carie (Jeff) Hoeser, Dawn (Ricky) Snyder, Kelley Niederhauser, Dave (Sara) Manecke, Dawn (Rob) Simpson, Ann (Adam) Victor and many dear friend and cousins that she cared about deeply.

She was preceded in death by her parents, Vincent and Vitalia Manecke; her husband Jerry's parents, Harold and Alice Griebeler; her brother, David Manecke; grandchildren Timothy Gendron, Matthew Gendron, Jessica Smith, and Vincent Laduron; great-grandson Kolton Lemma; brothers-in-law Wayne Graesing, Jerome Volkman, and Fred Winters; sister-in-law Georgia Winters; nephews Robert Graesing and Ty Marienthal; and niece Denise Mascari.

The family extends their sincere gratitude to Dr. Christopher Peterson, Oncology Specialist at Aspirus Cancer Center, as well as Haley Breaker, Lacie Beltran and the entire Aspirus Hospice nursing team, for their compassion, dedication, and exceptional care during this difficult time. Your support and kindness brought great comfort to our mother and to all of us, and it is deeply appreciated.

Judi has peacefully departed to be reunited with Jerry, together "gone fishing" once again.

records.name: Randy Reissman
records.summary: Randy Reissman, age 68, of Wittenberg passed away on June 12, 2026.
records.body:
Randy Reissman, 68, of Wittenberg, died on Friday, June 12, 2026, at his home, surrounded by his family. He was a kind man with the biggest heart.

Randy was born on April 30, 1958, the son of Ervin Reissman and Delores Bernitt.

On June 6, 1986, Randy was united in marriage to Mary Grulkowski in Wausau. The couple just celebrated 40 years of marriage.

Randy worked as a carpenter for Low Brothers Construction and Dun-Rite Exteriors. Randy made many lasting friendships throughout his years in construction and often said that working for these companies felt like being part of a family. He took great pride in his work and valued the relationships he built with his coworkers over the years.

While he enjoyed his work, his true passion was farming. Randy loved spending time outdoors, whether he was ice fishing or hunting. Some of his most treasured memories were made on hunting trips to Colorado with his brothers and friends and his trip to Alaska with Mary and friends. He also enjoyed watching the Packers and playing Sheepshead and took great pride in teaching the game to his grandchildren. Randy cherished the time he spent with his family. Attending his children’s and grandchildren’s sporting events brought him immense joy. He loved his kids and all of the kids he raised. He was incredibly proud of his son when he became a United States Marine and later decided to work in the agriculture field. Randy took great pride in watching his children and grandchildren grow and achieve their goals.

Randy is survived by his wife, Mary; his father, Ervin; two children, Samantha Grulkowski and Matt (Stephanie Laug) Reissman; seven grandchildren, Alexis (Josh Newbury) Lorbecke, Jersey Bradham, Haiden Reissman, Bryce Reissman, Qiarie Burns, Riven Reissman and Dax Reissman; three great-grandchildren, Braxton Lorbecke, Xavier Monrreal and Nova Newbury; mother-in-law, Veronica Grulkowski; siblings, Pam Thiex, Nancy Reinke, Lori (Dave) Hedtke, Larry (Diane) Reissman, Julie (Chuck) Natzke, Shirley (Chuck) Wendler, Richie (Liz) Reissman and Paul (Holly) Reissman; brother-in-law, Joe Grulkowski, sister-in-law, Jesse Sadenwasser and many nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends.

Randy was preceded in death by his mother, Delores Haase and father-in-law, Robert Grulkowski.

A Funeral Service will be held at 11 AM, on Friday, June 19, 2026, at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg. Vicar Jim Hartleben will officiate. Visitation will be on Thursday, June 18, 2026, from 5PM until 7PM. Visitation will continue on Friday, from 9:30 AM until the time of service, all at the funeral home.

The family would like to thank Dave and Sue and Bruce and Joy for their company and playing cards with Randy over the last six months, it brought Randy so much comfort. Also, a thank you to Zach and Chuck for helping with the farm work.

Memories and messages of support may be shared at schmidtschulta.com

records.name: Shawn Ardith Carlson
records.summary: Shawn Ardith Carlson, age 74 passed away on Jun 12, 2026.
records.body:
Shawn Ardith Carlson, a 74-yr old Michigan-UP “Yooper”, passed away on June 12, 2026. She was the youngest daughter of Edward and Dolly (Johnson) Carlson.

She enjoyed activities with her daughters, roller skating, making pasties or cornflake cookies, and watching da-Bears. She was a proud Grandma, bonding over long walks, singing silly songs, grabbing lunch, and supporting developmental programs.

Shawn was committed to education, earning 2 Associates degrees (while working and raising her family). Her strong work ethic and attention to detail led to accounting positions including Arachnid (IL) and payroll positions including PGA (FL) - as a nice tie-in to her golf interest (beyond golfer Tom Watson crush!).

Shawn spent time snowmobiling, bowling, and reading books. Known for her sass and quick wit (“if you burn meal, just add water” or “I’d be terrible at Sales – can’t sell water in desert!”). Yet her tender heart showed through when someone needed an ear. Music was a constant, particularly her favorites: Neil Diamond, Cher, and Kenny Rogers (and yes Guns & Roses Welcome to the Jungle!).

She leaves two daughters, Tamara (son-in-law Robin) O'Dell and Kimberly Stanford, and three grandchildren, Ashley O'Dell, Amanda O'Dell, and Matthew Stanford; sisters, Gloria McMahon, Linda (Bill) Brantley, and Carole Moore; sister-in-law Janey Carlson; along with many nieces and nephews.

Shawn was preceded in death by her parents, brothers Arden and "Artie" Carlson, brothers-in-law Tom McMahon and Jerry Moore, as well as nephews Billy, Danny, and Jason, and niece Christine.

The family extends their heartfelt thanks to the Aspirus Hospice team’s amazing empathetic care and Sylvan Crossings compassionate care.

In lieu of flowers, the family asks that donations be made to a charity of your choice.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Dennis Lee Leader
records.summary: Dennis Lee Leader, age 73, of Schofield passed away on June 13, 2026.
records.body:
Dennis Lee Leader, 73, of Schofield, passed away at his home on Saturday, June 13, 2026.

Dennis was born July 19th, 1952, in Bell Gardens, California, to the late George and Mary (Wick) Leader. He served our country in the United States Marine Corps in 1971, in Los Angeles, California, earning the National Defense Service Medal. He married Laura (Olerich) Leader on December 9, 1979, in Denver, Colorado.

Dennis worked as an auto mechanic for many years. He worked on a lot of cars over the years but especially loved the nearly 50 model cars that he took care of right at home. He was also a fan of John Wayne movies and the music of The Beach Boys. He loved and was loved by his family and will be dearly missed.

He is survived by his wife, Laura Leader; sons, Dennis (Kala Bloomer) Leader, Jr., Matthew Leader, and Joseph Leader; grandchildren, Malialani Attina, Ethan & Brandon Leader, and Michael Leader; great-grandson, Barron Attina; sister, Pam Rivers. He is preceded in death by his parents; and siblings Bill Wick and Jackie Randolph.

Private services are being held at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care with interment in Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Kronenwetter.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Donald R. Tremelling
records.summary: Donald R. Tremelling, age 95, of Marshfield passed away on Jun 13, 2026.
records.body:
Donald R. Tremelling, 95, Marshfield, passed away on Saturday, June 13, 2026 at Wells Nature View, Marshfield.

A memorial service will be held at 11:00 am on Friday, June 26, 2026 at Rembs Funeral Home, Marshfield with visitation from 9:30 am until the time of service. Chaplain Randy Mantik will officiate. Burial will follow at Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Marshfield where military honors will be conducted by American Legion Post 54 – Marshfield.

Don was born on September 8, 1930 in Marshfield to Lyman and Eleanor (Brandt) Tremelling.

As a young man, Don worked for Charles Perkel, a John Deere dealer. He was a veteran of the United States Army, serving from 1951 until his honorable discharge in 1953. On October 25, 1952, he was united in marriage to Betty A. Kaufman at Our Lady of Peace Catholic Church in Marshfield. She passed away on March 14, 2010.

Don worked for Mitten’s Home Appliances for fifteen years. In 1967 he became employed at Hub City Foods working there until his retirement in 1993 when it was known as Fleming Foods. After retirement Don worked part time at Figi’s in Marshfield.

He was a member of St. Patrick’s Catholic Church, American Legion Post 54 and the Fraternal Order of Eagles Badger Aerie 624 in Marshfield.

Don enjoyed working in the woods, metal working, fishing trips, sauerkraut feeds, mowing the lawn, restoring John Deere tractors, doing puzzles, and watching the Green Bay Packers. He also loved spending time with his grandchildren and great grandchildren.

He is survived by his daughter, Geri Weinfurtner of Mosinee, Robert (Teresa) Tremelling of Mosinee, Paul (Joan) Tremelling of Marshfield, and Jon (Linda) Tremelling of Mosinee, grandchildren, Tara Tremelling (Andy Swenson), Tasha Weinfurtner, Tia Tremelling, Michelle (Andy) Lerch, Mike (Jena) Swanson, and Becky Swanson, and great grandchildren, Madelyn and Olivia Slaughter, Dax and Mia Swanson, Veda Kremer, and Roman Tremelling-Swenson.

He is further survived by his brother, Jim (Nancy) Tremelling and sister in law, Arlene Kaufman.

He was preceded in death by his parents, wife, Betty, son in law, Tom Weinfurtner, grandson, Travis Weinfurtner, sister, Arlene Sternweis, and brothers in law, Al Sternweis and Herman Kaufman.

In lieu of flowers, a memorial will be designated at a later date.

The family would like to extend a special thanks to the staff at Wells Nature View on 21st Street and the St. Croix hospice nurses for the excellent care.

records.name: Gary Lee Tullberg
records.summary: Gary Lee Tullberg, age 61 passed away on June 13, 2026.
records.body:
Gary Lee Tullberg passed away on Saturday, June 13th, 2026, after a valiant battle against diabetes. He was surrounded by family always and at the time of his death. Gary lived in Wittenberg most of his life and lived a full and adventurous life. Gary was a great cook and baker, bartender, decorator, artist, Gardner, dog whisperer, and doting Uncle. Gary was generous, always making sure the kids in the family grew up learning about animals and parades and the circus, to name a few. He literally traveled with a circus exploring the US. He kept current on life events and was passionate about many things.

In recent years, he enjoyed attending Polka festivals in the summer because in his words, “everyone there is so happy.” He enjoyed his trips to the casino and constantly tried new games to play and gained new friends wherever he went.

Gary was preceded in death by his father, Ronald Lee Tullberg, paternal grandparents, Raymond and Dorothy Tullberg; maternal grandmother, Winifred Foreman, and aunts, uncles, and cousins. Gary is survived by his mother, Greta Tullberg; sisters, Julie (Dale) Grulkowski, Tina Tullberg, Tammy Tullberg and brother, Jamie Tullberg. He is also survived by nephews, Andrew, Joshua, Jonathon, Marcus, and niece, Dior, and great nephews and niece, Matthew, Isaac, and Maple. Gary is survived by many cousins, three aunts and an uncle. He is also survived by many special friends who he remained close with: Tom, Luane, Leslie, Kerry, Tabitha and Luke, as well as his special dogs, Molly and Prince.

A Celebration of Life Picnic will be held at Washington Park, 104 Park Street, Wittenberg, WI 54499, on Sunday, June 28, 2026, from 2-4PM.

In lieu of flowers, please donate to the diabetes foundation.

https://diabetes.org/?form=Donate&utm_source=google_search&utm_medium=ppc&utm_campaign=fundraising_totepremium&utm_content=search&utm_term=non_brand_diabetes&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=19608957597&gclid=CjwKCAjw6MPRBhBTEiwAd-7Mr_EyDmz0FpR6okh8tPVkzu1M_prCcbyQcd_ihbm0E0Ml6obEnNO40BoCkCsQAvD_BwE

Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg is assisting the family with arrangements. Memories and messages of support may be shared at schmidtschulta.com.

records.name: Jerome Steven Sharkey
records.summary: Jerome Steven Sharkey, age 76, of Phillips passed away on Jun 13, 2026.
records.body:
Jerome “Jerry” S. Sharkey, 76, Phillips, passed away on June 13, 2026.

It is with great sadness that we announce the sudden passing of our brother, uncle, and friend Jerry Sharkey.

Jerry was born to Sylvester and Irene (Marco) Sharkey in the small town of Mosinee, Wisconsin. He grew up in Mosinee, attending Mosinee Schools, where he graduated. After graduation, Jerry entered the Military and proudly served in the U.S. Air Force from May 1969 to May 1975, including 2 ½ years overseas in Vietnam. After his honorable discharge, he worked as a carpenter.

In retirement, Jerry stayed busy and was always willing to lend a hand. He enjoyed nature, a good fire in the fire pit with great conversations and storytelling. Fishing and boating his summers away in Wisconsin and traveling to spend his winters in Florida and Arizona.

Jerry was preceded in death by his brothers, Leroy, Eugene, Jim, and Adrian Sharkey, and sisters, Mary Ann Seidler and Vonnie Albert. He is survived by his brother Pat (Joan) Sharkey, sister, Debra (David) Beske, along with many nieces, nephews, and dear friends.

A Celebration of Life will be held at a later date. Beste Funeral Home, Mosinee, is honored to be assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be directed to the family at www.bestefh.com.

records.name: Kathleen "Kathy" R. Miskowiak
records.summary: Kathleen "Kathy" R. Miskowiak, age 76 passed away on Jun 13, 2026.
records.body:
Kathleen R. Miskowiak, 76 of Wausau, passed away on Saturday, June 13, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

She was born October 20, 1949, in Wausau. Kathy was one of seven children of the late Frank and Virginia (Konkol) Miskowiak.

Kathy was employed at Mount View Care Center of Wausau. She always enjoyed taking her nieces shopping while not working.

Survivors include four brothers, Gary (Patty) Miskowiak, Yukon, OK, Gene, Russell (Lenore), and Pat (Connie) Miskowiak, all of Wausau; sister-in-law, Diane Miskowiak, Wausau; Kathy’s nephews and nieces, Douglas and Michael Miskowiak, Christine Burton, Meredith Nelson, Jerry Otto, Ryan, Aaron, and Katie Miskowiak, Tara De La Cruz, Curtis and Jacob Miskowiak, Sandra Larson, Scott Miskowiak, Steven Miskowiak, Jody Radke; uncle, Robert (Donna) Konkol.

Besides her parents, she was preceded in death by a brother, Alan, sister, Vienna Dallman and her nephew, Chad Miskowiak.

Graveside service will be 11:00 a.m. Tuesday, June 23, 2026, at Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau. Visitation will be Tuesday from 10:00 a.m. until the time of burial in the Memorial Chapel Mausoleum, Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Brian R. Dye
records.summary: Brian R. Dye, age 50, of Wisconsin Rapids passed away on Jun 14, 2026.
records.body:
Brian R. Dye, 50, Wisconsin Rapids, passed away on Sunday, June 14, 2026 at Evergreen House, Wisconsin Rapids.

Funeral services will be held at 12:30 pm on Friday, June 19, 2026 at Rembs Funeral Home, Marshfield with visitation from 11:00 am until the time of service. A livestream of the service will be available on Brian’s obituary page at rembsfh.com. Deacon Nate Mueller will officiate. Burial will take place at Mound Cemetery, Pittsville.

Brian was born on May 31, 1976 in Marshfield to Richard and Sandra (Masanz) Dye.

He worked at ODC in Wisconsin Rapids for a number of years, as well as Crockett Septic. Brian enjoyed his time in Special Olympics and his fishing had no boundaries. Brian loved to collect hats and was very friendly with everybody - especially if there was some type of ball cap. He would wrangle that hat from the owner.

He is survived by his parents, Richard and Sandra Dye, and special furry friend, Vito.

He was preceded in death by his two sisters, Shannon and Michelle Lee Dye, grandparents, Margaret and Leonard Hoffman, aunts, Sharon Dye and Roxanne Masanz, uncles, Donald Masanz and Gary Meyer, and nephew, Robert Dye.

The family would like to extend a special thank you to all the doctors and nurses who were with Brian during his fight with cancer, including Evergreen Adult Family Home, and all his extended family who Brian admired and loved.

records.name: Chris Dale Severt
records.summary: Chris Dale Severt, age 67 passed away on Jun 14, 2026.
records.body:
Chris Dale Severt, was born on January 18, 1959, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to Donald and Lois Severt. He passed away on June 14, 2026, leaving behind a legacy of joy, creativity, and inspiration that will be fondly remembered by all who had the privilege of knowing him.

Chris graduated from the Wisconsin School for the Deaf in Delavan, where he was known for his athletic spirit, playing center on the football team, competing as a heavyweight wrestler, and participating in basketball and track. After graduation, he attended Northcentral Technical College’s two-year program for welding. A special thank-you to Karen Akey as his interpreter and good friend. Throughout his life, he embodied a “can-do” attitude, never letting his disability hold him back.

For 45 years, Chris worked as a welder at 51 Sports Shop, where his talent for fabrication was unmatched. He could turn any idea into a reality and was passionate about woodworking as well. An active member of Calvary Lutheran Church in the Town of Pine River, Chris's strong faith was a guiding light in his life.

Beyond his professional and spiritual pursuits, Chris was an outgoing and artistic soul who loved the outdoors, trout fishing, drumming, and dancing. His infectious spirit and ability to make everyone around him smile were hallmarks of his personality.

Chris is survived by his siblings, Mark Severt, Debra (Todd) Schwartzman, Patrick (Jane) Severt, Dianne (Brad) Detert, and Robert "Bob" Severt; nieces, Bethany, Katelyn, Tiffany, and Morgan; nephews, Adam, Dale, Craig, and Devon. He also leaves behind several cousins, and close friends who will miss him dearly.

He is preceded in death by his parents, grandparents, brother, Thomas "Tom" Severt, great-nephew, Theodore Kirchberger; and several aunts and uncles.

A special thank you goes to Compassus Hospice for their kindness during Chris's final days. We invite friends, family, and community members to join us in celebrating Chris's life.

Celebration of Life will be held at Pine River Town Hall in Merrill, Sunday, July 19, starting at 1pm, where we will share stories and memories.

Memorial donations can be made in Chris's name to the Wisconsin School for the Deaf , an institution that held a special place in his heart.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Darlene A Bunkelman
records.summary: Darlene A Bunkelman, age 88, of Marshfield passed away on Jun 14, 2026.
records.body:
Darlene A. Bunkelman, 88, of Marshfield passed away the evening of Sunday, June 14th at Stoney River Memory Care.

A Private Funeral Service will be held. Darlene will be interred in Immanuel Lutheran Cemetery next to her husband.

Darlene was born on December 29, 1937 in Medford to Jacob and Minnie (Neumann) Knippel. She was raised in Medford and attended public schools there, graduating in 1955 from Medford High School. Darlene was united in marriage to Duane "Duke" Bunkelman July 20, 1957 At Immanuel Lutheran Church in Medford. He sadly passed in 2009. Following their marriage, Darlene & Duke made their home in Marshfield. She had worked for Weyerhauser until her retirement.

An outdoor woman, Darlene loved tending her flower garden, dining out, and snowmobiling with Duke and their friends. She was an avid book reader and dog lover. Darlene took much joy in riding along with her husband when he worked as an OTR Truck Driver. Following Duke’s death, Darlene found peace sharing regular lunches with her friends at The Wrinkle Room.

Darlene is survived by her children: Lori (Hugh) Nikolai of Marshfield, Jeanne (Steve) Canney of DePere, and Jeff (Martha Steinhart) Bunkelman of Marquette. She is also survived by five grandchildren: Megan (Steve) Colter, Adam (Margaret) Nikolai, Nicole Canney, Jackie (Misael) Ornelas, and Mariana Steinhart; and four great-grandchildren. Darlene is further survived by her sister Lorelei Rubeck.

She is predeceased by her parents and her brothers: Melvin, Clyde, Algernon, Leon, and Darrell Knippel.

records.name: Donna Rae Annis
records.summary: Donna Rae Annis, age 81, of Rhinelander passed away on June 14, 2026.
records.body:
Donna Rae Annis, age 81, of Rhinelander, died on June 14, 2026 at the Howard Young Medical Center in Minocqua, Wisconsin. She was born on September 3, 1944 in Merrill, Wisconsin. She married her high school love, Dennis Annis on May 18, 1963. They moved to Rhinelander in 1967. Donna was employed at National Foods, Cirilli's Supermarket, and J.C Penney.

Donna has enjoyed bowling, playing bridge, and golfing with her friends. She also liked to sit at her computer and write. She found joy in spending time and activities not only with her husband but also with family members, children, grandchildren, great grandchildren, and friends. She found much enjoyment and relaxation by rearranging flowers and decorating for holidays.

She is survived by her husband Dennis Annis, four children, Kimber Macdonald, Rick (Diane) Annis, Randy Annis, Jayme (Troy) Rustad. She has ten grandchildren and seven great grandchildren with three great grandchildren on the way.

She was preceded in death by her parents.

There will be a celebration of life for Donna on Saturday, August 15th, 2026 starting at 1:30 PM at the Pizza Haven in Rhinelander. You may leave your private condolences for the Annis family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Annis family.

records.name: Ervin F. Jagodzinski
records.summary: Ervin F. Jagodzinski, age 95 passed away on Jun 14, 2026.
records.body:
Ervin F. Jagodzinski, 95, of Weston, formerly of Edgar, passed away on Sunday, June 14, 2026, at Wausau Manor, under the care of Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services.

He was born on July 7, 1930, to the late Paul and Balbina (Knetter) Jagodzinski in Edgar. Ervin married Arlean Butalla on April 7, 1953, at St. Mary Catholic Church in Marathon, and recently celebrated 73 years together.

Ervin will be remembered by those close to him as a hard worker, a person that was physically and mentally strong, and one that had a great sense of humor. His contribution to the cheese and dairy industry was highly regarded and appreciated. For 38 years, Ervin ran a state-of-the-art cheese factory that was a model for production. Ervin was a licensed cheese maker and was a lifetime member of the Central Wisconsin Cheesemakers and Buttermakers Association. Over the years he had cheesemakers from all over the world through his factory to see how new equipment could be used in their own factories.

Outside of work, Ervin was a lifetime member of St. John the Baptist Catholic Church, a member of the Holy Name Society, a 50+ year member of the Edgar Lions Club, and an early member of the Poniatowski Dare Devils. He loved snowmobiling with family and friends, working in the woods, four-wheeling, and cutting the grass. He also enjoyed making firewood with his sons and son-in-law. Ervin was quite the dancer in his early years and loved dancing polkas and waltzes with Arlean. He enjoyed a good game of Sheepshead with family and friends as well.

Survivors include his wife, Arlean; children, Dale Jagodzinski, Diane (David) Konsella, Daryl Jagodzinski (Brenda Williams), and Daryn Jagodzinski (Mary Rosier); grandchildren, Kathleen (Michael) Maher, Kristine Konsella, and Jennie (Davin) Konsella-Norene; great-grandchildren, Priela and Matthew Maher and Madelyn, Dylan, and Parker Norene; sisters, Joan Wagner, Janet Trawicki, and Theodora Theiler; and other relatives and friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents and numerous brothers and sisters-in-law.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. on Tuesday, June 23, 2026, at St. John the Baptist Catholic Church, Edgar, with Rev. Alan Wierzba presiding. Visitation will begin at 9:00 a.m. until the time of service at the church. Burial will follow in St. Johns Catholic Cemetery, Edgar. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

A special thank you from the family to the nurses and staff at Wausau Manor and to Nina and Dawn from Aspirus Palliative Care for the compassionate care that Ervin received.

Memorial donations are suggested to St. John the Baptist Catholic Church or School, Edgar.

records.name: Jamie Ann Mendrzycki
records.summary: Jamie Ann Mendrzycki, age 71 passed away on June 14, 2026.
records.body:
Jamie Ann (Westberg) Mendrzycki 71, died peacefully at Dimensions Living of Wausau on June14, 2026. She was married to Michael Mendrzycki in Wausau, WI on October, 1981. They were married 35 years until the death of her husband. They had three sons: Nathan (Wisconsin Rapids, WI); Troy (Minneapolis, MN); and Brian (Madison, WI).

They made their home in Wisconsin Rapids, WI. Jamie was a member of Calvery Baptist Church in Wisconsin Rapids, WI. She worked at Copp’s Foods in the deli department for many years. She loved her family and serving others. We loved her smile.

Funeral arrangements at Bethany Baptist Church in Weston, WI on Saturday, June 27 with 10:00 visitation and 11:00 service. She will be buried at Veterans Cemetery, King, WI next to her husband.

She was preceded in death by her husband Michael Leo Mendrzycki; her father John (Jack) Westberg; her mother Betty (Fenhaus) Westberg; two brothers Jay and Kris (Lori) Westberg. She is survived by her sisters Bonnie (Kenneth) Hall (Lakeland, Florida & DeForest, WI); and Kari Cline (Wausau, WI) and a brother Jonathan Westberg (Pewaukee, WI).

The family would like to thank the devoted staff at Dimensions Living of Wausau and Interim Healthcare of Wausau-Hospice for their loving care.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family. Family and friends may go to MWCS.WS to leave words of remembrance.

records.name: Arthur Warren Haynes
records.summary: Arthur Warren Haynes, age 78, of Weston died on June 12, 2026.
records.body:
He was born January 23, 1948 in Lawrence, Massachusetts, son of the late Arthur and Arlene (Willard) Haynes. He moved to Orange County, California where he fell in love with the sun, sand and Carol Ann Miller, his sweetheart of nearly 55 years. They married on a sunny day in Garden Grove, California on August 11, 1973.

He was a dependable and committed employee of the California State Hospital system where he worked in facility operations, retiring after 40 years of service and relocating with Carol to Wisconsin. Early retirement afforded him the opportunity to spend time doing what he loved. He was an avid fisherman, with an impressive collection of over 300 fishing rods and reels and a story to go along with each. He also loved sports of every kind, but was a die-hard LA Dodgers fan and was looking forward to a three-peat World Series performance this October. He was coach for many youth sports teams and was a natural.

Art was a loving family man with a strong Christian faith. He received the Lord in his life in 1972, baptized in Pirates Cove in Corona Del Mar, California. He lived an honest life of hard work and generosity and will be remembered as someone who never met a stranger. He was not a man of few words and could talk your ear off, which made him even more endearing.

Survivors include, his wife of almost 53 years, Carol Haynes, Weston; two children, Dennis Russell, Gays Mills, Wisconsin and Melissa Haynes, St. Louis, Missouri; brother, David Haynes, Arizona; sister Shirley Kelley, Arizona; two sisters-in-law, Kathy Vanderhoof, Ogema and Eva Nichols, Wausau; numerous beloved nephews and nieces as well as his cats Scooter and Patches.

Besides his parents, he was preceded in death by two siblings, Nancy Bartlett and Skip Haynes.

A celebration of Arthur's life will be held on Friday June 19, 2026 at Christ Community Church, 8100 Alderson Street, Schofield. Rev. Arnold Allison will officiate. Visitation will be at 10:00am with service following at 11:00 am at the church.

In lieu of flowers, please consider a donation to the Pitch In For Baseball and Softball https://pifbs.org/ an organization supplying sports gear to youth sports teams in need.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Bob E. Pfaff
records.summary: Bob E. Pfaff, age 80, of Wausau passed away on June 9, 2026.
records.body:
Bob was born on August 11, 1945, in Medford, Wisconsin, to Arthur and Edna Pfaff. On August 16, 1975, he married Barbara Ovel and together they had two daughters, Cindy and Laura. He is further survived by his granddaughter, Emma; his sister, Lucille Miller; four sisters-in-law, Pat Wunsch, Kathy Johnson, Joan Pfaff, and Ann Ovel; and numerous nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents; his brother, John Pfaff; and three brothers-in-law, Leonard Miller, Dan Ovel, and Jack Wunsch.

Bob was a veteran of the United States Army and served during the Vietnam War. He worked as a heavy equipment mechanic for various construction companies throughout his 41-year career, retiring at the age of 62.

Bob was an avid fisherman who enjoyed fishing, especially on the Great Lakes. He was also an accomplished hunter, enjoying both bow and rifle hunting. He liked playing pool, listening to music, attending live shows and concerts, and watching stock car races. He had a passion for anything with an engine, especially cars, tractors, and construction equipment. He also enjoyed camping, road tripping throughout the country, and trying his luck "pulling levers" at the casino.

Bob was a kind and generous man who was always willing to offer his help and share his knowledge when needed. Larger than life, he had a warm and welcoming nature that made everyone feel comfortable. He was an amazing person whose kindness, humor, and presence left a lasting impact on all who knew him.

A celebration of life will be held beginning at 1:00 p.m. on Sunday, July 26, 2026, at Big Eau Pleine County Park, Enclosed Shelter #19 (main shelter near the beach), 135695 Eau Pleine Park Road, Mosinee. Please join the family for lunch and a time together to remember and celebrate Bob. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Chad Robert Seivert
records.summary: Chad Robert Seivert, age 50, of Weston passed away on June 11, 2026.
records.body:
Chad was born on January 3, 1976, in Wausau, to Robert and Patricia (Koenig) Seivert. He was baptized and confirmed at First English Lutheran Church in Wausau, a graduate of the D.C. Everest Senior High School class of 1994, and further pursued his studies at Northcentral Technical College.

From his earliest days, Chad showed a remarkable mechanical mind and a generous spirit. For nearly a decade, Chad worked as a skilled Tool and Die Maker at Gordon Aluminum.

To those closest to him, he was a wonderful son and a special friend to many. Known affectionately as the "Mayor of Schofield," he knew and loved so many people and was quick to make friends wherever life took him. His great sense of humor and open heart drew countless people into his life. The numerous long-lasting friendships are a testament to the warmth and kindness he radiated.

Family was central to Chad's life. He was always there for his parents, treasuring the time spent together. Chad enjoyed fishing from an early age, especially with his dad and best friend, Bryan. Chad also had a passion for his Harley-Davidson motorcycle and had a knack for building or fixing anything. As an avid Green Bay Packers and Wisconsin Badger Fan, Chad hosted many fun Packer parties. He was especially proud of his home, where he took care of beautiful renovations; but his pride and joy was the bar he handmade from barn wood. It was beautiful!

A dream close to Chad's heart was his land in the Upper Peninsula of Michigan, where he had hoped to someday build a cabin and enjoy a peaceful retirement surrounded by nature. He leaves behind countless memories, friendships, and the legacy of a hardworking, loving individual whose humor, love, and zest for life will be terribly missed.

He is survived by his parents, Bob and Pat Seivert, Ringle; sister, Kristi; uncle, Tim (Alma) Koenig; aunts, Kathy Ellis, Joanne Woodrich, and Joleen Seivert; significant other, Sheila Beyer; best friend, Bryan DeLucia; special friend, Kelly Hicks; as well as several cousins and extended family. He is further survived by his cats, Ernie and Venus. Chad is preceded in death by his grandparents; uncles, Mike, George, and Glenn; and cousins, Chi and Josh.

A Memorial Service will be held at 1:00 PM on Saturday, July 18, 2026, at First English Lutheran Church, 402 N 3rd Avenue, Wausau, WI 54401. Pastor Mary Zupansic will officiate. Visitation will be from 10:00 AM until the time of the service at the church. In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed in Chad's honor to First English Lutheran Church or the American Cancer Society.

The family wishes to extend their gratitude to the Aspirus Oncology Staff as well as the staff in the MSICU at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Colleen Rita Kurtzweil
records.summary: Colleen Rita Kurtzweil, age 87, of Tomahawk passed away on Jun 15, 2026.
records.body:
Colleen Kurtzweil, 87, of Tomahawk, WI was called to rest on June 15th, 2026. She was born December 27th, 1938, to the late Reginald and Lucille (Senoraske) Kluck.

Colleen graduated from D.C. Everest Senior High and Wausau Vocational School. She began her career working as a legal secretary and later joined Kolbe and Kolbe Millwork as an Accounts Receivable Manager until retiring in 2004.

Colleen and her husband, Ken Kurtzweil, took a break from their careers to venture to Mosinee, WI where they purchased and renovated an old tavern into the Stein ‘n’ Baum. They made many new friends and enjoyed being part of the community for several years, before returning to the Wausau area, and later relocating to Tomahawk.

Upon her retirement Colleen enjoyed bartending. She cherished the friendships she made at the local gathering places in Tomahawk, partaking in her signature Bud Light with a glass of ice, and occasionally throwing a buck in a gambling machine. She and Ken were active members of the local snowmobile clubs.

Colleen filled her days cooking a good meal, gardening, feeding the birds, and working on jigsaw puzzles. Colleen always enjoyed fishing, as a young girl with her father, and later on in life after connecting with Bob Rosemurgy. She and Bob took several trips to Canada to visit friends and wet a line.

In recent weeks, Colleen endured a brief battle with colon cancer. ‘God saw you getting tired when a cure was not to be, so He wrapped his arms around you, and whispered, “Come to me”.’

Colleen is survived by her daughter Jodie Connor of Mosinee, WI, her sister Karen Kennedy of Las Vegas, NV, her grandchildren Ryan (Jamie) Connor of Kronenwetter, WI and Lindsey (Ethan) Hudson of Sullivan, WI, and her great grandchildren Sam Connor, and Quinn and Nora Hudson.

Colleen was preceded in death by her husband, Ken Kurtzweil, in 2009, her niece, Heather Kennedy, in 2011, her son Randy Kurtweil, in 2018, and her significant other, Bob Rosemurgy, in 2025.

Visitation will be held at Beste Funeral Home in Mosinee on June 30th beginning at 11:30 a.m., followed by a service at 12:30 p.m. A Celebration of Life will begin at 1:00 p.m. at Paper City Lanes in Mosinee. Online condolences may be directed to the family at www.bestefh.com.

records.name: Dave Wanta
records.summary: Dave Wanta, age 86, of Bevent passed away on June 8, 2026.
records.body:
He was born in Bevent on May 27, 1940, to the late Anton and Mattie (Cebula) Wanta. He fought a courageous battle with Parkinson's disease for several years.

Dave married Nancy Kurszewski of Bevent in 1961, and they had two sons during their marriage. They divorced in 1988.

He was a mason contractor by occupation and a musician at heart. Music was in Dave's blood, and his first love was the concertina. He was a band member with Norm Dombrowski's band, the Happy Notes of Stevens Point, Wisconsin, as well as the Polka Knights of Mosinee, Wisconsin, and the Polka Stars of Stevens Point, Wisconsin.

Dave loved to golf and also to travel, especially to Las Vegas to see his sons. Bingo and trips to the local casinos were among his favorite pastimes.

He is survived by his significant other, Jennie Awana of Schofield, WI; son, Troy (Ginger) Wanta of Las Vegas, NV; granddaughters, Mandy and Angie Wanta; and great-grandsons, Trentin, Casen, and Cash, all of Las Vegas. He is further survived by his brothers, Jack (Sue) Wanta and Ron Wanta, all of Bevent; goddaughter, Heidi Krause of Green Bay; along with many close friends and relatives.

He was preceded in death by his parents; his son, Todd; sister, Adeline; brother-in-law, Kelly Wanta; sister-in-law, Debbie Wanta; and nephew, Bryan Wanta.

The family would like to extend our heartfelt gratitude to Dave's significant other, Jennie, for all the love and care she gave to Dave over the past 30 years. Thank you also to the wonderful staff at Mount View Care Center for the wonderful care, love, and support to Dave and the family. You will all be in our hearts forever.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 AM on Friday, July 17, 2026, at St. Ladislaus Catholic Church, Bevent. The Rev. Augustine Bentil will preside. Visitation will be held from 9:00 AM until the time of Mass at the church. Interment will be in the Parish Cemetery, with a meal to follow at the church.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Dennis Lee Leader
records.summary: Dennis Lee Leader, age 73, of Schofield passed away on June 13, 2026.
records.body:
Dennis was born July 19th, 1952, in Bell Gardens, California, to the late George and Mary (Wick) Leader. He served our country in the United States Marine Corps in 1971, in Los Angeles, California, earning the National Defense Service Medal. He married Laura (Olerich) Leader on December 9, 1979, in Denver, Colorado.

Dennis worked as an auto mechanic for many years. He worked on a lot of cars over the years but especially loved the nearly 50 model cars that he took care of right at home. He was also a fan of John Wayne movies and the music of The Beach Boys. He loved and was loved by his family and will be dearly missed.

He is survived by his wife, Laura Leader; sons, Dennis (Kala Bloomer) Leader, Jr., Matthew Leader, and Joseph Leader; grandchildren, Malialani Attina, Ethan & Brandon Leader, and Michael Leader; great-grandson, Barron Attina; sister, Pam Rivers. He is preceded in death by his parents; and siblings Bill Wick and Jackie Randolph.

Private services are being held at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care with interment in Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Kronenwetter.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Joyce E. Rakow
records.summary: Joyce E. Rakow, age 82, of Wausau, Wisconsin passed away on April 29, 2026.
records.body:
Joyce was born February 10, 1944 to the late Gilbert Hieronimus and Lucia Hieronimus. Joyce married Thomas (Tom) Rakow of Wausau, WI on October 2, 1965 at Trinity Evangelical Lutheran Church in the township of Stettin. Tom and Joyce operated a dairy and ginseng farm in the town of Stettin until their retirement in 2004. They were married for 56 years prior to Tom's passing.

In addition to her spouse and parents, Joyce was proceeded in death by a sister Nancy Hieronimus, her in-laws Lewis and Ella Rakow, sister-in-law Ruth Kohlwey, brother-in-law Ron Zahrt, brother-in-law John Rakow and niece Kristen Schumann.

Joyce is survived by her daughter Jennifer (Tony) Podjaski; grandchildren Allison and Harvey; sister Lois Zahrt; sister Sue (Dan) Thelen; sister-in-law Sue Rakow; brother-in-law Terry Kohlwey and several nieces and nephews.

A Graveside service for Joyce Rakow will be held on Thursday, June 18 at 11:00 am Zion Cemetery in the town of Stettin.

records.name: Judy May Kivlin
records.summary: Judy May Kivlin, age 90 passed away on Jun 15, 2026.
records.body:
Judy May Kivlin was born on June 15, 1936, in Antigo, Wisconsin, to Joseph Andrew Baraniak and Magdeline Barbara (Schwingle) Baraniak. She passed away peacefully in Wausau, Wisconsin, on June 15, 2026-her 90th birthday.

She was preceded in death by her beloved husband, Larry Kivlin; her parents; her maternal and paternal grandparents; her three brothers and their spouses, Robert (Carol), Jack (Arlene), and Richard (Marge); and two nephews, Dean and Jeff.

Judy and Larry were married in May 1963 and shared a devoted life together until his passing in December 2008.

Judy lived a life grounded in family, friendship, and quiet dedication to the people and places she cherished. She found joy in simple pleasures, especially walking and spending time outdoors, where she felt most at peace. Music was also an important part of her life, and she especially enjoyed playing the piano.

She deeply valued her friendships and looked forward to her regular breakfast gatherings with friends at Coppertop in Madison, Wisconsin. Judy took great pride in her professional work with the State of Wisconsin and found meaning in her time volunteering at St. Mary's Hospital in Madison, where she was able to serve others and build lasting connections. She also supported her husband in operating the Kivlin Livestock Company.

Judy is survived by five nieces and four nephews, who will remember her for her warmth, steady presence, and the love she extended throughout her life.

She will be remembered with deep gratitude by all who knew her and will be dearly missed.

A Celebration of Life Service will be held at a later date.

records.name: Kenneth W. Boneske
records.summary: Kenneth W. Boneske, age 91, of Athens passed away on March 17, 2026.
records.body:
He was born on March 20, 1934, in Medford, to the late Arnold and Margaret (Merwin) Boneske.

Kenneth worked for many years as a dairy farmer and was also employed at Jahnke Construction. After his retirement, he drove road grader for the towns of Bern and Holton. When he wasn't working, he enjoyed his 32 tractors. Kenneth served his country in the U.S. Army in the late 1950's.

Survivors include his brother, Roy (Pat) Boneske of Athens; sister, Darlene Zietlow; stepson, Randy (Chris) Apfelbeck of Edgar; stepdaughter, Crystal (Perry) Williamson of Athens; daughters, Connie Boneske (fiancé, Randy Murkowski) of Athens and Cherie Boneske of Colby; 11 grandchildren, Stephanie (Jim) Halvorsen of Edgar, Jeremy (Kristina) Apfelbeck of Edgar, Matt (Karen) Apfelbeck of Edgar, Tiffany (Travis) Hart of Rozellville, Ciera (Josh) Grueneberg of Berlin, Jessie Venzke of St. Petersburg, Florida, Jackie (Patrick) Venzke of Edgar, Kimberly Venzke of Athens, Stevie (Jenna) Boneske of Cornell, Shane Boneske of Wausau, and Selena Boneske of Athens; and 14 great-grandchildren.

He was preceded in death by his parents; brother, Jerry Boneske; and brother-in-law, Don Zietlow.

Services will take place later this year. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

The family would like to thank the first responders for their care and support.

records.name: Marlene D. Heinz
records.summary: Marlene D. Heinz, age 83, of Wausau passed away on June 10, 2026.
records.body:
She was born October 2, 1942 in Wausau, daughter of the late Aloysius and Anna (Lang) Schilling. On July 7, 1962, she married the love of her life, Milton "Smokey" Heinz in Wausau. He preceded her in death on June 23, 2025.

Marlene's greatest passion was gardening. She loved tending to her flowers both indoors and outdoors. She liked knitting, crocheting and hosting family gatherings for all holidays, birthdays and family reunions. Marlene was a longtime member of Holy Name Catholic Church and enjoyed donating items for several church events. Her faith was strong and she was always by her husband, Smokey's side.

Survivors include two special nieces, Lisa (Brian) Buchberger and Chris Wintersteen; and many nieces, nephews, relatives and friends.

Besides her parents and husband, Smokey, she was preceded in death by six siblings, George Schilling, Armella Bendele, Orville Schilling, Evelyn Winter, twin sister-Darlene Kapitz and Gary Schilling.

Funeral services will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Thursday, June 18, 2026 at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau. Rev. Woodrow Pace will officiate. Burial will be in Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau. Visitation will be on Thursday from 9:00 a.m. until time of service at the funeral home.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Princeton Alexander Ferguson
records.summary: Princeton Alexander Ferguson was born still on June 8, 2026.
records.body:
Princeton was born still on Monday, June 8, 2026, to his mother, Ariana Ferguson, at Marshfield Medical Center – Weston.

Princeton is loved by his mother, Ariana; grandma, Crystal (Todd Jones) Dalbec; aunt, Jazelle Ferguson; uncle, Treyvon Jones; and great-grandmothers, Bonnie (Dwayne Ricci) Wadinski and Elaine Dalbec. He is preceded in death by his aunt, Lexi Dalbec.

Funeral service will be held at 6:30 PM on Wednesday, June 17, 2026, at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston, WI, 54776. Visitation will be held from 5:00 PM until the time of service at Ascend.

Princeton's family wishes to extend their gratitude to the Birthing Center staff at Marshfield Medical Center – Weston for their special love and care.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Randy Reissman
records.summary: Randy Reissman, age 68, of Wittenberg died on June 12, 2026.
records.body:
Randy was born on April 30, 1958, the son of Ervin Reissman and Delores Bernitt.

On June 6, 1986, Randy was united in marriage to Mary Grulkowski in Wausau. The couple just celebrated 40 years of marriage.

Randy worked as a carpenter for Low Brothers Construction and Dun-Rite Exteriors. Randy made many lasting friendships throughout his years in construction and often said that working for these companies felt like being part of a family. He took great pride in his work and valued the relationships he built with his coworkers over the years.

While he enjoyed his work, his true passion was farming. Randy loved spending time outdoors, whether he was ice fishing or hunting. Some of his most treasured memories were made on hunting trips to Colorado with his brothers and friends and his trip to Alaska with Mary and friends. He also enjoyed watching the Packers and playing Sheepshead and took great pride in teaching the game to his grandchildren. Randy cherished the time he spent with his family. Attending his children's and grandchildren's sporting events brought him immense joy. He loved his kids and all of the kids he raised. He was incredibly proud of his son when he became a United States Marine and later decided to work in the agriculture field. Randy took great pride in watching his children and grandchildren grow and achieve their goals.

Randy is survived by his wife, Mary; his father, Ervin; two children, Samantha Grulkowski and Matt (Stephanie Laug) Reissman; seven grandchildren, Alexis (Josh Newbury) Lorbecke, Jersey Bradham, Haiden Reissman, Bryce Reissman, Qiarie Burns, Riven Reissman and Dax Reissman; three great-grandchildren, Braxton Lorbecke, Xavier Monrreal and Nova Newbury; mother-in-law, Veronica Grulkowski; siblings, Pam Thiex, Nancy Reinke, Lori (Dave) Hedtke, Larry (Diane) Reissman, Julie (Chuck) Natzke, Shirley (Chuck) Wendler, Richie (Liz) Reissman and Paul (Holly) Reissman; brother-in-law, Joe Grulkowski, sister-in-law, Jesse Sadenwasser and many nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends.

Randy was preceded in death by his mother, Delores Haase and father-in-law, Robert Grulkowski.

A Funeral Service will be held at 11 AM, on Friday, June 19, 2026, at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg. Vicar Jim Hartleben will officiate. Visitation will be on Thursday, June 18, 2026, from 5PM until 7PM. Visitation will continue on Friday, from 9:30 AM until the time of service, all at the funeral home.

The family would like to thank Dave and Sue and Bruce and Joy for their company and playing cards with Randy over the last six months, it brought Randy so much comfort. Also, a thank you to Zach and Chuck for helping with the farm work.

Memories and messages of support may be shared at schmidtschulta.com

records.name: Thomas J Wolfe
records.summary: Thomas J Wolfe, age 79 passed away on June 15, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas J. Wolfe (Wolfeman) passed away Monday, June 15, 2026, while under Comfort Care at Aspirus Hospital. He was born April 9, 1947, to Norman and Katherine Wolfe who preceded him in death. He is survived by his wife, Paula, with whom he celebrated their 56th wedding anniversary on April 25, 2026, and their son Jermey, the pride of his life, and daughter-in-law, Tiffany. He is also survived by his siter Karin (Bob Thomas); brothers and sisters-in-law, Roseanne Helm, Colette and Bob Monroe, and Rita and Randy Romanoski; and nieces and nephews.

Tom was a graduate of Newman High School. After high school, he served in the U.S. Air Force and was stationed in Grand Forks, ND as a jet engine mechanic. This is where he met Paula and got married. After the birth of Jermey, his service in the Air Force ended and he returned to the Wausau area with his family, eventually settling in Kronenwetter in 1976, where he lived until his passing.

Tom worked mainly in construction and as an over-the-road truck driver for WH Transportation for most of his career. He loved to tell stories of his travels to anyone that he met, and anywhere he went, someone always seemed to know him.

Tom played softball, volleyball, golf, and pool leagues. He also enjoyed hunting and hanging out with the crew at the hunting shack. He loved tinkering with his train set, fishing, and woodworking. He was an avid fan of the Packers, Brewers, and Bucks, and he followed auto racing, and many other sports. Vacationing in Puerto Vallarta, greeting the morning with a tequila sunrise and watching the waves, was a trip he looked forward to almost every year.

Burial will be at Peaceful Pines, Mosinee, WI.

A gathering of friends will be held at Relocation Pub, 1801 County Road XX, Kronenwetter, on Tuesday, July 14, 2026, from 4:00 pm until 7:00 pm. Please join his family and share your stories with them. A light meal will be served.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family. Friends and family may go to MWCS.WS to share stories and leave words of remembrance.

records.name: Wynn O. Jones
records.summary: Wynn O. Jones, age 94, passed away on June 10, 2026.
records.body:
Wynn was born on March 16, 1932, in Oshkosh, WI, to Gomer "Pappy" D. Jones and Grace "Dot" (Means) Jones. He graduated from Horicon High School and went on to attend Carroll College in Waukesha, WI and later graduated from the University of Wisconsin – Platteville. Wynn was also a Korean War Veteran who served in the U.S. Navy as a Seaman based in the Pacific from 1951 to 1953. In May of 2019, he had the privilege of taking the Never Forgotten Honor Flight out of Wausau, WI.

On December 19, 1953, Wynn married Joan A. Knutson. He loved to tell people the story of how they "met." Joan was one of his sister Jean's bridesmaids, and he had been assigned to "take care of her." He would smile and tell people that he never stopped. They were married for 71 years and had three boys: Paris O., Hugh E. and Wynn "Casey" O. Jones, II. Wynn and Joan raised them in Schofield, WI, retired and moved to Rhinelander, WI in 1997 and wintered in Hot Springs Village, AR as "snowbirds" for 30 years. They also really enjoyed travelling. Included in their international adventures were over 25 cruises as well as trips with each of their 6 grandchildren for all their high school graduation presents.

Wynn worked in sales and laboratory design with Milwaukee Equipment Co. for 23 years before becoming their president. In 1982, he started Wynn O. Jones & Associates, Inc., grew it successfully, remained involved until 2002, and his company continues to flourish. He was also very active in the community, including Rotary, elder at First Presbyterian Church of Wausau, WI, member of Village Bible Church, Hot Springs Village, AR, and as a founding member of Covenant Community Presbyterian Church, Schofield, WI. He was a faithful choir member in all the churches he attended.

Family was always very important to Wynn, who was also affectionately called "Taid", which means grandfather in Welsh. His Welsh heritage was not the only thing that bound the Jones family together. In the 1920s, his father, "Pappy" purchased land on a lake near Rhinelander, WI, where 6 generations have come together over the years, built cottages and homes, and continued family traditions; playing cribbage, picking blueberries, playing kick-the-can, getting thrown in the lake with your clothes on when you turn 12, and getting a silver dollar when you swim the lake's channel without assistance. Relatives travel to Jones Point from a variety of states for picnics on all three summer holidays, where homemade ice cream is served and the doxology sung, just as it has been for over 100 years.

Wynn loved to work with his hands and was always ready to start or help with a project. He was an outdoorsman who played golf, flew private planes, loved watersports, and was an avid hunter and fisherman. He was also known for always having a Golden Retriever by his side, taking a daily "morning dip" in the lake and playing cribbage with Joanie every night before supper. Wynn made sure to invest time in playing with his grandchildren/great-grandchildren and regularly charted their growth by marking it on a door frame. Most importantly, Wynn was a man of faith and said at his and Joanie's 50th wedding celebration, "If you have Jesus Christ, you can get through…just anything."

Remaining to cherish Wynn's memory are his wife Joan; and their sons, Paris (Lora-jean), Hugh (Diane), and Casey (Cindy) Jones; their grandchildren, Nichole (Scott) Anderson, Gwen (Dan) Rizk, Leah (Jed) Lechleitner, Evan (Christy) Jones, Owen (Jenna) Jones, Bennett (Christina) Jones, and 17 precious great-grandchildren. He is preceded in death by his parents, Gomer and Grace Jones; his two sisters, Sue (Bruce) Gohl and Jean Radke; and brother-in-law Tom Radke.

A memorial gathering to celebrate his life on earth and now in Heaven with His Lord and Savior will be held at 11:00 AM on Monday, June 22, 2026, at Covenant Community Presbyterian Church, 1806 Weston Avenue, Schofield, WI. Pastors Jim Gates and Larry McKensie will officiate. Visitation will be held from 10:00 AM until the time of the service at the church. Military Honors will be accorded immediately following the service, with a luncheon to follow in the church fellowship hall. Inurnment will take place in Pine Grove Cemetery, Wausau, at a later date.

In lieu of flowers, donations in Wynn's memory can be made to Covenant Community Presbyterian Church.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Debra Lynn Narloch
records.summary: Debra Lynn Narloch, age 71 passed away on June 16, 2026.
records.body:
It is with heavy hearts that we announce the passing of Debra Lynn Narloch, our beloved mother and grandmother, who left us on the afternoon of June 16, 2026. She passed away peacefully, surrounded by her loving family, after a fierce and courageous battle with kidney failure and subsequent health complications.

Born March 21, 1955, in Wausau, WI. Deb was the daughter of Raymond Kolbeck and Elaine Carpenter. She attended Wausau West High School and beautician school in Green Bay, WI and later moved to West Allis, Wi with the love of her life, Donald. The couple was united in marriage on July 19, 1975, at Mt. Calvary Lutheran Church in Rothschild, WI. After starting their family in West Allis, they returned to Rothschild where they raised their two children.

From a young age Deb started working. Her parents’ business Coral Lanes Bowling Ally, Coal Lanes Root beer stand, The Palms Supper Club, and a hairdresser. She cut family and friends’ hair throughout her life.

After several years, Deb and Don embarked on the adventure of owning and operating their own business, Quality Carpets of Wausau and QCF Supply, for approximately 22 years before retiring. Over the 22 years Deb enjoyed being a member of the Wausau Westside Business Association and assisting in returning the annual Christmas Parade to Wausau and joyed that it continues.

Following retirement Deb and Don relocated to their little piece of heaven in Tomahawk, WI. Deb enjoyed her beautiful gardens, generational flowers, canning, and campfires. Family time was important to Deb. She would make sure that all the right (in excess) snacks were packed for her cherished time with her grandchildren, family, and friends on Lake Nokomis, cruising, fishing (the competitions), and “beaching it”. Deb found her best times in life with the love of her life Don in Tomahawk, traveling, berry picking, and spending time together hand in hand. She kept busy with friends at Taco Tuesday, strong bodies, and enjoying card parties with her good friends during annual “hunting widows weekend” and Birthday celebrations.

Deb is survived by her son Bradey (Milissa) Narloch Ringle, Bethany Narloch Wausau, Hunter Narloch Weston, and Addison Narloch Weston, Sister Linda Kent Weston, Brother Mike (Mary) Kolbeck, Sisters Deb Mortensen, Kitty Kramer, and Pam Carpenter.

Deb is preceded in death by her husband Donald, parents, Raymond Kolbeck and Elaine Carpenter (Klemp), older sister Dianne Lynn, and Brother Steve Kolbeck.

Special Thanks to many in moms declining health. Aspirus Dialysis Team (especially Nate), long term kidney doctor Dr. Abrego, Dr. Raymond Hardtke and the GI Associates team.

Special thanks to Debs best friend Mary Detterming.

Special thanks to Pastor Kathy for her spiritual support to Deb, the family, and for officiating.

A celebration of Deb’s life will take place on July 18th, 2026, at Mt. Calvary Lutheran Church in Rothschild, WI with Pastor Kathy officiating. Visitation 9am-11am Service at 11am. Mid-Wisconsin Cremation Society assisted the family with cremation services. Online condolences may be given at MWCS.WS

In lieu of flowers, the family requests that cards and memorial contributions be directed to them.

records.name: R. Ross Plant
records.summary: R. Ross Plant, age 96, of Kronenwetter passed away on June 16, 2026.
records.body:
R. Ross Plant Jr., of Kronenwetter, passed away peacefully on June 16, 2026, at his home.

Ross was born on May 24, 1930, to the late Russell and Eleanore (Scheel) Plant in Wausau, WI. He married the love of his life N. June Zich on April 12, 1952. June died earlier this year. They were married almost 74 years.

Ross worked for American Can/James River for 35 years in mostly supervisory positions. He worked in Wausau WI, Chambersburg PA, and Neenah WI.

Ross, along with June, loved to fish and travel. They traveled to Canada for forty years to go fishing and enjoyed great success. Ross and June also spent many winters in Florida after Ross' retirement. They were also able to travel to many other destinations in the US.

Ross enjoyed woodworking, especially carving. He carved a collection of whimsical wooden figures that were displayed in the house.

Ross and June were never without a dog or two by their side. They had dogs of all sizes from small terriers to Great Danes throughout their married life.

He is survived by his brother, Wayne (Judy) Plant and former sister-in-law, Diane Plant and his children; son, Michael (Cynthia) Plant; and daughters, Cheryl (Paul) Jahns, Susan (Rick) Paszkot, and Barb (Keith) Friedlein; grandchildren, Kelly (Wade) Puntney, Christopher (Kirby) Plant, Matthew Plant, Brian (Amy) Jahns, Andy (Carly) Jahns, Aimee (Kyle Buehler) Paszkot, Jonathan (Jaewoo) Paszkot, David (Piper) Pozorski, Laura Friedlein, and Lucas Friedlein; great-grandchildren, Lana, Ethan, Tenley, Harlow, Henry, Violet, Emily, William, Ryan, Rhys, Lucy, Vincent, and Trinity. He is preceded in death by his parents and wife June.

A memorial service for both Ross and June will be held on Saturday, July 11, 2026, at 11:00 A.M., at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston. Visitation will be held from 10:00 A.M. until the time of service.

"Our family would like to thank Ross and June's closest neighbors for all their help and support".

We would like to give a special thank-you to Kelly Miller for helping to care for our mom and dad over the last 6 months. Also, thank you to Cornerstone caregiving, Compassus Hospice, and Interim Hospice for all the care and support given to Ross and June.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Robert "Bob" Ronald Hoff
records.summary: Robert "Bob" Ronald Hoff, age 82, of Berlin passed away on Jun 16, 2026.
records.body:
Robert 'Bob' Ronald Hoff of Wausau, age 82, went to his heavenly home on June 16, 2026, at his home. He was born February 15, 1944, in the town of Berlin, to the late William and Dorothy (Oelke) Hoff. He was united in marriage to LouAnn D. Seehafer at St. Johns Lutheran Church, in the town of Rib Falls, WI, on July 3, 1965.

Bob worked hard to provide a good life for his family, not only having a full-time job but growing ginseng, helping maintain his in-laws farm, all while building his and LouAnns' dream home. Bob was a social man, and was well known in the neighborhood for being willing and eager to talk to anyone. No one stayed a stranger for long around Bob. His greatest joy, however, was spending time with his grandsons, Bobby and Joe.

Bob is survived by his children Lynda (Victor) Smith of Yelm, WA; Robin (Deb) Hoff of Marathon, WI; and Renea (Zachary) Kania of Marathon, WI; Grandchildren Eric Smith, Kytaira (Casey) Sowles, Robert A. Hoff, and Joseph Kania; step-grandchildren Thomas (Nicole) Cimino, Jr., Don Cimino, and Amanda (Tim) Schmidt; Step-Great-Grandchildren Amelia Cimino; Layla and Carter Schmidt; and Connor and Kenley Cimino; He is also survived by many brothers and sisters, as well as nieces and nephews.

Robert was preceded death by his parents, his beloved wife LouAnn; brothers Donald Hoff and James Hoff; and sisters Elaine Schade and Pearl Voigt.

A memorial service will be held at St. John Immanuel Lutheran Church, 5304 North 44th Avenue, Wausau WI 54401, (Village of Maine), on Monday, July 20, 2026, at 11:00 AM. Reverend Maggie Westaby will officiate. A visitation will be held at church from 9:30 AM until the time of the service.

Helke Funeral Home & Cremation Center of Wausau is honored to assist Bob’s family with the arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.helke.com.

records.name: Thomas James Hintz
records.summary: Thomas James Hintz, age 65 passed away on June 16, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas James Hintz of Rosholt, passed away peacefully on June 16th, 2026, at the age of 65 surrounded by his loving family after a tough 3 year battle with a rare B-cell Lymphoma cancer in the brain.

Tom was born June 3rd, 1961, in Milwaukee, WI to the late Raymond and Henrietta (Singer) Hintz. He grew up in the Rosholt area, attending St. Adalberts Catholic School and Rosholt High School, graduating from Rosholt High School in 1979.

On September 12th, 1987, Tom was united in marriage to Jacqueline (Wierzba) Hintz at St. Ladislaus Church in Bevent. They shared just shy of 39 wonderful years together making memories with their 3 children and 5 grandchildren.

Tom worked as a Line Leader at Greenheck Fan in Schofield for 32 years. In his free time, Tom found great joy in the outdoors. He loved hunting, fishing, camping and working out on his land making food plots and firewood. In his earlier years he enjoyed ice fishing, snowmobiling and traveling out west and up to Canada on hunting trips with friends and family.

As his family grew, he passed those traditions on to his children, teaching them not only how to hunt and fish, but also to appreciate nature and the simple joys of life. He made it a point to take his family camping several times each year, exploring campgrounds and national forests treasuring the peace and quiet of the outdoors. He always made sure to make special memories filled with family fun, laughter and excitement.

Tom had a gentle spirit and an exceptionally kind heart. He was the kind of person who would drop everything to lend a helping hand to anybody who needed it. His words carried wisdom, sincerity and trust; everybody always knew they could count on him.

Tom’s unwavering love, quiet strength and selfless devotion to his family will never be forgotten. His legacy lives on in the memories he created, the values he instilled and the countless lives he has touched.

Tom is survived by his wife Jackie; his Children: Margaret (Sam) Napiwocki, Kimberley (Zack) Boettcher and Travis (Taylor Krogwold) Hintz; his grandchildren: Wesley, Oaklee and Jaxon Boettcher and Lincoln and Walker Napiwocki. He was further survived by his siblings: Scott (Susan) Hintz, Lisa (Jim) Richter, Diane (Wayne) Garski, Jenny (Chad) Malek; and his brothers and sisters-in-law: (Diane) Wierzba, (Pauline) Wierzba, Norbert (Bernie) Wierzba, Jeanette Gollon, (Russell) Miller, James Wierzba, (Elaine) Wierzba, Joe Wierzba, Marcie (Dennis) Hintz, Joyce (Jeff) Krukowski, Joan (Patrick) O’Neil, Judy (Larry) Osowski, Mary (Tom) Lewandowski, Norman (Brenda) Wierzba and many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents Raymond and Henrietta Hintz; his father and mother in-law John and Margaret Wierzba; his brothers-in-law: Johnny Wierzba, Myron Wierzba, Wayne Gollon, Jerome Wierzba; sister-in-law Marlene Miller, and nieces Nicole Richter and Sammy Jo Kurdydyk.

A Funeral Mass will be held at 11:30 AM, at St. Ladislaus Catholic Church in Bevent, WI on Monday, June 29th, 2026. Rev. Augustine Bentil will officiate. Visitation will be held from 9AM until the time of Mass at the church.

The family would like to thank Dr. Lawler and his team at Marshfield Clinic; Dr. Durani and her team at Mayo Clinic in Rochester, MN; and Compassus Hospice Care team for their care and compassion throughout Tom’s journey.

Memories and messages of support may be shared at schmidtschulta.com

records.name: Barbara J. Beck
records.summary: Barbara J. Beck, 82, a long-time resident of Marshfield, Wisconsin, passed away on June 12, 2026.
records.body:
Barbara J Beck (nee Kappler), 82, a long-time resident of Marshfield, WI passed away at Woodland Court Elder Services of Merrill, on June 12, 2026. Barbara was born September 1943 to Alvin and Hazel Kappler in Jackson, MI. She was a graduate of Manchester High School class of 1961. She married the love of her life, John Beck, on May 18, 1974.

Barbara was an exchange student in the Netherlands upon graduation, and she volunteered many years for Youth for Understanding, including serving on the National Volunteer Advisory Council. She worked in tourism and hospitality for many years including as a travel agency manager, and hotel sales and banquet director. Barbara was a Board Member of the Marshfield Convention and Business Bureau for 12 years and was a volunteer at many events. She also worked as Personnel Officer and Employee Benefits Manager at Valley Bank in Appleton.

Barbara was active in Business & Professional Women/USA for many years. She received many awards including State Woman of Distinction 2005, Regional Woman of the Year 2006, State Member of Inspiration 2007, and State & National Issues Management Award 2007.

Barbara was an active member of Immanuel Lutheran Church in Marshfield and a member of the Women's Guild. She was a proud member of the Daughters of the American Revolution.

Survivors include her husband John Beck of Merrill, brothers-in-laws David (Betty) Beck of Menomonee Falls, Keith Beck of Wausau, and sisters-in-law Mary (Dale) Gohdes of Wausau, Suzanne Beck of Aniwa; and numerous nieces and nephews. She was preceded in death by her parents, Alvin and Hazel Kappler, brother James Kappler, and brothers-in-law Robert and Allen Beck, and nephew James Beck.

A Celebration of Life Service will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, June 20, at Immanuel Lutheran Church in Marshfield, Wisconsin. Family and friends may call at the church beginning at 9:30 a.m. until the time of service. Private burial will be at Central Wisconsin Veterans Memorial Cemetery in King, Wisconsin at a later date.

The family would like to thank the staff of Pine Crest NH, Woodland Court Elder Services, and St Croix Hospice for their kind attentions to Barbara. In lieu of flowers, donations to Immanuel Lutheran Church, Marshfield are appreciated.

Family and friends may go to MWCS.WS to leave words of remembrance.

records.name: Ellanora L. Bessey
records.summary: Ellanora L. Bessey, 99, of Merrill, passed away on June 10, 2026.
records.body:
Ellanora L. Bessey, age 99, of Merrill, passed away Wednesday, June 10, 2026.

Ellanora was born April 23, 1927, in the Town of Corning, daughter of Henry and Martha (Voigt) Kienbaum. She married John E. "Jack" Bessey on May 22, 1948, at St. Stephens United Church of Christ, Merrill. He preceded her in death on February 8, 1991, while residing in Texas.

After their marriage, Ellanora and Jack lived in Wisconsin Rapids for a short time before moving to California. They lived in California for 25 years and then moved to New Mexico where they lived for six years. In 1982, they moved to Texas. Ellanora resided in Texas until April of 2014, when she moved to Merrill to be closer to her siblings.

Ellanora worked as a cook at various restaurants, and while in New Mexico, she leased a gourmet crepe restaurant. Ellanora worked in Alaska at Denali National Park for nine summers. Most of her work was done at the gift shop at the park. The last summer Ellanora spent in Alaska was 2008.

Ellanora enjoyed cooking and baking, especially birthday cakes for family members. She also enjoyed sewing and knitting and taught knitting class in California. Ellanora volunteered at a hospital in Longview, Texas and was a 50+ year member of the Eastern Star Masonic Lodge in California.

Survivors include her son, David Bessey of Woodville, Texas; grandson, Kevin (Kendra) Bessey of The Woodlands, Texas; brother, Gordon Kienbaum of Birchwood, Wisconsin; four great-grandchildren, Clara, Cayman, Coralee, and Chance; two great-great-grandchildren, Wrenlee and Everleigh; step-grandson, Quentin (Stephanie) Trautner of Winnsboro, Texas; step-great-grandchildren, Laney, Kason, Kinley, and Addison; daughter-in-law, Virginia Bessey of Longview, Texas, and several nieces and nephews.

Ellanora was preceded in death by her husband; parents; daughter, Tamara Dillard; granddaughter Brandy Baldridge; step-grandson, Robert Coleman; sisters, Rosemary (Art) Reichelt and Dorothy (Edwin) Bahlow; brother, Henry (Eldina) Kienbaum; sister-in-law, Louise Kienbaum.

Visitation will be held at St. Stephens United Church of Christ in Merrill, on Saturday, June 27, 2026, from 9 am to 11 am, Funeral service to follow at 11 am.

Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Ervin F. Jagodzinski
records.summary: Ervin F. Jagodzinski, 95, of Weston, formerly of Edgar, passed away on June 14, 2026.
records.body:
Ervin F. Jagodzinski, 95, of Weston, formerly of Edgar, passed away on Sunday, June 14, 2026, at Wausau Manor, under the care of Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice Services.

He was born on July 7, 1930, to the late Paul and Balbina (Knetter) Jagodzinski in Edgar. Ervin married Arlean Butalla on April 7, 1953, at St. Mary Catholic Church in Marathon, and recently celebrated 73 years together.

Ervin will be remembered by those close to him as a hard worker, a person that was physically and mentally strong, and one that had a great sense of humor. His contribution to the cheese and dairy industry was highly regarded and appreciated. For 38 years, Ervin ran a state-of-the-art cheese factory that was a model for production. Ervin was a licensed cheese maker and was a lifetime member of the Central Wisconsin Cheesemakers and Buttermakers Association. Over the years he had cheesemakers from all over the world through his factory to see how new equipment could be used in their own factories.

Outside of work, Ervin was a lifetime member of St. John the Baptist Catholic Church, a member of the Holy Name Society, a 50+ year member of the Edgar Lions Club, and an early member of the Poniatowski Dare Devils. He loved snowmobiling with family and friends, working in the woods, four-wheeling, and cutting the grass. He also enjoyed making firewood with his sons and son-in-law. Ervin was quite the dancer in his early years and loved dancing polkas and waltzes with Arlean. He enjoyed a good game of Sheepshead with family and friends as well.

Survivors include his wife, Arlean; children, Dale Jagodzinski, Diane (David) Konsella, Daryl Jagodzinski (Brenda Williams), and Daryn Jagodzinski (Mary Rosier); grandchildren, Kathleen (Michael) Maher, Kristine Konsella, and Jennie (Davin) Konsella-Norene; great-grandchildren, Priela and Matthew Maher and Madelyn, Dylan, and Parker Norene; sisters, Joan Wagner, Janet Trawicki, and Theodora Theiler; and other relatives and friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents and numerous brothers and sisters-in-law.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. on Tuesday, June 23, 2026, at St. John the Baptist Catholic Church, Edgar, with Rev. Alan Wierzba presiding. Visitation will begin at 9:00 a.m. until the time of service at the church. Burial will follow in St. Johns Catholic Cemetery, Edgar. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

A special thank you from the family to the nurses and staff at Wausau Manor and to Nina and Dawn from Aspirus Palliative Care for the compassionate care that Ervin received.

Memorial donations are suggested to St. John the Baptist Catholic Church or School, Edgar.

records.name: Gary Lee Tullberg
records.summary: Gary Lee Tullberg passed away on June 13, 2026.
records.body:
Gary Lee Tullberg passed away on Saturday, June 13th, 2026, after a valiant battle against diabetes. He was surrounded by family always and at the time of his death. Gary lived in Wittenberg most of his life and lived a full and adventurous life. Gary was a great cook and baker, bartender, decorator, artist, Gardner, dog whisperer, and doting Uncle.
