Gary was generous, always making sure the kids in the family grew up learning about animals and parades and the circus, to name a few. He literally traveled with a circus exploring the US. He kept current on life events and was passionate about many things.

In recent years, he enjoyed attending Polka festivals in the summer because in his words, "everyone there is so happy." He enjoyed his trips to the casino and constantly tried new games to play and gained new friends wherever he went.

Gary was preceded in death by his father, Ronald Lee Tullberg, paternal grandparents, Raymond and Dorothy Tullberg; maternal grandmother, Winifred Foreman, and aunts, uncles, and cousins.

Gary is survived by his mother, Greta Tullberg; sisters, Julie (Dale) Grulkowski, Tina Tullberg, Tammy Tullberg and brother, Jamie Tullberg. He is also survived by nephews, Andrew, Joshua, Jonathon, Marcus, and niece, Dior, and great nephews and niece, Matthew, Isaac, and Maple. Gary is survived by many cousins, three aunts and an uncle. He is also survived by many special friends who he remained close with: Tom, Luane, Leslie, Kerry, Tabitha and Luke, as well as his special dogs, Molly and Prince.

A Celebration of Life Picnic will be held at Washington Park, 104 Park Street, Wittenberg, WI 54499, on Sunday, June 28, 2026, from 2-4PM.

In lieu of flowers, please donate to the diabetes foundation.

records.name: Jamie Ann (Westberg) Mendrzycki
records.summary: Jamie Ann (Westberg) Mendrzycki, 71, died peacefully at Dimensions Living of Wausau on June 14, 2026.
records.body:
Jamie Ann (Westberg) Mendrzycki 71, died peacefully at Dimensions Living of Wausau on June14, 2026. She was married to Michael Mendrzycki in Wausau, WI on October, 1981. They were married 35 years until the death of her husband. They had three sons: Nathan (Wisconsin Rapids, WI); Troy (Minneapolis, MN); and Brian (Madison, WI).

They made their home in Wisconsin Rapids, WI. Jamie was a member of Calvery Baptist Church in Wisconsin Rapids, WI. She worked at Copp's Foods in the deli department for many years. She loved her family and serving others. We loved her smile.

Funeral arrangements at Bethany Baptist Church in Weston, WI on Saturday, June 27 with 10:00 visitation and 11:00 service. She will be buried at Veterans Cemetery, King, WI next to her husband.

She was preceded in death by her husband Michael Leo Mendrzycki; her father John (Jack) Westberg; her mother Betty (Fenhaus) Westberg; two brothers Jay and Kris (Lori) Westberg. She is survived by her sisters Bonnie (Kenneth) Hall (Lakeland, Florida & DeForest, WI); and Kari Cline (Wausau, WI) and a brother Jonathan Westberg (Pewaukee, WI).

The family would like to thank the devoted staff at Dimensions Living of Wausau and Interim Healthcare of Wausau-Hospice for their loving care.

Family and friends may go to MWCS.WS to leave words of remembrance.

records.name: Kathleen R. Miskowiak
records.summary: Kathleen R. Miskowiak, 76, of Wausau, passed away on June 13, 2026.
records.body:
Kathleen R. Miskowiak, 76 of Wausau, passed away on Saturday, June 13, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

She was born October 20, 1949, in Wausau. Kathy was one of seven children of the late Frank and Virginia (Konkol) Miskowiak.

Kathy was employed at Mount View Care Center of Wausau. She always enjoyed taking her nieces shopping while not working.

Survivors include four brothers, Gary (Patty) Miskowiak, Yukon, OK, Gene, Russell (Lenore), and Pat (Connie) Miskowiak, all of Wausau; sister-in-law, Diane Miskowiak, Wausau; Kathy's nephews and nieces, Douglas and Michael Miskowiak, Christine Burton, Meredith Nelson, Jerry Otto, Ryan, Aaron, and Katie Miskowiak, Tara De La Cruz, Curtis and Jacob Miskowiak, Sandra Larson, Scott Miskowiak, Steven Miskowiak, Jody Radke; uncle, Robert (Donna) Konkol.

Besides her parents, she was preceded in death by a brother, Alan, sister, Vienna Dallman and her nephew, Chad Miskowiak.

Graveside service will be 11:00 a.m. Tuesday, June 23, 2026, at Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau. Visitation will be Tuesday from 10:00 a.m. until the time of burial in the Memorial Chapel Mausoleum, Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Kevin Richard Damask
records.summary: Kevin Richard Damask, age 53 passed away on June 17, 2026.
records.body:
Kevin R. Damask, 53, of Hatley, passed away unexpectedly on Wednesday, June 17, 2026.

Kevin was born on June 7, 1973, in Wausau, the youngest son of Lawrence and Susan (Esker) Damask.

He graduated from Wittenberg-Birnamwood High School and later attended Northcentral Technical College, where he earned a degree in Agricultural Technical Mechanics.

Kevin spent several years helping his parents on the family farm. Throughout his life, he worked in a variety of occupations, including concrete work, pole building construction, and most recently in maintenance at First Impressions. A gifted problem solver, Kevin took pride in his work and used his talents to build a beautiful gate for his parents.

Kevin had a deep love for horses and worked hard training and breaking several of them. He also enjoyed spending time outdoors, especially when he could be with his horse, Monty.

Kind-hearted and devoted to those he loved, Kevin cherished his family, particularly his niece and nephews. He will be remembered for his generosity and caring nature.

Kevin is survived by his parents, Lawrence and Susan Damask; his brothers, Ken (Shannon) Damask and Chris (Brenda) Damask; his niece and nephews, Brandon, Melanie, Dalton, and Coleman; along with many other relatives and friends.

He was preceded in death by his grandparents.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00am, on Friday, June 26, 2026, at St. Florian Catholic Church in Hatley, with Father Jerome Patric presiding. Burial will follow in the parish cemetery.

Visitation will be held Friday at the church from 9:00 a.m. until the time of Mass.

Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home of Wittenberg is assisting the family. Memories and messages of support may be shared at www.schmidtschulta.com.

records.name: Merle LaVerne (Bud) Schultz
records.summary: Merle LaVerne (Bud) Schultz, 94, died on April 1, 2026, in Ketchikan, Alaska.
records.body:
Merle LaVerne (Bud) Schultz, 94, died on April 1, 2026 in Ketchikan, Alaska.

He was born on April 3, 1931, in Wausau, Wisconsin. He attended Wausau East High School and Wisconsin Technical School.

Mr. Schultz served in the U.S. Marine Corps from 1951 to 1954.

On March 21, 1954 he and his wife, Beverly were married in Anaheim, California.

He was employed by Wisconsin Public Service as a lead lineman from 1956 to 1977, in both Wausau and Rhinelander, Wisconsin.

Mr. and Mrs. Schultz then moved to Ward Cove where he resided for 49 years.

The couple and their son, Jeff, and daughter, Debra, owned and operated the Ward Cove Market for many years before deciding to officially retire and concentrate on family, hobbies and traveling.

In addition to travel, he enjoyed hunting, fishing and woodworking. He was also well-known as the maker of the "Bucka Deer Call" which was sold in Alaska and in other states.

He was a member of the Clover Pass Community Church, which he helped to build.

Mr. Schultz was preceded in death by his father, Erwin Schultz, mother, Mabel (Ramthun) Schultz, and daughter, Debra Louise Burkhart.

He is survived by his wife, Beverly, of Ward Cove; sons, Jeffrey (Nancy) of Ketchikan and Jay (Jean) of Wasilla, AK, son-in-law, Don Burkhart of Ketchikan; grandchildren, Aaron, Jennifer, Jeremy, Jacob and Joshua (Sarah) Schultz, and Taylor Burkhart; great-grandchildren, Azariah (Emily), Jhaelah, Alaina, Aria, Mason, Myah, Calla and Albert; and great-great-grandchild, Jeannie Marie.

A memorial service will be announced at a later date.

records.name: Reese Anthony McCoy-Giltner
records.summary: Reese Anthony McCoy-Giltner, age 24, of Indianapolis passed away on Jun 17, 2026.
records.body:
Reese Anthony McCoy-Giltner, age 24, of Indianapolis, Indiana, and formerly of Mosinee, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly on June 17, 2026.

Born on October 29, 2001, to Nora McCoy and Ethan Giltner, born in Tampa, Florida. Reese was a proud 2020 graduate of Mosinee High School. He went on to the University of Wisconsin-Stevens Point, where he accomplished something truly remarkable—earning two degrees simultaneously: a Bachelor of Arts in Political Science and Government and a Bachelor of Science in Public Relations, Advertising, and Applied Communication, and a minor in Creative Writing graduating in May 2024 with a 4.0 on the Dean’s List.

Throughout his college years, Reese threw himself into campus life with the same passion he brought to everything. He served as a Student Government Associate, finding his voice in advocacy and policy. He nurtured his creative soul through the Rising Voices Poetry Club and the Writer's Club, where his words often left rooms quiet and moved and also won writing competitions where his poetry was published. He connected with fellow adventurers through the VGE/Video Game Guild Club and remained actively involved in PAO—always building community wherever he went.

But Reese didn't just study change—he lived it. He was deeply involved in his community, guided by an extraordinary gift for showing up when it mattered most. He ran political campaigns for different candidates here in Wisconsin and Indiana. At the time of his passing, Reese was working for Easterseals in Indianapolis, where he dedicated himself to supporting individuals and families navigating their deepest and heaviest moments and advocating for their needs to better their lives. He had a rare and quiet strength — the kind that didn't need to fill the silence, but simply stayed present, offering steady encouragement and a hand to hold when life felt overwhelming.

Outside of work, Reese found joy and adventure in the sprawling worlds of role-playing games. He was a natural storyteller, weaving intricate tales that captivated his fellow players. You may know him as Reese, Morrigan, Raven, or other gamer tags but no matter which name used each was a reflection of his complex, guiding, and fiercely protective nature and huge heart, often known as a gentle giant. Just as he championed his companions in fantastical realms, Reese was a tireless advocate for those struggling in the real one, including helping found an organization Strong Neighbor in the Indy area helping provide food and necessities and even helping those escape domestic violence environments.

Reese was just beginning to find his way in this big, complicated world, and the path he was carving was one of profound purpose. He will be deeply missed beyond words.

Reese is survived by his devoted parents, Nora McCoy and his stepfather Chris Gulsvig, Ethan Giltner and stepmom Airin, brothers Giordan, Orion, and Sloan, and stepsister Lili Gulsvig, his Gram-Beth McCoy, grandparents James and Ann Marie Giltner, aunts and uncles cousins and even more extended family; and a wide circle of friends who will forever remember his sharp wit, his fierce loyalty, and the gentle way he carried others through their storms.

Please join us for his visitation being held from 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM on August 15, 2026, at Flowers of the Field (162203 County Rd C, Mosinee, WI 54455), followed immediately by a Celebration of Life ceremony and luncheon reception.

Reese's legacy of compassion, presence, and selfless service will forever echo in the lives he steadied during their hardest days. May his spirit always soar.

records.name: Thomas J. Wolfe
records.summary: Thomas J. Wolfe passed away on June 15, 2026, while under Comfort Care at Aspirus Hospital.
records.body:
Thomas J. Wolfe (Wolfeman) passed away Monday, June 15, 2026, while under Comfort Care at Aspirus Hospital. He was born April 9, 1947, to Norman and Katherine Wolfe who preceded him in death. He is survived by his wife, Paula, with whom he celebrated their 56th wedding anniversary on April 25, 2026, and their son Jermey, the pride of his life, and daughter-in-law, Tiffany. He is also survived by his sister Karin (Bob Thomas); brothers and sisters-in-law, Roseanne Helm, Colette and Bob Monroe, and Rita and Randy Romanoski; and nieces and nephews.

Tom was a graduate of Newman High School. After high school, he served in the U.S. Air Force and was stationed in Grand Forks, ND as a jet engine mechanic. This is where he met Paula and got married. After the birth of Jermey, his service in the Air Force ended and he returned to the Wausau area with his family, eventually settling in Kronenwetter in 1976, where he lived until his passing.

Tom worked mainly in construction and as an over-the-road truck driver for WH Transportation for most of his career. He loved to tell stories of his travels to anyone that he met, and anywhere he went, someone always seemed to know him.

Tom played softball, volleyball, golf, and pool leagues. He also enjoyed hunting and hanging out with the crew at the hunting shack. He loved tinkering with his train set, fishing, and woodworking. He was an avid fan of the Packers, Brewers, and Bucks, and he followed auto racing, and many other sports. Vacationing in Puerto Vallarta, greeting the morning with a tequila sunrise and watching the waves, was a trip he looked forward to almost every year.

Burial will be at Peaceful Pines, Mosinee, WI.

A gathering of friends will be held at Relocation Pub, 1801 County Road XX, Kronenwetter, on Tuesday, July 14, 2026, from 4:00 pm until 7:00 pm. Please join his family and share your stories with them. A light meal will be served.

Friends and family may go to MWCS.WS to share stories and leave words of remembrance.

records.name: Whitney R. Lindwall
records.summary: Whitney R. Lindwall, 94, of Wausau, passed away on June 8, 2026.
records.body:
Whitney R. Lindwall, 94, Wausau, passed away on Monday, June 8, 2026 at Mount View Care Center, Wausau, under the care of St. Croix Hospice. He was born on June 5, 1932 in Hinsdale, Illinois, the son of the late Ralph and Nanna (Swanson) Lindwall. He married Beatta "Bea" Allemann on March 17, 1956 at Zion Lutheran Church, Minneapolis.

Whitney was a Medical Specialist in the U.S. Army having completed Medical Field Service School Training at Fort Sam Houston, San Antonio, TX, and achieved the rank of First Lieutenant. He also served as Team Captain of the U.S. Army Pistol Team. After completing his doctorate in Food Science at the University of Minnesota he began his career in food science and product development holding key positions at Schweigert's, General Mills and International Multifoods. His hobbies and interests were many, and included traveling, home remodeling, landscaping, gardening, collecting antiques, playing Bridge and Scrabble, and extensively studying our family's genealogy.

Survivors include his wife, Bea, Wausau; one daughter, Karen (Theran) Rusch, Wausau; two grandsons, Matthew (Diana) Rusch, Papillion, NE, and David Rusch, Wausau; two great-grandsons, Finnigan and Liam, three great-granddaughters, Eliany, Diannelys, and Adielys, and his nieces and nephews.

Besides his parents, he was preceded in death by one daughter, Laura Lee, and one sister, Carol Holm.

No public services will be held in accordance with Whitney's wishes. Private burial will take place at a later date at Fort Snelling National Cemetery in Minnesota. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

Donations preferred to Rib Mountain Lutheran Church, 227150 Harrier Ave, Wausau, WI 54401.

records.name: Dale Hanne
records.summary: Dale Hanne, age 75, of Gilman passed away on June 18, 2026.
records.body:
It is with great sadness that we announce the passing of Dale W. Hanne, 75 of Gilman, Wisconsin. He passed away peacefully Thursday, June 18th, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Palliative Care with his daughter, Sheila, at his side, after losing a courageous health battle.

Dale was born January 23rd, 1951, to Elroy and Cecelia (Kramer) Hanne Sr. in Wood County, Wisconsin. He attended Colby Schools and became a truck driver immediately after, making it his lifelong career of over 45 years. He drove all over the continental United States loving every mile. He lived life to the fullest enjoying classic old vehicles, hunting, fishing, and spending time with family, good friends, and neighbors. He was always the life of the party, telling jokes and laughing. He loved making venison, grilling out, watching old western movies, making sausage, and listening to country music and the oldies with “Surfin Bird” as one of his favorites. He will be missed by everyone who had the pleasure of knowing him.

He leaves behind his loving wife and soulmate, Lynette, Daughters; Sheila (Jerry) Hauck, Heather (Kurt) Kulas, Jackie (Joel) Oechler and Hillary (Adam) Williams. Sons; Jeremy Heiden and Ricky Sazama. Grandchildren; Tyler, Kyle, Jaxson, Noah, Aaron, Hunter, Evelyn, and Bethany.

He was preceded in death by his parents, grandparents, daughter; Kristie and brother; Elory Jr.

A weekend of celebrating his life was held by his family per Dale’s request.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Donald A. Pyke
records.summary: Donald A. Pyke, age 76, of Arpin passed away on Jun 18, 2026.
records.body:
Donald A. Pyke, 76, Arpin, passed away on Thursday, June 18, 2026 at St. Vincent Hospital, Green Bay surrounded by family.

Funeral services will be held at 11:00 am on Thursday, June 25, 2026 at Trinity Ev. Lutheran Church, Marshfield with Rev. Tod Vertz officiating. Burial will follow at Arpin Cemetery, Arpin.

Visitation will take place from 4:00 pm - 7:00 pm on Wednesday, June 24 at Rembs Funeral Home, Marshfield and from 10:00 am until the time of service at church.

Don was born on September 28, 1949 in Milwaukee to Charles and Alice (Cline) Pyke. He was united in marriage to Annette Esser on October 16, 1971 at St. Francis Catholic Church in Arpin.

Don enjoyed working on classic cars, making maple syrup, boating, scuba diving, and spending time in Door County. Whether he was building, repairing, or inventing something with wood, metal, or motors, Don always seemed to be working on something. He was a true jack-of-all-trades. A self-taught musician, he found great joy in playing and listening to music. Don was a man of strong faith, often engaging in meaningful conversations around the campfire. Above all, he cherished his role as dad and grandpa and treasured every moment spent with his family. Don had a magnetic personality that naturally drew family and friends to him, and they knew they could always count on having a good time when he was around.

He is survived by his wife, Annette, daughters, Angelia (Gerald) David and Stephanie (Michael) Henning, grandchildren, Kaleb (Leah) David, Carson (Layla) David, Kiana David (fiancé - Evan Ellis), Ava Henning, Emmitt Henning, Maverick Henning, and Micah Henning, and great grandchildren, Daxton and Desmond David.

He is further survived by his mother, Alice, sisters, Sharon (Don) Brehm and Barbara (Zac) Bushmaker, and many nieces, nephews, and brothers and sisters in law.

He was preceded in death by his father, Charles.

The family would like to extend a special thanks to Pastor Vertz from Salem Ev Lutheran Church in Sturgeon Bay for his numerous visits and comforting words.

records.name: Janie M. Allen
records.summary: Janie M. Allen, age 65, of Merrill passed away on Jun 18, 2026.
records.body:
Janie M. Allen, 65, of Merrill, passed away Thursday, June 18, 2026, at her home under the care of her family and hospice services.

Janie was born April 6, 1961, in Geneva, Illinois, daughter to Robert and Patsy (Graves) Hinterlong. She graduated from the former Batavia Senior High School in Illinois. She married David G. Allen on July 9, 1983, in Batavia, Illinois. He preceded her in death on January 28, 2009. Janie worked as a short order cook at various restaurants. She moved to the Merrill area nine years ago to be closer to family. While in Merrill, she worked at Bell Tower Residence and County Market. Janie loved sitting on the front porch and watching nature.

Survivors include her siblings, Misty Holm of Crystal Falls, MI, Benita (George) Marriott of Merrill, Robert (Khonsawan) Hinterlong of Merrill, and Roberta Armstrong of Crystal Falls, MI; and many nieces and nephews. Janie was preceded in death by her husband, David Allen; and her parents, Robert and Patsy Hinterlong.

Burial will take place at a later date in Evergreen Memorial Cemetery, Crystal Falls, MI.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family. Online condolences are may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Jeanette Ann Reed
records.summary: Jeanette Ann Reed, age 90 passed away on June 18, 2026.
records.body:
Jeanette Ann Reed, age 90, of Rhinelander, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on June 18, 2026, at Friendly Village Nursing Home. She was born on July 6, 1935, in Medford, Wisconsin, to David and Alberta (Krickenberg) Anderson.

Jeanette was a loving mother, grandmother, great-grandmother, sister, and friend. She was a hard worker throughout her life and devoted herself to caring for her family. Raising four children, she spent countless hours walking wherever she needed to go, often pulling the younger children in a wagon. Her determination, resilience, and unwavering love for her family were evident in everything she did.

Known for her sense of humor, Jeanette was a legendary "backseat driver." Although she never had a driver's license, she was never shy about offering directions—even if they weren't always the right ones. Her family will forever cherish the laughter and stories that came from her confident navigation advice.

Jeanette was a lifelong Elvis fan, but more importantly, she was devoted to her family. She was spunky, strong-willed, and had a sparkle that made her uniquely her. Looking around at the women in our family, we can see pieces of her in each of us. That same spark, determination, and spirit continue to shine through, and when we see it, we know it's a little bit of Jeanette.

She was preceded in death by her husband, John Reed; her son, Jimmy Wismeth; her daughter, Helen Wismeth; her grandson, Jim Lawrence; her parents, David and Alberta Anderson; and her siblings, Harlan, Sheryl, Darlene, and Jerry.

Jeanette is survived by her daughters, Jeanne (Eugene) Belanger, Joanne (Todd) Lawrence, and Judy (D.J.) Deschler; her son, Jeffrey (Tina) Wismeth; and her siblings, Jenny (Ollie) Kitchen, Sherrie (Mike) Ertl, and Sandy (Rick) Salzwedel.

She also leaves behind her beloved grandchildren: Nichole Braaten, Trisha Meyer, Melissa Lawrence, Michelle Zimmerman, Matthew Deschler, Tiffany Deschler, and Aya and Henry Wismeth.

Jeanette was blessed with many great-grandchildren, including Brodie and Emily Braaten, Evan Meyer, Arcelia, Onyx, and Willow Deschler, Torrance and Cadance Lacrosse, James Lawrence, Cynthia Zimmerman, along with many other family members and friends who will miss her dearly.

A funeral service will be held on Wednesday, June 24, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. at Evergreen Cemetery, located at Wisconsin Highway 64 and Cran Road, Medford, WI 54451. Family and friends are invited to gather in remembrance and celebration of Jeanette's life. Following the burial, the family will host a Celebration of Life at Sackett's Lake, where loved ones can share stories, memories, and fellowship in honor of Jeanette. In lieu of flowers, Jeanette requested that memorial donations be sent to: Judy Deschler at 446 Hoffmann Ct. in Manawa, WI 54949Memorial contributions will be used to support research efforts related to Dementia and Alzheimer's disease in Jeanette's memory.

The family would like to extend their heartfelt gratitude to the dedicated staff of Friendly Village Nursing Home, especially the 900 Wing team, for the compassionate care, comfort, and support they provided to Jeanette during her stay. A special thank you is also extended to the activities staff for helping keep her engaged and bringing joy to her days.

As we say goodbye, we find comfort knowing that Jeanette's fingerprints are all over our lives—in the memories she gave us, the lessons she taught us, the love she shared, and the family she helped shape. Her love, laughter, strength, and unmistakable sparkle will continue to live on through the generations. She will be deeply missed, forever loved, and never forgotten.

You may leave your private condolences for the family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Reed family.

records.name: Lori Jane Steinke
records.summary: Lori Jane Steinke, age 64, of Wausau passed away on June 18, 2026.
records.body:
Lori Jane Steinke, 64, of Wausau, passed away on Thursday, June 18, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

Lori was born on February 13, 1962, in Wausau, to Ernest and Verona (Obernberger) Steinke.

Lori worked as a Registered Nurse for 30 years, caring for patients in Surgery, Oncology, Home Health, Short-Term Rehab, Clinic Management, Memory Care, Hospice, and Home Health Care. Notably, she worked for GI Associates in Wausau, where she was instrumental in the development of the endoscopy center. She loved a challenge and took pride in her ability to care for others. She loved her profession and the many co-workers along the way.

Lori’s interests included crafting, any outdoor summer sport, and she especially loved going to her grandsons' sporting activities. She loved making things for others and giving - just because. She also had a love for music, often listening to Prince, Journey, Aerosmith, and more. Lori had many unwavering friendships and special bonds with countless memories from over the years. She also loved animals and had 3 cats, one surviving - her special emotional support cat, Lucy.

Lori is survived by her children, Jerry Robert Klemp, Amanda Rae (Klemp) Burtch (Matt) (their father, Jerry A Klemp), and Katrina Ann Raskie (Nik Watral); one grandson, Levi John Dahl (Alex), and one granddaughter, Kennedy Alice Burtch. She is further survived by her mother, seven sisters, aunt and uncle, nieces and nephews, as well as many cousins. Lori is preceded in death by a son, Dallas James Raskie (Steve); Steve M. Raskie; her father, Ernest Steinke; former mother-in-law, Alice Klemp; and numerous loved aunts, uncles, and cousins.

No formal services are being held. In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to the family for support, causes special to Lori, and for her grandchildren.

Lori believed we should all be kind, compassionate, and inquisitive, as you never know what any person, at any time, is going through. She would want us to stay present, to listen, to give loved ones and strangers an empathetic ear, and for us all to share meaningful time with those we love.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Lucinda “Cindy” J. Kopp
records.summary: Lucinda “Cindy” J. Kopp, age 99, of Stratford passed away on Jun 18, 2026.
records.body:
Lucinda “Cindy” J. Kopp, 99, Stratford, passed away on Thursday, June 18, 2026 at Colonial Health Services, Colby, the last of 15 children.

Lucinda was born on September 1, 1926 in Marshfield to Joseph Sr. and Mary (Dieringer) Stini.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 am on Tuesday, June 23, 2026 at St. Joseph’s Catholic Church, Stratford where the visitation will start at 9:00 am with a rosary service at 10:00 am. Rev. Aaron Becker will officiate. Burial will follow at St. Joseph’s Cemetery, Town of Eau Pleine, Marathon County and serving as pallbearers will be Matt, Mike, and Sam Redetzke, Brandon Kopp, Alan and Dylan Grawien, and Byron Thompson. Sauter/Rembs Funeral Home, Stratford is assisting the family.

Lucinda attended Lone Elm School in Bakerville. She was united in marriage to Clarence “Shorty” Kopp on January 28, 1953 at Corpus Christi Catholic Church in Bakerville, and they celebrated 57 years together until his passing on January 10, 2011.

Lucinda enjoyed baking pies, babysitting grandchildren, great grandchildren and neighborhood children, embroidering, having family over, making meals, and tending her beautiful flower gardens. In her earlier days, she helped run the family farm, vegetable gardened, and canned. She also worked at the shoe factory before marriage and seasonally at Figi’s.

She is survived by her children, Doreen (Peter) Mayer of Cleveland, WI, Dennis (Janell) Kopp of Loyal, and Darla (Doug) Redetzke of Stratford; 10 grandchildren, Patty (Alan) Grawien, Amy Thompson, Kelly (Mike) Palushik, Brandon (Naomi) Kopp, Mikayla Kopp, Matt (Brittany) Redetzke, Mike (Gina) Redetzke, Sam Redetzke, Hannah Redetzke, and Rachael Redetzke, and 15 great grandchildren.

She was preceded in death by her parents, husband, Clarence, son, Darrell Kopp, grandson, Jacob Kopp, and great granddaughter, Ava Redetzke.

A memorial will be designated at a later date.

The family would like to extend a special thank you to Colonial Center Health Services - Memory Care Unit in Colby and Northshore At Home Hospice for their wonderful care.

records.name: Betty Lou Fleischman
records.summary: Betty Lou Fleischman, age 78, of Pittsville passed away on Jun 19, 2026.
records.body:
Betty Lou Fleischman, 78, of Pittsville passed peacefully into the presence of her Lord on June 19, 2026. She was born on August 24,1947 to Clem and Rose (VanKirk) Trader, and lived a life marked by love, devotion to family, faithful service to those around her and a strong work ethic.

She leaves behind a loving family who will cherish her memory: her children, Brenda (Paul) Veldman, Scott (Darla) Fleischman, Dennis (Deb) Fleischman, Denise (Mike) Bodendorfer, Tim (Cathy Maslowski) Fleischman, and Tammy (Steve) Pozega. She is also survived by her sister, Carol (Norb) Konrardy, and her brother, Joe (Tina) Trader and brothers and sisters in law; Rita Rudie Smazal, Walter (Virginia) Fleischman, Annette Fleischman, Mary (Jim) Krause and Doris Meacham.

Betty's greatest joy was her family. She was blessed with 21 grandchildren and 33 great-grandchildren, each of whom brought immeasurable happiness to her life. Her love, wisdom, and caring spirit will continue to live on through the generations she leaves behind.

Betty was preceded in death by her beloved husband, Donald Fleischman; her parents, Clem and Rose Trader; and her siblings, Archie Trader, Robert Trader, Phil Trader, and Diane Trader.

Though she will be deeply missed, her family finds comfort in knowing she has been reunited with her beloved, Donnie.

A celebration of Betty’s life will be held on Tuesday, June 30th at Bluff View Church, 8095 Bethel Road, Arpin, with visitation from 9:00 to 11:00 am and service at 11:00 am.

"Well done, good and faithful servant." — Matthew 25:23

records.name: Colleen R. Kurtzweil
records.summary: Colleen R. Kurtzweil, 87, of Tomahawk, Wisconsin, was called to rest on June 15, 2026.
records.body:
Colleen Kurtzweil, 87, of Tomahawk, WI was called to rest on June 15th, 2026. She was born December 27th, 1938, to the late Reginald and Lucille (Senoraske) Kluck.

Colleen graduated from D.C. Everest Senior High and Wausau Vocational School. She began her career working as a legal secretary and later joined Kolbe and Kolbe Millwork as an Accounts Receivable Manager until retiring in 2004.

Colleen and her husband, Ken Kurtzweil, took a break from their careers to venture to Mosinee, WI where they purchased and renovated an old tavern into the Stein 'n' Baum. They made many new friends and enjoyed being part of the community for several years, before returning to the Wausau area, and later relocating to Tomahawk.

Upon her retirement Colleen enjoyed bartending. She cherished the friendships she made at the local gathering places in Tomahawk, partaking in her signature Bud Light with a glass of ice, and occasionally throwing a buck in a gambling machine. She and Ken were active members of the local snowmobile clubs.

Colleen filled her days cooking a good meal, gardening, feeding the birds, and working on jigsaw puzzles. Colleen always enjoyed fishing, as a young girl with her father, and later on in life after connecting with Bob Rosemurgy. She and Bob took several trips to Canada to visit friends and wet a line.

In recent weeks, Colleen endured a brief battle with colon cancer. 'God saw you getting tired when a cure was not to be, so He wrapped his arms around you, and whispered, "Come to me".' 

Colleen is survived by her daughter Jodie Connor of Mosinee, WI, her sister Karen Kennedy of Las Vegas, NV, her grandchildren Ryan (Jamie) Connor of Kronenwetter, WI and Lindsey (Ethan) Hudson of Sullivan, WI, and her great grandchildren Sam Connor, and Quinn and Nora Hudson.

Colleen was preceded in death by her husband, Ken Kurtzweil, in 2009, her niece, Heather Kennedy, in 2011, her son Randy Kurtweil, in 2018, and her significant other, Bob Rosemurgy, in 2025.

records.name: Edwin "Ed" W. Duerr
records.summary: Edwin "Ed" W. Duerr, age 97, of Marshfield passed away on Jun 19, 2026.
records.body:
Edwin “Ed” W. Duerr, 97, Marshfield, passed away on Friday, June 19, 2026 at House of the Dove, Marshfield with his family by his side.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 am on Wednesday, June 24, 2026 at Sacred Heart Catholic Church, Marshfield with Rev. Jared Clements officiating. Burial will follow at Brooklawn Memory Gardens Cemetery, Town of Richfield, Wood County where military honors will be conducted by American Legion Post 54 and VFW Mueller-Hintz Post #1866 - Marshfield. Serving as pallbearers will be Ernie, Ray, Bob, and Brady Duerr, Kevin Weiler, and Ralph Czaikowski along with honorary pallbearers, John, Dennis, and Wayne Duerr, and Lori Brinkmann.

Visitation will take place from 4:00 pm - 7:00 pm on Tuesday, June 23 and from 9:00 am until the time of service on Wednesday, all at Sacred Heart Catholic Church.

Edwin was born on September 7, 1928 in Athens, Wisconsin to Charles and Erna (Strege) Duerr.

He served in the United States Army from 1946 until his honorable discharge in 1948 with a rank of T-5. He then worked for Roddis Plywood in Marshfield for 13 years. On July 17, 1954 Edwin married Irene Sydow. From 1962-1964 Edwin and Irene ran the Hilltop Bar in Granton. In addition to running the bar, in 1963 Edwin started working for Trieweiler Construction where he was an operating engineer until his retirement. Following his retirement he continued to do side jobs well into his 80’s. Edwin was passionate about baseball where he played in many leagues over the years. He played in the Beer and Lunch league for his son’s team, The Viaduct Bar until he was 75. Even at 75, Edwin was still one of the best first basemen around. He was honored with a plaque for first baseman and hitting player in the 45 and older league. Edwin loved his family and was very proud to have 12 great, great, grandchildren resulting in 5 generations.

Edwin was a member of and past commander of The VFW. He was also a member of the American Legion, Marshfield Eagles Club, and the Klondike Fox Trackers.

Edwin is survived by his son, Marvin “Butch” (Mary Lou) Duerr of Marshfield, daughter in law Elaine, grandchildren Lori (Roger Brinkmann), Dennis (Candy Brandl), Wayne (Lisa), Ray (Nancy), Bob (Molly), John, Ernie (Tanya), Ralph, Steve, and Amy. 18 great grandchildren and 12 great great grandchildren.

He was preceded in death by his wife Irene and sons Ken, Ray “Karl”, Donny, & Jeff. His parents, and brothers, Willard, Clarence, Marvin, Roy, & George.

Our family would like to thank St Croix Hospice, Compassus Hospice, Wells Nature View, and House of the Dove for the great care they provide in his last days.

records.name: Louis "Louie" G. Reissman
records.summary: Louis "Louie" G. Reissman, age 91 passed away on Jun 19, 2026.
records.body:
Louis “Louie” G. Reissman, 91, village of Maine, passed away on Friday, June 19, 2026, while under the care of the wonderful Palliative Care Unit at Aspirus Wausau Hospital. He was born February 6, 1935, in Birnamwood. He was the son of Paul and Elizabeth Reissman. Louie was married to the late Kathie Reissman.

Saying that Louie was a hard worker was an understatement. He retired from Marathon Electric and had worked for 42 years as a machinist. When he came home from work, he would start working in his Ginseng garden and was a grower for many years. He did the majority of the work himself and had the help of his family. After harvesting his crop of Ginseng, he would plant Christmas trees for a cut your own tree farm. The tree farm was his true love, from planting the seedlings to shearing, and then watching people come out with their families to pick the perfect tree. He loved watching all the memories families were making. Up until two months ago he planted 500 tree seedlings. What 91-year-old does that? Louie does.

Louie was a veteran of the United States Army. He served as an Army Engineer and Mail Clerk. He loved delivering the mail to all that served in his unit. He was stationed in Okinawa and Guam October 19, 1953, until October 19, 1956, during the Korean War.

He was a very spiritual man and loved his church and faith so much. Everyone in church he called his friend and loved them all. His favorite thing to do was to donate the Christmas tree for all the congregation to enjoy.

Louie had many special quotes. His morning greeting was always, “Daylight in the swamp.” Another famous saying was, “I’m easy to get along with, if you don’t believe me…just ask me!” Also, “Don’t sweat the small stuff.” Lastly, “Don’t do as I do, do as I say!”

Family was extremely important to Louie, from his daughters and sons-in-law to his grandchildren and great-grandchildren. A birthday never passed without getting a phone call with him singing Happy Birthday. He enjoyed polka music every Sunday and was a true NASCAR fan.

Survivors include his two daughters, Terry (Randy) Hillman and Jody (Stuart) Gehrke; two step-children, Shelly and Shawn; grandchildren, Stephanie, Brittney, Sam, Brayden, and Lonnie; great-grandchildren, Augie and Eliza; step-grandchildren, Emily, Hayden, Wyatt, Savannah, Sydney, and Vincent; brothers, Elmer and Ervin; sister, Bernice; and brother-in-law, Lloyd. He is further survived by his two loving companions, his dog, Goldie and cat, Kitty.

In addition to his wife and parents, he was preceded in death by two sons, Steven Paul and Lonnie; brother, Raymond; and sisters, Dorothy and Marge.

The funeral service will be at 11:00 a.m. on Thursday, June 25, 2026, at the Round Barn at Willow Springs, 6905 North 60th Avenue, Wausau, with Rev. Maggie Westaby officiating. Visitation will begin at 9:00 a.m. until the time of service in the Round Barn. Burial will follow in St. John’s Evangelical Lutheran Cemetery, Village of Maine, with military honors accorded by the American Legion Montgomery Plant Dudley Post #10. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Mary S Smith
records.summary: Mary S Smith, age 80 passed away on June 19, 2026.
records.body:
Mary S. (Salzman) Smith, of Wausau, passed away on June 19, 2026, one day shy of her 81st birthday. She was born on June 20, 1945.

Forty-eight years ago, Mary married the love of her life, James Smith, in Wausau, Wisconsin. Together they shared a life filled with love and devotion.

Mary is survived by her three siblings, Louise, Zookie, and Jim. She is further survived by many loving nieces and nephews, and many great nieces and nephews. Closest to her heart was Cece Upton, who was like a daughter to her and lovingly helped care for her for more than 15 years. Cece's children became the grandchildren Mary never had and brought great joy to her life.

Mary also leaves behind her two beloved fur babies, Rick and Marty.

She was preceded in death by nine siblings and her very special puppy, Dennis.

Per Mary's wishes, no services will be held.

Mary was a wonderful person with a big heart who was always giving to others. Her kindness, generosity, and love for family and friends will be remembered by all who knew her. She will be deeply missed.

The family would like to thank Mid-Wisconsin Cremation Society for their assistance at this time.

Online condolences may be expressed at MWCS.WS.

records.name: Matthew William Reif
records.summary: Matthew William Reif, 54, of Verona, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly on June 9, 2026.
records.body:
Matthew William Reif, age 54, of Verona, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly on Tuesday, June 9, 2026. He was born on January 16, 1972, in Tarrytown, New York, to Bill and Mary Nell Reif. Most of his growing up years were spent in Wausau but included many visits to northern Minnesota to visit grandparents and Reif, Randall, and Hoolihan cousins.

A 1990 graduate of Wausau East High School, Matt went on to receive a Bachelor of Arts degree from Hamline University in St. Paul, Minnesota. He was captivated by music at an early age. He loved to sing and knew all the lyrics from his childhood favorite "Bad Bad LeRoy Brown" to all the music he later came to love. Matt was a talented pianist and spent countless hours at his many keyboards performing and sharing his passion with others. Over the years he played in multiple bands in college, and in Phoenix, Wausau, and Madison. Lately, he was devoting time to composing original music as well as recording his own versions of music by his favorite musicians.

Matt and Holly Schmidt were married in June of 2006, and their family grew to include three boys, Myles, Evan, and Nolan. Matt and Holly lived in Madison while Matt worked for Music Notes and Holly taught Music at the Wingra School. They returned to Wausau in 2013, and Matt worked for Wausau Coated Products for two years. Subsequently, the family moved to Madison where Matt took on the role of a stay-at-home dad and performed with a local Madison band.

More than anything else, Matt cherished being a father to Myles, Evan, and Nolan and was recently excited about introducing them to the sports he loved as a child. He was deeply committed to supporting, encouraging, and loving his three boys and treasured every moment he spent with them.

Matt was preceded in death by his maternal grandparents, Alice and Jerry Hoolihan, and his paternal grandparents, Jane and Myles Reif.

He is survived by his wife and the mother of his three children, Holly Reif of Verona, Wisconsin; his sons, Myles, Evan, and Nolan, his parents, Bill and Mary Nell Reif, his brothers, Ben Reif (Amy), Will Reif (Laura), and nieces and nephews, Patrick, Benjamin, Allie, and Christopher Reif and Shea, Ryan, and Billie Reif, all of Wausau., as well as beloved cousins, aunts and uncles, many extended family members, dear friends, fellow musicians, and all whose lives were touched by knowing him.

Matt was kind, thoughtful, sensitive, empathetic, and creative, traits which were evident from the start. Most importantly he will be remembered for his devotion to family and particularly his three boys.

Visitation will be from 4pm to 5pm at the Church of the Resurrection in Wausau on Friday, July 10. The service will begin at 5pm followed by a reception at the Wausau Club. All are welcome.

Memorials may be directed to a charity of your choice or to the Community Foundation of North Central Wisconsin for the "Matt Reif Music Scholarship" for students of Wausau East, Wausau West, and Newman High School. The scholarship will also support younger musicians who need help attending the Wausau Conservatory of Music.

records.name: Michael 'Mike' D. Stiver
records.summary: Michael 'Mike' D. Stiver, age 70, of Somerset passed away on Jun 19, 2026.
records.body:
Mike Stiver passed away peacefully at his home in Somerset, WI, on June 19, 2026, at the age of 70, after a final week of joy with his family. Mike was born in Merrill, Wisconsin, on May 21, 1956, and he lived in Merrill for most of his life, eventually meeting and marrying Amy Lemon.

For Mike, family wasn’t just a part of his life—it was his entire universe. For 39 beautiful years, he shared a life with his beloved wife, Amy. Mike loved being married to Amy. They had so much fun together raising a family, creating a home, traveling, and living out their vows.

Together, they raised three children who were his pride and joy: Rachel, Paul, and Sam. One of the greatest honors of Mike’s life was adopting and raising Rachel as his own daughter, sharing a bond built on fierce love and a healthy dose of their shared stubbornness. Paul is his firstborn son. When Paul was born, Mike strutted around the bar proudly—and that's how he lived his whole life, celebrating his children as blessings from God. Sam came last and held perhaps the most tender place in Mike’s heart, which was evident in the way Mike always had his back.

Mike worked in maintenance for much of his career and thrived at using his mechanical intelligence. He loved his co-workers and showed that by sharing his time in and outside work with them, mentoring and passing on his knowledge, and being a hard worker. But his career, intelligence, and incredible work ethic weren’t his motivation. What motivated Mike was what came at the end of his 15-minute drive home (he was speeding): his wife and kids. He loved his family, and doing what it took to support them was what fueled him. Witnessing Mike support his family was to see him in his sweet spot. There are countless stories of his care, consideration, and willingness to do whatever it took for his family that have left an indelible mark on our lives.

Mike was a charismatic and warm man. One of his most beautiful qualities was his rare ability to open up and make friends with anyone. His generous spirit overflowed in the way he brought people together, most notably through sports. In his younger years, an avid love and skill for foosball helped him forge a tight circle of lifelong friends. As a father, he was ever-present for his kids and their diverse interests, proudly coaching their youth sports—from Little League to softball to soccer. His love of sport naturally extended to his children, who all cheer endlessly for Wisconsin teams and especially the Green Bay Packers. Whether bonding over a recent game or hosting his memorable Fourth of July parties in Merrill, Mike used sports and his knack at conversation to build a loving community full of laughter and belonging around him.

In his retirement years, Mike and Amy moved to Somerset, WI, to be present in their grandchildren's lives. Mike was gifted at many things, but he was born to be a grandpa. He flourished in loving his grandsons. He loved chasing them around, encouraging them, and teaching them about fishing, sports, life, and how to eat ice cream. It is almost inexpressible the ways that seeing his grandchildren grow before him filled his heart and brought him joy.

Finally, Mike loved and trusted God. Although he didn’t always understand God’s ways, Mike was a prayerful and thoughtful man and trusted the “man upstairs” with even the most difficult things in life. For all these reasons and many more, we will deeply miss him. We are thankful for his life, and his legacy will live on through his faith and his family.

Mike is preceded in death by his mother, Carol (Lambrecht), and his father, Bernard. Mike is survived by his wife Amy, daughter Rachel, sons Paul (Allison) and Sam (Javi), grandsons Chase and Brooks, and his siblings: Joanne (Mark) Bronsteatter, Deb (and the late Nick) Mattson, Mary (Carl) Moscherosch, and Dan (Martina) Stiver.

A funeral remembrance will take place at 1:00 P.M., Saturday, September 5, 2026, at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation - 301 Eagle Dr., Merrill, WI. The family will receive friends beginning at 10:00 A.M. Saturday at the funeral home. In lieu of flowers, please consider making a donation in Mike’s name to the Special Olympics of Minnesota or to your local animal nonprofit.

records.name: Myron "Mike" M. Trzebiatowski
records.summary: Myron "Mike" M. Trzebiatowski, age 94 passed away on June 19, 2026.
records.body:
Myron (Mike) Maximillion Trzebiatowski passed away peacefully on June 19, 2026, surrounded by his loving family. He was deeply grateful to remain in the home where he was born, where he built a life and raised a family with Arlene, and where he proudly continued the family farming tradition until his death.

Myron was born on September 12, 1931, the fourth child of the late Max and Rose (Wierzba) Trzebiatowski.

Myron grew up in Amherst on the family farm, the original Trzebiatowski homestead. Through eighth grade, he attended Fountain Grove School, a small one-room neighborhood school which was just down the road from the farm. After completing his education, he continued helping on the family farm. During the winter months, he and several of his siblings would take the bus to Milwaukee to find work to support the family. He worked at places such as Trostel Tannery, Advanced Tool & Die, and Continental Can Company.

Myron joined the United States Army on November 13, 1952, and was honorably discharged on November 30, 1960.

Myron met the woman of his dreams, Arlene Marie Ebel, when she walked up to him at a wedding. She mistakenly thought Myron was Clarence, her girlfriend MaryJane’s boyfriend. It turned out Myron was Clarence’s brother, and this serendipitous mistake kicked off the beginning of an exceptionally long and happy marriage of nearly 69 years. They were united in marriage on June 29, 1957, at St. Stanislaus Catholic Church in Stevens Point.

Early in their marriage, Myron worked as a milkman for Fisher’s Dairy, delivering milk to schools and families in the middle of the night. His true calling, however, was farming. In 1959, Mike and Arlene, and their growing family, moved back to the family farm. It is here that they carefully cultivated the land, but also lovingly raised their family. Together, they received the Century Farm Award, recognizing their devotion to both the land and their faith.

Myron will be remembered as an avid Brewers and Packers fan, for his enthusiasm for hunting, his ability to be a strategic Sheepshead player, for his love of pool and bowling, and his commitment to his church, where he was a member of St. Mary Holy Name Society.

Myron is survived by his beloved wife, Arlene; his children, Allen (Betti), Steve (Sandy), Donald (Maggie), Terry (Bob) Gahl, John (Lori), Karen (Bill) Desing, and Ryan (Jamie); his grandchildren and step-grandchildren, Isaac, Orean, Uriah, Marissa, Ashlee, Nathan, Gwen, Coleman, Audra, Henry, Julie, Dave, Zach, Rachel, Kyle, Stacey, Miranda, Chelsey, Eleanor, Vincent, Jillian, Lucy, and Wesley; and twenty-two great-grandchildren and step-great-grandchildren, with another on the way. He is also survived by his brother, Rudy, his sisters Arlene Wroblewski and Jan (Jim) Roska, and many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his son Matthew; his parents, Max and Rose; and his siblings, William, Chet, Clarence, Jerry, Nancy, Lucille Patoka, and Lorraine Wroblewski.

A funeral mass will be held on Saturday, June 27, 2026, at St. Mary of Mount Carmel, 3995 County Road K, Amherst, WI, presided by Father Jerzy Rebacz. The Rosary will begin at 8:45 a.m. followed by Visitation until time of service. Service will begin at 11:00 a.m. John J Buettgen Funeral Home is assisting the family. Condolences and words of remembrance may be left at www.honorone.com.

In lieu of flowers, a donation may be sent to St. Jude’s Foundation or to St Mary of Mount Carmel Restoration Fund.

A special thank you to the many people who helped Myron during his last few years, and especially to Heartland Hospice and Synergy Health who provided wonderful care and compassion. Their support allowed Myron’s family to grant his final wish—to remain in his home for the remainder of his life.

records.name: Olive Esther Smith
records.summary: Olive Esther Smith, age 79 passed away on June 19, 2026.
records.body:
Olive Esther Smith, dearly beloved sister, aunt, mother, grandmother and friend, passed away on Friday, June 19, 2026 at University Hospital in Madison. Olive was born on March 6th, 1947 in Rhinelander, WI., the 4th child of David and Olive Ross.

Olive is survived by her son Wayne Garrow Jr. and granddaughter Alexandria Garrow. Her brother John Ross, her sisters Ethel Craig, Victoria (Howard) Kalkofen and Donna (Thomas) Zimmerman. Along with many nieces and nephews and great-nieces.

Olive was preceded in death by her parents, her infant son William Smith, her sister Nancy Squires and her step-son Daniel Resch.

Olive graduated from Rhinelander Union High School in 1965. Soon after graduation she moved to Washington DC and worked for the FBI. Olive later returned home to Rhinelander and worked at 1st National Bank and then as a telephone operator for AT&T. In 1968 Olive married Wayne Garrow, they had a son in 1972, Wayne Garrow Jr. After they divorced, Olive attended Nicolet College, earning an Associate in Accounting Degree. She was a wiz with numbers and letters, including IRS tax returns (having a VITA tax certificate) and playing Scrabble. Upon graduation Olive worked for Teshendorf & Schrock and was also the Treasurer for Hodag Sports Club. She purchased a home. Olive later ran a very successful business for close to 30 years.

Olive had a great faith and was a devoted member of Saint Augustine Episcopal Church. She was very active in her church, filling many roles; treasurer, President of the ECW and a reader at church services.

Ollie enjoyed her Ross family traditions; birthdays, holidays and sister days. She had beautiful gardens, flowers were her love, and knew the names of plants and flowers. Especially delighting in rare fruits and flowers. Ollie was beloved by her family and friends. Ever kind, caring, accepting, a bit stubborn, and possessed of a wonderful sense of humor.

We will forever miss our Ollie, who had such courage, faith and hope. Suffering greatly, while attempting to recover and live from a very difficult and extended hospital stay. The love and devotion of her niece and nephew, Kerry and Mike, her niece Laura, and her great-niece Sarah, gave Ollie great comfort during her hospital stay. She and her family were also grateful for the care and kindness of the nursing staff and chaplains of B4/3 shown to Olive. Most especially we thank Father Hal, the Episcopal chaplain at UW Madison hospital. He supported, visited and prayed with Ollie and her family innumerable times.

An obituary with services times will follow shortly. You may leave your private condolences for Olive's family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving Olive's family.

records.name: R. Ross Plant Jr.
records.summary: R. Ross Plant Jr., 96, of Kronenwetter, passed away peacefully on June 16, 2026.
records.body:
R. Ross Plant Jr., of Kronenwetter, passed away peacefully on June 16, 2026, at his home.

Ross was born on May 24, 1930, to the late Russell and Eleanore (Scheel) Plant in Wausau, WI. He married the love of his life N. June Zich on April 12, 1952. June died earlier this year. They were married almost 74 years.

Ross worked for American Can/James River for 35 years in mostly supervisory positions. He worked in Wausau WI, Chambersburg PA, and Neenah WI.

Ross, along with June, loved to fish and travel. They traveled to Canada for forty years to go fishing and enjoyed great success. Ross and June also spent many winters in Florida after Ross' retirement. They were also able to travel to many other destinations in the US.

Ross enjoyed woodworking, especially carving. He carved a collection of whimsical wooden figures that were displayed in the house.

Ross and June were never without a dog or two by their side. They had dogs of all sizes from small terriers to Great Danes throughout their married life.

He is survived by his brother, Wayne (Judy) Plant and former sister-in-law, Diane Plant and his children; son, Michael (Cynthia) Plant; and daughters, Cheryl (Paul) Jahns, Susan (Rick) Paszkot, and Barb (Keith) Friedlein; grandchildren, Kelly (Wade) Puntney, Christopher (Kirby) Plant, Matthew Plant, Brian (Amy) Jahns, Andy (Carly) Jahns, Aimee (Kyle Buehler) Paszkot, Jonathan (Jaewoo) Paszkot, David (Piper) Pozorski, Laura Friedlein, and Lucas Friedlein; great-grandchildren, Lana, Ethan, Tenley, Harlow, Henry, Violet, Emily, William, Ryan, Rhys, Lucy, Vincent, and Trinity. He is preceded in death by his parents and wife June.

A memorial service for both Ross and June will be held on Saturday, July 11, 2026, at 11:00 A.M., at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston. Visitation will be held from 10:00 A.M. until the time of service.

"Our family would like to thank Ross and June's closest neighbors for all their help and support".

We would like to give a special thank-you to Kelly Miller for helping to care for our mom and dad over the last 6 months. Also, thank you to Cornerstone caregiving, Compassus Hospice, and Interim Hospice for all the care and support given to Ross and June.

records.name: Richard J. Austin
records.summary: Richard J. Austin, 89, of Rib Mountain, passed away June 10, 2026.
records.body:
Richard (Dick) J. Austin, 89, of Rib Mountain, passed away June 10, 2026 at Aspirus Hospice House. Dick was born August 11, 1936 in Sturgeon Bay, WI and moved to Wausau in 1956 to attend Wausau Vocational School. Dick's career spanned over 60 years in various roles, working primarily for the Schuette companies in Wausau. He married Sandra (Sandy) in 1960 and their son Jeffrey was born in 1961.

Dick was an avid golfer and fan of the Packers and Brewers. He also enjoyed a good Friday fish fry and morning coffee with the guys at The Red Apple and Mosinee Cafe. He and Sandy were longtime supporters of the Humane Society of Marathon County. As a memorial to his late wife, Dick provided the vision and drive that resulted in the creation of Sandy's Bark Park in Rib Mountain.

He is survived by his son, Jeff (Jennifer) Austin of Minocqua; his sister, Jayne (Mark) Baumann; nieces and nephews, Jon (Dawn) Marsh, Julie (Randy) LeMieux, all from Sturgeon Bay and Jacob Baumann of Plover. He is also survived by friend Mary Cole of Rib Mountain and many great nieces, nephews and cousins. He was preceded in death by his wife, Sandy; brother, John; and parents, Walter and Lucille.

In keeping with Dick's wishes, there will be no funeral service. In lieu of flowers, please consider a donation in his name to the Humane Society of Marathon County or Aspirus Hospice House where he received the best of care.

records.name: Ryan Paul Johnson
records.summary: Ryan Paul Johnson, age 52, of Wausau passed away on Jun 19, 2026.
records.body:
Ryan Paul Johnson, age 52, of Schofield, Wisconsin passed away Friday, June 19, 2026. He was born June 20, 1973 in Owatonna, Minnesota, the son of Susan Johnson and the late Gary Johnson.

Ryan graduated from D.C. Everest High School in 1992. After high school, Ryan moved to Missoula, Montana. Shortly thereafter, he relocated to Greer, South Carolina, where he worked alongside his father as a salesman in the glass industry, running their warehouse. Returning to the Wausau area, he started his own landscaping business.

For those who knew Ryan, he was definitely not a Cheesehead. He preferred his purple and gold and loved harassing his Wisconsin friends- SKOL! In addition to football, Ryan also enjoyed hockey, fishing, flying his drones and above all, spending time with his friends. Ryan never missed an opportunity for a good time.

In addition to his maternal and paternal grandparents, Ryan was preceded in death by his father, Gary J. Johnson (South Carolina); brother, Jason; uncles Richard, Kevin Johnson and David Hansen. Ryan is survived by his mother, Susan J. Johnson (Schofield, WI); sister, Amy Haenggli and brother-in-law, Remo Haenggli (Switzerland); nieces, Kiehla and Annika Haenggli (Switzerland); stepmother Karen Johnson (South Carolina); along with many loving aunts, uncles, cousins and friends who loved him dearly and will sadly miss him.

As you think of Ryan throughout your life, remember his loving heart and kind soul; his zest for life and his love to be surrounded by family and friends.

A Celebration of Life for Ryan will be held on Saturday, June 27, 2026, from 2:00 p.m. until 5:00 p.m. at Brainard Funeral Home, 5712 Memorial Court, Weston, WI 54476.

The family wishes to thank the amazing and kind staff at the Aspirus Wausau Hospital, in the various departments that Ryan was admitted to throughout his time there.

In lieu of flowers, memorials are encouraged.

Brainard Funeral Home & Cremation Center in Weston is honored to assist Ryan’s family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.brainardfuneral.com.

records.name: Thomas James Hintz
records.summary: Thomas James Hintz, 65, of Rosholt, passed away peacefully on June 16, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas James Hintz of Rosholt, passed away peacefully on June 16th, 2026, at the age of 65 surrounded by his loving family after a tough 3 year battle with a rare B-cell Lymphoma cancer in the brain.

Tom was born June 3rd, 1961, in Milwaukee, WI to the late Raymond and Henrietta (Singer) Hintz. He grew up in the Rosholt area, attending St. Adalberts Catholic School and Rosholt High School, graduating from Rosholt High School in 1979.

On September 12th, 1987, Tom was united in marriage to Jacqueline (Wierzba) Hintz at St. Ladislaus Church in Bevent. They shared just shy of 39 wonderful years together making memories with their 3 children and 5 grandchildren.

Tom worked as a Line Leader at Greenheck Fan in Schofield for 32 years. In his free time, Tom found great joy in the outdoors. He loved hunting, fishing, camping and working out on his land making food plots and firewood. In his earlier years he enjoyed ice fishing, snowmobiling and traveling out west and up to Canada on hunting trips with friends and family.

As his family grew, he passed those traditions on to his children, teaching them not only how to hunt and fish, but also to appreciate nature and the simple joys of life. He made it a point to take his family camping several times each year, exploring campgrounds and national forests treasuring the peace and quiet of the outdoors. He always made sure to make special memories filled with family fun, laughter and excitement.

Tom had a gentle spirit and an exceptionally kind heart. He was the kind of person who would drop everything to lend a helping hand to anybody who needed it. His words carried wisdom, sincerity and trust; everybody always knew they could count on him.

Tom's unwavering love, quiet strength and selfless devotion to his family will never be forgotten. His legacy lives on in the memories he created, the values he instilled and the countless lives he has touched.

Tom is survived by his wife Jackie; his Children: Margaret (Sam) Napiwocki, Kimberley (Zack) Boettcher and Travis (Taylor Krogwold) Hintz; his grandchildren: Wesley, Oaklee and Jaxon Boettcher and Lincoln and Walker Napiwocki. He was further survived by his siblings: Scott (Susan) Hintz, Lisa (Jim) Richter, Diane (Wayne) Garski, Jenny (Chad) Malek; and his brothers and sisters-in-law: (Diane) Wierzba, (Pauline) Wierzba, Norbert (Bernie) Wierzba, Jeanette Gollon, (Russell) Miller, James Wierzba, (Elaine) Wierzba, Joe Wierzba, Marcie (Dennis) Hintz, Joyce (Jeff) Krukowski, Joan (Patrick) O'Neil, Judy (Larry) Osowski, Mary (Tom) Lewandowski, Norman (Brenda) Wierzba and many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his parents Raymond and Henrietta Hintz; his father and mother in-law John and Margaret Wierzba; his brothers-in-law: Johnny Wierzba, Myron Wierzba, Wayne Gollon, Jerome Wierzba; and sister-in-law Marlene Miller.

A Funeral Mass will be held at 11:30 AM, at St. Ladislaus Catholic Church in Bevent, WI on Monday, June 29th, 2026. Rev. Augustine Bentil will officiate. Visitation will be held from 9AM until the time of Mass at the church.

The family would like to thank Dr. Lawler and his team at Marshfield Clinic; Dr. Durani and her team at Mayo Clinic in Rochester, MN; and Compassus Hospice Care team for their care and compassion throughout Tom's journey.

records.name: Tommy Lee Gatz
records.summary: Tommy Lee Gatz, age 77 passed away on June 19, 2026.
records.body:
Tommy ‘Pearly’ L. Gatz, 77 of Birnamwood, died on Friday, June 19, 2026, at Rennes Health and Rehab, Weston, under the care of Aspirus Comfort Care & Hospice.

Tom was born on February 4, 1949, in Wausau, the son of Stanley and Meta ‘Mickey’ (Jensema) Gatz.

In 1972, Tom was united in marriage to Linda Hanson in Mineral Point. They later divorced.

Tom graduated from Eau Claire Memorial High School before earning a degree in Police Science from UW–Platteville. Dedicated to serving and protecting his communities, he began his career as a Wisconsin DNR warden before continuing in law enforcement with the Shawano County Sheriff’s Department. Tom also proudly served for 30 years with the Birnamwood Fire & Ambulance Department. He enjoyed hunting, fishing, being outdoors, shooting and collecting guns and spending time at his cabin. Tom loved traveling to Alaska, where he would pan for gold.

Tom is survived by two children, Daniel (Rachel) Gatz and Theresa Gatz; three grandchildren, Jaxon, Evelyn and Frank; siblings, Jeanne Gatz, Jim Gatz and David (Diane Williams) Gatz; brother-in-law, Gary Menard; sister-in-law, Ann Pretzel, as well as many cousins, nieces and nephews.

Tom was preceded in death by his parents; brother, Dean Gatz and sister, Patricia Menard.

A Time of Sharing will be held at 4PM on Thursday, June 25, 2026, at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Birnamwood. Burial will be in Forest Cemetery, Birnamwood. Visitation will be on Thursday, from 1PM until the time of sharing at the funeral home.

The family would like to thank Aspirus Comfort Care & Hospice for their kind and compassionate care given to Tom.

Memories and messages of support may be shared at schmidtschulta.com.

records.name: Cecelia "Cis" Baker
records.summary: Cecelia "Cis" Baker, age 81 passed away on June 20, 2026.
records.body:
Cecelia (Cis) Ann Baker, age 81, of Rhinelander, WI peacefully joined her Heavenly Father on June 20,2026, surrounded by her loving family, from natural causes related to a progressive lung disease she courageously fought and endured the past six years. The youngest of three siblings, she was born June 26, 1944 in Middleton, WI to Rev. Edward T. and Alice (Graser) Reiff. Cis graduated Grafton High School in 1962, where she was active in Girls Athletic Assn. and 4-H. She received a BS degree in Education from Oshkosh State University, where she was a charter member of Alpha Phi sorority, leading to lifetime supportive friendships and sisterhood reunions. At OSU, Cis was also active in intramural and women's sports, initiating her desire to teach girls physical education.

Cis's first teaching job was in elementary physical education in West Allis, WI. After an 11-week tour of Europe the following summer with two sorority sisters, she taught girls physical education at Burleigh Junior High in Brookfield, WI. During this time, Cis met her future husband, Don, who lived in the same apartment building, prophetically on Lovers Lane Rd. in Milwaukee! They were married in Neenah, WI on August 7, 1971. After Don accepted the first of his banking job transfers with The Marine Corporation, to Beaver Dam, WI, Cis taught high school girls' physical education in nearby Waupun. While there, Title IX of the Education Amendment of 1972 was enacted, ensuring equal opportunity for girls in all facets of education, including sports. Cis became one of the original girls' varsity sports coaches. At Waupun H.S. she was a successful head (and only) coach of its first girls' volleyball, basketball, softball and track & field teams, not for the $100 total compensation, but for the opportunity, challenge and love of girls' sports.

When Cis and Don's first of three sons were born, she became a stay-at-home mom. When their last son started Neenah (WI) High School, Cis began substitute teaching there, followed by Oconto Falls, WI where she also coached girls' varsity golf, and finally Rhinelander, WI where she retired from teaching in 2009. Throughout her career, Cis was a dedicated teacher and coach, well-liked by her students and players, whom often sought her counsel, as well as by fellow faculty members. She was a leader and active member of many organizations in the communities she lived in during Don's banking career. They included charitable, educational, athletic, social and ELCA church congregations. Cis was especially fond and proud of her involvement in the Neenah-Menasha Emergency Society while in Neenah.

During her lifetime, Cis had a passion for golf, having learned it from her father at age 12. She was a student of the game, continually learning, from articles, videos and frequent practice, and became very proficient at it. She founded and for many years led a junior golf program at North Shore Golf Club in Menasha, WI and won a women's championship there, as well as women's championships at River Island Golf Course in Oconto Falls and Northwood Golf Club in Rhinelander. Her determination kept her playing, with her lifetime golf partner Don, even after contracting her lung disease, until at age 79 she reluctantly stopped when no longer physically able. While raising a family, Cis kept up with Don and her sons on fishing, camping and canoe trips in Wisconsin, Upper Michigan, Minnesota's Boundary Waters and Canada. In retirement, Cis and Don enjoyed long-awaited travel, especially cruises and a memorable Tanzanian safari. Together, they saw all 50 states, 42 national parks and over 20 countries on five continents. In recent years, they spent their winters with friends in Green Valley, AZ.

Cis was a unique woman who easily and quickly connected with anyone she met. Through her lifetime of volunteerism, generosity and humor she touched many lives in positive ways. Cis was an incredible wife, mother, grandmother, homemaker, cook, teacher, mentor and friend. Her passing will leave an empty sadness, but many loving memories in the lives of her three sons and their wives, all of whom she was extremely proud, her six grandchildren, whom she adored, and especially Don, whom she was very supportive of throughout the location moves during his banking career. She liked to use the phrase “new job, new house, new baby,” which happened to her three times! Cis will be greatly missed, but long remembered, by all who knew her.

Cis was preceded in death by her parents, brother David Reiff and his wife Sara, and brother-in-law Greg Haines.  She is survived by her husband of 54 years, Don, sons Brock (Lynette, children Mikayla and Malia), Derek (Meagan, children Catherine and Benjamin) and Ross (Stephanie, children Sloane and Reese), sister Mary Wessel (Dr. Gilbert), brother-in-law David (Krisan) Baker, sister-in-law Karen Haines, and sister-in-law Kathy (Dean) Tappa, and nieces, nephews and cousins.

A visitation will be held Wednesday, July 8 from 10:00 to 11:00 AM at Trinity Lutheran Church, 235 N. Stevens St., Rhinelander, WI, with a funeral service immediately following. A private graveside service will be held at Wanderers Rest Cemetery, Gillett, WI. In lieu of flowers, in honor of Cis, please consider a donation to the Rhinelander Area Food Pantry or the Northwoods Alliance for Temporary Housing. You may leave your private condolences for the Baker family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Baker family.

records.name: Dewain “Shorty” L. Ellis
records.summary: Dewain “Shorty” L. Ellis, age 89, of Marshfield passed away on Jun 20, 2026.
records.body:
Dewain “Shorty” L. Ellis, 89, Marshfield, passed away peacefully on Saturday, June 20, 2026 at House of the Dove, Marshfield.

Dewain was born on March 16, 1937, in Hickory Grove, Grant County, Wisconsin, to Graydon and Louisa (Roth) Ellis. He attended Gays Mills High School and went on to serve in the United States Army. On November 17, 1962, he married the love of his life, Donna Rotar, at Corpus Christi church in Bakerville, WI.

Dewain worked as a backhoe operator and was a proud member of the Operating Engineers. He spent many years working for Mueller Pipeliners and then, after suffering a back injury that ended his ability to operate his backhoe and do clean up work for Mueller Pipeliners, he went on to work for five years at the YMCA in Marshfield until his retirement.

At the age of 56, he went back to school and earned his HSED. Outside of work, Dewain enjoyed attending sporting events involving his grandchildren until his back pain limited his ability to attend them. He also loved returning to Boscobel, where he grew up, and enjoyed picking watercress with his Uncle Dale. Dewain and his wife Donna also enjoyed getting away in their motorhome and wintered in Texas for a few years. Known for his sweet tooth, Dewain looked forward to his morning coffee and doughnut and was never opposed to sneaking a few cookies at night.

Dewain is survived by his wife, Donna Ellis; his children, Randy (Irene) Ellis of Marshfield and Debra (Rick) Long of Marshfield; six grandchildren, Adam (Natalie) Carolfi, Jason (Lindsay) Carolfi, Travis (Jennifer) Ellis, MacGyver Ellis, Nicole (Aaron) Cepress, and Aimee Feint, and numerous step grandchildren; 18 great grandchildren, Donevan, Jayde, Madden, Sofia, Savannah, Shyanne, Hunter, Peyton, Braylon, Lincoln, Deitrik (Abbie), Malachi, Tahneya, Aliyah, Adrian, Archer, Auden, and numerous step great grandchildren and step great, great grandchildren. He is further survived by his sister-in-law, Joyce Ellis, and several nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his maternal grandparents, Bessie and Henry Roth; parents, Graydon and Louisa Ellis; granddaughter, Jill Ellis; his brother, Arnold Ellis; his sisters in law, Darlene Franklin and Betty Bohman; his mother and father in law, Frank and Veronica Rotar; brother in law, Joseph Bohman; and special uncle, Dale Roth.

The family extends a special thank you to the compassionate nurses and staff of Compassus Hospice and House of the Dove in Marshfield for their exceptional care and support.

A graveside service with military honors will be held at 10:30 am on Monday, June 29, 2026 at Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Marshfield.

records.name: Mavis  Louise Perry
records.summary: Mavis  Louise Perry, age 97 passed away on June 20, 2026.
records.body:
Mavis Perry, a lifelong resident of Rhinelander, died on June 20, 2026, in Ellsworth, Wisconsin. She was born on January 4, 1929 in Rhinelander, the daughter of Harold and Maren (Fremstad) Christiansen. Mavis attended schools in Rhinelander, graduating from the Rhinelander High School in 1947. She married Eugene Perry on June 2, 1951 and he preceded her in death on July 10, 2018. The Lord blessed them with 67 years of marriage.

After her schooling Mavis went to work for the Rhinelander Paper Company and then stayed at home to raise her family. Eventually she went back to work for Consolidated Papers.

Mavis was a long-time member of the Immanuel Lutheran Church. She was proud of her Norwegian heritage and in her spare time she enjoyed gardening and traveling. She loved hosting family gatherings especially at Christmas time. She loved her family, her friends and more than anything, her Lord and Savior.

Mavis is survived by her daughters Janet (David) Harmon, Sandra (Roy) Egelhoff and Barbara (Gregory) Schmidt, her daughter in law Karen Perry, her grandchildren Becky, Sarah, Eric, Kara, Beth, Robert, Tim and Brianna, her five step grandchildren, her ten great grandchildren, nieces, nephews, other family and many friends. he was preceded in death by her husband Eugene, her son James, her parents, her sister Anna and her brothers Donald, Eugene and Norman.

Mavis' family would like to thank the staff at Grace Lodge, Preferred Senior Living and Adoray Hospice for the loving care they provided their Mother.

Visitation for Mavis will be held on Friday, June 26, 2026 from 9:30 AM to 11:00 AM with services following at 11:00 AM, all at the Carlson Funeral Home. Interment will be in the Forest Home Cemetery. You may leave your private condolences for the Perry family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Perry family.

records.name: Ricky Miles Swanson
records.summary: Ricky Miles Swanson, age 77, of Mosinee passed away on Jun 20, 2026.
records.body:
Ricky M. Swanson, 77, Mosinee, passed away peacefully on Saturday, June 20, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital, with his loving family by his side.

He was born on July 25, 1948, in Ipswich, South Dakota, son of the late, Harris and Loree (Rickard) Swanson. He married his lifelong love Veronica F. Bertsch on June 2, 1973, she survives.

A fun-loving free spirit, Ricky touched many lives and always willing to share his faith. From farming to fishing & hunting to camping, the outdoors was a way of life for him. His loyal and dedicated work ethic extended beyond his professions of farming, cooking and carpentry to the organizations close to his heart. A former 4-H leader, volunteer firefighter, church board member, and active supporter of Carpenters for Christ, Ricky was never one to shy away from hard work, especially if it meant enriching others’ lives. Ricky was most proud of his role as a loyal & dedicated husband; and devoted & active father. His final greatest achievement was spending time with his grandchildren, passing down his love for woodworking and the outdoors.

Ricky is survived by his loving wife, Veronica; his oldest daughter Ericka and her husband Matthew Napoli and their two children Leo and Isabella; and his youngest daughter Elizabeth and her dog Lexie.

Besides his parents, Ricky was preceded in death by his brother Jack, brother Harris and wife Sharron, and nephew James Swanson.

The family will plan to have two celebrations over the coming weeks in memory of Ricky. Beste Funeral Home, Mosinee, is honored to be assisting the family.

A Memorial Service will take place at 11:00 a.m., on Friday, July 10, 2026, at Christ Community Church, 8100 Alderson St., Schofield, WI 54476. Pastor Arnold Allison will officiate. Visitation will be at the church from 10:00 a.m., until the time of the service. Reception to be held after at Gorski’s on the patio.

A second Memorial Service will be held on Monday, July 27, 2026 at United Church of Christ, 203 N Fourth St, Ipswich, SD. Visitation will be 10:00 a.m. and service at 11:00 a.m. Reception will be held at the Swanson Farm.  To place an order of remembrance flowers for this service you may visit www.thebostonfern.com.

Online condolences may be directed to the family at www.bestefh.com.

In lieu of flowers, donations are also welcome in Ricky’s name to the organization of your choice in one of these areas important to him and his family: Drug and alcohol rehabilitation; Faith-based mission work; Cancer patient treatment.

records.name: Donna C. Drake
records.summary: Donna C. Drake, age 73, of Mosinee passed away on Jun 21, 2026.
records.body:
Donna C. Drake, 73, Mosinee, passed away peacefully on June 21, 2026, at Pine Meadows, Mosinee.

Donna was born in Wausau and grew up in Mosinee.
