Donna was the 14th of 15 children, of the late Claude and Elizabeth (Bressete) Drake. 10 of her siblings preceded her in death, Mary Jane, Sheldon, Richard, Dawn, Marvin, Chester, Joseph, John, Marcella and “Baby Drake”. Donna is survived by her two daughters, Linda Drake, Sue Drake, and grandson Mike Drake. Donna’s surviving siblings include; Carolyn (Lloyd) Roberts, Denver, IN, Clinton (Barbara) Drake, Schofield, David (the late Betty Drake), Wausau, and Roseann (Andy) Boron, Mosinee.

The family would like to extend their heartfelt gratitude to Linda and all the caring and compassionate caregivers at Pine Meadows Assisted Living. Each one of Donna’s caregivers played a significant role in making her final days so comfortable and content.

We are especially grateful to Cheyenne, Donna’s hospice caregiver, whose patience, flexibility, and willingness to adjust schedules whenever Donna wasn’t feeling up to care never went unnoticed. We also sincerely thank Malayna, the social worker, for her guidance, encouragement, and support during this difficult time. A special thank you to Amanda, RN, and Wendy, RN, and the entire Interim Hospice Team, your dedication, kindness, exceptional compassion, and unwavering care meant more to our family than words can adequately express, and we will always be grateful for the dignity and comfort you provided Donna.

A private family memorial service will be held for Donna. Beste Funeral Home, Mosinee, is honored to be assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be directed to the family at www.bestefh.com.

records.name: Jack Herbert Bartelme
records.summary: Jack Herbert Bartelme, age 83 passed away on June 21, 2026.
records.body:
Jack H Bartelme Sr, age 83 of Rhinelander,  died Sunday, June 21, 2026, at Aspirus Hospital. He was born on October, 29 1942, in Rhinelander, Wisconsin to Earl and Fern Bartelme. He graduated Rhinelander High School, served 4 years active duty in United States Navy and was forward deployed for the Viet Nam War. After honorably discharged, he worked the rest of his working career at The Paper Mill in Rhinelander for nearly 40 years. Jack loved to hunt, fish, camp, watch the Packers, and do gardening for activities. He is survived by his wife Karen, 3 sons Tim, Jack Jr, and Tom, 4 grandchildren, and 4 great grandchildren. He was preceded in death by his parents and sister (Janet Sorgatz).

A visitation for Jack will be held on Tuesday, July 7, 2026 from 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM, at the Carlson Funeral Home. You may leave your private condolences for the Bartelme family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Bartelme family.

records.name: Kathleen  Kelly
records.summary: Kathleen  Kelly, age 72 passed away on June 21, 2026.
records.body:
Kathleen Joy Kelly (nee Mosetter) passed away peacefully on June 21st, 2026 at the age of 72.

Kathleen was born on July 19th, 1953 in Rapid City, SD. After that she was raised in Milwaukee

with her sisters where she graduated South Division High School in 1971 and moved to

Rhinelander in the late 1970’s. She met her beloved husband Tom shortly after moving to

Rhinelander, and together they raised four children in the area. What was especially nice for her

was that her sister Vicki also lived in the same town, and their collective children were able to

grow up together, something she always cherished.

Mom has always been a stubborn woman with a strong work ethic. She kept a clean home,

even with all her children in it, enjoyed cooking and baking, and loved watching her soap operas

and painting her nails after a long day at work. She worked hard and while we didn’t have much

growing up, we always had a roof over our head, food on the table, and warm hugs before bed.

As she got older and all her kids were gone, she preferred listening to Celtic music and enjoying

her peaceful, quiet home while Dad sat at the table doing crossword puzzles. Mom was quite

the collector of things: she loved anything I Love Lucy, Celtic trinkets, teapots, books, and

anything lighthouse related. She firmly believed in the value of education, always pushing her

children to read, get good grades, and attend college, which they all did. Something unique

Mom always took pride in was that one of her children (Rex) and her great-niece (Morgan)

shared a birthday with her. Three generations, same birthday, something she always liked to tell

people. This year’s birthday will feel different, but we will all celebrate her in our own way. She

loved and was proud of her children and their spouses, as well as their children. Mom was so

happy all of her children found amazing life partners who would love us and support us the way

we deserved. She always told us she wanted to be a better mother than what she had growing

up and have better opportunities than what she had, and Mom, you did a wonderful job with

that.

Mom worked for Holiday Acres for a few short years after she moved to Rhinelander and then

worked at Petersen Health Care for over two decades before having to take an early retirement

in the late 2000’s due to health issues. Unable to work and being an avid reader, she devoted a

significant amount of time reading books and doing book reviews for authors and publishing

companies. She was very proud of her book reviews and blogs and made a lot of new friends

along the way. She also enjoyed being able to spend more time with her husband Tom, taking a

few vacations and riding in their Corvette.

In 1999, Mom and Dad were introduced to the Milwaukee Irish Festival and fell in love with the

fest, music, and experience overall. They attended Irish Fest for about 10 years before their

health worsened and they could no longer make the trip. Both Mom and Dad were able to visit

Ireland twice, and both trips were highlights of their lives, so much so they want their ashes to

be scattered in Ireland, a special place for both of them.

When Dad passed in 2024, Mom was never the same. She missed him so much and has

looked forward to seeing him again. The last three years after her renal failure diagnosis have

been hard on her body, mentally and physically, but we are so grateful to have gotten this extra

time with her. We believe Mom and Dad are back together again, and that brings us all peace

and comfort.

Kathleen is survived by her four children; Jen (Casey) Wolf, Erik (Emilse) Kelly, Rex (Emily)

Kelly, and Kate (Brian) Yonker. She is also survived by her sister Vicki (Tom) Shultz, her nieces

Heather, Amy, and Nicci, nephew Jeffrey, grandchildren Cobi, Dylan, Ethan, Gavon, and

Charlie, great nephews Grady, Liam, and Ajent, great nieces Morgan, Savannah, MaKenzie,

and Mya, and last but not least, her precious cat Baby, whom she and Dad both adored. She

was preceded in death by her husband Tom Kelly, sister Jackie Mosetter, and parents.

Mom has always been adamant about not having a funeral or memorial service, so we are

honoring her wishes. We instead would like to encourage anyone (in lieu of flowers or simply in

her memory) to make a donation to the Oneida County Humane Society or to the National

Kidney Foundation. We would also like to extend our sincere gratitude to the staff at DaVita

Kidney Care, both in Rhinelander and Minocqua, and Careview Health & Rehab in Minocqua for

taking such good care of her and treating her with the utmost respect, dignity, and compassion

that she deserved.

Mom, thank you for raising us right and loving us in the best way you knew how. We are happy

that you and Dad are back together again. We miss you, and we love you more.

You may leave your private condolences for the Kelly family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Kelly family.

records.name: Carolyn Simonis
records.summary: Carolyn Simonis, age 71 passed away on June 22, 2026.
records.body:
The family of Carolyn Simonis sadly conveys her passing on June 22, 2026, at the age of 71. She is a beloved mother, grandmother (Gma), great-grandmother, sister, and aunt.

Carolyn was born on January 1, 1955, in Wausau WI, the daughter of Margaret Farrar and Edgar Miller Jr. She resided in Missouri. She is survived by her children; David Miller (Wausau); Jessica Rainville (Brock) Wausau; Desiree Hunt (Billy) Piedmont MO; Grandchildren; Jordan, Gracie Alexis, Margaret (Andrew); Damien, Darius and Santino. Her great-grandchildren Taylor, Linnea, and Oaklyn; as well as her siblings Ed Miller, Laurie Wade, Brian Miller (Brenda), and Janelle Coulter (Harley). All will miss her dearly.

Carolyn is predeceased by her parents, her daughter Linnea, and stepfather Willie Farrar.

Carolyn was a free-spirited down to earth person who lived a simple life. Her vibrant colored tie-dyed shirts were her trademark. She enjoyed playing cards with friends, fish fry’s with John, going to Bingo at the casino and gambling after. She loved living in Piedmont MO in her tiny home right next to her daughter Desiree’ and family. She enjoyed sitting on her porch smoking a cigarette and keeping an eye on everything.

Carolyn’s family expresses their gratitude to the compassionate caregivers of the Aspirus Hospice House. Their attentiveness and proficiency during this difficult time for Carolyn, her family, and friends is commendable. Her cremation will be arranged by Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society, Schofield. A date for Carolyn’s celebration of life is yet to be determined. Online condolences and words of remembrance may be given at MWCS.WS

The following is a heartfelt note from “Gma’s “granddaughter Alexis.

The world lost some of its color. My Gma’s free soul is exploring the “spirit world” as she called it. My mom once said my Gma’s is the type that can just have 2 Pennie’s in her pockets and still be happy, and boy was that true - add a tie dye shirt, some quarters to play cards, and a pack of smokes, and she was set for life. She was always hard to pin down as she never stayed in one spot too long, but that is when she was the happiest. At the age of 16 she lived with the circus for 2 years. Does it get more free than that? She taught me how to fish, how to catch fireflies, milk a cow and then had me and my sister shake the milk in a mason jar to make butter (yes, it was a lot of shaking). We will never know all the roads she roamed, but the ones we do know about are pretty crazy stories. Words aren’t enough, but the memories are forever. Peace Gma, I’ll love you forever!

records.name: David A Pautz
records.summary: David A Pautz, age 68 passed away on June 22, 2026.
records.body:
David Pautz, 68, of Irma, Wisconsin, passed away at this home on June 22, 2026. David was born in Medford, Wisconsin, to Arlyn Pautz and Thelma Klein. He married Linda Schreiner in 1991.

Throughout his life David worked for the US Forest Service and on several farms, but his real passion was baking and cooking. He and Linda owned a bakery in Marathon City, and a secondhand store in Minnesota, then ran Dave’s Diner in Rib Lake for years.

In his retirement, David loved his chickens, fishing, his dogs and cats, working in the woods, and his garden.

He is survived by his wife, Linda; parents, Fred and Thelma Klein of Wausau; sisters, Cindy (Scott) Knoff of Medford, Dawn (Jeff) Hein of Medford, Christeen (Bill) Collins of Great Falls, Montana, Cheryl Kohler of Michigan, and Laurie (Ted) Dahl; brother Fred Klein Jr of Minneapolis; his children, Brenda of Merrill, WI, and Andrew Thums of Rib Lake; as well as his grandchildren, Isaac and Ahna.

David was preceded in death by his father Arlyn Pautz and brother Terry Pautz.

He will be remembered for his picking on his sisters and his world-famous potato salad.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family. Online condolences may be given at MWCS.WS

records.name: Donald D. Aspinwall
records.summary: Donald D. Aspinwall, age 83, of Schofield passed away on June 22, 2026.
records.body:
“Well done, good and faithful servant.”

With grateful hearts and abiding faith, we celebrate the life of Donald D. Aspinwall, who was called home to be with the Lord on June 22, 2026.

Don Aspinwall was born on September 28, 1942, in Ironwood, MI, to the late George and Esther Aspinwall. Don was the youngest of their 5 children. Growing up, Don enjoyed all the activities that living in the U.P. had to offer. While in high school, he met the love of his life, Jeanie. One of their favorite places to spend time together was at Little Girl’s Point. They would enjoy walking the cobblestone beach looking for agates, making a driftwood fire, and dreaming about their future together. Don graduated from L.L. Wright High School in 1960. He continued his education at Northland College. In 1964, he graduated with a B.S. degree. On June 27, 1964, Don and Jeanie got married and moved to Wausau.

Don began his teaching career as a Social Studies teacher at John Muir in 1964. In 1970, he made the career choice to work at Lincoln Hills School in Irma, WI. He started at Lincoln as a social worker, but the classroom is where his heart belonged. He loved history and the lessons that can be learned by studying it. He taught history for the remainder of his 29-year career at Lincoln Hills School. Don educated hundreds of youth, bringing the past to life through meaningful lessons, but his true gift was the connection that he was able to make with the youth. He was a trusted teacher, an inspiration to make positive life choices, a confidant, and a person who truly cared about them. Don received many letters from his former students after they left Lincoln Hills. The letters were full of gratitude for having been in his class and the impact that Don had on their lives.

Don and Jeanie raised three daughters. As a young family, they shared many wonderful times in the U.P. They went camping at Lake Superior and Lake Gogebic, snowmobiling, unforgettable days with grandparents, and family trips to Florida, California, and Oregon.

In retirement, Don and Jeanie loved to travel. Don was his own GPS. He knew every route by heart, where the best rest stops were, and which little towns to stay the night. Together they traveled to Florida, Arizona, Laughlin, Las Vegas, New York, Texas, and Colorado. They visited Ground Zero, Ellis Island, and the Rio Grande Valley in Texas. Don was always loving the drive and went wherever Jeanie wanted to go.

After Jeanie died in 2011, Don spent time volunteering in his middle daughter’s 2nd grade classroom. He would teach lessons on history, play card games, show the kids his magic thumb trick, or how he could wiggle his ears. He always drew his famous Snoopy on the board before he left. The students were always so excited for his visits. He enjoyed working with his oldest daughter, making pizzas for the Pizza Kiln, and visiting at the Clay House. He was a professional dough roller. Don assisted his youngest daughter in supporting local and regional cancer awareness efforts. He saw how positivity could grow from adversity. Don also volunteered with the Lions Club and The Never Forgotten Honor Flight for several years.

Don traveled to Montana for sport hunting of mule deer, a fishing trip to Woodland Caribou Provincial Park in Canada, and elk hunting in Idaho. Don was amazed to watch the sheep herder bring his flock of sheep down the road and how the dogs kept them together. He enjoyed going to the D.C. Everest football games and watching Badger and Packers games. He was always ready to play Smear or a competitive game of Doghouse.

His 7 grandchildren and 7 great-grandchildren adored him. “Papa Jack Hammer” made you feel happy and loved. He was always ready to challenge you in a thumb war, an arm-wrestling contest, or give you a ride on his knee. Don took many day trips to Bay Beach with the grandkids. His favorite ride was the tilt-o-whirl. He always supported the kids in their activities. As the grandchildren got older, he still stayed closely connected. He was a fabulous listener and was sincerely interested in what was happening in their lives. He offered advice in the form of a question because he wanted to guide them, and not to tell them what to do.

Don’s heart was not only filled with love for his family, but also for his pets. Throughout his life with Jeanie, and even after, he loved his dogs. Ellie and Maggie Mae were his companions in the later years. He called them ‘his girls,’ and they kept each other company. He would often chuckle about the howling session the three of them had each morning.

Don is survived by his daughters, Gretchen (Lee) Kluz of Ringle, Sarah (Kent) Kwick of Kronenwetter, and Amy (Steve) Zondlo of Schofield; grandchildren, Brittany (Nic) Lotzer, Paige (Steven) Button, and Hope (Mike) Rebman, Jordan Kwick, McKenzie (Jordan) Baltz and Brandon Kwick and Jack Zondlo; great-grandchildren, Gavin, Emmett, Finnley Lotzer, August Button, Peter and Penelope Rebman and Tennessee Jean Baltz. He is further survived by his brother, Dennis Aspinwall.

He is preceded in death and reunited with his wife, Jeanie Marie (Margetta) Aspinwall; brothers, George and Howard Aspinwall; sister, Marilyn Butson; and special mother-in-law, Gladys Margetta.

Memorial service will be held at 11:00 AM on Monday, July 20, 2026, at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston. Pastor Lance Hoelscher of Mount Olive Lutheran Church, Weston, will officiate. Visitation will be held from 9:30 AM until the time of the service at Ascend.

Don’s presence will be deeply missed. He truly was a ‘seed planter’ and parts of him will live on through us. Anyone who had a chance to know Donald Aspinwall has been truly blessed. As Don would say, “Keep Your Eyes on the Prize.”

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Judy May Kivlin
records.summary: Judy May Kivlin, 90, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on her 90th birthday.
records.body:
Judy May Kivlin was born on June 15, 1936, in Antigo, Wisconsin, to Joseph Andrew Baraniak and Magdeline Barbara (Schwingle) Baraniak. She passed away peacefully in Wausau, Wisconsin, on June 15, 2026-her 90th birthday.

She was preceded in death by her beloved husband, Larry Kivlin; her parents; her maternal and paternal grandparents; her three brothers and their spouses, Robert (Carol), Jack (Arlene), and Richard (Marge); and two nephews, Dean and Jeff.

Judy and Larry were married in May 1963 and shared a devoted life together until his passing in December 2008.

Judy lived a life grounded in family, friendship, and quiet dedication to the people and places she cherished. She found joy in simple pleasures, especially walking and spending time outdoors, where she felt most at peace. Music was also an important part of her life, and she especially enjoyed playing the piano.

She deeply valued her friendships and looked forward to her regular breakfast gatherings with friends at Coppertop in Madison, Wisconsin. Judy took great pride in her professional work with the State of Wisconsin and found meaning in her time volunteering at St. Mary's Hospital in Madison, where she was able to serve others and build lasting connections. She also supported her husband in operating the Kivlin Livestock Company.

Judy is survived by five nieces and four nephews, who will remember her for her warmth, steady presence, and the love she extended throughout her life.

She will be remembered with deep gratitude by all who knew her and will be dearly missed.

A Celebration of Life Service will be held at a later date.

records.name: Julia  Katheryn Minter
records.summary: Julia  Katheryn Minter, age 66 passed away on June 22, 2026.
records.body:
Julia Katheryn Minter (Mitchell) ‘Julie’ passed away peacefully on June 22nd at the age of 66 surrounded by the love of family and friends after a courageous and short battle with ALS. Julie was born on December 31, 1959, to Joan and John Mitchell and raised in Des Moines, Iowa. She was the youngest of four girls. In her younger years, she enjoyed time at the family farm in Winterset, Iowa playing with her siblings and cousins. Julie went to Roosevelt High School in Des Moines. A city girl who always had a love for nature and the country.

Julie met her husband Dan in Iowa, Christmas of 1991. They married soon after on June 13, 1992, and together built a beautiful life centered on faith, family, and adventure. They were blessed with two daughters, Christine in 1995 and Catherine in 1998, who were the greatest joys of her life.

Julie dedicated her life to serving others through recreation, ministry, healthcare, and compassionate care. She earned her bachelor’s degree in Recreation from the University of Northern Iowa. From 1974-1981 Julie worked with Moingona Girl Scout Camps. From 1982-1987 she was the YWCA Camp Wahpaton Director. Next, she was the director of the Black Hawk County YMCA day camps as well as the membership and public relations director from 1988-1992.

In 1992, she began working with her husband at One Stop Travel in Humboldt, Iowa and eventually they moved their family to Wausau, Wisconsin where they started their own business, Vacation Center etc., which they ran until 2008.

She then followed her calling to ministry and served as a youth minister for 5 years in Medford, WI and Clintonville, WI from 2008-2013 before becoming the director of the Catholic Life Center in Kohler and Sheboygan Falls, WI. She later answered another calling as a hospital chaplain with HSHS St. Vincent’s and St. Mary’s in Green Bay, WI. In this position she had the special privilege of serving people alongside her daughter, Christine, during the COVID-19 pandemic. She then concluded her professional ministry as a hospice chaplain with Aspirus in Central Wisconsin, providing comfort, dignity, and hope to countless patients and families.

Julie embraced life with enthusiasm and curiosity. She and Dan loved traveling together in their early years, making treasured memories at Disney with their children, throughout Europe, and on many cruises. In recent years, her love of travel was rekindled as she enjoyed several Caribbean cruises with her daughter, Catherine. Julie also discovered a creative passion for mosaic art, sharing many joyful hours creating alongside friends.

In October 2025, Julie was officially diagnosed with ALS. Though the disease progressed quickly, she continued to live with remarkable grace, courage, and faith. During her final months, she moved in with Christine, Michael and Ruth, where she cherished precious time with her granddaughter Ruth—watching her take her first steps, hearing her first words, and simply delighting in being "Oma."

Julie spent her final days at the Aspirus Hospice House in Wausau, WI, the very place where she had once served others as a hospice chaplain. It was a fitting full circle moment for a woman whose life's work was caring for those in their greatest moments of need.

Julie is preceded in death by her father John (1997) and mother Joan (2004). She is survived by her husband, Dan, daughter Catherine, daughter Christine (Michael), and treasured granddaughter Ruth. She is also survived by her siblings: Margaret (Steve), Mary Pat (nephew Michael), Sue (Vince, nieces Lizzie and Julie). Julie also leaves behind her beloved dogs, Hazel and Bella.

As a family we would like to thank the Mayo Clinic ALS Clinic team, Team Gleason, and the nurses and staff at the Aspirus Hospice House who kept her comfortable in her final moments.

In lieu of flowers, the family requests that memorial donations be made to Team Gleason in honor of Julie's courageous fight against ALS. Team Gleason provided many resources to our family to help care for Julie in her last months.

A funeral service will be held at Sacred Heart Catholic Church in Nekoosa, WI on Friday, July 3rd. There will be a visitation where we can share stories of Julie starting at 9 am with mass at 10 am. Lunch to follow.  A celebration of Julie's life will also be held in Iowa later this fall, where family and friends will gather to celebrate and remember the incredible woman she was, and the lasting impact she made on so many lives. A private burial for immediate family will occur at Restlawn Cemetery in Wausau, WI.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family. Online condolences and words of remembrance may be given at honorone.com.

records.name: Kevin R. Damask
records.summary: Kevin R. Damask, 53, of Hatley, passed away unexpectedly on June 17, 2026.
records.body:
Kevin R. Damask, 53, of Hatley, passed away unexpectedly on Wednesday, June 17, 2026.

Kevin was born on June 7, 1973, in Wausau, the youngest son of Lawrence and Susan (Esker) Damask.

He graduated from Wittenberg-Birnamwood High School and later attended Northcentral Technical College, where he earned a degree in Agricultural Technical Mechanics.

Kevin spent several years helping his parents on the family farm. Throughout his life, he worked in a variety of occupations, including concrete work, pole building construction, and most recently in maintenance at First Impressions. A gifted problem solver, Kevin took pride in his work and used his talents to build a beautiful gate for his parents.

Kevin had a deep love for horses and worked hard training and breaking several of them. He also enjoyed spending time outdoors, especially when he could be with his horse, Monty.

Kind-hearted and devoted to those he loved, Kevin cherished his family, particularly his niece and nephews. He will be remembered for his generosity and caring nature.

Kevin is survived by his parents, Lawrence and Susan Damask; his brothers, Ken (Shannon) Damask and Chris (Brenda) Damask; his niece and nephews, Brandon, Melanie, Dalton, and Coleman; along with many other relatives and friends.

He was preceded in death by his grandparents.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00am, on Friday, June 26, 2026, at St. Florian Catholic Church in Hatley, with Father Jerome Patric presiding. Burial will follow in the parish cemetery.

Visitation will be held Friday at the church from 9:00 a.m. until the time of Mass.

records.name: Louis G. Reissman
records.summary: Louis G. Reissman, 91, of the village of Maine, passed away on June 19, 2026.
records.body:
Louis "Louie" G. Reissman, 91, village of Maine, passed away on Friday, June 19, 2026, while under the care of the wonderful Palliative Care Unit at Aspirus Wausau Hospital. He was born February 6, 1935, in Birnamwood. He was the son of Paul and Elizabeth Reissman. Louie was married to the late Kathie Reissman.

Saying that Louie was a hard worker was an understatement. He retired from Marathon Electric and had worked for 42 years as a machinist. When he came home from work, he would start working in his Ginseng garden and was a grower for many years. He did the majority of the work himself and had the help of his family. After harvesting his crop of Ginseng, he would plant Christmas trees for a cut your own tree farm. The tree farm was his true love, from planting the seedlings to shearing, and then watching people come out with their families to pick the perfect tree. He loved watching all the memories families were making. Up until two months ago he planted 500 tree seedlings. What 91-year-old does that? Louie does.

Louie was a veteran of the United States Army. He served as an Army Engineer and Mail Clerk. He loved delivering the mail to all that served in his unit. He was stationed in Okinawa and Guam October 19, 1953, until October 19, 1956, during the Korean War.

He was a very spiritual man and loved his church and faith so much. Everyone in church he called his friend and loved them all. His favorite thing to do was to donate the Christmas tree for all the congregation to enjoy.

Louie had many special quotes. His morning greeting was always, "Daylight in the swamp." Another famous saying was, "I'm easy to get along with, if you don't believe me…just ask me!" Also, "Don't sweat the small stuff." Lastly, "Don't do as I do, do as I say!"

Family was extremely important to Louie, from his daughters and sons-in-law to his grandchildren and great-grandchildren. A birthday never passed without getting a phone call with him singing Happy Birthday. He enjoyed polka music every Sunday and was a true NASCAR fan.

Survivors include his two daughters, Terry (Randy) Hillman and Jody (Stuart) Gehrke; two step-children, Shelly and Shawn; grandchildren, Stephanie, Brittney, Sam, Brayden, and Lonnie; great-grandchildren, Augie and Eliza; step-grandchildren, Emily, Hayden, Wyatt, Savannah, Sydney, and Vincent; brothers, Elmer and Ervin; sister, Bernice; and brother-in-law, Lloyd. He is further survived by his two loving companions, his dog, Goldie and cat, Kitty.

In addition to his wife and parents, he was preceded in death by two sons, Steven Paul and Lonnie; brother, Raymond; and sisters, Dorothy and Marge.

The funeral service will be at 11:00 a.m. on Thursday, June 25, 2026, at the Round Barn at Willow Springs, 6905 North 60th Avenue, Wausau, with Rev. Maggie Westaby officiating. Visitation will begin at 9:00 a.m. until the time of service in the Round Barn. Burial will follow in St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Cemetery, Village of Maine, with military honors accorded by the American Legion Montgomery Plant Dudley Post #10.

records.name: MaryAnn H. Luther
records.summary: MaryAnn H. Luther, 84, passed away on May 20, 2026.
records.body:
MaryAnn H. (Faillaci) Luther, 84, passed away May 20, 2026.

She was born September 28, 1941, daughter of the late Santo and Frances (Campo) Faillaci. On October 11, 1969 she married Melvin Luther Jr. and he preceded her in death on March 16, 2020.

Survivors include her daughter, Malanie Burley; grandchildren, Isabella Javidan, Benjamin Javidan and Charles Sparrow.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated 4:30 pm Friday, June 26, 2026 at St. Michael Catholic Church, Wausau. Rev. Tom Lindner will preside. Visitation will be Friday from 3:30 pm until the time of service all at St. Michael Catholic Church, Wausau.

records.name: Myron M. Trzebiatowski
records.summary: Myron M. Trzebiatowski, 94, of Amherst, passed away peacefully on June 19, 2026.
records.body:
Myron (Mike) Maximillion Trzebiatowski passed away peacefully on June 19, 2026, surrounded by his loving family. He was deeply grateful to remain in the home where he was born, where he built a life and raised a family with Arlene, and where he proudly continued the family farming tradition until his death.

Myron was born on September 12, 1931, the fourth child of the late Max and Rose (Wierzba) Trzebiatowski.

Myron grew up in Amherst on the family farm, the original Trzebiatowski homestead. Through eighth grade, he attended Fountain Grove School, a small one-room neighborhood school which was just down the road from the farm. After completing his education, he continued helping on the family farm. During the winter months, he and several of his siblings would take the bus to Milwaukee to find work to support the family. He worked at places such as Trostel Tannery, Advanced Tool & Die, and Continental Can Company.

Myron joined the United States Army on November 13, 1952, and was honorably discharged on November 30, 1960.

Myron met the woman of his dreams, Arlene Marie Ebel, when she walked up to him at a wedding. She mistakenly thought Myron was Clarence, her girlfriend MaryJane's boyfriend. It turned out Myron was Clarence's brother, and this serendipitous mistake kicked off the beginning of an exceptionally long and happy marriage of nearly 69 years. They were united in marriage on June 29, 1957, at St. Stanislaus Catholic Church in Stevens Point.

Early in their marriage, Myron worked as a milkman for Fisher's Dairy, delivering milk to schools and families in the middle of the night. His true calling, however, was farming. In 1959, Mike and Arlene, and their growing family, moved back to the family farm. It is here that they carefully cultivated the land, but also lovingly raised their family. Together, they received the Century Farm Award, recognizing their devotion to both the land and their faith.

Myron will be remembered as an avid Brewers and Packers fan, for his enthusiasm for hunting, his ability to be a strategic Sheepshead player, for his love of pool and bowling, and his commitment to his church, where he was a member of St. Mary Holy Name Society.

Myron is survived by his beloved wife, Arlene; his children, Allen (Betti), Steve (Sandy), Donald (Maggie), Terry (Bob) Gahl, John (Lori), Karen (Bill) Desing, and Ryan (Jamie); his grandchildren and step-grandchildren, Isaac, Orean, Uriah, Marissa, Ashlee, Nathan, Gwen, Coleman, Audra, Henry, Julie, Dave, Zach, Rachel, Kyle, Stacey, Miranda, Chelsey, Eleanor, Vincent, Jillian, Lucy, and Wesley; and twenty-two great-grandchildren and step-great-grandchildren, with another on the way. He is also survived by his brother, Rudy, his sisters Arlene Wroblewski and Jan (Jim) Roska, and many nieces and nephews.

He was preceded in death by his son Matthew; his parents, Max and Rose; and his siblings, William, Chet, Clarence, Jerry, Nancy, Lucille Patoka, and Lorraine Wroblewski.

A funeral mass will be held on Saturday, June 27, 2026, at St. Mary of Mount Carmel, 3995 County Road K, Amherst, WI, presided by Father Jerzy Rebacz. The Rosary will begin at 8:45 a.m. followed by Visitation until time of service. Service will begin at 11:00 a.m. John J Buettgen Funeral Home is assisting the family.

In lieu of flowers, a donation may be sent to St. Jude's Foundation or to St Mary of Mount Carmel Restoration Fund.

A special thank you to the many people who helped Myron during his last few years, and especially to Heartland Hospice and Synergy Health who provided wonderful care and compassion. Their support allowed Myron's family to grant his final wish—to remain in his home for the remainder of his life.

records.name: Richard 'Dick' L. Ashbrenner
records.summary: Richard 'Dick' L. Ashbrenner, age 82, of Merrill passed away on Jun 22, 2026.
records.body:
Richard ‘Dick’ L. Ashbrenner, 82, of Merrill, passed away Monday, June 22, 2026, at Pine Crest Nursing Home, Merrill, surrounded by his family.

Dick was born December 6, 1943, in Merrill, son of Edward and Ruth (Morrison) Ashbrenner. He graduated from Merrill High School with the class of 1962. After graduation, Dick served his country in the Army National Guard. He married Judith ‘Judy’ Westfall on August 31, 1968, at St. Anthony’s Catholic Church in Athens. Dick attended NTC for welding and mechanic courses. He worked for Anderson Bros. & Johnson Company for over 40 years. Five of those years, Dick was part owner of the company. Later, Anderson Bros. & Johnson sold to Michel’s Corp and Dick continued working there until his retirement.

In earlier years, Dick enjoyed fishing and hunting. He especially looked forward to fishing with his family at his in-laws cabin on Island Lake in Mercer. Most of all, Dick enjoyed being at home. Whether it was tending to his lawn or garden; cutting, splitting and stacking firewood; or putzing in the garage, home is where he preferred to be. Dick loved his family; his son and granddaughters are his legacy and the purpose of his life.

Dick enjoyed reading, listening to various types of music, and was an avid Green Bay Packers fan. Dick liked working alongside his son, Rich, setting monuments and making firewood. As a child, Dick grew up on a farm and liked the animals, especially ducks and chickens. In later years, he had a fondness for pet crows.

Survivors include his wife of 57 years, Judy Ashbrenner of Merrill; son, Rich (Julie) Ashbrenner of Merrill; granddaughters, Ashley Ann, Lydia Mae, and Maria Judith; sister-in-law, Dallas Ashbrenner of Rhinelander; brother-in-law, Dennis Stiver of Merrill; sisters-in-law, Jean Westfall of Marathon, Geraldine (Larry) Natzke of Eau Claire, Carol Berndt of Merrill, Irene (David) Mroczenski of Marathon, and LuAnn (Mark) Schneider of Athens; and many nieces and nephews.

Dick was preceded in death by his parents, Edward and Ruth Ashbrenner; father-in-law and mother-in-law, Gordon and Katherine Westfall; sister, Marilyn Stiver; brother, Jim Ashbrenner; and brothers-in-law, John Westfall and James Berndt.

The Funeral Liturgy will take place at 11:00A.M., Tuesday, June 30, 2026, at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation – 301 Eagle Dr., Merrill. Father John Long will preside. The family will receive friends beginning at 9:00 A.M., Tuesday at the funeral home. Burial will take place in St. Francis Xavier Catholic Cemetery, Merrill, with graveside military rites conducted by V.F.W. Post 1638, Merrill.

In lieu of flowers, memorial in Dick’s name may be made to St. Francis Xavier Catholic Church or School in Merrill.

The family would like to thank the staff at Pine Crest Nursing Home Memory Care Unit and Aspirus Comfort Care & Hospice Services for their care.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Tommy L. Gatz
records.summary: Tommy L. Gatz, 77, of Birnamwood, died on June 19, 2026.
records.body:
Tommy 'Pearly' L. Gatz, 77 of Birnamwood, died on Friday, June 19, 2026, at Rennes Health and Rehab, Weston, under the care of Aspirus Comfort Care & Hospice.

Tom was born on February 4, 1949, in Wausau, the son of Stanley and Meta 'Mickey' (Jensema) Gatz.

In 1972, Tom was united in marriage to Linda Hanson in Mineral Point. They later divorced.

Tom graduated from Eau Claire Memorial High School before earning a degree in Police Science from UW–Platteville. Dedicated to serving and protecting his communities, he began his career as a Wisconsin DNR warden before continuing in law enforcement with the Shawano County Sheriff's Department. Tom also proudly served for 30 years with the Birnamwood Fire & Ambulance Department. He enjoyed hunting, fishing, being outdoors, shooting and collecting guns and spending time at his cabin. Tom loved traveling to Alaska, where he would pan for gold.

Tom is survived by two children, Daniel (Rachel) Gatz and Theresa Gatz; three grandchildren, Jaxon, Evelyn and Frank; siblings, Jeanne Gatz, Jim Gatz and David (Diane Williams) Gatz; brother-in-law, Gary Menard; sister-in-law, Ann Pretzel, as well as many cousins, nieces and nephews.

Tom was preceded in death by his parents; brother, Dean Gatz and sister, Patricia Menard.

A Time of Sharing will be held at 4PM on Thursday, June 25, 2026, at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Birnamwood. Burial will be in Forest Cemetery, Birnamwood. Visitation will be on Thursday, from 1PM until the time of sharing at the funeral home.

The family would like to thank Aspirus Comfort Care & Hospice for their kind and compassionate care given to Tom.

records.name: Adyn Noyce Roberts
records.summary: Adyn Noyce Roberts, age 23, of Schofield Barracks, Hawaii passed away on June 23, 2026.
records.body:
SPC Adyn N. Roberts, 23, passed away on Tuesday, June 23, 2026, while on active duty with the United States Army stationed at Schofield Barracks, Hawaii.

Adyn was born on April 21, 2003, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to Joshua Roberts and Niki Glisch. He graduated from D.C. Everest Senior High in 2021.

Adyn was known for bringing humor and sarcasm into every conversation. He was happiest when he was goofing around, making others laugh, and turning ordinary moments into fun. He had an answer for everything and seemed to know how to test the limits. Adyn also loved eating Ramen, BMX biking, video games, snowboarding, and was an avid fan of the Pittsburgh Steelers. Most of all, he loved his two children more than words can express.

He made the choice to better himself and his family by enlisting in the United States Army in 2023. He was currently stationed in Hawaii, proud of his role as a 13B Field Artillery Cannon Crewmember, working as part of a team responsible for operating and maintaining field artillery weapons.

From a skater boy with a man bun to a soldier – Adyn and his smile will never be forgotten by those who knew and loved him.

Adyn is survived by his wife, Trinity Roberts; their children, Tru and Rio; his father, Josh (Megan) Roberts; his mother, Niki Glisch; his siblings, Maddi, Will, Rhianan, Roman, Alissa, and Brenyn; and nephew and nieces, Colt, Eevie, and Malia. He is further survived by his grandparents, aunts, uncles, cousins, and other relatives and friends.

Funeral service will be held at 5:00 PM on Sunday, July 19, 2026, at Mount Olive Lutheran Church, 6205 Alderson Street, Weston. Visitation will be held from 2:00 PM until the time of service at the church. Military Honors will be accorded by the United States Army Honor Guard.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: April Louise Sazama
records.summary: April Louise Sazama, age 73, of Hatley passed away on June 23, 2026.
records.body:
April Louise Sazama, age 73, of Hatley, entered into the arms of her Savior on Tuesday, June 23, 2026, surrounded by her family at her home.

April was born on July 15, 1952, to the late Richard and Mary (Hull) Nuber. She married Keith A. Sazama on July 26, 1975, in South Milwaukee.

A nurturing individual with a servant's heart, April lived her life full of doing things for others and teaching her children to do the same. As a licensed practical nurse (LPN), she spent 38 years caring for patients in the intensive care unit and also specializing in clinical care. She most recently cared for residents at Rennes Health & Rehab in Weston.

Leading by example, April was a wife, mom, and grandma who put her family first; homeschooling some of her children and grandchildren, and educating them on the importance of volunteering. A notable experience for her children would be spending Christmas Eve volunteering to visit and sing for shut-ins. Further, April spent many years in civic work, serving her community as a Census taker and at the polls in Ringle for many elections. She often contributed annually to Operation Christmas Child to support the work of Samaritan's Purse.

April was an avid reader and among her many interests were sewing, gardening, canning, camping, her chickens, and doing anything to be frugal. She loved crafts, and it gave her joy to preserve family memories and create a legacy that lives on through scrapbooks and heritage albums. She was also an active member at Highland Community Church in Weston for over 25 years, planning events and participating in small groups at the church. She loved her bible studies with Keith and the many activities she helped organize or participated in.

She is survived by her husband, Keith; children, Jeremy (Laurie) Sazama, Jackie (Ted) Hahn, Brian (Nicole) Sazama, and Renee (Jason) Bostwick; as well as nine grandchildren, with one on the way. She is further survived by her siblings. She is preceded in death by her parents.

April's Celebration of Life will be held at 3:00 PM on Sunday, July 12, 2026, at Highland Community Church Weston Campus, 6615 County Rd. J, Weston. Pastor Dan MacDonald will officiate. A reception with cake, donuts, ice cream, and fellowship will follow the service. As April loved sunflowers, guests are welcome to wear sunflowers or sunflower colors in her honor.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Janet Eva Motz
records.summary: Janet Eva Motz, age 86, of Knowlton passed away on Jun 23, 2026.
records.body:
Janet Motz passed away peacefully on June 23, 2026, at the age of 86.

She was born in Faribault, MN. on August 5th, 1939, oldest daughter of the late Hugh and Vera Kenney. Over the years she built a life centered on faith, family and friends.

Jan was known for beautiful blue eyes and smile. She found joy in sewing, cooking and baking. She expressed her love through delicious home-cooked meals and baked goods. Family gatherings were especially dear to her. Her life was full of blessings, most notably her marriage of 66 years with her husband David.

Janet is survived by her children; Charles (Julie) Motz, Robert (Annette) Motz, Kathryn (Dave) Clark, Mary Ledebuhr.

Her grandchildren, Melissa Motz, Andrew Motz. Kyle (Ashley) Motz, Heather (Edvard Joseph) Motz, Cory Motz, Phillip Becker, Justin Becker, David Becker, Jacob Ledebuhr and Ryan Ledebuhr. Great grandchildren, Lincoln Caldero, Grayson Motz, Teddy Motz, Berkley Motz, Hadley Motz and Emmett Joseph. Also survived by her sister Kathleen Huffman and brother-in-law Arlyn Proksch. Besides her parents Janet was preceded in death by her brother James (Dorry) Kenney, sister Mary Kay Proksch and brother n law Dennis Huffman.

A Mass of Christian Burial will take place at 11:00 a.m., on Monday, June 29, 2026, at St. Francis Xavier Catholic Church, Knowlton. Father James Trempe will officiate. Burial will take place at St. Francis Xavier Cemetery. Visitation will be from 9:30 until 10:45, all at the church. Beste Funeral Home is honored to be assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be directed to the family at www.bestefh.com.

records.name: Patricia Krake
records.summary: Patricia Krake, age 88, of Scholfield passed away on Jun 23, 2026.
records.body:
Patricia Krake, 88 of Weston, WI passed away on June 23,2026 after a lengthy battle with dementia. She was born on March 13th, 1938, to the late Walter and Clara Kempf.

Patricia graduated from Wausau High School. She married David Krake on May 19th, 1963. Patricia spent most of her adult life as a homemaker, helping to raise the couple's three children. Patricia enjoyed cooking, canning and reading, but her absolute favorite thing to do was to go up to the family cottage near Lake Tomahawk, WI and go fishing.

Survivors include her husband David (Weston, WI), daughter Kelly (Coupland, TX), son Keith (Weston, WI), granddaughters Trista (Dallas, TX) and Kiera (Austin, TX), sisters Ellen Rustad (Rhinelander, WI), Bonnie Fisher ( Kronenwetter , WI), Sandy Marquardt (Wausau, WI) and Cathy Szekeress (Schofield, WI).

Patricia is preceded in death by her parents, Walter and Clara Kempf, son, Kevin Krake, brothers, Marvin Kempf and Donald Kempf, sisters, Carol Hess, Mary Ruplinger and Linda Kempf.

Funeral services will be 11:00 am Monday July 6th at St. Agnes Parish in Weston, WI. Visitation will be from 10:00 am until the time of service. Burial will follow at the parish cemetery.

The family would like to thank the staff at Mount View Care Center in Wausau, WI for the compassion and caring of Patricia in her final years.

records.name: Barbara L. Ortman
records.summary: Barbara L. Ortman, age 82, of Athens passed away on Jun 24, 2026.
records.body:
Barbara L. Ortman, 82, Athens, passed away Wednesday, June 24, 2026 at Marshfield Medical Center, Marshfield.

She was born May 6, 1944 in Wausau, daughter of the late Klenka and Elva (Venzke) Hargraves. On June 13, 1964 she married Jack Ortman at Bethlehem Lutheran Church, Milan. He preceded her in death on April 18, 2019.

Barbara was a proud homemaker and enjoyed the small things in life like playing solitaire on her iPad, enjoying crafts; especially making cabbage patch dolls and care bear dolls and spoiling her dogs. Barbara loved her grandchildren dearly and was fortunate enough to watch her grandson grow up since he was a baby.

Survivors include her children, John Ortman, Wisconsin Rapids, Joel Ortman, Wausau and Tammy Ortman, Athens, her loving grandchildren, Kasandra Lesieur, Wausau and Sequoia Miles, Athens. Barbara is further survived by her siblings, Chris (Roger) Heldt, Florida, Veronica (Ron) Wolf, Racine, Sherry Dissmore, Racine, Glenn (Cindy) Hargraves, Racine, Richard and Tommy Hargraves, both of Racine and Caryl Micetich, Chicago and numerous nieces and nephews.

Besides her parents and husband, she was preceded in death by and infant daughter, Vickie.

Memorial services will be held at 11:00 a.m. Saturday, July 11, 2026 at Bethlehem Lutheran Church, Milan. The Rev. Michael Mathey will officiate. Burial will be in Milan Lutheran Cemetery. Visitation will be on Saturday, July 11, 2026 from 9:00 a.m. until the time of services all at the church. Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Athens is in charge of arrangements. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Dale LaVern Hoffman
records.summary: Dale LaVern Hoffman, age 87 passed away on Jun 24, 2026.
records.body:
Dale Hoffman, 87, of Merrill, Wisconsin, finished his earthly journey and walked into the arms of the Lord on June 24, 2026, surrounded by his loving family.

Dale was born in Spirit, Wisconsin on July 22, 1938, to LaVern and Erna (Brietzke) Hoffman. When he was 17 years old, he talked his mother into signing consent and he enlisted into the United States Army, serving his country in the armored infantry. He told his family many stories about the military days when he operated tanks and represented his Army unit as an avid boxer. He had been stationed in Germany when he met the love of his life, Katherine (Filas) while home on leave, they were married on December 27, 1958 in Rib Lake, Wisconsin. The newlyweds made their home and raised their family in Merrill, Wisconsin, where he worked at Semling & Menke until his retirement in 1990. Katherine preceded him in death nine months prior, family and friends know he died of a broken heart and they are now together dancing in paradise.

Dale was an avid hunter and outdoorsman and will be remembered for his hunting and fishing skills. He taught his children how to survive and live off the land if needed. Dale was always the life of the party, family and friends will remember him for his outgoing personality, quick wit, and offering a beer to everyone that walked through his door. Their home was always the hub for family occasions and reunions. Many Saturday nights were spent playing poker and other card games with groups of family and friends.

Dale is survived by his children, Mary (Tim) Stevenson, Merrill and James (Tracey) Hoffman, Medford. He was so proud of his grandchildren, Brooke Stevenson, Merrill; Kyle (Miranda) Stevenson, Rib Mountain; Ashley Stevenson, Wausau; Aaron Hoffman, Merrill; Eric (Esther) Hoffman, Merrill; Lesa (Trevor) Vogel, Wausau; Aimee (John) Becker, Merrill; Kendra (Scott) Hoffman, Dodgeville; Jason (Jess) Hoffman, Medford; Andy Hoffman, Athens; Robert (Denise) Hoffman Jr, Merrill; Tracy (Travis) Thompson, Illinois; brother, Glen Hoffman, Montana; sisters, Myrna (Andy) Holmquist, Brantwood; Patricia (Emery) Mueller, Oak Creek; Luann (Daryl) Lind, Ogema; Nancy Kraeger, Texas; Linda (Jerry) Sanchez, Kansas; Rita (Scott) Renn, Tomahawk; brother-in-law, Frank (Rosie) Filas, Medford. Along with many great-grandchildren.

He was preceded in death by his wife, Katherine; son, Robert Hoffman Sr., Merrill; Parents, LaVern Hoffman, Antigo and Erna Kabalin, Brantwood; brother, Charles Siroin Sr., Clintonville, along with many other family and friends who greeted him in Heaven.

Family and friend gathering is scheduled at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, 301 S Eagle Drive, Wednesday, July 1, 2026 from 10am-1pm. Military burial will follow the gathering at the Lake View Cemetery in Rib Lake, Wisconsin.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: David A. Pautz
records.summary: David A. Pautz, age 68, of Irma, Wisconsin passed away on June 22, 2026.
records.body:
David Pautz, 68, of Irma, Wisconsin, passed away at this home on June 22, 2026. David was born in Medford, Wisconsin, to Arlyn Pautz and Thelma Klein. He married Linda Schreiner in 1991.

Throughout his life David worked for the US Forest Service and on several farms, but his real passion was baking and cooking. He and Linda owned a bakery in Marathon City, and a secondhand store in Minnesota, then ran Dave's Diner in Rib Lake for years.

In his retirement, David loved his chickens, fishing, his dogs and cats, working in the woods, and his garden.

He is survived by his wife, Linda; parents, Fred and Thelma Klein of Wausau; sisters, Cindy (Scott) Knoff of Medford, Dawn (Jeff) Hein of Medford, Christeen (Bill) Collins of Great Falls, Montana, Cheryl Kohler of Michigan, and Laurie (Ted) Dahl; brother Fred Klein Jr of Minneapolis; his children, Brenda of Merrill, WI, and Andrew Thums of Rib Lake; as well as his grandchildren, Isaac and Ahna.

David was preceded in death by his father Arlyn Pautz and brother Terry Pautz.

He will be remembered for his picking on his sisters and his world-famous potato salad.

records.name: Diane Veronica Dombeck
records.summary: Diane Veronica Dombeck, age 73 passed away on June 24, 2026.
records.body:
Diane V. Dombeck, 73, of Eland, passed away on Wednesday, June 24, 2026, at Bellin Hospital in Green Bay.

Diane was born on October 11, 1952, in Stevens Point, the daughter of Dominic and Viola (Kontney) Richter.

On October 2, 1971, Diane was united in marriage to Robert J. Dombeck at Sacred Heart Catholic Church, Polonia. Together they built a life centered on faith, family, and love until Robert's passing on March 27, 2009.

Diane wore many hats throughout her life, approaching every role with dedication, creativity, and compassion. For many years, she worked as a Home Interiors and Gifts sales consultant, where she formed many lasting friendships. She later spent 12 years providing administrative support at River View Construction.

Her creativity was a gift she generously shared with others. She designed wedding decorations that helped celebrate life's happiest moments and lovingly created hundreds of beautiful floral arrangements for gravesites that brought comfort to families as they honored the memories of their loved ones.

Diane found joy in life's simple pleasures. She loved traveling, reading a good book, diamond painting, and expressing her creativity through embroidery. She also shared a special bond with her horses and treasured the time she spent caring for them.

Her faith was a guiding force throughout her life and was reflected in the kindness, compassion, and generosity she showed to everyone she met. Above all, Diane found her greatest fulfillment in caring for others. She had a remarkable ability to offer comfort, encouragement, and strength, becoming a source of peace for many during times of loss and grief. She generously gave of her time through volunteer work in her community and never hesitated to lend a helping hand. She had a special way of making people feel welcomed, cared for, and loved, and her kindness left a lasting impression on everyone who knew her.

Nothing brought Diane more happiness than her family. She was a devoted mother and a proud grandmother who always made time for the people she loved. Whether sharing everyday moments or celebrating life's milestones, she cherished every opportunity to be together. Diane will be remembered for her kind heart, generous spirit, creativity, and the unconditional love she gave so freely to her family and friends.

Diane is survived by her two sons, Scott (Theresa) Dombeck of Elderon and Derek Dombeck of Eland; one daughter, Dawn (Patrick) Cherek of Rosholt; seven grandchildren, Victoria, Isaiah, Lauren, Emma, Makenna, Robbie, and Lexi; one great-grandson, Anthony; her sister, Debbie (Wally) Sell; her brother, Dale (Jenny) Richter; her loving partner of 16 years, David Grooms; as well as many nieces, nephews, brothers-in-law, sisters-in-law, extended family members, and dear friends.

She was preceded in death by her parents, Dominic and Viola Richter; her husband, Robert; and her beloved son, Kevin.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 12:00 noon on Thursday, July 2, 2026, at St. Ladislaus Catholic Church, Bevent. Fr. Augustine Bentil will preside. Burial will follow in the parish cemetery. Visitation will be held on Thursday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of Mass at the church, with a Rosary being recited at 9:00 a.m.

Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg is assisting the family with arrangements. Memories and messages of support may be shared at schmidtschulta.com.

records.name: George A. Lawrie
records.summary: George A. Lawrie, age 87 passed away on June 24, 2026.
records.body:
George Lawrie, a devoted husband, father, grandfather, great-grandfather, great-great-grandfather, veteran, and friend to many, passed away surrounded by the love of his family.

George proudly served his country in the United States Air Force, carrying with him the values of dedication, hard work, and service throughout his life.

George shared a beautiful life with his loving wife, Ginger Lawrie. Together they celebrated 65 years of marriage and built a life filled with love, hard work, and family. Their greatest blessing was the family they created together — 7 children and a legacy that grew to include 81 grandchildren, great-grandchildren, and great-great-grandchildren. Through every season of life, George and Ginger faced challenges, created memories, and built a family they were proud of.

George was a man with a kind heart, gentle smile, and hands that could fix just about anything. If something was broken, everyone knew who to call — George. Whether it was a home project, a repair, directions when someone was lost, or simply a helping hand, George was always there. He remodeled homes, repaired what others thought couldn’t be fixed, and taught his family the importance of hard work, patience, kindness, and taking care of one another.

When George wasn’t working on a project or helping someone else, he enjoyed the simple things in life. He loved spending time outdoors, especially hunting and fishing. The quiet moments, stories, and memories made along the way were something he treasured and shared with those he loved. More than anything, George loved his family. His greatest legacy isn’t found in the things he fixed or the houses he worked on — it is found in the family he helped create, the lessons he taught, and the love he gave so freely.

Family gatherings were always special, and anyone who knew George knew his famous line when the evening was winding down: “Well, I’ve got paperwork to do.” Sometimes there was paperwork waiting… sometimes there wasn’t — but everyone knew with a smile that it was George’s way of saying it was time to head home.

The children of George would like to extend a special thank you to their mother, Ginger. For 65 years she stood by his side as his wife, partner, and best friend. Through these last years, months, and days, she continued to show her love through her care, dedication, and devotion to him. The comfort, strength, and commitment she gave George throughout his journey is something we will forever be grateful for.

The family would also like to extend a heartfelt thank you to Ari and Gina, George’s caretakers, for the kindness, compassion, and care shown to George during this journey. Your support, patience, and the comfort you brought him will never be forgotten. A special thank you to the VA and hospice team for their guidance, support, and compassionate care, not only for George, but for our entire family during this difficult time.

George spent his life taking care of others, fixing what was broken, serving his country, and building a beautiful life with the people he loved most. His tools may be set down, but the love he built, the lessons he taught, and the memories he created will live on forever.

George leaves behind his beloved wife, Ginger, and their children: Dawn, Lori, George (Deb), Bill (Christine), Shelly, Brenda (Shannon), and Melissa (Kevin). He also leaves behind his many grandchildren, great-grandchildren, and great-great-grandchildren who will continue to carry on his love and legacy.

George was preceded in death by his parents; his in-laws, Ginger’s parents; his sisters, Sally and Judy; his sons-in-law, Rodney and Mathew; and other loved ones who passed before him.

A mass of Christian burial will take place at 11:00 am on Saturday, July 11, 2026, at St. John the Baptist Catholic Church, N4887 WI-17, Merrill, WI 54452. Visitation will take place from 10:00 am until the services at the church. Burial and military honors will follow the mass. John J. Buettgen Funeral Home is assisting the family. Online condolences and words of remembrance may be left at www.honorone.com.

And now, with a smile and a heart full of love, we can almost hear George saying one last time…

“Well, I’ve got paperwork to do now.”

records.name: Harold G. "Coke" Kohlbeck
records.summary: Harold G. "Coke" Kohlbeck, age 84, of MARSHFIELD passed away on Jun 24, 2026.
records.body:
Harold G. “Coke” Kohlbeck, 84, passed away peacefully the morning of Wednesday June 24, 2026 at the House of the Dove Hospice with his family at his side.

A funeral service will be held at 3pm on Monday, June 29, 2026 at Rembs Funeral Home, Marshfield with Deacon Nate Mueller officiating. A livestream of the service will be available on Coke’s obituary page at www.rembsfh.com. The visitation will follow until 7pm. Interment will be at a later date.

Born on September 7, 1941, in Marshfield, Wisconsin, Harold lived a life defined by hard work, service, and devotion to his family and community. Affectionately known as “Coke” since the age of three, a nickname that stayed with him throughout his entire life, he was known and loved by generations of family, friends, customers, and fellow veterans.

Harold was the beloved son of the late Wenzel and Laura Kohlbeck. He proudly served his country as a veteran of the United States Army. Following his military service, he remained dedicated to supporting fellow veterans and his community through his involvement with the American Legion. He served as Commander of the Marshfield American Legion, a position that reflected his patriotism, leadership, and commitment to helping others.

Harold was also an avid fan of the Green Bay Packers and the Milwaukee Brewers. He enjoyed following his favorite teams through the years and rarely missed an opportunity to cheer them on. Whether celebrating victories or enduring tough seasons, his loyalty to Wisconsin sports never wavered.

A skilled craftsman and hardworking entrepreneur, Harold owned and operated Kohlbeck Upholstery for most of his working life. Through his business, he earned a reputation for quality workmanship, honesty, and attention to detail. He took great pride in his craft and in serving the many customers who became friends over the years. He continued his work until retirement, leaving behind a legacy of dedication and craftsmanship that will be remembered by many throughout the community. Whether they knew him as Harold or simply as “Coke,” they knew a man whose life was marked by loyalty and kindness.

Harold was a devoted husband to his wife, Barbara, and a loving father to his four sons: Doug (Carol), Mike (Carolyn), Greg (Diane), and Ryan. He leaves behind his cherished grandchildren, Candice, Zachary, Amanda, Erin, Jack, Taylor, and Joe, as well as his beloved great-grandchildren, Calvin, Cece, Tucker, and Cooper, who brought him tremendous joy and pride.

Harold is also survived by his sisters, Janet Diers, Marilyn (Paul) Zimmerman, and Patti VerKilen. He was preceded in death by his parents, Wenzel and Laura Kohlbeck, siblings, Donna (Harold) Anderson, Roger (Gerri), and Bob (Molly), and inlaws Al Diers, Dennis Tessmer, and Tom VerKilen.

Family was the cornerstone of his life, and he cherished every opportunity to spend time with those he loved.

Harold’s memory will live on in the hearts of all who knew and loved him. He will be deeply missed and fondly remembered.

“Well done, good and faithful servant.”

In lieu of flowers donations may be made to The Highground Veterans Memorial Park in Coke's memory. https://www.paypal.com/us/fundraiser/charity/3799

records.name: Julia Katheryn Minter
records.summary: Julia Katheryn Minter, age 66, passed away on June 22, 2026.
records.body:
Julia Katheryn Minter (Mitchell) 'Julie' passed away peacefully on June 22nd at the age of 66 surrounded by the love of family and friends after a courageous and short battle with ALS. Julie was born on December 31, 1959, to Joan and John Mitchell and raised in Des Moines, Iowa. She was the youngest of four girls. In her younger years, she enjoyed time at the family farm in Winterset, Iowa playing with her siblings and cousins. Julie went to Roosevelt High School in Des Moines. A city girl who always had a love for nature and the country.

Julie met her husband Dan in Iowa, Christmas of 1991. They married soon after on June 13, 1992, and together built a beautiful life centered on faith, family, and adventure. They were blessed with two daughters, Christine in 1995 and Catherine in 1998, who were the greatest joys of her life.

Julie dedicated her life to serving others through recreation, ministry, healthcare, and compassionate care. She earned her bachelor's degree in Recreation from the University of Northern Iowa. From 1974-1981 Julie worked with Moingona Girl Scout Camps. From 1982-1987 she was the YWCA Camp Wahpaton Director. Next, she was the director of the Black Hawk County YMCA day camps as well as the membership and public relations director from 1988-1992.

In 1992, she began working with her husband at One Stop Travel in Humboldt, Iowa and eventually they moved their family to Wausau, Wisconsin where they started their own business, Vacation Center etc., which they ran until 2008.

She then followed her calling to ministry and served as a youth minister for 5 years in Medford, WI and Clintonville, WI from 2008-2013 before becoming the director of the Catholic Life Center in Kohler and Sheboygan Falls, WI. She later answered another calling as a hospital chaplain with HSHS St. Vincent's and St. Mary's in Green Bay, WI. In this position she had the special privilege of serving people alongside her daughter, Christine, during the COVID-19 pandemic. She then concluded her professional ministry as a hospice chaplain with Aspirus in Central Wisconsin, providing comfort, dignity, and hope to countless patients and families.

Julie embraced life with enthusiasm and curiosity. She and Dan loved traveling together in their early years, making treasured memories at Disney with their children, throughout Europe, and on many cruises. In recent years, her love of travel was rekindled as she enjoyed several Caribbean cruises with her daughter, Catherine. Julie also discovered a creative passion for mosaic art, sharing many joyful hours creating alongside friends.

In October 2025, Julie was officially diagnosed with ALS. Though the disease progressed quickly, she continued to live with remarkable grace, courage, and faith. During her final months, she moved in with Christine, Michael and Ruth, where she cherished precious time with her granddaughter Ruth—watching her take her first steps, hearing her first words, and simply delighting in being "Oma."

Julie spent her final days at the Aspirus Hospice House in Wausau, WI, the very place where she had once served others as a hospice chaplain. It was a fitting full circle moment for a woman whose life's work was caring for those in their greatest moments of need.

Julie is preceded in death by her father John (1997) and mother Joan (2004). She is survived by her husband, Dan, daughter Catherine, daughter Christine (Michael), and treasured granddaughter Ruth. She is also survived by her siblings: Margaret (Steve), Mary Pat (nephew Michael), Sue (Vince, nieces Lizzie and Julie). Julie also leaves behind her beloved dogs, Hazel and Bella.

As a family we would like to thank the Mayo Clinic ALS Clinic team, Team Gleason, and the nurses and staff at the Aspirus Hospice House who kept her comfortable in her final moments.

In lieu of flowers, the family requests that memorial donations be made to Team Gleason in honor of Julie's courageous fight against ALS. Team Gleason provided many resources to our family to help care for Julie in her last months.

A funeral service will be held at Sacred Heart Catholic Church in Nekoosa, WI on Friday, July 3rd. There will be a visitation where we can share stories of Julie starting at 9 am with mass at 10 am. Lunch to follow. A celebration of Julie's life will also be held in Iowa later this fall, where family and friends will gather to celebrate and remember the incredible woman she was, and the lasting impact she made on so many lives. A private burial for immediate family will occur at Restlawn Cemetery in Wausau, WI.

records.name: Lori Jane Steinke
records.summary: Lori Jane Steinke, age 64, of Wausau passed away on June 18, 2026.
records.body:
Lori Jane Steinke, 64, of Wausau, passed away on Thursday, June 18, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

Lori was born on February 13, 1962, in Wausau, to Ernest and Verona (Obernberger) Steinke.

Lori worked as a Registered Nurse for 30 years, caring for patients in Surgery, Oncology, Home Health, Short-Term Rehab, Clinic Management, Memory Care, Hospice, and Home Health Care. Notably, she worked for GI Associates in Wausau, where she was instrumental in the development of the endoscopy center. She loved a challenge and took pride in her ability to care for others. She loved her profession and the many co-workers along the way.

Lori's interests included crafting, any outdoor summer sport, and she especially loved going to her grandsons' sporting activities. She loved making things for others and giving – just because. She also had a love for music, often listening to Prince, Journey, Aerosmith, and more. Lori had many unwavering friendships and special bonds with countless memories from over the years. She also loved animals and had 3 cats, one surviving – her special emotional support cat, Lucy.

Lori is survived by her children, Jerry Robert Klemp, Amanda Rae (Klemp) Burtch (Matt) (their father, Jerry A Klemp), and Katrina Ann Raskie (Nik Watral); one grandson, Levi John Dahl (Alex), and one granddaughter, Kennedy Alice Burtch. She is further survived by her mother, seven sisters, aunt and uncle, as well as many cousins. Lori is preceded in death by a son, Dallas James Raskie (Steve); Steve M. Raskie; her father, Ernest Steinke; former mother-in-law, Alice Klemp; and numerous loved aunts, uncles, nieces, nephews. and cousins.

No formal services are being held. In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to the family for support, causes special to Lori, and for her grandchildren.

Lori believed we should all be kind, compassionate, and inquisitive, as you never know what any person, at any time, is going through. She would want us to stay present, to listen, to give loved ones and strangers an empathetic ear, and for us all to share meaningful time with those we love.

records.name: Mary S. Smith
records.summary: Mary S. Smith, age 80, of Wausau passed away on June 19, 2026.
records.body:
Mary S. (Salzman) Smith, of Wausau, passed away on June 19, 2026, one day shy of her 81st birthday. She was born on June 20, 1945.

Forty-eight years ago, Mary married the love of her life, James Smith, in Wausau, Wisconsin. Together they shared a life filled with love and devotion.

Mary is survived by her three siblings, Louise, Zookie, and Jim. She is further survived by many loving nieces and nephews, and many great nieces and nephews. Closest to her heart was Cece Upton, who was like a daughter to her and lovingly helped care for her for more than 15 years. Cece's children became the grandchildren Mary never had and brought great joy to her life.

Mary also leaves behind her two beloved fur babies, Rick and Marty.

She was preceded in death by nine siblings and her very special puppy, Dennis.

Per Mary's wishes, no services will be held.

Mary was a wonderful person with a big heart who was always giving to others. Her kindness, generosity, and love for family and friends will be remembered by all who knew her. She will be deeply missed.

records.name: Robert "Bob" John Swita
records.summary: Robert "Bob" John Swita, age 57, of Mosinee passed away on Jun 24, 2026.
records.body:
Robert “Bob” J. Swita, 57, died on Wednesday, June 24, 2026, at his home.

Bob was born on March 26, 1969, in Wausau, son of the late, John and Dolores (Stark) Swita. He married Patrice Zientara on August 15, 1998, at St. Paul Catholic Church, Mosinee, she survives.

Bob found his greatest joy in the outdoors, whether he was hunting, fishing, or exploring the trails on his snowmobile. A devoted animal lover, he cherished his faithful Labrador companions, Sophie and Buddy, and took exceptional care of Patti’s beloved horse, Lucy. He also enjoyed showing horses and proudly competed at regional events over the years. Known for his remarkable work ethic and generous heart, Bob was someone others could always count on. He never hesitated to lend a helping hand to family, friends, or neighbors, whether it was tending to the family’s large garden or assisting wherever he was needed. His quiet acts of kindness and caring nature left a lasting impression on all who had the privilege of knowing him.

Bob is survived by his wife, Patti; siblings, James Swita, Beveryly (Joe) Niescior, Thomas Swita, Charles Swita, Marie Swita, John Jr. Swita; nephew, Clayton (Nichole) Niewiadomski; sisters-in-law, Pam (Jerry) Krall, Kathy Freitag, John (Ann) Zientara, Mary (Tony) Suardini, and Ann (Jerry) Badeau; as well as many nieces and nephews.

A Mass of Christian Burial will take place at 10:30 a.m., on Friday, July 3, 2026, at St. Paul Catholic Church, Mosinee. Father Aaron Becker will officiate. Burial will take place at St. Paul Cemetery. Visitation will be held on Thursday, July 2, from 4:30 p.m., until 6:00 p.m., then will be again Friday, from 9:30 until the time of the service, all at the church. Beste Funeral Home, Mosinee, is honored to be assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences can be directed to the family at www.bestefh.com.

records.name: Robert Ronald Hoff
records.summary: Robert 'Bob' Ronald Hoff, age 82, of Wausau passed away on June 16, 2026.
records.body:
Robert 'Bob' Ronald Hoff of Wausau, age 82, went to his heavenly home on June 16, 2026, at his home. He was born February 15, 1944, in the town of Berlin, to the late William and Dorothy (Oelke) Hoff. He was united in marriage to LouAnn D. Seehafer at St. Johns Lutheran Church, in the town of Rib Falls, WI, on July 3, 1965.

Bob worked hard to provide a good life for his family, not only having a full-time job but growing ginseng, helping maintain his in-laws farm, all while building his and LouAnns' dream home. Bob was a social man, and was well known in the neighborhood for being willing and eager to talk to anyone. No one stayed a stranger for long around Bob. His greatest joy, however, was spending time with his grandsons, Bobby and Joe.

Bob is survived by his children Lynda (Victor) Smith of Yelm, WA; Robin (Deb) Hoff of Marathon, WI; and Renea (Zachary) Kania of Marathon, WI; Grandchildren Eric Smith, Kytaira (Casey) Sowles, Robert A. Hoff, and Joseph Kania; step-grandchildren Thomas (Nicole) Cimino, Jr., Don Cimino, and Amanda (Tim) Schmidt; Step-Great-Grandchildren Amelia Cimino; Layla and Carter Smith; and Connor and Kenley Cimino; He is also survived by many brothers and sisters, as well as nieces and nephews.

Robert was preceded death by his parents, his beloved wife LouAnn; brothers Donald Hoff and James Hoff; and sisters Elaine Schade and Pearl Voigt.

A memorial service will be held at St. John Immanuel Lutheran Church, 5304 North 44th Avenue, Wausau WI 54401, (Village of Maine), on Monday, July 20, 2026, at 11:00 AM. Reverend Maggie Westaby will officiate. A visitation will be held at church from 9:30 AM until the time of the service.

records.name: Ryan Paul Johnson
records.summary: Ryan Paul Johnson, age 52, of Schofield, Wisconsin passed away on June 19, 2026.
records.body:
Ryan Paul Johnson, age 52, of Schofield, Wisconsin passed away Friday, June 19, 2026. He was born June 20, 1973 in Owatonna, Minnesota, the son of Susan Johnson and the late Gary Johnson.

Ryan graduated from D.C. Everest High School in 1992. After high school, Ryan moved to Missoula, Montana. Shortly thereafter, he relocated to Greer, South Carolina, where he worked alongside his father as a salesman in the glass industry, running their warehouse. Returning to the Wausau area, he started his own landscaping business.

For those who knew Ryan, he was definitely not a Cheesehead. He preferred his purple and gold and loved harassing his Wisconsin friends- SKOL! In addition to football, Ryan also enjoyed hockey, fishing, flying his drones and above all, spending time with his friends. Ryan never missed an opportunity for a good time.

In addition to his maternal and paternal grandparents, Ryan was preceded in death by his father, Gary J. Johnson (South Carolina); brother, Jason; uncles Richard, Kevin Johnson and David Hansen. Ryan is survived by his mother, Susan J. Johnson (Schofield, WI); sister, Amy Haenggli and brother-in-law, Remo Haenggli (Switzerland); nieces, Kiehla and Annika Haenggli (Switzerland); stepmother Karen Johnson (South Carolina); along with many loving aunts, uncles, cousins and friends who loved him dearly and will sadly miss him.

As you think of Ryan throughout your life, remember his loving heart and kind soul; his zest for life and his love to be surrounded by family and friends.

A Celebration of Life for Ryan will be held on Saturday, June 27, 2026, from 2:00 p.m. until 5:00 p.m. at Brainard Funeral Home, 5712 Memorial Court, Weston, WI 54476.

The family wishes to thank the amazing and kind staff at the Aspirus Wausau Hospital, in the various departments that Ryan was admitted to throughout his time there.

In lieu of flowers, memorials are encouraged.

records.name: Donald "Donnie" Maass
records.summary: Donald "Donnie" Maass, age 53 passed away on Jun 25, 2026.
records.body:
Donnie passed away on June 25, 2026, at 3:42 a.m.

He was a reverend and an auto mechanic who generously helped members of his community repair their vehicles. During Christmas, he brought joy to many by dressing as Santa Claus. Donnie was know for his wonderful sense of humor, always making people laugh.

He is survived by: -Daughter: Cheyenne Olivia Maass -Sisters: Wonda and Candie -Brother: James -Other living relatives

records.name: Gladys Lorine Iczkowski
records.summary: Gladys Lorine Iczkowski, age 88, of Mosinee passed away on Jun 25, 2026.
records.body:
Gladys L. Iczkowski, 88, Mosinee, passed away peacefully on Thursday, June 25, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

She was born on January 3, 1938, in Ida, Louisiana. Daughter of the late John and Marie (Heflin) Tyra. She married Sylvester Iczkowski on June 27, 1957, in Bossier City, Louisiana, he survives.

Gladys loved gardening, watching hummingbirds and putting puzzles together. She had quite the collection. Most of all, she loved spending time with her children and grandchildren. Often telling them about her life in Louisiana. Even after all these years, she still had her southern accent. Gladys was loved by all and will be deeply missed.
