Gladys is survived by her husband, Sylvester; children, Daryl (Shirley) Iczkowski, Sharon (Jason) Laska, Janet (Dennis) Levandowski, Kenny (Diane) Iczkowski, Dale (Sheila) Iczkowski, and Greg (Stephanie) Iczkowski; daughter-in-law, Beth Iczkowski; 13 grandchildren; and 11 great-grandchildren. Besides her parents, Gladys was preceded in death by her children, Brenda, Randy and Terry Iczkowski; grandson, Blake Iczkowski; and her sisters, Geneva and Marie.

A Mass of Christian Burial will take place at 11:00 a.m., on Monday, July 6, 2026, at St. Paul Catholic Church, Mosinee. Father Aaron Becker will officiate. Burial will take place at St. Paul Cemetery. Visitation will be from 9:30 a.m., until the time of service, all at the church. Beste Funeral Home, Mosinee, is honored to be assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences can be directed to the family at www.bestefh.com.

A special Thank You to the Comfort Care team at Aspirus Hospital for the excellent care they showed mom in her time of need.

records.name: Michelle L. Zinser
records.summary: Michelle L. Zinser, age 57 passed away on Jun 25, 2026.
records.body:
Michelle L. Zinser, 57, of Weston, passed away on Thursday, June 25, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center in Marshfield. She was born on February 25, 1969, in Wausau, to Henry Zinser and Eileen Cook.

She was a proud employee of CTech Manufacturing, and her work was a large part of her life. Outside of work Michelle enjoyed crafts, tanning, motorcycles, and car racing.

Survivors include her daughter, Randi (Joe) Bowman; grandchildren, Koda, Gavin, and Savanna; mother, Eileen (Donald) Neumann; father, Henry (Deborah) Zinser; and brothers, Brian (Jen) Zinser and Curtis (Deanna) Zinser. She was preceded in death by her son, Donald.

Private services will take place at a later date. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Robert J. Hoheisel
records.summary: Robert J. Hoheisel, age 89, of Norway, MI passed away on Jun 25, 2026.
records.body: Robert James “Bob” Hoheisel passed away peacefully at Marshfield Medical Center in Marshfield, WI on June 25, 2026, at the age of 89. Bob was born on June 28, 1936, in Norway, the son of the late Emmanuel “Emil” and Josephine (Tavernini) Hoheisel. He graduated from Norway High School in 1954 and later from Michigan Tech University with a Drafting degree. He worked for Lakeshore Engineering in Kingsford until his retirement in 2001. He served his country as an infantryman in the U.S. Army in Germany from 1957 to 1959. Bob married Mary Yourkoski on September 3, 1960, in St. Barbara’s Church in Vulcan. Bob loved being outdoors, especially walking the woods at camp, The 4 Bucks Chalet, founded with his 3 sons in 1991. He loved his children but his grandchildren and great grandchildren were his joy. They benefited from his baking skills with “Grandpa Buns” and his special Apple Cinnamon kuchen ready and waiting on the counter when they arrived for a visit. Family, friends and neighbors could also rely on a gift of Christmas fudge at holiday time. We love you so much and miss you. You will be forever in our hearts. Bob is survived by his wife of 65 years Mary; 5 children: Kathy (Jeff) Barnett, Carol (Tim) Mooney, Susan (Mike) Bovee, Dan (Deb) Hoheisel, Karl (Patsy) Hoheisel and beloved daughter-in-law, Christa Hoheisel; 16 Grandchildren: Joshua, Sara, Emily, Claire, Hannah, Maria, Alexander, Chase, Kaitlyn, Trevor, Adam, Nicholas, Lucas, William, Patrick and Sophie; 8 Great Grandchildren: Lance, Leo, Julia, Noah, Stefan, Luca, Rhys, and Wesley; many nieces and nephews. In addition to his parents, Bob was preceded by his son David (Christa) Hoheisel; his sisters Marlea Bray and Marjorie Meiner; and his brother Jerome Hoheisel. Family will greet friends on Thursday, July 9, 2026 from 10-11 a.m. at St. Mary’s Church in Norway. A Memorial Mass will be celebrated on Thursday, July 9 at 11 a.m. at St. Mary’s Church with Fr. Abraham Mupparathara, officiating. Interment will follow at the Norway Township Cemetery, followed by a luncheon at St. Mary’s Parish Hall.

records.name: Susan J Surprise
records.summary: Susan J Surprise, age 73 passed away on June 25, 2026.
records.body:
Susan J. Surprise, 73, of Weston, passed away on June 25, 2026, at her home.

Susan worked at Kolbe and Kolbe for over 30 years before her retirement. She married Jim Surprise. He preceded her in death.

She is survived by her children, Beth (Chris) Flenory, of Milwaukee, Angie (Randy) Holz, and Ricky of Wausau; as well as her grandchildren.

There will be no services held at this time, and she will be cremated per her wishes.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family.

records.name: Adam  C Elsmo
records.summary: Adam  C Elsmo, age 65 passed away on June 26, 2026.
records.body:
Adam C. Elsmo, 65, passed away peacefully on June 26, 2026.

Adam was born on May 23, 1961, to the late Todd and Mary (Clark) Elsmo. He later married the love of his life, Jamie Babicky, and together they shared a life filled with love, happiness, and countless memories.

Adam proudly served his country in the United States Air Force before beginning a career as an over-the-road truck driver. He later worked in construction as a concrete laborer, where his strong work ethic and determination were evident to all who knew him.

A lifelong learner, Adam had an endless curiosity about the universe and the mysteries of the unknown. He enjoyed reading, spending quiet time at home, and finding peace in the solitude of country living. He especially loved planting trees, working in his yard, and creating a place of comfort and tranquility for his family. Adam was known for being stubborn and always believing he was right, but beneath that determined exterior was a devoted husband, loving father, and proud grandfather who cared deeply for those he loved.

He is lovingly survived by his wife, Jamie Elsmo; his daughter, Ashley; his cherished grandchildren, Devin and Lydia; his siblings, Alan Elsmo of Hartford, Alisia Albright of Athens, Wisconsin, Annette (Shawn Barke) Elsmo of Birnamwood, Wisconsin, and Angela Elsmo; along with several nieces, nephews, extended family members, and friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Todd and Mary (Clark) Elsmo; his mother- and father-in-law, James and Anna Mae Babicky; his brother-in-law, Joe Albright; and his brother, Aaron Elsmo.

Adam's family will forever remember his unwavering determination, his inquisitive mind, and the quiet joy he found in nature and home. Though he will be deeply missed, the memories he created and the love he shared will remain in the hearts of those who knew him.

A celebration of life will be held at a late date.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences and words of remembrance may be left at MWCS.ws.

records.name: Andrea D. Firkus
records.summary: Andrea D. Firkus, age 58, of Amherst passed away on Jun 26, 2026.
records.body:
Just after sunrise on Friday June 26th, 2026 at Marshfield Medical Center - Weston, Andrea “Andi” Firkus, 58, took her last breath and went home to be with loved ones lost before. Andi was surrounded by family and friends during her final hours and was showered with love from all who stopped by.

Andi was born on April 6, 1968 in Stevens Point to Thomas and Rose (Schill) Porter. She was united in marriage on March 24, 2001 to Jeremy Firkus. They were married for over 18 wonderful years before Jeremy passed away in 2020. Andi graduated from beauty school and enjoyed her time working as a beautician.

Andi enjoyed spending her time camping with friends and family and going for motorcycle rides. She loved crafting, playing with all of her kitties and spending time with her children and grandchildren. Andi also enjoyed traveling to The Badlands in South Dakota and all of her trips to tropical areas. Special times for Andi were always having people over for bonfires and 4th of July fireworks shows. Andi was known for being sassy and sarcastic, and would always have a witty comment for everyone that she talked to. Andi was an animal lover and would take in numerous animals to love on.

Andi is survived by her children, Tiffany (Christopher Bowden) Gumney of Pittsville, Thomas (Rhea) Firkus and Trinity (Adam) Firkus of Amherst. Grandchildren Ethan and Emma. Boyfriend Andrew “Itch” Kizewski. Step-mothers Linda Porter and Joanne Arndt. Her sisters Michelle (Michael) Gumney and Dawn Porter. Special neices Deidre and Shannon Gumney. Many aunts and uncles, brothers and sisters-in-laws, as well as many nieces, nephews and friends.

Andi was preceded in death by her parents, Thomas and Rose Porter, husband Jeremy, twin brothers Theodore and Thomas.

A celebration of life will be held on Saturday, July 25th from 2pm – 6pm at Smileys in Plover

The family wishes to thank the staff at the Marshfield Hospital ICU in Weston for taking such great care of Andi until the end. They were truly amazing.

records.name: April Louise Sazama
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: April Louise Sazama, age 73, of Hatley, passed away on June 23, 2026.
records.body:
April Louise Sazama, age 73, of Hatley, entered into the arms of her Savior on Tuesday, June 23, 2026, surrounded by her family at her home.

April was born on July 15, 1952, to the late Richard and Mary (Hull) Nuber. She married Keith A. Sazama on July 26, 1975, in South Milwaukee.

A nurturing individual with a servant's heart, April lived her life full of doing things for others and teaching her children to do the same. As a licensed practical nurse (LPN), she spent 38 years caring for patients in the intensive care unit and also specializing in clinical care. She most recently cared for residents at Rennes Health & Rehab in Weston.

Leading by example, April was a wife, mom, and grandma who put her family first; homeschooling some of her children and grandchildren, and educating them on the importance of volunteering. A notable experience for her children would be spending Christmas Eve volunteering to visit and sing for shut-ins. Further, April spent many years in civic work, serving her community as a Census taker and at the polls in Ringle for many elections. She often contributed annually to Operation Christmas Child to support the work of Samaritan's Purse.

April was an avid reader and among her many interests were sewing, gardening, canning, camping, her chickens, and doing anything to be frugal. She loved crafts, and it gave her joy to preserve family memories and create a legacy that lives on through scrapbooks and heritage albums. She was also an active member at Highland Community Church in Weston for over 25 years, planning events and participating in small groups at the church. She loved her bible studies with Keith and the many activities she helped organize or participated in.

She is survived by her husband, Keith; children, Jeremy (Laurie) Sazama, Jackie (Ted) Hahn, Brian (Nicole) Sazama, and Renee (Jason) Bostwick; as well as nine grandchildren, with one on the way. She is further survived by her siblings. She is preceded in death by her parents.

April's Celebration of Life will be held at 3:00 PM on Sunday, July 12, 2026, at Highland Community Church Weston Campus, 6615 County Rd. J, Weston. Pastor Dan MacDonald will officiate. A reception with cake, donuts, ice cream, and fellowship will follow the service. As April loved sunflowers, guests are welcome to wear sunflowers or sunflower colors in her honor.

records.name: Carolyn Simonis
records.summary: Carolyn Simonis, age 71, passed away on June 22, 2026.
records.body:
The family of Carolyn Simonis sadly conveys her passing on June 22, 2026, at the age of 71. She is a beloved mother, grandmother (Gma), great-grandmother, sister, and aunt.

Carolyn was born on January 1, 1955, in Wausau WI, the daughter of Margaret Farrar and Edgar Miller Jr. She resided in Missouri. She is survived by her children; David Miller (Wausau); Jessica Rainville (Brock) Wausau; Desiree Hunt (Billy) Piedmont MO; Grandchildren; Jordan, Gracie Alexis, Margaret (Andrew); Damien, Darius and Santino. Her great-grandchildren Taylor, Linnea, and Oaklyn; as well as her siblings Ed Miller, Laurie Wade, Brian Miller (Brenda), and Janelle Coulter (Harley). All will miss her dearly.

Carolyn is predeceased by her parents, her daughter Linnea, and stepfather Willie Farrar.

Carolyn was a free-spirited down to earth person who lived a simple life. Her vibrant colored tie-dyed shirts were her trademark. She enjoyed playing cards with friends, fish fry's with John, going to Bingo at the casino and gambling after. She loved living in Piedmont MO in her tiny home right next to her daughter Desiree' and family. She enjoyed sitting on her porch smoking a cigarette and keeping an eye on everything.

Carolyn's family expresses their gratitude to the compassionate caregivers of the Aspirus Hospice House. Their attentiveness and proficiency during this difficult time for Carolyn, her family, and friends is commendable. Her cremation will be arranged by Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society, Schofield. A date for Carolyn's celebration of life is yet to be determined.

The world lost some of its color. My Gma's free soul is exploring the "spirit world" as she called it. My mom once said my Gma's is the type that can just have 2 Pennie's in her pockets and still be happy, and boy was that true – add a tie dye shirt, some quarters to play cards, and a pack of smokes, and she was set for life. She was always hard to pin down as she never stayed in one spot too long, but that is when she was the happiest. At the age of 16 she lived with the circus for 2 years. Does it get more free than that? She taught me how to fish, how to catch fireflies, milk a cow and then had me and my sister shake the milk in a mason jar to make butter (yes, it was a lot of shaking). We will never know all the roads she roamed, but the ones we do know about are pretty crazy stories. Words aren't enough, but the memories are forever. Peace Gma, I'll love you forever!

records.name: Charles F. Kolbeck
records.summary: Charles F. Kolbeck, age 90 passed away on Jun 26, 2026.
records.body:
Charles F. Kolbeck, age 90, Marshfield, passed away on Friday, June 26, 2026, at Wells Nature View Assisted Living.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 am on Friday, July 10, 2026, at Our Lady of Peace Catholic Church, Marshfield, where visitation will be from 9:00 am until service time. Burial will be in Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Marshfield, where military rites will be conducted by the American Legion Post 54 of Marshfield. Rembs Funeral Home is assisting the family.

Charles was born on May 11, 1936, in Marshfield to Anton and Elsie (Ottelien) Kolbeck. He attended St. Andrew’s School in Rozellville, Purdy Junior High School, Marshfield, and Marshfield Senior High School. He entered the United States Army in May of 1955, as a specialist 3, serving at Fort Hood, Texas in the 6th Armored Division. He was honorably discharged in 1957.

After his discharge from the Army Chuck drove granite truck for his father for two years. He then drove semi-truck delivering farm machinery for H & S Manufacturing for 27 years. He retired in 1994 and drove part time for V & H Automotive delivering trucks throughout the United States, He also drove part time for AgSource in Stratford and hauled granite for Roehl Transport, Marshfield.

He married Phyllis Bohman on October 9, 1957, and they divorced. He later married Carmela J. Zais on November 4, 1972, at St. Michael’s Catholic Church in Hewitt.

Chuck enjoyed boxing, football, especially the Green Bay Packers, and NASCAR racing, especially watching his favorite driver, Jeff Gordon who he met in person several times. He was proud of the fact that he drove a regular NASCAR car at the Chicagoland Speedway.

He was a member of Our Lady of Peace Catholic Church, its Holy Name Society, and The Knights of Columbus.

He is survived by his wife, Carmela, a daughter, Sheila (Kenneth) Leitheuser, West Palm Beach, FL, a son, Ryan (Tera) Kolbeck, Cambridge, MN, grandchildren, Kenneth II (Gina) Leitheuser, West Palm Beach, FL, Dustin (Jessica) Leitheuser, West Palm Beach, FL, and Aiden and Hannah Kolbeck, Cambridge, MN, and twin great grandchildren, Clayton and Kyler Leitheuser, West Palm Beach, FL. He is further survived by brothers, Lyle (Janet) Kolbeck, Marshfield, and Verlin (Gloria) Kolbeck, Stratford, and many nieces and nephews and cousins.

He was preceded in death by his parents, brothers, LaVern and Jerome Kolbeck and sisters, Ardelle Powell and Jeraine Best.

The family would like to thank Wells Nature View, Heartland Hospice and Inclusa staffs for their care, concern, and support for Chuck and family.

records.name: Diane V. Dombeck
records.summary: Diane V. Dombeck, age 73, of Eland, passed away on June 24, 2026.
records.body:
Diane V. Dombeck, 73, of Eland, passed away on Wednesday, June 24, 2026, at Bellin Hospital in Green Bay.

Diane was born on October 11, 1952, in Stevens Point, the daughter of Dominic and Viola (Kontney) Richter.

On October 2, 1971, Diane was united in marriage to Robert J. Dombeck at Sacred Heart Catholic Church, Polonia. Together they built a life centered on faith, family, and love until Robert's passing on March 27, 2009.

Diane wore many hats throughout her life, approaching every role with dedication, creativity, and compassion. For many years, she worked as a Home Interiors and Gifts sales consultant, where she formed many lasting friendships. She later spent 12 years providing administrative support at River View Construction.

Her creativity was a gift she generously shared with others. She designed wedding decorations that helped celebrate life's happiest moments and lovingly created hundreds of beautiful floral arrangements for gravesites that brought comfort to families as they honored the memories of their loved ones.

Diane found joy in life's simple pleasures. She loved traveling, reading a good book, diamond painting, and expressing her creativity through embroidery. She also shared a special bond with her horses and treasured the time she spent caring for them.

Her faith was a guiding force throughout her life and was reflected in the kindness, compassion, and generosity she showed to everyone she met. Above all, Diane found her greatest fulfillment in caring for others. She had a remarkable ability to offer comfort, encouragement, and strength, becoming a source of peace for many during times of loss and grief. She generously gave of her time through volunteer work in her community and never hesitated to lend a helping hand. She had a special way of making people feel welcomed, cared for, and loved, and her kindness left a lasting impression on everyone who knew her.

Nothing brought Diane more happiness than her family. She was a devoted mother and a proud grandmother who always made time for the people she loved. Whether sharing everyday moments or celebrating life's milestones, she cherished every opportunity to be together. Diane will be remembered for her kind heart, generous spirit, creativity, and the unconditional love she gave so freely to her family and friends.

Diane is survived by her two sons, Scott (Theresa) Dombeck of Elderon and Derek Dombeck of Eland; one daughter, Dawn (Patrick) Cherek of Rosholt; seven grandchildren, Victoria, Isaiah, Lauren, Emma, Makenna, Robbie, and Lexi; one great-grandson, Anthony; her sister, Debbie (Wally) Sell; her brother, Dale (Jenny) Richter; her loving partner of 16 years, David Grooms; as well as many nieces, nephews, brothers-in-law, sisters-in-law, extended family members, and dear friends.

She was preceded in death by her parents, Dominic and Viola Richter; her husband, Robert; and her beloved son, Kevin.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 12:00 noon on Thursday, July 2, 2026, at St. Ladislaus Catholic Church, Bevent. Fr. Augustine Bentil will preside. Burial will follow in the parish cemetery. Visitation will be held on Thursday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of Mass at the church, with a Rosary being recited at 9:00 a.m.

records.name: Donald Raymond Turowski
records.summary: Donald Raymond Turowski, age 79 passed away on Jun 26, 2026.
records.body:
Donald Raymond Turowski of Athens, Wisconsin, died Friday, June 26, 2026 at age 79 years.

He was born January 19, 1947 in Bremerton, Washington to Ervin R. and Marie E. (Striebel) Turowski. He was united in marriage to Marilyn A. Haynes on June 13, 1970 in Suquamish, Washington. They were blessed by the Lord with 56 years of marriage.

Don enjoyed working in the woods as a forester for many uears. In 1996, he and his family moved to Wisconsin where he spent the next 30 years hand-crafting furniture.

In his youth, Don received Jesus Christ as his Lord and Saviour. He was a member of the Athens Mennonite Church, where he was ordained a deacon in 1998. He also served as deacon at Lena, Wisconsin for five years, returning to Athens in 2007.

He is survived by his wife, Marilyn, and three daughters, Anna (Noah) Amstutz of Athens, Sarah (Sidney) Halligan of Athens and Grace Turowski of the family home. He is survived by 12 grandchildren and is also survived by his brother T. James (Michele) Turowski of Seabeck, Washington and sister Catherine Lucas of Poulsbo, Washington.

He was preceded in death by his parents.

Don was a loving husband, father, and grandfather.

He lived for the good of others and will be greatly missed.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Michael "Jaws" Raymond Perkins
records.summary: Michael "Jaws" Raymond Perkins, age 67 passed away on Jun 26, 2026.
records.body:
Michael Raymond Perkins “Jaws”, 67 passed away unexpectedly Friday, June 26, 2026.

He was born March 29, 1959 in Ladysmith, son of the late Raymond and Jean (LaBelle) Perkins. On June 30, 1984, he married Gail Ellis at Schmidt’s Ballroom.

Mike enjoyed gun collecting, camping, kayaking and biking. He definitely had the gift for gab and loved checking up on his friends. He also enjoyed having coffee at the truck stop in Edgar and talking with his buddies. .

Survivors include his wife, Gail Perkins; son, Brandon (fiancé, Hailey Mathwich) Perkins and daughter, Ashley (Garret Leichtman) Perkins; brothers, Chris (Lori) Perkins and Nathan Perkins; nephew, Michael (Dana) Perkins; niece, Brittany (Eric) Braunreiter; mother-in-law, Audrey Rechner.

He was preceded in death by his parents.

Funeral service will be 7:00 pm Thursday, July 2, 2026, at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Athens. Rev. George Graham will officiate. Burial will be in Athens Village Cemetery. Visitation will be Thursday from 3:00 pm until the time of service all at the Athens funeral home.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Naomi C. Hardman
records.summary: Naomi C. Hardman, age 82, of Ringle passed away on Jun 26, 2026.
records.body:
Naomi Hardman was a redeemed child of God who believed that, by His grace, God sent his son Jesus to earth to become our substitute, to live a perfect life, to suffer and die in our stead, to save us from our sins, and to rise again showing us the promise of heaven. Naomi was God's gift to her Christian parents, Pastor Edwin and Mrs. Irmgard (Pape) Breiling, on December 13, 1943, in Geneva, NE. She entered God's Kingdom through Holy Baptism on December 25, 1943, at Grace Ev. Lutheran Church (Geneva). "Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved." Mark 16:16 . After receiving instruction in the doctrines of God's Word, Naomi was confirmed in the faith into which she had been baptized at Our Savior's Lutheran Church (Two Rivers, WI) on April 14, 1957. Her confirmation verse is Ephesians 2:10 "For we are God's workmanship created in Christ Jesus unto good works which God hath before ordained that we should walk in them."

Naomi attended elementary school at Randolph Elementary School (Randolph, WI) and St. John's Lutheran School (Two Rivers, WI). She attended Manitowoc Lutheran High School (Manitowoc, WI) for the first three years of high school and finished her final year in 1961 at Northwestern Preparatory School (Watertown, WI). She graduated from Dr. Martin Luther College (New Ulm, MN) in 1965, where she had trained for the teaching ministry.

She served the Lord teaching children the good news of Jesus at Grace Lutheran School (Yakima, WA), Immanuel Lutheran School (Tawas, MI), and St. Peter Lutheran School (Schofield, WI). She was also privileged and blessed to serve as organist, Sunday School teacher, and Vacation Bible School teacher in various WELS congregations. Helping with church functions, altar guild, and serving as a counselor at Camp Basic brought joy to her heart.

On June 17, 1972, God provided her with a faithful, God-fearing John Clay Hardman as her companion in this life. Her father united them in marriage at St John's Ev. Lutheran Church (Bay City, WI). Their marriage was lovingly blessed with three children. It was her special joy and delight to watch them grow in the Lord and serve Him. It was also her great joy to see the "children of her heart" (former students) bringing their young families to church. "I have no greater joy than to hear that my children are walking in the truth." 3 John 4

The Lord faithfully has delivered Naomi out of this world of sorrow by taking her soul to be with him in heaven on June 26, 2026. His amazing gift of her time of grace numbered 82 years, 6 months, and 13 days. We pray that the Lord would " teach us to number our days aright that we may gain a heart of wisdom." Psalm 90:12.

Naomi was preceded in death by her husband of 49 years. She is survived by their three children - Jennifer of Tempe, AZ, Aimee of Milwaukee, WI, and Christopher (Denise) of Ringle, WI, along with two grandchildren, Nicole (Tristin) and Neil Hardman. She is also survived by one sister, Janet (Joel) Fredrich of New Ulm, MN, and five nieces and nephews. She was also preceded in death by both her parents and a sister and brother-in-law: Karen and Ronald Shilling. Jesus said, "I am the resurrection and the life. He who believes in me will live, even though he dies; and whoever lives and believes in me will never die." John 11:25

The service of Christian funeral will be held at St. Peter Ev. Lutheran Church, 2822 N 6th St, Wausau, WI 54403, on July 6, 2026, at 11:00 am with a visitation prior at 10:00 am. Her mortal body will be laid to rest in Pine Grove Cemetery to await the glorious resurrection. Jesus promised, "Because I live, you also will live." John 14:19b

I KNOW THAT MY REDEEMER LIVES!

records.name: Robert Gerold Seidler
records.summary: Robert Gerold Seidler, age 89 passed away on June 26, 2026.
records.body:
Robert G. Seidler, age 89, passed away on June 26, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born on March 2, 1937.

A visitation will take place from 10:00 am until 12:00 pm on Thursday, July 9, 2026, at John J Buettgen Funeral Home, 948 Grand Ave, Schofield, WI 54476. Burial will military honors will take place at St. Paul Catholic Cemetery, Mosinee, Wisconsin.

John J Buettgen Funeral Home is assisting the family. Online condolences and words of remembrance may be left at www.honorone.com

records.name: Scott Michael Bouchard
records.summary: Scott Michael Bouchard, age 63, of Marathon passed away on June 26, 2026.
records.body:
Scott Michael Bouchard, 63, Marathon City, passed away peacefully on Friday, June 26, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital's Palliative Care Unit, surrounded by his sons, daughter-in-laws, and brother, Thomas.

He was born on October 9, 1962, at USAF Hospital Lackland AF Base in Texas to Velma Mae Bouchard and Donald Alvin Bouchard. He graduated from Newberry High School in May 1981. In 1995, he became a proud father to Dylan Bouchard and then again in 1999, to Brett Bouchard.

Scott worked at Marathon Cheese for 35 years. He retired in 2024. In his free time during retirement, he’d spend time with his grandchildren. He loved teaching his grandchildren how to play the drums and enjoyed listening to them rock out on the drum set. He was an avid lover of music, specifically the drums, which he played for most of his life. He played in numerous bands from high school, into his adult life. He was also an avid Green Bay Packers fan. He loved golf, which he spent much time playing with his friends. He played up until he physically couldn’t anymore. Other interests include Baseball, Hockey, cooking, movies, The Simpsons, and most importantly, spending time with his kids, and grandkids, and family.

Scott is survived by his children, Dylan Bouchard (Sami) of Merrill, WI; Brett Bouchard (Blanca) of Milwaukee, WI; grandchildren, Ava, Nicholai, Bailey, and Owen; as well as his mother, Velma Bouchard, of Newberry, MI. He is further survived by his siblings, Patricia Simi of Sault Ste. Marie, MI, David Bouchard (Jennifer) Bixby of OK, Richard Bouchard (Diane) of Newberry, MI, Thomas Bouchard of Tulsa, OK; as well as many nieces and nephews. He is preceded in death by his father, Donald Bouchard.

A Celebration of Life will be held at on Sunday, July 26, 2026, at 1:00 P.M. at the American Legion Post 74, 7964 State Highway M123, Newberry, MI.

The family wishes to thank Scott's old coworkers at Marathon Cheese for the card and well wishes during his time in the hospital, as well as a special thank you to his friends that visited him in the hospital.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Wendell Allen Hackworth
records.summary: Wendell Allen Hackworth, age 88 passed away on Jun 26, 2026.
records.body:
Wendell (Jake) Allen Hackworth, 88, passed away peacefully on Friday, June 26, 2026, at Aspirus Hospice House in Wausau, Wisconsin.

Jake was born on February 22, 1938, in Paintsville, Kentucky, to Ransom and Elva (Tackett) Hackworth. He was the second of ten children and was raised with the values of faith, hard work, and devotion to family that guided him throughout his life.

On October 10, 1959, Wendell married the love of his life, Connie Van Buskirk. Together they shared a loving marriage spanning more than six decades. Jake and Connie settled in Rocky River, Ohio to raise their only child, Craig. They were surrounded by many wonderful friends and family.

After leaving the family farm in Vanlue, Ohio, Jake attended technical college receiving a degree in medical technology. He worked in hospital laboratories and teaching until joining Fisher Scientific in 1968. He would start as a sales representative and ultimately ascend to assistant branch manager for the Cleveland office.

Throughout his adult life, Jake enjoyed golfing and fishing. Jake was active in Free Masonry throughout his entire life. He was a member in good standing with Lakewood Lodge No. 601 and the Al Koran Shrine until his passing.

Jake was preceded in death by his parents Ransom and Elva (Tackett) Hackworth and eight of his nine siblings.

He is survived by his beloved wife, Connie; his son, Craig Hackworth (Sarah); and his cherished grandchildren, Samuel(Renea) and Olivia.

The family wishes to express their sincere gratitude to the compassionate staff of Aspirus Home Hospice and the Hospice House for the exceptional care and kindness they provided during Wendell's final days. Donations in Jake's remembrance should be made to the Aspirus Wausau Hospice House.

records.name: Clifford  John  Hatz
records.summary: Clifford  John  Hatz, age 63 passed away on June 27, 2026.
records.body:
Clifford John Hatz, age 63, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly on June 27, 2026. He was born on May 7, 1963, in Merrill to the late John and Berdina (Luedtke) Hatz. Clifford graduated from Merrill High School in 1981.

Clifford had a lifelong passion for aviation. He was a self-employed aircraft craftsman specializing in restoring fabric covered antique and classic airplanes. Many of his restorations won prestigious awards at national shows. A good singer he was often called on to perform at weddings, parties and memorial services.

He is survived by his brother, Lyman (Judy) Hatz and his sister, Barbara Hatz; his nephews, Wyat Hatz and Jordan Lesky; and his niece, Angela (David) Jirovec. He will be deeply missed by his family and all who knew him.

Clifford was preceded in death by his parents and his brothers, Aaron Hatz and Allan Hatz.

His memory will live on in the hearts of those who loved him and in the skies he so dearly cherished.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family. Family and friends may go to mwcs.ws to leave words of remembrance.

records.name: David James Jones
records.summary: David James Jones, age 70, of Mosinee passed away on Jun 27, 2026.
records.body:
David J. Jones, 70, Mosinee, passed away unexpectedly on Saturday, June 27, 2026, at his home.

David was born on February 25, 1956, in Milwaukee, WI, a son of the late James and Winifred (Ladwig) Jones. He was a graduate of Reno High School and worked as a laborer, machinist and custodian for many years.

David was a man of remarkable talent whose creativity seemed to know no bounds. Gifted in leatherworking, woodworking, and craftsmanship, he had an incredible ability to transform an idea into something beautiful with his own hands. Whether creating custom leather pieces, building with wood, or tackling a new project, his inventive spirit and keen eye for detail were evident in everything he made. He found his greatest peace outdoors, where he enjoyed hunting, fishing, and embracing the beauty of nature. A cowboy at heart, David never tired of a good Western and admired the values of hard work, loyalty, and independence they represented. Above all, he cherished his family and friends and was especially proud of his three grandchildren, who brought immeasurable joy to his life. Though he often insisted he didn’t enjoy cooking, those closest to him knew better—he was the one behind the grill and in the kitchen at family gatherings, creating memorable meals that brought everyone together.

David is survived by his wife, Julee, two sons, Emmett (Amanda) and Garrett (Brianna); and three grandchildren, Coralee, Charly and Jameson. He was preceded in death by his parents and grandparents.

A Celebration of David’s life will take place at 2:00 p.m., on July 9, 2026, at Beste Funeral Home, Mosinee. Deacon Kevin Breit will officiate. Visitation will be from 12:00 p.m., until the time of service, all at the funeral home. Online condolence may be directed to the family at www.bestefh.com.

records.name: George J. Clements
records.summary: George J. Clements, age 84, of Weston passed away on June 27, 2026.
records.body:
George J. Clements, 84, was called to rest on June 27, 2026.

George is survived by his wife, Carla; sons, Ja (Jennifer), Lee (Pam); daughters, Shirley (Don); Kayla and Marion.

According to George's wishes, no services will be held.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: June L. Cizek
records.summary: June L. Cizek, age 96, of Weston passed away on June 27, 2026.
records.body:
On April 2, 1930, June L. Cizek was welcomed into her home and into the hearts of her parents, Erna (Frederick) Juhlke and Herbert Juhlke, and 96 years later, on June 27, 2026, her loving Savior, Jesus, welcomed her into her forever home.

She graduated from Park School - a one room schoolhouse, then Merrill High School, and later went on to receive her teaching degree from UW Eau Claire. On June 22, 1952, she married Rev. CJ "Cy" Cizek. He preceded her in death on April 1, 1996. She was baptized, confirmed and married in her home church, Trinity Evangelical Lutheran Church, Town of Berlin. There she began playing the organ and singing in their choir.  June taught elementary school, kindergarten and preschool, retiring in 1992 from Rib Mountain Lutheran Church.

Music always had a special place in her life. Along with music, she enjoyed dancing, traveling, reading and spending time with family and friends. She enjoyed working crossword puzzles, playing card games with her friend group, and working in her flower garden. She was a member of Lutheran Women's Missionary League and also volunteered at Bethesda Thrift Shop for many years. She was a member of Mount Olive Lutheran Church, where her husband served as pastor, and she had been their organist and choir director.

Survivors include her two children, Coralee "Cori" (Stephen) Foster of Wausau, and Curt (Linda) Cizek of KY; three grandchildren, Casey Foster, Lauren Foster, and Andrew Cizek; and her sister, Dawn Evanson of Tomahawk; and her nieces, Dawn Anderson of Johnson Creek and Debbie Kurtz of Waukesha.

In addition to her parents and husband, she was further preceded in death by her infant brother, Victor and her sister, Darlene (Tootie) Slewitzke.

Funeral service will be held at 11:00 A.M. on Thursday, July 9, 2026, at Mount Olive Lutheran Church, 6205 Alderson St., Weston.  Pastor Lance Hoelscher will officiate.  Visitation will be from 9:00 A.M. until the time of service at the church.  Entombment will take place at Merrill Memorial Park Cemetery.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be given to Mount Olive Lutheran Church's Music Department.

The family would like to thank the caregivers of Aspirus Hospice, especially nurse Haley and social worker, Carrie.  They'd like to also acknowledge Inner Peace Home Health Services - Nicole, Zory and Peggy for their kindness and care, as well as June's longtime physician, Dr. Vicky Baker, and Young's Drug Store in Wausau.  Also, a very special thank you to June's sister, Dawn, who has lived with and cared for June in her final three years.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements.  Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Lyle R. Hotchkiss
records.summary: Lyle R. Hotchkiss, age 83, of Rothschild passed away on Jun 27, 2026.
records.body:
Lyle Richard Hotchkiss, 83 of Rothschild Wisconsin peacefully passed away on June 27, 2026.

Lyle was born March 24, 1943, to Lester and Ruth (Plautz) Hotchkiss. He was a graduate of Wausau High School class of 1961. He married his wife Darlene Lepak, who survives him, on September 18, 1965, in Wausau at Holy Name Church.

Lyle served his country in the Army Reserves and was honorably discharged as a staﬀ sergeant. Lyle began working as a route driver for Topper Dairy. His trucking career started with Paul Radtke trucking. He then moved on to long haul driver for Wausau Metals delivering windows to the East Coast. He then joined the sales staﬀ for Wausau Metals and eventually retired from his estimator position at Old Castle Glass. Lyle was also a dedicated Amway consultant for many years.

He was a longtime member of Saint Mark Catholic Church. He volunteered his time as an Usher, assisting with ticket sales for the June Bug Festival and calling Bingo with his wife, Darlene.

Lyle had many hobbies, including cross country skiing and snowmobiling with family and friends, he loved to bowl and was a member of a couple of league teams with Darlene. Lyle enjoyed spending time with his cats Amy and Scooter and sunny summer days floating in the backyard pool. He was an avid sports fan of both the Packers and Brewers.

Lyle was proceeded in death by his parents, In-laws Lawrence and Cecilia (Busko) Lepak brother Les, Jr. (Sharon Niewolney) and sister-in-law Dianne (Smith) Lepak.

He is survived by his wife, Darlene of 60 years, son Brad (Sally) stepsons Al and Mitch, daughter Beth (Rob) Southwick, three granddaughters Carly (Greg) Grasse, Megan (Ryan) Vance and Rachel Southwick, brother Larry (Sally), and sister Connie (Mike) Vaningan, brothers-in-law Larry Lepak (Sue White), Randy Lepak (Jennifer) and Glenn (Dianne) Smith along with many nieces and nephews.

Funeral service will be held on Friday, July 10 at Restlawn Memorial Chapel. Visitation will begin at 10 am with service to follow at 11am. Entombment with follow immediately at the Restlawn Memorial Chapel.

Helke Funeral Home is honored to be assisting the family during this time.

The family wishes to thank the staﬀ at Lili Gardens Assisted Living for their patience and kindness in caring for Lyle, especially Charlie ;-) and the compassionate and caring staﬀ from Heartland Hospice.

records.name: Celestine Tesch
records.summary: Celestine Tesch, age 78, of Wausau passed away on June 28, 2026.
records.body:
Celestine Tesch, also known as Tudy, age 78, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on June 28, 2026.

Born on September 3, 1947, in Holcomb, Wisconsin. The daughter of Henry and Melba (Towle) Pahl. Celestine was, above all, a person devoted to her family. She cherished every moment spent with her children, grandchildren, and great-grandchildren, holding each member of her family close to her heart and making them feel valued and loved in her special way. Those she cared for will remember her unfailing kindness, her curiosity, and her joy in both everyday moments and family milestones.

A gifted jewelry maker, Celestine had a creative spirit and a gentle hand. She found her passion in crafting beautiful, meaningful pieces, which she often gifted to loved ones. Her talents were a reflection of her character unique, thoughtful, and always shining with her warmth.

Tudy had a reputation as a good Samaritan and a genuinely good person. She was always willing to go out of her way to help others, embodying compassion in her actions and her words. She delighted in the simple pleasures of life, from running errands with zest earning her the affectionate title of "runner arounder" to browsing her favorite shops. Shopping was more than a pastime for Celestine; it was a source of connection and joy, an opportunity to think of others and perhaps find a small treasure to brighten someone’s day. Those who knew Tudy will remember her most for her unwavering love, her giving nature, and her joyful presence.

She is survived by her grandchildren and great-grandchildren. Celestine is preceded in death by her parents, husband; Terry, and her children; Scott Tesch, Shelly Alsteen, Shawn Tesch and Sarah Janka.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Elizabeth Ann Otteson
records.summary: Elizabeth Ann Otteson, age 44 passed away on June 28, 2026.
records.body:
In loving memory of Elizabeth Ann Otteson (Lieder), age 44 of Rhinelander. After a courageous and unwavering battle with cancer Beth passed away on June 28, 2026. Beth was born on October 24, 1981 to Peter Lieder and Barbara Dankert in Hartford Wi. On November 21 of 2007 Beth married Karl Otteson, she would have said she was under the influence but we all know it was because they loved each other deeply.

Beth graduated Three Lakes High School in 2000 and started her health care career, in 2019 Beth got her TMA/Med Tech certification. She worked over 25 years with care and compassion for others.

Beth is survived by her loving and devoted husband Karl Otteson, and her children Izabella and Ian Otteson. She will be deeply missed by her father Pete Lieder, Ute Stampfl her second mom, her siblings Bruce, Jason, and Rebecca Lieder; She will also be missed by her parents in-law Diana and Larry Sparling along with Kurt and Gayle Otteson and many other family and friends. Beth was preceded in death by her mother Barbara Dankert.

She enjoyed kayaking, fishing, and exploring numerous waterfalls with her family. Beth cherished the first big family road trip to the black hills of South Dakota. She loved her pets no matter the species, she also loved going out to dinner with her friends.

Beth was a loving mother, wife, and friend. She was the hardest and most stubborn worker throughout her life and devoted herself to caring for others.

The family would like to give their deepest gratitude to all the staff who helped Beth with her battle and journey with cancer. Beth’s strength, kindness, and radiant spirit will forever live on in our hearts. She will be greatly missed, but long remembered by all who knew her.

A celebration of life will be held at a later date. You may leave your private condolences for the Otteson family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Otteson family.

records.name: Frank D Lanier
records.summary: Frank D Lanier, age 62, of Marshfield passed away on Jun 28, 2026.
records.body:
Frankie D. Lanier, 62, of Marshfield passed away Sunday, June 28, 2026 at his home unexpectedly.

A memorial service will be held 11:00 am Wednesday, July 15th at the Rembs Funeral Home in Marshfield with Pastor Preston Tippen officiating. The family welcomes relatives and friends for visitation from 9am until time of service at the funeral home. Military Rites will follow provided by the VFW Mueller-Hintz Post #1866 of Marshfield. Interment will take place later in Joliet, IL.

Frank was born May 31, 1964 in Chicago, IL to Dean Ivory and Bernice Morris. He was the 4th of 6 children. The family resided in Joliet, IL during his formative years and he attended public schools there, graduating from Joliet East High School. Following his schooling Frank enlisted in the Marine Corps and served as a cook from 1984 to 1988. 20 years later he felt the call to serve again and joined the Army from 2007-2009. Frank was a man of faith and strong force in the family. He was humble and it was his honor to serve at his church. He enjoyed fishing and football, especially cheering on the Bears. He will be remembered lovingly as a kind man and a jokester.

Frank is survived by his wife Barbara at home with his fur babies Cali, Faith and George as well his children Josh and Cori Lanier. He is further survived by his siblings: Vivian (Larry) Crawford of Joliet, IL, Paul (Carla) Lanier of Columbus, OH, James Rosée Lanier of Palm Bay, FL, Mia Tammy (Shawn) Banks of Limerick, PA; and half-siblings Carolyn and Denise. And his step daughter Linda (Adam) Rappley, step-grandchildren Kylie Lindner, Jonah Rappley, and Gracelynn Rappley. He is also survived by his siblings-in-law John (Ann) Weis, Gary (Laura) Weis, Amy Reading, Bethany (Dan) Reading, Sheri Reading, and Scott Reading.

He was preceded in death by his parents, his brother Sylvanus "Vaan" Lanier, and his brothers-in-law Michael Weis and Justin Reading.

records.name: Harold "Harry" J. Gust Jr.
records.summary: Harold "Harry" J. Gust Jr., age 84, of Marshfield passed away on Jun 28, 2026.
records.body:
Harold “Harry” Joseph Gust Jr, aged 84, peacefully entered into eternal life on June 28, 2026, surrounded by his family. He was born on June 18, 1942, a son of the late Harold and Sara Gust Sr.

Harry was a dedicated entrepreneur and the proud owner of Gust Decorators for over 50 years. Beyond his work, Harry enjoyed the outdoors - snowmobiling, hunting, fishing, camping, and spending time with his family.

Harold is survived by his loving wife of 63 years, Shirley (Newmier) Gust; one son, Gregory (Amy) Gust of Wisconsin Rapids; three grandchildren, Noah Gust (Alissa Meronk), Heidi (Matthew) Gochee, and Abbey Gust; and two great grandchildren, Anthony and Isaac.

Harold is also survived by 5 siblings; Nelda Wolf, SaraLyn Gust-Heiting , Dan (Maria) Gust, all of Marshfield; Duwayne Gust of La Crosse, WI; and Mary Gust of Chaska, MN.

The family wishes to extend their heartfelt gratitude to the dedicated staff at North Shore Healthcare Center and Heartland Hospice for the exceptional care they provided.

A private celebration of life will be held.

records.name: Jerome "Mike" Wozniczka
records.summary: Jerome "Mike" Wozniczka, age 66 passed away on June 28, 2026.
records.body:
A lifelong resident of Rhinelander, Mike Wozniczka, 66, passed away on June 28, 2026. Mike

married his wife, Carol, on October 15, 1983 in Green Bay, Wisconsin. He started working at

Campbell’s Clothing as a young boy and to this day the core group of employees are still

friends and meet once a month for dinner. Mike cherished his Campbell’s Family. Mike retired

from AVFCO Supply on August 29, 2025. He was a simple man in the best way. Enjoyed fishing

with his Uncle Doug who was really more like a brother to him. He enjoyed visiting with friends,

meeting with his coffee clutching guys every morning, cooking out in the backyard and

spending time with his wife, son, favorite daughter-in-law, and had a very special relationship

with his grandson.

Mike recently suffered two strokes (March and May) and was recovering from those. On

Sunday, June 21, 2026 he received the news that he had stage 4 pancreatic cancer that had

metastasized throughout his body. He went home under hospice care on June 24, 2026 and

died peacefully at home with his family.

Mike was able to communicate his wishes to Carol and Nicholas and they were able to say

their goodbyes. He was loved by many, and no one ever had a bad word to say about him. In

true Mike form, even with the seriousness of his diagnosis, he didn’t feel sorry for himself. He

was more concerned about his family and how they were doing.

Carol, Nicholas, Breanna and Charley thank everyone for their kind thoughts and prayers. A

special thank you goes out to the nurses, doctors, and CNA’s at Aspirus Rhinelander Hospital.

They treated the family with dignity, compassion, and kindness. The family also wants to

recognize Aspirus Home Hospice nurses who helped them keep Mike comfortable at home.

One of the last things Mike said to Carol and Nicholas was “love each other”, and that’s what

the family wishes for his family and friends.

Visitation for Mike will be held on Friday, July 10, 2026 from 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM, at the Carlson Funeral Home. You may leave your private condolences for the Wozniczka family at www.carlsonfh.com. A complete notice will follow shortly. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Wozniczka family.

records.name: Kim Allen Koval
records.summary: Kim Allen Koval, age 68 passed away on June 28, 2026.
records.body:
Kim Allen Koval, 68, passed away peacefully on Sunday, June 28, 2026, at Aspirus Hospice House, surrounded by the love of his family.

Kim was born on September 18, 1957, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to Wesley and Julie Koval. As the oldest of five children, he embraced the role of big brother and remained devoted to his family throughout his life.

If something was broken, Kim could probably fix it. And if he couldn't, he'd keep it just because “you never know you might need it someday”. A true "jack of all trades," he had a knack for solving problems, working with his hands, and taking on projects. There weren’t many things he didn’t think he could fix with little determination, the right tool or in most cases a random tool he would just make work. Whether helping family, friends, or neighbors, Kim was always the first to lend a hand and rarely expected anything in return.

Kim found his happiest moments outdoors. Whether camping with family, sitting quietly in a fishing boat, or heading into the woods for hunting season, nature was where he felt most at home. He loved the simple things in life, the fresh air, a good campfire, and stories shared long after the sun went down and the occasional red dog. Those adventures created memories his family will treasure forever.

Above all, Kims greatest pride was his family. He is survived by his wife and best friend of 34 years, Debbie (Deb, as he called her), his three younger siblings, Shari Sickinger and husband Pat, Carla Hutchison, and Gregg Koval and wife Mary; his eight children Adrian Bixby and her husband Jerald, Chris Rice and significant other Susan, Brenda Nemitz and husband Matthew, Kristy Lemmer and husband Troy, Greg Rice and wife Michelle, Ryan Tappan and husband Luke, Richard Rice and wife Julia, and Cody Koval; eighteen grandchildren, and four and a half great-grandchildren, all of whom were blessed by his love, guidance, and sense of humor.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Wesley and Julie Koval, and his youngest sister Patti.

Kim will be remembered for his hardworking spirit, generous heart, practical wisdom, and unwavering devotion to those he loved. He leaves behind not only countless memories but also a legacy of resilience, kindness, and the reminder that there's always another way to fix a problem. While his family will miss him deeply, they take comfort in knowing his stories will continue to be told around campfires, family gatherings, and every project that requires a little ingenuity.

The family would like to extend their heartfelt gratitude to the compassionate staff at Aspirus Hospice House for the exceptional care and kindness they provided during Kim’s final days.

There will be a celebration of life held on Saturday July 18th at Wildlife Bar and campground in Birnamwood.  The celebration will start at 3pm, the address is 183327 Schwede Lane in Birnamwood WI. Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society assisted with cremation services. Online condolences may be given at MWCS.WS

Forever loved, forever missed, and forever remembered.

records.name: Mark D. Norris
records.summary: Mark D. Norris, age 70 passed away on Jun 28, 2026.
records.body:
Mark Norris, age 70, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on Sunday, June 28, 2026.

Born on February 28, 1956, in Platteville, Wisconsin, Mark was the son of Jerry Norris and Jean Dean. He grew up with a love for athletics and attended Ripon High School, where he proudly wore jersey #21 and excelled as a standout football player, wrestler, baseball player, and boxer. His competitive spirit, determination, and leadership were qualities that remained with him throughout his life.

Mark’s professional career spanned sales and entrepreneurship, but his greatest accomplishments were found outside the workplace. A gifted carpenter, he took pride in building with his hands and helping others. He devoted 11 years to coaching youth football, including coaching his two sons, instilling in countless young athletes the values of teamwork, discipline, and perseverance. He also had a passion for motivational speaking, encouraging others to pursue their goals with confidence and purpose.

An enthusiastic lifelong fan of the Green Bay Packers and Milwaukee Brewers, Mark rarely missed an opportunity to cheer on his favorite teams. He also shared a special bond with his beloved pets—Reese, Runty, Wolf, and Callie—who brought him much joy.

Above all else, Mark was a devoted husband, father, grandfather, son, brother, and friend. His greatest love was his family. He cherished his wife, Tracy, and took immense pride in his children and their families. Nothing brought him greater happiness than spending time with his grandchildren, who were the light of his life and whom he loved beyond measure.

Mark is lovingly survived by his wife, Tracy Norris; his children, Lindsey Norris (Cameron), Tyler Norris (Mel), and Trevor Norris (Whitney); his cherished grandchildren, Arowyn Norris, Watson Norris, and Navie Norris-Naab; his mother, Jean Dean; his siblings, Steve Norris (Kari), Sherri Rompala (Mike), Dave Norris, Pat Wilcox (Brea), and Brian Wilcox (Elizabeth); along with many nieces, nephews, extended family members, and dear friends.

He was preceded in death by his father, Jerry Norris, and his grandparents, Harold and Irene Norris, and Stanley and Edna Smyth.

Mark will be remembered for his unwavering strength, generous heart, sense of humor, words of encouragement, and the lasting impact he made on everyone fortunate enough to know him. His legacy lives on in the family he loved so deeply, the lives he influenced through coaching and mentorship, and the countless memories shared with those who loved him.

Though he will be profoundly missed, Mark’s love, wisdom, and example will continue to inspire his family and friends for generations to come.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Barbara L. Ortman
records.summary: Barbara L. Ortman, 82, of Athens, passed away on June 24, 2026.
records.body:
Barbara L. Ortman, 82, Athens, passed away Wednesday, June 24, 2026 at Marshfield Medical Center, Marshfield.

She was born May 6, 1944 in Wausau, daughter of the late Klenka and Elva (Venzke) Hargraves. On June 13, 1964 she married Jack Ortman at Bethlehem Lutheran Church, Milan. He preceded her in death on April 18, 2019.

Barbara was a proud homemaker and enjoyed the small things in life like playing solitaire on her iPad, enjoying crafts; especially making cabbage patch dolls and care bear dolls and spoiling her dogs. Barbara loved her grandchildren dearly and was fortunate enough to watch her grandson grow up since he was a baby.

Survivors include her children, John Ortman, Wisconsin Rapids, Joel Ortman, Wausau and Tammy Ortman, Athens, her loving grandchildren, Kasandra Lesieur, Wausau and Sequoia Miles, Athens. Barbara is further survived by her siblings, Chris (Roger) Heldt, Florida, Veronica (Ron) Wolf, Racine, Sherry Dissmore, Racine, Glenn (Cindy) Hargraves, Racine, Richard and Tommy Hargraves, both of Racine and Caryl Micetich, Chicago and numerous nieces and nephews.

Besides her parents and husband, she was preceded in death by and infant daughter, Vickie.

Memorial services will be held at 11:00 a.m. Saturday, July 11, 2026 at Bethlehem Lutheran Church, Milan. The Rev. Michael Mathey will officiate. Burial will be in Milan Lutheran Cemetery. Visitation will be on Saturday, July 11, 2026 from 9:00 a.m. until the time of services all at the church.

records.name: Dennis L Fairchild
records.summary: Dennis L Fairchild, age 85 passed away on June 29, 2026.
records.body:
Dennis L. Fairchild, 85, passed away peacefully on June 29, 2026, under the care of Compassus Hospice at the House of Dove Hospice House.

Dennis was born on January 22, 1941, on a farm in Iowa to the late John and Gladys (Hartung) Fairchild. His passion for chemistry, water quality, and sales eventually brought him to Wisconsin, where he built both his career and his family.

Water was more than Dennis's profession—it was his life's work. He believed deeply in the importance of clean, high-quality water for both people and animals. Throughout his career, he helped develop formulas that improved the quality of silage and water used by dairy farmers, contributing to healthier cattle and improved milk production. He took great pride in knowing his work made a meaningful difference for agricultural communities. Dennis ate, breathed, and lived water.

Dennis was a man of quiet strength. Though reserved by nature, he was generous with his time, knowledge, and support. He was intelligent, compassionate, and had a lifelong interest in politics and current events. Those who knew him looked to him as a steady source of wisdom and strength.

Above all else, Dennis treasured family. He loved not only his biological family but also the family he gained through his dear friend, Jean Burt. Jean and her family, especially Kim Meer, held a special place in his heart and enriched his life with their friendship, love, and support.

Dennis is lovingly survived by his children, David Fairchild of Colorado, Darcy (Phil) Flynn of Indiana, and Tonya (Randy) Jones of Kentucky; his many grandchildren and great-grandchildren; and his dear friend, Jean Burt, along with her family.

He was preceded in death by his son, Dennis Fairchild Jr.; his wife, Kay Fairchild; his parents, John and Gladys Fairchild; two brothers, Gordon and Gerald; and one sister, Nola Jean.

In accordance with Dennis's wishes, no services will be held at this time. His final resting place will be at Lone Tree Cemetery, Sioux Rapids, Iowa.

Family and friends are invited to visit MWCS.WS to share memories and words of remembrance.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family.

records.name: Donald D. Aspinwall
records.summary: Donald D. Aspinwall, 83, was called home on June 22, 2026.
records.body:
With grateful hearts and abiding faith, we celebrate the life of Donald D. Aspinwall, who was called home to be with the Lord on June 22, 2026.

Don Aspinwall was born on September 28, 1942, in Ironwood, MI, to the late George and Esther Aspinwall. Don was the youngest of their 5 children. Growing up, Don enjoyed all the activities that living in the U.P. had to offer. While in high school, he met the love of his life, Jeanie. One of their favorite places to spend time together was at Little Girl's Point. They would enjoy walking the cobblestone beach looking for agates, making a driftwood fire, and dreaming about their future together. Don graduated from L.L. Wright High School in 1960. He continued his education at Northland College. In 1964, he graduated with a B.S. degree. On June 27, 1964, Don and Jeanie got married and moved to Wausau.

Don began his teaching career as a Social Studies teacher at John Muir in 1964. In 1970, he made the career choice to work at Lincoln Hills School in Irma, WI. He started at Lincoln as a social worker, but the classroom is where his heart belonged. He loved history and the lessons that can be learned by studying it. He taught history for the remainder of his 29-year career at Lincoln Hills School. Don educated hundreds of youth, bringing the past to life through meaningful lessons, but his true gift was the connection that he was able to make with the youth. He was a trusted teacher, an inspiration to make positive life choices, a confidant, and a person who truly cared about them. Don received many letters from his former students after they left Lincoln Hills. The letters were full of gratitude for having been in his class and the impact that Don had on their lives.

Don and Jeanie raised three daughters. As a young family, they shared many wonderful times in the U.P. They went camping at Lake Superior and Lake Gogebic, snowmobiling, unforgettable days with grandparents, and family trips to Florida, California, and Oregon.

In retirement, Don and Jeanie loved to travel. Don was his own GPS. He knew every route by heart, where the best rest stops were, and which little towns to stay the night. Together they traveled to Florida, Arizona, Laughlin, Las Vegas, New York, Texas, and Colorado. They visited Ground Zero, Ellis Island, and the Rio Grande Valley in Texas. Don was always loving the drive and went wherever Jeanie wanted to go.

After Jeanie died in 2011, Don spent time volunteering in his middle daughter's 2nd grade classroom. He would teach lessons on history, play card games, show the kids his magic thumb trick, or how he could wiggle his ears. He always drew his famous Snoopy on the board before he left. The students were always so excited for his visits. He enjoyed working with his oldest daughter, making pizzas for the Pizza Kiln, and visiting at the Clay House. He was a professional dough roller. Don assisted his youngest daughter in supporting local and regional cancer awareness efforts. He saw how positivity could grow from adversity. Don also volunteered with the Lions Club and The Never Forgotten Honor Flight for several years.

Don traveled to Montana for sport hunting of mule deer, a fishing trip to Woodland Caribou Provincial Park in Canada, and elk hunting in Idaho. Don was amazed to watch the sheep herder bring his flock of sheep down the road and how the dogs kept them together. He enjoyed going to the D.C. Everest football games and watching Badger and Packers games. He was always ready to play Smear or a competitive game of Doghouse.

His 7 grandchildren and 7 great-grandchildren adored him. "Papa Jack Hammer" made you feel happy and loved. He was always ready to challenge you in a thumb war, an arm-wrestling contest, or give you a ride on his knee. Don took many day trips to Bay Beach with the grandkids. His favorite ride was the tilt-o-whirl. He always supported the kids in their activities. As the grandchildren got older, he still stayed closely connected. He was a fabulous listener and was sincerely interested in what was happening in their lives. He offered advice in the form of a question because he wanted to guide them, and not to tell them what to do.

Don's heart was not only filled with love for his family, but also for his pets. Throughout his life with Jeanie, and even after, he loved his dogs. Ellie and Maggie Mae were his companions in the later years. He called them 'his girls,' and they kept each other company. He would often chuckle about the howling session the three of them had each morning.

Don is survived by his daughters, Gretchen (Lee) Kluz of Ringle, Sarah (Kent) Kwick of Kronenwetter, and Amy (Steve) Zondlo of Schofield; grandchildren, Brittany (Nic) Lotzer, Paige (Steven) Button, and Hope (Mike) Rebman, Jordan Kwick, McKenzie (Jordan) Baltz and Brandon Kwick and Jack Zondlo; great-grandchildren, Gavin, Emmett, Finnley Lotzer, August Button, Peter and Penelope Rebman and Tennessee Jean Baltz. He is further survived by his brother, Dennis Aspinwall.

He is preceded in death and reunited with his wife, Jeanie Maire (Margetta) Aspinwall; brothers, George and Howard Aspinwall; sister, Marilyn Butson; and special mother-in-law, Gladys Margetta.

Memorial service will be held at 11:00 AM on Monday, July 20, 2026, at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston. Pastor Lance Hoelscher of Mount Olive Lutheran Church, Weston, will officiate. Visitation will be held from 9:00 AM until the time of the service at Ascend.

Don's presence will be deeply missed. He truly was a 'seed planter' and parts of him will live on through us. Anyone who had a chance to know Donald Aspinwall has been truly blessed. As Don would say, "Keep Your Eyes on the Prize."

records.name: Donald R. Turowski
records.summary: Donald Raymond Turowski, 79, of Athens, Wisconsin, died on June 26, 2026.
records.body:
Donald Raymond Turowski of Athens, Wisconsin, died Friday, June 26, 2026 at age 79 years.

He was born January 19, 1947 in Bremerton, Washington to Ervin R. and Marie E. (Striebel) Turowski. He was united in marriage to Marilyn A. Haynes on June 13, 1970 in Suquamish, Washington. They were blessed by the Lord with 56 years of marriage.

Don enjoyed working in the woods as a forester for many uears. In 1996, he and his family moved to Wisconsin where he spent the next 30 years hand-crafting furniture.

In his youth, Don received Jesus Christ as his Lord and Saviour. He was a member of the Athens Mennonite Church, where he was ordained a deacon in 1998. He also served as deacon at Lena, Wisconsin for five years, returning to Athens in 2007.

He is survived by his wife, Marilyn, and three daughters, Anna (Noah) Amstutz of Athens, Sarah (Sidney) Halligan of Athens and Grace Turowski of the family home. He is survived by 12 grandchildren and is also survived by his brother T. James (Michele) Turowski of Seabeck, Washington and sister Catherine Lucas of Poulsbo, Washington.

He was preceded in death by his parents.

Don was a loving husband, father, and grandfather.

He lived for the good of others and will be greatly missed.

records.name: George A. Lawrie
records.summary: George A. Lawrie, a devoted husband, father, and veteran, passed away surrounded by the love of his family.
records.body:
George Lawrie, a devoted husband, father, grandfather, great-grandfather, great-great-grandfather, veteran, and friend to many, passed away surrounded by the love of his family.

George proudly served his country in the United States Air Force, carrying with him the values of dedication, hard work, and service throughout his life.

George shared a beautiful life with his loving wife, Ginger Lawrie. Together they celebrated 65 years of marriage and built a life filled with love, hard work, and family. Their greatest blessing was the family they created together — 7 children and a legacy that grew to include 81 grandchildren, great-grandchildren, and great-great-grandchildren. Through every season of life, George and Ginger faced challenges, created memories, and built a family they were proud of.

George was a man with a kind heart, gentle smile, and hands that could fix just about anything. If something was broken, everyone knew who to call — George. Whether it was a home project, a repair, directions when someone was lost, or simply a helping hand, George was always there. He remodeled homes, repaired what others thought couldn't be fixed, and taught his family the importance of hard work, patience, kindness, and taking care of one another.

When George wasn't working on a project or helping someone else, he enjoyed the simple things in life. He loved spending time outdoors, especially hunting and fishing. The quiet moments, stories, and memories made along the way were something he treasured and shared with those he loved. More than anything, George loved his family. His greatest legacy isn't found in the things he fixed or the houses he worked on — it is found in the family he helped create, the lessons he taught, and the love he gave so freely.

Family gatherings were always special, and anyone who knew George knew his famous line when the evening was winding down: "Well, I've got paperwork to do." Sometimes there was paperwork waiting… sometimes there wasn't — but everyone knew with a smile that it was George's way of saying it was time to head home.

The children of George would like to extend a special thank you to their mother, Ginger. For 65 years she stood by his side as his wife, partner, and best friend. Through these last years, months, and days, she continued to show her love through her care, dedication, and devotion to him. The comfort, strength, and commitment she gave George throughout his journey is something we will forever be grateful for.

The family would also like to extend a heartfelt thank you to Ari and Gina, George's caretakers, for the kindness, compassion, and care shown to George during this journey. Your support, patience, and the comfort you brought him will never be forgotten. A special thank you to the VA and hospice team for their guidance, support, and compassionate care, not only for George, but for our entire family during this difficult time.

George spent his life taking care of others, fixing what was broken, serving his country, and building a beautiful life with the people he loved most. His tools may be set down, but the love he built, the lessons he taught, and the memories he created will live on forever.

George leaves behind his beloved wife, Ginger, and their children: Dawn, Lori, George (Deb), Bill (Christine), Shelly, Brenda (Shannon), and Melissa (Kevin). He also leaves behind his many grandchildren, great-grandchildren, and great-great-grandchildren who will continue to carry on his love and legacy.

George was preceded in death by his parents; his in-laws, Ginger's parents; his sisters, Sally and Judy; his sons-in-law, Rodney and Mathew; and other loved ones who passed before him.

A mass of Christian burial will take place at 11:00 am on Saturday, July 11, 2026, at St. John the Baptist Catholic Church, N4887 WI-17, Merrill, WI 54452. Visitation will take place from 10:00 am until the services at the church. Burial and military honors will follow the mass.

records.name: Mark D. Norris
records.summary: Mark D. Norris, 70, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on June 28, 2026.
records.body:
Mark Norris, age 70, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on Sunday, June 28, 2026.

Born on February 28, 1956, in Platteville, Wisconsin, Mark was the son of Jerry Norris and Jean Dean. He grew up with a love for athletics and attended Ripon High School, where he proudly wore jersey #21 and excelled as a standout football player, wrestler, baseball player, and boxer. His competitive spirit, determination, and leadership were qualities that remained with him throughout his life.

Mark's professional career spanned sales and entrepreneurship, but his greatest accomplishments were found outside the workplace. A gifted carpenter, he took pride in building with his hands and helping others. He devoted 11 years to coaching youth football, including coaching his two sons, instilling in countless young athletes the values of teamwork, discipline, and perseverance. He also had a passion for motivational speaking, encouraging others to pursue their goals with confidence and purpose.

An enthusiastic lifelong fan of the Green Bay Packers and Milwaukee Brewers, Mark rarely missed an opportunity to cheer on his favorite teams. He also shared a special bond with his beloved pets—Reese, Runty, Wolf, and Callie—who brought him much joy.

Above all else, Mark was a devoted husband, father, grandfather, son, brother, and friend. His greatest love was his family. He cherished his wife, Tracy, and took immense pride in his children and their families. Nothing brought him greater happiness than spending time with his grandchildren, who were the light of his life and whom he loved beyond measure.

Mark is lovingly survived by his wife, Tracy Norris; his children, Lindsey Norris (Cameron), Tyler Norris (Mel), and Trevor Norris (Whitney); his cherished grandchildren, Arowyn Norris, Watson Norris, and Navie Norris-Naab; his mother, Jean Dean; his siblings, Steve Norris (Kari), Sherri Rompala (Mike), Dave Norris, Pat Wilcox (Brea), and Brian Wilcox (Elizabeth); along with many nieces, nephews, extended family members, and dear friends.

He was preceded in death by his father, Jerry Norris, and his grandparents, Harold and Irene Norris, and Stanley and Edna Smyth.

Mark will be remembered for his unwavering strength, generous heart, sense of humor, words of encouragement, and the lasting impact he made on everyone fortunate enough to know him. His legacy lives on in the family he loved so deeply, the lives he influenced through coaching and mentorship, and the countless memories shared with those who loved him.

Though he will be profoundly missed, Mark's love, wisdom, and example will continue to inspire his family and friends for generations to come.

records.name: Mary Kaehne
records.summary: Mary Kaehne, age 66, of Weston passed away on June 29, 2026.
records.body:
Mary Kaehne, age 66, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly but peacefully at her home on Monday, June 29, 2026, with her companion, Bella, her German Shepard, by her side.

Born on February 27, 1960, in the vibrant city of Chicago, Illinois, Mary’s journey was marked by unwavering compassion, an ardent love of learning, and a steadfast dedication to those she held dear.

Mary distinguished herself academically, earning double bachelors, an achievement emblematic of her intellectual curiosity and relentless pursuit of knowledge. Her educational path laid a foundation for a vocation that would become not just her profession, but truly, her calling.

As a cherished nurse with Aspirus, Mary embodied the essence of care and advocacy. Her devotion to the well-being of her patients transcended duty; she brought a gentle, healing touch into every interaction, and her colleagues and patients alike felt buoyed by her compassion and unwavering commitment. To Mary, nursing was as much an art as it was a science a way to bridge empathy with expertise, and the community she served will long remember her for this rare gift.

Mary’s passions extended far beyond the walls of her workplace. She was an avid reader, deeply engaged in holistic medicine, exploring the benefits of essential oils, and continually seeking knowledge that could enrich both her life and the lives of others. Her love for animals was evident in the affection she poured onto her beloved dogs and cats, whose companionship brought her great joy. Bird watching became a source of serenity and wonder, each encounter with nature nurturing her spirit. She also had a lifelong appreciation for jewelry, delighting in the beauty and craftsmanship of each unique piece.

Mary is survived by her beloved daughter, Starr Kaehne, and her treasured granddaughter, Emily Kaehne, both of whom were the lights of her life. She is also survived by her siblings; Nancy, Sue, Marc, Ron, and Bill, and her good friend Edward. She is preceded in death by her mother.

Per Mary's wishes, no formal services will be held.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Michael R. Perkins
records.summary: Michael Raymond Perkins, 67, passed away unexpectedly on June 26, 2026.
records.body:
Michael Raymond Perkins "Jaws", 67 passed away unexpectedly Friday, June 26, 2026.

He was born March 29, 1959 in Ladysmith, son of the late Raymond and Jean (LaBelle) Perkins. On June 30, 1984, he married Gail Ellis at Schmidt's Ballroom.

Mike enjoyed gun collecting, camping, kayaking and biking. He definitely had the gift for gab and loved checking up on his friends. He also enjoyed having coffee at the truck stop in Edgar and talking with his buddies.

Survivors include his wife, Gail Perkins; son, Brandon (fiancé, Hailey Mathwich) Perkins and daughter, Ashley (Garret Leichtman) Perkins; brothers, Chris (Lori) Perkins and Nathan Perkins; nephew, Michael (Dana) Perkins; niece, Brittany (Eric) Braunreiter; mother-in-law, Audrey Rechner.

He was preceded in death by his parents.

Funeral service will be 7:00 pm Thursday, July 2, 2026, at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Athens. Rev. George Graham will officiate. Burial will be in Athens Village Cemetery. Visitation will be Thursday from 3:00 pm until the time of service all at the Athens funeral home.

records.name: Nancy Ann Pries
records.summary: Nancy Ann Pries, age 91 passed away on Jun 29, 2026.
records.body:
Nancy Ann Pries, age 91, passed away peacefully on June 29, 2026, in Wausau, Wisconsin, surrounded by the love of her family. Born in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, on May 22, 1935, Nancy lived a life defined by faith, service, compassion, and deep devotion to those she loved.

Nancy graduated from West Milwaukee High School, where she met the love of her life, Howard "Howie" Pries. Their connection was immediate, and they were married on October 19, 1957. Together they shared nearly 68 years of love, partnership, and cherished memories until Howie's passing on August 18, 2025. Their journey began in their first home, affectionately remembered as the "Little House on the Alley," where they laid the foundation for a life rich in family, friendship, and laughter.

Together, Nancy and Howie raised their three children-Betty, Bonnie, and Michael-in a home filled with love, encouragement, and wisdom. Nancy was a devoted wife, mother, grandmother, great grandmother, and friend whose gentle guidance and caring heart touched countless lives.

Nancy possessed many talents and gifts. She played the accordion, prepared memorable family meals, and offered a listening ear and thoughtful advice not only to her own children but also to their friends. Her kindness knew no bounds. Whether volunteering at church, serving as a leader for Girl Scouts and Boy Scouts, or volunteering at St. Clare's Hospital, Nancy was always willing to lend a helping hand. Her warm smile, compassionate spirit, and genuine care brought comfort and joy to everyone fortunate enough to know her.

Nancy found great happiness in the outdoors. She enjoyed camping, hiking, boating, and water skiing with family and friends.
