Family vacations at Lynn Ann's Campground in St. Germain became treasured traditions and created lasting memories for her grandchildren. Yet some of her greatest joys were found in life's simplest moments-sitting at her kitchen table with a cup of coffee, watching the birds gather at the feeder and laughing at the occasional squirrel that stopped by.

Faith was the cornerstone of Nancy's life. She lived her beliefs through service, kindness, and fellowship. Alongside Howie, she was an active member of her church community for many years, participating in family groups, youth ministries, women's circle, and countless church activities. She especially enjoyed gathering with friends for card club, where a spirited game of Sheepshead was always a favorite.

Nancy will be remembered for her unwavering faith, generous heart, welcoming home, and the love she shared so freely with family, friends, neighbors, and strangers alike. Her legacy lives on in the values she instilled, the lives she touched, and the countless memories cherished by those who knew and loved her.

Though her family grieves her loss, they find comfort in knowing that Nancy and Howie are together again, reunited in the presence of the Lord they faithfully served throughout their lives.

Nancy's life was a blessing, her memory a treasure, and her love a gift that will remain in the hearts of her family forever.

Nancy is survived by her daughters Betty Rasmussen (Douglas) and Bonnie Surkin (Barry); son Michael Pries (Maryrose); grandchildren Claire Chamberlain, Michael Surkin (Chelsea), Jason Surkin (Kenzi), Cooper Pries, and Johnny Pries; and great-granddaughter Margot Surkin. Her legacy of love and laughter continues through each of them.

Visitation will be held at Brainard Funeral Home, 5712 memorial ct., Weston, WI 54476 on Tuesday, July 7, from 4 to 7 p.m. Funeral services will take place Wednesday, July 8, at 11 a.m. with a Funeral Service at Covenant Community Presbyterian Church, 1806 Weston Ave, Schofield, WI 54476 with a visitation one hour prior to the service and interment at Pine Grove Cemetery, 1501 Grand Ave, Wausau, WI.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to Covenant Community Presbyterian Church,1806 Weston Ave, Schofield, WI 54476.

records.name: Patricia Krake
records.summary: Patricia Krake, 88, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on June 23, 2026.
records.body:
Patricia Krake, 88 of Weston, WI passed away on June 23,2026 after a lengthy battle with dementia. She was born on March 13th, 1938, to the late Walter and Clara Kempf.

Patricia graduated from Wausau High School. She married David Krake on May 19th, 1963. Patricia spent most of her adult life as a homemaker, helping to raise the couple's three children. Patricia enjoyed cooking, canning and reading, but her absolute favorite thing to do was to go up to the family cottage near Lake Tomahawk, WI and go fishing.

Survivors include her husband David (Weston, WI), daughter Kelly (Coupland, TX), son Keith (Weston, WI), granddaughters Trista (Dallas, TX) and Kiera (Austin, TX), sisters Ellen Rustad (Rhinelander, WI), Bonnie Fisher (Kronenwetter, WI), Sandy Marquardt (Wausau, WI) and Cathy Szekeress (Schofield, WI).

Patricia is preceded in death by her parents, Walter and Clara Kempf, son, Kevin Krake, brothers, Marvin Kempf and Donald Kempf, sisters, Carol Hess, Mary Ruplinger and Linda Kempf.

Funeral services will be 11:00 am Monday July 6th at St. Agnes Parish in Weston, WI. Visitation will be from 10:00 am until the time of service. Burial will follow at the parish cemetery.

The family would like to thank the staff at Mount View Care Center in Wausau, WI for the compassion and caring of Patricia in her final years.

records.name: Ramona Theresa Hanson
records.summary: Ramona Theresa Hanson, age 94, of Wittenberg passed away on June 29, 2026.
records.body:
Ramona T. Hanson, 94, of Wittenberg, passed away peacefully on June 29, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center-Weston.

Ramona was born on August 4, 1931, to A.N. and Elithe (Chapin) Brunner. Raised in Leopolis, she attended local elementary schools before graduating from Marion High School. She continued her education at UW-Madison, where she served as president of her sorority, competed as an Alice in Dairyland finalist, and earned her bachelor's degree.

On August 21, 1954, Ramona married Roger Hanson at St. Mary's Catholic Church in Leopolis.

Ramona began her career teaching high school English in Tigerton and later worked as a substitute teacher in Wittenberg. Alongside her husband, Roger, she co-founded and operated Hanson Sanitation & Excavating, Inc.

Even while co-managing a business, Ramona was deeply involved in her community.

She served on the local school board, the Homme Home for the Aging board, and the Holy Family parish council. She was also instrumental in developing a residential subdivision in Wittenberg. Ramona possessed a vibrant personal life; she loved playing the piano and encouraged her grandchildren to play. She also enjoyed bridge parties, reading, discussing politics, and was one of the first female golf members at Maple Hills.

Her remarkable work ethic was evident well into her later years-she tended a garden for decades and was still mowing her own lawn at age 93.

Ramona was preceded in death by her beloved husband, Roger Hanson, on June 4, 2022, and her infant son, Joseph Hanson.

Ramona is survived by three sons: Steve (Jane) Hanson of Marathon City, Stuart (Lynne) Hanson of Eland, and Eric (Kay) Hanson of Rocklin, CA; 10 grandchildren:

Nick, Angie, Brian, Jason, Jeremy, Nicole, Keegan, Carl, Evan, and Megan; 20 great-grandchildren; and one expected great-great-grandchild.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00am on Saturday, July 18, 2026 at Holy Family Catholic Church in Wittenberg. Father Mark Rydell will preside, with burial to follow at Forest Home Cemetery, Wittenberg.

Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home is assisting the family with arrangements. Memories and messages of support may be shared online at schmidtschulta.com.

records.name: Richard "Dick" T. Schmeltzer
records.summary: Richard "Dick" T. Schmeltzer, age 82, of Merrill passed away on Jun 29, 2026.
records.body:
It is with heavy hearts that we announce the passing of Richard "Dick" T. Schmeltzer, who left us on June 29, 2026, at the age of 82. Born on December 7, 1943, to the late Otto and Catherine (Chartrand) Schmeltzer, in Melrose Park, IL. The family eventually moved to Merrill, WI, where Dick attended and graduated from Merrill High School with the class of 1961. He met Sharon Chartier, and the two were united in marriage on February 3, 1962, a loving union lasting over 64 years. She survives.

Dick was a dedicated worker and a loyal friend, known for his strong work ethic and his love for his family. Dick worked for numerous years at the Wausau Paper Mill, until his retirement.

During his younger years, both he and Sharon were involved with coaching Little League in Merrill for over 30 years. Dick loved sports, and was a lifelong fan of the Chicago Cubs and Chicago Bears. He also coached all of his sons and grandchildren and continued to attend games after his coaching career. Family and friends will forever remember him for his kindness, sense of humor, and the lively spirit he brought to every gathering.

Dick leaves behind his beloved wife: Sharon, 6 children: Jeff (Shevon), Tim (Sandi), Todd (Theresa), Thomas, Chris (Tracy) and Mitchell, 15 grandchildren, 18 great grandchildren and sister: Mary Lou Kramer. He was preceded in death by his parents, and 4 sisters.

In honor of Dick's life, a private family gathering will be held.

Rest in peace, Dick. You will be deeply missed, but your spirit will live on in all of us.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation Services of Merrill is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences are encouraged and may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com.

records.name: Robert "Bob" J. Swita
records.summary: Robert "Bob" J. Swita, 57, died on June 24, 2026, at his home.
records.body:
Robert "Bob" J. Swita, 57, died on Wednesday, June 24, 2026, at his home.

Bob was born on March 26, 1969, in Wausau, son of the late, John and Dolores (Stark) Swita. He married Patrice Zientara on August 15, 1998, at St. Paul Catholic Church, Mosinee, she survives.

Bob found his greatest joy in the outdoors, whether he was hunting, fishing, or exploring the trails on his snowmobile. A devoted animal lover, he cherished his faithful Labrador companions, Sophie and Buddy, and took exceptional care of Patti's beloved horse, Lucy. He also enjoyed showing horses and proudly competed at regional events over the years. Known for his remarkable work ethic and generous heart, Bob was someone others could always count on. He never hesitated to lend a helping hand to family, friends, or neighbors, whether it was tending to the family's large garden or assisting wherever he was needed. His quiet acts of kindness and caring nature left a lasting impression on all who had the privilege of knowing him.

Bob is survived by his wife, Patti; siblings, James Swita, Beveryly (Joe) Niescior, Thomas Swita, Charles Swita, Marie Swita, John Jr. Swita; nephew, Clayton (Nichole) Niewiadomski; sisters-in-law, Pam (Jerry) Krall, Kathy Freitag, John (Ann) Zientara, Mary (Tony) Suardini, and Ann (Jerry) Badeau; as well as many nieces and nephews.

A Mass of Christian Burial will take place at 10:30 a.m., on Friday, July 3, 2026, at St. Paul Catholic Church, Mosinee. Father Aaron Becker will officiate. Burial will take place at St. Paul Cemetery. Visitation will be held on Thursday, July 2, from 4:30 p.m., until 6:00 p.m., then will be again Friday, from 9:30 until the time of the service, all at the church.

records.name: Susan J. Surprise
records.summary: Susan J. Surprise, 73, of Weston, passed away on June 25, 2026.
records.body:
Susan J. Surprise, 73, of Weston, passed away on June 25, 2026, at her home.

Susan worked at Kolbe and Kolbe for over 30 years before her retirement. She married Jim Surprise. He preceded her in death.

She is survived by her children, Beth (Chris) Flenory, of Milwaukee, Angie (Randy) Holz, and Ricky of Wausau; as well as her grandchildren.

There will be no services held at this time, and she will be cremated per her wishes.

records.name: Wendell A. Hackworth
records.summary: Wendell A. Hackworth, 88, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully on June 26, 2026.
records.body:
Wendell (Jake) Allen Hackworth, 88, passed away peacefully on Friday, June 26, 2026, at Aspirus Hospice House in Wausau, Wisconsin.

Jake was born on February 22, 1938, in Paintsville, Kentucky, to Ransom and Elva (Tackett) Hackworth. He was the second of ten children and was raised with the values of faith, hard work, and devotion to family that guided him throughout his life.

On October 10, 1959, Wendell married the love of his life, Connie Van Buskirk. Together they shared a loving marriage spanning more than six decades. Jake and Connie settled in Rocky River, Ohio to raise their only child, Craig. They were surrounded by many wonderful friends and family.

After leaving the family farm in Vanlue, Ohio, Jake attended technical college receiving a degree in medical technology. He worked in hospital laboratories and teaching until joining Fisher Scientific in 1968. He would start as a sales representative and ultimately ascend to assistant branch manager for the Cleveland office.

Throughout his adult life, Jake enjoyed golfing and fishing. Jake was active in Free Masonry throughout his entire life. He was a member in good standing with Lakewood Lodge No. 601 and the Al Koran Shrine until his passing.

Jake was preceded in death by his parents Ransom and Elva (Tackett) Hackworth and eight of his nine siblings.

He is survived by his beloved wife, Connie; his son, Craig Hackworth (Sarah); and his cherished grandchildren, Samuel(Renea) and Olivia.

The family wishes to express their sincere gratitude to the compassionate staff of Aspirus Home Hospice and the Hospice House for the exceptional care and kindness they provided during Wendell's final days. Donations in Jake's remembrance should be made to the Aspirus Wausau Hospice House.

records.name: Patricia "Patsy" Josephine Bruyette
records.summary: Patricia "Patsy" Josephine Bruyette, age 90 passed away on June 30, 2026.
records.body:
Patricia “Patsy” Josephine (Miller) Bruyette, age 90, passed away peacefully at home on June 30, 2026, surrounded by her large and loving family.

Born in Rhinelander on April 14, 1936, to Lawrence and Genevieve (Bliss) Miller, Patricia was raised on the family farm off Lassig Road. Her youth was shaped by those she loved; inheriting her father’s physical strength, while her mother gifted her with wisdom. She was “schooled” by her beloved grandmother, Addie, who taught her the skills of womanhood, including sewing, knitting, crocheting, and tatting. Her life on the farm included milking cows early each morning before heading to school.  She was involved with the riding stable/club and enjoyed riding her own horses.

She met Leonard "Louie" Bruyette in high school and was perplexed that a handsome athlete like Louie would pursue her. She graduated in the Top 10 of her class from Rhinelander Union High School in 1954. On July 31, 1954, wearing a wedding dress she and her grandma made together, Patricia and Leonard were wed at St. Mary’s Church in Rhinelander. They danced to polkas and waltzes at their reception on the family farm and continued to dance together throughout their marriage.  They honeymooned traveling around Lake Superior.

Patricia stayed home to raise eight children, using her talents to make clothing, create Halloween costumes, decorate the house for every holiday, bake from-scratch birthday cakes, crochet dolls and Afghans.  She managed the bookkeeping for the family excavating business.  She taught herself to play guitar and was known for singing to all her grandkids or breaking into a song that fit every situation.

Patricia took pride in her beautifully mowed lawn, flower gardens, trees, pond, and a bountiful vegetable garden. Together, the family made maple syrup. She and Len spent time snowmobiling, playing horseshoes, and traveling by car across the United States. She was also an avid pool player, played softball, and rode horseback at age 80.

Her greatest joy came with the arrival of her grandchildren. For them, she enhanced her beautiful backyard to create endless fun with a playhouse, trampoline, and swing set to her grandkids, nieces, and nephews, “Grandma” or “Aunt Pat” wasn’t just a person—she was everyone’s version of “home,” offering love, security, and acceptance. From jukebox music in the basement to playing pool, board games, and crafts, her home will always be remembered as a fun place to be. She “adopted” anyone who needed a parent or grandparent. She attended every sporting event and school program for every grandkid, saved newspaper clippings, provided rides, and endlessly babysat. As they grew older, she loved family game nights with them, especially playing Password.

In her downtime, she was a passionate genealogist, pursuing details of generations past. She visited libraries and courthouses to unearth records, eventually tracing ancestors back to their arrival at Ellis Island, and neatly organized numerous letters, postcards, and photos into dozens of family albums she was deeply proud of.

Patricia is survived by her children: Roxane, Rachel, Pauline, Audrey (Scott), Jolene (Kevin), Lance, and Christopher (Jean); daughter-in-law Julie; son-in-law Rob; sister Bliss (Dick); and sister-in-law Kathy. She is the cherished grandmother of Patrick, Sierra, Justin, Savannah, Nathan, Jonny, Kalon, Jade, Aaron, Kianna, Samantha, Lance, Addison, Mitchell, Shannon, Cortney, Phillip, Mariah, Makala; along with Gage, Ernest, and Celeste, whom she adopted in her heart. She leaves behind great-grandchildren: Bryce, Sean, Jayden, Vera, Marigold, Dawson, Skyler, Madison, Kaeden, Konner, Karsen, Lillie, and Lauren; and great-great-grandchild, Evelyn.

She leaves behind several special friends, including Ray, Charlotte, Kathy, Mike.  Each of them touched her life and she treasured their friendship.  Charlotte, a Native American, is Nishiime–”sister” to Patricia, and gave her an Ojibwe name, “Ozhawaashko” which means Blue Sky Woman.  Giizhigokwe.  She has all the spirits of the sky...the winds, the sun, rain, water, snow that make plants thrive.

She was preceded in death by her husband, her parents, her brother, David; and her son, Lawrence. The first person she wanted to see at the gates of Heaven was her son, Lawrence.

A celebration of life will be held August 9, 2:00–6:00 PM as an open house/yard/pond visitation at the Bruyette home, 5877 Hwy 8.  Please contact Jolene Kopplin or any family member if you would like to help. In her honor, we will share plants from her property.  Visitors are asked to bring a container and a small spade or shovel. In lieu of gifts, please plant a flower or tree in her memory in your favorite place and think of her when you visit.  Please post photos of your plantings on her Facebook page.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting with the cremation. Family and friends may go to MWCS.WS to leave online condolences and words of remembrance.

records.name: Rosaline Isabel Gaines
records.summary: Rosaline Isabel Gaines, age 76 passed away on June 30, 2026.
records.body:
Rosaline Isabel Gaines, 76, passed away peacefully on June 30, 2026.

She was born on October 17, 1949, in Sacramento, California, to the late Frank and Dorothy (Dalske) Pierson. After moving to Wisconsin and beginning her life as a wife and mother, Rose embraced farm life. She and her first husband operated a hobby farm, and later, with her second husband, she helped run a dairy farm that remained close to her heart throughout her life. Rose was incredibly proud of the years she and her children spent on the farm, especially the time she spent working in the fields and driving the tractor. Those experiences shaped the strong work ethic, independence, and determination that defined her for the rest of her life.

Rose dedicated her life to helping others. After farming, her daughter encouraged her to go back to school, where she got her degree, and she began her career in social work as an AODA counselor, where she found purpose in supporting people through difficult times with compassion and understanding. Her entrepreneurial spirit later led her to own and operate a deli, “Bubba’s Bayou Deli,” a neighborhood bar, “Bubba’s Country Crossroads”, a coffee shop, a Food Wagon, and finally a U-Haul business and bait shop, all of which she did alongside her husband, Bubba. Whatever role she took on, Rose's greatest gift was connecting with people.

After losing her husband, Bubba, Rose never lost her independence. She learned the City Bus route and would use it to get downtown. Even in later years, she could often be seen riding her scooter to CVS, Krist Station, and the Yummy Deli, sharing smiles with everyone she met along the way—and perhaps leaving behind a trail of sunflower seeds. She took great joy in watching those flowers bloom and was heartbroken when the city mowed down the beautiful sunflower path she had lovingly created.

Rose had a generous heart and a zest for life. She loved going out to eat, horseback riding, traveling, visiting the casino, and trying her luck with scratch-off lottery tickets—especially  the crossword scratchers. She was a hopeless romantic and a dreamer, always talking about the next place she hoped to visit and looking forward to the next adventure.

An exceptional cook, Rose shared her love through food. She passed her cooking skills on to her children, and they will forever cherish the family meals she prepared with so much love and care. Her Indian tacos were legendary and will be especially missed around the family table.

She found joy in life's simple pleasures, whether feeding the birds and squirrels outside her home or sitting down to hear someone's life story. Rose never met a stranger. She welcomed everyone with open arms, and by the end of every conversation, she had made a new friend. She believed everyone had a story worth hearing, and she genuinely wanted to know it.

Her generosity extended far beyond her family. Rose faithfully supported multiple charities and was always willing to lend a helping hand. If you needed a break from life's challenges, she was the friend who would tell you to hop in the car so the two of you could escape for a good meal, a few laughs, and a chance to forget your worries, even if only for a little while.

Rose's sense of humor was unforgettable. She loved making people laugh and often joked that she was proud to have outlived "all of her ex-husbands." Her quick wit, contagious laughter, and playful spirit brought joy to countless lives.

More than anything, Rose loved her family. She was a loving and devoted mother to Jennifer (Tom Malone) Lukes, Damian Betts, Gabrielle (Julio) Rojas, Georgianna (Jeff) Szymanski, and Rorydale (Shannon) Genrich. Her family was the center of her world.

She was the proud grandmother of Morgan, Kaitlin, Zandria (Austin), Jilisa, JadenRose, Jonathan, Reyden (Caitlyn), Isaiah, Xavier, Nicholas, Johnathon, Lilyan, Violet, Dayton, Dominic, and Lincoln, and the treasured great-grandmother of Levi and Layni.

Rose is also survived by her siblings: Dennis (Jeanette) Pierson, Toni (Len) Malik, Delphine (Tom) Taft, Karen (Dave Barrick) Cichucki, and Geri (Joe Trevino) Armijo, along with many nieces, nephews, extended family members, and countless friends.

Rose was an integral part of the community she called home. She will forever be remembered for the kindness, friendship, laughter, and generosity that she and her beloved, Bubba, shared with everyone they met. Her door was always open, her table always had room for one more, and her heart always had space for another friend.

She was preceded in death by her parents, Frank and Dorothy (Dalske) Pierson.

Although her presence will be deeply missed, Rose's adventurous spirit, unwavering generosity, infectious humor, and unconditional love will live on in the hearts of all who knew her. The lessons she taught, the meals she shared, the stories she listened to, and the friendships she created will remain a lasting legacy for generations to come.

The family would like to thank Wood Aven Health and Rehabilitation, Cedar Creek Manor, and Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice for the exceptional care they gave Rose. They would also like to thank Ashley Salzwedel and Roseanne for being such a big part of Rose's life; as well as the countless friends and neighbors who would offer to help over the years. You were all a blessing to Rose.

A Mass of Christian Burial will take place. Please check back for service and burial details. John J Buettgen Funeral Home is assisting the family. Family and friends may go to www.honorone.com for service details and to leave online condolences and words of remembrance.

records.name: Adam C. Elsmo
records.summary: Adam C. Elsmo, 65, passed away peacefully on June 26, 2026.
records.body:
Adam was born on May 23, 1961, to the late Todd and Mary (Clark) Elsmo. He later married the love of his life, Jamie Babicky, and together they shared a life filled with love, happiness, and countless memories.

Adam proudly served his country in the United States Air Force before beginning a career as an over-the-road truck driver. He later worked in construction as a concrete laborer, where his strong work ethic and determination were evident to all who knew him.

A lifelong learner, Adam had an endless curiosity about the universe and the mysteries of the unknown. He enjoyed reading, spending quiet time at home, and finding peace in the solitude of country living. He especially loved planting trees, working in his yard, and creating a place of comfort and tranquility for his family. Adam was known for being stubborn and always believing he was right, but beneath that determined exterior was a devoted husband, loving father, and proud grandfather who cared deeply for those he loved.

He is lovingly survived by his wife, Jamie Elsmo; his daughter, Ashley; his cherished grandchildren, Devin and Lydia; his siblings, Alan Elsmo of Hartford, Alisia Albright of Athens, Wisconsin, Annette (Shawn Barke) Elsmo of Birnamwood, Wisconsin, and Angela Elsmo; along with several nieces, nephews, extended family members, and friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Todd and Mary (Clark) Elsmo; his mother- and father-in-law, James and Anna Mae Babicky; his brother-in-law, Joe Albright; and his brother, Aaron Elsmo.

Adam's family will forever remember his unwavering determination, his inquisitive mind, and the quiet joy he found in nature and home. Though he will be deeply missed, the memories he created and the love he shared will remain in the hearts of those who knew him.

A celebration of life will be held at a late date.

records.name: Bernice Connor
records.summary: Bernice Connor, age 89 passed away on Jul 1, 2026.
records.body:
Bernice T. Connor, 89, Athens, passed away Wednesday, July 1, 2026 under the care of St. Croix Hospice at Athenian Living Community, Athens.

She was born October 15, 1936 in Mosinee, daughter of the late Frank and Elizabeth (Springer) Purull. On August 21, 2004 she married Lawrence “Woody” Connor and he preceded her in death on April 19, 2023.

Bernice worked at Marathon Electric and enjoyed playing softball, and dancing especially the polka, waltz and jitterbug. She was also an avid Packers and Brewers fan.

Survivors include her son, Scott Roesler; grandchildren, Chad Roesler, Tucker (Emily) Roesler and Treyton (Katie Hart) Roesler; great-grandchildren, Xander, Caston, Willow, Waylon, Braxton, Kinsley and Maverick Roesler; step-grandchildren, Peter and Cale Kurszewski and Jonathon and Eric McCunn, and step-daughter Vicki (Tom) Rheinschmidt.

Besides her parents, she was preceded in death by her husbands, Melvin Roesler, Charles Robinson and Lawrence “Woody” Connor.

Memorial service will be 11:00 a.m. Wednesday, July 8, 2026, at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens. Rev. George Graham will preside. Burial will be in St. Patrick Cemetery, Minocqua. Visitation will be Wednesday from 9:30 a.m. until the time of service all at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Jason  D.  Beall
records.summary: Jason  D.  Beall, age 43 passed away on July 1, 2026.
records.body:
Jason D. Beall, 43, passed away unexpectedly on July 1, 2026, after suffering a sudden medical event while working in the yard at his second home in Ontonagon, Michigan.

Jason was born on January 5, 1983, in Germany while his father was serving in the United States Civil Service. From an early age, he developed the adventurous spirit, determination, and compassion that would define his life.

At just 17 years old, Jason met the love of his life, Danielle Teetzen. He married the girl who captured his heart at their first jobs, and together they spent the last 26 years building a life filled with unwavering love, countless adventures, laughter, and what seemed like a new hobby every week. Together they created a home centered on family, friendship, and making the most out of every day.

Jason proudly served his country in the United States Marine Corps. Following his military service, he earned his Bachelor's Degree in Psychology while building his career as a Tool Specialist with Viking Electric. To Jason, Viking Electric was far more than a workplace—it was a second family, and he cherished the friendships and camaraderie he found there.

Jason had an enormous heart and never hesitated to help someone in need. Whether it was stopping to assist a stranded motorist, standing up for someone who needed an advocate, or lending a hand to a friend or stranger, Jason believed in doing the right thing without expecting anything in return. His generosity extended even further when he twice grew out his signature thick, curly red hair to donate to Wigs for Kids—first donating 19 inches, then later another 16 inches—to help children in need.

Known affectionately as "Red," Jason was blessed with an incredible circle of friends who loved him for his loyalty, authenticity, positivity, and larger-than-life personality. He wasn't one to take any guff, and he always stood up for what he believed was right.

Jason was a true hobby connoisseur. If something caught his interest, he dove into it wholeheartedly. Over the years he explored rock climbing, 3D printing, leatherworking, photography, woodworking, and countless other pursuits. He loved collecting rocks, playing disc golf, gathering around a table for games of Magic: The Gathering, hiking, practicing jiu-jitsu, riding adventure motorcycles on winding trails, and spending time near the water. His taste in music was as diverse as his interests, and he found joy in discovering something new wherever life took him.

Above all else, Jason's greatest pride and greatest joy was his family. He adored his nieces and nephews, was incredibly proud of his son, Jacob, and treasured the beautiful life he and Danielle built together. Danielle and Jacob will forever remember

his huge heart, his unwavering love, his willingness to help anyone, and how hard he worked to make life better for those around him.

Jason is survived by his beloved wife, Danielle Beall; his son, Jacob; bonus children, Trinity and Bowden; his grandmother, Martha; his stepfather, David Triggs; his father-in-law, Alan Teetzen; his aunt and uncle, Corky and Debbie; his brothers and sister, Patrick (Keturah), Samantha, and Mandy (Kegan); along with many cherished nieces, nephews, extended family members, and dear friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents, David Beall and Caroline Triggs; his sister, Jenny; and his mother-in-law, Patricia Teetzen.

Family and friends are invited to gather for visitation from 3:00 p.m. until 6:00 p.m. on Wednesday, July 15, 2026, at John J. Buettgen Funeral Home, 948 Grand Avenue, Schofield. Military Honors will be conducted at 6:00 p.m. at the funeral home.

Online condolences and words of remembrance may be shared at www.honorone.com.

Though his time with us was far too short, Jason leaves behind a legacy of service, kindness, courage, generosity, and adventure. He lived fully, loved deeply, and never missed an opportunity to help someone else. His memory will live on in every trail explored, every act of kindness extended to a stranger, every new hobby embraced with enthusiasm, and in the hearts of all who were fortunate enough to know and love him.

records.name: June L. Cizek
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: June L. Cizek, 96, passed away on June 27, 2026.
records.body:
On April 2, 1930, June L. Cizek was welcomed into her home and into the hearts of her parents, Erna (Frederick) Juhlke and Herbert Juhlke, and 96 years later, on June 27, 2026, her loving Savior, Jesus, welcomed her into her forever home.

She graduated from Park School – a one room schoolhouse, then Merrill High School, and later went on to receive her teaching degree from UW Eau Claire. On June 22, 1952, she married Rev. CJ "Cy" Cizek. He preceded her in death on April 1, 1996. She was baptized, confirmed and married in her home church, Trinity Evangelical Lutheran Church, Town of Berlin. There she began playing the organ and singing in their choir. June taught elementary school, kindergarten and preschool, retiring in 1992 from Rib Mountain Lutheran Church.

Music always had a special place in her life. Along with music, she enjoyed dancing, traveling, reading and spending time with family and friends. She enjoyed working crossword puzzles, playing card games with her friend group, and working in her flower garden. She was a member of Lutheran Women's Missionary League and also volunteered at Bethesda Thrift Shop for many years. She was a member of Mount Olive Lutheran Church, where her husband served as pastor, and she had been their organist and choir director.

Survivors include her two children, Coralee "Cori" (Stephen) Foster of Wausau, and Curt (Linda) Cizek of KY; three grandchildren, Casey Foster, Lauren Foster, and Andrew Cizek; and her sister, Dawn Evanson of Tomahawk; and her nieces, Dawn Anderson of Johnson Creek and Debbie Kurtz of Waukesha.

In addition to her parents and husband, she was further preceded in death by her infant brother, Victor and her sister, Darlene (Tootie) Slewitzke.

Funeral service will be held at 11:00 A.M. on Thursday, July 9, 2026, at Mount Olive Lutheran Church, 6205 Alderson St., Weston. Pastor Lance Hoelscher will officiate. Visitation will be from 9:00 A.M. until the time of service at the church. Entombment will take place at Merrill Memorial Park Cemetery.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be given to Mount Olive Lutheran Church's Music Department.

The family would like to thank the caregivers of Aspirus Hospice, especially nurse Haley and social worker, Carrie. They'd like to also acknowledge Inner Peace Home Health Services – Nicole, Zory and Peggy for their kindness and care, as well as June's longtime physician, Dr. Vicky Baker, and Young's Drug Store in Wausau. Also, a very special thank you to June's sister, Dawn, who has lived with and cared for June in her final three years.

records.name: Mark D Scheuer
records.summary: Mark D Scheuer, age 60, of Marshfield passed away on Jul 1, 2026.
records.body:
Mark D. Scheuer, 60, of Marshfield passed away the early morning of Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at the Marshfield Medical Center in Marshfield, WI with his family by his side.

A Celebration of Mark’s life will be held at a later date.

Mark was born September 26, 1965 in Marshfield to Richard and Dolores (Weigel) Scheuer. He attended local schools graduating in 1984 and, in his early years, was active in 4H having won many awards as an exhibitor at the Fair. Mark later attended the UW in Madison earning a B.S. in Biochemistry. He was proud to have worked as a tutor while in Madison and while wintering in California. Mark had a clever sense of humor that could light up a room. He was also a prolific gardener, card player, and chess master.

He was truly one of a kind and meant so much to so many. He gave us countless memories filled with laughter, shenanigans, and unforgettable moments that always kept life interesting. Time and again, he beat the odds, and despite all that he faced, he continued to make the most of life with remarkable resilience. He will be deeply missed and forever remembered by all who had the privilege of knowing and loving him.

Mark is survived by his siblings: Larry (Cindy) Scheuer of Marshfield, Duane (Delores) Scheuer of Marshfield, Linda Langman of Palm Desert, CA, Debra (Tom) Sinn of Chili, Rick Scheuer of Marshfield, and Cheryl (Dave) Swensen of Auburndale. He is further survived by nieces and nephews Eric Langman, Ryan (Grace) Langman, Greg Scheuer, Aaron Swensen, Kelly Silbernagel and Michelle Sinn and 2 great-nieces: Emma and Addison Moen, and one on the way.

He was preceded in death by his parents and a nephew: Nick Scheuer.

The family wishes to extend their deepest appreciation to the staff of the CCU for the loving care provided to Mark and the family during his final hours.

records.name: Marlene Joyce Sawczuk
records.summary: Marlene Joyce Sawczuk, age 84, of Mosinee passed away on Jul 1, 2026.
records.body:
Marlene Sawczuk, age 84, passed away peacefully on July 1st, 2026, at Belltower Residence in Merrill WI.

She was born July 30th, 1941, in Wausau WI. Her parents were the late Henry and Alma Baravetto. Marlene married Roy Sawczuk on April 9th, 1976.

In her younger years, Marlene worked at Rondele’s cheese in Mosinee WI. Later she babysat several children for 20+ years. Marlene and her daughter Lisa started their own cleaning business that Marlene occupied until retirement.

Marlene loved her family so much. She loved family get-togethers where she could see and be with all her precious grandchildren. She enjoyed going for walks with the neighbor ladies and going to the casino. Marlene loved country music. She was known for singing to her family and friends, “I love you, a bushel and a peck, a bushel and a peck and a hug around the neck. A barrel and a heap and I talk in my sleep about you!” “About you!”

Marlene was proceeded in death by her husband Roy and her sister Jean (Ray) Romatowski. She is survived by 4 children, Terry (Mary Ann) Wasniewski, Rick (Chris) Wasniewski, Lisa Terry Woller, Tracey (Colette) Sawczuk, 14 grandchildren and 9 great-grandchildren

A Mass of Christian Burial will take place at 10:30 a.m., on Wednesday, July 8, 2026, at St. Paul Catholic Church, Mosinee. Father Eric Mashak will officiate. Burial will take place at St. Paul Catholic Cemetery, Mosinee. Visitation will be from 8:30 a.m., until the time of the service, all at the church. Beste Funeral Home, Mosinee, is honored to be assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be directed to the family at www.bestefh.com.

records.name: Michelle L. Zinser
records.summary: Michelle L. Zinser, 57, of Weston, passed away on June 25, 2026.
records.body:
She was born on February 25, 1969, in Wausau, to Henry Zinser and Eileen Cook.

She was a proud employee of CTech Manufacturing, and her work was a large part of her life. Outside of work Michelle enjoyed crafts, tanning, motorcycles, and car racing.

Survivors include her daughter, Randi (Joe) Bowman; grandchildren, Koda, Gavin, and Savanna; mother, Eileen (Donald) Neumann; father, Henry (Deborah) Zinser; and brothers, Brian (Jen) Zinser and Curtis (Deanna) Zinser. She was preceded in death by her son, Donald.

Private services will take place at a later date.

records.name: Naomi C. Hardman
records.summary: Naomi C. Hardman, 82, of Wausau, passed away on June 26, 2026.
records.body:
Naomi Hardman was a redeemed child of God who believed that, by His grace, God sent his son Jesus to earth to become our substitute, to live a perfect life, to suffer and die in our stead, to save us from our sins, and to rise again showing us the promise of heaven. Naomi was God's gift to her Christian parents, Pastor Edwin and Mrs. Irmgard (Pape) Breiling, on December 13, 1943, in Geneva, NE. She entered God's Kingdom through Holy Baptism on December 25, 1943, at Grace Ev. Lutheran Church (Geneva).

After receiving instruction in the doctrines of God's Word, Naomi was confirmed in the faith into which she had been baptized at Our Savior's Lutheran Church (Two Rivers, WI) on April 14, 1957. Naomi attended elementary school at Randolph Elementary School (Randolph, WI) and St. John's Lutheran School (Two Rivers, WI). She attended Manitowoc Lutheran High School (Manitowoc, WI) for the first three years of high school and finished her final year in 1961 at Northwestern Preparatory School (Watertown, WI). She graduated from Dr. Martin Luther College (New Ulm, MN) in 1965, where she had trained for the teaching ministry.

She served the Lord teaching children the good news of Jesus at Grace Lutheran School (Yakima, WA), Immanuel Lutheran School (Tawas, MI), and St. Peter Lutheran School (Schofield, WI). She was also privileged and blessed to serve as organist, Sunday School teacher, and Vacation Bible School teacher in various WELS congregations. Helping with church functions, altar guild, and serving as a counselor at Camp Basic brought joy to her heart.

On June 17, 1972, God provided her with a faithful, God-fearing John Clay Hardman as her companion in this life. Her father united them in marriage at St John's Ev. Lutheran Church (Bay City, WI). Their marriage was lovingly blessed with three children. It was her special joy and delight to watch them grow in the Lord and serve Him. It was also her great joy to see the "children of her heart" (former students) bringing their young families to church.

Naomi was preceded in death by her husband of 49 years. She is survived by their three children – Jennifer of Tempe, AZ, Aimee of Milwaukee, WI, and Christopher (Denise) of Ringle, WI, along with two grandchildren, Nicole (Tristin) and Neil Hardman. She is also survived by one sister, Janet (Joel) Fredrich of New Ulm, MN, and five nieces and nephews. She was also preceded in death by both her parents and a sister and brother-in-law: Karen and Ronald Shilling.

The service of Christian funeral will be held at St. Peter Ev. Lutheran Church, 2822 N 6th St, Wausau, WI 54403, on July 6, 2026, at 11:00 am with a visitation prior at 10:00 am. Her mortal body will be laid to rest in Pine Grove Cemetery to await the glorious resurrection.

records.name: Patricia Krake
records.summary: Patricia Krake, 88, of Weston, passed away on June 23, 2026.
records.body:
She was born on March 13, 1938, to the late Walter and Clara Kempf.

Patricia graduated from Wausau High School. She married David Krake on May 19, 1963. Patricia spent most of her adult life as a homemaker, helping to raise the couple's three children. Patricia enjoyed cooking, canning and reading, but her absolute favorite thing to do was to go up to the family cottage near Lake Tomahawk, WI and go fishing.

Survivors include her husband David (Weston, WI), daughter Kelly (Coupland, TX), son Keith (Weston, WI), granddaughters Trista (Dallas, TX) and Kiera (Austin, TX), sisters Ellen Rustad (Rhinelander, WI), Bonnie Fisher (Kronenwetter, WI), Sandy Marquardt (Wausau, WI) and Cathy Szekeress (Schofield, WI).

Patricia is preceded in death by her parents, Walter and Clara Kempf, son, Kevin Krake, brothers, Marvin Kempf and Donald Kempf, sisters, Carol Hess, Mary Ruplinger and Linda Kempf.

Funeral services will be 11:00 am Monday July 6 at St. Agnes Parish in Weston, WI. Visitation will be from 10:00 am until the time of service. Burial will follow at the parish cemetery.

The family would like to thank the staff at Mount View Care Center in Wausau, WI for the compassion and caring of Patricia in her final years.

records.name: Robert E. Clevenger
records.summary: Robert E. Clevenger, age 65, of Galesburg, IL passed away on Jul 1, 2026.
records.body:
Robert E. Clevenger Jr., 65, of Galesburg, Illinois passed away the evening of Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at the Marshfield Medical Center in Marshfield, WI following a heart attack on the job.

A celebration of Robert’s life will be held at a later date.

Robert was born on September 14, 1960, in Streator, Illinois, to Robert Sr. and Ruth (Persong) Clevenger. After completing his education, he proudly served as a cook in the United States Marine Corps from 1978 to 1982. Following his honorable discharge, he worked as both a welder and a truck driver. Robert had a lifelong passion for loud music and fast cars. He was also a devoted Harley-Davidson enthusiast and was nearing completion of the restoration of his 1965 Panhead at the time of his passing.

He is survived by his fiancé Kathly Beasley, his son Levi (Jacklyn) Clevenger, grandchildren Henry & Callen Clevenger, and his nephew Jason "Jake" Rager.

records.name: Robert G. Seidler
records.summary: Robert G. Seidler, 89, passed away on June 26, 2026.
records.body:
He was born on March 2, 1937.

A visitation will take place from 10:00 am until 12:00 pm on Thursday, July 9, 2026, at John J Buettgen Funeral Home, 948 Grand Ave, Schofield, WI 54476. Burial with military honors will take place at St. Paul Catholic Cemetery, Mosinee, Wisconsin.

records.name: Stanley "Stan"  G Szymanski
records.summary: Stanley "Stan"  G Szymanski, age 83 passed away on July 1, 2026.
records.body:
Stanley "Stan" G. Szymanski passed away surrounded by the love of his family on July 1, 2026. He was born on May 23, 1943, to the late Stanley A. and Elizabeth (Knetter) Szymanski.

Stan married Angela Burger in 1964, and together they built a life on the family farm, raising their children while tending the fields and caring for the animals. Angela preceded him in death in 2014. In 2015, Stan found love again and married Sharon Malueg. Together, they shared many happy years traveling and enjoying their love of polka dancing.

Stan was a retired dairy farmer whose passion for farming never faded. He loved caring for the dairy herd and especially enjoyed spending time out on the tractor. Farming was more than his livelihood—it was his way of life.

Away from the farm, Stan embraced life with enthusiasm. He loved polka dancing, traveling worldwide, writing poetry, and playing cards, especially a good game of sheephead. He also enjoyed watching sports on television and was an avid Milwaukee Brewers fan.

Stan never met a stranger. He was a social man who loved striking up conversations with anyone and everyone. His children fondly remember many occasions when they had to wait patiently in the car because Dad had stopped to chat with someone he knew—or had just met. His warm personality and genuine interest in others left lasting impressions on countless people.

Dedicated to both agriculture and his community, Stan was actively involved in many organizations throughout his life. Among them were the Gladiola Society, Holstein Association, Wisconsin Rural Leadership, Wausau Jaycees, Master Farmers, and the Agriculture Society, to name just a few. His commitment to serving others reflected the same work ethic and dedication he brought to his family and his farm.

Above all else, Stan was a devoted husband, father, grandfather, great-grandfather, brother, and friend. He was a hardworking family man whose love, generosity, and kindness will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved him.

He is survived by his beloved wife, Sharon; his children, Sheila (Ted Rude) Szymanski, Jeff (Anna) Szymanski, Christopher (Ana) Szymanski, Gail (Danny) Wilde, and Tracy (Rod) Kaiser; his stepchildren, Craig Stern, Shelly (Cory) Martin, and Ross (Dannette) Stern; 22 grandchildren: Tom (Shauna) Szymanski, Amanda (Jesse) Fischer, Nicole (Joe) Westberg, Tyler (Alexis) Szymanski, Isaiah Genrich, Xavier Genrich, Nicholas Szymanski, Johnathon Szymanski, Lilyan Szymanski, Violet Szymanski, Johnathan Rodriguez, Clarissa Rodriguez, Deanna Cotty, Erika Wilde, Carmen Wilde, Shelby Wilde, Kaitlyn Kaiser, Marissa Kaiser, Alex Kaiser, Seth Martin, Ashley Martin, and Madison Stern; and 10 great-grandchildren.

He is further survived by his siblings, Jacki (Terry) Pyan and Ted Szymanski; his brothers-in-law, Kenneth Lepak, Paul (Sandy) Burger, Steve (Jane) Burger, Joe Burger, and Peter Burger; his sisters-in-law, Linda Szymanski, Carolyn Martino, Emily Noll, Pauline (Alan) Babler, Elaine Schlender and Pam Oswald; as well as many nieces, nephews, cousins, extended family members, and friends.

In addition to his parents, he was preceded in death by his sisters, Sandra Lepak, and Patricia (Joe) Oswald; brother Terry Szymanski; brother-in-law, George Burger; and sisters-in-law, Margie Nagdeman and Barbara Olander.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Thursday, July 9, 2026, at Sacred Heart Catholic Church, 222761 County Road S, Marathon City, WI 54448. Visitation will be held at the church from 9:00 a.m. until the time of Mass. Burial will follow in the parish cemetery.

John J. Buettgen Funeral Home is assisting the family. Family and friends may visit www.honorone.com to share online condolences and words of remembrance.

records.name: Barbara A. Jennison
records.summary: Barbara A. Jennison, age 82, of Weston passed away on Jul 2, 2026.
records.body:
Barbara Ann Jennison, Weston, WI, died the morning of July 2, 2026, from the effects of vascular dementia. Barb was born in her family home in Amherst, WI, on November 4, 1943, the daughter of Phillip Charles Hamilton and Blanche (McGinley) Hamilton. Her brother, Phillip, Jr., arrived in August 1945 and together they experienced the hospitalization of their mother six weeks later to recover from tuberculosis, the divorce of their parents soon after, and a move with their mother to Wisconsin Rapids, where they settled. There, Barb’s Catholic faith was formed in childhood as she attended SS Peter and Paul Elementary and later the Our Lady Queen of Heaven School before graduating in 1963, alongside her brother, from Assumption High School.

After graduation, Barb worked at Emmons Stationary and moved to Minneapolis in 1964 to attend the Humboldt Institute, where she completed a course in Airline and Business Training in 1965. There, a lifelong passion for traveling began, strengthened during her first job at Northwest Orient Airlines as a reservation agent. Barb returned to central Wisconsin as a ticket agent for Northwest Orient in the Wausau Airport, and in August 1966 she married Lewis W. Perkins, moving to Schofield where they raised their three children, Scott William, Sarah Jean, and Shelley Lynn. They divorced in 1979.

At the American Automobile Association, Barb experienced the business side of the travel industry by managing the travel agency in their Wausau office. Travel became a career path that she would continue to follow throughout her life, and she began roaming across the United States and around the globe—passport in hand—to share reviews with her clients and better inform her sales technique. Souvenirs from Alaska, Hawaii, Germany, Ireland, and Mexico lined her office shelves. Barb met James L. Jennison through friends, and the pair married in November 1982. In Jim, she found a partner just as interested in the travel business, and when Barb ventured out on her own to open The Travel Agency (later Jennison Travel) in Rothschild, he joined as the part-time office manager.

Through her travel agency, Barb organized and escorted group tours, coordinated destination weddings, planned honeymoons, booked young families on their first vacations, and made many friends—most beginning as clients. She became a Certified Travel Agent, and later joined MAST, a Midwest consortium to educate, train, and support travel agents, earning many awards from them for her business successes and dedication to the field. With the change in the travel reservation business increasingly moving toward phone and internet reservations, Barb brought her business into her Schofield home in 2001, a year that further changed the travel industry. For over twenty years she grew Jennison Travel, eventually closing when her health, and Jim’s, began to decline.

As much as she loved to travel, she perhaps more so enjoyed time at home with family. It wasn’t uncommon for her and Jim to enjoy a glass of wine as they watched the sun set from their deck, and the grill was always ready to go for a family meal to celebrate life events and birthdays. Fridays were often spent waiting for a table at Carmelo’s for fish fry, or at Peking or The Palms Supper Club, the latter a favorite for regular dinners with her friends. Always one to enjoy a party, she celebrated her 80th birthday in 2023, just as the effects of her dementia began surfacing, surrounded by cousins from the McGinley, Lemke, and Hamilton clans, close friends, and neighbors.

Barb was predeceased by her parents, Blanche Hamilton and Phillip Hamilton, Sr.; her stepmother Delores Hamilton; her husband, Jim Jennison; her brother Phillip Hamilton, Jr.; her step-son Jay Jennison; her granddaughter Ellianah Bolduc, and nephew Joe Hamilton. Surviving her are son Scott Perkins, Pittsburgh, PA; daughters Sarah (Ken) Maciaz, Kronenwetter, and Shelley (Ryan) Wucherer, Green Bay; and stepdaughters Jami (Joe Mershon) Traska and Jill (Tim) Bolduc. Missing their “Gramma Babs” are her grandchildren Liz (Luke) Hilleshiem, Peri Maciaz, Madi Wucherer, Ben Wucherer, Duke Wucherer, Halle (Mathew) Axling, Courtney Bolduc, and Brooke Jennison. The youngest family members, her great-grandchildren, Vivienne, Ava, and Riley Hilleshiem, will hear stories of great-grandma Barb for years to come. Her sisters-in-law, Donna Landeen and Dorie Hamilton, niece Niki Hamilton, and nephew Phil Hamilton III, will miss catching up with her, as will lifelong friends Mary Towle and Sharon Lorenzen. Too many to name, Barb’s cousins from the McGinley, Lemke, Wirtz, Ecker, Skibbie, and Buehning families will remember the sparkle in her eye, her laugh, strong family devotion, and polka dancing at their weddings.

In her last weeks, Barb was comforted by the caregiving team from St. Croix Hospice, Wausau. Her family wishes to extend their gratitude to Becky, Brenda, and Jess at St. Croix as well as the entire team at Careview Transitional Care (formerly Pride TLC), Weston, for providing long-term assisted living care.

Brainard’s Funeral and Cremation Center, Wausau, will coordinate her last wishes. Visitation will be on Friday, July 31, 2026, at 9:30am at St. Anne Catholic Parish, Wausau, followed by a 10:30am funeral service led by Fr. Al Slowiak.

In lieu of flowers, the family asks you toast to Barb’s life on your next trip—she would have loved to hear about where you stayed, what you ate, and all of the interesting sites you experienced while away. The many “Having a great time! Wish you were here!” postcards she received over the years speaks to her place in all of our hearts.

records.name: Sally Mae Matson Herrmann
records.summary: Sally Mae Matson Herrmann, age 89, of Tigerton passed away on July 2, 2026.
records.body:
Sally Mae Matson-Herrmann, 89, of Tigerton, passed away peacefully on Thursday, July 2, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

Sally was born on August 2, 1936, in Tigerton, the daughter of Tom and Ada (Omitt) Johnson. Following the death of her father, she was lovingly raised by Edward Polley, who became a devoted father figure in her life.

On October 30, 1954, Sally was united in marriage to John "Kenny" Matson Sr. in Tigerton. They shared more than 53 years of marriage before Kenny preceded her in death on January 12, 2008. Sally was blessed to find love again and married Bruce Herrmann on September 12, 2009, in Wittenberg. Bruce preceded her in death on March 9, 2018.

Sally grew up in the Town of Germania and cherished her country life on the farm near her childhood home. She had a passion for rummage sales and was always on the lookout for hidden treasures. Sally became well known for hosting one of the area's best annual rummage sales, where she especially enjoyed visiting with customers and friends. She also loved browsing secondhand and thrift stores.

Some of Sally's happiest moments were spent relaxing on her deck, taking in the peaceful surroundings. She delighted in watching the birds, tending to her flower, herb, and vegetable gardens, and enjoying the playful antics of her chickens, geese, cats, and horses.

Sally looked forward to lunch dates with friends and treasured every opportunity to spend time with her family. Her children, grandchildren, and great-grandchildren brought her immeasurable joy and filled her heart with love.

Sally is lovingly survived by her children, Barbara (David Yonker) Matson, John "Kippy" Matson Jr., and Laurie Matson; her grandchildren, Becky (Brandon) Merckes and Chris Matson; and her great-grandchildren, Nicolas and Parker Merckes.

She was preceded in death by her husbands, John "Kenny" Matson Sr. and Bruce Herrmann; her parents, Tom Johnson and Edward and Ada Polley; and her siblings, Edward "Hardrock" Johnson, Pam Polley-Dankmeyer, Roger Polley, and Sharon Dedolph.

A funeral service for Sally will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Tuesday, July 14, 2026, at St. John Lutheran Church in Tigerton, with Pastor Dean Suehring officiating. Visitation will be held at the church on Tuesday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service.

Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home of Wittenberg is assisting the family with funeral arrangements. Memories and messages of support may be shared at www.schmidtschulta.com.

The family would like to thank the Peritoneal Team at Aspirus Wausau Kidney Care for all of the wonderful care they provided.

records.name: Sister Mary Angela Sackmann
records.summary: Sister Mary Angela Sackmann, age 101, of Merrill passed away on Jul 2, 2026.
records.body:
Sister Mary Angela Sackmann, a Sister of Mercy of the Holy Cross, 101, died July 2, 2026 at Bell Tower Residence in Merrill, Wisconsin.

Jeannette Sackmann was born May 15, 1925, in Medford, WI, to John and Elizabeth(Massoth) Sackmann. She entered Holy Cross Convent in Merrill, Wisconsin, September 14, 1946, and professed her vows on July 17, 1948. Sister was in her 78th year of ministry at the time of her death.

She graduated from Medford High School. Before entering the convent, she attended the Taylor County Normal School for 2 years and taught in a one room school. She received her B.S. in Elementary Education from Edgecliff College in Cincinnati OH. She taught school at St. John School, Deer Park OH, St. Mary’s School, Antigo WI, St. Joseph’s School, Rhinelander WI and St. Robert’s School, Merrill WI. Sister Mary Angela worked in the Diocese of Superior CCD office. She was the Director of Religious Education in Luling, Hahnville, Vacherie, and Baton Rouge LA.

In 1984 she received her CPE Certificate from St. Louis University. She then worked as Chaplain of Our Lady of the Lake Regional Medical Center in Baton Rouge LA.

During her first retirement in 1998, she volunteered at food pantries, nursing homes, St. Anthony’s Retreat Center in Marathon, WI and as Spiritual Assistant to the Secular Franciscan St. James Fraternity in Wausau WI.

In 2008, Sister Mary Angela along with Sister Joelle accepted a call from the USA Province to work with the poor in Kentucky. They worked there until 2015 when she retired again and moved back to Merrill. In 2010, she received an award from the Franciscan Federation for “Passionately Embracing Gospel Living.”

Sister has also served the Holy Cross Sisters as the Coordinator of the Senior Sisters. Sister Mary Angela loved to travel and bake and to spend time at the lake with her family.

Preceding her in death are her parents John and Elizabeth, her brothers, William(Eva), Joseph(Edna), Valentine(Lucille), Robert(Lillian), Kenneth, Clarence(Elizabeth), Herbert and sisters, Annabelle(Glenn) Moorhead, Caroline(Robert) Viel, Dorothy, Nora(Paul) Hornak, and Leona(Harold) Hornak.

She is survived by her family of Holy Cross Sisters and associates, as well as her sister-in-law Jane Sackman and many nieces, nephews, as well as great and great-great nieces and nephews.

A Funeral Mass for Sister will be held on July 10th at 11:00am in Holy Cross Chapel in Bell Tower Residence. Father Charles Hoffmann will officiate. Visitation will be held July 10th at 9:30am until time of the liturgy. Burial will follow in the St Francis Catholic Cemetery, Merrill, WI.

Special thanks to chaplains John Bourke and Jen Hoffman and the staff of Bell Tower Residence along with Interim Healthcare hospice nurse and staff for assisting in the spiritual and health care of Sister Mary Angela. An extra thanks to Holy Cross Sister companions Jamely and Sherry.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Thomas Joseph Knetter
records.summary: Thomas Joseph Knetter, age 76, of Wausau passed away on July 2, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas J. Knetter passed away on July 2nd, 2026, at the age of 76, leaving behind a legacy of service, hard work, and independence.

He was born on December 8, 1949, in Wausau, son of Alex and Alice Knetter.

Tom spent most of his life in Rib Mountain, Wisconsin, before later moving to Schofield. A graduate of Newman Catholic High School, he was known for his strong work ethic, resilience, and straightforward nature.

A proud Vietnam Veteran, Tom served in the United States Marine Corps, attaining the rank of Corporal. His military service was a defining chapter of his life, and he remained deeply proud of his country and his fellow veterans. As a founding member of the Man of Honor Society, he found meaning in recognizing and supporting those who served. One tribute that reflects the values he held dear reads:

“You have served so that others may live. You are a true American Man-of-Honor. The people of the United States are grateful.”

Following his military service, Tom built a career as an independent over-the-road truck driver, spending years navigating highways across the country. He later worked for the Town of Rib Mountain, serving the community he called home for much of his life, before continuing his career with B&K Trucking. He embraced the freedom and challenges that came with life on the road. Among the memories he shared with his family were the occasions when he took his son, Jason, along with him on trucking trips. Though those opportunities were not frequent, they became cherished memories that Jason would carry with him throughout his life.

Tom had a special love for dogs and found great companionship in them over the years, especially his beloved Raven.

Tom is survived by his son, Jason Knetter (Janet Curin); his sisters, Sandy Thompson and Pam (Roger) Norton; his grandsons, Kole (Brandy) Knetter and Karson (Melissa) Knetter; and his great-grandchildren, Mikah, Miley, and Klara. He also leaves behind friends, fellow veterans, and others whose lives crossed his over the years.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Alex and Alice Knetter; and his brother, Terrance "Terry" Knetter.

Tom will be remembered for his service to his country, his years spent on the road, his dedication to his work, and the independent spirit that guided him throughout his life. His family and friends will remember him in their own ways and cherish the moments they shared with him.

Semper Fidelis — Always Faithful.

Private services are being held. Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: William C. Sliwicki
records.summary: William C. Sliwicki, age 85 passed away on Jul 2, 2026.
records.body:
Dear Husband of Kathryn (Nee Moha) For 51 years. Kind generous loving dad “Popsy” to the late Jennifer Zengle (Rob), Julie Anderson (Al), And Joe (Katie). Papa of Norah and Jack Sliwicki whom he loved very, very much. Brother of the late Robert (and late Carollyn), and Ray. Brother-in-law of John, Chris, Joe, Carla, and Ann Moha.

Fondly Remembered by many other relatives and friends.

Bill served in the Navy before working at Harnischfeger Corp for over 40 years.

He will be missed by all the Presque Isle property deer, birds, chipmunks, squirrels, and other wildlife he was able to make friends with these past 20 years.

As per Williams wishes – No services will be held

records.name: Bernard "Bernie" A. Kreuzer
records.summary: Bernard "Bernie" A. Kreuzer, age 81 passed away on Jul 3, 2026.
records.body:
Bernard “Bernie” A. Kreuzer, 81, of Rib Mountain, passed away on Friday, July 3, 2026, at Aspirus Hospice House. He was born in Milwaukee on July 2, 1945, to the late George and Lucille (Dietzler) Kreuzer. Bernie graduated in 1963 from Brookfield Central High School and later married Patricia “Patti” Waldinger on April 26, 1969, at St. Mark Catholic Church in Rothschild.

Bernie worked as a route salesman for Frito-Lay and retired after 28 years of calling on his many clients. After his retirement, he worked part-time for Brickner’s and later for City Delivery. Bernie liked to stay busy and enjoyed feeding the birds, fishing, ice fishing, small game hunting, deer hunting, golfing, and bowling. Over the years he bowled in many leagues including Knights of Columbus men’s and couple’s with Patti. Bernie’s different leagues also had him bowling at many of the bowling lanes (former and current) in the surrounding area like the Wausau Bowling Center, Mountain Lanes, Tanglewood Lanes, and Day’s Bowl-A-Dome. He celebrated the day he rolled a 298 game. Bernie was a life member of the Knights of Columbus Council 1069 and a member of Holy Name of Jesus Catholic Church.

Dogs were a big part of Bernie and Patti’s life. During Bernie’s early hunting years, he had two Beagles that he hunted rabbits with. More recently they had the companionship of two Shih Tzus, Pixie and Molly.

Survivors include his wife, Patti; sister and Irish twin, Nancy (Don) Van Sleet; brother, John (Sue) Kreuzer of South Carolina; brothers-in-law and sisters-in-law, Elyse (Dave) Wiegand, Joanne Genrich, John (Chris) Waldinger, Robert “Bob” (Pat) Waldinger, and Mary (Jim) Scholes; and many nieces, nephews, and extended family.

He was preceded in death by his parents and brother, Jim Kreuzer.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:30 a.m. on Tuesday, July 21, 2026, at Holy Name Catholic Church, 1104 South 9th Avenue, Wausau, with Rev. Woodrow Pace presiding. Visitation will begin at 10:00 a.m. until the time of service, all at the church. Private burial will take place at a later date. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

The family requests memorial donations be made to Newman Catholic Classical School at Holy Name.

records.name: Bernice T. Connor
records.summary: Bernice T. Connor, 89, of Athens, passed away on July 1, 2026.
records.body:
Bernice T. Connor, 89, Athens, passed away Wednesday, July 1, 2026 under the care of St. Croix Hospice at Athenian Living Community, Athens.

She was born October 15, 1936 in Mosinee, daughter of the late Frank and Elizabeth (Springer) Purull. On August 21, 2004 she married Lawrence "Woody" Connor and he preceded her in death on April 19, 2023.

Bernice worked at Marathon Electric and enjoyed playing softball, and dancing especially the polka, waltz and jitterbug. She was also an avid Packers and Brewers fan.

Survivors include her son, Scott Roesler; grandchildren, Chad Roesler, Tucker (Emily) Roesler and Treyton (Katie Hart) Roesler; great-grandchildren, Xander, Caston, Willow, Waylon, Braxton, Kinsley and Maverick Roesler; step-grandchildren, Peter and Cale Kurszewski and Jonathon and Eric McCunn, and step-daughter Vicki (Tom) Rheinschmidt.

Besides her parents, she was preceded in death by her husbands, Melvin Roesler, Charles Robinson and Lawrence "Woody" Connor.

Memorial service will be 11:00 a.m. Wednesday, July 8, 2026, at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens. Rev. George Graham will preside. Burial will be in St. Patrick Cemetery, Minocqua. Visitation will be Wednesday from 9:30 a.m. until the time of service all at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens.

records.name: Clifford John Hatz
records.summary: Clifford John Hatz, 63, of Wausau, Wisconsin passed away unexpectedly on June 27, 2026.
records.body:
Clifford John Hatz, age 63, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away unexpectedly on June 27, 2026. He was born on May 7, 1963, in Merrill to the late John and Berdina (Luedtke) Hatz. Clifford graduated from Merrill High School in 1981.

Clifford had a lifelong passion for aviation. He was a self-employed aircraft craftsman specializing in restoring fabric covered antique and classic airplanes. Many of his restorations won prestigious awards at national shows. A good singer he was often called on to perform at weddings, parties and memorial services.

He is survived by his brother, Lyman (Judy) Hatz and his sister, Barbara Hatz; his nephews, Wyat Hatz and Jordan Lesky; and his niece, Angela (David) Jirovec. He will be deeply missed by his family and all who knew him.

Clifford was preceded in death by his parents and his brothers, Aaron Hatz and Allan Hatz.

His memory will live on in the hearts of those who loved him and in the skies he so dearly cherished.

records.name: Debra Lynn Narloch
records.summary: Debra Lynn Narloch, 71, of Rothschild, Wisconsin passed away on June 16, 2026.
records.body:
It is with heavy hearts that we announce the passing of Debra Lynn Narloch, our beloved mother and grandmother, who left us on the afternoon of June 16, 2026. She passed away peacefully, surrounded by her loving family, after a fierce and courageous battle with kidney failure and subsequent health complications.

Born March 21, 1955, in Wausau, WI. Deb was the daughter of Raymond Kolbeck and Elaine Carpenter. She attended Wausau West High School and beautician school in Green Bay, WI and later moved to West Allis, Wi with the love of her life, Donald. The couple was united in marriage on July 19, 1975, at Mt. Calvary Lutheran Church in Rothschild, WI. After starting their family in West Allis, they returned to Rothschild where they raised their two children.

From a young age Deb started working. Her parents' business Coral Lanes Bowling Ally, Coal Lanes Root beer stand, The Palms Supper Club, and a hairdresser. She cut family and friends' hair throughout her life.

After several years, Deb and Don embarked on the adventure of owning and operating their own business, Quality Carpets of Wausau and QCF Supply, for approximately 22 years before retiring. Over the 22 years Deb enjoyed being a member of the Wausau Westside Business Association and assisting in returning the annual Christmas Parade to Wausau and joyed that it continues.

Following retirement Deb and Don relocated to their little piece of heaven in Tomahawk, WI. Deb enjoyed her beautiful gardens, generational flowers, canning, and campfires. Family time was important to Deb. She would make sure that all the right (in excess) snacks were packed for her cherished time with her grandchildren, family, and friends on Lake Nokomis, cruising, fishing (the competitions), and "beaching it". Deb found her best times in life with the love of her life Don in Tomahawk, traveling, berry picking, and spending time together hand in hand. She kept busy with friends at Taco Tuesday, strong bodies, and enjoying card parties with her good friends during annual "hunting widows weekend" and Birthday celebrations.

Deb is survived by her son Bradey (Milissa) Narloch Ringle, Bethany Narloch Wausau, Hunter Narloch Weston, and Addison Narloch Weston, Sister Linda Kent Weston, Brother Mike (Mary) Kolbeck, Sisters Deb Mortensen, Kitty Kramer, and Pam Carpenter.

Deb is preceded in death by her husband Donald, parents, Raymond Kolbeck and Elaine Carpenter (Klemp), older sister Dianne Lynn, and Brother Steve Kolbeck.

Special Thanks to many in moms declining health. Aspirus Dialysis Team (especially Nate), long term kidney doctor Dr. Abrego, Dr. Raymond Hardtke and the GI associates team.

Special thanks to Debs best friend Mary Detterming.

Special thanks to Pastor Kathy for her spiritual support to Deb, the family, and for officiating.

A celebration of Deb's life will take place on July 18th, 2026, at Mt. Calvary Lutheran Church in Rothschild, WI with Pastor Kathy officiating. Visitation 9am-11am Service at 11am.

In lieu of flowers, the family requests that cards and memorial contributions be directed to them.

records.name: Kim Allen Koval
records.summary: Kim Allen Koval, 68, passed away peacefully on June 28, 2026.
records.body:
Kim Allen Koval, 68, passed away peacefully on Sunday, June 28, 2026, at Aspirus Hospice House, surrounded by the love of his family.

Kim was born on September 18, 1957, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to Wesley and Julie Koval. As the oldest of five children, he embraced the role of big brother and remained devoted to his family throughout his life.

If something was broken, Kim could probably fix it. And if he couldn't, he'd keep it just because "you never know you might need it someday". A true "jack of all trades," he had a knack for solving problems, working with his hands, and taking on projects. There weren't many things he didn't think he could fix with little determination, the right tool or in most cases a random tool he would just make work. Whether helping family, friends, or neighbors, Kim was always the first to lend a hand and rarely expected anything in return.

Kim found his happiest moments outdoors. Whether camping with family, sitting quietly in a fishing boat, or heading into the woods for hunting season, nature was where he felt most at home. He loved the simple things in life, the fresh air, a good campfire, and stories shared long after the sun went down and the occasional red dog. Those adventures created memories his family will treasure forever.

Above all, Kims greatest pride was his family. He is survived by his wife and best friend of 34 years, Debbie (Deb, as he called her), his three younger siblings, Shari Sickinger and husband Pat, Carla Hutchison, and Gregg Koval and wife Mary; his eight children Adrian Bixby and her husband Jerald, Chris Rice and significant other Susan, Brenda Nemitz and husband Matthew, Kristy Lemmer and husband Troy, Greg Rice and wife Michelle, Ryan Tappan and husband Luke, Richard Rice and wife Julia, and Cody Koval; eighteen grandchildren, and four and a half great-grandchildren, all of whom were blessed by his love, guidance, and sense of humor.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Wesley and Julie Koval, and his youngest sister Patti.

Kim will be remembered for his hardworking spirit, generous heart, practical wisdom, and unwavering devotion to those he loved. He leaves behind not only countless memories but also a legacy of resilience, kindness, and the reminder that there's always another way to fix a problem. While his family will miss him deeply, they take comfort in knowing his stories will continue to be told around campfires, family gatherings, and every project that requires a little ingenuity.

The family would like to extend their heartfelt gratitude to the compassionate staff at Aspirus Hospice House for the exceptional care and kindness they provided during Kim's final days.

There will be a celebration of life held on Saturday July 18th at Wildlife Bar and campground in Birnamwood. The celebration will start at 3pm, the address is 183327 Schwede Lane in Birnamwood WI.

Forever loved, forever missed, and forever remembered.

records.name: Lyle Richard Hotchkiss
records.summary: Lyle Richard Hotchkiss, 83, of Rothschild, Wisconsin passed away on June 27, 2026.
records.body:
Lyle Richard Hotchkiss, 83 of Rothschild Wisconsin peacefully passed away on June 27, 2026.

Lyle was born March 24, 1943, to Lester and Ruth (Plautz) Hotchkiss. He was a graduate of Wausau High School class of 1961. He married his wife Darlene Lepak, who survives him, on September 18, 1965, in Wausau at Holy Name Church.

Lyle served his country in the Army Reserves and was honorably discharged as a sta? sergeant. Lyle began working as a route driver for Topper Dairy. His trucking career started with Paul Radtke trucking. He then moved on to long haul driver for Wausau Metals delivering windows to the East Coast. He then joined the sales sta? for Wausau Metals and eventually retired from his estimator position at Old Castle Glass. Lyle was also a dedicated Amway consultant for many years.

He was a longtime member of Saint Mark Catholic Church. He volunteered his time as an Usher, assisting with ticket sales for the June Bug Festival and calling Bingo with his wife, Darlene.

Lyle had many hobbies, including cross country skiing and snowmobiling with family and friends, he loved to bowl and was a member of a couple of league teams with Darlene. Lyle enjoyed spending time with his cats Amy and Scooter and sunny summer days floating in the backyard pool. He was an avid sports fan of both the Packers and Brewers.

Lyle was proceeded in death by his parents, In-laws Lawrence and Cecilia (Busko) Lepak brother Les, Jr. (Sharon Niewolney) and sister-in-law Dianne (Smith) Lepak.

He is survived by his wife, Darlene of 60 years, son Brad (Sally) stepsons Al and Mitch, daughter Beth (Rob) Southwick, three granddaughters Carly (Greg) Grasse, Megan (Ryan) Vance and Rachel Southwick, brother Larry (Sally), and sister Connie (Mike) Vaningan, brothers-in-law Larry Lepak (Sue White), Randy Lepak (Jennifer) and Glenn (Dianne) Smith along with many nieces and nephews.

Funeral service will be held on Friday, July 10 at Restlawn Memorial Chapel. Visitation will begin at 10 am with service to follow at 11am. Entombment with follow immediately at the Restlawn Memorial Chapel.

The family wishes to thank the sta? at Lili Gardens Assisted Living for their patience and kindness in caring for Lyle, especially Charlie 😉 and the compassionate and caring sta? from Heartland Hospice.

records.name: Marilyn Catherine Bins
records.summary: Marilyn Catherine Bins, age 88 passed away on Jul 3, 2026.
records.body:
Marilyn Catherine Bins, age 88, passed away peacefully on Friday, July 3, 2026, in Wausau, Wisconsin.

Marilyn was born on September 16, 1937, in Green Bay, Wisconsin, to Walter and Harriet (Collie) Fabry. She lived a life centered on love, family, and creating cherished memories with those closest to her. Marilyn found great happiness at the family cabin in Three Lakes, Wisconsin, and at their winter home in Ft. Myers Beach, FL; but nothing brought her greater joy than spending time with her family. Whether gathered for holidays, celebrations, or simple visits, Marilyn treasured every moment with the people she loved.

She is lovingly survived by her two sons, Daniel and David; brother Mike (Carol) Fabry; grandchildren Aly (Andy) Stahlman and Andy (Lauren) Johnson; her two great-grandchildren, Margot Stahlman and Beau Johnson; son-in-law Doug (Cheryl) Johnson, as well as other relatives and friends. Her love, kindness, and devotion to her family will be remembered always

She was preceded in death by her maternal and paternal grandparents; her parents, Walter and Harriet Fabry; her beloved husband, Gerald; and her daughter, Julie Bins Johnson.

A visitation will be held on Friday, July 17, 2026, from 9:30 a.m. to 10:30 a.m. at Church of the Resurrection. A Funeral Mass will follow at 10:30 a.m., with Father Tom Lindner officiating. Burial will take place after at St. Joseph Cemetery.

records.name: Nancy Ann Pries
records.summary: Nancy Ann Pries, 91, passed away peacefully on June 29, 2026, in Wausau, Wisconsin.
records.body:
Nancy Ann Pries, age 91, passed away peacefully on June 29, 2026, in Wausau, Wisconsin, surrounded by the love of her family. Born in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, on May 22, 1935, Nancy lived a life defined by faith, service, compassion, and deep devotion to those she loved.

Nancy graduated from West Milwaukee High School, where she met the love of her life, Howard "Howie" Pries. Their connection was immediate, and they were married on October 19, 1957. Together they shared nearly 68 years of love, partnership, and cherished memories until Howie's passing on August 18, 2025. Their journey began in their first home, affectionately remembered as the "Little House on the Alley," where they laid the foundation for a life rich in family, friendship, and laughter.

Together, Nancy and Howie raised their three children-Betty, Bonnie, and Michael-in a home filled with love, encouragement, and wisdom. Nancy was a devoted wife, mother, grandmother, great grandmother, and friend whose gentle guidance and caring heart touched countless lives.

Nancy possessed many talents and gifts. She played the accordion, prepared memorable family meals, and offered a listening ear and thoughtful advice not only to her own children but also to their friends. Her kindness knew no bounds. Whether volunteering at church, serving as a leader for Girl Scouts and Boy Scouts, or volunteering at St. Clare's Hospital, Nancy was always willing to lend a helping hand. Her warm smile, compassionate spirit, and genuine care brought comfort and joy to everyone fortunate enough to know her.
