Nancy found great happiness in the outdoors. She enjoyed camping, hiking, boating, and water skiing with family and friends. Family vacations at Lynn Ann's Campground in St. Germain became treasured traditions and created lasting memories for her grandchildren. Yet some of her greatest joys were found in life's simplest moments-sitting at her kitchen table with a cup of coffee, watching the birds gather at the feeder and laughing at the occasional squirrel that stopped by.

Faith was the cornerstone of Nancy's life. She lived her beliefs through service, kindness, and fellowship. Alongside Howie, she was an active member of her church community for many years, participating in family groups, youth ministries, women's circle, and countless church activities. She especially enjoyed gathering with friends for card club, where a spirited game of Sheepshead was always a favorite.

Nancy will be remembered for her unwavering faith, generous heart, welcoming home, and the love she shared so freely with family, friends, neighbors, and strangers alike. Her legacy lives on in the values she instilled, the lives she touched, and the countless memories cherished by those who knew and loved her.

Though her family grieves her loss, they find comfort in knowing that Nancy and Howie are together again, reunited in the presence of the Lord they faithfully served throughout their lives.

Nancy's life was a blessing, her memory a treasure, and her love a gift that will remain in the hearts of her family forever.

Nancy is survived by her daughters Betty Rasmussen (Douglas) and Bonnie Surkin (Barry); son Michael Pries (Maryrose); grandchildren Claire Chamberlain, Michael Surkin (Chelsea), Jason Surkin (Kenzi), Cooper Pries, and Johnny Pries; and great-granddaughter Margot Surkin. Her legacy of love and laughter continues through each of them.

Visitation will be held at Brainard Funeral Home, 5712 memorial ct., Weston, WI 54476 on Tuesday, July 7, from 4 to 7 p.m. Funeral services will take place Wednesday, July 8, at 11 a.m. with a Funeral Service at Covenant Community Presbyterian Church, 1806 Weston Ave, Schofield, WI 54476 with a visitation one hour prior to the service and interment at Pine Grove Cemetery, 1501 Grand Ave, Wausau, WI.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to Covenant Community Presbyterian Church,1806 Weston Ave, Schofield, WI 54476.

records.name: Patricia Krake
records.summary: Patricia Krake, 88, of Weston, Wisconsin passed away on June 23, 2026.
records.body:
Patricia Krake, 88 of Weston, WI passed away on June 23,2026 after a lengthy battle with dementia. She was born on March 13th, 1938, to the late Walter and Clara Kempf.

Patricia graduated from Wausau High School. She married David Krake on May 19th, 1963. Patricia spent most of her adult life as a homemaker, helping to raise the couple's three children. Patricia enjoyed cooking, canning and reading, but her absolute favorite thing to do was to go up to the family cottage near Lake Tomahawk, WI and go fishing.

Survivors include her husband David (Weston, WI), daughter Kelly (Coupland, TX), son Keith (Weston, WI), granddaughters Trista (Dallas, TX) and Kiera (Austin, TX), sisters Ellen Rustad (Rhinelander, WI), Bonnie Fisher (Kronenwetter, WI), Sandy Marquardt (Wausau, WI) and Cathy Szekeress (Schofield, WI).

Patricia is preceded in death by her parents, Walter and Clara Kempf, son, Kevin Krake, brothers, Marvin Kempf and Donald Kempf, sisters, Carol Hess, Mary Ruplinger and Linda Kempf.

Funeral services will be 11:00 am Monday July 6th at St. Agnes Parish in Weston, WI. Visitation will be from 10:00 am until the time of service. Burial will follow at the parish cemetery.

The family would like to thank the staff at Mount View Care Center in Wausau, WI for the compassion and caring of Patricia in her final years.

records.name: Ramona T. Hanson
records.summary: Ramona T. Hanson, 94, of Wittenberg, passed away peacefully on June 29, 2026.
records.body:
Ramona T. Hanson, 94, of Wittenberg, passed away peacefully on June 29, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center-Weston.

Ramona was born on August 4, 1931, to A.N. and Elithe (Chapin) Brunner. Raised in Leopolis, she attended local elementary schools before graduating from Marion High School. She continued her education at UW-Madison, where she served as president of her sorority, competed as an Alice in Dairyland finalist, and earned her bachelor's degree.

On August 21, 1954, Ramona married Roger Hanson at St. Mary's Catholic Church in Leopolis.

Ramona began her career teaching high school English in Tigerton and later worked as a substitute teacher in Wittenberg. Alongside her husband, Roger, she co-founded and operated Hanson Sanitation & Excavating, Inc.

Even while co-managing a business, Ramona was deeply involved in her community. She served on the local school board, the Homme Home for the Aging board, and the Holy Family parish council. She was also instrumental in developing a residential subdivision in Wittenberg. Ramona possessed a vibrant personal life; she loved playing the piano and encouraged her grandchildren to play. She also enjoyed bridge parties, reading, discussing politics, and was one of the first female golf members at Maple Hills.

Her remarkable work ethic was evident well into her later years-she tended a garden for decades and was still mowing her own lawn at age 93.

Ramona was preceded in death by her beloved husband, Roger Hanson, on June 4, 2022, and her infant son, Joseph Hanson.

Ramona is survived by three sons: Steve (Jane) Hanson of Marathon City, Stuart (Lynne) Hanson of Eland, and Eric (Kay) Hanson of Rocklin, CA; 10 grandchildren: Nick, Angie, Brian, Jason, Jeremy, Nicole, Keegan, Carl, Evan, and Megan; 20 great-grandchildren; and one expected great-great-grandchild.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00am on Saturday, July 18, 2026 at Holy Family Catholic Church in Wittenberg. Father Mark Rydell will preside, with burial to follow at Forest Home Cemetery, Wittenberg.

records.name: Rosaline Isabel Gaines
records.summary: Rosaline Isabel Gaines, 76, passed away peacefully on June 30, 2026.
records.body:
Rosaline Isabel Gaines, 76, passed away peacefully on June 30, 2026.

She was born on October 17, 1949, in Sacramento, California, to the late Frank and Dorothy (Dalske) Pierson. After moving to Wisconsin and beginning her life as a wife and mother, Rose embraced farm life. She and her first husband operated a hobby farm, and later, with her second husband, she helped run a dairy farm that remained close to her heart throughout her life. Rose was incredibly proud of the years she and her children spent on the farm, especially the time she spent working in the fields and driving the tractor. Those experiences shaped the strong work ethic, independence, and determination that defined her for the rest of her life.

Rose dedicated her life to helping others. After farming, her daughter encouraged her to go back to school, where she got her degree, and she began her career in social work as an AODA counselor, where she found purpose in supporting people through difficult times with compassion and understanding. Her entrepreneurial spirit later led her to own and operate a deli, "Bubba's Bayou Deli," a neighborhood bar, "Bubba's Country Crossroads", a coffee shop, a Food Wagon, and finally a U-Haul business and bait shop, all of which she did alongside her husband, Bubba. Whatever role she took on, Rose's greatest gift was connecting with people.

After losing her husband, Bubba, Rose never lost her independence. She learned the City Bus route and would use it to get downtown. Even in later years, she could often be seen riding her scooter to CVS, Krist Station, and the Yummy Deli, sharing smiles with everyone she met along the way—and perhaps leaving behind a trail of sunflower seeds. She took great joy in watching those flowers bloom and was heartbroken when the city mowed down the beautiful sunflower path she had lovingly created.

Rose had a generous heart and a zest for life. She loved going out to eat, horseback riding, traveling, visiting the casino, and trying her luck with scratch-off lottery tickets—especially the crossword scratchers. She was a hopeless romantic and a dreamer, always talking about the next place she hoped to visit and looking forward to the next adventure.

An exceptional cook, Rose shared her love through food. She passed her cooking skills on to her children, and they will forever cherish the family meals she prepared with so much love and care. Her Indian tacos were legendary and will be especially missed around the family table.

She found joy in life's simple pleasures, whether feeding the birds and squirrels outside her home or sitting down to hear someone's life story. Rose never met a stranger. She welcomed everyone with open arms, and by the end of every conversation, she had made a new friend. She believed everyone had a story worth hearing, and she genuinely wanted to know it.

Her generosity extended far beyond her family. Rose faithfully supported multiple charities and was always willing to lend a helping hand. If you needed a break from life's challenges, she was the friend who would tell you to hop in the car so the two of you could escape for a good meal, a few laughs, and a chance to forget your worries, even if only for a little while.

Rose's sense of humor was unforgettable. She loved making people laugh and often joked that she was proud to have outlived "all of her ex-husbands." Her quick wit, contagious laughter, and playful spirit brought joy to countless lives.

More than anything, Rose loved her family. She was a loving and devoted mother to Jennifer (Tom Malone) Lukes, Damian Betts, Gabrielle (Julio) Rojas, Georgianna (Jeff) Szymanski, and Rorydale (Shannon) Genrich. Her family was the center of her world.

She was the proud grandmother of Morgan, Kaitlin, Zandria (Austin), Jilisa, JadenRose, Jonathan, Reyden (Caitlyn), Isaiah, Xavier, Nicholas, Johnathon, Lilyan, Violet, Dayton, Dominic, and Lincoln, and the treasured great-grandmother of Levi and Layni.

Rose is also survived by her siblings: Dennis (Jeanette) Pierson, Toni (Len) Malik, Delphine (Tom) Taft, Karen (Dave Barrick) Cichucki, and Geri (Joe Trevino) Armijo, along with many nieces, nephews, extended family members, and countless friends.

Rose was an integral part of the community she called home. She will forever be remembered for the kindness, friendship, laughter, and generosity that she and her beloved, Bubba, shared with everyone they met. Her door was always open, her table always had room for one more, and her heart always had space for another friend.

She was preceded in death by her parents, Frank and Dorothy (Dalske) Pierson.

Although her presence will be deeply missed, Rose's adventurous spirit, unwavering generosity, infectious humor, and unconditional love will live on in the hearts of all who knew her. The lessons she taught, the meals she shared, the stories she listened to, and the friendships she created will remain a lasting legacy for generations to come.

The family would like to thank Wood Aven Health and Rehabilitation, Cedar Creek Manor, and Aspirus Comfort Care and Hospice for the exceptional care they gave Rose. They would also like to thank Ashley Salzwedel and Roseanne for being such a big part of Rose's life; as well as the countless friends and neighbors who would offer to help over the years. You were all a blessing to Rose.

records.name: Stanley G. Szymanski
records.summary: Stanley G. Szymanski, 83, passed away on July 1, 2026.
records.body:
Stanley "Stan" G. Szymanski passed away surrounded by the love of his family on July 1, 2026. He was born on May 23, 1943, to the late Stanley A. and Elizabeth (Knetter) Szymanski.

Stan married Angela Burger in 1964, and together they built a life on the family farm, raising their children while tending the fields and caring for the animals. Angela preceded him in death in 2014. In 2015, Stan found love again and married Sharon Malueg. Together, they shared many happy years traveling and enjoying their love of polka dancing.

Stan was a retired dairy farmer whose passion for farming never faded. He loved caring for the dairy herd and especially enjoyed spending time out on the tractor. Farming was more than his livelihood—it was his way of life.

Away from the farm, Stan embraced life with enthusiasm. He loved polka dancing, traveling worldwide, writing poetry, and playing cards, especially a good game of sheephead. He also enjoyed watching sports on television and was an avid Milwaukee Brewers fan.

Stan never met a stranger. He was a social man who loved striking up conversations with anyone and everyone. His children fondly remember many occasions when they had to wait patiently in the car because Dad had stopped to chat with someone he knew—or had just met. His warm personality and genuine interest in others left lasting impressions on countless people.

Dedicated to both agriculture and his community, Stan was actively involved in many organizations throughout his life. Among them were the Gladiola Society, Holstein Association, Wisconsin Rural Leadership, Wausau Jaycees, Master Farmers, and the Agriculture Society, to name just a few. His commitment to serving others reflected the same work ethic and dedication he brought to his family and his farm.

Above all else, Stan was a devoted husband, father, grandfather, great-grandfather, brother, and friend. He was a hardworking family man whose love, generosity, and kindness will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved him.

He is survived by his beloved wife, Sharon; his children, Sheila (Ted Rude) Szymanski, Jeff (Anna) Szymanski, Christopher (Ana) Szymanski, Gail (Danny) Wilde, and Tracy (Rod) Kaiser; his stepchildren, Craig Stern, Shelly (Cory) Martin, and Ross (Dannette) Stern; 22 grandchildren: Tom (Shauna) Szymanski, Amanda (Jesse) Fischer, Nicole (Joe) Westberg, Tyler (Alexis) Szymanski, Isaiah Genrich, Xavier Genrich, Nicholas Szymanski, Johnathon Szymanski, Lilyan Szymanski, Violet Szymanski, Johnathan Rodriguez, Clarissa Rodriguez, Deanna Cotty, Erika Wilde, Carmen Wilde, Shelby Wilde, Kaitlyn Kaiser, Marissa Kaiser, Alex Kaiser, Seth Martin, Ashley Martin, and Madison Stern; and 10 great-grandchildren.

He is further survived by his siblings, Jacki (Terry) Pyan and Ted Szymanski; his brothers-in-law, Kenneth Lepak, Paul (Sandy) Burger, Steve (Jane) Burger, Joe Burger, and Peter Burger; his sisters-in-law, Linda Szymanski, Carolyn Martino, Emily Noll, Pauline (Alan) Babler, Elaine Schlender and Pam Oswald; as well as many nieces, nephews, cousins, extended family members, and friends.

In addition to his parents, he was preceded in death by his sisters, Sandra Lepak, and Patricia (Joe) Oswald; brother Terry Szymanski; brother-in-law, George Burger; and sisters-in-law, Margie Nagdeman and Barbara Olander.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Thursday, July 9, 2026, at Sacred Heart Catholic Church, 222761 County Road S, Marathon City, WI 54448. Visitation will be held at the church from 9:00 a.m. until the time of Mass. Burial will follow in the parish cemetery.

records.name: Allan Johnson
records.summary: Allan Johnson, age 74, of Mosinee passed away on Jul 4, 2026.
records.body:
Allan Wayne Johnson, age 74, passed away on Saturday, July 4, 2026, at his home in Mosinee, with his loving wife by his side and under the care of Compassus Hospice. He was born on December 7, 1951, to the late Robert and Ila (McCumber) Johnson in Madison, WI. Al attended and graduated from high school and went on to earn his bachelor’s degree in broadcasting. He met the love of his life, Nancy Louise Ranum, and the two were united in marriage on February 3, 1973 in Mount Vernon, WI. She survives.

Al was an installer for various sound systems, and enjoyed working on computers and electronics. He was very good at solving issues with them and helped everyone he could. He had an incredible smile and was very friendly and outgoing. He made friends wherever he went. Al’s passion was broadcasting for local radio stations, which he did for many years. He enjoyed fishing, riding his bicycle, often times, to and from church, and was dedicated to his church, Fellowship Baptist Church, where he assisted with wiring and some of their sound system needs. Al had recently been driving bus for Lamer’s part-time. He was an active member of the HAM radio organization, and enjoyed the camaraderie with his fellow radio co-workers. Al will be sadly missed, but his love and personality will live on in family and friends who knew and loved him.

Al leaves behind his wife: Nancy Johnson, son: Nathan Johnson and 2 grandsons: Mason and Grant. He was preceded in death by his parents, son: Andrew Johnson and an infant sister.

The funeral service for Al will be held on Friday, July 10, 2026, at 12:00 p.m. at the Fellowship Baptist Church, 1130 Field Road, Kronenwetter, WI 54455. Pastor Brent Moodie will officiate. Visitation will be held on Friday, July 10, 2026 from 10:00 a.m. until the time of the service, also at the church. The burial will take place on a later date at the Perry Lutheran Cemetery in Mount Horeb.

In lieu of flowers, please direct donations in Al’s name to either the Fellowship Baptist Church or to the HAM radio club.

A special thank you to all of Al’s friends, family, and his church family, especially, Pastor Brent Moodie, for all of their prayers, support and comfort that was shown to Al and Nancy during his final days.

Brainard Funeral Home & Cremation Center of Wausau is honored to assist Al’s family with arrangements. Online condolences may be made at www.brainardfuneral.com.

records.name: Jean Ann Marcis
records.summary: Jean Ann Marcis, age 72, of Knowlton passed away on Jul 4, 2026.
records.body:
Jean A. Marcis, 72, Mosinee, passed away on July 4, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center, Marshfield.

Jean was born on May 22, 1954, in Wausau, WI, daughter of the late Arthur and Elizabeth (Benz) Blumenstein. She married Sherman Marcis on October 12, 1974, he survives.

Jean graduated from UW Stevens Point in 1987 as a Medical Technologist and retired from Marshfield Clinic in 2019.

She enjoyed vacationing with family, water aerobics, gardening, canning vegetables, reading, quilting, and spending time on Lake DuBay riding on the pontoon boat, fishing, and swimming at the beach.

Even in their grief, Jean’s family celebrates the beautiful certainty of her faith, knowing that Jesus Christ has welcomed her home for eternity.

Jean is survived by her husband, Sherman; children: Michelle (Aaron) Winowiski, and Robert Marcis; grandchildren: Hawi Winowiski, Misikir Winowiski, Preston (Lily) Winowiski, Isaiah (Kaitlin) Winowiski, and Sophie Winowiski; great-grandchildren: Sparrow and Evangeline Winowiski; brother, Walter (Jeri) Blumenstein, and sister, Joanne Pryga. Besides her parents, Jean was preceded in death by her sister, Delena Blumenstein, brother, Ronald Blumenstein, and brother-in-law, Chet Pryga.

A Celebration of Life will take place at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, August 1, 2026, at Beste Funeral Home, Mosinee. Son-in-law, Pastor Aaron Winowiski will officiate. Visitation will be held from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service, all at the Funeral Home. Burial will take place at Mosinee Cemetery at a later date. Online condolences and memories may be shared with the family at www.bestefh.com.

records.name: John Frederick Pilgrim
records.summary: John Frederick Pilgrim, age 79, of Homer Glen passed away on July 4, 2026.
records.body:
John Frederick Pilgrim, age 79, of Homer Glen, IL, died on Saturday, July, 4, 2026 at the St. Vincent Hospital in Green Bay, WI.  He was born on May 23, 1947 in Sheboygan, WI to Orville and Dolores (Kelm) Pilgrim.

John is survived by his wife June, other family and many friends. A complete obituary will be available shortly.

A memorial service for John will be held on July 24th, 2026 at 11:00 AM at the Carlson Funeral Home with a visitation from 9:30 till the time of the service. You may leave your private condolences for the Pilgrim family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home(715-369-1414) is handling the services for the family.

records.name: Kathleen M. Hale-Mundt
records.summary: Kathleen M. Hale-Mundt, age 87 passed away on Jul 4, 2026.
records.body:
Kathleen Mary Hale-Mundt, passed away on July 4, 2026. Born on October 5, 1938, in Racine, Wisconsin, Kathleen was a devoted wife, mother, grandmother, and great-grandmother, who touched the lives of everyone around her.

Kathleen, affectionately known for her love of flowers and spending time outdoors, found joy in watching the birds in her yard and creating a warm home atmosphere. She cherished gatherings with her loved ones, especially her grandchildren and great-grandchild, Oliver Hale. Kathleen had a knack for keeping her home tidy and was often busy cleaning something, but she always made time for the things she loved.

After many years working with Merrill Candy, Kathleen spent a significant part of her career as a receptionist at Marshfield Clinic in Merrill, where she greeted everyone with a smile. She was a proud member of St. Mary’s Catholic Church in Tomahawk and was a woman of deep faith.

On September 26, 2002, Kathleen married her beloved husband, Gerald Mundt, who survives her, along with her son Christopher (Ellen) Hale; grandchildren: Michael Hale, Bradley (Tom Thornton) Hale, Brittany (Joe Norton) Hale, and Katherina (Tim Oleson) Hale. Kathleen is also survived by her sisters, Yvonne Nichols and Jane (Tom) Wilson, and many loving nieces and nephews.

Kathleen was preceded in death by her previous husband, Brad Lee Hale; her son, William Hale; her sister, Barbara Klebenow; and brothers-in-law, Charles Nichols and Gerald Klebenow.

Visitation will be held at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral and Cremation, 301 S. Eagle Dr, Merrill, on Tuesday, July 21 from 4 PM to 7 PM, and on Wednesday, July 22, at St. Mary’s Catholic Church in Tomahawk from 11 AM to 12 PM. Following the visitation, a Mass of Christian Burial will take place at 12 PM, with a burial to follow at Woodlawn Cemetery in the Town of Rock Falls.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Kelly Melanson
records.summary: Kelly Melanson, age 59, of Kronenwetter passed away on July 4, 2026.
records.body:
Kelly Melanson, age 59, of Kronenwetter, Wisconsin, passed away in the arms of her husband on Saturday, July 4, 2026, at her residence after a two-year battle with cancer.

Born on July 11, 1966, in Wausau, Wisconsin, Kelly was the daughter of Sandra Halverson. Kelly’s journey was marked by warmth, laughter, and a deep devotion to those she cherished.

Throughout her years, Kelly embodied the very essence of kindness and generosity. The world knew her as loving, caring, and outgoing.

Family stood at the core of Kelly’s life. She shared a beautiful marriage with her husband, Roy, whose steadfast companionship was evident to all who knew them. Together, Kelly and Roy found joy in the simplicity of working on their property, moments filled with nature, laughter, and the quiet satisfaction of shared achievements.

Work was not merely a job for Kelly but an extension of her caring spirit. As a dedicated bus operator for Metro Ride with the City of Wausau, she served her community with diligence and heart, touching the lives of countless riders over the years. Her professionalism and conversation were daily gifts to those who crossed her path.

Kelly found her true joy in the great outdoors. Whether she was fishing, hunting, or simply enjoying time in nature, she felt most alive with wind in her hair and soil beneath her boots. She and Roy’s shared love for their land was a source of happiness, adventure, and connection. Kelly’s fond membership in Bill’s Musky Club, where she was recognized as a lifetime member, stands testament to her enduring passion for angling and the friendships that grew from her love of nature.

Being a grandmother was among Kelly’s greatest joys. Her granddaughters, Amelia and Elizabeth Kroeplin, lit up her life. She cherished every moment spent with them, creating loving memories in which they will forever find comfort. Kelly’s adoration for her granddaughters was rivaled only by their admiration and affection for her.

Kelly is survived by her husband Roy; son, Kenneth (Kou Yang) Kroeplin; brother, Charles Kroeplin; and sister, Tamie (Joe) Williams. She is also survived by her sister-in-law, Brenda (Cary) Pellowski; granddaughters, Amelia and Elizabeth Kroeplin; uncles, Roger Frost and Richard Frost; many nieces, nephews, other relatives and many friends. She is preceded in death by her mother, Sandra Halverson.

A funeral service for Kelly will be held on Friday, July 10, 2026, at 11:00 AM, at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston. Visitation will take place from 9:00 AM until the time of service at 11:00 AM. Kelly will be laid to rest following the service at Stettin Christian Cemetery.

As we gather to celebrate the life of Kelly, we invite those who wish to honor her memory by wearing camouflage attire.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Leona D. Carr
records.summary: Leona D. Carr, age 97 passed away on Jul 4, 2026.
records.body:
Leona D. Carr, 97, of Wausau, formerly of Marathon, passed away peacefully on Saturday, July 4, 2026 at Mount View Care Center in Wausau, under the care of St. Croix Hospice.

She was born November 5, 1928 in Mosinee, daughter of the late Albert and Stella (Blaszak) Zawislan. Leona graduated from Mosinee High School in 1947.

Leona had a fulfilling and diverse work history which she often talked about. Her earliest job included housecleaning in "several beautiful homes on Wausau's East side". She was part of the 'Rosie the Riveter' era when she welded for Muskegon Piston in Schofield. She owned Carr's Bar in Town of Stettin. Leona moved on to what would be her primary career until retirement of being a CNA at Sunny Vale and Mount View Care Center. She also managed and bartended for American Legion, VFW, and Thunderbird bars in Wausau and worked in ginseng gardens. She spent a year living in Green Bay where she was the manager of a Hallmark Store. Leona had stories and enjoyed aspects of every job.

After retirement she was able to enjoy hobbies and her family found out how creative and crafty she was. She had always been great at cooking and sewing, but when she gardened, painted the most beautiful ceramics, and made some grandchildren baptismal gowns, it was amazing to see someone who spent their life often working two jobs at a time get to enjoy and express herself. Leona had an incredible work ethic and instilled that in her children.

Leona is survived by her children, Carol, Diane (Bob), Gary, Randy (Cheri), Brian (Judy), Lynn (Lynn) and her daughter-in-law, Char. Her grandchildren, Kim, Kris, Brian, Michael, Amy, Carrie, Christy, Casey, Dana, Gina, Kevin, Kelly, Kory, Kyle, Kody, Garret, Allison, Lucas, and Samantha. Leona has many more cherished great and great-great grandchildren. Another passion was to boast about all of her grandchildren's accomplishments or "how smart they were" when they were little.

She was preceded in death by her parents and siblings, John, Marie, Angeline, Ted, Sophie, Ben, Helen, Edward, Clara, Stella, and Bernice. Three sons, Wayne, Dale, and Patrick. A daughter-in-law, Doreen, son-in-law, Len, and granddaughter, Alicia also preceded her in death.

In accordance with Leona’s wishes, there will be a private service and burial at St. Mary's Cemetery in Marathon.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Martin E Burkhardt
records.summary: Martin E Burkhardt, age 89 passed away on July 4, 2026.
records.body:
Martin E. Burkhardt passed away, leaving behind a legacy of love, service, and lifelong learning. He was born in Rock County, Wisconsin, to the late Martin and Georgina (Schneider) Burkhardt.

Martin met the love of his life, Kathleen Ihlenfeldt, and the two were married on January 28, 1961. Together they shared 65 wonderful years of marriage, building a life centered on love, family, and unwavering devotion. They raised two sons, Craig and Brian, and created countless cherished memories together.

A man with an unquenchable thirst for knowledge, Martin believed that learning was a lifelong pursuit. He earned two Bachelor of Science degrees, one in Agriculture and another in Mechanical Engineering—before going on to receive his Master's degree in Agricultural Engineering. His education laid the foundation for a distinguished career dedicated to helping others.

Martin began his professional career teaching Agricultural Engineering, first at UW Platteville, Wisconsin, and then SUNY Cobleskill, NY. He then spent more than 20 years as a Safety Consultant with Wausau Insurance, where his expertise and commitment to workplace safety earned him the respect of colleagues and clients alike.

Service to others was an important part of Martin's life. He was a proud member of the Shriners and, for more than a decade, volunteered countless hours transporting patients to Minneapolis, Minnesota, so they could receive the medical care they needed. He was also a dedicated Mason and an active member of the Merrill Historical Society. During the past five years, he found great joy in researching local history for the society, spending many hours uncovering and preserving stories from the past.

Martin enjoyed working with his hands and could often be found fixing things around the house or tending to his yard. He took pride in solving problems and making things better, whether it was repairing something broken or helping someone in need.

Above all else, Martin was a devoted husband, father, and grandfather. Until the very end, his greatest concern was making sure Kathleen was cared for. He was a loving and supportive father to Craig and Brian, and he took immense pride in his grandchildren, celebrating each of their accomplishments with genuine joy.

Martin will be remembered for his quiet strength, generous heart, sharp mind, and steadfast dedication to his family and community. His life was one of purpose, kindness, and service, and he leaves behind a legacy that will continue to inspire all who knew and loved him.

He will be deeply missed and forever remembered. He is survived by his wife, Kathleen; sons, Craig (Joan), and Brian (Kerri); and grandchildren, Brandon, Karissa, and Jillian; as well as several nieces and nephews.

In addition to his parents, he was preceded in death by his mother and father-in-law, Millard and Louise Ihlenfeldt; as well as his sister, Janette Jenson.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family with cremation services. Family and friends may go to MWCS.WS to leave condolences and words of remembrance online.

In accordance with his wishes, no services will be held. May his memory be of comfort to all that knew him. Memorials in his memory may be directed to the UW Foundation, Excellence in Cardiovascular Medicine Fund.

records.name: Roger Anthony Eisenman
records.summary: Roger Anthony Eisenman, age 101 passed away on Jul 4, 2026.
records.body:
Roger A. Eisenman, born June 12, 1925, in Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on July 4, 2026, leaving behind a legacy of service, dedication, and love for family, community, and faith.

He was the son of Arthur, Sr and Elizabeth (O'Neil) Eisenman. Roger was preceded in death by his parents, his brothers, Arthur Eisenman, Jr. and Mark Eisenman, his sister, Betty Eisenman and his beloved wife Patricia.

Roger married the love of his life, Patricia Bauman, on August 7, 1951. Together they built a devoted family and were blessed with three children: Louie (Cheryl) of Schofield, Wisconsin; Anne (Russ) Johnson of Buckeye, Arizona; and Amy Meyer of Scottsdale, Arizona. They also have four grandchildren: Louie (Camelia) Eisenman of Eau Claire, Wisconsin, Lisa (Jeff) Suppon of Wausau, Wisconsin, Ryan Eisenman of Washington D.C., and Madysen (Mike) Masino of Sunrise, Florida, as well as two great grandchildren, Jennifer and Fernando and many nieces and nephews.

A proud combat veteran of World War II, Roger enlisted in the U.S. Coast Guard at the age of 17. He served aboard the USCG Cutter Woodbine as a radar operator throughout the Pacific Theatre , including Saipan, the Mariana Islands, and Okinawa. After the war, he was honorably discharged, returned home and completed his education, graduating from Wausau High School in 1947.

Roger Spent the majority of his career with WSAU Radio, beginning in sales in 1953 and rising to station manager in 1976. He was respected for his leadership, integrity, and strong work ethic.

Roger loved sports, especially baseball. He played in the Marathon County League, Wisconsin Valley League, and City Fast Pitch Softball League. He also coached youth baseball for many years, serving boys of all ages in Youth Baseball Minors, Little League, Pony League, Senior League, Babe Ruth, and American Legion. He served as President of Little League and was inducted to the Wausau Youth Baseball and Softball Wall of Honor in 2020. He was also active in the community as President of Grant School PTA and the Citywide PTA Council. In addition, he served as Publicity Director for the Wisconsin Valley Fair. Roger also liked to bowl and golf.

Music also had a big influence on his life. He was a member of the Wausau Barbershoppers for 75 years. He performed with the River Valley Harmonizers, performing in the annual Barbershop show, singing Valentines, the annual Christmas concerts, many weddings and other various events. He was named Barbershopper of the Year three times and was affectionately known as "Mr. Barbershopper."

Most importantly, Roger's faith was central to his life. He was active from an early age in St. James Church and later in the Church of the Resurrection, where he sang in the choir most of his adult life. His faith was an enduring source of strength and guidance throughout his life.

Roger will be remembered for his devotion to his family, his service to his country, his contributions to his community, and his lifelong love of music, sports, and faith. He leaves behind a legacy that will be cherished by all who knew and loved him.

In his last weeks, Roger was comforted by the caring team from Aspirus Hospice. His family wishes to extend special gratitude to Kari, Carrie, Jason and Chris at Aspirus, as well as Char and the entire team from Careview Transitional Care (formerly Pride TLC) for providing exemplary long term assisted living care.

In lieu of flowers, donations may be forwarded to the following: Aspirus Hospice, 520 N 32nd Ave, Wausau, WI 54401, Barbershop Harmony Society at barbershop.org or 110 7th Ave, Nashville, TN 37203, VFW Post 338 at 212 E Randolph St., Wausau, WI 54401, or the American Legion Post 10 at 1001 Golf Club Rd., Wausau, WI 54401.

A visitation will be held July 16th, 2026 from 9:30 am to 10:30 am at the Church of the Resurrection in Wausau. Funeral Mass to follow with Father Tom Lindner officiating. Burial will follow at Restlawn Memorial Park.

records.name: Tony Lenard
records.summary: Tony Lenard, age 74 passed away on Jul 4, 2026.
records.body:
Tony Lenard, age 74, of Marathon, WI passed away at home, surrounded by his wife, son, and sister, along with a blanket of many prayer wishes from other family members and friends on July 4, 2026. Tony was born in Wausau, WI and raised on a dairy farm north of Edgar. He was the son of Regina and Phil Lenard. Tony married Mary Wilichowski on Dec. 18, 1976.

Tony was a successful building salesman, owning Advantage Building Systems. He sold over 3,000 buildings throughout his more than 30 year career. He took pride in his projects and made a lasting impression of honesty and integrity in working with his customers.

Tony had a passion for motorcycles, whether they were complete bikes or various parts in a box. He loved cycle racing and the thrill of the finish line. In the area, Tony was known as a “legend in the field of biking.” Tony was community-minded, being involved in projects at St. Mary’s, Marathon High School, and Town of Stettin. He was a gifted craftsman, inventor, and problem solver. If someone had a question, Tony had the answer. Tony’s son, Ryan, brought an immeasurable joy to his life. Tony never missed supporting Ryan with advice or sharing his 100% presence at music, academic, or sports events.

Survivors include his wife, Mary, and son, Ryan (Katie.) Tony’s grandson, AJ (Anthony) was named after his grandpa. He delighted Tony as only a 3 ½ year old child can.

Besides Tony’s parents, Tony was preceded in death by one sister, Margaret Treu.

A Celebration of Life will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Saturday, August 29, 2026 at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Ave., Wausau. Rev. Msgr. Joe Diermeier will lead the service. Visitation will be from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service all at the Stewart Avenue funeral home. Private burial will be at St. Mary’s Cemetery.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

Any memorials may be directed towards the Edgar/Marathon Circle of Joy.

“For every joy that passes, something beautiful remains.”

records.name: Warren M. Schmidt
records.summary: Warren M. Schmidt, age 89 passed away on Jul 2026.
records.body:
Warren M. Schmidt, born June 12, 1937, to Clarence and Erna (Oestreich) Schmidt and passed on to our Lord and Savior after a short courageous battle with cancer July, 2026.

Warren was confirmed in 1949 at Immanuel Lutheran Church. In 1955, he graduated from Merrill High School. He served active duty in the Wisconsin Army National Guard in 1961 during the Berlin Crisis. Warren married Marlene Balschun in 1962.

He began work at Northern Door Manufacturing in Merrill from 1956 until 1982. In 1982, Warren and Marlene moved to Roseburg, Oregon where he took a position as plant manager at Garage Door Manufacturing. In 1987, they moved back to the area and Warren returned to Northern Door Manufacturing. He worked there until the company closed. In 1991, he began working with Powder Tech Inc and retired from there in 2000. Warren was the caretaker of WOODLODGE in the Town of Birch from 1997 until 2026.

Survivors include his wife, Marlene Schmidt; brother, Ken (Carla) Schmidt; sister, Marie McCarty; and many nieces and nephews. Warren was preceded in death by his parents, Clarence and Erna Schmidt; his in-laws, Gus and Marie (Arseneau) Balschun; and Dale Fisher and Pat McCarty.

The memorial service for Warren will be held at 11:00 A.M., Monday, July 13, 2026, at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation – 301 Eagle Dr., Merrill. Rev. Scott Gustafson will officiate. V.F.W. Post 1638, Merrill, will provide military rites immediately after the service. Visitation will begin at 10:00 A.M. Monday at the funeral home.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

Revelation 3:11 “I am coming quickly; hold fast what you have, so that no one may rob you and deprive you of your crown.”

records.name: Carol J. Berry
records.summary: Carol J. Berry, age 85, of Marshfield passed away on Jul 5, 2026.
records.body:
Carol J. Berry, 85, Marshfield, passed away on Sunday, July 5, 2026 at home.

A visitation will be held from 5:00 - 8:00 pm on Tuesday, July 21, 2026 at Rembs Funeral Home, Marshfield. Interment will take place at McMillan Memorial Gardens Cemetery at a later date.

Carol was born on October 1, 1940 in Montevideo, Minnesota to Carl and Myrtle (Zielsdorf) Smith. She attended school in Lindstrom, Minnesota and then went on to serve in the United States Army from 1958-1962. She was united in marriage to Elmer Berry on June 8, 1961 in Heidelberg, Germany. He passed away on April 30, 2021.

Carol enjoyed gardening, needlepoint, crocheting, knitting, plastic canvas, taking care of the cats, Solitaire, and Soduko.

She is survived by her children, Marie (Todd) Cattanach of Chili, Ellen Berry and Dean Berry, both of Marshfield. She is also survived by a granddaughter, Shanna Berry and a great grandchildren, Aaron and Sophia Lynn Wiesner.

She is further survived by her brother, Harold (Darla) Smith of Spencer, Carl Smith Jr. of Osakis, MN, and Barbara Walsch of Colby.

She was preceded in death by her parents, husband, son Edward, sisters Shirley (Rose) Gose and Marilyn (Jim) Werner, sister in law Phyllis Smith, stepmother Konstancia Rekis-Smith, and stepbrother Ojars Rekis.

The family would like to extend a special thank you to Heartland Hospice, nurse Miranda, therapy dog Barkley and his handler John for their excellent care.

records.name: David E. Raymond
records.summary: David E. Raymond, age 88, of Weston passed away on Jul 5, 2026.
records.body:
David E. Raymond, 88, Weston passed away peacefully on Sunday, July 5, 2026 at his home.

He was born October 7, 1937 in Wausau, son of the late Harold and Angeline (Scutkowski) Raymond. On June 13, 1970 he married Connie Haight at Holy Name Catholic Church, Wausau. She survives.

For many years Dave was an electronic technician at AT&T. Some of his favorite pastimes included being outside, fishing, cutting wood and traveling. Dave had a passion for bear hunting and would be known to spend over a month in the bear woods alongside Connie.

Dave proudly served his country in the United States Air Force.

Survivors include his wife, Connie Raymond, Weston, one sister, Rosemary Wianecki, Eagle River and nieces and nephews.

At Dave’s request there will be no formal services. A private service will be held at Fellowship Baptist Church, Kronenwetter at a later date.

Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Wausau is in charge of arrangements. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Pamela "Pam" M. Leahy
records.summary: Pamela "Pam" M. Leahy, age 83, of Schofield passed away on Jul 5, 2026.
records.body:
On Sunday, July 5, 2026, Pamela Maureen Leahy, surrounded by compassionate care from Compassus Hospice, completed her earthly journey and was welcomed into the Kingdom of Heaven.

Pam was born on July 18, 1942, in Fresno, California. From the very beginning, her life was guided by a steadfast faith in God and a deep, unwavering love for her family.

A devoted and determined mother, Pam worked tirelessly to provide for her four sons. As a proud single parent, she often held three jobs at once. Working full-time at Foremost Dairy as a member of the Teamsters Union, waitressing, and selling Mary Kay cosmetics. Through hard work, sacrifice, and unconditional love, she always made sure her boys had everything they needed.

Later in life, Pam followed her family to Schofield, Wisconsin, where she enjoyed working for Krueger Floral. She found great joy in her grandchildren and treasured the time she spent with each of them. Family was always at the center of her life, and her love, guidance, and faith will continue to live on through the generations she helped shape.

Pam in lovingly survived by her sons, Martin Bottomley, Mitch Bottomley (Michelle Opal), Joe Bottomley, and Jared Bottomley; her grandchildren, Ryan Bottomley (Adam Lupton), Dana Thurston (Bud), Alexis Bottomley, Payton Trzcinski (Loukas), Blake Opal-Wahoske (Brett), Jason Wolfe, Jordan Monette, Morgan Bottomley (Joe Walkowiak), and Madison Bottomley; and her cherished great-granddaughters, Mara Thurston and Birdie Opal-Wahoske.

She is also survived by her sister, Jeryl Techman (John); her brother, Michael Leahy (Rhonda); and many beloved nieces, nephews, extended family members, and friends.

Pam was preceded in death by her soulmate, Donald Schendel; her parents, Maurice and Vivian Leahy; and her brother, Stanley Leahy.

Her family extends their heartfelt gratitude to the staff and caregivers of Mount View Care Center and Compassus Hospice for the kindness, dignity, and compassionate care they provided throughout her final years. Your dedication and generosity of spirit will never be forgotten.

A private family gathering will be held to celebrate Pam’s life.

Brainard Funeral Home & Cremation Center of Weston is honored to assist the family with arrangements. Online condolences may be shared through their website.

records.name: Patricia Lois Rogers
records.summary: Patricia Lois Rogers, age 82, of Marshfield passed away on Jul 5, 2026.
records.body:
Patricia L. Rogers, 82, of Marshfield passed away Sunday, July 5, 2026 at Northshore-Marshfield Health Services in the presence of her sons.

A Funeral will be held at 11:00am on Monday, July 13th at the Rembs Funeral Home with Reverend Mark Krueger officiating. Interment will follow in Hillside Cemetery next to her daughter Sheila. Relatives and friends are welcome to join the family for a time of visitation from 9am until time of service at the funeral home.

Patricia was born August 5, 1943 in Rockford, Illinois to Rudolph and Esther (Salmon) Bomback. She was a hardworking woman whose strength and determination were evident throughout her life. Never one to let obstacles stand in her way, she walked wherever she needed to go, never having a driver's license. She dedicated more than 18 years to Chips Diner, where her strong work ethic and friendly nature were appreciated by coworkers and customers alike. At home, she found joy tending to her garden, taking pride in the flowers and plants she lovingly cared for. Above all, she was a devoted mother and grandmother who cherished her family, always offering her love, support, and unwavering care.

Patricia is lovingly survived by her sons Craig, Kenneth, and Edwin (MarLonna Petruska) Rogers. She is further survived by grandchildren: Eric & James Rogers, Nadine Cottingham, Ashley Rose Klaver, Precious Stashek, Destinie (Erick) Laspa, Michael (Samantha) Rogers, Angela (Travis) Wilkinson, and Shaine Rogers; and her siblings: Judy Rogers and Roger Bomback.

She was preceded in death by her parents in 1977, her daughter Sheila at the age of 6 in 1972, and her brother Thomas Bomback in 2019.

records.name: Dennis L. Fairchild
records.summary: Dennis L. Fairchild, 85, passed away peacefully on June 29, 2026.
records.body:
Dennis L. Fairchild, 85, passed away peacefully on June 29, 2026, under the care of Compassus Hospice at the House of Dove Hospice House.

Dennis was born on January 22, 1941, on a farm in Iowa to the late John and Gladys (Hartung) Fairchild. His passion for chemistry, water quality, and sales eventually brought him to Wisconsin, where he built both his career and his family.

Water was more than Dennis's profession—it was his life's work. He believed deeply in the importance of clean, high-quality water for both people and animals. Throughout his career, he helped develop formulas that improved the quality of silage and water used by dairy farmers, contributing to healthier cattle and improved milk production. He took great pride in knowing his work made a meaningful difference for agricultural communities. Dennis ate, breathed, and lived water.

Dennis was a man of quiet strength. Though reserved by nature, he was generous with his time, knowledge, and support. He was intelligent, compassionate, and had a lifelong interest in politics and current events. Those who knew him looked to him as a steady source of wisdom and strength.

Above all else, Dennis treasured family. He loved not only his biological family but also the family he gained through his dear friend, Jean Burt. Jean and her family, especially Kim Meer, held a special place in his heart and enriched his life with their friendship, love, and support.

Dennis is lovingly survived by his children, David Fairchild of Colorado, Darcy (Phil) Flynn of Indiana, and Tonya (Randy) Jones of Kentucky; his many grandchildren and great-grandchildren; and his dear friend, Jean Burt, along with her family.

He was preceded in death by his son, Dennis Fairchild Jr.; his wife, Kay Fairchild; his parents, John and Gladys Fairchild; two brothers, Gordon and Gerald; and one sister, Nola Jean.

In accordance with Dennis's wishes, no services will be held at this time. His final resting place will be at Lone Tree Cemetery, Sioux Rapids, Iowa.

Family and friends are invited to visit MWCS.WS to share memories and words of remembrance.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family.

records.name: Dorothy E. Sorensen
records.summary: Dorothy E. Sorensen, 96, of Cedarburg, Wisconsin, died peacefully on June 21, 2026.
records.body:
Following a fulfilling and inspiring life, Dorothy Elizabeth (Thompson) Sorensen of Cedarburg, WI died peacefully on June 21, 2026. Dorothy (Dottie) was born on a dairy farm on June 15, 1930 to Arthur and Alma Thompson. Dorothy's husband Gordon and sister Mildred preceded Dorothy in death.

Dorothy is survived by children; Heidi (Don) Nelson, N. Peter (Mary Jo) and Mark; and her granddaughters Laura Nelson (Greg) Furdek and Sarah Nelson (Dan) Goeckermann; and four great grandchildren; Jack and Annika Furdek, and Jay and Ross Goeckermann. Dorothy's loving family was the light of her life.

Dorothy, with her husband Gordon, served United Methodist Churches in Richmond, Lowell/Juneau, Beloit, Appleton, Beaver Dam, Wausau, and Elm Grove, Wisconsin. She influenced many students as a caring educator after her Bachelor and Master's degrees in education from UW-Stevens Point. A true lifelong learner she traveled to 20 countries.

A celebration of life will be held at Pilgrim United Church of Christ, 1621 2nd Ave., Grafton, WI on July 9 at 5:00 PM. A visitation with the family will be held prior to the service beginning at 3:30 PM. The service can be viewed at https://www.youtube.com/@pilgrimuccgraftonwi346. Following Dorothy's wishes, her body has been donated to the Medical College of Wisconsin in support of medical education and research. In lieu of flowers or memorial gifts the family suggests donations to historic restoration at her childhood church in Curtiss, WI, 54422 St Paul's Old Norwegian Lutheran Church or online to the Dorothy Sorensen Memorial Fund at the UW Extension Milwaukee County Urban Agriculture Program (Dorothy.Sorensen.my.canva.site).

records.name: Jason D. Beall
records.summary: Jason D. Beall, 43, of Ontonagon, Michigan, passed away unexpectedly on July 1, 2026.
records.body:
Jason D. Beall, 43, passed away unexpectedly on July 1, 2026, after suffering a sudden medical event while working in the yard at his second home in Ontonagon, Michigan.

Jason was born on January 5, 1983, in Germany while his father was serving in the United States Civil Service. From an early age, he developed the adventurous spirit, determination, and compassion that would define his life.

At just 17 years old, Jason met the love of his life, Danielle Teetzen. He married the girl who captured his heart at their first jobs, and together they spent the last 26 years building a life filled with unwavering love, countless adventures, laughter, and what seemed like a new hobby every week. Together they created a home centered on family, friendship, and making the most out of every day.

Jason proudly served his country in the United States Marine Corps. Following his military service, he earned his Bachelor's Degree in Psychology while building his career as a Tool Specialist with Viking Electric. To Jason, Viking Electric was far more than a workplace—it was a second family, and he cherished the friendships and camaraderie he found there.

Jason had an enormous heart and never hesitated to help someone in need. Whether it was stopping to assist a stranded motorist, standing up for someone who needed an advocate, or lending a hand to a friend or stranger, Jason believed in doing the right thing without expecting anything in return. His generosity extended even further when he twice grew out his signature thick, curly red hair to donate to Wigs for Kids—first donating 19 inches, then later another 16 inches—to help children in need.

Known affectionately as "Red," Jason was blessed with an incredible circle of friends who loved him for his loyalty, authenticity, positivity, and larger-than-life personality. He wasn't one to take any guff, and he always stood up for what he believed was right.

Jason was a true hobby connoisseur. If something caught his interest, he dove into it wholeheartedly. Over the years he explored rock climbing, 3D printing, leatherworking, photography, woodworking, and countless other pursuits. He loved collecting rocks, playing disc golf, gathering around a table for games of Magic: The Gathering, hiking, practicing jiu-jitsu, riding adventure motorcycles on winding trails, and spending time near the water. His taste in music was as diverse as his interests, and he found joy in discovering something new wherever life took him.

Above all else, Jason's greatest pride and greatest joy was his family. He adored his nieces and nephews, was incredibly proud of his son, Jacob, and treasured the beautiful life he and Danielle built together. Danielle and Jacob will forever remember his huge heart, his unwavering love, his willingness to help anyone, and how hard he worked to make life better for those around him.

Jason is survived by his beloved wife, Danielle Beall; his son, Jacob; bonus children, Trinity and Bowden; his grandmother, Martha; his stepfather, David Triggs; his father-in-law, Alan Teetzen; his aunt and uncle, Corky and Debbie; his brothers and sister, Patrick (Keturah), Samantha, and Mandy (Kegan); along with many cherished nieces, nephews, extended family members, and dear friends.

He was preceded in death by his parents, David Beall and Caroline Triggs; his sister, Jenny; and his mother-in-law, Patricia Teetzen.

Family and friends are invited to gather for visitation from 3:00 p.m. until 6:00 p.m. on Wednesday, July 15, 2026, at John J. Buettgen Funeral Home, 948 Grand Avenue, Schofield. Military Honors will be conducted at 6:00 p.m. at the funeral home.

Online condolences and words of remembrance may be shared at www.honorone.com.

Though his time with us was far too short, Jason leaves behind a legacy of service, kindness, courage, generosity, and adventure. He lived fully, loved deeply, and never missed an opportunity to help someone else. His memory will live on in every trail explored, every act of kindness extended to a stranger, every new hobby embraced with enthusiasm, and in the hearts of all who were fortunate enough to know and love him.

records.name: Mari J. Olive
records.summary: Mari J. Olive, 63, of Minneapolis, Minnesota, passed away unexpectedly on June 24, 2026.
records.body:
Mari Jane Olive passed away unexpectedly in Minneapolis, MN on June 24, 2026. She was a huge Green Bay Packer fan and proudly displayed her affection for the team by her outside decorations and jerseys she'd wear on game day. If someone else in the Twin Cities was wearing anything Packer related, Mari would be the first one to greet them with a huge smile on her face.

Mari was born August 30, 1962, in Wausau, WI to the late Richard and Patricia Jesse, who she loved to be called "daddy's girl". Mari went on to graduate from Newman High School in Wausau WI in 1980.

While working at BNOX Jewelry Studio in Pepin WI she met the love of her life, Andy Olive, and they were married on Sept. 12, 2009, at St. Clement Catholic Church, he survives. She is also survived by her son, Andrew, who she loved dearly with all her being.

Mari had a soft spot for her furry babies, treating each and every one like a member of the family; never missing their birthdays or the anniversary of their passing.

Mari will always be remembered for singing along to her favorite songs and yelling at the TV when Packers were playing…sometimes she would lose her voice doing both! We'll miss Mari every day and her memory will live on in the hearts of all those who loved her.

Mari was preceded in death by her parents Richard and Patricia Jesse, and is survived by her loving husband, Andy Olive, son Andrew Koehler, sister Julie (Dale d.) Bouvat, Laura (Don) Reinhard, Kathy Reinhard, Rich (Michelle) Jesse and many nieces, nephews, aunt, cousins, her Olive brothers- and sisters-in-law and their families, and all her friends.

Washburn-McReavy Cremation is in charge of arrangements. The funeral Mass will be at 11:00 am Saturday July 11 at St. Helena's Catholic Church in Minneapolis, MN with a visitation at 10:00 AM.

records.name: Maybelle E. Woiak
records.summary: Maybelle E. Woiak, age 88 passed away on Jul 6, 2026.
records.body:
Maybelle E. Woiak, 88, went on to be with her Lord on Monday, July 6, 2026.

A memorial service will be held at 11:00 am on Saturday, July 25, 2026 at Rembs Funeral Home, Marshfield with visitation from 10:00 am until the time of service. Pastor Todd Trapani will officiate. Burial will take place in City Point Cemetery at a later date.

Maybelle was born on July 24, 1937 in Springfield, Illinois to John and Verna (Eddy) Jones. She was united in marriage to Clarence S. Woiak on November 7, 1959 at St. Joachim Catholic Church in Pittsville. He passed away on April 20, 2019.

Maybelle worked as a bookkeeper for several years and was also known as the Avon Lady in Nekoosa. She enjoyed playing Scrabble, creating and sending out homemade cards, cooking and baking for family, Sudoku, camping, and feeding birds and deer. Most of all, she loved spending time with her family and always opened up her home to her childrens’ friends and was “Mom” to many.

She is survived by her children, Gregory (Mary) Woiak of Red Granite, James Woiak of Pittsville, Shelley Sweney of Superior, Andrew (Carmen) Woiak of Athens and April (Jeffrey) Peckham of Eau Claire. She is also survived by 16 grandchildren and 22 great grandchildren.

She is further survived by her sister, Johna Mae Kozemczak of Houston, MO.

She was preceded in death by her parents, husband, and son, David Lee Jones.

The family would like to extend a special thanks to Heartland Hospice, April and Jeff Peckham and family, Shelley, and Maybelle’s sister Johna for the compassionate and loving care they gave to our Mom.

records.name: Patricia J. Bruyette
records.summary: Patricia J. Bruyette, 90, passed away peacefully at home on June 30, 2026.
records.body:
Patricia "Patsy" Josephine (Miller) Bruyette, age 90, passed away peacefully at home on June 30, 2026, surrounded by her large and loving family.

Born in Rhinelander on April 14, 1936, to Lawrence and Genevieve (Bliss) Miller, Patricia was raised on the family farm off Lassig Road. Her youth was shaped by those she loved; inheriting her father's physical strength, while her mother gifted her with wisdom. She was "schooled" by her beloved grandmother, Addie, who taught her the skills of womanhood, including sewing, knitting, crocheting, and tatting. Her life on the farm included milking cows early each morning before heading to school. She was involved with the riding stable/club and enjoyed riding her own horses.

She met Leonard "Louie" Bruyette in high school and was perplexed that a handsome athlete like Louie would pursue her. She graduated in the Top 10 of her class from Rhinelander Union High School in 1954. On July 31, 1954, wearing a wedding dress she and her grandma made together, Patricia and Leonard were wed at St. Mary's Church in Rhinelander. They danced to polkas and waltzes at their reception on the family farm and continued to dance together throughout their marriage. They honeymooned traveling around Lake Superior.

Patricia stayed home to raise eight children, using her talents to make clothing, create Halloween costumes, decorate the house for every holiday, bake from-scratch birthday cakes, crochet dolls and Afghans. She managed the bookkeeping for the family excavating business. She taught herself to play guitar and was known for singing to all her grandkids or breaking into a song that fit every situation.

Patricia took pride in her beautifully mowed lawn, flower gardens, trees, pond, and a bountiful vegetable garden. Together, the family made maple syrup. She and Len spent time snowmobiling, playing horseshoes, and traveling by car across the United States. She was also an avid pool player, played softball, and rode horseback at age 80.

Her greatest joy came with the arrival of her grandchildren. For them, she enhanced her beautiful backyard to create endless fun with a playhouse, trampoline, and swing set to her grandkids, nieces, and nephews, "Grandma" or "Aunt Pat" wasn't just a person—she was everyone's version of "home," offering love, security, and acceptance. From jukebox music in the basement to playing pool, board games, and crafts, her home will always be remembered as a fun place to be. She "adopted" anyone who needed a parent or grandparent. She attended every sporting event and school program for every grandkid, saved newspaper clippings, provided rides, and endlessly babysat. As they grew older, she loved family game nights with them, especially playing Password.

In her downtime, she was a passionate genealogist, pursuing details of generations past. She visited libraries and courthouses to unearth records, eventually tracing ancestors back to their arrival at Ellis Island, and neatly organized numerous letters, postcards, and photos into dozens of family albums she was deeply proud of.

Patricia is survived by her children: Roxane, Rachel, Pauline, Audrey (Scott), Jolene (Kevin), Lance, and Christopher (Jean); daughter-in-law Julie; son-in-law Rob; sister Bliss (Dick); and sister-in-law Kathy. She is the cherished grandmother of Patrick, Sierra, Justin, Savannah, Nathan, Jonny, Kalon, Jade, Aaron, Kianna, Samantha, Lance, Addison, Mitchell, Shannon, Cortney, Phillip, Mariah, Makala; along with Gage, Ernest, and Celeste, whom she adopted in her heart. She leaves behind great-grandchildren: Bryce, Sean, Jayden, Vera, Marigold, Dawson, Skyler, Madison, Kaeden, Konner, Karsen, Lillie, and Lauren; and great-great-grandchild, Evelyn.

She leaves behind several special friends, including Ray, Charlotte, Kathy, Mike. Each of them touched her life and she treasured their friendship. Charlotte, a Native American, is Nishiime–"sister" to Patricia, and gave her an Ojibwe name, "Ozhawaashko" which means Blue Sky Woman. Giizhigokwe. She has all the spirits of the sky…the winds, the sun, rain, water, snow that make plants thrive.

She was preceded in death by her husband, her parents, her brother, David; and her son, Lawrence. The first person she wanted to see at the gates of Heaven was her son, Lawrence.

A celebration of life will be held August 9, 2:00–6:00 PM as an open house/yard/pond visitation at the Bruyette home, 5877 Hwy 8. Please contact Jolene Kopplin or any family member if you would like to help. In her honor, we will share plants from her property. Visitors are asked to bring a container and a small spade or shovel. In lieu of gifts, please plant a flower or tree in her memory in your favorite place and think of her when you visit. Please post photos of your plantings on her Facebook page.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting with the cremation. Family and friends may go to MWCS.WS to leave online condolences and words of remembrance.

records.name: Sally Mae Matson-Herrmann
records.summary: Sally Mae Matson-Herrmann, 89, of Tigerton, passed away peacefully on July 2, 2026.
records.body:
Sally Mae Matson-Herrmann, 89, of Tigerton, passed away peacefully on Thursday, July 2, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

Sally was born on August 2, 1936, in Tigerton, the daughter of Tom and Ada (Omitt) Johnson. Following the death of her father, she was lovingly raised by Edward Polley, who became a devoted father figure in her life.

On October 30, 1954, Sally was united in marriage to John "Kenny" Matson Sr. in Tigerton. They shared more than 53 years of marriage before Kenny preceded her in death on January 12, 2008. Sally was blessed to find love again and married Bruce Herrmann on September 12, 2009, in Wittenberg. Bruce preceded her in death on March 9, 2018.

Sally grew up in the Town of Germania and cherished her country life on the farm near her childhood home. She had a passion for rummage sales and was always on the lookout for hidden treasures. Sally became well known for hosting one of the area's best annual rummage sales, where she especially enjoyed visiting with customers and friends. She also loved browsing secondhand and thrift stores.

Some of Sally's happiest moments were spent relaxing on her deck, taking in the peaceful surroundings. She delighted in watching the birds, tending to her flower, herb, and vegetable gardens, and enjoying the playful antics of her chickens, geese, cats, and horses.

Sally looked forward to lunch dates with friends and treasured every opportunity to spend time with her family. Her children, grandchildren, and great-grandchildren brought her immeasurable joy and filled her heart with love.

Sally is lovingly survived by her children, Barbara (David Yonker) Matson, John "Kippy" Matson Jr., and Laurie Matson; her grandchildren, Becky (Brandon) Merckes and Chris Matson; and her great-grandchildren, Nicolas and Parker Merckes.

She was preceded in death by her husbands, John "Kenny" Matson Sr. and Bruce Herrmann; her parents, Tom Johnson and Edward and Ada Polley; and her siblings, Edward "Hardrock" Johnson, Pam Polley-Dankmeyer, Roger Polley, and Sharon Dedolph.

A funeral service for Sally will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Tuesday, July 14, 2026, at St. John Lutheran Church in Tigerton, with Pastor Dean Suehring officiating. Visitation will be held at the church on Tuesday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of service.

Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home of Wittenberg is assisting the family with funeral arrangements. Memories and messages of support may be shared at www.schmidtschulta.com.

The family would like to thank the Peritoneal Team at Aspirus Wausau Kidney Care for all of the wonderful care they provided.

records.name: Stanley G. Szymanski
records.summary: Stanley G. Szymanski, 83, passed away on July 1, 2026.
records.body:
Stanley "Stan" G. Szymanski passed away surrounded by the love of his family on July 1, 2026. He was born on May 23, 1943, to the late Stanley A. and Elizabeth (Knetter) Szymanski.

Stan married Angela Burger in 1964, and together they built a life on the family farm, raising their children while tending the fields and caring for the animals. Angela preceded him in death in 2014. In 2015, Stan found love again and married Sharon Stern. Together, they shared many happy years traveling and enjoying their love of polka dancing.

Stan was a retired dairy farmer whose passion for farming never faded. He loved caring for the dairy herd and especially enjoyed spending time out on the tractor. Farming was more than his livelihood—it was his way of life.

Away from the farm, Stan embraced life with enthusiasm. He loved polka dancing, traveling worldwide, writing poetry, and playing cards, especially a good game of sheephead. He also enjoyed watching sports on television and was an avid Milwaukee Brewers fan.

Stan never met a stranger. He was a social man who loved striking up conversations with anyone and everyone. His children fondly remember many occasions when they had to wait patiently in the car because Dad had stopped to chat with someone he knew—or had just met. His warm personality and genuine interest in others left lasting impressions on countless people.

Dedicated to both agriculture and his community, Stan was actively involved in many organizations throughout his life. Among them were the Gladiola Society, Holstein Association, Wisconsin Rural Leadership, Wausau Jaycees, Master Farmers, and the Agriculture Society, to name just a few. His commitment to serving others reflected the same work ethic and dedication he brought to his family and his farm.

Above all else, Stan was a devoted husband, father, grandfather, great-grandfather, brother, and friend. He was a hardworking family man whose love, generosity, and kindness will be deeply missed by all who knew and loved him.

He is survived by his beloved wife, Sharon; his children, Sheila (Ted Rude) Szymanski, Jeff (Anna) Szymanski, Christopher (Ana) Szymanski, Gail (Danny) Wilde, and Tracy (Rod) Kaiser; his stepchildren, Craig Stern, Shelly (Cory) Martin, and Ross (Dannette) Stern; 22 grandchildren: Tom (Shauna) Szymanski, Amanda (Jesse) Fischer, Nicole (Joe) Westberg, Tyler (Alexis) Szymanski, Isaiah Genrich, Xavier Genrich, Nicholas Szymanski, Johnathon Szymanski, Lilyan Szymanski, Violet Szymanski, Johnathan Rodriguez, Clarissa Rodriguez, Deanna Cotty, Erika Wilde, Carmen Wilde, Shelby Wilde, Kaitlyn Kaiser, Marissa Kaiser, Alex Kaiser, Seth Martin, Ashley Martin, and Madison Stern; and 10 great-grandchildren.

He is further survived by his siblings, Jacki (Terry) Pyan and Ted Szymanski; his brothers-in-law, Kenneth Lepak, Paul (Sandy) Burger, Steve (Jane) Burger, Joe Burger, and Peter Burger; his sisters-in-law, Linda Szymanski, Carolyn Martino, Emily Noll, Pauline (Alan) Babler, Elaine Schlender and Pam Oswald; as well as many nieces, nephews, cousins, extended family members, and friends.

In addition to his parents, he was preceded in death by his sisters, Sandra Lepak, and Patricia (Joe) Oswald; brother Terry Szymanski; brother-in-law, George Burger; and sisters-in-law, Margie Nagdeman and Barbara Olander.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Thursday, July 9, 2026, at Sacred Heart Catholic Church, 222761 County Road S, Marathon City, WI 54448. Visitation will be held at the church from 9:00 a.m. until the time of Mass. Burial will follow in the parish cemetery.

John J. Buettgen Funeral Home is assisting the family. Family and friends may visit www.honorone.com to share online condolences and words of remembrance.

records.name: Thomas J. Forst
records.summary: Thomas J. Forst, age 76, of Hewitt passed away on Jul 6, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas J. Forst, 76, Hewitt, passed away on Monday, July 6, 2026 at House of the Dove, Marshfield.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 12:00 pm on Friday, July 10, 2026 at St. Michael’s Catholic Church, Hewitt with visitation from 9:00 am until the time of service. Father Angi will officiate. A livestream of the service will be available on Tom’s obituary page at the time of service. Burial will follow in the parish cemetery. Rembs Funeral Home is assisting the family.

Tom was born on May 15, 1950 in Marshfield to Stephen and Mary (Weissenbeck) Forst. He was united to Georgia Kath on April 27, 1974 at St. Phillip’s Catholic Church in Rudolph. She passed away on June 15, 2006.

Tom lived life to the fullest and raised his family in the small town of Hewitt, Wisconsin. He worked as a truck driver for Northern Auto Parts/Carquest for 40 years. He was also a volunteer firefighter for the Hewitt Fire Department. After retiring, he continued to operate a small beef farm with his cousin Gary Bredl. Tom enjoyed working on the farm and was very proud of his cows, his dog Smokey, his equipment and machinery, and his ability to fix just about anything.

People often turned to him for advice, and he never hesitated to help someone who needed a hand. Tom recently took up baking pies, which he enjoyed sharing with family and friends. Above all, Tom loved his family. His grandchildren were his pride and joy, and he treasured every opportunity and moment he could share with them.

He is survived by his children, Matthew Forst of Hewitt, Charity (John) Schug of Spencer, Jason Forst of Spencer, and Brent Forst of Hewitt, six grandchildren, Brandon, Terrall, and Colton Schug, and Emmalee, Cooper and Olivia Forst, and great granddaughter, Lillian Schug.

He is further survived by his siblings, Richard (Sue) Forst of Stratford, Joan (Mark) Elfe of Spencer, David Forst of Marshfield, Mike (Judy) Forst of Marshfield, Robert (Deb) Forst of Marshfield and Judy (Brian) Caldwell of Marshfield.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Stephen and Mary, the love of his life Georgia, and his beloved dog, Buddy.

The family would like to extend a special thanks to the House of the Dove for their excellent care.

records.name: Edward "Eddie" W. Ellefson
records.summary: Edward "Eddie" W. Ellefson, age 27 passed away on Jul 7, 2026.
records.body:
Edward “Eddie” Winthrop Ellefson

August 23, 1998 – July 7, 2026

Edward Winthrop Ellefson went home to be with the Lord on July 7, 2026, at the age of 27.

Eddie was a unique, complicated, passionate spark in the lives of everyone who knew him. He was kind-hearted and loving, creative and funny, witty and intelligent, a deep thinker with a talent and adventurous spirit all his own.

He loved music above almost anything. A gifted musician, Eddie taught himself to play a wide range of instruments, and he was precise and particular about the music he made, always chasing the sound he heard in his head. He was easy and warm in conversation, equally at home talking with someone twice or half his age. He was a visionary who saw things differently than most, and he left his mark on the people around him.

But his mind was never quiet. Eddie struggled with the debilitating grip of addiction on his quality of life, a battle that shaped so much of his story. His family is devastated by this loss. They prayed he would accept the help that was offered him, so he could find peace here on earth. That peace wasn’t meant to be found in this life. He is now in the arms of Jesus.

Eddie is survived by his loving parents, Meg (Meyer) and John Ellefson, and his brother Davy, along with aunts, uncles, cousins, and many friends and acquaintances who loved him.

He was preceded in death by his beloved grandparents, John “Grandpa Jack” and Mary Meyer, and Richard and Barbara Ellefson; his great uncle Bill Field; and dear friends lost too soon.

Eddie was deeply loved by his mom, dad, and brother Davy, and by many friends and acquaintances whose lives he touched.

Memorial service will be 11:30 a.m. Saturday, July 18, 2026 at River City Church, Wausau. Visitation will be Saturday from 10:00 a.m. until the time of service all at River City Church.  We invite all family and close friends to please attend.

Please dress in something bright and happy to honor Eddie’s unique style.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Woodrow Gerrow
records.summary: Woodrow Gerrow, age 80, of Kronenwetter passed away on July 7, 2026.
records.body:
Woodrow James Gerrow, "Woody", age 80, of Kronenwetter, Wisconsin, completed his journey to Heaven on July 7, 2026, surrounded by his loving family.

Woody was born on November 9, 1945, in Wausau, Wisconsin, the son of Emory & Gertrude (Stachura) Gerrow. After high school he went onto obtaining an associate degree from NTC. He married the love of his life, Janet Woehlert, on June 24, 1967, and the couple just celebrated 59 years together.

He carried his hard work ethic into a long and fulfilling career. Woody dedicated 36 and a half years to Weyerhauser, and after a remarkable tenure, began a second chapter with 20 plus years at J&D Tube Benders. Woody also worked as a bartender at both the Kelly Club and Angelo’s Pizza on Ross Avenue. He relished the camaraderie and stories that came with these roles, endearing himself to patrons and colleagues alike.

Beyond his professional accomplishments, it was Woody’s personal warmth and infectious zest for life that defined him. He was widely known for his big smile, big hugs, and laughter that lifted the spirits of those around him. His patience and generosity were evident to all who encountered him, always willing to lend a hand and offer encouragement. Family was his pride and joy; the unconditional love he gave was the heartbeat of many cherished memories. Woody found delight in life’s simple pleasures: he enjoyed the calm of sunrises, the morning songs of birds, and the hopeful promise each new day brought.

His love for sports was ever present. He played, coached, and watched countless games of softball, and relished time spent on the golf course or playing racquetball. Whether mentoring a teammate or cheering from the sidelines, his enthusiasm was contagious, and his encouragement was unwavering. Woody also enjoyed shooting pool and watching NASCAR.

For many years, Woody delighted family and friends by donning the red suit as Santa Claus, bringing joy and laughter to all ages. His love was truly unconditional, his outlook positive and upbeat. In every chapter of his life, he embraced each day with gusto and gratitude.

Those who knew Woody would agree he was an amazing human, loved by so many. His legacy lives in the stories shared, the values he instilled, and the countless ways he brightened the lives around him.

He is survived by his wife, Janet; children, Kurt (Kelly) Gerrow, Kim (Dean) Waldvogel and Kelly (Sue Heil) Gerrow. Woody is also survived by his grandchildren, Aaron (Randi) Felch, Kori (John) Gerrow, Adam Gerrow, Kaitlin (Steve) Heil, Jaden (Kate) Heil; great-grandchildren, Madalyn, William, Isabelle and Rory; his sisters, Diane (Pat) Sippl and Nancy (Ron) Smith; and many nieces, nephews, other relatives and many friends. He is preceded in death by his parents.

A funeral service for Woody will be held on Saturday, July 18, 2026, at 12:00 PM at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston. Pastor Lance Hoelscher will officiate. Visitation will be held from 9:00 AM until the time of service at Ascend. Woody will be laid to rest at Restlawn Memorial Park at a later date.

The song "What a Wonderful World" by Louis Armstrong now has a new meaning, for all of those who loved and knew Woody.

The family would like to give a special Thank You to the staff at Aspirus ICU for all of the special care given to Woody in his final days.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Allan Wayne Johnson
records.funeral_home: Brainard Funeral Home & Cremation Center of Wausau
records.summary: Allan Wayne Johnson, age 74, of Mosinee, passed away on July 4, 2026.
records.body:
Allan Wayne Johnson, age 74, passed away on Saturday, July 4, 2026, at his home in Mosinee, with his loving wife by his side and under the care of Compassus Hospice. He was born on December 7, 1951, to the late Robert and Ila (McCumber) Johnson in Madison, WI. Al attended and graduated from high school and went on to earn his bachelor's degree in broadcasting. He met the love of his life, Nancy Louise Ranum, and the two were united in marriage on February 3, 1973 in Mount Vernon, WI. She survives.

Al was an installer for various sound systems, and enjoyed working on computers and electronics. He was very good at solving issues with them and helped everyone he could. He had an incredible smile and was very friendly and outgoing. He made friends wherever he went. Al's passion was broadcasting for local radio stations, which he did for many years. He enjoyed fishing, riding his bicycle, often times, to and from church, and was dedicated to his church, Fellowship Baptist Church, where he assisted with wiring and some of their sound system needs.
