Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation Services is assisting with the arrangements. Online condolences are encouraged and may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com.

records.name: Thomas J. Knetter
records.summary: Thomas J. Knetter, 76, passed away on July 2, 2026.
records.body:
Thomas J. Knetter passed away on July 2nd, 2026, at the age of 76, leaving behind a legacy of service, hard work, and independence.

He was born on December 8, 1949, in Wausau, son of Alex and Alice Knetter.

Tom spent most of his life in Rib Mountain, Wisconsin, before later moving to Schofield. A graduate of Newman Catholic High School, he was known for his strong work ethic, resilience, and straightforward nature.

A proud Vietnam Veteran, Tom served in the United States Marine Corps, attaining the rank of Corporal. His military service was a defining chapter of his life, and he remained deeply proud of his country and his fellow veterans. As a founding member of the Man of Honor Society, he found meaning in recognizing and supporting those who served. One tribute that reflects the values he held dear reads:

"You have served so that others may live. You are a true American Man-of-Honor. The people of the United States are grateful."

Following his military service, Tom built a career as an independent over-the-road truck driver, spending years navigating highways across the country. He later worked for the Town of Rib Mountain, serving the community he called home for much of his life, before continuing his career with B&K Trucking. He embraced the freedom and challenges that came with life on the road. Among the memories he shared with his family were the occasions when he took his son, Jason, along with him on trucking trips. Though those opportunities were not frequent, they became cherished memories that Jason would carry with him throughout his life.

Tom had a special love for dogs and found great companionship in them over the years, especially his beloved Raven.

Tom is survived by his son, Jason Knetter (Janet Curin); his sisters, Sandy Thompson and Pam (Roger) Norton; his grandsons, Kole (Brandy) Knetter and Karson (Melissa) Knetter; and his great-grandchildren, Mikah, Miley, and Klara. He also leaves behind friends, fellow veterans, and others whose lives crossed his over the years.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Alex and Alice Knetter; and his brother, Terrance "Terry" Knetter.

Tom will be remembered for his service to his country, his years spent on the road, his dedication to his work, and the independent spirit that guided him throughout his life. His family and friends will remember him in their own ways and cherish the moments they shared with him.

Semper Fidelis — Always Faithful.

records.name: Todd Lewis Brown
records.summary: Todd Lewis Brown, age 69 passed away on July 10, 2026.
records.body:
Todd Lewis Brown, age 69, passed away unexpectedly on July 10, 2026.

Todd was born on June 26, 1957 in Tigerton, WI son of Lewis and Betty (Wesener) Brown. He graduated from Wittenberg-Birnamwood High School in 1975. In 1977 he earned a degree in Electronics from North Central Technical College in Wausau.

Todd started working at the Waupaca Foundry in 1979 doing electrical maintenance. He continued his education, while working, to become an Electrical Engineer. His expertise added to the Foundry’s ability to expand their company across several states. After 35 years Todd retired.

Todd married Connie (Spieth) on April 26, 1980 in Tigerton, WI and together they had three children. Todd and Connie moved to Waupaca in 1985 where they raised their family.

Todd had a wide range of skills and could fix virtually anything. He was incredibly detail-oriented and took great pride in doing things the right way. He loved the challenge of rebuilding, repairing or refurbishing equipment. His expertise and knowledge of electrical was well known. From 1985 to present day, Todd worked as the electrician for several carnival companies where he would often travel with his family to county fairs to fix various rides.

Todd’s true passion ignited in the mid 80’s while automating privately owned hydroelectric dams enabling the owners to remotely manage water flow and turbine generation without continuous human intervention. Todd and Connie enjoyed traveling across multiple states in their motorhome while Todd worked those projects.

Todd played ball all his life, only stopping because work got in the way. His love of the game was passed down to his children and now carried on with his grandboys. He often told his grandboys, “I hit home runs so I didn’t have to run the bases.” He enjoyed watching his grandboys play sports any chance he got.

Todd will be greatly missed by his family and friends both local and around the country.

Todd is survived by his wife of 47 years, Connie, his children Derek (Sarah) Brown of Waupaca, WI, Melanie (Ryan) Gilles of Stevens Point, WI and Ryan (Christan) Brown of LaCresent, MN, and seven grandboys Keenan, Kelson & Kollin Gilles, Chapman & Channing Brown, Bronson & Dekker Brown, his brother Chris Brown, sister Sharla (John) Brown and several brother & sister in laws, nieces & nephews and extended family.

Todd is preceded in death by his parents, Lewis and Betty Brown, his in-laws Walter and Irene Spieth along with many of his aunts, uncles and cousins.

Visitation will be held Friday, July 17th from 4p-8p and Saturday, July 18th from 9a until the funeral service at 11a at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home in Wittenberg, WI. Burial will follow in the Evergreen Rest Cemetery in Elderon, WI

Memories and messages of support may be shared at schmidtschulta.com.

records.name: William Clark
records.summary: William "Bill" Clark, born January 15, 1934, passed away surrounded by the love of his family on July 8, 2026.
records.body:
William "Bill" Clark, born on January 15, 1934, to the late Thomas and Frances (Pryga) Clark, passed away surrounded by the love of his family on July 8, 2026.

Bill married the love of his life, Phyllis Krueger, and together they shared 57 wonderful years of marriage before Phyllis' passing in 2016. They were truly the perfect pair. Phyllis was the one who took charge, while Bill was content to go with the flow—a balance that served them well throughout their life together.

A quiet, easygoing man, Bill found joy in life's simple pleasures. He enjoyed ice fishing, hunting, gardening, playing cards, going out to eat, and, above all, spending time with his family and friends. His gentle nature and steady presence will be fondly remembered by all who knew him.

Bill worked for many years at the paper mill in Mosinee, Wisconsin, and proudly served his country in the United States Army, followed by the National Guard.

He will be dearly missed by his daughters, Betty (Mark) Martino and Judy (Dennis) Tiry; and his beloved granddaughter, Melissa (Andrew) Bella.

Bill was preceded in death by his parents, Thomas and Frances Clark; his beloved wife, Phyllis; and his daughter, Eydie Clark.

Private graveside services will be held at Gate of Heaven Cemetery, with military honors conducted by Montgomery-Plant-Dudley American Legion Post 10.

The family extends their heartfelt gratitude to the staff of Mount View and Aspirus Comfort Care & Hospice Services for the exceptional care, kindness, and compassion they provided to Bill.

records.name: Woodrow J. Gerrow
records.summary: Woodrow James Gerrow, "Woody", age 80, of Kronenwetter, Wisconsin, completed his journey to Heaven on July 7, 2026.
records.body:
Woodrow James Gerrow, "Woody", age 80, of Kronenwetter, Wisconsin, completed his journey to Heaven on July 7, 2026, surrounded by his loving family.

Woody was born on November 9, 1945, in Wausau, Wisconsin, the son of Emory & Gertrude (Stachura) Gerrow. After high school he went onto obtaining an associate degree from NTC. He married the love of his life, Janet Woehlert, on June 24, 1967, and the couple just celebrated 59 years together.

He carried his hard work ethic into a long and fulfilling career. Woody dedicated 36 and a half years to Weyerhauser, and after a remarkable tenure, began a second chapter with 20 plus years at J&D Tube Benders. Woody also worked as a bartender at both the Kelly Club and Angelo's Pizza on Ross Avenue. He relished the camaraderie and stories that came with these roles, endearing himself to patrons and colleagues alike.

Beyond his professional accomplishments, it was Woody's personal warmth and infectious zest for life that defined him. He was widely known for his big smile, big hugs, and laughter that lifted the spirits of those around him. His patience and generosity were evident to all who encountered him, always willing to lend a hand and offer encouragement. Family was his pride and joy; the unconditional love he gave was the heartbeat of many cherished memories. Woody found delight in life's simple pleasures: he enjoyed the calm of sunrises, the morning songs of birds, and the hopeful promise each new day brought.

His love for sports was ever present. He played, coached, and watched countless games of softball, and relished time spent on the golf course or playing racquetball. Whether mentoring a teammate or cheering from the sidelines, his enthusiasm was contagious, and his encouragement was unwavering. Woody also enjoyed shooting pool and watching NASCAR.

For many years, Woody delighted family and friends by donning the red suit as Santa Claus, bringing joy and laughter to all ages. His love was truly unconditional, his outlook positive and upbeat. In every chapter of his life, he embraced each day with gusto and gratitude.

Those who knew Woody would agree he was an amazing human, loved by so many. His legacy lives in the stories shared, the values he instilled, and the countless ways he brightened the lives around him.

He is survived by his wife, Janet; children, Kurt (Kelly) Gerrow, Kim (Dean) Waldvogel and Kelly (Sue Heil) Gerrow. Woody is also survived by his grandchildren, Aaron (Randi) Felch, Kori (John) Gerrow, Adam Gerrow, Kaitlin (Steve) Heil, Jaden (Kate) Heil; great-grandchildren, Madalyn, Wiliam, Isabelle and Rory; his sisters, Diane (Pat) Sippl and Nancy (Ron) Smith; and many nieces, nephews, other relatives and many friends. He is preceded in death by his parents.

A funeral service for Woody will be held on Saturday, July 18, 2026, at 12:00 PM at Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care, 2809 Jelinek Avenue, Weston. Pastor Lance Hoelscher will officiate. Visitation will be held from 9:00 AM until the time of service at Ascend. Woody will be laid to rest at Restlawn Memorial Park at a later date.

The song "What a Wonderful World" by Louis Armstrong now has a new meaning, for all of those who loved and knew Woody.

The family would like to give a special Thank You to the staff at Aspirus ICU for all of the special care given to Woody in his final days.

records.name: Daniel Jakubowski
records.summary: Daniel Jakubowski, age 74, of Weston passed away on July 11, 2026.
records.body:
Daniel Jakubowski, age 74, of Weston, Wisconsin, was called home to be with Jesus on Saturday, July 11, 2026, at home surrounded by his loving family.

Born on August 9, 1951, in Wausau, Wisconsin, Dan was the son of John and Joyce (Eggebrecht) Jakubowski. He married the love of his life, Ruth Bahlow, on July 23, 1983, and the couple enjoyed nearly 43 years together.

Dan built a career that reflected both skill and dedication, working for Wausau Homes in the construction and electrical fields. In his professional life, he was recognized for his work ethic and commitment to excellence. His hands helped shape not just buildings, but the lives and memories of countless families who came to call those structures home.

Away from work, Dan truly thrived in the pursuits that brought him joy and peace. An avid sportsman, he could often be found fishing along quiet banks or hunting in the early hours, taking in the natural beauty of Wisconsin’s woods and waters. He delighted in tending his garden, nurturing life from the earth with the same care he showed to those around him. Woodworking was another of Dan’s passions, and he approached each project with creativity, patience, and pride, transforming simple materials into works of usefulness and art.

Dan’s love for his favorite sports teams was well known, and he rarely missed the chance to cheer on the Brewers and Packers, following their seasons with unwavering enthusiasm. His wit was quick and ever-present; friends and family recall a man who could light up a room with a clever turn of phrase or a well-timed joke. Above all, Dan cherished the time spent with his loved ones, never missing an opportunity to share his warmth, humor, and, as he often joked, his unique ability at “driving his wife crazy.” All who knew Daniel will remember him as witty, kind-hearted, and steadfast. His presence enriched the lives of those around him, leaving behind a legacy of laughter, devotion, and honest work.

Dan is survived by his wife, Ruth; and son, Ryan Jakubowski. He is also survived by his sisters, Judie (Dave) Rheinschmidt, Annie Ohrmundt, and Cindy Hoenisch; his brother, Jon (Renee) Jakubowski; brothers-in-law, John (Susan) Bahlow and Charles Bahlow; nephew, Christopher (Cara) Bahlow; and niece, Tina Bahlow.

He is preceded in death by his paternal grandparents, John and Tekla (Bermbenck) Jakubowski; maternal grandparents, Fredrick and Emily (Natschke) Eggebrecht; his parents, John and Joyce (Eggebrecht) Jakubowski; parents-in-law, Elmer and Erna (Kurth) Bahlow; sister-in-law, Cathy Bahlow; and nephew, Zachary Bahlow.

A memorial service for Dan will be held at 11:00 AM on Saturday, July 25, 2026, at Our Savior's Ev. Lutheran Church, 703 Flieth Street, Wausau. Pastor Nathan Wranovsky will officiate. Visitation will be held from 10:00 AM until the time of service at the church. Dan will be laid to rest at a later date.

In lieu of flowers, memorials in Dan's name may be directed to Our Savior's Ev. Lutheran Church in Wausau, or Northland Lutheran High School in Kronenwetter.

The family would like to say a special thank you to Interim Healthcare Hospice Services and the wonderful team who cared for us, including Wendy, Jen, Marrol, Nick, Jill, Chaplain Mike, and Ashley, all of whom went above and beyond to care for us like family.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Debra Ann Olson
records.summary: Debra Ann Olson, age 60, of Mosinee passed away on Jul 11, 2026.
records.body:
Debra "Deb" A. Olson, 60, of Mosinee, passed away peacefully after a courageous 16-year battle with glioblastoma on Saturday, July 11, 2026, at her home, surrounded by her family.

Deb was born on March 23, 1966, in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, to Dennis Hess and Diane (Kemmerling) Klein. She married Corey M. Olson on July 7, 2007. He survives.

Deb was diagnosed with grade 4 glioblastoma in 2011 and was given a short time to live. However, she far exceeded that expectation. You would never have known she had cancer because she lived every day to the fullest while undergoing chemotherapy and radiation treatments. She never let cancer slow her down, even on the hardest days. She kept going for her family and remained positive through it all. She radiated that positivity to everyone around her.

She was a loving wife and mother who was incredibly selfless and caring. She poured her whole heart into being a mom, always making sure her children were cared for, even if that meant working three or more jobs. She loved taking her children to Lake DuBay Beach when they were young and spending one-on-one time with each of them. She made sure they always knew how special and deeply loved they were. She was so proud of her children, and they learned how to be loving, devoted parents because of the example she set.

Deb always gave the warmest hugs and had a contagious laugh and smile that her family and friends knew so well. She made it a priority to bring the family together for holidays and never missed celebrating a birthday. Not a single birthday card was ever forgotten. She was patient, understanding, and made everyone feel heard, valued, and important. Deb was truly one of a kind, with a heart of gold.

She also cherished being Corey’s wife. Their love was evident in the way they looked at each other. They enjoyed bike rides, traveling, dancing to their favorite songs without caring who was watching, and spending time together playing cribbage, golf, bowling, bean bags, and countless card and board games. They loved boating, gathering with friends, and sharing meals at Pit Stop. They never took their time together for granted and always made each other feel loved and appreciated. Their love was truly one of a kind and will live on forever.

Deb loved soaking up the sun, whether she was sitting on her porch, relaxing at her patio table, or taking long walks with friends. She treasured the simple things, listening to the birds sing and watching butterflies flutter by. She also enjoyed working on butterfly puzzles whenever she had the chance to relax. Everyone who knew Deb knew how much she loved butterflies. Her home was filled with them, as they became a symbol of hope after her brain cancer diagnosis.

Deb worked as a Special Education Paraprofessional for 40 years, a role she carried out with tremendous passion. Everyone knew how much she loved the children she worked with. It wasn't just a job to her; it was her calling. She was fully invested and had a natural talent for bringing out the best in every student she worked with. Going the extra mile to see them shine and succeed wasn't an option for her; it was simply who she was. She took enormous pride in every milestone and achievement her students reached. No matter how big or small, she was their biggest cheerleader, and even on the toughest days, she never stopped believing in or caring for them.

She also enjoyed working in the middle school library for two years before retiring.

After graduating from high school in 1984, Deb began pursuing her bachelor's degree at Walden University. She later took time away from her education to focus on raising her children before resuming her studies in 2010. The following year, she was diagnosed with brain cancer. Faced with that challenge, she briefly questioned whether she should continue her education, but her determination quickly prevailed. She returned to her studies and, in 2013, proudly earned her bachelor's degree in psychology with a focus on preschool child development.

Deb is survived by her husband, Corey Olson; her children, Keisha (Reese) Anderson, Jessica (Jeremy) Ferrin, Rebecca Sandoval, Ryan (Emily) Bell, Sarah Olson, and Brittney (Nash) Schoff; and her cherished grandchildren, Naomi, Scarlett, Madison, Dawson, Joyce, Landon, Maisie, Emma, Braxton and Koah. She is also survived by her siblings Lisa, Tammy, Kris, Tiffany, Andrea and Julie and her parents Dennis (Merry) Hess and Diane (Francis) Klein.

A Celebration of Life will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Friday, July 24, 2026, at Beste Funeral Home in Mosinee. Pastor Phyllis Smoot will officiate. Visitation will be held from 9:00 a.m. until the time of the service at the funeral home.

In honor of Deb, the family invites everyone to wear bright colors or anything with butterflies on it. To leave the family a condolence or share a memory of Deb, visit www.bestefh.com.

records.name: Jimmy Harvey Moon
records.summary: Jimmy Harvey Moon, age 78, of Merrill passed away on July 11, 2026.
records.body:
Jimmy Harvey Moon, 78, of Merrill, passed away peacefully at his home on Saturday, July 11, 2026, with his loving family by his side.

Jimmy was born on March 19, 1948, in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, to the late Charles and Catherine (Murphy) Moon. He was deeply proud of his Native heritage and honored to be a member of the Stockbridge-Munsee Band of Mohican Indians. His heritage was an important part of who he was, and he carried that pride throughout his life.

On July 3, 1976, Jimmy married the love of his life, Debbie Laske. Their time together was far too short, as Debbie passed away on December 8, 1987, from complications of lupus. Although she was taken from him much too soon, Jimmy continued to love her every day for the rest of his life. This year would have marked what would have been their 50th wedding anniversary.

Jimmy had a deep appreciation for the outdoors and for living a simple, meaningful life. He was a lifelong Green Bay Packers fan and rarely missed cheering on his team. He loved hunting and took great pride in tanning his own deer hides. Horses were one of his greatest passions, and many of his family's favorite memories were made riding trails together. Those times spent on horseback brought laughter, adventure, and countless stories that will be cherished forever.

As a young boy, Jimmy raised sheep and, with patience and determination, made a blanket from the wool himself. It was just one example of his hardworking nature and his ability to create something meaningful with his own hands. He and his daughter Jessie also raised pigs together when she was a young girl, and Jimmy was looking forward to Jessie getting a new house built so the two of them could again raise pigs together.

Above everything else, Jimmy treasured his family. Nothing brought him greater happiness than spending time with those he loved. He was incredibly proud of his daughter, Jessie, and his grandchildren, who were truly the light of his life. He loved each of them unconditionally and always wanted to make things better for them. Whether offering advice, lending a helping hand, or simply being there when he was needed, his family always knew they could count on him. They were his everything.

His family will forever be grateful for his unconditional love, steady guidance, and the many lessons he shared throughout the years, like: "If you're riding and see a bear in the woods, just hold on—the horse knows where to go." It perfectly reflected his trust, wisdom, and calm approach to life.

Jimmy is lovingly survived by his daughter, Jessie “Baby”- Jessica Zulliger; his grandchildren, Shylo Moon and fiancée Rebecca, Kyle (Jenna) Zulliger, Kira (Tyler) Stone, and Daniel Zulliger and his significant other, Heather; his great-grandchildren, Matthew, Riley, Nate, Adalee, and Ellie; his brothers, Charles Moon and Delbert Moon; and many nieces, nephews, cousins, great-nieces, and great-nephews.

In addition to his parents and his beloved wife, Debbie, Jimmy was preceded in death by his sisters, Nora (Robert) Hooker, Charlene Moon, and Laura Moon; and his sister-in-law, Linda Moon.

A graveside service will be held at 1:00 p.m. on Thursday, July 16, 2026, at Woodlawn Cemetery, W15626 Woodlawn Road, Birnamwood, WI 54414, in the Town of Hutchins. Family and friends are invited to gather afterward for food and refreshments at Hutchins Town Hall, W15698 Red River Rd E, Birnamwood, WI 54414.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family with arrangements. Words of remembrance and online condolences may be left at MWCS.WS.

Jimmy will be remembered for his quiet strength, kind heart, unwavering devotion to his family, and the love he carried for those closest to him throughout his life. His legacy will live on in the memories he created, the values he instilled, and the stories his family will continue to share for generations to come.

records.name: Mary E. Knippel-Stachowiak
records.summary: Mary E. Knippel-Stachowiak, age 92 passed away on Jul 11, 2026.
records.body:
Mary Knippel- Stachowiak, age 92, passed away peacefully on July 11, 2026 at Mount View Care Center in Wausau. She was surrounded by her family.

Mary was born in Wausau on July 29, 1933 to Joseph and Theresa (Sneigoski) Jakubowski. She was the second of four daughters. On July 7,1956 she married William Knippel. They were married until his death on September 23, 1998. In 2001, she found happiness and companionship again by marrying Fabian (Buck) Stachowiak. He preceded her in death on May 5, 2021.

Mary worked for Wisconsin Public Service, at a law firm as a legal secretary, and years later, after raising her children, in the circulation department of the Wausau Daily Herald. Many of her friendships later in life were established from her job at the Daily Herald.

Outside of work Mary enjoyed gardening, attending church picnics and traveling. In her early years, she and William enjoyed roller skating together. Bill had a profound interest in photography and often used Mary as his model. She and Buck enjoyed taking cruises and together sailed over 30 times. Mary was an avid walker and could be found outside walking regardless of how hot or cold the Wisconsin temperatures were. She was firmly planted in her faith and consistently attended mass with both William and Buck. Mary and Bill volunteered for years at St. Mary’s Church in Wausau helping with bingo, and later she and Buck loved going polka dancing at church festivals in between attending mass.

Mary is survived by her six children, Robert Knippel, Wausau, Kathy (Randy) Froehlich, Wausau, Ann (Gary) LaPorte, Kronenwetter, Joe (Sherrylynn Mueller) Knippel, Wausau, Monica (Andy) Lukas, Brookfield, Laura (Greg) Weyandt, Chetek, stepsons Fabian and Scott Stachowiak, 16 grandchildren and 7 great grandchildren. She was preceded in death by her husbands William and Buck, and her three sisters Virginia, Margie, and Annie.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:30 a.m. on Wednesday July 22, 2026 at St. Michael’s Catholic Church in Wausau. Burial will be in Restlawn Memorial Park in Wausau. Visitation will be Wednesday at St. Michael’s Catholic church from 10:30 a.m. until the time of service.

The family wishes to extend a very special thank you to all of the staff, caregivers, and volunteers at Mount View Care Center for all of the care, activities, and trips that they provided when family was no longer able to do so. The care and interaction that Mount View provided was exceptional and was deeply appreciated by both Mary and her entire family.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Patricia "Trish" M. Newman
records.summary: Patricia "Trish" M. Newman, age 67 passed away on Jul 11, 2026.
records.body:
Patricia “Trish” Marie Newman, 67, of Wausau, passed away on Saturday, July 11, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital. She was born on November 17, 1958, in Kenosha, Wisconsin, to the late Matthew and Eleanor Skender.

Trish was a very loving and caring warm-hearted woman and loved many things about life. She had health problems for the last 12 years and became wheelchair mobilized for about nine of them. She never let that stop her and made the best of what she could and had to work with to remain happy. She had a lot of memorable events in her 67-year history and a very astounding working career.

She graduated from Tremper High School in Kenosha. While a teenager she worked at Taco Bell, Pizza, Hut, and Villa Decarlo Pizza and Restaurant. During her 20s she continued to work as a cook and pizza maker at the aforementioned restaurants and was also a waitress at 3 Coins downtown restaurant and Sambo’s. Trish met her future husband, John Newman, a neighbor in Kenosha, when they were both 25 years old. Together they moved to the Upper Peninsula of Michigan in 1985, and they bought property along a trout stream in Ironwood Township. John and Trish remodeled and modernized a 100-year-old cabin that was designated on the historical register in the Ottawa National Forest. She then began working and cooking at White Cap Mountain Ski Hill as a cooking supervisor.

Following her time at the ski hill, Trish went to work at the Grandview County Hospital in Ironwood, Michigan, in the housekeeping and environmental services departments. While at the hospital she took over the position of employee chairperson for the AFL-CIO county employee’s union for all hospital workers. She was proud of the work she did for her fellow coworkers and was able to get many employees reinstated to their jobs from wrongful discharge including backpay compensation. She also attended Gogebic Community College while working at the hospital and received her Legal Business Degree after two years. She graduated with high honors and a 4.0 grade point average.

Upon completion of her schooling, Trish became a paralegal in the local the Ironwood-Bessemer-Wakefield Michigan area working for two attorney’s offices and a property title company. She also was united in marriage to John Newman on July 2, 1994, having a big, beautiful wedding at Our Lady of Peace Catholic Church in Ironwood. They celebrated with a wonderful reception at the Ironwood Township Hall.

In 2000, they moved to Wausau and purchased a home together. Trish began working at Eastbay in the Investigative Fraud Department for two years and then moved on to Wausau Insurance. She worked there for five years as a customer service representative until they closed. Trish continued for another 10 years with WPS Insurance at the Westwood Center and then for two years with Tri-Care military insurance until her retirement due to health issues.

In addition to her work, Trish enjoyed music and many hobbies. She saw many musicians in concert including: Ringo Starr, Paul McCartney, Bruce Springsteen, Charlie Daniels, George Thorogood, Blondie, Cheap Trick, Alice Cooper, Billy Joel at Lambeau Field, and many others just to name a few. She enjoyed sewing, cooking, artwork, music, rock concerts, country music, boating and fishing on the Great Lakes and inland lakes of Wisconsin, pets, birds and animals in the yard, gardening, plants, travel, vacations, touring sports stadiums, computer games, board games, playing cards, movies, T.V. shows, and going to local county fairs. Trish was a big fan of the Green Bay Packers, Milwaukee Brewers, and the Wausau Woodchucks.

Survivors include her husband of 32 years, John Newman; brothers, Matthew “Buzzy” and Michael; sister, Jenny; many nieces and nephews, Jenny, Jamie, Michael, Mitchel, Justin, Kim, Todd, Tammy, and Dawn; and many other family that she truly and dearly loved.

She was preceded in death by her parents and siblings, Mary, Kathy, and Charles “Chuck.”

The graveside service will be at 11:30 a.m. on Friday, July 24, 2026, in the Restlawn Memorial Park Chapel with Deacon Peter Burek presiding. Visitation will begin at 11:00 a.m. until the time of service in the chapel. Entombment will take place following services in the Restlawn Memorial Chapel Mausoleum. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Ronald Arthur Jaeger
records.summary: Ronald Arthur Jaeger, age 86, of Merrill, Wisconsin passed away on Jul 11, 2026.
records.body:
Ronald Arthur Jaeger, age 86, of Merrill, passed away on July 11, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital in Wausau.

He was born on January 14, 1940, in Pine River, son of Wilbert and Bernice (Billerbeck) Jaeger. He married Rosalie Baumann on November 21, 1987, at Grace Baptist Church in Irma, WI – she preceded him in death in 2025.

Ronald worked as a Granite Polisher for Anderson Brothers and Johnson and later for SEMCO prior to his retirement. He enjoyed gardening, fishing, Sheepshead and other card games, sports, spending time with his family and friends and listening to old country music. He will be missed by all who knew and loved him.

Ronald is survived by his children, Kevin (Tara) Jaeger, Bruce Jaeger, Kraig (RaDell) Jaeger, James Jaeger, Jamie (Daniel) Lemke, Paul Jaeger (Holly), Peggy (Joan) Jaeger, Joshua (Christina) Jaeger, Dawn (Allan) Lemke, Joseph Gleason, Jerry (Jesse) Duginski, Linda Goodman (Greg Baierl), Jason (Shannon) Duginski; siblings, Arthur (Joreen) Jaeger, Phillip Jaeger, Glen (Jackie) Jaeger; many grand and great-grandchildren, as well as several nieces, nephews and other extended relatives.

He is preceded in death by, his parents, Wilbert and Bernice; his first wife, DeJon LaMountain; his second wife, Mary Ann Jaeger; his third wife, Rosalie; son, Brian; brothers, Richard and William; sister-in-law, Connie Jaeger; stepson, Leon Duginski; grandchildren, Robert Goodman and Brandon Duginski.

The funeral service for Ronald will be held on Friday, July 17, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral Home at 301 Eagle Drive, Merrill. Pastor Andy Perry will officiate. Visitation will be held from 10:00 a.m. until the time of the service, also at the funeral home. Burial at Merrill Memorial Park Cemetery will immediately follow the service.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation Services of Merrill is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences are encouraged and may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com .

records.name: Marjorie Mae Zdun
records.summary: Marjorie Mae Zdun, age 100, of Pittsville passed away on Jul 12, 2026.
records.body:
Marjorie Mae Zdun, 100 was called home to her beloved Lord and Savior on Sunday, July 12, 2026 surrounded by her loving family. Marjorie was born on April 9, 1926 in Milwaukee, WI and adopted by Stephen & Therese Lampe. From an early age, music filled her life and touched all who knew her. A lifelong resident of Pittsville, WI she attended Pleasant Corners & St. Joachim Grade School and graduated from Pittsville High School in 1944.

Faith, family and service were the hallmarks of her extraordinary life. After high school Marjorie worked at the Pittsville Post Office where she met the love of her life, Emil Zdun. Following a whirlwind romance they were united in marriage on August 19, 1950 at St. Joachim Catholic Church in Pittsville. So began 46 years working together: first on farms in Pittsville and Auburndale and later as owners of Pittsville Farmers' Feed & Supply. She had a truly profound faith in God and was an enthusiastic member of St. Joachim Church and the PCCW. She was also actively involved with the Pittsville Historical Society and a lifetime member of the American Legion Auxiliary and Disabled American Veterans.

Always active, Marjorie would be seen energetically riding her bicycle until age 81 or walking well into her 90's. Marjorie loved people and giving back to help others in need. Rarely home, she would always leave an individualized, cardboard note on the floor in her apartment so that friends and family knew where she had gone. When home you could hear her playing her accordion or piano.

However, her greatest delight stemmed from her role as mother, grandmother, great grandmother and great-great grandmother to her family. For decades as her family grew, she delighted in preparing and sharing Sunday chicken dinners, a tradition that spanned more than 32 consecutive years. Birthdays and special occasions were marked by a personalized phone call and her cherished, handwritten cards. Her warm smile and favorite farewell "keep lovin" became the mantra that inspired generations.

Marjorie is survived by her children, Therese (Sonny Bornstein) Zdun, Susan (Gregg Steffel) Zdun, Stephen (Deborah) Zdun, Thomas (JoAnn) Zdun, Evelyn (Thomas) Chaltry, James Zdun, Laura (David) Dolan, Margaret (Darrell) Smazal, Patrick (Carolyn) Zdun, and Sheila (Todd) DeDecker. She is also survived by 25 grandchildren, 41 great grandchildren and 6 great-great grandchildren. She is further survived by daughter-in-law Sherry Zdun, brother Tom Newman and sister-in-law Lu Hein.

She was proceeded in death by her husband Emil Zdun, son Timothy Zdun, grandson Christopher Zdun, her parents Stephen and Therese Lampe, brothers Leo Lampe & Philip Hein, and birth mother Susan Wierl.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held at 11:00 am on Monday, July 20th at St. Joachim Catholic Church in Pittsville with Father Leo Raphael officiating. Visitation will be held at the church from 8:00 am until the time of service. A rosary will be prayed at 8:30 am. Per Marjorie's request, lunch will be immediately following Mass. Interment will be held at St. Joachim parish cemetery following lunch.

The family wishes to thank family and friends for their loving support and generosity through these amazing years. A special thank you to the staff of St. Croix Hospice, Nekoosa Court Assisted Living and everyone who helped her in her life. In lieu of flowers, mass intentions can be sent to St. Joachim Church.

records.name: Michael Duane Ekern
records.summary: Michael Duane Ekern, age 79, of Eland passed away on July 12, 2026.
records.body:
Michael D. Ekern, 79, of Eland, died on Sunday, July 12, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center, Weston.

Mike was born on May 25, 1947, in Rice Lake, the son of Duane and Mary (Reckenthaler) Ekern.

Mike was a United States Army Veteran, serving from 1968 until 1970. Mike was a proud veteran and was able to go on the Honor Flight in 2025, with his son, Scott.

On September 30, 1978, Mike was united in marriage to Katie Verkuilen.

Mike attended UWSP and graduated with a degree in Natural Resources. He worked for the DNR, traveling to South Dakota. Mike later discovered his love for building and working with his hands, which led him to his carpentry career. He worked as a Union Carpenter for Carpenters Local 804, until his retirement. Mike enjoyed hunting, fishing, traveling, being outdoors and making maple syrup. He was active in his community as an assistant boy scout leader and president of the Eland Historical Society. Mike loved spending time with his family and attending the kids’ sporting activities.

Mike had a heart that always put others first, and he found joy in helping others. His compassion and kindness were shared with everyone he met. He leaves behind a legacy of love, selflessness, and genuine goodness that will never be forgotten.

Mike is survived by his wife, Katie; three children, Laura (Derek Grove) Ekern, Lindsay (Greg) Cole and Scott (Kristin) Ekern; eight grandchildren, Alex, Luke, Gavin, Landon, Lydia, Owen, Olivia and Jack; siblings, David (Debbie) Ekern, Maureen (Jim) Mantor and Marilyn (Ted Hegstorm) Brenna; brothers and sisters-in-law, Barbara Ostrowski, Betty (Elroy) Koskey, Richard (Julie) Verkuilen, Pat Miller and Joyce Verkuilen and many nieces, nephews and other relatives.

Mike was preceded in death by his parents and mother and father-in-law, Leslie and Hattie Verkuilen.

A Funeral Service will be held at 1 PM on Friday, July 31, 2026, at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg. Pastor Wes Jedras will officiate. Visitation will be on Friday, from 11 AM until the time of service at the funeral home.

records.name: Virginia M. Zastrow
records.summary: Virginia M. Zastrow, age 99, of Wausau passed away on Jul 12, 2026.
records.body:
Virginia M. Zastrow, 99, town of Wausau, passed away on Sunday, July 12, 2026 while under the care of St. Croix Hospice at Careview Transitional Care of Weston.

She was born December 21, 1926, in Wausau, daughter of the late August and Marie (Behnke) Tietz. On May 22, 1943 she married Fred Zastrow in Wausau. He preceded her in death on June 26, 2000.

Mom belonged to the Highland Grove Grade School Mothers Club and the Highland Homemakers Club. She could fix the electric cord on her clothes iron as well as adjust the carburetor on the car. Holidays were always special with decorations, gifts and special meals.

Mom and Dad enjoyed fishing trips to Canada later in life and spent years bowling and going to casinos.

After dad’s passing, she enjoyed textile painting, Nintendo Wii, bowling, writing poetry and word find puzzles. Her treasured dog, Slick, kept her company.

She remained in the beloved farm home of 83 years until 10 days before her passing. Mom attended Grant, Franklin and Highland Grove grade schools in Wausau through the 8th grade. She was baptized and confirmed at St. Paul’s United Church of Christ in Wausau. She attended Sunday School regularly.

Mom met dad because she was a good friend to Delores “Toots” Zastrow, dad’s sister, during school days. Romance blossomed and they married on May 22, 1943 at a pastor’s house in Wausau. A honeymoon trip to LaCrosse followed.

A family came along and Mom and Dad became farm owners in 1949. Through lean years Mom helped with hand milking, cooking, canning meats, fruits and vegetables and cared for her large family. Mom enjoyed doing beautiful pencil drawings, writing poetry and keeping a notebook of song lyrics in her younger years. After raising the family Mom began an extensive doll collection which she enjoyed thoroughly.

Survivors include her children, Linda Schroeder, Wausau, Donna Kufahl, Wausau, Elroy (Sharon) Zastrow, Wausau, Vicki Deckert, Wausau, Arlin Zastrow, Wausau, her grandchildren, Todd (Jane) Schroeder, Troy (Tammy) Schroeder, Ted (fiancé Michelle) Schroeder, Tiffany (Jeremy) Knopow, Blake (Beckie) Kufahl, Brett Kufahl, Brooke (Bob) Frenkel, Jennifer (Brad) Altergott, Scott Zastrow, Steven (Amanda) Zastrow, Karen Zastrow, Rod (Stacey) Zastrow, Kelly Zastrow, Alec (Bianca) Zastrow, Brittanie (Matt) Schulz, Amelia Zastrow, Dustin Dudek, Travis (Shania) Wistrom, Ryan (Meghan) Wistrom, Erika (Dan) Staffeil, Cody Smoker, Kyle Smoker, her great grandchildren, Adam (Amanda), Brad, Corwin, Griffin (Amanacer), Willow, Hudson, Hazel, Alexia, Alivia, Brock, Jadden, Logan, Samantha (Tyler), Brandon (Emilee), Morgahn, Siena, Oakley, Odessa, Natalee, Julian, Chanelle, Cole, Jenna, Brooke, Klaire, Gracie, Ember, Bria, Allie, Alex, Chase, George (Megan), Saxon (Aylissa) and great great grandchildren, Teagan, Llane, Mason, Lilah, Emma, Nora and numerous nieces and nephews.

Virginia was preceded in death by her parents, her husband Fred in 2000, her children, Wayne Zastrow in 1972, Garry Zastrow in 2009, Robin Zastrow in 2018 and Kathy Wistrom in 2026. She was also preceded in death by her sons-in-law, Richard “Dick” Schroeder, Gary Wistrom and Bruce Kufahl and her siblings, Henry (Ella) Tietz, Clarence (Marge) Tietz, Lorinda (Ralph) Wimmer, Hilda (Claude) Pettit, Harold (Christa) Tietz, August (Cathleen) Tietz, Emil Tietz and Verna Tietz.

Funeral services will be held at 11:00 a.m. Wednesday, July 22, 2026 at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau.  Chris Ewan will officiate. Burial will be in Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau. Visitation will be held on Wednesday from 9:30 a.m. until the time of services all at the Sixth Street funeral home. In lieu of flowers a memorial fund has been established in Virginia’s name. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Blake R. Mosher
records.summary: Blake R. Mosher, age 22, of Mattoon passed away on Jul 13, 2026.
records.body:
On Monday, July 13, 2026, Blake R. Mosher, age 22, passed away unexpectedly, leaving his family and friends to mourn his loss. Blake was born on May 8, 2004, to Tim and Lorraine (Spinhirne) Mosher, in Antigo, WI. The family moved to Wyoming in 2011, and returned to Merrill in 2016, where he attended Merrill High School. Blake worked at Geiss Meats and Northern Wire, both in Merrill, but most recently, he was a processing operator in the logging industry. Blake loved being outdoors, which made his job perfect for him. He looked forward to deer hunting all year, fishing in the summer and winter, swimming and camping. Blake loved his family and friends dearly, he was excited to go to Rockfest this year, as well as celebrating his parents’ anniversary next month and a trip to Vegas. He was a true “Mr. Fix-It”, he could tinker and work on most anything! Blake had a passion for racing, whether it was off-road racing or ice racing, he just loved being out there! He would also enjoy spending time with his brothers gaming and just hanging out, and taking trips to casinos to gamble. His family remembers him as a funny, smart and loving guy, that always had a prank or joke, and was easy to get along with. Blake was close with his family and friends, and although he is gone, he will never be forgotten.

Blake leaves behind his parents: Tim and Lorraine Mosher, siblings: Keith (Grace), Brooke (Corey) and Kyle Mosher, paternal grandparents: Barb and Lyle Mosher, uncles: Andy (Dana) Mosher, who were also Blake’s godparents, Jeff (Jodi) Mosher and James (Kristy) Spinhirne, aunts: Heather (Butch) Fleischmann, Rebecca (Craig) Sinnen and Lucy (Dave) Fraki. He is further survived by his girlfriend: Anna Westfall, many cousins and good friends, as well as his dog: Ryder. He is preceded in death by his maternal grandparents: Terry Spinhirne and Katherine Hulda.

A Celebration of Blake’s Life will take place at a later date.

The family wishes to thank all of those that have supported through this tragedy and the Aspirus Wausau Hospital for their efforts and care shown to Blake and his family, especially nurse, Emily. You love and kindness will never be forgotten.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation Services of Merrill is honored to assist Blake’s family with the arrangements. Online condolences are encouraged and may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com.

records.name: Donald James Koeller
records.summary: Donald James Koeller, age 91, of Weston passed away on Jul 13, 2026.
records.body:
Donald “Don” J. Koeller, 91, Marathon, passed away peacefully on Monday, July 13, 2026, at Rennes Health and Rehab in Weston, under the care of Aspirus at Home Hospice.

He was born on December 23, 1934, in Wausau, son of the late Erwin and Lucinda (Harder) Koeller. On June 26, 1954 he married Lois H. Buttke at Trinity Lutheran Church, Stettin. The couple recently celebrated 72 years of marriage.

Don was a 1952 graduate of Wausau High School and played football under coach Win Brockmeyer. He started his career at Marathon Cheese working in the basement of Ray Goldbach’s family and eventually went on to become a supervisor at Pauly Cheese in Wausau. He continued working as a painter for Goetsch’s Welding and Machine into his 70’s. Don kept busy as the Treasurer for the Town of Rib Falls for 54 years in addition to serving in many positions at St. Matthew Lutheran Church.

He enjoyed listening to music, especially polkas, waltzes and German Brass bands. At age 16 he began drumming for his high school band which extended to a 42 year career with the Jerry Goetsch Orchestra. Through the orchestra the travelling group Polka Productions was formed which allowed him and his wife to travel world-wide enjoying the sights, music and making new friends. He had an opportunity to perform at numerous Octoberfest celebrations in Munich Germany.

Don was a man of deep faith and love for his family. Among his favorite pastimes, he enjoyed playing Sheepshead and Cribbage with his children, grandchildren and great-grandchildren.

He was an avid sports fan and loved to watch the Wisconsin Badgers, Milwaukee Brewers and was a Green Bay Packers season ticket holder for 63 years. He never missed an opportunity to watch his grandchildren’s sporting events all through their careers at Marathon High School.

Survivors include, his wife, Lois; four children, Gail (Brian) Gumtz, Karen Koeller, Bruce (Brenda) Koeller and Brian Koeller; six grandchildren, Nicholas (Christy) Gumtz, Michelle (John) Tatera, Brett (Lisa) Koeller, Caleb Koeller, Kolby Koeller and Jaden (Claire) Koeller; five great-grandchildren, Alexander and Dominic Gumtz, Jack Baumer, and Abigail and Benjamin Tatera; one brother, Robert Koeller; and many nieces, nephews.

Besides his parents, he was preceded in death by two brothers, Kenneth (infant) and Gerald; and his infant grandson, Brandon Koeller.

Private services will be held at St. Matthew Lutheran Church in Marathon.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to St. Matthew Lutheran Church Building Fund or the Little Lions Child Care in Marathon.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Francis E. Fischer
records.summary: Francis E. Fischer, 88, of Antigo, Wisconsin, passed away on July 8, 2026.
records.body:
Francis Edward "Bud" Fischer, 88, of Antigo, Wisconsin, passed away at home on July 8, 2026.

Bud was born on June 21, 1938, in Antigo, Wisconsin, to Frank Fischer Jr. and Marie Fischer (Smith).

He married his wife Joyce (Wickey) on November 14, 1959, in Milwaukee. Together they built a life and a family that reflected everything he valued.

Bud served his country in the United States Marine Corps, where he worked as a truck mechanic. After his service, he went to work for Bultman Trucking in Milwaukee. He later spent ten years at Consolidated Freightways in Wausau before returning to Bultman Trucking, where he remained until he retired after thirty years of driving with the Teamsters.

Anyone who knew Bud knew that his work didn't stop when he retired. He was a man of many talents — a self-taught carpenter, electrician, mechanic and welder who could build or repair just about anything. He never hesitated to lend a hand to anyone who needed it, and he took pride in passing on those skills and hard-working attitude to his children.

Bud was happiest outdoors. He loved to hunt and fish, work on his tractors, and he was most at home in the woods. You could always find him in his shop building and fabricating just about anything. He spent many summers at the family cottage on Oxbow Lake in Presque Isle, Wisconsin, and loved camping in Michigan's Upper Peninsula near Lake Superior. And wherever Bud went, you could always find him with a can of Skoal in his chest pocket.

He is survived by his wife Joyce; his daughters Yvonne (Scott) Nutter and Dianne (Mike) Kohler; his son Jerry (Wendy) Fischer; his sister Shirley Laab; four grandchildren; and four great-grandchildren.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Frank Fischer Jr. and Marie Fischer; his sister Joann Gehrke; and his great-grandson Loxley.

A celebration of life will be held in the fall. Details to follow.

records.name: Frank Decker
records.summary: Frank Decker, age 80, of Weston passed away on July 13, 2026.
records.body:
Frank Decker, also known as Joe, age 80, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully at home, surrounded by his loving family, on July 13, 2026.

Frank was born on July 12, 1946, in Wisconsin, the son of Frank and Neva (Sulik) Decker. On May 27, 1967, he married the love of his life, Judy Feldbruegge, and together they shared 59 wonderful years of marriage.

Throughout his life, Frank found joy in life's simple pleasures. He had a passion for woodworking, hunting, fishing, and camping, reflecting his love of both nature and craftsmanship. Whether working with his hands or enjoying the quiet beauty of the outdoors, Frank was truly at home. Music—especially polka—held a special place in his heart and often filled family gatherings with laughter, dancing, and togetherness.

Frank was blessed with a remarkable sense of humor that drew people to him and endeared him to everyone who knew him. His presence brought smiles and laughter, making even ordinary days memorable. Above all, Frank cherished time spent with his family, treasuring every opportunity to create lasting memories together.

Frank is lovingly survived by his wife, Judy; his children, Angela (Kevin) Holz, Julie (Kenny) Meis, and Jason Decker; his grandchildren, Nicholas, Christopher, Amber, and Kayla; his great-grandchildren, Barbara and Conner; and his siblings, Rachel and Mike.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Frank and Neva Decker, and his siblings, Fred, Kris, and Claudette.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held in Frank's honor on Friday, July 24, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. at St. Therese Catholic Church, 113 W. Kort Street, Schofield. Fr. Janusz Kowalski will officiate. Visitation will be held at the church from 10:00 a.m. until the time of Mass. Frank will be laid to rest following the service at Gate of Heaven Cemetery in Kronenwetter.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: James Bornbach
records.summary: James Bornbach, age 82 passed away on Jul 13, 2026.
records.body:
James “Jim” Bornbach, 82, of Marathon, WI, passed away peacefully at his home on Monday July 13, 2026 with his loving wife by his side. He was born in Rozellville, WI on October 22, 1943 to John and Eleanor (Linzmeier) Bornbach.

Jim leaves behind a life filled with family, friends and more love than we could capture in just a short reading. He loved being outside, working and being “on the go.” He spent his early years working on the family farm and then moved to Iowa to continue farm work. He spent 3 years in the service from 1962-1965 studying mechanics.

In 1965, Jim met the love of his life, JoAnn Aschenbrenner. They were united in marriage at Sacred Heart Catholic Church in Marshfield on June 18, 1966.

After working for years in the tire business, custodial services and water conditioning business, he decided to start his own water treatment business, Water Excellence. His business became a big success from its start in 1997 until his retirement in 2026 (the business will continue under Jim’s grandson, Nick Schill).

Jim leaves behind his wife, his lifelong love JoAnn, six children: Cheryl, Daniel (Stacy), Cynthia, Connie, Christina, Darren (Danielle); 18 grandchildren and 3 great-grandchildren. He is further survived by brothers, Alvin, David (Donna), Eugene (Genny), sister, Delores, brothers-in-law, Gary (Bonny) and Ken (Nancy), and sisters-in-law, Helen and Kathy.

Jim was preceded in death by his parents, John and Eleanor, parents-in-law, Robert and Lovilia Aschenbrenner, brothers, Orville and Gerald, sisters, Joan (Ed Stockheimer) and Alice (Arthur) Schallock; two brothers-in law, Roger and Robert; and sisters-in-law, Betty and Bev.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. on Friday, July 17, 2026 at St. Mary Catholic Church, Marathon, with full military honors to follow conducted by the American Legion Post No. 469, Marathon. Rev. Msgr. Joseph Diermeier will preside. Visitation will be on Thursday from 4:00 until 7:00 p.m. at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home in Marathon and again on Friday from 9:30 a.m. until time of services at the church.

Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com
