records.name: Jeanette Switlick
records.summary: Jeanette Switlick, age 93, of Athens passed away on Jul 13, 2026.
records.body:
Jeanette Switlick,93, Athens passed away on Monday, July 13, 2026.

She was born January 29, 1933 in Athens. On May 26, 1951, she married Stanley Switlick at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens. He preceded her in death on March 31, 1996.

Jeanette was a proud homemaker and truly enjoyed raising her children and grandchildren and anyone who came through her door. Some of her favorite pastimes included embroidery, making quilts, playing cards, cooking, shopping, gardening and enjoying the company of her grandchildren and great grandchildren. Jeanette made baby quilts for all the grandchildren and great grandchildren.

She also enjoyed blueberry picking, visiting with people and living life to the fullest.

Survivors include her children, Mike (Kathy) Switlick, Lorraine (Donald) Sobjeck, Mark Switlick, Daniel (Laura) Switlick, Leanne (Victor) Smith, Francis (Jennifer) Switlick and Lori Switlick, her grandchildren, Kristine (Jose Argueta Granados) Switlick, Josh (Korina) Switlick, Justin (Tania) Switlick, Jeremy (Jessica) Switlick, Erin Sobjeck, Kyle Sobjeck, Kimberly (Joe) Ellis, Matt Switlick, Tiffany (Andrew) Lukasko, Melanie (Jordan) Locher, Jonathon Patton, Cassandra (AJ) Switlick, Nadine (Casey) Aho, Ryan (Samantha) Switlick, Russell (Amanda) Switlick and Megan Switlick, her great grandchildren, Blake, Brock, Luke, Jake, Landon, Eli, Everly, Oliver, Lyla, Madden, Finnley, Ashlyn, Emma, Holden, Elsie and soon Baby Girl Locher and her special cousin, Mabel Drewek.

Besides her parents and husband, she was preceded in death by three sons, Richard, Florian and Jerome “Mego” Switlick and her grandchildren, Keith Boehm, Amanda Boehm and Jennifer Patton and a great granddaughter Adrian Handrick.

The Funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. Tuesday, July 21, 2026, at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens. The Rev. George Graham will preside. Burial will be in Calvary Cemetery, Athens. Visitation will be held on Tuesday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of services at the church. Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Athens is in charge of arrangements. Online condolences be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Lauretta A. Tauschek
records.summary: Lauretta A. Tauschek, age 97, of Marshfield passed away on Jul 13, 2026.
records.body:
Lauretta A. Tauschek, 97, Marshfield, passed away on Monday, July 13, 2026 at Wells Nature View in Marshfield.

A private memorial service will be held.

records.name: Mariah Rusch
records.summary: Mariah Rusch, age 23, of Merrill passed away on Jul 13, 2026.
records.body:
Mariah Marie Hope Rusch, age 23, of Merrill, Wisconsin, passed away on July 13, 2026. She was born on August 1, 2002, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to her loving parents, Jason and Jennifer (Sturm) Lemmer.

A bright light in the lives of those who knew her, Mariah was a graduate of Merrill High School, where her creativity and passion began to shine. She furthered her education by earning an Associate's Degree in Marketing from Northcentral Technical College in Wausau, allowing her to cultivate her talents as a Digital Marketing Specialist and Photographer.

Mariah (Mira) was a beautiful, loving, creative, thoughtful, supportive lover of life. She loved capturing beautiful moments for her clients. She also loved traveling with her husband to see new places, the mountains were her favorite.

She loved reading, writing, drawing, her work, her family, and her dog Oslo. Her husband Brandon was the love of her life and completed her in every way.

She started to have a love of gardening, raising her five chickens, and loved her peaceful life.

She was a coffee connoisseur and enjoyed making coffee for others at Ana Blair’s and recently at the Pickle Jar. Making many friends along the way.

ML Creative was her dream job, and starting this job was one of her most exciting adventures. She traveled to multiple states making beautiful memories for many people.

Mariah was loved by everyone she met. She left this life way too soon and will be dearly missed.

Survivors include her husband, Brandon Rusch of Merrill; parents, Jason and Jennifer Lemmer of Merrill; in-law, Marcel and Jamie Rusch of Gleason; brothers, Cody Lemmer (Allison Palecek) of Merrill and Caden Lemmer of Merrill; paternal grandparents, Jan Lemmer of Merrill and Mike (Cindy) Lemmer of Petersburg, TN; sister-in-law, Jerzie Rusch (Mason Thomaschefsky) of Gleason; and brother-in-law, Jaden Rusch (fiancée Bailey) of Weston. Further surviving are aunts and uncles, Jason (Terri) Sturm of Wausau, Jamie (Chad) Mathwich of Little Chicago, Jeremy (Katie) Sturm of Wausau, Jamie (Kent) Bronsteatter of Merrill, and Jenny Lemmer (Pam) of Cleveland, OH; many cousins including her lifelong best friend, Hailey Mathwich; and her special friends, Olivia and Mackenah ‘Mack’. Mariah was preceded in death by her maternal grandparents, Mark and Patricia Sturm.

A celebration of Mariah’s life will be held on Sunday, July 26, 2026, at the Corning Town Hall. Friends may gather from 1:00 P.M. until 5:00 P.M.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Marilyn C. Bins
records.summary: Marilyn C. Bins, 88, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on July 3, 2026.
records.body:
Marilyn Catherine Bins, age 88, passed away peacefully on Friday, July 3, 2026, in Wausau, Wisconsin.

Marilyn was born on September 16, 1937, in Green Bay, Wisconsin, to Walter and Harriet (Collie) Fabry. She lived a life centered on love, family, and creating cherished memories with those closest to her. Marilyn found great happiness at the family cabin in Three Lakes, Wisconsin, and at their winter home in Ft. Myers Beach, FL; but nothing brought her greater joy than spending time with her family. Whether gathered for holidays, celebrations, or simple visits, Marilyn treasured every moment with the people she loved.

She is lovingly survived by her two sons, Daniel and David; brother Mike (Carol) Fabry; grandchildren Aly (Andy) Stahlman and Andy (Lauren) Johnson; her two great-grandchildren, Margot Stahlman and Beau Johnson; son-in-law Doug (Cheryl) Johnson, as well as other relatives and friends. Her love, kindness, and devotion to her family will be remembered always.

She was preceded in death by her maternal and paternal grandparents; her parents, Walter and Harriet Fabry; her beloved husband, Gerald; and her daughter, Julie Bins Johnson.

A visitation will be held on Friday, July 17, 2026, from 9:30 a.m. to 10:30 a.m. at Church of the Resurrection. A Funeral Mass will follow at 10:30 a.m., with Father Tom Lindner officiating. Burial will take place after at St. Joseph Cemetery.

records.name: Mary Ann Schuld
records.summary: Mary Ann Schuld, age 88 passed away on Jul 13, 2026.
records.body:
Mary Ann Schuld, 88, of Metamora, IL, formerly of Macomb, IL, Mosinee, WI, and Wausau, WI, passed away with her family by her side on Monday, July 13, 2026, at OSF Richard L. Owens Hospice Home in Peoria, IL.

She was born on April 8, 1938, in Wausau to the late Jacob and Sarah (Walters) Reuter. She married Ernest R Schuld on May 23, 1964, at St Mary Catholic Church in Wausau. He preceded her in death on February 5, 2018. Survivors include one son and two daughters, Sara (Brad) Payne of Metamora, IL; Alan (Marianne) Schuld of Huntsville, AL; and Dana (Tim) McVicker of Metamora, IL; nine grandchildren, Ashley (Steven) James, Austin and Alex Payne, Erin and Riley Schuld, Ashley and Luke Daniel, and Kaelyn Ray and Connor McVicker; one sister and one brother, Audrey Reuter of Wausau, WI, and Leon (Irene) Reuter of Lancaster, CA; two sisters-in-law and one brother-in-law, Irene Reuter, Joyce Schuld, and Michael Stack; and many nieces and nephews. She was preceded in death by her parents; her husband; four brothers, Norman, Robert, Edwin, and Ralph Reuter; two sisters, Laverne Reuter and Delores Hamerla; several sisters- and brothers-in-law, several nieces and nephews, and many aunts and uncles.

She was very active in Girl Scouts, serving as a troop leader for 50 young girls, and was a church volunteer prior to marriage. She attended Viterbo College, a Franciscan institution in La Crosse, Wisconsin. She and her husband fostered high school children for several years before raising their own children. She was an owner and actively involved in the family manufacturing businesses in product development, accounting, and marketing roles. She also took pride in maintaining a pristine home and preparing tasty meals for her family. She was an avid Packer fan; and enjoyed sewing, camping, gardening, traveling, reading, knitting, playing cards with family and friends, and art in many forms including woodcarving and sketching.

The Funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 am on Thursday, July 23, 2026, at Holy Name of Jesus Parish, Wausau. Fr. Woodrow Pace will preside. A noon lunch at the Church will follow the services, followed by a Burial with graveside prayers in Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau.

Visitation will be on Wednesday, July 22, 2026, from 3:00-7:00 at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau, and also on Thursday, July 23 from 10:00 until time of services at the church.

Memorial donations may be made to OSF Richard L Owens Hospice Home, Peoria, IL. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Michael J. Ringwelski
records.summary: Michael J. Ringwelski, 83, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on July 10, 2026.
records.body:
Michael "Mike" J. Ringwelski, 83, Wausau, passed away peacefully surrounded by his family on Friday, July 10, 2026 at Aspirus Wausau Hospital.

He was born March 13, 1943 in Wausau, son of the late Michael F. and Helen (Langhoff) Ringwelski.

Mike spent many enjoyable years hunting, fishing and making firewood at his cabin in Harshaw. He always knew where the secret fishing spots were. Mike was an avid Packers and Brewers fan and he made sure he got in on every pool.

The family would like to extend a special thank you to all his many friends at his two favorite local pubs, Tic Toc and Chatterbox, who were there to assist him in any way.

He will be missed by his many friends and family. Mike fought a good fight and is now forever in God's care.

Survivors include five siblings, Richard "Ringo" (Delores) Ringwelski, Wausau, MaryAnn Schmidt, Schofield, Florian "Bud" (Barb) Ringwelski, Tomahawk, Floyd Ringwelski, Wausau and BettyJane "BJ" Kimps, Harshaw; one sister-in-law, Evelyn Ringwelski, Ringle; and many nieces, nephews, relatives and friends.

Besides his parents, he was preceded in death by two siblings, Nancy Wilde and Dennis Ringwelski; three brothers-in-law, David Schmidt, Dennis Kimps and Jerome "Pork" Wilde; and one sister-in-law, Nancy Ringwelski.

In accordance with Mike's wishes, private burial will be held.

records.name: Pamela M. Leahy
records.funeral_home: Brainard Funeral Home & Cremation Center of Weston
records.summary: Pamela M. Leahy, 83, of Schofield, Wisconsin, passed away on July 5, 2026.
records.body:
On Sunday, July 5, 2026, Pamela Maureen Leahy, surrounded by compassionate care from Compassus Hospice, completed her earthly journey and was welcomed into the Kingdom of Heaven.

Pam was born on July 18, 1942, in Fresno, California. From the very beginning, her life was guided by a steadfast faith in God and a deep, unwavering love for her family.

A devoted and determined mother, Pam worked tirelessly to provide for her four sons. As a proud single parent, she often held three jobs at once. Working full-time at Foremost Dairy as a member of the Teamsters Union, waitressing, and selling Mary Kay cosmetics. Through hard work, sacrifice, and unconditional love, she always made sure her boys had everything they needed.

Later in life, Pam followed her family to Schofield, Wisconsin, where she enjoyed working for Krueger Floral. She found great joy in her grandchildren and treasured the time she spent with each of them. Family was always at the center of her life, and her love, guidance, and faith will continue to live on through the generations she helped shape.

Pam in lovingly survived by her sons, Martin Bottomley, Mitch Bottomley (Michelle Opal), Joe Bottomley, and Jared Bottomley; her grandchildren, Ryan Bottomley (Adam Lupton), Dana Thurston (Bud), Alexis Bottomley, Payton Trzcinski (Loukas), Blake Opal-Wahoske (Brett), Jason Wolfe, Jordan Monette, Morgan Bottomley (Joe Walkowiak), and Madison Bottomley; and her cherished great-granddaughters, Mara Thurston and Birdie Opal-Wahoske.

She is also survived by her sister, Jeryl Techman (John); her brother, Michael Leahy (Rhonda); and many beloved nieces, nephews, extended family members, and friends.

Pam was preceded in death by her soulmate, Donald Schendel; her parents, Maurice and Vivian Leahy; and her brother, Stanley Leahy.

Her family extends their heartfelt gratitude to the staff and caregivers of Mount View Care Center and Compassus Hospice for the kindness, dignity, and compassionate care they provided throughout her final years. Your dedication and generosity of spirit will never be forgotten.

A private family gathering will be held to celebrate Pam's life.

records.name: Roger A. Eisenman
records.summary: Roger A. Eisenman, 101, of Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on July 4, 2026.
records.body:
Roger A. Eisenman, born June 12, 1925, in Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on July 4, 2026, leaving behind a legacy of service, dedication, and love for family, community, and faith.

He was the son of Arthur, Sr and Elizabeth (O'Neil) Eisenman. Roger was preceded in death by his parents, his brothers, Arthur Eisenman, Jr. and Mark Eisenman, his sister, Betty Eisenman and his beloved wife Patricia.

Roger married the love of his life, Patricia Bauman, on August 7, 1951. Together they built a devoted family and were blessed with three children: Louie (Cheryl) of Schofield, Wisconsin; Anne (Russ) Johnson of Buckeye, Arizona; and Amy Meyer of Scottsdale, Arizona. They also have four grandchildren: Louie (Camelia) Eisenman of Eau Claire, Wisconsin, Lisa (Jeff) Suppon of Wausau, Wisconsin, Ryan Eisenman of Washington D.C., and Madysen (Mike) Masino of Sunrise, Florida, as well as two great grandchildren, Jennifer and Fernando and many nieces and nephews.

A proud combat veteran of World War II, Roger enlisted in the U.S. Coast Guard at the age of 17. He served aboard the USCG Cutter Woodbine as a radar operator throughout the Pacific Theatre, including Saipan, the Mariana Islands, and Okinawa. After the war, he was honorably discharged, returned home and completed his education, graduating from Wausau High School in 1947.

Roger Spent the majority of his career with WSAU Radio, beginning in sales in 1953 and rising to station manager in 1976. He was respected for his leadership, integrity, and strong work ethic.

Roger loved sports, especially baseball. He played in the Marathon County League, Wisconsin Valley League, and City Fast Pitch Softball League. He also coached youth baseball for many years, serving boys of all ages in Youth Baseball Minors, Little League, Pony League, Senior League, Babe Ruth, and American Legion. He served as President of Little League and was inducted to the Wausau Youth Baseball and Softball Wall of Honor in 2020. He was also active in the community as President of Grant School PTA and the Citywide PTA Council. In addition, he served as Publicity Director for the Wisconsin Valley Fair. Roger also liked to bowl and golf.

Music also had a big influence on his life. He was a member of the Wausau Barbershoppers for 75 years. He performed with the River Valley Harmonizers, performing in the annual Barbershop show, singing Valentines, the annual Christmas concerts, many weddings and other various events. He was named Barbershopper of the Year three times and was affectionately known as "Mr. Barbershopper."

Most importantly, Roger's faith was central to his life. He was active from an early age in St. James Church and later in the Church of the Resurrection, where he sang in the choir most of his adult life. His faith was an enduring source of strength and guidance throughout his life.

Roger will be remembered for his devotion to his family, his service to his country, his contributions to his community, and his lifelong love of music, sports, and faith. He leaves behind a legacy that will be cherished by all who knew and loved him.

In his last weeks, Roger was comforted by the caring team from Aspirus Hospice. His family wishes to extend special gratitude to Kari, Carrie, Jason and Chris at Aspirus, as well as Char and the entire team from Careview Transitional Care (formerly Pride TLC) for providing exemplary long term assisted living care.

A visitation will be held July 16th, 2026 from 9:30 am to 10:30 am at the Church of the Resurrection in Wausau. Funeral Mass to follow with Father Tom Lindner officiating. Burial will follow at Restlawn Memorial Park.

records.name: Sandi K. Higgins
records.summary: Sandi K. Higgins, age 75, of Edgar passed away on Jul 13, 2026.
records.body:
Sandra K. “Sandi” Higgins, 75, Edgar passed away on Monday, July 13, 2026 while under the care of St. Croix Hospice in Edgar.

She was born May 4, 1951 in Saginaw, Michigan, daughter of the late Lyle and Mary (Szarenski) Roberson. Sandi graduated from Midland High School in Midland, Michigan. She married Howard Higgins Jr. in 1970 in LaPorte, Michigan. They lived in Oskaloosa, Iowa, Ossineke, Michigan, Ironwood, Michigan and traveled in a motor home since 2000. Her summer home was in Edgar near family.

Sandi enjoyed camping, watching her children and grandchildren play sports, traveling, machine embroidery, sewing, cross stitch and other crafts. She was a lifelong camper and had the chance to visit 47 of the 50 states. Sandi worked for Dow Chemical in Midland, MI CLOW Corporation in Oskaloosa, Iowa and managed an insurance office in Ironwood, MI for 8 years and also worked for and the Michigan Bowling Association.

Survivors include her husband of 56 years, Howard Higgins, her sons, Michael (Debbie) Higgins, Spencer, and their children, Leah, Chase and Judd, Corey (Tina) Higgins, Edgar and their children, Caden, Carver, Cael and Caius, her brother and sisters, Mary (Gregg) Johns, Midland, MI, Debbie (Wayne) Rivette, Freeland, MI, Ed (Thelma) Roberson, Midland, MI, Connie (Dave) Varner, Midland, MI, Jenny (Dick) Gordert, Midland, MI, Tom (Sue) Roberson, Rhodes, MI, Chris (Claudio) Roberson, West Olive, MI, sister-in-law, Sue Roberson, Freeland, MI and numerous nieces and nephews.

Sandi was preceded in death by her parents, Lyle and Mary Roberson, her sisters, Linda Roberson and Karen Fitzgerald, her brother, Randy Roberson, her sister-in-law, Karen (Fitzwater) Roberson and niece, Stefanie Roberson.

At Sandi’s request there will be no formal services. Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Edgar is in charge of arrangements. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Tina M. Andrasek
records.summary: Tina M. Andrasek, age 56 passed away on Jul 13, 2026.
records.body:
Tina Andrasek, 56, passed away unexpectedly on Monday, July 13, 2026.

She was born on January 24, 1970, in Chicago, Illinois, the daughter of the late Edwin Formas and Barthalamia Formas. Tina married Kevin Andrasek, her childhood neighbor and the love of her life, on July 8, 1995. Together they created a family and a home based on love, support, and compassion.

Tina enjoyed cooking, baking, making stained glass, fishing, and reading, but most of all she loved her family fiercely. She always loved to prepare and host meals with Kevin, her daughters, and family and friends. She savored spending time on the lake with the people she loved, and she consistently caught the biggest fish.

Tina was vivacious, funny, passionate, kind, and brave. We will truly miss her laugh, her jokes, and her support.

Survivors include her mother, Barthalamia Formas; husband, Kevin Andrasek; daughters, Mia and Macy Andrasek; sisters, Christina Egan and Paris Casey; and many other nieces, nephews, extended family, and dear friends.

She was preceded in death by her father, Edwin Formas; and brother, Edwin Formas Jr.

The Funeral Liturgy will be at 12:00 p.m. on Saturday, July 18, 2026, at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Avenue, Wausau, with Deacon Ervin Burkhardt presiding. Visitation will begin on Friday, July 17 at 4:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. and continue on Saturday beginning at 10:30 a.m. until the time of service, all at the funeral home. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Donna  Jean Srb
records.summary: Donna  Jean Srb, age 84 passed away on July 14, 2026.
records.body:
Donna Jean Srb, born on June 21, 1942, to the late Harry and Ruby (Genke) Paul, passed away peacefully on July 14, 2026, under the compassionate care of Compassus Hospice, with her loving family by her side.

Donna had a deep love for all animals, especially her birds, whom she faithfully cared for every day, and her beloved dog, Oscar. She loved Oscar so much that she often told her family he was the reason she chose to undergo chemotherapy. That was Donna—sassy, spunky, and witty, with a personality that brought smiles to those around her.

Family meant everything to Donna. She especially cherished her grandchildren, who loved her as much as she loved them. She also grew to love her neighborhood, where friends became like family.

Donna is lovingly survived by her children, Jennifer (Val) Cihaski, Kim (Todd) Shnowske, and Steven Srb and his significant other, Emily Normand; her grandchildren, Megan, Matthew, Wade, and Molly; and her cherished fur baby, Oscar.

In addition to her parents, Donna was preceded in death by her grandson, Trent Shnowske; her sisters, Joan Regan and Marjorie Beaudoin; and her husband, George.

The family would like to extend their heartfelt gratitude to Dr. Ontitillo and his team, Compassus Hospice, and the staff at CareView for the exceptional care, compassion, and kindness they showed Donna throughout her journey.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences and words of remembrance may be shared at MWCS.ws.

records.name: Gregory Edward Bethke
records.summary: Gregory Edward Bethke, age 83, of Rhinelander passed away on July 14, 2026.
records.body:
Gregory E Bethke, age 83, of Rhinelander, died on July 14, 2026, in Rhinelander. He was born on September 25, 1942 in Milwaukee, Wisconsin the son of Gregory and Loretta (Packee) Bethke. Greg was a Civil Engineer for the Wisconsin DOT for over 42 years. Outside of his family and work, Greg's pride and joy was his ZR1 - 635 hp - Corvette.

Greg is survived by his wife Judy, his daughter Melanie (Jason) Towner his son Gregory (Amber) Bethke, his grandchildren Hudson and Norah Bethke and Greyson and Dylan Towner, his great grandchild Colt Bethke, his sister Jeanne (John) Burkhardt, his stepchildren Nikki, Mou and Andrea and his special friends Ben Marquardt and Deborah Pueschner.

Visitation for Greg will be held on Tuesday, August 11, 2026 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM, with a Mass of Christian Burial following at 11:00 AM, all at the Nativity of Our Lord Catholic Church. Father Jerome D"Souza will be officiating. Interment will follow in the Nativity of Our Lord Catholic Cemetery. A complete notice will follow shortly. You may leave your private condolences for the Bethke family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the Bethke family.

records.name: Kelley Marie Heidemann
records.summary: Kelley Marie Heidemann, age 67 passed away on Jul 14, 2026.
records.body:
Kelley Marie Heidemann passed away on July 14, 2026, at the age of 67. Born on December 21, 1958, in Antigo, Wisconsin, to Harold and Pearl (Jensen) Boyle, Kelley was a true gem who will be dearly missed by her family and friends.

She married the love of her life, Timothy Heidemann, on May 7, 1993, at Our Saviours in Merrill, and together they created a warm and loving home filled with laughter and joy. Kelley's spirit was infectious; she had the remarkable ability to listen without judgment and was a loyal friend to many. Outgoing and sociable, she embraced life with a fun-loving attitude.

Kelley had a soft spot for animals, especially her beloved Lhasa Apso dogs. She loved exploring flea markets, craft shows, and antique markets, and had a fondness for the Irish Heritage. A strong-willed woman, Kelley dedicated over 25 years to her career as a Technical Analyst at Church Mutual.

She is survived by her husband, Timothy; children, Heath Heidemann and Heather (Jason) Wolff; grandchildren, Cade Heidemann, Aaron Wolff, and Allison Wolff. Kelley is also survived by her sister-in-law, Luanne Boyle.

Kelley was preceded in death by her parents, Harold and Pearl; her son, Justin Ames; her brothers, Dwane Boyle and Robert "Bob" Boyle; her sister, Kathleen Watson; and her brother-in-law, Richard Watson.

In honor of Kelley's love for animals, the family kindly requests that, in lieu of flowers, donations be made in her name to the Lincoln County Humane Society.

A special thank you goes out to the wonderful staff at Pine Crest Nursing Home and the Aspirus Merrill Hospital for their exceptional care.

Visitation will be held July 20th at Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, 301 S. Eagle Dr, Merrill from 9am-11am, with service to follow at 11am. Burial will follow the service at Merrill Memorial Park Cemetery.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Sandra Jean McKitrick
records.summary: Sandra Jean McKitrick, age 77, of Rhinelander passed away on July 14, 2026.
records.body:
Sandra J McKitrick, age 77, of Rhinelander, died on July 14, 2026, at her home. She was born on October 4, 1948 in Manitowoc, Wisconsin the daughter of Paul and Marjorie (Plinske) Aumann. Sandra's survivors include her husband Dr. Larry McKitrick, her sons Thomas (Charlene) McKitrick and Scott McKitrick and her grandchildren Haley and Scott.

Arrangements are incomplete at this time. A complete notice will follow shortly. You may leave your private condolences for the McKitrick family at www.carlsonfh.com. The Carlson Funeral Home (715-369-1414) is serving the McKitrick family.

records.name: Adyn N. Roberts
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: SPC Adyn N. Roberts, 23, of an unspecified town, passed away on June 23, 2026.
records.body:
SPC Adyn N. Roberts, 23, passed away on Tuesday, June 23, 2026, while on active duty with the United States Army stationed at Schofield Barracks, Hawaii.

Adyn was born on April 21, 2003, in Wausau, Wisconsin, to Joshua Roberts and Niki Glisch. He graduated from D.C. Everest Senior High in 2021.

Adyn was known for bringing humor and sarcasm into every conversation. He was happiest when he was goofing around, making others laugh, and turning ordinary moments into fun. He had an answer for everything and seemed to know how to test the limits. Adyn also loved eating Ramen, BMX biking, video games, snowboarding, and was an avid fan of the Pittsburgh Steelers. Most of all, he loved his two children more than words can express.

He made the choice to better himself and his family by enlisting in the United States Army in 2023. He was currently stationed in Hawaii, proud of his role as a 13B Field Artillery Cannon Crewmember, working as part of a team responsible for operating and maintaining field artillery weapons.

From a skater boy with a man bun to a soldier – Adyn and his smile will never be forgotten by those who knew and loved him.

Adyn is survived by his wife, Trinity Roberts; their children, Tru and Rio; his father, Josh (Megan) Roberts; his mother, Niki Glisch; his siblings, Maddi, Will, Rhianan, Roman, Alissa, and Brenyn; and nephew and nieces, Colt, Eevie, and Malia. He is further survived by his grandparents, aunts, uncles, cousins, and other relatives and friends.

Funeral service will be held at 5:00 PM on Sunday, July 19, 2026, at Mount Olive Lutheran Church, 6205 Alderson Street, Weston. Visitation will be held from 2:00 PM until the time of service at the church. Military Honors will be accorded by the United States Army Honor Guard.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Bernette Bernice Steffenhagen
records.summary: Bernette Bernice Steffenhagen, age 95 passed away on Jul 15, 2026.
records.body:
Bernette Bernice Steffenhagen, age 95, passed away peacefully in the Aspirus Merrill Emergency Room with family by her side on July 15, 2026. She was born February 26, 1931 to the late Erna (Waldow) and Bernard Barkow in Athens, Wi. She graduated from Athens High School. Bernette was married to Alvin Steffenhagen on September 1, 1956 at Trinity Lutheran Church in Athens.

Bernette retired from Wausau Insurance Company in 1992 from the supervisory position which she held for 42 years. She was a member of the Wausau Insurance Retired Women’s Club and Wausau Insurance Quarter Century Club. During her retirement she was an election poll worker for the town of Berlin for 19 years. She is a member of Trinity Lutheran Church Merrill, Wi.

Prior to moving to Pinecrest she enjoyed going to polka dances, going out to eat, and going on road trips with her husband and friends. Bernette enjoyed embroidering, doing crafts, baking and cooking for her family, putting puzzles together, and doing word search books. Bernette meticulously manicured her lawn and was very proud of her yard and home. She loved spending time with her grandchildren and great grandchildren.

Bernette is survived by her daughter, Dawn Teske of Merrill; grandsons, Josh (Casi) Teske of Rib Mountain, Tyler Teske of Merrill; great-granddaughters, Brooklyn and Brianne Teske. She is also survived by her brother, Dale (Rosie) Barkow of Athens and brother-in-law, Lowell (Marie) Steffenhagen of Merrill, along with many other relatives and friends.

She is preceded in death by her husband, Alvin; her parents, brother, Marvin Barkow, Sister in-law Evelyn Barkow and son-in-law, David Teske.

Visitation will be held on Monday, July 27, 2026 from 9am-11am at Trinity Lutheran Church Merrill. Service to follow at 11am with Rev. Jonathan Chapa officiating. Burial will immediately follow the service at St. Paul’s Cemetery, Naugart.

The family would like to thank the staff at Pinecrest Nursing Home for their exceptional care that they provided for Bernette for the past year and one-half. A special thank you to Olivia Barker, a CNA at Pinecrest, whom she formed a special bond with. Also Thank You to the Paramedics and the staff at Aspirus Merrill Hospital.

Taylor-Stine-Waid Funeral & Cremation, Merrill, is assisting the family. Online condolences may be made at www.taylorstinewaid.com

records.name: Betty Jane Tasse
records.summary: Betty Jane Tasse, age 93, of Granton passed away on Jul 15, 2026.
records.body:
Betty Jane Tasse, 93, formerly of Granton, was called home on July 15, 2026, at Clark County Rehab & Living Center in Owen.

A private memorial service will be held with a public celebration of life taking place at a later date.

Betty was born on March 27, 1933, on the family farm in Lynn to George and Dorothy (Heinrich) Schmitz. She attended Granton schools and Marshfield High School. On August 19, 1952, she was united in marriage to Cyril Tasse at Corpus Christi Catholic Church in Bakerville. They were married 49 years until Cyril’s passing in 2001.

Betty worked several jobs including Figi’s, farming, and cheese sales at Lynn Dairy. Once retired, she continued doing what she loved - cleaning houses with her daughter Barb until 2020.

Betty loved gardening, baking, cooking, and most of all - spending time with her grandkids and great grandkids. She was honored as the Granton Grandparent of the Year in 2008. Betty was best known for her Sunday chicken dinners with rice tators, and all the fixings, dumplings, and sauerkraut. She was also known for her delicious chocolate chip cookies, Christmas cookies, and candies.

Betty enjoyed bowling for Lynn Dairy and playing softball and volleyball at Chili Corners.

She was a member of St. Stephen’s Catholic Church in Chili, where she sang in the choir and was active with the PCCW.

She is survived by her children: Jim (Amy), Tom (Barb), Barb (Bob), Mary (Brian), Lori, and Deb (Tim); 9 grandchildren: Erin, Amanda (John), Ben (Jackie), Mandy (Alex), Zac (Lauren), Libby (Erik), Hannah (Jake), Jenny (Josh), and Isabelle; and 15 great grandchildren: Braden (Syd), Austin, Peyton, Brynlie, Jack, Stella, Andrew, Penelope, Sloan, Collins, Peter, Salvatore, Simon, Clara, and Elle.

She is further survived by many nieces, nephews, and great nieces and nephews.

She was preceded in death by her parents, George and Dorothy Schmitz; husband, Cyril Tasse; granddaughter, Rebecca Christensen; son-in-law, Mark Schroeder; and brothers and sisters in law, LaMar (Thelma June) Schmitz, Ronnie (Shelby) Schmitz, Jerry (Lila) Schmitz, Fred Tasse, Edwin (Dorothy) Tasse, Greg (Coletta) Tasse, Sylvester (Lorraine) Tasse, Cecilia (Rich) Weyer, Dolores (Joe) Czaikowski, and Innocence (John) Rhodes.

The family would like to extend a special thank you to the amazing staff of 1 South & 2 West at Clark County Rehab & Living Center - especially Ren, Cherry and Joe, St. Croix Hospice, Falon, Kirsten, Roxy, Caleb, and Chaplain Mike, and the David and Sadie Borntreger family.

In lieu of flowers, memorials will be established in Betty’s name to Feast of Crispian, St. Jude’s Children’s Hospital, and Special Olympics.

records.name: Daniel Jakubowski
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Daniel Jakubowski, 74, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on July 11, 2026.
records.body:
Daniel Jakubowski, age 74, of Weston, Wisconsin, was called home to be with Jesus on Saturday, July 11, 2026, at home surrounded by his loving family.

Born on August 9, 1951, in Wausau, Wisconsin, Dan was the son of John and Joyce (Eggebrecht) Jakubowski. He married the love of his life, Ruth Bahlow, on July 23, 1983, and the couple enjoyed nearly 43 years together.

Dan built a career that reflected both skill and dedication, working for Wausau Homes in the construction and electrical fields. In his professional life, he was recognized for his work ethic and commitment to excellence. His hands helped shape not just buildings, but the lives and memories of countless families who came to call those structures home.

Away from work, Dan truly thrived in the pursuits that brought him joy and peace. An avid sportsman, he could often be found fishing along quiet banks or hunting in the early hours, taking in the natural beauty of Wisconsin's woods and waters. He delighted in tending his garden, nurturing life from the earth with the same care he showed to those around him. Woodworking was another of Dan's passions, and he approached each project with creativity, patience, and pride, transforming simple materials into works of usefulness and art.

Dan's love for his favorite sports teams was well known, and he rarely missed the chance to cheer on the Brewers and Packers, following their seasons with unwavering enthusiasm. His wit was quick and ever-present; friends and family recall a man who could light up a room with a clever turn of phrase or a well-timed joke. Above all, Dan cherished the time spent with his loved ones, never missing an opportunity to share his warmth, humor, and, as he often joked, his unique ability at "driving his wife crazy." All who knew Daniel will remember him as witty, kind-hearted, and steadfast. His presence enriched the lives of those around him, leaving behind a legacy of laughter, devotion, and honest work.

Dan is survived by his wife, Ruth; and son, Ryan Jakubowski. He is also survived by his sisters, Judie (Dave) Rheinschmidt, Annie Ohrmundt, and Cindy Hoenish; his brother, Jon (Renee) Jakubowski; brothers-in-law, John (Susan) Bahlow and Charles Bahlow; nephew, Christopher (Cara) Bahlow; and niece, Tina Bahlow.

He is preceded in death by his paternal grandparents, John and Tekla (Bermbenck) Jakubowski; maternal grandparents, Fredick and Emily (Natschke) Eggebrecht; his parents, John and Joyce (Eggebrecht) Jakubowski; parents-in-law, Elmer and Erna (Kurth) Bahlow; sister-in-law, Cathy Bahlow; and nephew, Zachery Bahlow.

A memorial service for Dan will be held at 11:00 AM on Saturday, July 25, 2026, at Our Savior's Ev. Lutheran Church, 703 Flieth Street, Wausau. Pastor Nathan Wranovsky will officiate. Visitation will be held from 10:00 AM until the time of service at the church. Dan will be laid to rest at a later date.

In lieu of flowers, memorials in Dan's name may be directed to Our Savior's Ev. Lutheran Church in Wausau, or Northland Lutheran High School in Kronenwetter.

The family would like to say a special thank you to Interim Healthcare Hospice Services and the wonderful team who cared for us, including Wendy, Jen, Marrol, Nick, Jill, Chaplain Mike, and Ashley, all of whom went above and beyond to care for us like family.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Donna J. Srb
records.summary: Donna Jean Srb, 84, of an unspecified town, passed away on July 14, 2026.
records.body:
Donna Jean Srb, born on June 21, 1942, to the late Harry and Ruby (Genke) Paul, passed away peacefully on July 14, 2026, under the compassionate care of Compassus Hospice, with her loving family by her side.

Donna had a deep love for all animals, especially her birds, whom she faithfully cared for every day, and her beloved dog, Oscar. She loved Oscar so much that she often told her family he was the reason she chose to undergo chemotherapy. That was Donna—sassy, spunky, and witty, with a personality that brought smiles to those around her.

Family meant everything to Donna. She especially cherished her grandchildren, who loved her as much as she loved them. She also grew to love her neighborhood, where friends became like family.

Donna is lovingly survived by her children, Jennifer (Val) Cihaski, Kim (Todd) Shnowske, and Steven Srb and his significant other, Emily Normand; her grandchildren, Megan, Matthew, Wade, and Molly; and her cherished fur baby, Oscar.

In addition to her parents, Donna was preceded in death by her grandson, Trent Shnowske; her sisters, Joan Regan and Marjorie Beaudoin; and her husband, George.

The family would like to extend their heartfelt gratitude to Dr. Ontitillo and his team, Compassus Hospice, and the staff at CareView for the exceptional care, compassion, and kindness they showed Donna throughout her journey.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family with arrangements. Online condolences and words of remembrance may be shared at MWCS.ws.

records.name: Frank Decker
records.funeral_home: Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston
records.summary: Frank Decker, also known as Joe, 80, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away on July 13, 2026.
records.body:
Frank Decker, also known as Joe, age 80, of Weston, Wisconsin, passed away peacefully at home, surrounded by his loving family, on July 13, 2026.

Frank was born on July 12, 1946, in Wisconsin, the son of Frank and Neva (Sulik) Decker. On May 27, 1967, he married the love of his life, Judy Feldbruegge, and together they shared 59 wonderful years of marriage.

Throughout his life, Frank found joy in life's simple pleasures. He had a passion for woodworking, hunting, fishing, and camping, reflecting his love of both nature and craftsmanship. Whether working with his hands or enjoying the quiet beauty of the outdoors, Frank was truly at home. Music—especially polka—held a special place in his heart and often filled family gatherings with laughter, dancing, and togetherness.

Frank was blessed with a remarkable sense of humor that drew people to him and endeared him to everyone who knew him. His presence brought smiles and laughter, making even ordinary days memorable. Above all, Frank cherished time spent with his family, treasuring every opportunity to create lasting memories together.

Frank is lovingly survived by his wife, Judy; his children, Angela (Kevin) Holz, Julie (Kenny) Meis, and Jason Decker; his grandchildren, Nicholas, Christopher, Amber, and Kayla; his great-grandchildren, Barbara and Conner; and his siblings, Rachel and Mike.

He was preceded in death by his parents, Frank and Neva Decker, and his siblings, Fred, Chris, and Claudette.

A Mass of Christian Burial will be held in Frank's honor on Friday, July 24, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. at St. Therese Catholic Church, 113 W. Kort Street, Schofield. Fr. Janusz Kowalski will officiate. Visitation will be held at the church from 10:00 a.m. until the time of Mass. Frank will be laid to rest following the service at Gate of Heaven Cemetery in Kronenwetter.

Ascend Funeral Home & Cremation Care of Weston is assisting the family with arrangements. Condolences may be shared at www.ascendweston.org.

records.name: Jimmy H. Moon
records.summary: Jimmy Harvey Moon, 78, of Merrill, passed away on July 11, 2026.
records.body:
Jimmy Harvey Moon, 78, of Merrill, passed away peacefully at his home on Saturday, July 11, 2026, with his loving family by his side.

Jimmy was born on March 19, 1948, in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, to the late Charles and Catherine (Murphy) Moon. He was deeply proud of his Native heritage and honored to be a member of the Stockbridge-Munsee Band of Mohican Indians. His heritage was an important part of who he was, and he carried that pride throughout his life.

On July 3, 1976, Jimmy married the love of his life, Debbie Laske. Their time together was far too short, as Debbie passed away on December 8, 1987, from complications of lupus. Although she was taken from him much too soon, Jimmy continued to love her every day for the rest of his life. This year would have marked what would have been their 50th wedding anniversary.

Jimmy had a deep appreciation for the outdoors and for living a simple, meaningful life. He was a lifelong Green Bay Packers fan and rarely missed cheering on his team. He loved hunting and took great pride in tanning his own deer hides. Horses were one of his greatest passions, and many of his family's favorite memories were made riding trails together. Those times spent on horseback brought laughter, adventure, and countless stories that will be cherished forever.

As a young boy, Jimmy raised sheep and, with patience and determination, made a blanket from the wool himself. It was just one example of his hardworking nature and his ability to create something meaningful with his own hands. He and his daughter Jessie also raised pigs together when she was a young girl, and Jimmy was looking forward to Jessie getting a new house built so the two of them could again raise pigs together.

Above everything else, Jimmy treasured his family. Nothing brought him greater happiness than spending time with those he loved. He was incredibly proud of his daughter, Jessie, and his grandchildren, who were truly the light of his life. He loved each of them unconditionally and always wanted to make things better for them. Whether offering advice, lending a helping hand, or simply being there when he was needed, his family always knew they could count on him. They were his everything.

His family will forever be grateful for his unconditional love, steady guidance, and the many lessons he shared throughout the years, like: "If you're riding and see a bear in the woods, just hold on—the horse knows where to go." It perfectly reflected his trust, wisdom, and calm approach to life.

Jimmy is lovingly survived by his daughter, Jessie "Baby"- Jessica Zulliger; his grandchildren, Shylo Moon and fiancée Rebecca, Kyle (Jenna) Zulliger, Kira (Tyler) Stone, and Daniel Zulliger and his significant other, Heather; his great-grandchildren, Matthew, Riley, Nate, Adalee, and Ellie; his brothers, Charles Moon and Delbert Moon; and many nieces, nephews, cousins, great-nieces, and great-nephews.

In addition to his parents and his beloved wife, Debbie, Jimmy was preceded in death by his sisters, Nora (Robert) Hooker, Charlene Moon, and Laura Moon; and his sister-in-law, Linda Moon.

A graveside service will be held at 1:00 p.m. on Thursday, July 16, 2026, at Woodlawn Cemetery, W15626 Woodlawn Road, Birnamwood, WI 54414, in the Town of Hutchins. Family and friends are invited to gather afterward for food and refreshments at Hutchins Town Hall, W15698 Red River Rd E, Birnamwood, WI 54414.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family with arrangements. Words of remembrance and online condolences may be left at MWCS.WS.

Jimmy will be remembered for his quiet strength, kind heart, unwavering devotion to his family, and the love he carried for those closest to him throughout his life. His legacy will live on in the memories he created, the values he instilled, and the stories his family will continue to share for generations to come.

records.name: JoAnn VerBunker Plano
records.summary: JoAnn VerBunker Plano, age 89 passed away on Jul 15, 2026.
records.body:
JoAnn V. Plano was born on December 22, 1936, as an only child to Carl O. Olson and Josephine M. Olson (VerBunker) in Wisconsin Rapids, Wisconsin. She grew up in Port Edwards, Wisconsin and graduated from high school there. After high school she married James E. Faehling and had three children, Mary Jo, James C. and Michael J. Faehling.

After she and James divorced, JoAnn went on to attend U.W. Stevens Point where she graduated with a Bachelor of Science degree. During this time, she met the love of her life, Franklin V. Plano and they married in 1971. They lived in Wausau for their entire marriage. JoAnn worked for the State of Wisconsin Job Service until she retired in 1999.

JoAnn spent many years tracing her family genealogy and self-published 8 books under "Good Heart Publications" on these lineages. She visited extended family sites in Canada and France. She completed much of her writing at their cottage on Lake Tomahawk, Wisconsin. She also enjoyed entertaining at the lake and at home serving elaborate dinners for family and friends.

She and Frank traveled to all of the contiguous U.S. states during summer vacations. Frank often said that JoAnn knew every shopping mall, restaurant, hotel, motel and rest stop between Bangor, Maine and San Diego, California.

JoAnn loved her family and she always made sure to remember birthdays of her three children, 8 grandchildren, and 16 great grandchildren. She was a loving wife, mother, grandmother and will truly be missed.

JoAnn is survived by her loving husband of 54 years, her sons James Faehling (Joan) and Michael Faehling (Becky). She is preceded in death by her parents and her daughter Mary Jo Winnie.

The family would also like to thank Sylvan Crossings on Evergreen Road, Wausau for the assisted living care they provided JoAnn the past 2 ½ years.

A visitation will be held Saturday, July 25th, 2026, from 10 am - 11 am at Helke Funeral Home with a memorial service to follow at 11 am with Deacon Peter Burek officiating.

records.name: Michael D. Ekern
records.summary: Michael D. Ekern, 79, of Eland, passed away on July 12, 2026.
records.body:
Michael D. Ekern, 79, of Eland, died on Sunday, July 12, 2026, at Marshfield Medical Center, Weston.

Mike was born on May 25, 1947, in Rice Lake, the son of Duane and Mary (Reckenthaler) Ekern.

Mike was a United States Army Veteran, serving from 1968 until 1970. Mike was a proud veteran and was able to go on the Honor Flight in 2025, with his son, Scott.

On September 30, 1978, Mike was united in marriage to Katie Verkuilen.

Mike attended UWSP and graduated with a degree in Natural Resources. He worked for the DNR, traveling to South Dakota. Mike later discovered his love for building and working with his hands, which led him to his carpentry career. He worked as a Union Carpenter for Carpenters Local 804, until his retirement. Mike enjoyed hunting, fishing, traveling, being outdoors and making maple syrup. He was active in his community as an assistant boy scout leader and president of the Eland Historical Society. Mike loved spending time with his family and attending the kids' sporting activities.

Mike had a heart that always put others first, and he found joy in helping others. His compassion and kindness were shared with everyone he met. He leaves behind a legacy of love, selflessness, and genuine goodness that will never be forgotten.

Mike is survived by his wife, Katie; three children, Laura (Derek Grove) Ekern, Lindsay (Greg) Cole and Scott (Kristin) Ekern; eight grandchildren, Alex, Luke, Gavin, Landon, Lydia, Owen, Olivia and Jack; siblings, David (Debbie) Ekern, Maureen (Jim) Mantor and Marilyn (Ted Hegstorm) Brenna; brothers and sisters-in-law, Barbara Ostrowski, Betty (Elroy) Koskey, Richard (Julie) Verkuilen, Pat Miller and Joyce Verkuilen and many nieces, nephews and other relatives.

Mike was preceded in death by his parents and mother and father-in-law, Leslie and Hattie Verkuilen.

A Funeral Service will be held at 1 PM on Friday, July 31, 2026, at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home, Wittenberg. Pastor Wes Jedras will officiate. Visitation will be on Friday, from 11 AM until the time of service at the funeral home.

records.name: Michael Kuehn
records.summary: Michael Kuehn, age 56 passed away on July 15, 2026.
records.body:
Michael Kuehn, age 56, passed away peacefully on July 15, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital, after a long battle of illness.

Born on February 26, 1970, at Holy Cross Hospital in Merrill. He was the son of John and Elinor (Rowl) Kuehn. He worked at various factory jobs in his younger years. He enjoyed music, playing electric guitar, drawing, painting, working on old cars, metal working, and reading.

He attended Merrill High School and married his high school sweetheart, Becky Hoffman, on August 19, 1995.

He is survived by his wife, Becky; brother, Jon (Milagros) Kuehn; and loving dachshund, Winnie.

He was preceded in death by his parents, John and Elinor Kuehn; brother, Carl Kuehn; and loving dachshund Max.

The family would like to thank the Staff at Aspirus Merrill Hospital, Froedtert Hospital in Milwaukee, and Aspirus Wausau Hospital MAPS unit for the wonderful care.

Per Michaels request, there will be no services.

Mid Wisconsin Cremation Society is assisting the family. Online condolences and words of remembrance may be given at MWCS.WS.

records.name: Paulette A. Patzer
records.summary: Paulette A. Patzer, 78, of Wisconsin Rapids, Wisconsin, passed away on July 9, 2026.
records.body:
Paulette Ann (Panfil) Patzer, 78, of Wisconsin Rapids, Wisconsin, passed away on July 9, 2026. Born April 22, 1947, in Milwaukee County, Wisconsin, Paulette was the beloved daughter of Sigmund John Panfil and Florence (Iverson) Panfil.

Paulette was preceded in death by her fiercely beloved husband, Glendon Irvin Patzer; her father, Sigmund John Panfil, and her mother, Florence (Iverson) Panfil.

She is lovingly survived by her son, Sheldon Patzer, ex-daughter-in-law Connie Patzer; her daughter, Shannan Siekert, and son-in-law, Dennis Siekert; her grandchildren, Ryan Patzer, Bryanna Patzer, Leyton Patzer, Zackery Patzer and his wife, Cheyanne, Amanda Lynn Patzer, Ashton Patzer, Zade Patzer and his wife, Heidi, Kassie Schenck, Mackenzie Parr and her husband, Matthew, and Maverick Siekert; 17 cherished great-grandchildren; three siblings; and many extended family members, friends, former coworkers, and individuals whose lives were touched by her compassion and care.

Paulette was a woman defined by compassion, an unwavering work ethic, and a heart devoted to caring for others. Early in her career, she worked in the building now known as the U.S. Bank Center in Milwaukee, (was the First Wisconsin building) the tallest building in Wisconsin, and later worked at Sequoia Supply, in Wausau, Wisconsin. She eventually found her calling in healthcare, serving as a Certified Nursing Assistant at The Renaissance for nearly 20 years.

For Paulette, caring for others was never simply a job. She walked alongside people during some of the most challenging and vulnerable moments of their lives, offering dignity, comfort, and compassion when they needed it most. Her experience and wisdom extended beyond those entrusted to her care. She freely shared her knowledge and advice with the nurses and caregivers around her, helping shape stellar healthcare professionals who carried her lessons and compassion forward. Her influence lives on not only in the patients she cared for, but also in the caregivers she helped inspire.

Above all else, Paulette loved her family fiercely. The love she shared with her husband, Glendon, was equally fierce—a deep and enduring devotion that remained an extraordinary part of her life and the story of their family. Their love, commitment, and life together created a legacy that continues through their children, grandchildren, and great-grandchildren.

Throughout her life, Paulette found joy in creating and nurturing beautiful things. In her earlier years, she enjoyed sewing and painting figurines. She also possessed a long-standing green thumb and found happiness working in her garden. Her love of gardening became a passion she passed on to her daughter, Shannan, and, in time, Paulette found herself learning even more from the daughter to whom she had first given that gift. It was a beautiful reflection of their relationship and the way Paulette's lessons continued to grow through those she loved.

Those who knew Paulette will remember her compassionate spirit, her strength, and her incredible work ethic. She spent a lifetime caring for others—as a wife, mother, grandmother, great-grandmother, caregiver, mentor, and friend. She gave her time, her wisdom, and her heart freely.

The measure of Paulette's life is found in the countless people she comforted, the caregivers she inspired, the family she fiercely loved, and the legacy she leaves behind. Her hands cared for many, but her heart always belonged to her family.

Though deeply missed, Paulette's love will continue to live through every lesson she taught, every life she touched, and every memory her family carries forward. Her legacy will continue to bloom for generations, much like the gardens she so lovingly tended.

Family and friends are invited to gather in celebration of Paulette's life on Thursday, July 16, 2026, at 4:00 p.m. at Memorial Park in Wausau, Wisconsin 54403. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

Please join Paulette's family in celebrating her life. Guests are encouraged to bring a lawn chair, a memory, and a dish to pass, if they wish, as family and friends gather to share stories, remember, and celebrate a life so deeply loved.

records.name: Sandra K. Higgins
records.summary: Sandra K. "Sandi" Higgins, 75, of Edgar, passed away on July 13, 2026.
records.body:
Sandra K. "Sandi" Higgins, 75, Edgar passed away on Monday, July 13, 2026 while under the care of St. Croix Hospice in Edgar.

She was born May 4, 1951 in Saginaw, Michigan, daughter of the late Lyle and Mary (Szarenski) Roberson. Sandi graduated from Midland High School in Midland, Michigan. She married Howard Higgins Jr. in 1970 in LaPorte, Michigan. They lived in Oskaloosa, Iowa, Ossineke, Michigan, Ironwood, Michigan and traveled in a motor home since 2000. Her summer home was in Edgar near family.

Sandi enjoyed camping, traveling, machine embroidery, sewing, cross stitch and other crafts. She was a lifelong camper and had the chance to visit 47 of the 50 states. Sandi worked for Dow Chemical in Midland, MI CLOW Corporation in Oskaloosa, Iowa and managed an insurance office in Ironwood, MI for 8 years and also worked for and the Michigan Bowling Association.

Survivors include her husband of 56 years, Howard Higgins, her sons, Michael (Debbie) Higgins, Spencer, and their children, Leah, Chase and Judd, Corey (Tina) Higgins, Edgar and their children, Caden, Carver, Cael and Caius, her brother and sisters, Mary (Gregg) Johns, Midland, MI, Debbie (Wayne) Rivette, Freeland, MI, Ed (Thelma) Roberson, Midland, MI, Connie (Dave) Varner, Midland, MI, Jenny (Dick) Gordert, Midland, MI, Tom (Sue) Roberson, Rhodes, MI, Chris (Claudio) Roberson, West Olive, MI and numerous nieces and nephews.

Sandi was preceded in death by her parents, Lyle and Mary Roberson, her sisters, Linda Roberson and Karen Fitzgerald, her brother, Randy Roberson and her sister-in-law, Karen (Fitzpatrick) Roberson.

At Sandi's request there will be no formal services. Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Edgar is in charge of arrangements. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Shirley A. Philipp
records.summary: Shirley A. Philipp, age 90, of Tomahawk passed away on Jul 15, 2026.
records.body:
Shirley A Philipp

July 15, 2026

Shirley A Philipp, Tomahawk (and formerly from Wausau), 90, passed away peacefully on Wednesday July 15, 2026, under the care of the Aspirus Wausau Hospital Palliative unit. Shirley was born on November 16, 1935, in the township of Texas, Marathon County, WI to the late Max and Martha (Johlke) Hartig.

Shirley is survived by her daughters Patricia (Bob) Jumbeck and Pamela (Jim) Daniels, her Grandchildren Matthew (Jerica) Philipp, Stephanie (Jeff) Stone, Ashely (Tony) Davis, Jessica (Nick) Davis, and her Great Grandchildren Mason Koepke, Nova Haasch, Max Haasch, Cecelia Stone, Jaxton Philipp, Parker Philipp, Jedrix Philipp, Jaylex Philipp, and Maddix Philipp. She is proceeded in death by her late husband Robert Philipp, her 5 brothers and 5 sisters, her daughter Karen Philipp, and her son Robert Philipp.

Shirley and her late husband Robert owned and operated Northern Service Toyota in Wausau. After selling the dealership, Shirley continued her career in the Auto Industry as the Service Manager for Jim Buck Toyota and finally retired after serving in the health care industry at North Central Health Care.

Shirley had a passion for her time up north, being at the lake, playing cards, tending to her plants and flowers, having family and friends visit, steak and lobster on Christmas Eve, and a cold Miller Lite.

There will be a friends and family gathering in honor of Shirley on Sunday, August 2, 2026, from 2pm to 5 pm at 4 Father's Pub, 1724 Merrill Ave, Wausau, WI 54401.

records.name: Tina M. Andrasek
records.summary: Tina M. Andrasek, 56, of an unspecified town, passed away on July 13, 2026.
records.body:
Tina Andrasek, 56, passed away unexpectedly on Monday, July 13, 2026.

She was born on January 24, 1970, in Chicago, Illinois, the daughter of the late Edwin Formas and Barthalamia Formas. Tina married Kevin Andrasek, her childhood neighbor and the love of her life, on July 8, 1995. Together they created a family and a home based on love, support, and compassion.

Tina enjoyed cooking, baking, making stained glass, fishing, and reading, but most of all she loved her family fiercely. She always loved to prepare and host meals with Kevin, her daughters, and family and friends. She savored spending time on the lake with the people she loved, and she consistently caught the biggest fish.

Tina was vivacious, funny, passionate, kind, and brave. We will truly miss her laugh, her jokes, and her support.

Survivors include her mother, Barthalamia Formas; husband, Kevin Andrasek; daughters, Mia and Macy Andrasek; sisters, Christina Egan and Paris Casey; and many other nieces, nephews, extended family, and dear friends.

She was preceded in death by her father, Edwin Formas; and brother, Edwin Formas Jr.

The Funeral Liturgy will be at 12:00 p.m. on Saturday, July 18, 2026, at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 3400 Stewart Avenue, Wausau, with Deacon Ervin Burkhardt presiding. Visitation will begin on Friday, July 17 at 4:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. and continue on Saturday beginning at 10:30 a.m. until the time of service, all at the funeral home. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com.

records.name: Todd L. Brown
records.summary: Todd Lewis Brown, 69, of Waupaca, Wisconsin, passed away on July 10, 2026.
records.body:
Todd Lewis Brown, age 69, passed away unexpectedly on July 10, 2026.

Todd was born on June 26, 1957 in Tigerton, WI son of Lewis and Betty (Wesener) Brown. He graduated from Wittenberg-Birnamwood High School in 1975. In 1977 he earned a degree in Electronics from North Central Technical College in Wausau.

Todd started working at the Waupaca Foundry in 1979 doing electrical maintenance. He continued his education, while working, to become an Electrical Engineer. His expertise added to the Foundry's ability to expand their company across several states. After 35 years Todd retired.

Todd married Connie (Spieth) on April 26, 1980 in Tigerton, WI and together they had three children. Todd and Connie moved to Waupaca in 1985 where they raised their family.

Todd had a wide range of skills and could fix virtually anything. He was incredibly detail-oriented and took great pride in doing things the right way. He loved the challenge of rebuilding, repairing or refurbishing equipment. His expertise and knowledge of electrical was well known. From 1985 to present day, Todd worked as the electrician for several carnival companies where he would often travel with his family to county fairs to fix various rides.

Todd's true passion ignited in the mid 80's while automating privately owned hydroelectric dams enabling the owners to remotely manage water flow and turbine generation without continuous human intervention. Todd and Connie enjoyed traveling across multiple states in their motorhome while Todd worked those projects.

Todd played ball all his life, only stopping because work got in the way. His love of the game was passed down to his children and now carried on with his grandboys. He often told his grandboys, "I hit home runs so I didn't have to run the bases." He enjoyed watching his grandboys play sports any chance he got.

Todd will be greatly missed by his family and friends both local and around the country.

Todd is survived by his wife of 46 years, Connie, his children Derek (Sarah) Brown of Waupaca, WI, Melanie (Ryan) Gilles of Stevens Point, WI and Ryan (Christan) Brown of LaCresent, MN, and seven grandboys Keenan, Kelson & Kollin Gilles, Chapman & Channing Brown, Bronson & Dekker Brown, his brother Chris Brown, sister Sharla (John) Brown and several brother & sister in laws, nieces & nephews and extended family.

Todd is preceded in death by his parents, Lewis and Betty Brown, his in-laws Walter and Irene Spieth along with many of his aunts, uncles and cousins.

Visitation will be held Friday, July 17th from 4p-8p and Saturday, July 18th from 9a until the funeral service at 11a at Schmidt & Schulta Funeral Home in Wittenberg, WI. Burial will follow in the Evergreen Rest Cemetery in Elderon, WI

Memories and messages of support may be shared at schmidtschulta.com.

records.name: Virginia M. Zastrow
records.summary: Virginia M. Zastrow, 99, of the town of Wausau, passed away on July 12, 2026.
records.body:
Virginia M. Zastrow, 99, town of Wausau, passed away on Sunday, July 12, 2026 while under the care of St. Croix Hospice at Careview Transitional Care of Weston.

She was born December 21, 1926, in Wausau, daughter of the late August and Marie (Behnke) Tietz. On May 22, 1943 she married Fred Zastrow in Wausau. He preceded her in death on June 26, 2000.

Mom belonged to the Highland Grove Grade School Mothers Club and the Highland Homemakers Club. She could fix the electric cord on her clothes iron as well as adjust the carburetor on the car. Holidays were always special with decorations, gifts and special meals.

Mom and Dad enjoyed fishing trips to Canada later in life and spent years bowling and going to casinos.

After dad's passing, she enjoyed textile painting, Nintendo Wii, bowling, writing poetry and word find puzzles. Her treasured dog, Slick, kept her company.

She remained in the beloved farm home of 83 years until 10 days before her passing. Mom attended Grant, Franklin and Highland Grove grade schools in Wausau through the 8th grade. She was baptized and confirmed at St. Paul's United Church of Christ in Wausau. She attended Sunday School regularly.

Mom met dad because she was a good friend to Delores "Toots" Zastrow, dad's sister, during school days. Romance blossomed and they married on May 22, 1943 at a pastor's house in Wausau. A honeymoon trip to LaCrosse followed.

A family came along and Mom and Dad became farm owners in 1949. Through lean years Mom helped with hand milking, cooking, canning meats, fruits and vegetables and cared for her large family. Mom enjoyed doing beautiful pencil drawings, writing poetry and keeping a notebook of song lyrics in her younger years. After raising the family Mom began an extensive doll collection which she enjoyed thoroughly.

Survivors include her children, Linda Schroeder, Wausau, Donna Kufahl, Wausau, Elroy (Sharon) Zastrow, Wausau, Vicki Deckert, Wausau, Arlin Zastrow, Wausau, her grandchildren, Todd (Jane) Schroeder, Troy (Tammy) Schroeder, Ted (fiancé Michelle) Schroeder, Tiffany (Jeremy) Knopow, Blake (Beckie) Kufahl, Brett Kufahl, Brooke (Bob) Frenkel, Jennifer (Brad) Altergott, Scott Zastrow, Steven (Amanda) Zastrow, Karen Zastrow, Rod (Stacey) Zastrow, Kelly Zastrow, Alec (Bianca) Zastrow, Brittanie (Matt) Schulz, Amelia Zastrow, Dustin Dudek, Travis (Shania) Wistrom, Ryan (Meghan) Wistrom, Erika (Dan) Staffeil, Cody Smoker, Kyle Smoker, her great grandchildren, Adam (Amanda), Brad, Corwin, Griffin (Amanacer), Willow, Hudson, Hazel, Alexia, Alivia, Brock, Jadden, Logan, Samantha (Tyler), Brandon (Emilee), Morgahn, Siena, Oakley, Odessa, Natalee, Julian, Chanelle, Cole, Jenna, Brooke, Klaire, Gracie, Ember, Bria, Allie, Alex, Chase, George (Megan), Saxon (Aylissa) and great great grandchildren, Teagan, Llane, Mason, Lilah, Emma, Nora and numerous nieces and nephews.

Virginia was preceded in death by her parents, her husband Fred in 2000, her children, Wayne Zastrow in 1972, Garry Zastrow in 2009, Robin Zastrow in 2018 and Kathy Wistrom in 2026. She was also preceded in death by her sons-in-law, Richard "Dick" Schroeder, Gary Wistrom and Bruce Kufahl and her siblings, Henry (Ella) Tietz, Clarence (Marge) Tietz, Lorinda (Ralph) Wimmer, Hilda (Claude) Pettit, Harold (Christa) Tietz, August (Cathleen) Tietz, Emil Tietz and Verna Tietz.

Funeral services will be held at 11:00 a.m. Wednesday, July 22, 2026 at the Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau. The Rev. Chris Ewan will officiate. Burial will be in Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau. Visitation will be held on Wednesday from 9:30 a.m. until the time of services all at the Sixth Street funeral home. In lieu of flowers a memorial fund has been established in Virginia's name. Online condolences may be expressed at www.petersonkraemer.com

records.name: Donald J. Koeller
records.summary: Donald "Don" J. Koeller, 91, of Marathon, passed away on July 13, 2026.
records.body:
Donald "Don" J. Koeller, 91, Marathon, passed away peacefully on Monday, July 13, 2026, at Rennes Health and Rehab in Weston, under the care of Aspirus at Home Hospice.

He was born on December 23, 1934, in Wausau, son of the late Erwin and Lucinda (Harder) Koeller. On June 26, 1954 he married Lois H. Buttke at Trinity Lutheran Church, Stettin. The couple recently celebrated 72 years of marriage.

Don was a 1952 graduate of Wausau High School and played football under coach Win Brockmeyer. He started his career at Marathon Cheese working in the basement of Ray Goldbach's family and eventually went on to become a supervisor at Pauly Cheese in Wausau. He continued working as a painter for Goetsch's Welding and Machine into his 70's. Don kept busy as the Treasurer for the Town of Rib Falls for 54 years in addition to serving in many positions at St. Matthew Lutheran Church.

He enjoyed listening to music, especially polkas, waltzes and German Brass bands. At age 16 he began drumming for his high school band which extended to a 42 year career with the Jerry Goetsch Orchestra. Through the orchestra the travelling group Polka Productions was formed which allowed him and his wife to travel world-wide enjoying the sights, music and making new friends. He had an opportunity to perform at numerous Octoberfest celebrations in Munich Germany.

Don was a man of deep faith and love for his family. Among his favorite pastimes, he enjoyed playing Sheepshead and Cribbage with his children, grandchildren and great-grandchildren.

He was an avid sports fan and loved to watch the Wisconsin Badgers, Milwaukee Brewers and was a Green Bay Packers season ticket holder for 63 years. He never missed an opportunity to watch his grandchildren's sporting events all through their careers at Marathon High School.

Survivors include, his wife, Lois; four children, Gail (Brian) Gumtz, Karen Koeller, Bruce (Brenda) Koeller and Brian Koeller; six grandchildren, Nicholas (Christy) Gumtz, Michelle (John) Tatera, Brett (Lisa) Koeller, Caleb Koeller, Kolby Koeller and Jaden (Claire) Koeller; five great-grandchildren, Alexander and Dominic Gumtz, Jack Baumer, and Abigail and Benjamin Tatera; one brother, Robert Koeller; and many nieces, nephews.

Besides his parents, he was preceded in death by two brothers, Kenneth (infant) and Gerald; and his infant grandson, Brandon Koeller.

Private services will be held at St. Matthew Lutheran Church in Marathon.

In lieu of flowers, memorials may be directed to St. Matthew Lutheran Church Building Fund or the Little Lions Child Care in Marathon.

records.name: Jeanette Switlick
records.summary: Jeanette Switlick, 93, of Athens, passed away on July 13, 2026.
records.body:
Jeanette Switlick, 93, Athens passed away on Monday, July 13, 2026.

She was born January 29, 1933 in Athens. On May 26, 1951, she married Stanley Switlick at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens. He preceded her in death on March 31, 1996.

Jeanette was a proud homemaker and truly enjoyed raising her children and grandchildren and anyone who came through her door. Some of her favorite pastimes included embroidery, making quilts, playing cards, cooking, shopping, gardening and enjoying the company of her grandchildren and great grandchildren. Jeanette made baby quilts for all the grandchildren and great grandchildren.

She also enjoyed blueberry picking, visiting with people and living life to the fullest.

Survivors include her children, Mike (Kathy) Switlick, Lorraine (Donald) Sobjeck, Mark Switlick, Daniel (Laura) Switlick, Leanne (Victor) Smith, Francis (Jennifer) Switlick and Lori Switlick, her grandchildren, Kristine (Jose Argueta Granados) Switlick, Josh (Korina) Switlick, Justin (Tania) Switlick, Jeremy (Jessica) Switlick, Erin Sobjeck, Kyle Sobjeck, Kimberly (Joe) Ellis, Matt Switlick, Tiffany (Andrew) Lukasko, Melanie (Jordan) Locher, Jonathon Patton, Cassandra (AJ) Switlick, Nadine (Casey) Aho, Ryan (Samantha) Switlick, Russell (Amanda) Switlick and Megan Switlick, her great grandchildren, Blake, Brock, Luke, Jake, Landon, Eli, Everly, Oliver, Lyla, Madden, Finnley, Ashlyn, Emma, Holden, Elsie and soon Baby Girl Locher and her special cousin, Mabel Drewek.

Besides her parents and husband, she was preceded in death by three sons, Richard, Florian and Jerome "Mego" Switlick and her grandchildren, Keith Boehm, Amanda Boehm and Jennifer Patton and a great granddaughter Adrian Handrick.

The Funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 a.m. Tuesday, July 21, 2026, at St. Anthony Catholic Church, Athens. The Rev. George Graham will preside. Burial will be in Calvary Cemetery, Athens. Visitation will be held on Tuesday from 9:00 a.m. until the time of services at the church. Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, Athens is in charge of arrangements.

Lois E Wetterau, age 88, of Rhinelander, died on July 17, 2026, in Rhinelander. She was born on February 2, 1938 in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, the daughter of Raymond and Gertrude (Meier) Meyer. Survivors include her sons Jon (Patricia) Wetterau and James (Lori) Wetterau.
