records.name: Mary A. Schuld
records.summary: Mary A. Schuld, 88, of Metamora, Illinois, formerly of Mosinee and Wausau, Wisconsin, passed away on July 13, 2026.
records.body:
Mary Ann Schuld, 88, of Metamora, IL, formerly of Macomb, IL, Mosinee, WI, and Wausau, WI, passed away with her family by her side on Monday, July 13, 2026, at OSF Richard L. Owens Hospice Home in Peoria, IL.

She was born on April 8, 1938, in Wausau to the late Jacob and Sarah (Walters) Reuter. She married Ernest R Schuld on May 23, 1964, at St Mary Catholic Church in Wausau. He preceded her in death on February 5, 2018. Survivors include one son and two daughters, Sara (Brad) Payne of Metamora, IL; Alan (Marianne) Schuld of Huntsville, AL; and Dana (Tim) McVicker of Metamora, IL; nine grandchildren, Ashley (Steven) James, Austin and Alex Payne, Erin and Riley Schuld, Ashley and Luke Daniel, and Kaelyn Ray and Connor McVicker; one sister and one brother, Audrey Reuter of Wausau, WI, and Leon (Irene) Reuter of Lancaster, CA; two sisters-in-law and one brother-in-law, Irene Reuter, Joyce Schuld, and Michael Stack; and many nieces and nephews.

She was preceded in death by her parents; her husband; four brothers, Norman, Robert, Edwin, and Ralph Reuter; two sisters, Laverne Reuter and Delores Hamerla; several sisters- and brothers-in-law, several nieces and nephews, and many aunts and uncles.

She was very active in Girl Scouts, serving as a troop leader for 50 young girls, and was a church volunteer prior to marriage. She attended Viterbo College, a Franciscan institution in La Crosse, Wisconsin. She and her husband fostered high school children for several years before raising their own children. She was an owner and actively involved in the family manufacturing businesses in product development, accounting, and marketing roles. She also took pride in maintaining a pristine home and preparing tasty meals for her family. She was an avid Packer fan; and enjoyed sewing, camping, gardening, traveling, reading, knitting, playing cards with family and friends, and art in many forms including woodcarving and sketching.

The Funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:00 am on Thursday, July 23, 2026, at Holy Name of Jesus Parish, Wausau. Fr. Woodrow Pace will preside. A noon lunch at the Church will follow the services, followed by a Burial with graveside prayers in Restlawn Memorial Park, Wausau.

Visitation will be on Wednesday, July 22, 2026, from 3:00-7:00 at Peterson/Kraemer Funeral Home, 1302 Sixth Street, Wausau, and also on Thursday, July 23 from 10:00 until time of services at the church.

records.name: Mary Knippel-Stachowiak
records.summary: Mary Knippel-Stachowiak, 92, of Wausau, passed away on July 11, 2026.
records.body:
Mary Knippel- Stachowiak, age 92, passed away peacefully on July 11, 2026 at Mount View Care Center in Wausau. She was surrounded by her family.

Mary was born in Wausau on July 29, 1933 to Joseph and Theresa (Sneigoski) Jakubowski. She was the second of four daughters. On July 7,1956 she married William Knippel. They were married until his death on September 23, 1998. In 2001, she found happiness and companionship again by marrying Fabian (Buck) Stachowiak. He preceded her in death on May 5, 2021.

Mary worked for Wisconsin Public Service, at a law firm as a legal secretary, and years later, after raising her children, in the circulation department of the Wausau Daily Herald. Many of her friendships later in life were established from her job at the Daily Herald.

Outside of work Mary enjoyed gardening, attending church picnics and traveling. In her early years, she and William enjoyed roller skating together. Bill had a profound interest in photography and often used Mary as his model. She and Buck enjoyed taking cruises and together sailed over 30 times. Mary was an avid walker and could be found outside walking regardless of how hot or cold the Wisconsin temperatures were. She was firmly planted in her faith and consistently attended mass with both William and Buck. Mary and Bill volunteered for years at St. Mary's Church in Wausau helping with bingo, and later she and Buck loved going polka dancing at church festivals in between attending mass.

Mary is survived by her six children, Robert Knippel, Wausau, Kathy (Randy) Froehlich, Wausau, Ann (Gary) LaPorte, Kronenwetter, Joe (Sherrylynn Mueller) Knippel, Wausau, Monica (Andy) Lukas, Brookfield, Laura (Greg) Weyandt, Chetek, stepsons Fabian and Scott Stachowiak, 16 grandchildren and 7 great grandchildren. She was preceded in death by her husbands William and Buck, and her three sisters Virginia, Margie, and Annie.

The funeral Mass will be celebrated at 11:30 a.m. on Wednesday July 22, 2026 at St. Michael's Catholic Church in Wausau. Burial will be in Restlawn Memorial Park in Wausau. Visitation will be Wednesday at St. Michael's Catholic church from 10:30 a.m. until the time of service.

The family wishes to extend a very special thank you to all of the staff, caregivers, and volunteers at Mount View Care Center for all of the care, activities, and trips that they provided when family was no longer able to do so. The care and interaction that Mount View provided was exceptional and was deeply appreciated by both Mary and her entire family.

records.name: Michael Kuehn
records.summary: Michael Kuehn, 56, passed away on July 15, 2026.
records.body:
Michael Kuehn, age 56, passed away peacefully on July 15, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital, after a long battle of illness.

Born on February 26, 1970, at Holy Cross Hospital in Merrill. He was the son of John and Elinor (Rowl) Kuehn. He worked at various factory jobs in his younger years. He enjoyed music, playing electric guitar, drawing, painting, working on old cars, metal working, and reading.

He attended Merrill High School and married his high school sweetheart, Becky Hoffman, on August 19, 1995.

He is survived by his wife, Becky; brother, Jon (Milagros) Kuehn; and loving dachshund, Winnie.

He was preceded in death by his parents, John and Elinor Kuehn; brother, Carl Kuehn; and loving dachshund Max.

The family would like to thank the Staff at Aspirus Merrill Hospital, Froedtert Hospital in Milwaukee, and Aspirus Wausau Hospital MAPS unit for the wonderful care.

Per Michaels request, there will be no services.

records.name: Patricia M. Newman
records.summary: Patricia "Trish" Marie Newman, 67, of Wausau, passed away on July 11, 2026.
records.body:
Patricia "Trish" Marie Newman, 67, of Wausau, passed away on Saturday, July 11, 2026, at Aspirus Wausau Hospital. She was born on November 17, 1958, in Kenosha, Wisconsin, to the late Matthew and Eleanor Skender.

Trish was a very loving and caring warm-hearted woman and loved many things about life. She had health problems for the last 12 years and became wheelchair mobilized for about nine of them. She never let that stop her and made the best of what she could and had to work with to remain happy. She had a lot of memorable events in her 67-year history and a very astounding working career.

She graduated from Tremper High School in Kenosha. While a teenager she worked at Taco Bell, Pizza, Hut, and Villa Decarlo Pizza and Restaurant. During her 20s she continued to work as a cook and pizza maker at the aforementioned restaurants and was also a waitress at 3 Coins downtown restaurant and Sambo's. Trish met her future husband, John Newman, a neighbor in Kenosha, when they were both 25 years old. Together they moved to the Upper Peninsula of Michigan in 1985, and they bought property along a trout stream in Ironwood Township. John and Trish remodeled and modernized a 100-year-old cabin that was designated on the historical register in the Ottawa National Forest. She then began working and cooking at White Cap Mountain Ski Hill as a cooking supervisor.

Following her time at the ski hill, Trish went to work at the Grandview County Hospital in Ironwood, Michigan, in the housekeeping and environmental services departments. While at the hospital she took over the position of employee chairperson for the AFL-CIO county employee's union for all hospital workers. She was proud of the work she did for her fellow coworkers and was able to get many employees reinstated to their jobs from wrongful discharge including backpay compensation. She also attended Gogebic Community College while working at the hospital and received her Legal Business Degree after two years. She graduated with high honors and a 4.0 grade point average.

Upon completion of her schooling, Trish became a paralegal in the local the Ironwood-Bessemer-Wakefield Michigan area working for two attorney's offices and a property title company. She also was united in marriage to John Newman on July 2, 1994, having a big, beautiful wedding at Our Lady of Peace Catholic Church in Ironwood. They celebrated with a wonderful reception at the Ironwood Township Hall.

In 2000, they moved to Wausau and purchased a home together. Trish began working at Eastbay in the Investigative Fraud Department for two years and then moved on to Wausau Insurance. She worked there for five years as a customer service representative until they closed. Trish continued for another 10 years with WPS Insurance at the Westwood Center and then for two years with Tri-Care military insurance until her retirement due to health issues.

In addition to her work, Trish enjoyed music and many hobbies. She saw many musicians in concert including: Ringo Starr, Paul McCartney, Bruce Springsteen, Charlie Daniels, George Thorogood, Blondie, Cheap Trick, Alice Cooper, Billy Joel at Lambeau Field, and many others just to name a few. She enjoyed sewing, cooking, artwork, music, rock concerts, country music, boating and fishing on the Great Lakes and inland lakes of Wisconsin, pets, birds and animals in the yard, gardening, plants, travel, vacations, touring sports stadiums, computer games, board games, playing cards, movies, T.V. shows, and going to local county fairs. Trish was a big fan of the Green Bay Packers, Milwaukee Brewers, and the Wausau Woodchucks.

Survivors include her husband of 32 years, John Newman; brothers, Matthew "Buzzy" and Michael; sister, Jenny; many nieces and nephews, Jenny, Jamie, Michael, Mitchel, Justin, Kim, Todd, Tammy, and Dawn; and many other family that she truly and dearly loved.

She was preceded in death by her parents and siblings, Mary, Kathy, and Charles "Chuck."

The graveside service will be at 11:30 a.m. on Friday, July 24, 2026, in the Restlawn Memorial Park Chapel with Deacon Peter Burek presiding. Visitation will begin at 11:00 a.m. until the time of service in the chapel. Entombment will take place following services in the Restlawn Memorial Chapel Mausoleum.

records.name: Patricia "Patty" A. Jahnke
records.summary: Patricia "Patty" A. Jahnke, age 68, of La Crosse passed away on Jul 17, 2026.
records.body:
Patricia “Patty” A. Jahnke, 68, La Crosse, passed away on Friday, July 17, 2026 at Marshfield Medical Center.

A graveside service will be held at 2:00 pm on Tuesday, July 28, 2026 at Calvary Cemetery in Prairie du Chien.

Patty was born on March 14, 1958 in Milwaukee to William “Bill” and Elizabeth “Liz” (Nolan) Jahnke. She graduated from Columbus Catholic High School in 1977.

Patty worked various factory jobs and volunteer work through the years. She loved cats, collecting knickknacks - especially cat figurines, and spending time with her family.

She is survived by her brother, Edward Jahnke, and nieces and nephews, Maya, Jackson, Alexander, and Jonah.

She was preceded in death by her parents, Bill and Liz Jahnke.

records.name: Shirley A Philipp
records.summary: Shirley A Philipp, 90, of Tomahawk and formerly of Wausau, passed away on July 15, 2026.
records.body:
Shirley A Philipp, Tomahawk (and formerly from Wausau), 90, passed away peacefully on Wednesday July 15, 2026, under the care of the Aspirus Wausau Hospital Palliative unit. Shirley was born on November 16, 1935, in the township of Texas, Marathon County, WI to the late Max and Martha (Johlke) Hartig.

Shirley is survived by her daughters Patricia (Bob) Jumbeck and Pamela (Jim) Daniels, her Grandchildren Matthew (Jerica) Philipp, Stephanie (Jeff) Stone, Ashely (Tony) Davis, Jessica (Nick) Davis, and her Great Grandchildren Mason Koepke, Nova Haasch, Max Haasch, Cecelia Stone, Jaxton Philipp, Parker Philipp, Jedrix Philipp, Jaylex Philipp, and Maddix Philipp.

She is proceeded in death by her late husband Robert Philipp, her 5 brothers and 5 sisters, her daughter Karen Philipp, and her son Robert Philipp.

Shirley and her late husband Robert owned and operated Northern Service Toyota in Wausau. After selling the dealership, Shirley continued her career in the Auto Industry as the Service Manager for Jim Buck Toyota and finally retired after serving in the health care industry at North Central Health Care.

Shirley had a passion for her time up north, being at the lake, playing cards, tending to her plants and flowers, having family and friends visit, steak and lobster on Christmas Eve, and a cold Miller Lite.

There will be a friends and family gathering in honor of Shirley on Sunday, August 2, 2026, from 2pm to 5 pm at 4 Father's Pub, 1724 Merrill Ave, Wausau, WI 54401.
